name: Ananda - Organic farming plus yoga and meditation center
description:
Ananda Putta Bhumi is an integrated development project of Ananda Marga Poland, with a primary focus on agriculture, spiritual practices, yoga, and meditation. Our community currently includes two full-time monks, a Ukrainian refugee family consisting of a grandfather, parents, and two children, as well as a volunteer from Sweden. We also care for four cows, one calf, and two dogs.

Our focus is on growing organic produce. Daily activities vary depending on the planting season and may include tasks such as seedling preparation, planting, weeding, harvesting, and preserving the harvest. You may also find opportunities to explore cheese production, beekeeping, and other agricultural practices.

At Ananda Putta Bhumi, we adhere to a strict yogic sentient diet. Our meals vary in style, ranging from Indian, Polish, and Ukrainian to Asian cuisine, and are all vegan or vegetarian, prepared without onion, garlic, or mushrooms. Additionally, the farm maintains a drug- and smoke-free environment.

You are welcome to join our daily spiritual practices, including chanting and yoga sessions. Our farm is also ideally situated near a national park and a majestic mountain, offering excellent opportunities for hiking and adventure.

name: Diana - Organic arabica coffee farm
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
We are planting the arabica coffee by the seeds so you can join us to plant since the seeds still in the polybag to the land. And we are preparing all the organic fertilizer to support the soil to keep it not only fertile but healthy too. Instead we are planting the plants as the food forest such as banana, cassava, potato, chili, paddy field, and so on. 
Our farm beside the hills and the valley. You can do hiking by the hills and forest if you want. 
Inside the jungle there are hawk, wild boar, wild chicken and so many monkeys there. and you can feed the chickens and buffalos everyday. Beside that we have many dogs with us especially the border collie dog named Billy 🐶😁

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
1. Making the planting hole for the coffee 
2. Planting the coffee seeds
3. Making the organic fertilizer at our own jungle
4. Feeding the livestock at the farm such as chickens, buffalos, dogs, etc
5. Learning together with us to cook how to make the signature food of Batak Toba ethnic
6. Operating the tractor for the land clearing
7. You can explore the Toba Lake, and the Batak Toba community arround north Tapanuli.

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals:
Our accommodations are based in our farm. Its made of woods. There is kitchen and bathroom too. There is spring in the farm so the water gonna be enough for the whole family, worker and wwoofer's.

4. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?
The wwoofer's can join us to do the activities in the farm with our own workers. We hope that the wwoofer's can learn together with us how to build the organic farm. 

5. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.):
Holla its funny to play the
_wwoof_travel: One hour from the Silangit International Airport, near to the Siborong-borong bus stations.

name: Cornelis Herman de - Family farm
description:
We live on an old farm beside a small salmon river in Southern Lapland, Finland. we have some forests, grow our own vegetables and heat our house with fuel wood, fish in the rivers and lakes, and pick berries and mushrooms. Our vegetable garden uses no chemicals, only compost and leaves, we grow leaf vegetables, potatoes, root plants, and strawberries. We have 2 children, a cat, and in most summers, 8 or 10 sheep. We rent out 2 wood cabins for nature lovers, fishing enthusiasts and hunters.

Organic methods: no fertilizer exept dung and compost and ashes. forest thinnings and meadow cleaning for firewood. no insecticide or whatever chemicals. Chicken and cats partially fed with bycatch from fishing.

Certifications: am registered sheepholder in Finnish system

Current projects: meadow cleaning of bushes and trees.

re-constructing hay loft

forest thinning

fish traps in no ice period

gardening for vegetables and potatoes

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Between Ranua (32 km) and Simo (60 km), beside Simojoki river.

name: Jack - Alternative Art and Grow Project in the Mojave Desert
description:
The PROJECT

Our property, Villa Anita, is taking an inter-disciplinary approach to stewarding the land. We are artists, designers, dog-carers, builders, chefs, and farmers living in one of the hottest deserts in the world, trying to create a self-sustainable art site. We are exploring how we can influence the aesthetic of growing while finding ways to incorporate art, architecture, renewable energy, recycled waste products.

We are Jack and Carlo (couple and owners), and our nephew, Weston, who has joined the team for the last 2 years. We live here and will work alongside you everyday. Along the way, you will meet other guests and travelers that stay for nightly stays on the property as well.

What We Need From You
Our ideal WWOOFer looks like this:
*required-non cigarette smoker 
*required-loves dogs 
*required-healthy and strong 
.-wakes up early, self-contained 
– has a passion that he/she can enjoy on off hours
- wholesome, down-to-earth, non-convoluted
Bonus:-can work with power tools-has construction background-can plumb-can run electrical-can weld-has experience with renewable energy

Our work day looks like this:
We wake up at 7 a.m. and work together to make breakfast. We eat communal breakfast at 8 a.m. We work until lunch. Then we work again until an hour before sundown and then we take the dogs for a run in the desert, and sometimes finish the day by getting clean at the local hot springs’ resorts. Then, we cook and eat dinner together, and the rest of the evening is at your disposal. It’s a simple life set in a brilliantly beautiful backdrop. Working towards common creative goals is the order of the day. With your help, we are creating a haven for alternative design, where art, life and nature converge.

Projects within the PROJECT
1. Designing a long lasting desert foodscape.
2. Removing adobe soil in exchange for sandy loam.
3. Building shade structures to protect plants from the sun
4. Maintaining/Removing/Transplanting invasive bamboo roots
5. Buil

name: Heidi - Small organic sheep farm by the ocean
description:
Hi! 
I'm Heidi (35) and I live full time on the farm together with my husband (34) and our baby. My mother (59) who lives close by runs the farm with us. We are on our fifth year of living here. The farm house was originally built in the 1850-1880s and is located in a village that is traced back to the stone age. You can visit historical burial mounds and sacred sites from the bronze and iron age withing walking distance from the farm. 
We chose to move here to be close to the wild of nature, to keep heritage sheep and to preserve old wisdom of stewarding land as well as (slowly but surely) incorporating new, modern ways of thinking.
The farm is certified organic through Debio. We are enthusiastic about animal welfare and no-till farming, and work to incorporate elements of permaculture design. We would love to learn from you! 

We have 27 sheep, 10 ducks, and plan to get chickens again once we have built a new chicken coup. The animals live outside all year, but have access to the barn in winter.

Sheep meat and skins are for sale. We may slaughter on site for our own use, both sheep and birds. We forage berries and mushrooms in the forest, as well as herbs. Local hunters hunt deer for us so we are basically sustained on meat. In the spring and summer we fish in the sea.

The nature and weather conditions can be rough, but it definitely makes you feel alive! Eagles, foxes,wild birds, deer, seals, otters can all be spotted regularly.
If you brave the cold you are welcome to take a dip from our beach, or enjoy hikes along the coast or in the mountains that surround us. The beach is also a popular spot for wind surfing.

Woofing has been important experiences for me personally and I would love to be able to give back to this community as a host and hopefully keep learning together. We will try to accomodate your wishes when it comes to the work you would like to do, however this is very dependent on the season so please show understanding for that.

Potential tasks:
_wwoof_travel: Florø airport -->Florø bus and ferry stop --> ferry to Villevik kai.

name: Justus - We are a small 2 acre ashram based on Vedic tradition.
description:
We are Justus(husband) and Nicole(wife)  and Ravindra(2 year old son), a family working to cultivate a small ashram and healing center amongst the redwoods.  We are located in Albion, CA, located about 5 minutes from the Pacific Coast Highway and about 20 minutes south of Mendocino, about 2 hours from Santa Rosa.  We are working on clearing the land and creating a clean and beautiful space for people to come find solace.

Farming activities include construction of raised beds from local materials, building greenhouses.  This includes landscaping, burning brush, various construction projects (decks, 10x12 units, composting toilets, etc.), stacking/chopping wood, splitting rounds, etc.

There is an onsite cabin with bathroom and outdoor shower attached.  Food will be simple and Ayurvedic in nature (porridges, kitchari, simple curries, etc)

Justus is an Ayurvedic practitioner and Nicole has a specialty in trauma release,  We both sing and play music.  We both enjoy cooking and hiking.  Nearby there are ocean bluffs on which to hike, beaches to enjoy, forests to walk through.

name: Cristian - Finca orgánica
description:
Hola Soy Cristian Solarte con mí esposa vivimos en una finca orgánica, producimos alimentos para nuestra familia y criamos nuestros dos hijos dé una forma sana y en un ambiente natural y libre de químicos, tenemos tres perros y veinte gallinas y muchos cultivos.

Generalmente hacemos mantenimiento a los cultivos de aguacate hass y café, también deshierbar las huertas y hacer mantenimiento a la carrera, abonar los cultivos aplicar sustratos, microorganismos y hacemos alimento para nuestras aves, recolección de café y aguacate y transformar el aguacate en aceite, también hacemos algunas construcciones en guadua y tierra.

Estamos abiertos a compartir las comidas y llegar a un acuerdo personal, la habitación es independiente con una hermosa vista a las montañas, el baño es compartido con la familia.

Me gusta cuidar los cultivos, hacer música, tocar varios instrumentos, escribir poesía, en la finca hay un hermoso bosque para caminar y nadar en un riachuelo, caminatas por zonas rurales visitar otras experiencias similares de amigos y amigas.

name: Ali Haydar - Doğada Organik bal ve keçi çiftliği
description:
Hello WWOOF volunteers,

My name is Ali Haydar, I am 43 years old, and I live in a highland area in Türkiye. After spending some time living abroad, including in Canada, I decided to return to my ancestral land to build a simple, natural, and meaningful life.

Here, I run a small-scale, traditional farm focused on natural and organic practices. I work with honeybees and produce natural bee products. I also take care of goats (including young kids), chickens, and grow seasonal crops on my land.

My animals are raised in a natural environment — goats graze freely every day in the highlands, and everything is done with respect for nature and traditional methods.

This is a quiet, rural lifestyle away from the city. Life here is simple, sometimes physically demanding, but deeply connected to nature.

I live here myself, and you will be sharing this environment with me and the animals on the farm.

If you are interested in experiencing real village life, learning about beekeeping, animal care, and natural food production, you are welcome.

Looking forward to meeting you.

On my farm, daily life is based on simple, natural routines connected to the land and animals.

Volunteers will have the opportunity to learn about traditional village life, animal care, and small-scale natural farming. This includes working with goats, feeding and caring for young kids, collecting eggs and taking care of chickens, and supporting basic beekeeping activities depending on the season.

A typical day usually starts in the morning with feeding the animals and checking their water. Goats are taken out to graze in the highlands, which is an important part of the daily routine. During the day, there may be tasks such as cleaning animal areas, helping with simple farm maintenance, preparing food, or assisting with household tasks.

Beekeeping activities vary depending on the time of year, but volunteers may observe or help with basic tasks when appropriate.

Work is usually around 4–5 hours per

name: Michael - Eu estou transformando uma área degradada em uma agrofloresta.
description:
Eu sou Michael Stojek e eu moro no interior do Amazonas. A minha cidade, Novo Airão, fica ao lado do Parque Nacional de Anavilhanas e esta se tornando um ponto turístico. O meu projeto consiste em criar uma agrofloresta no meu terreno e reflorestar seletivamente as espécies nativas que foram extraídas nos últimos anos. A parte disso estou juntando o lixo da beira dos rios para reciclar esse lixo adequadamente. 
Na minha casa tem 3 cachorros que alegram a minha vida.

As atividades agrícolas consistem basicamente em preparar a terra para o plantio, limpar o terreno e colher os frutos.
Mas as atividades variam bastante devido ao clima e a época do ano.
A parte das atividades agrícolas também tem atividades para melhorar a moradia e para fazer a casa mais eco-amigável (coleta de agua de chuvas, energia renovável, etc...)
No tempo livre gosto bastante passear na beira dos rios e juntar lixo, pois é uma das melhores formas para ver animais silvestres e para relaxar.
Essa atividade considero como hobby e quem quer participar seja bem vindo...

A alimentação seria conforme as pessoas que estão comigo, pois comemos todos juntos e gostaria que preparamos a comida juntos. 
Teria uma refeição fria (o café da manhã) e duas refeições quentes (o almoço e o jantar).

Eu gosto bastante de música e fica muito difícil definir um só estilo de música que eu gosto. No meu tempo livre também gosto muito passear pela natureza e praticar esporte.
_wwoof_travel: O aeroporto mais próximo é de Manaus e tem carros de lotação que levam as pessoas diretamente para a minha localização.

name: Anshul - 60-year-old Renerative-Organic-Agroecological farm in Auroville (south-east coast of India).
description:
AuroOrchard is a community farm based in Auroville, on the southeast coast of Tamil Nadu. Auroville is an international township founded in 1968, created as a living experiment in human unity and as a place of unending education and progress.

AuroOrchard began in 1968 with the vision of “growing food for Auroville. We steward around 25 acres and contribute to the community’s food system through fruits, vegetables, and eggs. We practice natural-farming and keep refining our systems with agroecological and regenerative methods to develop a healthy ecological, social and resilient community food culture.

Volunteers join us to learn by doing—through real farm work, shared routines, and simple living in a multicultural environment on an intense production farm. We welcome people of goodwill who are interested in re-discovering their relationship with the land, with food and themselves. We aim for clear communication, respect for the farm's rhythm, mutual care, and a grounded exchange that nourishes both the volunteers and the land.

We need help in the vegetable gardens and orchards to maintain the plantations, work on raised beds, weeding, mulching, pruning, harvesting, sorting, planting and so on. Depending on the interest and commitment from the volunteers, they could get involved in designing new plantations, working on documenting different strategies.
The goal is that people help the farm but at the same time use the time to develop themselves on the farm. We are open for personal research projects that align with the work and values of the farm.

We have simple and clean dormitory style accommodations available for volunteers. They have common bathrooms and kitchen.
Breakfast is cooked for all volunteers of the farm and volunteers are expected to cook their lunch and dinner themselves in the community kitchen. The ingredients can be sourced from the farm.

Cultural exchange is part of everyday farm life. We work in Auroville, an international township in South I

name: Marc - Smallholding, tree nursery, livestock farm, croft
description:
This Farm has lots of interesting and innovative  projects on the go through out the year. This is a journey of upcycling, recycling, and general sustainability throughout the farm. We have Sheep(not ours, but soon will have Shetland Sheep, Chickens, Ducks,  to keep us busy plus, we focus on a very wide range of  vegetables that are grown in a seaweed based soil. using raised beds
We will always make you welcome
Projects for April is the straining posts for the 350 trees coming in May
May is tree planting
June is prepping for goats, pigs
July is more solar panel, wind turbine construction with tyres,pebbles, pallets etc
August is digging for victory, and hopefully making fences.
September will be a finishing off, and tidy declutter of the recycling field.
October is planting winter crops.

Thanks for visiting our profile.

Here is a general link on the Shetland Islands
https://www.shetland.org/
https://www.shetland.org/blog/the-great-shetland-bucket-list

https://www.shetland.org/blog/the-great-shetland-bucket-list-part-iii

If you like walking,great food, excellent water, views, peace, and fresh air then Papa Stour Paradise Island could be a great stay.

Organic methods: Everthing is grown natural, organic, and free range, with a view to excellent soil mineral improvement through Seaweed, and Stinging Nettles.

Certifications: Everything Organic, natural, we do not, and will never use chemicals.

Current projects: Tyre Walls with old fence wire, and local pebbles above high tide.
Tyre tracks!! Tyre roofs.

We now have 2 ponies on Forewick 

South facing geothermal wall for the Barn 

New Forest on the way, prepping, and trials

Building Chicken Tractors

Seaweed fed Sheep enclosure.

Roofs for two old Croft houses for shelter, human and animals.

Fencing and Alternative fencing upcycling

Raised beds

Greenhouse construction, and tyre methods.

Wall building dry stone. This training is done at the Walls Croft. Mainland

Producing Seaweed fertilizer and then sprayin

name: Carolyn - A small, certified organic and Real Organic Project–certified vegetable farm in southeast Minnesota.
description:
We are a small, certified organic and Real Organic Project–certified diversified vegetable farm in the Driftless region of southeast Minnesota, growing everything we sell on less than one acre, including three high tunnels and a Deep Winter Greenhouse built in partnership with the University of Minnesota. We prioritize minimal tillage, natural pest management, and biodiversity through native hedgerows and prairie plantings, and we are steadily increasing cover crops while reducing plastics. Our produce is sold through farmers markets, an online store with home delivery, a local grocery store, a food hub, and several restaurants.

My name is Carrie, I'm 40 years old and manage the farm alongside my mid-20s co-manager, who has been with me for three seasons, with help from a small part-time seasonal crew of one to three people. My mother, a retired teacher, spends most of her week at the farm during the growing season helping with planting, harvesting, and market work. Although my husband has a full-time job, he pitches in when he can, especially on weekends. Our two-and-a-half-year-old son is usually at daycare during the workday but loves farm life and helps out in his own way. We are expecting another baby in mid-March! We also have two rescue dogs who, although initially shy around new people, quickly warm up. They spend most of their days lounging, exploring the farm, or occasionally chasing rabbits out of the fields.
Farming is a second career for me—I came to it without a formal background, but with a long-standing passion for growing food, spending time outdoors, and cooking with unique, seasonal ingredients. In 2018, when my husband transitioned to a remote job, I left a ten-year career with the federal government to pursue organic vegetable farming. I have learned through hands-on experience, books, podcasts, webinars, field days, and trainings such as the Land Stewardship Project’s Farm Beginnings Program and online masterclasses, and I continue to expand m
_wwoof_travel: La Crosse, WI; Rochester, MN; Winona, MN

name: Virginia - One-acre urban community garden in the hills of Southern Dallas
description:
Innovate Water-Saving Agriculture & Community Living — Dallas, TX
Enjoy the outdoors in the hills of Southern Dallas on our 2+ acre property. This is a pilot site for nutrient recycling and community farming, combining urban agriculture, water-saving innovation, and alternative building. Our goal is to create a model of “futuristic living in existing structures” — and we need motivated, self-driven volunteers to help build it.

🔧 Current Tasks & Projects
Hydroponics & aquaponics lab – setup, testing, and water-quality analysis

Irrigation & plumbing – laying lines, water-saving systems, filtration, cistern building

Soil building & composting – Bokashi, wood chips, fermentation, and microbial inoculants

Fermentation – turning organic waste into plant-available nutrients (Terra Boost™)

Wood cutting & splitting – fuel and small construction needs

Beekeeping – help with pollinator care and habitat

Tiny house construction – finishing carpentry, fittings, trim

Gardening & food forest – raised beds, planting, care, and harvesting

Alternative building projects – greywater recycling, compost toilets, biofilters, rocket stoves

🏡 Accommodation
Tiny house (under construction and almost finished—help finish it and stay in it!) 

Or bring your own camper

Outdoor shower & composting toilet  

Tent/camping space and rustic outdoor kitchen

Washing machine available; indoor shower sometimes available

Suburban woods with owls, hawks, big cedars, oaks, and a creek trail

🕑 Schedule
4–5 hours/day, 5–6 days per week (including Saturdays)

Work is outdoors in Texas heat & winter cold — boots required

Flexible shifts: mornings preferred, but can split into shorter morning + evening blocks

Evenings are for lighter tasks (lab, indoor, finishing work)

🌍 Who We’re Looking For
Visionary interns, students, or post-grads in sustainability, agriculture, biology, or building

Hands-on, self-motivated, and community-oriented individuals

Comfortable with rustic living, teamwork, and in
_wwoof_travel: DFW 20 miles Love Field 15 miles Bus Station 10 miles

name: Vílzeth - Huerta de vegetales, cultivo de aguacates🥑 y aceite
description:
Located in a beautiful valley in the heart of the Peruvian Andes, we are running an ever-expanding organic farming project at an altitude of 1700m. with a warm climate and a 100'000m2 land (currently focusing on the growing and harvesting of avocados).

Please let us know or use Google Translate (translator) if you don't speak Spanish !!

Some of what we offer include a warm, welcoming community of people to learn and work with and a chance to live in an incredible, remote and peaceful location in the Andes Mountains (see photos!).Tucked high up in the mountains and away from the rest of the world, wake up every morning to remarkable mountain views, the sounds of birds and fresh food. There are many things to do in the region, plenty of free avocados and a chance to experience an unseen side of Peru while living an alternative, sustainable, natural lifestyle removed from society, with a great group of people. 

My name is Vilzeth, and I am the founder of the organic farming project. I am 44 years old and am an easy going person that loves to laugh, play chess, explore the mountains, write and read. I have a strong vision for this project and a keen interest in modern entrepreneurship, organic farming and sustainable living. I am looking to begin new enterprises using the farm as a base including a new project for camping in the mountains, a small local organic restaurant and lodge, and producing other types of avocado based products. 

Currently, Coco, our Volunteer Manager is managing the online/volunteer component of the project (English/Spanish, lo que quieras!) so all contact will be with her, unless you prefer to have direct contact with Vilzeth. Although she is not at the farm right now, she knows how it all works as she stayed at Vilzeth's place for some time. Feel free to ask her any questions you might have, she knows the details of the project and will relay all the information to me. 

I live on a large property in the Andes Mountains (1 hr from Celendin)

name: Julie - Organic farm where we grow farm-to-table vegetables for our restaurant and raise animals.
description:
Welcome to our organic farm, Bio-Benin! I'm Julie, a Canadian from Montreal, and my Beninese partner Guillaume and I run our farm in the northern hilly region of Benin, West Africa. We also have a farm-to-table restaurant and a craft brewery, where we make our own gluten-free beer from sorghum. We have two dogs and a large farmhouse to accommodate Wwoofers.

The morning chores involve providing food and water to our rabbits, guinea fowl, chickens and ducks. We then collect greens to feed the rabbits and take a break for morning coffee and a game of chess or Scrabble. Work on the farm each day will vary according to the season - cutting grass, hoeing land, planting seeds or seedlings, harvesting and sometimes delivering produce to the restaurant. Soon we hope to add a fishpond so that will be an additional learning experience. We're also planning a medicinal herb garden and would love to get you involved in planting it.

Both Guillaume and I are good cooks and we like to eat well! A full breakfast is served at the farmhouse, mostly vegetarian but meat is available. Lunch is take-out from the restaurant, delivered to the farm. Dinner is eaten at the restaurant, probably the best one in town worthy of 5 stars! Housing at the farmhouse with two bedrooms can accommodate two couples or two men and two women, sharing a bathroom with running water, flush toilets and hot water for showers. I mention this because it's not always the case in this part of Africa.

We enjoy hiking or bicycling around the countryside. This is also excellent bird-watching country. Time permitting, we like to play chess or scrabble each morning and have plenty of books, also WiFi and Netflix. Swimming and riding are available nearby but for a fee. We think you will be happy spending time with us!
                                                                                                         ________________________
Je m'appelle Julie et je suis une canadienne de Montréal. Mon partenaire bé

name: Robert Kevin - Join our coastal hub: Build impact, learn freediving! Join our movement from the ground up.
description:
I am a multi-sport coach (basketball, boxing, freediving) and entrepreneur. My mother and I manage our 1-hectare farm daily. She is the heart of the land, working from 6 AM to 5 PM, while I balance the coaching and the "Life Hub" vision. We are joined by Princess, our resident dog.
The Project & Location
Our farm is a "blank canvas" 15 minutes from our Blade Freedivers HQ at the Butong Baywalk. We also have synergistic sites in Pansol and Mindoro. We are in the "Foundational Phase"—building a self-sufficient ecosystem that will eventually house a specialized restaurant and co-working spaces for wellness entrepreneurs.

Life here is active and tactile. Common tasks include:
• Orchard Care: Watering and harvesting eggplants, papayas, mangoes, bananas, guyabano, and avocados.
• Herb Craft: Gathering leaves for our various natural teas.
• Building: Working our existing vermicast pit, starting mushroom cultivation, and literal "ground-up" construction projects.
• Digital Growth: We need help marketing our freediving school and "Life Hub" vision. If you have media/editing skills, you’ll help us turn our raw athletic and farm content into a compelling story.

The Stay: A-Frame & Coastal Synergy
You’ll be based in our A-frame cottage, a breezy, communal space that sleeps up to 10 people (dorm-style). For projects centered at our freediving headquarters, we also have accommodations by the beach. Both locations are built for creators, featuring high-speed internet and plenty of flexible workspaces (fold-out tables and shared areas) for your architectural or digital projects.
The Food: Building the "Life Hub" Kitchen
Nutrition is the next frontier for our project. While we are still developing our formal "Life Hub" meal systems, your experience will be grounded in authentic local living:
• The Source: We harvest directly from our 1ha orchard (mango, papaya, avocado, guyabano) and supplement with fresh finds from the local Taal market.
• The Cuisine: We serve traditional Filipi

name: Jacqueline - Vineyard; olive grove; forest, fruits and flowers
description:
Olá Wwoofer, welcome at our beautiful small estate in rural and central Portugal!
We, my husband Leendert, dog Yara and I, Jacqueline  are located at the outskirts of a quaint little village in the Ribatejo Area. We have been running  this agricultural farm now for almost 5 years.  We like to receive friends and family at our place and now we are ready to receive new friends from around the world. We would love to share our experience and adventure of organic produce growing in Portugal and would be very happy with eager minds and well working hands.

Our Vineyard ( 1 ha) does require the most attention and work.
All year round there is something going on. Like pruning, fertilising, mowing, maintenance, harvesting, and in the fall getting ready for the WinterRest. 
The Olive grove, the forest, the flower garden and the fruits do require care as well like watering, mesting, pruning and off course the harvesting.
Our goal is to create and maintain large biodiversity and apply optimum care for the environment. " Minimal intervention and maximum attention"

A  normal day can look like this:

Outdoor Yoga practice in the morning (besides a farmer, I am also a yoga teacher)
Healthy and hearty breakfast 
Working on the land, activity depends on the season and your interest and capacity
Enjoying lunch together on our panoramic terrace
Siesta; swimming (we are so lucky to have a large salt water pool) reading: relaxing in the hammock in the forest or just do nothing!
Maybe a few more hours on the land or assisting in preparing for dinner.
After dinner, enjoy the evening by star gazing or other preferred activities
Noble Silence!

You Wwoofer, will have a nice and comfortable guesthouse at your disposal.
The guesthouse has a spacious bedroom for 2 people and a private bathroom.
I like everything clean and cozy so our guests will feel at home.
From the guesthouse you can dive directly in the pool. How Cool!

For breakfast I offer home made yoghurt with fruit and self made gran

name: John - Organic produce farm producing all vegetables in season, specializing in carrots and watermelons..
description:
Since the late '90's, I have dabbled at organic farming and animal husbandry, though I never felt confident about attempting to  become a full-time farmer until meeting Laura in 2019.   We both had always dreamed of living in a way more like our grandparents did, growing our own food, being generally resourceful as a lifestyle.  Both of us had been looking for someone to share that vision with.    Our strengths complimented each other, and we had the land available here in this sandy, fertile river valley, so we naturally started taking advantage of the opportunity. 
     In some ways, the COVID pandemic offered us an ideal chance to undertake our first season raising pigs, chickens, and growing vegetables on about ten acres.  The farmers' markets were shut down, so we concentrated on our farm stand, an old Ashland gas station that was in desperate need of repair.   In 2022 we finally finished work on a commercial kitchen there, which allowed us to market value-added products like lacto-fermented sauerkraut, pickles, and salsa.  We also set up a butcher shop, so we can make our own hickory-smoked bacon, ham, and country sausage for retail.  
     In 2023 we became certified organic, and started hosting WWOOFers.  The first two years, our housing accommodations were limited to camping on the farm, or utilizing one of our guest bedrooms.  Over the winter of '24-25, we built a cabin on the north side of our propagation greenhouse, so our guests can retreat to their own private, air-conditioned space without fending off the bugs and storms.
     After six seasons growing together, we can almost say that many of the activities are a tradition by now.   The season starts in November, when we plant garlic that will be harvested the following July.  On December 26 we plant tomatoes under grow lights beside the wood stove in our basement.   About the first week of February we move the tomatoes to a heated table in our propagation greenhouse.   Around that same time, we start
_wwoof_travel: Bloomington for Greyhound.  Louisville for plane.

name: Patrick - Petite ferme, avec petits élevages, moutons, volailles, pigeons, cailles, porcs, jardin potager,
description:
Bonjour, je m'appelle Patrick,
J'ai une petite ferme au pied du Moyen Atlas, au Maroc, dans la région de FES.
Il y a un petit élevage familial, et un grand potager. Tout est absolument bio. 
Aziz et Rahma s'occupent de gérer tout ce petit monde, volailles, moutons, chèvres, cochons et ont l'habitude de recevoir des Woofers, (c'était avant le Covid ) 
Je n'ai pas créé cet environnement dans un but lucratif, mais pour faire profiter à ma famille et mes amis de produits sains et non contaminés par des pesticides.

Les woofers que nous avons reçus avant le Covid, ont tous pratiquement quitté la ferme les larmes aux yeux, tellement Aziz et Rahma les ont bien reçus.
Le travail qu'ils ont fournis c'est moins de deux heures par jour, aide à la nourriture des animaux, entretien du potager etc...

Rahma prépare de délicieux repas, et les hôtes sont logés dans des chambres confortables, salle de bain, toilettes, eau froide et chaude.
Rahma communique volontiers ses recettes de plats marocains, peut apprendre à faire le pain et les fromages de chèvre frais.

Il y a de belles ballades à faire dans les environs , les paysages sont superbes et c'est une région du Maroc qui n'est pas encore polluée par le tourisme. Les gens sont authentiques et très chaleureux. Nous sommes en pays berbère.

name: Jose Geraldo - Family farm
Verena - Permakulturhof
description:
Wir benötigen ab sofort Hilfe! Am besten längerfristig mit Chancen auf Projekteinstieg. Wenn Du dir das vorstellen kannst, schreibe das doch gerne mit in die Nachricht!

LAGE:

Unsere Solidarische Landwirtschaft befindet sich in Mammendorf - einem Dorf, das zwischen Augsburg und München gelegen ist. Der Hof selbst befindet sich im Ort und der Acker, auf dem wir das Gemüse anbauen rund 1,5 km davon entfernt vor dem Ortseingang.

BESCHREIBUNG:

Als Solawi am Donihof haben wir 2016 begonnen, einen kleinbäuerlichen Hof in Mammendorf (zwischen Augsburg und München) nach den Prinzipien der Solidarischen Landwirtschaft wieder zu beleben. Nach den Anfängen des Anbaus im Garten des Hofs, bepflanzen wir mittlerweile eine gepachtete Ackerfläche von insgesamt 3,5 Hektar, was der Fläche von knapp fünf Fussballfeldern entspricht. Mit dem Erntejahr 2019 sind wir somit zu einer Gruppe aus rund 50 Ernteanteilen gewachsen, wobei ein Ernteanteil etwa ausreichend für zwei bis drei Personen ist.

Die Produktion von ökologischem Gemüse, Obst und Kräutern wird gemeinschaftlich organisiert und finanziert – auch Risiken werden zusammen getragen. Grundsätzliche Entscheidungen treffen wir gemeinsam auf regelmässigen Mitgliederversammlungen. Wöchentlich versorgt uns das Hof-Team mit der Ernte, die bedarfsgerecht und solidarisch aufgeteilt wird.

Wir sind mit unserem Projekt auf einem guten Weg, aus unserem Acker ein landwirtschaftlich genutztes und trotzdem ökologisch wertvolles und lebendiges Biotop mit Obstbäumen, Wildhecken und Blühlfächen zu formen. Als nachhaltige und sinnvolle Unterstützung der Handarbeit und Alternative zum Traktor werden auf dem Acker auch unsere Großesel mitarbeiten.

Organic methods: Mulchen, Permakultur, Eseln, Terra Preta ... 

Certifications: -

Type of work: Je nach Jahreszeit sind wir hauptsächlich mit den unterschiedlichen Schritten des Gemüseanbaus für die Solidarische Landwirtschaft beschäftigt. Das beinhaltet die Planung des Erntejahrs, das Vorber

name: Kimbee - Smallholding, Aquaponics/Bioponics farm, Farm
description:
Bethany Farm is a family farm in Ipoh, Perak. Surrounded by trees, and nestled a little ways off the main roadways, it is a small oasis that’s still close to all that Ipoh has to offer. We are a young farm, with our main business being free range chickens, but we are building and planting the property with vegetables, herbs, fruit trees, and fish ponds. Our chilis are spicy, our bougainvillea are colourful, our durian is the talk of the town, and we’re planting more! This is a great time to get involved.

Some projects we’re working on:

	Developing vegetable beds	Creating an aquaponic system	Grafting fruit trees	Starting a greenhouse nursery	Constructing a kampung-style chalet	Carving out jungle treks	Building bee hivesBut of course, we’d also love to hear about you and your expertise or experiences. Because we are still young and in a phase of research and development, we are thankful for ideas from the outside world. This is one of the main reasons we signed up for WWOOF. So tell us what projects you might like to work on and we’ll show you what we’ve been up to so far. Your contributions will be a part of the place for years to come! 

 

Some things to note:

We have 9 dogs on the farm who roam freely with us while we work. They are friendly, but as our watchdogs they are also territorial. Because of this, we can’t accept WWOOFers with pets, and it is something to note for those with a fear of dogs. They won’t bother you, but we understand if dogs give you pause.

And speaking of animals. Our forest is home to the wild boar! Encountering one would surely be an experience… They are out trekking at sunrise and sunset so keep your wits about you especially during those times, but also know that our trusty watchdogs will protect you. 

Near the farm are a few tourist attractions you can explore. Lost World Hot Springs and Night Park, The Haven, Dong Hua Cave Temple, and Tambun Sai Baba Cave Temple are all within walking distance. Plus a variety of sma

name: Olga - Hospedaje rural y agroecológia
description:
¡Ven a Tekoa! Tradición, orquídeas y el mejor yogur artesanal de Arcabuco 🌸🥛
Arcabuco, Boyacá, Colombia
¡Te esperamos con impaciencia!
¿Buscas una inmersión auténtica en la cultura campesina colombiana? ¡En Tekoa estamos ansiosos por conocerte! Somos una finca familiar agroecológica (yo, mi esposo y nuestra hija) de 3 hectáreas situada a 2580 metros de altura, en el municipio de Arcabuco, rodeado de montañas y aire puro y la calidez de nuestro hogar te harán sentir parte de la familia desde el primer día.
En nuestra finca, la vida fluye entre el cuidado de 4 hermosas vacas lecheras (Limousine y Normande), 60 gallinas felices y una huerta diversa con más de 15 especies de hortalizas y frutales, y también contamos con perritos y gatitos. Además, somos guardianes de la naturaleza: tenemos un área de restauración de bosque nativo dedicada exclusivamente a la preservación de orquídeas. Todas esas experiencias se reúnen en nuestro hospedaje rural.
¡Estamos deseando compartir nuestros saberes contigo!

Tus tareas y aprendizajes: ¡Manos a la obra!
¡En Tekoa siempre hay algo emocionante por hacer!:
•	Magia en la cocina: Ayúdanos en la transformación de lácteos para crear nuestro delicioso yogur griego, quesos y mermeladas artesanales.
•	Secretos de la tierra: Aprende sobre alelopatía en la huerta y descubre cómo funciona nuestra "biofábrica" de abonos orgánicos y biopreparados.
•	Conexión animal: Participa en el cuidado de nuestras vacas y gallinas.
•	Exploración y naturaleza: Ayúdanos a mantener los senderos y participa en los procesos de reforestación.
•	Cultura Boyacense: ¡Te enseñaremos a preparar los tradicionales amasijos y lo mejor de la cocina local!

Tu hogar en las montañas
Te recibiremos en una habitación muy acogedora al lado de nuestra casa familiar, equipada con una cama doble, un camarote y sábanas de algodón muy suaves. Tendrás tu propio baño completo y disfrutarás de una alimentación básica campesina (omnívora) con productos fresquitos de la finca.

Yo estoy
_wwoof_travel: Centro urbano Tunja o paradero intermunicipal Arcabuco, Boyacá

name: Jurgen - Organic mountain guest farm and wilderness hiking trails
description:
Way back in 1999, Jurgen arrived in this remote valley and started to create a down to earth, off-grid and eco-friendly 2000ha mountain retreat. A unique hideaway to rest, relax and recharge ones weary soul away from all the tech stuff of modern living.

This secluded valley is habitat to countless medicinal plants and abundant natural wildlife with small rock pools, a hiking trail network of over 50km, guided outdoor activities such as: canyoning, wilderness trekking, rock climbing and abseiling.
The goal is to become as self sufficient as possible, living healthy off the land, harnessing nature's sustainable energies for electricity and hot water.

Situated in the Langeberg (Long Mountain), 50km West of Montagu in the Western Cape of South Africa, a 3 hours drive from Cape Town. Here, life begins at the end of the road!

WWOOFers are invited to experience a remote, eco-friendly, sustainable and organic way of life and share their dreams, experience, culture and knowledge.
_wwoof_travel: The nearest bus stop is Ashton ±60km from Simonskloof.

name: Bruno - Alternative co-operative community, Family home and garden - urban, Educational Centre
description:
O sitio recebe voluntários para o desenvolvimento de vivencias agroecológicas na área : apicultura ,( atividades de colheita de mel, manutenção de apiário) , atividades de agroflorestais( , ajudar na restauração de solo, plantio de mudas , produção de mudas ,manutenção do viveiro,) mão de obra pra oficinas de cosmetologia , ajudar na colheita de alimentos , ajudar no desenvolvimento de desidratados (linha de produção) , entre outras atividades de Permacultura(bio construção , na realização da construção de chale ecologico, banheiro seco, casa de reza , oficina de bamboo e pu a pique! e de lazer horas vagas conhecer a regiao com muitos atrativos naturais (cachoeira, lago, represa..) Juquitiba e região são lugares Magicos e místicos , temos uma rede agroecológica do vale do ribeira, estrangeiro seja bem vindo trazendo sua energia, pra nós ajudar na restauração da mata atlântica, agregamos  propriedades too em Embu Guaçu 

Muito prazer temos um terreno Ecopedagogico em Embu Guaçu, Zona Sudoeste da Grande São Paulo, onde 100% de seu território está inserido em Área de Proteção de Mananciais 💦 

Buscamos conservar e regenerar os 16hectares de terra através de processos naturais e agroecológicos, na qual os hóspedes podem experienciar vivencias que os anfitriões co-criam 🌱

Dentro da Ecovila, temos uma confortável Casa de Fazenda que locamos inteira ou quartos individuais e compartilhados para finais de semana ou Gardenoffice 🏡 

Para conhecer um pouco mais desse projeto socioambiental convidamos a nos seguir pelas redes sociais e experiênciar uma vida mais próximas Natureza  🌳 

“Sonho q se sonha só é só um sonho, q se sonha junto vira realidade” Que possamos nos encontrar nesse paraíso verde 

Organic methods: biodinamico , agroflorestal , sintropico

Certifications: organico agroflorestal

Current projects: medicinas botanicas , agroflorestas, apicultura , meliponicultura, desidratados ,cosmetologia , gastronomia, bio construir 

Internet access available

name: Emiliano - Homestead, Farm and plant nursery
description:
Join Us as a WWOOF Volunteer on Isla Solarte

We’re a small organic farm on Isla Solarte, reachable only by boat from Bocas Town ($10–$16 round trip). If you’re interested in sustainable agriculture, simple living, and learning practical skills, we’d love to hear from you. We ask for at least a two-week stay to ensure you get a solid experience and we can plan projects effectively. Please send your WWOOF application and complete the volunteer form on our website.

What You Give:

Volunteers contribute approximately 25 hours per week helping with farm work plus general household tasks, including washing dishes, setting up meals, and maintaining cleanliness and organization.

What You Get:

 -In exchange for your time and effort, we provide:

✅ Shared hardwood cabin with a bed and mosquito net
✅ Two balanced, home-cooked meals per day (note: diets including animal products only)
✅ Unlimited coffee, tea, and drinking water
✅ Wi-Fi access 
✅ Hot showers.
✅ Use of jungle trails, dock, and swimming area

What to Bring;

🔦 Headlamp (rechargeable recommended)
🦟 Bug spray.
☀️ Sunscreen.
🥾 Sturdy, closed-toe shoes 
👕 Clothes that can get dirty
🌧️ A rain jacket.
🛁 A lightweight, quick-dry towel.

Current Volunteer Opportunities:

🐓 General Farm Help – Assist with planting, harvesting, composting, soil preparation, aquaponics, feeding animals, trail maintenance, weeding, and mulching.
🛠️ Carpentry & Trades – Help with construction, repairs, plumbing, and electrical work.
🌳 Arborist – Chainsaw experience needed for tree maintenance and branch cutting.
🪴 Plant Nursery & Green Thumb Work – Assist with seed planting, propagation, pruning, grafting, organizing plants, and preparing orders.
🐕 Dog training - We have 4 young dogs at the farm that are ongoing training. If you have experience in this field or are interested in learning, please let us know.

Important Notes:

⚠️ Remote Location – Isla Solarte has no roads, shops, bars, or restaurants; access is only by boat.
🌿 Jungle Envi
_wwoof_travel: No walk-ins are allowed. Please arrange transportation directly with the Host.

name: Juan - Hobby Farm
description:
Granja Algarrobo espera ser un camping agroecologico, donde los visitantes puedan disfrutar zonas comunes durante el dia y tambien poder alojar en nuestras instalaciones para disfrutar los cielos estrellados y luminosos que nos da el Desierto de Atacama. Actualmente estamos plantando arboles nativos y frutales con tierra preparada in situ.

Certifications: Estudiante del curso de Agroecologia Holistica de la Doctora Anita Muñoz

Internet access available

name: Zachary - 5-acre field school campus surrounded by 23,000 acres of wilderness near Glacier National Park
description:
Welcome to Southeast Alaska! We are located in the heart of the Tongass National Forest at the doorstep of Glacier Bay National Park.  We purchased our property (a 5-acre fishermen's homestead surrounded by 23,000 acres of wilderness) in January 2018 and we're now converting it into a field school campus.  

We are currently looking to ramp up our “homesteading”, sustainability, and education capabilities; expanding gardens and planting fruit trees, reviving the forge, building soil, an outdoor kitchen, and much more!  We are searching for WWOOFers to help with this transformation and can host year-round.

Work this spring/summer/fall includes, but is not limited to:

CONSTRUCTION: wood shed/shop, 4-bin composter, smokehouse, floating greenhouse/sauna, composting toilet modifications, dock/grid improvements.

GARDENING: new garden design & construction, implementing hugelkultur/permaculture practices, seed planning/acquisition/saving/starting, integrating compost tea irrigation system, planting fruit trees and edible perennials, improving composting systems (humanure, fish emulsion, seaweeds, kitchen/garden scraps, etc.), maintaining worm bins.

FOOD SECURITY: harvesting from both wild (seaweeds, berries, mushrooms, etc.) and domestic (garden & greenhouse veggies, flowers, fruit trees, etc.) plants and preserving them through canning/dehydrating/smoking/salting/pickling/fermenting/freezing/etc., baking everything we can onsite (bread, bagels, english muffins, rolls, tortillas, crackers, scones, muffins, etc. etc.), harvesting and preserving local proteins (salmon, halibut, deer, etc.), poultry (duck & chicken) care, and basically ANYTHING that lessens our dependency on “outside” goods.

FIREWOOD, FIREWOOD, FIREWOOD: using a chainsaw to put up (fall, limb, buck, transport, split, and stack) firewood, use a chainsaw mill to turn trees into lumber for various construction projects.
**One of our missions is to be fossil fuel free as soon as possible. Due to

name: Noel - Farm
description:
Building communities capacities, increasing chances for international exchanges and prioritizing communities self-discovered ways of developments are the backbones of true african development. 
The Farm has a vision of bringing young and adults from all backgrounds together to identify key paths to reducing poverty. 
GF has a variety of activities from dairy products such as milk to maize cultivation as well as school gardening. 
We are also breeding sheep, goats, guinea fowl and pigs. 100% bio, all the livestock are fed on natural elements grown in the farm. 
The Farm is now aiming at implementing officially in the next couple of months an agricultural vocational training center for rural communities; 
If you have some time to spend, then you are invited to come to GF, we highly appreciate your time and value your presence in our farm. 

Renforcer les capacités des communautés, accroître les chances d’échanges internationaux et prioritiser les solutions internes de développement communautaire sont les socles pour un développement durable. 
Goshen Farm a pour objectif de rassembler des jeunes et des adultes de tous les horizons pour identifier les principaux moyens de réduire la pauvreté.
GF mène diverses activités, allant de la production laitière, de la culture, de l'éducation et de l'élevage diversifié. 
100% bio, tous les animaux sont nourris avec des éléments naturels cultivés dans la ferme.
GF vise maintenant à mettre en place officiellement dans les prochains mois un centre de formation professionnelle agricole pour les communautés rurales; 
Si vous avez du temps à consacrer, alors vous êtes invités à venir chez GF, nous apprécions hautement votre temps et apprécions votre présence dans notre ferme. 

Organic methods: We use animal feces for fertilizers 

Current projects: Set up agricultural vocational training center in the farm.

name: Raymonde - Plantation arboricole
description:
Nous sommes Raymonde et Bastien et en 2015, après avoir passé plusieurs années à pêcher, Bastien a décidé de monter un projet agricole sur un grand terrain à Haume. Aujourd'hui, nous avons des cocotiers, des citronniers, des pamplemoussiers, et beaucoup, beaucoup d'autres arbres.

En ce moment, nous avons besoin d'aide pour la récolte et l'entretien des terrains. Nous vous montrerons comment nous avons organisé nos terrains et quels techniques de plantation nous avons développé, certaines nous ayant demandées des années de travail. Les journées varient selon le travail mais nous préférons nous focaliser sur une tache pendant plusieurs jours afin de ne pas s’éparpiller. Vous serez surtout avec Bastien, qui s'occupe le plus souvent des terrains.

Il ne faut pas prendre l'avion pour venir faire du WWOOFing. Notre projet s'adresse plutôt à des woofers qui arriveront en bâteau.

Pour les woofers, nous aimerions compter sur des personnes indépendantes. Nous avons une maison à A'akapa que nous mettrons à votre entière disposition. Elle est spacieuse et tranquille, et la vue vous donnera envie d'y boire votre café pendant plusieurs heures. Nous vous accompagnerons pour le travail sur Haume mais les matins et les soirs, vous serez dans la maison de A'akapa. Des personnes autonomes, qui ne fument pas, qui ne picolent pas....

Quand nous ne sommes pas occupés, nous aimons passer des moments en famille autour d'un bon repas. Pour les randonnées, plusieurs sont possibles depuis A'akapa. Aussi, la plage est à 5 minutes de marche et si vous voulez aller pécher, nous avons tout ce qu'il faut pour que vous alliez chercher vous-mêmes votre poisson.

name: Randy - Organic farming in mountains, chicken ranch for egg production, food production in certified kitchen
description:
Hello my name is Randy.  I live in Sapello, New Mexico.  I have a permamnent working family that lives  on the property.  My sister lives on the property and is my secretary and worker when needed.  we have been here since 2013.  We have three border collies Elroy, Jane and Judy.

I own a free range chicken ranch that are for egg production.  We feed, water them and gather eggs three times per day.  We also water and feed the cows, horses and sheep.  Egg washing and packing are also done from 12 to 4.  We also have an elephant garlic garden that is in full run in the summer we use the garlic scapes for making pesto and eggs for pickling.  All of this will be part of learning including working the kitchen for tamales and tortillas which also go to Farmers Market.  We have 2 freight farms that are planned to go up soon and those are hydroponic  for organic vegetables.

I have just developed a new RV Park and the housing is for Woofers.   They are stocked will all bedding, restroom items' and basic kitchen needs.  Food will be provided once an arrangment of likings is complete.

I like walking the property and enjoying the animals.    I like reading, watching tv and news. 
My sister likes beading, embroidery and crocheting.
_wwoof_travel: Las Vegas, New Mexico for train or bus and Albuquerque, New Mexico for airport

name: Bianca - Organic ocean-front fruit, veggie and herbal farm wit ornamental gardens nestled in a boutique hotel
description:
Our organic farm and gardens are located in an ocean front boutique hotel in the Dominican Republic's north coast. We receive guests who retreat, relax and enjoy a nourishing lifestyle. Our spa and restaurants benefit from the organic produce we grow in our organic garden.

We grow herbs and salads, vegetables and tropical starches like yuca and plantain as well as fruits. We are looking for somebody that is a team leader, has deep knowledge of tropical plants and is already experienced with our daily activities which include: organizing the organic residues generated from the restaurants, creating compost, fertilizing and making sure the plants are healthy, aiding the plants that aren't looking healthy, watering sustainably, trimming sensitively, building natural structures for plants to climb on and maintaining the ornamental gardens looking beautiful. Aside from the edible garden, the hotel is surrounded by nature which also needs to be maintained. If you consider yourself a team leader and a visionary, please apply.

We have a beautiful and recently renovated private bedroom with private bathroom and A/C. Three meals a day are provided.

I enjoy surfing in the walking distance nearby beaches, stand-up paddling straight out from our property when the wind is low, swimming, mountain biking and taking part in the yoga classes and temple activities offered at our hotel.

Nuestro huerto orgánico esta ubicado frente al mar en la costa norte de la República Dominicana. Siendo parte de un hotel boutique con dos restaurantes y un spa, las hierbas, frutas y vegetales que cultivamos en el huerto dan vida a las creaciones culinarias y relajantes que ofrecemos a nuestros huéspedes. Necesitamos a alguien con conocimiento en plantas tropicales, experiencia en manejar huertas y jardines, con visión y liderazgo. Aparte del huerto comestible, el area del hotel está rodeada de verdes jardines ornamentales.

name: Erasmo - Huerta familiar dónde producimos bayas y verduras libre de pesticidas.
description:
Somos una familia que cree que es posible hacer agricultura sostenible y vivir dignamente de ella en condiciones de la sierra peruana. Nos gusta la abrobiodiversidad,  en base a ello nos enfocamos en producir alimentos de calidad , poniéndo énfasis en la inocuidad de los mismos. Para ello contamos con dos espacios: Una ubicado a 2200 msnm, donde producimos hortalizas y zarzamoras, lúcumas, chirimoyas, guayabas, etc. en este espacio se ubica nuestra vivienda (tres personas) , la de mis padres  y la vivienda de los huéspedes (para cuatro personas), tenemos amplias áreas verdes, en las que viven libremente, 4 perros, 2 alpacas, dos cabras. La otra, ubicado a 2800 msnm, donde hacemos agricultura de alta montaña, en la cual cultivamos maíces, tarwi, sauco, zarzamoras y tenemos bosques de protección.

- Manejo de vivero.
- Producción de tomates en invernadero.
- Producción intensiva de hortalizas (lechugas bbs, zuchini, berenjena, espinaca, arúgula, cebolla, coles)
-Preparación de sustratos para plantines.
-Manejo integrado de Plagas.
-Mantenimiento de sistemas de riego.
-Buenas Prácticas Agrícolas.
-Buenas Prácticas de manufactura en hortalizas.

7 horas de trabajo al día, con 2 horas de descanso para el almuerzo (12-2 pm) 5 días a la semana, participando en diversas actividades de la finca de acuerdo al plan de entrenamiento (o interés del voluntario) previamente concertado.

4. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.): Nos gusta cocinar, escuchar música diversa, hacer caminatas en las montañas, como hobby, me gusta la construcción sostenible!!! y el paisajismo.

name: Lucio Valentin - Farm organica, hacemos agro-turismo desde una pequeña empresa familiar.
description:
Es una Finca en Coronel Moldes, hacemos actividades agricolas de Temporada (Tabaco, Maiz, Sorgo...) y también criamos Vacas para la venta, además criamos y hacemos Queso de Cabra y tenemos Caballos que usamos.
Vendemos alojamiento en la casona de la finca y en cabañas. 
Complementamos los Servicios con Cabalgatas guiadas y con un Museo del Tabaco.
En la Finca vivimos Carlos y Valentina, y en sus casas las familias de los hijos Lucio, Julian y Josefina. Somos una familia que comparte mucho tiempo así que pueden encontrarse los nietos o alguien siempre dando vueltas.

Podrías aprender a mover un cerco eléctrico, sencillo, suplantando a quien debe hacerlo todos los días. Tambien ayudarías a alimentar a las cabras en el corral
En temporadas puedes recoger frutas o verduras y aprender a preparar abonos, fertilizantes e insecticidas orgánicos. 
No precisamente, pero entre Julio/Septiembre podrías ayudar amamantando cabritos con suplemento.

Te quedarias en una habitacion para ti, dependiendo la epoca podrias compartir con otros wwoffers pero no es lo comun, y tendras una heladera y una pequeña cocina para ti donde puedes desayunar.
Almorzarias con Lucio o Julian la mayoria de los dias, y puedes llevarte para cenar entre semana. Los fines de Semana Comemos casi toda la familia por lo menos una vez juntos, y estas siempre bienvenido, aunque si no estamos o nos vamos te dejariamos para cocinar.

Estamos a solo 1 colectivo de la Ciudad de Salta, a 60km. Tambien puedes ir en 1 bus a Cafayate. 
Tenemos a solo 3km un Lago, y a 7km de un pequeño lago. Tambien cerca puedes caminar por caminos entre fincas en un pueblo muy tranquilo. Ademas puedes conocer toda la finca como quieras.

name: Nick - Agritourism Destination
description:
On the farm we have 1 owner/visitor cottage, an open plan kitchen and deck shared by all. Tents and bathrooms. Free range and penned chickens and 5 bee hives. There are bikes and a beautiful beach about 3 miles from the Farm. It is a very organized and well maintained Neem tree farm with a wide variety of tropical fruit and flowering trees and above ground veggie and herb gardens.

 

Organic methods: A variety of health, beauty, pet and agriculture Neem products. And honey.

A variety of Caribbean fruits

Certifications: Quality Certification Services in Gainesville Florida, USA

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: 13 miles south of Marsh Harbour airport

name: Anis - A Centennial Olive Grove Thriving on Dry Farming Since 1907
description:
I'm  Anis a 5th generation olive farmer. 
Passionate about agriculture, heritage and slow way of life we welcome curious, respectful WHOOFers from around the world who are engaged to learn and live simply in harmony with the land.
At our centennial olive grove you will experience dry farming, a traditional and sustainable method where olive trees grow without irrigation, relying solely on natural rainfall. this technique not only conserves water but also encourages strong, deep roots and high-quality fruit. 
We also use organic composting, natural weed control, and seasonal pruning to maintain soil health and biodiversity.
We inherited the way of taking care of this century olive grove and we would like to share these inheritage and know how with you.
Our farm is located in the olive forest of the region  of Sfax.
We are a dedicated  to excellence.

Daily life on the grove
Harvest olives ( you will be joining a lovely team )
Preparing natural compost 
Trim the olive trees ( will teach you how to )
Sorting and labeling olive oil bottles
Cooking with local produce
Classify the archives

You will be hosted in to our guesthouse, the food will be cooked by locals and served 3 times a day

You will have the opportunity to experience the full silent, the land is flat you can run, walk. 
We have a fly yoga, a hammock and a dedicated place to meditated

We believe sustainable agriculture is not just a method , Since 1905 we have been maintaining ethical and sustainable ways  growing olives. it respects nature's rhythm, values quality over quantity and leaves space for future generations to grow. We hope to share our knowledge and keep this way of life alive.
_wwoof_travel: Airport Sfax  or  Airport Tunis Carthage

name: Virginia - Family home and garden - rural
description:
Soy Virginia y vivo cerca de la ciudad, pero tenemos una casa solar, autónoma de la red eléctrica de la ciudad. Estamos en el medio del bosque, pero a 15 minutos caminando del centro de la ciudad. 

Estamos tratando de tener nuestra propia comida de las hordas orgánicas. Y nuestra propia energía, con cocinas solares, calentadores y electricidad. ¡Estamos aprendiendo cómo vivir de manera saludable, sostenible y enjugable!

Estamos construyendo un invernadero y un taller de barro solar. Necesitamos ayuda para eso y para el tiempo ... para estos tiempos. ¡Estamos muy contentos de reenviarte y compartir lo que sabemos aquí!

Organic methods: Usamos macerado de ajo, aceite de lavanda, jabon blanco

Certifications: No certifico

Current projects: Los proyectos actuales son una nueva plantación de frambuesas y frutillas.
Y la construccion de un nuevo modelo de cocina solar. Hay trabajo de mantenimiento de las construcciones de barro y de los jardines.
Siempre muy variado y sin demasiado esfuerzo físico.

Internet access available

name: Sarwar - It is a multipurpose Agro & Fish Farm located in Feni. 135 KM From Dhaka towards Southeast
description:
It is a multipurpose Agro & Fish Farm located in Feni. 135 KM From Dhaka towards Southeast. The Bay of Bengal is only 48 km from the Feni.

Cattle farming and aquaculture.

Living with host family withing the farm.

Cooking and fishing, hiking

name: Rick - Southeast Alaska remote island homestead near Juneau
description:
We purchased 4 acres of beachfront, old growth, Tongass rain forest on a remote island near Juneau, Alaska in 1987.  We saved, built, and labored until we could finally move onto our dream land permanently in 1995. Our journey is what many of our WWOOF volunteers describe as their own dream also. When you visit, you will be helping us plant seeds to grow our gardens.  On a deeper level we hope to help you plant seeds to grow your dreams.  

We were formerly a remote fishing lodge. Now we are retired and grow and wild harvest for our living. Karen is Admiral of all she surveys. She is the green thumb, a Master Gardener and is looking forward to working with you in her green houses, nestled in her environmentally friendly garden beds. She will share her disdain for slugs and encourage conservation in all things. Rick is Captain of the boat, chief builder, and enjoys making music, sharing good books, cooking, and playing on the tractor. We are open-minded and thrive on the satisfaction of a good day’s work. We welcome volunteers who are interested in sharing and learning the values of living, and making a living, in harmony with the earth.

 We value the ideals of permaculture and sustainability and attempt to employ them in our daily lives. We have operational solar and wind power as well as a diesel generator. We have catchment for water and tend to our own waste disposal. I would say we are off the grid, but it has been so long (30 years) --what is a grid anyway?

Skill building can include self-sufficient living practices, renewable energy, gardening, growing and working with herbs, tincture and tea making, building construction, firewood gathering, composting, outdoor survival, fishing, small boat handling, chickens and understanding the nature of nature.

We invite you to share in our tasks and rewards. Depending on current projects our workdays are about 5 hours. The rewards include good, healthy food and a cozy little cabin to call your own. The work can be phy
_wwoof_travel: Juneau Airport, Juneau Alaska Marine Highway terminal at Auke Bay

name: Odile - Organic farm close to Quito
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
It is a small farm where we started to cultivate in permaculture we try to have diversity of vegetables, and fruits. For the moment we don't have animals  (accept dogs and cat) but we plane to have some in the future.
For the moment I live alone with a gardener who comes 2 or 3 days a week
I am conected with people of the village trying to share services

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
The woofers will help in weeding, the place who is already cultivated, and new place to  prepare the land, prepare fertilizer (like bocashi, compost...), prune trees, water plants if needed and participate sometimes in cooking,

3. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?
The working day of the woofers is starting at 7h45 (after a breakfast independent) , at 10h30 there is a break of 15mn with a snack and work till 13h00. We have lunch together and in the afternoon the woofer can do what he want (hiking, resting, or doing something in the land if he wish). In the evening he prepare his own meal (I provide basic food to cook, and you can find extra food like more vegetables and fruits in the village))



4. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.):
_wwoof_travel: Airport at 30 minutes by car.    Bus stop at 20 min walking

name: Enrica - Splendida masseria vitivinicola con ampio agrumeto e uliveto
description:
Ciao, mi chiamo Enrica, ho 41 anni e assime al mio ragazzo Marcello e la mia famiglia, gestisco una masseria meravigliosa che produce vino, olio e arance.
Siamo la terza generazione a produrre qui in Basilicata e siamo orgogliosi di continuare un progetto iniziato con i miei nonni basato sul rispetto della natura e della condivisione. Nonostante la bellezza di questa collina che ogni giorno mi offre la vista meravigliosa dei vigneti su mare e mi appaga per il lungo lavoro, 
in passato ho sofferto moltissimo l'isolamento rurale e non è stato facile per me vedere tutti i miei amici partire alla ricerca di un lavoro migliore verso regioni più ricche. Ho deciso così di restare, di non partire ma di lavorare sodo per trasformare questo posto nella masseria più accogliente del mondo, o almeno ci provo,  e di poter ricevere e conoscere persone che come me credono ancora fortemente nella natura, nei rimedi naturali, nello scambio, nella condivisione autentica di conoscenze.
Fino ad ora questo scambio culturale è stato vitale, per questo dopo aver ospitato volontari per quindici anni con altri portali ho deciso di aderire anche al woof, per offrire le mie competenze specifiche in materia di sostenibilità e produzione di vino anche qui e avere un numero sempre più alto e diversificato di aiuto. Con molti dei volontari che abbiamo ospitato è rimasto un rapporto di amicizia e affetto, alcuni di loro tornano a trovarci, nonostante la distanza a volte davvero importante, questo mi fa capire che siamo sulla strada giusta.
Il mio primo complice è sicuramente Marcello, ingegnere informatico che vive con me e mi aiuta ogni giorno nella gestione della masseria, poi c'è la mia mamma Anna, ex insegnante di inglese che vive a duecento metri da noi e ogni tanto passa a trovarmi con una buona focaccia, e poi ci sono le mie sorelle, Isabella, agronoma e mamma di Giuseppe e Pasquale, il suo compagno Francesco , e Teresa, avvocato, che torna con la sua famiglia solo nel periodo estivo perché
_wwoof_travel: Stazione di Metaponto/ aeroporto di Bari-Palese

name: Annabelle - Nubian goat farm surrounded by Saguaro cactus🌵 in Valley of the Sun, AZ
description:
We will not be accepting WWOOFing applycations after April 1st. 

Due to health issues,  the ranch will be closing soon, perhaps during the Spring or before the summer begins.

This has been a hard but necessary decision.

It has been an awesome experience. 

Thanks!

Annabelle
_wwoof_travel: Greyhound, Eloy, AZ and Phoenix Sky Harbor International

name: Ha - My farm is nearby Đà Nẵng city and Hội An city.
description:
I’m Ha. I was born and raised in a poor village in the middle of central Vietnam and as a child, I dreamt that one day I could make a change within my village and help my local people to live better lives. After graduating from university in Vietnam, I spent more than 5 years living and studying abroad in places such as Israel, Singapore and Denmark. I worked as an agricultural and tourism volunteer in more than 20 countries around the world and at the end of 2021, I returned to my village and started an organic farming and education project.

The mission of this project:
- Create an environment for cultural exchange between international volunteers, Vietnamese interns and the local Vietnamese community through sharing cuisine, cultural practices, and language.
- Provide the village children with free access to English classes; allowing them to learn another language, understand different cultural perspectives, push themselves outside of their comfort zones, build confidence, and develop themselves so that one day, they too can make a change to their hometown.
- Preserve traditional agricultural methods, practices, and values by inviting children to visit the farm for a day and experience what it’s like to be a farmer.
- Share our knowledge of agriculture and agricultural tourism with the local community and demonstrate how these can help people escape poverty, sustainably.

Jobs you can help with: taking care of fruit trees in the garden, keeping the lawn green, creating biodiversity in the garden. Taking care of pets. Use animal feces to raise earthworms. You can learn how to take care of livestock and how to utilize its manure to feed earthworms, then use the earthworm manure to fertilize the garden, harvest fruit in the garden during the harvest season, then learn how to Preparing and soaking traditional wine. There are also activities to help children in the village learn English and help local people with farm work

You will stay in the dorm. With fan, sleepin

name: Elin arneberg - Hobby  farm. Horse breed. Horsemanship. Vegitables, herbs, berries.  Firewood produktion.
description:
Would you like to expirience  life on a small farm north of  the polar circle? 

Our little farm has in total 17 hectar land, mainly forest and moorland, only about  3 hectar hilly meadow/ grassfields. We are logging in the woods, making firewood. For self sufficiency we grow potatos and vegetables and herbs in a small scale kitchen garden. We also grow a variety of berries,  have some fruit trees.  If the time allows, we pick wild berries, mushrooms and herbs. 
Growing vegetables this  far north is challenging. Fishing and livestock is a  natural and important part of farming and sustainable living due to our climate and soil and fish, seafood, meat, cheese and other milkproducts are a natural part of our nutrition.  A vegan diet will not be possible with us.  An ovo- lacto- pesco- vegetarian   diet may be possible during summer, but during winter it is too difficult for us.  
We are minor breader of the very old and rear north norwegian horse bread  "nordlands/lyngs horse" which is FAQ listed as extinction threatened.  It is very versatile, has a calm and gentle nature and is in size and exteriour similar to the icelandic horse. If  there will be an opportunity for volontiers to ride horses is depending on how long you are staying,  your riding skills, you choosing to put effort into  building a relationship with the horse,  our work load atvthe farm and spare time for following up and making sure you are able to safe and kind riding and horse handling. It is off course also depending on the current  status of mares/foals and, due to beeing small horses, also your body size.  We will unfortunately not have the time to give beginners an opportunity riding horses. Prior expirience with horses is preferable, but not abslutely nessesary to stay with us during the summer.  If you would like to join us during the winter though, prior horse expirience is needed and prior riding scills would be preferably.
There is always a lot to do at the farm and the work is physically

name: Isobelle - Experience Flower Farming and Nearby Wonders
description:
Situated in the rolling hills above Homer beyond the Wynn Nature Center, at 1,500 feet above sea level. Our primary activity this upcoming season includes taking care of a 1/2 acre peony field with 4 high tunnels of other flowers/plants, both annual and perennial. Typical activities include composting, weeding, and keeping our plants healthy. We also have an organic sustenance vegetable garden, egg laying chickens, and honey bees. 

We are looking for 2 or more WWOOFers, couples welcome, but no pets or children, April through end of September, three weeks plus, participating in a flexible schedule of 25 hours a week.

Accommodations consist of a group campsite. WWOOF options include (a) 2018 Keystone Hideout multi-bed camper trailer; (b) 1968 Frolic Travel Trailer; or (c) Cabela's Alaskan Guide Model Geodesic 8-Person Tent that includes cot, sleeping pad, and sleeping bag per person with option to bring own equipment, if desired. Host is in a separate trailer and has two English Springer Spaniel dogs. Outhouse bathroom on site. Camp shower available (NEMO Helio portable pressure shower in privacy shelter). Laundry is off-site. Shared potluck family style meals. Frequent transportation to Homer provided. There are also opportunities to visit other farms.

This area is a highly sought after tourist destination for wilderness hiking, kayaking, canoeing, ocean and river fishing and beach walking. Many nearby attractions! Including: the Anchor River, and Homer, with many local beach and forest trails, Kachemak Bay State Park, Kenai National Wildlife Refuge, and within a half day, Kenai Fjords National Park, Seward and Exit Glacier. Transportation options available.

Certifications: Because we are in a remote area of Alaska, it is difficult and expensive to be certified. We try to be as organic as possible, practicing permaculture and sustainable methods whenever feasible. We are conscientious and intentional to steward land under our care.

We welcome all visitors who ar

name: Dada - A yoga and meditation ashram  and small organic farm in the countryside west of Copenhagen
description:
We are a community of people practicing Ananda Marga meditation and yoga. We run an ashram and retreat center on a 13 hectare organic farm near Vig with a small orchard and garden and two mid-sized polytunnels. We have an organic bakery which delivers vegan and gluten free products to our shops in Copenhagen and Rørvig. The core of our project is providing our guests, retreat participants and volunteers with an experience in yogic lifestyle focusing on spirituality, community and sustainability. Our vision is to be a self-reliant regional hub for culture, education and spirituality. 

We are four monks (from Canada, India, Germany and Norway), four long term volunteers (from the Netherlands and Denmark), four bakers and one driver (from Poland, Greece, Romania, Egypt and India). We normally have two to six short term volunteers and a few guests, however, during retreats and Erasmus programs,  we may have up to 35 additional participants.  As a community we do chanting and meditation together in the mornings and evenings and we provide our guests and volunteers with the opportunity to learn a personal yoga and meditation routine. If you already have your own practice you are welcome to do that.

Currently we are only active in the garden, the orchard and the polytunnels. The rest of the land is registered as “Climate Grass” which we cut once a year. We focus on growing things that are fairly easy to grow and which we also like to eat. 

None of us are expert gardeners or working full time in the garden but we have been lucky up till now to get volunteers with some skill who have stayed with us for quite a while. Our ability to teach people really depends on whether or not we have a skilled volunteer who is playing the role of team leader. One of our past volunteers prepared a really good manual which describes our whole growing season in detail with good advice about the what, the how and the when, nevertheless, if you are a raw beginner you will be a bit lost unless
_wwoof_travel: Bus: Sneglerupvej, Train: Vig,  Airport: Copenhagen

name: Nicola - Ecofarm Park- Education/ Tourism/ Agriculture
description:
Hello Wwoofers, my name is Nicola. My husband  Dario and I are the owners of Rio Muchacho Organic Farm  on the coast of Ecuador. The farm is a social enterprise dedicated to teaching about sustainable agriculture. It weaves Agriculture, education and agroecotourism
Crops are grown year round, but change according to the seasons, dry and wet. The dry season is  cooler and perfect for cool climate vegetables while the wet season is hotter and good for tropical vegetables. The farm has a sustainability trail with 20 information stations, and lots of other cultural activities (like crafts, and chocolate making ) that visitors take part in.
