Wwoofers usually get to do many of these activities while there. The farm can accommodate around 40 people.

Wwoofers would be involved in animal care, agriculture  (and education if they chose to and have the language skills)
The activities vary from day to day including seed planting / transplanting/ weeding/ irrigating/ compost making/ soil preparation/ harvesting.

A cook makes tasty vegetarian food ( with as much supplied by the farm as possible)
Meal times are 7.30am 12pm and 6pm
Accommodation is in one of our creatively build bamboo and wood cabins.

The farm is 20 mins from the beach, where you can enjoy the warm tropical water and surf. There are surf boards for hire, great restaurants  and places to stay on the weekends. The are trips you can do to wetlands  and mangroves.
At the farm there is a sustainability library, internet,  a shallow river for cooling off in,  great nature and wildlife and lots of hammocks!

name: Trish - A small mixed vegetable farm, no-till, human & bicycle powered
description:
Hello! My name is Trish, my partner, Jeremy and I live at and manage this farm - a small, mixed vegetable farm in Spearfish Valley. We have a super sweet dog named Miga. We raise a variety of vegetables, herbs and flowers, and fruits primarily for a CSA, as well as for an on-farm farm stand, and a local natural foods store. In previous years we've pastured lambs, and typically have chickens and ducks (for meat and eggs), but this year we are taking a break from poultry and reassessing feed supplies and our sales & marketing.  The farm is 3 acres, with about 1 acre in vegetables and 1 acre in fruit trees/pasture. We work mostly by hand and make deliveries by bicycle.  We began farming on this property in 2012.

WWOOFers will engage in seasonal tasks ranging from soil blocking and seeding for spring and summer successions, weeding and bed prep throughout the growing season, seed collection and cleaning in the fall/winter. There are also daily chores, CSA and market harvests. A typical day for a WWOOFer would likely be breakfast together, then out to the field or greenhouse for the morning, lunch together and then the afternoon off to rest or explore Spearfish and the Northern Black Hills on your own.  Weather here can be unpredictable and our schedule of daily to-dos varies a lot with the forecast.

We have a small guest house available (full bed, bathroom with shower, kitchenette (toaster oven & electric kettle, no stove)). WWOOFers are welcome to join us for meal prep and feasting, breakfast, lunch and dinner. We eat seasonal meals, primarily (but not strictly) vegetarian. Otherwise, we are close to two natural food stores, a larger grocery store, and folks are welcome to use our kitchen too.

Jeremy is a bird watcher and volunteers at the local community bicycle collective, he's also interested in woodworking and insects, and learning more about electronic beat making. I like making block prints and clothes and am learning more about water coloring. We both have tr
_wwoof_travel: Greyhound stop in Spearfish; airports in Gillette, WY and Rapid City, SD (both ~1hr away)

name: Laura - Family farm and ranch in rural Arizona near Zion National Park specializing in agritourism.
description:
Hello!  We are the Lounsbury family: husband and wife Wesley and Laura and our four children plus two cats and two dogs.  Our kids range in age from 13-21 and we are expecting our first grandbaby in September!  We have lived on our working farm for the past 14 years in rural northern Arizona near Zion National Park.    We have been raising milk goats and chickens for egg production/consumption for 12 years.   Apart from our numerous milk goats (and their babies) and chickens, we have numerous animals used as pets in our farm tours including two Nigerian dwarf goats (Scoobie & Bella), one horse (Sundance) and three bunnies (Asher, Flopsy and Mopsy).   In addition to the animals we have multiple gardens and a goji orchard as well as a year round greenhouse.  We have also been Airbnb hosts for 8 years.  

Typical activities include weeding, feeding and water the animals, goat milking, planting and harvesting in season, food preservation or preparation, raking and weed cleanup, mowing and weed whipping, fencing repair, building projects like animal shelters, and even an earthbag greenhouse we are working on.   If no outside work is necessary during a given day then chores may include dishes and light house cleaning, laundry, breadmaking, etc. We are also in need of website building and social media marketing if you have those skills.   The schedule and activities would depend on the time of year that we are hosting.   In the hottest months (July-August) getting up early for chores is essential because of the heat of the day in the desert.     Expect to be up by 6:30 and working no later than 8:00.   Around 11:00 it's typically too hot to work so wwoofers would have the hottest parts of the day to themselves and be inside.    Around 5 or 6:00 PM work can resume and last until 8 or 9.    So much of the time spent outside depends on the weather and temperature.   Animal chores are the most consistent thing taking approximately 2 hours per day, one hour in the morning and o
_wwoof_travel: Saint George Utah (1 hour) or Las Vegas, Nevada (3 hours)

name: Sergio - Family farm
description:
Hola!! Somos Sergio y Alice, recientemente hemos vendido nuestra finca y compramos otra propiedad cerca de la montaña, aquí esta todo por hacer! Debemos reformar las casas,  nuestro trabajo principal es la elaboración de productos lácteos, quesos, dulce de leche, yogurt, tambien hacemos nuestro propio vino y mermeladas de frutas,  tenemos una huerta con verduras para nuestro consumo, ademas tenemos, gallinas, abejas para la miel, árboles de fruta... Tenemos un salón donde recibimos a nuestros clientes donde vendemos nuestros productos. Nos gusta cocinar y compartir nuestro lugar con voluntarios que pueden hospedarse en nuestra casa, felices de mostrarle nuestro lugar y nuestra cultura ( nos gusta bailar folklore de nuestro pais) y por supuesto tambien aprender de ustedes. Nuestra granja se encuentra muy cerca del pueblo de San Javier , provincia de Córdoba.  
 Los esperamos!!
Pd: Recibimos voluntarios por un mínimo de 15 dias ya que creemos que es el tiempo necesario para conocernos y que la estadía sea productiva para ambos.

name:
Geneviève - Association.
 Une fermette privée
description:
Idéalement située entre campagne et mer, dans la vallée de la Canche, à 4 km de la ville historique de Montreuil sur Mer, et 12 km de la mer, notre ancienne fermette à colombage se trouve au centre du village, dans une zone de marais. 
Dans le cadre de notre projet de « retraités actifs », nous y avons créé un jardin pédagogique basé sur le maraichage et les fruits du verger. Il est agrémenté d’'une mare et d’un bosquet. L'environnement boisé et marécageux donne beaucoup du charme au lieu avec un maximum de biodiversité. Nous avons aussi aménagés 2 chambres d'hôtes avec tables d’hôtes autour des produits du jardin en les complétant par des produits bio de notre réseau local. Nous vous invitons à venir partager nos réflexions et nos pratiques, par une vie au quotidien en respect  avec la nature, en les mettant en pratique dans notre jardin et notre verger et  par l'aménagement de notre étang. 
Nos échanges nous permettront de compléter et d’'améliorer nos animations pour les enfants (pyramide de la biodiversité, apprentissage de la germination et de la pollinisation des fleurs, les habitants de la mare  et les insectes du potager…). Nous aimons partager et échanger sur la nature. Nous pensons que nous pouvons tous y apporter notre contribution dans le respect de la terre, des  plantes et  de ses habitants.
Nous parlons : 
 Français,
 anglais et
 espagnol.
Activités proposées : 
 Maraichage et
 cueillette.
Autre activités : Verger. Jardin pédagogique autour du jardinage et de la biodiversité (Agrément Accueil Paysan).
_wwoof_travel: Ville la plus proche avec gare : Etaples. Nous pouvons venir vous chercher à la gare.

name: Ralph & Norma - Close to a river  and road  have a large garden and 3 greenhouses.
description:
We live beside a river in central Yukon about 4 hours north of Whitehorse.  Our farm is a mixed farm with animals and hay land. We have a large garden, 3 greenhouses and animals, mainly chickens, to look after. There is a separate house for WWOOFrs to sleep in and a fifth wheel trailer. Cooking is done in main house, usually by the host.  

Working on our farm means working in the garden and the greenhouses beside feeding and watering the animals. We also take part on the Farmers Market in Mayo where we sell our goodies. After harvesting, we can process, preserving the vegetables together. 

Also we have a canoe and boat to use to go upriver. There are hiking trails near by as well as plenty of wildlife to see and will be taken sightseeing too. It's worth it to take a one day Trip to Keno City, the end of the road in the heart of the Yukon, another part of history to explore. We accept WWOOFErs from May through September. We are looking for motivated WWOOFer who are helping with the gardens and helping looking after the animals. Hope to hear from you soon.

name: Abraham - We're small farming group, we do Animal, Vegetables, Banana, and other organics farming
description:
Greetings from Nasih Farm
We grew vegetables and other organics farming, we also do animal farming. Currently, we have 45 pigs 25 ducks and 27 chickens our farm is manage by our permanent staff.  Our farm doses't work on Sunday or National Holidays, we are currently working to complete our buildings so we can be able to host many Woofing volunteers we only have two bedroom free at the moment, and we want to add it and make our lodging facilities more comfortable for our guest. 

Our place is highly secure and peaceful, we work and have fun time, we also have one dog name "Lion" we also have children on the farm for this we don't allow smoking, we're Christian base Religion group of individuals so we only accept people from religious background all the different faith are welcome to our farm. Kindly note that if you are to come before January 25, 2025, we will suggest that you to take a guest house very close to our farm, and we will be responsible for your transportation to the farm everyday only. The guest houses are affordable, and the reason for this is that we are still working on our guest apartments on the farm.  We sincerely apologize for this, but in any beginning, it's difficult to get everything at the same time. 

Thank you!

The Management
Nasih Farm
Liberia
_wwoof_travel: Division 31, Block Path, Benla Clan, Kakata, Margibi County

name: Elizabeth - Herbal Homestead and farm with a Cow, Horse, Chickens, Dogs
description:
We're a family living out in the country north of Billings, Montana. We have 21 acres we're homesteading on.
During the school year I work at the local school, but in summers we are working on growing organic herbs to sell locally and online. We are just getting started but I have a lot of experience from before I moved here, so is not new to me. I’ve had 10 plus years working with an herbalist and in an herb garden/business etc.
As far as living accommodations, you can tent camp on the property (lots of trees), or stay in a small camper (needs fix up as well, but is an option—there will be clean sheets and a bed).  There is a tiny house that will be available until we start renting it out.
Our focus for summer of ‘24 is getting our herbal spots started and set up. Each week, we will have different projects we’re working on, depending on our priority at the moment. There will be some days that we are baking for local farmers market, some days we build or fix on things for a day, some days we do clean up and a fire, and sometimes we’ll work on landscaping. We’re also working on crafts for farmers market to keep our farm going, but you are not required to help with that if you’d rather do garden/farm stuff. Chickens have to be fed/watered every morning, eggs gathered.
We will be building a small house or log cabin on the property hopefully soon, if you would like to help with that. Or, if you would like to do more gardening, canning, or animals, we will be doing that as well. We'll also be building a root cellar and pond/waterfall at some point down the road. This summer our building project is a gazebo/library out of pallets and used windows. We have a couple friends willing to help as well.
I've had experience in farming, homesteading etc. I worked in a greenhouse 5 years and am an herbalist. Come along if you want to help and enjoy the beauty out in the country. 
We are trying  to become totally organic but unfortunately will take some time before we are set up to

name: Naveen - We cultivate rice and fresh vegetables with care and respect for the land. Our cows are raised with
description:
We are a small farming family living in a quiet rural village surrounded by farmland and nature. Our project focuses on growing rice and seasonal vegetables using natural and sustainable farming methods, along with caring for cows as part of our daily farm life. The people living here are family members who work together on the farm and share meals and responsibilities. We have cows and other friendly farm animals, which are an important part of our home and farming system

Our daily farming activities include working in the rice fields, planting and harvesting seasonal vegetables, watering crops, and caring for our cows (feeding, cleaning, and milking). We follow natural and traditional farming methods and would like to teach WWOOFers about basic crop cultivation, soil preparation, composting, and animal care. A typical day usually starts early in the morning with farm work, followed by breakfast and lighter tasks during the day. Evenings are calmer, with animal care, discussions about farming life, and time to relax together

We provide comfortable, simple accommodations for WWOOFers, either in a dedicated guest room or a cozy shared space close to the farm. Meals are homemade using fresh vegetables from our farm and locally sourced ingredients. We follow a balanced and wholesome diet, and guests are welcome to join us in cooking or helping prepare meals. Breakfast, lunch, and dinner are included, and snacks and fresh water are always available.

In our free time, we enjoy cooking traditional meals, listening to music, and sharing stories around the farm. We also love gardening and experimenting with natural farming techniques. Nearby, there are plenty of opportunities to connect with nature, such as walking through rice fields, hiking in the surrounding countryside, and exploring local villages. It’s a peaceful place to relax, enjoy fresh air, and experience rural life up close

name: Rosana - Elevage de poules, potager et plantation d'AçaÏ, Macaxeira inspiré de la forme syntropique
description:
Bonjour les amis WWOOFeurs, je m'appelle Rosana et avec mon mari, Christian nous vivons au Para, Brésil. Je suis d'origine de cette région et après 30 ans passés en Suisse, j'y ai ramené mon époux. Nous exploitons une petite ferme, avec une trentaine de poules, un cheval trois chiens et 1 chat, tous domestiqués et très sympas!
Notre objectif est de reboiser notre terrain de 24 hectares, sur le principe de la syntropie et de vivre le plus possible de nos produits. 
Pour ce faire, nous avons planté 800 Açaï, 600 eucalyptus, une centaine d'autres arbres et de la macaxeira, ainsi que fait un potager.
Nous avons rénové la maison principale, fait une maison pour l'employé et sa famille ainsi que des dépendances.
Nous avons également construit trois chalets, pour faire maison d'hôtes.

Nos activités principales sont:
- S'occuper de l'irrigation et de l'entretien de son réseau
- S'occuper du potager
- Surveiller les plantations et au besoin, tailler et traiter de manière naturelle
- S'occuper et surveiller les poules et au besoin, traiter de manière naturelle
- Nettoyer le poulailler
- S'occuper du compostage

Notre nourriture est le plus souvent végane, mais nous faisons volontiers du poisson une fois par semaine (en fonction des prises de nos voisins pêcheurs) et de la viande ou volaille de temps en temps.
Généralement nous prenons un gros petit déjeuner, un copieux déjeuner en début voir milieu de l'après-midi et un encas le soir.

Nous aimons le kitesurf et les balades en kayak (nous pouvons partir, en fonction de la marée, depuis la mangrove en bas de notre terrain) pour atteindre des plages sauvages.
Rosana et passionnée de cuisine et diététique.
Les plages ne sont qu'à 16 km de la maison par la route.

Olá amigos WWOOFeiros,
Eu me chamo Rosana e, junto com meu marido, Christian, vivemos no Pará, Brasil. Sou originária dessa região e, após passar 30 anos na Suíça, trouxe meu esposo para cá. Administramos uma pequena fazenda com cerca de trinta galinhas, um cavalo, trê

name: Mikus - Summer farm for happy people!
description:
We are organic farm - beekeeping, gardening, woodworking, log building, foresting and we were keeping chickens, sheep and goats, maybe in future will keep again.

We were travelling a lot, specially in winter time, so this is summer farm and if you come over you are more then welcome, but dot not expect luxury :) Everything is simple and need some improvements :)

Organic methods: No chemicals!

Certifications: We are not using pesticides and we can organize this by our selves :)

Internet access available

Sundays always off!
_wwoof_travel: If you are coming by plain, then have to take 2 buses or I will pick you up at airport... anyway its not going to be a problem to meet and come to farm.

name: Godfrey - Sustainable, climate-resilient farm linked to an NGO, livestock farming
description:
Our farm is part of a non-govermental organisation with a mission to  empower the large number of people affected by malnutrition, HIV/AIDS, Genocide and poverty in the community.

On our farm we're growing several types of vegetables, fruits and herbes. We are also producing animal products from running a fish, a pig and a cow farm, as well a Black Soldier Fly Project. Typical activities for volunteers include planting, harvesting, weeding and other practical farming tasks. If you like, you can also help us in the Organization or with our Saturday Children Program. 

The employees at the farm are people from our Village of Hope, which helps them to generate income while also learning about sustainable cycles in agriculture. Some of our harvests are given to the people in the community and to a nearby school. 
While working with local people, you will maybe be able to learn some words of Kinyarwanda, our local language, and you will definitely get to know the life in the small villages of Nyanza, far away from the touristic hotspots in Rwanda.  

Besides sustainable farming and agriculture, our Organization runs a Program where we construct kitchen gardens, carry out capacity building trainings and provide laying hens and fruit trees to every household within the village while teaching them climate-resilient agricultural practices and saving skills or even arts craft.

Your accommodation will be at my (Godfrey's) house, near the city center of Nyanza. We are well prepared to host volunteers, providing four guest rooms with included bathroom. Typically, we are all eating breakfast and dinner together at home and take lunch with the team at work. If you get tired of Rwandan food after a while, there is also a small kitchen which you can feel free to use at any time. Apart from my wife Diane, my two sons (who might be in university/boarding school when you are here), and our two home helpers, we are regularly hosting longstay volunteers, for example from Germany, the U

name: Joni - 480 acre environmental education center nestled in unique canyons of the Sangre de Cristo Range
description: We are a 480 acre environmental education center nestled in unique canyons of the Sangre de Cristo Range and the beautiful Wahatoya Mountains, at an elevation of 8,000 feet. We are in a forest environment an hour away from any town. Trinidad is the local town for supplies. Earth Mountain's 40 acre base camp offers a wealth of learning opportunities, to local and national schools and organizations. We welcome you to come and be a part of the Earth Mountain family co- creating social renewal, healing the land, and ourselves!  We educate on the farm and in our local communities and outreach to create sustainable community development projects such as Community gardens/farms, co-ops, and school gardens. We are also a CSA(Community Supported Agriculture) and an Outdoor School which hosts a Permaculture Design Certification Course, and a variety of workshops. We have been a WWOOF host farm for over 10 years. We prefer full season volunteers but are open to hosting with a 2 month minimum stay. Our season runs from March- November. We ask for 5 to 6 hours a day with 2 days off a week. All chores are shared. Most meals are communally prepared in a pleasant outdoor kitchen. Special diets may need to supplement. Earth Mountain has humble off-grid solar electricity, limited phone reception and wifi ability, must provide your own hotspot through a phone service. Basecamp has a summer solar shower, from May to October. Otherwise it's the old fashion way of heating water on your woodstove. Accomodations vary from, tipis, tent camping, and small cabins. You own transportation is strongly encouraged.  We are looking for volunteers who share the passion for simple living, education, youth and community.  We raise chickens, have milk goats, and a horse.  We grow, raise, and hunt most of our food and wild craft medicine.We are currently 1 family with 2 boys, dedicated to Earth Mountain's work and cottage industry.  We seek to  build long-term community, and love short term exchange. Th

name: Matej - Educational Centre, Agritourism Destination, Intentional Community, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures
description:
We're an ecologically regenerative project based on permaculture near Banská Štiavnica, Slovakia. We are currently working on making a beautiful and welcoming environment for visitors and our animals. Though we’re in a relatively early phase we hope to create a space for agrotourism and self-sufficiency using silvo-pasturing and keyline design concepts. We currently have a cow, several sheep and goats, ducks and geese and a very friendly cat and border collie.

Current projects: Stone and wood construction, care of livestock, setting up water and road infrastructure. 

Our worldview
We realise certain fundamental truths: 
a) Humans don’t stand apart from nature; we’re part of an interconnected whole with complete dependence on the functioning of the planet’s life-supporting processes
b) Disrupting these life-supporting processes to the point where they can’t regenerate is self-terminating
c) Human survival (along with many other living beings) lies in us becoming a regenerative rather than destructive agent in the living system.
d) We become regenerative by applying our creativity through regenerative design principles, which results in a positive and meaningful engagement with life.
_____________________________________________________________________________
Our mission 
1. To engage in healthy and self-reliant living by acting as an ecologically regenerative, resilient, decentralised, local producer instead of a dependent consumer.
2. To practice and promote regenerative design thinking in
creating human habitats, food production, construction, economic & social organisation

Internet access available

name: Elisa - Building a self sufficient family owned land
description:
Hi, I’m Elisa 
We are a family owned small farm. I’m a mother of two young children, a birth doula, and a woman deeply connected to nature and the natural rhythms of life. I lived abroad for over 10 years, wwoofed many times and now I feel truly happy to be the one hosting travelers to learn, share, and grow together. Traveling has taught me so much, and I’m deeply grateful for those experiences.
We moved to our land almost 2 years ago. It was an abandoned land and it became our small self sufficient little farm. We just bought more land and we definitely need your hands to more light into this new project. We are very happy to welcome you to our little "Paradise land".

——-Volunteer Tasks——-

-> Farm & Outdoor Work
* Gardening and watering
* Tree planting and tree care
* Carrying materials and organizing spaces
* Chopping wood and clearing land
* Composting and soil preparation
* Building garden beds and outdoor structures
* Trail and land maintenance
* Harvesting fruits, herbs, and vegetables
* Weed management and general farm maintenance

-> Building & Maintenance
* Painting
* Sanding and wood restoration
* Basic carpentry
* Organizing tools and storage
* Small repairs around the property
* Helping with construction projects
* Fence fixing and outdoor maintenance

->Home & Family Support
This is also a family home, so some volunteers may help with:
* Cleaning and organizing
* Cooking or meal prep
* Babysitting and playing with children
* Creating a calm and supportive environment
* Helping maintain harmony in shared spaces

-> We value:
* Respect
* Communication
* Initiative
* Emotional maturity
* Flexibility
* Care for the environment
* Presence over perfection

->Accommodation:
- Bed + table to work in our Moroccan tent + bathroom
OR
- You can bring your own tent and set anywhere you feel like

->We offer:
- Access to the community kitchen + you can have any fruit /veggies available on the land;
Also you’re welcome to make yummy goodies and share meals with us
_wwoof_travel: Small town / 10 min from the closest bus stop

name: Jim - Eco Retreat
description:
TO THE GARDEN is a functioning centre for creative arts study and retreat and is essentialy an arts project incorporating and introducing the concept of personal life as a creative process. Our guiding pilosophy is one of individualism and self reliance and our activities attempt to explore the problems of self motivation and self employment as a means towards greater freedom of personal expression.

 We have, over the course of fifteen years been involved with the rehabilitation of five acres of disused farm land according to principles of dry land reclamation and have successfully employed Permaculture and methods developed by the Mediteranan Garden Society to establish a fertile ‘forest garden’ environment. 

Volunteers are invited to help establish, maintain and learn about: Rain water harvesting systems, windbreaks, composting, earthworks and organic gardens.

We try as far as possible to provide tasks that are suitable for a person and our practical ethics are that tasks undertaken should not extend into monotony but should be informative and fun. In consequence WWoofers should be prepared to be more than normally awake and able to follow instruction. A focused and energetic wwoofer can learn a great deal in a short time. We have also found that generally, women are ‘at least’ the equal of men in most tasks.

The winter here is mild and there are no frosts so it is very good for gardening.

We especially value people who are good with their hands and volunteers are also given the opportunity to learn and develop technique and creative approach in such fields as woodwork, stone laying, tiling, decorative concrete, painting and furniture restoration as an aid to developing their own self employment (landscapers, builders and decorators). 

TRAVEL : Irini makes frequent trips to Parikia and is always ready to accomodate personal shopping requests or take a passenger if convenient. Otherwise bycycles are available and there is a local bus service which we can be u

name: Jessica Purks - Certified Organic veggie and cut flower farm in Vermont's Green Mountains
description:
Hi there, we are Jess and Connor (34 & 32 years old) - the farmers of this tiny farm nestled in Vermont's Green Mountains in the little town of Shrewsbury. We just got going here in the fall of 2022 and we're now in of our fourth season! Our market garden is nearly 1 acre and producing certified organic cut flowers and diverse vegetables. We grow our vegetables and flowers using minimal tillage, human-power, and regenerative land ecology practices.  We also raise a small flock of sheep and alpacas for fiber, meat, and fertility.  

​We sell our produce through our little farmstand, local retailers, and at two weekend farmers market during the summer. Our farm backs up to the Green Mountain National Forest and the Appalachian Trail, so our farmstand also serves the hiking community with essentials and farm-produced delights to support their adventure.   After a decade of fun and traveling the world (with LOTS of wwoofing along the way!), we're ready to return the favor by hosting  wwoofers on our farm. 

How you'd be part of what we are doing here:
Wwoofers will participate in the daily tasks of this operational veggie and flower farm.  In the field, they'll experience transplanting, harvesting, weeding, compost spreading, and plant care. In the wash and pack space, they can experience bouquet making, veg and flower processing, and order packing. 

Activities are dynamic and may also include tasks related to the farm stand, perennial crop maintenance, greenhouse work, animal chores, and building infrastructure.  We are flexible and happy to curate a tailored learning experience for our wwoofers - let us know what you'd like to learn while you are here or what skills you have to share! 

We live in a big old farmhouse, 1790s old. Fortunately, its been decently renovated and features more rooms than we can use. There are two furnished guest rooms in the house on the second level. One room is suitable for couples and another room has two twin beds. Wwoofers will have th

name: Julie - A Newfoundland harbour lifestyle
description:
We are very excited to start hosting wwoofer's in our community. This a great opportunity to be joining us in creating an off the grid lifestyle in its beginning stages.

We live in a small harbour town , Fleur de Lys, on north east coast of Newfoundland called iceberg alley where Newfoundland English is spoken and traditions are shared and cherished. We live where moose are abundant with coyotes and bears visible at times.

Nearly everyone who lives in the harbour is related to Neil.  Julie is from originally from rural New Zealand.

We live in a Newfoundland style house which is under construction in some areas. We have a trailer and off the grid cabin. We use our own boats, sawmill, argo and tractors. In the winter our main mode of transport is by snow mobile. We eat mainly from the land so eat local Newfoundland traditional meals utilizing locally grown vegetables with locally harvested fish, seafood, rabbit or moose.

We are wanting to host people who want to be immersed in a local harbour community, work alongside us as we create our ideal lifestyle of returning to our roots by living within the natural environment. Our intention is to live off the grid and be self-sufficient. Our goals for next few years are to complete the off the grid cabin and house/fishing boat, make raised garden beds, plant fruit trees and much more.

A typical day is gathering and harvesting food and materials from the local area, working on building projects (currently off the grid cabin and house/fishing boat), creating using wool or recycled materials, playing musical instruments, spending time with family and friends and taking part in community activities. 

Activities in the area lots of hiking trails with beautiful views. We are within walking distance of the waterfront. There are opportunities for hiking, boating, swimming, bird watching, animal sightings, whale watching, fishing, hunting, and wild berry harvesting.

WWOOF'ers should bring proper attire which consists of work c

name: Brett - Compassionate Living Hub
description:
The Compassionate Living Hub is a regenerative learning community rooted in vegan ethics, permaculture, and creative expression. Presiding in the lush valley of the Great Swartberg, our farm is evolving into an intentional community where compassion guides every action—from growing food to raising consciousness.

We welcome motivated vegans and the vegan‑curious to join us in co‑creating a care giving society that lives in harmony with non‑human nature.

The Gardens & SlowFood Culture
Our gardens are the beating heart of the Hub. Volunteers will experience:
Harvesting gourmet vegetables – an extraordinary range of seasonal produce grown in abundance.
Cultivating herbs – aromatic and medicinal plants that enrich both meals and healing practices.
Garden‑to‑table cooking – preparing food directly from the land, celebrating freshness and creativity.
Communal meals – exceptionally delicious shared feasts that embody our culture of togetherness.
Food here is not just sustenance—it is a celebration of abundance, creativity, and community.

What We Offer
Agroecological farming: Hands‑on experience in permaculture, soil regeneration, and market gardening.
Vegan education: Courses and workshops in philosophy, nutrition, and inventive plant‑based cooking.
Animal communication: Learn sensory‑based approaches to deepen relationships with animals.
Holistic practices: Yoga, meditation, and authentic living rooted in non‑violence.
Creative arts: Poetry, music, and visual arts as expressions of regenerative culture.

New Initiatives
Applied Zoopharmacognosy & Indigenous Plant Medicines. We are developing a Zoopharmacognosy program to empower animals to self‑select natural remedies. Volunteers can help:
Cultivate indigenous plant medicines.
Document traditional knowledge and integrate it with holistic veterinary practice.
Support workshops and research in animal‑guided healing.

Land Regeneration Project
Following recent flood erosion, we are launching a team project to restore degra

name: Vikas - Khajuraho Permaculture Farm is located right in the UNESCO World Heritage city of Khajuraho, India
description:
Hi, we are Permaculture enthusiasts building a half-acre food forest farm near the UNESCO listed temples of Khajuraho, Madhya Pradesh, India. Inspired by sustainable living and cultural exchange, I love hosting curious souls who appreciate sustainable living, eco-projects, local heritage, and off-grid vibes. 

Our Half Acre permaculture farm is located right in the UNESCO World Heritage city of Khajuraho, India, a unique blend of nature, culture, and history.

Here, you’ll not only work with the land but also learn sustainable farming practices that can be applied anywhere in the world. Volunteers have the chance to connect with like-minded people, discover local traditions, and explore one of India’s most iconic historical sites in Khajuraho.

We are a small team passionate about sustainable living and community building. Our goal is to create a self-sufficient space that respects the land and supports those who work on it. As a volunteer, you’ll be welcomed like family, guided in your tasks, and encouraged to share your own ideas and skills. 🤝 We believe in open communication, cultural exchange, and working together in a way that’s respectful, educational, and enjoyable for everyone.

Gardening, Building & Repairing Farm Stuff, DIY and building projects, Painting & Decorating, Helping around the house.
Sustainable farming methods WWOOFers can learn here = Polyculture and intercropping, Permaculture Principles. Organic farming, No Till Farming, Crop rotation, Integrated Farming etc.

Daily routines from 9 AM to 6 PM blend sustainable farming, animal care. WWOOFers dive into hands-on tasks like sowing, weeding, composting, agroforestry maintenance, while enjoying Khajuraho temple visits, fresh meals, nature hikes, and digital nomad perks such as Wi-Fi. This immersive volunteer program fosters skills in eco-farming, community bonds, and spiritual retreats amid peaceful biodiversity.

You will sleep in a tent/bamboo a frame at farm camping site.

Traditional Indian fo

name: Amaïlys - Micro ferme familiale
description:
We are a Belgian family who settled in the beautiful Kleena Kleene region of British Columbia in September 2024, with our children Leïla (5), Noah (11), and Mircko (21).
Our property is crossed by a river and surrounded by mountains and breathtaking nature. We truly live out in the wilderness, with the nearest town about 2.5 hours away. It is the perfect place to completely disconnect and recharge.
Here, we are building our dream: an organic micro-farm aimed at maximum self-sufficiency. It is a small-scale private family project that we are developing only for ourselves. Because of how remote we are, we make as much as possible ourselves. We do not sell anything, but we produce our own cheeses, butter, cream, yogurt, bread, pasta, pastries, prepared meals stored in advance, and our own hay.
Our little farm is home to three lovely dogs, six very affectionate cats, including one who often comes walking with us, six goats that have kids every year starting in April-May and that I milk, one buck, fifteen hens and two roosters for eggs, pigs every year from April to November for meat, as well as three cows that calve in spring and that I also milk. Every other year, we also raise meat birds or ducks, and we plan to have sheep in the future.
In daily life, we take care of the animals and their enclosures, milk the goats and cows in the morning, turn the milk into cheese, butter, or yogurt, work in the large vegetable garden and greenhouses, maintain the field used to produce hay for our animals, cook, bake, and do all kinds of practical jobs around the property. There is always something to do.
My partner is very hands-on and enjoys building, repairing, and improving things. We have a sawmill, so we can build everything ourselves from the trees on the land. If you would like to learn practical building or DIY skills, he will be happy to share everything he knows.
Our project is still relatively new, and we are very much still learning. It is always a pleasure to share wha

name: Ereneo - Mwanamonga farm is located at Mwanamonga village in Songea District in Ruvuma Region in Tanzania.
description:
I operate my farm at Songea Region South part of Tanzania with varieties of crops including maize, beans, groundnuts and vegetables both for domestic consumption and commercial. My farm project is also very helpful in feeding students in my own School located in Songea town Ruvuma region and South part of Tanzania (Namanditi South Secondary School). By accepting Volunteers i am sure that we will improve in production organically through exchange of experience and skills.

The tools used in the project are very local ones which might be new to volunteers but as we grow in production we are expecting to improve machines and tools for better production. Volunteers who will be interested with teaching in my Secondary School are also invited only during extra hours after farming activities. We have very good and clean accommodations which will be used by volunteers as homestays living together with my family in a very local lifestyle to be adopted by both as one family.

Ruvuma has many opportunities including fishing in Lake Nyasa near Malawi whereby Volunteers will have a chance to visit the lake and for leisure while experiencing all activities taking place around. 

Organic methods: crop rotation, green manure

Certifications: Not ready for now

Current projects: 1. Farm Project; Farm preparation, planting, crop management, weeding, harvesting and many more

2. School teaching (An option during Extra Hours)

Internet access available

name: Jocean - Educational Centre, Agritourism Destination, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat, Woodlot
description:
We are an educationally oriented, simple gourmet agroforestry project. We currently focus our production on salad greens and herbs for guests & select Jakarta restaurants, and have a B & B/ guest house to accommodate jakarta cement refugees/paying guests, foodies, & students, young and old, people interested in reconnecting with more natural patterns of life, reinvigorating local cultures, and exploring combinations of Western and Indonesian spices. We're growing more fruits & vegetables for our guests & you to eat on the farm these days. Exchange housing will be in modified West Javanese rice barns. Ideally, wwoofers would have some experience in agriculture, construction, hospitality or culinary fields, but all hard working, willing enthusiasts of tropical forests are encouraged to contact us, free loaders, soory, but go to jalan jaksa saja, protecting the fair trade lovely balance of wwoofing is important to us.

Organic methods: Tropikal Komposting Agroforestry, peeing on the citrus & papaya trees, traditional compost teas & pesticides, chickens & children running about.

Current projects: Rainy season replanting of trees, saving the lettuces from torrential rains, turning an office into guest bedrooms, battling the forest & building it, and taking care of guests...

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Foothills of mount Salak, 2-3 hours South of Jakarta

name: Bomba - Community farm
description:
Salamo aleikom,
We are Ahmed and Bomba, cousins living in the Sagalil Valley, located 30 km south of Atar city in the heart of Mauritania. Our family has owned this home and farming land in the historic region of Adrad for over five generations.

Currently, we are in the process of building a garden on our farm and striving to create a diverse and sustainable community. We warmly welcome volunteers from all over the world to join us, exchange ideas about Mauritanian culture and way of life, and share their own experiences.

Our farm is surrounded by lush palm trees and various other types of vegetation. Despite receiving limited rainfall in recent years, the year 2022 brought more rain than we have seen in nearly two decades, rejuvenating the river and bringing it back to life.

Our goal is to transform our ancestral place into a guest house, providing a haven for travelers, artists, and digital nomads. Here, they can relax, explore the hidden oases of the Adrar plateau, and embark on adventures such as riding the famous Iron Ore Train and visiting nearby destinations.

We value any knowledge and expertise our volunteers can bring, and we appreciate any help offered. Your contributions will be invaluable in our pursuit of biodiversity and sustainable farming practices, including the introduction of various animals on the farm.

As our farm is currently not registered with the local administration, we kindly request your assistance in becoming the first registered host in the entire country of Mauritania.

One of our current projects involves renovating our family's old houses, constructed using clay, hay, and palm tree branches. This will enable us to accommodate more people and, hopefully, establish our own Airbnb, generating income that can be reinvested in the development of the farm.

We are eagerly looking forward to meeting you, sharing experiences, and learning from each other. If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to reach out to us. We will respo
_wwoof_travel: The village of Ain Ehel Taya

name: Eleonora - Piccola comunità agricola vicino al centro di Anghiari, Toscana
description:
Ciao!
Siamo Eleonora (1994) e Sam (         ) e viviamo in una fattoria di mezzo ettaro appena fuori le mura di Anghiari. Abbiamo scelto di vivere seguendo i principi della permacultura, sia nell’ambito sociale ed economico sia nel modo in cui coltiviamo il cibo e ci prendiamo cura della terra.

Io, Ele, sono appassionata di giardinaggio e particolarmente interessata all’agricoltura rigenerativa. Ho studiato agronomia e agroecologia e, oltre al percorso universitario, negli anni ho seguito diversi corsi legati alla facilitazione di gruppo, alla comunicazione non violenta, alla permacultura e allo yoga. Ho conosciuto il mondo degli ecovillaggi attraverso progetti Erasmus+ e vari raduni, esperienze che hanno influenzato profondamente il mio modo di vedere la vita comunitaria. Ogni mattina pratico yoga e meditazione, e nel tempo libero amo ballare, correre e fare escursioni.

Sam lavora come tuttofare nella zona. Si occupa principalmente di falegnameria, muratura e lavori manuali di vario genere. Nel tempo libero ama suonare la chitarra e cantare. È un vero sportivo e adora fare trail running insieme alla nostra cagnolina Maggie.

Fino a giugno vivranno ancora con noi Laura e Bea, arrivate circa due anni fa tramite Workaway. Nel frattempo hanno trovato una casa un po’ più grande qui vicino, dove presto si trasferiranno per continuare il loro percorso.

Abbiamo comprato questo posto  cinque anni fa e fin dall’inizio lo abbiamo immaginato e vissuto come uno spazio comunitario. Negli anni sono passate di qui molte persone, alcune per poche settimane, altre per periodi molto più lunghi. Il progetto nasce dal desiderio di creare una casa che accolga persone con la voglia di sperimentare una vita comunitaria, mettendo al centro l’autoproduzione, la collaborazione e il benessere collettivo.

Per questo, oltre ai volontari, ospitiamo spesso amiche e amici che hanno bisogno di un luogo dove stare per un po’. Organizziamo eventi e momenti di convivialità che mettono insieme pers
_wwoof_travel: Stazione di Arezzo / Aeroporto di Perugia/Firenze

name: German - Agritourism Destination, Homestead, Orchard, Family farm
description:
Es una Granja familiar, estamos a 4 km del pueblo de El Bolsón, sobre el faldeo del Cerro Piltriquitrón. Cultivamos principalmente frutas finas, Frambuesa, cerezas, guindas, frutillas, también nueces. Tenemos un rebaño de ovejas, aves de corral, perros y gatos. También trabajamos con Turistas y ofrecemos una Casa de Campo para hospedarlos. Entonces necesitamos ayuda para: -Labrar la Tierra (Septiembre-Octubre-Noviembre-diciembre) -Cosechar las frutas, elaboración de Dulces/conservas (Enero y Febrero) -Cosechar Nueces (Abril-Mayo) -Arreglar la casa de los Voluntarios WWoofers. -Trabajo de Campo (Septiembre a Mayo). Podemos recibir Voluntarios los meses de Septiembre, Octubre, Noviembre, Enero, Febrero, Marzo, Abril y Mayo. La estadía mínima son 3 semanas. Esperamos Voluntarios respetuosos, con Buena Onda, que se adapten a la vida del lugar, que cumplan con su estadía pactada, lo que significa que no se vayan antes de lo acordado. Si quieren alargar su estancia  y de común acuerdo es posible. Ofrecemos una aventura de Campo típica de la zona Andino Patagónica, en un lugar bellísimo con una familia acogedora

Hospedaje

Ofrecemos alojamiento en una casa grande , 2 dormitorio con 3/4 camas, una cocina amplia, un estar  y un baño con ducha de agua caliente. Damos ropa de cama, mantas, acolchados. Se deberá mantener la limpieza de la casa que se les ofrece durante la estadía y luego al retirarse debe quedar en las condiciones de limpieza para los próximos voluntarios   Si quieren también pueden hacer campamento con sus propias carpas. La cantidad de voluntarios depende de las actividades y la época de la cosecha entre enero y febrero es mayor la cantidad que se puede recibir.

Comida

Por el trabajo del día, ofrecemos el desayuno (pan y dulces caseros) y el almuerzo (cocina colectiva). La dieta no es vegetariana, pero es adaptable. La realización de la comida es comunitaria y rotativa.

Somos una familia con 3 integrantes, nuestras perras y gatos. Las ovejas y gallinas.

name: Souleymane - Ferme de production maraîchère et production avicole
description:
Bonjour, je m'appelle Souleymane DIOP, j'habite dans la région de Saint-Louis au nord du Sénégal. J'habite dans un village à quelques kilomètres de la ville de Saint-Louis, à Sanar Peulh, à côté de l'université Gaston Berger. J'habite avec ma famille (ma femme, mes 5 fils et ma fille) dans une maison familiale où nous possédons des chèvres et quelques poulets. Nous partageons la cour avec d'autres familles (foyers de a 3 personnes). Mes terres se trouvent à quelques kilomètres de mon habitation (25mn de marche). 

Il n'y a pas de journée type. Nous vivons au rythme des saisons. Aux champs, nous produisons principalement des oignons, des tomates, des poivrons, des arachides et des concombres (en fonction de la période). Nous produisons de la pépinière jusqu'à la récolte. En fonction de la période où vous arrivez et la durée de votre séjour, vous pourrez voir évoluer la culture.  Les activités sont principalement l'arrosage, l'amendement et le désherbage. Nous avons pour projet le développement d'une activité avicole afin de faire des poulets de chairs et des pondeuses. Lors de l'hivernage, nous faisons la traite des vaches et des chèvres afin de vendre le lait.

Dans le cadre d'échanges linguistiques , c'est un plaisir de faire des initiations à la langue locale le Wolof et le Poular.
Je suis également en train d'apprendre l'anglais afin de recevoir des woofeurs anglophones.

Du point de vue hébergement, des chambres seront mises à votre disposition. Chaque repas sera pris en famille, vous pourrez ainsi participer à la vie familiale. Vous pourrez apprendre à cuisiner des plats locaux comme le thieboudienne ( plat national ), la yassa, le mafé, le domoda ainsi que d'autres plats. Le partage et l'échange sont des valeurs auxquelles nous sommes très attachés. 

Travaillant également a l'université de nuit comme gardien, je vous propose de passer des soirées ensemble sur le campus, pour échanger, rencontrer les collègues, partager le diner et faire Ataya, pour les plus d

name: Emmanuel - Alternative co-operative community, Educational Centre, Agritourism Destination, Eco Retreat
description:
Asaase Green Project is an organisation that is into Permaculture and community development in Ghana. We undertake various green activities, which includes sharing and planting fruit trees in schools, communities,public spaces to improve the environment and mitigate climate change. As well as do school and community gardens both in rural and urban areas. In a way to combat hunger, malnutrition, poverty,etc, which help us to contribute towards the achievement of sustainable development goals.
 We aim to revolutionize the practice of agriculture with the application of Permaculture design principles and appropriate technologies to create organic food in abundance and improve the well-being of the people in communities.

Organic methods: Application of Permaculture design principles and using natural resources in food production

Certifications: Food and drugs board

Current projects: The organization is undertaking a green project to plant million trees in homes,schools, communities, public spaces,etc to mitigate climate change and also contribute towards the achievement of sustainable development goals.

We are also doing Permaculture garden for homes, schools and communities. To Promote sustainable agriculture,reduce hunger and achieve food security.

We are involve in recycling project to reduce plastic pollution and greening communities with eco-friendly materials

Internet access available

name: Shannon - Regenerative garlic and small chicken farm
description:
Come learn and experience farm life on our farm, we are two families that bought our new farm together in 2024, the sole family that actually farms is the younger family with 3 young boys 4-1 in age its a crazy time here and we will be welcoming a 4th baby this summer, we Luke and Shannon have been growing garlic for 4 years now. Luke works a day job just in Warburg but he’s the main gardener that has the most knowledge. Goal is to make enough profit from farm for Luke to stay home and do farming full time.  So we are working towards that. Shannon is the stay at home farmer and mom is also in charge a lot and would be the one teaching a lot and doing lots of the projects. The other family is a senior couple Max and Carolyn and their older son Gavin with disabilities they don’t do much with the farming side of things on the property Max has a small mechanic shop that he does with his brother . We also have a friend Nora living with us for almost 2 years now and helps with child care and all other aspects in the farm she’s a big part of the family.  

We have an older holiday trailer that will be parked near the house. We just ask if you can use the house bathroom as the holiday trailers bathroom is out of order. We also have a spare bedroom in the main house. We eat all together we are very resourceful in our meals and try to provide hardy and healthy food. We always have lots of mouths to feed. We all work together to cook and clean up at meal times. 

Looking for individuals who are looking to learn the basic skills of working on a small scale garlic/garden and small egg business. Being self motivated and a fast problem solvers helps alot as some projects are solo with instructions. 
We dont have set hours of work pre day we work random hours depending on weather and family life. Morning and evening chores of feeding and milking animals are normal daily we typically start at 7:00 or sometimes a bit later depending on week days and weekends in the mornings. Then fin

name: Brigitte - There is an empty place where we will set up a Cacao/Mixed fruit Foodforest
description:
A fallow hectare wishes for fruit trees 
and a vacant house wants to be revitalized.

Creation of a vibrant biodiverse mixed foodforest with cocoa, fruit trees, and native species. 
The house will become a place for workshops, volunteers, and sustainable daytourism.

Become a Pionier!

We are transforming a fallow hectare into a species-rich mixed cocoa forest and renovating the existing stone house – for sustainable agriculture, biodiversity, and a home for people and animals. 

Become part of our vision!
For the land: Revitalization of biodiversity, support for nature and climate.
For the house: Making the place for living, working, and teaching shine again.

Who is behind it?
Brigitte: Initiator, enthusiast for sustainable agriculture, food forest, and Terra Preta. She has over 200 edible plants on two hectares, most of them self-planted, and is constantly learning about local vegetables, fruits, and medicinal herbs. She has lived in Suriname for 20 years.

Stefan: All-rounder and craftsman with experience in various disciplines. He has lived in Suriname for three years.

Hubert: Local construction expert and all-rounder, born and raised in Suriname, familiar with local farming methods.

Why do I wish to host WWOOFers on my farm? 
Our project thrives on people who want to roll up their sleeves with us. 
Whether preparing, planting, building/renovating.
We are looking for curious, committed people who want to be part of this journey.
Working together is always more fun...

What will WWOOFers be expected to help with?
On the land: 
All preparatory work for the creation of the new cocoa plantation and food forest.
At the house: 
Cleaning and repair work in and around the house, painting, sorting materials (wood) for processing...

Where will you be hosting potential volunteers? 
Accommodation is in a simple, typical local house, currently with rudimentary furnishings.
 It includes a kitchen with a gas stove and a shower with cold and hot water.

What will potential

name: Warren and Jamie - Ranch/ Livestock Farm, Family farm
description:
Located near the beautiful Moose Mountains in southeast Saskatchewan we operate a small scale, grass based livestock farm on native prairie. Our farm offers a wonderful opportunity to explore our tree filled ravines and discover evidence of life in Saskatchewan before European settlers, such as First Nations teepee rings and buffalo rub stones. We are trying to live a self sustaining life so our farm is constantly evolving. We have a variety of animals such as meat and egg chickens, turkeys, pigs, beef cows and Jersey milk cows. All animals live on pasture and are regularly moved to fresh grass. Jobs range from feeding and moving animals to milking cows, yard and garden work. There is work for people of all skill levels. We are experienced travelers and understand what it is like to be in a new land. We love meeting people and sharing in their customs. Warren can speak basic  German and Spanish. Welcome!

Organic methods: We don't use pesticides of any kind on our farm and we employ the Holistic method of grazing.

Certifications: We are not certified organic

Current projects: Milking cows, feeding and moving animals, caring for chicks and calves, building fence.

Internet access available

name: Meri - Family farm
description:
SPRING 2026 ONE PLACE FREE APRIL 19 - MAY 13 

We , I and my husband Sauvo,  have a small farm in Eastern-Finland now with about 20 sheep, two horses and one cat. We offer bed and breakfast troughout the year, the busiest time of the tourism is the summer. We have beautiful sceneries from our hill. Some parts of our area is in the Natura2000-protection program.  We enjoy of living in the countryside and doing outside work. We try to live ecologically and learn old ways of working. Sauvo works in our forests only with manual tools. Our sheep work during the summertime to keep the sceneries open. One horse we use also to work. Our animals are original  Finnish races, sheep are rare Kainuu grey-sheep. Our house is originally built in 1894, now it has all modern facilities, we keep it warm by wood and we have also solar energy panels to give us some extra electricity. 
In the  spring beginning in April there is a lot of work: taking care of lambing and the lambs, shearing the sheep, making/repairing fences for the sheep and horses, cleaning the whole house for the summer season, also baking for the summer, making daily meals for everybody, some garden work and our favorite - working in our forest. In the end of August to September is another busy time. 
We work together with You and learn You all tasks, You can also tell us what kind of work You like to do. 
Your accommodation is in a room for one or two,  sheets and towels included. You get working overall and boots, bathrobe here,  we have washing machine,  so you don't need to bring too much clothes but anyway something warm for cold weather even in the summertime,  in April it can still be real winter.  In the summer sunscreen/sunglasses may be useful.
We eat normal Finnish food - breakfast, lunch, dinner - vegetarian or gluteinfree meals are possible.  Everybody makes meals sometimes and attend household works. 
You are learned to visit Finnish sauna.  Nearest lake is 1,6 km away for swimming.

DEUTSCH

Wir haben e
_wwoof_travel: Nearest railway station and airport in Joensuu, from Joensuu by bus 64 km to Maukkula, Ilomantsi, we meet you at the bus  stop Maukkulan kantatie l.

name: Paul - Diverse 160-acre family farm producing market vegetables, grassfed Highland beef, honey & eggs
description:
Our farm sits on 160 acres, located 12 miles west of Bemidji MN amid the lakes and conifer forests of the north woods. We are a diverse family farm producing market vegetables, Highland beef, honey, eggs, small fruit, mushrooms and microgreens. Depending on the season, WWOOFers do planting, cultivating, harvest, packing and distribution of vegetables; building and maintaining fencing; using a tractor to maintain pasture; daily livestock chores, bee keeping; farm building construction; rotational grazing setup and operation; and firewood harvest. Our facilities include a small earth shelter greenhouse, two high tunnels and a solar powered drip irrigation system.  We have two families on the farm.  Paul and Becky are the long time owners.  Bridget and Robert and their two young children joined the farm last year.

Stays from one week to all season are okay. May to October is our busy time. Depending on length of stay you may use our spare bedrooms (bunk beds), use a sleeper sofa in the family room, or tent (yours or ours). We can host up to four people for 2-3 weeks, 2 max for a longer stay. 

We have a few dairy goats, a few sheep, a horse and pony, six cats, three dogs and a snake in addition to our herd of 32 highland cattle. Children are welcome. 

We treat our WWOOF visitors as part of the family during their stay. That means we'll ask your help with meals and household chores at times in addition to farm work. We are happy to cook vegetarian meals and can accommodate vegan or other special diets.  Some meals are eaten together while for others visitors are on their own. WWOOF visitors have full access to our well stocked kitchen and pantry. 

The farm work is quite varied. We can keep you as busy as you want to be. Our only requirement is that on harvest days we need all hands on deck. Other days we will spend anywhere from 2 to 12 hours in the field depending on weather, the season and family life. You can join us according to your abilities and interests.

 Ar
_wwoof_travel: Bus and air service to Bemidji MN. train in Detroit Lakes MN. There is bus service from MSP airport in Minneapolis to Bemidji, for a longer stay pickup from MSP is possible..

name: SCHRLEY - Communal Living Group, Educational Centre, Smallholding, Eco Retreat
description:
O Célula OCA Simpatia Baraúna,  está iniciando uma jornada de Auto Suficiência Alimentar, Energia buscando a Floresta Nova Nação como Aprendizado. Aqui, suscita Autonomia, Criatividade e Solidariedade à todos os seres na cultura e ancestralidade da comunidade que os cerca, a todos criadores contemporâneos que desejam aprender uns com os outros e produzir no tempo do agora através do Livre Educação’ e Educação Selvagem. Na Chapada Diamantina/Bahia, há três quilômetro do Rio capivara e cinco quilômetros da Comunidade Quilombola, Remanso, a Floresta Nova Nação fica localizada, e nós seremos os Guardiões  de seu ambiente, aprendendo e convivendo com todos os seres da floresta, Aprender sobre a Água, o fogo o Ar e o Solo (terra).
Com perspectiva de autossuficiência pelos princípios da Agroecologia -  Agricultura Sintrópica e Permacultura, na coexistência às situações específicas de cada ecossistema e dos seres existentes deste local. Simplicidade e acolhimento são princípios básicos.
Um projeto de Educação Livre e Selvagem*, educação para o desenvolvimento dos Instintos e Intuição.
A integração entre os dois tempos o ancestral e atual, gera uma interseção, que é esse corpo/ser contemporâneo que carrega todas as memórias biológicas e o corpo com seus aprendizados do mundo moderno, tudo isso a seu favor. Nesta perspectiva, produzir/empreender e ser abundante na cultura, fortalecer nossa identidade e a dos povos que carregamos. 
A primeira construção é uma cúpula, sua Planta baixa circular e com representação dos 4 elementos e piso formato Yin e Yang com cimento e instalação inicial com grama. Idealizada e intuída a partir das moradias indígenas, as Ocas, onde todos tem acesso ao espaço de forma democrática e aberta. Além de aproximar as pessoas que vivem nela, ajudam a desenvolver um maior senso de comunidade e traz mais liberdade – já que na maior parte das vezes, além de pequenas, são vãos abertos e integradas à natureza.


Organic methods: Agricultura Sintrópica, Permac

name: Christian - Alternative co-operative community, Communal Living Group, Educational Centre
description:
Obrobibini is a mix between two words in Twi (local language in Ghana): "Obroni" means "white man", "Obibini" means "black man". Obrobibini represents the union of people from all origins. By bringing people together to share experiences and learn from each other, we develop healthy environments that are necessary for a decent human life with a healthy body and mind.

OPC is an association of people from high- and low-income countries that support the freedom and happiness of the people. We love to share our experiences and learnings to foster the healthy environments that are necessary for a decent human life with a healthy body and mind.

By maintaining centres of learning, OPC provides platforms for people to unfold their full potential. Thereby, we focus on people from low-income countries who do not have the necessary resources to realize their ideas. Learn to live in peace and harmony with your fellow human beings and nature.

OPC's first centre is located in Ghana. We aim at contributing to the eradication of the root causes of poverty by building a centre of learning to support the health of the people and the environment through practical education in the areas of sustainable agriculture, healthy diet and sports, herbal medicine, natural sanitation, waste recycling and renewable energy.

Organic methods: Everything is organic, we are in conversion

Certifications: Established in 2018, in conversion

Current projects: Dynamic agroforestry with main crops oil palm, avocado, lemon and soursop (Annona muricata)

Organic vegetable garden

Sustainable building projects with AirCrete inspired by DomeGaia (https://www.domegaia.com/)

Renewable energy plant

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Turn left at Afrokoma junction/OPC road just before entering Busua township, the farm is right at the end of the road (OPC access road, only 400m long)

name: Roxana - Agritourism Destination
description:
LLevamos adelante un emprendimiento de Turismo Rural Sustentable en estas pampas argentinas. Tenemos una Granja Ecológica Educativa formada por una Huerta Orgánica, Granja, Paseo de Plantas, Bosque Recreativo y Ecoludoteca, un prototipo de casa sustentable y espacio lúdico. Realizamos talleres, encuentros, jornadas y visitas educativas en el ámbito de la permacultura y la agroecología.

Organic methods: Buscamos un desarrollo permacultural utilizando bioconstrucción, energías renovables, agroecología, lombricultura y principios de biodinámica.

Certifications: Nuestra producción es pequeña, para autoconsumo y ventas por menor

Current projects: Estamos realizando una segunda etapa de Bioconstrucción de un Salón de Usos Múltiples en donde se realizan talleres, cursos, encuentros y actividades con otras asociaciones del pueblo.

Internet access available

name: Saleh - A private farm producing dates, figs and vegetables
description: I travel to many countries, including America, China, India, Hong Kong, the Emirates, Qatar, and many Arab countries. I live on a small farm in the Qassim region that produces dates, figs, and vegetables. They are all organically grown, and we do not sell any of them. They are all for self-sufficiency, and there are a few sheep, camels, horses, chickens, ducks, and rabbits. And the bathroom. There is a garden in which there are camps for rent. The work will be for two or three hours only in mowing the grass, collecting eggs, or cleaning the place. There are several options for housing. There are private rooms and a shared bathroom. There is an air-conditioned tent with a bathroom and a private kitchen. There is also all kinds of food and the kitchen can be accessed. At any time, in free time, we can participate in cooking, cycling, horse riding, or walking. We can visit the city every day to go to the supermarket, restaurants, or visit historical monuments. I have a cat shelter with 15 cats.
_wwoof_travel: I am an hour away from Qassim Airport and an hour and a half away from Qassim Train Station. I will receive you at the airport at any time.

name: Lothar - Family home and garden - rural, Nature preserve
description:
Little Hobby Farm in the beautiful South Shore of NS, we are about 6 km outside of Shelburne, across beautiful  Shelburne Harbor.  About 2.5 hrs. away from Halifax (Airport/Busses)  and about 1.5 hrs. away from Bridgewater where Busses from Halifax go ( Maritime Bus, check online) and from where we can pick you up.  We may also would pick up from Halifax Stanfield International Airport if we have to go to Halifax .
We have Gardens, two little Green Houses, Chickens, Ducks, we raise almost all of our own meat, Eggs, Vegetables, Berries, Bread (homemade Wood fire bake oven) , lots of homemade here like german Spaetzle, Pasta, Sausages etc. etc.  Somebody interested could learn a lot here about Food in general, we are known here for good Food :), I am a retired Chef 
 and my wife still works casual.
Cooking, canning, butchering, fishing, gardening, everything Wood working big and small (full big Workshop), knowledge about many animals, ( Bread) baking, Beer and Wine making (mostly from just a kit), Wild mushroom knowledge.
We speak german and english.

We would like if a Wwoofer stay's at least 15 days with us (and longer:)  One week is to short.
We prefer one at a time, but consider couples too, depending on time and work. The Guestroom has two  separate beds.

Organic methods: I don't use Pesticides on the property, we only produce  Meat, Vegetables and Berries for our self.

Certifications: We just produce for our self.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Bridgewater from Halifax for Bus,  Halifax  for Stanfield International Airport (and Busses)

name: Eftychios - Community World
description:
Gia sas friends! I am Eftychios and I live in a beautiful village called Katydata situated on the foot of Troodos mountain. It has a unique position in the island, with access to the sea and the mountain, near popular villages like Kakopetria and Kalopanayiotis, it is an hour's drive away from the capital of the island, Nicosia. The area is known for its fertile lands and a river that runs through the village all year through, situated next to the buffer zone and the archaeological site of Skouriotissa.  
I am an organic farmer that have left the city few years ago to live in the countryside, to become autonomous and live in harmony with nature. I have a field here we grow vegetables/fruits trees and one field with olive trees.  
The field with the vegetables and the fruits trees is 6000 square meters where many kinds of seasonal vegetables are grown.  The field with the olive trees is about 5000 square meters.
I am looking for people with a positive attitude and good energy, who care for the land and the environment, who enjoy being in nature and the quiet life of the village. People who like to work on the land, doing a range of agricultural activities, from cultivation to harvesting, composting and construction.  Willing to help with all preparations and processing of our produce and the selling process.
You will be given the opportunity to learn how to grow and cultivate a variety of organic vegetables,trees and herbs

Organic methods: Composting using free range manure, grass cuttings and other available organic waste.
Microorganisms and Bokashi
Certified organic pesticides 
Co-cultivations and crop rotation.

Certifications: The vegetable farm is certified organic by Lacon Ltd

Current projects: I usually need help with the daily chores of the field, weeding, pruning, planting, making seedlings and collecting vegetables. In October and November,is a harvesting period olives. Also some times there is some processing of the vegetables. There are always some smal

name: Stefanie - Experience the midsummer sun on our small regenerative farm with cattle in north Sweden
description:
Hi!

We, Beren and Stefanie and our daughter Sara, live on a beautiful little farm on the Swedish countryside. We share the farm with a growing flock of heritage breed cattle, heritage breed chickens, two senior dogs and a large orange cat. 

Surrounding the farm is mostly forests, lakes, small villages and if the cows and chickens are quiet, silence! There is a great lake to swim 1,5 km from us and the nearest village is 15 km away and has a grocery shop, school and a gas station. 

With regenerative agriculture we build topsoil, increase biodiversity and take care of the landscape and everything that lives in it overall. In practice that means a lot of fencing work and moving the cattle through the landscape!  Another branch we will start to work with this year is the production of wildflower seeds. 

Things that we need help with (depending on the period):

-Cuddling with the cows and checking on them. Especially during calving season in May-June we need to keep a close eye on them. 
-Setting up temporal wire fencing for the cows. 
-Mucking out.
-Mowing and weeding (around the cultivation of wildflowers for example)
-Cutting the grass under the fences.
-Cleaning fields of encroaching bushes.
-Growing vegetables/gardening.
-Sometimes building /maintenance projects. 

Please write what you want to learn and why you want to be on our farm! We would also like to hear which skills you want to contribute to. You don´t have to be an expert in farming or have a lot of experience. Most important is that you are genuinely interested and motivated to learn new things. 

There´s a cabin located 400m from our house which you can use. It has electricity and (hot) running water during summer and has two bedrooms with double beds, a small kitchen and living room. Please note that the toilet is for now just a bucket with a seat which must be emptied in the closed compost bin! We want to build an outdoor toilet as well but that is a nice future project. 

If you like hiking in tra

name: Crystal - Be a tour guide and tend to the land on our dragonfruit farm in Maui
description:
UPDATED NOVEMBER 2025

*** NO  PETS OR KIDS 

This family owned business began in 2009 and has since hosted 100s of wwoofers from all over the world. We are the first commercial organic dragonfruit farm on Maui. Located on the west side, the farm is a 10 minute drive from Lahaina town and our house (accommodations) is a 10 minute walk to the beach. We are looking for volunteers that can commit to a 3 month stay with opportunities to extend. The stay begins with a two week trial period to ensure our farm is the right fit for both ends. 

Our primary crop is dragonfruit but we grow a variety of different exotic fruits like bananas, pineapples, lilikoi, papayas, mango, fig, guavas, soursop and citrus. As well as a variety of tea crops that we incorporate into our tours and offer guests who visit the farm. In addition to being a fully operational fruit farm we also offer farm tours, alpaca tours, ziplining and aqua ball. 

In exchange for food and accommodation, wwoofers will participate in farm activities for 20 hours a week broken up into 5 days. (Monday - Friday from 8:30am to 1pm w/ a 30 minute lunch break included) That being said, the wwoofers here participate in multitude of different tasks ranging from planting to weeding to pollinating and picking fruits as well as facilitating tours, tending to the alpacas and operating the zip line and aqua ball. 

As fall and winter approach, the island is going to see an increase in tourists and so will our farm. We are looking for individuals who are willing and able to not only do farm work but also give tours. Our tours are a great opportunity for the WWOOFers to get immersed in the plants on our farm and the culture around the islands. Even better, the tours are an incredible way to bring awareness to visitors about Hawaii's rich culture and the ways we can help protect the Aina as we enjoy it. As tour guides we are representing the farm and all the work that has been done prior. We are looking for individuals that are

name: Didier - Maison de maitre et son grand parc, et surtout : potager, verger, petit bois, poules et ânes
description:
Je vis avec deux jeunes ados dans une grande maison de maitre et son ancien parc, un potager et un verger, en partie hérités d'anciennes plantations. Deux ânesses occupent la majorité du parc. Le potager inclut une serre circulaire et environ 100 m2 de parcelles.

Les activités sont très multitâches : contenir le lierre, faucher et débroussailler des ronciers du parc, rendre visite aux ânes,  potager (tout un sujet), récolte des pommes et organiser le pressage, s'occuper des poules et des poulets, participer aux repas, s'occuper des abeilles et de la récolte de miel, entretiens et plantations d un jardin a l anglaise. Tout cela dépend forcément des saisons.

Pour faciliter la venue sur plus qu'une journée ou un week-end, il serait mieux de m'appeler qq jours ou qq semaines à l'avance afin que je puisse caler mes congés en fonction. Pour les week-ends, c'est bcp plus simple à organiser.

Repas du matin/midi/soir en commun (ou non !). Une grande (et belle) cuisine avec cuisinière à buches est disponible, ainsi qu'un espace bbq, un four solaire extérieur, etc.  Nous faisons notre pain au levain plusieurs fois par semaine.

La maison dispose d'une grande chambre d'amis dont la sdb est partagée mais deux autres sont éventuellement disponibles. Il y a également une chambre d'hotes (avec sdb séparée).

Les activités comprennent principalement des randonnées autour du village (éventuellement accompagné d'une ou deux des ânesses), mais aussi des sorties en vélo, voiture, train ou via Clermont-Ferrand, vers le reste de l'Auvergne (train de l'Aubrac, train des Cévennes, train vers le nord/l'Allier, train vers Thiers depuis Vertaizon). Il y a un ancien bassin circulaire alimenté par une source et très à l'ombre, dans lequel on peut plus ou moins nagé en fonction du débit de la source. Nous sommes à qq km de bourgs plus importants avec bcp d'activités populaires. Le village dispose de qq clubs sportifs, notamment le badminton (dont je fais partie), boulangerie, pharmarcie, bar.

name: Geoffrey - Organic dairy farm with Holiday Cottages on the South Devon Coast
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
We have a herd of 500 dairy cows plus followers and eight holiday cottages. We are lucky enough to farm right on the coast between the villages of Newton Ferrers and Holbeton, 10 miles East of Plymouth. 5 of us work with the cows and younger animals and a team of two on the holiday cottages plus the cleaning team. We have a busy office and there is always lots going on. We always  have conservation jobs on the farm 

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
Some work with the dairy cows depending on experience and enthusiasm
Gardening
Holiday Cottage preperation
Conservation work such as planting hedges
Collecting wood for the winter and to provide wood for our biomass boilers

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals:
Accommodation will be in a small one bed flat on the farm. We also have a mobile home available. 


4. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?
The day starts early with the cows, with milking starting at 0500, and we milk twice a day. Otherwise the working day begins at 0830, and your day will be defined  depending on the work and who you are helping. 


5. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.):
There are many lovely beaches in the area, and in the summer plenty of tourists too. The South West Coast Path goes across our farm. We are passionate about food provenance, producing healthy food and giving our holiday visitors a great time. 
We also have two horses and two dogs, a cocker spaniel called Chutney and a labrador called Maybe.
Our children are 20, 16 and 14 so there is always plenty going on on the farm.
We have 20 organic hens and so eggs are often on the menu!
Breakfast is taken early, about 0730, lunch at 1300 and

name: Marie - German family - Guest Lodge and organic self-sufficiency Farm bordering the Add ElephantNationalpark
description:
