We are a German family who immigrated to South Africa and now live on a 200-hectare game farm on the border of Addo Elephant National Park in the Eastern Cape. Besides running our small guest lodge, we are building a self-sufficient and sustainable lifestyle based on permaculture and organic farming principles.

We grow fruits, vegetables and herbs, produce our own eggs and dairy products, and care for a variety of animals including cattle, sheep, goats, horses, donkeys, chickens, geese, dogs and cats. Wildlife such as zebras and many native animals can also be seen on the farm.

Our goal is to live as sustainably as possible while sharing this beautiful place with guests and volunteers from around the world.

Life on the farm is varied and every day is different. Typical tasks include feeding animals, collecting eggs, gardening, planting, weeding, harvesting vegetables, maintaining fences, cutting firewood, bush clearing, repairing buildings, painting, and helping with renovation projects in our guest accommodations.

Depending on the season, volunteers may also assist with milking, making dairy products, maintaining irrigation systems, caring for horses and other animals, or general farm maintenance.

We are happy to share our knowledge about self-sufficiency, organic gardening, permaculture, animal care, sustainable living, and the realities of running a farm and lodge in rural South Africa.

WWOOFers stay in comfortable accommodation on the farm and have access to bathroom facilities, hot showers, electricity and Wi-Fi when available. Please note that we live in a rural area and occasional power or internet interruptions can happen.

We provide three meals per day. Whenever possible, meals include fresh produce from our gardens, eggs from our chickens, dairy products from our animals, and other home-grown ingredients. We enjoy sharing meals together and volunteers are welcome to join family life while also having their own private time.

We enjoy horse riding, g

name: Christina - Family home and garden - rural, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Farm, Family farm
description:
Shamba Miti Mingi is a new Permaculture project.  We wish to show an example how we can grow a lot of vegetables  and fruit trees dispite  big challenges, like poor sandy soil, long dry periods and a very hot climate. 
By tradition most shamba owners grow Coconuts, Mango and Cashewnuts.
Seldom you see any biodiversity of trees and very few vegetables. In fact most of the shamba land around us lies idle.
We are now two people who have done the Permaculture Design Course. We feel very inspired as these methods of farmig fit very well to cope with our challenges. 
Make good soil and lots of mulching, recreate the forest structure!
We hope that more people will come and see our work  to deveop their precious land.
We have two donkeys and a cat and a dog to help us.

Organic methods: Biological pest management, organic gardening, permaculture, syntropic agroforestry

Certifications: Our requests for certification is under application process

Current projects: 1. Renovation of eco houses
2. Renewing raised beds and mulching 
3. Construction of nursery beds and seedlings

Please it is good to stay for a while. Minimum 3-4 weeks!

Internet access available

name: Astrid - Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures
description:
Important:  THE LODGE IS CLOSED FROM MAY TO SEP. DONT APPLY FOR THESE MONTHS. ITS OUR WINTER TIME AND WE DONT HAVE AN OPERATION RUNNING.  ALSO: WE DO NOT, AND WILL NOT, ACCEPT APPLICATIONS FOR LESS THAN 25 DAYS. WE WILL NOT MAKE ANY EXCEPTION. 

We will privilege curious, outdoorsy, adaptable, intuitive and experienced people. If you have NO experience with gardening, plants, animals, please do not apply. We are not a teaching institution. We REALLY need the helping hand.

Described by The New York Times as ´Argentina's Private (and Secret) answer to The Galapagos´, Bahia Bustamante is a seaside Lodge surrounded by 360° of pure nature, located in Patagonia, in an area recognized by its relevant biological biodiversity. Only in a few places in the world its possible to find such a number and diversity of seabirds and marine mammals, which is why it has been included in ´Patagonia Austral´  National Park & ´Blue Patagonia´ UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. It is also an IBA (Important Birding Area).

We are a Lodge with 25 years of operations and 11 cabins for guests. Several activities of wildlife exploration in 60 kmts of seaside coast. 

WWOOFERs are welcomed since 15 years ago (only from October to April), and thanks to these collaboration exchange we have been able to make the best of our veggie production and hens and eggs breeding. Wwoofers also enjoy the paradise where we live in and aquire language skills and conservation concepts as well as some organic farming experience. 

We have space for up to 5 wwoofers per month in single or double rooms with shared bathrooms. We offer 3 meals (simple cooking, not gourmet), washing machine, wi fi, nature experiences, and free pass to fabulous beaches. 

VERY IMPORTANT INFORMATION: 
We expect 5 hours of work per day. Divided in 2 turns. 6 days a week. 
Working days start very early in the morning. If you are not a morning person, this is not your place. 

WE are a place of work. Our staff works on a very intense pace, many hou
_wwoof_travel: Bus from Comodoro Rivadavia or Trelew Cities in Chubut Province (DON OTTO Line) to Garayalde Gas Station in Route 3. Communicate day and time of arrival and we pick you up on the gas station to take you to the farm.

name: Céline - Ferme regenerative et centre de retraite
description:
To receive the description in English, please contact us. 

Coucou! 

Ce projet est né en 2010 et a connu depuis plein d'âges différents. 
Nous nous sommes installés sur cette terre très belle bordée de forêt et tout près de la mer (à 10 min à pied). Nous sommes assez isolés, le village le plus proche se trouve soit à 20 min de voiture ou 30/40 min de marche jusqu'à la route principale puis 10 min de bus ou stop). L’espace est sauvage,  dès lors si vous aimez la solitude et le calme ce lieu a de grande chance de vous séduire. Si par contre vous souhaitez vadrouiller, nous vous recommandons de trouver une voiture à louer. 

Des pâtures embroussaillées d'où nous avons commencé, une jolie forêt fruitière prend racine. De nombreuses idées nous habitent et trouvent petit à petit leur place sur le terrain. La diversité et la résilience aux catastrophes constituent nos principaux objectifs dans la mise en place de cet espace où il fait bon vivre. 
Après avoir travaillé comme des forcenés pendant des années pour faire émerger de la douceur de ce champ de soleil et d'épines, nous apprenons à prendre plus grand soin de nous même et par là même notre approche au travail a beaucoup changé. L'observation, la réflexion et la simplicité tendent à présent à prendre le pas sur l'action constante.  Plein de petits projets naissent, certains demeurent en suspends des années, certains voient enfin les finitions de leur mise en œuvre s'opérer. 
Dernièrement, Céline Nour est seule sur le terrain, et une nouvelle reconversion de l'activité s'opère. Dès lors les différentes activités que nous mettions jusque là en place sont pour la plupart en suspends en attente d'âmes passionnées qui souhaiteraient les faire vivre. 
Le lieu s'ouvre à l'accueil de retraites pour se ressourcer dans la beauté de cet espace connecté aux éléments. 

HEBERGEMENT :

Les wwoofeurs sont en ce moment hébergé dans un grand pavillon, autonomes, avec eau chaude, toilettes sèches et cuisine. Ils partagent une grande p
_wwoof_travel: Le Vauclin (5 Km) ou à l'entrée de la sortie Macabou (2km). Aéroport le plus proche : Le Lamentin (40 Km)

name: Vaillant - Permaculture, Ecotourism, Retreats & Community in the mountains of Grecia, Costa Rica
description:
Greetings, I am Vaillant, a native of France with a deep-rooted connection to the land. Over two decades, I embarked on an entrepreneurial journey within the dynamic realm of Silicon Valley in the United States. However, the winds of change led me to Costa Rica in March 2020, where I now reside.

In my quest for a life aligned with nature's rhythms and driven by heartfelt inspiration, I birthed the haven known as Theleme. Within its embrace, we cherish values that encompass sustainability, autonomy, the delicate equilibrium between liberty and accountability, human kinships and bonds, personal evolution, mindful communication, spirituality, entrepreneurial spirit, authenticity, fun, and the spirit of reciprocity.

On our idyllic grounds thrive a charming community of 6 goats, 15 chickens, 8 ducks and 3 turkeys. A flourishing greenhouse paints a canvas of vibrant vegetables and fragrant herbs. Our present endeavors revolve around the principles of permaculture and sustainable construction. Intermittently, we orchestrate retreats that delve into personal growth, a testament to our holistic approach.

The individual best suited for us would be a harmonious blend of ethical diligence, practical adeptness, a spiritual compass, and the gift of self-governance. We're in search of those who yearn to engrain themselves within our community's fabric on a more prolonged basis. Our current pursuits span the development of economic frameworks, including ecotourism and the distribution of farm-grown produce.

For those drawn to volunteer, your commitment entails five morning sessions of communal labor each week. The afternoons generously unfold for personal introspection and creative exploration. Nourishment and shelter are offerings extended in exchange for your dedication. Still, we remain receptive to alternative forms of reciprocity.

Should the notion of this endeavor resonate with you, I invite you to establish contact. We prefer long-term volunteers. Stays are 1 month mini
_wwoof_travel: SJO is one hour away, and bus terminal from Grecia 25 minutes

name: SAMBA CIRE - Ferme maraîchère et élevage avec visite du pays Bassari possible.  https://Gofund.me/15c2ea2f8
description:
Bonjour, je suis Samba Cire Diallo, professeur et directeur à l'école du village Ibel. Pendant l'hivernage je pratique le maraîchage avec ma famille, nous cultivons du maïs (très consommé ici), des arachides, des poivrons, des aubergines sénégalaise, ect
Les woofeurs seront hébergés dans une case traditionnelle à proximité de la maison familiale. Ils profiteront d'un endroit calme dans une nature préservée et pourront apprendre la cuisine traditionnelle, la boulangerie et la construction et réparation de cases ou greniers (en saison sèche). Les repas sont partagés avec la famille et le but de cet accueil n'est pas d'avoir des ouvriers agricoles mais bien de se rencontrer, de partager nos cultures et surtout de faire visiter à des étrangers la plus belle région du monde! En effet je connais le pays Bassari comme ma poche et serait ravi de vous montrer nos magnifiques cascades, nos immenses baobabs et nos villages autoconstruits en matériaux locaux (bambous, terre, herbes sèches et feuilles de palmiers).

Les activités sont très variées : semis en pépinière, semis en plein champ, repiquage, désherbage, récolte, réparation de la clôture, contrôle, arrosage, ect

Les repas sont partagés avec la famille, nous mangeons des plats simples, à base de semoule sénégalaise, riz ou spaghetti.

Les woofeurs pourront proposer des activités à l'école primaire et des cours particuliers pour les collégiens.
Beaucoup de fêtes traditionnelles sont organisées pendant l'année, j'espère que vous pourrez y assister!

Hello, I am Samba Cire Diallo, a teacher and headmaster at the Ibel village school. During the rainy season, I practice market gardening with my family. We grow corn (a staple here), peanuts, peppers, Senegalese eggplants, and more. WWOOFers will be hosted in a traditional hut near the family home. They will enjoy a peaceful setting surrounded by unspoiled nature and will have the opportunity to learn traditional cooking, baking, and the construction and repair of huts or gran

name: Youssef - Community farm
description:
Haj Moulein is a living experiment in sustainable agriculture and ecological harmony. Located at the edge of the stunning Siwa Oasis where fertile land meets the Sahara, the farm is dedicated to reviving the land's vitality and empowering local farmers to reclaim their food sovereignty through sustainable practices.

Spanning 20 acres, our farm features a diverse mix of palm, olive, and fruit trees alongside seasonal vegetable plots. We also care for chickens, ducks, and donkeys in our animal shed. The farm is fully powered by solar energy and uses eco-conscious water systems. All structures on the land are built from local materials or created through experimental natural building techniques.

Haj Moulein is not just a farm—it's a community. We welcome volunteers from around the world to join us in cultivating the land, exchanging knowledge, and living in harmony with nature. Whether your passion lies in organic farming, environmental education, art, culture, meditation, or ecological living, you’ll find a place here to learn and contribute.

Our facilities include three houses and three guest rooms, accommodating up to 25 people. There's a shared kitchen and a spacious pergola perfect for gatherings, workshops, and communal meals.

At Haj Moulein, we believe that restoring the Earth begins with responsible action, shared learning, and deep connection—to the land, to others, and to ourselves.

Current projects: We are looking mainly for people that are handy, creative and resourceful, to help with maintaining the farm, improving the accommodations, building, and hosting.

Possible projects:

- farming
- building
- improving irrigation systems
- artistic creation and decoration of hosting facilities.
- marketing for the accommodations
- cooking
- and many more

name: Lorena - Somos productores de Cerezas y Frutillas orgánicas, Crianza de Corderos y Bosque Nativo.
description:
Hola, soy Lorena y vivo con mi pareja y nuestros 8 tiernos perros en este lindo campo. Es un sector rural donde estamos inmersos en la belleza natural de cerros con bosques nativos de maitenes, boldos y quillay. Tenemos corderos, unas cuantas gallinas, vacas y caballos. Plantamos cerezos y frutillas orgánicas para exportación. Estamos enfocados en la relación entre lo agrícola y el medioambiente. Por eso, nos enfocamos en la restauración del bosque nativo y el suelo con microorganismos benéficos.

Las actividades del campo dependen de la temporada. Un día en Los Maitenes podría incluir: 
- el corte de brotes de cerezos (10 hectáreas)
- aplicación de protección de poda en pintura que sirve también para ahuyentar conejos en el campo de cerezos
- cosecha de cerezas en la última semana de noviembre o primera semana de diciembre
- raleo de flores, sacado de maleza y cosecha en media hectárea de frutillas
- fertilización  y nutrición
- cosecha de manzanas, membrillos, damascos, limones, ciruelas, peras, duraznos y almendras en el mini huerto cerca de la casa
- sacar y colocar cintas de riego 
- poda sanitaria de arboles 
- recolectar leña ya cortada para el invierno
- replantar arboles nativos y agrícolas
- instalar o reparar cercos
- y mucho más!

Alojamiento: Te quedarás en nuestra casa conmigo y mi pareja. El alojamiento consiste de un dormitorio privado con una o dos camas. Si vienes con un amigo o pareja, puedan compartir el mismo dormitorio. Tendrás un baño privado con ducha. Por favor, no tires el papel higiénico en el inodoro. Poner en el basurero. 

Espacios comunes: Tenemos 3 Shih Tzus quien viven en la casa. Tenemos 5 otros perros que viven afuera de la casa. Todos son muy amables. Somos amantes de perros y esperamos que seas también. Estás bienvenido compartir con nosotros en la sala living y la cocina. El agua de canilla/llave es potable; viene del subterraneo y tiene un sabor riquísimo. Cuidamos el medioambiente y te pedimos ser consciente del uso de agua y
_wwoof_travel: Terminal de Buses de Talca, Terminal de Tren de Talca

name: Kitzia - Educational Centre, Agritourism Destination, Eco Retreat, Market Garden, Orchard
description:
Living genealogy seed bank and training centre.


Since 1999, our botanical reserve features over 3000 plant species from the tropical and dry tropical regions of the world.

All organic.

We are 2 miles away from the beach, 45 minute radio from coral reef, hotsprings, waterfalls and the Los Cabos International airport.

Organic methods: Compost making
Integrated pest management
cover crops
foliar feeding
companion planting
mulch
biochar
natural pest control herbal preparations



Certifications: We dont have a need to have a paper. We believe in the benefits of organic. All the farmers in the East Cape are organic.

Current projects: SEASON OCT 2022 TO MAY 2023

1. Maintenence of Tropical Tree Orchards, fertilization, prunning, harvest, seed saving

2. Landscaping. Weeding, fertilizing, planting flowers, prunning, foliar feeding

3. Seasonal gardens. Sacred geometry designs, Preparing garden beds, companion planting, integrated pestmanagement, planting, foliar feeding, thining, seed saving, harvest.

4 Construction
Composting toilets

5. Community outreach activities
Elementary and high school workshops on art and gardening
International volunters

6. Eco Resort
Wellness and art  Retreats

name: Ola - Family home and garden - rural, Agritourism Destination, Flower Garden Farm
description:
The farm is in a valley between the highest mountains in Norway, in a marvellous landscape with dry climate. We work bio-dynamical, but cooperate much with other producers, restaurants and hotels to create regional development. We have cows, and grow many sorts of vegetables. Our speciality is growing of herbs, we make teas and species and sell all over Norway. We have a herb-garden for visitors and shop, and a new building for cultural events in summer. "We" are a family with 5 children (19 -28) - and 2 - 5 co-workers and practicants. We need help from beginning of April til end of October, you get an own room and we make and eat the meals together. We want people to stay at least a month. Now we need help from the beginning of September to the end of October! We welcome interest and questions!

Certifications: Debio certified, also Demeter

Internet access available

Our french students that have been for the summer is soon leaving. We need some more hands from the beginning of September to the end of October. We have had enough people for the summer season, and it is always rather busy. So I am sorry for not being clever answering peoples requests. Now I will surely answer!

Autumn between the highest mountains in Norway can be a wonderful experience...
All the best, Ola
_wwoof_travel:
In Lom, 500 meters over sea level, the highest mountain in Norway (2468) nearby.

Direct connection with bus from Oslo, Bergen or Trondheim. Eventually train from Oslo to Otta, then change to bus.

name: Benjamin - A permaculture fruit forest and cinnamon plantation offering paddy farming and eco-glamping.
description:
🏡 1. Our Place

We are located in a  remote sinhala village near Monaragala.
The property has mountain views, a great biodiversity, and untouched forests stretching along the streams that surround our property. It is the perfect place to disconnect, relax, and immerse yourself in nature. While this isolated landscape is ideal for purely organic cultivation, managing the local wildlife is actually a major challenge for farming! 

Our land is also perfectly suited for Eco-tourism/Agro-Tourism/Glamping. We have nature trails running throughout the property, which have incredible potential—but they currently require major maintenance. It is a lot of hard work!

🌦️ Seasons & Climate

    The Rainy Seasons: Heavy rains usually arrive around November/December and again around April.

    The Summer Dry Period: September and October require a lot of preparation to get the land ready before the next rains arrive.

    The Waterfall Season: From January to July, small waterfalls pop up everywhere. This is the absolute perfect time to bathe in the streams.

🚀 Current Projects & Vision

We are currently in the planning /beginning stages of establishing a large fruit forest/ cinnamon plantation/ camping sites/glamping for eco-tourists and a dedicated nature trail.

Additionally, we run a fruit-export business that we recently moved directly onto the farm. We pack and export fresh fruit harvested from our land and surrounding village networks and shipping directly to end-consumers in Germany.
 
🕷️ Nature & Living Conditions (Please Read Carefully!)

Because we live in the heart of nature, we share our space with plenty of insects, snakes, and spiders around the house. Mosquitoes pass through occasionally, and we rarely—but sometimes—see very small scorpions. You must be comfortable with this environment (and always shake out your boots before putting them on!). If you have a snake or spider phobia, this is not the right stay for you!

You also need to be happy with very basic fac

name: Alessandra - Fazenda agroecológica, produção de cacau, açaí, cupuaçu e outros em SAF e uma RPPN
description:
Meu apelido é Guxa, tenho 51 anos, advogada de formação, apaixonada pela terra, por fazer amigos, acredito que viver de forma regenerativa é possível e é por isso trabalho todos os dias, me entendendo parte da natureza e sigo descobrindo com ela a ser um agente regenerativo, gosto de dançar, cozinhar e cuidar

Na MoVera plantamos de forma orgânica e regenerativa, temos uma reserva ambiental, fazemos parte da Rede Povos da Mata (Grupo de certificação orgânica participativa) e iniciamos a produção de chocolate, geleias e frutas desidratadas com o que produzimos nos nossos SAFs, aqui podemos compartilhar tudo que aprendemos sobre agricultura regenerativa, produção de Bioinsumos e agroindústria

A casa mãe da MoVera tem um quarto coletivo que abriga até 5 pessoas, as refeições serão feitas juntos

Temos uma cachoeira a 30 minutos de caminhada da casa, uma piscina natural a 5 minutos de distância da casa com uma bica d’água pra refrescar do calor, Taperoá fica a 7km, 15 minutos de carro, com uma orla bem bonita, alguns restaurantes mas uma vida social pacata, na fazenda os pássaros nos chamam cedo, aos fins de semana podemos ir à praia temos 2 praias a 1 hora de distância, é uma região linda do baixo sul baiano, gosto de estar com amigos por isso sair para conhecer os arredores é algo que fazemos com frequência
_wwoof_travel: Aeroporto de Salvador, rodoviária de Taperoá

name: Joao - Family farm
description:
Finca El Aullador (The Howler Farm) is a 10-hectare conservation and multiple use area. The farm is located near Mompiche on the coast of Esmeraldas Province in Ecuador, a premier surfing and tourism destination. The farm is 2 kilometers from one of the best "left point-break"in Ecuador.  The farm is managed by a father and son team (João and Ivan). Pichi and his family are responsible for day-to-day tasks.  Our goal is to combine production and conservation to save the biodiversity of the coastal Chocó region of Ecuador.

Out of the 10 hectares, 7.5 are under primary and secondary forest.  Two-and-a-half hectares are dedicated to organic shade grown cacao (criollo and fino de aroma) fruit trees, and interspersed short-cycle crops. At the farm we are trying different ways of integrating conservation and agriculture.

The owners do not live in the property but nearby.  Your day-to-day supervisor will be Francisco (Pichi). The work can be hard and being inmersed in a tropical forest, you will have to contend with bugs and other inconveniences.  This is a life changing experience but not for those that expect comfort. Accommodations are spartan, there is no regular road access, no electricity. You will ahve to do most of your own cooking, albeit most often people joing forces with Pichi. We are flexible given the unique characteristics of our farm. 

Organic methods: The Howler Farm is an experimental conservationa and multiple use zone. Besides not using any chemicals, we work with natural vegetation processes to integrate agroforestry and conservation. Our focus on organic shade grown cacao allows us to maintain a high level of biodiversity while producing enough for basic food and cash needs of the family. Our actions are relatively recent, so many of our initiatives are only now beginning to take shape.

Certifications: We are not commercial, too small. We are organic by deep conviction

Current projects: Currently our goa is to enhance cacao and food production an

name: Jim - Large gardens above Shuswap Lake
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
We have lived here for 54 years, growing our own food and living a sustainable lifestyle. Our property has a greenhouse for starting our plants, a large compost box, three vegetable gardens, many flower beds, ornamental trees and numerous fruit trees, plus a large raspberry patch. We recently had some logging done, so there is plenty of work required for the clean-up. Other than using a rototiller, all the gardening is done by hand.  We are vegetarian, but I enjoy fish as well. We would be happy to add meat to some of the meals if requested. Take a virtual tour of our homestead here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GeqCbzezAp4&t=74s

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
Gardening, yard care, building, mulching and fence maintenance, construction projects and food storage and preparation.

3. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?
Weeding, mulching, hoeing, mowing, pruning, fencing, maintenance, and clean-up work after logging.  A typical day would include some weeding, harvesting, logging debris clean-up and/or hoeing.  During their free-time, WWOOFers could go for a hike, a swim or go canoeing. If they are a musician, there are other musicians in the neighbourhood they might enjoy jamming with. If they want to do some writing, I am an author and an editor and would be happy to help with suggestions and editing. Photography opportunities abound.  One of Kathi's hobbies is baking, and they could learn about baking cakes, cookies and squares. They can help with food preparation and later in the year, with canning and freezing. If they are fortunate to be here when we host one of our dance parties, they can help with set-up and clean-up, plus enjoy a night dancing to great live music. Our first show this year will be on July 8th.

4. Play the guide! Talk about your ow
_wwoof_travel: Kamloops, B.C. has an airport and buses arrive in Chase and Sorrento.

name: Pro-Life and Kirsten - USDA Certified Organic strawberry and sugar-snap pea farm
description:
We are USDA Certified Organic, big in strawberries and sugar-snap peas.  We plant February to May, our production is May to November.  We are mostly-vegetarian, Christian, moral activists.  We have a separate home for guests with full accommodations.  About 60% of our sales are u-pick.  We are close to the mountains, the Snake and Payette Rivers.  The Boise airport is about 50 minutes away.  We treat our guests like family and we are looking for future partners in farming.  We do not work on Sunday, we attend church-  non-denominational.  Most churches are businesses, which we believe is offensive to God.  If you follow your conscience, you will feel at home with us.  Visit for any length of time from February to November for farm work, or even winter- just to visit.  Feel free to call us with any questions.  Thanks for being interested in good food, the environment and all truth.  Our farm has some of the cleanest water and soil in the Mountain West.

Organic methods: We use Lacewings and Minute Pirate bugs to control aphids and other fruit destroying insects. 

Certifications: Idaho Department of Agriculture- Organic Division

Internet access available

name: Jerry - Regenerative, organic, pigs, chickens, cows and vegetables, located in the wilderness of Alberta
description:
WE WELCOME REFUGEES FROM UKRAINE! 

How lucky we are and how much we would like to share.  The freshest air, the healthiest food, the opportunities to learn about vegetable and protein production in an organic way. Now more than ever we are focusing on becoming self sufficient. What does that look like? 

We produce our own meat, vegetables, eggs, and dairy. We have started to produce honey, mill our own flour, bake bread, preserve vegetables, and make pasta, cured meats, butter, yogurt, kefir, and cheeses. We are looking for a volunteer who would appreciate learning these skills or teach us some of their own.  

Need something different in your life?  Pigs, bison, beavers, music, milk cow, northern lights, wilderness, tractors, gardening, organic, turkeys, trees, swans, swimming, skiing, solitude, chickens, community, canoeing, campfires, choir, concerts, cats, cows, cuisine, festivals, fishing, rodeos, etc. etc. etc.   Lots happening!  No two days are the same!

A long time ago (>30 years) we signed up to the WWOOF program. We were the second farm in Alberta to do so.  
Certified organic for the past 34 years, First Nature Farms is considered an ecological reserve of over 3000 acres of mostly aspen forest, three creeks and several beaver ponds.  Organic since 1990 and WWOOF hosts for almost as long,  our diverse ranch includes grass finished Belted Galloway cattle, Plains bison, free range chickens and turkeys but our specialty are the Berkshire pigs. We raise heritage breeds in a gentle environment from farrow to finish, outside, year round. "Empress" the milk cow is a close member of our family, supplying all our dairy needs.  Easy to milk too! We also have two large dogs, Figaro and Magnifico (super friendly), one inside cat, Ghost and one turtle (38 years old).

Help is appreciated throughout the year with most jobs centred around caring for the animals (no shit shovelling as all animals are free range on pasture). Accommodation is either in our log house (5 b
_wwoof_travel: Grande Prairie AB,  Dawson Creek BC

name: Shayna - Family home and garden - rural, Homestead, Ranch/ Livestock Farm, Mixed Farm, Farm, Family farm
description:
We are a family owned and run lifestyle/ farm start-up. Located in rural Canada in the Northern Rockies on a beautiful 36 acre property. 

We are a very active and busy family with growing sheep and goat herds, general repairs and maintenance of both land and out buildings, fencing, and a large commercial greenhouse. There is always something to do and there is never a dull moment. We as a family have a great appreciation for the land and all it gives us. We are continually finding ways to reduce waste and reuse materials, and conduct any maintenance required for both structures and land in a sustainable, efficient, and low-cost fashion. 
Since moving onto the farm in 2020, our livestock has doubled in such a short period of time. Thus, doubling the work load and consuming more time. 
Our long-term goal is to be fully self-sufficient and eco-friendly (solar energy, etc..) as well as selling organic meat products, herbs, baked goods, and natural health/ beauty products. 
This has not been fully reached yet. Being a start-up, the farm is very much still in transition from being a dated log home on 36 acres that had not received any care for many years previous; to becoming the fully functioning and sustainable farm. We are a hard working family of three passionate about farming, animals, and the many benefits hemp/cannabis have to offer.  Each day we take small steps towards achieving our goals. 

Rustic Duck Run Is only a few hours drives from the Liard Hotsprings- one of our main attractions here in the Northern Rockies! Along the way to the hot springs there are many hiking trails, camping, and plenty of fishing. As an outdoors family we love camping, hunting, and fishing in this area. We’ve been here for years and still have yet to grow tired of all the breathtaking mountains, lakes, and wildlife that surrounds us. Our location is perfect for those who are also on their way or wanting to see Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Alaska!

Organic methods: Ecolo

name: Andrew - Family home and garden - rural, Agritourism Destination, Hobby Farm, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures
description:
Antbear Lodge is one of those smaller owner run establishments with 15 accommodation units as either mountain view suites or garden view suites. Sustainability and responsible tourism are a huge part of the ethos of our lodge. And we try to do just about everything ourselves. It starts with our alternative structures and ends with the cheese on your breakfast table.

The lodge includes a restaurant, small bar and lounge. We cook all our meals from scratch and have our own organic vegetable garden. The meals we prepare have our South African twist to them and we cater for all dietary requirements with absolute pleasure. We have our own chickens for eggs, cows for milk and bake our own bread. And as I already mentioned we even make our own cheese. Yes Antbear Lodge is really a special place.

Organic methods: permaculture using compost, home made charcoal

Certifications: We are supplying the lodge and ourselves so there is no benefit 

Current projects: Restoring old dairy into self catering cottages.
Horse training using natural horsemanship
wood working creating artistic funiture

Internet access available

name: Bjarni - Family farm
description:
We are two I and my wife on the farm, sometimes with some staff and our sons try and help us out as much as they can, we are mostly growing berries herbs and vegetables. We are also making jams, sauces, smoking fish and cheese and other food products from what we are growing. There is a small store on the farm where we sell our products and we also have a small restaurant in an old barn (only pre-ordered for groups) we don't have a lot of animals but fishes, ducks and chicken.

About Help:

We are looking for wwoofers to help us with our farm. Main work on the farm is gardening, planting and harvesting picking and packing berries and wild mushrooms, cleaning weeds and all normal farming jobs. There is also kitchen work (kitchen for the shop) involved, for exmaple packaging and labeling or cleaning and just assisting.

It would be nice to have your own rubber boots and warm clothes for the work outside.

We are working from 13 pm - 18/19 pm (it depends how much work and which day).
Normally we are working 6 days a week, so there is one day off, which you can choose after discuss with us. But we are flexible and trying to make everything possible.

About Food:

We offer free food, but you have to help yourself with breakfast and lunch.
At dinner we all eat together and we will cook for you. No problem if you are vegetarian or vegan or whatever.

About Accommodation:

You will have your own private cabin/cottage if possible (single or for 2), shared bathroom with other wwoofers We have enough towels and bed sheets so you don't need to bring your sleeping bag. Accommodation is heated and you can use the washing machine at any time.

About Transport:

From Reykjavik you can take a bus or plane to Akureyri, then you can go by bus to Dalvík or we can lift you up in Akureyri if we will be available.

Organic methods: planting & harvesting

Certifications: not certified but everything is growing organic

Current projects: Growing berries herbs and vegetables,feeding the fish

name: Jeremy and Megan - Family Farm in Coastal CA - Chickens, Goats, Cows, Garden & Construction
description:
We have immediate openings!

Join us on the beautiful Central Coast of California for a fully immersive farming experience from livestock to gardening, farm stand and farmers market, and construction mentoring if you're interested!

Our farm is located in a beautiful canyon in Lompoc, California, only a few miles from Surf Beach, and the Vandenberg Space Force Base launch pad where NASA and SpaceX launch rockets into space . We specialize in raising chickens, and are known for helping others get started with growing their own food. We build chicken coops and offer poultry supplies for those just starting with chickens. We also operate a local Farm Box Subscription (CSA) Program, run an Honor System Farm Stand, and participate at our local Farmer's Market on Sundays. In addition to chickens, we have Jersey dairy cows and dwarf Nigerian goats for visitors to enjoy. 

Your Focus: WWOOF-USA guests primarily work in our garden and with the animals. Daily tasks, referred to here as "rounds," include feeding and watering the livestock, including laying hens, young chicks, cows, and goats. Regular tasks include harvesting, washing, and packaging produce and eggs, packing produce boxes, and stocking the farm stand.  Weekly tasks include cleaning stalls for chickens, moving chickens into larger spaces as they mature . Occasional tasks may include milking the cows, settling baby chicks into their brooders, gardening, staffing the farmer's market, and other farm maintenance and construction projects. 

Committment and Hours: We require a minimum stay of two weeks, but give priority for those who can join us long-term. A typical week is 8:00am-3:00pm with an hour lunch Monday through Friday, and 9:00am - 10:00am on Saturday and Sunday just for feeding/watering animals and collecting eggs for the chickens. The schedule will shift if you staff the farmer's market on Sunday. We also send our guests for pick-ups at our local partner farms and farmer's markets, so you'll have an oppo

name: Denys - Family home and garden - rural
description:
We are Denys and Anastasia and we are a young family which decided to live far from the city and to build ecological permocultural farm in a beautiful place. Near we have a forest and a few lakes, a lot of views and places to walk and relax. In our new farm we already have beehives, crater garden, young forest, vegetable garden, lake, terraces for planting. We built a beautiful house of natural materials. For the guests we can offer private little house with kitchen and bedroom. Shower and WC are outside. Would you like to live in a quite zone among the nature, to communicate with new culture, to feel atmosphere of Ukrainian village and to do interesting things? If you are ready to see something new you are welcome!!
                                        PROGRAM for VOLUNTEERS 2022!

Projects for 15 of March - 15 of April:

1. To plant trees to forest-garden above the Crater Garden
2. To pine-trees forest
3. To make new greenhouse beds.
4. To prepare staring-house

Projects for 15 of April - 15 of May:

1. Making Japanese beehives
2. To plant orchad forest.
3. Building dome-greenhouse in the Crater Garden
4. To plant vegetables.
5. To do tasks with bees. 

Projects for 15 of May - 1st of July:

1. To plant vegetable garden
2. Building of Greenhouse
3. Building of compost toilet and shower in the upper area
4. Building of constructions with supports for blackberries and grapes
5. Widening of the bee-garden, work with new families.   


Many tasks are creative and are appropriate both for women and men.

What do you need to do in order to participate in our progects?

1. To contact us and make small interview in skype call to tell about your goals to come to our farm.

2. 5-6 hours of helping per day

3. To have a sober lifestyle

We are always open to give you tasks which are the most interesting for you.

Don't be shy to contact us and we are sure that you will have interesting experience of natural life!

Organic methods: we

name: Kylik - Agritourism Destination
description:
It is our mission to provide visitors from all over the world with an authentic experience of the unique atmosphere and culture of our home in Canada’s North. As a well-established tourism company, we are diversifying our impacts and create a more resilient business. Doing this is not only fulfilling our long-term mission of developing a self-sustaining Arctic Eco-Village, but also investigating innovative solutions to major obstacles facing communities in the North: food and housing insecurity, decolonizing our economy, capacity development and cultural revitalization.

Sustainable farming is not the only focus we have here, but we are continuously developing ideas and trying new farming methods. We are happy to find people from all over the world who are as motivated as we are to make living in the Arctic more sustainable.

Organic methods: In the summer time, when we enjoy 24 hour sunshine, we grow our plants in greenhouses without the use of any chemical/artificial fertilizers. We reuse our compost and fish compost and our farming equipment is on its way to being 100 % solar powered soon.
Since the community of Inuvik is located so far north, it is dependent on regular shipments from the South and not self-sustainable which has a huge impact on the environment and food prices. Our approach is contributing to a more sustainable life in Canada's Arctic.

Certifications: The Okpik Arctic Village is still in the stage of development. We are going to apply for an official certification once we are set up properly.

We welcome anyone who is legally allowed to work in Canada and understands the following:

- We are an off-grid bush camp which means we do not have running water and no consistent power supply
- Nothing is automatic – everything has to be done manually, including chopping wood, hauling water, shovelling snow, building and maintaining cabins/tents, cleaning the dog yard ... etc.
- Farming is only possible in summer. If you stay with us in winter, there wil

name: Veronika - Quirky family home and garden on beautiful Haida Gwaii
description:
The Two Horizons Farm (or maybe one day full farm again--) is located on the north end of Tlell, a small community on Haida Gwaii in Northern BC. This entire archipelago is the unceded traditional territory of the Haida Nation and has a population of about 4200 people spread out over 10 different communities. To get to Haida Gwaii,  there is a nice, long (7h) ferry ride across the Hecate Strait from Prince Rupert or you can fly to either Masset or Sandspit from Vancouver. Our 15 acre farm is about 2km from the ocean on the east coast of Graham Island, almost exactly halfway between Masset and Daajing Giids (Queen Charlotte). The land has been mostly cleared by the previous owners, but there are some stunning forests all around the property, including creeks and a beautiful surrounding bog. 
- On the property  we live with three humans , two cats and a varying number of sheep and chickens. We moved in about five years ago and have been steadily rebuilding the place since then. One of us is a barista with a love for  music.  I have a background in horticulture and landscape architecture - love to build, tinker and enjoy the outdoors. The third human lives in her school bus on the property and is a vagabonding fiber and tattoo artist. The property was at some point previously a cattle farm and more recently had a semi-commercial greenhouse operation. We are working towards developing a small-scale, mixed-use  farm with some cooperative elements. Depending on your skills, experience and interest you will be helping with a variety of tasks around the farm. 
We are looking for self-motivated and mature people that like to learn and work hart. 
Your accommodation:
You will have your own space in the small bunkie - an  addition attached to my cabin. The bunkie is a self-contained unit with basic kitchen equipment, a single loft bed and a comfy chair. There is an outhouse nearby, an outdoor shower and the sauna in the main garden.
Depending on my schedule we will share lunch
_wwoof_travel: Masset Airport and the ferry terminal in Skidegate

name: Kellie - Commercial Goat Dairy and Creamery located south of Austin Texas
description:
**We are currently looking for a wwoofers for this summer and fall.  **  **We prefer month or  longer stays.  Require a minimum of 2 weeks stays.  We prioritize longer stays  **

We are a family-run farm raising dairy goats for milk and cheese, along with cattle, chickens, and pigs. As a Licensed Grade A Goat Dairy, we produce and sell grass-fed beef, pastured pork, and handcrafted goat milk soaps and lotions. Everything we do is rooted in hands-on care, quality, and a commitment to building a sustainable farm system.

We’re seeking WWOOFers  who are willing to jump into daily farm life and help with:
• Morning and evening feeding and animal care
• occasional Milking and milk bottling
• Caring for baby goats during kidding season 🐐
• Pitching in on various farm projects as needed

This is hands-on, real farm work, and every day is a little different!

In exchange, you’ll gain valuable, real-world experience, including:
• how to take care of goats, pigs, cows, chickens
• Safe handling and processing of goat milk products
• Animal health, nutrition, and basic veterinary care
• pasture management
• Making goat cheese and other dairy products
• Principles of regenerative agriculture

Spring and summer are especially fun—with lots of baby goats to help care for and socialize!

Accommodations

WWOOFers stay in a dedicated bunkhouse (newer double-wide home) that also serves as our goat nursery in the spring.
• 3 bedrooms / 2 bathrooms 
• Full kitchen and living space
• Internet access
• TV with streaming (Prime & Netflix)

We provide groceries, and you’ll have your own kitchen to prepare meals. We can accommodate most dietary needs, but we love sharing the food we raise on the farm.

The ideal WWOOFer is:
• Curious and eager to learn
• Hardworking and responsible
• Engaged and positive
• Open to sharing ideas and experiences

We require a minimum 2-week stay, but prefer one month or longer.

🚫 No dogs allowed

Our farm is located in a remote area between Bastrop and Lockha

name: Laurence - Located on the shores of Guatemalan's Caribbean, we are building a small homestead in a 4 acres land
description:
We are Laurence and Elisa, 2 nature passionate. Laurence is from Canada and has co-founded a cacao social enterprise in Guatemala 5 years ago. Elisa is from Guatemala and is a bamboo architect. We are passionate in farming, natural construction, and social justice. We are two very pro-active people that always have a few projects going on around the country. Life has been incredibly generous by gifting us a beautiful piece of land on the Atlantic Ocean of Guatemala. The land is PARADISE. We have a 50m white sand beach, a beautiful house, a private cabin, a large piece of land to plant lots of trees. Our dream: to transform this land into a food forest with cacao, of course, and bamboo. create a beautiful permaculture garden that can keep this place self-sustainable within the next 5 years. 

We have 3 dogs and chickens. We dream of adding bees, goats and horses.

A regular day,  At 10 AM we start with the farm work or natural building, which can vary from month to month depending on the project of the moment. We have worked so far on the compost systems, the chicken house, bamboo biochar, compost toilet, experimental garden, tree planting and more. The current and upcoming projects are a volunteer kitchen made of palette and cob also met to be use for processing food from the garden, large-scale garden including 3 sisters and madela garden, irrigation (get it ready before next dry season), herb garden, a tea house/ nursery dome, new designed compost toilet and more. 

Schedule usually looks like this: 
5 days/week
10 AM- 1 PM : Farm work ( Planting, building with bamboo or cob, working on a project) 
2 PM -4 PM: Creative Project ( Finding a creative project for a unique contribution) 
6 PM:  Family Dinner -Included

For Dinners, we usually do rotations between everyone involved in the community for the cooking and cleaning. 

We can teach a lot about cacao and bamboo, some knowledge in permaculture/agroforestry and natural building but we are also figuring it out as
_wwoof_travel: Puerto Barrios Litegua Bus Station or Airport.

name: Mark - Organic growing space in historic Algiers neighborhood of New Orleans
description:
This is a small organic growing space located in the historic Algiers neighborhood of New Orleans. It is located 1 block from the scenic Mississippi River Levee and 1 mile from the Algiers Ferry which runs to and from the French Quarter throughout the day. We are a laid back operation involved in lots of community projects such as hosting a community fridge, having part of the garden dedicated as a free self-harvest area, and promoting alternative economies via bartering produce with several local restaurants, markets, and individuals. Guests stay in a private tiny house. We also have space for campers and moderately sized RVs. People of all skill/knowledge levels are welcome to apply although precedence will be given to individuals with prior food-growing experience.  Day wwoofers and couples welcome.  LGBTQ friendly.        

Organic methods: Soil building utilizing food scraps from local restaurants.
Natural pesticides only.
Water harvesting.
Rain garden utilizing native plants.
Utilization of non-monetary economy via bartering with local gardeners, restaurants, markets, individuals, etc. 

Certifications: I have not yet applied for certification

Current projects: Wwoofers can expect a varied work experience incorporating basic gardening tasks, light carpentry/building, and maintenance/cleaning of community resource areas.  For most of the year we only host one wwoofer at a time so much of the work will be done on your own and can be done at whatever time of day you prefer to work.

name: Brenda - Sustainable farm with horses, dogs and cats, eco tourism in the desert
description:
Welcome in the beautiful desert of Wadi Rum. 

On the edge of the village at the desert of the Wadi Rum there is a special place in progress, where animals, guests, locals and you will meet together. The 7 horses, 4 dogs, 7 cats and me are living already on ths beautful spot in the desert. We, (my local colleague and his family) started to work on our dream from scratch. 
The next phase is almost ready, creating an organic garden with fruit trees and shrubs, a vegetable garden and (medicinal) herbal garden with flowers. Also beautiful shady spaces  and a Bedouin tent for our guests are in use. We're ready to welcome different secies of animals, chickens, sheep and goats to live comfortably with and next to each other. The sun is our friend but in summer sometimes too hot for some plants, animals and us. We found many solutions to create shade and a very good working airco inside the home and the tent to make the life comfortable. Also in wintertime we use heathers inside and wood fire outside to stay warm and cosy.

At this moment we’re are looking for female volunteers for a very special reason. As we like to encourage the local women in the village to come by and learn or get help to start their own organic garden, have some animals and how to treat them well. The women will unfortunately mostly stay away if male company is present. It is part of the culture here in a traditional desert village, in the cities you will see this already different. While our intention is to make women in the village who were not able to study further, more self-sufficient through education, both for themselves and their families. By breathing new life into old crafts techniques. Turning tribe and desert patterns into art objects based on which treasuries the desert will show. To create a sustainable environment accessible to more locals and good for the desert. Once the project is up and running and the women and their family are at ease we will start to welcome all volunteers. I hop
_wwoof_travel: Village of Diseh at the Wadi Rum desert

name: Diedhiou - Farm
description:
The Burok project is a small polyculture farm created in 2013 in Casamance on 2-3 hectares. Vegetables (onions, aubergines, tomatoes, okra, etc.), trees (lemon, cashew, mango, etc.), sheep and poultry are grown. And at the moment a resource house is being created. It will be a place of training, learning, culture, with a library, an educational garden...

Burok is a project open to all interested villagers, and open to the world, thanks to supporters living abroad and wwoofers received for many years, in the family house. You will be welcomed, housed and fed in exchange for a hand in the garden. And above all, you will have a great time and meet some very nice people, I promise!

Certifications: certification wouldn't have any benefits for us

Current projects: The Burok project started in 2012. Between the germination of an idea and the harvesting of results, a lot of water has flowed under the bridge!

The objectives of food self-sufficiency, reducing the hardship of rural life, diversifying the diet, initiating income-generating activities, developing local potential and contributing to the future of young people have already been largely achieved. However, none of us want to stop there!

Let's take stock:
Phase 1 : vegetable farming
We started the project with vegetables who used to be imported from the north or from abroad, or more often not eaten at all! So we helped people from the area to organize vegetables production.

At the same time, the family house was rebuilt

It now provides accommodation for the family, hosts traditional and family events, and accommodates visitors in good conditions, but also receives foreigners and in particular wwoofers.

Phase 2: breeding

Later on, the construction of a shelter for the animals allowed the development of livestock farming. The aim was to increase the workers' income, but it also provided fertiliser for the market gardening.

Today, the farm produces local and broiler chickens, guinea fowl, ducks and sheep. The

name: Daniel - Family farm
description:
Our farm is located in Dalen, West-Telemark.
We (Daphne and Daniel) are living together on Underdal with, (half of the time ) Daniels kids : Johanna (12) Kari (11) and Lambert (9).
 
We live without electricity on the farm because we like to be as self sufficient as possible :) That means that we cook on fire and  keep the house warm with fire. 

Tokke is a small mountainous area reaching from close to sea level to immaculate eminence. Here the aim is to build a sustainable life, living of the land, with our animals. We gain a lot of our knowledge from studying the new settlers in the states and early farming culture in general, as we see a big value in self- sufficiency. Telemark is a region with a rich history related to the work we do, so we are proud of being able to keep a lot of these traditions alive. Daniel and Daphne are working with a museum of cultural history from spring to fall, where they also maintain fields with grains, potatoes and flax. We also plow fields with the horse and grow vegetables the traditional way. Surrounding the farmyard there are pastures where the animal's grass and a forest where we get materials and firewood. As for the animals we have a small number of sheep and hens, three horses, two cats and two dogs. The sheeps are ranging free in the mountain during the summer, and we spend the first part of September bringing them home again. There's no tractor at the farm so the horses are helping a lot with different tasks, for instance haymaking, plowing, harrowing, pulling timber and transport.
A homesteader must acquire a lot of different skills, so by working with us you can expect to learn a lot depending on the season. Some things we do throughout a year: shear sheep, butcher, tan hides, process wool, work with textile, fish with nets, make firewood, do forest work, carpentry, woodcraft, haymaking, collect seeds, forage mushrooms, berries and medicinal plants. Daniel is also very passionate about folk music, building instruments an

name: Musa - Cacilia garden is found in mkwajuni Songwe Tanzania 🇹🇿
description:
My name is Musa, I'm 28 years old. My wife is Marietta And my son is Christian. I'm an organic farmer from mkwajuni Songwe district.

I'm living with my family. I have two rooms for WWOOFers interested to join and support my farm project, live together as a family member, while experiencing our local lifestyle.

We eat food together with volunteers as family including; breakfast, lunch and dinner.

We grow crop varieties including maize, groundnuts and beans as well as poultry at the farm. Main activities at the farm includes land cultivation with local tools, weeding, planting, harvesting and feeding chickens.

I would like to teach WWOOFERS how to grow bean, maize and groundnuts. They will also learn how to manage farm activities through organic methods which includes; crop rotation, biological pest control, compost manure and preservation of seeds.

Apart from my family organic farm project, I also work at my school's organic farm project with poultry and crops, for feeding the children. My school's name is Cacilia Primary School.

During free hours after farming activities, WWOOFers will have opportunities of visiting places of nature including SAZA waterfalls, KAWETERE Viewpoint 131.1km known for its scenic views of the surrounding landscape often described as a "Tourist Jewel in Development" a good destination for enjoying nature and the unique geographical features of Songwe area (It takes 2hrs 46min from Mkwajuni to Kawetere mountains). 

During free hours after work, WWOOFers will have an opportunity of visiting the world's eighth meteorite "MBOZI METEORITE" weighing an estimated 25 metric tonnes, it's around 3m long and 1m tall. Mbozi Meteorite is the most popular tourist attraction found in Songwe region (It is about 61km from Mkwajuni). Visiting SAZA mining about 2km from the farm, 15 minutes' drive by car or motorcycle, is part of an opportunity around Mkwajuni.

name: Frank - Community farm
description:
A fazenda tem 2 hectares e está localizada em uma região exuberante da floresta tropical (mata atlântica) com vários rios e cachoeiras. O projeto ainda está em pleno desenvolvimento com bioconstruções e SAFs. Realizamos atividades relacionadas à permacultura e agroecologia, entre elas, estamos construindo a primeira casa no local (biobuilding), cultivamos algumas mudas de árvores nativas e frutíferas que visam uma área experimental de agrofloresta. Nós temos um camping instalado, a casa já é utilizável com um mezanino para dormitório, possuindo uma cozinha coletiva provisória com fogão de pedra a lenha, um banheiro seco e um banheiro confortável. O sitio já possui eletricidade onde a casa está sendo construída. Precisamos de pessoas de grande determinação que se identifiquem com um ambiente muito natural, dispostos a viver coletivamente, abertos para interagir com as diferenças e com pouca infra-estrutura. Nosso projeto é excelente para quem quer aprender as primeiras práticas de agroflorestas e bioconstruções.

Possui área de acampamento, onde o conforto dependerá da estrutura que o voluntário tem (como barraca e equipamentos) e também um mezanino instalado na casa com um bom conforto e vista para o Canyon Índios Coroados onde o hospede poderá instalar-se. Podemos receber dois voluntários por vez.

cozinha coletiva e aberta para demais tipos de alimentação.

dois amigos que são nativos da região, iniciando uma fazenda orientada principalmente pela permacultura. Já estamos desenvolvendo projetos em ecoturismo e aventura (passeio aos cânions da região). Nós enxergamos uma chance de reconciliar atividades de permacultura e agroecologias com o potencial do turismo da região e, assim, expandiremos ainda mais o foco dado a essas atividades.

Acreditamos que o voluntariado pode aprender sobre uma bioconstrução (atividade que está sendo desenvolvida no momento) e que a vida é um projeto natural com a mesma facilidade de trabalho com a música, poesia e cachoeira. Somos
_wwoof_travel: A fazenda está localizada na cidade de Praia Grande, SC, também conhecida por capital dos canyons, justamente por possuir dois parques nacionais (aparados da serra) contento a maior cadeia de canyons do Brasil. A Vila da fazenda é a Vila Rosa que fica loc

name: Obi Jaunito - Let's Grow Trees: Reforestation, Community Activities + Organic Permaculture Farm + Tea & Coffee
description:
NOTE:  WE ARE FULL THROUGH 2026 
At this time we are not hosting short term stays.
Thank you for those who have taken an interest and supported our projects.

We are Guardians and stewards of the earth and the soul.

We are a small reforestation, land regeneration, and natural farming project in the cloud forest. We are active in our community and on our farm. Trees, plants, animals, good people, gratitude, and good experiences are our passions here.

We live in the cloud forest with a big open kitchen, dining room, and living room. The roof is tin. There are several dorms and shared rooms. Sometimes we are lucky and see the toucans in our yard, always we hear the birds - including the giant flocks of green parrots who love to fly by.

We love ethical, kind, and responsible people. We are looking for positive, independent, motivated people who can take initiative. People who can appreciate a laid-back, fun, rustic, simple homey lifestyle.

We are located in an area of Peru famous for being the main producer of tea in the country. It is less than a 4 hour drive from Cusco and just 3 from the Sacred Valley. It is a simple, uncomplicated farming community with a lot of wild life and the climate is not too hot and not too cold. We are trekking distance to Machu Picchu OR just a short 2-hour walk along the hydroelectric path which is just 85 minutes away by car.  The area has many waterfalls and great hikes.  A hours drive away are  the hot springs of Cocalmayo used since the Incan times for both relaxation and rejuvenation. 

We are a vegetarian project and focus on education about making conscious choices to positively effect global health through the consumption of less meat products (Meat and feed companies contribute directly to the destruction of Amazonian forests). If needed, there are many nearby restaurants in Huyro where you can find inexpensive, non-vegetarian meals.

Being Close to Nature: Planting seeds, maintaining land, and learning about organic pest mana

name: Suman - ​sikone homestead: rural assam, riverside with wi-fi, near guwahati and bhutan
description:
​i live alone on a peaceful homestead in a natural village in rural assam, dedicated to rural development and sustainable living. my project focuses on organic agriculture and animal husbandry in a serene, natural setting. i share this space with a variety of farm animals and pets, including cattle, goats, rabbits, a rooster, ducks, chickens, and my friendly dogs and cats. the property is an expansive three-quarters of an acre (0.3 hectares) featuring a small pond, walking paths, and seasonal fields of wheat and mustard.

your time here will be spent engaging with the daily rhythms of farm life. activities include caring for our livestock and poultry, managing the vegetable and fruit areas, and maintaining the mustard and wheat fields. i have many plans to develop this homestead, the nearby village environment, and even broader initiatives for humanity, and i am looking for the energy and help to make them a reality. i am happy to share my knowledge of rural development and traditional assamese farming while we work toward these goals together.

accommodation is provided in a minimal and open housing style, designed to stay connected with the natural environment. the area is secured with netted fencing for safety and comfort. the residence is equipped with electricity, wi-fi, and clean water from a private tubewell. i would love to cook for you, or alternatively, we can cook together using local grains and vegetables. feel free to have your meals alone or together as you like. please note that i maintain a strictly smoke-free, alcohol-free, and drug-free environment on the farm.

​i am an avid learner, currently studying languages and music through apps like duolingo and ling. i love listening to audiobooks, podcasts, and music while i work. i have many active hobbies—including table tennis, badminton, jogging, weight lifting, and cycling—as well as spending time in the nearby river water. while i haven't practiced these as much lately due to a lack of companions, i
_wwoof_travel: Public auto;jailuchi village(100 meter),bus;muktapur(5 kilometer),train;rangia(15km). airport;guwahati (50km)

name: Gianpiero - Fattoria di famiglia, Castagneto, Frutteto, Bosco
description:
News (leggi bene):

Per problemi e condizioni familiari possiamo ospitare per questa stagione persone che cerchino o siano disposte ad una situazione di maggiore autonomia rispetto al solito. Il tempo di condivisione sarà principalmente quello del lavoro - il mattino - e quasi sempre il pranzo insieme. Resta invece più libero il tempo del pomeriggio e soprattutto la cena/sera. Per la cena e i pasti non condivisi è a disposizione dei wwofer uno spazio rustico con cucinino da campeggio, lavandino e qualche mobiletto, per potersi gestire in autonomia. Per maggiori informazioni o esigenze scrivete pure.

Ecco le nostre prossime disponibilità:

- a LUGLIO, disponibili solo per esperienze brevi di pochi giorni, da concordare. Lavori di taglio erba, pulizia, carpenteria.

- ad AGOSTO, come spiegato nelle news. Lavoro principale spaccare ed accatastare legna, oltre a raccolta frutta, gestione degli animali e sfalcio erba

- a SETTEMBRE, per almeno 3 settimane, cerchiamo persona che abbia piacere a stare qui più in autonomia del solito perché andando al lavoro fuori casa non saremo sempre presenti. Ecco i lavori che ci saranno : taglio erba, spacco legna, raccolta e gestione dei frutti di Asimina Triloba, gestione degli animali. Possibile gestire in autonomia i tempi, anche perché sono periodi in cui io lavoro a scuola quindi non sono sempre a casa

- a OTTOBRE: per almeno 3 settimane, cerchiamo persona che abbia piacere a stare qui più in autonomia del solito perché andando al lavoro fuori casa non saremo sempre presenti. Ecco i lavori che ci saranno : raccolta, gestione e lavorazione delle castagne. Mese impegnativo ma anche molto ricco perché si può partecipare alle iniziative culturali e sociali dell'associazione di Castanicoltori di cui facciamo parte
-------------

Viviamo in una piccola casa fattoria immersa nei boschi di una piccola valletta non lontana dalla città di Bergamo (10 km). Con noi (marito e moglie) vivono un gatto, una coppia di asini e un piccolo gruppo
_wwoof_travel: Aeroporto Bergamo (Milano) Orio al Serio; stazione di Nembro Centro

name: Dálida - Somos una huerta regenerativa de jóvenes campesinos, producimos gran diversidad de alimentos.
description:
Nos dedicamos a brindar alimentos de calidad, sanos y frescos; provenientes de una producción agroecológica libre de agroquímicos, basados en la biodiversidad como forma de respetar e integrar todas las formas de vida, y en la conservación y regeneración del suelo. Adquirimos además un compromiso social de generar procesos de aprendizaje donde toda la comunidad puede ser parte, pues es nuestra tarea como jóvenes fortalecer producciones agrícolas de forma regenerativa, para así asegurar un empalme generacional en el campo, la permanencia de nuestras vocaciones en el territorio, y la vida misma en el planeta.

Generalmente hacemos actividades diarias, por ejemplo sembrando semillas en el vivero de plántulas, regando las huertas, preparación de terrazas para sembrar, trasplante de hortalizas, cosechar hortalizas y participar de la labor de la post cosecha, deshierbar las terrazas.

la alimentación es basada en comida vegetariana en su mayoría de la huerta agroecológica, incluida comida típica de la región como el desayuno: calentado de frijoles, arepa, huevos, queso , café o chocolate o aromáticas , almuerzo: sopas de verduras, cremas, verduras salteadas, frijoles, arroz, ensaladas   y jugos de frutas naturales, para la cena: pastas, refritos con arepa, sopas, focaccia, sándwiches  vegetarianos, hamburguesas vegetarianas y jugos naturales. El alojamiento es campesino cómodo con habitaciones privadas, cocina común,  un baño compartido con agua caliente,  con Wifi,  no hay televisor, tenemos paneles solares.

Estamos ubicados a solo 15 minutos caminando del parque principal del pueblo de Jerico , es un pueblo hermoso, colonial, tranquilo, seguro y muy cultural, hay programaciones casi todo el año de eventos culturales, por ejemplo conciertos de música clásica, folclórica y popular . Estamos cerca de museos de la música,  museo de antropología, teatro municipal, museo de arte moderno, Jardín botánico, senderos naturales y un rio limpio con cascadas.
_wwoof_travel: Terminal de buses del Sur (Medellin)  Taquilla Jericó

name: Angela - Ox-powered market garden and cow sanctuary
description:
Hello! Our farm is a small organic market garden located near Stockholm, Sweden. My name is Angela (34) and I run the farm full-time with the part-time help of my partner Uttam (42) and our two oxen, Madhu and Chandra. We are environmentalists, animal activists, and are dedicated to the sustainable stewardship of our land.

**For the 2026 season we are fully booked. However, we may consider hosting extra wwoofers who have a lot of farming experience and truly align with our mission**

Every morning and evening we milk our two rescued cows by hand. The cows and oxen are the center of our farm. The manure they create becomes the compost for our vegetables, the oxen help us with farm chores, and the cows provide a surplus of milk and cream.  WWOOFers can expect to learn all aspects of compassionate cow care and have many interactions with our calm and cuddly companions. 

We grow vegetables for the Stockholm Farmers’ Market which runs 12 weeks from August through October. We focus primarily on heirloom tomatoes, pumpkins, squash, beans, pots of herbs and whole chili plants. Although we also grow a diversity of other vegetables including radishes, kale, turnips, leeks, cucumbers, etc. WWOOFers will be involved in all garden tasks such as seeding, transplanting, and weeding. Those who visit during market season will also join in harvesting and potentially selling with me at the market.

We offer incredible homemade vegan and lacto-vegetarian meals and desserts. Uttam comes from an ethnically diverse family and enjoys cooking Indian, Spanish, and French cousine. Throughout the summer we love making pizza from scratch in our wood-fired stone pizza oven. With all of our cooking we only use non-violent milk products from our own cows - milk, yoghurt, butter, ghee, cheese, and cream.  We are happy to adapt to vegan WWOOFers. For breakfast we provide homemade breads, jams,  milk/cereal, etc. 

WWOOFers will stay in a  small simple one-bedroom cabin next to our family home. Eve

name: Kris - Medicinal Herb Farm, Educational Centre, Reskilling Festival, Agritourism Destination, Eco Retreat
description:
*Note: We only offer outdoor communal spaces in our campground, with a covered outdoor kitchen, fire pit, cook-stove, and well-appointed compost outhouses with running water wash stations. Because of this, we will only accept WWOOFers during summer months, unless our camper is available during spring months.

We welcome you to our prairie homestead – 80 acres of poplar and pine forest, marsh, and meadow, where we run a medicinal herb farm, Awaken Herbs, retreat spaces, and more. Situated on the boundary of farmland and cottage country, the farm is a stone’s throw from Lake Winnipeg and her beautiful white-sand beaches, as well as breathtaking boreal hiking experiences. It’s a place where you can come to plant your feet on the ground, open your heart to the wide open prairie sky, and reconnect with skills of the past.

The farm lies on the original lands of Anishinaabeg, Cree, Oji-Cree, Dakota, and Dene peoples, and on the homeland of the Métis Nation, on Treaty 1 territory and we are neighbours to the Brokenhead Ojibway Nation. We are city folk who have ventured out to the country, to live closer to the land, and we have a heart to facilitate reconnection with nature through land-based experiences, reconnection with others through a vibrant and loving community, and reconnection with important skills through workshops on homesteading and permaculture topics, retreats, and our festival-conference-summer camp experience, Take Root Fest. Our family is comprised of two parents, three young adults, and one teen who learns at home. Willow is a gentle giant Great Pyrenees who is an amazing companion and protector for our children and their hens. She loves visitors and warms to them quickly. She is training up Morel, our new Great Pyrenees puppy.

The farm is roughly half pasture/meadow and half forest. There are several different forest ecosystems right here on the farm, from a boggy area with mosses, ferns and many other unique plants and fungi, to mixed poplar and alder

name: Mohammed - Multiple farming and well established organic farming
description:
Mr. Matinur Rahman
Matinurs Farm is a large big home-centered organic farm at Boalmari, Faridpur. It is just near the largest Padma Bridge, only three hours journey from Dhaka. Other than homestead farming, it is producing organic vegetables, fruits, spices, rice, banana and jutefibre. Three large ponds with fishes; several milking cows, goats, chicken, ducks and pigeons are reared. The farm is very near to Faridpur city. The village has more than two hundred farmers who are farming at their homesteads which are very rich with multiple cropping cultures and all are organic. WWOOFers are enjoying the site, especially the mighty river Padma, the downstream of the great Ganges.

Tropical monsoon agricultural practices are done through out the year.  Crops, cattle farming, fish farming all are in the homestead farm.

The wwoofers will have food with the host family and also the accommodations in the same house.

My hobby is farming, hiking and swimming facilities are available there in the near by river

name: Melissa - Authentic Wellness Retreat Ranch in the forest
description:
Welcome…our ranch in the woods is called Authentic Wellness Experiences…We have organic gardens, horses and alpacas, a woodworking shop and Art studio as well as a homemade nature spa Center…Our 50 acre property surrounds a our beautiful private lake.  There is always on going up keep to the property as there are many buildings and fences…We are Natures Artists here, we follow the sun and watch the sky’s turn the soil, harvest the fruits, we have lovely spots for camping and or putting a small camper…and also have options for lodging…we run a business of wellness experiences here where there is a lot of guest services and nature education opportunities…we look forward to meeting people who want to learn to live in tune with themselves and the world around them ✨

A typical day in the life of a WOOFER here would be...Depending on how you like to start your day you will have option for eggs and a homemade pastry of sorts for Break...a smoothie or we can discuss what your preference for your ideal breakfast would be...you will come to the office to get your meals...I will be making basic stews and soups, burritos sandwiches etc for your meals...The days activities will be based on who is working with you or if you are working alone...The Jobs include Artistic Fencing around the property with our live edge wood, farm work such as grooming animals and cleaning up the stable area...There is also flower and vegetable gardens to weed, plant and maintain on the property...along with an apple and pear tree orchard for harvesting...You will be working with Todd property owner and or Melissa Property manager...There will be live edge sign making for the art walk on the property you will get to participate in as well as other woodworking opportunities...

You will Learn skills such as Working with wood working tools and machinery...chain saws, skill saw, hammer, drill, sander planer etc.  You will also get to learn some wild food on the property as well as plant identification a

name: Louise - Goat farm and cheesery/Homestead
description:
We are a french couple, living in a remote village in outer-Kakheti (David Gareji area).
We work all year long (gardening, foraging, sheperding) to have good food in our plates and make our animals (mainly goat) happy.

We can teach how to milk and make cheese, but we usually don't ask volunteers to do those main tasks. They'll more be include in lighter tasks like sheperding (1/2 day), gardening, cooking and participating in our everyday life. 

For May, there's a old house 5min to walk from our, with no current water but electricity. Showers and laundry are taken to our place. 
Most of the time we cook and take meals together (3 times a day), but if anyone feels to be on his own there are some essentials to cook in the other house.
Then we want to build a tiny house for volunteers and we might need help in this task. During the building period, volunteer will be host in our house, which is small, but it will be a personnal room.

We love cooking good food, and we have a musician in the house (and many instrument)

name: Scott - Agritourism, Homestead, Vineyard, Aquaponics / bioponics farm, Nursery, Orchard
description:
Lives change and paths diverge and so it is with the Riverside Food Forest in California! Having been here since 2017, it is time to move on to bigger and better things! From a compacted dirt lot, I leave it a nature rich environment with bounty of food still growing and expanding.

This change comes with some great new adventures and the opportunity for those truly interested in seeing a homestead grow from the wilds of nature. This time in the experience will be near the coast of North Carolina on a 1 acre forest lot!

First thing you need to know is that we're not your average farm in any sense of the words! We use a wide array of sustainable practices and incorporate strategies that you won't find at other locations. Permaculture, Back To Eden, and hundreds of Sustainable methods are used here. With many techniques created here.

Riverside was the Beta Testing Site for many of the techniques that will be used to develop the new property into a rich and very diverse environment of health and learning! 

NOW ACCEPTING DAY AND SHORT VISITS ONLY!

Set in the picturesque costal region of North Carolina, this wild, heavily brushed wooded 1 acre property will progressively transition into a bounty of food producing and nature encompassed paradise.

Focused on an agro-tourism format, with a Farmers Market, Backyard Nursery and commercial Moringa operation on the development schedule, this is a REAL example of what it takes to create an edible landscape and homestead. Hands-on experience here will give you a better understanding of not simply the work involved, but hundreds of techniques involved in integrating 5,000+ edible and medicinal plants successfully into a smaller space, with plenty of room for guests, backyard nursery, farmers market and Moringa operations.

Please understand, do to a lack of accommodations we are only able to host Day visits or those that are willing to camp out. If you have your own home on wheels, we are working to accommodate slightly longe

name: ( Niní) Maria Agostinha - Produção de Mudas orgânicas
description:
