Olá,  meu nome é Maria Agostinha (NINI), cuido de um projeto de produção de mudas orgânicas junto com o meu companheiro Jorge. Produzimos diversas variedade de mudas, como: ervas medicinais, árvores frutíferas, hortaliças e PANCs.

A Agricultura Orgânica desempenha um papel crucial na preservação da saúde ambiental e
humana, salvaguardando as gerações vindouras da contaminação desencadeada pelo uso
excessivo de produtos químicos na produção de alimentos. Esta prática consciente não
apenas protege o ar, o solo e a água, mas também preserva a integridade dos
ecossistemas, promovendo a harmonia entre os seres humanos, os animais e a natureza.
Em nossa fábrica de hortas, nosso compromisso vai além: disseminar os saberes populares
e tradicionais da agroecologia, aliando-os ao desenvolvimento de mudas orgânicas. Assim,
aspiramos a um futuro onde a coexistência entre o homem e o meio ambiente seja não
apenas equilibrada, mas também próspera e enriquecedora para todos os envolvidos.

Por aqui, normalmente tomamos café da manhã, almoçamos e jantamos juntos. Estando sempre abertos a trocar experiências na cozinha.

Gostamos bastante de uma boa conversa, de participar de encontros agroecologicos. Como ficamos próximos a Belo Horizonte, existem bastantes atividades culturais na cidade e bastante atividades de natureza nas cidades próximas.
_wwoof_travel: Estação de Metro Eldorado, Estação rodoviária de Belo Horizonte e Aeroporto COFINS

name: Asser - Fruit tree and date tree farm in the outskirts of Cairo
description:
Hello Woofers,
My name is Asser and I live in my farm with my wife and son. We have 2 donkeys, 2 goats, 10 chickens, 1 ostrich and 1 dog. We grow date trees, oranges and Mangoes as well as a variety of vegetables. We are just learning to be organic and we are experimenting with different methods.

Our typical day would start by feeding and watering the animals and letting out the chickens. Then we could do some spraying the mango leaves with a dish soap mixture, water the plants and collect some produce and maybe planting some seeds  .Before the sun goes down we feed them animals again and put some of them to bed.

We have a shared kitchen space where we provide all the basic necessities including food and cookware. Each member of our household and woofer will need responsible for coming one meal a week. Most of the time we will eat separately but we love to occasionally sit together for a nice meal and gathering. The woofers will have their own private room and bathroom.

I my family and I enjoy traveling, animals, cooking and karate.  There is tons of activities and places to see around our place. You can easily access Cairo and ask them historical sites on your days off. We can help you to access public transportation and also there is something like Ubers here too. There are supermarkets in our area also and you can get all you need near by.

name: Stephane - Ecole de Permaculture en environnement tropical humide - Bahia
description:
Olá, sou Stephane, professor de Permacultura, seja bem-vindo ao nosso projeto de regeneração de terras e agrofloresta. Estamos localizados no estado da Bahia, no litoral brasileiro, próximos à praia. O foco do projeto é o ensino da Permacultura, que inclui uma grande panóplia de práticas, incluindo floresta de comida e bioconstrução. Nós nos concentramos na sustentabilidade e no Carbon Farming.

Trabalhomos 6 horas por dia  5 dias por semana. Nao temos atividades repetitivas. A ideia nao é producao intensiva, é ensinar. Os voluntarios sao considerados como estudantes. Implementamos varios tipos de sistema agroflorestais; Agricultura Sintropica, Processos tipo Fukuoka, Jardim da Amazonia. e varios tipos de bioconstrucao; Hiperadobe, Adobe, Pau a Pique
As atividades praticas incluem explicações sobre a Permacultura e encima disso tem 4 horas por semana dedicadas a teoria da Permacultura.

O alojamento basico é barraca de camping em ambiante florestal protegido. O voluntário precisa levar sua barraca.  Voluntarios participam nas refecoes (ovo-vegetariano) e atividade comunitarias.

Ficamos 900 m das magnificas praias da Bahia e 1500 m do centro turistico , famoso no brasil.

O Voluntário deve ter já uma experiência em cultivo e vida na roça. 

Hello, I'm Stephane, Permaculture teacher, welcome to our project of land regeneration and agroforestry. We are located in the Bahia state in Brazilian litoral close to the beach. The focus of the project is Permaculture teaching which include a large panoply of practice including food forest and greenbuilding. We focus on sustainability and Carbon Farming. We work 6 horas por dia, 5 dias por semana. We do not have repetitive activities. The idea is not intensive production, it is teaching. Volunteers are considered students. We implement several types of agroforestry systems; Agricultura Syntropica, Fukuoka-type method, Jardim da Amazonia. and various types of greenbuilding; Hiperadobe, Adobe, Pau a Pique. 4 hours per week are d

name: Violeta - Organic cattle farm
description:
1. Finca La Chaya is a 400ha Cattle Farm since 1882. My husband Peter and me grew up in Germany with a almost "green" education. When we decided to manage the farm, we moved from Germany to Mexico in 2009. Since then, the farm is managed in a completely organic way. We are looking for people who can contribute with their knowledge to improve our methods and become more efficient. Please, please, please...it's only recommended for hard working people. Nature here is rough, sun burns, insects  chase you, reptils can come out of the nothing. Strong physical work 💪 is our daily bread.
We nearly do all by ourselves and Wwoofers can join the activities: seed corn, get the milk from our mother cows (by hand!), making cheese, move the cattle from land to land, process the meet in our little butcher store, repair maschines etc etc. There are a lot of things to do. As there is such a variety of work to do and it takes time to explain and show you around, we only accept a duration of stay of minimum 4 weeks.
The next city is only five minutes away (by car) and there our Wwoofers can rest and get to know a typical mexican small town.

name: Gukhee - <GB122>하회, 함께 만들고 나누며 삶이 자라는 곳/Hahoe, where life grows through sharing and creating together
description:
✅ 호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
저는 농사를 짓는 방식으로 작업하는 아티비스트 입니다. 안동 하회마을 인근에서 4년째 농사를 기반으로 다양한 시도를 이어오고 있습니다. 처음에는 제 몸과 마음을 돌보는 작은 시작이었지만, 지금은 “조금씩 농부”들이 모여 함께 일하고 나누는 공동체가 되었습니다.
밭에서의 노동은 때로 놀이가 되고, 축제가 되고, 자연스럽게 관계를 만들어왔습니다. 이곳에서의 경험이 누군가에게 작은 선물이 되는 것을 여러 번 지켜보며, 이제 더 많은 사람들과 이 시간을 나누고자 호스트가 되었습니다.
저희는 5살, 9살 두 아이와 함께 살고 있으며, 농사와 작업을 함께하는 팀원들이 있습니다. 하회마을 인근의 밭에서는 계절에 따라 다양한 작물을 기르고, 매년 10월에는 땅 위에서 열리는 작은 축제 ‘토제’를 엽니다. 또한 밭 옆에는 아티스트 레지던시이자 숙소인 ㅊ집이 있어, 머무르며 쉬고 작업할 수 있습니다.
아이들과 함께 오시는 분들도 이곳에서 자연스럽게 어울리게 됩니다. 흙을 만지고, 뛰어놀고, 작은 것들을 함께 발견하는 시간 속에서 아이들끼리도, 어른들끼리도 조용히 연결이 만들어집니다. 저 역시 그런 만남을 늘 기대하고 있습니다.
이곳에서는 씨앗을 뿌리고 수확하는 일부터, 자연 속에서의 시간, 그리고 그 경험을 글이나 그림으로 풀어내는 작업까지 함께합니다. 밭에서의 시간 이후, 각자의 방식으로 머무르고 표현하는 순간을 소중히 여깁니다.

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2022년
✅ 농장 형태 : 치유농장, 농사공동체, 예술공동체
✅ 농장 주작물 : 섬유용대마, 메밀, 홉

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 저에게 농사는 단순한 생계 방식이 아니라, 작업의 주제이자 하나의 예술적 실천입니다. 농사를 선택한 이유는, 그것이 가장 건강하고 지속 가능한 방식으로 삶과 작업을 함께 만들어가는 과정이라고 믿기 때문입니다.
환경을 위한 다양한 활동을 이어오고 있지만, 무엇보다 중요한 것은 일상 속에서 자연과 조화를 이루는 태도라고 생각합니다. 농사를 짓는 일 자체가 이미 환경을 돌보는 일이 되기를 바라는 마음으로, 그 과정을 나누고 함께 경험하는 자리를 꾸준히 만들어왔습니다.
저는 『채식하는 호랑이 바라』를 쓴 작가로, 도서관과 학교 등에서 환경에 대한 이야기를 나누어 왔고, 지난 3년 간 “조금씩 농부”들이 생겨나기를 바라는 마음으로 다양한 농사 프로그램을 진행해 왔습니다. 지금은 약 20명의 팀원들과 함께 ‘풍요로운 농사’라는 이름으로 지속적인 공동체 활동을 이어가고 있습니다.
작은 실천이 모여 더 많은 변화를 만들어낼 수 있다고 믿습니다. 이곳에서의 농사가 누군가에게는 새로운 감각이 되고, 또 다른 시작이 되기를 바랍니다.
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 : 아이들과 함께 농사를 지으며 살아가고 있습니다. 처음부터 생산량보다 농사짓는 방식이 더 중요하다고 생각해, 농약과 화학비료는 사용하지 않고 있습니다.
밭에서는 자연의 흐름을 최대한 따르려 노력하며, 풀과 흙, 벌레와 함께 살아가는 방식을 배우고 있습니다. 올해는 퍼머컬처 농부님께 배우며, 박스를 활용한 관리 방식 등 다양한 시도를 이어가고 있습니다.
생활 속에서도 가능한 한 덜 소비하고, 다시 쓰고, 나누는 방식을 선택하려 합니다. 완벽하지는 않지만, 작은 실천을 이어가는 것이 중요하다고 믿습니다.

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 시각 예술가, 두 아들
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려동물 : 없음
✅ 반려동물 허용: 실내 동반 취침 불가
✅ 우퍼가 하는 일 :
-11월 ~ 3월 (동절기) : 하회마을 안에서 밭을 준비하고, 흙과 거름을 돌보는 시간을 가집니다. 부엽토를 만들고, 박스를 활용한 흙 관리 등을 함께하며, 짚풀 공예와 같은 일상적인 손작업도 이어집니다. 농장 내에서 진행되는 다양한 생태 치유 프로그램에도 함께합니다.
-4월 (봄의 시작) : 파종을 준비하고 밭을 정리합니다. 작은 온실을 함께 만드는 작업도 이 시기에 진행될 예정입니다. 마을 안에서 이루어지는 다양한 생태 체험 프로그램에도 참여합니다.
-5월 ~ 7월 (초여름) : 잡초를 관리

name: Konswan - We are an on grid small scale farm that serves the local community.
description:
Work Hard and Play Often! Permaculture Farm with beautiful view, reasonable work, and nice amenities. My two sons and I are doing a Permaculture style farming experience from the beginning.

I am an organic farmer doing Permaculture, a registered artisan (a fiber artist), a trained massage therapist and a highly educated Jane-of-all-trades. I started a new sustainable living project and try to do things with a small carbon footprint. I have eleven years experience living off grid, doing an independent living project with my ex-husband. Now I am a single mother with two boys, ages 12 and 14, Odin and Mitra. 

I need help in the gardens, feeding ducks, moving goats pasture to pasture, and general farm work and maintenance. We’ll build rabbit hutches and garden structures with wood. 

.Work exchange with me means that you will have a bed of your own, work about four days (total of 25-30 hours a week), have three days off, have all meals provided, all housing, and you will be integrated as a family member. You will be treated with respect and dignity and nothing will be asked of you that I myself cannot or will not do. You will be treated as a nephew or niece who travels to stay with an aunt with adolescent boys.  You will learn to garden, to cook, possibly to slaughter animals, to survive on a medium income, to have fun and get many things accomplished in your day. 

You will learn what you choose and nothing will be obligated to you to learn. My plan for the future is to open a food truck similar to a farm to table bistro open three days per week, and continue massage therapy three days. Currently I host Food Experiences on Airbnb. I love to cook, to entertain, to have fun, and to get to know people and share life stories. Essentially, I love to host! 

I live on a beautiful island, a photographer's delight, a nature reserve full of waterfalls, natural swimming pools both saltwater and freshwater, two beaches, nature parks, camping spots, epic hikes, whale watching, s
_wwoof_travel: Get to Flores island, Açores, Portugal.  Airport code FLW.  A week before your flight, contact me and make arrangements for me or a taxi to pick you up from the airport.

name: Markus - Permaculture on the southern coast of Crete
description:
Kamara Garden is a small farm on the southern coast of Crete,  therefore the climate is rather arid and hot in summer. We are located 150 meters above sea level, 30-minutes walk down to a beautiful beach in a lovely small village, Tertsa. We are a small family, Markus, my wife Lisa, and our daughter Saya, who is 5 years old. Lisa is a Shiatsu practitioner and hiking guide. We share the space with 2 small dogs and 3 cats. We hope to have chicken this year as well.
We started all that in November 2018 after moving here from Vienna, Austria. Before that, we never had anything to do with farming, so we are still learning daily and the place is still much in development. 

The main fields of production are olive oil, herbs as well as fruits and vegetables for ourselves.

Duration of stay: It takes about a week to get familiar with the place, so we accept requests for a minimum of two weeks. 

What we do:
Repairing old farm terraces (I love the work with stones) and preparing them for farming; structuring the entire field with paths, stairs, and small swales to reduce erosion; working on Olive trees, herb cultivation, planting Mediterranean forest and other trees (i.e. Moringa), working in the vegetable garden, water harvesting and distribution; building a storage room and a lot more.

We have a very quiet life here, especially in winter.

name: Sampada - Biodiverse farm following agroecology, permaculture and local practices
description:
We are a homestead aiming for self sufficiency. We are married partners (Madhu and Sampada) who have moved away from the hustle bustle of city life to this quite village along the western Ghats of India. We are with the help of friends and family, trying to build a permaculture farm which will be self sustainable.
Though both of us are Engineers by educational qualifications, we moved away from our core field of study and worked in the sector of education for children coming from less privileged background for more than 15 years. We continue to teach and try to create a welcoming and open learning space for the kids in the village. As we explored various aspects of life, living on a farm is something that stayed with both of us and hence we dived all in. We are conscious and thoughtful of background of people around us and try our best to not judge or put you in any brackets. We are not religious but big fan of Kabir songs and bhajans (Bhakthi poetry). We look forward to the music and movie recommendations from anyone who visit us. We also look forward to learn, cook and exchange recipes with all our visitors. Come prepared with a playlist and a unique recipe that represents the place you come from. We are LGBTQ+ friendly, look forward to challenge existing social, ecological and economic power structures through the lives we lead. Food is a very important part of the exchange and we shall happily accommodate your special needs like Vegan or Jain choices.

We started working on our land on 2022 March. In the last 3 years, we are thorough with basic systems like water facility, major tree plantation, drip irrigation, facilities around living space for us and the volunteers and agro tourism setup. We now need help in sustaining these systems, troubleshooting as and when needed, planting and managing vegetable fields, harvesting and processing various fruits, grains, pulses, vegetables and more.
Some activities are yet to start like, making bunds to arrest soil erosion

name: Teresa - Azienda agricola che coltiva in modo naturale piante officinali, aromatiche, ulivi
description:
Qui, sull'isola di Sant'Antioco, parte sud ovest della Sardegna, viviamo tutto l'anno io e mio marito, con i nostri due cani ancora adolescenti e giocherelloni, decisamente molto espansivi e un gatto terribile! Non condividono lo spazio con gli ospiti (sia paganti sia wwoofers), quindi non preoccupatevi se non amate i cani, ma il gatto gira ovunque e non ci sono recinzioni che tengano. Abbiamo 150 anni in due, ma amiamo condividere quello che chiamiamo "il nostro piccolo angolo di paradiso" con ospiti che vengono da tutto il mondo, e che apprezzano la nostra ospitalità, all'interno delle tende più naturali, stile glamping, o all'interno delle camere. Siamo in campagna, ma in una piccola isola, all'interno di un parco naturale, in un ecosistema ancora incontaminato. Abbiamo una vista sul mare splendida, il mare si trova a poco più di un chilometro. Le nostre coltivazioni sono assolutamente naturali, con piante spesso autoctone, rispettiamo la biodiversità e abbiamo un piccolo vivaio che condividiamo con gli ospiti. Dalle piante ricaviamo olii essenziali, idrolati, molte essenze vengono semplicemente essiccate, anche ad uso condimentario. Facciamo assaggiare agli ospiti il nostro olio, fatto con olive sane che vengono raccolte da piante non trattate, e portate al frantoio e spremute a freddo in giornata per ricavarne un prodotto di qualità eccellente. Purtroppo negli ultimi due anni la siccità ha messo a dura prova anche le piante più aridoresistenti.

Mi piacerebbe condividere con i WWOOFer la mia passione per le piante officinali, e raccontare il percorso dal vivaio fino alla realizzazione dei prodotti. Vorrei che parlassero con gli ospiti e che raccontassero questo percorso. Io parlo un inglese scarso, e a volte non riesco a trasmettere l'essenza del mio lavoro agli ospiti, ed è un peccato perchè c'è un intero mondo da scoprire, che un madrelingua riuscirebbe a spiegare molto meglio. All'interno del nostro agriturismo c'è un piccolo shop, esclusivamente con nostri
_wwoof_travel: Stazione ferroviaria di Carbonia, aeroporto di Cagliari Elmas. Per arrivare da noi, e muoversi in zona, è indispensabile l'automobile. Siamo a 13 km da Sant'Antioco e Calasetta. Per chi arriva in aereo noleggiamo un'auto, indispensabile la patente x guida

name: Cheryl - Help needed for May! Feeding animals  walking dogs feeding sheep and alpacas!
description:
Looking for helpers for July and August for animal care,soap making and wool spinning  weeding grass cutting barn cleaning dog walking.. 
 My goal is to be  sustainable on an acre and a half!  Our property is located in the rolling hills of Northumberland outside the small village of Castleton.  I have bees if you’re interested and I harvest honey and render the beeswax to use in our skin care products and we make beeswax wraps for food. 
I  have 4 sheep and just rescued two young alpacas and will need help processing wool! I can teach you how to spin and knit! 
I sell my wool and knitted items out of the Hutt at the end of our driveway. 
We are called "The Hutt On Morganston”. We sell homemade soaps, hand creams, lip balm, beard cream, homemade deodorant and toothpaste., knitted socks, slippers, local maple syrup , honey and other handmade local gifts. 

If we have time i can teach you how to make soaps, creams, ( learning to make all natural sunblock and deodorant) and all natural toothpaste. 
I have a spinning wheel and can teach how to hand spin wool and knit sweaters, socks, mitts, and hats. 
I have two rooms in the basement and looking for folks who can work on their own as I work outside of the home more now. 
there is internet in the main house, It is sporadic depending on the weather.i enjoy having dinners together and occasionally with our neighbours 
 So what to bring; I would suggest  warm clothes , boots, gloves if you need them, a hat, books, if you have your own car it will be needed as we are 15 and 30 minutes from a town by car and try to only go shopping for groceries once a week. If you don’t have a car we can pick you up at the train station in Cobourg or Belleville. I like to meal plan together and we respect unique diets and will best try to accommodate you for dinners, provided we can source what you need locally.  I am vegetarian.  So there is lots of veg dinners For breakfast and lunches we usually fend for ourselves or if someone is making

name: Kristen - 15-acre homestead B&B in Northern Michigan wine country.    Nature. Homesteading. Yoga.
description:
Our land is a natural landscape nestled among the rolling hills and fresh waters of Leelanau County.  A smallish (15 acre) hobby and horse farm, intentionally crafted as a labor of love to lay foundation for mindful connection opportunities in appreciation of yoga, nature, and community. Rōming Roost is a hybrid Bed and Breakfast and Homestead for those who seek to rōm, rest, and renew.   We are located in Suttons Bay, Mi.  1/2 mile from the infamous Tart Trail biking system.  3.7 miles from the village of Suttons Bay and 10 miles to Traverse City.  Our community is rich in farming (mostly cherries and apples), fresh water, conservation land and love.  We are 20 minutes from Sleeping Bear National Lakeshore and a stones throw from a plethora of wineries and cider houses.  

This homestead is a family affair.  We are a faith focused family.  Myself  my husband, our three kids (who are home schooled) three dogs, two cats, two horses and many chickens,  are the stewards of this land, learning as we grow.  We are first generation farmers, on organic soil, and are trying our best to let the Earth and Mother Nature guide us.  We have a 20X70 garden with a mix of raised beds and in the Earth planting.  We plant mostly from seed and grow the foods that we love to eat and share.  We do have a farm stand to sell from, but we also use the foods for breakfast offerings for our B&B guests.   We have a flock of about 30 free range chickens who so graciously share their eggs with us.    We have Bed and Breakfast units on property, this is where WWOOFERS stay when we do not have B&B guests.  Our goal is to build out a dedicated space just for WWOOFERS, with do time.  

We are flexible on length of stay for our WWOOFers, between three nights  and 8 weeks for the months of April -October.  We welcome our WWOOFers as one of the family, but they would need to be comfortable with children and animals (our kids are 6-13 years old).  WWOOFING days are spent tending to with our hobby farm

name: Leila - We are now a homestead, since the fire.  We are focused on gardens, foul, & rebuilding campgrounds
description:
We hold State and County Organic Certification. We are year round. But because of the Dixie fire, we are presently more like a homestead. All of our retreat (campsites) burned.

We are located in the middle of Plumas National Forest, which is a 1,000,000-acre forest. 120 of our 130-acre farm work here is forest management, the other half is orchard, crop, and animals. We sit at an altitude of 3,980’ (1,213.1 meters).  

We were dramatically affected by the Dixie Fire. Because of that, there is a great deal of work being done around the land management with the Forest Service, the Natural Resources Consevation Service (NRCS), Federal Emergency Management Agency FEMA, the Office of Emergency Services (OES), the Farm Services Bureau, and others. That is pulling about 1/2 my time, but I see it as a rare opportunity to learn. 

This is a food desert, and we are looking for WWOOFERs with great attitudes to help us rebuild and establish Local Produce Markets. 

UPDATES:
May, 2024, we have re-skinned the greenhouse/hoophouse, and are about 1/2 way through installing 64 raised bed coldboxes.  10 boxes are going to be for Wine-cap mushrooms.  We are also hoping to finish the oyster mushroom grow area, and include a section for wine making, and growing mealworms for the turkeys.
It is also that time of year that we have to start mowing, raking, and scraping weeds and grasses in preparation for fire season. 

October, November,  2023, we are doing winter prep for what is expected to be a dramatic winter. 

November 2023
** We received an ARPA grant to build, install, and plant 40 Cold Boxes. We are repairing 25 outdoor raised beds and converting them to Cold Boxes. There is a bit of construction involved with converting those. Then everything in the hoop-house will be built and or retrofitted to Cold Boxes (another 15 Cold Boxes). 
** The last week in October, we received an NRCS grant to install a new solar well, and we have just begun the planning. 
** There is a new HUD gran

name: Tommy - Traditional 1840s mountain farm with cows, forest and great views. Welcoming LGBTQ+
description:
We are Tommy and Kent, a gay couple living on a mountain. We live on a small farm in the middle of nowhere, as early WWOOFers used to joke. We have cows, horses, chickens, goats, a dog and two cats.

We have three guest bedrooms, so most WWOOFers will probably have their own room.

A Day on the Farm
A typical day starts with breakfast, followed by moving the cows to their grazing spots. Then we tackle various tasks, such as carpentry (both indoors and outdoors), chopping firewood, repairing fences, or handling whatever else needs to be done.

Our farm is a typical Swedish farm, resembling how farms looked about 100 years ago. It was built in 1842, is surrounded by forest, and offers beautiful views from its higher spots.

We hand-milk our cows morning and evening (part of the year, the calves get most of the milk). The farm provides us with milk, meat, and eggs for personal use, as we are not commercial farmers. Recently, we’ve also started a small vegetable garden.

Welcoming Environment
LGBTQ+ people are very  welcome-26

Food and Allergies
Since we mainly eat what we produce, our meals consist of our farm’s own meat, milk, eggs, and vegetables. Unfortunately, we are unable to accommodate vegetarians or vegans.
For allergy sufferers, please note that we have dogs and cats. If your allergies prevent you from doing work on the farm, our farm is probably not the right place for you.

On the days you are off, you can take care of your own food.

Farm Life
To maintain a good sense of well-being, we all pitch in to clean shared spaces, such as the kitchen and bathrooms, and we take turns cooking meals. Lunch is a communal event, and afterward, we might take a short trip to explore nearby places.

There’s a small lake in the forest nearby, perfect for swimming on warm days. Evenings are often spent with friends who come by for dinner made with homemade ingredients or just to chat.

Current Projects
Maintaining an almost 200-year-old farm is a never-ending process. Curren

name:
Lyse - Organic market garden
Jardin maraîcher biologique
description:
Hello, my name is Lyse, me and my partner Normand acquired a property in northern British Columbia 6 years ago. We have several gardens  sow and maintain to feed a few families. Our goal is to be as self-sufficient as possible. We have dogs, cats, chickens, one goat and a horse who all live in harmony. 
Most of the days are spent outside. Different tasks have to be done on a farm depending on the season; prepare the land, sow, weed, water, harvest, manage all the vegetables... 
Most of the time we all eat together for lunch and dinner, breakfasts can be together or by yourself . 
We have a small trailer with fridge and stove to accommodate WWOOFers. 
We love hiking with our dogs. Harvesting small wild fruits and mushrooms are also part of our activities. There are several beautiful hikes possible nearby.

Bonjour, je m'appelle Lyse, moi et mon partenaire Normand avons acquis une propriété dans le nord de la Colombie Britannique il y a 6 ans. Nous avons plusieurs jardins à semer et entretenir pour nourrir quelques familles. Notre objectif est d'être le plus autosuffisant possible. Nous avons des chiens, des chats, des poules, une chèvre et un cheval qui vivent tous en harmonie.

Les journées se passent majoritairement à l'extérieur. Différentes tâches sont à faire sur une ferme selon les saisons; préparer la terre, semer, sarcler, arroser, récolter, gérer tous les légumes...

La plupart du temps nous mangeons tous ensemble le midi et au souper, les déjeuners peuvent être au choix ensemble ou chacun chez sois. Nous avons une petite roulotte avec frigo et cuisinière pour loger les WWOOFers.

Nous aimons faire des randonnées. La récoltes des petits fruits sauvages et champignons font aussi parties de nos activités. 
Il y a à proximité plusieurs belles randonnées pédestres possibles.

name: Rancho Agrosol - Rancho Escuela Agrosol: Aprende Agricultura Regenerativa en el Corazón de Veracruz
description:
Hola! Somos Rancho Agrosol, una granja regenerativa y centro de formación agropecuaria ubicado en La Pitaya, cerca de Xalapa y Coatepec, Veracruz, México.

Somos una cooperativa y uno de los proyectos pioneros en México en trabajar con un modelo de aprendizaje práctico enfocado en formar a la próxima generación de técnicos agropecuarios. Nuestra filosofía se basa en el principio de “aprender haciendo”, combinando experiencia directa con prácticas sostenibles y regenerativas.

En el rancho conviven estudiantes, instructores y miembros de la cooperativa, creando un ambiente colaborativo y de aprendizaje constante. También contamos con animales de granja como vacas, gallinas y otras especies que forman parte de nuestro sistema integral

Un día típico en Rancho Agrosol depende de la temporada y de los proyectos en curso, pero siempre está basado en el aprendizaje práctico y el trabajo en equipo.

Los voluntarios pueden participar en actividades como siembra, trasplante y cosecha de cultivos, elaboración de compostas y fertilizantes naturales, manejo del suelo, y cuidado de animales.

También hay oportunidad de involucrarse en procesos de transformación de alimentos (como lácteos o conservas) y en proyectos ecológicos como permacultura o bioconstrucción.

Cada día combina trabajo, aprendizaje y convivencia dentro de la comunidad

Ofrecemos alojamiento compartido en un entorno rural sencillo y rústico, rodeado de naturaleza.

La alimentación es una parte importante del proyecto. Las comidas se preparan con ingredientes frescos y naturales, muchos de ellos producidos en el mismo rancho. Generalmente compartimos los alimentos como comunidad.

La vida aquí es sencilla, por lo que es importante tener disposición y apertura a este estilo de vida

Nuestro estilo de vida está enfocado en la conexión con la naturaleza, la sustentabilidad y la vida en comunidad. Valoramos el aprendizaje, el intercambio cultural y el trabajo colaborativo.

La región donde se encuentra el rancho es

name: Gautur - Family farm
description:
Gay couple in their 50 very open minded, well travelled, welcoming a newbie or PRO for a great experience. We enjoy to meet different people from different countries worldwide. Living in a sheep farm not far from Djúpivogur village with our border collie dog Prada and cat Miezekatze. We provide normal food, so if you can’t eat it, we can’t host you. We try to live a minimalist lifestyle so if you are into consumerism, look for someone else.

You will live in Icelandic farm getting to know everyday reality from the first hands. You will be able to share and exchange the experience from your own country, not only teaching us something, but learning yourself as well. Not only the language, but the culture, way of living, working and enjoying everyday life not in Icelandic, but international way. We have travelled to around 223 countries, so we are at least familiar and open to mostly everyone from every country in the world.

Organic methods: Livestock manure used on grass fields and potato garden.

Certifications: https://ici.radio-canada.ca/premiere/emissions/style-libre/segments/chronique/95024/woofing-voyage-ferme-travailler-valises

Current projects: Cutting lawn, fixing fences, wood work, making hey, tractor 🚜 driving. May is lamming season while September-October is sheep herting season, so it´s perfect for hyper-active people and this is when we need most help (3 p. max.) !!! You have to be able to drive a standard car 🚘 , cook regular food and be tiny as your mother will be not working there with you! You need to be able to manage basics alone after you have been teached how to do the things i. e. daily feeding, as we will be not all the time around. A big plus if you have similar experience and can drive tractor.  We prefer one person or couple who is independent with some brain and don’t need to be told every time what to do, who is responsible and not afraid to be alone (with Prada 🐕 and 🐈 Miezekatze and sheep 🐑 thou) in remote conditions. Once you are done
_wwoof_travel: We live in a sheep farm 10 min. away from Djupivogur village.

name: Hacienda - Family farm
description:
Hacienda Herrera – Voluntariado en la Selva Amazónica (Tambopata, Madre de Dios, Perú)
Somos una familia local aliada de la ONG CECCOT (Centro de Educación, Ciencia y Conservación del Tambopata). Juntos desarrollamos actividades sostenibles que nos permiten conocer mejor nuestro bosque y vivir en armonía con la naturaleza.
La familia Herrera lleva más de 30 años dedicada a la agricultura familiar. Estamos muy felices de compartir nuestra experiencia con voluntarios y viajeros interesados en la Amazonía. 
Actualmente trabajamos en un proyecto  de ecoturismo que nos ayuda a sostener un proyecto de agroforesteria en el que hemos destinado 5 hectáreas de bosque al manejo de sistemas agroforestales y a una huerta orgánica. Además, realizamos monitoreos de fauna silvestre que visita nuestras plantaciones, lo que permite observar aves, mamíferos y otros animales en su ambiente natural.
La energía de la casa proviene de paneles solares, lo que reduce nuestro impacto ambiental y hace que la experiencia sea aún más sostenible.
Métodos orgánicos
• Sistema agroforestal amazónico  citrícos y cacao
• Uso de cáscaras de castaña (nuez de Brasil) como abono  
• Producción de humus orgánico para enriquecer nuestros cultivos  
Certificaciones
Actualmente no contamos con certificación orgánica oficial, pero trabajamos con prácticas sostenibles y respetuosas con el bosque.
Proyectos y tipo de experiencia
¿Buscas una experiencia vivencial auténtica en la selva amazónica?
Nuestros programas de voluntariado y pequeños proyectos de investigación pueden ser exactamente lo que estás buscando.
Aquí podrás:
• Aprender sobre aves tropicales y participar en observaciones de campo  
• Colaborar en el monitoreo de fauna silvestre  
• Fortalecer tus conocimientos en agricultura orgánica y agroforestería amazónica  
• Vivir y trabajar junto a una familia local, compartiendo cultura, conocimientos y vida diaria  

Si tienes interés, preguntas o necesitas más información, no dudes en ponerte en contact
_wwoof_travel: Esta ubicado en el rio bajo madre de dios km 2, a 10 minutos de la ciudad  de Puerto Maldonado en bote.

name: Cesare - Azienda agricola in sicilia, a pochi km da Cefalù
description:
Ciao, mi chiamo Cesare, ho 35 anni e vivo nel comune di Tusa a circa 17km da Cefalù.
Conduco la mia azienda agricola aperta da pochi mesi da solo, i miei compagni di vita e lavoro per ora sono Chaos e Athena due meravigliosi e dolcissimi pittbull.
In questo momento mi occupo prevalentemente della produzione di agrumi ed olio extravergine di oliva, oltre a questo al momento ho fatto un orto per produrmi il cibo in autonomia.
Ho appena aperto l'azienda e attualmente dei miei quasi 5 ettari solo 2 sono in attività produttiva, i restanti 3 sono completamente da riprendere perchè il terreno era in stato di abbandono da circa 6 anni e, trovandomi al confine con il meraviglioso bosco di Tardara (bosco costituito prevalentemente di splendide quercie da sughero), la vegetazione è inesorabilmente avanzata nel terreno, rendendo i meravigliosi ulivi secolari presenti difficilmente accessibili.

In base al momento dell'anno i lavori principali sono:
potatura ulivi, potatura agrumi, ripristino della parte di terreno in abbandono ( pulitura terreno da rovi con potatura alberi), piantumazione e mantenimento orto, lavori di recinzione della proprietà.

nella casa insistono 2 camere da letto una matrinoniale ed una singola, possibilità anche di 1 divano/letto matrimoniale in cucina.

Mi piace stare in estremo contatto con la natura, vicino alla mia azienda agricola ci sono delle meravigliose spiagge, per lo più poco affollate, nelle quali godersi tramonti incredibili, sentieri per fare passeggiate a piedi o in bicicletta che passano in mezzo al meraviglioso bosco di Tardara che con le sue caratteristiche querce di sughero vi accoglierà con sentieri che arrivano fino Tusa, un meraviglioso borgo con un panorama dal quale si vedono tutte le isole eolie.
I 2 paesi più vicini sono Castel di tusa a circa 7km, Finale di Pollina 10km, e Tusa 9 km, mentre a 16km troviamo S.Stefano di Camastra (famosissimo per le sue ceramiche) e Cefalù a 18km famosissima meta turistica della Sicilia che vi ac

name: Hugo - Join us in an isolated eco-jungle project on the shore of Lake Atitlan, Guatemala
description:
Stay with us by a beautiful lake, wake up to the sound of birds, enjoy the sunrise from our beach, disconnect from the noise, become more sustainable, feel the essence of nature and climb the volcano where we live.

We are looking for self-sufficient and collaborative individuals to join our team of four volunteers dedicated to maintaining and guarding the property. If you value independence, don't mind routine tasks, love being surrounded by nature and embrace a self-directed way of living, this could be the perfect fit for you!

--------------------------------------

📢 📢 𝗣𝗟𝗘𝗔𝗦𝗘 𝗣𝗟𝗘𝗔𝗦𝗘 𝗣𝗟𝗘𝗔𝗦𝗘: 𝗕𝗲𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘂𝘀, 𝘄𝗲 𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗹𝘆 𝗮𝘀𝗸 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝘁𝗼 𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗳𝘂𝗹𝗹𝘆 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗱 𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗿𝗲 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗳𝗶𝗹𝗲. Hello! My name is Hugo and I live on the slopes of San Pedro volcano in Guatemala, in a wild and truly isolated area of Lake Atitlan, accessible only by water. There are no roads, no close neighbors and no noise. Just pure nature, the songs of birds and a stunning beach. I’m on a journey to build a home in this paradise. Most of the time, I share this space with volunteers, some chickens, a cat and two dogs.

Over the past four years, I’ve welcomed more than 100 volunteers, and together we’ve worked on a variety of projects aimed at becoming more self-sufficient and sustainable, while continuously expanding our knowledge along the way. The following activities won’t be part of your assigned tasks, but during your free time, you’ll have the chance to explore them and deepen your understanding if you're interested:

-Beach creation: we constructed two massive natu

name: Fatih - Smallholding
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Bir sabah uyanıp madem imkan var, ne işim var benim bu koca şehirde, “hadi gel köyümüze geri dönelim” diyen biri olmadığım kesin. Hayatın bir şekilde köklerime doğru yol almamı sağladığı biriyim. Tarımla geçinmem gerektiğinden, baba topraklarına 22 yıl sonra dönüş yapmanın şaşkınlığını yaşadığım gerçeğini de inkar edemem. Diyarbakır’a 45 km uzaklıkta bir çiftlikte annemle beraber yaşıyorum. Geniş ve kısmen verimli arazilerde kendi yağımızda kavruluyoruz dersek yanlış olmaz.

Elimdekileri paylaşınca mutlu olan biriyim, bu nedenle bu zihin beden sağlığına dileyenlerin dahil olmasının derdindeyim. 22 yıl büyükşehirde yaşadıktan sonra kırsal hayatın nimetlerini makul ve gerçekçi biçimde paylaşmak doğru bir hareket olur düşüncesindeyim. 
“Yapabileceğim nedir?” diye düşünüp sıraladığımda, çokça bir şey olmadığının farkındayım. Tavuk, yumurta, eritme peynir, mevsiminde bamya, domates ve biber salçası, patlıcan ve biber kurusu, tam buğday unu, elma ve üzüm sirkesi, keşkeklik, aşurelik, içli köftelik, pilavlık, sarmalık buğday, elma kurusu, nane, fesleğen, maydanoz kurusu, üzüm marmelatı, pekmez, pestil, üzüm sucuğu, şarap gibi kendi ev ihtiyacımız ve dostlara çam sakızı çoban armağanı bir şeyler...

OCA-ŞUB: Hayvan bakımı, kar küreme 
MAR-MAY: Hayvan bakımı, toprak işleri, tohum ekimi, fidan dikimi 
HAZ-TEM: Hayvan bakımı, bahçe işleri 
AĞU: Hayvan bakımı, bağ işleri 
EYL: Hayvan bakımı, bağ bozumu 
EKİ: Hayvan bakımı, şarap ve pekmez yapımı 
KAS-ARA: Hayvan bakımı

---

I am not someone who just woke up and said "what am I doing here in the city, let's go back to the village". Life led me to my roots. I had to earn my life with agriculture, so I turned back to the land of my father after 22 years. Now I live together with my mother in a farm 45 km away from Diyarbakır. We stand on our own feet on our vast and partly fertile land.

It makes me happy to share what I have, so I want to invite people to join us enjoy the mental and physical well-being.
_wwoof_travel: Diyarbakır havalimanı, Batman havalimanı, Bismil tren garı, Bismil otobüs terminali

name: Claire - Family farm, Community farm
description:
We are a couple who met here in Zambia in 2008 and fell in love with each other, the place, the people, and the culture. We live on a small farm with our Baby Boy Bodhi, our daughter Ayla, and our Zambian neighbors and friends. We feel very fortunate to be hosted by the community where we stay. We have formed meaningful and co-dependent relationships with our neighbors who we are now lucky to consider family. We speak local language and are trying our best to adapt to the lifestyle of our hosts and friends. We have WWOOFed ourselves extensively in Latin America and are happy to have a home and farm to share with WWOOFers. We live off-the-grid on the farm where we have planted many fruit trees and have an organic garden . As the farm has not reached full production, we do not produce enough food to be self-sufficient (although we have lots of fresh veggies). For this reason we ask visitors to offer any form of contribution towards food that they feel called to. We also run a social project in Kasama town which is 12 km away. Learn more about that project at http://www.bakashana.org or more about us and the farm at www.lovenlightlukupa.com

Organic methods: Chicken Manure, Compost

Certifications: Visiting a farm is the most reliable way to trust how your food is grown.

Current projects: Fruit tree orchard, garden, flowers, love

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: an easy taxi from Kasama or we can take you if we are in town.

name: Michael - Help Rescue Horses in the beautiful mountains of Southern CA
description:
Located in the heart of Southern California, 40 minutes from Hollywood there is much to see and do. From star tours to the Pacific coast beach line, the weather is perfect for outside activities all year round. We are a non profit horse rescue, we work to train and rehabilitate all horse breeds, we have a few small animals including goats that are companions to the horses, and chickens for fresh eggs.  Construction projects are also a daily part of working on the ranch.

Your trailer guest quarters is set up with all modern amenities, including high speed internet in exchange for approximately 4.5 hours work per day. You are able to learn horse riding and natural horsemanship training skills, as well as other horse care requirements. There is train and bus stations close by that can take you anywhere in Los Angeles and beyond. 

Organic methods: We recycle and reuse everything from the ranch operation, brush is collected and turned to mulch, all animal droppings are collected, aged, and eventually used as a gardening compost.

Certifications: We have not pursued any official certifications.

Current projects: Current projects that need your help include:

1.) Numerous rescue horses in need of care, feeding, cleaning, exercise and human bonding.

2.) Finding homes for several rescue horses that have completed their rehabilitation requirements.

3.)  Construction projects currently in progress for new rescue facilities incorporated with a local community equestrian center.

4.) Developing new after school no cost, childrens horse activities and lessons program.

Internet access available

name: Heidi - Eco-Homestead, Retreat & Adobe houses: land and garden projects in a prestine rural setting.
description:
We live in a peaceful area in the highlands of Michoacán, Mexico, supporting a small regenerative farm and community space surrounded by forest. The main family’s property combines gardening, tree planting, natural building, and caring for animals, giving volunteers a chance to connect with the land and learn practical skills for rural living.

The community is on a 70-hectare property divided into 13 sustainable off-grid plots. Currently, two families live here, with more homes completed and families gradually moving in. Several charming, climate-adaptive adobe houses are built, with others in progress. The forest is full of native plants and wildlife — armadillos, coyotes, rabbits, raccoons, skunks, eagles, and many small birds. In the evenings, crickets fill the air, and tall oaks host seasonal orchids.

Before the pandemic, the main house ran a small farm supplying organic tomatoes and lettuce to nearby restaurants. Today, two greenhouses and outdoor garden areas provide hands-on learning in gardening and small-scale food production.

A special part of the project is the ongoing construction of traditional adobe homes. WWOOFers can participate in different stages of natural building, from preparing materials to seeing these beautiful, climate-adaptive houses come together.

Volunteers live and work alongside two families: one with a couple and their two adult children, and another with a single woman. Both homes share the land with friendly dogs, cats, and ducks.

At the heart of the project is a vision to develop the land in harmony with nature through gardens, trees, animals, and simple rural living. We welcome curious, self-motivated volunteers who want to experience everyday life on a working property while contributing to the ongoing development of the land.

Please write and tell a little about yourself, your interests, and what you hope to learn during your stay. Volunteers who are genuinely excited to be part of the land and community are always welcome.
_wwoof_travel: Morelia International Airport (MLM) – approx. 60 minutes by car.  Morelia Central Bus Station – major lines to Mexico City, Guadalajara, and other destinations, 30 minutes by car.

name: Rosa - Family farm
description: We have a large country house with organic gardens for family consumption only. The entire property is surrounded by mountains. We are located in the Elqui Valley, 25 kilometres from the city of La Serena and 45 kilometres from Vicuña, in the Elqui Valley. We have a room available in the house and a good place to camp if you prefer. Meals are served with the family when we are at home and we are open to different diets. We usually eat a vegetarian menu, but there is no problem if there is meat from time to time. Homemade bread and simple but nutritious meals are guaranteed. We are a married couple, our daughter and her family live in France, and our son and his family live in Germany. We are both retired. We are not farmers, but we learn every day by trying to follow the traditions of our land. Experience in wwoofing is valued, and if you have knowledge of organic farming, you are welcome. Tasks will depend on the season, but most will consist of weeding, harvesting, planting seeds, watering, pruning, and general gardening. We prefer volunteers to stay for a minimum of 2 weeks. We do not accept smokers or drinkers of any kind. We do not have Wi-Fi at home, so everyone must provide their own connection. We do not speak English, but we are willing to learn. We will communicate by email to organise your arrival.

name: Cindy - Traditional plant medicines on north-western shores of Lake Superior, on Anishinaabeg homelands.
description:
Hello WWOOFers, my name is Cindy and I live here with my 6 pets: 2 dogs - Charlie and Buddy and 4 cats - Oscar, Spyder, Gracie and Angel. I live in Neebing, Ontario, Canada, 10 minutes north of the Minnesota border and 45 kilometers south of the closest city Thunder Bay. I respectfully acknowledge this Sacred Land known to me as Niibing (it is summer) on the western shores of Gichigaming (the great lake), the traditional homelands of the Anishinaabeg.  It is between Animikii-Wiikwedong (Thunder Bay), the traditional homelands of Fort William First Nation, and Gichi Onigaming (Portage) the traditional homelands of Grand Portage Band of Lake Superior Chippewa.

I am growing and harvesting traditional plant medicines such as sage, sweetgrass, tobacco and cedar to share with the community together with Anishinaabe practices. I am in relationship with this land that loves me, protects me, sustains me, entertains me, teaches me and so much more.

There are 13 new cabins in development and a common area for your convenience. Common Area Amenities: Drinking water provided; Fridge and freezer to store your food; Electric stove top – please do not leave unattended; 3 sets of tables and chairs to gather; Garbage and recyclables to be deposited in appropriate bins; Give Away Bin – you may find something you would like; or you may have something to offer in return; and, Kettle, Keurig, tea and coffee. Also included: Firepit with firewood include; Six picnic tables available; Porta Potty with sink and flush toilet available for your washroom needs; Portable Shower available otherwise Lake Superior is a short walk further down the road (approximately 20 minutes); There are 13+ tent sites; and, the 13 cabins. The number 13 is significant as I am sharing the teachings of the 13 lunar moons also known as the 13 grandmothers. 

My personal space and time are very important to me. I expect that space to be respected and I will respect yours. I enjoy doing 1,000 piece puzzles, painting,

name: Santiago - ¡Bienvenidxs! Cocreate on our growing agroecological food forest around Lake Chapala, MX
description:
Hello, Santiago and Saku here!

Together with our family, we are creating a small co-housing project in the lively town of San Juan Cosalá, by Lake Chapala, Mexico. The land is shared with family, a few neighbors, our rescued pups Werita and Naya, and often friends, visitors, and volunteers.

Our intention is to grow a lush fruit, medicinal herb, and vegetable forest using permaculture and agroecological practices, while also building communal outdoor spaces and gathering areas for community life.

We are looking for self-sufficient, curious, and collaborative people who wish to co-create this project. We value the diverse skills each person brings and enjoy learning, sharing, and implementing them through ongoing, hands-on work.

Life here includes gardening, building, creating, waking up to birdsong, enjoying sunrises and sunsets over the lake, disconnecting from city life, and moving the body through garden work and mountain trails.

JUNE – 2026
Current activities include:
○ Landscaping around a recent build
○ Transplanting fruit and native trees
○ DIY build of a solar dehydrator
○ Creating a worm compost system
○ Hosting workshops, yoga, shared meals, movie nights, and potlucks
○ Processing harvests into jams, ferments, teas, and floral salads for local markets (seasonal)

__DAILY LIFE
We have a plant nursery, a hands-on hose-based irrigation system, and a composting area, with plans to expand into worm composting. Garden tasks follow lunar cycles when possible. Two shared meals are prepared on workdays, with cooking and cleaning shared equally.

__ACCOMMODATION
We offer a covered campsite with tents (max two at a time), compost toilet, outdoor shower, bedding, electricity, WiFi, and shared facilities. Vanlifers are welcome.

__COMMON AREAS AND AMENITIES
○ Fully equipped outdoor kitchen
○ Outdoor dry toilet and solar-heated shower
○ Open garden paths and central gathering area
○ Large terrace for yoga, movement, arts, and workshops (8:00–20:00)

Work starts earl

name: Mang'uli - Welcome WOOFER'S Mafinga Iringa and  support farming activities to surrounding community
activities: Unterkunft: bedroom, trailer, campsite, yurt-tipi, shack
description:
My name is Mang'uli
I'm a project Director living with my family, Teachers and Other workers. We have a small Organization dealing with using organic materials on cultivating different  types ofcrops helping to feed teenage and children.
 Our project is Located at Mafinga -Mufindi -Iringa-Tanzania Southern part of Tanzania in East Africa.
We kindly welcome everyone from different parts of the world to come and share different knowledge and skills with us. Hence, we have our new farm of 2 hectors which are ongoing for permaculture, we need your effort, energy on how to develop this farm on different stages.

name: Christopher (Charu) - 20 acres with gorgeous temple,  residential facility, parrots, peacocks, llamas and small cows in UT
description:
South of Spanish Fork, Utah, in a semi rural environment, we have 15 acres (5.85 hectares) upon which sit a gorgeous temple, a 6,000 sq foot residential facility with comfortable double rooms, two outdoor stages, a large covered pavilion, and amenities for parrots, peacocks, llamas and small cows. 

There is a 200,000 gallon lake with waterfall from which we gravity feed one acre under cultivation. The acreage produces chemical free tomatoes, eggplants, varieties of chilis, squash, melons, corn, cabbage, broccoli, zucchini, cucumbers, spinach, radishes, green peppers, potatoes and flowers for worship.

We sponsor lots of festivals. March of 2025 we hosted the 30th annual Festival of Colors (Holi) which is the largest Color Festival in North America.  A good video of the Holi Festival in Spanish Fork: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zqsRFAJVN9c."" Devin Graham did one called ""World's Biggest Color Party"" (6.0 million views to date) http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hh-o5g4tLVE and Goodline films another called ""Holi Festival."" http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HltruTVcTYE. Other annual events are the  Krishna Janmastami in August/Sept., & Diwali, Festival of Lights in October/November.
To follow us on facebook; Caru Utah or Sri Sri Radha Krishna Temple (Event Venue)

We teach yoga 4 evenings a week and have classes on the Bhagavad Gita 6 am and 6 pm as well as daily kirtans. These programs are optional for the guests, though the more they attend, the more they will be informed about the 5,000 year old spiritual culture and practices of ""Bhakti Yoga"".

The only MANDATORY spiritual  program occurs every Sunday 5 pm - 6 pm when there is an open house with kirtan, vegetarian feast, and spiritual discourse illustrated by power point presentation. This is an extremely 'seeker friendly' weekly event and VOLUNTEERS ARE REQUIRED TO ATTEND.  Additionally, Agni Dev, who leads the chanting most Sundays, is a world class kirtan leader. Caru Das, the main speaker has 350,000 downlo
_wwoof_travel: Several airlines fly into Provo, Utah, from major US cities. Otherwise, Salt Lake City airport is 65 miles away.

name: Robin - 6-acre beautiful remote rainforest property with vegetables and tropical fruit bordering reserve
description:
I am excited about sharing my farm once again and meeting new people to both teach and learn from.  I had a lot of awesome people come and share space here in the past  . Made lifetime friends . I am looking for some Helpers who are interested in country island  living and organic farming as we have large fruit trees, vegetable gardens (want more)  , tropical flowers. We also have animals including dogs , cats, a turtle and chickens. I also have a very large lava tube / cave on site for spelunking  that has been used in movies twice now .  I am looking for 4-5hrs / 5 days a  per week of help , with meals offered and a fun day once a week where we will go see the islands beaches , volcano national park, nightlife etc . 

I have six acres of beautiful remote rainforest property that borders the forest reserve.  I also grow vegetables and have several large producing tropical fruit trees . Exotic flowers too. We do alot of preserves I also make sourdough bread weekly  .;-).I just returned to the farm after being gone for 2 years due to surgeries  . Everything is a Little  bit over grown but still awesome and we are already on it getting it nice again . I am going to be sharing my space with a few volunteers to help me get it back to perfection. I am a very family type of person and am looking forward to meeting new people . I have a bedroom in main house , a new cabin, and lots tent camping available. 

Who lives at the farm? Currently there is myself , a man and 2 children . 

Any animals or pets?

4 dogs , a bunch of chickens, a couple cats and a turtle

How many hours a weeks will you ask guests to help?

20-25 depending on weather 

What kind of tasks will you require them to do?

Yard work ( weedeating , weeding, planting, chainsaw work , fruit picking , pruning , caring for a chickens and coops , gardening  etc , preserving baking , inside domestic chores if raining . 

What is the food that you provide for them?

Off the Land, as well as farmers market . Can acc

name: Vasile - Fermă ecologică de legume
description:
EcoParadis este o fermă în Drăgușeni - satul meu de baștină unde am revenit cu familia mea după ce am lucrat peste hotare. Sunt eu, soția mea și doi copii. Am revenit din Italia în 2015 după mai mulți cărți de dezvoltare personală și permacultură. Am fost nou în agricultură dar am vrut să fac permacultură în loc unde sunt deja a 5-ea generație a familiei Niculiță care face agricultura. Aici în primul rând am început să producem hrana pentru familia. Și am reușit să ajungem la 90% de auto-aprovizionare. În 2015 am început și studii în domeniu agriculturii ca să știu exact ce și cum trebuie de făcut.  La moment gospodăria noastră se află în circuit ecologic.  Avem 9 parcele, 6 din ele sunt cu culturi de câmp: ceapa, cartof, toate organizate în funcție de familie botanică pentru a avea rotația culturilor completă pe 7 ani.  Un terenuri se află in repauz, acolo este plantată lucerna, și în al doilea an vom pune acolo găinile, care vor îmbogățiți solul cu îngrașămînt. Anul acesta am introdus găinile  ca să creștem acest nivel de autonomie, ca să fie un ciclu închis. Ele mănâncă buruienele, dar încă trebuie îngrijite. Deci, încă învățăm cum să lucrăm mai eficient cu ele. Pentru a oferi familiei și altora un coș cât mai divers, producem 30-35 de culturi pe parcursul anului ca să avem culturi de sezon și ca să avem vânzări tot anul. Acum avem și sere, am mărit capacitatea de vânzări. Avem roadă din martie cu verdețuri pînă în noiembrie și decembrie când  avem cartofi și rădăcinoase (morcov, sfeclă, praj, usturoi), 4 feluri de ceapa, varza kale, conopida, broccoli. Vindem produse direct la consumator prin două canale: livrare la domiciliu și la piața. 

Wwooferii pot participa la diferite activități legate de producere și realizare: de la sămînat, răsad, pregătirea compotului, pînă la realizarea produselor la piața agricultorilor organizată o dată per saptămînă în Chișinău. Totodată, oaspeții pot participa și învăța cum se face conservarea produselor agricole și pregătirea b

name: Agnes - Small mixed farm with a vegetable CSA, grassland and some animals
description:
My name is Agnes and together with my husband Jan we manage a small diverse farm in southern Transilvania. We do a vegetable CSA (SoLaWi, AMAP) with 16 families, weekly delivering in Sibiu. We have a few sheep, chickens and runner ducks. Recently we planted a small forest/park and a small energy forest (poplar trees).

Volunteers at our farm work 5 hours a day (weekend free). Mostly in the morning and in the afternoon or evening an our or so, depending on what are the urgent jobs. We eat a warm lunch together and are happy with wwoofers that are cooks :).  Preparing meals is work.
In the growing season we weed and harvest a lot. Also other jobs like repairing fences, making hay, chopping wood.

In our yard we have 2 houses. One of them is hosts our volunteers and /or guests. It has a living, kitchentte and bathroom. The attic has up to 5 beds available. Camping is also a possibility.
The warm lunch is our main meal and we enjoy that together in our outside kitchen. For breakfast and evening meals we provide the ingredients and you are free to eat together or individually. We do not buy much, eat mostly our local food.

Our farm is in a small village near the foothills of the Carpathian mountains. A great place for hiking. The historic town of Sibiu is accessible by train (trainstop 2,4km from our house).

name: Ibrahim - Kola Nut Organic and Sustainable Indignous Farming Community
description:
My name is Ibrahim Mansaray, and I am a member of Slow Food International and the Kola Nut Producer Association. I live in the Kenema District of Sierra Leone, an area known for its rich agricultural heritage and vibrant farming community, primarily composed of indigenous peoples. My project focuses on sustainable organic agriculture that is good, clean, and fair. Our farms are characterized by their rich biodiversity and practice mixed farming, which enhances soil health and resilience. We are committed to promoting sustainable living in this remote region, particularly around the Gola Forest,  where we work to balance agricultural productivity with environmental conservation.
In my home, I live with my family, who are actively involved in our farming practices and community initiatives. We also have a few pets that bring joy and companionship to our lives, further enriching our connection to the land and the vibrant community around us.

Typical Farming Activities and Teaching Opportunities for WWOOFers

As a member of the Kola Nut Producer Association, my typical farming activities revolve around sustainable organic agriculture. Here’s what an average day might look like and what I would like to teach WWOOFers (World Wide Opportunities on Organic Farms) about:

 A Typical Day on the Farm

Morning:
- 6:00 AM - Sunrise Routine: The day begins early with a morning stretch and some light breakfast, often consisting of local fruits and porridge.
- 7:00 AM - Farm Inspection: We start by walking through the fields to inspect crops, checking for pests, and assessing overall health. This is an opportunity to teach WWOOFers about organic pest management and the importance of biodiversity.
- 8:00 AM - Planting and Sowing: We often plant seasonal crops such as cassava, yams, and various vegetables. WWOOFers will learn about seed selection, planting techniques, and soil preparation.

Midday:
- 11:00 AM - Weeding and Maintenance: After a mid-morning break, we focus on weeding

name: Fabienne - Family home and garden - rural, Homestead, Intentional Community, Orchard
description:
Willkommen im Kleinod der Landkultur. Dieser Hof ist bereits seit 35 Jahren Bioland-bewirtschaftet. Die Böden sind sandig jedoch pestizidfrei. Wir wollen ein zukunftsfähiges, enkeltaugliches Projekt aufziehen. Wir werden Tiere haben. Uns ist ein gesunder Kreislauf wichtig. Wir lieben diese Erde und wollen zum Erhalt beitragen. Kommt und helft uns - gemeinsam ist alles schöner!

Organic methods: Wir gestalten das Gelände sukzessive um in einen Permakultur-Garten. Wir stellen Kompost mit Pflanzenkohle her (Terra Preta). Uns ist ein Gleichgewicht zwischen allen Lebenwesen wichtig. Kreislaufwirtschaft, Agroforst, Waldgarten, Milpa, Syntropie - wir sind bereit alles mögliche auszuprobieren. Im Mittelpunkt steht immer der Aufbau des Bodens. Wir freuen uns drauf!

Type of work: Als nächstes wollen wir ein Syntropie-Beet anlegen. Unsere Spirale (siehe Fotos) braucht Überarbeitung. Unsere Draußenküche soll entstehen. Der Folientunnel wird umziehen. Wir ziehen unsere eigenen Pflänzchen vor. Dafür haben wir die letzten Jahre Räumlichkeiten geschaffen. Sukzessive gestalten wir das Gelände um.  Zu tun ist hier immer was ;)

Internet access available, Fahrräder zu verleihen, Freibad um die Ecke. 

Ihr schlaft in unserer Diele. Dort gibt es einen Aufenthaltsraum mit großer Bibliothek und Musikanlage. Wir haben 4 Betten, 2 Schlafsofas, 5 Matratzen.  Der Raum ist nicht beheizbar. Deswegen nehmen wir WWoofer gerne zwischen Ostern und Oktober auf. Wenn ihr zu einer anderen Zeit kommen wollt, sprecht uns gerne an. Bitte bringt Euren eigenen Schlafsack, Arbeitsklamotten und ein Handtuch mit! Wir haben einige Gummistiefel und Handschuhe zu verleihen. Wir sprechen Französisch, Englisch, Spanisch . Wir freuen uns auf viele Menschen!

Before you leave we will ask you to vacuum clean the area, clean the bathroom and kitchen so that it is nice for the next wwoofers to come. All the necessary gear we will provide. Thank you!

name: Micah - Small Organic farm at the heart of a Waldorf inspired school, kindergarten to grade 8
description:
We live in Blockhouse Nova Scotia, we are a family of 7, ages 1 to 15. We run a Waldorf school 3 days a week. At the heart of the school is our farm. We have pigs, goats, sheep, geese, chickens, bunnies, a pony, we also have a green house and vegetable garden.
Our ideal woofers are willing to work in the garden, planting, weeding, and watering the vegetable patch and greenhouse. 
Willing to assist in fence building and light barn maintenance. 

Our farming activities are feeding and caring for the animals, fence building, gardening and building new structures on the property for the farm life and school.

Our woofer will have their own clean and comfortable room. We cannot offer exclusive use of the room, it has a shared closet and our storage access. 

hobbies include music, dance, martial arts, hiking, beaches, camping, swimming in the lakes.
We eat dinner together as a family and each take turns making dinner.
_wwoof_travel: 12 minutes to Lunenburg, Bridgewater, 60 minutes to halifax/Airport

name: Charlotte - Organic permaculture-inspired nonprofit project in beautiful Finland, MN
description:
If you are interested in volunteering with us in 2026, please reach out! We ask for a minimum of 2 weeks for all stays.

The Land - We are an organic permaculture-inspired project on the edge of the village of Finland, MN. Of the 15 acres, 3.5 acres are developed for perennial and annual food production. On the land, there is a house, barn/event space, Deep Winter Greenhouse (DWG),  and the main office for the Organic Consumers Association (our parent organization). A 1/4 acre garden is being developed into an intensive vegetable production area for demonstration and education around growing successfully in this northern climate. A larger perennial garden (3 acres) is established with terraced medicinal herb gardens and berms and swales full of fruiting trees and shrubs.

The Work - Our work consists of planting and caring for a mixture of vegetables, herbs, flowers, and fruiting trees and shrubs in a way that works with the land here in the Boreal Region of northeastern Minnesota. Throughout the season, tasks vary and may include establishing compost piles, hauling mulch, manure, and soils, picking and hauling rocks for bed construction, fixing our tamarack log deer fence, mowing, planting, weeding, harvesting, etc. We often sell our produce at the Finland Farmers Market, Finland Cooperative, and community events.

Educational Opportunities - WWOOFers will be able to learn firsthand about Permaculture and AgroEcology methods, soils, seeds, planting,  finances, tools and equipment. Additionally, the AgroEcology Center will host trainings throughout the summer on a variety of topics including mushrooms, bees, and medicinals which WWOOFers are welcome to attend.

Additional Benefits -  Finland is located in the heart of the Lake Superior North Shore region of Minnesota.  Amazing nature hikes alongside wild rivers and waterfalls abound, we are only miles away from the Boundary Waters which offer a wonderful backwoods experience for hiking, camping, and canoeing.

Livin

name: MARCIO - Sustainable, Organic & Regenerative Agroforestry: Building a real Living from the Land in Pernambuco
description:
Hi, I’m Marcio and for the last 8 years, I’ve been dedicated to a mission that is as challenging as it is rewarding : turning 10 hectares of degraded sugarcane land in Ipojuca into a thriving, syntropic food forest.

I live here with my son (part-time), and Im right in the middle of a massive "re-building phase." This isn't a finished, postcard-perfect farm—it’s a living, breathing project where we deal with the real, grit-under-the-fingernails challenges of rural life in the Brazilian Northeast.

Why we need you right now
The forest is already giving back, and we are producing amazing things like fresh vegetables, honey, dehydrated fruits and artisanal tapioca. But to be honest? We are still figuring out how to scale everything. I need people who don’t just want to follow a "to-do" list, but who want to put their brains and hearts into the process.

I’m looking for someone who wants to help us:
- Scale the Field: Improve our planting and maintenance routines to increase production while staying true to the soil.
- Establish the Business: We need help organizing our sales, refining our marketing, and professionalizing our CSA (Community Supported Agriculture) deliveries.
- Build Resilience: From small constructions to solving the daily puzzles of rural logistics—we are building a life of happiness here, one challenge at a time.

Rhythm & Flexibility
Life on a farm doesn't always follow a 9-to-5 schedule. Around here, Saturday and Sunday can be normal workdays, depending on the weather, the needs of the land, or the harvest. However, your two days off are flexible. We coordinate everything in advance, ensuring you have clear time to rest and explore. It’s all about balance and clear communication.

Table: Nutrition and Truth
We believe that regeneration starts with what we put on our plates. Our foundation is what the earth gives us as much as possible: plenty of fruits and vegetables, roots (cassava, yams), eggs, beans, honey and the traditional "cuscuz." We also co
_wwoof_travel: Ipojuca or Cabo de Santo Agostinho

name: Jacques - Production de plantes médicinales dans les Hauts de la Réunion
description:
Ma ferme propose une activité de production de plantes médicinales ainsi qu'une pépinière de plantes indigènes. 
Je suis installé depuis 2015 dans les hauts de St Paul sur la côte ouest de l'île à 1 100m d'altitude. Pas de forte chaleur en janvier/février (max 25°) et du frais en hiver (min 5°). Endroit calme, on entend plus les oiseaux que les voitures.
Niveau activité il y a de quoi faire sur la pépinière (désherbage, repiquage, rempotage) et sur les plantations (entretien, récolte, défrichage). Je demande un coup de main les matins entre 8h et 12h, après-midi et week-end libre.
Mon logement est juste à côté (100m) avec une chambre indépendante pour le(s) wwoofeur(s) avec draps, couette et couverture; cuisine et salle de bain/wc accessibles en permanence. Repas végétariens, on cuisine et on mange ensemble.
A la maison: 2 chats, 3 poules, des abeilles et un potager.
Dans le coin: randonnées (à pied ou à cheval), accrobranche, point de vue (Maïdo à 30min en voiture).

name: Robert - Organic baby leaf greens farm
description:
Background
Hi there, my name is Rob. I am owner and operator of Good Time Greens farm, which is located near Berwick, Nova Scotia, about an hour and 15 minutes from downtown Halifax, and from the Halifax airport. I’m the only person who lives here, occasionally I have a boyfriend and family who come to stay. The farm is a home-based business that I run part-time and I sell baby greens to grocery stores, while I work full-time.

Typical Schedule
Typical farming activities during the day include harvest first thing in the morning. Then, there is packaging and cleanup. After that, there is tending to the fields. Weeding, watering, and other miscellaneous tasks. Following that there is bed preparation and seeding for the next crop. This usually occurs in the morning and there is only about 4 hours worth of work in the mornings, over the weekdays, with weekends off.

The House
There is a house on the farm that has five bedrooms. Three of them have beds and I only use one of them so there’s additional accommodation for a visiting farmer as well as another guest. There is a large kitchen with a dining room. Obviously, there are a lot of greens in the field, and I otherwise make food and have prepared foods ready for eating for convenience. It gets quite hot in the summer, but I have an air conditioner to ensure comfort.

Transportation
I'm in Halifax once per week and so pick ups and drop offs are possible, if I am going in. There is also a bus from Halifax to Wolfville, which is the closest town with an intercity bus stop, I can do runs to pick up and drop off there at any time. There is a municipal bus service that strings along all of the towns in the  Annapolis Valley, if you want to explore. A bike is available if you need wheels while here and Berwick (with all services including grocery and restaurant and pubs) is 5 km away. I do have a truck and I'm happy to drive you somewhere, if I am going there. Sadly I cannot let you drive the truck as my insurance does not al

name: Andre - Mixed vegetable farm, small orchard and chickens
description:
Hello WWOOFERs, we are a small organic farm in the central Kootenays of beautiful British Columbia. We grow fruits (berries, mixed fruit trees) and vegetables as well as raise chickens for eggs. We specialize in Indigenous food systems and also forage for traditional foods.

We do daily activities on the farm including weeding, harvesting, washing and packing produce for market. I would like to teach WWOOFers how to get ready for market and run a market stall, how to make compost in bulk, how to use compost tea, and how to forest garden as well.  On the non-farming side, we have a lot of forest and are cleaning up the forest to create mulch for the garden and for foraging. Chicken care is also a daily task. Keeping the property tidy and welcoming is also a priority.

We have most meals together including lunch and dinner and are looking for someone to participate in shared meals by taking on the meal making a day a two a week. We have lots of farm produce so meals are full and varied. We are not vegetarian but do enjoy vegetarian meals.

The Kootenays is an extraordinary place. There is recreation everywhere. Although we don't play any instruments, there are many locals who do and have open mic sessions. We like to hike and hang out on the local beach on hot summer afternoons.  There is plenty of mountain biking around as well. The food scene in Nelson is excellent as is the arts scene so even though this is rural living, we get the best of city life.

name: Cole - Organic farm at a beachfront hotel
description:
Hello WWOOFers, we are a lovely little hotel on a quiet beach in the Nicoya Peninsula, one of five Blue Zones in the world. We generally focus on yoga retreats, but occasionally we are open to the public as well. The tranquility, silence, and nature here are unparalleled. We don't keep any pets, but it doesn't mean we are without friends- birds, butterflies, iguanas, red squirrels, foxes, raccoons, howler monkeys, pozotes, and a myriad of others live on our property. Its not uncommon to find local bull and a couple of his friends wandering in off the beach to have a drink from our salt water pool with a couple of his friends in tow.

Our property has seemingly endless trees that give off fruit depending on the season (limes, mandarins, grapefruit, starfruit, guanabana, hibiscus, ylang ylang, marignon, mangos upon mangos, kumquats, mangosteens, merenga, it seems every day a new one is coming into fruit or flower). Currently we have a well sized greenhouse and a garden on property to help grow our own organic food for use in our restaurant. The main focus of any WWOOFers would be the tending to, rotation, and harvesting of this greenhouse & garden on a day to day basis with integration in the culinary program in mind. This would be lead by our lead gardener who is a Tica and raised in the area, so she knows the land and best practices well. 

We have on site housing that is dedicated for volunteers. If we have the availability during the stay, we will also rotate people into hotel rooms so they can enjoy the place from the view of our guests. We have a fully functional, local, seasonal kitchen that puts out three meals a day for our guests. These meals will also be available for WWOOFers! Local seafood, fresh veggies, rice and beans (wouldn't be Costa Rica without it!), and other lovely fare. Our entire culinary program is gluten and dairy free, and vegetarian/vegan friendly as well.

In Costa Rica its all about the nature. The "office" from which we are typing this d

name: Mary - Veggies, Chickens, No till No chemicals, Vermiculture and Spiritual & Educational Center
description:
Hi  WWOOFers!   Welcome to our awesome farm in a quite peaceful part of South Jersey USA.  
We are now hosting WWOOFers to help bring our farm back to life. Some of the help we need is chicken care, vermiculture assistand, weeding, and planting. This person will learn about feeding and caring for our 60 chicken flock. For the summer short term is preferred. Many wwoofers find our farm a great place to relax and regroup.
Also seeking a farm assistant manager apprentice. Learn what we do on our micro farm and have an active role in sharing this magical place with others. 

We are a teaching farm where you will learn about sustainable, regenerative methods of growing food and restoring the soil.  

The property is 13-acres and we have two big gardens where we grow veggies, berries & herbs. We raise 60 free-range, pasture chickens for eggs. We also have a worm farm where we raise worms for castings/fertilizer.   You can visit the Medicine Wheel, the Labyrinth & Fairy Grove on walking paths in the woods surrounding our farm .  We have 2 energetic dogs, 1 sweet older cat, 2 fun-loving twin cats.  

Only natural, no-till, beyond organic (more-ganic) farming methods have been used for the past 45 years! Hügelkultur, Vermiculture, Lasagna gardening, vertical growing, raised beds, Thermophilic compost, woodchip gardening, mulching and more!

We welcome WWOOFers who have a positive outlook on life and are enthusiastic about learning sustainable, regenerative growing practices.  Camping experience is useful. Some gardening experience is beneficial but not necessary. Knowledge of how to do minor repairs, work with power tools & a chainsaw is a plus, but not necessary. We have most of the tools needed to do these repairs. 

WHAT YOU WILL  LEARN: 
You will have fun with  "boots-in-the-dirt", "hands-on" learning experiences. See all the activities that go into having a small, regenerative farm/garden. The longer you stay, the more you will learn.  We have an extensive library of ga
_wwoof_travel: Philadelphia - Bus #410 stops half mile from farm

name: Scholastika Namutenya - We grow trees, vegetables, and raise animals.
description:
Hello Wwoofers!!!
Iiyimati Homestead is a family-run initiative committed to self-sustainability and environmental consciousness. In partnership with Living Earth Namibia, we strive to inspire our community to embrace eco-friendly practices while producing nutritious food for both humans and animals.
We are based in northern Namibia, in the Oshana Region—less than 7 km from Oshakati along the D3642 gravel road. Our homestead is a vibrant family space, home to two friendly dogs and a cat. We warmly welcome visitors, community members, volunteers, and businesses interested in learning and sharing.

Our 4-hectare homestead supports a variety of livestock including chickens, ducks, goats, quails, and pigs. We grow most of the animals’ feed ourselves using traditional methods. At Iiyimati, you will experience the richness of Oshiwambo cultural traditions and meaningful cultural exchange.
Our farming approach blends traditional practices with modern, sustainable techniques, with a strong commitment to environmentally friendly methods. We practice agroforestry and uphold a zero-waste philosophy in everything we do.

Facilities & Accessibility:
•	A sleeping room & bed, shared showering facilities, and toilet
•	Clean drinking water
•	Electricity and internet access
•	Cellphone network coverage
•	Public transport available from 06:45 to 19:00
Diet:
•	Mainly omnivorous meals
•	Vegetarian options available (please note that meat plays a significant role in Namibian culture)
Meals:
•	Breakfast 
•	Heavy lunch as the main daily meal
•	Light Dinner

We enjoy:
- exercising: cycling, jogging, and walking; 
- visiting the local markets 
- taking part in communal activities
- cooking

name: Nanelyn - Sustainable homestead on 31 acres with 30+ waterfalls nearby
description:
💠 Welcome to Our Farm 💠

Our farm was purchased in 2016 with a clear intention: to grow tea (Camellia sinensis) and other marketable crops within a regenerative, education-centered system—and to do it slowly, thoughtfully, and well.

We live on 31 acres surrounding a home built in 1907. About 15 acres are open pasture, and the rest is hardwood forest—quiet, shady, and full of life. It’s a place where you can work with your hands in the morning and take a reflective walk in the woods afterward.

Even though a major agricultural university is less than five miles away, this place feels wonderfully removed from town life.

💠 Forests, Water & Rest 💠
