Beyond our property are thousands of acres of protected university forest. There are more than 30 waterfalls within an hour’s drive, three within five minutes, and at least one within walking distance.

Seasonally, WWOOFers are also welcome to enjoy our heated saltwater pool—a favorite way to unwind after a morning of good work.

💠 How We Farm 💠 

✅ Our farming practices are rooted in care, observation, and stewardship of God’s creation. Organic principles, permaculture design, and regenerative methods guide everything we do.

✅ We don’t spray pesticides or insecticides. Instead, we rely on:

🔹compost and soil building
🔹mulching, chop-and-drop, and polycultures
🔹biological and mechanical solutions

We love explaining why something works—not just asking you to do it.

💠 What You’ll Be Helping With 💠

Work here changes with the seasons, but WWOOFers often help with:

🔹Tea leaf harvesting, withering, rolling, and processing
🔹Seed collection, germination, potting, and planting
🔹Garden and orchard care (weeding, watering, mulching)
🔹Composting and soil improvement
🔹Caring for animals (dog, cats, chickens)
🔹Nursery and shade-structure work
🔹Harvesting fruits, vegetables, and herbs
🔹Food preservation projects when timing allows

Many of the projects you’ll touch are about building for the future, not just quick results.

Work can
_wwoof_travel: Clemson Amtrack Station 10 minutes away -- GSP International airport 50 minutes away

name: Hakan - Peyniriyle ünlü köyde aile çiftçiliği / Family farm in the village with a cheese museum
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Hakan Yazıcılar ve ailesi, Kars'ın 2300 m rakımlı Boğatepe (eski adıyla Zavot) köyünde yaşıyorlar. Kökleri Kafkaslar'a dayanan aile, temel geçim kaynakları olan hayvancılık işini kuşaklardır sürdürüyor. Zavot ırkı inekleri, tertemiz bir havası olan bölgede çeşit çeşit bitkinin yetiştiği çayırlarda özgürce otluyor. Hakan Bey sert kış mevsiminde ineklerini yazın biçtiği yulaf, arpa ve otlarla besliyor. Sağlıklı beslenen bu ineklerden sağılan sütü köy mandırasına veriyor ve mandıra bu sütle Kars'ın meşhur kaşar ve gravyer peynirini üretiyor. Geleneksel kaşar ve gravyer yapımının halen sürdüğü birkaç köyden biri olan Boğatepe/Zavot köyünde peynir kültürünü belgelemek, yaşatmak ve gelecek nesillere aktarmak için eski bir mandıra restore edilerek Türkiye'nin ilk ve tek peynir müzesi kurulmuş.
Gürcistan ve Ermenistan sınırına yakın olan bölgede birçok kültürün izi var. Boğatepe/Zavot köyü üretiminden mimarisine kadar birçok açıdan kültürünü korumayı başarmış ender köylerden. Kışın ısınmak için sadece tezek kullanılıyor. Etkileyici bir doğası olan bölgenin coğrafi yapısı ve kültürü gereği mutfağı daha çok hayvansal ürünlerden oluşuyor.

HAZ-EYL: Süt sağımı ve ev işlerine yardım (kadınlar için), yulaf, arpa, ot biçme, biçilen otların taşınması ve yığılması

---

Hakan Yazıcılar and his family live in Boğatepe (also known as Zavot) village in Kars, at an altitude of 2300 m. The family, with their roots in Caucasus, have been dealing with cattle breeding as livelihood for many generations. Their special Zavot cows graze freely on the meadows where many different wild herbs grow. In the harsh winter of Kars, the host feeds his cows with barley, oat and herbs that he harvests in the summer. The milk of these healthy cows go to the cheesery of the village and turns into the famous Kars 'kaşar' and gruyère. The first and only cheese museum of Turkey is located in this village, with the aim of documenting and preserving the Zavot cheese culture and

name: Paphatchaya - Building a food forest and self-sufficient homestead in rural Thailand. Home to sheep, geese, ducks,
description:
Hello WWOOFers,

My name is Nok. I am a Thai pharmacist currently living and working in the Netherlands, but my heart project is a small food forest and self-sufficiency homestead in Sisaket, northeastern Thailand.

Over the past five years, I have been transforming 6 rai (0.96 hectares) of land into a place where fruit trees, animals and nature can thrive together. The property includes a pond, a solar-powered water system, hundreds of trees, and a growing food forest.

Our animal family currently includes sheep, geese, ducks and many wild birds that visit the land throughout the year.

The vision of Nok’s Nest is to create a peaceful and sustainable place where people can reconnect with nature, learn practical skills and experience authentic rural Isaan life.

Most of the time my mother helps care for the property while I manage the project from the Netherlands and visit Thailand regularly.

Daily activities vary depending on the season.

WWOOFers may help with:

* Feeding and caring for sheep, geese and ducks
* Planting fruit trees and native trees
* Watering and maintaining the food forest
* Harvesting fruits and vegetables
* Building simple farm structures and fences
* Improving habitats for wildlife
* General gardening and land stewardship

We are especially interested in creating a regenerative ecosystem where animals, trees and water systems support each other naturally.

A typical day starts early in the morning before the tropical heat. We usually work for a few hours, take a long lunch break, and continue with lighter activities in the late afternoon.

WWOOFers can learn about tropical fruit trees, small-scale animal care, food forest design and rural Thai culture.

WWOOFers will stay in simple accommodation on the property. The setting is peaceful and surrounded by nature.

Please note that this is a rural village environment, not a resort. Life here is simple, relaxed and connected to nature.

We value mutual respect, curiosity and a willingness to lear

name: Adriana - Cultivamos hortaliças, criamos poedeiras, fazemos geleias e doces. Sistema agroflorestal.
description:
Somosnum.casal "neorural":uma veterinária que fez transição para a carreira agrícola,em 2021. Meu esposo é um técnico de informática que fez o mesmo..nascidos e criados em cidade grande, hoje moramos num sítio que nos traz alegria e desafios, também .Temos uma nascente e muita área verde,natural e cultivada; nosso projeto é de sustentabilidade, regeneração e rentabilidade e nossa única fonte de renda. Somos apenas nós dois. Temos 2 gatos e 3 cães, amigáveis. Aqui encontramos uma vida saudável, cheia de curiosidades e aprendizado.
Cultivamos hortaliças, árvores frutíferas, principalmente mangueiras. Criamos galinhas poedeiras, fazemos geleias variadas, molhos e conservas. Doces de leite e de frutas. E até queijos eventualmente.
Adoraríamos receber pessoas interessadas realmente em observar, cuidar e aprender com a Natureza. Aqui temos tranquilidade, beleza, ar puro e tudo isso sem estar isolados. Estamos a 8 km da cidade. A rotina é cuidar dos animais, das plantações, fazer os alimentos processados. Buscar esterco e capim picado nos vizinhos (de carro).Fazer consertos necessários nas benfeitorias do sítio , eventuais cuidados da saúde dos animais e as limpezas de rotina. Além das paradas para.descanso e curtir esse lindo lugar.
Os arredores são aprazíveis para caminhadas.
Duas vezes por semana vendemos nossos produtos na cidade.
Temos internet boa e usamos bastante para estudar e para lazer.

Temos disponível 1 quarto com um banheiro exclusivo. As refeições geralmente são organizadas de acordo com o tempo do serviço. (Geralmente 2, mais lanche). Logo cedo tomamos chimarrão.  Comemos de tudo. Não temos televisão. Mas assistimos filmes no notebook. Gostamos de ler,  Ouvimos música variada.
Faço Liang Gong em casa, 3 vezes por semana.. Não gostamos de som alto. Não fumamos. Gosto de cozinhar, fazemos pães variados para o nosso café. Gosto de testar receitas novas e também inventar novas combinações de sabores.
Venha nos conhecer e curtir nosso paraíso.

name: Juha - We need help with garden and historical park.  Cattle Ranch, Mixed Farm,  Family farm,
description:
We are cattlefarm in southern Finland one hour from Helsinki. We have been farming organic, biodynamic and permaculture 25 years. We have large natural meadows and landscapes around the farm. We are repairing old buildings in the farm. 

Organic methods: composting, crop rotation, using cattle to maintain biodiversity and soil structure & fertility.

Certifications: luomu, official orcanic production

Current projects: We are starting to repair and renovate buildings and houses

Internet access available

name: Ryan - Off-grid homestead by the sea - a true oasis of sustainable living!
description:
Thanks for checking out our page! On 20 acres of forested foothills, our off-grid homestead is a true effort in sustainable living. We're passionate about growing our own food and living off the land, and we welcome volunteers who share our love of self-sufficiency.

Note: one of our daughters has just had a baby and simultaneously been diagnosed with a rare genetic disease and requires one of us to be with her four days a week for the foreseeable future (that would be me, Donna). Because of this, we have decided to put our wwoofer program on hold for the most part. We could potentially host someone who is independent, a self-starter, with a vehicle and some gardening experience, who doesn’t mind working alone.  Ryan would still be available to teach off-grid systems. 
If this description fits you and your desires, please read on. Thanks!

Now back to the description:

Prepare to immerse yourself in the rhythms of nature, working alongside us as we tend to our large permaculture-inspired garden, care for our  pets (two very snobby ducks, young chickens, three talented mousers, three large friendly dogs), and do our best to live in harmony with the environment. Our power comes from a small hydro system, solar, wood fire, and propane. You'll have the chance to learn all about alternative energy systems. We operate our homestead with less power than what most people are accustomed to, so keep in mind that while we can definitely charge our phones, and lights, we can't run electric heat or other large loads. We have high-speed satellite internet.

We believe in living simply and in harmony with the earth, and we welcome volunteers who share our values. 

As a volunteer, you'll have the opportunity to get your hands dirty and contribute to a sustainable way of life. Depending on your skill level and interests, you can help us with a variety of chores, some mundane and some more enjoyable. 

Here are just a few examples:

* Gardening: Our large garden is the heart of our

name: Javiera - La Lechuza Ecolodge es una granja ecológica y área natural protegida en la zona central de Misiones.
description:
Hola! Somos una familia argentina-suiza compuesta por dos adultos y tres hijos (dos adolescentes y un niño). Vivimos en una hermosa y muy diversa granja rodeada de selva misionera con arroyos y cascadas. Nuestro trabajo es muy diverso y por eso siempre necesitamos manos que nos ayuden.

Las tareas rutinarias de la granja son el cuidado de los animales, ordeñe de vacas, limpieza de las jaulas. En la huerta hay que regar, sembrar, cosechar. Tenemos huerta agroforestal y hay mucho trabajo de poda de arboles tambien. En la cocina hacemos queso dia por medio ,  cuando hay cosecha de frutas o en la huerta ., se prepara para la elaboracion. Elaboramos mermeladas, pickles, escabeches. Criamos animales y se hace la carneada en la granja.

los voluntarios tienen un pequeño departamente fuera de la casa. Es un monoambiente con 2 camas, ecritorio y un lavatorio. El departamento cuenta con wifi.  Los voluntarios cuentan con las 3  o 4 comidas. Comemos generalmente juntos.

Somos una familia que nos gusta la vida en la naturaleza, nos gusta disfrutar de nuestro entorno natural. No encanta pasar tiempo juntos , en la piscina de la granja o en nuestro salon de juegos.

name: Hamish - Flower grower, tree nursery, woodland, house and garden
description:
WE ARE FULLY BOOKED UNTIL THE 6TH OF SEPTEMBER 2026. 

The estate has been in the family for over 100 years and 15 years ago I started to transform 60 acres of my woodlands into an arboretum. The main idea is to plant trees from all over the world. The aim of the project is to understand the needs of the trees that can take the place of lost native species in the future due to climate change but also to create a beautiful amenity. The Burgie Arboretum is my personal stand against climate change. All trees and shrubs are grown from hand collected seeds and cuttings. The work varies from planting, weeding, labelling, estate maintains including fencing, road repairs, water works,... 

WWOOFers will stay in simple accommodation with private bathroom and share the kitchen and lounge area. A washing machine is available.

WWOOFers will be self catering and eat their meals independently, but food of your choice will be provided. I can take up to 6 WWOOFers at any one time. 3 pairs provided they are prepared to share a room.

4G is bad here but it is possible in certain parts of the estate.
There are a lot of activities that can be done from this part of Scotland of outstanding beauty and mild climate such as cycling, walking, canoeing and dolphins watching. Touring at the week ends is encouraged and there is a vast amount of adventures that can be done. There is a good bus and rail services locally.
Cheap car rental can be arranged too but you will have to have the necessary documentation for driving.

The whole object of coming to BURGIE is to have FUN!

Organic methods: Planting, seeds propagating, weeding, mulching, ...

Current projects: The arboretum is on going with lots of planting, developments, planning and some constructional work most of the time

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Fly or Train to Inverness or Aberdeen. Train or Bus to Forres, then A96 by car to Elgin - 4 Miles on A96 road to south sign posted in wrought iron "Burgie Mains". Further details as to House etc by phone.

name: Antoine - Granja de hortaliza ecológica
description:
Cultivamos 4 hectáreas de hortaliza orgánico certificado.
Vendemos exclusivamente en el mercado local. Organizamos "ventas en la granja" 2 veces a la semana, y repartimos a  restaurantes.

Nuestros métodos de cultivo se inspiran en varios tipos de escuelas (permacultura, biodinámico, método Fortier, tradiciones locales), en las que tomamos lo que nos parece más relevante en función de nuestros objetivos de Biotop en el que trabajamos.

Nous cultivons environ 4 hectares en maraichage certifié biologique.
Nous vendons exclusivement sur le marché local, des particuliers qui viennent à nos "vente à la ferme" 2 fois par semaine, à des magasins de proximité, et des restaurants.

Nos méthodes de cultures sont inspirées par plusieurs types d'écoles (permaculture, biodynamic, méthode fortier, traditions locales), dans lesquels nous prenons ce qui nous semble le plus pertinent en fonction de nos objectifs de du biotop dans lequel nous travaillons.

name: Noemi - Educational Centre, Agritourism Destination, Fruit tree nursery, Mixed Farm, Community farm
description:
Be part of our experimental agroecological farm in the Bolivian country side

Buenas, I am Noemi and I came to Bolivia to start this heart project of mine over 25 years ago. I’m running an experimental agroecological farm with 23 hectares in 3 different places located within the semiarid valleys of Cochabamba. My team consists of a few local employees working on the farm and we’re joined by international and local students who are researching and writing their thesis within their own projects and last but not the least by the woofers that join me through this platform - all co-working, exchanging and learning together about agroforestry and agroecology. Through this work, the 23 hectares that form this place are recovering their ecological value, thanks to the application of agroforestry systems coupled with agroecological measures. 
Here in Mollesnejta we are proving that it is possible to produce fruits and vegetables without agrochemicals, simply by applying the ancestral knowledge about sustainable agriculture that agroecology is providing us with. This technique has proven to be able to improve the productivity of the land and at the same time being ecologically sustainable. We have therefore contributed not only to recover soils and biodiversity at the semiarid valleys of Cochabamba, but also to promote organic food and we are aiming to achieve economic stability. 
In 2017 our project suffered a terrible fire. From that moment on, our team decided to reformulate the project and transform it into an “Institute for Andean Agroforestry”. This decision was founded in the desire to use all the wisdom accumulated during all these years, as well as our strong motivation to keep promoting agroecology as a tool for recovering the vulnerable ecosystem of the Semiarid Andean valleys of Bolivia. 
In 2023 we also lost half of our agroforestry plots in a fire. The entire mountainside was in flames. Without the natural vegetation, there was a landslide during the rainy seaso
_wwoof_travel: From the Plaza Bolivar en Quillcollo take the trufi 208 to COMBUYO until "La estación final arriba pasando por la casa de Don René"

name: Jacobiene - Agritourism Destination, Eco Retreat, Orchard
description:
I am a Dutch citizen and have been visiting Kyrgyzstan for over 30 years. For my work as consultant for a Dutch engineering company, I drafted a strategy for sustainable development for the Issyk-Kul region and the village of Grigorievka. After that, I decided to execute a part of the strategy myself, in partnership with local NGOs and local people. At Bel-Zhan we focus on sustainable tourism and fruit processing. Both can add substantial value to the present level of income. I like to support the villagers in developing their livelihood in a sustainable way, using environmental friendly materials and technologies and doing community based business. In June-October we harvest, process (mainly dry) and pack fruits from the garden (raspberries, apricots, cherries, prunes, apples, pears)  and wild berries (seabuck thorn and berberis) in order generate income and preserve the bushes that are important for the ecosystem, and thus add value to the local economy and enhance sustanaible development. For summer 2022 (June-October) we are looking for WWOOFers who can help rharvest, process and pack fruits and berries. We can also use hands in April-May in building up the lodge .
The fruits grow in our own garden , the seabuckthorn berries and berberis near the lake shore. The lodge has a house in which I live myself, and with a small seperate room for a volunteer. Toilets (waterflushed) and showers (hot water from a solar boiler) are in the garden. We have a cook, a driver, some technical help and a cleaning person.

Certifications: In the course of aranging certification for sea buck thorn berries - fair wild, complicated procedure, expensive and the first time in Kyrgyzstan

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Coming form direction Bishkek, pass the center of Grigorievka village, turn right at the second street after the school with soccer field on your right hand.

name: Cheri - Honey and blueberry producer located about 45 minutes from New Orleans
description:
Hello from southeast Louisiana.  

We are a small farm located about 45 minutes from New Orleans, focusing on bees, chickens, flowers, & blueberries. Our operation sits on 50 acres of mixed hardwood forest, nestled between a wildlife refuge, a State Park, & a 30-mile-long Rails-to-Trails bike path. Opportunities to hike, bike, & canoe abound, & should you want world-renowned dining & a vibrant music scene, New Orleans is a short drive from the farm.

We are an official Monarch Waystation and partner with the USDA in the Conservation Stewardship Program, which includes increasing pollinator habitat. We seed to feed the pollinators, so in addition to migrating Monarchs, you will see a kaleidoscope of butterflies, bees, & hummingbirds here. As a special treat, fireflies can be seen many evenings May - September.

We have limited indoor lodging. There is a loft in the climate-controlled barn, an RV, and plenty of room for campers & tents if you can handle rustic. There is an indoor kitchen and shower/bathroom, as well as outdoor showers and composting toilets. Internet access is available at the barn. 

Keep climate in mind when you consider a stay in the Gulf South.  It can be hot & humid from May through September. Conversely, we can see freezing temps from November to March, with January and February being the coldest. Hurricane season is June through October, with late August/early September the most active. April, October, & November are, as Goldilocks famously said, usually just right. 

We evacuate for hurricanes.  If you come during hurricane season, please be prepared for the possibility of evacuation. You can discuss your plans with us if you are unsure what this entails.  Being stuck without power & AC in the bug-infested southern heat is apocalyptic at best.

The workload is light, and WWOOFers are here to learn & work. We expect you to contribute 4-5 hours a day, 4-5 days a week, or a similar agreed-upon contribution.

The best stay length right now would b
_wwoof_travel: Train station in Slidell or New Orleans, or New Orleans International Airport. We can pick up in New Orleans on weekdays only.

name: Cinzia - Agriturismo, Azienda Agricola, Progetto di Recupero e Conservazione del Paesaggio
description:
Lavoriamo dal 2005 sui nostri 24 ettari per ripristinare biodiversità ricreando un ecosistema che comprenda anche l’uomo. 
Siamo a 350 metri slm, fuori dal centro abitato, a circa 6km dal mare, 15km dal cratere dell’Etna, 20km da Taormina e 30km da Catania. A cento metri si ferma la circumetnea, un piccolo treno che collega tutti i paesi intorno al vulcano. 
Ci dedichiamo secondo i principi della permacultura, e tecniche di agric. rigenerativa, alla cura dell'agrumeto preesistente, e del vigneto, frutteto, oliveto e melograneto e almeno un centinaio di differenti altre varietà di alberi e  cespugli impiantati in questi anni. L’orto sinergico quando riusciamo a curarlo è destinato principalmente all’autoconsumo e agli ospiti dell'agriturismo.
Vivono con noi gatti, cani, e un modesto pollaio quando riusciamo a risparmiarlo alla volpe.
Il cuore è un vecchio palmento ristrutturato, e ospitiamo i turisti in tre mini appartamenti. 
Il nostro obiettivo è trovare un modello di vita alternativo a quello urbano, senza rinnegare il progresso. Prestiamo attenzione al risparmio energetico, alla riduzione dei consumi e alla produzione di energia alternativa. 
La gestione dell'agriturismo impegna buona parte del nostro tempo quindi in base ai periodi PUO' CAPITARE DI DOVER fare le attivita' DA SOLI, ci fa comodo aiuto anche per la cura della casa, e degli animali, e in base alle stagioni per altri lavori come;
NOVEMBRE e DICEMBRE si raccolgono le clementine e in cantina si etichettano le bottiglie
Nel mese di GENNAIO/FEBBRAIO serve ripulire il frutteto dalle potature e si raccolgono i mandarini e i limoni
MARZO/APRILE/MAGGIO si raccolgono i capperi, e se c'è tempo si imposta l'orto, e iniziano a spuntare le fragole
Nel mese di GIUGNO si lavora in vigna 
LUGLIO e AGOSTO fa molto caldo e  ci si prepara per la vendemmia di SETTEMBRE
Da OTTOBRE si raccolgono melograni e mele cotogne

Quando abbiamo iniziato eravamo un piccolo gruppo di amici con il tempo ognuno ha preso strade diverse
_wwoof_travel: Stazione di Fiumefreddo di Sicilia / aeroporto Fontanarossa di  Catania

name: Leikny Sofie - Small scale farm,  many different vegetables and berries.
description:
1. How to get here:  By flight to Tromsø or by Train to Narvik and buss to Tromsø. To come here from Tromsø, you either use bus 428 or 430. Or hitchike. The busstopp is called Bråthen.

2. Are you interested of learning about growing vegetables, berries , herbs, potatoes in this arctic areas close to Tromsø ? 
3. Our surrounding:
We live in the land of the midnight-sun and  in the land of the Northern light. Bright, clean fresh and Cold. Living in the nature, borrowing from nature- without destroying. Good area for hiking, lots to do on your spare time.
We are close to city of Tromsø.  

4. Food: We do seasonal and lokal food  for the meals.  We have one hot meal every day (dinner at about 5 pm). Traditionally norwegian eat quite mutch bread for all meals except for dinnermeal.
5. Making dinner / hot food: we make   typical norwegian., seasional  food. You will get chance to make some food from your place if  we can get the engreedience for a useable price. Cheese is very expensive in Norway,  and specially foreign cheese cost a lot. If you dont like the food you get here, you have to by alternativ option and prepare for yourselves.

Staying here:  Our genuine interest for organic farming made us open our home for wwoofers. We like to shere knowledge.  And we experience that wwoofers can to be interested about learning and sharing their knowledge with us.  You have to share beedroom with other wwoofers.  

All wwoofers help keeping household nice and tidy evereywhere, every day.  hoowering, clining after yourselves, specially in bathroom to avoid illness.  Keep your bedroom clean and tidy.  

Certifications: Debio

Current projects: Making more areas for growing berries and herbes weeding and planting.  

Internet access available. 
Televition in one of the bedrooms. Two old bikes, very "second hand" quality.

We also have a boat for rowing. If you like to do fishing, you can bring with your own fishingtools and lifebelt. And you have to pay a depose for the time yo
_wwoof_travel: Bus or flight to Tromsø. Farm is 10 km from  airport.  Narvik got train from Sweden. Bus from Narvik to Tromsø 5 hours. I can get wwoofers from airport in daytime on sundays.

name: Juan Guillermo - Its a Coliving. Based in biology, we do aquaponics and environmental education in Tayrona
description:
(No incluyas estas líneas de texto, te aconsejamos usarlas sólo como guía)
1. Describe tu proyecto, el lugar y sus habitantes (incluídos los animales):

El Proyecto trabaja en 14 de los 17 Objetivos de desarrollo sostenible de la Onu. Trabajamos Acuaponia, piscicultura sostenible, tenemos un semillero y un invernadero.  Nos ubicamos en la entrada al Tayrona, Santa Marta, Colombia. 

Los habitantes somos hongos, semillas, Plantulas, plantas de tomate, berenjena, cebollín y pimiento.  También hay un pez africanp (tilapia) y un pez nativo: el bocachico. DE las comunidades tenemos de la costa, de Cali y comunidades indígenas de la Sierra Nevada.  También hay Nomadas digitales ocasionales que disfrutan el coworking.

2. Haz una lista de las actividades en las que participarán los WWOOFers y qué quieres enseñarles:
Las actividades son: 
Piscicultura, 
Acuaponia,
Hidroponia

3. ¿Cómo sería el día de un WWOOFer en tu lugar (horario de participación en actividades, expectativas...)?
Se trabajan 4 horas al día por la estadía. 
En la mañana tipicamente rociamos y nutrimos las plantas, alimentamos los peces, en el intermedio hacemos mantenimiento y limpieza, incluso de zonas de aseo, barrer y trapear zonas comunes para el bienestar de toda la comunidad o nuevos visitantes  y al final algunas cosas creativcas o miniproyectos que se le ocurren al Dr Juan.

4.  Habla sobre tus propios intereses (música, cocina, etc.) y las posibilidades de los alrededores (senderismo, natación, etc.):

Hay posibilidad de hacer bici hasta Minca, Donama o Calabazo, caminatas a Bonda o Bahía concha en el Tayrona. Si se quiere ir un poco más lejos solo es necesario tomar un bus en frente de la granja.
_wwoof_travel: Del aeropuerto se toma un bus a Bonda y se bajan en la "Ye de Bonda" , allí se pueden recojer a los WWOOFers. o pueden tomar una moto par llegar un trayecto corto. También puede

name: Camille - Inti Eco Lodge es un ecolodge autosuficiente en la Amazonía peruana
description:
Vivimos en un ecolodge autosuficiente ubicado en plena selva amazónica peruana, a unas 3h30–4 horas de Iquitos (bus + bote). El lugar se extiende sobre aproximadamente 30 hectáreas, gran parte de ellas de selva primaria. Desde 2012, desarrollamos aquí un proyecto de vida sencillo, ecológico y respetuoso del territorio.

En el lugar viven las personas que gestionan el espacio en el día a día, así como voluntarios según los períodos. Trabajadores locales también están presentes de lunes a viernes. La vida es tranquila, comunitaria y no festiva, y requiere autonomía, responsabilidad y capacidad de adaptación a un entorno aislado.

🌿 Estilo de vida e infraestructuras

El sitio funciona en modo off-grid:
energía solar,
agua potable filtrada de un pozo,
baños secos (el compost se utiliza en el jardín),
cocina a gas,
iluminación con lámparas solares, velas y linternas frontales por la noche.

El Wi-Fi solar está disponible solo durante el día y se apaga por la noche. La señal telefónica es limitada. Es un lugar propicio para una desconexión voluntaria.

🛏️ Alojamiento de los voluntarios

Los voluntarios se alojan en dormitorio compartido, en cama individual con baúl de almacenamiento, y comparten los espacios comunes (cocina, baños y duchas exteriores). La vida es sencilla y colectiva.

🥗 Alimentación

La alimentación en el lugar es simple y 100 % vegetariana, adaptada al contexto del sitio (sin refrigeración permanente). Se basa en productos locales como arroz, pasta, verduras, papas, huevos, legumbres y frutas.
Los voluntarios participan en la vida de la cocina. Cualquier restricción o alergia debe comunicarse con antelación.

⏰ Organización del voluntariado
5 horas por día, de lunes a viernes, de 7:00 a 12:00

Tardes libres

Los voluntarios trabajan con gran autonomía, sin supervisión constante. Se realizan intercambios breves al inicio o al final de la mañana. Hay fichas de tareas detalladas disponibles (francés, inglés y español).

🔧 Actividades propuestas

Las tareas
_wwoof_travel: Aeropuerto Internacional Coronel FAP Francisco Secada Vignetta (IQT)

name: Hue - An organic farm run by a young and passionate team who share vision about sustainable lifestyle
description:
We are a young and passionate team who come together to run a lifestyle that helps us grow, teaches us various aspects of life, and gives us opportunities to contribute. Community values are something we treasure and guide us in our daily activities.

We are running different projects including:
-organic gardening and farming from a to z, 100% outdoor
-creative and non-stressful education for kids (only summer time)

We highly value community time spent together but we also respect when one member needs their own space, solitude and privacy.

The following is some important info about us that you should know before making decision to come and join us:
-We are an organic farm, located 30km from the center of Dalat. To get to our farm from Dalat, you can either take a taxi or a public bus. The public will stop at a station that is 7km from the farm. From there you can take taxi to our farm.
-The small town that we are in is not very popular for tourists as there are not much around to see. 
-We grow veggies and herbs on the farm, 100% outdoor. 
-We all eat vegetarian on the farm, and no smoking is allowed here. If you need to eat meat, you can go to foodstall or the market near our farm.
-There are many work on the farm such as planting new plants, watering, making new veggie beds, harvesting the seeds, processing the seeds.
-We provide basic accommodation (shared room). If you are looking for anything fancy (materially) for your stay, perhaps our place is not for you as we live very simple life here on the farm.
-We will provide vegetarian groceries and you will cook your own food. The groceries will include local seasonal veggies, beans, tofu, flours, rice, eggs...
-There are a lot of mosquitoes, and insects on our farm. If you are afraid of them, you will have a hard time living on the farm.
-Working hours is 5-6 hours per day. If you need to take a break or to take days-off, we can discuss in details after you start. Thursdays will be off day every week. 

In the

name: Noni - Community Farm at Beachside in Lombok
description:
Welcome to our regenerative, family-run project dedicated to permaculture and the principles of sustainable, self-sufficient living. Located on the stunning tropical island of Lombok, Indonesia, we are committed to fostering a harmonious ecosystem while promoting the slow living philosophy.

Our farm activities include really a lot of stuff: 
- Goats & Friends
- Seedlings at our nursery
- Vegetables, flowers, plants and trees
- Games and sports in our sports court in the farm
- BBQNights
- Surfing at Mawun Beach (3mins from the farm)

FAQs

Meals

We provide our guests 3 times meals. Our food is mostly local food cooked at Noni's Farm Cafe using mostly farm grown products.

Accommodation

We offer private wooden cabins in the farm and access to outdoor shower / toilet.

name: Chelsea - Vegetable, fruit farm and animal rescue ranch on 10 acres in FL
description:
Welcome to Marando Farms and Ranch 🌿
Our family farm sits on 10 acres of nature, surrounded by a lake that wraps around the property. We grow a variety of tropical fruits on part of the land (mangoes, avocados, bananas, papayas, guavas), and we also sell many kinds of plants in our nursery. A large part of the farm is dedicated to our organic and local farm market.
We share this space with our educational farm, where horses, chickens, and other rescued animals live. Families can visit the farm and feed the animals, which makes this place lively and welcoming.
The farm is open to the public Monday through Friday from 8 AM to 6 PM, and on weekends from 9 AM to 5 PM. We are closed on Wednesdays.
We also host events in our barn on some evenings or weekends (weddings, birthdays...). Every other Sunday, we offer a free yoga session in a peaceful natural setting. We enjoy creating a family-style atmosphere where everyone takes part in farm life with a spirit of teamwork and sharing.
On site, you will also find an organic juice and smoothie bar open to the public six days a week (closed on Wednesdays).
We are ideally located about 20 minutes from Fort Lauderdale Beach and about 20 minutes from the beautiful Florida Everglades. Public transportation is available, with a bus stop located right in front of the farm.
🌱 Life on the Farm
We are looking for motivated volunteers to take part in the daily life of the farm: seeding, planting, harvesting, some weeding, building and improving soil, as well as various small maintenance and repair projects.
Animals are also an important part of the farm: daily feeding, care, collecting eggs, cleaning stalls, and maintaining fences.
Depending on the needs of the moment, help in the kitchen with food preparation is also greatly appreciated.
We only use organic and natural methods: cow, horse, and chicken manure; compost made from fruit and vegetable scraps; Integrated Pest Management (IPM); vermiculture; diatomaceous earth; fish emulsion;

name: Charlie (Charles) - Organic 20 acre homestead located in the Jocko River Valley on the Flathead Indian Reservation, MT
description:
We are currently looking for WWOOFers for the growing season from May through September, short stays are welcome.We are an organic 20 acre homestead located in the Jocko River Valley on the Flathead Indian Reservation about 25 miles north of Missoula, Montana and 10 minutes from the small town of Arlee. Our property is a mix of agricultural field, forested hillsides, and meadows with the Jocko River running along the edge. We have a small organic orchard of 14 trees, a large raised bed garden, and the beginnings of a vineyard consisting of 124 vines. We are not a commercial operation but would like to sell grapes in the future.

For 20 hours of work a week we will provide camping in a small camper, a sun shower, composting outhouse, outdoor kitchen with table & chairs, propane cook stove, cooler and ice, bear-proof food box and a fire pit with wood. We'll provide breakfast food and food for meals that you prepare yourself. We provide family sit-down dinners 2x/week (vegetarian options available).

We provide transportation to and from the airport if necessary. 

Work may include weeding, mowing, garden/soil prep, mulching, pruning, harvesting, irrigation, fence construction, trellising and training of vines, freezing and drying of fruits and veggies, canning, wine making, and misc. tasks. We are looking for hard working, respectful, happy :) individuals that love camping, enjoy the outdoors and diverse weather conditions. We can accommodate 2 people, no pets or children please, and ask that you give dates of arrival with an estimated length of stay. We are offering a 1-2 week stay and if the ""fit"" is good, we will work with you to negotiate  longer stays. We reserve the right to ask you to leave at anytime. A telephone interview is required.

The homestead resides in a beautiful small valley in Montana, surrounded by many hiking and mountain biking trails. Kayaking on pristine lakes and rivers is just a drive away, as well as some of the best fishing in the west.

name: Morgan - Remote Alaskan horse ranch & veg farm surrounded by wilderness
description:
Hi Woofers, 
Now Interviewing Woofers for 2025.  Especially looking for two woofers who can arrive on May 20,  but still have space throughout the summer and Fall.

Very Remote, fly-in only, Alaskan wilderness homestead seeking adventurous, responsible, and safety-minded WWOOFer(s). Homestead includes market garden, kitchen garden, high tunnel, Icelandic horses, chickens, dogs, and wildlife surrounded by boundless and breathtaking true Alaskan wilderness.  Emphasized learning opportunities include: planting, harvesting, making hay, making silage, horsey stuff, animal husbandry, outdoor skills, bush construction, wild blueberry picking and wild medicinal plant harvesting.  

Very remote setting perfect for those with a love of solitude, basic knowledge of living outdoors, and a positive attitude--  hiking, backpacking,  abundant wildlife (including bugs), and bouldering all right outside your tent flap.  No stores, no nightlife besides a campfire, or cell service.   Internet access is available.  Ample opportunities to pursue your interests-- art, bushcraft, writing, bouldering, music, exploring. There is an extra guitar and mandolin.   Woofer lodgings are large expedition tents with cots and pads/mattresses in the forest.  No running water— water is hand pumped from a well or from a creek, outdoor shower in the high tunnel or get in the creek, and use an outhouse.  Meals are predominantly shared, though breakfast and lunch may be prepared separately depending on the day.  

IMPORTANT NOTES:

Wwoofers are only needed during the brief but beautiful  growing season-- May-Late Sept, though that season can be stretched into winter a bit for folks with the right skills. 

*WWOOF's algorithm puts the farm in the village of McGrath.  It is in fact very remote, McGrath is the closest village by air and is 70 miles away* 

DO YOU HAVE HORSE TRAINING EXPERIENCE? Especially interested in any wwoofers who have significant experience training  horses.  Please read the rest of the

name: Alissa - We are a regenerative family-run farm eco farm in Sinai
description:
We’re Ahmed and Alissa, former city kids working to build a regenerative farm in the Sinai desert. Ahmed (36) is from Cairo, and Alissa (39), with Ukrainian roots, grew up in Berlin. We have two little daughters, ages 3 and 6. Our farm is home to over 300 animals such as ducks and chickens, goats, sheep, dogs, cats, and turtles. We live close to nature, growing food sustainably and raising animals ethically, creating a space for hands-on learning, eco-living, and community connection.

Our daily farm life includes gardening, eco-projects, and maintaining the farm. Right now, we’re building an eco-friendly bathroom and kitchen for guests. Volunteers can help with the family household, gardening, artistic projects, farm maintenance, and sustainable construction.

Volunteers stay in a wooden hut if one is available. If not, we provide a tent or a bedroom in the farmhouse. All accommodations are simple and close to nature, with access to a shared bathroom and a communal kitchen.  

We provide ingredients for volunteers to prepare their own breakfast and offer one cooked meal per day which we hopefully will eat together most of the time. Volunteers are also welcome to use the kitchen to prepare additional meals if needed.  

Since we have small children and a shared living space, we appreciate help with keeping common areas tidy.

Ahmed is passionate about philosophical conversations, while Alissa loves music and cooking. We both enjoy spending time in nature, and since we have small children, most of our daily life revolves around them.  

Our farm is surrounded by stunning mountains, perfect for hiking and exploring. A 15-minute walk takes you to beautiful beaches with breathtaking coral reefs, great for swimming and snorkeling. In the evenings, we often gather around the fire, share meals, and enjoy good conversations under the stars.

name: Ronald - We are an Ecosystem Organic & Agroforest Farm and animal husbandry  in Zambia .
description:
Hello my name  is Ronald Solami aged 42 ,Iam a Nurse holding a bachelor of Science in Nursing from  Zambia and im passionate about the environmental and its sorroundings in line with its  nature ,ecology and its ecosystem.
Iam a change agent of the environment in respect with Organic farming .
Our project is located in the Western part of Zambia of Central Africa.We are an organic ecosystem in livestock ,Homested,Agritourism and Agriforest farming organisation.
We keep  Pigs, Rabbits,Guinea pigs,guinea fouls and chickens  .We enhance organic gardening and orchading through the collection of manure from our piggery.
We plant plants for fruits and vegetables  to enhance  food security and to fight against the effects of climate change in our space through the planting of trees and vegetables.
We support the use of organic farming and educate our communities about the environmental education for the benefits of organic farming and health  living.
We promote Agriforest by planting many trees in our farm and  other villages.
Our objective is to promote organic farming for health food security in our areas and combat the effects of climate change in Zambia through an integration of Agriforest  and Livestock farming.
At our Organic  farm we have  Dogs ,Cats,Pigs and Rabbits
We have our community local volunteers ,our two managers who stay at the farm.
We produce organic manure from  our pigs and  Rabbits later make organic compost manure depositions for our garden and orchard.
We are practicing AgriForest using ecosystem approach.Our animals and birds produce  organic manure which is intergrated into ecosystem of organic farming thereby making our plants  and trees benefit from organic manure.The trees produce fruits like mangoes,oranges,lemons,guava,cashews nuts and other wild fruits in which some fruits  are given to our pigs and Rabbits.
We would love to teach WWOOFers  about Agroforest ,Ecosystem  and Organic farming  and how  we integrate ,Livestock,crop and  Agrofore

name: Tobias - Agroecology, Intensive Horticulture, Ecotourism Family Farm in the cloud forests of El Salvador
description: We have a small, diversified, agroecological family farm where we also run a small ecotourism project.  We raise diverse vegetables, beans, fruit and nut trees, and raise sheep and chickens on our farm. We also have several natural buildings/cabins where we run an ecotourism business. Always looking for people interested in natural farming, agroecology, indigenous agricultural systems, etc. to come and learn with us.

name: Floriane - Ferme écologiques d'arbres fruitiers et légumes
description:
Hello, dear Woofers!
My name is Floriane, I just bought this wonderful finca and dream of turning it into a little ecological paradise and establishing a lifestyle closer to self-sufficiency. But it's not quite organized yet, and not done. That’s why I need help setting it up and organizing it all step by step. The fruit trees are already large (banana, papaya, avocado, and others), and the vegetables are already planted and already need some care. I live here alone for now, but I really want to share this place with Woofers and others. I have six chickens and three cats who have lived here longer than I have! I have a professional project to sell locally processed products made from the farm's fruits and vegetables, but it will take a few months before it can be done. The farm is located in the north of Tenerife, a few kilometers from Bajamar, and right next to the Anaga Nature Reserve! Hiking trails are accessible directly from the finca, as are seawater pools; it's a paradise for nature lovers! And yet, it's not isolated: Bajamar and Punta del Hidalgo are popular seaside resorts, and the town of La Laguna is a half-hour drive away. There's also a bus stop a 10-minute walk from the finca!

There's no real "typical" day because this is all so new, but generally speaking, taking care of the trees and vegetables, protecting them from insects, trimming branches, removing and cutting back unwanted plants, cutting up bananas and then banana trees, helping me with carrying and tinkering to improve the organization of the space and the reception of current and future woofers.

The kitchen, toilets and bathroom, as well as the living room are shared. The woofers will have a private tent covered by a roof. Currently, there's a large standing tent (for one person or a couple) and a small, low-slung two-person tent. I plan to buy a second, tall one. As for meals, I personally don't eat breakfast but can provide the woofers with what they need (within reason). I like to cook a

name: Sylvain - Ferme biologique en Ardèche - Cochons, brebis, châtaignes, ...
description:
Bonjour,

Nous sommes une famille de 5, Noémie, Sylvain et nos 3 enfants âgés de 2 à 13 ans. Nous avons toujours des projets plein la tête et nous aimons bricoler, jardiner, cuisiner de bons produits de notre ferme.
Nous aimons notre métier et tout ce qui en résulte. 

Nous sommes installés dans un petit village non loin d'Aubenas, en Ardèche, à 550m d'altitude et nous élevons des cochons en plein air, des brebis / agneaux et nous récoltons des châtaignes.

Il y a beaucoup de choses à faire et donc beaucoup de possibilités comme par exemple :
Donner à manger aux animaux, s'occuper des brebis, faire des clôtures, débroussailler, faire petit potager, ramasser les châtaignes, faire des produits à base de cochons,...

L'emploi du temps est relativement libre en fonction des WWOOFeurs et des travaux du moment. Nous vous proposons de faire partager notre quotidien dans une ambiance décontractée et conviviale. 

Un petit logement indépendant est mis à la disposition des WWOOFeurs pour le couchage et le repas du soir.

Notre village se situe dans le Parc Naturel Régional des Monts d’Ardèche. Idéal pour les sports de plein air et découvrir de magnifiques paysages.

Sylvain, Noémie et leurs trois enfants.

name: Gerry - Smallholding, Family farm
description:
We are a family living on a twenty hectare Organic Farm.
Our aim is to be as self-sufficient as possible and farm in a morally responsible manner, placing value on our environment above profit. Our kids were home-birthed, home-schooled, and home-fed in our home-built house.
We have no aspirations to compete with commercial agriculture, but would rather sit back and enjoy some delicious homegrown and homemade food cooked with fuel from our farm.
Although we do eat meat produced on our farm, we support vegetarians and can easily cater for them.
We have a small herd of cattle that helps feed us and brings in some income.
Our herd of goats is for land-clearing and milk and cheese, while their manure feeds the bio-digester for gas in the kitchen.
Our horse, mules and donkey are for grazing rotation and parasite control while the Great Danes, besides being pets, are for breeding.
We also have a few hives of bees for honey and a generally overgrown, out of control vegetable garden that always needs weeding. Our garden is managed using our adaption of permaculture methods which we have adapted over the last 25 years.
We are lucky enough to live in an area with abundant fruit, so a big job in summer is to process and preserve fruit for the rest of the year.
Having experimented with self-sufficiency on the farm since 1995, we have managed to grow and produce many of life's essentials (and luxuries), but still battle to produce all of them all the time.
Our eco project is in response to the very visible increase in air-pollution in our once pristine valley, and the shocking evidence of climate change. We have started our own atmospheric carbon harvesting program in the form of a sustainable reforestation project. We have planted the first 300 or so indigenous trees and hope to establish a new mistbelt forest.
We greatly appreciate the way that volunteers help us to finish our projects and give us inspiration to start new ones as well as refreshing our enthusiasm for our goals.

name: Fiete - Eco Village / Artist in Residence / Free spaces
description:
Eco Village / Residences / Free Spaces
Welcome to the “Agriao Forest” project — a becoming eco-village and artist-in-residence space on the Azores - Sao Miguel (main island). From the beginning we understand this place as a consciously political project: clearly left, anti-racist, feminist and queer-friendly, committed to climate justice and collective liberation. We (mostly white, cis and living with a lot of privilege) are not “experts”, but in an ongoing process of learning and unlearning. We invite people who come here to share this curiosity and to be willing to reflect, listen, make mistakes, give and receive feedback, and practice non-violent and accountable ways of living together.

My name is Fite, I am an architect based in Berlin. After a long time of searching, a large piece of forest next to the ocean has now been saved from commerce and exploitation. What you hear and see are the trees in the wind, the waves, the birds, the bees — not much more. It is a truly peaceful spot that offers a high level of privacy, nature and distance from noisy roads, barking dogs and neighbours. The view over the ocean from many spots of this land is simply breathtaking ✨.

This is pioneer work — and we mean that literally.
The project started in April this year. We are building something from scratch, in a real forest, with our hands. So far we have built a communal open-air kitchen a big eating spot, a dry toilet and shower with hot water. We sleep mainly in caravans that are very comfi but also professional bell-tents are available and a new container with an indoors kitchen for rainy days:)
There is no blueprint, no finished product — just land, ideas, and people willing to show up and figure it out together.
If you like getting your hands dirty, working with wood, soil and living things, spending long days outdoors and going to bed genuinely tired — this might be exactly your kind of place.

What there is to do:
There is real, physical, meaningful work here for people

name: Murat - Organik fındık üretimi / Organic hazelnut orchard
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Çiftçi, bölgesinde kurulmuş olan Organik Üreticiler Birliği yönetimindedir ve temel olarak fındık yetiştiriciliği yapmaktadır. Bunun yanı sıra aileye yetecek kadar sebze ve meyve yetiştirmektedir. 35 dönüm üzerinde kurulu fındık bahçesi ve 1,5 dönüm üzerine kurulu sebze bahçesi vardır. Çiftçi, 2006'dan beri sertifikalı organik tarım yapmaktadır. Eşiyle, ikiz çocuklarıyla ve yaz aylarında dönemsel olarak gelen akrabalarıyla birlikte sertifikalı olarak yetiştirdikleri fındıkların hasadını yapmaktadırlar. Asıl mesleği inşaat olan ev sahibi Temmuz ve Ağustos aylarında gönüllü kabul etmektedir. Her mevsim yeşil kalan ormanlarla çevrili bu coğrafyada birçok tecrübe yaşamak mümkündür. Yaban hayatı gözlemi, doğa yürüyüşleri ve şenlik havasında geçen fındık hasadı çiftliğin sunduğu olanaklar arasındadır.

Temmuz: Budama
Ağustos: Fındık hasadı

---

The farmer is in the management board of the organic producers association in his region and is mainly engaged in hazelnut production. Fruits and vegetables are also produced for the family’s own consumption. There is a hazelnut garden of 35 decares and a vegetable garden of 1.5 decares. The farmer has been engaged in certified organic farming since 2006. He, with his wife and twins, and with his relatives coming in the summer season, is harvesting the certified hazelnuts produced. The actual profession of the host is construction and he is accepting volunteers in July and August. It is possible to have different experiences in this area covered with evergreen forests. Wildlife observation, trekking and hazelnut harvest with its festival spirit are some of the opportunities the farm offers.

July: Hazelnut pruning
August: Hazelnut harvesting

name: JULIUS KAGWA - "Organic Farming" & other farming skills
description:
Lusaka is a fairly modern city with most of the creature comforts that are available in most modern cities of the world. There are shopping centers, supermarkets, museums, restaurants and a variety of attractions for our free time exploration. 
Our farm is not automated and as a result requires a lot of community labor from the nearby villages.
We also care for and teach children,youth and widows all the work and planting that we perform as we learn and grow. The community that we are teaching will one day engage in various planting schemes on their own farms.

We are developing a community of exporters who will be able to export the produce outside Zambia in a few years.
Work involved includes composting, land restoration, planting, weeding, seed collecting, grafting, and other planting and harvesting functions. WWOOFers can choose a daily four (4) hour work schedule between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 14:00 p.m. Monday thru Friday.

WWOOFers will be accommodated in a roomed house on the farm, vegetables and chickens
grown on the farm will be available to feed on.

I love Music, Cooking, adventure, swimming (though am still learning (:,
Movies, and Learning new cultures

name: Shams - Farm
description:
About Om Sleiman farm

We at Om Sleiman Farm have been running a CSA farm in Bil’in, Palestine since 2016. Our work has been an important connection that ties us to our land with an emphasis on collectively  (re)creating our means of resistance. Reclaiming our space(s) of imagination aspiring to showcase a vision of a regenerative, just and independent future in Palestine. 

How the story began
The idea of establishing Om Sleiman Farm began when the co-founders one from Jerusalem and the other one from Gaza met by coincidence in Ramallah. Through in-depth discussions on Palestine’s agricultural and economic conditions, they envisioned a farming model that would reconnect people with the land—emphasizing chemical-free cultivation, preservation of local seeds, community involvement, and food self-sufficiency.
Their search for suitable land led them to Abu Alaa Mansour the landlord from the village of Bil’in, who generously offered them use of his land free of charge for five years, then extended it to as much as needed. 

The current team now:

Yara from Jerusalem, joined in 2018  and Raghad from Tulkarem in 2022. They are both managing the farm at the moment. Yara takes care of projects, popular education programs, research and development while Raghad handles all the day to day details of the field management and CSA production. The third member of the team is Majdi who does administrative work and helps the team connect the dots with office work, coordination and communication tasks. Along the core team we have members who work on a seasonal basis helping with the harvest, workshops cooking, and infrastructure mainly from the village of Bil’in.
 
Our community consists of our current team – the hands cultivating the land, previous team members  who worked at the farm and maintain connection to it, as well as our members who receive weekly baskets from us and share the risks with us. Our community at large is the welcoming and kind people of Bilin, other farmers in

name: Robin - A natural sanctuary creating a world that works for Earth and Her beings
description:
We are a non-profit farm on 95 wooded acres in rural Kansas dedicated to providing our community with a sanctuary for personal and spiritual development.  We offer retreats, events and workshops on many weekends with a focus on alternative healing support and spiritual development. The farm itself is in a constant state of expansion and we are always seeking more sustainable ways to support this growth. We are devoted to learning, living, and growing in partnership with Nature.   

Most of the buildings are over 125 years old and have been repaired or rebuilt from scratch. We have an earthbag building, a small strawbale building, and an Aircrete Dome and we are getting ready to begin working on a string of cabins for our retreat guests.  We currently trying to finish up two of these projects.:  a small strawbale building that still needs a floor and electricity and an Aircrete dome that we are completing earthen plastering of the interior and sealing and painting the exterior.  There is also a trailer that is getting an upgrade as well as some minor repairs from old water damage and multiple painting projects, that could use a hand or two. On most days there is some kind of gardening project that will need attention.  There is no shortage of things to do that's for sure!      

We have hosted woofers for a long time.  It would be nice to have some folks that can help assist us in this time of expansion as we just entered our 30th year as an all volunteer non profit.  Basic gardening knowledge, physical strength, and an interest in learning a more sustainable life-style is a must.  You must be able to work independently on most days, however, there will be plenty of opportunity for group projects as well.  

We are in need of people who have proficient garden knowledge and can work independently transplanting, preparing beds, and taking care of watering and composting.   We would love to have produce to attend a weekly market this year, but need help to make this hap
_wwoof_travel: Kansas City airport (MCI)  Greyhound in Lawrence, KS

name: Lokesh - Parbat district (≤60 km from Pokhara)
description:
In the scenic district of Parbat, host Lokesh Subedi offers volunteers a unique opportunity to experience the harmony of modern agriculture and traditional Nepali lifestyle. His farm is home to a thriving bee enterprise with more than 30 hives, alongside diverse fruit orchards and agricultural fields that sustain the community.
Volunteers are welcomed into a classical Nepali house, where daily life unfolds in an ethnic village setting rich with cultural diversity. Here, the rhythms of farming blend seamlessly with traditions, creating a living fusion of heritage and innovation. The location lies less than 60 kilometers from the Pokhara Valley, making it accessible yet deeply rooted in rural authenticity.
This placement is ideal for those who wish to:
• 	Learn practical skills in beekeeping and orchard management.
• 	Experience a homestay that connects agriculture with cultural immersion.
• 	Discover the balance of modern farming practices within a traditional village lifestyle.
Volunteer Capacity
👥 2–3 volunteers at a time

name: hogie - Farmin' in Cajun country
description:
i am a renaissance woman who grows much of my food on a small lot in a small town in south Louisiana Acadiana area known for Cajun and Creole music and culture and i play zydeco, r&b, and Cajun music with local bands. dancing is one of my passions too and i can teach it. i think outside the box and like to deal with other creative free conscious people.

 i do most of my maintenance on my old cypress house property is always a work in progress. i am into sustainable living and consciousness work, art, music, foraging, kayaking, swimming, and fishing.

 Your work could involve anything from gardening to maintenance chores, and anything that we want to create or that you are good at and want to contribute.i am an idea person and tend to generate projects and welcome assistance.

 i prefer to work with someone who is an independent worker and knows how to use and maintain tools. i need people who have   gardening skills, building maintenance, and a cleaner/organizer person.

 Over the past few years, the universe has blessed me with a ""family of choice"" many of whom i have met by being a host.

Some of my dear friends have returned multiple times even bringing friends and i am truly grateful for the help and even more for the connections made with people who all seem to be related in some way:)

 If we choose, your stay may be extended for any length of time. People have stayed for a year by mutual agreement.

 i eat healthy, mostly organic whole foods and i'm a pretty good cook .i buy you whatever groceries you like to eat. i know about cultural events involving music and can show you the inside of the local cajun and zydeco scene. there are kayaks and bikes here and the lake is close.

 currently looking for permaculture gardeners and or handy builder types.  Rainwater to irrigation system is needed.

 

Organic methods: permaculture, organic , wild foraging. etc.

Certifications: dont want to pay for certification and the mosquito truck sprays on my street althoug

name: Michael - Charitable community based organization focus on economic and environmental sustainability
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
Umoja Foundation Group is a grassroot transparent registered charitable community based and lead volunteer placement organization.Located in Kakamega County,Likuyani sub county,Western region in the Republic of Kenya.The organization is inhibited by mixed tribes of the local community who are small scale mixed farmers.The farmers practice dairy farming but not successful because their livestock breeds are not the artificial insemination breeds.The crop planting farmers practice mixed crops where they use bulls in ploughing their farm and don't plant the certified seeds thus poor farm produce. There's need to introduce local farmers to techniques that prevent soil erosion, introduce new skills on organic farming to reduce use of chemical and provide, demonstrate best artificial insemination breeds to local livestock farmers.

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
The organization  has many activities that are of community need and WWOOFers may be interested to participate: support vulnerable children and families, environment related issues, education, water and sanitation, agriculture, sports,HIV/AIDS, support different abled persons youth and women empowerment, health,the aged and alleviate poverty.We strive to promote socio-economic activities for food production and income generating activity for sustainable life 

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals:
Umoja Foundation Group has a permanent house with a restroom equipped with a flat LED TV, laptop, internet WIFI, toilet, bathroom,water, outside latrine,24hrs electric supply powered with a generator for backup.WWOOFers will be provided with a spotlight in case of blackout.we have well fenced compound with security Umoja Foundation Group will provide the WWOOFers with nice bedrooms.
Three meals daily that's breakfast, lunch and
_wwoof_travel: Jomo Kenyatta International Airport Nairobi -Eldoret Airport Eldoret town - Matunda

name: Roshan - Agriculturist with Farm house
description: An Agriculture Officer, manages a grape vineyard , seasonal vegetable farming and some of the flowers. His work also supports ethnic groups in the village at a governmental level, making his host profile both agricultural and socially impactful. Volunteers can expect lodging facilities, nearby hiking trails, and homestay opportunities. The farm provides a balance of agricultural learning and cultural immersion, with a focus on community support and sustainable farming practices.

name: Arturo - Organic essential oils destillery and farming
description:
"La Colo " are Cecilia and I, with our daughter Luisa (4)and my children Inti (15)and Violeta(17), We live on a 4-hectare farm with 2 other families, 30 minutes from the center of Montevideo, near a fishing village call "La Colorada" located within the protected area" humedales del Santa Lucia" and in front of the "Rio de la Plata" .
We have a small family destilerie of organic essential oils,  cultivation and production of organic food as well. 
Actually our project aims mainly to improve our family buisnes of essential oils.

We can offer accommodation (see photos): It consists of a rustic house with a large living room and integrated bedroom, bathroom and kitchen, with electric light, refrigerator, stove and cold water, also tent. And the following meal plan: lunch, dinner and breakfast Normally the guests make their own meals, they have everything they need to cook, we can also share some of the meals. What we ask in return is commitment to the planned work activities, which are normally 5 hours a day, although it can vary depending on the activities.

We are aligned with the spirit of woof: The experience of living on a farm is not a vacation, we are not interested in receiving people who only are looking for cheap accommodation while traveling.
We are looking for working, committed and proactive people with some experience in rural life and who want to continue learning about  agroecological practices in the production of food and essential oils, and also share your experience, knowlege and culture with us in order to  promote an educational exchange, and build a global community conscious of ecological farming practices.

Our intention is to live in contact with nature,  under the principles of permaculature: respect for the earth, for people, sharing what life offers us with respect, joy and fairness.

The main tasks carried out in our farm are: prune the aromatic plants that we use for the production of organic essential  oils, destilation, maintenance and

name: Volodymyr - Alternative way of living in the countryside
description:
Since 2013 I decided to live simple life in the village next to city.
My family consists of three people. I'm Volodymyr (47), my wife Zhanna (44), our daughter Tonya (8) they live currently in the city.
The idea of sustainable living is on topic. I use solar power for fridge, power tools, electric bicycle. As I'm an engineer I can teach you how it works.
I also practicing permaculture gardening, natural-building, traditional knowledge and technology.
The small organic garden 0.1 ha. Surroundings area is agriculture fields, two big estuaries (Khadjibeisky and Kuyalnitskiy).
Gardening tasks are dependent on season and weather. Spring, early summer main activities are around prepareing beds, soil, planting and watering. Soil requires mulching, different layers or composting materials.
Some watering especially in summer is always needed.

You'll know how ho to grow healthy soil and environment for plants. What is good to grow in this type of soil and weather conditions.
How to make jam without sugar.
Also you have possiblity to learn more in my mom's project in another region of Ukraine. Free transportation. There is a big livestock of goats. You'll learn how to make feta (homemade cheese), whole process, to grow plants.

Accommodation will be in the house (3 separate beds -zone in the studio -room with kitchen an bathroom.
Also additionally there is outside building with bed, and another mini - building for private living there.
Power consumption 220V from solar panels (basic), and grid from my neighbour to use, optical fast internet, WiFi.
There is always enough food and kitchen for cooking snacks, light meals anytime you need.
Property has outside compost toilet, 1 well, 2 tanks of water.

The place located far from frontline zone with russian army. But sometimes you'll hear sirens (drones attacks or rockets). Here is small concrete shelter in the ground with metal cap for this purpose. Hope this russian-ukrainian conflict will be melted soon

Use bicycle or public t

name: Francisco - Emprendimiento en la Quebrada de Humahuaca, con cultivo ancestrales y deshidrado solar
description:
Soy Francisco Milhas, dirijo un emprendimiento de triple impacto social, ambiental y económico. Iniciamos en 2017 en la Quebrada de Humahuaca, Jujuy, Argentina, y el proyecto se enfoca en ofrecer herramientas de bajo costo a pequeños productores de economías regionales.
Omaguaco brinda a los productores agrícolas las herramientas y el conocimiento para dar valor agregado a su cosecha mediante el uso de energía solar y el reciclaje, generando así un impacto positivo en las comunidades rurales del norte de Argentina.

Producir alimentos andinos deshidratados con energía solar,  autosustentabilidad y valor agregado a la producción agricola. 
Reciclar barriles metálicos descartados por la industria de alimentos, para transformarlos en deshidratadores y hornos solares.
Aprovechar los desperdicios agrícolas de las cosechas que de otra manera se perderían, transformándolos en productos como barras de cereal, galletitas y sopas.
Utilizar la energía solar para el proceso de deshidratación.

Cabaña ecologica tipo refugio de montaña con 2 habitaciones, capacidad para 6 camas tipo literas, un baño adentro junto a las habitaciones y otro afuera en el campo, con biodigestores y ducha electrica. Se ofrece desayuno, almuerzo y cena, consistentes en infusiones y bollos caseros, verduras y granos de estacion con carnes, pollo y otras proteinas.

Somos emprendedores, ecologistas, naturalistas, comunitarios, pero por sobre todo amamos ser anfitriones y buscamos ser sustentables. Vivir en la Quebrada de Humahuaca es una experiencia única, marcada por la profunda conexión con la tierra, las tradiciones ancestrales y la imponente naturaleza. La economía local se basa en la agricultura y el pastoreo de animales como cabras, ovejas y llamas. Los cultivos de maíces, papines andinos y quinua son pilares de su alimentación y subsistencia. Los principales pueblos de la Quebrada, como Purmamarca, Tilcara y Humahuaca, son atractivos en sí mismos. Cada uno ofrece plazas pintorescas, mercados de ar
_wwoof_travel: Aeropuerto de Jujuy - Gobernador Horacio Guzmán

name: Amy - Forestry and community gardening
description:
We are a retired couple living two miles from the west gate of Rocky Mountain National Park in Grand Lake, Colorado. Although we have never hosted a working volunteer, we have hosted professionals and students from other countries. We are hoping to build a chicken coop and get chickens this summer. Though we like dogs, we don't have any right now. Our project is a restoration and fire preparedness project with the hope of developing the space and resources for more gardening and chickens

Our area suffered through the East Troublesome Fire in 2020. It burned over 170,000 acres of forested land including 80 acres of our property. The mild winter suggests a significant threat for more fires in the fall of 2026. A WWOOFer could help us plant trees in the burned area and willows along the Colorado River that flows through the property. We are still working to build fence, plant trees and grasses, make slash piles to burn, etc. Our home sits on three acres that did not burn, but we are working to make this area more "fire wise". A WWOOFer could spend a few hours each day cutting out juniper bushes, downing old trees, cleaning debris, and planting native grasses, shrubs, and flowers. A WWOOFer would learn about erosion control, forest management, native plant restoration, etc.  We could also use help restoring an old spring for a future garden.

We have two extra bedrooms. Each has its own balcony and a shared Jack & Jill bathroom.  The kitchen is available for use, and we are happy to cook or share something that the WWOOFER would like to cook.  Our dining is casual, because we are usually busy during the day. We rarely eat breakfast or lunch together, but always share dinner. We will provide food for three meals and honor a WOOFER's food request as best we can. The WOOFER(s) can decide whether or not they want to join us or prepare their own meals.  This is a non-smoking house. In past years, the county has also included outdoor smoking as part of a fire ban. The nation

name: Berker - Educational Centre
description:
Ambar projesi, 2020 yılında, Türkiye'nin doğal mirasını koruma altına almak ve gelecek nesillere taşımak amacıyla, Bolu'nun verimli topraklarına sahip Mengen İlçesinde hayata geçirilmiştir. Bu öncü girişim, tamamen gönüllülük esası üzerine kurulmuş bir sosyal sorumluluk projesidir. Atalık tohumların dünyanın değişen iklim şartlarına karşı korunması, çoğaltılması ve sürdürülebilir bir şekilde gelecek nesillere aktarılması bu projenin temel amacını oluşturur. Bu sayede, yerel çeşitliliğin ve biyoçeşitliliğin korunması hedeflenmektedir.

Ambarın özelliği, tohumların uzun süreler boyunca canlılıklarını koruyabilmeleri için en ideal saklama koşullarının yaratıldığı bir yapıya sahip olmasıdır. Proje ekibi, tohumların gereksinim duyduğu doğal iklim koşullarını titizlikle inceleyerek, bu ihtiyaçları karşılayacak bir saklama alanı tasarlamıştır. Toprak altı mahzeni, doğal yalıtım özellikleri sayesinde, tohumların bekledikleri uygun sıcaklık ve nem dengesini yıl boyunca sürdürmelerine olanak tanır. Bunun yanı sıra, herhangi bir nem dengesizliğinin önüne geçmek amacıyla, tohumlar vakumlu paketler içerisinde saklanmaktadır. Bu sayede, dış etkenlerin olumsuz etkilerinden maksimum derecede korunmaktadırlar.

Ambar'ın bir nevi yaşayan kütüphane olma özelliği taşıdığı söylenebilir. Her bir tohum çeşidi, detaylı bir envanter çalışması ile kayıt altına alınmıştır. Bu kapsamlı kayıt sistemi sayesinde, tohumların hangi koşullarda en iyi şekilde çimlenip büyüyebileceği gibi değerli bilgiler de arşivlenmektedir. 10.05.2024 tarihi itibarı ile projenin envanterinde, 561 farklı tür tohum çeşidi bulunmaktadır. Bu, biyoçeşitliliğin muhafazası adına büyük bir başarı olarak kabul edilmekte ve proje, yerel topluluklar başta olmak üzere, ulusal ve uluslararası pek çok kurum ve örgütten takdir toplamaktadır.

name: Leisha - Family farm
description:
Hello and Bonjour! We are a small family Farm that grows vegetables, herbs, fruit and flowers - everything is herbicide and pesticide free. We operate a CSA with delivery twice per week, as well as sell produce to local restaurants and a weekly food hub.

We can take 1 single or a couple - will sleep in our child's bedroom with a double bed and nightstand.  We can provide food for supper, with WWOOFER preparing own breakfast and lunch. Job duties will vary throughout the season - some weeks may be super busy while other times will be less busy. 

Organic methods: We are herbicide/pesticide free, with some use of a mixture of chemical fertilizer and organic amendments to the soil.

Certifications: Herbicide and pesticide free, not certified organic

Current projects: helping gather and prepare twice weekly CSA, weeding, planting, berry-picking, making jams for market, gathering produce for weekly CSA boxes, more.

ideally we’d like a minimum of 1 week stay - we are a family with two small children (11 years and 6 years). Jeremy is the farmer and could use help throughout the farming season - depending on the time of year it may include weeding and planting (either with tractor or by hand), preparing weekly CSA boxes,  tending to tomatoes, preparing flower bouquets. 20 hours per week of work on average gives you lots of time to explore St. John’s. We live in a city parkland area with trails to the main part of the city. Pond on Farm for beautiful summer swimming. 4-5 hours of work per day on average, flexible, leaves lots of time for exploring our beautiful city. We live in a magical place!

Internet access available

There is no city bus access to our farm - however, we live in a greenbelt in the centre of the city of St. John's with access to park trails to take you into the city. We can likely also provide the occasional ride into town, and if the weather is good we often have folks enjoy the walk to town/or back to the farm (half hour-45 min through city park trai
_wwoof_travel: St. John's International Airport - airport code YYT

name: Julie - 8-acre organic farming compound with sled dogs in Alaska
description:
Francis and Julie run a Bed-and-Breakfast and sled dog kennel on the 8-acre small-scale organic farming compound. Owned and managed by Francis, an Alaskan resident of over 50 years, and Julie, a retired educator with 32 years of teaching experience. We are former USDA-NRCS EQIP participants and practice experimental and research-oriented cold-climate growing methods including composting, nutrient/pest management, and high tunnel food production. We also offer educational and consultation services to Yup'ik villages along the Kuskokwim River interested in upstart farming activities. 

Compound activities can include: seeding/transplanting garden and high tunnel, composting, salmon fishing, sled dog kennel maintenance, small construction projects, firewood preparation, meal preparation assistance--many places to fit in! You will have comfortable living accommodations and meals in exchange for four to five hours of work per day.  A minimum of 2 weeks is preferred. We speak English and eat real food, organic and locally acquired when possible. Because of the proximity to the dog kennel, pets are not allowed. 

Bethel has various seasonal opportunities for social, educational, and cultural experiences such as fishing and fish preservation, boating, canoeing, Saturday market, berry picking, bike riding, exploring the tundra, and community intramural sports events like softball, soccer, ultimate Frisbee, sand volleyball, and more. If you like meeting interesting people on the western edge of Alaska, join us! 

Things to know about Bethel and the compound: We work on the compound 7 days a week, however, WWOOFers get one day a week off. We meet in the morning over coffee or tea to prioritize activities and offer instruction. Sometimes you will be working independently on projects. Jobs require bending, stooping, lifting--and energy. Generally, breakfast and lunch are self-serve meals. Dinner is usually a group effort. Your cell phone may not work well in Bethel, however Wi-f

name: Simon - Homestead, Intentional community, Garden, Permaculture, Community, Vegan friendly
description:
The Garden Temple is now considering Wwoofers for winter season. Please contact Simon if interested in a very unique experience!
Our Garden is 2 acres near the active lava flow on the Big Island of Hawaii.  Located in the heart of Puna, close to the Kingdom of Hawaii in Kalapana and the nude beach at Kehena.  
Most of our gardening activities are weeding, bed preparation, creating a food/fruit forest, mulching, fertilizing, also some building and off grid, self sustained living construction. 
We are founded in BioDynamic and permaculture principals.
