We ask only 2 hours work in exchange for a tent space with vegetarian meals provided.  Accommodations are from tenting to possibly a cabin for hard workers with at least a three month commitment. 

The Big Island and Puna are vulnerable populations.  Our community is very social.  
Also, please in your message or visit request tell us why you want to work on our garden and live in our community, lower Puna, especially.  Messages with generic or short statements will most likely also be rejected.  
Mahalo for your kokua!
Research Puna, Kehena Beach, Kingdom of Hawai’i please! 
The Big Island is a very unique island. She is not for everyone! She can present challenges.  There are dangers in the beauty.  Expect a wild ride.  This is not the Hawaii you see on travel posters. We have black sand beaches, lava spurting out of the ground whenever, long periods of rain, sunshine, lots of dancing, nude beaches, pot smoking hippies, crazy people and one of the most friendly communities in the world!
 There are dangers and challenges here.  It rains a lot, like buckets.  Sometimes for months. Nice thing is you can go to the dry side of the island in 2 hours. 
The conditions are primitive at best.   Mostly tent in the jungle with a shared kitchen and shower.  
The ocean is also very dangerous especially in the winter when waves can and do kill swimmers.  
We are a community in recovery.  We suffered a recent lava flow that took man

name: Lan - Organic Farm and Education
description:
Hello Dear Wwoofers, 
My name is Nguyen Lan. I am the manager of an educational farm in Hanoi, Vietnam. My EduFarm is located on natural hills close to a natural forest. This makes the educational farm become a natural school for kids. Here, we have many fruit trees and vegetables which are naturally grown and we never apply any chemical fertilizers or pesticides. We just leave the trees and crops naturally grow and develop. We teach kids basic subjects and Emglish as well as natural farming skills in both Vietnamese and English. So, we'd like to invite you, Wwoofers, to visit my farm as volunteers. We offer you rooms to sleep with warm water for cold days and AC for hot days. We offer you all meals of the day with us. If you are a vegan or vegetarian, we have special food for you too. We aim to create an English learning environment to educate children by doing projects to practice agricultural sustainability, protect our environment, and maintain its integrity. 

Your typical day at our farmschool will look like this: you have breakfast together with our teachers and students at 7:30am. Then you can choose either to go to our farm on top of the hills to do the weeding and take care of vegetables and fruit trees or to go with our teachers to their classes to talk and share with our students about the organic farming and sustainable development in your countries or in tue places where you have lived and experienced. Morning break at 10am and usually all of us will have some fresh juice or fermented juice to drink together in the leaf house. We come back to work from 10:45 to 11:30am and have lunch together until 1pm. Afternoon work start at 1 to 2:30pm then break to 3pm. After 3pm there is no farming and teaching work but you are encouraged to help our staff to tidy up all materials and tools after a working day. From 4 to 6pm you are free and usually our volunteers walk in the village and go to local coffee shops to chat with each other and locals there. Dinner sta

name: Boon - Alternative co-operative community, Communal Living Group, Educational Centre
Kevin - Family farm
description:
Welcome to our Farm
As the seasons change so do the experiences and work on our mixed certified  organic farm. The farm is located in the southwest part of the Saskatchewan  prairie. The cultivated land grows:   cereal grains, pulses, oil seeds, and forage. In addition we raise certified organic beef.  
The native pasture is home to a variety of grasses ,forbs and sedges where during the growing season something new blooms weekly. An abundance of wild life roam the area (deer, coyote, badger, rabbits) . For the bird enthusiast  there are many species of bird including species at risk .
Spring tasks are diverse , in mid April the main focus is on the cattle as the new born calves are arriving.
Then on to preparation for seeding the crops and garden which involves readying equipment, seed planning and preparing the soil.
Summer is haying time and tending to the garden and a whole host of other jobs around the yard or farm( weeding , fencing....)

Fall brings harvest of crops and the garden and the opportunity to preserve the vegetables.
In the winter a fodder system is maintained that grows fodder for the cattle. Also, time is spent on   projects indoors.

The farm is a family operation, husband and wife and one daughter who helps out when she can during busy times ( seeding , haying, harvest , calving). the family members are active in organic organization  and groups. Often attending meetings, workshops and webinars.Always wanting to gleam and share knowledge with other organic folk.
Paperwork is integral part of organic farming , therefore, if that is something that a wwoofer would be interested in acquiring knowledge about that could be offered.

The local communities do offer some recreational activities :there is a museum and library within  ten minutes.There are two smaller cities within a hour and a larger city within two hours. The cities offer a variety of recreational activities including cultural, music, and sports.


Certifications: TransCanada Organic Ce

name: Justin - Alternative co-operative community, Communal Living Group, Family home and garden - rural
description: Owners:Justin has been actively learning and practicing permaculture since he founded a guerilla community garden in Vancouver BC between 2006 and 2010. He completed a PDC at OUR Ecovillage in 2008 and followed that up with teacher training courses at the Bullock Brothers’ Homestead and with Jude Hobbs. His interests include earthworks, ponds, food forests, integrating animals into the landscape, renewable energy, biochar, mushrooms, electric transportation, compost, black soldier flies, teaching, working with interns, soil fertility, large-scale earth restoration, all-season greenhouses, carbon sequestration, regenerative economics and just about everything permaculture. Justin has an MBA in Sustainable Development from Pinchot University.History:We are in our 8th year of opperation. The soil of our land was very intertile in the beginning, consisting of mainly sand. We've used permaculture principles and practices to build the fertility of our land of these last six years and now we are able to grow our own produce for private consumption and occasionally offer surplus at local farmer's markets. We are aiming to grow into a full fledged market garden as the fertility of our soil increases.We raise pastured chicken and eggs for our own consumption always keeping the comfort and wellbeing of our animals. We collect food waste from the local municipality and supplement this as feed for our chickens, closing up energy loops and reducing waste in the process.We have invested heavily in beautiful sustainable infrastructure for our homestead. We have a solar heated outdoor shower and laundry for wwoofers and interns. This water is sourced from Lake Huron and a grey water system is in place to return moisture to the soil while filtering the water. We've also constructed a timberframe compost toilet building. We use humanure composted over multiple years to fertilize trees and perrenial gardens. (Don't worry, not the annual vegetable gardens!)We are an ideal place for self
_wwoof_travel: 12 km west of Little Current on Hwy 540

name: Ulrim - <JN137>쉼과 문화가 있는 녹차정원/A Green Tea Garden of Rest and Culture
description:
✅ 호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :

안녕하세요, 제 이름은 오울림입니다. 저는 30대 여성이고, 한국 전라남도 해남에서 부모님과 함께 유기농 차밭을 운영하고 있습니다. 저희는 1997년부터 농약과 화학비료를 전혀 쓰지 않는 자연농법을 지켜오며, 자연과 가까운 삶을 실천해 왔습니다.

프로필 사진은 저희 부모님이며, 연락과 소통은 딸인 저 오울림이 담당할 예정입니다.

저희 다원은 단순한 차밭이 아니라, 씨앗부터 차나무를 키워 녹차, 발효차, 쑥차, 목련꽃차, 떡차 등 다양한 차를 만들고, 한옥스테이, 찻집, 전통문화 체험까지 운영하는 공간입니다. 어머니는 판소리, 풍물, 사물놀이 같은 한국 전통 음악 체험도 진행하여, 방문하시는 분들이 자연과 전통문화를 함께 경험할 수 있도록 돕고 계십니다. 가족은 저와 부모님, 그리고 순하고 사람을 잘 따르는 강아지 두 마리 영심이(10살)와 아롱이(7살)(모녀 강아지)와 함께 살고 있습니다. ★★2026년에는 어머니가 안식년으로 자리를 비우셔서, 아버지와 제가 함께 모든 일을 운영할 예정입니다.★★

저희는 농사만 하는 곳이 아니라, 삶 전체를 자연과 함께하는 방식으로 운영합니다. 농약·비료를 쓰지 않고, 제철 식재료 위주의 식사를 하며, 기계 사용을 최소화하고, 손으로 하는 작업을 많이 합니다. 이곳에서의 시간이 자연에 기대어 사는 삶을 느끼고, 경험하고, 돌아보는 시간이 되기를 바랍니다.

저희 차나무는 모두 씨앗부터 키운 나무이며, 생태적 공존을 위해 여러 나무와 풀, 꽃들과 함께 자랍니다. 우리는 토양의 생명력과 생태계의 균형을 가장 중요하게 생각하며, 자연이 회복하는 속도에 맞춰 농사짓는 철학을 가지고 있습니다. 이런 방식은 시간과 정성이 많이 들지만, 그만큼 깊고 순한 맛의 차와 느린 시간을 함께 나눌 수 있게 해줍니다.

우퍼 숙소는 차밭에서 도보 2분 거리에 있습니다. 상황에 따라 방이 바뀔 수 있으며, 보통은 우퍼 전용 독립 공간이지만 가끔은 다른 사람과 쉐어할 수도 있습니다. 전통적인 분위기 속에서 자연을 느끼며 지낼 수 있는, 조용하고 편안한 숙소입니다.

★★우리는 최소 2주 이상 머물 수 있는 우퍼와 함께하며, 장기간 머무는 우퍼를 더 선호합니다.★★

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 1991년
✅ 농장 형태 : 가족농이면서 체험교육농장
✅ 농장 주작물 : 녹차와 쑥, 목련 등
✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 2002년 부터 유기재배 인증 2005년 부터 유기 가공 인증을 받았고 지속적으로 실천하고 있어요.
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 : 적게 먹고 적게 쓰고 바르게 사용.
✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 가끔 둘째딸
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려 동물 : 강아지 2마리
✅ 우퍼가 하는일 :
이곳에서의 생활은 자연의 리듬에 맞춰 조용히 흘러갑니다.
우퍼가 참여하게 되는 활동은 계절에 따라 조금씩 다르지만, 주로 다음과 같습니다:

차밭 관리 (제초, 정리, 간단한 돌봄 등)
차 생산 과정 보조 (수확, 선별, 포장 등)
체험 프로그램 및 찻집 운영 보조
숙소 및 공용 공간 청소
공용 수건, 이불 커버 빨래
체험 보조 (다도 체험, 숲 체험 등)
함께 일하며 교류하는 시간이 이곳에서는 가장 소중한 배움의 순간이 되곤 합니다.

✅ 선호하는 우퍼상 : 장기간 우핑이 가능한 우퍼, 차분하고 맡은 일을 잘하는 우퍼 
✅ 우퍼에게 당부 메세지 : 일은 해치우는 것이 아니라 정성을 들이는 것이다. 특별한 기술이 필요하지는 않으며, 자연과 함께하는 삶을 배우고 싶은 마음이 가장 중요합니다. 
✅ 숙소에서 와이파이 가능여부 : 가능
✅ 술, 담배 가능여부 : 간헐적 외부 흡연, 및 음주 가능
✅ 호스트와 식사여부 : 하루 세끼를 함께 먹습니다. 
아침: 간단한 샐러드나 요거트, 과일 등 가벼운 식사
점심·저녁: 함께 만들어가는 식탁으로 모두가 요리를 하고 나눕니다. 
보통은 지역에서 난 좋은 농산물과 제철 재료로 만든 집밥 스타일의 한식 반찬과 밥
(채식이나 알레르기가 있는 경우 미리 알려주시면 조정 가능합니다.)

✅ 영어소통 : 기본적인 소통 가능
✅ 일주일

name: Dee - A Calladium and Herbs farm on an acre of land, by a hillside overlooking rice field. Huge gardens
description:
Hello there, my name is Dee and we run a Calladium and Herbs farm on an acre of land filled with tropical plants and trees on Langkawi Island, Malaysia. I live with my partner Marc and 3 cats namely Hugo, Poppy and Henry. Our 1 acre property is set in a cul-de-sac, in a village, 15 minutes walk to the nearest beach. Very private.

I'm an avid gardener. We have a collection of more than 300 Caladium plants plus other beautiful tropical plants and trees.  Wwoofers are welcome to learn and propagating these plants apart from helping us cleaning the gardens etc.

We're also propagating and planting Basil, Sage and other local herbs too. Wwoofers are expected to assist us in  this process too. 

A private fan only  room  measuring 15ft by 15ft with a double bed, sofa, writing desk, drawers with ensuite bathroom. Basic food like pasta, rice, bread, margarine, eggs, jam, noodles and local vegs are provided and Wwoofers prepare their own meals. Scooter is also available to use anytime  (with valid driving licence only). Work 4 hours a day with one day off in a week.

I like reading, socialising, morning walks and doing charity work especially fund raising events. Swimming and sitting on the beach are part of my weekly activities too.

name: Izabela - Private house located in a small village, surrounded with wildlife forrests and meadows.
description:
Hi, we are 2+2 familly= Izabela, Michał,  Stefcia, Florcia. 

We are one of those, who left the city to settle in a countryside, and we appreciate this decision every day.
Right now we are foccusing on our daughters (5yo and2yo) and this takes almost all our time :D
Minutes that are childrenfree we spend in the garden, improving our land. 
We've already build a prototype of external kitchen (far from beauty, but fully  working), external bathroom with grey water filter system, we have vegetable garden, orchard,  our own forrest and meadow. We keep chickens and one furry cat. 

We do our best to use the knowledge that we gained on Permaculture Design  Course with Peter Cow and Bram von Overbecke, 2022 in Tinos Ecolodge.

We invite you to stay with us. We offer a tipi type tent 20m2, furnished with beds and cupboards, fully equiped outdoor kitchen, outdoor composting toilet and bathroom, Also we have a playground  for kids, hammocks and big porch, to chill. During colder months sep-apr we can offer a parking for your camper.

Duties for our guests: The most important thing for us now is keeping us and our children company. I do my best to teach girls english, but its nothing comparing to speaking with natives, or people, who don't know polish. But don't worry, we are not looking for a nanny! It would be nice, if you like children and can play with them for a while, or even better bring your own kids to play with S&F
There is always a lot to do on our small farm: weeding, mulching, mowing, cooking, cleaning chickens coop etc., nothing, that recquires superpowers. 
During summer we are busy on making preservings for winter, our kitchen becomes a lab. 
Sometimes we make special projects, like building new greenhouse, preparing new raisedbeds, building toys for playground. The list of projects to do is endless, just let us know when and for how long are you coming and we will choose something interesting.

Out of farming we work as a dentist/stay at home mom (Iza) enginee
_wwoof_travel: We have train station 6km away, Warsaw airport 1hour by car

name: Jose - Family home and garden - rural, Agritourism Destination, Market Garden, Livestock Farm, Orchard.....
description:
Hello people

I'm Jose, a 47 years young Canarian guy who lives in an old self rebuilt stone house in a farm located in a quiet valley in Fuerteventura Island.

I have fotovoltaic system unplugged from public energy, so electricity is available but with responsable use. Internet with WIFI. Sun provides hot water.

Accomodation and organic vegetables from the farm are provided. You will sleep in a little apart room with your own kitchen (no fridge).... The water installations are still on work....there is a compost toilet, but shower has to be done in my house....

The farm is located in the middle of the island. Nearby are beautiful and inviting landscapes for hiking/running. The farm is 20-25 minutes walking away from the busstop, so you can go explore the island in the afternoons or your free days (saturday and sundays). The closest beach (no waves to surf in it) is 15 minutes by bus to reach. I live far away from surfing places and nightlife.

I am interested in serious and independent wwoofers who like to work in physical tasks....mostly with vegetable garden but also planting new trees or some construction tasks......
Please notice this is an professional horticultural farm with  no machines and body work is needed.....but here are works for all commited persons.....

I am asking for 5 hours 5 days a week (from monday to friday).

I need to work to produce organic veggies to sell in a market and to primary schools. So it cant be that is produced less with wwofing people in the farm then when I work alone... I untherstand you wanna learn/speak about lots of things....I´ll explain all what is related to the work that has to be done, but we cannot "chat" during working time....we can meet after working for a beer or walk or whatever and speak....in both directions....

Organic methods: I work with biodversity for pests control, low inputs, alive soil (organic matter and soil management)
In this world....in wich everyone wants his own names, definition, protagonism
_wwoof_travel: By plane from the principal european cities, by plane or ship from the other islands.  www.ryanair.com  www.spanair.com  www.vueling.com  www.aireuropa.com  www.easyjet.com

name: Monica - A holistic and diverse farm in the outskirts of Cabrera, Dominican Republic
description:
La Finca 

A 40 acre holistic farm highly motivated by animal welfare and biodiversity. In the animal world there are 26 dairy cows, Carlito the bull, 25 chickens, 20 ducks, 3 pigs, Pinta the rescue horse and her daughters Isabella Miranda the mule and Milagros the filly. There are 3 dogs (Ramona, Lindo and Frida) and Nick the cat. What farm house is a proper farm house without a cat?

We also have a small apiary and collect our own honey. 

Pathways lead you to many of our key installations in agriculture, including a small fruit forest planted in 2019, a Dominican canuco and our tangled organic garden. The farm is managed with holistic principles, a focus on biodiversity and implementing the ethics of permaculture; earth care, people care and fair share, and cared for by 3 local farm hands.

Since acquiring the farm it has been a vision to invite others to share in the experience and to inspire the locals in conscientious approaches to animal welfare and stewardship of the land. 

These are some of the areas that we will attend to:
milking
repairing fencing and pathways
gardening and cultivating
weeding
planting
harvesting 
composting
watering
animal handling

There are some ongoing projects requiring more advanced skill…
outdoor kitchen and dining area with traditional clay cookstove made of recycled glass bottle bricks
camping platforms with yurts
cheesemaking
shelf building
labyrinth
new ideas always welcome

Farm life waking up  is our alarm clocks and the most activity, delegated by priorities, is throughout the mornings, ex. 6:30-12:30pm. Midday(lunch) onwards is at leisure and we may come together again early evening (5-6pm) to share and interact. This could also mean some yoga and meditation, depending on the participants vibe. 

Dinner, family style, is served after sunset and the rest of the evening flows as the host and the visitors flow. This may or may not include a bonfire, reading, drumming, sharing, star gazing, or nothing (which is something) at a

name: Clement - Regenerative community for dreamers
description:
Our mission is resolute: to nurture a sustainable and regenerative future for our planet through education, innovation, and community-building.  The Dream Village Vision

Dream Village envisions a world where agriculture goes beyond basic sustenance and emerges as a powerful agent for healing our planet. Since 2017, we've transformed this vision into reality by annually training 20 dedicated students. Our mission is built upon three essential pillars:

1. **Education:** Knowledge is the cornerstone of change. Our college is dedicated to providing a comprehensive, hands-on education in regenerative agriculture. We empower our students with the skills and wisdom required to lead in sustainable farming practices, soil regeneration, and ecosystem restoration.

2. **Innovation:** Innovation is paramount in addressing the challenges of today's agricultural landscape. Dream Village serves as a hub for pioneering research and experimentation. We strive to develop groundbreaking solutions for regenerative agriculture, encompassing low-impact farming techniques and sustainable food systems.

3. **Community:** We recognize that sustainable change cannot occur in isolation. Our college fosters a sense of community among students, faculty, and the broader society. Through collaboration and shared values, we aim to create a network of regenerative agriculture advocates who can catalyze change within their communities.

Dream Village Farm offers a unique blend of agritourism, education, and eco-living experiences, emphasizing sustainability and community engagement. Key features include:

1. Activities: Dream Village offers hands-on regenerative agriculture training, where visitors and students can learn about organic farming practices, syntropic farming, composting, organic pest control, and vegetable and herb production. Other activities include mushroom and snail farming, small animal husbandry (rabbits and chickens), and practical skill-building workshops for local youth and v
_wwoof_travel: 8 hours from Accra by buss, easy transport

name: John (Patrick) - 1 acre Certified Naturally Grown flower and vegetable market-garden with a panoramic views
description:
**Current & Future Availability** We began our farming journey in 2011 and are in our fifth season as farm owners. Patrick operates the farm full time. Destiny works remotely for a garden education non-profit and assists often. (We are also hosting a foreign exchange student named Juan who is in the 11th grade).   Our property consists of 34 acres nestled between 2 ridges and alongside a creek. We operate a 1 acre Certified Naturally Grown market-garden with a panoramic view of trees.  We have 2 small propagation houses where we start most of our transplants, as well as four Farmer's Friend Caterpillar Tunnels, which allow us to grow year round. 

We grow specialty cut flowers and vegetables for wholesale, CSA, farmers market and weddings. This includes lots of hot peppers for our farm hot sauce line. Produce is always available for the kitchens and people on the farm.  Supporting our pollinators is a priority so you’ll see diverse flowers, birdhouses and bee hives around the property. Honey harvest day is always a crowd favorite; our jars are never empty. We also have a small shiitake mushroom operation.

We're not just looking to pass along undesirable tasks; we're looking to work as a team. WWOOFers tasks include: seed starting, transplanting, garden bed prep, weeding, irrigation set up, harvesting vegetables and flowers, and floral arranging. They may also include assisting in the Shiitake operation, assisting in farmers market, pest management, wheelbarrow work among other tasks.

OUR SITUATION IS IDEAL FOR: 1-3 WWOOFers at a time who know each other. This could be an individual, 2-3 friends or a couple. After a team breakfast, we head to work. We adjust the schedule throughout the year to accommodate for weather and ensure we’re bypassing the extreme Arkansas heat. 

HOUSING: We currently have one 30 ft long pop out RV with water, electricity, two full sized beds, a bathroom, and a kitchen stocked with the cooking essentials and vegetarian staples. Groups requ

name: Alexander - Educational Centre, Community farm
description:
Roshni Association is a Pakistani-German collaboration, East-West culture exchange project, and always volunteers from around the world work here. The number of Pakistanis working here is around 80. We work with persons with special needs, for education and natural farming. We have sheltered working places, like a bakery, wood workshop, textile workshop and gardening. Two houses provide space to our living community. We run a school inspired by Waldorf-Pedagogy. We have been cultivating this land (4 acre) since 2001 and have never used any chemical-synthetical fertilizers or pesticides. One agriculture engineer from Germoney and our local gardeners try to establish biodynamic farming here. We produce seasonal vegetables and green fodder for the 15-20 buffaloes and cows. There are also fruit trees. Further animals are 200 chicken, two donkeys and a couple of cats. We use most of the produce our own, but any surplus we market. The farm is not certified. We also provide advice to interested farmers and schools about organic farming. Your involvement in all of these activities is possible. We expect you to be a tolerant and honest person, willing to learn and to take initiative. We can attend up to 2 wwoofers at once. Accommodation would be in our community houses. Food is prepared daily for more than 60 people in our kitchens. We have transportation with vans, motorbike or bicycle available. The public transport passes nearby.

Certifications: Nil.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: We are situated in the Province of Punjab, around 0.5 hours drive South-East from the City of Lahore. Airport in Lahore

name: Jonathan - Organic farmstead surrounded by the mountians and wilderness of Northwest BC
description:
We are Jonathan and Jo and our farm and home is a 320 acre century-old farmstead in the Kispiox Valley, northwest BC.  This land we steward is within the unceded traditional territory of the Gitxsan.

We eat what we grow:  organic vegetables from the acre of gardens and greenhouses; bread and baked goods from the woodfire brick oven; milk and cheese from the Jersey cow; salmon from the river; and meat from the sheep, cows, and chickens that keep the farm fertile. 

After a  decade  primarily growing food for our local weekly farmers' markets, we have shifted to growing select crops for an our retailer friends and spring CSA (May, June), while directing our energy towards our seed,  garlic, and fermented salt products.  Our main focus continues to be on finding a measure of community-sufficiency in food, energy needs, and building materials here on the farm. Everyday work usually combines time in the garden, preserving food, tending the animals, preparing meals, and work on an ever-evolving list of building projects. **We are currently building a small house on the property, and some help with construction is a possibility depending on what stage we are at with the build**

Seasonal tasks include planting, lots of weeding, and harvesting vegetables throughout the growing season; bringing in the hay in mid-summer; processing garlic scapes and spruce tips; harvesting and cleaning seed and grain crops; and cutting firewood in winter. And of course, there's no shortage of repetitive tasks like pulling thistle, rock picking, brush cutting, mowing, pruning and washing and packing veggies, the perfect way to let the mind wander while keeping the hands and body busy. 

The Kispiox Valley is home to a vibrant, eclectic community of old-time ranchers, back-to-the-landers, First Nations, and all around interesting folk, and is host to the Kispiox Valley Music Festival in July.  On occasion there may be friends, neighbours, guests and wwoofers around; more often the farm is incr
_wwoof_travel: There is limited BC North Bus and Via Rail train service to New Hazelton.  The closest airports are Smithers and Terrace, from where there is limited BC Transit bus service to Hazelton.

name: Kaia - Family farm in a remote area
description:
Hello brothers and sisters from all around the Mother Gaia!
I'm Kaia, 28y old, so happy to have wonderful wwoofing experience in Cyprus and now I'm a host to you who are interested.
In my family's farm we live during may-october, here are my mother and father. Special member is  our cat who is actually leading the family and holds an eye over everything.
The Opaku Farm is more than 100 years old, built by my fathers ancestors. It was very big, now there are not many buildings but lots of space to get creative. Meadows, forests and river are surrounding it so it's a wild and clean place.
The core of our mission is to maintain and develop the farm and it's land while having the best summer vacations ; to grow our organic food in the permaculture garden ; to take good care of our forests and meadows so that wildlife would always be here and next generations would have even better future here ; to gather medicinal and edible plants every year ; to gather the historical materials of this farm and area. 
My future goals here are organic food forest, big permaculture garden, dendrological park, (sun)flower field(s), adventure tracks, hiking trails, camping possibilities and a weatherproof house with sauna. If the house will be there and I will move in then cats and dogs and other animals start to arrive fast! Yes, imagine the magical paradise, that's the frequency!

You can usually find me picking medicinal plants or doing something in the garden. Regular day in our family in the farm is free-flowing but we get together to eat and also for tea & coffee moments and bird watching.  Me and my mom do most of the work that needs to be done and there are tasks every day. Sometimes we have workers to do construction work with the house.
Average day for me includes yoga, garden and greenhouse work, long walk with a basket to gather something, cooking, reading.
I would teach you about gardening, permaculture, medicinal plants, forest, estonian language.

We share the same house. Th

name: Juri - Azienda Agricola polifunzionale, agriturismo.
description:
Reis è un luogo dove produzione e ristorazione vivono in simbiosi. 
Siamo in Borgata Chiot Martin, immersi nei boschi della Valle Varaita.
Juri è il fondatore, cuoco,contadino e allevatore. 
Con lui una squadra di ragazze e ragazzi che condividono la passione per un futuro alternativo.

Il fulcro del progetto è l'agriturismo, a cui attorno ruota un' sistema di autoproduzione agro-silvo-pastorale:

Alleviamo pecore, capre, conigli e galline per la produzione di carne e uova per il ristorante.
Grazie al pascolo ci prendiamo cura dei boschi circostanti creando equilibrio tra attività umane e natura, aiutando a mantenere la biodiversità presente in territori volti all'abbandono.

A livello contadino abbiamo un approccio rigenerativo.
Non cerchiamo solo di coltivare in modo del tutto naturale, ma abbiamo in primo piano la salute del nostro suolo.
Rotazioni, colture di copertura, consociazioni e lavorazioni quasi esclusivamente manuali ci permettono di donare struttura al terreno, di utilizzarlo al meglio senza sconvolgere l'equilibrio suo e dei suoi micro-abitanti.
Coltiviamo ortaggi in zone diverse della valle permettendoci di estendere il periodo di produzione, in borgata abbiamo diversi meli con cui produciamo sidro e un bosco di castagni.

Nei diversi periodi dell'anno non mancano le uscite volte alla raccolta di piante e frutti selvatici, altro fondamento della cucina di Reis.
---o----o-----0-----o----o---
Reis is a place where production and gastronomy live in symbiosis. 
We are in Borgata Chiot Martin, immersed in the woods of the Varaita Valley.
Juri is the founder, chef, farmer, and breeder. 
He is joined by a team of young men and women who share his passion for an alternative future.

The heart of the project is the farmhouse, around which revolves a system of self-production involving agriculture, forestry, and pastoralism:

We raise sheep, goats, rabbits, and chickens for the production of meat and eggs for the restaurant.
Thanks to grazing, we take care of
_wwoof_travel: Saluzzo, Savigliano, areoporto di Levaldigi o Torino

name: Santiago - Un bello oasis en el desierto. Cielos increíbles . Sol y buen clima todo el año. Vida en comunidad
activities: Unterkunft: bedroom, campsite, yurt-tipi, shack, trailer
description:
Hola! Me llamo Santi! Y junto con runachay , Somos un proyecto ecológico basado en los principios de la permacultura, la vida comunitaria y el cuidado de la tierra. El proyecto se encuentra en etapa de construcción, con la primera casa y una cabaña "ZOME", ahora expandiendo y construyendo nuevas cabañas y una casa con la técnica de súperadrobe , trabajando en el jardín , la huerta y el bosque de alimentos día a día, ideal para compartir conocimientos en bioconstrucción y agroecología.
Se encuentra en las afueras (1h) de la ciudad de Arequipa, Perú en el pueblo de La Joya, un valle desértico con un hermoso cielo nocturno y a solo una hora de la Costa.
Ofrecemos comida vegetariana Todos son bienvenidos a aprender y compartir en comunidad :)
Ven a ser parte de esta familia

Un lugar para compartir saberes en bioconstruccion, agroecologia, permacultura, vida sana en comunidad y conciencia.necesitamos ayuda con tarea de bioconstruccion, agricultura organica y mantencion de espacios verdes
Así también como carpinteros, bioconstructores y jardineros

Comemos 3 veces al día 
Desayuno Almuerzo y Cena
Nos gusta ser muy abundantes y brindamos la cocina a disposición de los voluntarios 
Una vez por semana prénselos en horno de barro y hacemos noche de pizza

Vivimos a 10 min del río y el patio trasero es un valle sin vecinos donde se puede ir a correr montar bicicleta o solo salir a dar una vuelta
Tenemos muchos instrumentos en casa y nos encanta tocar con el calor de un fuego 
Somos amantes de la buena cocina así que si sabes cocinar estaremos muy felices de compartir la cocina :)

We are a ecological project based on the principles of permaculture, community life and land care. The project is in the construction stage, with the first house, a cabin "ZOME", and a volunteers cabin Now expanding and building new cabins with the tecnique of Superadobe, working in the garden and food forest day by day, ideal for sharing knowledge in bioconstruction and agroecology.

It is located

name: Katrien & - Family farm, Community farm
description:
Dear volunteers 
Added to memory
Manage
We Are Open Again for 2026!
This summer, we have a very limited period available for experienced horse work helpers.
Between July 15–30, 2026, we are looking for motivated volunteers who can stay for at least two weeks and have experience in:
Natural horsemanship groundwork and riding (for very experienced riders)
Teaching English to staff
Working in the greenhouse
Our Philosophy
We follow organic and sustainable methods. Our goal is to be a truly eco-friendly lodge, located in a natural paradise, offering guests an unforgettable experience.
Certification
ViaVia Harganat lodge
_wwoof_travel: Taking a bus from dragon center in west of UB to Murun for 13h and take a taxi to Harganat ecolodge for 30min

name: Juan - Granja ubicada en una isla en Chiloe llamada Coldita
description:
Es una granja de 26 ha que se dedica a la crianza de ovejas. Hay algunas gallinas también. Es un lugar que no tiene caminos ni coches, todo se hace caminando o via marítima. Hay mucho bosque y avifauna tanto de tierra como marítima, avistamiento de delfines y lobos marinos. Hay pocos habitantes en la isla, es muy tranquilo y solo se accede por mar en una lancha que demora 1 hora desde el puerto de Quellon.
Las labores diarias son variadas, manejo de ovejas, reparación de cercos, recolección de leña, recolección de mariscos, buceo( solo en caso de ser apto), pesca, caminatas, trabajo con vecinos. Siembra  de hortalizas y cosecha. Poda de frutales, propagación de árboles y flores.
Labores de manejo de praderas.
Nuevos proyectos de construcción.
Me gusta la música de todo tipo, excepto reggaeton. Se come bastante carne de cordero y patatas, todo sale de la granja.
Fogatas en noches agradables.
Asados de cordero.
Reparación de instalaciones.
Hay kayaks disponibles, un velero, 
Recomendado para personas sin problemas de movimiento y movilidad, pues no hay caminos.
_wwoof_travel: Aeropuerto de Chiloe, puerto de Quellon

name: Viviana - Somos una familia agrícola, dedicada a la agricultura y la ganadería en la localidad de Cochrane
description:
Soy Viviana , hija de Campesinos en la localidad de Cochrane dónde vivimos en un campo que se dedica a la ganadería y agricultora. Tenemos variedad de siembras y labores que hacer de invernadero interior y exterior. También trabajamos con ovejas y vacunos todos los días. Tenemos mucho trabajo en el campo y queremos tener voluntarios para enseñarles este rubro y que nos cooperen con sus conocimientos. Buscamos gente que hable idealmente español y tenga experiencias en agricultura. Si es una pareja podemos recibirlos y darles alojamiento y alimentación en este lugar

Levantarse en la mañana tomamos desayuno y vamos al invernadero a limpiar y desmalezar . Luego riego en caso de ser necesario y en la tarde labores de cosecha de verduras , el tiempo es relativo y lo vamos acomodando

Ellos estarían en una casa independiente y nosotros les daremos la alimentación y ellos se organizan con sus horarios

Me gusta salir al campo andar a caballo y recojer frutas

name: Hynek - Pod Dračím kamenem
description:
Small family homestead located in the Jizera Mountains (Jizerské hory) Protected Landscape Area, near the cities of Frydlant and Liberec just a bit more than one hour from Prague. We focus on environment friendly farming, sustainable living. We aim for at least partial self-sufficiency. We are interested in traditional craftsmanship, permaculture, cheese making, tanning skinns and green building. We grow vegetables, keep sheep, goats, ponies, pigs, rabbits, chickens and bees, and take care of 7ha of land (pastures, orchard, garden, forest). WWOOFers could help with planting trees, building fences, shelters, with animals, garden, field, forest, housekeeping, household, cooking and many other activities ... but we understand the WWOOF project as well as an opportunity to meet interesting people, have fun and exchange experiences. Jsme malé rodinné hospodářství v CHKO Jizerské hory. Naším cílem je hospodaření v souladu s přírodou, trvale udržitelný život a s tím související alespoň částečná soběstačnost, vytváření zázemí pro osobní rozvoj, ohleduplná péče o okolní krajinu a spolupráce s místní komunitou. Zajímáme se o tradiční řemesla, permakulturu, výrobu sýrů, zpracování kůží a vlny, přírodní stavitelství. Hospodaříme na 7ha, chováme ovce, kozy, koně, včely, králíky a drůbež. Pěstujeme zeleninu, bylinky, zakládáme ovocný sad. WOOFery chápeme nejen jako vítanou výpomoc, ale také příležitost k výměně zkušeností.

Organic methods: We use only on farm inputs or certified organic on the farm.

Certifications: We are working only with natural product sin closed cycle, but for our homestead it makes no sense to be certified organic

Type of work: We are trying to make our homestead look nicer in this year.
_wwoof_travel: The best way to our farm is to get to city of Liberec and then by train to Raspenava.

name: Erica - Remote and quiet self sustainable farm and cattle operation in the southern mountains of California.
description:
Land Stewardship, Gardening, livestock production, homestead maintenance, and produce/meat harvesting
Bohna Creek Canyon, Hwy 155, Glennville, CA, Kern County, California
Dates: Flexible Openings as soon as July 1

This is a summer camp on the ranch where the family has lived for three generations. We are currently hiring WWOOFers to help with garden maintenance and various ranch projects. We have a few animals that WOOFers will learn to care for. WWOOFers will work directly alongside our ranch manager, who oversees the day-to-day operations of the property and will serve as the primary point of contact for all farm and facilities work. Campers and staff are also present on the property and will be part of the broader community WWOOFers interact with during their stay.

The ranch's animal residents at this time of year include a working cattle herd, a few horses, barn cats, and a ranch dog or two. WWOOFers should be comfortable working in close proximity to large animals,. WWOOFers should also be able to pass a background check and be willing to provide references.

Who We're Looking For
We are seeking two to three motivated individuals to join us from now through November 30, though the entire duration is not necessary. We welcome people with a genuine interest in hands-on agricultural and land stewardship work. Prior experience is appreciated, and hard skills are required; willingness to work hard and learn is important. Working alongside children and living inside a summer camp environment is required, so a willingness to explore a new kind of community is essential.

Work & Learning Opportunities:
WWOOFers will work alongside our staff in a variety of tasks, including:
Garden preparation, planting, and development
Greenhouse construction and project development
Firewood cutting and splitting
Fence repair and property maintenance
Animal care and tending
General facility cleaning and organization
Camp facility maintenance for the summer season

Our schedule is 5 d

name: Chris (Christopher) - Queers rehabbing an old ranch.
description:
We are out in the woods in the coastal range with a dream to transform an abandoned cannabis farm into a sustainable biofabulous vine garden. we are two dudes two dogs two cats and lots of wild horses.  We currently host a hipcamp here and we are looking for ways to diversify.

The work is decidedly unglamorous on a day to day basis..   Weeding.  Weedwacking,  making burn piles.  Filling potholes.  chopping wood, stacking wood. Moving wood splitting wood.  But sometimes we get into some more exciting stuff.  

We are a chef and ex pot farmer both love music and art.  We have two dogs and two cats .  . we have waterfalls and  wild horses.. need we say more?

Wwoofers will be housed in our tiny guest cabin and/or guest room in our house.   We generally prepare one family meal a day in the late afternoon which we would be happy to share with our wwoofers and our kitchen is open to prepare any additional snacks and meals they want.  

Our land is VERY remote and a long 45 minute drive from the nearest anything.  It can be very difficult for folks who are not accustomed to real isolation. 

WWOOFing hours are flexible, but we would generally expect folks to put in 4 hours per day while staying here :)
_wwoof_travel: Ukiah, CA 2.5 hours drive north of SFO and a solid 45 minutes from our land

name: Amalia - Homestead, Family farm
description:
Les invitamos a la Finca a cultivar su alma a traves de la agricultura orgánica/permacultura. Se ubica en el Macizo Colombiano, en las montañas fuera del pueblo de San Agustìn, Huila. Vivir y trabajar con nosotros requiere una caminata por las montañas y valles con mucha fuerza, inspiración y ánimo. 

Qué vengan a explorar con nuestra familia la hermosa naturaleza y espacio cultivado. Somos una familia bilingüe (ingles y castellano) agrícola y llevamos décadas explorando los diversos caminos de agricultura sostenible, biodinámica, sin petro-químico, eco-lógica.  Celebramos la vida a través de la comunión con la naturaleza, con música, arte y danza.  
Lo ideal para nosotros es que lxs visitantes de intercambio se quedan unas semanas mínimo para poder disfrutar la experiencia al máximo y estamos abiertos a que quedan varios meses si la energía fluye en una onda positiva. 

This magical place invites your spirit to grow with us through organic agriculture and permaculture. We are located in the Andian region of Colombia, known as the Macizo, in the mountains outside of San Agustín in the department of Huila.

Come explore with our family the beauty of natural and cultivated space. We are a bilingual (English/Spanish), vegetarian farming family, we have spent decades studying the many paths of sustainable agriculture including biodynamics, petrochemical free farming, land stewardship and permaculture.  We celebrate life through our communion with NaTuRe, music, art and dance.

Ideally you will be able to stay for at least two weeks in order to enjoy the experience to the max and we are open to you staying several months if the energy is flowing positively.  

Organic methods: Our goal is self-sustainability through permaculture and chemical free agricultural methods. We are currently focusing on planting green manures including the native bean tree balú (puruto, chachafruto), bean bush guandúl, native jungle bean frijol cacha. We have over 10 years experience with  verm

name: Gökhan - Family farm
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Doğu Akdeniz’de yer alan çiftlikte anne ve oğlu birlikte yaşamaktadır. Ev sahipleri evlerini yılın on iki ayı gönüllülere açmaktadır. Bahçe işleri, bakım, çitleme, mantar toplama gibi işlerde yardıma ihtiyaç duyan aile, toplamda 12 dönümde tarım yapmaktadır. Fasulye, nohut, domates, salatalık, ay çiçeği, ve mısır temel tarım ürünleridir. Çiftliğe yakın mesafelerde birçok tarihi ve doğal ziyaret noktaları bulunmaktadır. Aşılı, Yalatlı, Değirmendere Kaleleri ve Yumurtalık, Akyatan, Tuzla gibi sulak alanlar bölgede yer almaktadır. Doğu Akdeniz’de kar yağışı genel olarak görülmemekle birlikte kısa süreli kar yağışları rakım ve konum nedeniyle çiftliğin yer aldığı yakın bölgede görülmektedir.

---

A mother and her son live on this farm in the Eastern Mediterranean area. The hosts accept volunteers all year around. They need help with gardening, maintenance, fencing, and picking mushrooms on their 12 hectares of land. Beans, chickpeas, tomotoes, cucumber, sunflower and corn are the main crops grown on the farm. There are several historical and natural sites nearby. The castles of Asili, Yalatli and Degirmendere are also nearby. In general the Eastern Mediterranean region does not get snow but because of the height and the altitude there might be some snow in the area around around the farm.

Certifications: -

Internet access available

name: Fred - Historic House and organic vegetable garden
description:
This historic 300 year-old property is in the final stages of restoration with an intensive organic garden rooted in the principals of Permaculture and 'no-dig' methods. The owners, Fred & Sunita, have developed organic gardens in several locales, and are committed to developing the property to become self-sufficient in food and energy. The garden was established in 2019 and is maturing nicely. A passive solar greenhouse is also planned.

The property is situated on the edge of Haroldswick Bay in Unst, Scotland's most northern isle. When not participating in organised activities (usually 5-6 hours/day), WWoof guests can enjoy hiking the heath, exploring the hills and wildlife sanctuaries, or participating in aqua-sports. The island has a fully staffed health clinic and leisure centre with a gym, swimming pool and fitness classes. 

Fred teaches karate, and manages a tour company, www.greenolivetours.com and an affiliated advocacy organization, www.greenolivecollective.com. Sunita is a harpist and performs locally and internationally. The couple also produce retreats, concerts and events on Unst and other islands, and in Italy and the USA. Their daughter Maya is a yoga teacher and naturopathic doctor who visits in the summer and conducts yoga classes and workshops in Shetland.  The family has a friendly labrador dog. Laila.

You will have the opportunity to participate in activities such as gathering seaweed and animal manure for the compost piles, planting trees, helping build new raised planting beds, harvesting and preserving vegetables, and cooking meals. In addition if you are handy with woodworking and stone, then there are many carpentry and masonry projects on the property. 

Organic methods: Compost for the raised beds are built with layers of seaweed, pony and cow poo, cut grass, ashes, sawdust and kitchen & garden waste. They are turned after a few months with more layers added, then left to mature, and turned again after several months and covered for 6 m
_wwoof_travel: Ferry to Lerwick port or fly to Sunburgh Airport. Daily integrated buses and ferries to the northern isle of Unst.  Bus stops directly outside our house.  Can pickup from ferry if coordinated in advance and it fits our work schedule. Otherwise take a bus.

name: Emily - Small scale sustainable living.  My farm provides most of the food myself, and my family eat.
description:
Hello WWOOFERS!  My name is Emily, and I own a small self sustaining farm in western Wisconsin about an hour from the Twin Cities.  I have always been a Green Minded person, I even attended The School of Environmental Studies at the Minnesota Zoo as a junior and senior in high school, that was a long time ago, but my passion for the environment has never wavered.  In the house I have 2 dogs, Elsa and Greta.  Outside, I have many chickens.  

On my farm, I raise mostly poultry, chickens and turkeys (sometimes), but have raised a few mangalitsa pigs in the past and they were a joy.  I raised quail for a short time, but couldn't find a market for them and got out of that business for now.  Most of the food I raise goes to my family.  I trade poultry for meats that I can't get otherwise such as beef and venison.  

Typical farming activities differ throughout the year.  Below is a general breakdown of what activities occur throughout the year.
WINTER:  Planning the coming year's projects, gardens, chicken breeding pairings.  Starting some seeds, crafting, blending herbal teas and tonics
LATE WINTER/EARLY SPRING: Tap maple trees and make syrup.  starting seeds indoors, clean up property from winter
SPRING: prepare garden beds for planting, plant cold crops, start seeds indoors, foraging, begin working on project list items, begin chicken breeding plan, incubate eggs
SUMMER: Prepare beds for warm weather planting, harvest cold season crops, July is raspberry month collecting raspberries is a daily job.  Start ferments, wine, vinegar, hot sauce, etc.  Foraging, canning and food preservation begins
FALL: Craft show season. I do not accept WWOOFERS in fall as I am off selling crafts and herbal teas and tonics made from foraged plants.  Canning, and other food preservation continues

A general list of the things that I produce on my farm are:
Chicken and Turkey meat, wine, vinegar, hot sauce, salsa, herbal teas, body soap, laundry soap, items made from recycled materials, sou

name: Craig - Five beautiful acres of pine forest and permaculture style gardens with a lake for swimming
description:
Five beautiful acres of pine forest with a lake good for swimming on the south side. The gardens are set up along the lake, permaculture style. Forest gardens are the plan here. 

This is a mostly secluded piece of property located at the end of a gravel road near 2 state parks. 
Optional Yoga classes are held by request some mornings. They will be custom tailored to your capability. 

We have honey bee's in a Warre Hive to pollinate the gardens.
Want to learn how to buy inexpensive land and homestead it off grid? We can teach you! We are off grid as far as water goes and harvest rainwater. See a video of the system at this link https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a3p4B8WFfxw   We have grid tie solar electric. 
Town is only an 8 minute drive and Austin is about 45 minutes. 
We will grow as much of our own food as possible, work on improving the eco friendly cob buildings, water the gardens, prepare fresh organic food, and enjoy living! 
 
There is a flush toilette, hot shower, washing machine, and kitchen. 
We make our town runs to Austin using a diesel truck that is fueled with used cooking (vegetable) oil and I can teach you how it is done. 

We perform our tasks for 6 hours a day, five days a week, 10:00 am - 1 pm and 2 pm - 5:00 pm.  
We can pick you up from the airport or town provided the time is agreed upon in advance. . 
An extensive library of holistic health books and DVDs are here for you to look over. 
On work days we serve a nice lunch. We are vegetarian and ask that no meat be taken onto the land.  Organic eggs are supplied by request. Raw, gluten free, and allergy free meals are available (you prepare it). We purchase the food, you give us a list, provided we consider it appropriate and healthy.
There is a canoe that wwoofers are welcome to take out on the lake behind the house. 
The property is located in an endangered species zone for the Houston Toad and we will take precautions not to interfere with the toads.
We will be planting existing gardens, mak

name: Dohina - Farm-to-Table Inn con huerta organica
description:
Nuestra granja se ubiqua en el Callejon de Huaylas, una zona hermosa de los Andes entre la cordillera blanca y la cordillera blanca, la cual es llena de lagunas turquesa... Nos consideramos como un ecosistema de proyectos : una huerta organica, un hotel, un restaurante y un bar de botanicos. La huerta es la pieza maestra de todo, se produce aqui las verduras para nuestro restaurante y equipo. Producemos un poco de todo, gracias al clima increible de la zona : frejoles, espinacas, yacon, camote, manzanas, zanahorias, arugula, lechugas, flores, aromaticas...  Y transformamos plantas medicinales en productos cosmeticos (balsamo, cremas, jabones) y verduras en conservas y otros (fermentados, salsa picante, etc.) gracias a nuestra herbalista y nuestro cocinero.

Regar, sembrar, cosechar la huerta. Y reempezar. Eso es el trabajor cotidiano de la huerta, cada vez con plantas diferentes. Queremos siempre mejorar, buscar nuevas ideas... Si quieres experimentar : bienvenido.

El desayuno y la cena son en autonomia con acceso a la cocina. Compartimos el almuerzo, cocinamos junt@s, tomamos turnos o el jefe de cocina nos prepara algo.

La cordillera blanca es conocida por sus treks fabulosos (lagunas turquesas como mencionado), tambien hay varios sitios archeologicos pre-incas y algunas actividades simpaticas y relajante (caballos de paso peruano, banos termales...). Nosotros les ayudamos a organizar sus salidas en dia de descanso, le ponemos en contacto con transportes o otras personas para coordinar.

name: Stairway - Be A Part of Stairway Foundation’s Organic Farm in Puerto Galera, Oriental Mindoro, The Philippines
description:
The Stairway organic farm is a land area located in a beautiful mountain setting around 450 meters above sea level in the village of Baclayan, Puerto Galera, Oriental Mindoro, in the Philippines. The farm aims to inspire community members in sustainable and effective methods of agriculture. Stairway has been working in the Baclayan community for more than a decade, and the farm plays a significant role in terms of food production as well as a venue for training and inspiration for especially the children and youth in the community. 

The day in the Stairway farm starts at 7 AM with a meeting at the workshop with all the farmers to delegate responsibilities and agree on the day's activities. The farmers will discuss how best can the volunteers support them that day and the activities range from planting, harvesting, preparing the compost, weeding, irrigating, and taking care of the seedlings at the nursery, among others. Usually, the activities are decided based on the weather as well, for instance, if it is raining the farmers usually work at the compost facility or at the greenhouses and nursery. There are multiple gardens on the farm and each of them is at different stages of planting, so the activities vary substantially every week.

The most common tasks include preparing new plots as the farm is still expanding. This constitutes marking the new beds, preparing the soil, weeding, irrigating, and then preparing the seeds and seedlings to plant. Other activities include collecting bamboo in the mountain, cleaning and organizing the workshop and common areas, and building bamboo structures to protect the new plantings from the sun. The volunteers can also assist the farmers in calculating the anticipated weight of the next harvest. Transplanting of seedlings is usually done at 3 PM to avoid exposing the seedlings to the sun.

There are two 15-minute breaks throughout the day at 10 AM and 3 PM when the volunteers are given snacks, and the lunch break is from 11:30 A
_wwoof_travel: Ninoy Aquino International Airport, Manila, Philippines

name: Sarah - A hidden piece of paradise in the Wild West of Portugal
description:
SPECIAL PROJECT: 

Calling in people who want to help build a future rooted in nature.
                                                                    
At No Meio Do Nada, we are creating a place to slow down, reconnect and build something meaningful  together.

A place where people and nature support each other, where learning happens through doing, and where simple living becomes a source of richness.

We are looking for people who would like to spend some time  living and working on the land, helping us lay the foundations for a long-term, regenerative place.
This is not about perfection or hard productivity it is about presence, commitment and shared intention.

🌱 What we are creating together
An abundant vegetable garden, grown with care for the soil and local ecosystem

A natural spring and water system, where water can be stored, shared and used to nourish the land

A garden kitchen, where we process, preserve and prepare food directly from the land

Communal spaces where learning, rest and connection can naturally take place

Everything we build is meant to serve both people and the landscape, now and in the future.

🤲 What you will receive
Accommodation and nourishing meals in exchange for your help

Guidance and hands-on learning in regenerative living, gardening and land-based projects

Masterclasses and shared knowledge from local specialists and people working with the land

A communal living experience based on respect, simplicity and collaboration

Space to slow down, reflect and reconnect with yourself, others and nature

You will learn while working, and work while learning.

🌍 Who we are looking for
We are looking for people who:
Feel drawn to living close to nature

Are willing to contribute with care and responsibility

Are open to communal living and shared rhythms

Want to be part of something that is still growing and unfolding

Our intention is to form a small, aligned community with a shared goal:
 to learn, inspire, grow and carry this
_wwoof_travel: Train station Saboia - Santa Clara, 15 min. Faro Airport, 1,5 hour

name: Amy - Off-grid 90-acre family farm in central TN with goal to be self-sufficient 💪
description:
3/28/2026  Please note, we are currently almost entirely booked for 2026.  We currently have these slots  available:
4/19-4/23
5/27-6/1
6/30-7/5
7/14-7/19

Outside of these dates, we *may* be able to host you but as we only have one "nice" intern housing unit, you would either be in a somewhat defunct but functional camper (see below for details) or tenting out, your preference.  We don't book more than 2-3 WWOOFers at a time, so even if you are willing to tent, if we already have a couple of folks booked we likely won't be able to take you as well.  Thanks so much for understanding!!

We are a 15-year-old 90-acre family farm on a reclaimed strip mine in central Tennessee. Our primary goal is self-sufficiency (we are off-grid with solar power and well and rain water), but we try to provide quality meat, produce, and other farm products to the surrounding community (a food desert in several respects) by participating in several year-round farmer's markets and a food hub serving local restaurants.  We also want to return this abused property to a healthy, vibrant oasis of diverse life. We rotationally and intensively graze Scottish Highland cattle, meat goats, sheep, and alpacas. We also have ducks, geese, and occasionally honeybees. We are still growing our cattle herd (around 35), and we sell some goats and butcher a few for ourselves. I spin our sheep and alpaca wool for personal use and sell the raw fleeces online.  We sell duck eggs and some garden produce, especially garlic, and I also make soap and a variety of personal care products as an online business (website and Etsy shop). We would love help with a variety of farm jobs any time of year, including seasonally butchering goats or poultry, caring for baby poultry, spinning or knitting alpaca yarn, prepping fleeces, making soap, planting or harvesting garlic, canning, freezing or drying produce, maintaining the gardens and orchard, and a wide variety of chores pertaining to a garden and farm in general. We ha

name: Yasmina - Organic farm in an argan forest
description:
Salut les woofers !
Suite aux pluies diluviennes de cette année, nous reprenons notre activité à la ferme !
Notre projet est une ferme biologique de 13 ha située dans une arganeraie de l'arrière-pays proche d'Essaouira. Notre volonté est de faire de cette ferme un projet d'agrotourisme basé sur le respect de la nature dans la tradition de la permaculture, de la culture locale et de la valorisation de nos arganiers (extraction d'huile d'argan).
Faune : moutons, poules, ânes, chiens et chats
Flore : arganiers, oliviers, grenadiers, caroubiers, amandiers, figuiers et autres variétés
En tant que woofers, nous souhaitons vous proposer différentes activités.
Vous aurez l'occasion de participer à :
1. ARGAN EXP. (selon la saison)

Tailler/arroser les arbres
Marquer les arbres porteurs de fruits
Récolter les fruits
Répertorier les arbres
Sécher les fruits

2. Autres arbres fruitiers

Arrosage
Taille
Récolte
Traitement des maladies par des méthodes naturelles

3. Jardins et potagers

Création et entretien du potager
Entretien du jardin aromatique
Créer des bacs autour des arbres
Ramasser et empiler les pierres du terrain
Créer des murets/bancs en pierre
Baliser et créer les chemins d'accès

4. La vie à la ferme

Cuisiner pour les woofers
Entretenir les espaces communs
Déterminer des chemins de randonnée dans la propriété
Apprentissage sur la précieuse huile d'argan
Définir les activités pour les futurs woofers
Mettre en place des processus pour le bon fonctionnement de la ferme

Organisation de la journée :

4h/jour et 5 jours/semaine
Équipe du matin (2 personnes) : 8h-12h
Équipe de l'après-midi ou soirée (2 personnes) : 12h-16h
Après un bon petit déjeuner, établir des zones du terrain et répertorier les arbres
En saison de récolte, participer à la récolte
Participer de manière générale à la vie de la ferme dans la bonne humeur
Temps libre dans une zone calme et reculée

COMPORTEMENT ATTENDU :

Pas de consommation d'alcool sur les lieux
Respect du personnel sur place
Esprit

name: Giulia - Cerfiticated organic food production, agroforestry and medicinal plants
description:
Sítio Sou, located in Teresopolis, on the mountains region of Rio de Janeiro, is an agroecological production point that has as goal to practice and spread regenerative agriculture knowledges. Here we have different work fronts: certificated organic production, biodiverse agroforestry systems, essential oils production, particular order of organic food delivery, courses and experiences. At the moment, besides our employee, we both - Giulia and Pedro - live and work at the farm. We are a couple that believes in a better world.
The volunteer work consists of helping in daily activities, through those you can acquire agroforestry, rural management and agroecology knowledge. Such as:
Farming - soil preparation, fertilizing, planting, weeding, irrigation installation, harvest, plant nursery, agroforestry implementation; 
Processing - herb drying, essential oil destination. (eventually)
Conservation - water and energy supply care, conviviality spaces estruturation, mowing.
Selling - weekly particular order of organic food delivery mounting, supermarket cargo delivery.

What does the farm offer?
- Communal room accommodation (maximum 4 persons)
- Basic food itens (oil, rice, beans, pasta, coffee, sugar, salt, tomato sauce, corn flour, wheat flour, onions and garlic) and fruits/vegetables from the farm. If the volunteer needs anything else to eat, you need to provides it before arriving.
- Complete kitchen, bathroom, covered external area, shed and camping area. 
- We don’t have wifi in the farm but Tim, Vivo, Claro 4g works very fine. 
- The farm is locater near the Três Picos State Park and the Serra dos Órgãos Nation Park (PARNASO) - around 30 minutes to one hour by car - which have untouched nature, waterfalls and trekking to explore - but we don`t offer transport for volunteers private tourism.
- We have books avaiable for reading while staying here
- Social and cultural activities such as camp fire and friends farms visit

What does the volunteer offer?
- Stay a minim

name: Petra - Einsiedlerhof mit Direktvermarktung von Rindfleisch und Erlebnisbauernhof
description:
Hallo, Willkommen bei den Sollmanns.

Wir, das sind Stephan (Dipl. Agrar. Ing.), Petra (Dipl. Ing. Ernährung und Versorgungsmanagement), Johann (geb. 2015) und Moritz (geb. 2018) bewirtschaften seit 2013 unseren Hof. Dank der ursprünglichen Besitzerfamilie war es möglich, eine außerfamiliäre Hofübergabe zu verwirklichen.

Wir  haben eine Nebenerwerbslandwirtschaft mit Direktvermarktung von Rindfleisch.  Weiterhin sind wir ein Erlebnisbauernhof und besitzen einen großen Garten mit Obstbäumen und Gemüsepflanzen für den eigenen Bedarf. Neben den Kühen wohnen bei uns noch  Hühner, Kaninchen und Katzen.

Unser Hof liegt als Einödhof sehr idyllisch zwischen Nürnberg und München.

Wir freuen uns auf Deine Anfrage!

Organic methods: Wir arbeiten nach Richtlinien von Naturland.

Certifications: Naturland Betrieb seit 2018

Type of work: Wir gestalten im Moment unseren Hof etwas schöner. 
Dazu gehört das Streichen der Stallfassade etc. 
Das Dach vom Hühnermobil bedarf einem neuen Anstrich
weitere größere Projekte in Zukunft sind die Verlängerung des Stalldachs als Lagermöglichkeit und der Abriss des alten Wohnhauses. 

Internet access available

name: PIANG YOW - Educational Centre, Eco Retreat, Orchard, Mixed Farm
description:
We are a social enterprise sponsored under a private limited company Angkasa Nusantara Sdn Bhd to undertake this work. The main objectives will be food security and poverty alleviation through successional agroforestry and reforestation. A major part of our profits goes towards improving the land, education of the local community and the creation of social entrepreneurs.

Funding:
We are self-funded.

Communications:
Internet is available for smart phones with data plan. Our preferred mode of communication is by whatsapp or Telegram, which can be provided to those who email us. It is mandatory for WWOOFERS to have a smartphone for communication.

Accommodation:
We expect accommodation to be ready by Dec 2023.

Duration of stay:
Visitors are required to stay at least 2 weeks for their visit, which can be extended upon mutually satisfactory performance.

Covid-19 Virus:
All WWOOFERS are required to check on the conditions of entry into Malaysia before they depart from their country of origin and ensure that they have adequate vaccinations to meet the requirements of the Malaysian Health Authorities - https://covid-19.moh.gov.my/garis-panduan/garis-panduan-kkm. Failure to do so could result in the person not allowed into the country.

Currently (2022 Aug 12) the public are required to take 2 vaccinations and 1 booster shot. In general, everyone wears a mask in public. Other than that, life goes on as normal. Those who are infected by Covid-19 or are exposed to those with Covid-19 are required to go into home quarantine and report to the Ministry of Health (MOH) through a MySejahtera app. Duration of quarantine is set by the MOH - usually 7 days in isolation. Those with severe cases may be hospitalised as required by the MOH.

Location & Travel:
The farm is in Perak, Peninsular Malaysia, accessible by train and highways.

Organic methods: Successional agroforestry,

Woodcomposting & composting of market waste and oil palm waste (EFB - Empty Fruit Bunch).

Certifications
_wwoof_travel: Our farm is located at Lot 11878, Mukim Chenderiang, Daerah Batang Padang (Tapah), Perak. For waze, search for Taman Sri Kampar, Kampar, Perak.

name: Stefanie - Finca de café y cacao
description:
🌱 Bienvenidos a nuestra finca en Colombia
Hola, soy Diego, colombiano de 35 años, y mi pareja, Stefanie, es belga y tiene 39 años. Vivimos en una finca familiar en la cima de una colina, rodeados de naturaleza, junto a nuestros dos perros y dos gatos. Mis padres viven a poca distancia a pie, y mis hermanos nos visitan con frecuencia con sus hijos, así que siempre hay un ambiente familiar y acogedor.
Desde lo alto de la finca se disfruta de una vista espectacular. Muy cerca pasa el río Guaure, con su hermoso cañón y aguas cristalinas: un lugar perfecto para conectar con la naturaleza. El pueblo de San Gil, centro turístico de la región, se encuentra a una hora de aquí.

☕ Qué hacemos en la finca
La actividad principal es el cultivo de café y cacao bajo principios de permacultura y conservación de la vegetación nativa. También cultivamos maíz, yuca, plátano, piña, mango y frijol. Nuestra huerta orgánica se fertiliza con compost producido por nuestros conejos y gallinas.
Queremos ofrecerte una experiencia auténtica de la vida campesina en Colombia. Empezamos el día temprano con una taza de café, y te enseñaremos todo el proceso: desde la siembra de la semilla hasta tostar y moler tu propio café. El cultivo en Santander es distinto al de la zona cafetera tradicional, con una vegetación muy especial.
Nuestro nuevo proyecto de cacao también brinda muchas oportunidades para aprender sobre este fascinante cultivo.

🏡 Alojamiento y comida
Como voluntario de WWOOF, te ofrecemos una habitación privada dentro de nuestra casa. También puedes alojarte en casa de mis padres o acampar si lo prefieres: hay mucho espacio para ello.
Incluimos tres comidas al día y, por supuesto, café disponible a toda hora. Cocinamos con productos frescos de nuestra huerta, y nos encanta compartir la cocina típica colombiana. Si sigues una dieta vegetariana o vegana, no hay problema: Stefanie es vegetariana y estará encantada de preparar alternativas saludables.
Queremos que te sientas como en casa. P

name: Alexandre - A retreat at the mantiqueira mountains
description:
Olá, we are Ale and Tissy.
In 2019 we left São Paulo in search of something more. We found a special place, in the middle of the Mantiqueira mountains, in a small village in Monteiro Lobato, and started building our little piece of paradise: a permacultural yoga retreat center.

We fell in love with the amazing views, the civilization is distant, and we appreciate the silence. We've been planting a lot of native trees and gardens to bring back the biodiversity that belongs here. We love our community, our neighbors are mainly artists and organic farmers.

We love making things. Creating, drawing and building. We have been on this journey for 5 years almost non-stop. Our main goal is to materialize places where people can connect with themselves, others and with nature.

Throughout our projects we really connected with bioconstruction and building with wood. We've created two domes, one being a yoga hall, a COB house, small structures and we love doing green roofs.

We will be focusing on COB small structures to host more volunteers. Little cozy rooms with a simple project that uses only materials that we already have (pallets and dirt from our own land). Bioconstruction is a lot of fun, and very empowering because we reconnect with the tradition of centuries of communities building their own houses. It will be extra special for the volunteers to build the house for the next volunteers.

Let us know if you have experience working with power tools.
We also have a PAYED position for long term stays for our retreats (cleaning and helping with the kitchen).

The volunteers are hosted at the same rooms that we host our retreat guests.
We provide rooms with amazing hot shower, a lot of personal space and also a stunning view of the mountains. It's a great place to connect, rest and restore.

Since we are in the mountains, the village is 40 minutes walking, the place is more suited for quiet and chilled activities such as reading, resting, writing and contemplating.

Some i

name: Anne - Projet pilote explorant de nouveaux modèles de coexistence durable des humains en milieu tropical
description:
[Español 🇨🇷] 

La Finca Cattleya está situada en Costa Rica, en la península de Osa, a orillas del Golfo Dulce. Concretamente, a mitad de camino entre Chacarita y Rincón. Se encuentra en una zona protegida, un corredor animal entre los dos parques nacionales Corcovado y Piedras Blancas. 

En una finca de unas treinta hectáreas, se trabaja para desarrollar un modo de vida sostenible lo más autosuficiente y autónomo posible, inspirado en los indígenas Ngäbe, con quienes colaboramos estrechamente: se desarrollan técnicas agrícolas totalmente naturales (compostaje, fabricación de fertilizantes naturales a base de huesos de pescado y papaya, lombricultura, acolchado...) para trabajar una plantación agroecológica que intenta representar la colección más completa posible de plantas alimenticias locales tradicionales, variedades «olvidadas» de la región.

Esta también incluye plantas medicinales autóctonas y variedades de plantas destinadas a los animales salvajes que pasan por el corredor animal, que son bienvenidos en un modelo que incluye a todos los habitantes de este bosque primario.

También se desarrolla la apicultura de especies utilizadas en la farmacopea indígena local y el cultivo de vainilla con el fin de sostener el equilibrio financiero del proyecto. 
Tres familias trabajan para el proyecto. Dos de ellas viven allí, incluida una indígena cuyos conocimientos son muy valiosos para alinear el proyecto con el modo de vida tradicional de los antiguos aborígenes de la región. ¡Con ellos tendrás el placer de compartir tu día a día! 

Hay una habitación con cama doble disponible para los WWoofers :)

Actividades locales y cercanas
- Kayak en los manglares y en el Golfo Dulce
- Descubrimiento de la fauna y la flora de la selva tropical primaria
- Parque Nacional Corcovado (posibilidad de contactar con guías locales)
- Parque Nacional Piedras Blancas (lo mismo para los guías)
- Excursiones en barco por el Golfo Dulce y/o por el delta del río Sierpe (la zona de manglares

name: Tobie - Regenerative Farmstead near Santa Fe
description:
For 2026, we have a flower co-learner with us in the casita for the season.  For Spring, summer, and fall, we can host tents, campers, van-lifers. We have lots of shared spaces (outdoor dining, pool, outdoor office, indoor bathrooms). 

FLOWER FARM CO-LEARNER
We are setting up a 1/ 4 acre Flower mini-farm. You can learn alongside her and join in the fun, work, & frustration .   Come make marvelous mistakes and learn with us, gaining experience for your own dream.  We have a co-learner for the season, but welcome others as there is much ado! around the farm. 

We ask each WWOOFer to complete an intake form and we set up a zoom call to chat prior to finalizing a visit. We prefer stays of 3-4 weeks when using casita (and we welcome shorter stays for camping). To stay in the casita, we usually look for a minimum of three weeks.   We may be open to a VISIT exchange for 2-4 days if you are from e.g. Denver/Moab/ABQ/ and want to visit (and host a visit for us).   

OUR FARM
We are caretakers of an established farmstead outside of Santa Fe, New Mexico (25 minutes away). Surrounded by flowers, vines, and orchards, we are using a combination of New Mexican traditional, regenerative, and permaculture principles to create an updated working  farmstead. We are a bustling family juggling school, work, and the joys of  living on the land. We are excited to be stewards of this land and look forward to sharing the experience. We look forward to help, good company, and cheer. 
We value diversity and request you do the same. LGBTQ+ welcome.   
Please be ID verified.

The days are filled with pruning grapes, picking (or pickling) pears and apples, weeding, building new beds, fixing fences, caring for chickens, preserving the harvest and a myriad of other farmsteading tasks.   

We are grounded and relaxed people and seek harmony in our home. We do have some chaos with work, school, children--but we try to keep this positive. We offer personal engagement as well as space to be/explore--
_wwoof_travel: Santa Fe, New Mexico (SFA) (25 min) or ABQ is 1.5 hours away.

name: Jonathan - Organic farm Mountain, 30 min. from Sosua. 400k Meter.  Producing Fruit,Veg. Prefer Spanish speakers
description:
I'm Jonathan. Our farm is 11 years old and we have 1000 fruit trees, a greenhouse for vegetables. We produce Cacao and  Honey too. We have 2 full time employess, 4 horses, 4 dogs, 3 cats, 4 cows and a mule.  We have an .A frame casita with a new queen size bed, a composting toilet and an outdoor shower. A wood fired kitchen is available 70 meters from the A frame.

Woofers would help germinate seeds, feed horses, weed greenhouse, harvest produce and general farm organization.

Meals will be prepared by our neighbor Nicole at her restaurant a 3 minute walk from the A frame.

We have a wonderful spring for swimming and the area is beautiful for walking. We are aslo a 10 minute walk from a wonderful river.

Soy Jonathan. Nuestra granja tiene 11 años y cuenta con 1000 árboles frutales y un invernadero para hortalizas. También producimos cacao y miel. Contamos con dos empleados a tiempo completo, cuatro caballos, cuatro perros y gatos. Tenemos una casita con estructura en forma de A con una cama queen size nueva, un inodoro seco y una ducha exterior. Hay una cocina de leña a 70 metros de la casita.

Los woofers ayudarían a germinar semillas, alimentar a los caballos, desmalezar el invernadero, cosechar productos y organizar la granja en general.

Nuestra vecina Nicole preparará las comidas en su restaurante, a 3 minutos a pie de la casita.

Tenemos un manantial maravilloso para nadar y la zona es preciosa para caminar. También estamos a 10 minutos a pie de un río precioso.

name: Alexzander Felix - Community farm
description:
Somos una finca con diversidad en cuanto a fauna y flora, además de ello contamos con cultivos de café y cacao, cultivos que son eje principal de la economía de la zona. nos encontramos enmarcados entre reservas de biosfera, donde nuestro objetivo es la conservación del medio ambiente.


Certifications: certificación orgánica

Current projects: Instalación de tanques para crianza de peces, cosecha de café y cacao para realizar productos terminado.

Internet access available

name: Alejandro - Agritourism Destination
description: Somos Una Pequeña granja Destinada a la cría de animales ya la fabricación de quesos de cabra ... también criamos caballos ... cerdos..y llamas ... en epocas de baja de Producción Trabajamos con turismo..cabalgatas y ... gastronomía

name: Kuntur - Educational Centre and Master Unit (place of sustainable developement)
description:
Introduction:

We  propagate a complete philosophy of life based on universalism called Neohumanism. The project is established in the mountains of Córdoba, Argentina, in a previously uninhabited area and is also known as Ánanda Náráyańa (Blissful Divinity).

Since 2014, with increased efforts, we are becoming an ideal model of growth, following the guides on "Master Units" of Shrii Shrii Ánandamúrti, which are miniature forms of sustainable development based on spiritual values ​​and community inclusion.

Curious visitors, families and volunteers come from all over the world to enjoy the happiness that prevails in the place and its sustainable projects. Acting locally and thinking globally offers a new perspective of a progressive nature for society. 

We seek to be a perennial source of inspiration for all, through its exemplary commitment to its visions and selfless service in all areas.

Currently, the project houses some beautiful residential homes, a bakery and produces essential oils; cultivation of organic herbs and vegetables.

Unfortunately, right now we cannot receive any volunteers because the program is lacking subsistantial funds. As soon as the project gains collective support, the volunteers program will be re-opened and announced. So,

Stay connected!

Organic methods: We have a seed bank, produce our compost, apply biodynamic methods of cultivation and soil treatment. Our project is set in high mountain and is free of surrounding pollution and noise. 

Certifications: We are in the process to certify our products. Locally we didn’t get any problem to sell or provide our products in organic market where mostly we commercialize our items. 

Current projects: 1. Home constructions based on Permaculture methods 

2. Gardens’ (vegetables and medicine herbs) development and expansion. 

3. Herbal Medicine: natural medicine and essential oils distilling.  

4. Fruits trees. 

5. Apiary.

6. Community exchange and coworking.

7. Cultural exchange. World Fa

name: Md Lutfar - A patron of organic farming and a former tea planter has a beautiful bungalow with a nice homestead
description:
