a patron of organic farming and a former tea planter has a beautiful bungalow with a nice homestead organic farm producing organic tree fruits and vegetables. His farm is located at Kapasia 45 km away from the capital Dhaka. The village has 30 farmers who are all doing organic farming at their homesteads which are very rich with multiple cropping cultures and all are organic. The village is close to the river Sitalaska. From Dhaka, one can easily reach there by road and waterways.

Organic Orthodox Tea

Accommodations in the farm. Food will be cooked in the estate

Gardening

name: Rick - Beautiful, wet, and wonderful remote farm near Juneau
description:
Thank you all for your interest in our farm project! We are chock-a-block like they say in New Zealand, which means full full full for Summer 2024.  Sorry we are not accepting more volunteers until next summer.
Rick and Nels  run this farm near Juneau. It is a beautiful, wet and wonderful piece of property, very remote (2 hour kayak from Auke Bay), no cars, very little electricity (solar power). We raise vegetables in raised beds, live outdoors all summer. We need wwoofers from May 5 to October 5. We have built a small timber frame cabin from timbers milled on our property. Accommodation for WWOOFers is an apartment above the woodshed or in a big wall tent in the meadow. Or if you prefer, you can bring your own tent and use one of the tent platforms. Hot tub under the stars, community kitchen, outhouse in the blueberry bushes. Work includes gardening, soil building, pioneering, building projects, carrying materials from the beach, firewood, and many other creative projects. Volunteers must enjoy the simple things in life, be quite fit, community minded, ready to rough it softly, love to work, be OK with living remote. 5 hours of work per day in exchange for really good vegetarian and Alaska wild food. Minimum stay 2 weeks. You need this much time to really get the feel of the place. Maximum stay is 6 weeks your first year.

 We would love having you if you are the kind of person who wants a break from the internet and social media and is ready to really be open to the others on the farm. Our rule about your use of technology is that it should be your secret! :)  Good dogs welcome, children not out of the question, no tobacco use whatsoever please. All are welcome, gay people especially so. Call ahead so we can arrange transport. I speak fluent English, Spanish, German, Hungarian, and dabble in French. I hosted over 20 volunteers last year and consider my volunteer community the true wealth of my project .Please be a morning person- we meet at 8 am for breakfast and

name: Douglas - Peaceful Homestead & Retreat in Ecuador
description:
(This  insert is a small note from douglas: Crystal has done a beautiful and creative portrait of our space here in the amazonian highlands of Ecuador, this land is very special to us and for this reason we want to share it with all who are called by intuition.  The other day when I was hiking to one of the woofer cabins I had this thought, -it was a kind of dialogue as I was walking along with my machete chopping grass, noticing the dew covered webs of spiders, the tiny mushrooms scattered the path my feet were on, hearing the enormity/intensity of bird songs and distant waterfall, smelling the nectar of orchid and forest and grass, feeling the humid warmth of the amazonian mother hold me as I noticed.- the thought was of a dialogue with someone who just arrived to share space.  So excited I am to share with this new arrival all that I have learned in this short time, I know it is not us who grows food, it is Her our mother, we just help by planting a seed and watching, maybe throwing some compost down to encourage.  Being in gratitude. Simple tools like the machete for chopping green compost, (side, side, note***also kids, if it has been a lifelong dream of yours of how to sharpen a  machete, a local showed me how and I am happy to pass this wisdom along, please ask) the barretta a one meter iron bar with a point on one end and a flat gouge on the other essential for digging rocks, or new holes for trees, and of course hands and feet for feeling the earth and being intimate with plants, insects and funghi. Wow I guess my short note turned into a ramble, I guess more than anything i just wanna say, You are welcome here.  To in-joy similar dialogues, amazing food (thanks to Crystals genious with combining plants and sauces), or maybe just to ramble on in the midst of ineffable beauty. in love, douglas)  

Possible day trip adventures close by: hiking Podocarpus National Park; Swimming in Rio Bombuscaro; Touring the beautiful botanical gardens of Finca Monay (which i
_wwoof_travel: Zamora Bus Terminal, which is 1.5 hours from Loja

name: FRANCESCO - Agricoltura naturale a trazione animale
description:
Torre dei Mastro è una piccola realtà a conduzione familiare che da 4 generazioni si prende cura di qualche migliaio di ulivi secolari e millenari, in un meraviglioso fazzoletto di terra pugliese, situato tra Castellana Grotte e Monopoli.
Lo facciamo con amore, attenzione e profonda gratitudine verso il territorio che ci ospita: un luogo di inestimabile valore e incredibile bellezza.
Qui potrai trovare prodotti di qualità, coltivati senza veleni, nel rispetto della natura e dei suoi tempi: Olio Extravergine di oliva, Vino Primitivo Rosso e Verdeca Bianca, Ciliegie, Mandorle e Carrube. Sapori di una volta, che hanno origine in terreni vivi e floridi di biodiversità. 
La ricerca di soluzioni sostenibili, a livello ambientale, economico e umano, hanno da decenni guidato la nostra azione, per questo abbiamo deciso di dedicare tempo ed energie nell’esplorare nuovi – e antichi – modi di lavorare senza petrolio.
In quest’ottica, nel 2012, abbiamo accolto in masseria Lana e Deianira, due puledre CAITPR (unica razza da tiro pesante rapido in Italia). Grazie a loro le nostre attività negli uliveti, nei vigneti, nell’orto e nei frutteti, hanno un impatto sull’ambiente estremamente positivo.
Alla trazione animale si è ben presto aggiunta l’elettrificazione dei nostri strumenti da lavoro, così da ridurre ulteriormente l’utilizzo di combustibili fossili, ad oggi davvero irrisorio.
Crediamo nello scambio, nella filiera corta, nel baratto e nel consumo critico (pensato, sentito). Riteniamo preziosi gli antichi saperi e fondamentale il tramandarli, per questo organizziamo spesso corsi e giornate d'incontro. Ci prendiamo cura della terra e di ogni essere vivente che passa di qui: che siano i numerosi gatti ormai di casa, il cane Nino e gli altri suoi amici, le galline e i galli felici, a cui abbiamo costruito due pollai mobili per farli stare sempre all'aperto, le oche chiaccherone, i conigli, le api, i numerosi lombrichi che abitano i terreni, fino all'ultima formichina che ci buca

name: Bruno - Four acres of desert in early stage of development into a permaculture forest in Arizona
description:
Four acres of desert in early stage of development into a permaculture forest.

We are located less than a mile from the San Pedro river in beautiful southeastern Arizona. The area is noted for bird-watching and natural beauty that includes mountain forests, desert, grasslands and riparian environments. Close to historic Tombstone and Bisbee.

Currently the property is mostly desert. There is a small vegetable garden and a few young trees including figs, olives and Arizona walnut. 

Goals: food self-sufficiency through permaculture and small livestock, community education through workshops.

We need help with development of rainwater harvesting and distribution, garden construction and maintenance and a variety of projects that will arise in the course of growing a homestead.

Accommodations: One private bedroom for a single or couple. Shared bathroom, kitchen and living areas. The house is approximately 1700 square feet with a private well and solar (on grid) power.

Meals: One shared family-style dinner. Breakfast and lunch self-serve.

WWOOFers would be expected to help for 4-6 hours per day on projects related to permaculture development including laying out and digging rainwater collection and distribution system, preparing planting beds, planting and weeding, small construction projects.

Organic methods: No-till, composting and mulching, no chemical fertilizers or pesticides.

Certifications: Just getting started with property development, so food production is low. As the farm develops I will learn more about certification.

Current projects: designing and developing passive rainwater harvesting
developing garden beds
designing pasture and livestock systems

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Sierra Vista, AZ bus, Tucson AZ,  airport.

name: Cuong - Eco Retreat, Flower Garden Farm, Family farm
description:
Welcome to Our Family Farm!

Our family farm is located in the heart of Thai Hoa Town, Nghe An Province, covering an area of approximately 2 hectares. Our main crops include guava, pomelo, banana, and other seasonal fruits. We also raise fish, chickens, and ducks. Our family is working towards developing the farm into an ecological and organic farming model.

The farm is located near a natural forest and uses water from a natural spring for both agricultural activities and daily living. It is a peaceful place close to nature, suitable for those who would like to experience rural life and learn more about sustainable farming.

We are a family of four, consisting of myself, my spouse, and our two children: an 11-year-old son and a 7-year-old daughter. We are happy to welcome English-speaking volunteers to join us in organic farming activities and also help our family improve our English communication skills.

Besides the farming experience, we would like to offer volunteers the opportunity to immerse themselves in rural life, learn about local culture, and connect with the local farming community. We believe this cultural exchange will help volunteers gain a deeper understanding of rural life in Nghe An.

Come and become part of our family farm, where you can learn about organic farming, share your knowledge, experience a simple lifestyle, and create beautiful memories in the natural landscape of Thai Hoa.

Activities

Volunteers will help with suitable farm activities such as planting crops, taking care of plants, maintaining the garden, supporting some daily tasks, and joining family activities. The specific tasks will be discussed depending on the season, weather, and each volunteer’s ability.

Other Requirements

We prefer volunteers who are in good health, creative in sharing ideas, have a good sense of humor, are sociable, enthusiastic, and willing to take part in family life as well as farm activities.

As it is currently the summer holiday season, our family e

name: Merete - Homestead, Smallholding, Livestock Farm, Family farm
description:
The farm is under construction and there is a lot of work left before I’m done. The main building is very old, and is under total reconstruction, so here is primitive living.
Be aware that we most of the time will be alone, and one must endure being away from "civilization" without getting "cabin fever". You have to be comfortable with your own company.

The best part is living in nature. We have the midnight sun in the summer, and northern lights in the winter.
Here is nice to go fishing, and to go hiking in the mountains. If you want to go in the mountains on the main land, you have to be experienced and not go alone. If you want to go on a mountain trip, then I can try to help you find people you can go together with, or you can pay to go on a guided tour. There are several companies in the area who offer various guided tours.

Accommodation:
I have a room with two beds on the second floor. I also have a lavvo (like a tipi, but the Scandinavian type). The lavvo has max room for 10 people, but for comfort, it is best with less people inside. I also have a couple of hammocks and a tent that can be used if anyone wants to try to sleep outside in nature.
Note that because the house is under construction, everything here is primitive.

Here we eat food based on the farm's resources, so both meat and fish are used. My plan is to try to grow my one food, or else eat local and in season. The farm is alcohol and drug free.

Weather here in the north of Norway can be all sorts. So you need clothes and shoes for cold, rain and warm weather, even in the summer.

Remember to have a valid travel and health insurance.

What else...:
MRSA (bacterium that is resistant to most antibiotics) is a major problem in many countries, but not here in Norway. We want to keep it this way, and therefore I need to ask all workers to send a recent negative test result showing that you dont carry any MRSA bacteria. The test is taken at a doctor's office. If you test positive on MRSA, then unfor
_wwoof_travel: The farm is located on an island close to Ørnes, just above the arctic circle, and about 120 km south of the city Bodø. On the island there are only 12 residents, and closest living neighbor is about 3,5 km away. But in the summertime, there are summer gu

name: Robin - Small family owned organic farmstead with hi tunnels, located in beautiful Homer, Alaska
description:
We are a small family owned organic farmstead with two hi tunnels one is 20 x 92 feet the other is 16 x 20. We have the greatest good for woof help in April may. June July is project and maintained time. August is harvest and food storage. We are also developing small outdoor gardens for field crops.  The large tunnel and gardens  are being developed using permaculture principles including a small food forest with integrated chickens. Rare breed chickens are raised for eggs and breeding. Peak season is March - October.we also run a bed and breakfast hosting travelers from all over the world. We are expanding beyond inn keeping and creating yurts.  Our focus is providing loving tolerant community space for travelers from all over the world  we play music share food and play catan. We are located in Beautiful Homer Alaska, the beach, bay, and town are all walking distance. kayaking, hiking, sailing, music and arts are all here and accessible. 

There is a growing organic farm community and many woofers in the area. Accommodations are in the family home, with bay views and with family dinners, carpentry skills are appreciated. a couple is welcome long term agreements are available. 25 hours a week minimum is expected in return for housing and shared dinners, house chores are shared, beautiful view, beautiful place...firepit on site, hand drummers and musicians encouraged, slip cob builders would be celebrated!

I am willing to entertain the possibility of couples or pairs of friends with discussion first...call and we can talk...this is our sanctuary, no drugs alcohol on site. We are unapologetic nerd types.  With a drug and alcohol free home. 
25 hours a week; shared dinners and chores. Respectful and fun is wonderful  

Organic methods: Rotating crops,  ferments And teas for fertilizer 
Compost, cover crops, working chickens, plant community 

Certifications: Permacultirist organic not certified 

Current projects: Big renovations this year! 2021. On top of 2020 vill

name: Malcolm - Detox Retreat Centre with Organic Garden
description:
Hello WWOOFER, I am a retired airline pilot with a passion for healthy living and self sustainable farming practices. My Wife Christina and I founded the  Detox and Fasting retreat centre 2022, on our 24 hectre farm, located on the magnificant west coast of Sweden. We host Wellness Retreats for international and local guests. 

We have two cats that live with us and are an integral part of our family.  

We have a fully operational  8 metre green house and adjoining veggie garden that produces food for the Retreat Centre.

We transfer seedling from the heated main house into the green house around mid to end April. Seedling are placed into the garden beds a few weeks later. The green house and garden need daily love and care to ensure a successful production of food for the Centre. We also have a sprout house operating out of the Green house. 

There is plenty to see and do both here on the Farm and the surrounding penisual. The ocean is 3 km away (30 min by foot). We have two bicycles on the property available for use.  We enjoy food production, cooking, raw food/plant based.  I have a passion for herbal and natural medicine. We have an Ice bath and Sauna that we like to utilize along with Wim Hoff style breathing exercise's.  We enjoy meditation, Yoga, Hapey and exploring Spirituality.

There is much to see, do and explore on the nearby peninsula, with a few interesting and cute fishing villages nearby. Bus routes make travelling easy.

What kind of work are we mostly doing and what is needed around here?
We have a thriving greenhouse that is a source of great delight. In the garden, we are preparing the soil, working with compost, and bokashi, seeding, growing, weeding, and best of all the harvesting. We love to work as much as possible with vegan types of compost and mulch and implement different possibilities for creating long-term better soil and beds.

Who are we looking for?
Happy, healthy, open people who are into working with sustainable growing & living c

name: Charles "Chuck" - Creating a self sufficient organic farm and beginning a plant medicine retreat center.
description: Finca Medicina is 65 hectares/162 acres located in Olon, Ecuador. We are just under 6 miles/10 km inland from the beach of Olon. We are beginning to create an ecotourism and plant medicine retreat center which will also organically/biodynamiclly grow and produce all of our own food. When I say building , I mean creating from basically scratch. There is currently running water, a mineral spring fed swimming pool, a bathroom with flushing toilet, an outdoor shower, and a rustic cabin. We cook with a regular stove and oven and have a bbq. At this time there is no power or cell service/internet. We will be setting up some solar and have a small generator to run an hour or two in the evening for lights and charging. (Update 05/2026 solar panels and equipment just delivered. Also, starlink.) There are a few acres of banana and plátano, some yuca, papaya, mango, citrus, coconut palms, avocado, sugar cane, guava, etc. Someone will typically go to the market most every day and get proteins like fish, shrimp, chicken, and occasionally beef or pork ( at the moment this is by motorcycle so there is not usually an opportunity to take people, if so, no more than one person) Sleeping will be in tents or hammocks while we build Cabañas. (We now have a rustic house/cabin for guests. Also amazing tent sites and tents available. If you wish you can also bring a tent or hammock with a built in mosquito net. We are currently setting up a better showering area, setting up a kitchen, planting gardens, clearing and cleaning the land surrounding the cabin(s) and living area (mowing grass down and some brush) and building cabañas, building some pens for laying hens, goats, and a diary cow and acquiring the animals. Anyone who comes in the first stages will learn how to do pretty much everything you could need to do from scratch on your own place. With the food available people can more or less eat however they choose. Whether pescatarian, vegetarian, omnivore etc.  The property has both trop
_wwoof_travel: The nearest airport is in Guayaquil. Across the street from the airport is the bus terminal called the Terminal Terrestre there is a direct bus to Olon there called CLP upstairs on the 3rd floor above Gran aquí supermarket. It’s a 3 hour ride and $7

name: Eric - We raise whitetail deer
description:
Hello, my name is Eric. I’ve been in and out of the ranching world since I was a kid, and it’s a way of life I’ve always been drawn back to. Our place is located about six miles outside of Edgemont, South Dakota. Out here, we’re a good hour and a half from the nearest Walmart, but honestly—we like it that way. The quiet, the space, and the lifestyle suit us perfectly.

On the ranch, we raise whitetail deer, which means daily feeding and plenty of hands-on work. There’s always something that needs doing—whether it’s fixing fence, maintaining vehicles and equipment, or just the steady rhythm of day-to-day chores that keep everything running.

For those visiting, we have a comfortable lodge where you’ll be staying. You’ll have the option to prepare your own meals, or, if you’d prefer, you’re welcome to join us at the ranch house and share family-style meals.

I’ve always been an outdoorsman at heart. I love spending time watching wildlife, working with animals, and enjoying the simple beauty of ranch life. Outside of ranching, I enjoy shooting and traveling with my family whenever we get the chance.

name: Anita - Lavendrr, veggie and Meditation farm space in scenic countryside.
description:
Hi! I’m Anita, 51 and loving country life in rural Utah with my fantastic hubby/partner. We’re learning the ropes of conscientious land stewardship on our little 2.4-acre farm — dreaming of turning it into a thriving meditation retreat space and viable veggie and flower haven and farm!

Life on our little farm is a mix of laptops and dirt! I work part-time as an attorney  and meditation coach from home, so most of our planting, chorin' and dreaming happens in the evenings and from Thursday - Sunday. We're laid-back, still learning, and loving the journey of turning this patch of land into a magical community oriented space.

We eat well and both love to cook so you'll enjoy some gourmet food while you are a welcomed guest.  Nourishment is key, kiddos.  Have a pop-up A-Frame trailer, or 14' glamping tent for WWoofer's accommodations and we are building a large barn this summer.

We’re in the peaceful countryside of Milburn, Utah, just a few miles from the mouth of stunning Fairview Canyon. It’s quiet, starry, and blissfully far from highways and city noise. On your days off, you’ll find incredible adventures close by—Lakes, scenic mountains, Diamond Fork Hot Springs is just 45 minutes away for a relaxing soak, and Moab, Arches, and other epic Southern Utah landscapes are just a few hours down the road. Whether you love hiking, hot springs, or just soaking in mountain views, there’s plenty to explore from our little slice of paradise.

name: Hugo - Establecimiento agro vitícola con fines educativos y turístico
description:
Los anfitriones Griselda y Hugo les damos la bienvenida a nuestra pequeña finca escuela la cual compartimos con 3 perros y 2 gatos. 
Trabajamos desde 1994 como productores de uva y huerta orgánica.

Detalles de actividades:
Trabajos varios en huerta y en la uva dependiendo de la época del año.
Trabajo de mantenimiento general del establecimiento en tareas como limpieza de jardín, pintura, alimentar a los animales, cocina,  entre otras tareas similares.
Colaboración y ayuda de la creación de un museo de antigüedades.

Dependiendo de la temporada el horario de inicio de actividades variara por cuestiones climáticas.  
Un día en el establecimiento comienza con un buen desayuno. Luego se inician las actividades acordadas previamente durante la mañana y posteriormente finalizan al mediodía en el horario de la comida.

Me gusta enseñar a las personas que nos visitan sobre las actividades que desarrollamos cumpliendo tanto con las normativas ecológicas como así también respetando el entorno natural.
Somos un establecimiento orgánico certificado y recientemente obtuvimos autorización municipal para turismo rural.

La zona rural donde se localiza el establecimiento se encuentra cerca del cerro Pie de Palo lo que permite hacer actividades como senderismo. Tambien es posible visitar y realizar actividades en lugares cercanos como dique de ullum; bodegas; museos; granjas, tambos, etc.
_wwoof_travel: Terminal de autobus de Caucete, Aeropuerto de San Juan (UAQ)

name: Suzan - Farming our food whilst creating a beautiful forest.
description:
1. Beschrijf je project, je plek en de bewoners (inclusief dieren):
We, Peter and Suzan, have 10 hectares of land in the centre of beautiful Portugal right next to rio Mondego. We bought the land in 2019 and haven't regretted one second of moving here. We live a so called off the grid life with the comfort of internet, solar power, hot shower and toilet. Our veggie garden is producing, not as much as we would like, but we are getting there. We have 2 dogs who are guarding our 9 chickens and 4 geese during the day, and live in our house during the night. Our 5 cats keep the critters away and our chickens give us plenty of eggs.

2. Geef een lijst van de activiteiten waaraan WWOOFers zullen deelnemen en wat je hen wilt leren:
We are creating a mixed vegeteble garden, where everything grows, flowers, veggies and herbs.
On our land there are about two and a half million wildly growing mimosa trees which we want to turn into a forest. In order to do so we need you to help us cut and plant trees. (Diameter not more than 15 centimeter.) We have already done a lot, but there is still a lot to do. We find the work very rewarding as you can immediately see the results and it really makes a difference to the landscape.
Primarily we like help with a handsaw and different sizes of loppers. We also use brush cutters and different sizes of chainsaws and even a tractor with shredder to turn the cut down trees into mulch for the garden en to create pathways.
*To prevent the possible risk of insuries these powertools will only be handled by us. If your (travel)insurance covers it, we are happy to teach you how to savely handle powertools, but you can only handle them on your own risk and when we are around.

3. Hoe zou de dag van een WWOOFer er op jouw locatie uit kunnen zien (tijdspanne voor deelname aan activiteiten, verwachtingen...)?
In the summer we like to start at about 6 or 7 o'clock to get ahead of the heat, which means breakfast is even earlier, or we have a quick little bi
_wwoof_travel: You get of the train in Nelas when coming from the airport from Porto or Lisboa. We can pick you up from there. At the moment the trains aren't going to Nelas, in Coimbra you have to pick the bus to Nelas.

name: Max - Hobby Farm
description:
We live in a beautiful place on Vancouver Island, and we try to do what we can to work with nature.  Our farm is 2.5 acres and has all kinds of potential to produce more delicious organic food.  I am willing to work with WWOOFers to make this farm a little taste of paradise.  We have ocean view accomodation and the beach is only a 3.5 minute walk away. After work there is always the chance of farm based live music, and excursions for kayaking, mountain biking, hiking, and side trips to other local attractions such as Elk Falls Park Suspension Bridge, and just when you thought it couldn't get any better, there are no biting bugs around my farm, who wants to get disturbed by little vampires when you are trying to work or rest outside.  It will be my aim to make your stay here on my farm the best it can be.      

Organic methods: No pesticides or chemical fertizers are used on my plants, I do all I can to keep my plants as healthy as possible through organic composting and mulching, , and I believe they thank me come harvest time.  

Certifications: I am not certified organic, my property is relatively small and only functions as a hobby.

Current projects: Upgrading bluberry enclosure (soon, waiting for used materials, I like to recycle as much as I can). Planting at least two more fruit trees. New bridge to island on larger fish pond (preliminary stage). Weeding a current project?  It's more of a constant, ha ha.  Nice weather, hence weeds...fortunately, I am close to the ocean and receive free air conditioning, so one doesn't overheat while engaging those persistent weeds.  We're not averse to new ideas, this is a free speech zone.  

Internet access available

name: Miloš - Vineyard, Livestock Farm, Orchard, Mixed Farm, Family farm
description:
Vegetables and fruits are produced themselves.
We strive to create ecologically sustainable production in the garden we have. I am a member of Premakultur Community in Serbia. We devote a lot of time to the basket of grass and the preparation of feed for the animals.
  We have: chickens, pigs, sheep, cats and dogs. Water comes from a well, life on a rural farm is not only work in the garden, we have to maintain buildings, repair fences, prepare meals, winter storage, teas, juices, here we make wine and brandy. I also have my own forest where I provide firewood, but we also do afforestation and brush clearing.
Sometimes, depending on weather conditions, power outages may occur. Internet is provided. We have a lot of neighbors and they visit us very often. You will feel a very warm family atmosphere and enjoy good and healthy meals. We expect mutual respect and honesty. We are very simple and there is enough space for laughter and discussion. Your opinion is welcome.
  life on a rural farm is not just work in the garden, we have to maintain the buildings, repair fences, prepare meals, winter storage, teas, juices, here we make wine and brandy. I also have my own forest where I provide firewood, but we also do afforestation and brush clearing.

Organic methods: We feed the animals with the food we produce, the sheep go to our pastures and live freely, and so do the chickens. No chemicals are sprayed around the apiaries.

Certifications: We produce for our needs, and such production in our region is quite possible.

Current projects: Work in the apiary, barn, garden, during the summer we will collect fruit, collect hay, clean bushes, repair and erect fences, etc.

Internet access available

name: AINA - Centre d'accueil d'enfants errants et vulnérables/Champ-école
description:
Bonjour amis woofers,

Le Refuge d'Aina est situé à  Antetezambaro à une vingtaine de km de la ville de Tamatave à Madagascar.
Nous recherchons des woofers pour nous accompagner au quotidien avec notre équipe et les enfants recueillis au Refuge.  L'hébergement et la restauration sont gratuits contre service ou transfert de compétences au profit des bénéficiaires du Centre. 
Pour des raisons de sécurité sanitaire, les seuls frais demandés aux woofers sont l'achat de l'eau potable et les frais de participation pour le gaz (10 euros par semaine).

Créée en 2013, le Refuge d'Aina est un centre d'accueil permanent agréé avec 30 pensionnaires âgés de 3 à 15 ans qui comprend également :
- Une école primaire avec cantine scolaire de 88 enfants allant de la préscolaire à la CM2,
- Une ferme intégrée pour assurer l'autosuffisance alimentaire du Centre ainsi que la pérennité de la totalité du projet,
- Un site agroécologique à finalité de développement communautaire autonome en devenir.

Les bénéficiaires sont des enfants errants, orphelins, abandonnés, fruits de viol ou d'incestes et issus de familles vulnérables, et déscolarisés.

Les 4 piliers du projets sont les suivants :
1. Pensionnat (accueil et dignité humaine)
2. Site agroécologique (sensibilisation des bénéficiaires et viabilité du projet)
3. Education (développement humain)
4. Ferme intégrée (autonomie du projet et sécurité alimentaire)

Les activités sont les suivants :
- Activités (chants, jeux, travaux manuels, théâtre, ...) avec les enfants de l'école (de 3 à 15 ans)
- Soin des animaux de la ferme (porcherie, poulailler, ..)
- Soin des végétations et du potager
- Cuisine collective avec l'équipe du Refuge

Les équipes préparent les repas et mangent ensembles (cuisine collective au feu de bois)
Les woofers auront leur propre pavillon d'hébergement (chambre / cuisine / salle d'eau) et pourront s'ils le souhaitent préparer leurs propres repas

Loisirs : musique, chants, danse, cuisine, feux de camp ...
Le refuge ac

name: JennCaitlin - Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat, Horse Ranch, Yoga 🧘‍♀️, Orchard
description:
Ich bin Ärztin und „Hobby-Bäurin“ und genieße mein Leben in der Landidylle MeckPomms. Ich freue mich über Unterstützung im Garten, Obstgarten, bei der Altbaurenovierung, Zäune bauen, Pferde Versorgung, üblichen Hofarbeiten draußen und im Haus.

Badesee am Ort, Ostsee ça 40 Minuten, nächste grössere Stadt Rostock, nach Hamburg knapp 2 Std per Bahn/Auto, nach Berlin knapp 3Std.

Organic methods: alles ohne Gift

Type of work: 
Garten
Weidepflege
all about horses
gerne ausreiten 
Altbaurenovierung 
vegetarisch kochen

Internet access available

name: Dierk - Orchard
description:
Our place is a family farm in Puok, near Siem Reap and we mainly grow cashewnut and  mango trees. We use a chicken manure soup as fertilizer to improve the poor soil condition. For pest control we use vinegar and neem. You can help us in fruit picking, fruit pressing etc, general gardening, fruit tree care, watering trees and plants, soil preparation, going shopping on the local market, maybe some cooking when you like as well as playing  (football, sports, cards) also with our son. You can join AIKIDO with us.  Please get your own insurance.

Organic methods: We're mixing a chicken manure fertilizer soup and composting cashewnut tree leafs. Furthermore we use Neem leafs to make insecticide. 

Certifications: Organic Certification is not available in Cambodia

Current projects: Current projects are soil preparation, compost production, vegetable gardening, watering trees and waiting for the harvest season. 

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: We are located 20km outside of Siem Reap, in Puok. We can pick you up at the airport.

name: Magda - Apiary, agriculture, etc
description:
Hello woofers, we are a couple who have a apiary of about 300 bee hives. We have also greenhouse for tomato, cucumbers and peppers.
We have orchard of apricots and quinces.

We offer in the countryside, in the Big Island of Braila (Romania) the following:
1) -Beekeeping training (how to collect, extract honey, how to handle the bees, treat them, stimulate, care of the bees, melting of the beewax etc, depending on what is needed in the season of beekeping)
2)- Agriculture training : greenhouse for cucumbers, tomato, peppers), 
pruning of the orchard, etc
3)- Dehydration of tomato's, prunes and apricots, herbs etc
4)-Wood/log cutting and move/transportation.
5)-Vegan /Vegetarian cooking classes, even raw food.
We prepare the food together.
6)+/-Health lectures of lifestyle medicine and natural remedies.

For short-term: 2 weeks -1 month with possibility of extension.

I offer accomodation in the new house (large big rooms) with cozy cooking stove in the winter +/or RV or tent in the summer (if want more rustic than the house).

We offer therapies indirectly in a relaxed athmosphore, stress free, in the middle of the nature:
-Apitherapy: with Honey, Propolis, Polen, Bees Aerosol (for asthma, for which others pay 70 euro for 1/2 hr), 
-Heliotherapy (sun baths, tanning), 
-Hydrotherapy (in the Danube river, or sun warmed water in a bathtub in the garden) 
-Melotherapy (birds cirping), 
-Recognition and collection of medicinal plants. And much more...sky is the limit...

1 student/ volunteer at 1 time.

We eat vegan vegetarian

Nearby there is a Sulphur water thermal small pool with therapeutic properties
_wwoof_travel: Braila or Ploiesti (60 km from Bucharest)

name: Philippe - Ferme bio de culture de cacao et de transformation du cacao
description:
Nous sommes installés dans cette ferme depuis 2019. Nous sommes une grande famille d'une dizaine de personnes.
Ferme bio culture de cacao, poule pondeuse bio canard et cochon 
TRANSFORMATION DU CHOCOLAT ponctuellement parce que nous allons vraiment récolter pour la première fois en 2024. C'est le pays originaire du Chocolat. On récolte le chocolat toute l'année. Il y a des zones naturelles où on trouve des plants sauvages de cacao.

Les activités des WWOOFeurs :
Activité agricole de base  de la culture du cacao en milieu tropical je recherche aussi toute personne pouvant nous apporter son expérience en permaculture transformation du chocolat et autre 
récolte des œufs de poule et aussi couveuse électrique pour la naissance de poussin
La participation est le matin est libre l après midi pour découverte du milieux naturel autour de la plantation qui est entourée de foret primaire

Tout ce que offre l'Amazonie : randonnées pêche ballade  etc

name: Solomon - Eldo is an organic farm located in ELDORET and has its branches in kisumu.
description:
1.Eldo is a farm located in ELDORET and has got a branch in Kisumu here the main daily activity is to make land productive using the waste materials, AND NEVER PACKED MANURE OR HERBICIDES.
Eldo farm we don't just do mono-croping for the fertility of the soil we balance the types of crops to grow in each year..We use kitchen refuse as the best preferred manure and animal dung as the best fertilizer...To kill pest that has overstayed in our land we go the traditional way and this is how it's done; we aquire ashes from the cooking areas in large number, we had some water to dilute the ashes and then we filter the big and unwanted stones from it.. Lastly we do the application using hand sprays...
We also use bitter herbs for the persistent pests..
2.WWOOFers will participate in collections of animal dungs around the compound to locate them in a desired positions in the farm... interested persons will be tought on how to milk the animals that produces milk, cows and goats... interested woofers will also attend classes of learning our languages with there times off work.
3. We offer meals three times a day that is: Breakfast, lunch and super whereby the WWOOFers eat what they feel is comfortable with them...We also offer a single bedroom for woofers who travel alone and double bedroom for families and couples..Woofers can retire to bed anytime of there time after work.
4. Woofers should expect to be working for two house a day and be ready to work with the family members in the farm activity..
5. With woofers time of job there are historical sites around and places of hikes woofers can either go themselves or invite some one or two family members to accompany them.. making stories after every meal is the family hobby dancing music in the music all with granees is wat we enjoy.. drinking and smoking with friends and partying is what we are after when we are off job.

name: Laura Stefany - Granja ecológica, líder en proyectos de sostenibilidad.
description:
Hello, my name is Stefany, I really want to know and interact with different worlds. Currently I live with my animals and all the spirits of the mountain.

The last ones are our pets: three Pitbulls, two Creole dogs, a rooster, a hen and a chick.

Our vision of life is to sow seeds in the hearts of people who have the purpose of caring for the planet; as long as we remain in it. We know that we are transitory beings, but with immense responsibility not only with our own existence, but with the existence of all those who inhabit the same place.

In our farm you will find an ideal space to connect with nature, it is a place with a tropical climate, with an average temperature of 22° and a relative humidity of 50%, that is to say that you will be able to find a spectacular cloud forest phenomenon, we consider that It is really beautiful when occasionally the landscape turns a white that brings freshness to the mountain.

You will appreciate the song of innumerable birds, in addition to their beautiful shapes and colors. You will enjoy the food that the mountain itself produces, and that has been planted with all the love and care. (Banana, Plantain, Yuca, Orange, Lemon, Coffee, Aromatic)
You will find that the inhabitants are friendly, as well as being a safe place with adequate facilities to meet your basic needs. You will have good access to public and private transportation, in addition to having internet service to ensure communication with your loved ones.

The place offers you the opportunity to share experiences and knowledge around environmentally conscious practices, and with your physical and mental health, which means a significant contribution to the change and growth of all of us who live there. We are open to brainstorming, we want to share our desires and listen to your dreams, those purposes that move us and make us feel that we are here for a special reason.

We are a family business that connects and interacts with a community of people in search of n
_wwoof_travel: El Aeropuerto Internacional El Dorado (IATA: BOG, OACI: SKBO), más conocido como Aeropuerto de El Dorado, es el principal aeropuerto de Colombia.

name: Nancy Nathanya - If you are seeking a one of a kind learning opportunity, come to this off-grid goat dairy in NM 🐐
description:
- If you are seeking a one of a kind learning in living opportunity read below: 
-Come work and live with us to help co-create an off-grid oasis in the rugged, isolated mountains of New Mexico. We milk our bountiful goats to produce milk, cheese, yogurt, and custard for our own use! This fall we are getting less and less milk as the goats are pregnant . We will have lots of milk and baby goats starting the end of February. 

- Nancy has been living off-grid on the dairy for 40 years. She is a professional cheesemaker and was awarded the 2010 Organic Farmer of the Year by New Mexico Organic Commodities Commission. Nancy is in her 70s and enjoys sharing her lifelong knowledge of animals, living sustainably, and the world. 

- Accommodations include rustic, private, wood-burning cabins, step van, or bunkhouse. All food is provided; vegans and vegetarians will be accommodated thoughtfully. 

-There is limited internet and electricity on the dairy. All of the heating is with wood. The electricity is solar-powered. 

- Nancy and her husband Leroi run the dairy. Leroi is a great teacher with tools, locks, sewing, fixing broken infrastructure, and history. He is a loving and enthusiastic wise We are looking for folks to come at least a month but longer is ideal. 

- Spend time with the plethora of farm animals: free-range goats and their babies, Maremma guard dogs, chickens, and barn cats. 

- Love to hike and explore?! You can walk for hours, north of the Sawtooth Mountains and see no sign of other people! 

-We are 2 hours from Pie Town which is our mailing address and 4 hours from Albuquerque where pick up can be arranged. 

-WWOOFers are accepted year-round and we need help twice daily with chores, (letting the goats out to range, milking in season) as well as cooking, cleaning, wood splitting, and overall care. All WWOOFERS are weekly granted one day off from responsibilities. There is plenty of free time to create, learn, and explore independently or with others. 

-
_wwoof_travel: Grants New Mexico bus Albuquerque plane bus or train New Mexico

name: Bérenger - Pépinière modeste en agroforesterie
description:
Le but est d'entretenir un petit terrain et de produire/vendre des plantes,  perché sur une petite coline aux alentours de Saint-Laurent du Maroni.  Je suis installé depuis 3 ans. Vous serez hébergé dans un petit carbet (sorte de maison en bois, ouverte sur l'extérieur) se situant dans sur un terrain essentiellement constitué d'arbres fruitiers et de plantes pérennes, quelque peu organisé à partir des principes de la permaculture. 

Le terrain est situé à 15-20 minutes du "centre" de Saint Laurent, en voiture.  

Les activités possibles sont variées : plantation d'arbre ou de mise en place d'une zone maraîchère, entretient des arbres, amélioration du sol et nutrition des fruitiers ou des plantes en général, production de plans et entretien de la pépinière, taille et entretien des plantes de service, mais aussi parfois l'entretien de la piste d'accès, ce qui est le travail le plus amusant ... 
Cela peut concerner l'aménagement des espaces de travail comme l'atelier plante ou l'atelier bois, le travail du bois ou la conception de structures ou de meuble peuvent se faire aussi. 
bref... toute activité qui participe à la vie du lieu est possible, les idées de chacun sont bien sûr les bienvenues. 
 
Différents projets pourront mis en place dans les mois qui viennent : conception d'une cacaoraie, four à pain/pizza, plantation d'une zone en friche, mise en place d'une zone maraîchère ou pourquoi pas un projet construit et conduit ensemble.  

L'hébergement se fait en Hamac, dans le carbet. Une petit pièce du carbet est aménagé pour vous. Dormir sur un matelas est possible aussi, voir dans un lit. 

L'entretien du terrain, des arbres et la production de plants sont des activités secondaires puisque je partage mon temps avec mon métier salarié (prof)  et non salarié (éducateur sportif). 

N'hésitez pas à me contacter pour tout type de question. Pour ceux.elles qui arrivent en avion à Cayenne, il est possible également de vous faire acceuillir sur Cayenne. 

A bientôt.

name: Facu - Proyecto de permacultura en la Costa Uruguaya
description:
Hola! Somos Facu y Lala, buscando una vida mas sustentable y amable con el medio ambiente. Hace ya 7 años vivimos en el monte de La Esmeralda, donde compartimos el espacio con gallinas, conejos, perros y gatos. Aparte de una gran variedad de arboles y plantas.

En un dia normal nos ocupamos de los animales y plantas por la mañana principalmente, aprovechando las buenas horas para riego y luego organizamos alguna actividad que ayude a continuar nuestro proyecto. Las opciones son muy variadas, para todos los gustos y toda ayuda es bienvenida. Podemos enseñar a usar herramientas!

Actualmente contamos con un gran espacio para colocar carpas como asi tambien un quincho que funciona como espacio comun. El baño de momento es compartido dentro de la cabaña, pero tambien hay una ducha afuera que en verano puede usarse. 
Respecto de las comidas, brindamos siempre un buen desayuno antes de empezar las tareas y un almuerzo para compartir. Luego para el resto de las comidas pueden usarse distintos espacios.

Ambos somos personas de intereses diversos. Nos gusta cocinar y escuchar musica de todo tipo, tambien disfrutamos una buena proyeccion de peliculas bajo las estrellas.
Al estar cerca de la playa, este es uno de nuestro paseos favoritos para ver amaneceres y atardeceres, pero tambien saliendo del balneario, tenemos cerca la Reserva de Santa Teresa y la Laguna Negra, dos paseos que recomendamos muchisimo!

name: Sean - Orchard, Cidery, and Green Energy Homestead in SW Minnesota
description:
Blackshire Farms is host to a cidery, tasting room, multiple orchards, pumpkin patch, event hall and more! Nestled in the little town of Beaver Creek, our idyllic location boasts rolling hills, a pond, a historic one lane bridge, and a creek that meanders through the property.

A little about us: I studied horticulture at University and went on to become an organic squash farmer. After a couple other farm ventures and some moves including to California, I was given the opportunity to reclaim my family acreage as a full functioning farm. My partner Marcella is freelance writer and poet, working in college administration.  Since 2016, we have been working towards the goal of owning and operating a cidery.  In 2017 we planted our first orchard. In 2025 we opened our cidery! We press juice from trees in our orchards, and the orchards of our neighbors, producing traditional, craft hard cider.  

But our farm is so much more! We have a large plot of my great grandmother's raspberries. Mulberry trees and elderberry bushes abound.  We grow a pick your own pumpkin patch near The Cidery.   In addition, we partner with an El Salvadorian family to grow their native beans.

We are food preservation and foraging experts.  From canning to freezing, brining to smoking, we make some really amazing products!  We pride ourselves on making the best pickles you've ever had and our smoked trout wins lunch every time.  Our maple syrup is smoky from our open fire evaporation.  Spring and summer provides us with wild asparagus and many species of mushrooms, while fall brings harvest of honey and pumpkins!  

The sun is the ultimate energy source, so to respect that, we built a garage with a solar array that covers 85% of the farm needs.  

Guests can stay in a room in our unique turn-of-the-century farm home with us, or camp in our beautiful maple grove.  We are also just finishing a cabin to accommodate more privacy for future woofers. 

Area activites include hiking, swimming and kyaking

name: Nick - Organic farming systems incorporated fruits and vegetables, beekeeping , poultry and animals.
description:
Hello WWOOfers, I am Nick 40 years old, live with my partner Mercy and our two young boys in the farm. The other two boys are away in school. We live in Pap Onditi, Kisumu, Kenya. It's a semi-rural, peaceful and quiet environment. We do organic farming and our main focus is on beekeeping, fruits and vegetables, poultry and animals like sheep, rabbits, cows.
We have a dog called Bob and a cat.

Our farming activities include gardening, planting, weeding, watering, cleaning animals houses, maturing the garden, building and repairs, inspecting beehives/harvesting honey, feeding among others.
Some of these activities are done in the morning and others are done in the afternoon based on daily or weekly plans.
WWOOFers will be able to learn about our language( local and Kiswahili) our culture, different farming systems, some of the herbs....

We eat dinner, breakfast and lunch together as a family. We have specific houses as accommodation for the WWOOFers which are private and they can share when more than one.

I enjoy swimming, giving massage as a therapy, 
There is swimming pool nearby, a hiking, a walk and seeing around.

name: Hamid - Farmstay
description:
Hello I'm Hamid and my Amazigh family from Tagounite in southeast Morocco. Our family has lived in this desert region for generations, and we enjoy welcoming volunteers to share our daily life, culture, and the peaceful rhythm of the oasis.

We live in our family home in Tagounite and spend time in a traditional kasbah surrounded by palm trees. Our main focus is simple gardening work in the oasis. We take care of palm trees, water plants, clean the garden areas, and help maintain green spaces using traditional and natural methods adapted to the desert environment.

Volunteers help with gardening tasks such as watering, cleaning, planting, and general maintenance. No experience is needed—we explain everything step by step. We also live with friendly dogs and cats. Life here is simple, calm, and based on cultural exchange, nature, and learning from everyday desert life.

The work is mainly light gardening and daily care of the oasis. Volunteers help with watering plants and palm trees, cleaning garden areas, removing dry leaves, and general maintenance. Sometimes we plant small plants or help organize the green spaces. The work is calm and adapted to the desert climate. No experience is needed, and we always explain everything step by step.

A typical day starts in the morning with breakfast together. We usually work around 4 to 5 hours per day, mostly in the morning when it is cooler. After work, volunteers have free time to rest, read, walk in the village, or enjoy the oasis. Meals are shared together, and in the evening we relax, talk, and share stories about culture, life, and the desert. Life here is simple, peaceful, and based on exchange and respect.

WWOOFers stay in our family home in Tagounite or in the traditional kasbah, depending on the season and availability. Accommodation is simple and traditional, with shared rooms, mattresses, blankets, and access to a bathroom. The house is calm, clean, and part of everyday village life.

We provide three home-cooke
_wwoof_travel: CTM bus and Ozz airport ( ouarzazat)

name: Benjamín - Family farm
description:
We are a diverse family (5 at the moment, Benjamin, Mateo , Dani, Nahuel, and Caro) that live in a 1 hectare organic farm in Rio Seco, a coastal location nearby the city of Punta Arenas, Chile. We grow all sorts of vegetables in greenhouses and outdoors; considering the high latitude and harsh (but lovely) weather conditions we live in. We also raise animals such as pigs, or a cow depending on the season, about 60 chickens, and ducks, plus two dogs and two cats! 

We produce our own organic fertilizers, which is the main core of our farm fertility, and manage pest control with ecological and low environmental impact technologies. Our main goal as a farm is to contribute to our local food sovereignty through the ways of agroecology, sharing experiences, and learning from each other, together with practicing a healthier lifestyle.

Organic methods: Compost, bokashi, biopreparados, control biológico, biodiversidad, rotación de cultivos.

Certifications: No existe un agente de certificación local en Magallanes

Internet access available

name: Timothy - Flower and blueberry farm 24 miles north of Charlotte, North Carolina
description:
We are a flower and blueberry farm 24 miles north of Charlotte, North Carolina. The property is 36 acres, and we farm on 5 acres. The farm is in a hilly area with a spring fed creek.  Tim and Lis have been farming for 12 years and it is our full-time job. We grow flowers year-round in hoop houses and in fields using row covers. We don't use synthetic fertilizers, pesticides, or herbicides. It is important to us to not poison the land or us! 

Our family includes Charlie the lab and Socks the cat.

We are a production flower farm  and are looking for a Woofer to help with farm chores and projects. You will gain meaningful farm experience including planning, planting, seeding, harvesting, bed prep, and marketing/prep for our local farmer's market. Hours vary based on time of the year and in the summer we start early because of the weather/heat. We ask for 25 hours a week. If you are in the middle of a chore and there is clean-up to do - you are expected to finish, put away tools and sweep floors (if needed). Attending the farmer's market is extra hours. and not required. We have had a couple Wwoofer's that counted every hour they worked.

You will be living in a tiny home with a twin bed. There is a full bathroom, but no kitchen. Meals are included and we are not vegetarians (but can adapt to vegetarian or vegan diets). We will cook lunch together one or two times a week and there are miscellaneous clean-up and sweeping chores.

Charlotte is a lovely town, and we are 1 ½ hours from Asheville and the mountains. Take the time to explore the area on the weekend.

We welcome long-term stays with the possibility of joining our staff or earning a stipend.

name: Linda - Rehabilitate pond & orchard, resourceful solutions, maintain landscape
description:
We are 2 hours from Toronto near Collingwood & Georgian Bay with 125 acres of forests, apple orchard, fields, trails alongside the stream. Animals we have here are 9 Icelandic horses & 1 retired reindeer. Gardens consist of apple, pear, and plum trees, annual vegetables, and integrating perennials in a food-forest garden manner.  We are a family of grandparents with son & fiance running the business, you will also be accompanied by staff and other guests on property. 

Healthy help, with self-starter attitude is always needed for daily animal chores, and outdoor maintenance (landscaping, grass & snow) and housekeeping duties. Must be able to work outdoors in all weather for daily chores, other projects will be tailored for weather. 
Ongoing projects include trail/forest maintenance, fence building, bringing in Hay to the barn, garden creations with permaculture twist, pond maintenance, forest gardening. Experience/skills with mechanics, carpentry or chainsaws would be a welcome bonus! We are open all-season and alongside the daily chores brings unique projects for each season.

A picture of the seasons:
Winter: up-keep with snow removal on walkways, cleaning indoors, Christmas decorations and crafts, using the summer harvest creatively. Into early Spring brings maple syrup production (conditional upon help received and time). 
Spring: garden clean-up, prepping soil for planting, pruning trees, starting seedlings. Planting seeds in the ground, Apple orchard taming.
Summer: [Top Bar biodynamic beekeeping is tentative upon available time, Raising pigs and chickens for meat and eggs-not for 2026] . Flower bed maintenance, vegetable garden harvesting and weeding. Established lavender rows to be maintained and flowers harvested. Maintaining pool and outdoor furniture. 
Autumn: harvest apples and pears for baking and cider; tree pruning. Trail maintenance. Gardens to bed. Chopping wood for our fireplaces. Seed-saving.

We were a Natural Horsemanship facility having done (E

name: Hugh - Community Supported Agriculture (CSA), Education Centre &  Community Benefit Society (31409R).
description:
Our Farm is wholly owned and managed by  a Community Enterprises Limited, a charitable Community Benefit Society (31409R). We are a 36-acre farm, located ten minutes’ walk across a lovely footpath from the small busy town of St Just in West Cornwall, we are four miles from Lands’ End and one mile from the coast.  

Visit our website here: https://www.bosaverncommunityfarm.org.uk
Check our our flipbook for an overview of our farm:  https://online.fliphtml5.com/kakjx/xlzp/#p=16 

LOCAL ATTRACTIONS
There are lots of ancient settlements, stone circles, stunning coastal walks and famous surf beaches nearby, as well as an array of cultural attractions including galleries, museums, theatres, community festivals (Golowan https://golowanfestival.org/ in Penzance during June & Lafrowda festival https://www.lafrowda.co.uk/ in St Just in July), a sports centre, film nights, markets, Cornish folk dancing, music sessions and open mic nights.  

ACCOMMODATION & SUBSISTENCE
Accommodation is provided in our farmhouse where you will be living communally with other WWOOF guests from across the world for the duration of your visit. The house sleeps a maximum of ten people. There are 5 bedrooms, 2 of which are shared dormitories. There is a communal kitchen equipped with a washing machine, a cooker and a rayburn; there is a communal lounge with a log burner, TV and DVDs; internet access and a communal bathroom with hot shower. There are also three compost toilets elsewhere on site. As a working farmhouse in a rural area it is clean and functional but it is old and does need looking after. 

We provide your utilities and all your food basics (except for meat, fish, alcohol and luxury items which you are welcome to buy for yourself), with most of the fresh veg and eggs produced here on the farm. If you have any dietary requirements, or anything that may affect your visit to our farm, please let us know before arrival so we can make reasonable adjustments for you in advance.  

The provisi
_wwoof_travel: Bus number 17 from Penzance railway station. From the bus stop in St. Just we are a ten minute walk through town and across the fields. We will email directions of how to walk to the farm.

name: Le Mallouestan - Écolieu écocentriste et vegan
description:
Salut! Nous sommes Lau, Bastien, Antoine, Juliette et Cortney aux alentours de la trentaine et vivons au Mallouestan. C'est un écolieu qui accueille toute l'année des personnes en visite qui découvrent le lieu, nous donnent des coups de mains ou assistent à nos événements. Le projet est de développer un lieu solidaire envers les humain-es et plus largement le vivant. Nous travaillons quotidiennement dans une optique de gagner en autonomie vis à vis du système dominant (nous nourrir, produire notre bois, savoir construire, faire de l'artisanat etc.) Nous avons un mode de vie peu commun qui s'est défait de certains aspects du confort moderne (peu d'électricité, pas de machines électriques, pas de gaz etc).
Nous vivons avec 2 chiennes, Taga et Izzy, 2 chat-tes Suika et Muta ainsi que d'autres animaux dits "de ferme" que nous avons accueillis dans un contexte de refuge.

Tous les jours, nous nous organisons collectivement avec des tableaux pour se répartir les tâches du quotidien (vaisselle, balais, cuisine etc ) et les soins aux animaux. Il y a quatres jours dans la semaine dédiés à des chantiers divers (construction, jardin, entretien des espaces etc.) que nous décidons lors de la réunion du samedi en fonction de nos objectifs. Tous les matins des journées chantiers nous faisons un peu d'entretien dans le jardin.
Les trois autres journées sont dédiées à des temps plus calmes et des tâches d'entretien du lieu qui ne sont pas quotidiennes (nettoyer la salle de bain, laver le linge etc.)

Nous disposons de chambres partagées pour les personnes en visite et disposons de draps si besoin.
Toute la nourriture sur le lieu doit être végan. Il est interdit par notre charte de consommer des produits non-végan sur lieu.
Le petit-déjeuner est en libre-service (avoine, compote, pain, confiture, tisane)
Les repas du midi et du soir sont souvent collectifs mais n'ont pas obligation à l'être.
La cuisine se fait le soir par la ou les personnes inscrites.

Au Mallouestan on aime expérim

name: Grunde - Herrgård med ört, frukt och grönsaksodling
description:
I’m a gardener at Notsjö Mansion, with a focus on permaculture and herbs. The garden at Notsjö is uniquely designed as an ornamental space that combines edible, nutritious plants with aesthetic and medicinal flowers, all growing in symbiosis.

The garden is still in development. As part of a larger restoration project of the mansion and its cultural heritage, we welcome WWOOFers to join us in creating this one-of-a-kind garden concept.

Cultivating edible plants, bushes and fruit trees in a permacultural concept. Mov the lawn. weeding. Watering and composting. Further develop, design and build the garden.

WWOOFers are welcome to stay in the 18th-century mansion, which has been carefully restored in keeping with the style of its time. The historic building offers simple countryside living—with its charms and challenges—but also includes modern amenities such as running water, heating, and bathrooms, all of which have been thoughtfully integrated into the mansion. 

We provide breakfast at the mansion, lunch at the restaurant, and an evening meal back at the mansion.

I'm a Norwegian gardener and designer with a broad interest in culture. In my free time, I devote myself to art, music, natural and cultural heritage—and, last but not least, to socializing with family, friends, and anyone I meet along the way.

name: Maria - Family farm
description:
Tenemos una finca de 7 hectáreas, 5 de ellas son olivar de montaña, media hectárea es de huerta y el resto es monte, que forma parte del.parque natural de Cazorla Segura y las Villas.
Además tenemos almendros, Granados e higueras y algunos frutales recién plantados.
Convivimos con gastos, perros, una yegua y variedad de aves.

Llevamos 20 años en la finca y hemos recuperado un antiguo cortijo de principio del siglo pasado. Aún nos quedan cuadras que arreglar.

Actualmente tenemos una panadería ecológica y tienda de productos orgánicos en Cazorla, por lo que pasamos parte del tiempo en el pueblo. Además nuestros hijos van creciendo y es más sencillo así.
Necesitamos ayuda en el cuidado de animales, huerta y olivar, así como en avanzar con las obras, pero sería interesante que fueran personas a las que les guste pasar parte del tiempo solas, aunque nosotros estamos siempre pendientes de la finca y los animales.

Certifications: certificado ecológico 

Current projects: 
Necesitamos ayuda continua en la huerta y el olivar, así como los animales.
Estamos iniciando o por iniciar obra en las cuadras y el el jardín
Estamos poniendo en marcha la introducción de otros cultivos distintos del olivar.

Internet access available

name: Carmen - Ecodome Resort, boarding kennel & animal sanctuary for goats,sheep, kune kune pigs and chickens.
description:
Hello
We are Darren and Carmen.  We live on a private 5-acre lake front property just outside of Flin Flon Manitoba Canada.   We are new to farming and just started a small animal sanctuary.  We have a dozen goats, 2 sheep, 10 kune kune pigs, bunnies, chickens, and ducks.   We have 5 dogs and 7 cats!  You must love animals here!  We have a boarding kennel for dogs on our property as well as a beautiful 4 season eco dome we rent out.  We have goat walks  on the weekends, and they have become extremely popular!  We also have a small eco boutique on our property.  We have a beautiful barn to house our animals.
We have a small personal garden on our property we use for ourselves and our animals. 
We are 10 minutes from town.  Flin Flon is a small northern mining town with a cool culture and lots of interesting opportunities to join in on.

An average day is barn chores, walking the boarding dogs, yard work, special projects that are usually for the animals, and caring for the animals and taking care of the ecodome.  Our animals are our pets and we spend a lot of time with them.  
Going on goat walks with the public and educating them on the animals.
House chores. Cooking meals.
We are 420 friendly, we do not smoke cigarettes and only drink socially occasionally.

We do our own thing for breakfast and lunch time.  For dinners we eat together.  We enjoy downtime and relaxing just before bed with movies and snacks.
We have a nicely decorated and comfortable guest bedroom with a queen size bed for you.  You also have a guest bathroom that is yours to use while here.
I very much want you to have a special experience while visiting us, and I try my best to find activities you would be interested in while here.
In the summer, spring, and fall, we have kayaks and canoes for you to enjoy as we live on a lake.  In the winter we have snowshoes for you to enjoy as we have lots of wonderful trails in our area.
My hobbies are my animals, and I am very busy with my work around the pro
_wwoof_travel: WE ARE A REMOTE COMMUNITY.  Flin Flon  Manitoba has an airport but it is expensive to fly.  ( Winnipeg Manitoba or Saskatoon Saskatchewan are the closest major airports. We would need to plan pick up.  Bus service now available as well.

name: Aoife - Croft by the sea with native sheep, felting and berry orchard
description:
1. We are a husband and wife with a nine year old child. We have a Croft by the coast on the Isle of Skye. Most of the land is steeply sloping to the sea and is all permanent species-rich grassland. We keep around 100-130 Hebridean sheep for both their meat and wool. We sell directly to individuals and restaurants, so we can ensure the highest traceability and welfare standards for our animals and our customers. We use the best fleeces each year to produce felted items including rugs, tea cosies and nesting pods for birds. We also keep some hens for eggs for ourselves and have a working collie and pet Labrador, as well as two cats.

In 2023 we took on the care of over 600 well established blueberry bushes in our local village and we are looking forward to adding another string to our bow and ensuring this amazing orchard can remain productive. We also have a polycrub where we are trying to increase our self-grown food. This allows us to grow plants that would not normally survive well on Skye. 

Our main aims for the Croft are improving the plant biodiversity and maintaining the highest welfare standards for our animals. 

We are a pretty relaxed household, who enjoy spending time in the outdoors, curling up and watching films and cooking and eating good food. We eat both vegetarian and meaty food with toast/ porridge/ cereal breakfast, soup/ sandwich/ wrap type lunch and a hearty hot dinner. 

2. Visiting WWOOFers (depending on time of year) will help with feeding the animals, chopping firewood, maintaining fences, processing fleeces and maintaining blueberry bushes. We are also participants in a pilot study on the future of environmental support in agriculture in Scotland, so in the summer months undertake plant surveys on all our land. We would be very happy to help pass on our (modest but increasing) knowledge of local plants. 

3. A typical day could include feeding the animals, chopping firewood and pruning blueberry bushes or weeding in the garden/ polycrub.

name: Ingunn Rivertz - Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Family farm
description:
Hei !
Here is Ingunn and Mathieu. We are a Norwegian-French couple and since mai 2021 a happy family of three.

Our family house "Karnes Gaard" is a farm since the 16th century. In 1912, Ingunn's grand father gave part of the land for the Tromsø county to build "Solhov", prestigious boarding school and largest wooden building north of Trondheim.
When Ingunn took over the farm in 2009, running it organically felt something obvious. And when Solhov was for sale the next year, a dream became possible : sharing our love for the Lyngen Alps by hosting guests who seek breathtaking surroundings, healthy stay and organic local food, peaceful lifestyle and carbon-free activities.

Our dream is to serve as much as possible our organic and local products to these guests. 

Originally we grow potatoes and strawberries. We are trying several small scale production to see what is possible to grow here up north. At the moment, we are focusing in :
- increasing our honeybees park, hives and flowers 
- building a chicken coop
- 2-300 sqm building for the sawmill
- maybe to create a goat pen since our baby boy seems to really like goat cheese.

Everything here is weather/season dependent and you can read more about our global schedule/organisation in the "opportunity=>current projects" section

With the short summer, organic farming in the Arctic is quite challenging.
As is the administrative work to change the use of a 3000sqm building from a school to a hostel.
As is the intense skiing season with guests (march april).
As is taking care of all these old wooden buildings in the farm and the school. This is especially important after the rude winter, painting and fixing roofs are necessary to protect the timber. 
Challenging but so much rewarding, and with your help we will manage these interconnected projects.

Sami people says we have 8 seasons up north, and the farming activity last from the spring (end of april with seeding) to the harvesting season (beginning of september). Sinc

name: James - Family home and developing organic food forest and garden
description:
We have a 10.000m2 property on the magical island of Kythera, between the mainland and Crete. Our home overlooks the straight between Kythera and the mainland, with a young 35-tree food forest and the first efforts in planting a vegetable garden.  We have two ginger kittens and a lovely dog called Jojo and 3 ponds with frogs and lots of water and room and ideas for so much.

WHAT WE NEED: 
We really need people with some experience in landscaping, farming, carpentry, building and perhaps mural painting or mosaics, so people with skills like that have a definite advantage. But also help keeping the kitchen tidy, gardening, tree planting, painting, pruning, sharing the cooking, and entertaining the pets is part of the day as well. Five hours of help a day, 5 days a week on average would be expected for your room and board. If you are a nerd (like James), there is always some new project like evaporative solar desalination or using a pool heat-pump to heat the floors. James is even working on getting the island to net zero by 2028, and is currently programming a fire-safety volunteer app for the island. In your ample free time: this is the perfect place to be creative. Write a book or a screenplay, practice the visual arts, discover the island, and much more. We are both from the creative industries – filmmaking, book authors, designers, etc., so we are always working on something fun. And the island is full of creative, interesting people from all over the world. 

MEALS:
The fridge is always full of good things, and we take turns cooking. If you tell us what you like to eat we will make sure we have it (within reason). It would be good if you also enjoy cooking. 

YOUR DAY:
We are flexible about working times, but if you need a schedule, 10 am until 3pm, five days a week will work for us. Cooking and tidying up we all do every day of course. Depending on current projects  you would help one of us or do the job yourself. We also have a dam to maintain, and if you like
_wwoof_travel: The airport is 25 minutes drive away.

name: Ludivine - Élevage poule pondeuse, jument M, maraîchage biologique, agroforesterie cacao cafe vanille
description:
Sur le domaine, il y a de la polyculture et de l’élevage, principalement des poules pondeuses avec la production d’œuf, canard cochon et il y a deux Jument. du Maréchal en Bio, une petite surface. Et la création dans l’espace d’agro foresterie, plantation de café cacao Vanille
Il y a une rivière et un sous-bois que nous sommes en train de rendre cultivables.

Principalement, il y a le soin aux animaux, leurs alimentation , l’entretien de leurs habitat, la ’plantation de nouvelles surfaces, le désherbage
Il y a la possibilité de monter les juments et aussi d’accéder à la rivière

Il y a l’étage  du labo de conditionnement 
Qui offre une chambre et un espace personnel distinct de l’habitation principale 
C’est une propriété familiale avec chiens et chat 
Le repas est commun avec la famille et les grands parents

À proximité des randonnées d’une distillerie, d’une cascade très connu 
Dans le nord de l’île de basse terre

name: Lone - Northern Roots Gardens are home of Transform Your Yard into a Food Garden Mentorship program.
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
We live and work on Chief Drygeese Territory, treaty 8, the traditional land of the Yellowknives Dene First Nation.  Northern Roots has a respectful relationship with the land and its people. 
Our gardens provide organic vegetables for customers, for self-sufficiency and for making value added products (sauerkrauts, jams, jellies, pickled beets) . The gardens are also home to garden mentorship programs.  Each growing season from May to October visitors from schools, universities and the public come to learn more about how to grow vegetables. 
The garden mentorships and agricultural consulting keep us busy year around. We work with Indigenous communities’ local food skills through our Agriculture Training and Mentorship  programs. School gardening (including indoor gardening in winter in schools) also run year around. 
In 2026 my grandson wants to do more flower farming. I am sure he would love your help making bouquets for selling in his road side stand! 
We currently do not have any farm animals but may build a chicken coop in 2026! 
In 2025 we built a new garden and had the project filmed. Here is a 35 minute video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=goOEOXgt9BU
We were previously WWOOF host from 2012 to 2015 and it was a really good experience.  

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
During the long sunny days of spring and summer the vegetables will grow with great speed and intensity here at latitude 62.  
As a WWOOFER  you’ll be learning about our unique climate and the details of growing the most amazing vegetables on the planet. You’ll be working alongside me to  enhance soil health, seeding,  plant in spring and caring for food plants. We will go foraging on the land at certain times during the summer. You will also be assisting with workshops and garden events at schools, youth programs and adult programs. We may also go to an In
_wwoof_travel: There’s a domestic airport in Yellowknife

name: Jane - Family run high tunnel focused on local food along with harvesting firewood
description:
2026 is our first year accepting WWOOFers! We are a married couple living on 40 acres on the Kenai Peninsula in Alaska. We grow fresh produce and manage firewood as part of our land based lifestyle. All of our children are grown, with one 20 year old still living at home. Our place is shared with some chickens, a few small dogs and 2 cats.

On our 40-acre homestead on the Kenai Peninsula, our days follow the seasons and the needs of the land. In the high tunnel we plant, tend, and harvest tomatoes, zucchini, and other seasonal vegetables. Work also includes watering, pruning, weeding, pest monitoring, and keeping the growing space clean and productive. Outside the tunnel, we manage chickens—feeding, collecting eggs, maintaining coops—and we cut, split, and stack firewood for home and local use.

We can teach WOOFers hands-on skills in high tunnel growing, seed starting, transplanting, irrigation setup, basic plant care, harvesting, food handling, and building fences (to keep the moose out). Depending on the season, there may also be opportunities to learn about soil building, composting, greenhouse management, butchering moose, canning meats, fish or veggies, processing fish, and simple homestead maintenance. With the chickens, you can learn daily animal care, coop cleaning, and flock management. Firewood work includes safe tool use, splitting wood, stacking for proper drying, and understanding how wood is used as part of a semi-self-sufficient lifestyle.

An average day usually starts with morning chores like checking the greenhouse, watering, and tending animals. Midday is often focused on planting, weeding, harvesting, or larger projects like wood cutting or infrastructure upkeep. Afternoons may include finishing field tasks, cleaning, processing produce, or preparing beds for the next planting cycle. Days are active but flexible, shaped by weather and season, with a strong emphasis on practical skills, steady work, and learning by doing in an Alaska homestead se

name: Franck - Production maraîchère & fruitière certifiée Bio Pasifika
description:
Bonjour, 
Je m'appelle Franck, j'ai 41 ans et je suis installé en agriculture biologique depuis 2012. Je produis du maraîchage diversifié, des bananes, et de l'ail depuis peu. Toute la production est certifiée Bio Pasifika et je commercialise mes produits via une coopérative. Je travaille avec mon père avec qui je suis associé, et une employée. Ma mère, ma compagne et parfois même mes enfants, qui sont présents un week-end sur deux et pendant les vacances, participent aussi volontairement aux activités passionnantes de la ferme ! La pépinière pour l'autoproduction de  semences est située chez mon père au village de La Foa. Je vis avec ma compagne à Focola, commune de Farino. Ma compagne est ingénieure agronome,  gérante de bureau d'études, et sera ravie d'échanger aussi avec les wwoofers. Nous avons un chat et deux chiens. Les champs se situent à 5 min de la maison (bananes) et 25 min (maraîchage) en voiture.

En général, nous partons ensemble de la maison à 6h45 vers les champs et nous y restons la journée, en prévoyant un pique nique. Nous prenons une longue pause déjeuner sur place. Nous allons ensuite déposer les récoltes chez le transporteur en vue de la livraison à Nouméa, et nous rentrons à la maison pour 17h15. L'organisation est faite avec chaque wwoofer selon ses envies : la participation peut être de deux ou trois journées complètes par semaine, dont le lundi et le jeudi. Les tâches sont variées et peuvent être : récolte dans la serre ou dans les champs, nettoyage des fruits et légumes après récolte, semis en pépinière, plantation de maraîchage, désherbage des champs, etc...  

Nous vivons avec ma compagne dans une maison avec jardin. Le wwoofer aura un bungalow indépendant avec un lit 2 places, une petite étagère, une douche et des WC.
