Ma compagne se charge la plupart du temps de la préparation des repas du soirs et des piques niques du midi. Un coup de main en cuisine est bienvenu, étant donné qu'elle travaille aussi à temps plein. 
Nous consommons sur

name: Rafael - Horse and cow ranch experience in Laguna Llancanelo, Argentina
description:
Hello My name is Rafael I am 52 years old, I own a property of more than 15000 HA, I am dedicated to livestock in Laguna Llancanelo area, Malargüe, Mendoza, Argentina ... I also own an equine reserve with more than 400 horses that I take care of in the wild and more than 1000 cows.
We make horse taming and cow breeding.
Also rural tourism and bird watching.

Hola Mi nombre es Rafael tengo 52 años, soy dueño de una propiedad de mas de 15000 HA, me dedico a la ganadería en zona Laguna Llancanelo, Malargüe, Mendoza, Argentina...en mi propiedad cuido mas de 400 caballos en estado salvaje y mas de 1000 vacas.
Realizamos amanse de caballos y crianza de vacas.
También turismo rural y avistaje de aves.

After breakfast we start with the tasks of each day, which are usually moving cows with horses, repairing fences, visiting cattle pastures, taming wild horses, etc.
We also cook, clean and garden the place.

Luego del desayuno comenzamos con las tareas de cada día, las que suelen ser mover vacas con caballos, reparar alambrados, visitar aguadas del ganado , amansamos caballos salvajes, etc
También cocinamos, limpiamos y jardineria del Lugar.

We provide breakfast, lunch, snack and dinner
The meat is organic from our production.
We accept vegetarians.

Proveemos desayunos, almuerzo, merienda y cena
Las carnes son orgánicas de nuestra producción.
Aceptamos Vegetarianos

I tame horses, took care of more than 1000 cows, more than 400 horses and more than 200 donkeys. I also like to do yoga and meditate. 
I receive and attend tourists who do photographic safaris with wild horses and bird watching, more than 170 species surround our house.
Cow herding is also an available experience.


Amanso caballos, cuido mas de 1000 vacas, mas de 400 caballos y mas de 200 burros. También me gusta hacer yoga y meditar. 
Recibo  y atiendo turistas quienes hacen safaris fotográficos con los caballos salvajes y  avistaje de aves, mas de 170 especies rodean nuestra casa.
Arreos de vacas también es

name: Monica Tatiana - Entre la Sierra de Guara y la Hoya está Tierra Media en sus primeros pasos.
description:
Tierra Media es una finca de 2 hectáreas, una parte está dedicada a la apicultura, la otra tiene una huerta, arboles frutales y naturaleza salvaje.
Nuestra idea es organizar un sistema integral en Permacultura, pero de momento no vivimos en la tierra así que habitamos a tres kms de la misma, mientras estamos reformando una casita ya existente. 
Estamos mejorando y ampliando nuestra primera huerta, proyectando y realizando un sistema de recolecta del agua entre otras cosas.
Luis y yo, Mónica, somos los anfitriones, hemos hecho de nuestras pasiones nuestros trabajos: 
Mónica es danza terapeuta y  monitora de pilates, Luis es periodista y apicultor. Ambos hemos estudiado permacultura y continuamos aprendiendo a través de nuestra finca. 

Posibles actividades en las que participarán los WWOOFers:
- mantenimiento y ampliación de la huerta: organización y construcción de nuevos bancales, plantar hortalizas,
- construcción de un baño seco,
- ayudar en la reforma de la casita,
- hacer muros de piedras,
- realizar swales y balsas de agua,
- ayuda en la poda y para plantar nuevos árboles.

¿Cómo sería el día de un WWOOFer en nuestro lugar?
En los meses de invierno intentaremos aprovechar las horas de luz, evitando las horas mas frías, así que bajaremos a la finca por las 9,30 - 10, pero el horario puede variar según las tareas del periodo.
En los meses de verano intentaremos trabajar en la finca pronto por la mañana o tarde por la tarde, el horario puede variar según de las tareas y de lo que quiere hacer el Woofer en su tiempo libre.

Nuestros propios intereses y las posibilidades de los alrededores:

nos encanta pasear por la Sierra de Guara: cerca de la finca hay caminos para descubrir, sitios donde pasear, pozas en que bañarse, lugares donde hacer barranquismo, escalada y senderismo.

name: Karim - Désert, Nature & Partage à Olecactus
description:
We are a family-run organic farm in southern Morocco, passionate about sustainable farming and promoting local culture. Our farm, Olecactus, is located in a peaceful rural area where we cultivate olives, prickly pear, pomegranates, and aromatic herbs. We use traditional methods with a focus on environmental sustainability, and we warmly welcome volunteers to help us in our agricultural activities while learning about Moroccan heritage.

At Olecactus, volunteers will help with various farm activities, including planting, harvesting, and maintaining our olive trees, prickly pear cactus, and aromatic herbs. Other tasks may include building with natural materials, composting, 

“We provide our visitors with authentic Moroccan meals made from fresh, locally-grown ingredients. Meals include traditional dishes such as couscous, tagine, and freshly baked bread, all prepared with organic produce from our farm. We also offer seasonal fruits, olives, and freshly made herbal teas. Our goal is to offer a genuine taste of Moroccan culture while maintaining sustainability in everything we do.”

In addition to farm activities, we offer plenty of leisure opportunities to relax and enjoy the surroundings. Volunteers can take part in evening walks through the scenic countryside, relax by the campfire, or enjoy stargazing under the clear desert sky. We also encourage visitors to explore Moroccan culture through traditional music, storytelling, and cooking workshops. It’s a great chance to unwind and connect with nature and local traditions.

name: Gudny Halla - Mixed Farm, Family farm
description:
Hello my name is Gudny Halla Gunnlaugdottir  I live in the south of  Iceland  surrounding with amazing nature.
I´m married and we have 4 children,   3 boys 1 girl ,  
4 grandchildren.  Our youngest son 22 and he is at the moment living with us working away.  Our oldest son is 40 and lives with his family in Sweden. 
Our daughter lives in next town with her family. 
Our second youngest son lives in north.

I have  Studied nature Medicine and healing.  I love to practice and teach it but the time is not always there because this is a milking  farm with a lot to do each day by the cows.  

I have been breading Icelandic sheepdog  fore 30 years   were they were  in danger of dying out only 250 dogs left in the world.  Dogs from our Gerplu kennel can be found in many country's in the world. 
 Both of us have been grown up with Icelandic horses so it was only natural to start breeding them  and riding tge ponys are our hobbyes .   
But this breeding are on hobby level. 




Organic methods: Me and my husband Gudmundur 'olafsson have an organic dairy farm with around 50 Icelandic milking cows and cattle  in all age are around 50.  We milk morning and evening all days all year around.  We also have farmland were we make our organic hey and corn.  
The most help we  need on the farm including cleaning stables houses  and milking  the cows.   We need help in   household  etc. because we got  more tasks on  the farm.  like chickens, Icelandic  horses and Icelandic Sheepdogs breed from Gerplu Kennel. they are sold world wide,  

I also have company named Fridarmidstodin ehf ( Peacecenter).
The goal of the Peace center is:
Nature protection 
Personal guidance
Work creation in the spirit of organic and self supporting ideal.


Certifications: The Farm Buland is certified as organic  dairy farm  , by Vottunnarstofan Tun

Current projects:  
We like to have  that want to take care of cows and Milk with us.   
 Painting , cleaning the stables,  Help with hey,  renovation,  fences ,

name: Diane - Maison familiale et jardin - rural
description:
Comme on peut le lire dans le catalogue wwooff, ici, on dort dans une petite cabane ou plus loin dans un tipi, toilette à compost et douche au camping municipal. Il y a une petite roulotte, tout équipée pour les déjeuners et soupers des woofers, je sers les dîners Comme travail jardinage :on produit d'une façon artisanale des légumes racines et des laitues , il y a 2 petites serres  et de l'amanagement paysager , séchage de plantes pour tisanes et préparation de teinture-mère avec plantes médicinales. L'alimentation est végétarienne et biologique et la vie est communautaire. Le travail se fait de 9 hres le matin à 13 hres où est servi le dîner , puis les gens sont libres jusqu'au lendemain matin et ce 6 jours/semaine. Le dimanche est congé pour tout l'monde. Il y a des bicyclettes à la disposition des wwoofers.

Méthodes biologiques: compost et bois raméal fragmenté BRF - artes rourge et aulnes.

Certifications: non,  parce qu'on doit payer à chaque année pour la certification

Accès Internet disponible.
_wwoof_travel: Seulement autobus voyageur  au prochain village à 5 kl.

name: Mikhaelle - Nous habitons à Val-David à 5 minutes du village. Notre espace est situé dans la forêt
description:
Bonjour belles personnes ! Notre famille habite à Val-David au coeur de la forêt, mais à 5 min du village. Je m’appelle Mikha et mon amoureux est Alex. Nous avons une petite créature magique (notre enfant) qui se nomme Charlie. Nous attendons également un autre enfant à la fin mars (Lumi). Nous avons deux bergers australiens très gentils. Nous avons une forêt immense derrière notre maison avec des sentiers qui mènent à un lac sauvage. Nous aimerions débuter un jardin bio à l’arrivée du printemps 2026. Nos voisins sont des bons amis et ont déjà débuté ce projet commun.

Puisque nous aurons un bébé naissant et un jeune enfant de 18 mois au printemps 2026, nous aimerions partager notre quotidien avec des gens de coeurs qui pourraient nous venir en aide avec le jardin et/ou les enfants et/ou les animaux ! Nous rêvons d’une micro communauté d’entraide dans la simplicité.

Nous avons une chambre d’amis disponible dans notre maison et nous souhaitons partager l’ensemble de la maison et de l’espace avec les woofers. Nous adorons faire à manger. Nous allons au partage et solidarité chaque semaine qui est un organisme dont le but est de limiter le gaspillage alimentaire.

Nous adorons marcher en forêt. Nous avons tellement un bel espace dans la forêt derrière la maison. Nous aimerions d’ailleurs aménager des structures et modules en bois pour les enfants style arbre en arbre. Nous avons déja trouvé le bois. Il nous manque juste des bras :) Sinon, nous avons le parc régional Val-David, Val-Morin qui offre plusieurs sentiers et les meilleurs sites d’escalade des Laurentides. Nous aimons aller nous baigner au lac. Nous avons la piste cyclable avec la rivière du nord tout près.

name: Beatrice - Help join in our community project
description:
My name is Beatrice Mugeni, a proud member of the Ngeri Community and the Chairlady of the Ngeri Community Fish Cage initiative. Our project focuses on fish farming as a sustainable way to enhance food security and create employment opportunities for members of our community.

Through this initiative, we work together to raise fish in cage systems, promoting responsible aquaculture practices while supporting local livelihoods. I am passionate about community development, environmental sustainability, and empowering others through practical skills and teamwork.

We welcome volunteers who are eager to learn, share knowledge, and contribute to meaningful work that positively impacts both people and the environment. By joining us, you will experience authentic community life, gain hands-on experience in fish farming, and be part of a project that is making a real difference.

On our farm, we focus on small-scale, sustainable agriculture combined with fish farming to support food security and create opportunities for the local community. Our daily activities vary depending on the season, but they mainly include tending to crops, feeding and monitoring fish in the cages, maintaining the farm, and working on new agricultural projects.

A typical day usually starts around 7:30–8:00 AM with a simple breakfast. After that, we head to the farm where we work for about 1–2 hours in the morning while the weather is still cool. During this time, we may be planting, weeding, watering crops, repairing fish cages, or feeding the fish. Because the sun can be strong, we often take a break before midday.

In the afternoon, activities are lighter and can include checking on the farm, harvesting, cleaning equipment, or engaging in community-based projects. There is also time to relax, explore the island, interact with local people, or enjoy the natural environment.

WWOOFers who join us will have the opportunity to learn:

Basics of fish farming (fish cage management, feeding, and mainten

name: Greg - Homestead, Garden, Vegetables, Community, Ranch/Livestock
description: Update: recently and still cleaning up from recent wildfire. Could use a few good hands and souls to aid in the process... Due to an excess of recently cut down trees on property, I've decided to give mushroom cultivation a go. Lots of drilling and plugging to do. Have 6 honey bee hives to get through the winter as well.Thanks for your consideration     hello! thanks for taking a minute or two out of your life to check out the farm. trying to turn the 20 acres I'm currently living on and turning it into a global community. where anyone who has heart and soul and passion for making the world a better place will have a place to stay and harvest the fruits of their labors. I've been fortunate enough to travel throughout our great state of alaska and a little bit around the world and decided to settle here. though the climate can be a bit challenging (mostly in winter) the beauty is priceless to me. anyhow, starting to drift.  so we're looking for some good wwoofers .....right now we have about 28 chickens , 3 goats, and have 6 beehives . Nurpu is still under construction and there's all sorts of work to be done. but we try to live in tune with our bodies and nature and don't work ourselves to much either . looking for lots of positive energies. but I believe with the right people it will happen and already is...we're located in willow alaska, which is an hour and a half north of anchorage. besides being close to major road system, we're more or less surrounded by wild land... the beauty is priceless. I've put everything I have into building this place up, that's why I'd love to get some wwoofers who have some connection to the earth, who have some passion to help others and have a passion for coming-being in Alaska. thanks for your time and feel free to contact me with any questions. there's lots of awesome things to do around here if you enjoy nature. I have a kayak, 2 catarafts, a mini bike (motorized) and a 4 wheeler for wwoofers to use. the fishing is world class .

name: Jean-Pierre - Ferme biologique certifiée AB
description:
Bonjour, nous sommes situé dans le Nord, dans le Sud du département à110 Km de Lille, 10 km de la Belgique, 50 km des Ardennes dans une région bocagère : l'Avesnois classé "parc naturel régional". 

Nous sommes attachés à la notion d'agriculture paysanne familiale et pratiquons l'agriculture biologique depuis plus de quarante  ans (1982). Sur la ferme nous cultivons essentiellement des légumes et des arbres fruitiers. Il y a aussi quelques poules et lapins.

Passionnés par la biodiversité et le maintien du paysage bocager, nous avons envie de partager notre concept dans la convivialité et la simplicité, contre un coup de main ponctuel mais efficient. 

Nous préférons accueillir nos  wwoofeurs en dehors du weekend, vu notre emploi du temps chargé à ce moment, le lundi étantt notre jour de repos, nous préférons une arrivée, en fin de journée, ou le mardi.

Nous travaillons en couple sur la ferme avec notre fils Samuel qui a une bonne connaissance de l'anglais (car il a pratiqué le wwoofing quelques mois en Angleterre) et une main d’œuvre salariée occasionnelle saisonnière.

Nous vendons notre production sur les marchés le samedi et le dimanche ainsi que dans quelques magasins bio.

Nous sommes engagés dans diverses associations locales. 
Nous parlons Français.
_wwoof_travel: Peut aller chercher d'Avesnes Helpe (5 Km)

name: Barbara - Mixed Farm
description:
IMPORTANT NOTICE : DUE TO A NEW WWOOF CANADA FEATURE, THE CALENDAR SHOWS ORANGE WHEN A WWOOFER IS ACCEPTED, BUT SINCE WE WELCOME MORE THAN ONE PERSON, DO NOT HESITATE TO INQUIRE ABOUT ANY DATES YOU ARE INTERESTED IN.
Welcome to the Yukon! We have a large ranch/farm in the Takhini valley 30km outside Whitehorse, where we pasture cows and elk, have a large haskap berry orchard, a large organic garden and greenhouse, and raise pigs in the summer months. We have one young black Labrador male dog named Jimmy, and one tabby male cat named Puss Cat or Hunter.
Experiences we offer are helping with organic gardening, greenhouse maintenance, haskap field maintenance and harvesting and participating with farm market goods preparation, helping with packaging of meat products and making different types of sausage,  fencing, carpentry projects, wood cutting, and other seasonal tasks. 
Recreational activities that can be easily accessed are canoeing, wilderness trails, northern lights in the fall, mountains and climbing of same, berry picking and wild mushroom harvesting. 

Our accommodation we offer cabins which are heated with wood stoves, and a RV for your own little kitchen for breakfasts and suppers.  We provide all food, and have one shared meal a day, five days a week for lunch.  We offer organic food, all meat is raised on our farm, and vegetables in season .  There is a wash house with a shower and washing machine for your use.  It is preferred that you have your own vehicle, but if you need a car for some reason we have one that can be rented for a reasonable fee.  

People who will fit in with our farms needs are those with a positive attitude, and enjoy working with others in an outside environment.  If you have previous skills such as gardening, berry picking, carpentry, mechanical knowhow, animal husbandry, this is all appreciated, but you can also learn here.

Our haskap berries are a big part of our farm, especially in July and August, when they are ripe and we are
_wwoof_travel: We are located on the Alaska Highway 30 kms west of Whitehorse (on the way to Haines Junction NOT Dawson City!) We are at km. 1459.5 (there is a small hand painted green and white sign on the side of the highway).  Turn right and there you are!

name: Boimah - Community farm
description:
Hello, my name is Boimah C Korneh, I live in Liberia a country in West Africa. I am a farmer, I do farming to sustain my family and also send me to school and I also manage a community farm in Bomi County. My dream is to became a great farmer. I grow crops such as Greens, Corn, Tomato, Cassava, Pepper and etc. In collaboration with some Community youth and also raise cattle such as: Chicken and Sheep. You will get to know me more as we interact. Thank you, and you are most welcome to my farms.

Organic methods: I do cultivation before plantings

Certifications: I am certified with the Tubmanburg Rural Farmers Union

Current projects: I am doing a cassava farm project

Internet access available

name: Patrick - One-acre market garden
description:
Hello! This is Claire, Patrick, and Toad! We run a mixed vegetable farm. We are located on the edge of Laurel, Montana, which lies just 20 minutes west of Billings, the largest city in the state. We farm about an acre of mixed vegetables and sell our products in Billings, Laurel, and Red Lodge. We sell at farmers markets, run a CSA, and online through a local food hub. We - Claire and Patrick - are partners and Toad is our dog. We also have a roommate and bestie, named Nate.

Our farm tasks range from day to day, but generally follow a weekly schedule centered around our markets. Tasks on the farm include: planting, harvesting, greenhouse work, trellising, washing, packing, and of course, weeding. As the sole operators of the farm, Claire and Patrick will always be around to guide you through activities and keep you company. We love to share what we've learned and to learn from you, too!

For our first three years, we lived in a tiny house on the farm. In early 2025 we moved into a full-sized house on the property and the tiny house has sadly sat mostly vacant since then. This gave us the idea that we should open it up to WWOOFers! We've both WWOOfed in the past and found it to be an enriching experience. 

The tiny house has a kitchen, bathroom, and loft bedroom. The loft is accessed by a ladder so requires comfort with climbing a ladder. The toilet is a composting toilet so that is something to get comfortable with too if you’re only used to flush toilets.  The water for the tiny house is supplied by a small tank that we’re happy to fill as often as necessary, but we ask you to please be conscientious with your water usage. When we lived together in the tiny house we generally could go a week on a tank of water. 

The tiny house is a great place for one person or for a couple to share. 
We hope to share at least one meal a day with our WWOOFers and will provide groceries for the other meals of the day. Being a vegetable farm, you are welcome to whatever veggies we
_wwoof_travel: Logan international Airport, Billings MT greyhound stop

name: Noel - Family home and garden - urban
description:
We are a couple from Pará with one child (born in 2018). We live in a peri-urban area, inherited from grandparents, in a place with a great diversity of fruit trees and other Amazon food plants, mainly for self-consumption but also for sale. We have poultry, one pig and fish farms, a composting chamber, rainwater harvesting, ecological toilets and an oven and wood stove.
In addition to the farm work, we also organize and make weekly food deliveries from an agro-ecological network that involves small farmers and local consumers.
Our small house has a mezzanine available for a simple but comfortable accommodation. Welcome to our Amazônia.

Organic methods: permaculture, organic compost

Certifications: Grupo para Consumo Agroecologico (GRUCA)

Current projects: Fish-farming, Açaí juice

Internet access available

name: Clare and Edgars - Family home and garden - rural, Homestead, Fruit tree nursery, Mixed Farm, Family farm
description:
We make an elecctic array of products that arnt at your average homestead.   Our bread and butter is making weaving supplies which we sell all over the world.  You can find us around the internet as Harvest Looms.  But also you will find carpets, snowshoes and African clay folk instruments.  (and more:)

Rostes is nestled in the Forest near to the Estonian border/Baltic seaside with a river running on one border of the property. The river is the Salaca and has cute little red cliffs. Our land is actually part of a National forest. Its magical. We have a humble organic homestead, where we farm, craft, build, harvest and enjoy life the sustainable way as best we can.  Animals are sometimes changing but we always have the horses and chickens, sometime pigs, ducks, geese, or quials.  We are busy all year around. Our house is also the home to our studios. Clare is a weaver and whatever she can get her hands on kinda artist and Edgars potter/wood craftsmen. also a builder/inventor. So you can learn to weave a belt to making snowshoes. In the summer time we have a big garden and are harvesting many things from the forest/fields. A bigger respect goes to wild foods.  We grow, harvest, and make our own herbs, winter food supply, teas, medicine, etc (as much as we can). What we dont have we try to get from neighbors, like milk and butter.  We are well rounded countrysiders who have lived on different continents (and are also from different countries) and try to bring all that together here. We spent years on the road traveling until we threw the anchor down here.  Hitchhiked thousands of Km/miles and have even crossed the Atlantic Ocean on a square-rig ship with no engine! And the adventure continues here!

    We can always use extra hands in the spring, summer or fall with projects around the place, rather that be weaving a willow fence, collecting bilberries, could be anything! we always have some dream brewing. Summertime there are often fires, peeps coming and going, and
_wwoof_travel:
From Riga ride to Ainazi (bus is about 6euros, and  auto-stop free and normal here)
then  we can pick you up to bring you deeper into the countryside

name: Sharp - My farm is found in Bunyonyi
description:
Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals)
My project starts from one day old chick up to maturity and it found at lake Bunyonyi Kabale District Uganda 🇺🇬
2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
Activities:they will be helping me in the following;
1.feedding birds 
2.constructing some poultry houses
3.inspecting birds
4.vaccinating bird where necessary
What I will teach them are the following;
1. How to feed poultry birds
2.how to construct better houses
3.how vaccinate poultry birds
4. How to prepare chicken 

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals:
I will provide all meals and breakfast
Accommodation I have cottages
4. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?
It will be a wonderful day 🌹

5. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.):
Reading historical books, story teller, swimming, canoeing , listening to music

name: Thomas & Janie - Goat Dairy, Chickens, Vegetable Garden, Fruit Trees
description:
We have 22 of the most AMAZING Alpine Goats. 6 goats are currently being milked with our melasty 8 goat milking machine. Also we have about 20 Chickens, mostly heritage breeds. 

We teach goat husbandry, including milking with 8 goat milk machine, processing of milk, yogurt, and kefir along with hoof trimming and repairing electric fencing. We also teach kefir, yogurt, and cheesemaking when my wife feels up to it. 

We also welcome those with construction skills to help finish the plumbing and kitchen in the upper level of the barn for our FARM EDUCATION center. We also need help to build small cabins for housing for WOOFER’s who want to live on the property and help for an extended period of time. 

We do NOT do farm work except feeding and milking of goats on the Eternal Sabbath, Saturday. The farm shuts down on Sabbath and we rest. 

We currently host day and extended stay WWOOFERS and have a 30’ camper. It is suitable for MARRIED couples or a single person. It has a bathroom, shower, stove, double bed, a new fridge, nice couch in the living room. We can share some meals and provide food. 

WWOOFERS also MIUST  have a reliable Vehicle for Transportation along with Insurance.. The closest amenities are 10 min away. 

Activities: goat husbandry including milking, delivery of milk to drop off locations in Denver and Colorado Springs, cleaning  out sheds, trimming hoofs, training them on a leash, gardening, weed mitigation,  weed wacking and weeding, landscaping, mechanic work on farm vehicles. 

Hobbies we have are reading, hiking, sewing, listening to music, watching goats do their thing, working out, sewing, ect….

name: Buza - Sustainable marketgardening type horticulture with permacultural aspect in a community venue
description:
/Magyar leírás lejjebb/

1, Introduction

Dombfalva is located in a quiet valley of the Gödöllő hills, not far from Budapest. It is a small community initiative that operates as a program venue. Various events are implemented here, for example, international voluntary projects, artistic and cultural events (Organix Festival). The area was designed with a permaculture approach.
The continuation of a community garden in a new location is being realized. It is a rare opportunity that as a volunteer you can participate not only in the caretaking of the vegetable garden, but also in its making process.
By visiting us, you can become part of a friendly, open team that strives to cooperate with nature. Namely Dani, Bunya, Marci and Kecsó make up the team.
A bigger city - Gödöllő - is easily accessible from the location, where you can take part in cultural and other events. Budapest is not far away and can be reached by public transport. The lake in the nearby valley provides a swimming oppportunity and there are many hiking options available in the area.

2, What can you learn visiting us?

The activities strongly depend on the season and, of course, the current weather.

Early spring works:
Pruning, tillage, carrying manure and turning it into the soil. Preparation of irrigation system. Seedling care: sowing, weeding, watering, transplanting. Lawn mowing, repair and maintenance of machines and tools, relocation of bushes and trees, relocation of a plastic tunnel.

Spring and early summer activities:
Rotoilling, sowing, digging, planting seedlings, installing an irrigation system, weeding, hoeing, seedling care, thinning out, mulching, decoration, mowing.

Things to do in mid-summer and autumn:
Irrigation system supervision, weeding, succession planting, soil preparation, mowing, plant protection, harvesting, community canning  .

Due to our varied fields of activity, we also have other type of tasks. In addition to gardening, we also count on the help of volunteers in the

name: Pedro José - Family farm, Family home and garden - rural, Smallholding, Mixed Farm
description:
A história da família Lovatto, do Sitio Agroecológico Espaço do Sossego, reflete um modo de viver, de produzir e de se relacionar com seus semelhantes e com a natureza. A propriedade se converteu em uma das primeiras propriedades agroecológicas da cidade de Farroupilha/RS e uma das primeiras do estado. 

No dia 14 de outubro de 1989, estivemos presentes na primeira edição da Feira dos Agricultores Ecologistas (FAE), promovida pela Coolmeia em Porto Alegre/RS, feira que participamos até hoje, todos os sábados de manhã. 
Hoje, o fruto da parceira entre nós expressa o que chamamos de agricultura familiar agroecológica, que envolve o respeito entre as pessoas no trabalho diário e a terra onde se habita. 
O Sítio é muito diversificado: produz morango, mirtilo, framboesa, amora, alho, figo, pêssego, feijão, flores e diversas PANCS (plantas alimentícias não convencionais), sendo o verão (novembro a março) a época da colheita das frutas, um grande momento de aprendizado e conexão com essas plantas e um dos períodos mais importantes do ano no nosso sítio. Na agroindústria produzimos geleias e embalamos frutas vermelhas congeladas o ano todo. 

Certificação: Certificação orgânica.

Projetos em desenvolvimento:
- Trabalhamos na agroindústria, fazendo geleias e embalando frutas vermelhas congeladas;
- Há diversas colheitas de frutas no verão, principalmente mirtilo, morango, amora e framboesa;
- Participamos da feira de agricultores ecologistas no sábado de manhã em Porto Alegre/RS, onde vendemos nossos produtos diretamente aos consumidores;
- Em 2022 instalamos energia solar e nos tornamos autossuficientes em energia elétrica.
.
.
.
.
The history of our family, of Sitio Espaço do Sossego, reflects a way of living, producing and relating with each other and the nature. The family property became one of the first agroecological properties in Farroupilha/RS city and one of the first in the state. 

On October 14, 1989, we were present at the first edition of the Feira dos Agricul

name: Kostas - Family home and garden - rural, Agritourism Destination, Vineyard, Farm
description:
The majority of the property is an organic vineyard that has about 5000 vines from which we produce our own organic wine in the old-fashioned way: we still stomp the grapes and store the wine in wood barrels.

Besides this, we also have on the farm a vegetable garden, lemon trees, orange trees, fig-trees, olive trees, almond trees, and many other fruit trees. We also have livestock with chickens, goats, sheep, donkeys and horses.

There is also a wide variety of flowers.

We also host tourists on the farm who come to buy wine, cheese, and olive oil and take a little tour of the vineyard.

We are situated on the small Island of Lipsi, close to the Aegean Sea (about 100 meters) Thanks to EU subsidies, Lipsi is in the process of becoming the first Mediterranean green island (funding voted in April 2011), this will mean that the island after a transition period will have zero CO2 emissions, and will take its energy from solar and wind power; cars and scooters will be electric.

The farm has been in the family for over 200 years and is unique, and has hosted over 1500 students and volunteers in the last 12 year's 

Accommodation & Board

Two options:

1. At the Main Farm (14-person capacity)  With Electricity, Gas and Wood Cooking, and Electricity for For Lights and WiFi, full water desalination and rain water. 

Separate lodging upstairs from the family house, with a bathroom and separate entrance. The kitchen situated downstairs will be shared. Food will be provided and WWOOFers will be able to cook themselves. We will have set times for when we eat.

Breakfast is usually 1 hour to a half hour before we start.

Lunch is between 1.30 and 2.00 pm

Dinner is between 7.30 and 9.00 pm

2. at the Farm/Vineyard (2 person capacity)

This is a smaller version of the main farm (1000 grape vines, 15 fig trees, 10 almond trees, and 30 olive trees).

It is about 100 meters from the main farm. You can get to it from the main farm 2 ways through the field, or walk on the road which t
_wwoof_travel: Port of the island of Lipsi

name: Dennis - Organic farm near the Jersey Shore.
description:
Welcome to a growing community in Toms River, New Jersey, dedicated to cultivating healing, sustainability, and hope. Our farm is part of an innovative program by a non-profit mental healthcare company, combining sustainable agriculture, mental wellness, and recovery-based learning.

We’re a small team of passionate growers, mentors, and community members who believe in the power of working the land to restore both people and the planet. Our farm family includes Dennis (the live-in the project manager), seasonal interns, volunteers, and some friendly animals (coming soon).

At our core, this farm is about connection: to the earth, to each other, and to purpose.

Our farm blends sustainable agriculture, aquaponics, and hands-on learning. WWOOFers will have the opportunity to dive into:
Clearing land from fallen trees and branches
Planting, maintaining, and harvesting crops
Beekeeping
Organic vegetable and herb production
Building and maintaining greenhouses
Composting and soil health
Seed starting and greenhouse management
Aquaponic system care (fish and plants)
Permaculture and regenerative growing techniques
Helping with our youth mentorship and community outreach projects

A typical day starts around 10 AM with morning chores—watering, feeding animals, checking the aquaponics system, and harvesting for our CSA or local food donations. After a mid-morning break, we move into bigger projects such as planting, building greenhouses, or improving irrigation systems. Afternoons might include educational workshops or quiet garden work. We are just getting started and have not opened to the public, yet. As spring approaches, we are getting busy laying the groundwork for this season.

You’ll learn not only how to grow food sustainably but also how to grow community through service and compassion.

WWOOFers stay in our farmhouse. Our three guest rooms are simple but comfortable, with 2 shared bathrooms and a community kitchen. We have gym equipment in the basement and our v
_wwoof_travel: Atlantic City International Airport, Atlantic City Greyhound Station

name: Laura Jean - Large vegetable garden, Berry Orchard and layer hens as well as 3 horses and alpacas
description:
Hi, my name is Laura and my husband Len and I live on a 10 acre farm in the beautiful North Thompson. we are both hard working seniors. We have a large vegetable garden as well as a Haskap Berry Orchard, we have 90 layer hens, 3 horses and 3 Alpacas. We have a Border Collie named Babe and 2 very spoiled cats, Fred and Phaedra. 
I am a Bee keeper having 5 hives at the moment. I will probably grow to 8 by the late spring. Bee Keeping is a relatively new hobby but I love to share and Learn.

Each day starts with feeding the Hens and gathering eggs, then we are off to the garden before it gets too Hot! Planting in the spring, weeding all summer and harvesting in the fall .Our berry picking season starts in early June to the end of July. 
I have 63 bushes of berries and they are all hand picked , we pick only the ripe ones. 
I am an avid canner and Pickler and I make a lot of delicious Jam. I teach a community cooking / canning courses and enjoy sharing my knowledge with others.

Last year we completed a beautiful Studio Cabin. with a full kitchen , eco friendly incinerator toilet and a gorgeous Deck overlooking the North Thompson Valley.  
We typically don't eat breakfast but I would supply food of your choice in your kitchen for breakfast. Lunch typically includes a soup and sandwich and dinner is typically at about 730 pm. We don't have any dietary restictions but I can accomadate almost everyone

I am a pretty good cook, I like to experiment with different cuisines, we love to travel, having spent 6 weeks last winter in Spain and Portugal. For the past 25 years I have been a spinner of Fiber, in the past I have owned sheep, angora goats, Llamas and Alpacas. 
Len built me a she shed a few years ago where I can escape and just spin. I have a Loom that I am hoping to have time to learn to weave. Having had fiber animals in the past I have a supply of fiber to teach with. 
We live in the Beautiful North Thompson Valley which includes Wells gray Park that has 27 waterfall

name: Michele - Azienda agricola nel Conero
description:
ciao sono Michele ho 25 anni, gestisco un'azienda di 25 ettari nel parco del Conero. Vivo da solo (vicino al nostro agriturismo),  la mia famiglia composta da mio padre, madre e due fratelli vive nella nostra casa dentro il nostro agriturismo.

nella nostra attività agricola le lavorazioni principali sono la gestione del vigneto e l'oliveto, nella giornata tipo svolgeremo attività diverse in base alla stagione per gestire le due colture, dalla potatura alla raccolta. Per poi proseguire con le trasformazioni e imbottigliamenti in cantina. In alcuni periodi dell'anno verranno effettuate altre lavorazioni dei terreni per la produzione dei cereali, il taglio e la sistemazione della legna (sia dei residui di potatura e dalla periodica pulizia dei confini), la cura del verde della struttura (raccolta foglie, taglio siepi e prato ...), la preparazione - gestione dell'orto familiare e la manutenzione dei mezzi.

tutti i pasti partendo dalla colazione avverranno insieme, la sistemazione avverrà in un' appartamento in condivisione vicino alla nostra struttura, nella stagione invernale se disponibile e per visite di meno una settimana l'alloggio avverrà nelle stanze del nostro agriturismo, per chi dispone di un proprio mezzo camperizzato (riscaldato per la stagione invernale) e/o una tenda (presente in azienda) può campeggiare all'interno della struttura.

Nel tempo libero sono solito uscire con i miei amici e andare al mare.
---o----o-----0-----o----o---

hello I'm Michele I'm 25 years old, I manage a 25-hectare farm in the Conero park. I live alone (near our agriturismo), but my family comprising my father, mother and two brothers live in our house inside our agriturismo.

In our agricultural activity the main work is the management of the vineyard and the olive grove, in a typical day we will carry out various activities according to the season to manage the two crops, from pruning to harvesting. This is followed by processing and bottling in the cellar. At certain times of
_wwoof_travel: Stazione ancona / Aereoporto delle marche e bologna

name: Fabian andres - Elaboracion de cerveza artesanal, apicultura, construccion tradicional enmadera, gallinas
description:
elaboracion de cerveza artesanal, apiario, recoleccion de miel, cerezas, manzanas, hierbas aromariticas,  gallinas araucanas , huevos verdes, recoleccion de leña


2 actividades apicolas, elavoracion decerveza, recoleccion de hierbas aromaticas.


3. las actividades no son extrictas, empleamos el dia disfrutando de todas las tarea

4., Podemos hacer treking enlas montañas, disponemos de bicicletas ,  tambien en su tiempo libre pueden desfrutar del rio limay

name: Edmund - Organic Mix Cropping farm in the mountain
description:
Hello my name is Edmund and i am 35 years. I live in a town on the mountain in west africa Ghana called Kwahu. I live with my extended family where everyone is a farmer. we grow maize, cassava, plantain, cabbage, carrots and also poultry. There are dogs and cats living with us. I'm currently trying to run this new farm project to provide free food to children and the elderly who cant afford to work to feed themselves. It has always been my dream to run a free food joint or restaurant to feed as many people as i can for free and the only way to do this is by growing crops  to avoid buying costly market groceries for that.

As explained earlier, normal farming operations include cultivating, harvesting, minor drainage, plowing, and seeding. so i will use the opportunity to teach WWOOFer's a lot about how we grow our local crops and also make enquiries if is possible to grow some of the crops in their various countries if the climate won't be a problem. just like how the chinese has now leant how to grow one of our crop call palmnut in their country whereby the climate or weather isn't a problem.

There will be free Accommodation and free food for our lovely WWOOFer's but it any WWOOFer can help us by assisting pay for the hostel that will be nice since we are non profit organization and needs a lot of help and a lot of hands to feed the needy

I enjoy cooking and that is one of the main reasons why i choose to cook and supply food for free to the community and beyond if i can get there. there is much joy in doing what you like and seeing other happy by benefiting from it. there would be a lot of Ghanaian food cooking practising. Not only that, there would be african cultural to be thought, exchange of language teachings and a lot more fun stuffs. There would be a lot of tourist sites to visit as well.

name: Amanda - Hobby Farm, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat, Cattle Ranch, Horse Ranch
description:
BearPaw Ranch is a beautiful 160 acre family run guest ranch located in central Alberta Canada just 20 minutes from Drayton Valley, 1.5 hours from Edmonton and approximately 3 hours from Jasper National Park. 
From mid-April to late November the ranch is open to the public. They can choose to rent a rustic cabin, come camping, host a retreat, reunion or a wedding, or the very popular option, come out for the day. They also have the option to bring their own horses so they can ride on the 5km of forest trails on site or 80 km of river side trail 2km away from the ranch.
There is a lot to see, pet, and feed with all the farm animals on site including cows, horses, mini horses, ducks, bunnies, lambs, a llama, chickens, pigs, potbelly pigs, cats and dogs. There are always things to do for fun including horseback riding, fishing, canoeing, kayaking, hiking and wild life watching. We often have deer, moose, lynx, bobcats, bears, and sometimes mountain lions but don’t worry, they are more afraid of us than we are of them.
On site we also have a store that specializes in crystal holistic healing. It contains rocks, fossils and gems from all over the world.  We have a Spa onsite with a Himalayan salt room. As well as a treatment room where one could book a massage, reiki, spiritual response therapy and more. We will also run classes to teach different holistic modalities. 
With that being said, there is always work to be done here, such as: cleaning cabins, building maintenance, yard maintenance, farming (hay season June – September), caring for all animals (calving season March – June), large vehicle maintenance, horse trail/walking trail maintenance, horse training and exercising and general responsibilities of running a guest ranch. With the store on site you also have an opportunity to learn about holistic healing through oils and crystals, reiki and pendulum work. We also travel around to small towns to sell our crystals and you would be more than welcome to come. 
As y

name: Hunter - Cattle Ranch
description:
Hello, I’m Hunter, I am the manager of the ranch. Located outside Glendive Montana, we run a herd of 450 cattle. While we’re not officially certified organic, we strive to operate with organic principles and practices to the best of our ability.

Our days are a blend of traditional ranching tasks and the unpredictability of working with nature. Riding out to check on the herd is a daily ritual. This means saddling up and heading out to various pastures, keeping a keen eye on the health and well-being of our cattle. Whether it's ensuring they have enough grazing or spotting any signs of illness.
Herding cattle is another essential activity. This involves working with the herd to move them between pastures or to bring them in for vaccinations and other necessary care. It’s a dynamic process that demands both patience and quick thinking, as cattle have a mind of their own.

Fixing fences is an ongoing task that requires hands-on problem-solving. With miles of fence to maintain, we regularly inspect and repair any sections that have been damaged by weather, wildlife, or wear and tear. This is a great opportunity for WWOOFers to learn about practical skills like the use of various tools and materials essential for fence repair.

Maintaining water lines is another critical aspect of ranch life. Ensuring that our cattle have a steady supply of clean water involves inspecting and repairing water lines, checking tanks, and sometimes dealing with unexpected issues like leaks or blockages.

We don’t have a “typical” day because ranching is inherently unpredictable. One day might be spent entirely on horseback moving cattle, while another could focus on repairing fences or troubleshooting water issues. Weather conditions, cattle behavior, and other unforeseen factors can all shift our plans.

Through WWOOFing here, we aim to share the intricacies of ranch life and offer hands-on experiences that build practical skills. From the basics of horseback riding to understanding animal

name: Riitta-Miina - Family farm
description:
We have an ecological farm in eastern Finland. The farm is specialized in maintaining biodiversity in certain areas with the help of cows and sheep. At the same time we protect the threatened Finnish original cows called kyyttö. We sell the milk direct to the customers, make cheese and sell purebred calves for farmers. Our farm is very traditional and a bit old fashioned. I milk our small cattle (4-5 milking cows) with an oldfashioned machine. Our vegetable garden is big, because we try to grow the potatoes, carrots, artichotes etc. for our own table. If the volunteers are interested in our farm, they may help me to feed the animals, maybe to shear the sheep with me and repair the fences with my husband. In the summer time there is much weeding. In the autumn we pick up the berries in the forest as well as mushrooms.

Projects for the wwoofers: Feeding and taking care of the animals, weeding the vegetable garden by hand, shearing the sheep. 

PLEASE NOTE: We wish to have only wwoofers, who are already used to do physical work and preferably have also experience of animals. Taking care of animals requires the ability of pushing a wheelbarrow full of hey, lifting several 8kg water buckets and walking 5000 steps a day.  We can take only one wwoofer at the time, and the working periods are limited to summertime. We are not able to host wwoofers in the winter. 

About the house: Internet acces available. The voluntary workers get a room of their own. We serve ordinary, home-made food.

name: Jennifer - Diverse family farm & homestead
description:
We have been a small mixed farm since 2012. We grow much of our own diet and scale up to sell to a committed group of customers.  Along with large gardens, we have bee hives, laying hens, a milk cow and a flock of purebred Icelandic sheep. Most of the food that we don't produce ourselves is purchased from nearby farms (ie. grains, berries).  We make the majority of our meals from scratch and have many skills in food preparation and food preservation. We also grow and market flowers for beauty and for natural dyeing (we dye the wool from our sheep). 

We have both travelled and worked across Canada and the world and have been involved in local food sovereignty initiatives for many years. We are committed to a just food system for all the earth's citizens. Our two boys (ages 14 and 17) don't go to school but learn alongside us as we do our daily work.  Besides the farm, we have off-farm income teaching English and writing/editting (from home).

We eat breakfast on our own and lunch and supper together. We like meat but don't eat it every day. We expect you to help in the kitchen doing dishes and assisting with meal preparation. You are also welcome to take a turn at cooking lunch or dinner, if you wish.

Your room is in the basement of our large farm house where our oldest son also has his room. 

We make biweekly deliveries to Winnipeg and can pick WWOOFers up at this time.

We have bikes you can use. The Canada Trail and other hiking trails are a few miles away. We have a lake nearby for swimming. Morden is a nice town with coffee shops, restaurants, an art gallery, a dinosaur museum, an annual festival and a taproom.

Farming Practices: We care deeply for the health of the soil (regenerative agriculture). We work to integrate plants and animals so that the work of each benefits the other (permaculture). We rotational graze our cow, sheep and chickens. We practice no till gardening methods. We minimize fossil fuel use.

Current projects: After many years of infrastr

name: ZEZITO - Produção de café cogumelos frutas hortaliças
description:
Sou Zezito Duarte produtor de cafés especiais e cogumelos comestíveis e medicinais, em agrofloresta, acampamentos para adolescentes, bistrô e cafeteria, escola de cursos pontuais em agroecologia, cerimônias e retiros e grupos de autoconhecimento em técnicas e práticas de xamanismo dos nativos das Américas.

Meditação, alongamentos, café da manhã e depois 6 horas de trabalho nas atividades da fazenda, depois livre para trekings e banhos  decachoeiras

café da manhã almoço e jantar

Estamos junto ao Parque Nacional da Chapada Diamantina

name: Carlos - Family farm
description:
Nuestra Familia esta Compuesta por 4 personajes: Carlos 48 años de Edad, Samanta 45 Años de Edad, Mateo 11 y Tomás 3 años de Edad. 
Vivimos En Una Cabaña De Madera Que Construimos Con Los Árboles Del Lugar. TENEMOS UN huerto de 700m2, tenemos un invernadero. Un gallinero de 100 gallinas de campo. También criamos pollos de campo para la carne. 
Tenemos varios frutales. Actualmente estamos construyendo un estanque grande para sembrar peses.
Vivimos en el lugar donde producimos. Tenemos varias construcciones de materiales naturales. Además de Trabajos de carpintería.
 La Granja esta situada a 30 km de dos ciudades de Tandil y Ayacucho, en camino de tierra. No hay autobus para llegar al Lugar. 
Las opciones para llegar son taxi o vamos a recogerlos y compartirmos los gastos de combustible.

En caso de lluvia no se puede llegar o salir de la Granja.


Current projects: Estamos armando una pequeña granja ecológica de una hectárea, la ayuda que necesitamos es muy variada, 
nos encontramos en la etapa de la construcción de nuestra casa permanente en el encofrado de paja, madera y todo lo relacionado con las instalaciones de agua, luz y de la carpintería. 
También solicitamos mantenimiento del jardín, alimentación de pollos y gallinas. Fabricación de dulces y conservas. Mantenimiento general de la granja.

Estamos armando Una Pequeña Granja de eco Una hectárea, la ayuda Que precisamos es muy variada,  
Estamos en la Etapa de Construir Nuestra Casa permanente en paja encofrada, Madera y Todo Lo Que se refiera instalaciones de agua, luz y carpintería. 
También solicitamos mantenimiento de la huerta, Alimentar Pollos y gallinas. Fabricación de dulces y conservas. Mantenimiento en General de la Granja.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel:
Vivimos en La Constancia un paraje pequeño que pertenece al partido de Ayacucho, Provincia de Buenos Aires. A 350km de la ciudad de Buenos Aires.
 La granja queda situada a 30km de Tandil y de Ayacucho, en camino de tierra, por lo tanto no hay bus para l

name: Brian - Welcome to Briden Farm, our Regenerative Homesteading Farm in Bear River, Nova Scotia
description:
We enjoy a busy pace in a relaxing, country setting  here at Briden Farm, in Bear River, Nova Scotia, Canada. Briden Farm sits atop a Hillside, overlooking The Head of The Tide, where the freshwater river meets salt. The West Branch of the River helps form the Eastern property boundary and Franklin Brook runs through the back four acres of our 22 acres, before running into the West Branch. This land, it is believed, is that on which some of the earliest settlers first built and planted grapevines.

My Partner and I bought our 22 acres of paradise on the West Branch of Bear River, Nova Scotia in 2020, and have from necessity learned to recycle, Upcycle, and find creative ways of running our Homesteading Farm to make ends meet. 

We are basically a traditional farm, embracing newer, more earth-friendly methods. Practising rotational pasturing and regenerative farming methods. Working to turn our woodlands into pastures and gardens and we do so by employing our Goats, Pigs, Chickens, Ducks, Rabbits, etc. in helping us accomplish our goals. We’ve also been asked to have our livestock on neighbouring properties, by property owners who saw what we’re doing and wanted us to do similar on Their Property. We currently have animals on four properties and walk from pasture to pasture covering a Km or two daily! Even in the Winter we sometimes have to make the trek to get the chores done. Lugging feed and water buckets, We do most everything by hand, in old fashioned ways! Ways that will allow you to learn what to do even if the grid goes down! We look forward to sharing our experience and helping you learn affordable, sustainable, skills that you’ll be able to use in a number of future opportunities.  It’s hard work, cold temperatures in the winter, when we also have to cut, split, and process firewood for our wood stove. Yes, it’s hard work, cold temperatures, and demanding. Coming here you’ll learn a foundation for homesteading and farming that You’ll be able to build on in

name: Sofia - Olive trees, pomegranate  and carob trees up on a hill with many more
description:
Hello. My name is Sofia and I am 34 years old. In the begging of 2024 I choosed to come and live near nature. I bought land that has a wooden house and I live here all my self accepting friends family and travelers to come and stay. Together are around 6 dogs a because sometimes we have visitors and a big.

My farming activities is around olive picking, carobs and pomegranate. Also fixing the house of the animals is a plan with fencing and take care the trees . New projects are all welcome like composting and making a natural lake.

I offer a bedroom with 3 single beds . A bathroom with shower and inside toilet. Breakfast, lunch and bites for dinner are offering with some help in preparation.

I like almost every music and I listen depends on anytime mood.. I sing my self and learning how to play a traditional instrument. I sing mostly old Greek folk music. I don't really like loud music .

name: Elin - Cozy family homestead in small village close to Hedemora town
description:
We are two families in two households living on a smaller homestead: me, husband and two sons, and my sister and her two daughters. The children are aged 9–14 yrs old.

We focus on self-sustainability; growing vegetables and strawberries on a small scale. We also have chickens, quails, three friendly goats, two lovely horses, a couple of rabbits, guinea pigs, and three dogs :) We produce eggs and milk (from one goat) for our own consumption.

We use the horses for farm chores such as collecting firewood, but also for leisure rides in the forest or nice outings in the carriage around the local villages. If you are into horses, you will have the opportunity to participate in horse-related activities.

We have a small creek running through the property and the village, and we are about 10- 15 min walk away from the forrest. 25 min hike from the farm you will find a nice fireplace in the forrest.

We do all sorts of regular farm work such as gardening, caring for animals, maintaining the fields, building fences, etc. We need help with all of this according to season and what needs to be done. Some work can be a bit demanding physically. We also have regular jobs in town so we will be away working part of the weekdays so then tasks need to be carried out independently. You should be comfortable working and spending time at the farm alone some days.

We grow our own hay and have local farmers helping us with the harvest, this normally take place late june.

We milk one of our goats and produce some dairy products for our own consumption, so if you like, you can make your own yoghurt or cheese, if there is enough milk. Note that we only have one milking goat so its on a very small scale. 

I have a room in my house for you to stay in, with a double bed. You are free to use the kitchen, bathroom, and living areas. If you prefer camping that can be organized. We can set up a tent under roof in the barn. This is a cozy way to spend time here and might be needed if there are m
_wwoof_travel:
Arlanda airport 2h by train or car. Train station 10 min by car or bus. Bus stop just outside the farm but only few buses a day.

We can pick up from trainststion in Hedemora or if you want to come here by bus.

name: Krista - Tropical Flowers/Moringa/Orchard/Orchid/Bees
description:
WE ARE  ACCEPTING APPLICATIONS FOR THE FOLLOWING SEASONS IN 2026!
JUNE 2026 - AUGUST 2026
AUGUST 2026 - OCTOBER 2026
OCTOBER 2026 - DECEMBER 2026
 
There are often time slots that bridge the above. (We also consider longer-term stays once you arrive and if the situation suits all parties)

Work hard and get your hands dirty on a sustainable tropical flower  farm! 

At its core, we are in the business of producing and selling Tropical Flowers, honey, fruit, & Moringa. We encourage both international and US applicants.   

Please note, experience in farming is not essential. We work hard and it's rewarding work, but it can be rough in the heat and rain.

Location:  
We are situated on the East side of Maui just 1.5 miles before the town of Hana. It is very remote with no consistent public transportation (we are 2 hours from Kahului airport) and little access to modern conveniences. We have one general store, one bank, a post office, a few food trucks, and that's it! Beyond that, gorgeous beaches, Haleakala National Park 9 miles away, and lots of fun to be had. There are ways to get involved in the beautiful community and a few festivals to enjoy and participate in each year. 

What we provide: 

We provide your food staples (rice, salt, pepper) and then you decide what else you would like and we will provide within a communal budget. All the fruit and vegetables grown on the land are shared. We share a communal kitchen.  

The accommodations are clean and neat and dry, but not the most fancy. You may share a room with one other person for a time, but we strive to give everyone their own room.  It is possible that we have up to 8 WWOOFers at a time here.  

You share a bathroom with your fellow WWOOFers that includes a hot water shower.  There is an outdoor shower with cool water and other toilet facilities at a lower warehouse. There is a washer/dryer for your use and WiFi.
_wwoof_travel: Kahului Airport on Maui (OGG) or Hana Airport in Hana. Schedules must be coordinated because we only drive out once every 2 weeks. This is an ecological decision we live by.

name: Ari - Ferme agroforestière (gemmothérapie, forêt jardin et agriculture syntropique)
description:
Bonjour, je suis Ari, paysan queer féministe à vélo sur ma petite ferme bio de 3 hectares dans le Limousin. Depuis 2022, j'implante un jardin forêt et des lignes d'agroforesterie syntropiques dans le but d'apprendre, d'expérimenter et de produire des extraits de gemmothérapie (macérats de bourgeons d'arbres médicinaux). Si vous n'avez jamais entendu parler de gemmothérapie je vous explique tout sur le site de la ferme : www.lafermedessoeurs.fr 
En parallèle, je rénove la maison et les bâtiments agricoles et je continue de me former auprès d'autres paysan.nes. Je vis sur la ferme avec mes deux chats Nala et Toulouse.

Pour accueillir les wwoofeur.ses, il y a une chambre de 12m² avec deux lits doubles. La chambre ne dispose pas de son propre chauffage, elle sera donc plus confortable à partir de mi-mars. Elle est néanmoins disponible toute l'année et sera un peu réchauffée par la cuisinière à bois de la maison (moult couvertures et bouillottes fournies). La salle de bain de la maison est partagée ainsi que l'espace de vie. Les repas sont vegan et préparés ensemble ou à tour de rôle.

IMPORTANT : Pour venir à la ferme, il vous faudra idéalement une paire de bottes (ou chaussures de rando l'été) et une paire de sandales/claquettes pour entrer et sortir de la maison facilement. Des vêtements de pluie ou une casquette si la météo le demande et des vêtements qui ne craignent pas d'être abîmés. En été prévoyez également des t-shirts manches longues et pantalons (tiques et coups de soleil).

Les journées ne se ressemblent pas et mon temps de travail varie énormément d'une saison à l'autre. Nous travaillons ensemble le matin et vous pouvez décider de continuer ou non avec moi l'après midi. J'essaie de prévoir des activités les plus variées possibles et d'alterner certaines plus physiques avec d'autres plus tranquilles. J'aimerais accueillir des personnes curieuses et motivées pour partager mon quotidien mais aussi apprendre d'elleux ! 
Aucune forme de discrimination n'est tol

name: Emma - Community in south-west Scotland - rural, organic walled garden, animals, woodland, pasture
description:
*****We are currently fully booked for the 2026 summer season*****

We are a housing co-operative based in Dumfries and Galloway in south-west Scotland, currently home to 23 adults and 6 children. 

We live in and around a huge Victorian main house, with a large organic walled garden, surrounded by 180 acres of beautiful woods, pastures and wetlands, stretching from a loch to the north, to the best wee sledging hill in Galloway to the south. Living spaces use wood for heating and cooking. A hydro supplies much of our electricity. We grow as much fruit and vegetables as we can, keep cows, pigs, hens and bees, and do most of our own maintenance.

We have community mornings most days, which WWOOFers can join in with. These mornings are based on gardening, land maintenance, processing firewood, and house maintenance. There is always lots to do, particularly seasonal garden related activities such as weeding, harvesting and processing. 

There is lots of knowledge and experience amongst the residents, some of whom have lived here for 50 years! A great opportunity to experience life in a well established community and learn about many aspects of self-sufficiency.

WWOOFers will stay in a large, comfortable static caravan next to the main house, with access to a kitchen and bathrooms. We also have space for tents and a campervan.

We will provide you with food to cook for yourselves, including lots of fresh veg and fruit from the garden when in season. Most families here have their own kitchens, where WWOOFers may be invited for the occasional evening meal. We usually have a few communal BBQs through the summer too.

We are ideally looking for 2 WWOOFers at a time, for 2 week stays.

A few of us are keen volley ball players, with regular evening games through the summer. 
Our loch is a short walk through lovely woodland, has a small beach and is an excellent swimming spot. We are on the edge of Galloway Forest Park, with lots of excellent cycle routes, swimming spots and h

name: Germaine - Come grow organic food, learn to make pepper sauce and more on the southern coast of Trinidad
description:
Note: We have been active on carribean wwoof since 2014. Recently independants wwoof took over carribbean wwoof so we have been building our program since 2014 even though the site doesn't reflect that.

Our farm is a cooperative organic farm led by a loving Trinidadian family. The farm includes a food-preserving project, producing local fruit preserves, seasonings and hot pepper sauce. Volunteer activities include: working on the farm, selling at the market, making food preserves, baking in our clay ovens, clearing the pond & river, helping out at the local school, cleaning elderly people's yards, working in Giselle's home garden, and helping community members learn to plant. In your spare time, you will take a dip in the ocean, sway in a hammock, and dance with family and friends.

Nestled in the coastal range of southern Trinidad, the farm is the work of a small cooperative of local farmers. It is run by the most positive and supportive families, led by Noriga, his daughter Giselle, Giselle's husband, Ottis, and their son Jahshel. Crops under cultivation include banana, plantain, papaya, hot peppers, pimento peppers, cucumber, tomatoes, okra, rice, herbs, breadfruit, coconut, yams, casava, and eggplant. Plum, mangos, and pomme cythere are also sourced from the village and forest.

The warm and loving extended family of this farm is what renders this project an exceptional cultural, as well as agricultural, experience. Since few tourists or outsiders come to this community it is important to be both flexible and respectful of the family and local culture. Volunteers should also be flexible in terms of amenities, considering local conditions. The family believes in self-sufficiency and upliftment for their community. The experience of authentic immersion in a different culture is what makes this volunteer opportunity unique. You may find yourself dancing to reggae in the kitchen or reasoning on politics and philosophy in the garden. 

There are two farms, one off t
_wwoof_travel: Option 1: We can arrange a car to pick you up at the airport (for a fee) Option 2: You can travel by public transport, but we only recommend this between 5am-2pm. It will cost @100TT from the airport.

name: Jimmy - Quinta la amistad es un proyecto familiar
description:
We have space for you from today until Space available from today until January 1st.

This is a family project.
It is a charming place located in Horquetas,
a rural area known for its natural beauty and peaceful atmosphere.
The place is ideal for those looking to disconnect from their routine and
connect with nature, living with all the modern comforts surrounded by the morning sounds of howler monkeys some days and other times the sound of hundreds of monkeys welcoming the sunrise.

Horquetas is a picturesque rural community in the province of Heredia, surrounded by mountains, rivers, and tropical forests. We are neighbors to Braulio Carrillo National Park.
A rich biodiversity is very close to San José, an ideal place for hiking, mountain biking, or simply a rural area to escape the routine of the city or crowded tourist spots.

Living Nature:

Large green areas with gardens and fruit trees: orange, mandarin, mango, guava, lemon, sunflower seeds (coconut), grapes, and more. We have a tilapia farm and are planning to build a duck pond. This is an area home to secondary and primary forests in the mountains, which are home to a great diversity of flora and fauna.
The presence of rivers, waterfalls, and nearby streams is ideal for getting away from the routine.

My name is Jimmy (project manager), and I live and work on the project in Horquetas Sarapiquí.
I work with my uncle Miguel on his project.
I'm a very active builder, I'm very talkative, I love dogs, and crafts. I've worked on many projects with volunteers in different locations in Costa Rica, so I have a lot of experience hosting volunteers. I speak a little English and love meeting new people and sharing stories.
I'm a builder who loves permaculture, self-sustaining construction, exotic plants, mountains, and nature. I'm a bit off the grid; my life has been day-to-day living for the past 16 years.

This is a family project that has been formed with the help of many friends, family, and volunteers for almost 30

name: Connie - Die Oase ruft!
description:
1. Beschreibe dein Projekt, den Ort und seine Bewohner (einschließlich Tiere):
Wir leben etwas ausserhalb der City, trotzdem zu Fuss oder mit dem Fahrrad gut zu erreichen. Wir pflegen einen Garten von ca. 4000 m2 mit Olivenbäumen und Dattelpalmen. Ich lebe seit 2008 in der Oase und meinen ägyptischen Partner kenne ich seit 2010. Die Oase Siwa ist die grösste Oase Ägyptens. Die Menschen hier leben von tiefen Wasserquellen, das hier sehr beliebte Siwa Wasser. Im Salzsee wird seit einigen Jahren auch Salz abgebaut. Die Bewohner besitzen alle Ihre Grundstücke mit Dattelpalmen und Olivenbäumen. Natürlich sind die Jungen Männer lieber mit einem Allrad unterwegs und führen die Touristen in die Wüste.

2. Liste die Aktivitäten auf, an denen WWOOFer teilnehmen werden, und was du ihnen beibringen möchtest:
Wir arbeiten das ganze Jahr über im Garten und hegen und pflegen unsere Bäume auf organische Art. Das liegt uns sehr am Herzen. Unsere Datteln werden unbehandelt, teils handgelesen weitergereicht. Sehr beliebt sind sie von meinen Freundinnen und deren Familien in der Schweiz. Aber natürlich gehen die meisten direkt vor Ort in eine Kooperative.
Die Teilnehmer/innen, die uns bei der Ernte unterstützen werden, lernen viel über die Pflanzen. Aber ein Anliegen, das mir besonders am Herzen liegt, sind Weidearbeiten (Flechten) mit den dünnen Ästen der Palmen, wo die Früchte dran hingen. Da habe ich seit Jahren die Äste gebündelt und gesammelt, damit ich gemeinsam mit Freunden Basteln kann.

3. Wie könnte der Tag eines WWOOFers bei dir aussehen (Zeitfenster für die Teilnahme an Aktivitäten, Erwartungen...)?
Zeitig am Morgen ist es am angenehmsten Datteln und Oliven zu pflücken. Nach einem kleinen Frühstück gehts zum Pflücken. Nach ca. 3 Stunden gibt es dann richtiges Frühstück. Ist also nichts für Langschläfer! Nach dem Frühstück kann jeder Teilnehmende machen was er möchte. Der Swimmingpool lädt zum Baden ein, die Bibliothek hält Bücher bereit und es gibt allerlei Spiele, die dara
_wwoof_travel: Borg al Arab, Internationaler Flughafen Alexandria oder Intern. Flughafen Cairo

name: Miren Gurutz - Baserriko es nuestra forma de vida familiar, en contacto y respeto con la naturaleza
description:
Nuestra familia esta compuesta por mi esposo y nuestros dos hijos, quienes vivimos de manera autosustentable desde el 2004 en Pelvin. Esta es una hermosa zona rural muy cerca de Peñaflor, a solo 40 km. al oeste de Santiago, la capital del país. Hay locomoción pública  y tambien tenemos bicicletas. 

Con que contamos? En nuestra granja cultivamos hortalizas y árboles frutales. También tenemos animales de granja, como cabras y ovejas lecheras, gallinas, perros y gatos, todo de manera sostenible, en kilometro cero y con colaboracion de pequeños proveedores locales. Tenemos un taller de restauración para motocicletas y autos antiguos como pasatiempo. Hacemos queso en nuestra pequeña queseria y cava, pan, deshidratados, fermentados, destilados.  Utilizamos abonos naturales y reciclamos parte importante de lo que generamos, incluyendo las aguas grises.

Que necesitamos y podemos ofrecer a nuestros wwoofers? La ayuda que esperamos es de terreno, compostaje, animales, jardinería, manejo de humedales, construcción, elaboración de queso, mantenciones menores. También aceptamos con mucho gusto ideas y propuestas para mejorar lo que tenemos!!. El alojamiento es en una cabina sencilla con baño seco y ducha solar. La estadia puede ser desde 1 día hasta 1 semana; la  ayuda es de lunes a viernes, 5 horas diarias. Las comidas se elaboran principalmente con productos propios o productos orgánicos locales, sabores del campo de Chile y Euzkadi. Tambien permitimos que nuestros wwoofers, puedan cocinar y compartir su comida más tipica.

Que experiencias ofrecemos adicionalmente? Realizamos cursos personalizados de elaboracion natural de quesos, sin aditivos artificiales, con leche cruda y pasteurizada, la cual ordeñamos para entender el proceso desde su origen. Degustacion de los quesos y subproductos lacteos que elaboramos.

Estamos ubicados en una zona turística y vinícola, con muchas atracciones y lugares para caminar, a dos horas de las principales playas y montañas de los Andes y a
_wwoof_travel: Desde aeropuerto se puede tomar transporte publico a Santiago, luego el metro. Por medio de transporte publico se llega a Peñaflor, donde podemos recoger a nuestros wwoofers previa coordinacion

name: Linda , Francois, Vanja, - Family farm
description:
We are a family of four. Linda has an agricultural background, from a mountain dairy farm in Norway, ceramist from profession. François is french and a ceramist 2nd generation. We bought this farm 15 years ago, and are in the process of upgrading the numerous buildings on the farm, and establishing a large and varied organic orchard and vegetable garden and greenhouse based on permaculture.   Our sheep graze in the mountains in summer and stay at the farm in winter. We try to be as self sufficient as posssible. We have also built a large woodfired kiln and a ceramic workshop that now is turning.  Woofers are lodged independently in a comfortable cabin with the space for 2 persons. Couples or single travellers are all welcome. We all share the lunch meal, and you prepare breakfast and supper amongst woofers. We will supply you with local fish and meat and good homemade food. If you are vegetarian it is of course not a problem.

Certifications: Debio, 60 % of our land , including all orchards and vegetable gardens

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel:
Strandebarm, by the Hardanger Fjord, Norway

good bus connection from Bergen, bus stops by the farm

name: Leo - Culture d’açaí sous les tropiques
description:
1. Décrivez votre projet, votre lieu, et ses habitants (y compris les animaux) :

Surrounded by lush forests Fazends Monto Alegre is a farm planted with cocoa and açaí in an agroforestry system. It belongs to European associates who live happily in a rural and calm setting near the sea. the Belgian  associates of the farm practice agroecology and are defenders of the cabruca cropping system (plantations under the cover of the thinned forest). We are members of an association of small producers and a cocoa cooperative.

2. Listez les activités auxquelles les WWOOFeurs participeront et ce que vous désirez leur apprendre :

plantation maintenance (mowing, pruning) and harvesting. Diversified work and most often in the shade... Development of biofertilizers and use of forest humus . We also make artisanal chocolate.

3. A quoi pourrait ressembler la journée d'un WWOOFeur chez vous (plage d'aide aux activités, vos attentes...) ?

we like to diversify the activities, one hour of mowing, one hour of pruning, one hour of humus harvesting, etc. We usually share meals and like to cook together, and happy to discover your own culinary specialities.

4. Jouez le guide ! Parlez de vos propres loisirs (musique, cuisine, etc.) et ceux possibles aux alentours (randonnée, baignade, etc.) :
The fazenda is 12 km of dirt roads from Camamu where there is public transport where you can take boats to Barra Grande, a well-known seaside resort.

name: Art - A Little Oasis Farm in the Okanagan
description:
Hey there, fellow adventurer! Thanks a bunch for checking out our profile. Feel free to shoot us a message if you like what you see—we're quick to reply, usually within a day. When you drop us a line, share your preferred arrival and departure dates. If you possess any special skills or experience, such as digital marketing, mechanical aptitude, or gardening expertise, please make sure to highlight these in your profile. Your unique talents can greatly contribute to the diverse range of activities we undertake here. Dive into the details below, and don't hesitate to ask if you've got any questions on your mind!

Upon retiring from the business world, I found myself drawn to the principles of sustainability, as well as the pursuit of a healthier and more peaceful lifestyle. It's truly remarkable how much I've learned from engaging and interacting with the younger generation, including my own children. They have served as insightful spokespeople, emphasizing the urgent need to protect our planet and highlighting the importance of fostering harmony among all its inhabitants.

My farm spans a generous 8 acres of land. Currently, approximately half of this space is dedicated to flourishing apple trees. The remaining half of the farm is undergoing a gradual transformation, as I introduce a diverse range of fruit trees and cultivate a variety of vegetables. This process reflects my commitment to sustainable agriculture and my aspiration to create a harmonious ecosystem that thrives on diversity and balance. We don't have any farm animals, apart from my dog and the rescue cats we foster. As a non-commercial farm, we primarily donate our produce to local charities, contributing to the community in meaningful ways.

While staying with us, you'll find yourself engaged in many different tasks, not all of which are strictly related to farming or confined to the farm premises. Your responsibilities will encompass a wide range of activities, including planting, weeding, harvesting
_wwoof_travel: Nearest bus station is in Vernon and the nearest airport is in Kelowna.

name: Wayne - Organic, Regenerative and Resilient Living Ranch
description:
Our campus is in two locations in and near the Idaho Rocky Mountains where we have a small urban farm near Boise and a much larger regenerative ranch in the high desert near Marsing , Idaho (1 hour from Boise). We use the facilities for research and development of aquaponic systems, hydroponic systems, and regenerative farming. We strive to improve upon previous methods of intensive yet regenerative and sustainable agricultural systems. aquaculture (fish farming) and hydroponics. We are also in the early stages of creating an intentional community where spirituality and resilience are major goals and factors guiding the community development.

For almost 17 years, we operated a large ranch in Colorado, where we have hosted volunteers for almost 13 years. We have been here in Idaho for almost two years and we have recently acquired our Ranch near Marsing, Idaho.
