Our recent move to Idaho was to expand our regenerative living to a much larger ranch that we are early in the process 
of developing . Summers are wonderfully mild and dry while winters are cool and pleasant. We do have snow, but daytime hours are almost always above freezing. We have a wide array of climate changes.

Our base location is only a short  20 minute drive from Boise with skiing in winter, year around fishing, hiking, biking and all kinds of outdoor activities. Our site near Marsing is about an hour from our site in Boise and our lodging there is currently a nice RV with full year around services, including internet using Starlink. We have excellent cell  phone coverage and unlimited at both locations Wi-Fi availability. We can arrange for transportation for you while you are staying with us, in many cases. 

Guests stay in a modern two bedroom apartment with all the amenities of home while they are in our Boise location and for now we commute (about a 35 mile drive) to our Marsing site. You will have a fully stocked private kitchen and bathroom in the Boise area. When you stay overnight in Mars

name: Laura - We are a farm to table house hold
description:
Hello, my name is Laura. I’m 38 years old and live in rural northern Alberta with my husband Shayne (48) and our two boys, ages 8 and 15, on our 70-acre homestead that we built from the ground up.

Although we have beautiful scenery here, and there are many grand places in the world, that’s not really what this is about. This life isn’t something I put on display or try to capture. I don’t build it for an image — I live it. And what I’m offering is the chance to step into that and experience it for yourself.

It’s an immersed, lived, grounded, and wholesome way of life — not rushed because we have to, but motivated because we want to.

Some days are more relaxed, focused on basic chores and daily routines, and other days are more project-based where we work together to get bigger things done. We often have friends or family around helping as well, so it’s a balanced and social way of life.

My husband Shayne is incredible. He works less than half the year so he’s right here with me planning, creating & enjoying. 

We have access to a sawmill and woodworking equipment, and we have a large supply of lumber. A lot of what we build comes from what we have available, and you’ll get to see and be part of how we use it to create what we need.

Your experience here will be a slower, yet productive way of life. We are not off-grid, and we are not a large production farm. There is space here — space for autonomy, for learning, for exploring, and for gaining a true understanding of rural living and community.

We moved here from Ontario and built this life from the ground up. We left behind an urban lifestyle and chose northern Alberta to step away from light pollution, overconsumption, and the constant rush — not just for ourselves, but for our children. Piece by piece, we created something that feels real and honest to who we are.
My children do NOT have cell phones or tablets. We do have a TV and internet lol! 

My days are filled with what matters — the land, the quiet, my
_wwoof_travel: Bus Peace River. Airplane Grande Prairie

name: Lindsey - We are a small goat farm in Northern British Columba Canada
description:
THANK YOU EVERYONE FOR ALL YOUR INTEREST IN OUR FAMILY FARM.AS OF RIGHT NOW WE ARE FULLY BOOKED FOR WOOFERS NOW TILL THE END OF MAY OF 2027. 

 Hello WWOOFer's my parents (Terri 69 and Darryl 69 and myself (Lindsey 43)  run a small 36 acre, 30 Doe goat farm and Dog Boarding Kennel about 11 miles north of Fort Nelson BC Canada. We have both dairy goats (Nubian and LaManacha) and meat goats (Boer Kiko cross)  on the farm as well as 14 horses, 4 ducks, 2 geese, 20-30 chickens, 6 rabbits and even a pair of Peafowl. We also have 9 dogs and 3 barn cats that ALL love to scratch and played with daily We also plant a vegetable garden and have a few fruit trees which we can the fruit vegetables from each fall. Day to day we spend a lot of time outside whether is is sunny and warm or cold and blowing snow. In late spring summer early fall months Mid May to Mid September Fort Nelson get a huge amount of daylight hours and a lot of the time in July and August it doesn't get dark in Fort Nelson all so we have huge growing season for our gardens, so we able to grow huge variety of plants through out the summer. In the winter months we also get a lot of sunny days so it helps on those very cold days, and in the winter we will often have Norther Lights that is dance amazingly across the night skies. We are very lucky to live in a area in Northern Canada that is VERY  Diverse and so very Beautiful and breathtaking.  Thank you for taking the time to read though out host profile and we hope to hear from and even better get to meet as many amazing people as we can !!

On typical day on the farm we get up and have tea or coffee and then go out and check all the animals and feed the ones who need morning feeding. Than we come back in the house for Breakfast and make a plan for the day on what we are hoping to get done, which may or may not also get done but if it doesn't that OK! 
Fencing is always something we are working on as animals always seem to brake something.  We aren't a huge fa
_wwoof_travel: Fort Nelson Airport or Fort St John Airport

name: Alejandra - Organically-certified farm growing outdoor vegetables and raising sheep for dairy.
description:
Greetings! We are three families regenerating a farm with the aim of achieving food sovereignty in Iceland. We were motivated to leave our city life behind and focus on regeneration of the Earth and aiming for promoting sustainability on our community. So here we are, five years later, making our dreams a reality in our farm paradise in the beautiful Svarfaðardalur valley in the North of Iceland. We have beautiful mountains surrounding our farm which are perfect for hiking. We also have a river border which is a calming asset when working out in the field. Inside of our home, we have one cat and one dog living with us.

We are multicultural families with anthroposofical values, which we apply to our lifestyle and environment. We have experience with Waldorf education, Biodynamic farming and holistic medicine approach.

During this time of the year, we are looking for travelers with gardening experience and also with carpentry experience, since the home projects currently developing are building and fixing old infrastructure, fencing, building a greenhouse, etc.

We will provide a private room which is conveniently located close to the bathroom and kitchen. Food dynamic will work differently for each meal: Breakfast items will be available for anyone to help themselves. Warm lunch will be prepared by rotating members of the house and/or volunteers. Dinners can vary between warm meals or cold where anyone can help themselves.

Our volunteers enjoy of the perks of enjoying horseback riding, going to the local hot pools, or visiting art exhibitions in the local Culture House or art spaces in the region. We have a car available for local transport which we could borrow to volunteers with driver's license and experience driving manual car.

name: Bruce - We raise and train sled dogs
description:
My name is Bruce Noksana I am an Inuvialuit from Tuktoyaktuk, Northwest Territories near the Arctic Ocean. I own a touring company called Noksana Mushing Tours with 27 sled dogs.  I live with Michele Tomasino with her husky Saba.

I want to teach WWOOFers about the Inuvialuit culture and how to raise sled dogs. An average day with us includes feeding dogs twice a day and cleaning up after the dogs, depending on the day and weather, we also train and run the dogs. We also pull fish nets in the summer and fall to stock food for the dogs for the winter. We cook dog food in a large caldron at the dog yard or at the house depending on the season.

We have a 3 bedroom house with a private room for the Wwoofer. We provide food available all day and have dinner together on weekdays and all meals together on weekends.

We live a traditional lifestyle, with locally harvested food. For instance, we pull fish nets to provide us with fresh fish, and store fish for the dogs for the winter. We participate in local events and activities in the community that include traditional dancing and singing and sporting events, such as Northern Games. This is a small town of 950 people. There is a local gym and fitness center. There are hikes and beaches to explore to the Pingos and on the shoreline of the Beaufort Sea/Arctic Ocean. We travel by snowmobile and side by side to the dog yard and to events.

name: Douglas - Flower and vegetable garden
description:
ABOUT ME
I grow organic vegetables and flowers in a greenhouse to sell locally and do yard gardening.

Ideal Woofer 
I require a woofer who is pleasant and neat and helps in my garden. 

Typical Day 
Start 8 am and end 12 pm. Work duties would involve general chores to gardening which would include: watering, weeding, fertilizing, picking produce.
During the woofer's free time, the woofer can explore the islands with the provided bicycle. Host is willing to assist woofer in seeing the rest of the islands. 
 
About My Community
I am located centrally on Haida Gwaii. There are approximately 4500 people on these islands. There is access to all sorts of outdoor activities such as swimming, kayaking, fishing, hiking, crabbing, camping, cycling and surfing. To find out more ideas on what to do on Haida Gwaii, you can visit www.gohaidagwaii.ca. 

HELP NEEDED NOW AND ALL YEAR ROUND!I live in the middle of the largest of the beautiful Queen Charlotte Islands, Haida Gwaii.  live at the edge of Stewart Bay and operate a small garden .  Grow organic tomatoes, cucumbers, carrots and many flowers. Wwoofers have there own private room in my home and are expected to work 20 hours per week. Help is needed at all times of the year.Please contact me for more information.

I own a 1000 square foot greenhouse, have blueberries, strawberries, raspberries and fruit trees (apples, cherries, plums, apricots, peaches, pears, and nectarines).

Organic methods: Composting

SKILLS TO SHARE
Plant pollination, plant propagation, produce harvest, produce selling at farmer's market, basic cooking, food preservation (canning & freezing), composting.

name: Lorraine - Permacultural and inclusive homesteading, farming, beekeeping, and forestry in lovely Central Texas
description:
We’re a permacultural homestead, apiary, and small farm on a beautiful piece of land in rural Texas, full of lush green hills, towering pines, oak savannahs, and wild animals, about 1 hour from Austin. The population varies and includes first-generation Americans, native Middle Easterners, and LGBTQ+ people. Over time, we hope to build a sustainable community here, starting from our family and friend group and beyond. Lorraine is one of the original community members and landowner, and she and Jack will be WWOOFer's main point of contact and coordinator

We have a vegetable plot that we are expanding toward “small farm” status using permacultural techniques (plant interdependence, water catchment, on-site composting and bee + hand pollination, greywater, etc.). We planted about 30 native pecan trees this year and have many persimmons, with more fruit and nut trees to come. Our new chicken coop will be occupied soon. We also spend a lot of time on regenerative forestry and have planted about 3000 native, drought-resistant loblolly pine seedlings (for habitat restoration, not for timber), and a few hundred other native trees. This requires reclaiming some space from invasives as well as some watering (until the trees are established). There is also a lot to forage much of the year (dewberries, mustang grapes, mulberries, sticky weed).

Our beekeeping operation is growing, and we’ll expand to a dozen hives by the end of the year. Our primary focus is taking care of the bees and appreciating their hard work (which keeps us all fed), but we harvest honey as well when the rain is normal. We’re in the midst of a drought, so it can take a lot of work to keep the hives healthy and mite-free.

A typical day would include watering, weeding, pollinating, composting, etc., but also helping us expand this operation: setting up new garden plots, building raised beds, dealing with the bees, and maintenance tasks. The bugs and weed are voracious here, and we love them everywhere exc

name: Glen - Working Farm Education Centre
description:
Our focus is on helping the rural farming poor to live better by going organic. So, if you are interested in learning organic farming or more interested in a cultural immersion, you will find those opportunities here. This is a hands on trade school and NOT an academic school.  It is a working farm providing practical training to the rural poor.  It is a  working farm where you will work hard and get dirty every day.

We have sheep, chickens, rabbits as well as a wide variety of fruit and vegetables. We are also into herbal medicine and grow many of those plants. Our students are young adults, male and female, with rural backgrounds who are eager to learn organic farming. Occasionally, we host high school students for a day of learning about organic farming. 
 
If you are interested in beginning a farm and living in Honduras, we could offer some guidance because we have done it several times and one of our goals is help poor Hondurans do the same.

It is not all work and no play. Many weekends we get off campus to relax. Sometimes it is swimming in natural hot springs, sometimes it's hiking in the mountains, sometimes it is enjoying cold water swimming under water falls, sometimes it's visiting other towns for rural local competition like horse riding, sometimes it's for pizza or a football game between our students and woofers versus another local team.

We are located 15 min. from town and 1.5 hour drive from the San Pedro airport. We are open to WWOOFers 365 days a year. However, during the Christmas new year holidays, there was only a skeleton crew at the farm. In other words, you will need to do your own cooking.

Our students are from many different areas of Honduras. In other words, as you work with and eat and hang out with them, you will be exposed to various areas and cultures of Honduras.  Being on campus is like being with and visiting multiple different families in various areas from near the border of Nicaragua to near the borders of El Salvador and Gu
_wwoof_travel: Airport San Pedro Sula.  San Pedro Bus Terminal.  Bus station here in Quimistan, Santa Barbara.

name: Jennie - Nonprofit focused on small farm advocacy and education
description:
Acadiana Growers Alliance (AGA)
As a 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to supporting small family farms in the Acadiana region of South Louisiana, AGA fosters a thriving local food system through education, advocacy, and tangible market-opportunities for growers.

What we do:
	•	We deliver technical assistance, peer learning, and outreach to small-scale and beginning farmers, helping them adopt regenerative and ecological practices.
	•	Through our Growers On The Go program, we provide mobile, on-farm mentoring, pop-up training events, and shared equipment/resources so that small family farms can scale production, improve profitability, and connect with regional markets.
	•	We manage a shared farmers market tent at regional events and markets, giving small growers visibility and an affordable way to access direct-to-consumer sales channels. This helps growers build their brands, streamline logistics, and meet customers face to face.
	•	We promote market diversification, local supply chains, and collaborative networks among growers, ranchers, processors and consumers.
	•	When you WWOOF with us, you’ll gain hands-on experience in small farm operations, direct marketing, community engagement, and regenerative agro-ecosystems — all while supporting family-scale farms that form the backbone of our region’s food landscape.

What you’ll do:
	•	Assist in on-farm activities: planting, weeding, harvesting, post-harvest handling, market preparation.
	•	Participate in setting up and staffing market tent events, helping growers connect with customers, handle sales, and learn about direct marketing.
	•	Join workshops or pop-up training sessions under the Growers On The Go program: topics may include soil health, cover cropping, pest management, packing/labeling, market strategy.
	•	Engage with the local community and network of growers: you’ll learn from and contribute to our broader mission of strengthening the region’s small-farm economy.

What we offer:
	•	Accommodation and meals (
_wwoof_travel: New Iberia Greyhound or Amtrak, Lafayette Airport

name: Malick - Educational Centre, Fruit tree nursery, Community farm
description:
The Ubuntu Centre is a community-based project. Our mission is to combine agriculture, art and education on a 2,500 sq meter farm in Casamance, Senegal.
We are a Non-Profit Organization run by young artists from Ziguinchor. We grow veggies and citrus plants. We are also planning on building a small poultry section.

Organic methods: composting, rotation, permaculture

Certifications: Not certified but definitely organic

Current projects: Expanding accommodation capacities, building community training projects, building poultry section

name: Tone - Hobby Farm
description:
Ten minutes walk along the fjord we also have some Icelandic ponies and they would welcome some happy and calm woofers short term. Woofers will live in the little white cabin you see in the picture or in a giant greenhouse (bring tent :) ). The cabin is perfect for one:) but ok for two. The nature surrounding my garden is very nice and 200 meters from a nice path along the fjords. Downtown Stavanger is 15 minutes walk from the cabin. Check out "Gladmat", "Prekestolen", "Kjerag" etc. For me its ok if you want to work half time and then get your own food. There is a kitchen in the cabin and shops nearby. 

Certifications: Blog "elefantenemine.blogspot.no"
_wwoof_travel: Its 15 minutes walk from the center of Stavanger. If you like hiking we have Prekestolen and Kjerag here (daytrips) and lots of nature.

name: Mauricio - Organic Portuguese style farm with river in southern Brazil
description:
Hey how it’s going? I live in one of the most beautiful places in Brazil, between the ocean and the mountains, that is Imaruí in Santa Catarina, southern Brazil. We started a reforestation project a few years ago, we planted lots of native fruit trees, organic from the start and developing to become a Biodynamic and sustainable farm.

Usually what we like most is to prepare the soil, make it fertile and plant native trees.

We have a spare house just for guests and we can agree to have food together sometimes and other times you can cook by yourself

There are lots of amazing waterfalls nearby, river and some amazing beaches on the coast

name: Grober - Organización que trabaja con agricultores orgánicos en la Selva Peruana, Satipo.
description:
Hola, mi nombre es Grober y junto a mis compañeros en La Campiña Peru sac,  trabajamos en agricultura orgánica, biodinamica y Regenerativa con un gran número de agricultores en la Selva Peruana - Satipo.  Tenemos animales en nuestra granja y en la zona hay muchos lugares naturales para visitar en dias libres.

Son diversas actividades, desde siembra,  deshierbo, aporque, podamos, crianza de animales, tenemos cultivos de jengibre,  curcuma, cacao, naranja y también queremos aprender del wwoofer y compartir con los agricultores.

El alojamiento es cuarto privado con comodidades luz, agua, ducha, baño, Internet y cerca a centro de la ciudad.   Los alimentos se otorga todos los dias y se respeta si es vegano y otros gustos.

Nos gusta personas que quieran estar en la naturaleza, que quieran enseñar al mundo el trabajo de nuestros agricultores,  visitar las diferentes fincas.
_wwoof_travel: Aeropuerto Lima y Aeropuerto Mazamari - Satipo

name: Adélaïde - Ferme brebis laitière biologique de montagne
description:
Nous, Adélaïde et Quentin, venons de reprendre au 1er janvier 2026. C'est donc dans nos débuts de l'aventure que vous aurez l'occasion de partager un moment avec nous ! Cependant la ferme existe et accueille des woofers et stagiaires depuis longtemps, et nous avons déjà pu participer à ce fonctionnement. 

Le chapeau vert est une petite ferme Bio ecoconstruit à 1000 m d'altitude, en plein coeur du Parc Naturel Régional du Haut Jura. Située près de la Pesse, elle surplombe le village des Bouchoux. 

Aujourd’hui vivent sur la ferme soixante  brebis, dont le lait est transformé sur place,  2 chiens de conduite, 50 poules pondeuses, des canards, des chats, chevaux, bientôt des vaches... ainsi que nous, Adélaïde et Quentin.
Nous essayons d’élever de notre mieux ce petit monde et de trouver le meilleur équilibre entre leur nature, leurs besoins et les contraintes de l’élevage et de l’environnement.

Les activités de la ferme sont multiples: distribution du foin, traite,  fromagerie, récolte des oeufs, entretien des clôtures, travaux et bricolages sur la ferme, entretien des fleurs et du potager, partager quelques heures l'activité de berger… ou celle des foins en été.  Aide pour l'accueil des hôtes surtout le samedi, entrées, sorties, ménage. 

Nous pouvons vous accueillir du debut du printemps a la fin de l'automne pour échanger, vous faire découvrir notre vie paysanne et notre environnement de moyenne montagne, un sejour de 1 mois minimum est demandé. 

Nous privilégions des personnes avec des expériences / savoir faire d'artisanat, de bricolage, d'activités extérieures ou de soins des animaux. Bricoleuses, artisanes en tout genre, savoir faire dans les tissus, le bois, la laine, ou toute autre chose sont bienvenus !!  

Etre ok avec une certaine volonté d'autonomie, la consommation de notre propre viande ainsi que certaines réalités de la vie rurale (prédation, protection de nos animaux contre la faune sauvage...) 

Vie assez isolée, mais avec du monde sur le site en s

name: Audrey - Granja agroecologica
description:
Somos una pareja franco colombiana. Empezamos este proyecto de granja integral agroforestal en los Montes de María desde el 2021 , con la seguridad de que la transición comunitaria AGROECOLOGICA es fundamental para la construcción de paz.
Estamos aún en fase de adecuación de nuestra parcela de 7 ha hacia una producción de bosque de comida agroforestal y cría de cabras.
Nos gusta experimentar, aprender y compartir experiencias en pro de construir ese mundo más amable y resiliente...

Organic methods: Agroforesteria, abonos orgánicos, integración de especies menores, curvas de nivel, semillas nativas, policultivos.

Certifications: Aún en proceso de construcción del modelo

Current projects: Reforestación productiva: Estamos sembrando árboles frutales y maderables nativos del bosque seco tropical.

Huerta de autoconsumo.

Organizando un rebaño de cabras lecheras

Internet celular

name: David - Human scale Permaculture farming with hens, sheep, pigs and vegetables.
description:
Hi! We are David, and Tess! David is from Australia and Tess is from Uppsala. We have lived on our farm, Slinkom in Uppsala for 11 years, after leaving the restaurant business in Stockholm for a new more natural life with our two daughters and three cats. We have sheep, hens and pigs and make compost for our vegetables from their manure all of our produce we sell directly to our customers. We follow Permaculture and organic principles, all our work is done by hand. We are also currently building a production kitchen for processing local produce.

The work we do is varied from day to day and depends on the season. This includes moving sheep, collecting eggs, planting crops, packing orders for our customers or feeding the animals. 
We are currently working on a market garden and perennial food forest hedges,  building a production kitchen, renovating existing accommodation and a larger henhouse so there is plenty of opportunity to get involved in those projects.
We are both chefs and Tess is also a Sommelier, we spend most of our time raising food on our farm, gardening and cooking. We have a beautiful lake close by and we are there swimming as often as possible. 
Geographically we are located very centrally  with good connections so guests can easily visit other towns and cities.

Hej, vi heter David och Tess som driver denna gård. Vi flyttade ut till Slinkom för att leva ett mer naturligt liv med våra barn och katter. 
På gården finns det höns, får och grisar. Allt som vi producerar har på gården säljer vi direkt till kund, utan mellanhänder. Vi följer ekologiska och permakultur principer. Allt vårt arbetet utför vi för hand. 
Dom dagliga arbetsuppgifterna varierar. Det kan vara allt från att flytta djur, samla in ägg, plantera, packa beställningar till utfodring av djuren. 
Vi håller också på att anlägga en perenn skogsträdgård, bygga ett produktionskök, renovera gamla hus och bygga ut hönshuset. Så man kan även delta i dessa projekt. 
Vi kommer båda från restauran

name: Monika - Biodynamic vegetable farm and earth ship
description:
Welcome to a Manitoban prairie landscape with wild gras and poplar bluffs, immersed in endless skies.  In the midst of these vast landscape is nestled our complete off the grid earth ship. A passive solar house with special artistic features and fully independent.
We power our small farm with solar energy and collect and filter rain water in large cisterns.
The sun heats our building or the wood stove when it is cloudy. We have a small greenhouse as part of the earth ship where we grow all year round garden greens, herbs and trying some fruits.
Over the last 3 years we have developed a decent size garden for our own needs and market sales. We are expanding our growing area every year. 
We have pigs which we are raising healthy and happy and which support our gardening endeavours with digging and manure.
We are applying biodynamic practices on our farm and are enjoying the results in delicious and quality food and a nourished and improved healthy landscape. This year we are trying our first approach on natural beekeeping to integrate bees into our ecosystem.
We have seasonal events, farm to table dinners and a large camping area.
A barn, workshop/studios are in process of getting built.
You will mostly enjoy the quite wilderness at this location. You can enjoy a small pond in the camping area or drive just 20 min to one of the largest inland lakes, Lake Winnipeg with in the summer bustling events and recreation opportunities.
Not too far away is lake Manitoba with cleaner water and more majestic landscapes.
Overall our area is quiet but you can choose to join summer events nearby and some gatherings on our farm.
At the off grid farm are living me, Terry and our 2 dogs Morgan and Jasper. In the camping /tiny house area lives our friend Stephanie who works part time in the garden.
I am working mostly in the garden and in my handmade clothing production. 
There are building activities ongoing and the regular maintenance, weeding, planting seeding and harvesting in the m

name: Real - Maison familiale et jardin - rural
description:
Nous sommes un couple de personnes agé(e) moi 75 et Maryanne 71, nous nous intéressons autant au jardinage qu'a la foresterie, nous cultivons des jardins biologiques non certifiés, d'environ 1.5 acre. Nous avons des ruches, quelques arbres fruitiers -10 en tout- 150 espèces de fleurs vivaces, et plusieurs annuelles, nous cultivons nos légumes pour l'hiver et nos surplus sont acheminés vers un comptoir alimentaire (food bank).  Nous vivons dans une forêt de 105 acres dont une partie est de la forêt ancienne, nous coupons notre bois pour nous chauffer l'hiver, uniquement les arbres morts ou très malades sont coupés, nous aimons beaucoup essayer de faire pousser de nouvelles espèces de légumes ou de fruits. L'an passé ce fut les patates sucrées. Cette année nous avons planté deux nouvelles espèces de pommiers. 

Nous sommes tous les deux bénévoles d'un projet de production intensive de légumes pour les comptoirs alimentaires (food bank) de la région. Nous sommes très impliqués dans notre milieu et nous aimerions que les wwoofeur(es) travaillent  chez nous à la ferme et au jardin collectif, situé dans la petite ville de Mont-Laurier. 

Nous acceptons les woofers à partir de la  fin avril à la  mi-novembre. Nous avons l'accès à l'internet mais pas de wifi.

We are a couple in their seventies,. We are interested in gardening as much as forestry. We have non-certified organic gardens of about 1.5 acres. We have hives, a few fruit trees, 10 in all, 150 species, perennial flowers, and several annuals. We grow our vegetables for the winter and our surplus is directed to a food bank. We live in a forest of 105 acres which is partially old-growth forest. We cut our wood to heat for the winter, only the dead and dying trees are cut. We love to try to grow new species of vegetables or fruit. Last year it was artichokes and this year we tried sweet potatoes. 

We are involved with intensive vegetables production for several food kitchens. We are very involved in our community and

name: Besiki - I have a bio-organic farm, mini fruit tree plantation. Vineyard and winemaking family traditions
description:
We are friends Tata and Beso. We live in the village Kondoli (12 km from Telavi, Kakheti region, Georgia). Some days ago my friend gave me info about this site. The door for the guest are always open here. We are sure that our experience must be interesting for you dear guests. Two dogs and the 3 cats we have.

Сaring for plants, clearing the area of ​​weeds and stones, giving mineral medicines and nutrients to plants

Continental breakfasts we have and the local tea. Unlimited coffee. Local seasonal food for lunch, fruits in the evening.
we have 3 bedroom. Two rooms for 2 person, 1 room for 3 person.

We love growing and caring for plants, we love going on nature excursions and we love animals.

Мы друзья Тата и Бесо. Мы живем в селе Кондоли (12 км от Телави, регион Кахетия, Грузия). Несколько дней назад мой друг дал мне информацию об этом сайте. Двери для гостей здесь всегда открыты. Мы уверены, что наш опыт должен быть интересен для вас, дорогие гости. У нас есть две собаки и три кошки. Готовим вино и чачу - у нас свои виноградники.
Что мы делаем ещё: Уход за растениями, очистка территории от сорняков и камней, подкормка растений минеральными препаратами и питательными веществами. Мы любим выращивать растения и ухаживать за ними, мы любим ходить на экскурсии на природу и мы любим людей и животных.
У нас континентальные завтраки и местный чай. Неограниченный кофе. Местная сезонная еда на обед, фрукты вечером.
у нас 3 спальни. Две комнаты на 2 человек в каждом и 1 комната на 3 человек.

name: Skye (Marcus) - Organic Orchard on the Coast of NC
description:
We are starting blueberry, grape wine, mushroom and peach orchards on a 26 acre 🧑‍🌾 farm property.  

We may also do hemp and greenhouse plants. 

To start we are building a guesthouse (think AirBnB meets Hostel) on a one acre test farm near the main home for all our planned growth categories.

You'll help us with construction, clean up, feeding chickens 🐓 , pruning, planting, maintenance, irrigation, harvesting, cleaning grounds, clearing,  building & improving structures (carpentry,  trenching, land maintenance).

We have two campers at the test farm / guesthouse for Spring, Fall and Summer. 

Campers have working appliances and AC and can house 2-4 guests with pets. Pets will always stay with their owners in the campers.

We will dine together occasionally, and place food in refrigerators and storage for our WWOOFers to cook, eat and share.

A typical day starts early 600-730; you'll meet a project leader, go over the day plan, then work on a current project (e.g., planting the orchard, maintaining a building, fixing up a shed, cleaning an area, repair or grease a tractor, clearing land); we'll break at lunch and the rest of the day is off for our WWOOFers. Sometimes we may work a full day and give WWOOFers the next day off so they can explore the town,  nearby beaches or tourist sites. 

We expect willingness to work hard and get hands dirty, positive attitude, and independence. We have pets, so you should like dogs, cats, and chickens.

We are musicians and artists. We love hosting family get-togethers and music festivals up at the farm.  We have a few extra surfboards and bicycles available to WWOOFers for use at nearby Wrightsville Beach. You can also swim and kayak on the Cape Fear River across the road from the farm at Riverside Park. 

Eight miles away is historic downtown Wilmington, where you can take in world class live entertainment and visit WWII era Battleship USS North Carolina and star gaze at the Film & TV studies.

People (like us!) enjoy walking
_wwoof_travel: ILM International Airport, 8 miles. Greyhound station, Wilmington, NC

name: Vlad - Agroforesty organic small farm in the beautiful Jequitinhonha Valley
description:
Hello! My name is Vlad and I am an italian farmer who moved to Brasil two years ago, since i met this beautiful place , the Jequitinhonha Valley I understood that I belonged here. The "Cerrado" ecosystem, similar to the Savana is just magic, so much biodiversity, so resilient to the lack of rain.
I studied syntrophic agroforesty for many years and I had several experiences all around the world, then i decided to build my own farm here and the aim is to regenerate this land threatened by the Eucalyptus monoculture. I am part of the group of organic farmers of the region, and I live alone with a dog and a cat and tons of wild animals.

According to the period the work can be different, during the rainy season I take advantage of the soft soil to plant new agroforestry systems with the main focus on coffee plants, on Saturday I sell my products at the local market and when i have spare time i make some reforms at the house. To the Woofers I can teach how to plan, design and plant an agroforest system according to the objectives and the country.

Woofers will have their own room, every day fresh organic food is served, there is also a bakery oven where I like to make fresh bread and pizza, occasionally meat is served also.

The farm is surrounded by native forest and is close to the Araçuaí river with a very nice sandy beach. Turmalina is only 12 km from the farm and I go there at least twice per week, it's a very special and traditional region of the countryside of Mina Gerais, People are very nice and there are a lot of public events.

name: Mohamed - مزرعه خضروات  وفواكه
description:
"اسمي محمد يسري، عمري 32 سنة، من مصر وأقيم في محافظة البحيرة. أنا شخص نشيط وملتزم وأحب العمل الزراعي واليدوي، ولدي خبرة في زراعة الخضروات والفواكه. جوهر مشروعي هو أن أتعلم وأشارك في العمل الموسمي مثل زراعة الفراولة والخضروات، وأن أطور مهاراتي في الزراعة المستدامة. أعيش مع أسرتي المكونة من [عددافراد   6]، ولا أملك حيوانات أليفة."

"في الصيف أمارس أنشطة زراعية مثل زراعة الخضروات والفواكه، والاعتناء بالأرض والري والتسميد. أحب أن أشارك الآخرين خبرتي في العمل اليدوي البسيط والزراعة التقليدية، وأتعلم منهم طرق جديدة للزراعة المستدامة. يومي العادي يبدأ مبكرًا، أساعد في الأعمال الزراعية، ثم أرتاح قليلًا وأعود لإكمال العمل أو الاهتمام بالبيت، وأحب أن أقضي وقتي مع العائلة وأتعلم أشياء جديدة."

مكان الإقامة في بيت عائلي بسيط في محافظة البحيرة بمصر. الغرف مريحة وبها احتياجات أساسية. نقدم ثلاث وجبات يوميًا: الفطور، الغداء، والعشاء. الطعام مصري تقليدي يعتمد على الخضروات، الفواكه، الأرز، المكرونة، والفول، مع بعض الأطباق باللحم أو الدجاج. يمكن إعداد وجبات نباتية إذا رغب أحد الأعضاء."

"من هواياتي الاستماع إلى الموسيقى الهادئة، فهي تساعدني على الاسترخاء بعد العمل. كما أحب الطبخ وتجربة وصفات جديدة، خاصة الأكلات المصرية التقليدية. أجد متعة كبيرة في تحضير الطعام ومشاركته مع الآخرين."

---

أفضل أن يظل ملفي باللغة الإنجليزية، فهي لغة مريحة لي في التواصل."
أو لو تحب بالعربي:
"أشعر بالراحة مع اللغة العربية وأفضل أن يكون ملفي مترجمًا إليها بجانب الإنجليزية."

بالإنجليزي

name: Mariana - Sitio orgánico de piaçavas ,frutales,huerta,, jardín
description:
Soy Mariana y vivo en una pequeña facenda de piaçavas,frutales, diferentes cultivos,agua abundante de naciente y mata atlántica. Estoy muy cerca de diferentes playas hermosas. Años atrás he recibido woofers de distintas partes del mundo, en mi país de origen Argentina y de han generado lazos que muchos de ellos perduran hasta hoy!

2. Ayuda en general con los plantíos y limpieza del terreno

3.conocer la cultura y cultivos de Bahía Brasil 

Me gusta cocinar,hacer probióticos,comida variada  e ir a la playa que queda a 2 km de la Vila do Saguí . Proximamente comenzare con una.produccion de shitake y tu turas medicinales.Se puede ir a conocer varias playas q están cerca y también ciudades turísticas como Corona Vermelha,aldea de indios pataxos y Porto Seguro

name: Mamá - Una comunidad a 1h30 de San Cristóbal en medio de un bosque de 40 hectáreas en las montañas de Chiap
description:
Somos una comunidad dentro de otra comunidad indígena a 1h30 de San Cristóbal en medio de un bosque de 40 hectáreas en las montañas a 1700msnm. Estamos construyendo una vida alternativa,
Necesitamos apoyo con 
Permaculture, huertos organicos, bioconstrucción, carpintería, construir con materiales reciclados,juntar leña, cocinar a leña, hacer conservas, etc 
Nos reunimos todos a las 7 de la mañana en la cocina, tomamos un desayuno y distribuimos las tareas del día .
Tenemos una cueva grande y  20 hectáreas de bosque preservado para explorar, slakline, miradores, instrumentos de música, una pequeña biblioteca, hacemos esculturas de barro, a la noche nos gustan las fogatas, juegos de mesa creando siempre buen ambiente.
_wwoof_travel: Punta de reunión en San Cristóbal de las casas. Estamos

name: Eunmi - <CN123>아름다운 퍼머컬쳐 작은 농장 / A beautiful small permaculture farm
description:
✅ 호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
저희 농장은 퍼머컬쳐(지속가능한 농법)을 실천하는 농장입니다. 농장지기로 엄마 김은미(오실)와 딸 이서진(도담)이 함께 숲과 같은 밭을 가꾸고 있습니다. 저희는 도시에서 4년 전 귀촌하여 심신의 건강과 여유로움을 되찾게 되어 이를 계기로 농장을 지구 환경과 자연생태에 거스르지 않고 더불어 살 수 있는 환경으로 만들어 가고 있습니다. 또한 이를 사람들과 함께 공유하고 싶어 농촌 민박과 디저트카페를 함께 운영하고 있습니다. 

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2023년
✅ 농장 형태 : 가족농, 자급자족형
✅ 농장 주작물 : 베리류 및 과실나무

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 무농약, 무경운, 무화학 비료로 땅을 살리는 농법을 실천하고 있습니다, 인간의 최소한의 관여로 자연 스스로 공생 할 수 있는 후글컬쳐, 시트피복 등의 퍼머컬쳐 농법을 구현하고 자연에서 나서 다시 자연으로 돌아갈 수 있는 선순환 농법을 지향하고자 합니다.
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 : 잔여 음식물 퇴비화, 생태화장실을 통한 거름, 재활용분리수거, 플라스틱 최소화와 재사용, 팜투테이블, 자연에너지(태양광, 우수) 재활용

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 딸, 자주 방문하시는 친정어머니
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려동물 : 강아지, 고양이  
✅ 반려동물 허용: 실내 동반 취침 불가
✅ 우퍼가 하는일 :
-3~5월 : 일년생 쌈채소 파종 및 모종심기, 퇴비 주기, 가지치기 및 나무 가꾸기 등
-6월~8월 : 마늘 및 양파 수확, 퇴비 만들기, 옥수수 및 허브 가꾸기, 매실 수확 및 매실청 담그기 등
-9월~11월 :  깨 종류 수확, 수세미 수확 및 천연수세미 만들기, 가지치기 등
-12월~2월 : 온실 샐러드 채소 가꾸기, 봄 파종 및 거름 주기, 두둑 조성하기 등

✅ 선호하는 우퍼상 : 환경과 생명의 가치를 중요하게 생각하는 여성 우퍼 환영합니다. 
✅ 우퍼에게 당부 메세지 :  소규모 농장에서 다양한 체험을 해보고 싶으시거나 이 지역의 문화에도 관심을 가져주시는 우퍼

✅ 숙소에서 와이파이 가능여부 : 가능
✅ 술,담배 가능여부 : 간헐적 외부흡연, 및 음주가능
✅ 호스트와 식사여부 : 하루 한,두 끼만 함께 먹고 나머지는 식재료를 드려 직접 해 드셔야 합니다.
✅ 영어소통 : 기본 가능, 번역기 통해 가능
✅ 일주일 기준 우핑시간 : 하루5시간 주5일

======== English explanation ==========

✅ Host introduction and free farm introduction:
Our farm practices permaculture (a sustainable farming method). As farm keepers, mother Kim Eun-mi (Osil) and daughter Lee Seojin (Dodam) cultivate a field that is like a forest together. We moved from the city to the countryside four years ago and regained our physical and mental health and sense of ease, and from this experience we began to make our farm into an environment where we can live together without going against the global environment and natural ecosystems. We also wish to share this with others, so we run rural lodging and a dessert café together.

✅ Year Farming started: 2023
✅ Farm type: Family farm, self-sufficient style
✅ Main crops: Berries and fruit trees

✅ Practice of eco-friendly agriculture: We practic

name: Franca - Azienda  biologica
description:
Ciao sono Franca! 
Io e la mia grande famiglia viviamo in campagna e abbiamo una fattoria di cui occuparci.
Ho 1 figlio e 4 figlie, dai 10 ai 35 anni (sì, una grande differenza!) ma solo Elena, la piu piccola, vive con noi. Io e il mio compagno Massimiliano decidiamo di dedicare la nostra vita alla famiglia, prendendoci cura di loro scegliendo ogni giorno di realizzare tutto il possibile con le nostre mani. A me e alla mia famiglia piace avere un vero contatto con il mondo che ci circonda: con la natura, con il cibo e soprattutto con le persone.  Insieme a noi 3 vive pure il mio fratello Vincenzo che si occupa principalmente delle capre. Abbiamo un piccolo ristorante e 3 camere che affittiamo ai turisti, ma principalmente in estate. Ad agosto non prendiamo volontari perché dedichiamo molto tempo alla ristorazione.
Durante il resto dell' anno  coltiviamo ortaggi e alberi da frutto, se fa il tempo giusto raccogliamo le erbe spontanee commestibili. Alleviamo molti animali, molti dei quali proprio come animali domestici e altri per sostenere la nostra famiglia.

Da Gennaio a Maggio, si mungono le capre e si fa il formaggio.  Il resto dell'anno,  le attività sono diverse come dare da mangiare ai animali, l'orticoltura,  il diserbo, la manutenzione/pulizia delle stalle e la cura del giardino e del terreno. Se volete potete lavorare in cucina insieme a noi quando abbiamo gli ospiti.

 Vendiamo i nostri prodotti in azienda,  ai turisti a tavola!

Ci piace sperimentare e scambiare conoscenze.
Noi difficilmente  ci spostiamo dall'azienda e speriamo sempre di avere ospiti che ci portano le loro esperienze.
---o----o-----0-----o----o---

I have 1 son and 4 daughters, from 10 to 35 years old (yes, a big difference!) but only Elena, the youngest, lives with us. My partner Massimiliano and I decide to dedicate our lives to the family, taking care of them and choosing every day to acc

name: Barbara - A small homesteading farm with diverse production:  sheep, meat chickens, tree fruit, and vegetables
description:
Hello, WWOOFers--at our farm in Ithaca, NY, you will find two older (and hopefully a bit) wiser women who have built a small homesteading farm.  Our goal with the farm is to produce much of the food we eat as well as to provide a diverse ecosystem so that wildlife will flourish just as much as we do on these forty-two acres.  We have a small flock of sheep that we raise for fiber and meat, a nice vegetable garden, fruit trees, and we raise meat chickens once a year.  We have a very large and sweet dog, Huckleberry.

We offer two and three day weekend stays at our farm.  We do not have the ability to offer longer-term farm stays. We prefer to host folks over the weekend, either Friday through Sunday or Saturday through Monday.  Visiting for just the weekend is OK as well.

Farming work is season-driven, but tasks will include watering, weeding, harvesting and canning, helping with animal care, removing invasive plants and planting or caring for our young trees.  Both of us are retired horticulturists, so we have a lot of knowledge about the care of trees, shrubs and gardens to share.  We envision that we will work alongside you in the mornings, say from 9 am to noon, and then you will be free the rest of the day.

We have two spare rooms in our house that you can use.  If you prefer, we have a large tent we can set up by the pond for more privacy and intimacy with nature.

We are omnivores, but can cook vegetarian as well.  Vegan will take some coaching, input and participation on your part.  Much of our food will be from our farm, especially in the summer and fall.

Ithaca is a beautiful place--the Finger Lakes, waterfalls, lots of hiking, wineries and cideries are very close by.  We are about a 15 minute drive from the center of Ithaca.

We enjoy working with fiber that we get from our sheep--washing fleece, carding, spinning, dyeing and knitting.  We would be happy to teach you some of those skills.  We also love working in our woods and going for hikes.

name: Jessica - Former WWOOFer turned host 👩‍🌾 with new no-till regenerative fruit, veg and flower farm in IL
description:
Adama Farm is a new 2-acre no-till farm focused on organic and regenerative farming practices.

My name is Jessi — a former WWOOFer turned full-time farm owner and farmer. After several years of apprenticing and working on farms across the U.S., I now operate Adama Farm full time.

I am seeking WWOOFers who are genuinely interested in no-till production farming and are serious about taking their skills to the next level.

🌱 Commitment Expectations:

WWOOFing at Adama Farm is a serious commitment. Please do not apply if you are not ready to fully engage.

I rely heavily on WWOOFers to honor their commitments — it takes a village, and we operate as a team. In return, WWOOFers will gain extensive hands-on experience and leave with the skills needed to manage a small production farm or start their own.

Preferred stay:
• Full season: April 1 – November 1 (priority)
• Short-term stays: minimum 1 month

🌾 Apprenticeship-Style Experience:

This experience closely resembles an apprenticeship. WWOOFers will gain a full understanding of small-scale production farming through:

Daily field work

Rotating responsibilities

Managerial roles

Educational discussions and hands-on training

⏰ Work Hours:

Monday–Friday: 7:00 AM – 12:00 PM
(8:00 AM start early spring & late fall)

Saturday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM
(Farmers market, 1–2x per month. WWOOFers rotate working the Saturday farmers market)

Sundays off.

🧺 Farmers Markets:

WWOOFers rotate working the Saturday Park Ridge Farmers Market and receive modest pay. Markets provide valuable experience in customer interaction, sales skills, and understanding production planning.

🚜 About the Farm:

Approximately 1.5 acres are in active production, growing no-till vegetables and cut flowers for:

Farmers markets

Wholesale outlets

CSA program

On-farm store

WWOOFers will spend most days in the field with Jessi preparing beds, planting, weeding, pruning, and harvesting.

🍎 Fruit & Perennials:

In addition to vegetables and flowers, the f

name: Randy - Organic farm and vineyard located in the hills of the Willamette Valley in Oregon
description:
This is an organic farm and vineyard located in the hills of the Willamette Valley in Oregon. The vineyard is the main enterprise and is comprised of five main cultivars- Gruner Veltliner, Muller Thurgau, Riesling, Pinot Gris, and Pinot Noir. We turn most of our fruit into wine while selling the rest to other wineries in the area. We also have an acre garden, chickens, and sheep. We are a young couple and home to 3 dogs.

 We are eager to show how to bake, cook from scratch meals every night, and go hiking to share in all of oregon's beauty nearly every month. We are educators at heart and want to show and share everything we have. We are also learning ourselves and would love to learn from our skilled woofers!
Project- In addition to all of the maintenance and yearly vineyard tasks, we are expanding our garden and introducing more livestock this year. We will have chickens we raise for meat in addition to our current egg layers. We are a small family operation with never more than 1 or 2 employees. 
In January-March, the main task in the vineyard is pruning and brush pulling. In the garden, brassicas and preparing new beds for the upcoming season. Also baby chicks!
March-May- the most beautiful time of year where everything is in bloom. Shoot thinning and trellis management in the vineyard is the #1 task, Cherry harvesting, planting out the summer vegetables, and rearing chickens. We also do a big bottling run where we bottle all the white wines made in the previous year. 
June-August- In the hot summer is the height of growing season! Hedging, canopy management, and beautiful weather is in store for us. The garden is usually in full swing and we reap the benefits of our hard labor in the spring time. Rafting on the river is a weekly activity, and when we are finished with a hard days worth of work we BBQ and smoke meats for big meals in the evening! 
September - November - the most magical time! This is harvest season, and is the most rewarding time of the year. T

name: Silje - Family farm
description:
Dear Wwoofer

We are a family of 6 people (5 children), we have two dogs, two cats and rabbits at the moment (but are planning to also have a few goats in the future)

We have two small hobby-"farms". We live in Ulnes, Valdres, but we also have a small farm in Søndre Land (about 1 h 15 min drive) from where we live. We welcome you to participate in activities at both places together with us.

In both places will be similar task:  restauration of buildings, set up a vegetable garden, prepare the barn and outdoor area for having a few goats +++ , fruit and berry picking and conservation, firewood making etc.

Visiting us requires that you like children:) 
Accomandation will be in our house , but there is also space for your own camper if you have one. We will spend time together in the afternoons/evenings and in the weekends, since I work full time during the weekdays. 

We live close to beautiful mountain and forest areas - which gives you a lot of possibilities for exploring in all seasons. We also have lakes and fjords for swimming in the summer. 

Looking forward to hear from you! 

Silje

name: Sela - Eco Wilderness Retreat
description:
Hi,

Nuk Tessli is a secluded fly in (float plane) hiking cabin centre located in the backcountry wilderness of British Columbia, Canada.
It's located at 1,500 m high on the shore of the beautiful Whitton lake, at the edge of Tweedmuir park. 
There's no one else around Nuk Tessli. No other locals, tourists or trails.

Nuk Tessli has 3 cabins and one glamping dome for guests. In addition there's a cabin 12 km from Nuk Tessli and high in the alpine where we also host guests.
Our guests come for 3-7 days explore our area guided or unguided.
We maintain a vast hiking and canoeing trail network. Anything from 1-2 hours hike to strenuous scrambling days up the peaks around us.

Our staff is made of our family, a chef and a group of 4-5 volunteers  who come and help us for 4-6 weeks starting June 1st and ending in early October.

Our volunteers help us with everything: Building all kinds of stuff (Cabins, trails, docks etc), trail clearing, cooking, cleaning, gathering firewood, renovating the Wilderness Cabin and more.
In terms of food, we have all of our meals with the guests and they're very good =). Freshly baked bread, homemade yogurt, home-brew beer etc.

At Nuk Tessli you'll get a chance to experience true Canadian wilderness and living off grid and secluded. You'll get the opportunity to learn basic carpentry, off grid saw-milling, working with different tools like chain saws, drills etc, gathering firewood and more.
You'll get the chance to explore amazing lakes, mountains, true wilderness and so much more while also helping to operate a very unique place.

This place is our home and passion. Over the years we had over 200 volunteers. Lots of them came back to us for a second and even third volunteering period and all of our volunteers become a part of our family.

Volunteer accommodations :
A canvas wall tent (a big canvas tent where you can stand freely) on a small private island. 
You'll be getting a bed, a nice comfy mattress, duvet, sheets etc.

Current proje

name: Jairo - Alternative co-operative community, Communal Living Group, Agritourism Destination
description:
Nestled on 9 ha of cloud‑forest in Veracruz, our family‑run permaculture dairy farm is both a working showroom and a living laboratory. Our focus is dairy: fresh goat and cow milk transformed into cheese several times each week for our local market, with gardens supporting our work.

**Agrogym Boot‑Camp: Dairy Edition**
This isn’t a gentle retreat—it’s eight hours of daily hands‑on dairy work, strength‑building chores, and learning by doing.

**Your Daily Drill (6 days/week, 7 AM–3 PM)**

* **7:00 AM – 9:30 AM**:

  * Barn duty: clean goat feeders, feed kids, tidy milking area, cut & chop grass for fodder (gloves + rubber boots required).
* **9:30 AM – 10:30 AM**:

  * Breakfast break (self‑catered): prepare your own meals from basics we supply—rice, beans, eggs, cheese, seasonal veggies & fruit.
* **10:30 AM – 1:00 PM**:

  * Manure agrogym: shovel stall manure, spread as fertilizer on pastures and animal terraces.
* **1:00 PM – 2:30 PM**:

  * Milking prep & kid feeding: clean milking area, assist with milking setup, feed and care for kids.
* **2:30 PM – 3:00 PM**:

  * Wrap‑up: secure barns, tidy equipment, prepare for next day.

**Living Conditions**

* Yurt‑style dorm with raised bed platform—bring your own sleeping bag
* Compost toilet, river bath (no soap/chemicals—our frogs thank you!)
* Solar‑powered internet to check in

**Commitment & Culture**

* Minimum stay: 2 weeks (our sweet spot)
* Self‑catered meals on your schedule using supplied basics
* Bring grit, a sense of adventure, and readiness for rain, mud, insects—and transformation

**Who Should Apply**
If dawn‑to‑dusk dairy work—milking, feeding, cleaning, fertilizing—fires you up, join us. If you’d rather prune tomatoes than wrestle goat feeders, this isn’t your camp.

Ready for the ultimate dairy agrogym? Apply now!

name: Giuseppe - Fattoria mista
description:
Fattoria Familiare, allevamento bovini, orto per la vendita, fattoria mista , olivi, frutteto, ortaggi , 
Superficie della mia proprietà: 300 ettari
Certified Organic: Si dal 1999 SUOLO E SALUTE
Tecniche agricole: Controllo biologico dei parassiti, Compostaggio, Rotazione delle colture, Sovescio, Pacciamatura, Permacoltura.

Pratiche: foraggi- piccoli frutti - coltivazione, panificazione, erbe aromatiche, orticoltura, grani antichi, fieno/seminativo,  erbe medicinali, frutteti, conservazione di sementi, visite esperienziali.
Animali: bovini, conigli, pecore, galline, gatti, api, cani, cavalli

Prodotti:  pane,  pasta madre, uova,  miele, olio di oliva, conserve,  vino, carne e derivati

Ciao siamo una famiglia composta da me, Giuseppe,mia moglie Romina ed i nostri 3 figli . viviamo in fattoria nei pressi della Stazione di Vizzini (distante 60 km da Catania ), dove è situata la nostra fattoria.
Ci occupiamo di Allevamento e agricoltura Biologica nei nostri campi. Il nostro sistema di allevamento è transumante , spostiamo la mandria nel corso dell'anno in altri campi in provincia di Enna.
Insieme alla mia famiglia coltiviamo e alleviamo in biologico dal 1999. 
La  fattoria è formata da 300 Ha in totale ubicati in due località diverse Catenanuova (Enna)e Vizzini (Catania).
 Le attività che vorremmo condividere con voi sono: la gestione dei pascoli e della mandria, dalla semina alla raccolta dei foraggi e del grano, la cura dell'orto, la trasformazione delle conserve.
In azienda c'è sempre qualcuno, ma potrete passare dei momenti in cui sarà preferibile l'autogestione.
I settori di lavoro sono molti e specifici, quindi spesso ci ritroveremo a lavorare in ambiti differenti ma nello stesso posto. 
Preferiamo dunque: spirito di adattamento, forza fisica e mentale, interesse nei confronti del vegetale e sensibilità nei confronti dell'animale.
La nostra azienda è a ciclo chiuso, cioè all'interno dell'azienda si produce tutto, dai foraggi per gli animali alle sementi per i te

name: Clara - Agroforestry in existing miombo woodland
description:
Hi WWOOFers! My name is Clara, the initiator of the Minamba Research Farm where we look for ways to combine forest conservation with increased benefits for local rainfed forest users, ostrn subsistence farmers. We do both research and experiments testibg abd demonstrating regenerative and ecological techniques to increase yields, protect the soil and identify new forest products that can help generate a sustainable income throughout the year, so people are not forced to chop trees for charcoal or bricks to get some short term cash.

Besides using common permaculture techniques like digging swales, growing fruit and growing polycultures of organic vegetables, we also test how to propagate indigenous trees and set up activities around local knowledge. This includes sharing knowledge on the production of pesticides from local trees, making drinks from local fruits, drying mushrooms and many more. This may also include using branches to make tools or furniture, or using local materials to built an eco house.

We have a nearby house and some studios near the nearby Kambisa! community centre (which also has a forest gardens where we work at times). Bread is in the freezer, eggs and other foods are in the house. You can either cook for yourself of join us for lunch or dinner. If we cook, it is mostly traditional Zambian nsima with vegetables and a vegetarian protein source. We occasionally have chicken, fish or neat for those who like it, but please don't expect such on a daily basis.

The accommodation is off grid, using its own borehole and solar. Please indicate beforehand if you want to use heavy equipment like a heating device so we change to grid and preserve the batteries. 

We have games, books and DvD's and a community centre where you can organise activities with our neighbours.

name: Jennifer - We have gardens, native plants, and we are starting a vegetable garden.
description:
ABOUT US
We welcome you!  We need some help in the garden with cultivation, aromatic or medicinal plants, teas, pulling weeds, path work, mechanics, tools and technology, and irrigation. There are other jobs and we are open to other volunteer help. We can gratefully host one to four volunteers who have positive reviews. 

Our accommodation is a twin bunk beds in a trailer and two cots size beds in a trailer. There is camping available.  We offer a protein drink in the morning. A vegetarian lunch and dinner are offered daily. There is tea. There isn't coffee.  We do daily yoga together and go on walks together, if wanted. 

We are located close to the beautiful town of Julian in Southern California. We are located about an hour drive away from San Diego, and we are a few miles away from Julian. We are in the midst of gorgeous rural native trees and nature, away from urban life here. We are not affiliated with any particular spiritual ideology or philosophy with a central authority, but we are rather a community of truth seekers learning and growing in the mirror of relationship and intelligent questioning of everything; and love, we believe, is the only light in this spiritual journey. We Live in a beautiful piece of land at 3,350 feet elevation between Santa Ysabel, CA and the historic town of Julian, California. With many large stunning rock outcroppings and Metates carved into the rock, this has been the home to many before us. We have many varieties of native plants. 

The location here is very unique because we are located in a transition zone right in the middle of the low elevation coastal plants and the high elevation mountainous plants. On the walking paths here, one goal is to have the California Native Plants clearly labeled and we are looking for an artist to make these signs for us and a gardener to plant more plants regularly. We want this to be a demonstration garden for Southern California Native Plants. Some of the native plants are edible.  This are

name: Mariana - Rancho Sol Rosa es un centro de permacultura, agroecología y ecoturismo.
description:
Nuestra pasión es preocuparnos por el medio ambiente y siempre hemos buscado vivir en armonía con todos los seres vivos; entendemos que el estilo de vida que debemos seguir como sociedad, y nuestras formas de producción y consumo, han llevado a nuestra Madre Tierra a una crisis climática y a la injusticia social y económica.
Por eso queremos convertir este lugar en un espacio destinado a la educación ambiental, la permacultura y la sanación, que va de la mano con la sustentabilidad física, mental y espiritual.
Estamos ubicados en el estado de Colima, pero cerca de las faldas del volcán de fuego. 
Normalmente habitamos aqui Maui (yo) e Isaac, más un maximo de 3 voluntarios. 
5 perros, 3 gatos, un puerco vietnamita.

Las labores que realizamos constantemente es biocontrucción, pronto estaremos enfocandonos de nuevo en la agroecología y siempre tenemos labores de mantenimiento de las instalaciones. 
Aqui aprenderas las actividades tipicas que se hacen en la vida del campo. 
Además si te encanta cocinar buscamos a alguien que pueda ayudarnos en la cocina, preparando 2 comidas al día.

Podemos ofrecerte una habitación compartida, es para 3 voluntarios o una carpa para ti bajo techo que compartimos todos. Debemos respetar a nuestros socios y mantener nuestro espacio limpio y ordenado.
Nuestra alimentación generalmente es ovovegetariana, sin embargo no tenemos problemas con recibir personas con otro tipo de alimentación. 
Ofrecemos 3 comidas al día, más agua y fruta para tomar todo el día. Pero cualquier necesidad extra alimenticia que tengas corre por su cuenta ej. extra café por la tarde, tés, botanas, etc.

En nuestro tiempo libre nos gusta hacer yoga, ir a caminar hacia el volcán, ir a nadar a una laguna cercana, hacer fogatas y ocasionalmente bajar a la ciudad de Colima a algun evento cultural.

We have a passion for caring about the environment and have always aimed to live in harmony with all living beings; we understand that the lifestyle must people follow as a so

description:

name: Steve - Alternative co-operative community
description:
Welcome to North Branch Farms.    We started our community in 1972 and have been evolving these last 50 years.  Two families share the land (82 acres) having raised our children and we now provide some of those opportunities to our grandkids.   The grandkids still love playing in the hay loft, eating fresh organic peas, carrots and raspberries straight from the garden or delicious meals with the freshest ingredients (season dependent).

We are located 35 km from Thunder Bay deep in the boreal forest close to lakes and streams.  We started our community as an alternative to the mainstream consumer society.   We spent the first 16 years living off the grid and producing most of what we ate and needed - building our homes, gardens and homestead.  

 As we aged we gained skills that we now share with the larger community.   These include carpentry, pottery, health and healing, environment stewardship, community building and systemic advocacy.  While we eat meat, it is only a small part of our diet and we are comfortable with vegetian, lactose free and gluten free diets as some of our kids & grandkids need special diets.  Our guests will have the opportunity to share our lives and work in our gardens, woodlot and homes.

Organic methods: Composting, Green Manure, crop  rotation, no pesticides or herbicides, natural fertilizes only.

Certifications: We practice organic methods but are not certified.

Current projects: Gardens and Firewood.  Could include stone wall building for a new greenhouse.

Internet access available

name: Efrain - Promovemos la autosuficiencia con prácticas regenerativas, orgánicas y sintrópicas.
description:
¡Únete a nuestra granja y vive una experiencia única!

Estámos ubicados en las afueras de Guadalajara, México, la cuna del mariachi, el tequila y la charrería. A solo 5 km del encantador pueblo de San Agustín y cerca de la importante Avenida López Mateos, aquí podrás disfrutar de la tranquilidad del campo sin alejarte demasiado de la ciudad.

¿Qué ofrecemos?

Alojamiento cómodo: Contamos con una casa tipo hostal equipada con 4 camas y capacidad máxima para 4 personas, además de un baño completo.

Alimentación casera: El 60% de nuestra dieta proviene de lo que cultivamos y criamos. Preparamos deliciosa comida mexicana con maíz, frijoles, verduras y carnes frescas.

Proyectos inspiradores: En nuestras 2 hectáreas, nos enfocamos en la autosuficiencia y la sostenibilidad. Producimos alimentos orgánicos, plantas medicinales, jabones, semillas, frutas, y más, utilizando técnicas como la agricultura sintrópica y la homeopatía.

¿Qué buscamos?
Voluntarios comprometidos, con buena disposición, sentido común y ganas de trabajar en armonía con la naturaleza. Aquí convivirás con animales, plantas y todo lo que implica la vida en el campo. Si te apasiona aprender y contribuir en un entorno respetuoso y limpio, este lugar es para ti.

Horarios y estilo de vida:

Trabajamos de lunes a viernes, de 9:00 am a 4:00 pm, con desayuno a las 8:00 am y cena a las 5:00 pm.

Nuestra vida es sencilla, austera y pragmática, enfocada en la colaboración y el respeto mutuo.

¿Qué aprenderás?
Desde técnicas de agricultura orgánica y sintrópica hasta elaboración de productos naturales como jabones y extractos medicinales. También podrás participar en proyectos de producción de alimentos como carne de pollo y cerdo, así como en el manejo de plantas y cultivos sostenibles.

Importante: Si eres alérgico o te incomodan los insectos, el contacto con la tierra o la vida rural, este lugar probablemente no sea el ideal para ti.

¿Te animas a formar parte de nuestra comunidad agrícola?
¡Trae tu entusiasmo,

name: James - Avocado, citrus, exotic fruit orchard, vegan friendly
description:
Next opening will be in July 2026.  Please inquire only if you are interested in a stay of 8 weeks or longer.  Shorter stays are not suitable for this farm.    Thank you.

We will strive to give you new skills and experiences over time. A true interest in fruit crop production is a plus. Work ethic is essential! We love taking care of the earth and producing our crops.  If you ENJOY being productive, please apply. 

We are open to applicants of all races, religious backgrounds, sexual preferences, and you would share these attitudes.
 
This is a long established, quite diversified 5.75 acre farm at 2000' elevation in the Kona district of the Island of Hawai`i, producing mostly orchard crops: avocado, bananas, coffee, citrus, eggfruit, macadamia nuts, guava......black and white sapote and more.   Your primary work will be helping with our harvesting, packing, plant care and general maintenance. We provide excellent accommodations and food; we expect and do real work. We are tobacco, alcohol, and drug free. We have an almost-rural location, but we're close to post office, grocery, hardware store, farmer's market,  pizza and more.  Our farm is a few miles from the ocean; about 20 minutes drive. We  provide adequate and healthy meals and snacks, washing machine, internet, etc. We ask 25 hours help per week from each wwoofer. There is a wide range of work, so helpers will experience variety in their activities. Some of this work is routine or even boring, depending on your point of view. A candidate needs to be able to work independently, and to do mundane tasks. There's hand-weeding, hand cutting grasses and vines, watering, hand-spraying soap solution, etc. What a wwoofer can learn about: stewardship of the land, nature and care of a wide variety of subtropical fruits, nuts, and ornamentals... use of various small-scale farm equipment... organic fertilizers... mulch, natural weed control... pruning...plant propagation....use of catchment water systems.... native Hawaii

name: Doru - Raising and educating horses in a natural style
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
I have a farm where the horses are cared for and breed. Although I educate them, I do not work with them. Some suffered accidents and have various diseases, others were victims of diseases that unfortunately left traces. However, the vast majority are healthy. But the reason why I cannot work with them is the lack of serious and qualified personnel.
It can therefore be said that it is a sanctuary for horses and not a farm that produces income. I'm also afraid to sell them if the potential buyers can't assure me that they will love and take care of the horses. For me they are not pets but my non-speaking friends
I was alone with 40 horses for over 4 years. Paradoxically, the refugees from Ukraine that I accommodated on the occasion of the recent sad events, all contributed to my support. They all did something. Either in the house, or in the yard, or even at the horses. When the war started, I was among the first to offer help, totally disinterested. But paradoxically, the help given to me by those who hosted me was more significant than the one I offered. I do not have a non-profit organization, I do not receive donations. It is only a non-profit activity because it is not possible to make a profit without qualified and serious employees.
Currently, I have 48 horses, 8 of which are small, up to one month old. From the resources I have, I will continue to educate them in a natural style, so that I have friends and not slaves who execute commands out of fear. I also have a significant number of abandoned dogs. The first 30 horses were bought to save them from the slaughterhouse. The rest were born here. They live in a virgin area, in the middle of the mountains. Incidents with wild animals are inevitable. We have hay, but it is insufficient for our studs. The supply of hay is relatively difficult and costs a lot. But we managed. We all live free and happy. Currently, besides my non-speaking
_wwoof_travel: Predeal train station and Brasov airport (Ghimbav)

name: Nóra - Birtok a dél-alföldi pusztaságban /Farmstead on the Great Hungarian Praerie
description:
Dear Visitor,

I have been hosting since 2017 on the ranch I have been running since 2014. I update the volunteer pages around once every new year.

Today we are a community of organic ranchers and craftspeople in the praeries of Central Europe and are quite apt at self-sustaining without outside help. We occasionally also go volunteering to other places apart from or as a part of our professional life in ranching and crafting business.

If you wish to become a part of our community for a longer or shorter period of time we are continuing to be open. I am looking for independent people seeking refuge and joy in the environment created by human labour thoughout more than ten years. They must be able to self-sustain themselves, comfortable left to their own resources and take pride in contributing to the greater project. I am personally not able to entertain visitors on a day-to-day basis, however there is space, bed, bath basin, candles for lighting and wood-fire stove for cooking and heating in the winter. There’s a pantry and little space in the freezer for storing your food, but no refrigerator. There’s also electricity available for charging your devices and tools to use for projects around the farm.

In the summer the sun rises around 4am, with siesta time at possibly even 35-40C degrees between 10am and 4pm, and the sun sets around 10pm. There is very little rain or humidity.
In the winter the sun rises around 7am and sets around 4pm. We are currently having super warm winters around zero celsius degrees. It still can fall back to around -20C degrees. There can generally be very deep mud especially since the property resides in an Ice Age marsh in an indigineous habitat.

Throughout the years we had experimented with many forms of gardening beside animal husbandry. We experimented with many vegetables, onions and planted many fruit trees. Many of the fruit trees failed to thrive - and in this case we learned that there are foreign species, that are not fit for

name: Ibrahim A - Through our Sustainable Agriculture Program, SHARP provides:    No-interest seed loans from our seed
description:
Hello WWOOFers, my name is Ibrahim Alimamy Kamara. I live in Sierra Leone, West Africa, in a semi-rural community close to nature and local villages. I am a social entrepreneur, educator, and community organizer working on sustainable agriculture, food security, and eco-community development.
         The core of this project is to build a small but growing food forest and mixed farming system, combining crops, trees, and animals in a way that works with nature rather than against it. I also work closely with local youth and community members, so WWOOFers will experience real grassroots life in Sierra Leone.
           I live on site, and sometimes family members or local helpers are around. We keep simple animals such as chickens and goats, and there are always birds and wildlife around the farm.

Our daily activities depend on the season, but usually include:
Land preparation using low-cost, local methods
Planting and caring for crops such as cassava, vegetables, bananas, yams, and fruit trees
Composting, mulching, and “chop and drop” soil improvement
Feeding and caring for poultry and goats
Simple construction using local materials (animal shelters, fencing, garden beds)
        I would love to teach WWOOFers about tropical farming, permaculture principles, and how small-scale agriculture can support communities in West Africa. WWOOFers will also learn how we adapt farming to climate change and limited resources.

A typical day is 4–5 hours of farm work in the morning, with afternoons for rest, learning, cultural exchange, or exploring the area.

Our daily activities depend on the season, but usually include:
Land preparation using low-cost, local methods
Planting and caring for crops such as cassava, vegetables, bananas, yams, and fruit trees
Composting, mulching, and “chop and drop” soil improvement
Feeding and caring for poultry and goats
Simple construction using local materials (animal shelters, fencing, garden beds)

I would love to teach WWOOFers about tro

name: Karin - Young food forest, a traditional fruit orchard, and a wild, edible garden
description:
We live in one of the most beautiful parts of the Netherlands, right next to the floodplains of the Rhine, surrounded by nature and walking trails. Our home is a small paradise for people who love biodiversity, sustainable living, and good food.

We are creating an edible landscape that includes a young food forest, a traditional fruit orchard, and a wild, edible garden filled with herbs, berries, flowers, and native plants. The orchard is a shared community project that we maintain together with five neighbouring households, creating a wonderful sense of connection and stewardship.

Our goal is to combine food production with biodiversity, giving space to birds, bees, butterflies, and other wildlife. We love experimenting with edible perennials, mushrooms, natural gardening methods, and local food systems, and we are always happy to share our knowledge and learn from others.

If you enjoy working outdoors, appreciate nature, and would like to experience life in a beautiful Dutch riverside landscape, we would love to welcome you to our little corner of the world.

((We have 5 sheep and 5 chicken as well.))

Our activities vary greatly with the seasons, so no two weeks are exactly the same. Depending on the time of year, WWOOFers may help with pruning fruit trees, planting new trees and perennials, harvesting fruits and vegetables, weeding, mowing, repairing fences, maintaining the guest accommodation, or building simple structures to support climbing plants.

We also enjoy processing and preserving our harvest, so there may be opportunities to cook together, make jams, dry herbs, prepare mushrooms, or experiment with other forms of food preservation.

We love sharing knowledge about food forests, edible perennial gardens, biodiversity-friendly gardening, mushroom cultivation, and sustainable living. We believe in learning by doing, and we are always happy to explain not only *how* we do things, but also *why* we choose certain methods.

There is always time for a co

name: Darrin - Organic Farm
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
We do all things related to self-sufficiency on the farm. From growing & processing our own fruits and vegetables, maintaining  this all year round. Sometimes we get a steer for the summer. 

There are also some construction projects going on. Including renovating the main house, building a post & beam cabin. The main house is run off of solar. We are off grid.

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
WWOOFers will be participating in and learning, many things related to off grid living. Examples include, but are not limited to, growing food, harvesting, preserving, firewood, slaughtering and processing animals (including tanning hides). I have many self sufficient skills. Please enquire if you are looking for something specific.

3. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?
Generally work day starts between 8 & 9 am with the WWOOFer's day ending around 1 pm Monday through Friday. All food for meals will be included, but WWOOFer's are asked to help with cooking and/or clean up. As well as activities around the local area, including but not limited to. farmers markets, waterfall and hiking exploration. 

4. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.):
I love music and go to the local jams from time to time. I'm currently learning how to play the mandolin. There are lots of hiking trails with a fantastic swimming areas down by the river (5 min. bike ride).
_wwoof_travel: Bridgewater for bus, Halifax for Airport

name: Jay - Small farm producing heaps of healthy food 🥕 for the community in Minnesota's Central Lakes Region
description:
We are currently seeking WWOOFers to join us for the 2026 season!  Our season runs from April until early November. Please reach out, if you're interested in a stay with us this summer. We prefer wwoofers who can stay for at least 30 days. 

Note to prospective WWOOFers: We require a zoom video chat with any prospective WWOOFer(s) that are interested in living on our farm.  This helps us build trust with each other, and allows us to get to know each other better.  Communication is key. If you request a stay with us, and then your plans change. A simple message stating the fact is appreciated. 

2026 UPDATE: Many changes will be taking place this summer. Our farm is growing! In November 2024 we purchased a second farm just a few miles from our current farm. This new 175 acre property will allow us to expand our vegetable production greatly. This will be a season of transition for us, as we adjust to this new setup. WWOOFers will be housed in one of our two spare bedrooms in our new home. 

Located in Minnesota's Central Lakes Region, we're a small farm that sells produce at our on-site farm stand, local farmers markets, as well as to local restaurants and grocery stores. While we may be small, we produce heaps of healthy food for our community every summer. Broccoli, cauliflower, lettuce, beans, cucumbers, pumpkins, and so much more can be found growing in our fields. When we're not farming, we enjoy having a beer and conversation around the fire pit, dinner parties with friends, and enjoying the beautiful, mild summers of northern Minnesota. In addition to the farm income, one of us is an artist and carpenter by trade, and the other is a farm business management instructor. 

While not certified organic, we practice sustainable farming practices. Healthy soil, zero chemicals, and clean wholistic living are very important to us. Every summer season is a learning experience, and we hope to continue to grow and thrive here in our small community in northern Minnesota.

name: Julie - Projet mise en place de vergers  élevage et agrotourisme
description:
