Bonjour! Nous avons le projet de relancer notre petit Ferme en polyculture élevage et agrotourisme. Nous sommes dans une région très sèche de l’île.

Semis, bouturages, plantation d’arbres fruitiers,  désherbage, Défrichage…

Nos wwoofeurs sont hébergés dans une petite cabane rustique indépendante Les repas de la semaine sont collectifs mais les week-ends les wwoofeurs devront être autonomes

Plein d’activités sportives sont possibles à Saint-Leu, plage, surf, parapente, balades.   La ville est accessible par bus .

name: Josée - Séjours retraite éco-thérapeutique
description:
Séjours collaboratifs mi mai à mi juin, puis mi septembre à mi octobre, hébergementetrepasinclus. Possibilité de séjours en van (sans hébergement) à discuter.

L'opportunité de bénéficier d'une retraite dans un cadre exceptionnel, ce grâce à vos mains agiles, votre savoir-faire, votre créativité ou votre coeur à l'ouvrage !    

Je nous présente, Josée, François, Céleste, Stella et Moon. Nous avons le privilège de vivre sur l'île enchantée de Miscou en Acadie (Nouveau Brunswick), avec pour voisins renards et lièvres, chevreuils et orignaux. Une colonie de phoque habite non loin, quelques couples de colibris passe l'été avec nous, et les monarques font escale à la maison.   

Constituée de zones humides à plus de 50%, Miscou est bordée de dunes et de plages, de vastes étangs côtiers, de lacs limpides et de marais salés, avec en son centre une tourbière. Situé au coeur d'une réserve naturelle, la passerelle d'observation du Lac Frye fait face à votre hébergement. Ainsi pourrez-vous observer les grands hérons en traversantsimplementle chemin. 

Territoire sensible et sauvage, l'île accueille de nombreuses espèces d'oiseaux protégés en tant que station migratoire. Aussi connue pour ses nuits étoilées, loin de toute pollution lumineuse et ses aurores boréales.      

Parmi mes intérêts, l'éco-jardinage, l'étude des plantes médicinales, des lichens et champignons. François lui, s'intéresse au travail du bois, la mécanique et la soudure.    

Nous sommes enthousiasmes à l'idée d'accueillir des amoureux de la nature, et nous engageons à fournir un espace de vie confortable, convenant aussi bien à une personne, qu'à un couple ou deux amis.  

Ce en quoi vous pourriez contribuer durant votre séjour selon vos acquis ou inspirations : 

- Projet artistique ou artisanale mettant en valeur le lieu. 
- Semi (mai/juin), cueillette, transformation et mise en conserve (octobre). 
- Entretien du jardin et gestion des petites bêtes mange-feuille.   
- Construction de structure légères
_wwoof_travel: Aéroport Régionale de Bathurst (2h), Aéroport Internationale du Grand Moncton (3h),

name: Lauren - Organic herb and vegetable garden with greenhouse focusing on permaculture techniques.
description:
Our farm is surrounded by other farms and ranches. We have nearly 40 acres that was in winter wheat with peas  but now we planted field grasses.  We also have a 30 X 80  garden space that already has many perennials like horseradish, strawberries, lemon balm, asparagus and many more to come.  We have started to transition some space near the farmhouse into a Permaculture Food Forest. Spring of 2025 we planted 5 elderberry bushes, 5 ponderosa pine trees, 5 mountain ash trees, 5 red osher dogwoods and some native flowers and grasses in the field east of the house. Just the start of a process of reclaiming and regenerating some if the land we steward that had been conventionally farmed in the past. 

Located near Spokane in Eastern Washington, the farm is  ten miles from Riverside State Park and the centennial trail. Sixteen miles from North Spokane or about the same distance to the city of Airway Heights.   

Open for day-trippers to help out around the farm and long or short term stays. We have a guest bedroom for helpers who visit for less than a month but if you are traveling in a van or want to camp out I will be able to host you for several months.

Summertime work day starts early in the morning when the air is fresh and cool, long before it gets too hot.  This means we finish quite early in the day giving you long lazy afternoons or plenty of time to go exploring.  We provide ample and hearty vegetarian (vegan adaptable) food.  

Your actual farm chores will include helping with all kinds of garden jobs depending on when in the season you visit. Things like planting, weeding, drip watering system installation and management, harvesting the flowers, herbs and vegetables as well as preparing them to eat, and finally saving seeds for next year.  

In 2022 we finished the greenhouse installation and we are enjoying using it to start seeds early and extend the season.

We love the peace and quiet of the country life and have no interest in loud vexing disturbances.

name: JT (Jennifer) - A little spot for gardeners, artists, and land stewards  (LGBTQ+ and POC operated)
description:
Hello! We are a small community arts hub and homestead on 2.5 acres of land, about 25 minutes outside of Greensboro, North Carolina. As a collective of artists, land stewards and farmers, our mission is to strengthen local communities through art-making, political education and access to green space. Our few acres of land includes a woodlot, planter beds, fruit trees and brambles; we have six chickens and a rooster.   

In terms of housing, we have a vintage 15-foot trailer with a bed, dinette, propane oven/range and sink for 1 WWOOFer. We accept and welcome pets.  We have a portable solar panel and electricity to power small devices. WWOOFers will have full access to our home’s kitchen, fridge,  and bathroom (but can also use the outdoor compost-style toilet). There is WIFI on the property; cell service and internet can be slow. 

WWOOFing ACTIVITIES:

We compost and use no till, organic practices in our growing areas. We use hugelkultur in our planter beds and permaculture practices throughout the property. This WWOOFing placement would be perfect for beginners interested in learning the basics of working with seed planting and collection, different styles of growing, harvesting, and building small animal shelters.  

Current projects include: building planter beds, planting, compost turning, weeding, chicken care, tree and forest maintenance (1+ acre on property), trail blazing and beautification, and other building projects.

In addition to our land stewarding practices, we are committed to arts and organizing. We are particularly interested in supporting local, indigenous-led land back/ rematriation efforts. WWOOFers interested in art practice, Indigenous politics and learning about histories of colonization in North Carolina (or in their own home region) are particularly encouraged to apply. 

TIME & SCHEDULING:

WWOOFers will receive a tailored weekly plan with goals and expectations based on their interests, needs, and personal schedule. We are flexible with

name: MULONDO - Welcome to our Farm in Mutai! Nestled in the peaceful countryside of Mutai, Jinja City
description:
Hi! My name is Bimbasa, and I run a small, hands-on maize farm in the quiet village of Mutai, located in the heart of rural Uganda. I’ve been farming here for several years, focusing on sustainable and traditional methods of maize cultivation. The farm is not just a place of work—it’s a way of life that’s deeply connected to the land and the local community.

I live on the farm with my family members, e.g wife and two children,brother and we all take part in the daily work. We love welcoming new people from different parts of the world who are curious to learn about farming and experience our way of life.

We also share our home with a few friendly animals, e.g., “a loyal dog named Simba, a couple of cats, and some free-range chickens. They’re part of the family too and always enjoy the company of visitors.

At the core of our project is the goal to grow food in harmony with nature, preserve local farming knowledge, and create a space where people can connect, share skills, and enjoy a slower, more grounded lifestyle.

Life on the farm follows the rhythm of the seasons. Our main crop is maize, and we are involved in every step of its growth—from preparing the land and planting, to weeding, harvesting, and storage. Depending on the time of year, WWOOFers may take part in:
	•	Preparing planting beds and composting
	•	Sowing maize by hand
	•	Mulching and weeding
	•	Irrigation and water management (where needed)
	•	Harvesting and drying maize
	•	Sorting, shelling, and storing the harvest
	•	Helping with small construction or repairs around the farm
	•	Feeding and caring for our animals

We welcome WWOOFers to learn through doing. You’ll gain first-hand experience with traditional and sustainable farming techniques, and we’re always happy to share what we know about soil health, crop rotation, seed saving, and how maize is used locally for food.

A Typical Day on the Farm
	•	7:00 AM: Wake up and enjoy a simple breakfast together
	•	7:30 AM – 11:00 AM: Morning farm work—t

name: Nancy - Small livestock farm located near Amelia Island and beaches
description:
*Please note: We only accept stays of four weeks or more.  Also - we love them too but cannot take wwoofers with pets. *

Hi Woofers! My name is Nancy and I own an 11-acre farm in North Florida. We have goats, sheep, donkeys, alpacas, mini cows, geese, ducks and chickens. We also have barn cats and dogs at the house. The farm is  15 minutes away from the beach (by car) and about 20  minutes from Amelia Island and the tourist town of Fernandina Beach. We are about to go into peak season where the weather is beautiful!

Farming activities take about an hour and a half in the morning and about a half hour in the evening with an hour block sometime during the for clean up, weeding ,etc.  The day starts with feeding and watering the animals. In spring and sometimes in the fall, the goats and sheep will have lambs and kids. They will need to be milked. That is pretty much the morning. In the evening, everyone needs to be fed  and locked up again,. A few times a week, stalls and coops need to be cleaned out. I will teach the Wwoofers how to make sheep and goat cheeses as well as soap if we have goats in milk. .

We have an Airstream trailer with a bed, bathroom and kitchen area. There is an outdoor seating area with a grill and firepit. We have been renting it out through AirB&B and have great feedback.  Food will be provided for the Wwoofers for them to make their meals.

I haven't really had much time for hobbies - I think the farm is my actual hobby. I am a full time college professor, realtor and take care of the 50+ animals by myself, so I don't have a lot of spare time. I do love to cook and will be finishing up my culinary management degree this summer.

name: Kalina - Family farm, Community farm
description:
A country filled with ancient history, a unique culture, and yet undiscovered by tourists - these factors make traveling to Georgia an especially interesting and unforgettable experience. When travelling to Georgia you will experience wildlife and landscapes still untouched by man, which makes this country even more attractive to visitors. Georgians believe that "the guest is from the Lord" and therefore warm hospitality for our guests is a large part of the culture. We are small eco place in the very beautiful region of Georgia, it is higher Racha. Here We start to create Guest House for different people, and activites. We need help mostly from spring till December. We need now many help with house repairing,preparing wood, hay for winter, work with plants,vegetables, help with horses, pigs, sheeps, rabbits and with many birds. I am flexible and there is an interesting job for everyone. I will teach you how to take care of animals, mow hay and pick mushrooms and herbs.  We want  to create here a good all year place for people who like wild nature.

Certifications: we are not certified yet
_wwoof_travel: From Oni region center, to Ghebi village.

name: Sutima - Organic Farm im Isaan
description:
Hallo, ich bin Sutima aber man nennt mich wie in Thailand üblich bei meinem Spitznamen "Tuk". Gemeinsam mit meinem Freund und aus der Schweiz und unseren Angestellten betreibe ich unsere Farm. Rein biologisch bauen wir verschiedene Pflanzen und Gemüse an. Bekannt sind wir für unser Cannabis, welches wir legal mit einer entsprechenden Lizenz anbauen. Ebenso beliebt sind unsere Chilis und natürlich der Reis. Ich lebe in einem gemütlichen Dorf gemeinsam mit meiner Mutter und meinen zwei Söhnen (Teenager / fast Erwachsen) , sowie mit meinem Freund und unseren freundlichen Hunden. Zudem haben wir Hühner, Enten, Schweine und bald auch Rinder und Milchvieh sowie eine Crevettenzucht.

User Pflanzenanbau findet vom Samen bis zum fertigen Produkt rein "inhouse" statt. Wir klonen Pflanzen, ziehen Setzlinge, bestellen die Felder und stellen unseren eigenen Dünger her. Vieles erledigen wir traditionell per Handarbeit, weil es so noch immer am besten gelingt. Unsere Arbeit ist sehr abwechslungsreich.

Du bekommst ein Zimmer mit Klimaanlage und eigenem Bad. Die Mahlzeiten nehmen wir gemeinsam bei uns zu Hause ein. Hier hast du auch die Möglichkeit selber zu kochen, wenn du das möchtest. Gelegentlich essen wir am Abend gemeinsam im Restaurant.

Mein grösstes Hobby ist das Gärtnern, womit ich eigentlich mein Hobby zum Beruf gemacht habe. Bei uns kann man auch im Naturteich Schwimmen oder Fischen, Radfahren und vor allem die Ruhe geniessen und entschleunigen.
_wwoof_travel: Flughafen Ubon Ratchathani / Bus Terminal Ubon Ratchathani / Bus Terminal Amnat Charoen / Bus Terminal Phana - wir holen dich an diesen Standorten gerne ab.

name: Athaualpa - Regeneração da terra e resgate dos nossos alimentos ancestrais, tudo com muita alegria e amizade!
description:
(Descrição em português abaixo) We are a couple of farmers in a rural area near Brasília, following the principles of agroecology with the practices of agroforestry, animal integration, artisanal processing and cultivation of Unconventional Food Plants (UFP or PANC). Located in an ecotourism area with several waterfalls and the natural beauty of the cerrado biome. We believe in the rescue of UFP and in the preservation of native seeds, also acting as seed guardians. We currently take part in farmers markets and deliveries. 
We love waterfalls, hiking, parkour, ora pro nobis and jackfruit!
.
Somos agricultores do cerrado, seguimos os princípios da agroecologia com a prática de agrofloresta, integração animal, agroindústria artesanal e cultivo de Plantas Alimentícias Não Convencionais (PANC).  Estamos localizados em uma área rural próximo de Brasília, de fácil acesso. Região da chapada imperial, área de ecoturismo com várias cachoeiras e belezas naturais no coração do cerrado. Acreditamos no resgate das PANC e na preservação de sementes crioulas, por isso atuamos também como guardiões de sementes. 
Comercializamos em espaços de feira e entregas. 
Amamos cachoeiras, trilhas, parkour e gostamos sempre de companhia para aventurar conosco nessas práticas!
Trabalho em várias frentes  e contamos com a ajuda de outros colaboradores que atuam na propriedade para prestar apoio ao processo produtivo na nossa indisponibilidade de tempo.

Organic methods: Agrofloresta, rotação de cultura, fabricação de 100% do adubo utilizado, compostagem, vermicompostagem, integração com a criação de animais caipiras, ciclagem de resíduos.

Certifications: IBD, vigilância sanitária

Current projects: Aumento da área de cultivo em Agrofloresta. Melhoria da qualidade dos produtos produzidos; Desenvolvimento de novos produtos artesanais; plantio de mudas PANC e de espécies do cerrado na agrofloresta. Estruturação de nossa propriedade de pesquisa no cerrado na nossa propriedade na chapada dos veadei

name:
Jukka Arponen-, Verena - Family farm,
 wwoofing possible if you stay minimum for two weeks.
description:
Organic farm in sse Finland 95km from capital city Helsinki. 43 ha land. We have blueberries and strawberries, potatoes, vegetables, cereals(buckwheat, rye ). We have sheep,a mule and a donkey and  2 cats. Help needed from march to october for work in the fields, garden and repairing and constructing. Village in 5km distance, there library, shops, bank, post office and lakes for swimming. Good possibilities for cycling. Accommodation in our house and good organic food. Construction work also during the winter time.

Certifications: EU-Organic

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel:
95km from capital city Helsinki

Bus, train, bicycle, motorbicycle

name: Zoë and Tom - Family home and garden - rural, Horse Ranch, Ranch/ Livestock Farm
description:
We are located near Parksville BC with 100s of KM of hiking, biking and riding trails, beaches, kayaking, skiing and many other recreational activities. We have a 5-acre farm , Nubian goat herd, kunekune pigs, one cats and gardens. Zoe has a master’s degree in Animal Science, Diploma in Agriculture and is a certified riding instructor. 

Tom has a degree in electrical engineering and business and is a professional engineer. In addition to working with clients, he also instructs part time in a technology management degree program.  Chores would include helping with the animals during the morning and evening feedings, gardening, firewood stacking, painting, helping with meals and other general maintenance activities.  Summer and fall may also include helping and attending various goat shows as well as clinics typically around BC. 

Accommodation is a private bedroom with queen size bed. The room shares a large separate washroom. Laundry is available downstairs. High-speed Internet is available. Meals generally follow the animal feeding schedule and other activities with breakfast around 8 am, lunch about noon and dinner 6 or 7 pm and often outside during summer and fall. Meals are gluten and egg free. We enjoy vegetables, fruit, and meat from our own farm and from nearby farms. We attend various events locally and in the nearby towns including fireworks displays, world-class sandcastle competition, bathtub race.  WWOOFers are welcome to come with us. As we have livestock, we require the WWOOFers to carry full insurance; World Nomad Explorer (or equivalent). We do not expect WWOOFers to do/use any equipment that they are not comfortable operating. 

Organic methods: We  compost  manures as soil amendments for the garden. We do not use any herbicides or pesticides in the garden. We mix our own feed and minerals for the goats and do our best to avoid any GMO food/feed. In the garden, we are starting permaculture and coppicing trees and shrubs for fodder for the goats.

C

name: Nicole y Adrian - Harmonious Holistic Educational Centre in the jungle
description:
Somos Nicole y Adrián, junto a 1 perro y 3 gatos, decidimos dejar la vida de ciudad para venirnos a vivir en la selva paranaense subtropical, nuestro proyecto es un santuario de naturaleza, donde preservamos las especies nativas de flora y fauna y plantamos alimentos en armonía con el lugar, especialmente árboles frutales tropicales; también nos dedicamos a la profundización del conocimiento de la esencia del ser. Es un espacio en recuperación y regeneración de la biodiversidad, convivimos con Monos Titis, Tucanes, Pecaries, Arasarís, Zorros, Felinos, etc.

Las actividades las cuales nos gustaría que nos ayuden son:
* Realización de senderos por la selva.
   Se abren senderos podando arbustos y malezas 
* Huertos en camas altas, huerto Hügelkultur y huerto Keyhole.
 Armado de huertos desde cero
* Bioconstrución (terminación de casa, estufa rocket mass heater, pérgolas, etc.).
* Reforestación de flora nativa.
 Durante ciertos momentos del año plantamos árboles nativos y frutales
* Mantenimiento.
 Esta es la tarea que mayormente realizarás durante tu estadía, se podan algunas plantas y se colocan como cobertura (mulch) a otros árboles. 
*Abrir pequeños cocos (similar a abrir nueces) 
*Eliminar plantas invasivas que generan desorden en el ecosistema. 

Durante tu voluntariado usaras herramientas manuales como serrucho, tijera de poda, machete, azada, pala, etc.

Los visitantes podrán hospedarse en una casa con baño, carpa, casa rodante, etc.

Nuestro sueño es que la tierra sea un lugar donde podamos vivir en armonía, paz y amor; y queremos aportar nuestro grano en la transformación. Nos motiva participar en esta experiencia para compartir con los visitantes agradables momentos. En el lugar podrás explorar, fotografiar flora y fauna, meterte al rio con cascada y disfrutar como un niño.

              -----------------------------------------******************------------------------------------------ 

We are Nicole and Adrián along with 1 dog and 3 cats. We decided to

name: Rebecca - Family home and garden - rural
description:
We have availability now but we prefer WWOOFers who can spend at least a month with us.

We have an organic garden at Fraoch Lodge ; with a lot of fruit and vegetables. There are the usual maintenance tasks to be done - weeding, planting new seeds, netting the vulnerable vegetables etc and some years we have additional projects. At the moment we are working on replacing our raised beds and potentially refreshing the fruit cages. We're also already involved in harvesting from the garden and making as much as possible with the produce which is already growing through.

Some help in the house will also be required as we will all eat together so we'll all also help prepare and clear the meals too.

Internet access available

name: Pedro - Orchard, Mixed Farm, Family farm
description:
Ses Terres es una finca ubicada en el término municipal de Artà, Mallorca. Es una pequeña explotación agrícola familiar certificada en agricultura ecológica.

Creemos en un modelo basado en la agroecología, con productos de proximidad, calidad, de temporada y sobre todo ecológicos.

Buscamos gente con ganas de compartir experiencias, aprender unos de otros y sobretodo disfrutar.

Producimos producto fresco tal como verdura, hortaliza y fruta de temporada y carne de cordero. Por otra parte también producimos productos elaborados como aceites, confituras, conservas y deshidratados.

La explotación agrícola está formada por unas 25 hectáreas. La mayoría de la superficie se emplea para pastos de las ovejas. Unas 2 ha están ocupadas por olivar, 1 más para frutales y cítricos y 2 ha para la producción de verduras y hortalizas.

HUERTA
Hacemos aproximadamente 1 ha de huerta. Productos de temporada. Huerta de verano y de invierno
GANADERÍA
La finca se complementa con 50 cabezas de ganado ovino. Ovejas de reproducción de la raza Lacaune y mallorquina. La carne de cordero se comercializa a través de la cooperativa "Pagesos Ecològics de Mallorca"

Organic methods: Rotación de cultivos, asociación, abonos en verde

Certifications: CBPAE

Internet access available

name: CARLA - Estamos ubicados en Cochabamba, Bolivia. Dedicamos nuestras 3.5h a la agricultura orgánica.
description:
Hola, somos Carla y Horacio. Juntos administramos nuestra bella Granja Ecológica Polen, un espacio de 3.5h de agricultura orgánica. Trabajamos junto a 4 amigos que nos ayudan en el trabajo en campo. Juntos nos ocupamos de mantener un espacio verde, saludable y educativo. Destinamos este ambiente para producir hortalizas orgánicas, frutos orgánicos de temporada, lácteos orgánicos y tenemos animalitos de granja con los que convivimos (Peque nuestra vaca, Sara y Beto, nuestras cabras, Uña y Mugre nuestras ovejas, conejitos, patos y gallinas y nuestros amados perritos).  Nos dedicamos también a la agroforestería y permacultura como parte de nuestros principios agrícolas. También realizamos visitas guiadas de escuelas con niños y niñas para que puedan aprender de una manera lúdica y apreciar la importancia de la naturaleza y el origen de los alimentos que nos dan vida. 
Nos encontramos a 30 minutos de la ciudad de Cochabamba y a 8 minutos del pueblo de Tiquipaya. Existe transporte público cercano.

En la mañana, ordeñamos a Peque, nuestra vaca y alimentamos a todos nuestros animalitos de granja.
Según el trabajo planificado, sembramos, deshierbamos o cosechamos productos para la venta en el mercado semanal.
Lunes: procesamos lácteos (hacemos yogurt, queso, mantequilla, requesón)
Martes: Cosechamos verduras y las alistamos para la venta del día miércoles. 
Miércoles: Hacemos deliveries personales y participamos en la ciudad en 1 feria local. Los que estamos en la granja, nos quedamos a trabajar en las huertas.
Jueves: Trabajamos en campo, deshierbando o manteniendo espacios.
Viernes: Cosechamos verduras listas para la venta del día sábado en la feria de la ciudad. En las tardes, recibimos también vistitas que pueden comprar sus verduras frescas recién cosechadas en nuestra Tiendita Verde y pasear por la granja.
Sábado: Estamos en nuestro punto de venta en la ciudad. Los que estamos en la granja, nos quedamos a trabajar en las huertas.
Todos los días, llevamos a nuestros a

name: Sara - Medicinal Herbs, Permaculture, IntentionalCommunity, Solar Design, Fruits, Aquaculture, and more
description:
I’m glad you’re reading this. 

Our immediate need is for help getting  some solar panels exchanged on our roof. This needs to be done in May because it will be too hot on the roof after that. 
Here’s what we’re up to:
*moving third generation Tilapia into different tanks.
* adding more Solar panels
* planting seeds and transplants. 
* building tool shed inside Solar Barn.
* making doors for Solar Barn
* watering, mulching, weeding, mowing
* sowing seeds for transplants of herb plants (echinacea, bone set, etc)
* Swimming in the waterfall at the River.
* Projects change as completed, and with the seasons. So do check back. 

Our homestead’s central focus is sustainability and building resilient community!  On just under 5 acres, we steward medicinal plants, shiitake mushrooms, veggies & tree fruits, ducks & geese, Tilapia fish, solar home & solar business, intentional community, growing native plants, insect supporting perennials for our local Restoration Nursery, and permaculture design. The entire 84 acre community property has had almost no non-organic anything since property purchased in 1975! We are working on healing our selves/spot on the planet, right here, right now! We are engaged in self-illumination, cultural humility, and dismantling white privilege.  Doing Anti-racist work and reversing climate catastrophe are of paramount priority in our lives. Come be our guest and learn about these things along with us. 

 We have helped people *aspiring* to serve in the PEACE CORPS and we are a “soft-landing” for folks *after* serving in PEACE CORPS and other similar situations. 

We have Tilapia fish that live in 5 feet tall circular Solar Algae tanks in our living room.  We are nurturing our 3rd generation of Tilapia now.  
We have cultivated Elder bushes, blueberries, strawberries, apples, Asian pears, cherry, pomegranate, mulberry, and persimmon fruits.  Boysenberries are next.   

It’s never dull here and we intentionally make and take time for relaxation and
_wwoof_travel: Birmingham and Huntsville, AL   Both are over an hour away. No public transportation so we meet folks at airports and bus stations

name: Folino - Azienda agricola a conduzione familiare in collina
description:
Ciao sono Francesco, dopo aver viaggiato per 13 anni in giro per il mondo lavorando come chef, ho deciso e ho sentito il desiderio d tornare in Calabria x dedicarmi alla mia terra e cercare di creare qualcosa d nuovo e particolare! Vivo con i miei genitori che mi danno una mano nell accudire l'orto e gli animali. 
Nella mia piccola azienda alleviamo animali allo stato brado, coltiviamo ortaggi, produciamo olio d oliva, ospitiamo scolaresche nell arco dell anno, in più abbiamo un punto vendita degustazione in città a Cosenza!
Ci troviamo a 7 chilometro dal cento della città, ospito wwoofers tutto l'anno in appartamento o in struttura ecologica in fase d costruzione. Il pranzo sarà condiviso mentre per la cena sarà fornito tutto l occorrente.  E' richiesta autonomia nel cucinare perché io la sera sono impegnato con il punto vendita.
Non richiedo particolari esperienze, solo educazione, rispetto, voglia d fare e nuove idee!
---o----o-----0-----o----o---
Hi I'm Francesco, after traveling around the world for 13 years working as a chef, I decided and felt the desire to return to Calabria and devote myself to my land and try to create something new and special! 
I live with my parents who help me take care of the vegetable garden and animals.  On my small farm we raise free-range animals, grow vegetables, produce olive oil, host school groups throughout the year, plus we have a tasting store in town, in Cosenza!
We are located 7 kilometers from the city center, host wwoofers year-round in an apartment or ecological structure at the moment under construction. Lunch will be shared while everything will be provided for your dinner.  Independence in cooking is required because I am busy in the evenings with the store.
I do not require special experience, only education, respect, willingness to do and new ideas!

name: Anna-Karin - Small organic farm in the home of Swedish Lapland
description:
For this season 2026 we are now already fully booked!
We are a couple, Anna-Karin and Pelle who lives here on our farm. In the summertime my son also are home from school. Our animals on the farm are sheeps, some hens, one dog and two cats. In early spring the lambs are born and later they  are released to pasture with their mothers.  Need help with the general farm upkeep, house restoring including painting, gardening, non toxic products used. The farm is surrounded by beautiful lakes and mountains. You can do fishing, swimming and hiking here, in 24h sunlight in summer. In the winter you can go cross-country skiing, ride a Swedish "spark", walk or fish on the ice.  Easy 10min walk to local supermarket. I provide clean accomodation, shared bathroom, healthy food, barbeque, TV, non smoking indoors.
_wwoof_travel: The farm is approx. 2h bus trip away from AirPort or train station in Umeå, Skellefteå or Lycksele. More details available upon request.

name: Humberto - Educational Centre, Hobby Farm, Eco Retreat
description:
"Centro Energetico Hitugsungo"  is a permaculture sustainability project where we intend to create an Eco-village of people focused on healing ALL TYPES OF WOUNDS in the land, the environment, the ethnic groups, the families and the individuals.  We make our own organic soils and are working towards creating a food forest aiming at providing the majority of the needs of the Eco-village.
There is sufficient accommodation for 12 people in proper beds and there is ample room and good facilities for hammocks and tents.
Our land is located 15 minutes drive from some very popular beaches in the Colombian Caribbean coast between Barranquilla and Cartagena.
The 2.5 hectares of undulated land is located in territory with Ancestral Indigenous traditions and is surrounded by native dry forest,  We have some areas that will be dedicated to natural wild life and reforestation.
We welcome all volunteers interested in permaculture and bio-construction and more so if they have some spiritual background and interest in healing practices.
Long stays are welcome provided that we can create harmonious and prosperous relationships. 

Organic methods: We aim to grow continuously towards becoming food self sufficient, utilizing permaculture guidelines and making our own organic soils based in vermicompost, compost and other ingredients.  Use mostly Neem extracts for pest control 

Certifications: There are no internationally recognized certifying bodies in Colombia

Current projects: We are just starting our project so there is a lot to do.
Our main projects now are:
Make organic soil mixes based on vermi compost and other ingredients.
Plant more Aloe Vera
Plant more Moringa
Harvest, dry and prepare Neem concentrates
Plant more fruit trees
Expand vegie patch
Plant more native trees
Make walking trials

name: Mathieu - Permaculture farm spread across 8 hectares in Sumba
description:
Since 2016, the Sumba Hospitality Foundation (SHF) has provided vocational training to Sumbanese youth, with 64 students annually specializing in areas like Culinary, Food & Beverage, Front Office, Spa, and Housekeeping. Training combines practical experience at Maringi Sumba hotel and Makan Dulu restaurant with English, life skills, and sustainability.

The Foundation is located on an 8-hectare site, including nearly 4 hectares of gardens dedicated to sustainable agriculture and permaculture practices.

Key Responsibilities:
- Work in coordination with the Permaculture Manager
- Planting, weeding, watering, and general garden maintenance
- Harvesting fruits and vegetables
- Composting and soil preparation
- Basic construction (mulching, simple structures)
- Cleaning tools, equipment and maintaining work areas
- Assisting in daily farm routines and teamwork activities

Additional Responsibilities (for candidates with prior farm/agriculture experience):
- Train and mentor students in basic agricultural practices
- Monitor crop health, pest management, and irrigation systems
- Improve productivity through efficient planting, composting, and harvesting techniques
- Assist in planning seasonal crop cycles and farm production schedules
- Assess current farming methods and integrate permaculture principles
- Manage vegetable crops, including harvesting and processing
- Optimize green spaces for food production while preserving biodiversity
- Collaborate with landscaping to create productive and attractive spaces
- Conduct workshops and create educational materials on permaculture
- Support soil health, erosion control, and ecological balance
- Contribute to future sustainable landscape planning

Note: Working in Sumba’s climate requires strong physical fitness.

As part of the SHF family, airport transfer, housing and 3 meals a day are provided. Weekly cleaning of your room and laundry is also included.
_wwoof_travel: Tambolaka Airport, 1 hour flight from Denpasar Bali

name: Jon - 54-acre farm with sugar cane, vegetables, dairy, fruits, and water buffalo near Tampa, FL
description:
54 acre farm off main road; mostly natural pastures marshes, flatwoods. 1 acre for vegetables,sugar cane, non-certified organic vegetables, dairy and fruits for fresh markets. Small herd of Water Buffalo for milk, manure, and meat  (fencing). East of Tampa Fl. Hoping to find Woofers for more permanent membership, year round work. Interested in renewable energy with solar electric and water already in place. Winter season biggest, a couple needed. Have 2 large bedrooms in upstairs barn addition. Kids OK. Can pick-up from bus, train etc. Egalitarian type situation, but private ownership. We have one large common area kitchen open 6:30 am to 8:30 we share, cooking and cleaning responseable, food is mainly approved for an Weston A Price Diet. There's food available for special needs diets as gluten free, vegetarian, etc.  but animals are a big part of our permaculture farm and we eat meat. This is sometimes hard work, with HOT, buggy conditions, understand some will do better than others. We're raising our grandchildren and in our seventies, and looking for possible long term partners, that wanted to continue our sustainable farming efforts here.E mail, phone interview, submit our questionnaire.

Organic methods: permaculture, closed loop, sustainable, earth friendly methods

Certifications: Don't make enough money on our veggies to need certification

Current projects: Fencing, vegetable farming, nursery, fruit tree & perennial orchard, pest plant control, food processing, farm & building maintenance, dairy, etc.

Internet access available

name: Heidi - Small farm, raising horses, chickens, vegetables &  grassland - rural
description:
Hello Wwoof travelers, the farm is full until March 1, 2027 and is currently dealing with neighbours soil erosion deposits, so please understand that any requests that are for a stay from June 2026 until next March 2026, may go unanswered due to the unexpected clean up work at the farm and the fact that the farm is at capacity with Wwoof guests.  Requests for April 2027 and beyond will be responded to.  There will be updated information posted on this profile should there be any cancelations.

Spring and Summer 2027 wwoofing will include caring for horses, cleaning horse sheds, burdocking (its a thing), weeding, fixing fences, riding when the weather supports a pleasant ride, some years we welcome new foals to the farm, caring for the chickens, gardening (we grow amazing garlic), raspberry patch nurturing and more!  There are Working Equitation Shows I attend June, July, August and September, and I will need help getting ready for those. 

Summer 2027 wwoof guests  have the option to stay in the cooler main home large spare room that we are currently finishing.  There is room for one guest only unless you are a couple, and we share the bathroom.  Windy Coulee is a minimalist Ranch and Household, striving to reduce waste and reducing the use of plastic, as well as work and live more efficiently so we can spend even more time in nature and enjoying the horses and animals.  

The ranch is a great place to rest and "disconnect with the online human world".  Sourdough is a tradition Sebastien created in 2022 and the starter is hence named "Sebastien".  Most wwoof guests bake sourdough during their stay, I am happy to share Sebastien's recipe, with Lisa's additions, Ella and Ava's savoury and sweet additions, Sophia's experience and my tweaks and Ari and Francoise hot scoring tips.  In summer, the farm has Harvest Host Campers staying for a night.  The campers have become yet another fun tradition, with guest dog walks and interesting people traveling across Canada in lar

name: Namwaka - Organic agroforestry in a small Zambian village
description:
Our project: 

Rain Drops Community Foundation is situated in Silundu, a small off-grid village in the Western Province of Zambia, 12km from Mongu. In order to persue our fight against deforestation, we concentrate on local agro-forestry and raising awareness about climate change issues. We organise workshops in the surrounding villages and give out free trees we grow from seeds.
I live with my children Elijah (15 y.o) & Lydia (12 y.o). We have chickens and some pigs. 


Our activities: 

We firmly believe that the key in mitigating deforestation, is held by the local communities. It’s for this reason that one of the aims of our project is to make the community aware of the seriousness of the deforestation that our country is undergoing, as well as the impact that climate change has.
We would appreciate help establishing a new tree nursery, working in the vegetable patch, building reed huts and participating in the awareness campaigns in the villages (showing people how to plant trees, demonstrating how to make compost and charcoal briquettes from agricultural waste material). We also need help writing project proposals for grants and running the organisation.
We would be very happy to show you how to make these charcoal briquettes, how to plant seeds and share our Zambian culture with you.


A typical WWOOFer's day: 

Every day is different ! We can start work at 7 or 8 or 9 or 11, it depends on what you prefer and on the weather. There is no fixed time-table, and we tend to rest when it is too hot. We can eat together or separate for lunch, and generally sit together for dinner. It would be nice for you to join us when cooking/eating, to have nice interactions, share stories, etc.


What else? 

I love cooking local dishes & discovering new  foods. Baking cakes and scones is one of my favourite hobbies so come prepared with your nice sweet recipes. 
I love to collect & plant seeds, interacting with people from all over the world and would be very happy to share th
_wwoof_travel: Mongu bus station (direct bus from Lusaka and also other big towns)

name: Maya - Food forest in regenerative agriculture, between jungle and ocean, Costa Rica.
description:
We are a family of travelers who chose to settle in Costa Rica to live closer to nature and create a place for transformation. We live between jungle and ocean, in a simple ecological home made of earth and natural materials.
At the heart of our project, we host individual somatic retreats for people going through periods of transition or life changes. The space is designed to support deep rest, reconnection to the body, and a slower, more grounded rhythm.
In parallel, we are developing a food forest and a greenhouse, working toward food autonomy and land regeneration. The land, once degraded by intensive grazing, is gradually becoming a diverse and living ecosystem, including the use of local medicinal plants.
Our daily life is shared with the surrounding wildlife: monkeys, armadillos, raccoons, coyotes, frogs, snakes… and many others who are part of this ecosystem.
We live here as a family, guided by simplicity, nature, and the ongoing evolution of this place.

Our daily activities follow the rhythm of the seasons and the climate.
From April to August, as the rainy season begins, this is our main planting period. We focus on preparing the soil, bringing compost, planting trees and crops, sowing seeds, propagating plants (cuttings), and building simple structures to support the development of the food forest and garden.
The development of the food forest is done with a strong focus on native species, guided by the knowledge of a local elder who shares traditional techniques and helps us choose the right plants for this ecosystem.
Throughout the year, tasks also include maintaining the land (weeding, mulching, watering when needed), caring for young plants, harvesting, and observing how the ecosystem evolves.
We aim to share practical knowledge about regenerative agriculture in a tropical environment: soil restoration, plant diversity, food forest design, and the use of local medicinal plants.
A typical day usually starts early in the morning to avoid the heat. We w
_wwoof_travel: Tamarindo Airport / Liberia Airport / Santa Cruz bus station

name: Agata - Ecological finca with veggies and fruit garden, animals, events, retreats and visits
description:
Welcome to our eco-farm!
Our farm located on the Spanish island of Gran Canaria in the province of Las Palmas. We cultivate vegetables and fruits, organically grown and free from pesticides, chemical fertilizers and GMOs. We deeply believe in the idea that we can protect the environment with the practice of organic and regenerative agriculture. 

Values and aims: promote ecology, create connections between living beings, express creativity in harmony with nature, and build a self-sustainable community that spreads our values: our ethical decision is to take responsibility for our own existence and that of our next generation.
Our farm is a quiet place where enthusiasts of living together outside the network will find themselves perfectly. In addition to typical agricultural activities related to growing vegetables and fruits and cooking vegan food, we organize activities related to personal development, veganism, sustainability and eco-living.  We offer day events and group few days long retreats and workshops on personal and spiritual development, contact with nature etc. We open vegan restaurant offer once per week and also rent out the part of our house on airbnb and booking for tourists interested in such a sustainable, ecological tourism. 
We are looking for active, positive and world-curious people to work, who are not afraid of challenges and physical tasks. Working on a farm involves many demanding tasks, such as: preparing agricultural land for sowing, maintenance of the garden, harvesting crops, taking care of animals, helping with construction related tasks and house maintenance, including cleaning, as well as food preparation for the whole team and sometimes running some errands like shopping or organizing activities around the farm and events. 

Please be aware that there always is a lot of work and we are only looking for people that can keep up with us and help us build an amazing space and experience for everyone ;)

Work lasts 5 hours a day. 5 days

name: Xavier - Ferme pédagogique itinérante et élevage poulets fermier
description:
Notre ferme est située entre Toulouse et Castres. Nous élevons nos animaux pour notre activité de ferme pédagogique itinérante.
Nous avons toute sortes d'animaux qui peuvent participer à nos animations de ferme itinérante: âne, poney, chèvres, moutons, lapins, cochons, poules, canards, oies, dindon.
Nous élevons également des poulets fermier en plein air. Ainsi que des moutons Ouessant, des lapins et des poules d'ornements.
Si vous souhaitez découvrir la vie à la ferme avec nous : nourrir les animaux, le nettoyage quotidien, l'entretien de la ferme, couper du bois pour l'hiver, participer aux animations de la ferme itinérante ou simplement faire une balade en forêt ou découvrir la région, nous vous attendons avec plaisir dans notre ferme.

Nous avons également le projet d'ouvrir en ferme pédagogique fixe prochainement. Donc nous réalisons régulièrement des chantiers (en plus du travail de la ferme) afin de pouvoir accueillir du public : réalisation de clôture, modification ou création de bâtiments, aménagement dans la forêt... 

Nous aimons beaucoup la musique, la cuisine, la bonne humeur et la convivialité.
A la demande et selon les saisons, nous pourrons vous proposer de découvrir le fonctionnement de notre ferme pédagogique itinérante directement sur une animation. 

Français et un peu d'anglais parlés.
_wwoof_travel: Gare de Damiatte (8km); Gare de Toulouse (50 Km); métro sortie Balma;

name: Annemarie - Cedar Falls is a small farm; Centre of Living and Learning located in rural Mpumalanga province SA
activities: Unterkunft: bedroom, trailer, campsite, yurt-tipi, shack
description:
Cedar Falls is a small farm; Centre of Living and Learning located in rural Mpumalanga province of Southern Africa, Cedar Falls lies between Machadadorp/Entokozweni and Dullstroom. The land is 30 hectares (80 acres) in size, with blue-gum, cedar and eucalyptus forests of approximately 35 acres. Below the forest, the Taute River flows through the land. A magnificent waterfall with wooden deck and braai area lies well-hidden at the foot of the forest. There are three dams, one filled with koi. There are 6 houses on the land, occupied by two sisters, friends, family and staff.
Indigenous species include fish eagles, wattle cranes, wild pig, jackal, duiker, mountain reedbuck and scores of bird species. We have four horses (wild), we are surrounded by both game and cattle/sheep/goats farmed by neighbours, mostly renting out their lodges on weekends. 
Numerous food gardens have been established, with focus on medicinal plants, herbs and organic vegetables (and still growing). Established fruit trees include apple, plum, loquat, grapes, pecan, lemon, orange, prickly pear and granadilla. We harvest our own honey and gather free range eggs from our feathered fowl.
The fenced-off enclosed camp site (Camp Kaia) is one of the 6 houses; a space that offers a 3 sleeper including a kitchen-dining area as well as an indoor 'chill' space. Lodging options are Native American tipi's (sleeps 3 each) or two sleeper tents. All are fully equipped w/bedding, with outdoor solar lights. Ablutions are shared (and want to build more). A large circular fire-pit with seating is a prominent feature. 
We welcome volunteers at any time for periods of two (2) weeks plus. We do suggest that all volunteers take a day or two to settle into their new surrounds and to familiarise themselves with facilities, staff, pets, the town of Machadadorp. We welcome enthusiastic hands-on people keen to teach, assist, learn and implement a sustainABLE way of life, who enJOY nature and being away from the city buzz –

name: Clara Lala - Multidimensional habitat ꩜ Açores 🪱 protopia 🦶retreat 🐦song sanctuary 𓁿 Living Laboratory
description:
Welcome to Botanical Birdsong 🌿🕊️

I'm Clara Lala Espe, an ARTivist — an artist and activist working at the intersection of art, co-creation with nature, and social change — born, raised and socialized on the wild, free-spirited grounds of the electronic music mecca Berlin, now choosing to live in the middle of the Atlantic Ocean with my little family.

I'm part of the Design Science Studio (DSS), a worldwide artist collective working towards a world that works for all of life.
My mission: transforming a 4,500 m², 30+ year-old Atlantic garden into a flourishing permaculture project on the lush north side of São Miguel, Azores — just 20 minutes from the capital Ponta Delgada, but deeply immersed in nature.

The garden is already an oasis. Along a romantic alley lie 10 individual lots, each with its own unique character — majestic trees, exotic ornamental plants, wild edible herbs, wild flowers, fruit trees and so much more. At the heart of the land is a central plaza with a living altar of a gigantic phoenix palm. This is our outdoor living room: here we eat together, make bonfires, tend the plant nursery, and where I do my daily sunrise ritual for all of life.

We're located at the edge of the small village Farropo. I share this land with my 10-year-old daughter Emilia and our charming dog Kozmo. We live in a sweet Tiny House that made the journey with us all the way from Berlin.

We started on-site in 2025 — carefully pruning the giant trees, setting up permaculture food fields, vermicompost systems, wildflower meadows, and increasing biodiversity on all levels.

My work here is very practical and hands-on — but also deeply spiritual. Every sunrise I perform an ancient Vedic fire ritual called Agnihotra, using the ashes in a farming method called homa farming. Often, I play the symphonic gong in and for the garden — bringing resonance and joy to every living being here. 🌞🔔

What This Actually Is
This is a living land project — real work, real weather, real co-creat
_wwoof_travel: Airport Ponta Delgada (Self responsibility take a taxi, costs 20€)

name: Ufuk Erdem - Ali Çağanın anısını yaşattığım, kendine yeten, doğal dostu bir çiftlik
description:
Kurumsal iş yaşantısını bırakıp içinde büyüdüğüm çiftçiliğe Amasya/Merzifon/Karatepe köyünde devam ediyorum. 6,5 dönüm alan içine konuşlu 4 odalı bir köy evi, kümes, depolar, taş fırın, koyun ağılı, bahçecilik alanı ve ceşitli ağaçlar mevcut. Kedi köpek dostlarım ve yarı evcil kümes hayvanlarım var. Zaman zaman güvercin ve tavşan da besliyorum.

Özellikle doğal bahçeciliği, ata tohumu ile mahsul elde etmeyi, kompost yapımını, hayvan gübresi kullanımını, tahta işçiliği ve inşaat işlerini, koyun sürüsünü idare etmeyi ve beslenmelerini yönetmeyi, taş fırın kullanmayı, köy halkı ile kültür alış verişi yapmayı öğrenebiliriz. 
Sabah güne koyunları ağıllarında, kümes hayvanlarını yerlerinde, kedileri verandada beslemek ile başlarım. Akşama kadar olan zamanda kışın etrafı düzenlemek ve el işi yapmak ile geçirirken, yazın bahçe ile uğraşılır. Akşam da yine hayvanlar beslenir.

Metal kaynak, ahşap işleme, kerpiç yapımı gibi çiftlik fiziki faaliyetleri zaman oldukça yapıyorum.

Şubat: Tohumları toprakla buluşturma, ağaç dikme, budama
Mart: Bahçe hazırlama, fide hazırlama
Nisan: Arıcılık, ekim, kırlarda ot toplama, ağaçlara bakım, hayvan gübresi hazırlama 
Mayıs: Sebze dikimi, Hayvan bakımı, çobanlık, Arıcılık, yem bitkileri hasadı
Haziran: Meyve toplama, çapalama, sulama, arpa hasadı
Temmuz: Arıcılık, sebze hasadı, sulama.
Ağustos: Bal hasadı, kışlık sebze ekimi, salça yapımı, domates kurutma
Eylül: Meyve hasadı, sirke, kuşburnu,  çiftlik inşaat işleri, bağ bozumu
Ekim: Arıları kışa hazırlama ve bakımı,   sac ekmeği yapımı
Kasım: Kışlık gıda hazırlığı 

2 oda da toplam 2 gönüllü rahatça konaklayabilir. Daha fazla misafir olursa bahçe evinde de konaklama yapılabiliyor. Yemek pişirmeyi severim. Yöresel yemeklerde yapılır.  Beraber pişirir, beraber yeriz.

name: Gabriel Kwasi - A farm that works with nature and not against it, using holistic practices to nurture nature
description:
My name is Gabriel Kwasi  Asare, I’m a professional agronomist and nature systems designer for farms.
The core of the project is centered on stewardship, Independence and Multiplcation.
After being an employee for years, I’m transitioning with my partner to running our farm in Chingangauka, Mulobani Village, Zambia.
We want to improve the lives of the local communities that already have water in this area to engage in climate adaptation ans mitigation strategies to reduce poverty, starvation and access to basic education.  Don’t have pets yet as we are still organizing ourselves on the farm. I live with my partner. No kids yet.

Dry land farming, regenerative landscape farming and holistic agriculture incorporating all living organisms and creatures in the process.
Crop production, pasture poultry, building earthen structures, community engagement, field days with farmers and sales at market stalls.

The accomodations are decent and there’s access to electricity off grid for charging phones and laptops.
We try to be as traditional as possible, staying away from modern technology and lifestyle. As our living space works with nature and prefer communing more often with nature as much as possible.
Our philosophy is nature is the healer of mental health and nourishment to the body, mind and soul.

We love to do things as a group. Eating together, cooking together and working together, worship together as we are Christians and still embrace those of other faiths.
We have great natural spaces near the farm for exploration and we are along the road to Victoria falls. And the farm is near the house Dr David Livingstone built on his way to discover Victoria falls.

name: Rakan - Mixed Farm, Family farm
description:
Located at the village of Jureinah 4 km from the center of Madaba’s downtown, Carob Farm is a small 5-acre farm that aims at demonstrating the efficacy of ecological farming practices in producing an abundance of nutritious food while building thriving ecosystems.

To achieve that we are using principles of Permaculture design in harnessing renewable energy systems to diminish expensive agricultural inputs, and produce a wide variety of nutritious food. We basically use simple innovations to harvest rainwater, produce compost, rehabilitate soil, and cultivate intensively and efficiently — a farming solution that particularly appeals to small  and marginal farmers.

The farm acts as a demonstration site that helps educate our community on the vast possibilities of transforming their lands into productive ecosystems that have the natural ability to alleviate the harsh effects of climate change.
 
Our biggest hope is to inspire people to reconnect with their farming roots, revive our indigenous farming heritage, rehabilitate our soil and showcase that an abundance of clean, nutritious food is commercially achievable if we depart from soil-degrading, chemical agriculture, and embrace ecological farming practices. 

Here are a few examples of the innovative systems we are devising at the farm:
A multi-crop vegetable production system that produces 10 times the amount of food per square meter and uses 90% less water. All done naturally with compost made in-house – zero external inputs.
A low-cost rainwater harvesting/ tree growing system that also reverses soil erosion and collects organic matter.
An agroforestry system of native plant species that provide ecosystem services and much needed biomass that has the ability to sequester carbon dioxide, prevent soil erosion, provide wind and fire break, and mulch. 
A low maintenance composting system including liquid compost making using the farm’s own waste products. 

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Queen Alia International Airport, Madaba Town.

name: Neva (Paula) and Tobie - 105-acres of land located in north central Oklahoma on the U.S. "Great Plains"
description:
Hello and welcome!  Our 105-acres of land are located in north central Oklahoma, which is in the center of the U.S. and part of the “Great Plains.” It is heavily forested in places and has some open pasture and farm land as well. 
 We'd love to host a Future Farmer in 2025!

LOCATION. We are 3.5 miles from a small town (population 1,200), we are 17 miles east of a university town, Stillwater (population 100,000) and about 50 miles west of Tulsa, OK (population 403,000). If you travel by air or bus, you may choose Stillwater or Tulsa, Oklahoma for your destination. We can pick you up at the Stillwater Airport when you arrive and return you there when you depart.  **If you choose Tulsa—please see information below—under the heading “TRANSPORTATION.”

Our farm operations are small, consisting of a 1/2-acre vegetable garden; 5 Nubian milk-goats named  Ladybug, Polkie Dots, Delilahand their offspring, sometimes including bottle babies; a flock of about 25 free-range chickens and a few baby chickens; 2 guineas (in the Pea fowl family); large livestock guardian dogs (5 Great Pyrenees named Molly, Kiwi, Grey Ears, Teen Wolfman, & Blue oftentimes with puppies; and one cat, Stripey + her kittens.

ABOUT US.  We are dedicated to organic practices in raising animals and in our gardening. We use no pesticides, no herbicides, no chemical fertilizers, nor do we push production on our animals with lighting or hormones. We are intrigued with sustainability and self-sufficiency. We are also in the mid stages of designing an Eco-Village /intentional community and are studying the options. We are in retirement.  We enjoy having friends from all generations.

We have a small Health Food Store business that we have operated since 2000. We have down-sized and are transitioning it from its original location in town to our farm.

OUR CURRENT MAIN PROJECT is non-agricultural: Constructing a community building with a kitchen, dining room, four bedrooms, w/ an entry  mudroom & breezeway.

Also
_wwoof_travel: Stillwater, OK has small Airport serviced by American Airlines.  They also have a bus service to bring people to Stillwater from Tulsa, OK OSU Tulsa Campus, if someone flies into Tulsa.

name: Manish - We are located at roof of panchasey hills west of Pokhara.
description:
Hello WWOOFers,

Welcome to our peaceful farmhouse near Pokhara, surrounded by nature and breathtaking mountain scenery. We are passionate about cultural exchange, sustainable living, local tourism, and connecting with people from around the world.

Here, volunteers can enjoy hiking to nearby hilltops, explore the beautiful countryside, and experience stunning panoramic views of the majestic Annapurna Range. Our open farmhouse kitchen and small restaurant space create a warm and friendly environment where everyone can share meals, stories, and experiences together.

We enjoy gardening, collecting plants, exploring nature, meeting new friends, and sharing the simple joys of rural life. If you love mountain landscapes, local culture, outdoor activities, and meaningful cultural exchange, we would be happy to welcome you to our farm stay.

name: Samantha - Farm growing vegetables and cut flowers near New Orleans
description:
We are small scale farm growing annual vegetables, cut flowers, and planting a soon to be orchard. Located on the northshore of Lake Pontchatrain we are a short drive to local small towns with grocery stores, hardware stores, gas, restaurants, etc. New Orleans is an hour and 15 minutes away and our current market, so we take that drive weekly.  We are committed to sustainability and good food. Although we are not certified organic, we use organic practices. The current production field is .5 acres with more spaces needing to get prepped for further plantings. This is our 3rd season on this property so establishing healthy soil and infrastructure projects are priority. We are looking for hard working adventurers. We can't wait to hear from you!

Activities:
Harvesting food and flowers
Hand transplanting food and flowers
Seeding food and flowers
Weeding
Foraging
Pond building
Trench creation
Clearing and restoration of native areas on property
Walk in cooler build
Orchard Planing and Planting
Cover crop rotation and occultation 
Chicken Tractor builds
Chicken care and maintenance
Chicken Harvesting and Butchering
Fence Building
Floral Arranging
Drying Flowers
Flower crafts
Scrubs, salts, salve, lip balm creation
Menu planning

Potential Schedule:

Our expectations are minimal. Do your best, listen to guidance, and maintain a good attitude as we do here on the farm. 

7 ish wake up and hot breakfast is served

start work for 8am, although this can be earlier in the hotter months so we can take off during the mid day heat. We usually start off with harvesting whatever needs to be harvested, or laborious work in the field. 

break for a snack and tea usually around 10am and back to the field for 10:30

 work until about 12:30 where we will break for lunch (usually prepared on your own)

Usually after lunch depending on the weather we will do stuff inside or requiring less intense labor as the day moves on. 

Wwoofers can expect to be done with work around 2/3pm

A cooked

name: Rabindra man - Organic Dragon Fruit Farm
description:
Our farm is located in Thansing, Nuwakot, in a quiet and natural hill area surrounded by open skies and green landscapes. The place is peaceful, with fresh air, beautiful views, and a slow rural lifestyle. It is an ideal environment for people who want to live close to nature and experience simple village life in Nepal.

Before moving to Nuwakot, our farming journey began in Khopasi, where we practiced organic farming. Over time, our work continued in Nuwakot, where we now manage our farm and host volunteers. Since 2012, we have hosted many WWOOFers from around 12 different countries, creating a space for cultural exchange, shared learning, and friendship. Volunteers from different backgrounds have lived and worked together here, sharing experiences and skills.

The average daily work schedule is from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. Work is balanced and respectful, leaving enough time for rest and personal activities. Volunteers help with farm work and daily routines while learning about natural farming and simple living.

We provide neat and clean rooms and washrooms for WWOOFers. Accommodation is comfortable, well maintained, and suitable for a peaceful stay. Daily life on the farm is simple, clean, and friendly.

In addition to farm activities, there are opportunities for teaching, cooking, and hiking. Volunteers can share knowledge, learn local cooking, and explore the surrounding hills and villages through walks and hikes.

Our farm offers a calm and meaningful experience for those who value nature, simplicity, and genuine human connection.

name: Lucila - Agritourism Destination
description:
La Vigna is our piece of heaven on Earth. We escaped from a noisy Buenos Aires city 25 years ago, and started little by little to build our dream over an old building, where we created a small Farm Hotel and a Sheep, gotas and cows dairy farm and cheese factory… We went on with a small forest, vegetable gardens, animals in freedom, and lately bee hives. I am a psychologist and my husband an Eco Architect, and we have 3 teenage children . Little house on the prairie…Woofers are welcome to stay with us for one month approximately, and help with the farm, the cheeses, and with general duties of maintenance and feeding animals, milking, or cooking with us… Depending on everyone´s interests and time of the year, volunteers will be ask to help with the milking of the sheep and goats every morning, and during the afternoon sometimes help with the vegetable gardens, painting or helping fixing fences, gates…Our idea is to receive mainly couples, or up to a maximum of two volunteers at a time. Working 5 days a week during 6 hours per day. Woofers would be living in a small house next to La Vigna main house, by their own. It is a recycled shiping conteiner called Cactus Ranch, mainly built by volunteers! They will get the food that we produce in the farm every day, and they are expected to cook their meals (although they will be invited to join our family for meals during weekends if they wish to).We are really looking forward to receiving volunteers not only because we need they help, but mainly because we believe it is a wonderful exchange experience for everyone and it is exactly what needed in today´s world! Hopefully my kids will be joining woofing soon and also be able to travel volunteering as part of the real exchange adventure! Welcome to La Vigna Láctea!!!

Organic methods: Permacultura
Pastoreo racional voisine

Certifications: Red de Agroecología de Uruguay

Internet access available

name: Michelle - Homestead, Remote study opportunities, volunteer opportunities
description:
Our tree farm is 45 acres, surrounded by national forest, on the edge of Rocky Mountain National Park in Estes Park, CO. We welcome WWOOFers to our tree farm for a multitude of projects. 

**We are currently seeking two types of Wwoofers. We need someone (or a couple) who can build slash piles, and we need someone with chainsaw experience and wants to work hard. If you have chainsaw experience, we will talk ahead of time, as we have had many people claim to have experience and then leave trees hung up, or other dangerous activities.

Much of our land is heavily loaded with dead and down trees. Our goals for land management are written by a Colorado state forester. To accomplish these goals we build and burn slash piles, cut down dead trees, and thin overgrown areas. 

We maintain our ability to live off the power grid. This includes flowing water from a naturally occurring spring to our cistern, cutting firewood and maintaining our fully off-grid power system (solar). This means we are always conserving power and water.

For a work day, we would love 25-30 hours a week of your time and then want you to have an adventure exploring the area. 

Our favorite part of Wwoofing is having a communal table for three meals a day. We are omnivores/carnivores (we eat meat), using our smoker and BBQ grill for tasty meals. We are unable to accommodate vegans, gluten-free, or vegetarians at this time.

We have two rustic tent cabins with a queen bed in each, with an outhouse (in the summer). In the winter and summer, we have a shared bunk style accommodation, with heat, above the garage and composting outhouse.  Our two Bernese Mountain Dogs provide "security." We are not able to accept ANY outside animals.

Organic methods: We produce mulch from our horses, along with compost to use around our property to bring up the organic matter in our soils. We harvest mushrooms on our property and recently mulched a lot of our slash piles to propagate oyster mushrooms for harvesting through
_wwoof_travel: Denver International Airport is the nearest airport. There are a few transportation options to get to Estes Park, but we can also make arrangements if we are available.

name: Eray - Agritourism Destination, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat, Farm
description:
Merhaba, 
Çiftliğimize şimdiden hoşgeldiniz. Burada paylaşacağımız zaman ve mekanlarla ile ilgili sizlerle biraz bilgi paylaşalım istiyorum. 

Hayatın getirdiği alışkanlıklardan kurtulabilmek ve kendimize dönebilmek için, medeniyetin sunduğu konfor alanından uzakta, yabanda, bir köye yerleştik. İçinde olduğumuz vadide tek haneyiz. 

Burası, 900 mt yükseklikte, çevresinde ve içinde zengin bir hayat canlılığının olduğu, doğayla iç içe bir permakültür çiftliği. Henüz emekleme aşamasında ve sizlerin enerjisiyle daha da bütünleşecek.

Permakültür ilkeleri ile sürdürmeye çalıştığımız çiftlik faaliyetlerinde katkılarınızı almaktan memnuniyet duyacağız.

Off-Grid bir yaşamda, elektriği ve suyu kendimizin ürettiği bir yaşam içinde, doğayla uyumlanarak çiftlik faaliyetlerine destek olmak isterseniz bekleriz.

Mevsime ve ihtiyaca  göre, sürdürülebilir organik toprak işlerinden, mahsül toplamaya, ahşap işçiliğinden, taş örmeye, kompost yapımından, tohum toplarına kadar bir .çok şeyi tecrübe edebileceğiniz bir alan. 

Yoğun ve sıkı tempo çalışma gibi bir hedefimiz yok, amacımız severek güzel şeyler ortaya çıkarmak ve bilgilerimizi karşılıklı paylaşmak.

Gönüllülerimize max 2 kişilik, odalarda konaklayacaklar. Odalarda banyo ve sıcak su mevcut.
Yemekleri ortak alanda birlikte yapıyor olacağız.

Beraber yaşamı paylaştığımız, evleri dış ortamda, 3 adet çiftlik köpeğimiz; 4 tane de kedimiz mevcut. 

Burası doğadan sağlayabildikleriyle yaşayan bir yer. Elektriğimizi ve sıcak suyumuzu güneşten üretiyoruz. Tüketirken , zor şartlarda üretilen bir kaynak olduğunun bilinciyle seçimler yapmanızı dileriz. 

Tüm musluklardan temiz, ölçümü yapılmış içme suyu akmaktadır.

Banyolarımızda duş jeli, şampuan bulunmamaktadır. 
Havlu, çarşaf, nevresim gibi odalarda bulunması gereken diğer şeyler mevcut. 

Kapalı alanlarda ayakkabı kullanmadığımız için terliklerinizi yanınızda getirmenizi öneririz. Kapalı alanlarda sigara içimi yasaktır.

Çok keyifli vakit geçirmenizi dil

name: Jamila - Building collaborative community in tropical orchards and gardens
description:
Aloha 🦋
We are a  farm practicing Regenerative Agriculture and a homestead where we are creating the foundation for a community. On our farm we grow Taro(Kalo), Ginger, Turmeric, Banana, Papayas, Plantains and Coconuts+++ for our community. Our Homestead is 46 years old and contains extensive infrastructure(main house/community kitchen/ healing space, a giant wood-working shop, bath house(solar and wood fire heated), basic tiny cabins, Hearth (covered fire gathering space) simple yet beautiful).

We are calling in helpers as we form a nature-based family-type community on this Homestead.  We are still in the beginning stages of co-creation here, building a foundation for a family to live and thrive here for generations to come.  

While we are still building we are offering opportunities for working and learning in various realms of homestead life such as:

👨‍🌾 On our regenerative/syntropic ag farm with our wonderful elderman farmer which includes many facets of growing food and caring for the land, tool usage and Hawaiian history and practices, (and elder downloads about life in general).

🌱 Planting and weeding  in our homestead gardens and orchards, building soil, mulching, planting seeds, learning about tropical herbs, and tending our nursery.

🥑 Helping in our kitchen making plant-based, garden and farm-to-table meals for the crew 

🍷In our juice bar, making sugar cane juice, lemonade, morning chai and elixers

🔨 In our extensive workshop doing building projects like tree houses, tiny houses, instrument making or fixing machines , making furniture,  organizing and learning about different kinds of wood and tools. 

🥭Harvesting food from our orchards or in neighboring orchards that we work with and bringing the harvest to the community kitchen chefs.

🌴 harvesting, processing, and caring for Coconuts, making coconut cream, opening young coconuts for guests, and tending the coconut bar

Each day, each crew member works in one of these various realms of service, 5

name: Phạm - Organic daily farm in the hill
description:
I am an English teacher in primary school. I live in Bac Giang city with my family but I have a farm in my countruside where my parents live and do there. I want to make organic farm to serve our daily life and educate the children about farm, living skills for kids and make international communication environment for kids, too.

You can usually find me out feeding organic vegetables, clean atmosphere with paddy field and hill. We raise animal and plant fruit trees and the other healthy products, too.
You can work together, plant vegetables, fruit tree and take care our garden. On addition, you can help the children understanding about organic environment or take part in some activities with them, too.

We eat 3 meals together with products that we plant, grow and make. 
We have a bulgalow and full of accommodations there.

I like travelling, cooking and reading books. 
We can go hiking, go swimming and the other activities in the other places at the weekend when we have time.

According to the local police, WWOOFing constitutes a "grey area." It's still considered labor, not a tourist visa. Therefore, to be legal, you should voluntarily share a small portion of the daily food and utility costs with the homeowner. This would be legal under the "experiential tourism" model and would be risk-free for both of us. I'd like to ask for your opinion on this matter. Do you agree? Just $5-7 per day. You can find more information on this on Google; we only want to comply with the law and have no ill intentions. If you use work visa or labor visa, free for all.

Please understand it. Thanks a lot

name: Megumi - Regenerative Banana & Pig Circular Farm in the Tropical Rainforest
description:
Hello WWOOFers,

I am Japanese and my husband is from the indigenous Rungus community of Borneo. We live together with our extended Rungus family in a lively community of about ten people, including adults and children. Our farm is located in the tropical rainforest, surrounded by rich biodiversity. This forest is home to wildlife such as mouse deer, pangolins, and kingfishers.

We practice regenerative circular farming and aim to create a field where all living beings can coexist in balance, honoring the natural cycle of life that connects soil, plants, animals, and people.

Our daily work includes cutting grass in the banana plantation, harvesting bananas, chopping young banana trunks for pig feed, feeding and cleaning the pigs, and general farm maintenance.

We are developing systems to ferment and naturally purify animal waste so that nearby rivers are not polluted. Volunteers can learn about regenerative farming, circular nutrient systems, and agroforestry in a tropical rainforest environment.

A typical day usually includes working together in the morning for about half a day, followed by rest, swimming in the river, or spending time with the family in the afternoon.

Meals are mainly traditional Rungus dishes, including vegetables from our farm and seafood caught from the nearby sea.

Accommodation is currently in a strong and secure concrete building. In the future, we are planning to build a traditional Rungus bamboo house so that volunteers can experience indigenous architecture and live closer to the rhythms of the rainforest.

There are rivers and waterfalls nearby where we can swim and connect with nature. The surrounding rainforest is rich and peaceful. We enjoy sharing traditional cooking, stories, and daily life within the Rungus community.

こんにちは、WWOOFerのみなさん。

私は日本人で、夫はボルネオ島の先住民族ルングス族の出身です。現在は大人も子どもも含めた約10人のルングス族の大家族と共に暮らしています。私たちの日常は、先住民族の文化、家族のつながり、そして自然への深い敬意に根ざしています。

私たちの農園は熱帯雨林の中にあり、豊かな生物多様性に囲まれています。この森にはマメジカやセンザンコウ、ナンヨウカワセミなどが生息しています。私たちはこの生

name: Gordon - Festival grounds, Forest garden, Wilderness Adventures/Eco lodge, Woodlot & Educational Centre.
description:
We are take every precaution to ensure the safety of our guests, volunteers and staff.  This includes enhanced cleaning & sanitizing protocols of cabins, staff/volunteer areas & public common areas (Shower House, Straw Bale House, wash facilities and Sauna) 

We are a cultural, educational, ecological and recreational destination with the following values:
●	Diversity of People and Ideas
●	Sense of Community and Belonging
●	Awakening the Artist in Everyone
●	Connecting Nature and Art
●	Provide Meaningful Employment
●	Mindful Use of the Land we Occupy
●	Engaging With One Another

The site itself has camping and year-round cabin rentals along with numerous seasonal events such as four annual music festivals and music, art and ecological camps/workshops. 


Duties could include:

Garden maintenance, propagation, care and harvest
Event/Festival prep set up/take down

Planting trees, stand-tending, releasing

Gathering and hauling firewood

Poultry care, potential poultry  butchering and basic beekeeping

Trail Maintenance and development

Grounds maintenance – such as capturing biomass through mowing & mulching

Building projects

Snow removal

Housekeeping

Knowledge and skills some of our staff  have to share

Your host has over 40 years experience in the silviculture industry, owning and operating a successful tree planting company as well at Ness Core Ventures. I am very knowledgeable of the history of the land and passionate about the forest and the Ness Creek site operations. My work ethics, life history, guidance and knowledge is second to none.

My Manager has been a carpenter for most of his life and is a master craftsman builder. He owns and operates his own sawmill and is site project manager for all capital builds and renovations.  He is also the site Permaculture Manager. Through knowledge passed down from generations, life experience and general curiosity, he has a longstanding passion for gardening and permaculture techniques. He is also an avid wild mush

name: Annita - Permaculture Orchard and Homestead on Beautiful Bruce Peninsula
description:
Hello 👋 we are Frank and Annita and have recently moved to our dream homestead situated in the middle of beautiful Bruce Peninsula! Our property has an abundance of fruit trees and bushes which we are working on restoring to its former glory! We have chickens, ducks, goats, cats, a pet dog and our Livestock Guardian dog on site.  We are taking the Permaculture approach to caring for our orchard and garden. We love to learn and teach about the Permaculture way and take wwoofers and on our Permaculture tour when they arrive!

Annita is the early riser who typically begins the morning baking for the farm stand, then it's off to the chickens and ducks to feed, water, fill pools, collect eggs and clean coops if needed. Next, it's time to greet the goats, feed, water, milk the one milking goat and put outsome fresh cut brush. Our does are hopefully pregnant and if so, they will be kidding in the summer. If wwoofers would like to learn any of these animal husbandry skills,  we'd be happy to teach them. By the time Annita is finished, Frank has a delicious breakfast cooked and we discuss what needs to be done for the day. Wwoofers have breakfast in the trailer and then we  typically get started with the day's tasks  around 9:30-10am. We could be chipping branches, picking fruit, building a structure for the homestead, fixing machinery, haying (end of June and early September) harvesting from the garden, watering or maybe even something else not listed! Our days are often dictated by what our gardens or animals need and the weather! We often do many of these tasks in a day. 

In late May to early June, we are busy planting all of our seed starts out direct sowing into the gardens.  In late July through August, we have many blueberries to pick, so that's what keeps us quite busy.  Then late August until November,  we are into apple season. 

Through these seasons, Annita makes jams and preserves as we recently had our second kitchen certified by our local public health office

name: 선경 - <JJ127>자연과 예술, 사람을 엮는 유기농 감귤 농장 / Where nature and art meet people–organic tangerine farm
description:
✅ 호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
제주 동쪽 삼달리에서 유기농 감귤을 재배하며 살아가는 가족입니다. 자연의 리듬에 맞춰 농사를 짓고 흙과 함께 살아갑니다. 
우리 농장은 예술과 농업, 건강한 일상이 조화를 이루는 공간을 지향합니다. 귤밭 음악회, 피아노 수업, 밭에서 하는 예술놀이 등 다양한 활동을 통해 삶 속에 음악과 철학, 예술을 녹여내고 있습니다.
