도시를 떠나 제주에서 자연과 함께한 지 15년, 닭 2마리와 오리 3마리를 친구처럼 돌보며, 텃밭에서 기른 채소로 직접 요리합니다. 이웃 주민들의 방문도 잦아 저희 가족 외에도 다양한 분들을 만나고, 한국의 정을 깊이 경험하실 수 있을 거예요. 
저희 농장은 서귀포시 성산 일출봉, 표선 해비치해수욕장, 신천목장 등 아름다운 자연환경에 둘러싸여 있으며, 유기농 인증과 농촌융복합 인증을 받은 감귤농장입니다. 또한 농촌 체류형 쉼터와 캠핑 공간, 공용주방이 마련되어 있습니다. 쾌적한 공간에서 지내시면서 서로의 문화와 음식, 음악 등을 나누고 얘기하면 좋겠습니다. 우퍼들과 함께할 시간들을 기대합니다.

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2012년
✅ 농장 형태 : 유기농 인증, 농촌융복한 인증  농장
✅ 농장 주작물 : 감귤

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 2012년부터 삼달리 땅에서 시작된 딴따라농부는 유기농 인증을 받았으며, 화학비료나 농약을 일절 사용하지 않고, 자가 퇴비와 천연 자재로 토양을 돌보고 있습니다. 귤나무를 타고 올라가는 덩쿨 제거를 손으로 하고 있으며, 자연에 최대한 개입하지 않는 농법을 지향합니다. 
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 : 재사용과 재활용을 생활화하고, 아이와 함께 바다 쓰레기를 줍습니다.

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 자녀(여아 10살), 직원(생산관리자)
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려 동물 : 오리 3마리와 닭 2마리
✅ 우퍼가 하는일 :
3-5월 전정 후 나무 치우기
6-8월 덩쿨 제거 
12-2 귤 수확, 택배 포장, 귤 상자 나르기

✅ 선호하는 우퍼상 :
걷는 걸 좋아하고, 음악과 예술, 또는 운동을 사랑하는 분이면 좋겠어요. 
✅ 우퍼에게 당부 메세지 :
근처 5분 정도 걸어가야 버스 정류장이 나옵니다. 시골이라 교통편이 좋지 않습니다. 
일 할 땐 안전하게 일해주시고, 쉬는 시간엔 느긋하게 자연을 즐기실 분이면 좋겠어요.  
✅ 숙소에서 와이파이 가능여부 : 가능
✅ 술,담배 가능여부 : 간헐적 외부흡연, 및 음주가능
✅ 호스트와 식사여부 : 하루 한,두끼만 함께 먹고 나머지는 식재료를 드려 직접 해 드셔야 합니다.
✅ 영어소통 : 기본 가능
✅ 일주일 기준 우핑시간 : 하루5시간 주5일

======== English explanation ==========
✅ Introduction to the Host and the Farm :
We are a family living in Samdal-ri, on the eastern side of Jeju, cultivating organic tangerines in harmony with the rhythms of nature and the soil.
We aim to create a space where art, agriculture, and healthy daily life come together. Through tangerine orchard concerts, piano lessons, and art play in the fields, we weave music, philosophy, and art into everyday life.
It has been 15 years since we left the city to live close to nature in Jeju. We care for two chickens and three ducks like friends, and cook with vegetables grown in our own garden. We often welcome visits from neighbors, so you’ll not only meet o

name: Lisa - An isolated off-grid homestead on an island off Langkawi. Grass, forest, beach, dogs, sheep, ducks.
description:
Hi! We have an off-grid sheep farm on a tropical island in Malaysia. If you are traveling in South East Asia, this can be a working break between Penang and Thailand. 

If you love gardening, animals and woodwork, and are interested to learn about caring for a small flock of sheep, this may be the place for you! We are a retired couple, and our homestead is still under construction. We have a wood workshop, a small house, a sheep house and a vegetable garden...and pasture, forest and beach. We have 4 dogs and 3 cats, all very much involved in our everyday lives, so a visitor needs to be fond of animals to be comfortable. We are running entirely on solar power and filtered stream water, and our sheep are adding much-needed nutrients to our otherwise sandy soil. (We are NOT practicing agriculture, NOT producing food for sale, and are not a teaching example of organic vegetable farming, so please don't expect an internship in these fields. We merely have a small garden for our own veg and fruit, on a big isolated piece of land.)

We're looking for some help from a STRONG, HARDWORKING person, or couple, who can do gardening, woodwork, daily beach garbage clean-up, forest clearing etc. The work day is about 6 hours. Your free time can be spent relaxing, swimming in the sea, reading, exploring the beach or forest. Our place is isolated, and we rarely see people, so this is not really the place to learn about the local community and culture. We  appreciate our volunteers helping to keep the place clean, wash dishes and prepare meals.

The seasons affect the work here 
Mid Dec to mid April : Dry, no rain, very windy, leaves fall. 
                     Main Work : LEAF RAKING , MOWING & COMPOSTING.
Mid April to August : Daily rain, humid, grass grows fast
                    Main Work: GRASS MOWING , RAKING & COMPOSTING and beach garbage.
Sept to mid Dec : Rains all day everyday. Very humid. Floods. Not recommended to come
                   Main Work: Odd jobs, clear fallen
_wwoof_travel: Langkawi Airport ; Jetty Point Ferry, Kuah, Langkawi

name: Valentas - Eco-homestead for reuse, nature, gardening, machinery, and inventions.
description:
I am a hands-on creator and experimenter who owns a rural homestead in Varėna district, Lithuania, surrounded by forests, fields and plenty of space to build and test different ideas. My background is technical and creative. I am interested in mechanics, tools, simple construction and DIY projects, and I also create video content about rural life, creative processes and outdoor activities.

I live in this homestead mainly during the summer season together with my family, and during the rest of the year the place is continuously being developed through various projects and improvements. The homestead is gradually becoming an active rural experience space. It is not a traditional farm, but more like a living workshop and an open-air experimental space.

The core of my project is to create a place where people can take part in real, meaningful activities: building, repairing, improving the environment, testing creative ideas and learning through practice. Some days are physically active, others are calmer and more creative. The goal is to offer an environment where people can disconnect from city life and reconnect with simple, real living in nature.

People who come here are not just guests, they become part of the process. We create, improve, test ideas and share experiences together. In the future I plan to grow this place into a small community space for workshops, camps and experiential stays.

There are typical rural animals on the homestead, as well as a lot of wildlife around. The environment is calm, green and quiet, but the place itself is alive, constantly evolving and focused on activity rather than passive relaxation.

This place is ideal for people who enjoy practical work, creativity, outdoor life and who want to experience something real instead of just being tourists.

Our homestead activities are diverse and very natural to rural life. We have an orchard with fruit trees, where we take care of apple trees, pears, plums and other trees, as well as a ve

name: Angie - Small scale permaculture farm with budding sustainability and agriculture focused community.
description:
If you're seeking a tranquil tropical setting, enjoy working in and around nature and building community, then join us on our 160 acre farm in the mountains above Mayaguez with panoramic views of the foothills, the entire west coast from Cabo Rojo to Rincon and the Carribbean beyond with Desecheo and Isla Mona visible on sunny days. Our farm sits at 1500ft right off a federal mountain highway halfway between Mayaguez and Las Marias, with both great access and extensive privacy and tranquility. We offer a unique view into rural Caribbean life, despite being only a short 30 minute drive to downtown Mayaguez and several local beaches which we like to frequent on the weekends.

We are seeking people with a passion for projects and experiences that span sustainable agriculture, trail making, land stewardship, construction/restoration, and community living. We're working to revitalize what was once the primary Hacienda in the area and grow the space into a thriving co-living and volunteer community, that will in turn support the local community by promoting permaculture and sustainable living and building practices.

We’re a family who left city life behind to bring ourselves closer to the things that sustain us and regain the sense of community and direct involvement in nature we had been missing. This farm has been our dream and labor of love for over three years, and after much effort and major projects, we're finally beginning the journey of opening up to outside volunteers.

WWOOFers will be welcomed as part of our daily life on the farm, participating jointly and projects to advance or tasks to maintain, and sharing in meals and downtime activities at one's own discretion. While we balance farm work with raising two small children and remote jobs, we’re committed to creating an environment that is safe, welcoming, and an unfiltered window into rural tropical life in Puerto Rico.

Agricultural activities on the farm range from gardening, feeding and restocking water
_wwoof_travel: Aguadilla & Mayaguez (BQN/MAZ airports), any west-coast PR transit hub (Cabo Rojo to Aguadilla), or Linea Sultana van line from San Juan to Mayaguez (https://Www.facebook.com/P/Linea-Sultana-Inc-100063709653950/)

name: Matt - Educational Centre, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat, Mixed Farm, Farm
description:
Learn more from the Ghana - West Africa. In our Eco-project, You can also enjoy a natural environment, perform different field activities, cooperate with a project that is just beginning and aims to learn to develop and share experiences with the people of our community and its surroundings; it's very important to us that these projects can influence the population, people from different places can come to know our eco-project, bring their children, they can see animals and see how to develop a sustainable economy.

The Project(s):

Eco Farming, Eco building/constructions, Soccer project, School Aid - Teaching/Learning Projects, Language classes, Aged Care, Mobile Health Care, Clean Water Project, Youth Empowerment, Music project (Local and International), Creative Arts/Culture Project, Social learning ,Reading club project and eco tourism project.

Join us today for an amazing experience!

Organic methods: Permaculture/ Regenerative  principles, ethics and design system

Certifications: Permacultutre

Current projects: ECO FARMING
Earth works - Digging, Weeding (clearing), Site development/designing, Eco-buildings (natural sustainable buildings) for base camps and Eco-stay, preparing the soil, planting, organic production of vegetables: mulching and helping in maintaining and harvesting / collection, helping to create new seed and nursery and then transplanting to the field once this formed the plant, also with feeding the farm animals, Etc

Organic regenerative farming and Tree Planting

Schools and Learning,

Soccer Club (Soccer)
Ecopreneurship Project
Water project, Language 
Classes and Reading Club
Women and Youths' Empowerment
Community mobile Health care
Supporting the Less fortunate children and households
Music projects (Singing - Drumming - Dancing), 
Missionary projects
Community Environmental Initiatives

Internet access available

name: Sandy (Sandra) - Family operated horse ranch focusing on breeding, raising, training and mustang rescue 🏇
description:
Our ranch has been a family operated horse ranch for 30 plus years.  We are dedicated to breeding high quality horses with a focus raising, training, riding lessons and now also horse camps, birthday parties and weddings.  We also have a mustang rescue in the works.  We use intensive grazing and biological weed control through rotation of sheep, goats and chickens; so herding animals is part of our daily chores.  Most of our work is done outside or in barns and varies daily, the constant is feeding and watering animals, cleaning and maintenance and checking fences and repairs as needed.

We are  located in the Ozark foothills in Romance, Arkansas- about 45 mins. N. of Little Rock .  With 127 Acres of pastures, 6 ponds, and 3 creeks we provide our animals with natural free range and we use  rotation of animals and pastures.  We want to share our knowledge of animal husbandry, horse training as well as goat milking and sometimes yogurt and cheese making. We provide a welcoming environment and expect you to be open minded, dependable, handy and on time.
Requirements:
Positive attitude, interest in physical and mental labor, enjoyment of the outdoors, willingness to learn and participate in all aspects of ranch life and food prep and cleanup. You must be in good physical condition (you have to be able to lift min. 40lb) - we walk several miles daily.  No experience needed just willing to learn. We are open year round.  

Commitment:
Full time for three months preferred to be intern/apprentice; however we have had wwoofers short term also that have been amazing, so if you are a quick learner and eager and especially if you have some experience this is also acceptable.
Duties include:
Feeding and watering daily, bottlefeeding if needed, collecting eggs, milking goats - if able, learning and  working with horses and possibly riding, help with lessons and camp, and trailrides- you can learn alot; repair, move and build fences, garden work in spring and fall, winter haying a

name: Desmond - Family home and garden - rural
description: We are currently wildfire free and smoke free in our area. Unique house in a spectacular location in the wilderness. One acre cleared, 66 acres forested, property bounded by creek on S side, river/cold lake on W. Salmon, bears, moose. We need help  putting a new roof and skylights on the greenhouse, some construction upgrades.  Art studio. Beekeepers and organic subsistence farmers live close by. In the middle of First Nations culture. We are both retired teachers, non drinkers. We sponsor a local Young Naturalist Club, are members of BC Nature. We do citizen science projects like salmon enhancement, amphibian and bat monitoring. Dawn is an accomplished artist in love with trees; she will give free art lessons to woofers. She has a large, beautiful flower garden. We live on the edge of Lava Beds Provincial Park. We love animals; sometimes act as local animal rescue; we currently have 5 dogs, 4 cats, 2 budgies, and goldfish. The dogs and cats are all penned or house animals;  we do not let them loose to kill birds or chase other animals; they need to be protected from the wildlife! We can accommodate ovo or lacto vegetarians. We eat little red meat; most of our protein is chicken, eggs, or wild fish. We are growing  lettuce, parsley, lots of tomatoes, potatoes, carrots etc. for our own use. We have done a little experimental forestry; in 1995, 17 acres of our property were logged due to several circumstances including mountain pine beetle. We replanted 3000 seedlings in 1996. We heat the Art studio with wood in winter. For recreation, we hike, try to swim once a week in Terrace in the colder months, 2 or 3 X per week in Dragon Lake in summer. We have cross country skis to use when suitable snow in winter. I might get around to salmon and other fishing now that I am retired. We like cultural activities like Art shows, music and drama concerts in Terrace. We are about 80 km from the sea coast by road.  We enjoy reading, poetry and writing. We have satellite wifi and TV

name: Kay - Help us building a Tiny House as heart for a community place
description:
Our names are Sybille and Kay and we live on a 1 hectare farm in the south of Estonia. It is surrounded by beautiful nature and lots of peace and quiet. We have a solar system on our property that supplies us with electricity in the summer months. We have our own well that supplies us with water and since last year we have been building a permaculture garden. Our goal is to be able to provide ourselves with vegetables and fruit in the long term. At the same time, we offer people who love nature a place to camp. There is an outdoor kitchen and outdoor showers, as well as a composting toilet. This year our garden project will be supported with a greenhouse, which we want to build partly from old windows. We want to live our life in this place in harmony with nature and only use the resources that we really need. In the long term, an old farmhouse is to be restored to create a meeting place for people who are interested in living together in love and peace and in truly being in relationship with one another and with nature.

Our daily activities consist of gardening and the seasonal work that occurs there, such as planting young plants, watering, keeping wild plants in check and harvesting. The construction of a Benjeshäcke to protect some areas of the beds from the wind can also be integrated into the daily work.
We also look after the entire area, which has some wooded areas that have to be cleared regularly. We prepare firewood from fallen trees.
A sunny day in July begins with watering the vegetable garden. Then we take a tour of the garden and see what can be harvested and do some bed maintenance. We dedicate the rest of the day to a specific project, such as building a greenhouse, putting up a Benjeshecke or repairing the fence.

The WWOOfers are housed in a wooden tent. There is a mattress, bedding, electricity, heating and internet.
We provide the food and prepare one hot meal a day together.
We ourselves live in a 9 m² tiny house.
We use the kitchen, showers a

name: Lorenzo - Fattoria mista
description:
Sono un ragazzo di 30 anni che, credendo sempre in un’agricoltura rispettosa dell’ambiente e del consumatore, ha voluto dar corso a questa convinzione, fondando l'azienda. L'azienda è situata nella sub-regione della Barbagia, tra i comuni della valle del Cedrino. Ha indirizzo cerealicolo. Dispone di circa 5 ha, in cui vengono coltivati grani antichi (in particolare grano monococco) e leguminose da granella. Il grano viene coltivato senza utilizzo di prodotti chimici, per cui i momenti critici sono la semina (novembre-dicembre), la levata per il controllo delle infestanti (fine febbraio-aprile), la raccolta. L’azienda comprende anche le fasi successive, in particolare la pulizia del grano (con macchinari antichi), la macinazione a pietra, il confezionamento, la vendita di farine, pane, pasta. Grande risalto ha la parte di divulgazione, poiché, con la mia azienda, organizzo anche eventi mirati a promuovere, oltre al mio prodotto, anche il rispetto dell’ambiente. I periodi maggiormente critici sono ottobre, novembre, prima metà di dicembre (mercatini e semine), da metà marzo a metà aprile (diserbo manuale), da metà giugno a tutto agosto (pulizia del grano). Non sono una persona tassativa, cerco di rendere piacevoli anche i compiti in cui è richiesta maggiore concentrazione, tuttavia perché la convivenza sia fruttuosa credo sia importante la voglia di imparare. L'azienda si trova a pochi chilometri dal mare, in uno dei posti più incantevoli della Sardegna, un vero museo a cielo aperto. Per i WWOOFers è previsto l'alloggio in una stanza dedicata all'interno della casa, che si trova a 1 chilometro dall'azienda. L'organizzazione della giornata prevede il rientro a casa per cena, mentre il pranzo (e talvolta la colazione) vengono effettuati nel luogo di lavoro.

Metodi organici: Sovescio, rotazione, utilizzo della terra di diatomee per la prevenzione dei parassiti, minima lavorazione

Progetti attuali: Fare dei parcelloni per la riproduzione di antiche varietà di grano, ren

name: Javiera - Farm
description:
Granja agroecológica Liukenko.
Somos una granja de aprox. 60 has. ubicada a orillas del rio Claro, en los hermosos paisajes de la precordillera del Maule. Hace 20 años nos dedicamos al cuidado y regeneración de este territorio y el año 2019 nos incorporamos al área declarada Santuario de la Naturaleza Los Maitenes, buscando asegurar la conservación de sus valiosos ecosistemas y promover un manejo regenerativo de suelos y biodiversidad.
Hoy habitamos aquí 4 familias que buscamos acercarnos a la soberanía alimentaria, vivir en armonía y respeto con la naturaleza y sus ciclos.
Dentro de las actividades que se realizan en la granja, se encuentra la producción de cerveza artesanal (@labicicletacerveceria), pan (@casaquillaymaule)  quesos y derivado de la leche de nuestras vacas,  apicultura,  huerto, aves de corral y propagación de arboles y flora nativa (@viveroaliwenmaule)
Estamos trabajando experimentalmente con diversos sistemas que promueven la regeneración como agricultura sintrópica, ganadería regenerativa, bioconstrucción, reutilización de aguas residuales, restauración hídrica  y restauración de bosque nativo.
Buscamos personas que disfruten y amen la naturaleza, que colaboren con las distintas tareas que desarrollamos según la estación.
Nos gusta cocinar, compartir y disfrutar de la vida del campo.
Para las acomodaciones de los voluntarios contamos con una cocina-comedor comunitario, y una habitación con 4 cómodas camas. Proveemos tres comidas diarias, y el horario es de lunes a sabado de 9:00 a 13:00 horas, para tener tiempo para disfrutar del lugar, hacer camintas, ir a nadar en el río y descansar. Les esperamos.
_wwoof_travel: There is one bus from Cumpeo to Maitenes, departure from mondey to friday at 17:00. Contact with us for more information.

name: NATURALEZA - Native plant nursery and community gardening in Patagonia
description:
We are a small team of passionate people living in the southernmost region of Chilean Patagonia, in the remote village of Puerto Guadal. Our project, run by a non-profit organization, focuses on the regeneration of native ecosystems through community gardens, native plant nurseries, and educational landscapes. Most of us work in environmental education, landscaping, and native species propagation. We live surrounded by mountains, forests, and on the shore of one of the most beautiful lakes in Patagonia. We share the land with an organic farm, a few children, a friendly dog, and many wild birds.

Our daily activities include seed collection, growing native plants, preparing soil, planting in public spaces, and designing ecological gardens. Depending on the season, you might help with nursery tasks, garden maintenance, or even co-hosting workshops for children and local communities. We love to share knowledge about Patagonian flora, ecological restoration, and hands-on techniques for creating resilient landscapes. A typical day might involve morning tasks in the nursery, a shared lunch, and an afternoon spent planting or walking through the steppe to learn and teach about wild species.

WWOOFers stay in a small cabin with basic amenities during autumn/winter, or you can camp during the warmer months. We usually have a cook who prepares meals on workdays, and we eat together with the team from the organic farm we share the land with. Vegetarian options are always available, and our meals are simple but nourishing, using local and organic ingredients.

In your free time, we encourage you to explore nearby national parks, wild rivers, ancient forests, and connect with our vibrant local community. If you're curious about native ecosystems, outdoor learning, and creative environmental action, you'll feel right at home.

name: Mariae - Organic sustainable farm OR Theatre/Art  Wwoofing residency opportunity
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):

We are a self-sufficient, mostly off-grid, sustainable organic family farmstead located in the forest away from the main roads in the Kujawsko-Pomorskie province in central-northern Poland. We have a large organic vegetable garden as well as a fruit orchard, fish ponds, chickens, ducks, geese, and a functioning stable with 7 horses and a wee Shetland pony with lots of character! There are 3 dogs and 2 cats. As for the humans, there are 2  grown siblings, a father, and a 13-yr old boy part-time.

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
No-dig gardening as well as traditional gardening- weeding, raking, hoeing, watering, all the fun details of connecting to the land and the reward of watching food magically grow!

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals:
On the farm, we can provide  organic food from the garden and fresh eggs from chickens, supplemented by some store-bought food. We can eat together and/or provide you with food you can prepare yourself. Accommodation in a self-sufficient apartment with separate entrance, mini-kitchen, and own bathroom. This may be shared depending on how many wwoofers are helping.

4. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?

On the farm, you could help with various activities, for example, from 9am to 4pm with an hour break for lunch. In the summer, best to start early as it gets very hot. So it would depend on the weather and season partly. 

Example of a day:
9-9:30am - let out, feed and water chickens and ducks, geese
9:30-11 - weeding or watering vegetable garden
11-11:20 - coffee break
11:20-1pm - weeding and pruning in orchard
1-2pm - lunch
2-4pm - helping arrange firewood

There is also ANOTHER option for small groups of 3-4 Theatre artists, 
a Theatre Wwoofing Residency Opp
_wwoof_travel: Brodnica train/Bus station. Gdansk or Warsaw airport (3/3.5 hours away)

name: LIOR - Food forest in the high desert
description:
Hi! We are Lior and Jenny, a couple living with our two young children and a dog on a 1acre piece of land about 20 minutes from Lake Tahoe, Nevada. Lior is an environmental engineer working for the EPA of Nevada, and we're both passionate about ecological restoration and sustainability.
Our core project is to restore the local ecosystem by creating a small, sustainable food forest and replanting trees in this high desert area,  a place that was once a forest before being clear-cut during the gold rush era. We’re still in the early stages, so we’re looking for independent, kind, and respectful travelers or backpackers to help us with gardening, planting, light building.
Guests will stay in a cozy tiny house or, if preferred, a tent. Three basic meals will be provided daily. We love welcoming people from all walks of life, as long as they’re comfortable around kids and respect our home and values.

Our farming activities focus on regenerating the land and creating a sustainable, resilient food forest in a high desert environment. Typical tasks include planting and caring for young trees, mulching, composting, building raised beds, chopping firewood, light construction, painting and general outdoor maintenance.
 Indoors, help might involve organizing, tidying up while the kids are at school, or assisting with community-related projects.
We’d love to share our knowledge about:
Desert reforestation techniques

Principles of permaculture and sustainable land management
Practical DIY skills (tiny house building, irrigation systems, etc.)
Living off-grid or semi-off-grid with a low environmental footprint
An average day might look like this:

Morning: Wake up, enjoy your breakfast, and begin with about 3 hours of outdoor work (mostly by yearself), maybe tending to the garden, planting trees, or helping with a current building project.
Midday: Free time to rest, hike, explore the area, or just relax in nature.
Evening: Occasionally, we may share a family-style dinner if we’r
_wwoof_travel: Reno airport- Carson City bus station

name: Arjan - Ecovillage under construction
description:
We are 2 people, father and son,  living in a beautiful existing Portuguese village north of the Serra Estrela  among the original inhabitants. So you will most of the time only working with me, Arjan, together. 
We only have space for 2 wwoofers at the moment. 
For health reasons, we can't have people in our home who.are vaccinated.. I know this sounds strange and may be offensive. However, we must consider our own health first.

In this village there is a biodynamic farm, with cows, from our neighbors with whom we regularly work together and have roughly the same ideals. Sometimes we help with the cows .

My father need some help in the household.   We, you and I,  make food a few days a week and we are working on different things.  For example we work on a ruin to become a house.  We also make our own bio bread.  My father has three houses. All these houses need to be refurbished and/or renovated. 2 of them, including the volunteer house, are livable. 

Land: 
We have 4 pieces of land, 2 of which are close to home. This concerns the parental couple's backyard and a 2000m garden.
To be clear: We are building things up and that takes a lot of time and energy in addition to caring for my father.
So you can't expect an existing eco-village, but I try to stick to the core goals as much as possible and make them visible in and during the work.

Slowly more people, who want to work and live with nature, are coming to live in this in this environment.
They want to work and live with nature or are interested in the idee of Espacio da Vida. www.ecodorpportugal.nl 
Our very broad vision is biodynamic agriculture and art. 
We have interaction models such as on Tamera. We like to build a new family together with new people who are now coming to participate.

If you like you can learn a lot about b.d. agriculture and anthroposophy.
We, I and my neighbors,  offer also:
learn, or do, eurithmy.
And in the field of healing:
yoga.
massage
therapeutic eurithmy

Average program:
Star

name: Carolina - Agrofloresta e alimentação saudável
description:
Ola nós somos um coletivo que trabalha com agricultura orgânica, agrofloresta e vida sustentável. Temos 4 cachorros bem sociáveis com pessoas.

Nossas atividades são prioritariamente na agrofloresta, manejando, podando, montando canteiros, plantando, colhendo e processando.
Fazemos também processados em nossa cozinha, gostamos muito dos alimentos fermentados e germinados. 
Cuidamos de nossa saúde de forma integral e natural. Damos atenção a todos os alimentos consumidos. Os voluntários se alimentarão conosco, as 3 principais refeições, café da manhã, almoço e jantar. Os voluntários ficarão acomodados na área de camping, perto de banheiros confortáveis.

Adoramos sentar em volta da fogueira e conversar. Ficamos bem próximo ao parque arvorar que tem uma lagoa onde se pode banhar, caminhar e sentar para observar o pôr do sol. A praia mais próxima é a Prainha, a 10km.

name: Darren - Homestead, Farm
description:
Welcome to Tahsis Farm, a 187-acre property with 1.5 km of oceanfront, pebble beaches, waterfalls, all surrounded by lakes, rivers, caves, mountains, and many trails to explore. The property was logged 16 years ago and is in a state of healthy regrowth. Humpback whales (also orcas and grey whales) are here during the summer and fall. They can be seen swimming just meters from shore due to the steepness of the shoreline. Kayaks and SUPs are available to use; past volunteers have had incredible whale watching experiences. 

The Farm is very much in its infancy. Much of the work is general/physical labor, and revolves around essential items, such as power, water, and grey-water systems. Other tasks include making and maintaining trails and pathways, improving the soil in and around the garden by spreading woodchips, compost, and animal bedding/droppings, taking care of animals, assisting with building new structures, firewood processing, and watering and weeding the garden. More advanced jobs are available if you have the training and/or you are willing to learn.

I aim to have 2-5 volunteers at a time. Accommodation includes cabins and RV trailers. I provide food and accommodation in exchange for 5-6 hours of work per day, 5 days a week (25-30 hours a week). I try to give everyone their own project to work on, and I expect people to be able to research, plan, and complete their project (or milestone) before they leave.  There will also be some independent tasks to complete (cleaning, assisting myself or others, organising, etc). There is no public transport between Campbell River and Tahsis, and so I offer rides between Campbell River and Tahsis approximately every 2 to 4 weeks, depending on my schedule. 

*** Please note that I have a profile on other platforms and so the availability shown on the calendar is not accurate. 

A volunteer made a video from his summer 2023 experiences in Tahsis. You can watch it here:  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PmgoyFm8a9U

Organi

name: Ra.Ha - Alternative co-operative community, Agritourism Destination, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures
description:
The project concerns  to establish a community towards sustainable ways.  We construct an ecolodge for the hosting. We plant bio-organic food  for us and the travellers.  We began the project in 2017 by planting some trees, in 2018 we began to build a house with raw soil and woods. 

This year we are open for receiving our firsts WwooFers. We have to continue with the ecolodge designing and achievement. Planting, gardening are main activities. All around our domain is agricultor or farmer. The concept of permaculture and bio-organic lifestyle is my own goal to introduce to people for creating and managing one cooperative. we are in a wealth part of earth for the project but we are still in a political contradictory national situation.

Le projet vise à établir une communauté vers des voies durables.  Nous construisons un écolodge pour l'hébergement. Nous plantons de la nourriture bio-organique pour nous et les voyageurs.  Nous avons commencé le projet en 2017 en plantant des arbres, en 2018 nous avons commencé à construire une maison avec de la terre crue et des bois. 

Cette année, nous sommes ouverts pour recevoir nos premiers WwooFers. Nous devons poursuivre la conception et la réalisation de l'écolodge. La plantation, le jardinage sont les activités principales. Tout autour de notre domaine, il y a des agriculteurs ou des fermiers. Le concept de permaculture et de mode de vie bio-organique est mon propre objectif à présenter aux gens pour la création et la gestion d'une coopérative. Nous sommes dans une partie riche de la terre pour le projet mais nous sommes toujours dans une situation politique nationale contradictoire.

Das Projekt zielt darauf ab, eine Gemeinschaft zu nachhaltigen Wegen zu führen.  Wir bauen eine Ökolodge für das Hosting. Wir pflanzen bio-organische Lebensmittel für uns und die Reisenden.  Wir begannen das Projekt 2017 mit der Pflanzung einiger Bäume, 2018 begannen wir mit dem Bau eines Hauses mit Roherde und Wald. 

In diesem Jahr sind wir

name: Søren - Organic farm on a smal iland North off Tromsø
description:
Cultivation and collection

The small property has berry and vegetable cultivation as well as a lot of collection in nature Of plants, berries, mushrooms, seaweed, shells and fish. 
🌻
It is not a commercial operation but an ecological supplement for a disability insurance soon to be old-age insurance male sole resident on an island in the ocean off Tromsø in Norway.

The property  2000m² consists of a 65m² 1 1/2 storey wooden house from 1982 with a basement on the rock.

Family:

name: Christina - Regenerative farm with hemp, vegetables and free range chicken on a small island
description:
WELLCOME TO PEACEFUL AND BEAUTYFUL FEMØ! 

Note: For a longer stay, please write and ask.

Our regenerative hempfarm (CBD) is located on a beautiful small Island north of Lolland. There is only 120 people living here but many tourists in the summertime. To get here you take a small ferry and already on the ride you will feel the calmness. 

ABOUT US
We are a rainbow family in our mid-forties, Christina and Mette – living with our 13-year-old son Otto and our cocker spaniel, Elli. Christina works full-time on the farm, and Mette also works in Copenhagen.

Both our farm and the island are safe, welcoming places for queers and for women travelling alone. Femø has a long tradition of openness, care, and community, and many people come here to feel at ease, grounded, and respected — just as they are. Everyone is welcome here!

ABOUT YOU
Your age doesn’t matter to us. What matters is that you’re a capable, self-reliant person who feels comfortable taking care of yourself in everyday life.

You’ll be staying outdoors in your own camper at our small nature-based campsite, so you should genuinely enjoy being outside and living close to nature.You should also be happy to do physical outdoor work and be comfortable working in all kinds of weather. Life here follows the seasons, and being flexible and positive about that is important.

AVAILABILITY & PRIMARY TASKS
May 2 – June 11
•	Preparing hemp field (removing brambles, spreading compost, broadfork)
•	Seedlings & greenhouse care
•	Transplanting hemp and vegetables

August 10 – August 20
•	Hemp field and vegetable garden maintenance
•	Potato harvest
•	Greenhouse care

September 6 – September 17
•	Hemp field and vegetable garden maintenance 
•	Removing brambles
•	Greenhouse care

October 4 – October 22 (Looking for 6 WWOOFers)
•	Hemp harvest & trimming
•	Long hours of seated hand-trimming
•	No experience required

ABOUT THE FARM
We started our small regenerative farm in 2022 with industrial hemp, chickens, and a small vegetable

name: Mike - Self sufficiency farm in a resort
description:
We are a nature-based resort located on Phu Quoc Island, Vietnam, surrounded by tropical gardens, native plants, and a peaceful countryside environment. Sustainability and environmental education are at the heart of our project. We believe that reconnecting people with nature is essential for creating a more sustainable future, and we are passionate about sharing this philosophy with both our guests and the local community.

Our garden is an ongoing learning space where we grow herbs, vegetables, fruits, and flowers using natural and sustainable methods. We aim to reduce waste, encourage biodiversity, compost organic materials, and make responsible use of local resources. Sustainable agriculture is important to us because it helps protect the environment, supports healthy food production, and teaches future generations to value and care for nature.

WWOOFers can learn and participate in a variety of gardening activities depending on the season, including planting, watering, mulching, composting, seed propagation, soil improvement, harvesting, and general garden maintenance. We also welcome WWOOFers who are interested in environmental education and nature-based learning.

An important part of our project is helping children develop a connection with the natural world. WWOOFers may have the opportunity to assist with simple nature activities, gardening workshops, and outdoor learning experiences for local children and young learners. We especially welcome people who enjoy sharing knowledge about plants, sustainability, ecology, and caring for the environment.

Daily life here is relaxed and community-oriented. Alongside gardening and nature education activities, WWOOFers can participate in cultural exchange, learn about Vietnamese island life, and enjoy the natural beauty of Phu Quoc. Accommodation and meals are provided, and we strive to create a welcoming environment where people can learn, contribute, and grow together while fostering a deeper connection with natur

name: Sabine - Soul Sanctuary -  A place to nourish spirit, soul and body.
description:
Welcome to Samothraki, a very unique island in the Aegean Sea. It has been the seat of one of the main greek mysteryschools in ancient times, and many people are drawn to this power place for their own version of initiation.

We are originally from New Zealand (Rex) and Austria (Sabine) and have been travelling the globe for the last 10 years. When we first came to Samothraki a series of synchronicities happened, we fell in love with this island, bought a house and while we still travel a lot for work, Samothraki has become our base.

The name of our house is Chrysostomos, which means „golden word“ - and we treat it as a sanctuary for creativity and soul emergence. It is here to give nourishment for spirit, soul and body. 
The property is situated directly on the beach, which allows for deep communion with the sea and its wisdom. You will enjoy having a swim in the morning or sitting and musing on the shore in the evening to watch the sun set on the horizon.
Pomegranate- and Olivetrees and some beautiful plane trees give shelter in the property. And we enjoy to grow flowers and a vegetable garden.
Last but not least we want to mention Munzi, our cat. He is the true master of the house and he loves to be talked or sung to.

People who have come to our place have described it as magical. There is a stone mandala for meditation if that interests you, a nice platform to dance if you like and then there are all the plant beings that we look after. It is an honor for us to be custodians of this land and we hope you will enjoy its beauty as much as we do.

Usually we start the day with a swim in the sea - (which is directly in front of the house!) That means you walk only 20 meters through our garden and there you are: on our beautiful beach. Afterwards we enjoy good breakfasts to get us ready for the activities of the day. 
Rex loves the vegetable garden and Sabine the flowers around the property. So we attend to these areas as well as planting new trees. We prepare lunch
_wwoof_travel: Kamariotissa on Samothraki island (port for the ferry coming from Alexandroupolis)

name: Sophie - Jardin vivrier autour de la maison dans un village amérindiens
description:
Bonjour, je m'appelle Sophie , je suis née en France métropolitaine et je vis dans un village amérindien wayana du nom de Taluen, sur la commune de Maripa-Soula en Guyane. J'ai un grand jardin vivrier avec légumes, plantes aromatiques, plantes médicinales et plantes à parfums. Je vis avec un amérindien wayana et un chat. Notre logement est un carbet (cabane en bois, recouverte de feuilles de palmier). La partie réservée aux woofeurs est au rdc et elle est ouverte. L'hébergement se fait uniquement en hamac.

Les activités quotidiennes consistent a l'entretien de la parcelle: désherbage, amendements, tailles  des arbres forestiers qui la borde. Il y a aussi la collecte, et la transformation : fabrication de jus, séchage des fruits, confitures etc....
Il ne faut pas oublier les activités traditionnelles comme la fabrication de jus de palmier issus de cueillette en forêt ou de galette de cassave ( manioc) .  La pêche, la chasse et le dépeçage du gibier ainsi que son boucanage font aussi partie des activités.

Je prépare le repas seule (mais une aide est toujours la bienvenue) avec les produits issus de notre parcelle, de la ceuillette sauvage, de la pêche et la chasse a l'exception des céréales qui sont achetés  a l'extérieur.

Mes loisirs sont la natation et le kayak que je pratique sur le fleuve Maroni. Je pratique aussi quotidiennement le yoga. Je marche beaucoup par nécessité pour mes déplacements ( 5 a 10km par jour). Il y a possibilité de faire des petites randonnées aux alentours mais je n'en fait plus car j'ai un problème de ménisque.  Il est  possible jouer au volley et au football au village, il y a des entraînements tout les soirs a 17h quelle que soit la météo. A 19h à la nuit tout s'arrête, les gens se couchent tôt et il reste la lecture.
_wwoof_travel: L'aérodrome le plus proche se trouve a 2h de pirogue

name: PAUL ODIWUOR - Family home and garden - rural, Educational Centre, Fruit tree nursery, Family farm, Community farm
description:
🌿 Join Our Mission to Regenerate Lives Through Permaculture in Rural Kenya
By Paul Odiwuor Ogola | Permaculture Teacher & Designer
Founder, PermoAfrica Training Centre | Sector39 Certified | Organic Methods, Wales, UK

📍 About Us: A Vision Rooted in Soil and Hope
I am Paul Odiwuor Ogola, a passionate permaculture teacher and designer from Kawiya village, a remote community nestled in the northern part of Korayo Sub-location, Kochia Central, Rangwe District, Homa Bay County – Kenya. Our village, located within the Lake Victoria ecosystem, spans 10 square kilometers and is home to approximately 1,800 people. It is a humble yet vibrant community with one public school, three private schools, and 15 churches.

Our people rely primarily on subsistence farming, carpentry, blacksmithing, tailoring, and small-scale businesses. However, we face serious challenges – climate change, environmental degradation, limited resources, and isolation from key development networks.

That’s where permaculture steps in – not only as a solution but as a lifestyle that re-establishes our connection with nature and each other.

🌱 Our Mission
Our mission is to bring permaculture knowledge and regenerative farming technologies to our rural communities – to empower young and adult people to lift themselves out of extreme poverty through sustainable farming, community resilience, and environmental stewardship.

We envision a future where anyone willing to work and learn can provide for themselves and their families through practical skills, innovation, and shared values. Our focus is on open-source, decentralized permaculture education, rooted in the ethics of Earth Care, People Care, and Fair Share.

🛠️ Our Core Programs & Current Projects
We are actively developing and upgrading the following programs:

🌾 Permaculture & Organic Farming Trainings

🌿 School Children Permaculture Education

🏡 Home Gardening & Horticultural Farming

🌱 General Farming & Local Food Systems

🌞 Greenhouse Demonstratio

name: MaryKay - Christian Camp for Children off grid. Chickens, dogs, and cats. Off grid.mountainous wilderness.
description:
Hello, my name is MaryKay. I am 60 year old widow. I have been building a Christian camp for children. 
I have forty acres .  This is an old homestead. There is a lot of work that needs to be done before it is a fully functioning camp. Sometimes it is overwhelming in the big scheme of things but one thing at a time works well. The camp is off the grid so electricity comes from solar panels and or a generator .
Located in the wilderness there are mountain lions, bear, javelinas ,deer , elk , wild turkey , cottontail , jack rabbits and coatmundi. There are rattlesnakes, bull snakes and scorpions.
There are 13 laying hens, 5 dogs and four cats.  My current project and priority is building a new chicken coop and repairing the fences  eventually building structures. IE (outhouse, showers, garage, 
My typical day begins with reading devotional and the Bible. I wake up around 6:30 AM   I feed the chickens , dogs and cats. Every other Thursday is usually trash day and to town for supplies. On these days we will ear one meal in town. Saturday cleaning chicken coop in the morning. I work 4-5 hours a day except Sunday I watch church and relax.
 Every day is different. I work on various projects throughout the camp. Building cabins, outdoor showers ,weeding , cutting trees and firewood, sorting and organizing contents in the outbuildings. Working with the tractor.
 I am learning how to work on small engines like generators, weed eaters, rototillers and chainsaws.
I usually have three meals a day. I cook home style rarely from a box.  I am willing to accommodate if there is special food requirements.  
I have a spare bedroom with a set of bunk beds and a queen bed, a full size bed off the laundry room and a queen size bed in the enclosed porch. In the house. 
There is a cabin that has a queen size bed, a cabin that has a set of bunk beds and full size bed. The cabins have an outhouse nearby. The main house has a guest shower and toilet. There is WiFi,  satellite , television als
_wwoof_travel: Duncan Az bus station/  Phoenix Az 41/2 -51/2 hours drive away

name: Mario - Family ranch with forest conservation and sustainable grazing in the amazing Sierra Gorda
description:
Hi there! My name is Mario and I live on our family property with my 2 children (who I home-school) and my full-time helper, Pedro. Most of the property is dedicated to forest conservation but we also have grazing areas for the goats, cattle and horses. We have several garden spaces where we grow our own fruits and vegetables with organic and biodynamic practices. We have 3 outside dogs as well as an outside cat.

Our routine usually starts with milking the goats and feeding the animals, then the goats get taken out to graze and the horses turned out to pasture. We use the afternoons to make cheese and take care of the veggie gardens as well as general maintanence or projects. We are happy to teach people new skills but we do ask that only motivated people with good attitudes apply...

Accomodation is a self-contained apartment close to the main house and outbuildings. Some cooked meals will be shared, otherwise fresh fruits and veggies as well as home-grown meat will be provided.

Hiking trails through a beautiful forest, bird-watching, swimming in our pond, horse-back riding is an option for experienced riders, charro lessons once a week, exploring the nearby Pueblos Magicos, reading about history.

Senderismo por el bosque nativo, observacion de aves, nadar en la represa, montar a caballo (personas con conicimientos previos), clases de charreria, visitar los pueblos magicos cercanos, lectura sobre la historia.

name: Maxime - Permaculture en forêt tropicale
description:
Technicien agronome spécialisé en cultures tropicales. J'ai créé ce lieu pour en faire un espace d'échange et de formation

Nous avons construit notre maison avec les arbres qui se trouvaient là, ainsi qu'un bâtiment d'accueil, ayant 2 chambres. Il y a possibilité de poser des hamacs un peu partout également. 

Nos projets actuels sont: 
- la multiplication de nos fleurs (tropicales de sous bois)
- la mise en place de buttes
- l'entretient, la taille et la transformation en BRF pour paillage.
-  de nombreux autres projets autour de la diversification,  de l'irrigation, de la transformation et de l'autonomie alimentaire.
 Il reste du défrichage à faire, et de nombreuses plantations, autour des courbes de niveaux ( utilisation d'un niveau égyptien)
Il fait très chaud en Guyane, le travail peu être très physique, une bonne condition physique est conseillée car même un travail simple peu être difficile par 40° et 90% d'humidité :D

Nos passions sont, en dehors de la nature qui nous entoure, la musique, la danse et les arts en général. Nous sommes curieux de nature.

Nous sommes les parents d'un petit de 3 ans qui occupe bien notre temps et qui nous rend moins disponible qu'avant. Cela est à prendre en compte pour les fêtards qui pensent sortir en boite tous les soirs ;) (de toute façon y'a pas vraiment ça ici ... ). Nous essayons de manger assez tôt et ne faisons pas trop de folies en semaine.
Ma femme est assistante sociale et je suis prof de musique à mi-temps à cotés. Nos journées sont bien remplies et je suis donc absent soit la matinée, soit l'après midi.

Pour vous rendre jusqu'à chez nous, il faut, une fois arrivé à Cayenne, faire 3 heures de route, puis faire deux kilomètres de piste.
Nous sommes installés en lisière de forêt primaire, vous pourrez observer de nombreuses espèces d'oiseaux, des tatous, des agoutis, des téjus, des mygales... qui sont ici chez eux.
Ici vous pouvez faire de la rando, du kayak, de la salsa.... mais il vous faudra faire 20 min de voit

name: Elisabeth - Family home and garden - rural, Hobby Farm, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat, Vineyard
description:
About 100km away from Porto Alegre, we have our “farm“.
We work on reflorestation-projects, rainwater basins, biodynamic symbiotic plantations, sustainable gardening, bee-friendly bee-keeping, bio-construction with clay... and a lot of more 

Our ideia is to work and maybe invent new ways, to offer alternatives to common farming tecnics and to enhance biosymbiotics. 
My name is Elisabeth, I'm a biotecnologist, working on our project supported by my family

We expect interested and flexible Problem-solution-ers to join our everyday challenges. 
It's not necessary to have further experience. Everyone can contribute with ideas.
We offer a private acomodation, possibility to see a lot of native birds and other forest animals like apes, wildcats and deers, and last but not least learn and work together with us, enjoy some free-time playing guitar or do some art and crafts.
Who wants to visit us, needs to be a dog-proofed cat-lover, horse-understanding, goat-entertaining, bee-friendly person.

Just to mention it, we invite you into our family, you need to be ok with our kids and animals. 
We do not accept drugs. We don't tolerate disrespect towards people ou animals.
We reserve our right to end a visitors stay due to inconvenient behavior in any case.


Organic methods: Medical-herb extracts to control insects and other pests. 
Composting
More infos are coming...

Certifications: As part of our work we avoid chemicals.

Current projects: As we are right at the beginning and recently started we need support mainly for "base-camp construction":
General build-up of infrastructure including cabanas, vegetable garden, barn, rain water basins, fences, tree-planting, fruit-plantation...
Send us a msg and we check our availability and the next to dos on the list.

It would be nice to get some help.

Internet access available

name: Michael - Treatment Free Beekeeping
description:
I am one of the leaders of the natural beekeeping movement.  I live in Southeastern Nebraska.  I live in a 5,000 sq ft mansion with my wife, my son, my daughter-in-law, three grandkids, two Golden Doodles, a lot of chickens and a lot of bees.  I practice treatment free beekeeping.  Mostly my goal is to raise a lot of queens that don't require treatments.  I also usually harvest some honey.  I have an 18 acre farm and two out yards where I have bees.  I run around 200 hives in the peak of the season.

We do treatment free beekeeping and want to make treatment free queens available. Intern/Apprentice will receive training in all aspects of queen rearing, queen marketing and treatment free beekeeping, as well as room and board in exchange for work at queen rearing and other farm work. You will learn to set up “swarm box” queen cell starters, queen right cell finishers, queen mating nucs, graft larvae into queen cups, catch, mark and bank queens, ship queens, handle orders, build equipment, repair equipment, do splits (make new colonies), catch swarms, remove bees from houses, and many other aspects of running an apiary. You will have access to an awesome library of bee books and access to my discussion of any aspect of bees or beekeeping that you would like.

The room is in a beautiful solid brick house that was completed in 1895. It is five thousand square feet and has 7 people living in it currently. I need someone from April until September.   You can make yourself at home.  You will be part of the family.  You may have to forage for breakfast.  You are welcome to cook your own (plenty of food in the fridge) or have some cereal or toast etc.  Often my wife will cook breakfast and usually she will cook lunch and supper.  On weekends I usually cook breakfast.  The most often complaint I have had from interns in the past is that the food is too delicious and they eat too much.

I have too many hobbies to list, but playing guitar and singing would be high on the list.

name: Keghah Roger - A practical learning hub for climate-smart agriculture and food security.
description:
The AORW Agricultural Learning Site in Nakivale Refugee Settlement is a community-based demonstration and training space established to support refugee women and host community members with practical agricultural skills. The site promotes climate-smart farming, livestock feed production, agroforestry, kitchen gardening, and sustainable livelihood practices. It serves as a learning hub where women can gain hands-on experience, share knowledge, and adopt farming methods that improve food security, household income, and environmental conservation.

At our AORW agricultural learning site in Nakivale, we practice vegetable gardening, nursery work, compost making, agroforestry, fodder production, poultry keeping, and goat care. We would like to teach WWOOFers about climate-smart agriculture, organic farming, livestock care, and how farming supports refugee women’s livelihoods. A typical day includes watering crops, feeding livestock, weeding, planting, composting, and joining community learning sessions with refugee women.

WWOOFers will be hosted in simple, safe, and clean accommodation within or near the AORW learning site/community. Accommodation will be basic but comfortable, with access to a bed or mattress, mosquito net, bathing space, and toilet facilities. Meals will be shared with the host team and will mainly include local Ugandan foods such as posho, rice, beans, vegetables, matooke, potatoes, eggs, and seasonal fruits. We will do our best to provide three simple meals a day, and WWOOFers are welcome to support in meal preparation and share cultural food experiences.

We enjoy music, cooking, storytelling, gardening, learning from different cultures, and spending time with the community. WWOOFers can also experience local Ugandan food, visit nearby markets, interact with refugee and host community farmers, and explore the natural surroundings of Nakivale. Depending on availability and local guidance, visitors may enjoy walking, community visits, cultural exchan

name: Nour Eddine - Welcome to our small farm near the desert
description:
My name is Nour Eddine, I’m 24 years old and I have a deep love for nature. Growing up surrounded by the desert and the mountains, I developed a strong respect for the environment and for animals, I learned how to understand nature and live in harmony with it.
For a long time, I have been hosting volunteers through different platforms, and since my family has always worked in tourism, I’ve learned everything about welcoming people from all over the world and creating unique, meaningful experiences for them.

My dream is to build a sustainable farm. I’m taking the first steps now, but in the future I hope to become fully self-sufficient, grow my own food, and host animals, living peacefully in nature, managing resources responsibly, and sharing my knowledge with others.

The project is still at its beginning: we are starting to restore our house in Kasbah Oulad Ali and maintain the surrounding garden: simple but essential tasks open to anyone willing to help. Volunteers will contribute both to the renovation of the traditional house that hosts them and to the care of the garden, while learning about desert life and sustainable farming.
Our long-term vision is to build a small eco-farm capable of hosting animals and fruit trees, welcoming travellers and volunteers from all over the world to share knowledge, culture, and a more sustainable way of living in the desert.

Nestled near Tagounite, our home lies within a small rural village where time seems to have paused. The houses, built from sand, wood, and clay bricks, carry the warmth and wisdom of ancient traditions. 
Our, is a rural house in a village next to the desert: we live a nomad lifestyle. In the house there are 3 rooms, a kitchen and a bathroom. Water is a scarce and precious resource, we ask you to use it carefully and keeping in mind that we are in the desert.
For now we cultivate palms, but we want to increase the production including other species and crop production. We need help in managing and improvi

name: Fernando - Somos una granja de turismo rural  con animales  y trabajamos la tierra en forma sustentable.
description:
Hola wwooff es, mi nombre es Fernando , tengo 55 años y tengo una granja que se llama fundo San Fernando, me gustaría una ayuda trabajando la tierra y yo entregando mi cultura y alimentación más hospedaje a cambio, aquí se  trabaja con el turismo rural y aprovechamos la tierra para la agricultura que entregamos a los turistas que nos visitan, en sus desayunos, almuerzo u onces magallanicas,tenemos caballos y actividades de deporte aventura y cultural como un museo etnico , estamos ubicados. 42 km al sur de Punta Arenas, la ciudad más cercana de Magallanes, Patagonia chile.

Las actividades diarias es mantenimiento del recuento, ayudar en las labores de invernaderos , limpiando las hortalizas y cooperar en la vida diaria del campo.

El alojamiento es en una casa donde están los guías, la alimentación es desayuno , almuerzo y once cena al término de la jornada, comenzamos a las 08:30 a 12:30 y 14:00 a 18:00 un día a la semana se descansa.

Me encanta la música y la cocina, caminar y leer, tienes posibilidades de ir a ver los pingüinos rey, las ballenas , senderismo dentro del fundo y río para la pesca.

name: K’dee - Homestead, Woman-operated
description:
GARDENING  IS COMING! --

Looking for WOMEN ONLY at this time.

MUST BE ID VERIFIED please! 

Welcome to Our Homestead!

Hi there! I’m a homesteading wife living a full and rewarding life on our little farm, and I’m looking for a helping hand (or two!) to share in the daily rhythm of life out here. We raise chickens, pigs, a cow, and sheep — so there’s always something to do and plenty of animals to love.

My husband works full-time off the farm, so I manage most of the day-to-day and could use help in all areas — from feeding and tending to the animals, to maintaining our hard-working farm dogs, and even lending a hand when it comes time for butchering. Whether you’re experienced or eager to learn, I’d love to have you join in.

In return for your help, you’ll enjoy homemade meals — breakfast, lunch, dinner, and drinks (yes, we always have coffee on hand!). We also have Wi-Fi available, so you can stay connected while enjoying a peaceful, slower-paced lifestyle. We have two amazing guest room fully furnished with closet space and queen size bed and blankets, pillows and towels. 

I ask that you choose either opening up the farm in the morning or helping with the evening close-up — whichever suits your schedule best.

If you’re ready for good food, fresh air, and a taste of homestead life, we’d love to welcome you!

name: Jim - Hobby Farm, Market Garden, Family farm
description:
Our property is approximately 33 acres with 10 acres of organic vegetables grown for our own consumption as well as for fresh market. We attend local farmers' markets as well as supply local roadside stands and restaurants. We also have a sawmill on site and we do a lot of woodworking. In particular, we make wine and spirits barrels for wineries and distilleries. No livestock. We have one seasonal farm worker from Trinidad and during the summer months we have many visitors from around the globe. We have a quiet, forest and open field rural surrounding but we are only 10 minutes from the large community of Brantford for shopping, supplies and other activities. The Grand River is only a few kilometers away and has excellent rafting, kayaking and other opportunities. Bikes are available. Swimming pool on site. Wonderful neighbours. Lots of varying activities both farming and otherwise.

Organic methods: Plasticulture. Crop rotation. Biological and mechanical pest and weed control.

Certifications: Not certified because of adjacent non-organic farming.

Current projects: 10 acres of organic farming for our own consumption as well as for local markets. Starting in March with greenhouse seeding for transplanting in June.

Sawmilling and coopering as well as woodworking projects.

Solar panel systems design and construction.

Internet access available

name: Juan - Viva la soverania 🤠🥳
description:
My name is Juan Carlos I live in Rancho AMar La Baja sur Mexico, it’s a space in the middle of the desert we’re creating a place of healing, sustainability. The map shows La Paz because is the closest point of reference. I’m actually 38km north from La Paz and 18km to the nearest nearby town (Reforma Agraria) then 17km in dirt road into the pacific. Were on I-overlander as The Rabbit Hole if you wanna check out. 

Uncles and family live a few kilometers away other than that our nearest neighbor is about 20kilometers.2 cats 3 dogs 20 chickens 3 goats a regenerative garden
 
The fundamental practice is to regenerate the soil by creating organic matter. Creating diversity of microorganisms to break down mater for better plant availability. Identifying is important so you can select and intentionally grow. Afert selecting you give preference and use surrounding plants as fertilizers for the one selected. Close observation, the time in the season of diferent bugs insects possible plegs is a constant

Most food comes from here and some from local markets in the city of La Paz. Arrangements can be made to accompany to make this trips for basic needs the visitors should have. Theres a few cabins that I can host visitors to stay in. Include a bed clean sheets pillows. The average day here starts with the wake-up call from the roosters..fire up the fogón to get a tee or coffee, dedicate about an hour of exercise (trampoline,walk,run to the beach,yoga). By then the furry friends are hungry also our other farm animals maybe get some watering done for some plants by then is about time to start seeing about food for the humans. Depends what’s available in the garden but usually we’ve been blessed in a consistent number of egg production so a breakfast based on egg and seasonal vegies, sometimes baked goods and some beans is a typical breakfast. After there’salways something need of attending, maintaining, constricting, planting, de weeding, to entertain our self’s with. At 2:00 w

name: Greg & Teresa - Agritourism, market garden, orchard, farm
description:
Hello and welcome to Cusgarne Organic Farm! We are a fully organic farm in the heart of Cornwall, registered with Demeter. Your stay at Cusgarne would involve a lot of variety, we have 104 acres of growing fields, pasture, orchards, chickens, polytunnels and a farm shop.

Volunteer help has been central and hugely valued at Cusgarne for nearly 30 years. And we have been incredibly lucky to have some wonderful people give their time.

We are a commercially run operation, but on a small scale, so you will get a taste of all the elements it takes to keep an organic farm running. One day might be collecting eggs from the chickens, the next day moving cattle, the next day seeding and weeding and the next making preserves. 

We really want to make your stay at Cusgarne rewarding, inspiring and interesting too, so if there is a specific thing that you are passionate about, skilled in, or just want to experience, just let us know.

The farm is in a very quiet area of Cornwall, with small lanes and green fields. There is a small local pub, footpaths and a bus stop... not much else though! Falmouth and Truro are about 6/7 miles away as are the north and south coasts, which Cornwall is famous for.

We live a quiet and simple life here, eating seasonally and enjoying working with nature. You will meet inspiring and interesting people though, and hopefully leave with some funny and good memories.

Organic methods: Predators in tunnels, diamatacious earth, good rotations, liquid manure plant feed. Dock beetles on pastures All plant grown on farm. 90 different cultivars approx

Certifications: demeter

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: TRAIN/BUS: Truro/ Redruth www.nationalrail.co.uk www.nationalexpress.com www.megabus.com

name: Christian - Regenerative village at the Nile
description:
+++Please apply only if you can stay for at least 3 weeks+++

Welcome to Per Auset, a regenerative village and cultural restoration project located on an ancient island in the Nile. Guided by Mostafa, Jasmeen, and Chris, our mission is to breathe life back into a once-deserted Nubian village — honoring its heritage while cultivating a thriving, sustainable future.

🌱 Our Vision
We are restoring the village, planting 5,000 trees, and creating abundant gardens that will one day nourish a community of 100 people. This is more than a project — it is a living temple of regeneration, where ancient wisdom meets modern sustainability.
We invite volunteers who are skilled, self-aware, and genuinely dedicated to hands-on work, learning, and cultural exchange.

🛠️ Current Volunteer Roles
We are currently welcoming volunteers with skills and passion in:
- Permaculture & Gardening: Creating new food forests, vegetable gardens, and Blue Lotus ponds.
- Natural Building & Finishing & Architects: Artistic wall painting, decoration, and adding creative touches to our homes and lunch area.
- Social Media & Filming: Helping us share our story with the world.
- Yoga teachers: Offering Yoga classes to the community.

You can expect a mix of community work and individual projects, depending on your skills, interests, and the needs of the season. Each morning, after breakfast, we gather to plan the day together.
 We especially welcome women from Egypt, Africa, and the Middle East who feel called to connect with this sacred land, learn, and co-create in a supportive and inspiring environment.

🌞 Life at Per Auset
We live as a family, in a rhythm guided by the sun and the Nile. Our community includes donkeys and cats!
We share simple, healthy, mostly plant-based meals together — often home-cooked with vegetables from our gardens. Food time is sacred here: a moment to connect, laugh, and share reflections of the day.
You’ll sleep in shared rooms with shared bathrooms, or you can camp by the N

name: Vania - Livestock Farm
description:
Nous vivons à Saavedra , un petit village proche de Montero, à 1h30 de Santa Cruz. 
Nous sommes trois associés, ma sœur, son mari et moi-même. 
Nous avons une exploitation de vaches laitières, nous avons 300 bêtes dont environ 100 à la traite matin et soir. La ferme a 140 ha sur lesquels sont cultivés du maïs, du soja, du sorgho, du pâturage. Le lait est vendu à une entreprise qui collecte le lait.
Mon beau frère est français, ma sœur et moi sommes boliviennes et vétérinaires de formation. 
Nous aimons le contact et le partage avec les autres c'est pour cela que nous aimerions recevoir des wwooffers, afin de leur partager nos connaissances, notre culture et d'apprendre des leurs.
Ma sœur habite dans une maison sur l'exploitation, vous aurez votre propre chambre dans la maison, avec une salle de bain partagée. La nourriture est fournie, si vous voulez cuisinez vous-même c'est aussi possible vous aurez accès à la cuisine. 



Vivimos en Saavedra, un pequeño pueblo cerca de Montero, a 1h30 de Santa Cruz. 
Somos tres asociados, mi hermana, su esposo y yo. 
Tenemos una granja de vacas lecheras, tenemos 300 animales, de los cuales unos 100 se ordeñan por la mañana y por la noche. La finca tiene 140 ha en las cuales se cultiva maíz, soya, sorgo, pastos. La leche se vende a una empresa que recolecta la leche.
Mi cuñado es francés, mi hermana y yo somos bolivianos y somos veterinarios. 
Nos gusta el contacto y compartir con los demas, por eso nos gustaria recibir wwoofers, para poder compartir nuestros conocimientos, nuestra cultura y aprender de la de ellos.
Mi hermana vive en una casa en la finca, usted va a tener su propia habitación en la casa, con baño compartido. Se proporciona comida, si desea cocinar usted mismo, también es posible que tenga acceso a la cocina.



We live in Saavedra, a small village near Montero, 1h30 from Santa Cruz. 
We are three associates, my sister, her husband and myself. 
We have a dairy cow farm, we have 300 animals, of w

name: Alice - Petite exploitation fruitière  en AB
description:
Bonjour, 
je suis Alice, âgée de 45 ans bretonne d'origine, je vis sur une commune du littoral sur la cote d'émeraude. Installée en agriculture biologique depuis 5 ans. Mon compagnon, Pierre Emmanuel, me soutient et m'aide régulièrement sur mon activité professionnelle mais travaille à l'extérieur.  J'ai un chien, 2 chevaux et une chèvre.

Suivant la période de l'année, l'activité principale est la gestion des 2 ha de petits fruits, les récoltes se déroulent principalement du 15 juin au 15 septembre, le reste de l'année consiste à l'entretien et les tailles. L'hiver consiste à entretenir les haies bocagères sur les 32 ha de prairies, la taille des arbres et à l'entretien des prairies.

Les repas se feront ensemble, l'élaboration des repas se fera de façon collective. 
L'hébergement  est à 10 km de la ferme, dans la maison de mon compagnon, dans un cadre calme, agréable et verdoyant, loin de la foule de la cote. Les woofeurs peuvent venir en couple car les chambres sont équipées de lit double.

Longtemps passionnée d'équitation, mes passions s'orientent vers des choses plus simples et vers la nature.  J'aspire à une vie tranquille et harmonieuse. Je suis ouverte d'esprit et curieuse, je serais ravie d'apprendre également de mes woofeurs.

name: Mahendra - A food farm with fruit orchards (dragon fruit, moringa, lemon, apple, gooseberry, guava) North India
description:
My name is Mahendra Panwar, and I live in Udaipur. The farm is situated 25 km north of the city. I live here with my family , caretaker and a dog.

Since this is a food (fruits and vegetables) farm, the day involves sowing, planting, weeding and harvesting. Regular maintenance activities play a crucial role in keeping the farm well-maintained.

We offer the option of twin-sharing rooms with attached bathrooms, as well as a dorm with a shared bathroom. Three meals will be prepared on the farm and served.

I love cooking and serving and sharing about the historical monuments close by. Trekking on the Aravali mountains, birding, visiting lakes, temples, and historical forts.

name: Leena - Family home and garden - rural, Agritourism Destination
description:
Our place, situated in the southwestern archipelago of Finland, offer beekeeping, gardening and bed and breakfast accomodation. After renovating the buildings and garden (still going on) and encouraged by friends we started to develop the idea of offering low-priced accomodation to travelers. B & B is only secondary to our main jobs and focuses on summer time when beekeeping also keeps us busy.

Being and living in our property centres around the ideology of self-made and/or nearby produced activities and products. We aim to minimize our environmental burden, safeguard our natural garden and take biological biodiversity into account in everyday life as well as to convey these ideas to our guests. In producing food we try to be as self-sufficient as possible.

All the necessary renovations are carried out respecting traditional techniques without equipping old buildings with "unnesessary" conveniences.

Organic methods: We do not use in any artificial chemicals or pestisides. Instead we use usually horse manure and grass in our vegetable garden. Birds, frogs and small lizards help us with the pests. We circulate all possible materials and use the compost in our garden.

Our bee-keeping is more of a hobby, not professional.

Current projects: Gardening, picking berries, making food, baking. Forestry, repairing furniture, building. Beekeeping and centrifuging the honey in late summer. Also cleaning beekeeping equipments and preparing the garden for the winter.

Internet access available, own room for the woofers as well.
_wwoof_travel: Salo railway station/Paimio highway junction busstop, Helsinki or Turku airport

name: Pedro - Bosque en recuperación, Conexión, Armonía, salud, sensatez, sensibilidad, animales, olivos, frutales
description:
Conexión, Armonía, salud, alegría, sensatez, sensibilidad humana y ecológica, ecología profunda,... Son cosas que: he visto durante los últimos años (en los que he estado viajando y colaborando con proyectos con orientación ecológica y de desarrollo humano y grupal). Tras el anterior periplo, con 40 años de edad, me encuentro: de nuevo en mi tierra natal, ahora en solitario, con deseo de transformarla en algo así como lo que os he contado que viví lejos en años anteriores. Y agradezco compartir estas experiencia con voluntari@s a quienes les motiven estos sentires.

Bosque, Agua, meditación (no se exige, si bien el espacio invita a ello), diversidad, encinar en recuperación, fauna autóctona, pastos (herbáceos, arbustivos, arbóreos) que en temporadas son pastados por bellos caballos, olivar (y elaboramos nuestro propio aceite de oliva), frutales, terrazas-bancales,…

Se propone comenzar todo voluntariado con una semana de prueba, y si todo marcha bien (y si hay disponibilidad por parte de tod@s) se podría prolongar la estancia. 

Se requiere hablar español. 

Salvo que ciertas necesidades inflexibles se deban cubrir en determinados momentos, las horas de voluntariado semanales se pueden distribuir flexiblemente como acuerden el anfitrión y l@s voluntari@s. 

He llegado a comprobar que DIVER-sificando los tipos de actividad, nos cansamos menos y nos DIVER-timos más. Así, la mitad del voluntariado diario se dedica a tareas domésticas: elaboración de comida (se pide que woofer tenga experiencia en cocina de manera que se pueda desenvolver aquí por sí mism@), limpieza, acondicionamiento del alojamiento y su entorno, etc. Y, la otra mitad del voluntariado diario se dedica a tareas del campo: plantar (otoño-invierno), podar (invierno), gestionar hierba y/o cuidado de animales (primavera-verano), regar (verano), preparar cosecha de aceite (verano-otoño), recolección (sobre todo de aceituna en otoño),… mantenimiento, reparaciones, etc.

Las comidas corren de cuenta del anfit
_wwoof_travel: Woofer llega a Jaén, luego wwoofer puede coger autobús hasta la localidad donde se le recoge

name: Keith & Sandra - Mixed Farm and Honey Bees
description:
HI, we are looking for a easy going, responsible, independent person or couple to help on our farm. Volunteer should be able to work independently and do basic chores, check animals, help outside and inside. We have a few cows calving at the start of February and approximately 70 sheep lambing in March. 

 Keith and I run a small mixed farming operation in northeast Saskatchewan close to the Porcupine Forest. We both grew up on farms and can't imagine any other life. 

During February through April most of our work centers around daily chores to take care of the sheep,  cattle, goats, and birds. Once May comes, Keith is busy checking and uncovering his honey bees. He also makes his splits and puts new queens into the hives.  During the summer months, honey bees occupy most of our time.

We also have cows, sheep, goats, horses, chickens, turkeys, and working dogs on our farm. We plant a large garden and have a variety of fruit trees. If you are interested in preserving fruit and vegetables I also like to do that in late summer and fall. 

Although May-October is our key time, we always seem to keep busy. March through May we are busy with lambing and calving. We are close to a lake so we often canoe, kayak, or fish in our spare time during the summer.  We enjoy fishing and being out on the lake. 

In the winter months there is also a lot to do outside. Keith and I welcome anyone interested in learning and becoming part of our family while you are here. There is always something new to learn or experience. 

Keith has completed his second square timber cabin on our other quarter.  It is set up with solar power, an outdoor shower with a water tank and a wood burning stove. Keith  has a sawmill,  so he cuts the trees off our land, saws them, and then uses them to build.

Winters are not as busy as the rest of the year. We take time to relax, watch a few movies and read some books

Organic methods: We do not use sprays or insecticides on our property. We also raise our o
_wwoof_travel: Our farm is actually 42 km northwest of Preeceville.  You go north of Preeceville to Endeavour.  Drive through Endeavour.  Follow two curves: first right, then left.  Drive straight west for approximately 10km.  There is a train station in Endeavour.

name: Cheryl - Three different Crofts across Skye
description:
Looking for woofers May 26 ☺️

Crofting work at various locations on the Isle of Skye with Highland cows, sheep, bees, dogs, land management, small building works- tiny house, sauna project, fencing, tree planting, workshop tidying.
A car is needed as the accommodation is 40mins drive from the croft. 

FOOD
