I make a big lunch every work day and good coffee but otherwise no food is provided - the coop supermarket is a just a mile away. 

WORK
I ask for three full days help (daylight hours only) per week and the rest of the time you can explore or chill at your leisure.

ACCOMMODATION
In your own private room in a shared 6 bedroom house, wifi, heating, hot showers, kitchen, and good view from the living room over the sea and mountains. 

If you're a couple then great - if you are two individuals or a group then no bother as there are 4 spare bedrooms at the moment with two long term students staying who are studying on the local Outdoor Adventure course.  

AVAILABILITY
Preferably stays for at least a fortnight but flexible.
You'll be helping me, Cheryl, 36, a former teacher and now full time crofter.
Strictly no smokers, its a great time to give up here and have a wee detox.
You must have excellent English, unfortunately I'm rubbish at other languages and love a chat so while it's not a deal breaker, its best if you are pretty fluent.

BACKGROUND/CONTEXT

I look after a croft in Broadford where I rent a house (where you'll be based) and also have a croft in Portnalong where I am building a house of my own and live in a static caravan …and have just taken on a big croft in another part of the island with 200 sheep. We will travel between the the places as animals and jobs in each so you’ll get a good experience in lots of different parts of Skye, but based in Broadford which is the second largest settlement in Skye so lots of amenities for a rural location.

JOBS
Jobs include: tree planting, general tidying, shed organising, painting, gardening, fencing, drystane wall

name: Anna Sigurbjorg - Potatofarm with a stunning view
description:
We are Anna and Einar, we run a potato farm in south east Iceland. We have an arrange of animals living on our farm including pigs, goats, sheep, horses and dogs. We also have a few guesthouses. Our farm is surrounded by stunning views of glaciers, mountains and fields.

You can usually find us out in the fields gathering potatoes, this is a full time job. After gathering around 10-15 tons of potatoes (we use machines for this, don't worry), we take them inside to clean and pack them.
WWOOFers would learn about the Icelandic horse and how to care for the animals on the farm. They  can  learn all about the process of growing vegetables.  We produce all of the meat we consume ourselves and some of the vegetables we eat. They'll have a chance to learn about the nature and icelandic culture.
Our day begins with feeding the animals, that takes around 90 minutes. From there we move on to the potato field, we spend about 5-8 hours there each day. 
Two days a week we wash the potatoes, dry and pack them. After that we send them to the grocery stores.

WWOOFers would stay in a small cabin equipped with two bedrooms, toilet and a shower, a small kitchen and a sitting room. 
We provide four meals a day, breakfast, light lunch, afternoon coffee and a full dinner.

We love music, cooking, baking, spending time outside and just enjoy being alive. 
There is a small town close by, only a ten minute drive from our farm. There you'll find a swimming pool, a golf course, restaurants, a coffee house, a library and a bar. 
There are many walking paths around the area and you can also go fishing!

name: Beatrice - Help needed now! A family vegetable garden and an old house under the Baltic midnight sun
description:
Hi! We are a family of 3 people: Anton, Beatrice and our 2 year old son. We are bi-nationals French and Swedish so we will continue this presentation in French since we would be happy to welcome French-speaking volunteers.  

Nous habitons au bord de la mer Baltique du Nord dans le beau village de Siknäs. Nous aspirons à une vie proche de la nature et basée sur des principes écologiques. Nous ne produisons presque aucun déchat, nous avons des panneaux solaires, un chauffage pauvre en énergie, nous voyageons autant que possible sans énergies fossiles etc. Cela veut dire chercher des nouvelles solutions et de la curiosité, par exemple en réparant plutôt qu'acheter du neuf, ou acheter d'occasion. Nous cultivons nos légumes et nos oeufs (et parfois la viande) viennent de nos poules.

Nous parlons le suédois et le francais puisque Béatrice et notre fils sont bi-nationaux. Nos dates pour te recevoir seraient de préférence entre début mai et le 25 juin 2026. 

L'entretien d'un potager signifie, à part les tâches classiques, d'entretenir le composte, rassembler l'eau de pluie, faire de l'engrais. Cette année nous aimerions construire une clôture pour protéger les cultures des poules. La coupe de bois, la tonte du gazon sont des tâches qui reviennent régulièrement. Il faut quotidiennement s'occuper des poules et du coq. Nous aimerions partager ces grands et petits projets avec un.e volontaire. Nous voyons sa présence comme une agréable compagnie et bien sûr comme un précieux coup de main. Étant récemment devenus parents, il est parfois difficile pour nous de réaliser tranquillement une tâche de A à Z :). Notre volontaire nous suivrait aussi volontiers aux activités des villageaois, par exemple le nettoyage du port de plaisance chaque été, très convivial.

Tu seras hébergé.e à l'étage de notre maison. Notre chambre est au RDC. Tu auras en gros l'étage supérieur pour toi. La salle de bain et une grande cuisine sont en bas.
Dans une maison attenante, il y a également une deuxiè

name: Viveta - Organic farm (trees and veggies) and open market on Syros island
description:
Hello, we rent a beautiful place in a little village on Syros island, where we do organic agriculture. The farm is 15.000 sqm and has around 500 trees (oranges, mandarines, and lemons)  and around 5.000 sqm for cultivating seasonal organic vegetables. There is also a big wall full of capers.
We try to make our little plants, with organic seeds that we collect from around the country and from our plantations. Finally, we have an open market every Saturday inside the farm, where we sell our bio fruits and vegetables straight to our clients. Every Wednesday we deliver upon request our products to the town (Hermoupolis).  During the year we organize some little fiestas, bazaars, and music, or theater events.  

We will be pleased to host 1-2 WWOOFers for the summer period, sharing our experience, methods, and culture and having extra help in collecting capers and vegetables. As the farm has a little house with a kitchen and toilet (there is no bedroom in it) we can only offer a tent for sleeping under the trees in the period May - October.

During summer we are on the farm early in the morning and a few hours in the afternoon depending on the heat. Usually, we collect capers and vegetables 7-12 in the morning and then 6-8 in the afternoon (not every afternoon and not every day, depending on the crop and the demand).  We also help the vegetables to grow and stay healthy, by digging or cleaning from the weeds.
We can provide our hosts with vegetables from our garden and all the goods they need for breakfast, meals, and dinner. Hosts are responsible for cooking them. We would love to share our meals and we'll try to do it as often as possible, as we have family and a house not far from the farm. 

I'm sure you can enjoy your stay on our farm. The nearby village is very beautiful and during the summertime, there are concerts and festivals around the island. You can hike inside and outside the village or swim in the sea (10 mins by car, 30 on foot). There is a restaurant wit

name: Didier - Association qui promeut l'agriculture respectueuse de l'homme et l'environnement dans les Caraïbes
description:
Je m'appelle Didier et je suis paysan depuis 16 ans en Guadeloupe. J'habite avec ma compagne Galadriel et nos animaux : chiens, chat, poules, canards, oies, chèvres. 
Je produis des épices et notamment du curcuma que nous transformons en poudre de janvier à juin. Une partie de cette poudre est ensuite embarquée à bord de voiliers pour rejoindre l'Europe. 
La parcelle où je cultive est à quelques kilomètres d'où nous habitons. En revanche nous avons installé nos séchoirs et notre atelier de transformation juste à coté de la maison sur un grand terrain arboré où se trouve également 2 bungalows et des jardins partagés.

La base de l'activité c'est la transformation du curcuma. Notamment son lavage et son séchage. Vous pourrez également participer à toutes les autres étapes si vous le souhaitez, de la replantation à la mouture en poudre. 
Et bien sûr, en fonction de vos envies, tout l'éventail des activités d'une petite ferme : jardinage, nourrir les animaux, bricolage, mécanique, etc

Nous accueillons en général 2 à 4 woofeurs de janvier à juin. Nous avons 2 bungalows pour vous accueillir. L'un très bien équipé (il a même la clim alors que nous n'en avons pas à la maison ;), l'autre plus roots, au fond du jardin dans un endroit très nature. 
L'organisation autour de la cuisine et des repas est toujours à établir ensemble selon vos envies. En général nous prenons le déjeuner ensemble et on vous fournit ce que vous souhaitez pour prendre le petit déj et le diner en autonomie, les bungalows étant équipés d'une cuisine chacun. 
On est plutôt flexitariens mais on est habitués à accueillir tout type de gens (végé, végan, allergiques, etc). On s'adapte :)
On aime bien manger alors on vous fera à manger avec plaisir et on sera très heureux de découvrir vos meilleures recettes !!

La saison est assez rythmée de mon côté et ce n'est pas la période de l'année où j'ai le plus de loisirs. Mais en général, comme j'ai été moniteur de plongée dans une autre vie, il arrive que nous emm
_wwoof_travel: Aéroport Maryse Condé de Pointe à Pitre/Les Abymes

name: Cara Amy - A small off grid farm, foraging business and learning centre nestled in the sub arctic wilderness
description:
*****Availability: May 15 to Oct 30th***

About us:
Established in 2010 Laughing Lichen is a small 12-acre farm located 30 kms by road from the city of Yellowknife, NT. We operate a wildcrafting business, learning Centre and market gardens from our location. The entire farm is run via solar power and located in the boreal forest. Herbs and veggies are grown on site while wild mushrooms, berries and wild herbs are foraged in the surrounding wilderness.  Many of the products harvested on site are processed and dried in a timber frame herb drying barn. There are 3 large dogs, and a parrot on site, as well as a flock of laying hens, some pet sheep and quail, we also help run a dog rescue organization so are often fostering rescue dogs in addition to our own. In addition to foraging and growing local food, we are also a learning centre and offer training and workshops to others about sustainable foraging, harvesting wild foods, growing local food, food preservation, and also conduct research on traditional indigenous ethnobotany. 
The market garden aspect of the farm is a new operation and the gardens although small are productive. In 2026 we will have 1  acre of established market gardens, as well as two greenhouses. 
Laughing Lichen is a business with a hard-working team of dedicated staff, volunteers, as well as a family of three Amy, Ian and daughter Wren. Amy is the founder/Owner of Laughing Lichen, Ian is a timberframer/carpenter (https://sherpatimberworks.ca/). 

Activities and participation

WWOOFers will participate in the maintenance of the market gardens during the growing season from June to the end of September. This includes:
•	Planting, tilling soil, soil preparation, composting, cleaning, clearing of land for gardens, weeding, watering, pumping water and harvesting and preserving garden produce.
- feeding, watering, collecting eggs, cleaning and caring for Chicken + Quail + Sheep
•	Accompanying our team on forays into the forest to collect herbs, berries,
_wwoof_travel: Yellowknife, Northwest Territories 30 kms away by road

name: Kingsley - Family farm semi- rural, Duck farm, Sheep rearing, Mixed vegetable and fruit Farm
description:
We are a family run farm in Sarawak the Malaysian part of Borneo Island. Our farm is located in the capital city Kuching, roughly 1hour outside the city centre . We now have a duck farm (khaki campbell breed) for eggs on the 4-acre family land. We've only begun rearing ducks for about a yeart so if you have any experience with this it will be very beneficial. Most of the locations in  are with road access, between 1 hour drive The internet or wifi is available but unstable in the farm, however its close enough to town to get good connection if needed; but I'm sure there will be much better things to do while you're there. We can arrange a space at the farm if you're willing to stay, however the space is sharing with another male worker, but you will have your own room. Its basic (western style toilet),  but comfortable. Currently electricity by solar our generator so please bear this in mind. 
The past couple of years has encourage us to cultivate and work on the land to provide employment and help the struggling local economy. 
We also run a small coffee shop in town selling produce from our farm. Its nothing fancy, just a simple cafe with simple food from the farm, when we can.
What we can offer is a unique opportunity to explore Kuching as well as the surrounding area. We have previously hosted countless friends and guests from UK, Germany, Lithuania, Nigeria, Australia, New Zealand, and many other countries on our property. We too have had the opportunity to travel abroad for some time, where we came across wwofing, Now we would like to share this experience with those who are interested. Thanks!

Certifications: We had just started duck farming in the last couple of years. We are still new to farming and working towards certification.

Current projects:
We are in process of building a modern shelter for our ducks; also we have a small cafe in town selling our farm produce.

Internet access available at certain locations

name: Martha - Commercial Alaskan peony farm
description:
We are a family owned and operated commercial peony farm located in Willow, AK. We have operated our farm for 11 years and exclusively cultivate peonies as cut flowers for sale. Our property is 6 acres, one acre is peony production with the remaining land  being native forest and living space. We also have a vegetable/berry garden that is cultivated for private use. We have a fire pit/gathering area that most WWOOFers really enjoy as well as a weekly farmers market in town. 

Nathan is a Forester, so we often host his co-workers and industry professionals for a wide variety of projects ranging from drone mapping to sapling nursery and native species regeneration. Our farm is dedicated to assisting with educational research whenever possible, so we host visitors almost weekly to offer data and specimen collection. 

We'd love to show WWOOFers all aspects of a commercial peony operation depending on the week you plan on visiting. 
Tasks may include:

Testing and repairing irrigation systems 
Staking plants as they growRemoving side buds to encourage one terminal bud for commercial harvest 
Building boxes as we prepare for shipping
Harvesting and grading
Cooler management
Preparing shipments
Driving of deliveries/shipments
Field maintenance: weeds, integrated pest management, nutrient application, compost spreading, irrigation, mowing
Maintaining vegetable garden and berry patches (including sharing the bounty) Farm work is generally physical, laborious, repetitive and done in all sorts of weather. 

A flower farm sounds like a vacation, but it is still a working farm.
Harvest typically occurs the first 21 days of July and is our busiest time of year. We normally harvest every four hours.

Nathan is constantly building and upgrading systems.  Any system a WWOOFer is interested in learning more about, we'd be happy to teach as much as possible. We've built every piece of our farm from the ground up and learned a lot along the way. Martha is also the sales manager for a
_wwoof_travel: The Anchorage Ted Stevens International Airport is the nearest airport, about 1.5 hrs away, or a pick up/Drop off in Talkeetna if you travel by train.  There is a bus to take you from Anchorage to a stop close to the farm, where we'll pick you up.

name: Michel - Verger en perma culture transition vers agro syntropie
description:
Bonjour chers amis woof, je m’appelle Michel et j’ai 54ans. Je vis avec ma famille (mon épouse et nos 2 enfants, 3 chiens, 7chats et quelques poules) sur notre propriété de 4ha bord de mer en Nouvelle-Calédonie sur la commune de Bourail 98870.
Le climat est subtropical, une saison chaude de novembre à mai et une saison plus fraîche de juin à octobre.

Sur la propriété, les activités principales sont la production de vanille, l’arboriculture fruitière (agrumes, avocats et litchis) ainsi que le maraîchage en agriculture naturelle pour la famille (bananes, papayes, fruits et légumes)
Une journée type sur la propriété consiste à entretenir (désherbage), préparer (semis, repiquage, plantations, paillage) et récolter les cultures au fil des périodes et saisons, avec une préférence pour 4h de travail le matin (T° plus agréables).

L’organisation pour la journée commence la veille avec un point des travaux à réaliser. La journée peut se dérouler entre 6h et 10h, notamment durant la période de mariage des fleurs de vanille (septembre, octobre et novembre). Le planning sera à adapter selon les conditions météorologiques. L’après-midi est libre pour des balades entre La Roche Percée et Poé.

Le logement est une dépendance sur la propriété entièrement équipé (cuisine/frigo/congélateur/ lave-linge…)
Les repas sont pris ensemble de préférence le soir, ou à la convenance de chacun. 

Pour les loisirs, ils sont tournés vers les activités nautiques (spot de surf à proximité), la pêche en mer ou en rivière, la montagne (randonnées, VTT, équitation…) et la chasse, dans un rayon de 5km.

name: Miguel Angel Gastón - Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat, Flower Garden Farm, Fruit tree nursery
description:
Somos la familia Cassoli, Residentes En Meliquina Desde el año 1997 Pioneros Y en la Creación de la Villa Meliquina. Desde Hace Un tiempo desarrollamos la unidad ONU Proyecto Empresarial Sostenible Integrado por el siguiente Grupo de Empresas: Jardines de la Patagonia (Servicios y Vivero), Familia Cassoli ( Elaboramos chocolates, dulces, licores, y panificación) y Hostería Meliquina (Eco Hostería de Montaña) .

Basándonos en nuestra experiencia de 23 años en el lugar, y observando el crecimiento poblacional que está teniendo toda la zona, nos convertimos en una empresa que brinda servicios globales a los nuevos habitantes de la villa y alrededores. Nos encontramos en un momento de desarrollo y diversificación de iniciativas que tiene que ver con la incorporación de servicios que garanticen un crecimiento sostenible de la zona sumado a la concienciación ambiental de todos los habitantes. Para lograrlo, desarrollamos un sistema ecológico de crecimiento y evolución, no solamente del vivero, sino de todos nuestros proyectos comprometidos con el medio ambiente.

Nuestros Servicios: 
- Realización de Construcciones rústicas en piedra y madera. 
- Implementación de Sistemas de Energías Renovables. 
- Diseño y Cuidado de parques 
- Implementación de Sistemas de riego. 
- Forestación ONU Campos y loteos.
- Elaboración de productos de estación, chocolates y panificación

Hosteria de montaña en Patagonia alejada de la ciudad a 45 km de San Martín de los Andes. Sin medio de transporte para llegar, se puede acordar el traslado. Si Internet. Sistema de enegia alternativa. El lugar PUEDE padecer de ciertas carencias Que Tiene la Ciudad, PUEDE Haber nieve en el Camino Y PUEDE Que Internet no siempre funcione.

 

Organic methods: www.jardinesdelapatagonia.com
- We reproduce plants, native and others. 
- We have a greenhouse for our vegetables and aromatics
- We make Chocolate and Smokey Cheese
- Our energy is based in alternative energies, like solar panels and ba
_wwoof_travel: Dependiendo de la época del año, podemos buscarlos en San martin de los Andes, Bariloche o el cruce de Río Hermoso y Confluencia

name: Corentin - Petite ferme en cours d'installation
description:
Corentin, 30ans, 7 ans ouvrier apiculteur et Céline ma compagne. 
Nous avons une jolie ferme ou je lance mon exploitation à titre principal cette année dans le maraîchage. Je vais poursuivre également mon activité d'apiculteur.
J'ai également des poules pondeuses, mes abeilles et des cochons.
Je vends principalement au marché, en panier, et aux restaurateurs du coin
Le cadre est agréable et évolutif.

Vous participerez à la préparation des cultures, les plantations, les récoltes. Je produis mes plants, je prépare le marché le vendredi.

Maison avec chambre à part ou bien possibilité d'aménagement d'un lieu de vie dans séchoir à part pour plus d'intimité. Nous partagerons quasiment tous les repas ensemble. Nous discuterons de tout cela ensemble. 

L'été nous avons la possibilité d'aller à la piscine. 
Sinon beaucoup de beau endroit a visiter aux alentours ( château, village). Possible de prêter un vélo

name: Mourad - Le projet "Terroir Lblad" est une initiative ambitieuse visant à valoriser les produits de terroir
description:
Bonjour amis Woofleurs,je suis Mourad la Vision de mon projet est
Valoriser les produits de terroir locaux de la région de Boudnib Errachidia,  en les intégrant dans un système agroforesterie durable

Notre  projet, est en train de préparation : système d'irrigation, énergie solaire, bassin de stockage,
Clôture, plantation des arbres fruitiers, essai des cultures maraichers ...

Pour le moment le logement et nourriture sera à la maison (familiale)

J'aime la chasse, sports, randonnée,

name: Tyrfingur - Regenerative farm with sheep, beef, chicken and hens. On farm wool prosesing.
description:
About us
We are the third generation in our family on this farm, taking over in 2010, but the family moved to this farm in 1932.
We farm with ca 70 cattle, 90 sheep, 100 hens and numerous chicken and few pigs in the summer. We also run a mini mill for wool spinning at the farm, where 3 employees are working in the mill all week days.  We are 2 adults (husband and wife) and our 19 year old daughter living at the farm, and on the farm (in other houses) are also two of our other kids living with their families. There is a dog on the farm and a cat.
The farm is located in the south west of Iceland, one hour drive from Reykjavík. 
We like to cook good food and enjoy outdoor activities. Mostly that is done around the animals, so when we want to do something different, we take a drive and look at beautiful scenery or visit friends. We sing in the church choir and the lady of the house knits A LOT, where as Tyrfingur is a qualified welder and a mechanic, so making things that are needed is usually done at the farm.
We also do a lot of looking at videos,listening to lectures and read books about regenerative farming and sustainable living.
When we have some students or volunteers in our house, we take them for a sight seeing and tell them about our culture and country, when we can.

Accommodations and plan for meals.
Woofers will have a private room in our house and share bathroom with the family. Internet access is available. He/she will be with us in every meal of the day, which normally are 4. 
Our diet consists mostly of products grown or raised on the farm. We eat a lot of meat, eggs, dairy products and vegetables from our own vegetable garden. Unfortunately we cannot cater for particular diets.  Bear in mind that we are a meat producing farm that also slaughters animals here on the farm for our own consumption.  If you are not comfortable eating the diet described, this farm may not be the best option for you. However, we must point out that our main goal to treat our
_wwoof_travel: Keflavik airport. Bus station in Selfoss

name: Loh - Family farm, Community farm
description:
Seeds of change organization also focus more in Agriculture which is one of our main objectives .In a country where more than 60% are unemployed ,we see farming as a solution. this will help to bring more foods to the table and keep the youths more busy .Join is and make a difference in our community through farming. While with us in Cameroon or Ghana, you will-Assist us in fish , and poultry farming, animal feed manufacture etc.  
          Besides, growing  of organic vegetables and fruits are also part of our  activities .We carry workshops and Share common ideas on Agriculture with the poor farmers. while with us, you will also Visit our cattle ranch and assist in feeding and milking.  you will also learn how to cultivate crops  found mostly in Africa such as cassava and also how it is transformed into finish products,such as garri,beer,cassava flour,bohbolo, kokoi and accra cassava.-You will come across and assist in the cultivation of Cabbage, onion, carrot, maize and okro which are organically cultivated by farming groups.You would also join our team in raising awareness on  agricultural malpractices share ideas and create awareness on the importance of organic food. ACCOMMODATION :We have two volunteer accommodation in Cameroon and Ghana.Our homes are  comfortable with modern toilets and you are free not to share your room with other volunteers. This means that you get to eat, sleep, work, socialize and generally live like an African.  With Local families  just around, this will enable you to fully experience the local culture and tourism. Many of our volunteers remain friends with neighbors and members of the organization long after they leave Africa and in some cases they return to the country later especially to visit again."My neighbors - Hilda and Divine are lovely. The children are very sweet, and I feel extremely lucky to have with them.

name: Marie-Hélène - Petite exploitation, Agroforesterie, Verger, Champignons, Potager familial
description:
**Nous n'acceptons qu'un couple ou WWOOFeur à la fois, merci de regarder nos disponibilités avant de nous contacter**

Nous sommes une petite entreprise agroforestière située dans le Bas-Saint-Laurent, au Québec. Notre projet a débuté en 2016 avec la création de notre entreprise, l'acquisition de notre terre et la construction de notre mini-maison. En 2017, nous avons débuté nos activités avec l'achat de machinerie agricole et l'inoculation d'un millier de billots avec du mycélium de shiitake. En 2018, notre production de champignon débutait modestement et nous avons réalisé d'importants travaux (relier la maison à l'électricité, ouvrir des champs en friche, forage d'un puits, établissement d'un demi-hectare en verger diversifié, etc.). 2019 a été notre première réelle saison de production, nous avons fait plusieurs tests de cultures (haricots secs, quinoa, strophaire rouge vin) et nous avons débuté la cueillette sauvage de plantes médicinales, l'élevage de canards et d'oies (pour notre propre consommation et afin de lutter contre les ravageurs) ainsi que l'autoconstruction d'une grange qui abrite aujourd'hui un atelier de menuiserie/mécanique, un séchoir solaire et un hangar. En 2021, nous avons débuté l'autoconstruction de notre "vraie" maison, amélioré notre régie de culture afin d'augmenter nos rendements de shiitaké, fait d'autres tests au champ et augmenté nos volumes et diversité de cueillette sauvage. En 2022, nous avons agrandi le verger de petits fruits (sureau, gadelier, cassissier) et en 2023, nous avons ajouté une centaine de poiriers à notre verger.

Pour 2026, nous aurons un potager familial à entretenir, les poules, les oies et les canards à soigner (nous faisons du sylvopastoralisme dans notre verger),  beaucoup de cueillette sauvage à effectuer, la multiplication/entretien/récolte au verger (petits fruits principalement) ainsi que l'inoculation/entretien/récolte de la culture de shiitaké.

Au travers de tout cela, nous sommes également les parents

name: Lena - Ferme maraîchère
description:
Bonjour, je m’appelle Lena et je vis sur une micro ferme maraîchère sur le terrain familial. Ma mère, mon frère et mon conjoint y vivent aussi.

Nos activités quotidiennes sont très variées! Prendre soins des poules, récolter, semer, transplanter, désherber, tondre, faire des aménagements paysagers, s’occuper des plants, garnir le kiosque et aller au marché vendre les produits et rencontrer les clients!

En général le déjeuner est libre selon les goûts (fruits, pain, yogourt, céréales, œufs…). Pour le dîner, (petit déjeuner) nous prenons une pause sur l’heure du midi afin de se reposer et manger un petit repas simple (salade, sandwich, restants de la veille…).
Pour le souper, nous mangeons tous ensemble autour d’un bon repas. Un espace repas sera installé dans votre chambre afin que vous soyez quasi autonome. (Grille pain, four micro-ondes onde, bouilloire…)

Lors des journées de congé, j’aime relaxer autour de la piscine, profiter du soleil, faire du vélo et sortir en ville. 
Étant situé à 10 minutes de Sherbrooke, Qc, tout est à proximité: Restaurants, parcs, montagne, centre-ville, centre d’achat…

name: Seraphine - Organic Farming for Sustainable Lifestyle
description:
Hello WWOOFers, my name is Seraphine working in Igoma Organic Farm together with my other two Partners. I live at the lowlands of Same District nearby Mkomazi National Park. I live a local lifestyle with my friendly neighbours

Farm activities at Igoma Farm mostly include land cultivation, weeding, preparing manure including mulching, harvesting, cooking, irrigation and more organic farming practices. Working per day takes 4-6 hours depending on the day activities

There is a house at the farm for Accomodation, where all plans including cooking food are done at the farm where the house is. Meals will be the local food we cook and eat, sharing with WWOOFers as members of our family.

The farm is very close to Mkomazi National Park (Just a walking distance like 300m), whereby WWOOFers will get time to plan for visits during free hours and see the beautiful nature and animals available at the Park. I like hiking at the Pare mountains

name: Allie - We are an off-grid, small scale homestead farm located in the beautiful Matanuska Valley in Alaska.
description:
Off Grid -Human Powered- Nutrient Dense- Small Scale- Beyond Organic Farm and Homestead.
Seeking motivated individuals passionate about growing regeneratively and putting up food, learning new skills, and working hard.
Most skills require lots of repetition others don’t.

After reading all of this, if you are seriously interested, email us for more information.

Spring projects include: Prepping growing areas, building and fixing fence lines, prepping the gravity feed water system, installing pump for pond water, broad forking, amending beds, building and spreading compost, cleaning out chicken coop, maintaining wood heat in the greenhouse, making soil blocks starts, chopping wood, making ferments, and other various tasks. Note that this farm opportunity is not limited to the growing area, but a large portion of your time will be invested in all the workings of a homestead. 

Summer projects include: Transplanting, seeding, weeding, watering, harvesting for market, washing and processing vegetables, making value added products, harvesting herbs and making medicine, canning and freezing, smoking salmon, making ferments, building and turning compost, brewing compost tea, care of chickens, ducks, pigs, mixing, preparing, soaking and fermenting animal feed daily and/or weekly, building projects, etc. 

Fall Projects include: Vegetable harvest, berry picking, moose or caribou butchering and preserving, making ferments, pressing apple cider, food preservation and canning, clearing land for growing, cover cropping, laying silage tarps, chopping wood, and winterizing farm. Slaughter and processing of hogs in early Oct including traditional preservation techniques. Daily- taking care of pigs, making animal food for pigs, chickens, and ducks. Watering starts, maintaining gravity water system, managing and improving off grid systems, opening and closing venting systems for structures, rolling up sides on tunnels etc. and doing it all again in the evening. ---------------------

name: Kenneth (Ken) - Avocado farm🥑 with beautiful view, surrounded by redwoods, 30 minutes from Santa Cruz
description:
I am currently able to accommodate up to two people at a time for a Wwoof visit.  I will usually have a cottage available for housing and provide meals to all who are helping on the farm. Still a number of openings available for the coming months. If you are interested in a visit  please let me know.
     I am grateful to live in a beautiful place with a wonderful view and a climate that allows me to grow all kinds of delicious and healthy food throughout the year. Initially, my focus was on avocados and growing enough food to feed my family. In recent years that has shifted. In addition to feeding my family,  I am growing more and more for our small local market. So the growing beds have increased, there are more avocado trees, as well as a variety of other fruits, and creating healthy baked goods has become a mainstay as well. At this point my aspiration is to develop a replicable model in which a person could make a decent living by growing food and selling it directly to the consumer at a reasonable price. 

Over the years Wwoofers have been an integral part of this farm  and I  once again am enjoying meeting enthusiastic individuals from around the country and around the world while sharing the wonderful experience of life on this farm.  I will consider hosting a WWOOFER for as little as a week or up to a month, generally 3 weeks has proven to be ideal. Activities could include harvesting, mulching, composting, pruning, grafting, planting, digging, weeding, cultivating, irrigation, and perhaps a small construction project . Please let me know if you have other skills such as experience in construction or technical/computer skills as there are a  number of potential projects as well. We ask each visitor to contribute up to 6 hours a day, 5.5 days a week.  The main house is open for use during the day and evening.  Healthy vegetarian food will be provided with most meals prepared for you and access to the kitchen for others. We ask each wwoofer to plan on assisti
_wwoof_travel: Watsonville Transit Center is the nearest bus station. The nearest bus stops are on route 73 coming from Santa Cruz. The Norman Mineta Airport on San Jose is the nearest airport (SJC)

name: Julian - Finca Eco-Logica de Café organico, Cerveza artesanal y Joyería
description:
Soy Julian, nacido en Buenos Aires, Argentina llevo ya más de 7 años viviendo en Colombia, tengo 31 años. Tengo este proyecto de finca eco-lógica llamada Tantanakuy, que significa encuentro en quechua, y llevo casi  3 años transformandola de una finca de café comercial a una finca autosustentable.

La finca tiene una hectarea de la cual produzco café orgánico, cerveza artesanal a la leña y con agua de manantial de la finca, y joyería artesanal en plata con piedras semipreciosas.

Ademas cultivo organicamente muchos alimentos, tengo dos pequeños lagos con peces y dos colmena de abejas! Todo buscando la autosuficiencia que el presente demanda.

Hablo poco inglés pero lo suficiente para comunicarme. Mis dos hijitos pasan los fines de semana conmigo y también tengo una perra muy cariñosa llamada Chicha.

Actualmente estoy en la búsqueda de voluntarios para cuidar mi casa por unos meses mientras hago un viaje en los meses de julio y agosto.

Algunas actividades en la que vountarios me ayudan son por ejemplo:
◇ Bioconstrucción
◇ Cultivo de café
◇ Cerveza artezanal 
◇  Y todo tipo de tareas relacionadas con la agricultura consciente

Ofrezco un cuarto con una cama individual o pueden acampar al lado del río y bosque, que queda a unos metros de la casa.

La finca esta ubicada en la vereda Arauca uno, sector "el silencio" a 20 minutos en moto del pueblo de San Agustin, pueblo con mucho atractivo turístico y arqueológico. 

La casa la hice yo mismo, en un conjunto te tecnicas incluyendo bareque; hay baño seco,  y he decidido no tener wifi, aunque hay poca señal de telefono, ideal para desconectar.
_wwoof_travel: Terminal de transporte de San Agustín

name: Latif - Family farm
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Rafting ve kano gibi doğa sporlarının yapılabileceği muhteşem bir coğrafyada yer alıyor. Aynı zamanda kırsal turizm faaliyetlerinde bulunan ev sahibi üç ayrı evinde gönüllü ağırlayabiliyor. Kemaliye ile Apçağa Köyü arasında meyve bahçesinin bulunduğu evde dört kişinin kalabileceği iki oda, karşı mahalledeki ikinci evde iki ailenin rahatça kalabileceği iki oda, Kemaliye'nin içinde sadece yazın misafir ağırlayabileceği üçüncü evde ise 10 kişinin kalacağı iki oda var. Mart-Kasım ayları arasında yaklaşık 100 farklı bitki toplanıp kurutularak "zetirin" adındaki baharat yapılıyor.

MAR-MAY: Meyve ağaçlarında budama, aşılama, yabancı ot yolma HAZ-EKİ: Sulama, turşu, şurup, reçel yapımı, dut toplama, kurutma, pestil ve pekmez yapımı YIL BOYU: Kuzu, keçi, tavuk bakımı

---
	
The host, who is also engaged in rural tourism, accommodates WWOOFers in his houses in three different locations. 2 rooms for 4 people in his house in the fruit garden, 2 rooms for 2 families in his second house opposite his first house and 2 rooms for a total of 10 people in his third house in the town center (only in summer). The land is surrounded by beautiful nature which allow nature sports like rafting and canoeing. The special spice called 'zetirin' is made of nearly 100 plants picked between March and October.

MAR-MAY: Pruning fruit trees, grafting, weeding JUN-OCT: Watering, making pickles, syrup and jam, harvesting and drying mulberries, making pestil and pekmez (traditional sweets) ALL YEAR LONG: Sheep, goat, chicken care

name: Andy & Sabina - Croft
description:
We have lived on Papa Stour (approx 18 residents)for 52 years,, gradually building our house and Croft from an old stone ruin and raising a family. Be aware that As an older couple we are now less able to be alongside participating in Croft activities at all stages and you need to be happy to work alone or with other WWOOFER S. We have hosted Conservation Volunteers and WWOOFER’s from all over the world since the 1980’s, resulting in many enduring friendships.

Our Croft (small farm) is on the island of Papa Stour, which is in the Shetland group of islands, approximately 200 miles north of the Scottish mainland. We have a 40 breeding ewes, mostly Texel / Cllyn crosswhich are located both on the Croft .   Also a small flock of Shetland sheep on the ‘apportionment’ on the remote uncultivated side of the island. Lambing is usually one of the highlights of our year and is from late April into May.

We have several walled garden areas in which we grow a variety of vegetables, all according to Organic and Permaculture principles. We also rear and breed our own chickens .Finn  is our working sheep dog.

The property itself is adjacent to the sea, with its own beach and with various spectacular cliffs and caves nearby. We also manage our own native woodland eco burial site.

Tasks on the Croft varies according to the seasons; Anything from lambing, clipping, butchering (we eat our own produce, including lamb), egg production, composting, veg growing, fencing, cutting weeds, collecting seaweed, dry stone walling, hay making and peat cutting.

We have a number of rooms available in the main house, including a double en-suite. We also have a self contained wooden cabin for a couple. We are very sociable and main meals are usually taken communally.  We hope you will be prepared to take a  turn  of the cooking requirement.

For further information on Papa Stour please visit https://www.papastourhcg.org/
Ben Fogul Scotlands Sacred Island Series.
Certifications: Not certified due
_wwoof_travel: Overnight NORTHLINK ferry from Aberdeen on Scottish mainland to Lerwick in the Shetland Islands or 'plane: Aberdeen/ Sumburgh: Bus to ferry toWest Burrafirth: Ferry to Papa Stour.

name: Dayna - Farmstead in the beautiful Driftless area of southeastern MN.
description:
Intro: Nettle Valley Farm is in the beautiful Driftless area of southeastern MN. We focus on silvopasture establishment and invasive species control using a variety of practices. We also are starting to grow certified organic native and ornamental perennial flowers for seed production. We've also added two mobile saunas to the farm and we host community sauna sessions and private sauna parties. 

The Farm: The farm is 90 acres of woods, pasture, pine plantation, ponds and fields near Spring Grove, MN. Projects are different every day in every season, but can include tending to the seed crops (planting, weeding, harvesting), removing invasive species like garlic mustard and buckthorn, splitting wood using a hydraulic splitter, simple building projects, fermentation projects, helping our neighbors with various tasks, maintaining perennial beds, and more.

The People: NVF is run by Dayna and Nick. We're into simple but good living, doing work we love, and spending time with good people. Dayna runs the farm on a day to day basis and is into anything related to insects, saunas, birds, siestas, and learning new skills! Nick works full time as a remote programmer but is very involved in the homestead and business side of things. Nick is into playing violin, cooking up delicious pork dishes, playing cards, bonfires and podcasts. We love hanging out with our derpy dog Buddy (aka Budzilla).

WWOOFing with us: Above all else, we are hoping to connect with WWOOFers who are excellent communicators, pleasant, helpful, and positive contributors to our farm. Bring your skills, we'll teach each other! 

Flow of the day: We ask for 4 hours per day, 5 days per week. Depending on the weather and activities, we'll usually work on projects together from 8-noon or 9-1pm then break for lunch. In the afternoon, Dayna will likely need to work on solo projects or take a rest (we love naps here!), so afternoons and evenings are yours for relaxing, exploring the land or the area! We often head
_wwoof_travel: We can pick you up from Rochester, MN or La Crosse, WI.

name: Kasey - Stewardship education farm on 10 acres in Blanco, TX
description:
About the farm:

Previously Reverse Pioneers microgreens farm, host Kasey (that's me!) is transitioning the farm grounds into a visionary, multi-faceted venture offering an immersive blend of farm goods and tours, camping, event hosting, music, and education. Set on 10 acres in central Texas, all aspects of the farm operation are rooted in the principles of land stewardship, community, creative expression, and sustainable living. Built upon an ethos that connects human well-being with the health of our planet, Sound Ground is designed to attract eco-conscious travelers, outdoor enthusiasts, and creative communities who seek a genuine connection with nature and one another.

About your host: 

Kasey has lived on the property for 9 years, 8 of which she operated as co-founder of a microgreens business with her spouse at the time. Together with her two young children Louisa and Hugo, she aims to continue building a dynamic on-farm community of WWOOFers, short and long term renters, and community guests. She integrates permaculture, regenerative agriculture, holistic management, and other ecologically sound agricultural strategies in her land management practices. She is very mindful of her farm community and seeks to create safe and considerate spaces for all involved to work purposefully and grow together. This is primarily achieved through conscious communication and clear agreements, as well as thorough training and casual bonding opportunities.

Ethos and Philosophy:

People Are Nature. Recognizing that humans are an extension of nature, we emphasize the importance of reconnecting with our physical bodies, natural spaces, and living foods in order to avoid physical, emotional, and mental dis-ease. As such, land care is equal to self care.

Mission:

To steward this piece of earth into resilience and abundance, and teach others to do the same in their available spaces. To empower individuals to reconnect with themselves as nature, celebrate creativity, and drive sus
_wwoof_travel: Austin and San Antonio - both 1 hour away

name: Frank - 🌿Sustainable living center in the Amazon, learn permaculture & syntropic agriculture in the jungle.
description:
📌 Description:

Become part of the development of a sustainable living center in the Peruvian Amazon, located in the jungle of Tarapoto.

This is a personal project that is continuously growing; the vision is to work with nature and not against it: regenerating the forest, growing food that also serves as medicine, producing our own energy, practicing permaculture and syntropic agriculture, and building mainly with natural bioconstruction techniques.

It’s an authentic, simple, and real space. Here you’ll find jungle life, calm, mindful work, and constant learning.
I am currently developing a vegetable garden, a food forest, bioconstruction areas, water systems, trails, and community spaces — all with a vision of self-sufficiency and a deep connection with nature.

✨ People who come here learn:
🍃 Syntropic agriculture
🌿 Practical permaculture
🌱 Soil regeneration and management
🌾 Composting
🛖 Bioconstruction
🪵 Carpentry
🍳 Creative homemade cooking & Peruvian food
🌍 Techniques for simple and sustainable living in the jungle

We also share moments of calm and connection: hikes, bonfires, birdwatching, music, and time with the animals that live here.
Very close to the project, there are waterfalls and natural pools where we often go to swim and enjoy the water.

It’s the perfect environment for those seeking an authentic experience in the Amazon, learning by doing, and living in harmony with the forest.
My space is open to genuine people who want to learn, contribute, and be part of the process of creating a self-sustaining place in the jungle.

📌 Cultural exchange and learning opportunities:

In Purma Pérgola you’ll experience real jungle life, learning directly from nature and from the daily activities we do together.

🌿 Practical learning:
Permaculture basics, syntropic farming, natural building (clay & bamboo), composting, soil care, dry toilet usage, basic carpentry, wood-fired cooking, natural detergent with ash, and sustainable living principles.

🤝 Cultural exch
_wwoof_travel: Aeropuerto Guillermo del Castillo Paredes, Tarapoto, San Martin, Perú.

name: Denison - Small organic farm doing CSA and advocates healthy and sustainable consumption
description:
Den & Jean's Natural Farm Volunteer Program:
Our place is midlands, around 320masl - cool and breezy and surrounded by mostly non-productive agricultural land, so fresh and natural air. The farm has 4-6 workers at any point in time and will be tending to the vegetables and fruit trees. Your role will be to assist them as well as tend to your own assigned vegetable beds. We are an organic farm doing CSA with some farm animals on the side. 
====================
WORK:
1. Five (5) hours per day for 6 days a week
2. Doing mostly farm work - cultivating, weeding, watering, plant care, mulching, pruning, harvesting
3.  Some kitchen work/help if needed (during farm events or school tours or special guests/events) and house cleaning 
4. Other maintenance work like carpentry or trellis building (if applicable skills match)
5. Food processing work - washing, drying and packing of food products. 

TRANSPORTATION: 
We have regular vegetable delivery from Farm to Manila every TUESDAYS and THURSDAYS. So plan your trip based on these schedules to get free ride to and from the farm.

TUESDAYS / THURSDAYS DELIVERY SCHEDULE (unless holidays, inquire to confirm)
Leaves farm 4am going to Manila (Taguig City, 15mins from airport)
Leaves Manila around 11am-12:00nn going back to the farm

ACCOMMODATION :
1. We have 2 houses for Volunteers, depending on availability, you may be assigned to any of the houses. A Shared Room with 4 beds (2 bunkbeds) or Room with 1 Queen bed and 1 Queen pull out bed - whichever is available. Be ready to share with other volunteers. Ask in advance.
2. With Fan 
3. Blankets and Pillows
4. A big kitchen and dining area with complete cookware
5. A bathroom with shower (no hot water) 

AMENITIES:
1. We share assorted vegetables, seasonal fruits and herbs to volunteers 
2. Drinking water 
3. Tea (peppermint, Guyabano tea, lemongrass, galangal, tarragon)
4. Forage tons of amazing indigenous foods
5. There are provincial convenience stores (called sari-sari stores) 3-5
_wwoof_travel: Airport is 15mins from our delivery VAN (available every TUE and THURS 10am-12nn)

name: Angélique - Farm in the center of Portugal
description:
Hello
We are looking for motivated and energetic volunteers to help us with outdoor maintenance work on our rural property in Portugal.
After Storm Kristin, we have fallen behind on many tasks and we are now looking for extra help to bring the gardens and outdoor spaces back to life.
The work mainly includes:
Weeding and garden maintenance
Tree and hedge trimming
Fence repairs
General outdoor cleaning and maintenance
Small DIY and countryside projects
We are especially looking for people who enjoy nature, outdoor work, and are not afraid of physical tasks. Motivation and a positive attitude are much more important to us than experience.
In exchange, you will enjoy a peaceful stay in the countryside surrounded by animals and nature, while sharing daily life with our family.
If you love nature, simple living, and hands-on projects, we would be happy to welcome you!

name: Beverly 'Bev' - Specialty Cut Flower Farm
description:
We grow flowers on 1.5 acres and sell at 2 Indianapolis farmer's markets. We have a dog and 2 cats. My husband and I live here with our two teen daughters.

Activities:
planting, cultivation, harvest and sales. We'd love to teach them how to build a sustainable system and make money. We also dry our flowers and make items with these--the participant would have the opportunity for creative expression.

Expectation of 4-8 hours work in activities described in 2. We own 220 acres and the participant would be able to enjoy our whole property including a pond, woods and nearby Fall Creek.

We are 30 minutes from Indy, but also feel like we are in a remote place. We love good food, cooking,  reading, and woodworking

name: Reda - Organic Farm with Eco Constructions near Rabat
description:
2 hectares in front of eucalyptus and oak forest..in the process of building a small eco-village with tiny houses and bungalows in stone, clay, straw, wood, bamboo, earthbag...we have a carpentry workshop on site..Also expanding and organising the organic farming activities. We also have chicken, sheep, cows, a horse and two donkies and many cats and dogs. Will need help in all these activities..I am an engineer and I help design and layout all the processes

Agricultural work like sowing, planting, watering, triming, weeding, harvesting, working soil, putting up fencing...etc. Animal care like feeding, herding cattle, cleaning barn and moving, spreading manure (optional)..Construction:  help in building bungalows in all organic materials like stone, clay, straw, wood, bamboo, and recycled materials like bottles..

Detached house on site. Food is provided as required ore can be harvested from the garden.

20 mins from is Skhirate beach.
40 mins from Rabat.

My hobbies are live music (I'm a drummer), hiking in the forest, architecture and interior design.

-

2 hectares devant une forêt d'eucalyptus et de chênes... en train de construire un petit éco-village avec des maisons minuscules et des bungalows en pierre, argile, paille, bois, bambou, ecodome... Nous avons un atelier de menuiserie sur place... Nous développons également et organisons les activités agricoles biologiques. Nous avons aussi des poules, des moutons, des vaches, un cheval, deux ânes, ainsi que beaucoup de chats et de chiens. Il faudra de l'aide pour toutes ces activités... Je suis ingénieur et j'aide à concevoir et à organiser tous les processus.

Travaux agricoles comme semer, planter, arroser, tailler, désherber, récolter, travailler la terre, installer des clôtures... etc. Soin des animaux comme nourrir, conduire le bétail, nettoyer les étables et déplacer, épandre le fumier (facultatif)... Construction : aide à la construction de bungalows en matériaux entièrement organiques comme la pierre, l'

name: Rogeria - A family agroecology garden e aromatic field
description:
We are Rogéria Castro, aunt Eugenia and little Kaiuá - happy to share the dream of living in the countryside.
Espaço Trilha Viva has existed since 2018, located at the base of Serra das Aranhas mountains, in the village of Santa Cruz dos Godinhos, 15 km from Ervália city, in Minas Gerais State – southern region of Brigadeiro Mountains Chain.
In this small agro-ecological property, we care a natural garden, with a variety of plants around two lakes with pure water from the springs. In addition, we have a small seedling nursery, vegetable garden and crop field of aromatic plants used for essential oil production.
We are looking for people who can come and collaborate with us in gardening, bio-construction, agroecology, arts and general maintenance mainly.
The experience at Espaço Trilha Viva is ideal for those who want to explore the mountains, forests, waterfalls, gastronomy and rural lifestyle that are so outstanding in the State of Minas Gerais. 
Here you will be immersed in a totally natural Rainforest environment, surrounded by trees, flowers, mountains and wild animals. You will be able to meet people with a simple and extremely welcoming lifestyle, sharing a good chat by the fire, at the coffee table or at the wood stove.

Organic methods: We don´t use pesticides. We make our compost, we plant green manure and pruning to creat organic material for the crops,
Current projects: 
Soil Preparation for planting
Planting  and Haverst 
Essencial Oil extraction 
Compost care
Painting
Eco Art Projects to decoration
Gardening and landscaping
Trail construction

Internet access available

name: Sole - Organic Vegetable production,Animals,work with Kids and people with disability
description:
Welcome to Our Organic Family Farm in Veneto, Italy

We’re a young family of five — two adults and three small children (born in 2018, 2021, and 2023) — living and working on a small organic farm in the Veneto region. Our dream is simple: to live closely with the land, raise happy animals, grow real food, and share this way of life with others.

Our farm is 2 hectares in size and includes:

Seasonal vegetable gardens

A small processing lab for jams, sauces, and preserves

Chickens for meat and eggs

Three cows, a pony, a donkey, two dogs, and bees

During the week, we also collaborate with people with disabilities, who come to the farm to help with a variety of tasks. This is an enriching and meaningful part of our daily life, based on respect, inclusion, and shared experiences.

What We’re Looking For
We love having volunteers who are practical, curious, and willing to dive in. Experience isn’t necessary — we’ll teach you — but motivation and a good attitude are essential.

You’ll help with a variety of seasonal tasks depending on the time of year, such as:

Working in the vegetable gardens

Harvesting and processing fruit and vegetables

Helping at the farm shop or with local direct sales

Feeding and caring for the animals

Supporting us during slaughter days (only if you're comfortable with it)

Accommodation
We offer a mobile home with:

2 bedrooms (4 beds total)

Private bathroom

Kitchen

Air conditioning

We ask for a minimum stay of 3–4 weeks to allow time to settle in and become part of the farm rhythm.

Current and Ongoing Projects
We’re constantly improving and dreaming big. Some of our ongoing projects include:

Expanding our beekeeping

Building a new barn for our cows

Doing mechanical repairs on farm machinery

If you have skills in any of these areas — or want to learn — we’d love your help!

Free Time and Surroundings
We’re located near beautiful places worth exploring:

Padua, with its art, markets, and vibrant energy

The medieval walled town of

name: Ahmad Tarmizi - Farm,horse stables in a 15 acre of training camp by the mountain
description:
Hello WWOOFers. My name is Tarmizi, 45 years old. I live in a campsite at Gunung Pulai 2. I have 1 acre farm and horse range.

On a typical day at the farm, the day begins with early morning chores like feeding animals and harvest followed by cleaning, drying, and processing them. WWOOFers learn about organic farming practices, cultivation, and sustainable methods through hands-on activities. The day includes a mix of farm tasks, educational sessions, and communal meals, ending with relaxation and reflection on the day’s work. It’s a blend of practical work and learning, focused on growing and plantation sustainably.

WWOOFers will stay in a cozy hut with two double-decker beds and basic amenities. We’ll provide three nutritious farm-fresh meals daily, including dishes like vegetable stews, fresh salads, and herbal teas. There will be a shared dining area for enjoying meals and connecting. The goal is to offer a welcoming, homely environment that supports both learning and camaraderie.

I enjoy farming and harvest crops and horse riding.
_wwoof_travel:
Kulai Bus Station
Senai International Airport

name: Marcela - Organic avocado, coffee, mangosteen, egg and milk farm
description:
Hi my name is Marcela, my husband and I live on a farm in Colombia. We have 4 dogs a cat and lots of chickens. We also have  15 cows. We love that we live in a very peaceful place and we eat lots of fruits fresh eggs and milk.

A typical  day for us is waking early to feed all of our animals, milking the cows, picking eggs, picking avocados, plantains, cacao or mangosteen. In our farm you could learn how to plant, fertilize and harvest fruits. Take care of cows, milk them and see the beauty of a calf being born. Make products with our fresh milk, repair fences and get rid of ants.

We eat healthy meals. Breakfast usually consists of fresh eggs, baked bread, freshly squeezed orange juice, coffee and fruits and cheese. Lunch is usually a hot meal consisting of a protein vegetables and a starch.  Dinner is usually a light meal. We eat out on the porch where you can enjoy the peace and beautiful of the farm.

My husband and I enjoy daily hikes around the farm. We can also go horseback riding and swimming. Once a week we go down to town to go grocery shopping and enjoy an ice cream if we want.

En nuestra hermosa finca podrás encontrar vacas, caballos, perros, un gato y muchas gallinas. Comerás comida sana y fresca cultivada en la finca. En tu tiempo libre podrás ir de caminata ver cómo preparan la caña de azúcar en un trapiche o descansar al lado de la piscina. 
Te podremos enseñar a plantar, cultivar y recoger frutos como aguacate, plátano, cacao y mangostino. Aprenderás a ordeñar las vacas y cuidar de ellas. También podrás aprender a hacer productos lácteos con la leche fresca. 
Será una experiencia inolvidable.

name: MURILLO - Sítio com produção focada em café agroecológico, com uma nanotorrefação no local.
description:
Olá a todos! Me chamo Murillo, 37 anos. Sou um neorural, vim pra roça em fevereiro de 2019, com vontade de produzir água e focando, para isso, na produção de café especial orgânico. O sítio fica numa cidadezinha chamada Dom Viçoso, em Minas Gerais. Depois de aprender mais sobre agricultura, entendi que o orgânico em si era uma variação menos nociva do convencional, e passei a agroecologia. Trabalhava com dois funcionários, eram vinte mil pés de café. Hoje venho diminuindo a plantação para dar espaço às florestas e águas. Trabalho na maior parte do tempo sozinho e, às vezes, quando o trabalho aperta, contrato diaristas. Em 2024 montei uma nano torrefação aqui, pra poder controlar a qualidade do café e estar de acordo com as leis da produção orgânica. E nesse mesmo ano passei a receber visitas de turistas interessados em saber mais sobre o mundo do café. O sítio é certificado desde 2021, primeiro por auditoria, e agora pelo sistema participativo. Eu moro sozinho, mas tenho uma cachorra, a Rita, um cachorro, o Bresson, e uma gata, a Iolanda.

Os dias no sítio variam muito. No verão tem muita roçada e acumulação de matéria orgânica pra fazer, muita poda de bananeira. Quando chega o outono começa a colheita do café, a secagem no terreiro. Quando possível, o plantio do alho e da cebola. O inverno é tranquilo, focado na secagem e armazenamento do café. Uma colheita ou outra, mandioca, batata baroa. Na primavera recomeçam os plantios das anuais, as desbrotas dos cafezeiros. Além de ensinar os trabalhos do dia-a-dia, adoro falar sobre todo o processo que envolve o café, desde o pé até a xícara, todas as inúmeras variáveis que podem culminar num bom café ou num café ruim. Posso ensinar o processo de torra, já que torramos o café aqui. Amo falar sobre solo, sobre a importância da vida no solo.

As refeições são preparadas e consumidas juntos. Com exceção do ovo, as refeições são veganas. Há disponível um quarto com cama de casal. A casa tem dois banheiros, mas só um deles tem

name: Manuel - Living and learning together on a regenerative farm in Costa Rica
description:
We are a small community living in the Caribbean lowlands of Costa Rica, surrounded by jungle, wildlife, and food forests. Our home is a regenerative project focused on soil health, agroforestry, and simple, conscious living. Here, we grow food using permaculture and organic principles, care for the land, and share daily life with volunteers and learners from different cultures.

👥 Who lives here
Our community is made up of several adults that live here year round and short and long-term volunteers who come to learn, live and learn here. We live simply and work closely with nature. We live together, eat together, and share responsibilities in a respectful and friendly atmosphere. We speak both Spanish and English.

🌱 What we do on the land
Our daily activities depend on the season and current needs, but often include:
Planting and maintaining vegetable gardens and food forests
Making compost and taking care of soil life
Mulching, pruning, and harvesting
Caring for seedlings and nursery work
Natural building and small maintenance tasks
Help in the communal kitchen or cleaning shared spaces

WWOOFers help 5 hours a day, 5 days a week, and the work is always done alongside us or with clear guidance. Tasks are educational and hands-on,  supported by our team.

🍽 Food and accommodation
WWOOFers are provided with three healthy vegetarian meals a day. We cook mostly with fresh ingredients from the farm and local markets. Meals are shared with the community in a family-style setting.
Accommodation is in shared wooden cabins up to 4 people per room, there's shared compost toilets, and outdoor showers.

🎓 What you can learn here
We love to share what we know and encourage questions. Depending on the season and your interests, you can learn about:
Permaculture and regenerative agriculture
Soil health, composting, and microorganisms
Agroforestry and syntropic systems
Living in community and low-impact lifestyles
Medicinal plants and natural food preparation

We are not looking

name: Phileena & David - Family run ranch with lots of opportunities to contribute your skills
description:
WHO WE ARE

For over four decades, our ranch has been a haven for those seeking adventure, relaxation, and a genuine connection with nature. Our ranch, nestled amidst 37,000 acres of wildlife, has evolved into a pristine destination for hunters and nature enthusiasts alike.

We are looking for volunteers who have basic skills in building and vehicle maintenance, construction/building, and groundskeeping.

This is an ideal opportunity for people looking for a break from their everyday life who would like to exchange their skills and service for a remote cultural ranching experience in the breathtaking mountains of northern New Mexico.

We love to welcome kind, hardworking volunteers who are self-motivated, honest, trustworthy, and capable. An initial short-term WWOOF stay may be followed by the invitation to stay on longer term.

PROJECTS

Carpentry/Construction: 
We are looking for motivated, skilled, detail-oriented, hard-working hands to help us finish a couple renovation projects for old adobe dwellings. If you have experience with carpentry or construction, we would love to have you!

Maintenance/Mechanics:
We have other projects such as mechanical maintenance of ranch vehicles, if you're mechanically inclined or have experience with maintenance on trucks, tractors and off-road vehicles, we would also love your help!

Groundskeeping:
Beautifying the grounds and keeping up with maintenance.

Building Maintenance
Building and maintaining structures using conventional and alternative materials and methods.

Ranch Hand
Backcountry fence building and repair, maintaining roads and trails, feeding cattle

Other Opportunities: 
Housekeeping, grounds clean up, groundskeeping, creek maintenance, hiking trail maintenance, and organizing our Quonset hut and sheds. 

ACCOMMODATIONS and FOOD

We offer various comfortable housing options, but most likely individuals will share housing with other volunteers. 

Housing includes options of a private room and private bath, private

name: Pablo - Hi to all this farm is beautiful jungle full of native trees focused on syntropic agroforesty
description:
My name is Pablo iam into agroforesty i live here with my brother that is veterinarian we have 3 dogs 3 cats, tilapia fish, ducks, chickends, sheeps, horses, cows.

We want to keep expanding our syntropic farm goals and expand vegetable production!  Average day of work will be taking care of plans composting chop and drop and take care of animals.  Havest pruning ect

Plenty of space power internet hot water washing machine  you can camp on my tent or sleep in a bed

I love hiking and show around my guess others spots to enjoy nature

name: Catherine - Contamos con 3 chacras donde cultivamos distintos frutos para la venta y el consumo nuestro.
description:
Somo una familia de 4 personas, padre, madre, hija y nieta, tenemos chacras de fresa y huerta de distintas frutas, convivimos con animales como gallinas, gallos, pollitos, cuyes y 3 ovejas.

Nos dedicamos a la siembra y cosecha de fresas, como es la vida en el campo desde dar de comer a los animales que habitan en la casa hasta como tener una cultura de cuidado del medio ambiente.

Se cuenta con 1habitacion, acostumbramos a tener 3 comidas importante al día, por tradición de la zona agregamos 1 comida adicional llamada lonche que puede ser entre las 5pm antes de la cena.

Nos gusta la vida de campo, respirar aire puro y escuchar a los animales desde temprano y al rio que pasa muy cerca de la casa.

name: Diego - Somos un Eco-centro de Montaña, un espacio familiar al servicio de la vida.
description:
🌄¿Quiénes somos?
Somos una familia que habita y cuida este territorio de montaña desde hace más de 25 años.
 Con el tiempo, este espacio se transformó en un eco-centro de experiencias y retiros dedicados al autoconocimiento, la vida en comunidad y la conexión con la naturaleza. Nuestro propósito es ofrecer herramientas simples y prácticas para mejorar la calidad de vida y, al mismo tiempo, cuidar la Tierra.
 Trabajamos en proyectos de reforestación con especies nativas, cultivos adaptados al ecosistema serrano y diseño en permacultura, integrando saberes ancestrales y tecnologías apropiadas para habitar en armonía con el entorno.
El lugar cuenta con frutales, hierbas aromáticas y medicinales nativas, y compartimos la vida cotidiana con animales del entorno —ovejas, vacas, caballos— y con nuestras familias vecinas de la montaña.
 Actualmente el proyecto está conformado por 12 personas, algunas residiendo aquí y otras en proceso de transición desde la ciudad, fortaleciendo este puente entre mundos.

🌄¿Qué buscamos?
Pueblo Mágico es un espacio para el encuentro, la pausa y la regeneración.
 Nos inspira cocrear junto a la Madre Tierra nuevas formas de vivir, cuidarnos y hacer comunidad.
Creemos que la regeneración empieza en uno mismo y se expande hacia nuestras relaciones y modos de habitar el mundo.
 Por eso, estamos desarrollando una granja agroecológica basada en agricultura sintrópica y bosques comestibles, con la intención de avanzar hacia una forma de vida más simple y coherente con nuestros valores.

🌄 Programa de voluntariado
El Programa de Voluntades al Servicio invita a personas que deseen compartir, aprender y contribuir activamente a la regeneración de este espacio.
Buscamos generar intercambios significativos, donde cada persona aporte desde su experiencia y reciba aprendizaje, comunidad y conexión con la naturaleza.

 Las tareas varían según la época del año e incluyen:
* Actividades de huerta, siembra, compostaje, riego y recolección de semillas.
* Mante

name: NM - Mr. Noor M Babul, a high school teacher as well as an organic farmer. The farm is 140 km away from t
description:
Mr. Noor M Babul, a high school teacher as well as an organic farmer. The farm is 140 km away from the capital Dhaka and 40 km away from Bangladesh Agricultural University at Mymensingh. Very densely populated (4,500 /km²) village with warm hearted people. More than 100 farmers are doing organic farming especially in the homesteads. For field cultivation, they use traditional bull-plough. Pure and fresh milk from the domestic cows is available. One can try milking cows. Most of the farmers have large ponds for organic fish farming and aquaculture.

doing organic farming especially in the homesteads, Pure and fresh milk from the domestic cows is available.

Host family in the farm.

music, cooking,hiking

name: Louise - Family home and garden - rural
description: I would like to sponsor a Wwoofer to garden and experience planting a natural medicine garden. Â I am very interested in passing on my traditional knowledge in medicines. Â I also have arthiritus and need some help to move stuff around the yard so I need someone with a strong back.I am planting natural medicine and flower gardens around my house. Â This project will most likely last for 3 years. Â I plan on transplanting wild berry bushes by the lake in my last year. Â The Wwoofer will stay in a camper by the lake and will have lots of personal space. Â The wwoofer will be invited to participate in meal preparation. Â Special recipes are invited.

name: Freddy - Come to help us to grow sustainable economy in a native tribe in the Amazon.
description:
Translation in english below. 

Bonjour les ami(e)s, Je vis dans une communauté Shuar en Équateur, dans la province de Morona Santiago, près de Limon Indanza. Nous sommes au cœur de la forêt amazonienne, dans une forêt primaire où la sagesse est encore présente. Nous lançons des projets d'agroforesterie/permaculture afin de commercialiser des aliments amazoniens sains. Notre objectif est de construire un modèle économique en cohérence avec l'identité Shuar, dans le respect des valeurs ancestrales, sans détruire la nature. Ce que vous vivrez ici est un véritable échange interculturel.

Nous recherchons des personnes qui aiment vivre collectivement et dans des lieux éloignés de la ville. Pour venir ici, il faut savoir sortir de sa zone de confort. C'est une grande expérience d'apprentissage que de vivre dans une communauté indigène en  amazonien.  Nous sommes à 2h30 des premières commodités . Il faut aimer être proactif, travailler sur le terrain, apprendre sur les plantes et les systèmes d'agroforesterie/permaculture. Nous recherchons principalement des personnes pour s'occuper de la pépinière, de la plantation, du maintien des cultures en agroforesterie et d'autres travaux de construction.

Nous dormons dans un maison traditionnelle appelée « Jea » au milieu de la jungle. La douche est la meilleure au monde = une rivière pure avec de l'eau fraîche. Il y a des lits superposés à trois niveaux et la possibilité d'accrocher un hamac. Une cuisine bien équipée est disponible pour cuisiner. Un panneau solaire alimente la cuisine en électricité. Un mixeur et un extracteur de jus sont disponibles. Dans l'avenir, nous prévoyons d'installer un petit réfrigérateur pour le stockage d'aliments. Les repas sont généralement pris en commun avec les autres volontaires.

Pendant votre temps libre, vous pouvez profiter du territoire où je peux vous emmener pour de belles ballades, jouer avec les enfants, participer à la fabrication de poteries avec les femmes, faire de l'artisanat, jou

name: Antonella - Mini fattoria sul mare
description:
ZEN ○ nasce CON la passione PER L'autoproduzione e il piacere di avere contatto con la terra,abbiamo qualche animaletto,galline cani gatti .
Ci sono anche 2 adolescenti e una piccolina di 3 anni,oltre me.

Le attività quotidiane sono di vario genere: l'orto,la semina la raccolta,la trasformazione dei prodotti in conserve o pasti; raccolta delle olive e produzione di olio extravergine biologico destinato al consumo della mini fattoria; produzione di prodotti da forno a legna come pane,frise,focacce,pizza;
Chiedo aiuto con la cura dei piccoli animali domestici, collaborazione con la piccola Lara,con l 'orto e la produzione di prodotti da forno conserve,manutenzione degli spazi.

Tendenzialmente iniziamo a lavorare alle 8.30/9 fino alle 13.30/14 in base al piano della giornata e svolgimento delle attività necessarie, poi pranzo e tempo libero da gestire a piacimento tra mare citta e amici .

Le attività che propongo,in realtà,sono il mio hobby,fai del tuo hobby il tuo lavoro e non lavorerai un giorno della tua vita!!eheheh...è il mio motto;posso insegnare a riconoscere erbe spontanee e farne preparazioni,pasta fresca,pane pizza scolpire la pietra leccese e altro.
abbiamo la fortuna di vivere in un posto  dove escursionismo in boschi campagna o mare si possono tranquillamente fare anche in bici ,il salento  è una terra tutta da scoprire,nei luoghi e nella cultura.
---o----o----0----o----o---
ZEN ○ was born WITH a passion FOR self-production and the pleasure of having contact with the earth, we have a few pets, chickens, dogs, cats.
 There are also 2 teenagers and a little girl of 3 years old, besides me.

 The daily activities are of various kinds: the vegetable garden, sowing, harvesting, processing of products into preserves or meals;  olive harvest and production of organic extra virgin olive oil for consumption on the mini farm;  production of wood-fired oven products such as bread, frize, focaccia, pizza;
 I ask for help with the care of small pets, collaboration w

name: Clark and Barbara - Sustainable living and learning center in the high-desert forest of the Mt. Baldy foothills
description:
Lodestar Gardens, LLC is a demonstration site for small farm, high desert food and energy production located on 40 acres, 16 miles east of Show Low in Apache County.  Since 2000, Barbara & Clark Hockabout (along with dozens of farm volunteers and interns) have been experimenting with growing practices producing greater crop yield, perfecting more efficient energy production, implementing Permaculture and Biodynamic strategies, creating collaborations with other homesteaders, and offering educational opportunities to future homesteaders. Out of this experience the Lodestar Food Co-Op, established in 2011, continues to thrive; we are marketing produce to our local communities ; and we are establishing a Homesteading Apprenticeship Program (HAP).   In the past ten years, we have had six WWOOFers elect to stay a full year in the modestly paid capacity as Lodestar Farm Managers. All of them have gone on to find eco-careers all over the planet.

Our most recent experiments focus on small scale grain production for our animals, creating our own soil building amendments (bioreactors, biochar, biodynamic preps) and alternative building construction (steel container and straw bale).

We are within five hours of Phoenix, Tucson, Flagstaff, Sedona, Albuquerque, and Santa Fe. WWOOFers often spend their weekends exploring the geological, archeological, and historical sites nearby. We are off-grid; we use solar, wind and when necessary propane generator power sources. We have a ceramic studio that operates in the warmer weather. WWOOFers will help plant, cultivate, harvest and process produce for our  summer local market (May 31-Sept 30), food processing and preserving (canning, solar drying, salting, fermenting) and assist us in constructing a straw bale domicile. Housing for  WWOOFers varies according to a first come status and length of stay.  WWOOFers can be lodged in our Airstream trailer, (sleeps 2), the Lodestar Loft (sleeps 4), or our campground w/ water and toilet facilit

name: Anette - Organic smallholding by the sea
description:
We, a family of 4, have a small farm where we have sheeps, 2 horses of norwegian breed, henses, cats and 10 Alaska Huskies. We also have a big greenhouse and a kitchengarden. We're transmitting our farm into eco-certified now, but we have been practicing ecological principes for ceveral years.
We use solarpanels and bio-energy to heat up some buildings at the farm. We do a lot of forrest work, animal care, fencing and so on at our farm. We mostly eat vegetarian, but we sometimes eat fish and if we have voulenteers that eat meat we sometimes serve meat too.

We have been hosting voulenteers since 2018, and we have very good experience with hosting voulenteers from all over the world.
The voulenteers normally work 5 hours per day, 5 days a week. A normal day could be work from 9 - 13.30, and 30 minutes feeding animals in the evening. This depends on the time of the year and what we're working with. You normally start working the day after your arrival, work for 5 days and then have 2 days off.
We will help you to find activities for your days off if you want. If you love nature and hiking, there's a lot to do around here. You can also join us for our activities if you'd like. If you like city-life - our place is not for you since we live far away from the city.
You prepare and eat dinner with us in our house, and we provide for bread and toppings for your other meals that you can eat at your place. You have to bake bread yourself, we provide for flour and yeast. There is only one warm meal per day. If you'd like to have several warm meals you can of course buy ingredients yourself and make at your place.

As a hobby we have horses for rideing and dogs for dogmushing. If you're interested it's possible to learn about this in your sparetime while staying with us.

In winter you will live in a house or a cabin at our farm, togehter with other voulenteers. There is kitchen, bedrooms and bathrooms of good standard. In summertime you'll live in a caravan. There is toilets a
_wwoof_travel: Mosjøen. Take the bus to Leirfjord.

name: Catherine - 3-acre organic peony flower farm near breathtaking Homer, Alaska
description: Situated in rolling hills 14 miles from Homer, at 900 feet above sea level, in a farm neighborhood with fantastic views of volcanoes, glaciers, ocean inlet and bay, and fields of wildflowers, as well as wildlife, our farm offers adventure and opportunities for recreation as well as work.
