Our primary activity this summer/early fall includes working on a 3 acre organic peony field, including composting, weeding, and keeping our perennial peony plants healthy. We also maintain two small organic food and flower gardens, and will be cutting and putting up firewood. We are looking for 12 or more WWOOFers, couples welcome, but no pets or children, mid-May through end of September, two weeks plus, to work a flexible schedule 30 hours a week. Separate accommodations are provided: cabin or wall tent with amenities, in a private half acre area with sheltering trees, across a stream from the main house. We will also provide prepared breakfast, lunch, and dinner, and frequent transportation to Homer. Inexpensive bicycles can be purchased locally. French, Spanish, Bambara spoken here, Hindi and Chinese speakers welcome. We love the diversity of our Wwoofer community and encourage language learning. If you want to learn a language while you weed: free optional Chinese, Hindi, French or Spanish audio materials provided. Opportunities to learn spinning, weaving, felting and knitting. We are also part of a network of organic farms, including livestock farms with sheep, alpacas, yaks and other animals, and we make sure our woofers have opportunities to visit these farms as well, if they wish. This area is a highly sought after tourist destination for wilderness hiking, kayaking, canoeing, ocean and river fishing and beach walking. Many nearby attractions!  Including: the Anchor River, and Homer, with many local beach and forest trails, Kachemak Bay State Park, Kenai National Wildlife Refuge, Swan Lake and Swanson River Canoe Trails, and within a half day, Kenai Fjords National Park, S

name: Kalle - Alternative co-operative community, Family home and garden - rural
description:
In the middle of the small country side village of Uddebo lies a more than 100 year old factory called Väveriet. Since some years the factory is disused  and we (Liv, Kalle, Zam, Disa, Eyal, Camille among others) have made this factory our workplace and have set out to save the building from decay and fill the building with life and to new possibilities. 
There is something special going on in the village of Uddebo. The village is attracting young people from all over. Partly due to the many interesting and creative projects taking place, the diverse community and also due to cheap housing and all the indoor and outdoor space. 
In the weaving mill, we are turning the 5500 sqm factory building into a creative space for various activities such as a yoga studio, makerspace (or electronic playground), music and art studios, ceramic workshop and a whole bunch of other creative initiatives run by people from the village. 
This summer we will be doing the finishing touches on 2 art studios. There we will be building mosaic elements with clay and wood. We will paint walls and help set up a music and a farmers market event. 

We also started a cozy bakery in the building and Fridays we have pizza night with wood fired Neapolitan pizza. 
We have sheep who stay right next to the factory in the winter time and in the summer they are on other fields around the village. 

The accommodation would be in a separate room in the weaving mill and you will eat with the host family who also live in the building or in the big common kitchen. While you are here, during the week you would be involved in helping with various projects that happen at the weaving mill. Tasks here can vary greatly depending on what needs to be done, but it can be anything from garden work, painting windows, helping with the sheep to renovation and building maintanance. 
There is a lot to explore during your free time here. This summer, beside the weaving mill, new tiny houses are being built in different forms.
_wwoof_travel: From Gothenburg you can take bus 300 to Kinna. Change to bus 320 which takes you to Uddebo.  You can take the train to Limmared station where we can pick you up. Alternativet we can pick you up in Tranemo or Svenljunga.

name: Mark - 6500-acre grass-based beef ranch in South Dakota
description:
We are a working, grass-based beef ranch with a guest and hunting enterprise. We raise beef cattle, keep a dairy cow and chickens, and manage deer, elk, and bison as part of our hunting operation. At Sunrise Ranch, we welcome WWOOFers to work alongside us, learn, and experience life on the ranch.

Daily life here follows the rhythm of the animals and the seasons. Each day begins with the basics—making sure all animals have water and feed, and checking that everything is as it should be. Beyond that, no two days are the same. Some can be long and physically demanding, others are more relaxed, and there are almost always projects going on.

We generally spend most of our days working outside. In spring and summer, the focus is on livestock care—calving, fawns, and daily checks. In fall and winter, we run a significant hunting operation, and during that time Wwoofers have helped with everything from skinning and meat cutting to hospitality-related tasks such as cooking and cleaning. 
We’re constantly working to improve the ranch and make it more sustainable, which can include anything from bringing in new livestock and everything that comes with that, to irrigation work, building projects, and general repair and maintenance. Most Wwoofers find themselves learning plenty of new skills along the way. Handy skills are welcome but no professional experience is required—a good attitude, attention, and willingness to work and learn go a long way.

We use horses occasionally for cattle moves and leisure, but they are not part of the daily routine. It helps if WWOOFers have a drivers license, as it allows for more flexibility when working around our properties.

Outside of ranch work, we’re involved in the local community—my wife directs the community theatre, and depending on the time of year there may be opportunities to take part if that interests you. We don’t live fancy but we eat well. Between fresh eggs, milk, beef, lamb, pork, and chicken, nobody leaves here protein-de

name: Marley - Organic Family Garden Near Zion National Park Offering Heartfelt Hospitality
description:
Hello WWOOFERS! My name is Marley- I’m a 27-year-old yoga teacher & Mama to a little 1.5-year-old named Jasper.   Together, me, my husband, Ty, and our son tend to a growing garden and homestead.  We also have a little 12-lb cuddly pup named Pookie, soon-to-be 6 chickens, and hopefully a killer kitty property cat.

Each day, tending to the garden and the yard includes harvesting, planting, transplanting, weeding, feeding the chickens, turning the compost, and other random projects.  Inside, we are often drying, preserving, baking, cooking, pickling,  and using the harvest in as many ways possible!

We have a separate AirBnb portion of our home with its own private entrance, bathroom, kitchenette, and also access to the backyard and garden space.  We have a cold plunge/hot tub, sauna, and work out/yoga space in the garage.  For meals, we eat mostly home-cooked, organic, gluten free, and sugar free.  We would love to all share family dinners together ❤️

We love nature, hiking, camping, and going to the local river and hot springs.  I’m a yoga teacher and enjoy all related practices such as breathwork, meditation, and ecstatic dance.  My husband is a photographer and videographer with a niche in nature and conscious content.  We love traveling to wellness and yoga festivals as a family.  Nearby, there is SO much incredibly breathtaking nature, including iconic National Parks like Zion, the Grand Canyon, and Bryce Canyon.  Along with this, there are yoga studios, cafes, coffee shops, and all that good stuff! It’s a small town kinda vibe.  It is also a big place for mountain biking, rock climbing, boating, SUP, kayaking, canyoneering, ATVing- pretty much any outdoor activity- you name it, we’ve got it!
_wwoof_travel: St George Regional Airport, St George Shuttle Station (can take a shuttle here from Vegas/Harry Reid International Airport)

name: Luis Raul - Vineyard, Orchard, Mixed Farm
description:
Te invitamos a nuestra finca y bodega agroecológica para convivir compartiendo un mismo techo y trabajo. 
Por lo que refiere al techo, apuntamos a una convivencia de aprendizaje y de intercambio filosófico y cultural. La casa está preparada para recibirte con todas las comodidades (Cama, baño, agua caliente, cocina completa, WiFi). Además te esperamos con todas las costumbres Argentinas (¡Música, vino y amistad!) 
En cuanto al trabajo, requerimos entre 5 y 6 horas de tareas varias de huerta, campo y producción de alimentos y vino. 
No es necesario tener conocimientos previos ya que el aprendizaje será compañero nuestro todo el tiempo.

Estamos ubicados en Mendoza, San Rafael. Alejados a 25km del centro de la ciudad, en paraje Colonia Elena. 
La finca tiene 10 hectáreas totales donde se reparten el viñedo y una gran variedad de árboles frutales y verduras. 


Te leemos, te esperamos!!

Finca La Morera.

Certifications: No estoy de acuerdo con la certificación por institutos imperiales. Sin embargo, todos lo producido en la finca es orgánico y agroecológico.

Internet access available

name: Dom - Permaculture homestead in Central Portugal
description:
(( Now- harvest, food preservation, new seedlings - October : Olive harvest - from tree to Premium Olive Oil ))

Hello Wwoofers, my name is Dom and together with my partner I live on my homestead in Central Portugal.
We are living on our homestead at the very edge of a small hamlet, in the middle of an Olive Grove.
We left the busy life and jobs behind and came here to cultivate our own food. Coming as close as possible to Self-Sustainability is the goal. 
Come and experience another lifestyle. 

30 years permaculture experience. Medicine herb garden. Self sufficiency for delicious meals, Solar cooker . Visit Farmers Markets and our village  famous local wine cellars.We left the busy life and jobs behind and came here to cultivate our own food. Coming as close as possible to Self-Sustainability is the goal.
Come and experience another lifestyle.
Swimming lake in biking distance.
Every day in every season brings different tasks.  My work includes preparing seedlings, making beds, weeding etc. Composting. Harvesting Olives and pressing for oil. Pruning fruit trees. Planting trees. 
Permaculture is what you can learn here, I hold regular Permaculture Training Days, where you are welcome to attend.

name: Adam - Greenhaven is a 65-acre forest farm in the Comox Valley where craft, land, and community gather
description:
We are a multi-generational family farm and creative homestead located in the Comox Valley on Vancouver Island, Canada. Greenhaven is a 65-acre forest and pasture property where we are building a life rooted in land stewardship, skilled craft, seasonal food production, and meaningful community.

At the heart of the project is my mother, Eve Szoke, who will be the main liaison for WWOOFers. She is an avid gardener, cook, and poultry keeper, and tends much of the food production and daily rhythms of the farm.

I live here with my partner Jane, a baker, mother, artist, and jewelry maker. Together we are raising our four children — Aivi, Arjuna, Reyu, and Moise — who are growing up immersed in forest trails, gardens, and hands-on projects.

We share the land with a small extended community. Ryan, a timber framer and natural builder, lives here with his partner Larissa, an organizer and singer, and their new baby Wynter. Alex, an artist with an interest in tarot and astrology, lives here with his partner Carissa, a counsellor and writer. Zeno is an artisan, landscaper, and dedicated land steward who helps care for the property and ongoing projects.

The core of our project is to develop a regenerative farmstead that integrates food growing, poultry, orchards, natural building, woodworking, clay work, fermentation, and creative gathering. We are steadily building infrastructure — gardens, workshops, shelters, and community spaces — with a focus on real skills, shared work, and seasonal pace.

WWOOFers who join us become part of the daily life of a working homestead: tending gardens, supporting food preservation, helping with small building projects, caring for animals, and participating in the shared rhythm of land-based living.

Our farming activities follow the seasons and the real needs of the land, so no two days are exactly the same. We are a mixed forest and garden homestead, and tasks vary depending on time of year, weather, and individual skillsets.

Typical activ

name: Christine - Family home and garden - rural, fruit orchards, veggie beds & family pets / horses, dogs and cats.
description:
Our Projects for 2025.
We are needing help with removing dead and decaying trees from the Orchards, paddocks and our garden. 
Removing weeds that are not good for horses from their paddocks.
White washing the remaining trees with medicated lime-wash. Removing wild bushes from the orchard and paddocks.  

Also treating the wood structures and fences. 
Building fences.

 We live in a Remote and Rural location.  
Stunning views all around us.   Quite and in-sink with nature.   Although we are less than 45 mins from a city. 
Our local village is 7km down the hill.  Stolat is small and has Village life.  
Sarbolatesi is a small Hamlet above the main village situated on the hillside and is very rural.

Our home is made up of 2 parts.  The Old part is 95m of living space.  New part that is constructed but still being finished. 220 meters.  We are still building our dream home and are happy to share our dreams with you whilst you help us make it happen. 
We own 38.500 meters of farm land, woods and forest on a hillside
( Mixed fruit orchards : Cherry, Plum, Apples, Pears, Quince, Mulberry and others ).

We have a Rexton SUV  Old but works ( a Suzuki Soft Top but needs some work doing to her now) , A 7.5 tonne Iveco Horse Lorry, She's 31 yrs old and ... still runs.

We are located in a stunning location at the foothills of the Stara Planning mountains part of the Balkan mountain peninsula.  As our home is off the beaten track in the hills, you will have plenty of time to explore the natural beauty of this stunning area, so, If you love nature. deer, wild boar, foxes, badges etc you will love it here 

We want to share our experience of life outside the UK, our knowledge and care for horses and dogs and animals. We are always learning new skills this is where you as volunteers can help us too, this comes from sharing skills with volunteers who are happy to exchange knowledge and skills.

You can and should "Live your Dream" , Anything and everything is possible.

Our Location
_wwoof_travel: Sevlievo: Bus station 29km away, Gabrovo: Bus & train  36Km away. Veliko Tarnovo 1.5 hours: Bus & Train, Sofia 3.5 hours away Airport, Bus and Train.

name: Karlahof - Gemeinschaftliches Hofprojekt in der Uckermark
description:
Der Karlahof ist ein Gemeinschaftsprojekt in der Uckermark, auf dem mit kollektiver Subsistenz, nicht-kommerzieller Landwirtschaft und Formen eines gewaltarmen sozialen Miteinanders experimentiert wird.

Wir leben hier mit 13 Menschen zwischen 25 und 50 Jahren. Rund um unsere Hofstelle gibt es Grün- und Ackerland, auf dem hauptsächlich Getreide und Kartoffeln angebaut werden. Außerdem haben wir einen großen Garten mit Folientunnel, der uns ganzjährig mit Gemüse versorgt, eine Streuobstwiese, viele Beerensträucher und Waldstücke. Wir haben eine Verarbeitungsküche, eine kleine Imkerei und Werkstätten wie Tischlerei und Schlosserei.

Unser Zusammenleben dient nicht nur der gemeinsamen Organisation des Alltags. Es ist ein bewusster Versuch, die Kultur des sozialen Miteinanders, auch der damit verbundenen Konflikte, mit Mut und Sensibilität weiterzuentwickeln und inneres Wachstum zu ermöglichen. Insgesamt fühlen wir uns der Kultur der Tauschlogikfreiheit und der Commons verbunden.

Organic methods: Grundsätzlich humusaufbauende Anbaupraxis: Mischkultur, Fruchtfolge, Terra-Preta-Kompostierung, Mulchen, Gründüngung, Zwischen- und Untersaaten, Komposttee.

Certifications: EU-Bio

Gemüsebau (Ernten, Jäten, Pflege, Pflanzen)
Herstellung von Terra-Preta-Kompost
Obsternte
Brotbacken
Getreideernte 

Internet access available

name: Jenny - Agritourism Destination
description:
Located at Xom Moi hemlet, Bai Thom commune, Phu Quoc island, Kien Giang province, Vietnam.

Area of cultivation: 80 000 m2
Crops: pepper, fruits (banana, durian, pineapple, jackfruit, passion fruit, pomelo, etc...)
Animals: Pigs, dogs, chicken, cat, fish . . .
Development strategy: environmentally enhancing agriculture, supply organic agriculture products to our family and customers. 

Our missions:

·         "Say no" with chemicals and pesticides in permaculture.

·         Keep the traditional way to produce Phu Quoc pepper.

·         Self-supply and share our natural foods to everyone.

Number of volunteer can receive: 2 to 3 volunteers at the same time.
Working time: 6 hours/day and 6 days/ week.
Working period: 1 month minimum.
We cover accommondation, lunch, dinner and breakfast (coffee, tea, bread, eggs. but serve by yourself).

Phu Quoc Countryside is a farm located in rural Phu Quoc island, Vietnam, run by Jenny and her family. The farm is driven using unconventional methods with the goal of emotionally, economically, environmentally enhancing agriculture.
Phu Quoc Countryside operates a farm store on-site where customers go to pick up their products.
Activities: Volunteers can help us harvest fruit, pepper corn, growing trees, watering trees (if needed), will be a farm tour guide for visitors, explain to visitor how to grow and collect pepper corn.

Other requirements: love natute, farming, funny, sociable and enthusiastic.

Organic methods: We don't use chemical fertilizers or chemical pesticides for all plants and trees.

We use many kind of ants and they will help us to eat worms or other insects.

Certifications: We can't register organic farm for single farm (only our farm). Have to organize a group with many farm around and before making registering.

Current projects: Growing fruits trees, vegetable and herb. Those will be served for visitor in next future. Making lemongrass oil.

name: Krisztina - Family home and garden - rural
description:
We are a family of 4 with two daughters.. I am from Hungary, my partner is from Bulgaria. We have a small organic farm with garden and free range animals, chickens, ducks, dogs, cats and a very friendly rescue donkey and also bees. I enjoy to work with medicinal herbs, making creams from them using our own bee wax and herbs from our garden or make teas. My partner likes to work with wood and other natural materials. We live in a quite, authentic village 30 km from the Black Sea and Varna. Before we settled down here I was travelling a lot and now I would be happy to be the host of travellers who could help us to grow this small farm into a paradise for humans and animals.
We would gladly share our knowledge on organic farming and keeping of animals or working with natural materials just as much as we would be happy to learn new things from the volunteers.
We started our project not a very long time ago, so we have a lots of things which has to be done. We built a beautiful house for the chickens and to the donkey too, made our house livable and recently we got a washing machine too, yuhuuuu! Our garden is amazing this year, we have lots of fruits and vegetable and even though we had many fruit trees already we planted some more. But there is still a lot what has to be done,  for example we have to change the roof of the barn and we can not do that without some help. So if you want to join us on this journey of learning new things every day about nature and about how to live in it and if you enjoy to work in the garden and with animals and renovate we would be very happy to have you here with us :)

Certifications: We don't sell many products so we never went for being certified. But we don't use any chemicals, we try to make a ballance between plants and animals to keep our garden healthy or we use natural products.

Current projects: Renovating the barn. Planting in the garden. Prepare the nut garden for the chickens.

Internet access available

name: Jacob - Pratiques agricoles amazoniennes. Association développant des parcelles agroforestières.
description:
Vous participerez à un projet associatif visant à mettre en pratique une agriculture amazonienne et des systèmes en agroforesterie (SAF). 
Nous développons plusieurs parcelles en cultures associées et stratifiées (arbres fruitiers, ananas, canne à sucre, banane, tubercules,...) ainsi qu'une pépinière. Plusieurs projets agricoles sont en cours en partenariat avec des organismes de recherche  agronomique pour un développement durable de l'agriculture.

Basé en Guyane, à Saint Georges de l'Oyapock, ville frontalier avec le Brésil, le Centre Panakuh, possède un jardin fruitier d'un hectare ainsi que de deux hectares de parcelles agroforestières. 
Le site est composé de plusieurs carbets (hébergement hamac, cuisine, sanitaires, espaces de travail) et d'installations structurantes, un chalet bois. Vous avez accès à l'électricité et du wifi via une connexion Starlink.

L'objectif principal des productions est de permettre l'autosuffisance végétale de l'association en vue d'un accueil agro-touristique et de vente occasionnelle (préparation de repas, vente en circuit court...).
L'agriculture pratiquée se veut traditionnelle, donc reposant sur des pratiques ancestrales amazoniennes :
un approche holistique et durable sans intrants chimiques ni produits phytosanitaires. Utilisation de terra preta (terre noire contenant du biochar), d'engrais organique, paillage...

Le Centre est facile d'accès en bordure de la RN2 sur la berge de la rivière Gabaret, affluent de l'Oyapock. L'Oyapock est un fleuve transfrontalier qui relie la France et le Brésil entre la Guyane-Française et l'Etat de l'Amapa brésilien.
Vous y serez chaleureusement accueillis par Jacob et Tania, et vous pourrez aussi rencontrer toute l'équipe composée ponctuellement de stagiaires en agriculture tropicale, de volontaires en mission service civique et de bénévoles.

Vous pourrez travailler le matin, avec des activités variées (entretien des cultures, récoltes, plantation, fabrication de compost, de terra prêta, pai
_wwoof_travel: Aéroport International de Cayenne - Félix Éboué

name: Wasonga - A family-run organic farm offering hands-on  learning in poultry, gardening, and beekeeping.
description:
Welcome to Eden Agro-Ventures Farm!
Nestled on 20 hectares of scenic countryside, Eden Agro-Ventures is a family-run farm dedicated to sustainable agriculture and organic living. Certified organic since 2023, we believe in growing food naturally, caring for animals ethically, and living simply in harmony with the land.

Our location is peaceful and remote—far from towns and public transport—making it the perfect place to disconnect from the noise of city life and reconnect with nature. Wildlife sightings, starry nights, and fresh air come standard.

Our Farm Focus
We specialize in:

Large-scale poultry production (layers and broilers)

Horticulture (seasonal vegetables, fruits, herbs)

Beekeeping (seasonal honey and pollination support)

We also raise:

Rabbits (2–5, with young ones seasonally)

Sheep (15)

We cultivate both open-field crops and maintain a vibrant orchard and kitchen garden.

What WWOOFers Can Learn
Volunteers are welcome to join in and learn through hands-on participation in areas such as:

Poultry management (feeding, egg collection, cleaning)

Organic gardening and crop care

Beekeeping basics and honey extraction (seasonal)

Composting and soil building

Harvesting and preserving food

Basic maintenance and fencing work

We encourage cultural exchange, shared learning, and curiosity. No prior farming experience is required—just motivation, respect, and a willingness to get involved.

Accommodation & Daily Life
WWOOFers stay in a comfortable room on the farm. We share meals together as a family, including our two lively boys, so expect a warm, sometimes noisy, but always welcoming atmosphere.
You’re invited to help with simple household tasks like cooking or cleaning up after meals—it’s part of the shared experience.

Schedule & Expectations

5 hours per day, 5 days a week

2 days off per week for rest or local exploration

Flexibility depending on weather and farm priorities

What to Bring: sturdy shoes, work clothes, sun/rain protection, and a
_wwoof_travel: Nearest train station is in kisumu,bus stopin homa bay, air strip kabunde, kisumu international airport, airstrip kabunde in homa bay, and official bus stations in homabay

name: Aura - Eco Retreat
description:
Nos encontramos a 90 minutos en auto de Bogotá, sobre el camino real Zipacón y el Ocaso, colindando con el Río Apulo.
Hace 8 años decidimos venir a vivir en la montaña, sembrar nuestro alimento, respirar profundo y activar la vida consciente, cuidamos las semillas nativas, protegemos las abejas del territorio 6 especies, cultivamos  la tierra, ahora queremos compartir aprendizajes y recibir ayuda. Son muchas las tareas en un bosque comestible, cuidamos un micro lote de café especial de diferentes variedades Arábigas, Borbón, Caturra, Geisha, con sus procesos artesanales y orgánicos. Cada día una experiencia y aventura, diferentes.
Estamos emocionados por los nuevos aprendizajes, ¡bienvenidos!

Métodos Orgánicos:
- Permacultura y agroecología

Certificaciones: 
- Coexistimos con la naturaleza y todos sus habitantes, aplicamos abonos de la misma tierra y  procuramos respetar los procesos naturales, no hacemos control biológico, ni fumigamos.

Proyectos Actuales: 
- Aprender idiomas y culturas
- Mantenimiento de bosque comestible
- Semilleros, siembras, podas, abonos
- Procesos ruta del café
- Construcción de carpintería

Estamos trabajando en establecer una escuela informal para niños locales, donde puedan venir unas horas a la semana y aprender de nuestros voluntarios internacionales. De esa manera, esperamos aumentar el nivel de intercambio de conocimiento, también para fortalecer a nuestra comunidad. 

Alojamiento:
- Tenemos una pequeña tiny house donde duerme cómodamente 1 persona, o una pareja que se pueda ajustar al espacio pequeño. Además hay un cuarto privado con cama y escritorio.  Si en ocasiones hay más gente también tenemos una carpa bajo techo sobre piso de cemento. 
-  Buen acceso al Internet
- Agua potable
- Cocina al aire libre, con estufa de gas y estufa-horno de leña
- Tenemos un kiosko al aire libre donde compartimos comidas y actividades 

Comida: 
- Nos alimentamos lo más que podamos con alimentos, frutas, y vegetales del bosque comestible que ten
_wwoof_travel: Terminal El Salitre en Bogotá : bus a Cachipay, Cundinamarca con compañia Expreso Del Sol aprox $40k COP - varios veces al día, viaje 2h30m. El aeropuerto más cercano es El Dorado de Bogotá. O yo te puedo contactar un taxi privado: aprox $160k COP.

name: Shannyn - Homestead and family farm in the high plains of NM
description:
** We are looking for extra help for next years 2026 farmers market season.  Come play in the garden with me (Shannyn)! 
We are a small homestead/family farm located in the high plains of New Mexico.  We are starting our farm from scratch, helping turn raw land into healthy soil while building our own fun tiny house from RV's that we have collected.  Our gardens are still in the trial stages as we learn what varieties work best for our climate and practices, we love producing as much as we can from our farm, including drinks, herbs, and  medicines, along with our food. We are currently raising pigs, chickens,, and rabbits for meat and have two milk cows for fresh milk and cheese.

We garden organically using a small solar powered heated and cooled greenhouse and raised beds, utilizing covered row tunnels to help lengthen our short and somewhat tempermental growing season. We have several ongoing building projects, most that are necessary for farm functions such as barns and pasture fences. But some are just for fun! We try to use recycled products and have been able to successfully reuse items that others have discarded, such as in our horse barn and training ring, where we have utilized tires in the design and sheds built from the wood of old barns.  Our current major projects are building a wash/pack shed, new pig pens, a chicken egg mobile, fencing, a cool circular earthbag outhouse and shower (it's almost finished!), and remodeling the Dome, including plastering the exterior with stucco.  We are also growing native fruit trees, bushes, and grasses for windbreaks and hedging.We are completely off grid and utilize solar power and a wind generator.  We haul all of our water and setup rainwater harvesting setups on all buildings possible.  We are always needing strong, motivated, self starting, imaginative, creative people to help in the design and creation of projects.  It is rough country here and at over 7500 ft in elevation one must be able to adapt and adjust q

name: Vanessa - Ferme de 5 ha au coeur de la forêt amazonienne.
description:
Bonjour chers(es) amis(es) woofeurs(es), 
La Ferme biologique Bonne Semence a vu le jour en 2013.  Nous exploitons 5 Ha de terre avec une zone de maraîchage et une autre d'arboriculture. La forêt primaire exubérante et majestueuse qui nous environne fait de ce site un lieu particulièrement ressourçant, avec une faune et une flore encore préservée et toujours très surprenante ! 
Nous serions heureux de partager ce lieu accueillant et nos savoir-faire avec vous.
Lieu idéal pour reprendre confiance en soi, en l'autre et en la Terre Mère.

Les activités de la ferme sont très variées, mais nous retiendrons les principales qui sont 
l'entretien des cultures maraichères, des arbres fruitiers, 
le lancement de nouvelles cultures, 
le soin des animaux, 
la récolte de semence de  fruits, de légumes, de graminées... 
Respirer...être...simplement!

 Au plaisir de vous rencontrer,

Vanessa B

name: Benjamin - Organic and Regenerative Farm
description:
Sou Engenheiro Agrônomo e possuo uma propriedade rural com foco na produção orgânica de grãos (soja, milho, feijão, girassol e trigo mourisco) para comercialização, com certificação orgânica. Também sou professor de Agronomia, com ênfase em agroecologia e, pesquisador na área de solos e microbiologia agrícola. Assim, tento aliar a ciência e a prática agrícola, visando viabilizar a agricultura orgânica em escala na região da campanha do Rio Grande do Sul. Também trabalho com produção de ovos e hortaliças para consumo próprio. As áreas são manejadas com semeadura direta, rotação de culturas, plantas de cobertura e insumos biológicos visando aumentar a atividade biológica do solo. 

Organic methods: Controle biológico inoculativo e conservativo; semeadura direta, rotação de culturas, plantas de cobertura; Produção de insumos "On farm".

Certifications: certificação orgânica desde 2022.

Current projects: cultivo de  plantas de cobertura de inverno, cultivo de soja, milho, feijão e girassol e trigo mourisco orgânicos.


Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Caçapava do Sul, Santa Maria, Porto Alegre

name: Hannah - Organische Agroforstwirtschaft im dörflichen Westuganda
description:
Hallo! Wir sind Hannah und William, zwei Freunde, die im Herzen des ländlichen Westuganda arbeiten. Hier haben wir SLAC gegründet, eine gemeinnützige Organisation, die sich der Unterstützung von Kleinbauern durch Agroforstwirtschaft und nachhaltige Landwirtschaft widmet. Das Herzstück unseres Projekts ist unser beeindruckender Demonstrationswald in Kitengeto, Buseesa, in dem wir über 70 tropische Baumarten pflegen und zusätzlich Bananen, Gemüse und Gewürze anbauen. Diese Gewürze werden zu fair gehandelten ätherischen Ölen verarbeitet, die ein Stück unserer Farm in die Welt bringen! Außerdem züchten wir Hühner, Kaninchen, Bienen und Fische – und schaffen so ein wunderbares, kleines, sich selbst tragendes Ökosystem.

Wir laden dich ein, mit uns in dieses Abenteuer einzutauchen! Unterstütze unser kleines Team bei Aufgaben wie dem Schneiden von Bananenstauden, dem Umtopfen von Setzlingen, dem Pflanzen von frischem Gemüse und vielem mehr. Du wirst mitten im Geschehen sein und zu etwas wirklich Wertvollem beitragen. Deine Unterkunft befindet sich direkt auf dem Gelände, in einer sicheren und einladenden Umgebung, die mit WLAN, Solarstrom und unseren treuen (und sehr niedlichen) Wachhunden ausgestattet ist, die dich jederzeit beschützen.

Unsere Farm liegt in einer der abgelegensten Regionen Ugandas, wo das Leben einfach ist und Armut weit verbreitet. Wir haben uns deswegen ebenfalls für diesen einfachen Lebensstil entschieden: Wir duschen mit kaltem Wasser und nutzen Latrinen statt westlicher Toiletten – aber wir finden, das gehört zur authentischen Erfahrung dazu! Wir kochen unsere Mahlzeiten gemeinsam und genießen lokale Spezialitäten wie Posho, Matooke, Bohnen und frische Produkte aus dem Garten. Wenn du also nach einem echten, unvergesslichen Abenteuer abseits der üblichen Touristenpfade suchst, bist du hier genau richtig!

Während Hannah momentan nicht vor Ort ist, wird William dich mitnehmen und dir die atemberaubende Schönheit des westlichen Ugandas zeigen. Er führ

name: Hamid - Come help us to build our guest house family in Tagounite and discover berber culture
description:
Hello everybody! 
My name is Hamid i'm 29 yers old 
we are family berber live in south morocco in small village call Tagounite .my parents siblings moving from pasture to pasture in the vast desert, The knowledge of the berbercultur has been passed down to me. Attached to this harsh and alluring land, I would love to make your journey a haunting and magic experience desert life.
I live in Tagounite, and i start with my family home build guesthouse located in the palmtree garden area which is inviting you to take wounderful excursions between dates and pomegranates where people can experience desert life.
and we say welcome to anyone want to help us and we say welcome and serve people from all over the world..our interest is to share what we have with other..we are open minded and interested in exchanging ideas and experiences desert life

I am looking for volunteers who can help with general building, maintenence, gardening etc. keep the animal and help my familly in house.
You will cook with us, go on hikes, explore the area and party in our traditional way.

And we wish to exchange help at any thing and exchange our cultures with anyone from all the world

just contact us we will be glade to host you

We eat breakfast and lunch and dinner and w can have eting befor that or after

tagounite is small village in souht morocco in door the desert.Families are abandoning the nomadic lifestyle for various reasons. The borders being closed has blocked much of the trans-Saharan trade. Many nomadic families were forced to leave the farms to grow date palms and tagounite is now the home of a new settled way of life.I always wanted to have contact with other persons from other cultures. I now have many friends from all over the world , and I still want to get in touch with others who have not yet experience the desert life.
if you want to experience a totally different culture and different people you can take some time out of your life to forget the modern life and visit us.
_wwoof_travel: From the bus station and the taxi station , in thé village

name: Emilia - Vegetable little farm with horses
description:
1. Proyect has different activities.. Working on vegetable garden. Construct dome - bío construcción. Working in art and craft shop.  This year  we gonna also start  to make a green house. Fixing outdoor stuff. Maintenance. 
On a site live ar the moment 3 person. 10 dogs and 2 horses. 
We are ubicatated in open space clóse to Puerto Natales


2. Planting. Weeding. Protecting plants. Construccion. Bio- construcción. Maintrnance of fences and whatever comes by. (Taking care of 2 horses , 10 dogs,) Kitchen. Cooking.  
Working with wood..Using tools. 


3. Our Kitchen is all home made. Evererybody will take turns in
the kitchen ( 3 meals)
Accomodation - at the moment rustic acomodation un our construcción of the dome. Would be nice to bring a sleeping bag. If not we can organize some. 
No electticity, no shower. 
We go showering in the public shower in the swimming pool where we practice swimmking 4 times a week. 


4. Day of the participantes will vary of the needs of the day or weather. This year we will focus on construcción ( bio construcción, and green house) and lots of other work like gardening,  watering plants, weeding etc...


5. Our hobbies are: swimming, beeing outside. Horsebackridding  Playing with dogs .Arts and crafts. Cookng at home. Working in the garden.
Constructing.

name: Kelly - 21-acre farm focused on eco-conservatism, social liberalism, welcoming diversity, & kindness
description:
Open April 1st-October 15th / Closed Oct 16-Mar 31

VISION STATEMENT:
We strive to treat each other with kindness and respect, welcoming diversity in age, race, spiritual beliefs and sexual preferences while holding values of eco-conservatism and social liberalism paramount. 

We honor and take responsibility for stewarding the ecosystem and wildlife we cohabitate within and with, designing our impact on this ecosystem in the most harmonious way possible, with resilience and regeneration the ultimate goal.

We share resources and governance in a spirit of mutual respect and cooperation. We honor each other’s needs and autonomy by using healthy communication practices for deeper understanding and connection.

************************************************************************************************************************************
WHAT TO EXPECT 2026 SEASON:

This position is probably best suited for an individual who wants to live in Asheville for the summer and potentially work a side job if income is needed.  I am looking for more of a caretaker/assistant wwoofer this year in exchange for a place to live.  I'm not offering food/meals this year as I have several projects not farm related underway that will take a lot of my time. The camper accommodation for wwoofers is equipped with a full kitchen, and you're welcome to work your own vegetable patch if desired.  

I'm also expecting to have surgery on my left wrist that I broke last June and it hasn't healed properly.  So I will be out of working-commission for several months of recovery, and as of now, in March, I am tentatively scheduled for July surgery.
 I need an experienced wwoofer who is skilled, self directed and dependable.  It will probably be less of a learning experience than usual as I'm scaling back my herbal production this year, although there will still be some harvesting and processing of perennial medicinals for sure.   I will spend time with you teaching you how to operate equipment, an

name: Felicity - Organic vegetable gardens, & perennial flower beds at my home in beautiful  Elora
description:
I live in a 200 year-old century home in Elora Ontario.  I am an active female senior, unable to do all the physical upkeep it takes to look after the organic gardens, flower beds and property.

I love my home and spend most of my day outside. I need help any time in the months of April, May, June, July, August and October. The main project  is to care for my organic raised bed gardens and perennial flower beads.  I also need help with odd tasks around the property like painting. Each morning I would plan out a few hours work that I would need you to help with. 

Elora is a very beautiful and popular destination for people to visit. There are nature trails and parks around the Elora Gorge. Also unique and artsy shops to visit. Many artist  live here and sell their art, glass, wood carvings, and paintings in the shops. Everything is in walking distance from my home. There are lots of places to explore and lots of opportunity to meet other people.

I have a private room available for you with a comfortable single bed. 

I am pretty good at cooking up all kinds of meals. Homemade soups and stews, egg dishes, chicken dishes, salads, pasta, casseroles, pizza, burgers on the BBQ, the list goes on.......

WWOOF asked about my hobbies....Well,  life is my hobby. I like to make things and be self-sufficient. I make kombucha, tinctures, I dry herbs for teas, I distil my own water, I grow sprouts and indoor plants,  I sew and do alterations. I have my own line of natural creams etc, I use to teach breath work and meditation. I also work with energy and do energy healing.  I spend a lot of time working with my rife and scaler  frequency machines. Life is good :-)
_wwoof_travel: The nearest train station is in Guelph Ontario and the nearest airport is in Kitchener Ontario

name: Gill - House and garden, allotment group, campsite
description:
We live on the site of an old Nursery since 2020 and run a Greener Camping Club affiliated campsite on the land. We have 3 large greenhouses which are used to grow our own food, by members of our growers group and by some in the local community.  We have some no dig veg beds and continue to learn about permaculture principles which we hope will guide our growing and guardianship of the land. We have chickens for eggs, some new ducks hopefully for slug control ,and a hive which is primarily for pollination. We may take a little honey if there enough. This year we are starting to plant up a forest garden.
Our focus is an environmental one, We are aiming towards making ourselves resilient to the worst of climate change and hope to encourage others to do the same. Adi registered the country's first electric taxi to run on renewable energy,  and ran a bus company on sustainably sourced vegetable oil. 
We have a large solar system so the house and the campsite are off grid.

Organic methods: chemical free growing.

Certifications: not ready to be certified

Current projects: Growing veg and flowers. Greenhouse maintenance. Hens. Bees. 

Internet access available

name: Maria - Ferme biologique
description:
Hello fellow Wwoofers ;) 

Alain, my French husband, speaks only French and comes from a 300 year old French farming family. I, Maria (Mexican-American, Mexico City and Oregon, USA), speak English, Spanish, French, Italian, and Portuguese. Maria's vegetarian, meditates and practices yoga daily. We're music lovers and have 2 pianos, 4 guitars, many drums, pan flutes, ukuleles, etc. and love having great jam sessions around a campfire. We live in a beautiful 60 hectare circa 1735 farm house with one kilometer of small river front, in Normandy, France. We need help renovating two buildings that will become a music hall/jam sessions and a wellness center in this beautiful friendly region: time permitting, help with the orchard garden too. Finishing up the wellness center and then music hall are our priorities.

We live as naturally as possible. We give thought to the products we purchase and the food we eat.  We make conscious efforts to minimize the impact on our precious resources and buy as locally as we can. We enjoy playing guitar, piano and singing, sharing stories around a campfire, card and table games after dinner, etc.  We both share interests in a greener lifestyle, and enjoy learning and sharing our knowledge. Anything from sustainable native building materials to growing our own food in the garden. Knowledge and know-how are key, education to make informed choices. In the end it's up to all of us, and now more than ever, we feel a need to get back to the basics, find our roots, and plant new seeds. If we can help each other grow, we look forward to sharing our time.

Bonjour,

Alain, mon marie français, ne parle que français et vient d'une vieille famille d'agriculteurs français depuis 300 ans. Moi, Maria (américano-mexicaine, Mexico et Oregon, USA), je parle anglais, espagnol, français, italien et portugais, végétarienne, médite et pratique le yoga quotidiennement. 

Nous sommes amoureux de la musique et avons 2 pianos, 4 guitares, de nombreux tambours, de
_wwoof_travel: Gare de Flers , train station, then bus to Domfront

name: Ira - New small organic market garden
description:
Hello! My name is Ira, and I'm from Helsinki, Finland. I am starting a new market garden on an estate in Southern Finland, and am looking for 1 or 2 WOOFers to come and help me out with the first season on the farm! The farm will be a small (0.3 ha) market garden with over 100 varieties of vegetables, herbs and flowers, with a focus on heirloom varieties. The goal is to create a beautiful showcase of what small-scale, diverse, organic agriculture can look like - the focus is on rare varieties, growing for taste and biodiversity. We will be growing mainly for 3 customers: the manor house where the farm is located, a zero-waste restaurant with a green Michelin star in Helsinki, and consumers in downtown Helsinki. The farm will also act as a setting for education on the food system and biodiversity that we will be organising for companies and schools. I will be growing fully organically (not certified) with a focus on soil health and diversity. I'm trialling a lot of approaches from korean natural farming, and aiming for a fully closed nutrient cycle.

From late April to Late May we will be mostly sowing and transplanting, as well as making the  beds. August to October will be peak harvest season, so the work will be mostly harvesting and helping out with the wash/pack. You will learn (with me!) about how to lay out the beds, how to transplant, how to build compost piles as well as how to make biofertilizers from locally available resources.

I live in Helsinki, about 25 minutes away from the farm. You will be living in my summerhouse on an island about 15 minutes away from the farm, and you will have our cottage by the sea all to yourself. I will provide you with a rowing boat (it's 5 minutes accross, and I will show you how to do it) and a car (you need a driving licence!) for commuting to the farm, and obviously show you how everything works.  

I'm hoping this will be a really nice experience where you can spend your days on the farm with me, but enjoy the amazing

name: Claudia - Proyecto de Permacultura en Vélez Blanco, Almería.
description:
La Asociación para el Desarrollo de la Permacultura Los Vélez es una organización sin ánimo de lucro que nace en 2018 y tiene como fin último trabajar por una cultura sostenible y saludable poniendo en práctica los principios y técnicas de la Permacultura. 

Algunos de nuestros objetivos son la soberanía y autosuficiencia energética y alimentaria, la educación para el desarrollo, el autoempleo, la revitalización del mundo rural, la promoción del turismo sostenible, la participación social, la revalorización del patrimonio cultural y natural o la promoción de la economía alternativa. 

Para ello, hemos diseñado una Finca Demostrativa (Finca Quevedo) de una hectárea en el Parque Natural Sierra María-Los Vélez (Almería), gestionando de forma sostenible los recursos naturales a través del manejo holístico del terreno. Dentro de esta finca, la Asociación desarrolla programas de educación ambiental, bioconstrucción, compostaje, gestión de aguas, agricultura regenerativa, ecoturismo o voluntariado. 

Las personas que vengan a participar con nosotras convivirán con una pareja (Helena y Clau), un bebé y tres perras (Nala, Meiga y Huma). Las actividades que se realizarán con lxs WWOOFers se centrarán principalmente en el mantenimiento y cuidado de las distintas zonas de la finca (huerto, bosque comestible, olivar), así como la participación en las actividades concretas que se realicen en ese momento (siembra, poda, biochar, riego, bioconstrucción, cosechas, etc.), incluso en algún momento puntual podrían participar en el desarrollo de nuevos diseños o tareas de bricolaje. 
Disponemos además, de mucho material bibliográfico para aquellas personas que estén interesadas en el estudio y aprendizaje sobre diseño en permacultura.

Ofreceremos alojamiento en la finca en una cabaña para 2 personas, con agua caliente, cocina y baño y garantizamos los alimentos básicos necesarios para realizar tres comidas diarias. El trabajo se concentrará generalmente en 6h diarias de lunes a viernes

name: Christine - Family home and garden - rural,Dogs,Cats Horses, Nature, near lake.
description:
Come stay on the farm with us!  We live on 160 acres on the north end of Lake Dauphin In the prairies of Manitoba.  We could use help to look after our three horses and two outdoor dogs and three inside cats and one steer.  Some help with our garden and yard would be nice too.  I also do baking for markets, so there's times I can use some help with that as well.  And I need some taste testers!  We travel to different communities on weekends for the markets.  We are currently looking for wwoofers in 2026.  

In summer,  you'd work outdoors most mornings while it’s cooler, before it gets hot in the afternoon.  There is some gardening, some yard work, or looking after the animals, mostly the horses, cats or dogs.  
In winter, we get snow quite often, so keeping the walks and steps cleaned off would be helpful.  Also making sure the birds have feed.  You would have chances to ride a snow machine.  

If you like peace and quiet country life,  can do tasks alone without constant supervision, want to learn a little what country life is like, we'd be  happy to host you! We are semi retired but still live on our farm.  

We are Located on the north end of Lake Dauphin, a lovely scenic area, lots of wildlife, private lakeshore a mile away, for swimming, and bonfires, in our yard.  You can do other things in spare time, bikes, walks, (skating in winter), read, study, enjoy our incredible prairie sunrises and sunsets and, of course, the wide open spaces!!

Accommodation for wwoofers is in our spare bedroom main floor, that has two beds in it.  We do have another bed in the Semi-finished downstairs of our home, (well insulated, and warm).  This could be used if there is a Wwoofer overlap sometimes.  In summer, we can use our RV trailer as well.    Food is provided by us, and everyone helps themselves for breakfast and sometimes lunch too, but the big meal is in the evening, and I usually make it.  We do eat meat here.  Wwoofers can buy themselves specialized foods if they choose
_wwoof_travel:
Train is via rail Winnipeg to Dauphin, Manitoba. 
Bus is Kasper bus lines from Winnipeg to Dauphin Mb. More reliable to take bus as it’s always on time.

name: Nikki - Remote Smallholding & Horse-riding Adventures in West Scotland
description:
Hiya, my wife Cara and I have a small farm of about 100 acres at the far end of the beautiful Kintyre Peninsula that hangs off the far west of Scotland.  It is quiet and remote, about 2 miles from some golden beaches and surrounded by hills and forest. Our stone farmhouse is 170 years old and runs mostly on solar energy with a wood burning stove. Together we run horse-riding adventures with our characterful herd, and manage our own, self-sufficient small-holding with a few pigs, a wee flock of about 30 colourful Shetland Sheep, a few chickens, the dogs (Kiyo and Yeva) and three friendly farm cats.  We are looking for some help feeding and caring for the animals, growing produce for the farm, helping in the house and general activities around the farm.

We start in the morning, bringing in the horses, feeding the animals and getting everything ready for the day.  Jobs include helping in the garden with growing vegetables, animal feeding and welfare, tree planting, keeping the house clean and tidy and helping with the horses. If there are rides going out you may be able to join some of these depending on your ability.  We would love to show you our tree project on the farm, creating new woodland areas with native trees, horse dung and recycled tubes to restore the river banks and create wildlife habitats.  There are sometimes small building projects such as chicken housing and fence repairs.  In the farm we might bake bread, make butter and try and be as self-sufficient as we can be.

You will stay in the farmhouse in one of the two volunteer bedrooms that we have, with a shared bathroom.  All food is provided. Sometimes you will eat with us altogether and other times use the kitchen to cook for yourselves with food provided (you just need to let us know what you like to cook and any dietary requirements).

We are really interested in self-sufficiency, off-grid living and cooking from basics.  In the evenings we may listen to music, knit and read.  We are also fascina

name: JUKKA PEKKA - Organic farm in the South Lapland
description:
Hello WOOFERS. My name is Jukka, and I am a farmer in South Lapland. I live alone with my cute, cuddly cat! This farm has been in use since 1670, and I've been dairy farming for 36 years. At our operational peak, we had 20 cows for milking, now however, I only look after young calves and heifers.

I grow barley, hay, and pasture feed. I also take care of the young heifers and calves. I maintain Finnish heritage biotopes on my farm. 

I can teach you how to care for Finnish nature and our natural heritage biotopes, I can also teach you our local history and how to engage and look after an organic farm.

I have a bed for you in a private room in a warm Finnish wooden house. I cook simple, hearty Finnish food :)

I like hiking, skiing and swimming in our local river, really many kinds of sports.
I also like reading and music.

Nearby in the summertime, there are some hikes in the area, and it's good for fishing, kayaking, and water sports on the river. If you love cycling too, we have the Eurovelo 11 going right past our doorstep, going right up to Nordkapp, Norway! I've hosted many cyclists for a short period as a rest stop on their way.

name: Frangiskos - Agritourism Destination, Farm
description:
Eumelia, which means harmony, is a unique sustainable development and agrotourism accommodation that also runs workshops and events for wellbeing, agricultural activities, creative projects and gastronomy. Our organic farm is designed along the principles of Homeodynamic / Biodynamic agriculture, and our accommodation facilities are created using entirely eco-friendly materials and technology, such as recycling, compost, geothermic heating/cooling and water recycling.

We have organic vegetable gardens (1.500 sq. m.) with raised beds, olive grove (600 – 900 trees), almond grove (200 trees), vineyard (3.000 sq. m.) and another 50-100 fruit trees. We also have about 50 animals: chicken, geese, turkeys and rabbits, sheep (2) and cats and dogs.

Our production is partly for our own consumption, our visitors and some for exportation.

Located in the prefecture of Laconia near the village of Gouves, we welcome visitors from Greece and around the world to visit us throughout the year.

The team is comprised of 4 persons: a caretaker couple living here all year round, myself and another woman working with us.

Specific helping and/or living rules

Clean, organised, no heavy drinking, no drugs, respectful, follows directions, initiative, can work well with others. There is vegetarian option for food.

Months Needed: All year around

Organic methods: Organic farming

Certifications: DIO

Type of work: September - October: We have to prepare our vegetable gardens and plant the new season's vegetables. We also wish to build a small pond for our chickens and ducks. And we have to prepare the farm for the olive picking, cut the grass, pick the old branches etc.

November- December: Olive picking. The olive picking is an intensive and hard work but yet very interesting. We have the chance to experience the harvest of the olives for olive oil and also of the Kalamata edible olives. We visit the olive mil together and we try the freshly pressed olive oil. The volunteers also can par
_wwoof_travel:
We are situated in an amazing landscape, with view of Mount Parnonas & Taygetos, near the sea (15 min. by car). Our agrotourism farm is 200 m above sea level and is located near the of Village Gouves (2 km) with 300 inhabitants.
The nearest village is ca

name: Yohan - Nous produisons et utilisons des ressources végétales pour réhabiliter des sols pollués
description:
Nous vivons en Guadeloupe et développons un projet agricole innovant qui vise à réhabiliter des sols pollués par les activités agricoles passées grâce à des solutions végétales. Le cœur de notre projet est d’expérimenter, cultiver et montrer qu’il est possible d’associer production agricole et restauration de l’environnement.

Nos journées s’articulent autour de différentes activités agricoles et expérimentales. Selon la période, nous travaillons la préparation du sol, la plantation de nouvelles cultures et l’entretien général de l’exploitation (désherbage, taille, paillage, compostage). Une autre partie de notre travail concerne la recherche et l’expérimentation : nous réalisons des mesures et collectons des données scientifiques pour suivre l’évolution des cultures et l’impact de nos pratiques sur la réhabilitation des sols.

Une journée type peut commencer le matin par des tâches de terrain comme le soin aux cultures ou la mise en place de nouvelles modalités de plantation, puis se poursuivre l’après-midi par des activités plus calmes, comme la saisie de données, la réflexion sur les protocoles ou des échanges sur les techniques utilisées. Les WWOOFeurs peuvent ainsi découvrir à la fois les gestes agricoles concrets et une approche scientifique de l’agriculture durable.

L’hébergement des WWOOFeurs se fait dans un gîte situé à Deshaies, en dehors de l’exploitation. Le logement est confortable et permet de se reposer dans un cadre agréable après les activités sur l'exploitation. Les repas sont préparés et peuvent être pris en commun : le petit-déjeuner et le dîner se partagent au gîte, tandis que le déjeuner est organisé directement sur l’exploitation afin de profiter d’une pause conviviale au milieu de la journée de travail.

Le gîte, situé à Deshaies, offre un cadre idéal pour profiter de la plage, de la baignade et des balades côtières. Sur l’autre versant de l’île, à Capesterre-Belle-Eau, l’exploitation est entourée de nature : randonnées en forêt tropicale, c

name: Oliver - Homestead
description:
This is not a farm nor a lodge, but if you are looking at experiencing true Bush Life and the old, but now rare, Canadian wilderness lifestyle, along with some of the traditional ways of its First People, this would be the place to be.

We live very remote  in this vast Boreal Forest  of the barely populated Northwest Territories (the closest road is 180 km away and our closest neighbours are the isolated community of Lutsel K'e; so the ways of moving about out here are still the natural pathways of this Land and its waters...)

About ten years ago, I , Oliver , started building a cabin out here , 80 km away from the small isolated First Nation Dene village of Lutsel K'e where i had been living and learning for ten years by then, cause i wanted to be even deeper in the Bush and make a home from scratch and from the local materials. 
 So i started building this first little log cabin for my good friends from Lutsel K'e and started living out here.

I was trying to raise a dog team and needed fish to feed them and i while i was busy building so i was always fishing and would naturally dry whatever fish was extra into  traditional "dryfish" (once a staple of the diet of this Land s First People the Dene Suline (Chippewyan)). 
On my next trip to town (Lutsel K'e)  or later on to the city of Yellowknife (180km away) i would arrive with a pile of dryfish that people would be eager to buy at a friendly price...
And this how i naturally started fishing commercially...

So today , we still make a "living" by fishing and making dryfish, both as a trading item that we can sell to then buy things from town that we don t get or make here, but also of course , and before anything else ,  as a staple food for ourselves and however many dogs we have...

So we fish everyday , and we use whatever is available locally as much as possible , whether it be for building (logs and wood), or hunting for meat , working on hides and furs, collecting materials for making baskets, picking berri

name: Ammon - Regenerative Farm in Alaska
description:
We are a family owned and operated regenerative farm in the interior of Alaska.  Willow is a small community with many people living in remote places, off grid, with a common goal to be self-sufficient by raising animals and plants and living off the land.  We want to help feed Alaskans quality, local grown food, and we want to build a place for the community to gather for events.

The animals:  We have 2 Livestock Guardian dogs (Maremmas), an Australian shepherd, and a large hound dog mix!  2 cats keep mice and other rodents down.  And we raise chickens, turkeys, bees, and pigs!  Also, on our future list... beef cows, milk cows, and sheep!

Typical farming activities include watering and feeding livestock and poultry, cleaning coops and pens, weeding/watering in the greenhouse/garden, land clearing for future pasture (which works towards filling the woodshed as well as to gather lumber for building farm outbuildings), caring for and feeding new chicks, and property cleanup and maintenance.  Woofers will learn how to care for animals, wash, grade and package eggs, build and improve on the grounds, as well as participate in growing the property through building new infrastructure as needed, clearing land and improving roads and paths.

An average day would begin with 6:30AM chores, breakfast at the main house.  Late morning time in the garden, weeding, watering, planting, with egg gathering throughout the morning, dog walks, land clearing.  Lunch at 1PM and siesta (we like an afternoon break).  2:30 is feeding time again for animals.  Maybe working on one of the polebarns or the new livestock/poultry building.  6:30 we begin to put animals in, check waters one last time.  Evening off after that.  There is always baking going on.  If that interests you, plan on some time in the kitchen instead of outside!  A WWOOFer can enjoy as much of our day as they would like!

All of the ladies love to cook!  They will take turns preparing a nice breakfast and dinner for everyone

name: Belén - Proyecto autogestionado anarquista en mitad del bosque del pirineo
description:
¡Hola! Somos una pequeña comunidad intencional ubicada en el pirineo aragonés, rodeada de bosques de pinos y robles. 🌳⛰️✨

Estamos reconstruyendo un pueblo abandonado por un viejo proyecto de pantano que nunca se realizó... Nosotres lo hemos okupado, ¡y estamos devolviéndole la vida! 💪🏽🏘️

Recientemente hubo cambios importantes en la comunidad y ahora estamos en fase de reestructuración y sostén del proyecto entre pocas personas (solo 2 de momento), con la esperanza de que conforme construyamos espacios comunes cómodos y agradables, llamarán a gente que los habite 😊

Nuestra visión es un espacio de:
• Puesta en práctica de valores anarquistas.
• Aprendizaje y transformación personal y colectiva.
• Reconexión con la naturaleza.
• Refugio y cuidado ante el colapso del sistema que conocemos.
• Compromiso con la lucha social más allá de nuestro territorio, poniendo nuestro pueblo y recursos “al servicio”.

------ 10 CUESTIONES IMPORTANTES ------

(1) 🚬📱🍺Tenemos un protocolo de drogas. También hay un protocolo de agresiones, para cuidar que este sea un espacio lo más seguro posible desde la no impunidad ante violencias de cualquier tipo (género, racistas, gordofóbicas, capacitistas, etc.). Ambos protocolos te los haremos llegar por correo cuando nos contactes.

(2) ⚠️👉🏽En nuestro día a día hablamos castellano y catalán, y para nosotres es importante que se mantenga la lengua del territorio que habitamos (especialmente si es minoritaria), por lo que pedimos como requisito indispensable que quien quiera venir se maneje bien en castellano o catalán 🙏

(3) 🤝🕢 ¿Cómo trabajamos?
Dependiendo de si es verano o invierno, solemos quedar entre las 8:00-9:00 para estirar un poco, y luego trabajar hasta las 14:00-15:00 con alguna pausa intermedia. Luego echamos una siesta o descansamos un poco, y a menudo seguimos haciendo cosas para el pueblo entre las 18:00 y las 21:00. Pero a ti como woofer te pediremos que colabores con el pueblo 5h al día, 5 días a la semana a "pactar" dentro de
_wwoof_travel:
Para llegar hasta aquí lo mejor es usar autostop o BlaBlaCar. Podéis ir hasta Aínsa o Sabiñánigo (con bus) e intentarlo desde ahí.

Nosotros solo podremos recoger puntualmente si coincide que necesitamos ir (son trayectos largos).

name: Mehdi - Ecological Farming Project at the Foot of the Middle Atlas
description:
Hello my name is Mehdi, I am 43 and I would like to share with you this project which was born from a simple intention:
To create a resilient farming ecosystem that is energy-autonomous, water-efficient, and adapted to the natural topography.

Here are some of the principle leading us in this projet ;

Grow food without chemicals
Make and use compost
Rotate crops to protect soil
Plant trees and food forests
Save seeds
Care for free-range animals
Use natural pest control
Collect and reuse rainwater
Reduce farm waste
Use solar or renewable energy

Here are what is expected from the participants :

Daily life on our farm, part of the WWOOF, follows a simple routine focused on farming.
We start in the morning with a short meeting, then work in the fields or garden.

WWOOFers help with:
Planting and harvesting vegetables
Weeding and watering
Making and turning compost
Seeding and transplanting
Feeding animals and collecting eggs
Maintaining fences and farm areas
Work is hands-on and done together. We share meals, often with food from the farm.
The main focus is learning sustainable farming through daily practice.

Last but not least, I will be happy and be honored to learn from you during this original way of life teinted of frugal needs discovering and sharing in simplicity true pattern of resilience :

Off course we eat farm-fresh produce cooked in a traditional Moroccan style

Also I enjoy riding both road and mountain bikes to visit the country and exercise at the same time.

Of course I appreciate discovering various style of music and cultural aspect of the mankind.

name: Rébeka - Communauté coopérative alternative, Groupe de vie communautaire, Propriété familiale
description:
We are: a cooperative, bilingual, acadian, members of the National Farmers Union and La Via Capesina, interested in and concerned about food sovereignty, peasant agroecology, social justice, and the cooperative movement.

We have: an organic strawberry production, a community supported agriculture program, a market garden, cattle, pigs, turkey, broilers and hens, many acres in hay and grain production, large woodlots, and multiple construction and renovation projects on the go.

We love: good conversation and cultural exchange, delicious food, being active, and sharing with and learning from visitors.

There's tons to do and tons to discover and we would love to have you join us. This spring we will be preparing seedlings in the greenhouse in anticipation of the market gardening season. This summer, picking and eating the delicious berries and harvesting veggies for farmers markets and baskets. There is lots of flexibility in the work whether you're interested in field work, livestock care, or preserving and canning. 

We offer: 
- A house with two rooms. It's a common space during the day, where we have our collective lunches and do our admin work. In the evening, it's a space just for WWOOFers. 
- Your own room. You might have to share the house (toilet, kitchen, dining room...) with another WWOOFer.
- Interior toilet (shared during the day, just for WWOOFers in the evening). 
- Access to WIFI
- Recommandations of things to do and discover in the region.

Working hours:
- Monday to Friday, 8am to 2-3pm (we can be flexible if need be).  The employees and coop members work until 5pm.
- You have the evenings and week-end off to discover our beautiful region.

Come find us! Web site : www.fermeterrepartagee.org Facebook : La coopérative ferme Terre PartagéeTwitter : @TerrepartageeInstagram : fermeterrepartagee
------
Nous sommes: une coopérative, bilingue, acadienne, membres de l'Union Nationale des Fermiers et La Via Campesina, intéressées et concernées par la souver

name: Katerine - Le rêve western : la vie sur une écurie
description:
Svp prenez le temps de lire avant de nous envoyer un message ! :) 

Plage horaire disponible 2026

Février : COMPLET
Mars : COMPLET
Avril  : COMPLET
Mai : COMPLET
Juin : COMPLET
Juillet : COMPLET
Août : COMPLET
Septembre : 2 places 
Octobre : 2 places 
Novembre : 2 places 
Décembre : 2 places 

Nous sommes un couple francophone début trentaine.  Nous habitons sur une écurie et avons plusieurs autres animaux domestiques (chèvres, cochon nain, poules, lapins, chats, chien). Nous sommes en campagne mais à proximité d'une petite ville (3 km) , nous offrons des cours d'équitation et des camps de jour l'été. :)

Nous offrons des séjours de 4
semaines minimum. 

La maison est à 30 minutes de voiture de Montréal, où vous pouvez aussi y accéder via un arrêt de bus situé à 3 km. 

Nous offrons de l'hébergement dans une chambre de la maison partagée (2 lits) 

Projets actuels: Voici les tâches que vous pourriez faire  : 
-Nettoyer les enclos/box des chevaux
-Nourrir les animaux
-Animation lors des camps de jour
-Rentrer du foin au grenier
-Peinture et réparations 
-Tâches dans la maison
-Toutes autres tâches reliées à la vie de ferme
-Si votre niveau le permet, vous pourriez être amener à monter/entraîner certains chevaux 

:)

Nous cherchons des gens qui : 
-Veulent dépasser leurs limites 
-Qui n’ont pas peur du travail 
-Qui on à cœur le bien-être des animaux 
-Qui sont prêt à relever des défis 
-Qui ont une bonne capacité de communication 

C’est un travail très physique en général, plus besoin d'aller au gym ! 

Si vous pensez venir en vacances à petit prix, svp passez votre chemin. Nous cherchons des gens qui veulent vivre une expérience enrichissante et s’impliquer. 

Accès Internet disponible
Accès à un vélo

name: Neatness - Self Sufficient Organic Farm in Kilimanjaro region
description:
I am a woman with the passion of working for sustainability, open minded and very interested in meeting new people from whom we can share knowledge, skills and experience. I live in Moshi town just about 2.5Km from Moshi town city center. The core of my Organic farm project is to have a self sufficient healthy food from organic methods which are friendly to the environment. The people living around me are both friendly committed in business and development projects (Mostly from Chagga and Pare tribes)

Farming activities available includes, land cultivation, planting, weeding, processing compost manure and Harvesting. I am very interested to teach WWOOFers how to develop and manage farming activities through Organic methods from planting to harvesting. Working on the farm may take an average of 5 hours per day, but activities differ each day leading to changes in time. After completing day activities at the farm, WWOOFers may share with me their interests (Nature walk, Day trips, Exercises and any other they wish to be engaged in Moshi or any other neighboring districts)

WWOOFers will be living in a local lifestyle within my family house (Private rooms), sharing food (local food) and live friendly just like family members for the time agreed to stay together.

I like cooking food varieties, sharing knowledge, experience and skills on everything which seems productive. WWOOFers interested in hiking/ climbing mountains (Mt. Kilimanjaro) is nearby, swimming at the waterfalls, cultural tours and day trips to the lakes are all available.

name: Randy - Organic vegetable farm in the matanuska valley
description:
Good day Wwoofers. I wwoofed for the first time in Hawaii 15 years ago and fell in love with it. I still enjoy winter wwoofing and love to bring what I learn from other farms back to Alaska. We also enjoy hosting people from Worldpackers and Helpx, We love producing food for the local community. We have 5 markets a week. There is a magical cat named Mu on the farm.

Help on the farm varies by season. In the Spring we spend our time prepping the field, sowing seeds, growing transplants, cultivating those seeds in the field, weeding, and watering. As we move into summer and fall helpers will have the opportunity to harvest and sell the fruits and vegetables that have been growing the past few months at the local farmers’ markets..

Food is provided for all three meals I cook Breakfast and Dinner for wwoofers and we have a community dinner as well

I love to hike as there are plenty of hikes in the area as well as camping and cold plunges there is also have a sauna on the property.

name: Julie - Vie associative, permaculture, maraichage
description:
ENGLISH

Ekikrok is a non-profit organisation that has developed various activities from a large collective vegetable garden (65 ares) equipped with two greenhouses.

Usually, the activities in the garden are carried out with other volunteers, in a friendly atmosphere. A beautiful cabin with a kitchen provides a warm place to stay and the greenhouses allow to keep busy and dry when it rains. 

In addition to its productive dimension, the garden also has educational (permaculture), welfare (welcoming schools, associations, but also vulnerable groups) and festive (tomato festival, biodiversity festival, etc.) missions. So, there are many possible missions for the wwoofers, even if the priority is of course the market gardening activity... 

We produce different vegetables, but mainly tomatoes in our very large greenhouse. NB: There is also a grocery shop in the village which puts the products of the garden in the spotlight. Here is the website of the association to allow you to get to know it in more detail: https://ekikrok.be/

FRANCAIS

Ekikrok est une asbl ayant développé différentes activités à partir d'un grand jardin-potager collectif (65 ares) équipé de deux serres.

Habituellement, les activités au potager se font avec d'autres volontaires, dans un climat de bienveillance et de convivialité. Une magnifique cabane avec cuisine permet de s'abriter au chaud et les serres permettent de s'occuper au sec lorsqu'il pleut. 

Outre sa dimension productive, le jardin revêt aussi des missions pédagogiques (permaculture), de bien être (accueil écoles, associations, mais aussi de groupes fragilisés), festives (fête de la tomate, de la biodiversité etc). Donc, il en découle une multitude de missions possibles pour les wwoofers, même si la priorité est naturellement l'activité maraichère... 

On produit différents légumes, mais surtout des tomates sous notre très grande serre. NB: Il y a aussi l'épicerie dans le village qui met les produits du jardin à l'honneur. Voici le si
_wwoof_travel: EN : Accommodation is provided by one of the volunteers involved in the association, in Gembloux (1km from the station) and/Or in Bossière where the garden is located. For those who wish to do so, it is possible to camp in the garden. To reach Bossière

name: Rebecca - Family home and garden - rural
description:
We won’t be hosting folks for the 2026 season. 
~~~

Welcome to our home and garden! Please find lots of good and welcoming information below, but first, a few essentials:

** We are looking for wwoofers who are calm, mature, and who actually want to learn daily and work hard; casual travellers will not find our situation enjoyable. 
** Anyone who joins us must be comfortable with only 2 short showers per week. While water to/from the house is limited, the lake is nearby if you need daily dose of fresh (cold!) water.
** We are LGBTQ2S++ friendly, believe in fair treatment for all, and expect our visitors to be kind, open-minded, curious rather than judgemental, and interested in uplifting all humans, animals and environments. 
** For the 2026 season we will be focussing mostly on re-building our cob gardens (please google "cob") and on firewood gathering.  Garden maintenance (watering) and processing, and meal preparation/clean-up are also common tasks.

We have room for a couple or two friends - we do prefer you to come as a pair as the property can get lonely. 

We live on the shores of Lake Laberge, Yukon, 50km north of Whitehorse, Yukon's capital city. We are growing food and building permacultural systems into our half acre of land. We have good neighbours, but also immediate access to wilderness and water. 

Accommodation will be in our garden guest suite. Parking is available if needed. If you don't have a vehicle, we are happy to pick you up from the airport or  locally. 

We eat a mostly vergetarian diet; some fish if we can catch it ourselves (rarely). WWOOFERS will prepare their own breakfasts and one other meal. We will share lunch or dinner meals together. 

Activities available to woofers: canoe or kayak trips on the lake, fishing from the dock (if you have a license), hiking through the boreal forest (with or without our friendly dog Harriet), borrowing our bikes to explore the neighbourhood. Wwoofers can occasionally catch a ride to town with Chris o
_wwoof_travel: We live about 45 minutes north of Whitehorse just off the North Klondike Highway near Lake Laberge Territorial campground. We will give specific directions once we've been in contact.

name: Henri - Farm
description:
You've never been to the prairies?  You've probably been told there is nothing to see there! Think again!  Do you know there are antilopes in southern Saskatchewan? Have you ever seen a full double rainbow?  A full moon over a rolling field of wheat?  A storm coming from 40 km away and not an obstacle in the way to block your view?  A family of foxes  fooling around in the ditch?  Millions of birds you can watch at your leisure since they have nowhere to hide?  Why do you think Saskatchewan is called "Land of the living skies"!

I have been all over the world but have chosen to settle here, raised my family and grow our food. We have been farming 1120 acres of organic cereals, legumes and oil seeds (Spelt, French Green lentils, Brown flax, wheat) for over 35 years. We also have our own cleaning plant : we clean, bag and export our products to Europe and the USA.

For the past number of years, we have been building our own log house and doing everything ourselves with very little help. 

In the summer, we have laying hens and chicks to take care of. We have apple trees, choke cherry trees, black and red currants, Seabuckthorn, Saskatoons and raspberries which we harvest to make our own jams, jellies and syrups.  

We have a large garden to weed and care for as well as 20 000 trees we have planted over the years.  Not to mention lots of landscaping for our future home sight!  

In August, we start harvesting our lentils and garden. There is lots of work picking fruits and vegetables, cleaning  and, canning or baking. 

So, from farming, to building, gardening, landscaping, canning, homeschooling and jamming sessions (pardon the pun!), there is room for all the talents, interests and strong arms in the world!! Not to mention bird watching and taking a dip in Lac Pelletier after a hot day's work!
