French is our mother tongue, but English is also spoken fluently. Some German and Spanish is spoken. Musicians are welcome!  I will be alone with our two teenagers this summer
_wwoof_travel: Saskatoon, Regina or Medecine Hat bus station and/Or airport

name: Inga - Organic goat farm in the historical region of Zemgale, Latvia
description:
Welcome to our farm! 🌿🐐

Hello! We are Inga and Edgars, a family raising two young children on our organic farm in Zemgale, Latvia. Our 11-hectare farm is home to milk goats (with goatlings every second year), a horse, a vegetable garden, a greenhouse, fruit trees, and a scenic riverbank. We also share our space with a friendly dog and two curious cats (who try to sneak inside!).

Though we both grew up in the city, at a certain point in life, we made the decision to move to the countryside, believing that being close to nature brings us closer to one another and aligns with our faith in God.

We both have academic backgrounds—Inga has studied pedagogy and social sciences, while Edgars has studied culture, philosophy, and religion sciences. We integrate our education  into rural life by organizing various events and camps for children and young people, combining education, spirituality, and hands-on experiences in nature.

We are passionate about ecological education, sustainable living, and preserving the traditional Latvian rural lifestyle—all guided by our faith and values. As practicing Catholics and Europeans, we believe in the urgent need for a global ecological conversion and strive for a life of dignity, peaceful communities, social inclusion, and environmental responsibility. Faith plays an important role in our daily lives, and we welcome volunteers who respect our way of living.
What You Can Expect as a WWOOFer?

WWOOFers at farm engage in hands-on, meaningful activities that connect them with nature, sustainability, and local culture, including:
✅ Goat care, milking, and cheese-making 🥛
✅ Gardening, growing vegetables, and working in the greenhouse 🌱
✅ Traditional craftsmanship and farm maintenance 🔨
✅ Helping with daily farm life and seasonal projects 🌾
✅ Assisting in educational and cultural activities for children and youth 🎭

We offer a welcoming, family-friendly environment where you can learn new skills, experience Latvian rural traditions, and enj
_wwoof_travel: Ūziņi bus station (1.5 km from farm) and Riga Interational airport (70 km from farm)

name: Laise - Our garden is found on shores of lake Bunyonyi Kabale District Western Uganda
description:
My name is laise chosen and I'm a male Ugandan aged 27
I was raised around lake Bunyonyi Kabale District Western Uganda 🇺🇬
I live with my family and I own cottages for my volunteers pets I have a beautiful cat and dog

How to prepare good farm manure, how to apply it in the garden

All meals and breakfast to woofers are provided , I own nice cottages for them

I like swimming, canoeing, hiking , reading novels plus story telling and evening eating
_wwoof_travel: Enttebe airport travel to Kabale District by bus and boat on lake Bunyonyi Kabale

name: Steve - Cozy instructional/Cultural homestead with lots of sustainable project to share
description:
PLEASE NOTE: Communication with the host via a phone call is required for any requested stays. Cozy  instructional facility (not a farm) with room accommodations for 8. Pool table, darts, ice cream, snow cone and popcorn machines and an industrial working kitchen.  I will offer means of transportation when needed. I’m a retired Plant Sciences Professor and current High School instructor for the Construction courses.  I can offer my guests projects on irrigation, rain water catchment, welding, concrete work, greenhouse construction, home building, solar, wind power, soil enrichment, Aquaponics, plantings, safety and construction certification, canning and cooking. I have my guests choose a project that they can finish within their stay.  Artists welcome to do on site art work.

Organic methods: I use no chemicals for pest control.

Certifications: Not registered 

Current projects: Greenhouses building, planter beds, irrigation, art, sewing, home improvements, soil regeneration, wrought iron projects, fencing, sound system installation, lighting, deck renewal, home painting and home canning.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Colorado Springs and Denver train/Bus station and/Or airport.

name: Peter & Barbara - Permaculture farm, course centre in nature and we're growing a village in rural Croatia!
description:
Hello nature lovers! We welcome you to our green hilltop where we are creating a fulfilling life in an abundant edible landscape.

We are a Dutch family who left city life in The Netherlands to live in the Croatian countryside. Our aim was to live a life closer to (our) Nature, and to bring our permaculture knowledge into real practice. Over the past 17 years, this has grown into a rich and fulfilling life. We now care for a diverse landscape with animals, a self-sufficient garden, a nut orchard and 2 food forests. Along the way, we have explored natural building, hosted and facilitated permaculture courses, created a small eco campsite, and built up a blacksmithing workshop.

Barbara is the permaculturist and gardener. She got her permaculture Diploma from Looby Macnamara ("People and Permaculture") and now Barbara brings permaculture into practice on the land and in all ongoing projects. She loves design thinking

Peter is a creative visionary, a personal development coach and blacksmith. He is supportive and encouraging in everything he does.

Nol (2004) is a passionate cook and he is learning the craft of blacksmithing and knife making.

Fleur (2010) is attending graphic design school and brings a fresh creative energy to the place.

We share our home with two dogs (Nina the Labrador-retriever, and Nelson the border collie mix), four affectionate cats (Eep, Ellie, Lasagne, and Antenna), a changing flock of chickens and rabbits, and thousands of bees in one or two hives.

Volunteering with us means that you are part of our household. We work together on projects that are happening in that moment. We usually work on 1 big(ger) project, and 1 or 2 smaller ones, and then there are small tasks that always can be done. This gives you some space to do things together or alone, and you will be able to finish things (and celebrate).

On our homestead you can learn about permaculture and how to bring more permaculture into your life. You can learn about self sufficiency a

name: Agustin - Somos amantes de la Agrofloresta, la vida en comunidad, el rio, las casas sustentables, y mucho mas!
description:
“Acauapy” significa en guaraní “asentar cabeza” y hace referencia al momento en que los tupí guaraníes, comunidad nómada viajera, llegaron a la region de las cataratas de Yguazu, conectando con su abundancia, y asi se asentaron en tribu. De la misma manera, Acauapy es creada desde un espiritu viajero y está en vías de ser una pequeña comunidad abundante, nómada, regenerativa, revolucionaria, educativa, experimental e intencional. 

- Objetivos
Producir tanta comida y abundancia como sea posible.
Generar sistemas productivos de abundancia económica, social y ambiental.
Interactuar con los vecinos para generar una reforma agraria agroforestal en la región.
Ser un polo del conocimiento de la agricultura sintrópica y permacultura de la región.
Difundir la educación y el empoderamiento de las personas para que lleven consigo las herramientas necesarias para crear una vida abundante y plena para ellos mismos.
Tener tiempo y espacio para que las personas que habitan el proyecto se desarrollen y desarrollen el cómo ser seres humanos.

- Proyecciones
- Agroflorestas
- Casas modelo en bioconstruccion
- Elaboracion de alimentos y medicina.
- Centro pedagogico en permacultura

"La rama" representa al voluntario tipo WOOF que nosotros convocamos:

Una rama ya tiene un camino andado en voluntariar y trabajar en proyectos regenerativos de triple impacto y viene desarrollando su don, virtud, ikigai en cocina, bioconstrucción, agrofloresta y/o cualquier area que Acauaphy este precisando desarrollar.
Las ramas son miembros de la tribu accionaria que forman una relación esporádica y concubinal. Su rumbo, está destinado y dirigido por un tronco, es decir, que va a involucrarse en un plan dirigido dentro de Acauaphy . 
Las ramas están abiertas a colaborar en el flujo de energía de cualquier área de la chacra que sean necesarios.

- Tareas de voluntarios/as
En los trabajos tenemos enfoque en agrofloresta (podar, sembrar, cosechar,preparar y plantar, etc) y bioconstruccion (carpinteria, b
_wwoof_travel: Aeropuerto Internacional Iguazu a 30km. Puerto Libertad a 11km. Parada de colectivo de larga distancia en Gendarmeria Lago Uruguai, a 3 km.

name: Emmanuelle - Family home and garden - rural, Agritourism Destination, Family farm, Olive grove, Hobby Farm
description:
My husband and I have been living in Rhodes since 2009. My husband is from here and I am French and Swiss. We have four children born from 2000 to 2008. We love our property, which is on a hill, with sea view, not far from the beach. We like animals, that is why we have dogs, cats, donkeys, two sheeps, chickens, ducks, and many butterflies and bees. We have a piece of land where we grow fruit trees and a small garden. We make our olives and olive oil, as we have about 125 olive trees. Both my husband and I have studied tourism, and we like to welcome guests and exchange in an international atmosphere. We speak both 6 languages, and we like to learn from other cultures. We live 30 minutes walking from the sea. Bicycles are available. There is also a bus stop 20 minutes walking, to Rhodes town and to Lindos.

Organic methods: We use the dung of the horse and donkeys to fertilize our trees and flowers, and some paint made with natural cooper and limestone to protect our olives trees, also a mix of sweet drinks and theramon in bottles to catch the insects that harm the olives.

Certifications: We did not register as official private business, that is why we are not certified.

Type of work: Our current project is doing essential oils with the herbs we have in our garden, making preserves with the olives, and fruits. We rebuild the chicken coop. We clean the garden and paint where it needs to be painted. We also prepare some skin creams (with our olive oil, bee wax and essential oils.), and soaps.

Internet access available

name: Calvin - Organic vineyard and winery and blueberry farm the beautiful Catskill mountains.
description:
Hey! My name is Calvin and I am farming 3 acres of grapevines for winemaking and an acre of blueberries in the Catskill Mountains of New York. 
Before starting my own winery/vineyard, I apprenticed and wwoofed at different vineyards learning farming and winemaking for about 7 years. I am excited to share the beauty and charm of natural wine growing with anyone who is curious! 

Spanish speakers welcome. 

The Catskills are a perfect balance of nature and culture. There are many towns scattered throughout the mountains, all with their own personalities. There are many opportunities to see live music, go hiking, eat, drink, swim.  The farm is a six minute drive or 20 minute bike ride from Stamford, which has a welcoming and tightly knit community. 

I grew up in NYC and my girlfriend lives down there part time and part time up here, and we have a cat named Appa.
This is a great place for you if you’d like to learn about organic perennial fruit farming and natural wine, and if you like music and mountains and good food!

We’ll mostly be out in the vineyard or blueberries touching plants! Or in the winery making wine if it is harvest season. We will be administering organic and biodynamic cultivation methods throughout the summer as well. 

There is a four bedroom house where we will have our meals. Queen-sized beds and plenty of space to stretch out, and you will have your privacy as well. I live in a separate apartment above the winery, just up the hill. 

¡Hola! Me llamo Calvin y cultivo 1,2 hectáreas de variedades de uva norteamericanas y 0,8 hectáreas de arándanos en las montañas Catskill de Nueva York. Llevo 6 años cultivando uvas y elaborando vino natural. Crecí en Nueva York y mi novia vive allí parte del tiempo y parte del tiempo aquí, y tenemos un gato llamado Appa.
Este es un lugar ideal si quieres aprender sobre el cultivo orgánico de frutas perennes y el vino natural, y si te gusta la música, las montañas y la buena comida.

name: Bernard - A  Mountain side Bison/Buffalo ranch
description:
WE NOW have 11 month old Bison that  still bottle feed every second day always a rush . Now We would like to thank all the worker that helped us through this last year doing double duty looking after Tilly the baby Bison and all the other thing we managed to get done ,without their help we would not have  managed to get done what was needed and wish them good luck in their  future travels
We welcome refugees from Ukraine and would consider a longer stay
We are now booking for this year stating in April 20
We are now starting to book for wwoofers, couples or singles, AVAILABLE FOR Sept/Oct and Maybe the first 2 weeks of Nov

We will Normally  welcome wwoofers  starting in April through September with a break for july/ August as it get s to hot
Our ranch is situated 20 klms from Merritt at an elevation of 1,375 metres which gives us a scenic view of the Nicola Valley.  We have a small herd of Bison (buffalo) operating a cow/calf operation on 160 acres.  

We are looking for wwoofers to stay for up to 2 weeks, or longer,  if things work out.  Feeding the buffalo, checking, repairing and building fences where required, helping to clear fallen trees and cutting firewood are some of the areas we require help.  No experience is required but if you enjoy the outdoors and are prepared for all sorts of work ,working alongside us you will learn the basic workings of our small ranch.   

You will enjoy the wildlife in and around our property as well as gain an understanding and appreciation of the grandeur, strength and ease of caring for buffalo.  

We are located 'off the grid' and generate our own power with wind and solar, however, we do have all modern conveniences. Although there is no public transportation into town from our ranch, we go into Merritt 2 to 3 times a week.   

My interests are Black Powder shooting - We host a weekend  with 2 trail walks both with over 22 target that have to be repaired and repainted befor each event this is with muzzle loaded firearms use
_wwoof_travel: E Bus has a stop in town

name: Damiano - Lazy Farm è un Hub Agricolo che si occua di agricoltura, arte e creatività.
description:
Mi chiamo Damiano e ho un background in comunicazione e marketing ma negli ultimi anni mi sono dedicato alle mie passioni, principalmente legate all'agricoltura e al cibo buono, pulito e giusto. Dopo aver approfondito lo studio delle Scienze Gastromoniche ho deciso di tornare nel mio paesino di origine. Insieme a mio fratello Alfonso, che ha un background in regia ed effetti visivi, abbiamo iniziato un nuovo progetto che si chiama Lazy Farm.
Lazy Farm è un hub agricolo che ha come obiettivo quello di mettere in contatto attività diverse tra loro. Ci occupiamo di agricoltura, ma anche di creatività, arte ed education. La missione di Lazy Farm è quella di integrare all'interno del contesto agricolo attività trasversali. La visione è quella di istituire un luogo unico e un punto di riferimento per la creazione di valore economico, culturale e comunitario.
Viviamo in campagna lungo la fertile valle del fiume Sabato, a 6km da Benevento. Benevento è una citta molto bella, ricca di tradizioni, cultura e storia. Nella casa adiacente vivono i nostri genitori con il cane Enea e condividiamo il forno a legna ed un piccolo spazio verde di circa 2000 metri quadri con un piccolo uliveto e due orti rigenerativi e un pollaio con circa 10 galline. Abbiamo anche alcuni alberi da frutta tra cui meli, ciliegi e prugne.

Pratichiamo agricoltura organica rigenerativa e le attività sono divise a seconda della stagione. Tra Ottobre e Dicembre ci occupiamo principalmente della raccolta delle olive a mano con i teli e successivamente della potatura delle piante. Nello stesso periodo seminiamo grani tradizionali in un terreno vicino (circa un ettaro). Da Aprile a Settembre ci occupiamo della preparazione dell'orto rigenerativo, della concimazione naturale dei letti e successivamente della pacciamatura. Da Maggio in poi piantiamo principalemente colture tradizionali del territorio, tra cui pomodori, zucchine, melenzane, insalate, zucche, fagioli, piselli, patate e tanto altro. Le principali at

name: Samuel - We are a small family-held farm and forest reserve in rural Chiloé
description:
My name is Samuel, and along with my partner Jessica and our children, 8-year-old Eloísa and baby Damián, we moved to rural Chiloé 10 years ago. We've learned a lot about life here (especially about what we don't know). We have a vegetable garden, a greenhouse, chickens, and sheep, as well as a wooded stream that provides us with water. We also have Chaura and Travieso, our dog and cat. We aren't expert farmers and could learn a lot from WWOOFers visitors. However, our neighbors are lovely people with extensive generational knowledge of rural and self-sufficient living. We manage to shear the sheep, tend the vegetable garden, and search for eggs in the bushes, and we have many ideas and projects we'd like to bring to life. We're passionate about physically building and planning a sustainable rural life in southern Chile, in case you're interested in construction and architecture, which are our specialties.

Our days are mostly dedicated to architectural work and parenting, but we are always spending time in  our environment-related tasks, ranging from building shelters and cofts for the animals to opening and maintaining paths and the water system in the forest and tending the edible garden / greenhouse.

We have a small cabin for our guests that can accommodate up to two people (double bed), and we can fit a child's bed in the same room. It has a bathroom, a small kitchen with a wood-burning and gas stove, and a small dining/living area. We live in a nearby house and think we could have breakfast separately and perhaps share lunch or dinner at our house. This way, we can bake bread for the next morning or for a couple of days. The cabin has its own food supplies, and the idea would be to share one or two meals a day to get to know each other and discuss future projects.

Samuel loves building things and is always thinking about construction projects. He's an expert carpenter. Jessica and Eloisa love crafts. We explore the woods and hike to the nearby beach (a 20-mi
_wwoof_travel: Mocopulli MHC airport / Cruce Degañ bus stop / anywhere in Quemchi town

name: Nora - Family home and garden - rural, Homestead, Smallholding, Hobby Farm, Horse Ranch
description:
Hi! We are Gordon and Nora, and welcome all people interested in learning about sustainable, resilient, relation-respecting farming. We don't sell anything, mainly because we have yet to achieve total independence from the industrial food system (and that's our goal). Creating relationships and community are a core value. We welcome LBGTQ wwoofers with great enthusiasm.
We run an experimental, permaculture, regenerative subsistence farm about 45 minutes east of Edmonton in the Beaver Hills Biosphere (https://www.beaverhills.ca/). Our land is on the edge of the Beaver Hills moraine, which means we have a lot of clay, gravel and peat, and not a lot of black soil. This is why we have become quite expert at growing in poor soil. We also enjoy a very great deal of wildlife; you will likely see deer, moose and bison while you are with us (as well as a lot of smaller animals).
We grow mainly hay, vegetables, apples and berries, and also keep goats and sheep. We also have an older mare who soaks up all the attention she can get.
In May this year:
*Our sheep will give birth. We will also get a new milking goat, so there will be an opportunity to help the sheep have their babies (one needed her twins pulled last year), and then a lot of milking. We will teach you to milk by hand and also with our little milking machine. It is extremely fun. The babies are all bottle-fed (several times each day!) because it is healthier for them. They stay indoors for a couple of days and then live outside. They are ridiculously cute. 
*We need to build at least one new shed and build a new pen for our sheep/goats.
*We will install drip irrigation and mulch all our gardens. That is interesting and quite technical: a good opportunity to learn.
*We will continue working on new hugelkultur beds. This is an opportunity to learn how to build excellent gardens in difficult soils. This involves a lot of shovelling. You will definitely build muscles. We will also build mound garden containers using wa
_wwoof_travel: Edmonton International airport, and there is also a via rail terminal in Edmonton. There is no bus service to Tofield that we know of.

name: Paul - Small, no-till, permaculture farm
description:
Our farm is a 4 acre farm just outside of Minot, ND.  Approximately, 1/3 acre is utilized as a market garden, including a high tunnel, while the remainder is being developed as a food forest.  We do not till, use herbicides, pesticides or synthetic fertilizers.  In addition to my wife Cheryl, myself (Paul) and our dog Fitz, we have chickens and bees.

We have two well appointed basement bedrooms and a separate bathroom available for WOOFers.  We are happy to have WOOFers share our dinner meal which includes lots of our own produce and often includes locally harvested game.  We are happy to provide food for breakfast and lunch as well, although we may not necessarily prepare those meals.  WOOFers are welcome to prepare their own vegetarian or vegan meals.

Activities:
The list of projects is nearly endless.  In late spring, early summer we will be building additional no-till beds, planting fruit and nut trees, creating swales, and planting out crops.  The remainder of the season is given over to harvesting, weeding, watering, and composting.  Saturdays are market day at the local farmer's market.

Most days of the week the mornings are used for harvesting and afternoons are weeding, watering and various infrastructure projects.  Saturday mornings  and possibly Tuesday evenings are market day at the local farmer's market.

I am a retired biology professor with a Ph.D. in microbiology.  My passions are gardening and obstacle course racing.  Minot is a small city of approximately 50,000.  We have a vibrant arts scene for a city of our size.  There is an expanding number of hiking/biking/running trials. Minot also supports a zoo and children's museum.  There are also a large number of wildlife preserves and is one of the best bird watching site in the US.
_wwoof_travel: Minot International airport and the Minot Train station

name: Lydia - Great Plains Prairie Cattle Farm + hens + pigs *  taking Woofers April-November *
description:
Note - we are not able to reply to requests from outside of Canada that require a work VISA. These requests will be declined. If you want to apply to work and are from a partner country, please check out: https://awaytowork.ca/inbound/

About the farm: 

 We are a relatively remote (by most standards), hands-on cattle ranch in southwestern Manitoba. This is a real working farm, not a lifestyle ranch. Life here is real work—physical, weather-dependent, and outdoors. We are 45-60 mins from most urban conveniences (Killarney & Brandon MB). Wwoofing here is chance to experience true Great Plains ranching and farming. Fun here is outdoors. Beyond the farm, we enjoy hiking, fishing, camping, cooking,  bonfires in the backyard, reading. 

On our farm we raise cattle, hens, and hogs with the most extensive land base being used for cattle grazing. We do not have horses. 

 Our farm is pasture with wooded areas in the beautiful tiger hills of Manitoba. We farm together  with our 8 year old son and our neighbour.  We run a direct to consumer meat and egg business and also like to source local food from other growers. 

Accommodations & Food

Modest shared house (with other Woofers or trainees) in the town of Belmont, 5 miles from the farm; functional kitchen and bathroom. Cozy and clean.

Omnivorous diet with meat from the farm and local veg grown here or purchased from other farms when in season. Wholesome and simple. Ideally farm guests have some independent living skills including some basic cooking (know your way around a kitchen) and also the ability to wash dishes,  do own laundry and have an eye and take initiative to complete basic and fundamental housekeeping like sweeping the floor, wiping surfaces, doing dishes, clean a toilet (in shared housing) etc. 

Farm Work: 

Wwoofers help with picking eggs, checking livestock, checking cattle water and fence, helping in the garden or around the yard.  Wwoofers can also participate in gaining some hands-on experience running
_wwoof_travel: Our farm is located 200 km west of Winnipeg MB and 85 southeast of Brandon MB. We will send exact directions or arrange for pick up in Brandon or Winnipeg to those interested in coming to the farm.

name: Paulo - Fazenda em desenvolvimento nas montanhas do Vale do Capão, Bahia, Brazil
description:
Hello! Sitio Dos Milagres, a true paradise on the banks of a stream of pure water, waterfall in the garden, located in the mystical mountains of the Capão Valley, Chapada Diamantina, Bahia, Brazil.

We are guardians of water sources, practicing permaculture, doing bioconstructions and creating an agroforestry from which we can harvest medicinal, pure and nutritious foods for all beings.

Currently, our small farm blesses us with the production of:

Mango, Jackfruit, Cassava, Avocado, Passion Fruit, Cassava, Cherry Tomato, Holy Grass, beans, coconuts, cashew, mangaba etc. And, thank God, much more!

I'm Paulo living and building our project here. I am a teacher and researcher, permacultor, specialist in solidarity technosciences with emphasis on solar thermal energy, bioconstructions and valorization of local natural products. We have a friendly dog named Sol. We usually hire native workers to help.

Part of the time we develop activities on the site of miracles , but another part of the time we develop activities with coconuts, cashews, bioconstructions on another farm that is located on the coast of the State of Sergipe where we have a better infrastructure to receive volunteers.

The day begins when the sun rises, we make and share breakfast and organize the activities we need to do.

There are fixed daily tasks, such as taking care of plants, cooking for everyone, performing bioconstructions and helping to produce and sell our natural products.

We offer breakfast and lunch. We eat well with natural food, sometimes produced on our farm.

At the moment, we have a camping space, we also have 3 bedrooms with double beds and we share all the common spaces!

We love to make bonfires to sing and dance, walk to waterfalls, take care of our plants and simply be in contact with Nature...

name: Gabriela Cynthia - Family home and garden - rural
description:
Hola, soy Cynthia y recibo a voluntarios en mi chacra familiar cerca de Cañuelas, a 70 km de Buenos Aires. La casa es grande y ofrezco compartir el cotidiano. El campo tiene 3 hectáreas donde pastorean las vacas y cabras que son lecheras los fines de semana abro una pequeña casa de té en mi jardin y cuando es posible tenemos leche y hacemos queso, yogur) Lo que necesito es ayuda con la huerta, el jardin, y mantenimiento del campo en general. Realizamos las tareas juntos, el voluntario no necesita tener experiencia previa. Todas las comidas del dia estan incluidas, e intento proponer cosas variadas : vegetales , lácteos, huevos y legumbres como base , de vez en cuando carnes rojas y blancas. La casa esta en pleno campo y no tiene conexion wifi, pero para cualquier cosa puedo prestar mi celular con hay internet en tiendas y cafes en Cañuelas. En cuanto al transporte : Cañuelas esta a unos 70 km al suroeste de Buenos Aires y 45 km del aeropuerto de Buenos Aires (Ezeiza), y hay que tomar el tren de la linea Roca desde las paradas Constitucion (en el centro de Buenos Aires) o Ezeiza (cerca del aeropuerto). Una vez en Cañuelas, y como no cuento con movilidad propia, hace falta recorrer unos 6km caminando o en remis (que es un taxi local).Como el trabajo es puramente físico,lo que propongo son 2:30 horas  por la tarde y 2:30 la mañana (de esta forma se hace llevadera la labor ), tengo gran afición por tareas manuales . 
Los días libres pueden ser 2 o 3 según la conveniencia , estos jornales no se tomarán dentro de la chacra,( el huésped debe retirarse )
Tanto en horarios como en dias libres ,Soy bastante flexible ya que priorizo la convivencia.

name: Albert - Family run high bush blueberry farm with a commercial processing kitchen
description:
Hello WWOOFers, we are the Smith's!!!  Albert and Tracey and son Albert(Cub) manage our 2500 Blueberry Bushes.  We also have a commercial kitchen to process those fantabulous blueberries.  We use the kitchen also to can organic vegetables and now raise poultry to process for resale.  The web site listed is for the kitchen but check out the berry farm on Facebook.  The Blueberry Farm is in Norridgewock Maine and Poultry farm and kitchen is in Cornville.  We also have rescue horses and goats and cats that live in Cornville.

We are expanding into other berries so this summer will be busy with that as well.  Tracey (Blueberry Queen) spends time in the kitchen each morning then heads to the berry patch.

We have spare rooms at our home and Tracey always has baked goods on the counter.  We eat mostly only food we have put up and nothing pre packaged.  We connect every night for a sit down meal and lunches are packed daily.

We enjoy hiking, fishing, boating, kayaking, biking.  We have numerous kayaks at the house.  There is a wonderful lake minutes from the house.

name: Cetin - Nestled in the foothills of the Taurus Mountains in Antalya, it’s all about natural farming
description:
Living and working at Taurus Mountains Farm
We are an organic farm in the Taurus Mountains near Antalya, Turkey — avocado, olives, citrus, pomegranate, herbs. The farm started during the pandemic as a place to live close to the land, and over time it has grown into a working farm with a small, warm community. We care about doing things properly: organic farming, healthy soil, real food, animals that are well looked after.
Volunteers are an important part of our life here, and we'd love to hear from you if you can imagine being part of us for a while. To make sure it works well for both sides, we want to give you an honest picture of what life with us looks like.
This is not a holiday — but it is much more than just work.
The days on the farm are physical. We work six days a week, in two blocks of three hours — six hours a day in total. The exact times depend on the season. In the cooler months, we work during the day, from 9:00 to 12:00 and again from 13:00 to 16:00. As soon as it gets warmer, we shift the hours earlier and later — the hotter it is, the earlier we start in the morning. In high summer, we sometimes begin at 5:00 and work until 8:00, then again in the evening from 17:00 to 20:00. Working in the midday Antalya sun simply isn't possible — and you don't need to. But six hours of physical work a day, also in the heat, is real, and your body will feel it. Please consider this carefully before you apply.
In return, you get a lot back. We offer a beautiful environment, clean and well equipped rooms, and everything else you need to have a pleasant stay.
What there is to do.
Most of the work is physical — out in the fields, during harvest, in the orchards. Planting, pruning, picking, carrying, mulching, watering. This is where we need the most support, and this is where you'll spend most of your time.
Alongside that, we're always looking for someone to take special care of our dogs. Twelve dogs live with us — all mixed-breed livestock guardian dogs, big, loyal

name: Anders - Small lakeside farm in a forested area, currently being developed for permaculture and self-sufficie
description:
Dear WWOOFers,

I am a mountain and small-town doctor working part-time. I am 58 years old and live with my partner and our two children, aged 12 and 18, on a small farm that we are gradually developing through many enjoyable projects aimed at becoming self-sufficient. We have cats, chickens and rabbits, and we are planning to add a few sheep and a goat next summer, as well as a dog.

We moved here five years ago and have since built a greenhouse, created a fish pond, established vegetable gardens and orchards, and restored a neglected 19th-century log farmhouse with several outbuildings. We are actively involved in village life through local associations, the community hall and social gatherings such as “talko” – pleasant combinations of course, community time and shared work.

When we have guests and WWOOFers, we are usually full-time on the farm. The morning begins with an optional yoga session, followed by breakfast. Together, we then plan the day’s activities. During 2026, our projects include building stone walls and garden beds, sawing our own timber, constructing a chicken house and a tool shed, and fencing with local natural materials, including brushwood hedges for the future sheep. We aim to restore the farm’s original structure of meadows and grazing land.

We are interested in cultural history and traditional building methods, and we are inspired by older practices such as scythe mowing and traditional crafts. What we especially enjoy sharing is the circular system of a farm – how to creatively reuse materials and work with local, environmentally friendly resources. Within our family we have particular skills in forestry, scything, woodworking, organic growing, cooking, food preservation, self-sufficiency and home preparedness. We are also happy to show how to bake traditional Swedish flatbread in a wood-fired bakehouse.

We are curious people who love to keep learning, and we see WWOOFing as a true exchange of ideas and knowledge. In the long term, we

name: Brian - A homestead with over 400 varieties of edible tropical fruits, herbs, and veggies. Learn and grow!
description:
# THE OPPORTUNITY

This is an opportunity for an individual or pair to live and work on a tropical farm and homestead just minutes away from some of the world's most beautiful beaches. The 4-acre farm uses permaculture and natural/organic farming methods to grow about 400 varieties of edible fruits and vegetables, including coconuts, mangoes, avocados, lychees, jackfruit, egg fruit, ginger, turmeric, bananas, ashwagandha, papaya, dragon fruit, passion fruit, and many more! The farm specializes in growing dragon fruit, passion fruit, and spicy peppers. We grow for personal consumption, the sale of produce and value-added products, and donation to community groups.

We makeand sell hot sauce, fruit leather, dried herbs, juices, coconut water, coconut milk, sourdough bread, and more. All made in super small batches. We also sell fresh produce to restaurants, juice hales, small CSA program, and various food proveyors on island. Everything we do is small scale and done as a labor of love. But mostly, we are growing food for our family and the people who help grow the food .

Brian is a former business owner and Lauren is a former educator, and we are looking for work-traders who fit our values and lifestyle. We are looking for work-traders who can make a minimum three-month commitment.

# THE WORK

WWOOFing on the farm is a great chance to learn new skills in an idyllic setting. We expect WWOOFers to help a minimum of 25 hours per week on the farm. Work hours are 7:30 a.m. to 12:30 p.m., Monday through Friday.

Here are examples of the types of activities you may be asked to do: weeding, planting, building new veggie beds, harvesting, deliveries, food processing, mulching, indoor and outdoor cleaning, and more.

The farm is full-on. It's hot in the tropical sun. Some days we are working in the rain and mud. But we are also graced by beautiful weather, rainbows, and ocean views. When it gets too hot, you can take a quick dip in the ocean after work or cool off in the on-s

name: Pauline - Vaches allaitantes, chèvres laitières et transformation fromagère
description:
Bonjour, je m'appelle Pauline, et mon compagnon s'appelle Chete.  Nous avons 36 et 37 ans. Nous sommes installés tous les deux sur notre ferme située sur une butte en bout de route et entourée par les bois. Nous vivons avec notre fils Oscar qui a 3 ans et demi.

Chete et moi avons chacun notre troupeau: 
Lui en vaches allaitantes, conduites en plein air intégral et tout à l'herbe. C' est un passionné de la vie du sol, et son ambition consiste à régénérer le sol grâce au pâturage. Ce sont des vaches limousines.  Le quotidien pour lui c'est faire des clôtures mobiles, déplacer quelques fois le troupeau. Ponctuellement il y a aussi des tâches de soin ou des interventions sur le troupeau. Et puis l'été Chete s'occupe de faire les foins. 
Il passe aussi beaucoup de temps à m'aider avec les chèvres.
Quant à moi je débute avec mon petit troupeau de chèvres communes provençales. Nous entamons notre 2e saison! Mon quotidien c'est la traite, la transformation fromagère, la vente des fromages et les parcs et aussi et surtout la garde. Chaque jour, quand la météo est clémente, nous gardons les chèvres dans nos bois. Nous sommes d'ancien(e)s berger(e)s, et cette pratique nous tiens à cœur autant pour nous que pour nos chèvres.
Il y a également tout un tas de chantiers en projet et en réflexion sur la ferme (toitures, rénovations, agrandissement de la fromagerie...)

Nous avons une caravane bien équipée installée dans le hameau à 350 m de la ferme. Elle est reliée à l'eau et à l'électricité, chauffée avec un poêle à bois, il y a une chambre, une sdb avec douche un petit salon une cuisine et des toilettes sèches.
Concernant les repas c'est à voir avec les personnes qui viendront, certains repas peuvent être partagés chez nous, d'autres pris en autonomie dans la caravane avec des produits fournis par nos soins.

Nous avons repris cette ferme il y a 2 ans et malheureusement cela ne nous laisse pas beaucoup de temps pour les loisirs. Nous sommes à la recherche d'un ou une associée po

name: Doug - Woodland farm
description:
An unusual woodland farm on Dartmoor National Park. There's no actual farmhouse in the 46acres of ancient woodland &organic pasture. The 'hosts' (Doug & Claire) live nearby about 4miles away. Wwoofers mostly stay in caravans. There's a timber framed welfare barn / workshop with kitchen & living room.

2025/2026 tasks are diverse. We manage the woods and pasture, care for chickens & bees & have a small vegetable / fruit garden & occasional pigs sheep & cattle. Activities will include all sorts of things related to the active running of our woodland project. This might include carpentry and timber framing (from chicken sheds to timber framing skills), getting familiar with turning trees to timber on the sawmill, extracting trees from the woods & processing timber, managing woodland, &all sorts of other activities including some community events etc. Definitely lots of stacking wood!

Ideally suited to people with experience or passion for managing woods & working timber; & scope for lots of learning & other activities. We aren't able to help people whose primary interest is learning English. Currently main focus is forestry and the building of two small turf-roofed timber caravans / caravans.

We normally only accept people staying for two months minimum. This allows deeper understanding & engagement in woodland management. It also gives you a chance to settle in to a very different rhythm close to the land.

For longer stays there is the opportunity to take on an area depending on expertise. Construction, forest-garden, pathways etc. We will have plenty of carpentry projects requiring attention. Right now our focus is carpentry, forestry, and gardening.

Cooking is self-catering in a beautiful shared kitchen / living room in the workshop / welfare barn. A timber framed building we put up in 2020.  We eat simply but well. Filtered spring water to drink (usually).
You will be living fairly off-grid and close to nature, it's a special place to be and you are expected to
_wwoof_travel: Our farm lies on cycle path number 2. It is just under 2 miles from SouthBrent, 3 miles from Buckfastleigh and 7miles from Totnes. Public transport is the Stagecoach Gold bus every half hour or so to Plymouth / Totnes which offers the closest train servic

name: Gaël - Ferme en vergers, maraîchage certifiée AB et élevage de volailles
description:
*Ferme en vergers, maraîchage certifié AB et élevage de volailles*  

⚠️ *Vie simple et hors réseau* : pas d’électricité 24/24, pas de WiFi. 

*Bonjour !*  
Nous sommes Gaël et Laura, conjoints et responsables d’une exploitation agricole certifiée Agriculture Biologique.  
Nous serons ravis d’accueillir des voyageurs pour nous donner un coup de main et partager des moments conviviaux !

*L’exploitation*  
Située sur les hauteurs du quartier Lestrade, au Robert, dans le nord Atlantique de la Martinique.  
Vue magnifique sur la Presqu’île de la Caravelle, air ventilé venant de la mer.

Nous cultivons :
- *Un verger* : longanes, pommier cannelle, ramboutan, avocatiers, agrumes...
- *Du maraîchage* : ignames, aubergines, piment végétarien, jicama...
- *Un élevage* : poules pondeuses et dindes. Accueil de nouvelles volailles en projet.

Vous pouvez nous retrouver sur un marché hebdomadaire dans le sud de l’île. Vous êtes les bienvenus pour nous y accompagner, c’est l’occasion de rencontrer le terroir et la population martiniquaise !

Sur place, vous rencontrerez aussi le reste de l’équipe : notre fils de 5 ans et nos 2 chats.

*Les activités*  
- Soins aux volailles : alimentation, eau, herbe fraîche, ramassage des œufs.  
- Cultures : préparation du sol, entretien, arrosage, récolte, désherbage manuel et à la débroussailleuse.  
- Bricolage et amélioration de l’environnement de la ferme.  
- Accueil ponctuel de clients sur place pour vente directe et visite de la ferme.

*Conditions de vie*  
Contexte de vie rudimentaire :  
- Pas d’électricité courante. Groupe électrogène allumé 1h à 2h/jour, parfois plus selon l’activité.  
- Pas de WiFi. Prévoir batterie externe, chargeur solaire si besoin. Accès aux prises uniquement quand le groupe tourne.  
- Pour le soir : lampe frontale, lampe solaire ou à pile recommandées.  
- Moustiques coriaces : pantalon, chaussettes, manches longues indispensables pour le travail et le soir. Le soleil tape fort dès le matin.

Nous vivons e

name: MELISSA - Intentional Community
description:
Welcome 

Hello! We are Melissa (USA), my husband Jairo (Costa Rica), and our daughter Fiona. Together we are creating Haiku—a place where regenerative living, creativity, and meaningful connection come together.

Our home is located in the beautiful village of El Castillo, overlooking Lake Arenal and surrounded by rainforest, waterfalls, wildlife, and volcano views. Our land is still very much a growing project, and we love welcoming people who enjoy building something meaningful from the ground up.

Our vision is to create a small regenerative farm and learning space inspired by simplicity, Japanese aesthetics, and permaculture principles. We believe healthy land creates healthy people, and we are committed to caring for the earth using natural methods without pesticides or chemicals.

Our farm is home to chickens, goats, horses, fruit trees, medicinal herbs, native plants, and an expanding food forest. Every season we continue planting trees, improving soil, expanding gardens, and creating habitat for wildlife. While many of our fruit trees are still maturing, we are excited to see the land become more productive each year.

What you can expect

Volunteers become part of our daily life rather than simply completing tasks. Depending on the season, activities may include:
	•	Tree planting and food forest development
	•	Gardening and organic vegetable production
	•	Composting and soil improvement
	•	Caring for goats and chickens
	•	Trail maintenance and landscape restoration
	•	Natural building projects related to the farm
	•	Creating gardens, outdoor spaces, and artistic projects
	•	Painting murals and creative improvements around the property
	•	Learning about permaculture, sustainable living, and life in rural Costa Rica

We enjoy giving people the freedom to choose projects that match their interests while working together toward a shared vision.

Accommodation

We currently offer simple, comfortable accommodations on both of our properties. They are still evolv
_wwoof_travel:
SJO or LIR airports
Willing to pick up in La Fortuna

name: Dennis - Family home and garden - rural, Educational Centre, Orchard
description:
P.A.O is a small agricultural company that produces a variety of organic fruits. It is a family, all of them are dedicated to production work. We have one hectare of certified organic fruit trees. Quillota is a beautiful city located in the Aconcagua river valley, close to the beautiful city of Valparaíso and some beaches in Viña del Mar and Concón. The accommodation is in a cabin, open kitchen and Mediterranean food. Help expected in many things according to needs and time of year.

Organic methods: Control biológico de plagas, apicultura, reciclaje, etc

Certifications: Certificadora AGROBATO AG

Current projects: Asistance  to organic growers for pest control

name: Live - Family farm
description: We have started our journey towards being the world best farm in the fields of animal welfare, enviromnet and sosial media! :D We need help to get everything in place. The farm is located by the foot of Oslos highest plateau mountain Norefjell, by the lake Krøderfjorden. (1,5 hour drive from Oslo). We have free range Happy Pigs outdoors that have helped us prepare an area for new growing where we need help.  The farm is powered by our own bioenergy plant. We have sheeps, pigs, horses, dogs and cats. We would love help from wwoof’ers that have worked a lot manually before. One huge advantage would be knowledge of carpentry for some small projects. Some experience with machinery and fencing is welcomed. We have various big and small projects, and prefer wwooffers who will stay for a period of time and integrate in creating the dream. Our farms webpage is only in Norwegian, at nedreskinnes.no , but we run a English blog, you can check out farmgirlofnorway.com/about to learn more about us and our philosophy. We look forward to hear from you
_wwoof_travel: 1,5 hours from Oslo. 2 hour drive from OSL Gardermoen. 1 hour from Drammen. 45 min from Hønefoss. Very little  public transport. We can pick you up at Noresund or Krøderen bus stops.

name: JAN - Agroecological Bean to Bar Cocoa
description:
Dear friends of the earth and the heart,

Come, with your hands and souls, to embrace the magic that springs from the fertile soil! We are looking for passionate volunteers for a unique experience, where nature and tradition intertwine in a sweet embrace.
In the dance of the green leaves, we invite you to join us in harvesting cocoa, the sacred fruit that transforms the simple into the sublime. Under the warmth of the sun and the song of the birds, each fruit picked is a promise of flavor and connection.
From the tree to the table, we will walk together on the path of ceremonial cocoa. With respect and care, we will learn the art of transforming cocoa into a drink that nourishes the spirit and celebrates life. Each step will be a lesson, each moment, a celebration.
If you have an open heart and a desire to learn, come be a part of this journey! Here, under the starry sky and among the majestic trees, we will build memories, bonds, produce chocolate and lots of cocoa.
Join us! Bring your smile, your energy and your curiosity. Let's cultivate not only cocoa, but also friendship and respect for nature.


About Cabruca:
Organic production model Cabruca.
Cabruca is a production system that combines Atlantic forest, cocoa and other agricultural crops.
Valuing the cabruca and its products helps to prevent the advancement of cattle raising in our Atlantic Forest and the destruction of this rich and old ecological system.
Long live the cabruca !!

name: Chris - Family home and garden - rural, Horse Ranch
description:
2023 was our 50TH ANNIVERSARY! So we have been here a while.  As the farm has aged so have we. Nevertheless we are still excited to host returnees and to welcome new wwoofers from around the world! We are a  family farm breeding Canadian Warmblood horses. We have hosted over 1000 volunteers and are pleased to share our country life style with visitors from all over the world. Currently we are booking for the fall and winter. I will be going in for knee replacement surgery at the end of October so it would be great to have additional help for November and December.

Most of our horses live outside in small to larger herds which gives our volunteers an opportunity to learn first hand natural horse behavior. Year round there is always lots to do with our herd of 50 Warmblood horses on five quarter sections of land. Depending on the time of year, this includes training, breeding, foaling, weaning, stall cleaning, showing, feeding, grooming, selling, fencing etc. 

Other farm work includes repairs on buildings, equipment and machines, landscaping, gardening, haying,  building projects, logging, cutting firewood, cooking, housekeeping, and caring for sheep. Our focus is the breeding, training and selling of our horses for jumping, dressage and three day events. 

A renovated hip roof barn with additions serves as our home where we welcome our wwoofers in either single or shared  rooms. 

We produce some of our own meat.  Our non vegetarian meals can accommodate special needs of vegetarians, non wheat, non dairy, low carbohydrate, etc. most of the time. 

Each year, besides helping with the horses, we can use the talents of gardeners, internet savvy tech people and of course anyone with mechanical skills. We are also planning on a few construction projects. 

We invite you to be part of our 'Touchstone Family', share in our farm life experiences, have fun and meet people from around the world and make new friends.

Languages: English is our language but we do have some fam
_wwoof_travel: Whitecourt bus station / Edmonton Airport YEG

name: Risa Roe & - Horse care and gardening in Idaho
description:
Greetings!

Hello!  Thanks for checking us out! 
I just had a cancellation and that opened up a spot or two for mid summer!  
I have immediate openings from now (june 12th to September 1st) 
Otherwise my next openings are 

-January or Early February Arrival to Early March. 

To get a good idea of what your experience might resemble, check out this you tube video (credit, Teya, 
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=uUfIGjC3IEg
The process for acceptance:
Step 1: message me with your request for dates. 
Step 2: I will let you know if those dates are available. Keep in mind, I may be communicating with others about similar date inquiries. 
Step 3: if the dates you request are available, I send you paperwork to read, sign and return. 
Step 4: the wwoofer that responds back with paperwork filled out will get the spot. It is first-returned, first-booked.
Step 5. After I receive paperwork, i accept. You confirm.  Then it is a solid booking. 

Meet your Hosts, Risa and Lacie, 
Risa is the owner, providing quality horse boarding, horse motel services, small hay production and an environment for learning. Risa has been running businesses her entire adult life. Therefore we also have Jami on site as a business partner and mentor to many young individuals. 

Risa has partnered with Lacie to assist in the needs of assisting our wwoofers with more learning opportunities: 

Hi! My name is Lacie and I've been a boarder here for almost 6 years. My love for horses started when I was 15 and got a spicy Arabian for my birthday. The passion for horsey life only got more intense when my now 20 year old daughter took her first lesson at 7. Since then we have become active in the community with playdays, horse camping and drill team. I have been teaching beginner lessons here for 4 years now and look forward to the opportunity of riding with and helping others for more years to come.

With two hosts in one we have more opportunities to create a fun and learning environment.  

We will accept a o
_wwoof_travel: BOI airport is only 10 minutes drive.

name: Lisa - Homestead, Market Garden, Cattle Ranch, Ranch/ Livestock Farm, Mixed Farm, Farm, Family farm
description:
Ready to experience a mixed family farm?! We are a certified organic farm about an hour west of Grande Prairie, Alberta, and about an hour east of Dawson Creek, British Columbia. Donovan and Lisa have been farming since 2015.  There are cows, pigs, laying hens, a large garden. We farm on the same land as my husband’s  father, First Nature Farms, where we milk a cow to make cheese, yogurt and enjoy the fresh milk.  You can expect to learn about animal husbandry, from the arrival of all of the spring babies to marketing our meat at the local farmer's market.  You may find yourself washing eggs for market,  planting, weeding, and harvesting the garden, making amazing farm fresh meals, and so much more! We are really excited to have an opportunity to host WWOOFers!  It is a time for us to share our knowledge, food, and passion for sustainable agriculture.  We are very lucky to live in an area where we have a strong community of neighbors who also enjoy meeting new faces and are very welcoming.  In our free time, we enjoy potlucks with our community, concerts, going to music festivals, fishing, canoeing, and hiking. Almost all of these activities are within a one hour drive from our farm. We look forward to hearing from you!

Lisa and Donovan, Murray (6), Everett (4), and Levin (0). 

Certifications: Ecocert

Current projects: Every day is a new one. Feel free to contact us and we'll tell you what we're up to.

Internet access available

Check us out on Instagram @thehomestead2015.

name: Bill - Cattle Ranch
description:
Midsize cattle ranch 640 acres owned and 3700 acres crown grazing lease. Primarily a cow-calf operation. Also a large non commercial garden and experimental northern orchard. Draft horses. quarter horses, and poultry. South Peace River area of northeastern BC, foothills of the northern Rockies. Closest town Dawson Creek 26 miles. If you are serious about this type of agriculture, sustainability issues around food production, survival as a small farm, and the like, we would welcome you for several weeks to make the trip north worth while. We are actively looking for a person or persons for 2022 to help with the late summer and fall season. (Calving starts in March.) July, August, September and October are the most critical times with garden, haying, and then cattle handling in the fall, so a couple of great people committed for some or all of those months would be ideal. We are no longer certified organic, but are registered under BC Environmental Farm Plan, and operate using regenerative agricultural practices.No smoking or drugs on the property. What we are not - pony club, resort, entertainment center. This is a working ranch, always something to do, and if you can bring an attitude of 'how can I help' you will be treated with respect, fed well, and able to enjoy the many things this area has to offer.  You will be helping out with calving, preping the garden, fixing fences, some tractor work, checking cattle on range, all depending on the weather.  
Vaccination for covid required.  Medical and liability insurance also required, in case you break something of ours while you're here. Cell phone - if you have to be on your phone all day you may as well stay home and save yourself the airfare.
A farm can be a dangerous place, especially where livestock  is involved, you will be required to sign a waiver.
Facebook     ravenwoodfarmbc
Instagram     ravenwoodfarm_bc

Organic methods: Soil mineral balancing; composting; managed grazing; water management; drip irrigation;
_wwoof_travel: From Dawson Creek, 26 miles west on Hwy 97; 2miles south on Rd 267.

name: Heiða - Horse and sheep family farm
description:
Do to  cancellations we have space April till July. We are looking for someone how is interested in horses and riding for this summer. 
Me and my mom live on a farm in the north, aprox 15 min drive from the closest store.  We live on a family farm that has been in the family for a while. We have around 150 sheeps, 50 horses, 3 dogs, 2 inside cats and some chickens.

Dayly work evolves around the sheeps as they are feed twice a day and stable work around the horses, if you are experiensed you can ride on your own but if not realy experiensed then I would join you when I am not working.  We usualy go out around 9-10 etch morning to feed the sheeps and tend to the horses, then there would be lunch and after lunch we do what needs to be done on the farm and that higely depends on what time a year it is, in spring it is lambing season and then there is always plenty to do. Summer evolves alot around horses and haying  and what ever is needed to do, whinter we tend to the sheeps and horses that are keept inside. Later sheep feeding is usualy around 5-6 and after that would be dinner.

We usualy have breakfast, lunch and dinner, and beond that if people are hungry then we usualy have bread avalable. We are mostly a meat farm and can not acomidate for vegans ore vegetation . You will have a room to yourself unless there are 2 then there is a bunk bed to share the room. My mom usualy cooks for meals or there are always food in the fridge so noone should go hungry. We all live in the same house and live like a family and the pearson joining us would just be like part of the family.
Good internet connection.

Activities around are swimming pool in 15min drive and around the country side there are rivers with beautifull waterfalls, natural spring that you can enjoy when ever you feel like it, during summer there are rafting close by and Sauðárkrókur is in 30 min driving distant and there are museums, black sand beach, Grettislaug (natural hot spring).   There are farms around o

name: Ruby - Homestead harvesting berries, vegetables and raising meat birds.
description:
We’re Marty, Ruby, and Andrene, living with our dog Tonka on 12 acres located centrally on the North Island on Haida Gwaii, BC.  Our main interests are  gardening, camping, rock collecting and hunting. We have a 50 tree orchard, abundance of berry crops  (blueberries, raspberries, gooseberries, strawberries, and of course huckleberries), 2 green houses, and large root garden beds.  

If you choose to be a Woofer with us, your typical day involves watering and harvesting crops, with light weeding as needed. Depending on the time of year, other tasks may include transplanting, preserving food, preparing garden beds, maintaining yard areas, feeding chickens and repairing fences and garden structures as required. 

Most daily meals, include self-serve breakfasts (except blueberry pancake Sundays) and lunches and we host dinners together. Most of our meals are made from the meat/ fish and vegetables we harvested.  We can accommodate vegetarians; however, we do not know how to cook vegetarian meals. So if you are a vegetarian, we would need your assistance in preparing vegetarian dishes.  A private bedroom with a single bed is provided, however we can accommodate for a couple or if you'd like to share a room with a friend, we can put in a bunk bed.  We have Wifi, but there is no cell service on site.

On your free time, you can easily access to hiking the  Anvil or Pesuta Trails, fishing or swimming in the Tlell River and camping on the beach. coastline.  There is the option to join us when we go crabbing at North Beach.
_wwoof_travel: Masset and Sandspit airports and BC ferries in Skidegate

name: Aislynn - Experience Hands-On Regenerative Living and Permaculture gardening on a Texas Homestead.
description:
Welcome to Freedom Harvest Farms
Welcome to Freedom Harvest Farms, our 10-acre regenerative homestead in Taft, Texas, conveniently located between Corpus Christi, Rockport, Port Aransas, and Padre Island.
Freedom Harvest Farms is our home. It is where we live, grow food, care for animals, restore the land, and build community. It also serves as the home base for It's Just Aislynn, LLC. My business is centered around regenerative agriculture, local food systems, cooking, content creation, entrepreneurship, education, and community events.
When you stay here as a WWOOFer, you're not simply volunteering on a farm. You're stepping into the daily rhythm of a working homestead while participating alongside me in building a real small business. Throughout your stay, you'll experience how all of these pieces work together—from caring for the land and preserving food to preparing products, hosting events, creating content, and building systems that support both the homestead and the business.
Who Lives Here
Freedom Harvest Farms is home to three adults. I, Aislynn, am your host and operate It's Just Aislynn, LLC from the homestead. My parents live next door in their own private barndominium on the property. While we share the land, we each have our own homes and independent living spaces.
My parents are supportive of the homestead and occasionally have projects that volunteers may help with. My father also cares for our small vineyard, which is his personal passion project. They have two friendly dogs, and it's common for us to gather for coffee together most mornings before everyone heads off to their projects for the day.
The property is also home to chickens, potbellied pigs, dogs, gardens, a small orchard, a vineyard, and an ever-changing variety of native birds, butterflies, insects, and wildlife. Our fig orchard is one of our biggest seasonal successes each May and June, and our Spanish Lenoir grapes are transformed into homemade wine and naturally fermented red wine v

name: Damian - Retreat Educational and healing center
description:
Hello Woofers, at Abaton we’re focused on self sustainability we produce majority of our own crops which include local fruits, such as papaya, avocados, mangoes, plantains and casava. We also try to grow our own vegetables and farm honey as well. aside from myself it’s four other people living full time on site and four part timers, however it’s a growing community Our pets include 3 dogs and 4 cats.

A typical day at the farm includes checking on crops, cleaning, clearing weeds, composting, harvesting, and feeding the chickens and pets. We would like to teach WOOFERS about nature and the benefits different types of herbs, medicinal plants and healthy eating.

WOOFERS will have their own shared rooms with indoor bathrooms and showers and outdoor bathroom and showers as well. Meals will be provided for the first 3 days. After this they will be provided with materials to cook their own meals as well as full access to the kitchen. THE UTILITIES ARE PAID BY US , these include gas, electricity and water.

My hobbies are hiking, tending to the farm and making sure my flowers are tended to. We are located 3 kilometers from the sea so snorkeling, swimming, cave exploration, bicycling, and horse back riding can be done nearby.

name: Sebastian - Organic Farm in the middle of a forest
description:
We are a small group living on a farm in Senegal, where we are working to establish a forest garden. The environment is quiet and remote, surrounded by nature, with an emphasis on sustainable living and a simple lifestyle. Our goal is to create a harmonious space where people and the land can thrive together.

Our work focuses on maintaining and developing our forest garden. This includes planting, watering, composting, and experimenting with ways to enrich the soil. An average day often starts with tending to plants and making sure everything is in order. We are happy to share what we know about sustainable farming techniques and the principles of forest gardening. Afternoons may involve small projects, brainstorming new ideas, or learning opportunities related to the environment.

WWOOFers will stay in simple accommodations that reflect the natural environment around us. Rooms are basic but comfortable. Meals are prepared by us using locally sourced ingredients.

In our free time, we enjoy playing music with instruments like the handpan, drums, and flute. We also enjoy relaxing and engaging in creative activities. The area around the farm offers opportunities for walking and experiencing the quiet beauty of the natural surroundings. While the location is remote, it is an ideal space for those who appreciate a peaceful environment and a slower pace of life.

Nous sommes un petit groupe vivant dans une ferme au Sénégal, où nous travaillons à établir un jardin-forêt. L'environnement est calme et isolé, entouré par la nature, avec un accent sur la vie durable et un mode de vie simple. Notre objectif est de créer un espace harmonieux où les gens et la terre peuvent prospérer ensemble.

Notre travail se concentre sur l'entretien et le développement de notre jardin-forêt. Cela inclut la plantation, l'arrosage, le compostage et l'expérimentation de nouvelles méthodes pour enrichir le sol. Une journée type commence souvent par l'entretien des plantes et la vérification de

name: Anna Sofie - Community supported farm
description:
Prestegårdshagene is both a CSA (community supported Agriculture) and a flowerfarm.
We produce more than 30 different vegetables to the members of the coopretaive. We are located just outside the center of Porsgrunn. We are a cooperation with a board, a CEO (40%) and a gardener (100% in the season). We are around 140 members (both adults and children). We also work with different projects with kindergardens and schools, who use the farm as a "school garden" and a place to integrate theory and practise. We join different local festivals as Mersmak - a food festival in Skien by the end off August as well as national events as the national eco-week and GreenLight District - an artfestival with focus on transformation from industry to a more sustainable community.

Certifications: Certified in 2019

Internet access available

name: Wilman Karol - Agritourism Destination, Market Garden, Flower Garden Farm, Fruit tree nursery, Farm, Family farm
description:
Allyn Kawsay es Proyecto  familiar que inicio en setiembre del 2018, Hasta la Fecha Los Avances Que HEMOS Logrado han sido  satisfactorios un merecido  Agradecimiento a todos l @ s Voluntarios Que Pasaron por la casa y aportaron un granito de arena Gracias a Ellos Estamos Avanzando con Este sueño, lograr vivir  Una Eco-Aldea Sostenible y sustentable como siempre teniendo en cuenta la planificación, bajo, enfoque, agroecológico, respeto, Valores, Prácticas, permacultura., La Actividad de hortalizas. , El ecoturismo, la crianza de las abejas, la bioconstrucción, el de esparcimiento, meditacion, fruticultura, vivero, forestación, Forraje, muy pronto crianza de cuyes, TODAS ESTAS ACTIVIDADES estan sujetas AL Ciclo Natural de las Sierras Dinámica en EL Campo Nada Forzados Trabajamos viendo las temporadas Las Campanas y Las Necesidades,

 

Allyn Kawsay is a family project that began in September 2018, to date the progress we have made is very satisfactory and a well-deserved thank you to all the volunteers who passed through the house and contributed a grain of sand thanks to them we are making progress With this dream, achieve a sustainable and sustainable Eco-village as always taking into account a planning, low, focus, agroecological, respect, values, practices, permaculture., The activities we carry out in our plot are the following, The production of vegetables , ecotourism, bee breeding, bioconstruction, recreation, meditation, fruit growing, nursery, afforestation, fodder, very early guinea raising, all these activities are subject to the natural cycle of field dynamics, nothing is forced, we work by seeing seasons, campaigns and needs, are welcome to the Eco-village, Allyn Kawsay.

 

 

Allyn Kawsay es un proyecto familiar que comenzó en septiembre de 2018, hasta la fecha, el progreso que hemos logrado es muy satisfactorio y un merecido agradecimiento a todos los voluntarios que pasaron por la casa y contribuyeron con un granito de arena gracias a ellos. progres

name: Philipp - Family farm
description:
Vielfältiger Familienbetrieb.

Schwerpunkt sind Milchkühe und Ackerbau.

Außerdem werden Schweine und Hühner gehalten.

Zum Hof gehören auch ein Hofladen und Gästezimmer.

Organic methods: Biologisch, organische Bewirtschaftung.

Type of work: Baustelle, Herbstsaat...

name: Fabrizio - Hi, we are Fabrizio and Sebas, two brothers in an organic farm focused in regenerative agriculture.
description:
Hi we are Fabrizio & Sebastian, we have another worker called piña and Chu. We have dogs, hens and chickens.

We do germination of trays, transplant of seedlings, planting, harvesting, feeding animals, picking up eggs and making compost.

We have 3 houses and the volunteers will have their own lil house. There is a kitchen one of the houses which everyone can use. We make breakfast and lunch everyday for everyone.  Everyone else takes care of their dinner.

We like to cook, go for hikes, sometimes we like to gather around fire.

name: Kajsa - Creative smallholding on the East Coast
description:
Kajsa and Emil warmly welcome you to their beautiful farm, dating back to 1868, where they live together with their cat Mouche and sheep Bäatrice, Bärit, and Bärta. Here you'll also find chickens, bees, and a vegetable garden focused on self-sufficiency. The farm is continually evolving, and future plans include expanding our vegetable cultivation for local sales.

We cultivate more than just the land—we also nurture creativity, as our farm hosts regular workshops in vocal improvisation and vocal process art, led by Kajsa. Here, music and art blend seamlessly with farm life.

We have numerous dreams and projects where your help would be greatly appreciated! You might help build a new sheep shed, restore pastureland, or renovate farm buildings to welcome more visitors. Other exciting projects include creating a natural swimming pool or establishing a nut orchard. Self-sufficiency forms the foundation of our daily life, and activities range from everyday chores to long-term projects.

Activities you can participate in and learn at our farm include:

Sowing and planting

Cultivating and harvesting

Renovation and building maintenance

Baking, preserving, and food processing

Taking care of chickens, sheep, and bees

Fencing, clearing pastures, and tree care

Digging and creating garden beds

A typical WWOOF day is planned together the evening before. We work from a list of current tasks, and you can choose to work independently or together with us. Together we create a beautiful and functional environment where life and creativity flourish.

About us:
Emil is a morning person who usually works early in the day. Living with MS, he works part-time, dedicating his remaining time to rest, socializing, cooking, and daily life activities.

Kajsa is a singer and workshop facilitator who organizes cultural activities at the farm, integrating music and art with practical farm tasks such as gardening, cooking, and baking.

In our spare time, we enjoy hiking, mushroom picking, sw

name: Antoine - Pépinière d'arbres à fruit et à noix, Ferme Paysanne
description:
Bienvenue à Ripon !

Nous sommes deux amis qui démarrons un projet de pépinière et de ferme paysanne dans la Petite-Nation. Notre ferme, spécialisée dans les vivaces comestibles, produit des arbres et arbustes à racines nues. Nous élevons également des poulets de chair, des porcs et des poules en pâturage !

Antoine est un touche-à-tout. Biologiste de formation, il explore tous les aspects de la vie paysanne : dès qu’une activité lui permet de renforcer son lien avec le territoire qu’il habite, cela l’intéresse.

Victor, pour sa part, est plus spécialisé. Agriculteur de formation, il est passionné par les arbres et arbustes fruitiers de toutes sortes. Animé par la découverte et l’espoir de voir de nouvelles essences pousser dans notre climat, il est notre référence pour les variétés fruitières prometteuses.

Gourgane est notre chienne de ferme de 2 ans, une belle et grosse boule d’amour issue du croisement entre un Saint-Bernard et un Mastiff-Pyrénées-des-Montagne. Elle nous accompagne dans toutes nos aventures sur la ferme et veille sur nos animaux quand nous ne sommes pas là. Elle adore les câlins et les gâteries.

Les projets ne manquent pas : nous explorons plusieurs aspects de l’autosuffisance alimentaire et essayons de construire le plus de choses possible par nous-mêmes. Nous avons de nombreux arbres et arbustes fruitiers, des jardins et des animaux.

Notre région est riche en activités, allant du plein air aux événements culturels, et il nous fera plaisir de vous la faire découvrir.

Nous sommes très actifs dans notre communauté et chérissons l’effervescence que les gens de la région y créent.

Si vous souhaitez prendre part à un projet régénératif et nourricier au sein d’une belle communauté du Québec, notre humble aventure est pour vous !

Tout dépend de la saison !

La vie sociale et culturelle dans la région est riche, et nous avons un bon réseau de fermiers et fermières, donc les soirées et les fins de semaine se passent en bonne compagnie.

Le printemp

name: Jochen - Organic Farm,
description:
We are living in a safe and quite location around nature. We have almost summer in Kakheti, the cradle of wine. Everything is growing wonderful. We live in a wonderful area with an incredeble view to the Kaukasus Range in a mild mediterane climate. On 0,5 hectar we are growing vegetables, salad, mirco greens and herbs. By now we are starting to build rised beeds for our spring and summer crops, as tomatoes, fenel, green pea, chard, cucumber and much more in mixed cultures. We are working according to moon calendar and according to demeter rules. We do also some Marinade,  Fermentation, sourdough bread, Jam, and wonderful Juices. We would be glad to have woofers to join our project. we do, besides farming, lots of tests with natural remedy and we would appreciate to share our knowledge with interested people. 

Organic methods: mixed culture, nettle and others, crop rotation

Certifications: Caucascert

Current projects: we deliver mostly  in to our organic health food store and some other health food shops

We make juice, fermented vegetables like kimchi  and also marinade by ourselves

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel:
Tbilisi Airport
Bakurzikhe good connected with bus/Taxu

name: Nina - Syntropic agriculture farm/Reforestation project.
description:
Hi guys. We are mostly looking for either short term or long term volunteers. 

Volunteers will stay in their own house sometimes shared and we provide 3 meals per day. You will mostly cook your own meals. Some days we will cook together and relax by the fireplace etc but the majority of the time you will be living here 100% independent.

Our farm situated 2 hours from Rio de Janeiro. 45 minutes by plane to Vitória and 1 hour by car. We are located in the beautiful town Santa Teresa in the state Espirito Santo. We have 12 hectares of beautiful jungle (mata atlantica) and 6 hectares of farmland. We have a river with crystal clear water coming straight out of the national reserve passing 850 meters through our property with many natural pools and little waterfalls. Our neighbor is the amazing Augusto Ruschi National reserve with over 4000 hectares of protected forest. There are many mammals but it is most famous for birds . Augusto Ruschi was a famous Brazilian biologist specializing in hummingbirds. To the front you have an ocean view. We are 750 meters high so the temperature is comfortable.


In your spare time you can go hiking in the natural reserve, I'm also trying to climb trees and rocks. An experienced climber could probably open up about a dozen of new routes in the area it's full of big walls and boulders. We have our own little lake to swim. There is a very nice town about 15 km from us called Santa Teresa, it's a student town and there are lots of nice bars and restaurants. There are lots of waterfalls around, we are also building an ecological pool. I also like to surf, the beach is about 40km's from the town. We like to live our life in a way we don't need a holiday ! The public transport in Santa Teresa is excellent. From Vitoria it is extremely easy to take a plane to Bahia, Rio, Sao Paulo or anywhere you want to visit in Brazil !

Organic methods: Ernst Götsch's Syntropic Agriculture
Hügelkultur
Agroforestry
Biochar.

Certifications: Not yet certifie

name: Dong il - <GW129> 다름을 소중히 하고 나눔을 실천합시다./Let us cherish diversity and practice sharing
description:
✅  호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
* 이 곳에서의 먹거리는 가능하다면 자연농사,유기농으로 자급자족을 지향합니다.
*조금가지고 조금 사용한다면, 나와 모두와 지구가 더 좋아집니다.
*자본주의의 문제점을 벗어나고 싶은 곳입니다.
*저는 세상 정답 없으니 하고 싶은 것(환경과 생태적으로) 재미있게 실천적 실체적 실용적으로 살고자 합니다.
*노동은 모든 것에 우선합니다.
*다름을 인정하고 나눔을 실천하면 모든 것이 뜻대로 이루어 진다고 믿어요.

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2010년
✅ 농장 형태 : 가족농,체험농장 
✅ 농장 주작물 : 고구마 깨 콩 푸성귀 옥수수 감자

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 친환경 농업을 실천하고자 많은 것을 버리고 사는 사람이니 기대에 어긋나지 않을 것입니다.
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 : 모든세제는 친환경. 화장실에서 휴지 안 사용하기, 아나바다 실천하기,  적게 사용하고 적게 먹기

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 스님이 있어서 같이 생활 가능합니다.
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려 동물 : 개 4마리. 닭 10마리
✅ 우퍼가하는 작업
*4월~6월  파종하기. 산나물 채취. 제초작업
*7월~10월 수확과 제초작업
*연중 친환경 집짓기
*12월~3월에는 작업을 안합니다.
✅ 선호하는 우퍼상 : 진정성과 순수성만 있으면 됩니다.
✅ 우퍼에게 당부 메세지 : 저는 가식을 싫어하니 있는 그대로 일합시다.
✅ 숙소에서 와이파이 가능여부 : 가능
✅ 술,담배 가능여부 : 간헐적 음주가능
✅ 호스트와 식사여부 : 하루 한,두끼만 함께 먹고 나머지는 식재료를 드려 직접 해드셔야 합니다.
✅ 영어소통 : 번역기 통해서 대화 예정
✅ 일주일 기준 우핑시간 : 하루5시간 주5일

======== English explanation ==========
✅ Host introduction and free farm introduction:
* Food here aims for self-sufficiency through natural farming and organic farming if possible.
*If you use it a little bit, it will make everyone and the earth better.
*It is a place where we want to get out of the problems of capitalism.
*Since there is no correct answer in the world, I want to live what I want to do (environmentally and ecologically) in a fun, practical, practical, and practical way.
*Labor comes first with everything.
*I believe that if you acknowledge difference and practice sharing, everything will go as you want.

✅ Year Farming Started : 2010
✅ Farm Type : Family farm, experiential farm
✅ Main Crops : Sweet potatoes, sesame, soybeans, leafy greens, corn, potatoes

✅ Eco-Friendly Farming Practices : I’ve given up many things in life to pursue eco-friendly farming, so you can be assured that I am committed to this lifestyle.
✅ Eco-Friendly Lifestyle Practices :All cleaning products are eco-friendly, No toilet paper used in the bathroom, Practice of sharing and reusing (similar to Freecycle culture), Using and consuming le

name: Francesco - We are in the early stages of creating a regenerative land-based project rooted in nature, spiritual
description:
We are Francesco, Eros (8) and Isaac (5).
Francesco is a private chef, farmer and passionate natural builder, deeply interested in:
