earth construction
recycling and upcycling materials
low impact, hands on solutions
The children grow up close to nature and daily life on the land, so we welcome volunteers who are patient, kind and respectful of children, and comfortable sharing space with a family rhythm.
 and we are in the early stages of creating a regenerative land-based project rooted in nature, spirituality, community, and slow living, located in the countryside of Dolianova, South Sardinia, surrounded by olive trees, Mediterranean landscapes and silence.
Our long-term vision is to create a spiritual and nature-based retreat space, where people can reconnect with themselves, the land and each other.

The project will include:
🌱 a glamping area immersed in nature
🔥 a home restaurant focus on fire experience, with farm-to-table, local and seasonal food from local Sheppards
🧘 spaces for spiritual retreats, yoga, meditation and inner work
🌿 regenerative agriculture, gardens, herbs, bees and animals
🌊 natural water systems, ponds and ecological infrastructures
🤍 a community-oriented environment based on respect, presence and collaboration
At the moment, we are looking for open-hearted, conscious and hands-on people who feel called to help build this vision from the ground up.Our focus is on:
natural building (cob, earth, wood)
upcycling and reusing materials
simple, grounded living
food, fire and shared time
regenerating land and skills together

🔨 What we are working on (seasonal focus)
Depending on the season, activities may include:
natural construction (cob walls, wood structures, pallet reuse)
finishing outdoor bathrooms and shared spaces
olive grove care (pruning, grafting, cleaning)
firewood preparation
compost and grey water systems
garden preparation and food forest planting
creating paths, seating areas and common spaces
Work is physical, practical and mean

name: Adrienne - Lavender farm 💜 in semi-rural area of NJ, 1 hour from NYC
description:
Our farm is a 10 acre farm located in a semi-rural area of NJ that is only about an hour bus ride from NYC. Our base crop is lavender plus we have a 90 by 90 foot vegetable and flower garden within a deer exclosure, 2 dozen exotic chickens, 2 Dwarf Nigerian goats and 9 baby goats. The babies are our Goat Yoga stars and we have goat yoga sessions every weekend The most important time of the year for us is June through July, when the lavender is in it's first bloom and so it is super important to us to have  wwoofers to help with our lavender harvest in June.  The soil in our vegetable garden is made from our own compost and the beds need to be seeded, planted, weeded and harvested, depending on the time of year. We do have a Kubota front loader for the heavy duty work, but there is always plenty of hand work to do. 

We are open May through October from 10am to 2pm and we have lots of events and workshops on the farm. Our Tiny Shop lavender boutique is designed to look like a medicine show wagon and our chicken coop looks like a gypsy caravan. We always need help to label product for the shop and to prep and clean up events. We have a large camper that will sleep 2 people or  comfortably. Our wwoofers have complete access to the bath and kitchen facilities are within our home. Our meals are vegetarian and we are willing to accommodate other preferences. Because we have chickens, we do eat a lot of fresh eggs! 

The Delaware River is a 10 minute drive away, as well as several towns that have farmer's markets and are tourist attractions. Our language is limited to English but we welcome bilingual guestsWe all start work in the field or garden early in the morning to avoid the midday heat. Our wwoofers do not work more than 6 hours a day. If you are staying longer than a week, we arrange a day off per week.We take breakfast and dinner together and we all clean up the kitchen together. The midday meal is more casual but we always clean up after ourselves.In the evening w

name: Julia - Internship opportunities at this certified organic farm near Lake Michigan
description:
We are an OCIA certified organic farm with 1.5 acres of apples, one acre of blueberries, and two acres of vegetable/cut flower crop including one hoophouse.  We also run a small Airbnb farm cottage.  Duties will include seeding, transplanting, pruning, weeding, spading, harvesting, washing, packing, mowing and spraying of the apple crop, attendance at occasional farmers' markets.  With WWOOFer help, we hope to expand vegetable growing on two acres surrounding the hoophouse.  Housing in our farm house, kitchen privileges, communal meals usually.

Sodus is a farm community about 7 miles east of Lake Michigan and the St. Joseph/Benton Harbor, Michigan area which has beaches, children's museum, a carousel, water feature play area, breweries, restaurants and an art museum.  This is a high summer tourist area drawing vacationers to the local wineries and breweries too, which also help support weekly farmers' markets and monthly flea market on the St. Joseph bluff area overlooking Lake Michigan.   

Organic methods:  composting, crop rotation, biological pest control, mulching, organic-approved spraying and fertilizing of apple trees and blueberries, seed starting in organic approved soil mix.

Certifications: OCIA (Organic Crop Improvement Association) is our certifier since 2010

Current projects: Seed transplanting in preparation to planting in hoophouse, spraying trees, some blueberry bush pruning, tilling, spading.   

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Either Metra in South Bend, Indiana or Metra in downtown St. Joseph MI

name: Danelle - Natural building school and permaculture homestead in the Southwestern Oregon rainforest.
description:
We Have a several natural building projects and we are pushing to get a lot done during the summer.
It is an opportunity to dabble in a wide variety of off grid living.
 We’ll be building a yurt, which would include tasks, felling trees, logging and  milling on site from our 365 acre forest. We are totally off grid but have all you need to be comfortable including hot showers and electricity and Internet and all that stuff!

If you want to learn about goats, we  have a small herd of Nigerian dwarfs.
They are milking goats and we milk twice a day. 

We are a married couple with five boys between the ages of 16and 5year old, and a 7 month baby girl.
 We homeschool, so it would be good if you love the sound of giggling and sometimes fighting children. We enjoy the outdoors, good music, books, crafting and gardening.

Your lodging is a private natural wood cabin in its own little corner of the property. The cabin has all its own amenities including a place to cook, running water, electricity and a composting toilet.

We are happy to provide you with some bulk food and produce from the land and one home cooked meal per day. We are not vegetarians but we are vegetarian friendly. On Fridays we usually have a pizza party.

We are looking for someone who is really eager to learn and work hard. We are looking for help weekday mornings for four hours per day.

We are surrounded by a 365 acre forest filled with mushrooms and other edible berries.  We have a reservoir near our property that has trout and is a great way to cool off. Our boys go fishing all the time.
The ocean is about a 30 minute drive. The closest store is about 7 minutes away driving. We have loads of fruit trees and blueberry bushes.

Come help us build and learn about off grid living, and enjoy a hike in the surrounding forest, garden, bake apple pies, and take excursions to the ocean.
 Let us know if you want to come help!

Thanks for looking :)

In the summer it is best to get the work done in the morning.
_wwoof_travel: Nearest airport is about 30 minutes away in North Bend. We are about a 10 minute drive from Coquille, where you can find a bus system.

name: Ole Johan - Family farm
description:
We are looking for help with the farm  and our animals. We have a diverse and small farm with different animals such as cows, sheep, horses, pigs, ducks, some chickens, rabbits and 2 dogs.

 The daily work with  is around 5-6  hours per day. You have to be able to work independently. Can be possible for people who need farm practice such as veterinary-students or if u like to learn about livestock and General small-scale farming. In the spring we also might have puppies which needs attention socializing, could possibly also mean sharing home with cute puppies.

For extended stays at our farm the 4 first weeks will be a trial periode.

Our farm is around 10 hectares with grassland, 7 hectares of pastures for the animals, and 190 hectares of woodland. We use organic compost and natural manure. We welcome WWOOFers the whole year . We have 9 cows and their calves, 3 sows with piglets that are free range on the fields and in the forest. 

We also have 15 sheep and lambs which we need help with to observe them and maintain their fences. We keep around 20 chickens to produce eggs and need help to look after the chickens and collect the eggs. Also some few geese and ducks which need to be let out every morning and inside every evening. In addition we also have some rabbits. 

We have a cosy cabin for WWOOFers to use with a kitchen, bathroom with a hot shower, toilet, washing machine and clean drinkable tap water. There are 2 beds in the bedroom (1 double and 1 single bed).  

We use mostly organic food when making meals. Some meals will be prepared and eaten together with us, some meals you will prepare yourself with the food we provide. There will be from 1 to 3 WWOOFers at the farm at the same time, some times up to 4. Tasks/activities can be with animals - feeding and observing and some fencing. If we have time left we will work in forest, removing brushes and making new pastures for the animals. Their could also be other types of work for example painting, maintenance a
_wwoof_travel:
6 km from nearest village with shops; 16 km from Trondheim (160 000 citizens, third biggest in Norway).

bikes, car, walking, bus

name: Lisa - Regenerative farming in the mountains of Northern BC
activities: Unterkunft: bedroom, campsite, shack, yurt-tipi, trailer
description:
We are looking for someone to help this spring
 
We have many projects right now,  cutting firewood, clearing land,  preparing gardens for planting this summer and so much more! There is always something to do. 

We have just moved to a lovely 40 acres in the Bulkley Valley in Northern BC in summer 2022!

Our focus is on regenerative agriculture and we are seeking to transform this slightly run down property into something well cared for and flourishing.

We have pigs, chickens and a milk cow. We used the animals, especially the pigs to restore the land, by using rotational grazing.

We are working on clearing land for gardens. Unfortunately because of the move we had only a small garden this summer but are already looking ahead and planning for next year.

Because of our location it is common to see wildlife, there are many bears, moose, deer, and elk in the area.

There are many recreation opportunities in the area for days off. Spend time swimming, canoeing or fishing on the private lake on the property, if you want to get away, there are many camping sites close by. There are endless mountain biking opportunities right out the back gate as well as quality groomed trails in the near by towns. Bring your own horse and spend your time exploring the back country on horseback. There are seemingly endless wild berries waiting to be harvested. In the winter you are steps away from cross country skiing and snowshoeing and a short drive from the local downhill ski resort.
We are about 20 minutes from the nearest town.

Meals and cooking and cleaning are shared. We tend to eat a lot of meat, as it is all home grown or locally hunted. We also love beans and vegetarian dishes, we both come from a vegetarian background. We strive to make or grow as much as our food as we can, and if we can't we will source it as local as possible.
 
We speak Spanish, English, and French. 

There is no smoking and no drugs allowed on the property nor in the house. We do drink occasionally, bu

name: Gabriel - Espacio familiar que busca compartir prácticas de agricultura sostenible para autoconsumo
description:
¡Esto es Casa Kigelia!
Somos una familia de 5 personas (3 adultos, un niño y un bebé).  La gestión del espacio la hacemos Ana Florencia (ilustradora, tatuadora, artista gráfica, músico y clown) y Gabriel (consultor agroforestal).
Estamos en el pueblo de San Ambrosio Chalmita, Ocuilan, Estado de México. A 20 minutos de Malinalco. 
Aquí viven también 5 perros, 3 gatos, 2 caballos y varias gallinas. 
En este espacio gestionamos una huerta de hortaliza, un vivero agroforestal, un Sistema Agroforestal Sintrópico (SAFS), y un camp de perros (negocio de pensión de perros). Nos gusta mucho compartir lo que hemos aprendido y aprender más de quienes nos rodean.

Cada día hacemos:
-Alimentar y atender a los caballos y gallinas.

Y otras actividades como: 
-Manejo del huerto de hortaliza
-Podas de árboles frutales y forestales.
-Manejo del Sistema agroforestal sintrópico (SAFS)
-Elaboración de compostas
-Elaboración de fertilizantes naturales
-Siembra de plántulas y árboles en el vivero
-Acariciar perritos
-Recolectar semillas
-Mantenimiento a espacios y construcciones 
-Acariciar gatitos

En algunos casos puede haber:
-Proyectos de bioconstrucción

El alojamiento es un cuarto amplio, cómodo e independiente de la casa principal. 
Cuenta con cocina y baño (seco) propio con regadera (húmeda) caliente. 
Internet de buena velocidad. preciosa vista y un fogón de leña para el frío. 

En casa cocinamos diariamente alimentos saludables, deliciosos y vegetarianos/veganos con productos que cosechamos y compramos en el mercado local. 
Los desayunos y comidas serán con toda la familia, con cenas opcionales.

Nos gusta mucho la gente con mente y corazones abiertos a vivir la experiencia en la montaña. Tenemos muchos instrumentos musicales, nos gusta cocinar, sembrar, y acariciar animales.
Nos gusta montar a caballo (a nuestra yegua Mora), y podríamos organizar cabalgatas con los WOOFERs interesados. 

A 10 minutos a pie hay un gran bosque con muchos senderos, un río con pozas para nadar, y
_wwoof_travel: Estación de Autobuses de Chalma (Flecha Roja)

name: Caterina - Azienda agricola
description:
Sicilia Occidentale: nostra attività principale è la raccolta olive da metà ottobre ai primi di novembre a pochi chilometri dal tempio di Segesta (Trapani). Per il resto dell'anno svolgiamo le normali attività stagionali. Siamo una piccola azienda biologica che produce olio extravergine di oliva di altissima qualità, con raccolta precoce e a mano delle olive. La sistemazione dei soci-ospiti è prevista nella casa padronale in camere matrimoniali o a due letti, e prevede naturalmente i pasti. Punti di interesse: templi di Segesta e di Selinunte, città medioevale di Erice, Terme Segestane, isole Egadi, Mozia, Satiro di Mazara del Vallo, spiaggia di San Vito Lo Capo, riserva naturalistica dello Zingaro. Parliamo inglese, poco francese e spagnolo.
******************************
Western Sicily: Our main activity is olive harvesting from mid October - beginning November, for the rest of the year we carry out usual seasonal jobs. Situated at Calatafimi Segesta, a few Kilometres from the Temple of Segesta (Trapani), we are a family run farm which produces high quality extra-virgin olive oil, harvesting quickly and early by hand. Accommodation in the farm house, in double beds, or rooms with two singles, with meal eaten together. Points of interest: temples of Segesta and Selinunte, mediaeval city of Erice, hot springs of the Terme Segestane, Egadi islands, island of Mozia, Satiro di Mazara del Vallo, beach of San Vito Lo Capo, Zingaro nature reserve. We speak English a little French and Spanish.

Progetti attuali: Fruits ,Olives

name: Yelena - Organic Lemon and Avocado Orchard - 30min from beach, 1hr from LAX airport
Roberto - Family farm
description:
Es un centro de Aprendizaje Ecologico, que difunde una propuesta sanitaria-ecologica: el baño seco-portable, que no usa agua, y que los desechos se convierten en recursos. Se encuentra en proceso de construcción de un pulmón verde, con mas de 250 especies nativas o adaptadas a la localidad. Contamos con un invernadero que no está en funcionamiento en este minuto. Contiene muchas especies de semillas de cactus, árboles y plantas comestibles. 



Organic methods: Reciclaje de aguas, utilización de desechos orgánicos. 

Certifications: En proceso de certificación 

Current projects: Proyecto en curso: la construcción nuevas dependencias, activar el banco de semillas, y promoción y difusión del baño seco.
_wwoof_travel: Buses Interprovinciales en Vallenar.  Aeropuertos en La Serena y Copiapó.

name: Brian - Family home and garden - rural We are on  a large river with access to kayaks/canoes. Outdoor sauna
description:
Hello WWOOFers,

We are Brian and Elaine. We are both retired (Elaine is a retired physiotherapist and Brian is a retired home builder) and now spend our time caring for our 25-acre property, where we grow vegetables such as tomatoes, squash, and more.

We are former WWOOFers ourselves! We WWOOFed in Portugal about six years ago, and our daughter has also been a WWOOFer. We have traveled to many countries over the years and were Couchsurfing hosts for a long time as well. We truly enjoy meeting people from around the world and sharing experiences.

Our operation is small, and we use local organic growing methods, including organic compost and manure.

We are currently building a barn, caring for our vegetable gardens, and doing general maintenance around the property.

Internet access is available.

name: Jose Israel - Sustainable ecoturism property with organic gardens, animals, and passion for ecological practices.
description:
We are a rural family from Nayarit that lives and cares for Eco Finca Pachamama, a small natural sanctuary with animals, gardens, and mountains that has become our home and life project. After working more than 20 years in the United States, I felt the call of my land and the fields, returned to Nayarit with my wife and daughter, and purchased land that we transform into a paradise a little more each day.

We live a simple lifestyle close to the earth, where daily work in the fields, caring for water and trees, and community relationships with the local Cora indigenous people are part of our identity. We have animals including ducks, chickens, donkeys, goats, dogs, and a horse. We also maintain organic gardens, vegetable plots, and native trees for biodiversity and regeneration.

Our vision is to create a simple, self-sustainable place where people reconnect with nature, themselves, and the local community. We welcome visitors and volunteers like friends, sharing walks, campfires, farm activities, and weekend retreats - more like visiting friends than traditional tourism. We believe in respect for Pachamama, responsible ecotourism, and cultural exchange as tools for learning together and building a more conscious life.

Our typical day involves a mix of farm work and nature connection. You will help with:

Farm animals: Feeding and caring for ducks, chickens, goats, donkeys, dogs, and our horse. This includes daily feeding, water changes, and basic animal care.

Gardens and vegetables: Maintaining organic gardens and vegetable plots, learning about companion planting, seasonal harvesting, and sustainable food production.

Trails and maintenance: Walking the land, maintaining paths and access to the waterfalls, cleaning senderos (trails).

Construction and repairs: Depending on skills, helping with simple construction, carpentry, metalwork, and masonry to improve facilities and infrastructure.

Community and activities: Sharing campfires, meals, and conversations wit
_wwoof_travel: Tepic (30 minutes away) - Xalapa International Airport / Bus Station. Nearby: Atonalisco

name: Sylvanus - Avocado farming fruits
description:
Hello WWOOFers,my name is Sylvanus a 23 year old adult I with my partner deal with fruit farming commonly AVOCADOES and doing poultry, here in Njombe- Tanzania.

We manage avocadoes through irrigation, normally in the morning and evening hours.
We prefer using organic manure for better growth results in our farm, we use hand hoes (Jembe - local name), cow ploughing mostly but tractors are also available.

We arrange and eat together, breakfast, lunch and dinner

I enjoy listening music while cooking and weekends I go for swimming and have fun with friends and refreshing our minds for the better feelings 

However, we have good tourists attraction centres 
Like Luhuji (or Luhuiji) waterfall: perfect for picnics or filming , also Nyumba Nitu Forest & Caves
	•	A mystical natural forest near caves tied to local history and legends, a few kilometers from Njombe town  ￼.
	•	The nearby “Lwivala Stone” resembles the shape of Africa and even has mythic inscriptions .

Also Historical & Cultural Sites
	•	Visit old German-built structures: churches, courts, administrative buildings and war memorials in Njombe town.

Whats more special is knowing our social and cultural dances ,Traditional dance performances, Craft workshops & markets
Hands-on craft, social markets
And Beekeeping farm visits
Teachings on beekeeping tradition & income.

name: Nate (Nathan) and Tara - A cooperative ecological agriculture project using native wisdom and regenerative principals. CSA.
description:
***Please read our full farm description and profile before requesting a visit***

We are currently full for the 2026 season. We are accepting inquiries for the 2027 season, or self-contained volunteers with building experience. We give priority to full season stays. We may consider shorter visits, especially if you have pertinent experience. You'll get the most out of a longer stay.

We are holistic land stewards on our 16 acre regenerative agriculture project and CSA farm. We've been on this property since late summer 2015. It was previously an irrigated grass hay pasture that was cut, bailed and removed. We have been transforming it into a bio-intensive, diverse landscape, bursting with productive perennials, annuals, native plants, animals and habitat for wildlife. We implement various permaculture systems to promote sustainability, provide for ourselves and provide food for others in our community that share a vision of a healthy planet. We currently raise Nigerian Dwarf goats for milk, we have over 100 laying hens (along with their roosters), a breeding flock of heritage turkeys, sheep, other poultry, and a few cats.  We put our animals to work as a management strategy to build healthy soils. In return, they live a happy and healthy lifestyle on rotated pastures. We sell our farm products through Community Supported Agriculture (CSA) and online custom ordering. We have about 3.5 acres of alfalfa we grow organically as winter feed for our ungulates. There are always projects slated to work on.  We have a long wish list of projects to complete, such as seed starting house, improved wash & pack area, a farm store, yurt build, and many more. We also operate two 30' x 96' high tunnel greenhouses for season extension. We have an endless list of fun projects and tasks which we will accomplish with your help. We are a small-scale, slow-growth type of farm. Expect to be intimate with the animals and land here. We wish to be very intentional and diligent in our actions.

name: Kahualani - Rainforest Agroforestry
description:
Aloha! Here’s a little bit about us and what a visitor should expect. Please read the description all the way through, and respond accordingly if interested. 

Who:
Lani and Hobbes (dog) and Bob & Soli (jungle cats). I have been hosting people for decades and through WWOOF for the past year with mostly positive experiences. 

Where: 
Six+ acres at 2,000’ elevation on the slopes of Mauna Loa. The property is 25 miles south of Kailua-Kona. Please do an internet search to see where that is before contacting. It’s rural and if you wish to do outside activities you will likely want to rent a car.

We have been organically certified in the past but anyone who goes through the process can tell you it's an expensive, time consuming procedure with very little meat on the bones, so to speak. 

What:
Reclaiming the rainforest and buildings from invasive plants, planting food crops and reducing dependence on the current food system. I have identified over 150 edible plants on the property, many with medicinal and herbalist qualities, and love discovering new ways to prepare them. 

How: 
Weeding, clearing invasive plants, chopping firewood, repairing existing infrastructure.

I do not have a booth at the farmer's market or sell any products at this time. The property has been fallow for many years so more restoration projects than a typical farm or garden life one might expect. If you get here sooner, it’s building and painting. If you get here later, hopefully building a chicken tractor. 

What is expected from you: 
Cleanliness, flexibility, a good attitude and joy for life for six hours a day, two of which are to be spent gathering and processing food for meals. I try to accommodate individual skill sets and interests as much as possible while prioritizing the projects at the time. 

Where you will stay: 
In the rainforest and it's a jungle out here. This is not the Hawai'i most people imagine. Expect bugs. And mold and mud and spiders and slugs, pigs and rats and moss and m

name: ALIN - Bee farm  with big green house and reforestation plans
description:
Hello my name is Alin and I am passionate about beekeeping, this year I had my first try at collecting bee venom and I want to grow in this domain . 
I will start my activity in the spring of 2026 and will need help with the growing of fruits and vegs in my green house . Garden work and beekeeping mostly .

The activities will be garden related and beekeeping. There is always something to do

We will eat together what you feel comfortable with . Mostly local food from farmers

I love good music while working , cycling and sunshine

name: Lavinia - Small family homestead
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):

We are small family farm in the heart of Transylvania, run by me (Lavinia) and my son Joel. Since 1999 we have built the small organic farm from the ground up, and although we made some changes over the years, we strive to be as self sufficient and sustainable as possible. We have 2 dairy cows, Malva and Rosina, whose milk we turn into varying delicious dairy products (soft and hard cheese, yogurts, cream and butter), chicken, a dog, and three cats. 
We have a big garden that requires much work and dedication, we use heirloom seeds and help preserve local varieties. Every spring we harvest rose petals from our small damascus rose plantation and turn them into rose jam and other products. 

On our farm we have received volunteers 2004 and have enjoyed making friends and meaningful connections with people all over the world. The exchange of ideas, knowledge and culture that come with welcoming people into our family has enriched our lives. We can welcome a couple or a single person at a time, as we have one small room to provide as accommodation.

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:

If you are interested in homesteading skills you will benefit greatly from your stay with us, as we strive to be as self sufficient as possible. Among others you can learn and improve your:...
gardening 
animal husbandry
milk processing
preserving
bread making
field work
...skills.

3. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?

You will be welcome to participate in whatever areas interest you most, as we are very eager to include you in our family life. The activities vary from season to season of course, in general we need help with tending to the garden and the animals e.g. sowing, weeding, harvesting, mucking out, milking feeding, and of course the general upkeep of the farm, mending f

name: Christiane - Schamanischer Selbstversorgerhof mit Seifensiederei und Gemüsegarten
description:
Hast du Lust mit anzupacken? Bei Wind und Wetter draußen zu sein? Zu lernen und zu arbeiten? Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig.

Ich teile gerne all mein Wissen über Kräuter und Pflanzen, über Selbstversorgung und DIY, über Seife, Tiere und mein schamanisches Wissen und verschiedene Rituale und Zeremonien. 

1. Unsere Seifensiederei stellt natürliche Seife und Creme her und verkauft diese an Geschäfte, Privatpersonen und auf Kunsthandwerkermärkten, Festivals, oder Mittelaltermärkten.
2. In der Töpferei werden natürliche Tonprodukte mit Kräutern getöpfert und verziert. Es werden Kurse fürs energetische Töpfern oder fürs töpfern mit Pflanzenenergien gegeben.
3. In unserer Seelenflüsterei behandle ich auf Schamanische Weise Menschen und Tiere, ich leite Meditationen und Zeremonien an und bringe den Menschen in meinen Workshops verschiedene schamanische Techniken bei.
Es gibt Platz für:
-Bei uns sind Kinder und Tiere herzlich willkommen, müssen aber angemeldet werden 
-Eine Unterkunft bietet Platz für 1 Personen 
-Auch für deinen Van, Wohnmobil oder dein Dachzelt haben wir einen Platz 

Deine Unterkunft:

-Der Bauwagen bietet dir Platz für ein Bett 90x200cm und eine freie Fläche mit Meditationskissen und einem kleinen Ofen für ein entspanntes Feuer. Es gibt dort keinen Strom.
Essen:
Du bist herzlich willkommen als Teil unserer Familie bei uns zu essen, egal ob Vegan, vegetarisch, zuckerfrei oder als allesesser. Wir wechseln uns mit dem zubereiten der Mahlzeiten ab und erledigen auch die Küchenarbeit gerne gemeinsam. Wir wünschen uns ein fleißiges miteinander.
Möchtest du lieber in Ruhe essen, dann darfst du dir gerne etwas mit in deine Unterkunft nehmen und es dir dort gemütlich machen.

Das lernst du bei uns:
Ich bringe dir gerne mein Wissen über autarkes Leben und der Selbstversorgung bei, dazu gehört auch das häusliche herstellen von Brot und der Gemüseverarbeitung. Die Angebote sind verschieden und richten sich nach der Länge deines Aufenthaltes. Wenn es gerade pas

name: Chris - Homesteading & Seed Saving on the South Shore of Nova Scotia
description:
Come and WWOOF with us May-November, variable lengths of stay.  We welcome your interest!

Yonder Hill Farm is tucked away on a dead-end gravel road on the South Shore of Nova Scotia- 10 km to downtown Bridgewater, 20 km to Lunenburg, Mahone Bay, or LaHave, 25 km to beaches, and less than 100 km to Halifax. We have 20 acres of fertile soil on a gently sloping drumlin hillside, 200 acres of forests & wetlands to explore, and frontage on a pristine spring fed lake. Wildlife is abundant, though mostly kept from the main farm area by our trusty farm dog, and includes ospreys (nesting within view of the field), owls, loons, eagles, songbirds, deer, raccoons, porcupines, pheasants, wood cocks, coyotes, rabbits, and the occasional roaming bear or bobcat. We have a heritage post & beam barn, outbuildings and a 20'x48' greenhouse. There is a century old apple orchard & wild blueberries in the field, and we are in the process of establishing a diverse perennial crop system of fruit, berries, nuts, & herbs. We have around 2 acres in cultivation for annual vegetable & grain crops (1 acre fenced).

Our primary commercial product is ecologically grown vegetable, grain, herb & flower seeds- we currently maintain approx. 200 varieties which we sell through our website, retail locally, and wholesale to a regional seed company. We grow a homestead-scale vegetable garden, selling excess produce to local cafes. We also raise ducks (eggs & meat) and sometimes pigs for home use.

Our WWOOFing opportunity is ideal for people who love to spend most of their time outdoors, enjoy physical work, have interest in living off the land, enjoy the company of children, and are comfortable camping out. We work hard and like to relax by a campfire, play music, and swim in the lake. We have canoes & kayaks to take out on the lake, and bicycles available to borrow for sightseeing. We are well connected socially with the active & growing rural community on the South Shore. In a typical year, there are f

name: Carl - Join The Vineyard By Boudreau — where hard work, fresh air, and good food grow alongside the grapes
description:
About Us

Welcome! We’re so glad you’ve found our growing vineyards and small tent camping area.

We (Saeng & Carl) live right here on the property while building our home and winery. Saeng bravely escaped Laos as a teenager and brings her passion for gardening and growing exotic plants to the vineyard. She speaks three languages—Lao, English, and Spanish. Carl is a software architect with 20+ years of experience, following 21 years of service in the U.S. Coast Guard. He’s rescued lives at sea, intercepted hundreds of tons of illegal drugs, and even confiscated 200 parakeets (yes, really!).

These days, you’ll usually find us planting vines, tending the greenhouse, or building the structures that will support our future winery. We love meeting new people, learning their stories, and sharing a meal or a campfire when the day’s work is done.

How We House & Feed WWOOFers
    * WWOOFers can stay in a private tiny cabin or camp with our tents or their tents.  Fire rings are provided for quiet stargazing nights in the campground.
    *  Meals are included — we provide hearty home-cooked dinners several times a week, and  you can cook your own meals too. We enjoy sharing food from our garden and welcome you to join in cooking if you like. Saeng (loa\wife) - is trying to be vegan.

Typical Farming Activities
    * Spring: soil prep, planting vines, building trellises, greenhouse projects.
    * Summer: training shoots, weeding (we try to practice organic gardening, so weeding is a big part of the tasks), composting, irrigation, and canopy management.
    * Harvest (late summer–fall): picking grapes, fruit sorting, winemaking basics.
    * Fall Wrap-Up: mulching vines, winter prep, equipment cleaning and storage.

    * Days usually begin with a short briefing, followed by 4–6 hours of hands-on work with breaks. Afternoons are for lighter projects, personal time, or helping us with creative projects like marketing or building.

What You’ll Learn
    * We want WWOOFers to le
_wwoof_travel: Fly into St. Cloud MN. Regional Airport (STC), or bus into Pine City, MN.

name: JOHAN - Distillerie artisanale d'huiles essentielles  ⚗️🇷🇪
description:
La Fontaine Aromatique est une DISTILLERIE artisanale d'huiles essentielles, située sur l'île de La Réunion à Saint Leu. 
La propriété de plusieurs hectares est constituée principalement de plantes aromatiques, à parfum et médicinales que nous distillons en HUILES ESSENTIELLES.   Tout est distillé sur place grâce à  un magnifique ALAMBIC en cuivre bientôt centenaire! Régulièrement sont organisées des journées portes-ouvertes avec accueil touristique et pédagogique autour de la distillation. Il y a également des vergers de manguiers, papayers, fruits de la passion.
Je cultive et nous cultivons ensemble l'agroécologie! Distiller c'est une d'abord pour moi une passion et ensuite un métier. L'activité demande une vraie bonne condition physique et la tête sur les épaules... elle convient aussi bien aux femmes qu'aux hommes ou aux hommes qu'aux femmes! A partir du moment ou vous êtes motivés(ées), curieux(ses), courageux(ses), investis et partageurs(geuses) d'idées! Attention:  je reprécise, les missions nécessitent une bonne condition physique et résistance à la chaleur surtout l'été de Novembre à avril (il fait 30 à 35 degrés et 80% d'humidité sur le terrain). Dans la terre, la boue ou la poussière... Mais toujours avec le parfum de la nature ! 
Participer à l'activité agricole et à la distillerie ce n'est pas que distiller ! Nous abordons énormément de domaines:
apprentissage de la distillation des huiles essentielles, pédagogie, cueillette de plantes ou de fruits, semis, multiplication, bouturage, irrigation, plantation, entretien des champs et des vergers, désherbage manuel, entretien à la débroussailleuse à dos thermique, taille-haie thermique, récolte, distillation (the best!), mécanique, construction, cohésion d'équipe, accueil touristique lors des journées portes-ouvertes, conditionnement des huiles essentielles et hydrolats en flacons et  bien plus encore suivant la période et les actions du moment!
Les Wwoofers/ Wwoofeuses sont accueillis(ies) dans un grand par

name: Silas & Louise - Small-scale family farm with sheep and chickens (meat and eggs).
description:
We are Silas, Louise and our 12-year-old Hilda Plum. We live with two big indoor/outdoor dogs. We rotationally graze chickens and sheep producing high quality food (lamb, chicken and eggs) for our community, with great respect and thanks for our animals.  Pasturing on our own land and a couple of neighbouring properties, we focus on soil and animal health. We are not certified organic, but use organic methods and feed.

Silas spends his full days farming, while Louise and Hilda have homeschooling, camps, and travel on the go as well. Depending on the timing, you may find the whole family at home, or just Silas. We are also close friends with our nextdoor neighbours, who run a yurt business and have two kids. And we have a couple of friends who live full- and part-time on our land, sharing the kitchen and bathroom in the main house.

Silas studied at the Linnea School for Ecological gardening, has his permaculture certificate and over 20 years of experience gardening and farming. Opportunities to learn and participate on the farm include, caring for our pasture lands and animals, moving electric fencing and portable shelters, egg collection, watering and feeding, packing and delivering orders and most importantly, animal snuggles. Some other tasks may include building compost, growing and tending annual and perennial crops in the garden, tending fruit trees, blueberries and currants and processing food and herbs for the winter. We usually have some small building projects on the go as well, as we maintain and create new portable shelters for the animals.

WWOOFers will be invited to share and contribute to family meals in the main house. Breakfast is usually simple affair, and we sit down together for a proper lunch and supper prepared together. We try to eat local and seasonal foods whenever possible. Depending on the season and length of stay, accommodations may be available in a private room in our house, in a yurt, or in your own tent/camper. WWOOFers will have a

name: Patricia - Cattle Ranch, Ranch/ Livestock Farm, Family farm, Women-operated
description:
If you love mountains, wildlife and being outdoors then this may be the place for you. No previous farm experience is necessary just your enthusiasm at this small cattle ranch on the edge of the Rocky Mountains. Wwoofers who are energetic and willing to try their hand at everything will do well here. Our farm is situated in an isolated location so we are looking for mature people who are ready to participate in our daily life here. We have decided to instate a minimum age limit of 21 years. It is also necessary to be flexible as the daily tasks may change from day to day, and so much depends upon the weather. The tasks vary with each season. We like help for a minimum of 6 hours, 5 days a week. There are certain times of year when we are really busy when more help would be greatly appreciated, haying season, etc... Although many tasks are very physical on this farm we also need help with some gentler tasks such as weeding, cooking, and other odd jobs. We can usually accomodate all types of wwoofers. If you would like more information please email. And please no drugs or alcohol while staying on our farm.

Certifications: PACS 16-458

Internet access available

name: Mary Elizabeth - Our big Straw Bale House where the Blue Ridge Parkway meets Virginia's Skyline Drive.
description:
Hello Woofers, I am Mary Elizabeth and am married to David.  We are a committed Couple and both just beginning our senior years at age 70.  We have been hosting traveling guests and newcomers for several years since our two adult children are living independently.  We have a big beautiful Husky dog, Buddy and a very independent cat, named Felix and are a LGBTQ welcoming family. 

We have built our unique family home and resurrected this property to be what we share today.  We want you to feel at home and welcome you to learn about geo-thermal heating and eco-friendly building methods, as well as local farming in the beautiful, verdant Rockfish Valley of Nelson County.  We have lived here since 2000 and happy to be your hosts. We can much provide local information that is of interest to you. You’ll be learning much about our straw-bale home construction and it's R-50 natural insulation.  We are very familiar with all the area offers including the Shenandoah Valley and local communities.   

Our daily attention goes to the kitchen garden needs in cultivation and harvesting seasons.  The  surrounding grounds need care of the trees and pasture throughout the growing season.   We have a large brood of chickens that spend the summer in the fenced pasture on the north side and the winter months on the south side in the garden after it has been harvested.  Their roosting home is called a "Chicken Tractor" and is on wheels to be moved as needed  Collecting eggs and providing feed are daily needs with fresh hay required regularly.   These are the regular daily activities of our farm  we invite you to participate with.

Our free range hens provide fresh eggs for sale and nutritious meals.  We typically have a fresh smoothie drink to start our day - with fresh fruit, almond milk and kale or wheat grass!  Our pantry is stocked with cereal, and non-perishable grocery items for lunch and our dinner in usually a prepared and shared meal.   We have a big modern kitchen and shared ea
_wwoof_travel: Both Staunton and Charlottesville, Virginia have Amtrak stations. Charlottesville has intercity bus and regional airport

name: Brandi - 10-acre agritourism farm, petting zoo and row crops
description:
My name is Brandi. I live in Hephzibah, GA.  I am developing a 10-acre organic farm and seeking short-term and long-term WWOOFers who want to help build a sustainable, inclusive farm rooted in education, land stewardship, and agritourism. Wallace Farm is an established name in the CSRA (Central Savannah River Area), already operating as a traveling animal petting zoo for schools and community events, with plans to expand into a permanent on-site petting zoo and strengthen mobile programming.

An average day would look like feeding and maintaining the animals of the petting zoo. Working with the animals that are there for meat sales. Organically growing seasonal vegetables and herbs in crop rows with drip irrigation. Using regenerative farming practices to naturally amend soil.

Each WWOOFer receives a weekly omnivore food box containing healthy, basic staples intended to feed one WWOOFer for a minimum of seven days. Typical items include rice, pasta, dry beans or lentils, eggs, bread, cooking oil, canned vegetables, canned tuna or chicken, oatmeal or cereal, basic seasonings, and bottled water.
WWOOFers are encouraged to trade weekly staples with one another if it helps meet personal preferences. As harvests begin, farm-grown fruits and vegetables will be incorporated into the weekly food boxes. All meals are cooked and prepared individually by each WWOOFer. There are no group or community meals. Any additional food, snacks, or beverages are at the WWOOFer expense. Dietary preferences and restrictions, including vegan, omnivore, and allergies, are coordinated before arrival.

I love reading, teaching homesteading and farming practices, and trying experimental farm practices. 
Nearby, the things to do are the Augusta Riverwalk, local markets, restaurants, and live music, Phinizy Swamp Nature Park, Savannah River access, Clarks Hill (J. Strom Thurmond) Lake, Day trips to Aiken, South Carolina, and Columbia, South Carolina

name: Keto - Natural winery and farm-to-table restaurant in Imereti
description:
We are Gogita and Keto, a young couple running a small, artisanal winery and family-style restaurant in Terjola, Imereti (West Georgia).

Gogita is a generational organic winegrower and winemaker and a pioneer of Georgia’s modern natural wine movement. He founded a small artisanal cellar in 2008 and has been producing and exporting all over the world low-intervention natural wines.

Keto runs a women-led, farm-to-table restaurant in a 200-year-old traditional Imeretian Oda house with her sister-in-law, where guests enjoy village type dishes made of seasonal ingredients from local farms owned by female farmers and producers.

Since we own several plots of land our goal is to build our own organic veggie garden to supply our restaurant and later to employ locals to look after it, we'd also like to set up animal farms  and care for chickens, pigs, and cows.
Our home and restaurant are located in a 4000 sq.m yard and we need people to help us plan landscaping and do general yard work.

Our main work season is from March–April through the fall, and during this time we are urgently looking for help to plant, maintain, and develop our organic vegetable garden; build raised beds for herbs and flowers; and through different DIY projects help shape the landscape and organize yard.

Also, during this period we need to build wooden tables and chairs for the restaurant, to accommodate more people in our beautiful garden and support ongoing projects around the winery, restaurant, and garden through woodwork, carpentry, welding & general construction.

This is an ideal opportunity for people who want to live off of land, grow their own ecologically clean food, and take part in building and growing the community of Terjola.

At our place, you’ll gain cultural experience through:

– cooking and sharing traditional Imeretian home dishes, farm-to-table practices, seasonal eating, cheese adn khachapuri making,,and traditional food preservation (the way our grandmothers used to make the

name: Pariwut - Farmstay accommodation services with agricultural tourism activities
description:
🌾 Welcome to NachanSawang Farmstay

NachanSawang Farmstay is a small, family-run farm in Yasothon, northeastern Thailand. We don’t have fancy things—just an honest way of life, surrounded by fields, chickens, dogs, and the laughts.

We're not offering a perfect rural escape. We're inviting you to help us build it.

🏡 Life on the Farm
We live simply. No air-conditioning. Yes, it gets hot. There are insects. If you’re allergic to bugs or need modern comforts to feel safe and happy, this may not be the place for you.

But if you enjoy the sound of frogs at night, a breeze through banana trees, and sharing moments together, you will love it here.

We have a homey vegetable garden and plants to take care of in daily life. But if you are looking for RICE FARMING EXPERIENCE, you must visit us only in June. After that we will let the rice grow itself untill harvest time in November. SO! please ask us in advance about the season you’ll be visiting. However, we love keeping our hand busy and learning by doing. Which means we have many projects is going on such as :

A red ant egg collection zone (yes, ant eggs!)

A small vineyard

A seedling nursery

A chicken coop over fish pond

🍚 Food: Cook Together, Eat Together
We don’t have a chef, but we eat together often. And you are always invited! If you’re interested to learn Thai cooking, we can share it. And if you can share your tradition food, we would love try them!
We provide local ingredients, mostly from our farm—fresh veggies, rice and herbs, that you can prepare your own meals in our 
 kitchen. Otherwise, we are more than happy to give you a ride to get what you want but you must take response on the spend.

🛏️ Accommodation
Our rooms are basic, clean, and made out of wood. No air-con,  just fans and mosquito nets. It's very breezy in the morning and night.
REMINDER; we are in the nature. There will be heat. There will be some bugs.

🧠 Language
We speak Thai and very limited English. We use Google Translate, and a lot of

name: Jonathan - Regenerative Agroforestry project, learning to incorporate goats, mini-cows and chickens.
description:
Hey!  I'm Jonathan.  I live with my wife, Jessica on an 80 hectare property about an hour outside of Brasília, Brazil in the middle of the Brazilian Cerrado.  Our longtime friend, Sarah, partners with us.  We have one small indoor dog, 5 larger outdoor dogs, and 2 recovering feral cats (mostly outdoor)!
We raise goats, mostly for milk.  We just acquired a mini Jersey cow and bull.  We have several chicken yards - for eggs, pest management and at times meat.
Our primary agricultural project is our syntropic food forest.  We are attempting to establish a regenerative silvopasture system.  We also have raised organic garden beds for more traditional produce.  We have one neighbor who works for us, who tends to this area, and helps care for the livestock.  We do not use any synthetic fertilizers or pesticides and are currently working on our organic produce certification.
We love the Cerrado biome!  One of our goals is to incorporate as many native species into our production as possible.  We are constantly learning about "new" native fruits and plants!  We never stop learning!!

Typical activities include planting, pruning and weeding prepping compost bins, loosening soil for new beds, prepping rows for planting, gathering seeds etc.
We are also constantly trying to find creative ways to most fully use whatever is in abundance: drying, fermenting, freezing, processing into other products... we try to waste as little as possible.  
An average day could look like harvesting beans, then shelling... or pruning undergrowth or overgrowth to use as either fodder or to build soil biomass.  It could also involve collecting materials to build a static compost bin, then building the bin.  Another possibility could be to tend seedlings and weed inside the plant nursery....  The possibilities are nearly endless!  
We tend to work mornings until around noon and then only return to work outside after 3ish until dark.  So, any window in those times is great.  We normally stay indoors

name: Majvik - Communal Living Group, Mixed Farm, Farm, Woodlot, Community farm
activities: Unterkunft: shack, trailer, bedroom, campsite, yurt-tipi
description:
Majvik is a versatile biodynamic farm community in Sipoo right next to Helsinki. We cultivate vegetables (potatoes, carrots, parsnip, onions, beetroot, cabbages etc.), berries, apples, flowers, herbs and cereals on our fields the size of 20ha. We have a DIY greenhouse for tomatoes and cucumbers. We have lovely Finnish Eastern Finncattle cows (Kyyttö) as well as chickens, dogs and cats. In summer cows graze freely on the pastures. There is a self-service organic shop at the farm. Majvik has a long history with biodynamic farming. The previous farmers cultivated the land for 30 years and were pioneers of Finnish biodynamic farming. The current farmer, Atte has now cultivated in Majvik for 12 years and he follows the same principles.
 
Free time activities:
Life in Majvik is peaceful and communal. There are always customers, friends, schools and green-care groups stopping by.
There is also a lot of beautiful nature around the farm and it’s really close to the shore of the Baltic Sea. There is an old forest and bed rock outcrops surrounding the fields, and if you’re lucky you’ll meet a moose or deer family on the way to the fields. 
For your free time there is swimming and fishing possibilities nearby and we also have an old wooden sauna, which is normally warmed once a week.  We have a tepee tent where we gather for special evenings. There is 10min walk to a bus stop from where it’s a 20min ride to Helsinki, so the possibilities are almost endless. Sipoonkorpi national park is nearby. Porvoo is the second oldest city in Finland and is best known for its Old Wooden Town.

Job description:
Everything in Majvik is produced biodynamically, which means organic farming with close attention paid to the cycles of the surrounding nature. We use biodynamic preparations and follow the biodynamic sowing calendar. It’s also of great importance that our farming is self-sufficient, for example, we produce our own fertilizers. Most of our work is done by hand but we do use some old ma
_wwoof_travel: Farm can be easily reached by public transportation (busses 841, 850, 845 from East Central, Helsinki or 870, 840, 845, 835 from Kamppi bus station, Helsinki). We can also negotiate pick up from the Helsinki-Vantaa airport.

name: Chong Kee - Towards a post-capitalist regenerative community
description:
Important: Please read this through carefully before contacting me. Thanks!

Why: Why am I doing this? Because I want to live sustainably, improve the soil and land, and be more in touch with Mother Earth. I want to grow my food as much as possible, and to leave a legacy of at least this small parcel of land so that it will always be a permaculture homestead or even an international community for people after me. If I'm lucky I might be able to do even more, but achieving this will already make me happy. So come for a short or long visit, perhaps even stay long term and be part of this nascent ecovillage / homestead commons.

The farm: Our 16-acre farm is located on the shores of Lake Labish. This is the seventh year of my farming adventure. There is a sizable vegetable plot and a nascent (and expanding) orchard with about 30 fruit and nut trees, plus chicken, ducks, and goats.

The Work: Work includes lots of weeding and heavy shoveling, making compost, spraying with compost tea, planting, harvesting, etc.  We expect wwoofers to do 5-6 hours of work each day, 5 days per week. Weekends are rest days. Generally it is good form to expect the weekends off after you have worked for at least 5 days. Please do not come on Friday, leave on Tuesday and expect to have Saturday and Sunday as free time. We are happy to host you from Friday to Tuesday or other similar arrangements as long as you are willing to work for at least 5 days straight before taking your 2 rest days.  Typically work day is 7:30am - 12:30pm. We have a short powwow before the day begins to plan the work ahead and a short debrief at dinner to go over the work done and lessons learned.

Food: Diet is healthy organic with one meal per day consisting of animal protein (most likely, egg, chicken, pork, or fish), and the other meal being vegetarian. The cuisine style is mainly Asian (Chinese, Thai, Japanese, Indian) with a smattering of American and European cooking. We eat what we grow as much as possible and

name: Max - House and garden
description:
We are taking steps for a low-impact lifestyle. We are insulating our house and growing vegetables. We are also trying to establish a forest garden and getting our old watermill going again. Main tasks would be tree planting, maintaining tree guards and mulchmats, vegetable gardening, digging, chopping up firewood and maybe help in the house. We have no car but are happy to meet WWOOFers at the local bus stop and take luggage.
School holdays are our preferred time.
The mill stream runs next to the house in a quiet, sheltered, wooded spot. Very cold in winter, note we get about on bikes in the snow, but sometimes warm in summer. If you are very lucky you might see red squirrels, woodpeckers, wildcat and deer from the house. Borrow one our many and varied bikes and explore the rolling countryside. 9 miles from the coast where you can go surfing of you have a thick enough wetsuit. Room to park live-in vehicles. No midges.

People with lime mortar pointing skills very welcome, and keen learners too.

Host interested in Transition and peak oil research etc.

Organic methods: Food forest- we are experimenting with various perrennials including: bladder nuts, korean pine nuts, swiss stone pine nuts , heart nut, hazels , local variety apple grafting, siberian pea shrub.
_wwoof_travel: TRAIN: to Aberdeen or Inverurie. (Huntly station is closer but beware 303/308 bus only twice weekdays) BUS to Turriff or  ‘Scotston Crossroads’ by Aberchirder.   Frequent 35  bus to Turriff 5 miles. Less frequent 303 or 308 bus 'Scotstoun Crossroads'  2ml

name: Ludmila - Producimos diversas hortalizas de manera ecológica durante todo el año en poco más de una hectárea.
description:
Somos una familia de 6 personas: dos adultos, nenes de 10, 12 y 19  y una nena de 2 años.
Vivimos en Felanitx, a 45 minutos de la capital de Mallorca. Estamos en proceso de mudanza dentro del mismo pueblo a otra finca.
Convivimos con gatos, gallinas y dos caballos. Con uno de ellos trabajábamos la tierra, pero actualmente, por cuestiones de tiempo, lo hacemos con tractor.
Producimos hortalizas ecológicas todo el año para vender en el mercado local, a grupos de consumo y a la cooperativa ecológica de la isla. También cultivamos fresas, melones y sandías.
La idea, a futuro, es tener también árboles frutales, medicinales, aromáticas y un pequeñito bosque de alimentos. Hacer un baño seco y un biodigestor está incluido en los planes ;)

Actividades típicas: plantar, preparar la tierra, desherbar, desbrozar, cosechar, preparar pedidos.

Empezamos a trabajar a las 8 aprox. hasta la hora de comer. Algunos días seguimos por la tarde, pero solo necesitamos ayuda por las mañanas. 

La gran mayoría de los días, almorzamos y cenamos juntos y nos alimentamos siempre con lo que da el huerto; compramos algunas cosas como pan, carne (una o dos veces por semana), leche, yogurt, cafe, yerba mate, etc.
Para el alojamiento hay una habitación con ventana, mueble para acomodar la ropa, cama, escritorio, ventilador. 
Vivimos todos en planta baja.

Qué nos gusta? Leer, las plantas, conversar, juntarnos con amigos, tomar mate, comer trempo, la lana (aprender a hilar y tejer), el mar, lo político, la vida, la diversidad. No nos gusta el racismo y todo lo relacionado con eso.
_wwoof_travel: Estación de tren: Manacor, a 15 minutos en coche. Aeropuerto: el único de Palma de Mallorca a 45 minutos en coche

name: Susana - Comunidad transitoria
description:
Me llamo Susana, tengo 68 años, soy Sagitario y mono en el horóscopo chino. 

La huerta es mi apuesta en este entretanto. Es un espacio que ha sido compartido con muchas personas que llegaron de manera espontánea, ahora me abro al ciberespacio porque tengo la seguridad de que existen muchos otros más a la espera de este juego, que es estar en relación con el placer de comer, respirar, y escuchar directamente a la naturaleza.

La huerta pretende ser un proyecto auto sostenible, para consumo propio y comunitario, no comercial. Desde 2016, diversas personas han contribuido para que llegue a ser el lugar apacible que es hoy.

Vivimos en un entorno apacible en permanente contacto con la naturaleza. Estamos a 40 minutos en transporte público de la capital histórica de Bolivia: Sucre. La huerta está ubicada en un pequeño pueblo llamado Yotala.

Al ser un lugar de diversas tareas (tierra, bricolaje, reparación, carpintería, arte, animales, y otros), los voluntarios pueden elegir las que quisieran o que puedan ofrecer. Estos acuerdos se realizarán previa llegada.
Todas las actividades referentes al cuidado de la vida como, labrar la tierra, aporcarla, mirarla, regarla, cuidado de gatos.
La huerta espera de los voluntarios un compromiso real con el cuidado de este pedazo de tierra.

Al ser una comunidad transitoria se acuerda horarios y tareas con la nona por demandas en el momento, siendo el tiempo de trabajo 5 horas al día, 5 días a la semana, teniendo dos días de descanso a elección.

En la huerta de la nona todo es negociable excepto el consumo de drogas ilegales en el país.
Hablar español es indispensable a un nivel intermedio. 

El almuerzo esta a cargo del lugar y las demás comidas estarán a cargo de cada uno según sus culturas y costumbres.
La comida es vegetariana y sencilla, ofrezco fruta y verduras de temporada.  Sin embargo, las personas que necesiten comer carne pueden consumir en el pueblo donde hay todo tipo de ofertas.

La huerta está en una transición de lo o
_wwoof_travel: Tomar el bus a Yotala desde el mercado campesino de la ciudad de Sucre, bajar en la plaza principal del pueblo y dirigirse hacia el Teatro de los Andes, la huerta esta debajo.

name: Cheryl - A farm that incorporates a vegan lifestyle, art and oneness as a means to get back to nature
description:
Our farm incorporates growing fresh food, art and oneness as a means to get back to nature. Knowing that we are all one and that it takes all ages from 0 to 100+ to build a community!  We are surrounded by lush forest and have beautiful creeks and streams to explore filled with at least 7 different color clay's. Me and my husband actually got married here barefoot in the forest many years ago. We do bonfires, drum circles, art classes, Kayaking trips, we have even established 3 annual vegan potlucks. 2 of the 3 have evolved into 2-day camp outs.  We encourage attendees to read the labels and to strive for organic ingredients. To know what's in the food they are bringing. We participate in a monthly market that friends of ours run which is a beautiful network of local organic "friend" farmers who barter and buy from each other. We vend at Raw food festivals, markets, and events on neighboring farms.  

GOALS: 
Nourishment of the body and the soul! One of our main goals is to get to the point where we are eating only what we grow, forage, or buy from local organic farmers. As well as grow every ingredient we use in our dishes. My husband just recently mastered a raw plant-based sandwich loaf. He makes the loaf I make the bread, put it together and yum yum yum!!! I actually won 2nd place for my raw "pizzaz" in a contest in 2019 and 1st place in 2024! We want to educate people on what it means to eat for health and take care of ourselves. We also want to inspire COMMUNITY.

COMMUNITY:
Altogether there are about 11 people who live on the farm 
Jahi my husband teaches how to make jewelry, rain sticks and is very artistic, my son Zephyr (26 yrs old) is building his own house on the property. He is a songwriter, designer, and entrepreneur. My oldest son Kasheam  (43 yrs. old) who now lives in Montgomery is a master builder, electrician, etc. Our friend Jihad (26 yrs. old) has mastered jewelry making taught by my husband has began a jewelry company of his own called Aretrali
_wwoof_travel: Greyhound town next 5 miles from the farm and Atlanta Airport less than 2 hrs away. There is a shuttle service that comes 10 or 20 min away depending on cost

name: Alice - Vita in Natura
description:
A'NACA is a ECOproject immersed in the Agrò Valley (Sicily - Limina, ME). We want to promote a sustainable life style and be connected as much as possible with nature.
We are inspired by Permculture and do our best to be self sufficient or at least to be the most respectful as possible with the environment.
We started our project in 2021, and we are daily learning by doing!
In 2025 we renovated the rural house that is on the land and our son ELio was born!
Things changed a lot in the past year!
Now we are working to open an Agri Camping in summer!

A'NACA's team is composed by Mattias, Alice and Elio, but often Alice's parents come to help too!
We are a couple and our dream is to develop an Agri-Camping and live in our land.
Mattias is a professional chef (his food is delicious!), but he also likes a lot working with wood and doing tiring tasks! Alice studied psychology but after travelling some years she got very interested in ecovillages and sustainable communities. She has eyes on everything on the land, but her biggest passion is for the plants and flowers :)

Often there are friends and familiy members at our place and visitors can appear suddenly. This makes the days very felxible and funny too.

At A'NACA also live many cats and our dogs Miro and Lulù!

Often there are friends and familiy members at our place and visitors can appear suddenly. This makes the days very felxible and funny too.
At A'NACA also live many cats and our dog Miro!

Our days and activities are very random and depends on seasons, events, visitors and many other things. This means that we expect FLEXIBILITY and ADAPTATION :)
Works and tasks may depend a lot on the season.
In general we need help in gardening and taking care of plants, watering trees, picking fruits and transforming them in good jams, collecting hay, feeding the cats, ... 
Now, with the little Elio around, we may need help also with him! :)
Some building and painting projects may arise using recycled materials.
Work time i
_wwoof_travel: Stazione di S. Teresa di Riva / Aeroporto di Catania

name: Inske de - Smallholding, Mixed Farm, Family farm
description:
Selnes Gård is a small farm in Northern Norway at Balsfjord. The arctic summer is short and very busy. We have chickens, 40 sheep and about 8 icelandic horses and harvest hay for them. We have  a small no dig marked garden. We want to develope our garden more, so there are possibilities if you like to learn about gardening. We sell egg, meat and vegetables.

We also may have som pigs for the summer, who will dig up and improve the land before beeing slaughtered in the autumn. Composting is important part of summers work. We breed horses so we have no riding horses for guests and there is little opportunity or time for horseback riding in the summer. 

Most work in the summer is with the vegetable garden, haying, fixing fences, making compost and getting firewood for the winter. Wwoofers with skills and interest in carpentry are specially welcome since are always opportunities to participate in fixing around the house and shelter for the animals. We welcome Wwoofers June and July (and october) In 2025 we also need help in october for harvesting the garden and prepare the farm for the winter. We have one very small cabin with 3 beds and an old campervan, but if we have more than 3 Wwoofers some may have to sleep in a tent.. We speak Norwegian, German and English. Our kids are 17 and 20 years old an participate in the farm work when they are at home. We also have our very livly farm dogs 'Kita' and 'Laika'.

We are interessted in Holistic management and regenerative agriculture and experimenting about implementing this in our farming praxis.

NEW since 2019 because of the threat of Antibiotica-resistent bactiria all norwegian farm are obligated to provide a negativ MRSA-test from foreign visitors. (If you have been in contact with farm animals) Please contact your doctor so you can take this simple test. Lets all work together for healthy food and healthy animals. Thank you!

Organic methods: Our farm is certified from Debio which certifies after EU-demands. We have sh
_wwoof_travel: Our farm is located in Selnes, 55 km from Tromsø, 40 km from Nordkjosbotn and 20 km from Laksvatn. If you come by plane to Tromsø you can take a bus either directly to Selnes or to Laksvatn where we can pick you up. If you come by bus from south (Narvik)

name: Adam - Permaculture farm at 1500 masl Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta -Building with mud, glass and wood
description:
Hi all, I'm Adam, a 53-year-old US expat living in or near Minca since 2010.  Now with a small family in Minca, I'm looking for help at the farm (24km away) with minor farm maintenance and major help with bio construction.

Pruning, weeding, transplanting, watering and general basic tasks of farm maintenance are always general tasks. What is most desired now, however is help with renovating a 70 year old rammed earth house and additions in several techniques of bio construction.

Provisions will be provided, excluding meats and dairy, as we have no refrigeration on site.  Self-sufficiency is a must with this experience, as I will be physically present only 2 days per week.  Nearly all meals will be prepared by the same volunteers that stay at the farm. There is a good selection of food at the local stores.

My favorite hobby is as Daddy to my 5 year old daughter but prior to her existence I was a singer, songwriter, guitarist, and chef with a lifelong interest in world history.

Hola a todos, soy Adam, un estadounidense de 53 años de edad, radicado en Minca o sus alrededores desde el año 2010. Ahora que tengo una pequeña familia en Minca, busco ayuda en la finca (a 24 km de distancia) para tareas menores de mantenimiento y, sobre todo, para trabajos de bioconstrucción.

La poda, el deshierbe, el trasplante, el riego y las tareas básicas de mantenimiento agrícola son tareas habituales. Sin embargo, lo que más se necesita ahora es ayuda para renovar una casa de tapia pisada de 70 años de edad y para realizar ampliaciones utilizando diversas técnicas de bioconstrucción.

Se proporcionarán víveres, excepto carnes y lácteos, ya que no contamos con refrigeración en sitio. La autosuficiencia es fundamental para esta experiencia, ya que solo estaré presente físicamente dos días a la semana. Casi todas las comidas serán preparadas por los mismos voluntarios que se alojan en la finca. Hay una buena variedad de alimentos en las tiendas locales.

Mi pasatiempo favorito es ser p

name: Wayan - Permaculture Organic/Regenerative Farming & Workshops
description:
Welcome to our little secret hideaway hidden in the jungle just north of Ubud Bali ! We started Ubud Permaculture Community on our family land during COVID as we all lost our jobs in tourism. My name is Wayan and I run the Community with my brother Nyoman and our extended families. We all live in the local village about 500 metres from the farm, and if you decide to stay us you will have your own fully equipped tent (see photos). You will be staying with Susu & Taksu our cows, Happy & Lili our farm cats, Adam & Eve and their extended duckling family and the occasional monkey !

Our farm is totally based on Permaculture Organic principles and we have over 70 varieties of fruit and vegetables to tend daily.  A typical day starts as the sun rises by feeding the animals and watering the gardens, before collecting the manure from Susu's house to make compost. We would love to share with you all of the permaculture knowledge that we have picked up in the last few years, and invite you to help us with the many digging, gardening and maintenance activities needed to keep the whole place looking beautiful and productive. Staying with us you will step back in time, living essentially as a traditional Balinese farmer lives ! 

We also hold a Farmer's and Art Market on the last Sunday of every month where you will get to meet our wonderful local community of Farmers and Artists, and often our volunteers like to help us out with some of the children's workshops. 

You will be sleeping in one of our 4 large tents, each hidden away in the jungle with lots of privacy from the other volunteers. They are fully equipped with comfortable bed, sheets, pillow, tables and chairs. We will be sharing meals with you in one of the many outbuildings dotted around the site. To cool down after a days work you can either use our beautiful outdoor jungle shower (cold water only I am afraid!) or stand under our waterfall fed by the water from the Holy Spring !

Please note that the Calendar on this

name: Chris - Nonprofit community-supported urban organic farm and environmental education center in Tampa, FL
description:
Our farm was established in 1995 as a nonprofit community-supported urban organic farm and environmental education center in Tampa, Florida. We are located on 6 acres of suburban property along the Sweetwater Creek in Tampa’s Town ‘n’ Country neighborhood, in Hillsborough County, Florida.We operate a Community Supported Agriculture (CSA) Membership program, Sweetwater Sunday Farmers’ Market and Music Series open to the public, educational field trip programs for visiting groups, farm tours, workshops, outdoor movies, seasonal events, volunteer opportunities and more!Through our hands-on programs, visitors of all ages discover the wonders of nature, explore ecological interconnections, practice appropriate land stewardship and master sustainable agriculture techniques.  Our farm provides the freshest possible organic vegetables and herbs for hundreds of Tampa Bay residents each year.At Sweetwater, community members come together to grow and enjoy fresh organic food, acquire sustainable living skills, support local agriculture, enjoy nature, make new friends and be a part of the community. 

Open for volunteer days on Sunday 9am-12pm, all summer long. 

Organic methods: compost, mulch, 

Certifications: Florida

Current projects: Cafe
Co-op grocery store
Eco-Campground
Farm School
Farm marketing
Farm Fields Expansion

Internet access available

name: Julie - 70 acre certified organic farm just outside of St Ignatius, 45 miles north of Missoula
description:
We are a 70 acre certified organic farm just outside of St Ignatius, 45 miles north of Missoula, and have been in operation for 18 years. We produce a variety of vegetables and beef that we sell through the Western Montana Growers' Cooperative. We expect wwoofers to stay a minimum of a week or two, or longer if possible. There is housing provided with a sweet camper on the farm, as well as farm food provided in exchange for 5 half or full days of volunteering a week. Our busy season begins in March and ends in mid-October. We live in the Mission valley of western Montana, bordering the Mission Mountain Tribal Wilderness with endless outdoor activities.  Please let us know of your plans to volunteer with us at least one week in advance. We prefer no children and no pets. English, French and a little German spoken.

Certifications: MT Department of Agriculture

Internet access available

name: David - Homestead, Farm
description:
We are in the small community of Agua Fria. It's a relaxed place where most people still subside on some form of farming. It's almost always hot as hades, but aside from that it's paradise. In 2016, we finally got a little piece of land up on a hill in the jungle. Since then we've been slowly building a home where we can live and grow food. Native chiles, papayas, and passion fruit grow wild on the land and we continue to plant trees, vegetables, and herbs. While we will accept all sorts of WWOOFERs, we would most appreciate those with experience in building (carpentry, masonry, sustainability), design, and cultivation. All your accommodation will be covered while you work, but we expect you to share in chores and work full days like everyone else as to be a fair exchange. To confusion if your Spanish isn't strong: a normal work day here is 7AM -3PM. We are sharing our home, our food, and our little piece of the world with you, a stranger. If you are looking to sleep in and vacation at the beach, please look elsewhere. We don't care about your degree, gender, age, nationality... if you are eager to work, share your talents, and respectfully experience another way of living, you're welcome. Forest, beach, and waves are nearby. Some decent Spanish is essential. A few more details listed below. 

La comunidad se llama Agua Fría. Es un lugar tranquilo y sano. Un vistazo al pasado y un paraiso actual. En 2016, conseguimos un pedacito de tierra salvaje y off-grid. Cultivamos una porción mientras cuidamos una gran parte como bosque. Siempre hay todo tipo de trabajo entre la casa y los jardines pero nos ayudaría si tienes experiencia, o ganas, de construir con madera, concreto, o prácticas sostenibles. Te brindamos toda la accomodación y compartimos los deberes -- esperamos una jornada completa para que sea un intercambio justo. Una semana máxima para empezar y si todo bien podemos arreglar para una estadía prolongada. 

Visiones actuales y para el futuro: jardines, diseno,

name: Alexander - Permaculture - Retreat Farm  &  Seedkeeping
description:
ES
LaFINKA ALEXANDRIA se encuentra en la Gomera, la segunda isla mas pequena de las Canarias. La superficie total de la finca es de unas 2,5 hectareas de terreno de terrazas, a 800 metros sobre el nivel del mar (!) y orientada al sur. Estamos en la fase inicial de un proyecto agri­cola basado en la permacultura y buscamos a gente interesada en ayudarnos a construir las bases del mismo. Entre las actividades se encuentra la creacion de infraestructuras, la construccion y reconstruccion de muros de piedra natural, depositos de agua, estanques, corrales, etc. Ademas de sistemas de bancales y de cultivo alternativos, cortavientos y sistemas de riego. El alojamiento es en una casa particular cerca de la finca. La tarea principal consiste en disenar de forma creativa el jardin de acuerdo con nuestra idea de la permacultura. Actualmente somos tres personas en el proyecto y trabajamos 7 horas durante 5 di­as a la semana,(los Voluntarios 6 horas) ademas de alimentar a nuestros animales-en el momento son, (2 Gatos y Gallinas:). Construimos nosotros mismos practicamente todo y por eso podemos transmitir nuestros concocimientos artesanales en trabajos en piedra natural, madera y metal. Para estancias largas, se necesitan 2 semanas de prueba. 

DE
FINKA ALEXANDRIA - PROJEKTBESCHREIBUNG: Die Finca Alexandria befindet sich auf La Gomera,die zweit kleinste Insel der Kanaren.Die Gesamtfläche der Finca beträgt ca. 2,5ha terrassiertes Gelände auf 800 Meter Meereshöhe(!) in einer Südhanglage. Wir sind noch in der Anfangsphase eine Permakultur-Landwirtschaft aufzubauen und suchen im Moment Leute die in erster Linie am Grundaufbau interessiert sind. Hierzu gehören die Schaffung der Infrastruktur, das wieder oder neu Aufbauen von Natursteinmauern, Wassertanks, Teichen, Stallungen. Hinzu kommen alternative Beet und Pflanzensysteme, Windschutz und Bewässerungssysteme. Die Unterkunft erfolgt in einem Privathaus in der Nähe der Finca (15 min.). Die Hauptaufgabe besteht in der kreativen Garten

name: Sandrine - Ferme pédagogique dans le Lot
description:
Bonjour à vous, amis WWOOFeurs !

Notre association "Arts Scènes et Compagnie" (site web : https://www.artscenesetcie.fr/) a créé en 2014 la Ferme des Cerisiers afin de permettre à l’association de bénéficier d’un lieu fixe d’ancrage pour le développement de ses activités notamment celles autour de l’éducation à l’environnement (la ferme a été construite uniquement avec des matériaux recyclés).
 Située à Saint Vincent du Pendit (46400) à seulement quelques kilomètres de Saint Céré, la ferme d’environ 2 hectares bénéficie d’un environnement calme et verdoyant nichée au cœur du village. Composée d’une petite maison d’habitation, la ferme possède également un bel espace extérieur où se réalise une grande partie de nos des activités (ALSH, séjours, évènement culturel et chantier - atelier participatif). La ferme est un lieu inventif dédié aux jeux coopératifs pour les familles et les enfants. La ferme est composée de différents espaces notamment des emplacements de camping, un espace de vie et de jeux, un espace de restauration et sanitaire, un espace de réception pour les évènements recevant du public et des zones de maraichages et de cultures diverses.

Il y a 2 temporalités à la ferme :
