- La première de septembre à juin où l'on peut accueillir des wwoofeurs qui viennent aider les salariés, les volontaires en Service Civique et les bénévoles pour rénover, restaurer les locaux et relancer - entretenir les jardins de la ferme dans le cadre de chantiers participatifs ou encore de préparation d'évènement culturels comme la guinguette que l'on organise tous les ans au mois de juin.
- La 2de au mois de juillet et août où le temps est rythmé par l'accueil des enfants et jeunes dans le cadre de notre ALSH et nos séjours thématiques. Il y a beaucoup plus de monde à la ferme, outre les petits travaux quotidiens de la ferme, les wwoofeurs participe également à la vie collective (préparation des repas, nettoyage des locaux) et peuvent également nous aider s'ils le souhaitent à l

name: Julia - Organic Farm and we are also practice Permaculture.
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):

It’s paradise place from 8 hectares land in Chongoene Kumine Xai Xai in Mozambique. I’m Julia from Germany and my husband Feliz is from Mozambique. We have also a cute Son with the name Jaguar. 

We are a organic farm and also practice Permaculture.  

Further we have chickens, turtle and a dog with the name Wolf.

It’s very beautiful and quiet and peaceful here in Xai-Xai and it’s also close by very beautiful beaches. 🌊🏝🐬

It’s like paradise here, because everything grow here. The soil is so good that you can easy live from your own land. 🌻🌸🌴🍌🥑🍉🐿🦧🦜🌞 

One day people will wake up from they long sleep and will remember what is important in life.......to make your own paradise garden and to transform the whole planet again in one beautiful paradise. So as it was in the beginning so it shall be at the end. ☺️ (Anastasia)

Further we have a lot of land next to our land so we can helping people also who are interesting in to have there own land in Mozambique.  😉

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:

1-Working in the garden
2-Planting more vegetables and plants
3-Preparing beds for to plant
4-Giving plants water
5-Harvesting vegetables and fruits
6-Giving the chickens and animals food.

The volunteers can learn about organic farming and also about Permaculture. We also can practice different languages together. And we also can practice Tai Chi together for who like and want.

Further we also like to learn from our volunteers. 

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals:

We live in a beautiful wooden house. But for the volunteers we have separate a little beautiful cement house with a private bathroom also. The room for the volunteers have a beautiful view because the room is upstairs. 

Further we prepare 3 meals per day for the volunteers.


4. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at y

name: Gloria - Tea Plantation near the Atlantic Ocean
description:
*** Holistic – sustainable – community-based ***

Become part of an exciting project to build an organic tea farm on the volcanic island of São Miguel – the main island of the Azores, a Portuguese archipelago in the mid-Atlantic. Started seven years ago, the project now has a solid infrastructure: fertile land by the sea, our first tea plants ready for harvest, and a well-stocked nursery full of young plants ready to go into the fields. Two renovated houses – one on the plantation and one nearby – offer space for our team and guests.

The project follows a holistic approach, combining ecological, social, and economic sustainability. Our goal is to create a living, mostly self-sufficient example that grows through practical action – in line with the principle of “learning by doing.” Consumption and expectations take a back seat, while experience, community, and growth come first.

Our core team includes four local employees and two project leaders. Together, we are developing the farm: right now, we are working on a new dryer, to dry our harvest - which should just run with sun energy and the heat generating by composting. Also, we are setting up a new nursery with around 10.000 tea plants and as soon as possible, we will also create a nursery for other trees, which will become our windbreaks. 

What to expect:
Bring your ideas and energy! Help us with diverse tasks like weeding, planting, building paths, and caring for the plants. You’ll work three days a week (Monday–Wednesday) for 8 hours/day - it could be also five days a week, fewer hours per day.  We are flexible :) 

Living & Lifestyle:
The guesthouse has two private bedrooms – you're welcome to come with a partner or friend. It also includes a kitchen with dining area, one bathrooms, a garden with terrace, and a rooftop, where you can enjoy the sunrise and sunset. Washing machine and dryer are available. And of course, we have WiFi as well ;) 
You can reach the plantation by foot (40 mins), rental car/scooter

name: Mustafa - Family farm
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Çiftçi, bölgesinde kurulmuş olan Yüksek Yayla Tarımsal Kalkınma Kooperatifi başkanıdır. Temel olarak fındık, bunun yanı sıra aileye yetecek kadar sebze ve meyve yetiştirmektedir. 15 dönüm üzerinde kurulu fındık bahçeleri vardır. Çiftçi yıllardır sertifikalı organik tarım yapmaktadır. Eşiyle, oğullarıyla ve yaz aylarında dönemsel olarak gelen akrabalarıyla birlikte fındıkların hasadını yapmaktadırlar. Asıl mesleği muhasebecilik olan ev sahibi Temmuz, Ağustos, Eylül aylarında gönüllü kabul etmektedir. Her mevsim yeşil kalan ormanlarla çevrili bu coğrafyada birçok tecrübe yaşamak mümkündür. Yaban hayatı gözlemi, doğa yürüyüşleri ve şenlik havasında geçen fındık hasadı çiftliğin sunduğu olanaklar arasındadır. Eşinin mutfakta, Mustafa Bey'in ise bahçe işlerinde yardıma ihtiyacı vardır. Eşinin yaptığı turşular şimdiden bölgede nam kazanmıştır.

---

The host is the chair of the Yuksek Yayla Agricultural Development Cooperative in his region where the main product is hazelnuts. Enough fruits and vegetables are produced for the family and visitors. The size of the hazelnut field is 15 acres. The farmer has been practicing organic agriculture for years. He harvests the hazelnuts with his wife, his son and his relatives. The host, whose original profession is accounting, accepts volunteers in the moths of July, August and September. A variety of experiences are available in this location which is surrounded by evergreen forests. Wild life observation, nature trekking and the festive hazelnut harvest are among the possibilities that the host offers. Help is needed by Mrs. Akbulut in the kitchen and by Mr. Akbulut in the garden. Mrs. Akbulut’s pickles are already famous in the region.

name: Joslin - Agroecology food forest garden in the mountains with an arts residency & biomaterial lab
description:
We are TerraPods, an agroecology, art residency and biomaterials research space  nestled in the village of Baskinta, Lebanon on the foothills of the majestic Mount Sannine at an altitude of 1,600m and 45km from Beirut.

 Our project is deeply rooted in ecological regeneration, self-sufficiency, and community-driven innovation. We cultivate a food forest with a wide variety of fruits and vegetables, raise animals for fiber, honey, eggs, and meat and explore biomaterial development using mycelium, cellulose, plant fibers, and agricultural by-products. Our space integrates agroecology, biofabrication, and artistic expression, creating a vibrant and dynamic learning environment.

Our community includes two full-time Syrian farmers, artist and biomaterial designers in residency, a biomaterial lab material scientist, the host along with our three dogs and beloved farm animals, which provide companionship and play essential roles in our ecosystem.

Returning to ancestral land is a winding path of re-discovery in space. Our terraces are set between farmed lands; sharing water and pathways, definely set in stone with neighbouring farmers, just as our forefathers before us.

WWOOFers joining us will experience a diverse range of activities, including:

Food forest maintenance: Planting, pruning, composting, and harvesting.

Animal care: Depending on the animals we have for the season - grazing, feeding, milking, and habitat maintenance. In 2025 we have 15 beehives, 30 chickens and 3 dogs. We aim to get goats, sheep, rabbits and ducks for 2026. 

A typical day starts with a shared breakfast, followed by morning farm tasks, a break for lunch, and an afternoon focused on specialised projects. Evenings are for communal meals, open fire discussions and jams, and creative activities.

WWOOFers will stay in traditional bedouin tents made out of goat hair equipped with a two mattresses, fresh sheets, pillows, artisanal blankets woven in the village and can fit 2 persons in each tent

name: Christi - 80-acre farm owned by the Dominican Sisters of Peace
description:
Our volunteer application process takes approximately a week, so unfortunately, we cannot accept last minute WWOOFers.  We require a questionnaire to be answered and submitted back to us to review so we can see if we are a good fit. The next step is a zoom call to discuss our expectations, as well as yours. We hope to hear from you!  The questionnaire is now fully online and easy to complete!

WE HAVE A GREAT NEED FOR WWOOFERS IN JULY AND AUGUST!

We require all volunteers to have permanent residence.

While we do welcome children, they must be at least 9 years old so they can safely accompany you and participate in farm work. 

Our biggest need for volunteers is from March to September, though we are available year-round. This is when we plant, maintain, harvest, and preserve garden produce; process alpaca fleece, and do general farm maintenance. We welcome those who are self-motivated, as we encourage volunteers to find their own working rhythm. 

Smoking is strongly discouraged but will be permitted in designated smoking areas only. We are a drug-free and weapon-free zone.  This farm is very LGBTQ friendly and open minded.  You do not need to be religious to WWOOF here.  

ABOUT US:
We are an 80-acre farm owned by the Dominican Sisters of Peace, located in the heart of Kansas: 13 miles from the nearest town. We are a community with a Catholic Dominican Sister (recently retired and now a volunteer), a part-time Sister (who makes soap, jelly and other homemade items), and 5 employees. We generally have a couple volunteers living on the property. On the property we have numerous buildings: Spruce House(where the Sisters live), Hedge House(our guest space), Elm House(the volunteer quarters), Beech House(guest or volunteer space), Cedar House(where the Farm Manager lives), Strawbale Hermitage(guest space), Office/Workshop, pole barn, chicken coop, barn, Strawbale Arts Center, chakra silo, a couple small cottages(non-livable spaces), and garden pavilion.

Accommodation
_wwoof_travel: Hays, Dodge City or Wichita Kansas for Airport.  Hutchinson or Dodge City, Kansas for Train Station.  Hutchinson or McPherson, Ks for Bus Station.

name: Sushant - Volunteers can expect daily tasks such as animal care, dairy shop management, crop planting and harv
description: Near the Narayani and Rapati Rivers in Prembasti, Sushant Poudel, a BBA student from a local Brahmin family, manages a buffalo farm, dairy shop, and seasonal agriculture including rice, wheat, sesame, and mustard. He also raises goats and volunteers at Prembasti Secondary School, contributing to both education and community development.Volunteers can expect daily tasks such as animal care, dairy shop management, crop planting and harvesting, and goat farming. Accommodation is family-style, with traditional Nepali meals including dal bhat, milk, and seasonal produce. The cultural environment is rooted in Brahmin traditions, riverside communal life, and school volunteering.Learning opportunities include dairy management, buffalo care, crop cycles, and community volunteering. Volunteers are expected to work 5–6 hours per day, with flexible tasks and a minimum stay of one week preferred.

name: Brock (David) - Beautiful and productive 42 acres of land in south Louisiana with many hundreds of different plants.
description:
Hi there travelers, we are a beautiful and productive 42 acres of land in south Louisiana. We've been at it for 16 years now, and have established a beautiful space with great diversity. There are about three hundred fruit trees, lots of vegetables, flowers, 1/3 acre pond, and hundreds of different plants. I've also recently become a fan of cultivating and foraging for mushrooms, so you're likely to learn a bit about growing mushrooms on logs and go on some fantastic forest foraging forays.  

We've had a baby girl in July of 2023, and love hosting other families with children! 

Guests are accepted at most times of the year and for nearly any length of time. There are always a diversity of tasks through out the year that provide plenty of opportunities to learn. An emphasis is put on exchanging as much information as possible to all who come to the farm.  I'm keen on learning new languages, playing music, and experiencing new types of cuisine. We welcome curiosity and eagerness to learn. I'm quite an extravert and a conversationalist and greatly enjoy discussing deeper topics of philosophy, spirituality, politics, etc. We also have a shelf of books on permaculture and sustainable agriculture for you to thumb through at your leisure.

Accommodation is a school bus that has been converted into living quarters.  The bus is a magical space equipped with a queen sized bed, screened window in the bedroom, an air conditioner for the summer and a heater for the winter, a view of the pond, and an outdoor shower nearby. 

Plenty of fresh food is of course made readily available. Diversity is valued here, not only in the garden but in all respects. No matter your race, religion, nationality, sexual orientation, or whatever, all are welcome!! The farm is located 15 minutes outside of Lafayette Louisiana, which is a culturally rich area with live Zydeco and Cajun music/dancing and local Cajun cuisine. There's plenty of recreation within a short drive which include canoeing, hik

name: Surya - We offer comfortable accommodations with English-speaking support
description:
We currently grow chili, lettuce, cauliflower, and other vegetables across 20 ropanis of land, with plans to expand to 200 ropanis and plant 1,000 coffee trees. Our farm is home to 60 goats and 7 cows, along with herbs such as mint and thyme and a variety of medicinal plants. On my personal fields, I cultivate rice, maize, wheat, mustard, potatoes, buckwheat, and soybeans.

We warmly welcome you to our farm. Your activities will include helping care for the goats and cows and supporting vegetable and coffee farming. We provide comfortable accommodation, English-speaking support, and internet access in the home office. The area is rich in natural beauty, with nearby places such as the Mai River, Mai Pokhari (pond), Gajurmukhi and Pathibhara temples, Todke Spring, Sandakpur, and the Sriantu hills. I live on the farm with my wife, while our children live away from home.

name: Malena - Granja Biodinámica cerca de  Capilla del Señor
description:
1. Describe tu proyecto, el lugar y sus habitantes (incluídos los animales):
Hola! Somos 4 primos, tenemos una granja biodinámica cerca de Capilla del Señor. La granja es nuestra manera de obtener alimentos de la tierra, aprendiendo a su manera, sin químicos, sin monocultivos, con amor, conocimiento, paciencia y diversidad. Buscamos cuidar a las personas a través de los alimentos que generamos, proteger el suelo, cuidar el agua, preservar la vida, evitar agroquímicos, dar trabajo y restaurar ecosistemas.
En la granja tenemos caballos, vacas, pollos, gallinas y perros.

Hello! We are 4 cousins, we have a biodynamic farm near Capilla del Señor. The farm is our way of obtaining food from the land, learning in its own way, without chemicals, without monocultures, with love, knowledge, patience and diversity. We seek to care for people through the food we generate, protect the soil, care for water, preserve life, avoid agrochemicals, provide work, and restore ecosystems.
On the farm we have horses, cows, chickens and dogs.

2. Haz una lista de las actividades en las que participarán los WWOOFers y qué quieres enseñarles:
Los voluntarios pueden disfrutar de unos días en la naturaleza. Aprenderán de huerta, animales, el funcionamiento de una granja, los principios básicos de la biodinámica y como producir sus propios alimentos sin interceder negativamente en la naturaleza.

Necesitamos alguien que quiera aprender de huerta y trabajar en ella. También será parte de las tareas diarias como levantar huevos, ordeñar una vaca y mantenimiento general de la granja. Esperamos entusiasmo, ganas de aprender y trabajar, buena predisposición, responsabilidad y humor.

Volunteers can enjoy a few days in nature. They will learn about the garden, animals, the operation of a farm, the basic principles of biodynamics and how to produce their own food without interceding negatively in nature.
We need someone who wants to learn about the garden and work in it. You will also be part of daily
_wwoof_travel: Estación de tren Pilar y aeropuerto: Aeroparque.

name: Mo - Family farm specializing in raw dairy in CO foothills
activities: Unterkunft: shack, bedroom, trailer, campsite, yurt-tipi
description:
We are a small family and run a small raw dairy (with Jersey cows) located in the foothills of Berthoud between Loveland and Longmont about 40 minutes north of Boulder. We also raise sheep, poultry and keep a small herd of horses.  

We love being a WWOOF host farm and really enjoy helping folks learn about all things it takes to run a small diverse raw dairy and small animal-focused farm. Farm days are long, but fulfilling!  One day we really hope to be able to make farming our full-time jobs, but until then, we both have full-time jobs to support our little farm. We are very excited to welcome WWOOFers into our farm and help you learn about the ups, downs and in-betweens of running a farm. 

Our main focus is for WWOOFers to learn all-things-milking on our small dairy.  Our other main focus is daily care of the cattle, sheep, poultry and horses.  These are the day-in, day-out of WWOOFing at WiMo.

We raise our own lamb and occasionally poultry (quail, ducks, geese, turkeys, chickens) and pork.  We have been USDA certified organic for our (CBD) hemp operation (seasonally), but are currently in 2023/24 taking a break from hemp. 

We offer occasional opportunities to learn about and help with on-farm butchering (poultry and lamb). 

Duties include feeding and watering animals, cleaning pens and coops, milking, pouring milk, prepping and cleaning milk equipment and jars, and weeding, butchering, etc. And because we've only been in our current location for a few years, there are fencing and construction projects too (building, water lines, electric, cement, moving buildings, etc.).  Most WWOOFers end up learning how to use the skid steer, and occasionally the mini-excavator. For WWOOFers experienced with horses, there may be an opportunity to ride horses too.  However, our horses aren't "dude broke" so prior riding experience is pretty much necessary.  

Our expectation is for our WWOOFers to learn daily animal duties, and daily milking-related duties (milking if here
_wwoof_travel: Longmont bus station or Denver International Airport

name: NİLÜFER - Kadın Emeği Tarım
description:
Pandemi sırasında kurumsal hayatı bıraktım ve çocukluğumdaki yaz tatili ve bayramlara ait güzel karelerin geçtiği Kocaeli Akmeşe'de tarımla uğraşmaya başladım. 50 yaşındayım. İkiz çocuklarım ve eşim İstanbul da yaşıyor. Fırsat buldukça ben İstanbul'a, onlar bize yardıma buraya geliyor.  Akmeşe (eski adıyla Armaş Ermeni Köyü)  İstanbul' 1,5 saat, Sabiha Gökçen Havalimanı'na 1 saat Kocaeli merkeze de 30 dk lık mesafededir. 
Akmeşe'de yaşayan annem ve babamla birlikte yaklaşık 3 yıldır aile yadigari topraklarımızda, ata tohumları ile kimyasal zehir kullanmadan üretim yapıyoruz. Bunun yanında topladığımız mahsüllerden aldığımız ürünleri, köyümdeki kadınlarımızla birlikte hazırlıyoruz. 
Köpeğimiz karabaş, sokak köpeğimiz Çakır ve  4 kedimizle birlikte yaşıyoruz. 

Çiftliğimizde dönemine göre, sera hazırlığı, bakımı, fide yetiştirme, bakımı, çapa, ot temizliği, sulama sisteminin kurulması, dereden motorla su çekilmesi, tarlada ekim ve dikim, sebze ve meyve hasadı, budama, konserve yapımı, bokashi, soğuk ve solucan kompost yapımı, tohum alımı, ot biçme, fındık hasadı, gübreleme ve çiftlik bakımı yapılmaktadır. 

2 katlı evimizin üst kısmı bağımsız birimdir. 1 salon, 2 odası ile yaklaşık 5 kişiliktir. Kendisine ait banyo, balkon ve mutfağı vardır. Wifi,  TV ve buzdolabı mevcuttur. Kışın  doğalgaz yanında odunla yaktığımız sobamız vardır. Yemekler annem tarafından pişirilir. Yemek tercihimiz genellikle sebzedir. Dileyen misafirlerimiz kendi menüleri ve tarifleri ile anneme destek verebilir. 

Özellikle yaz aylarında güne 08: 30 gibi başlarız. Sıcak olan saatlerde genellikle evde dinlenir, akşam saatlerinde yine tarla, bahçe veya serada çalışırız. İşin yoğunluğuna göre günlük çalışma süremiz 6-7 saat arasında değişir. Misafirlerimiz boş zamanlarında, şayet yaz mevsimi ise  yaklaşık 1 saat uzaklıktaki Karadeniz de denize girebilir, göl veya mevsimine göre biberiye ve lavanta bahçesi ziyaret edebilir,  at çiftliklerini gezebilir veya her saat başı kalkan otobüslerle köyümüzden

name: Patrick - Organic Ulu {breadfruit) farm and  propagating native Hawaiian crops.
description:
Application introduction

 Folks who apply to my farm should be honest in their application.
i.e. if you are primarily interested in a subsidized vacation then say so
if you are claiming to be interested in farming and have absolutely no interest 
be honest...
Because it gets awkward for everyone when the fake premise of your application erodes and the real motivation for your 
application to join the wwoffing is not
  a "life long passion" for farm experience.

My farms keeps good its promise as stated on these pages,
To keep you safe and give you a wholesome farming environment.
And plenty time to enjoy our wonderful Hawaii.
Aloha
Looking forward to hearing from 


Located in rain forest a the 200 foot elevation. we enjoy trade winds and an average annual temp of 80F (27C).
Ulu tree propagation is a much sought after skill. We plant and harvest. Chicken coop needs to be elevated a few feet to keep the mongoose from the eggs. 

Our daily activities are determined by the weather. Typically the day starts after a  leisurely breakfast.

We are located near a free bus serving the entire island. We schedule weekends off as there are lots of open farmers markets, beaches , tidepools and community festivals
_wwoof_travel: Free bus service is five min walk away, Hilo international airport is 20 miles away,

name: Stewart - Hobby Farm
description: If you are wanting to learn about farm animals and work with farm animals then you will enjoy our farm. An interest in learning about farm animals is a MUST to WOOF here.  We are a small hobby farm, in the summer we have a garden, and in the fall several fruit trees that need to be harvested. We have a small herd of  goats(4 of whom will be having babies in a couple months) and  2 lambs and one baby goat all who are being bottle fed.  We also have miniature horses,  and two large riding horses (no we don't allow anyone to ride them, sorry); ,and one mule.  We also have chickens .  We will be hatching out ducks and chickens in  couple months.  Our animals are very close to us, and we tend to spoil them a little.  All our animals are very friendly and love to be petted.  We also have working livestock guardian dogs that protect our property and animals from wild predators like coyotes and bear and eagles.  They take their jobs seriously, but are very affectionate with humans.  We only moved last summer, and up until now we have spent the majority of our time setting up the farm to accommodate our animals.  A typical day here will include being up by approximately 06:00 am most days and start the day by feeding and watering the animals.  That takes about an hour depending on the time of year.  Then during the rest of the day there is always something that needs to be done, ranging from garden work to cleaning out barns or chicken houses, or yard/garden work.  Your accommodation will be in our home  in the guest room.  We do expect some help with cleaning up after meals or sweeping the floor if you notice it is dirty.  We enjoy our wwoofer to cook us a meal from their native country if they like.  We look forward to meeting you!  We do have a few rules, based on past experience (these make us sound gruff, but we really aren't):-First off we are not your parents and will not tolerate immature and disrespectful behavior, -We also ask that you be able to understand and spe

name: Sophie - Family Garden
description:
This is a one-acre property on Mudge Bay outside of the little village of Kagawong on beautiful Manitoulin Island. I'm not a market garden or a farm business, rather an experiment to be as self-sustaining as possible with food growing. I've been hosting WWoofers for almost ten years. In the last few years I've enjoyed hosting artists/writers and other creatives -- as a professional writer and artist, we end up having wonderful conversations that move between gardening and creative pursuits. 

I am slowly maximizing all the growing space on the property with vegetable gardens, some perennial flowers, fruit and nut trees, and mushroom logs. I'm hoping to get chickens this year. I grow a big mix of produce and do a lot of preserving and storing for the winter. I experiment with low or no water growing, layering, companion planting, and other practices. I use organic methods -- no pesticides or herbicides -- organic seed,  and seed saving. I have a greenhouse which I use to start plants, and to extend the growing season, and am integrating more and more permaculture strategies, and perennial foods (nut and fruit trees, berries etc.).

I'd love to host WWOOFers who have some experience with organic farming, permaculture, preserving food, and establishing perennial food crops, but I'm also happy to host folks without much experience, but who are keen to learn what I've learned!

This year my plans include:

- expanding the food forest/perennial foods, including planting many more nut and fruit trees
- perhaps getting chickens and building a coop
- moving and replenishing perennial beds (asparagus, strawberries, sunchokes, nut and berry bushes)
- adding additional perennial foods to the garden
- the usual cycles of planting, seeding, preserving, greenhouse tending, and Kombucha making!

I particularly need help in the spring (May and June), and the fall (September and early October). 

As I have a full house -- my daughter and a roommate -- I provide Wwoofers with a comfor
_wwoof_travel: Espanola for the bus, Sudbury for airport

name: 신섭 - <JJ128> 유기농 귤 과수원 / Meadow Orchard
description:
✅ 호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
2010년경부터 서울에서 직장생활을 하며, 주말과 휴가 등을 이용하여 서귀포시 삼달리 소재 귤 과수원에서 귤을 재배하기 시작했습니다. 
당시 귤 재배 등 농사에 대한 지식이나 경험이 전혀 없는 상태였으나, 가정에서 유기농농산물을 소비하고 있던 것이 계기가 되어 유기농법으로 귤을 재배하기 시작하였는데, 귤 재배에 전념하지 못하여 품질이 좋지 않고 나무도 많이 죽어 전체 공간의 1/3가량 어린 묘목을 심었습니다.
귤 재배 기술은 매년 작성한 영농일지와 농업기술센터의 기술 교육 등으로 귤의 품질과 맛이 좋아지고 나무도 유기농법 적응해서 매우 건강한 상태입니다. 다만, 아쉬운 것은 제 과수원과 붙어 있는 과수원이 화확농약 등으로 귤을 재배하여 유기농인증을 받지 못하고 있고, 앞으로도 이 상태가 계속될 가능성이 높다는 것입니다. 
2023년말에 직장퇴직으로 서울생활을 정리하고 본격적인 전업농으로 귤을 재배하고 있는데 약 15년간 지속가능한 원동력은 유기농농산물을 생산한다는 자부심과 사철 푸르름의 잡초의 꽃을 보고, 베고, 밟는 등의 활동을 통해서 땅이 전해주는 에너지를 받는 것이 아닐까 생각합니다. 또한 과수원 한쪽에 건물을 지어 게스트하우스로 운영중인데 정년퇴직후 내게 맞는 일자리를 만들어 날마다 휴가를 보내고 있는 것처럼 즐겁고 행복한 시간을 보내고 있습니다.
저와 같이 풀 밟고 바다를 즐기러 오세요!

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2010년
✅ 농장 형태 : 개인 과수원(숙박시설 이용자 체험 병행)
✅ 농장 주작물 : 귤, 한라봉, 황금향

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 유기농 비료, 유기농 약재로 귤 재배
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 : 재활용품 분리 배출, 전기자동차 운영

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 게스트하우스 투숙객
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려 동물 : 없음
✅ 우퍼가 하는일 :
-3~4월 : 귤 나무 등 과실수 전정가지 정리 및 잡초 제거
-10~12월 : 귤 수확 및 운반, 택배 포장, 포장물품 상하차, 전정가지 정리 등

✅ 선호하는 우퍼상 :
없음
✅ 우퍼에게 당부 메세지 :
제공되는 숙소에서의 식기, 침구 등 깔끔한 정리정돈
✅ 숙소에서 와이파이 가능여부 : 가능
✅ 술,담배 가능여부 : 금연이나 음주는 가능
✅ 호스트와 식사여부 : 하루 한,두끼만 함께 먹고 나머지는 식재료를 드려 직접 해 드셔야 합니다.
✅ 영어소통 : 번역기 통해 가능
✅ 일주일 기준 우핑시간 : 하루5시간 주5일

======== English explanation ==========
✅ Introduction to the Host and the Farm :
Around 2010, while working in Seoul, I began cultivating tangerines at an orchard in Samdal-ri, Seogwipo, using weekends and holidays.
At the time, I had no knowledge or experience in farming, but since my family consumed organic produce at home, I decided to grow tangerines organically. However, since I couldn’t fully devote myself to farming, the fruit quality was not very good and many trees died. As a result, I replanted about one-third of the orchard with young seedlings.
Through years of keeping farming journals and participating in training at the local Agricultural Technology Center, the quality and taste of the tangerines have greatly improved, and the trees have become strong

name: Alicia - Small BIO*charming HEMP farm
description:
My name is Alicia and together with my family & friends I run a small bio*charming*place. On our LoveField we specialise in sustainable, manual as well as joyful hemp plant cultivation [Cannabis Sativa L. ~ a legal one :].

You can learn here how to make medicaments out of hemp, how to take care on the soil & plants, how to collect, dry and process them into various products: CBD oil, hemp flour, dried leaves' tea, hemp butter, gummy bears.. and more.

Working on the field is very simple and you do not need any special skills to do it well, but I do care about good energy and some kind of passion*dedication as well as clear communication and honesty among all the helpers. The main tool for collecting hemp is secateur:) no big machines here as all my hemp products are handcrafted.

There'll be a lot of space to talk, laugh out loud together, to share all the goods (as well as meals). YES, if you like cooking I'll be more than happy to waive you my 'kitchen master' badge.

There's a variety of sleeping options. You can sleep in a big house (as all my Couchsurfers do), you can use small clay house, you can sleep in a barn on a straw*bed or use a tent. All depends on your preferences and availability. There is a kitchen, a toilet, a shower.. even a bath :O

My field lies in a very calm, clean and peaceful countryside area with plenty of wild woods with mushrooms. It's near Kielce, in a bit mountainous area, where you can climb, ski or visit the biggest cave in Poland, so called Paradise Cave. In your free time you can enjoy these treasures or you can visit our [wild] sauna in the forest... mm.

Organic methods: LoveField farm within pure*healthy*joyful vibes all around. If you have a while, come, smile:) and help me. In generaI I let my plants grow half-wild, so they are surrounded by poppies, cornflowers and bees from our own small apiary. We do hand sowing, the only fertilizer we use on the farm is horse manure, during harvesting we collect & process hemp plants by ha

name: Lisa - Farming the hills under the tropical sun, creating a syntropic edible forest
description:
In 2020, after a career in communications and living in cities, I started this permaculture project in the hills of Yabucoa, which I run with the help of two to four volunteers, neighbours and friends. 90+ different plants, mostly local fruit trees, but also (medicinal) herbs, are growing around the house and down the slope towards the creek. We experiment with natural ways of feeding and protecting the plants (syntropic methods made popular by Ernst Götsch), and enjoy eating and sharing the 100% bio/organic crop. I don't have pets, but you'll see and hear dogs, cats, roosters, chickens, iguanas, lizards, frogs, and birds, as well as (rarely) snakes, spiders, and (many) ants, and flying (also stinging) insects. Be aware that this is a 12+ growing zone, so extremely hot and humid, and not comparable with mainland USA farming.

We work together and individually, mostly in the early morning hours before lunch, and again after 3PM, in order to avoid the stinging sun. During a minimum of 25 hours per week you'll clear areas by cutting grass and bushes, deweed, mulch, prune trees, plant, harvest, apply compost, ... Most meals are prepared by me, but all household chores are shared gender equally.  There can be outages, but the fiber optic internet is rather reliable and I have solar power and a backup water cistern. There is no public transportation. An Uber ride to/from the farm to San Juan can cost anything between USD 30 to 100 one way. I pick up/drop off when possible.

On the ground floor there are two rooms with a total of four individual beds for up to four volunteers (or two guests and two volunteers) who share a bathroom. We share living room, dining room, and kitchen. I have my private room and bath upstairs. Field clothes, gloves, sun cream, bug spray, soap and shampoo, linnen and towels are provided. We wash and line dry together when necessary. Mindful use of resources expected. No phones during meals. Expect oats, soy milk, seasonal farm fruits, seeds and nu

name: Eli - Organic Farm, Eco lodge and Cabin in the mountains
description:
It all started for me as a hobby when I  planted my own food in my home garden and constructed my first few beehives to bee keep. Then later on I began to farm on a larger scale hence me planting coffee, tomatoes, okra, cassava, cocoyam, plantain, orange, dragon fruit and other food stuff. I have 2 acres of land surrounded by mountains, caves and a waterfall where I intend grow organic food and build an eco lodge. I have started to grow apples and dragon fruit and I am very excited.

In order to accomplish and grow further, we are in the lookout for WWOOFFERS with experience and expertise in Woodwork, Architecture, Construction to help us implement our building project. We need help with building cabins and natural buildings. If this is something you can do kindly message us, you will sure have a good time volunteering with us.

Are you into Rammed Earth building we are looking for you to help us a rammed earth structure on the farm.

WWOOFers will be helping with farm work like planting fruits, coffee, plantain, seedlings, seed nursery, watering etc. We need the help of WWOOFers with expertise in wood work example build tables, chairs, beds, and structures with wood. We are also looking for someone who are into natural buildings and architecture, we really need help of WWOOFers who has so much knowledge in building ecologically.
We are urgently in need of content creators and curators as well.

Food and accommodation are free. We have a dormetory and private room for 
WWOOFers.

WWOOFers spend four hours each day with break times and 4 or 5 days in a week.

name: Gerald - 60-acres of rolling hill bench land located near Ozark National Forest in NW Arkansas
description:
We have 60 acres of rolling hill bench land located in the plateau region of Northwest Arkansas. Our farm is under construction near the small town of Tilly. We are interested in permaculture and in alternative ownership or ways of sharing or passing land on to the next generation. The farm is located a few miles from the Ozark National Forest, which provides recreational activities such as camping, swimming, hiking, and biking (We provide mountain bikes). Nearby Marshall offers seasonal rodeos. We are about two miles from Tilly and a few miles from Witt Springs; the area is sparsely populated. We especially welcome WWOOFers in the spring and fall. Couples are welcome anytime. We are usually flexible, but your stay times may need to be arranged in advance. 

We have incorporated the Emilia Hazelip synergistic garden method for our vegetables. In our forest garden we grow perennials, berries, fruit trees, nut trees, and leguminous nurse crops. Ernst Gotsch has been a big influence for our forest garden design.  Our practices encourage natural pollinators. We practice water harvesting using roofs, ponds, tanks, swales, and French drains. We are interested and utilize various natural building methods. We are inspired by architect Peter Cowman's work and others. We harvest timber from our land sustainably and use our portable sawmill to saw the lumber.  We care for chickens and ducks who free range and forage throughout the property. Their contributions include tick control and nitrogen for compost, as well as delicious eggs and cuteness. 

Here is a partial list of our projects and future interests: remodeling a house; building a cabin with a rocket mass heater; planning on building more chicken houses, garden shed and a greenhouse; doing rockwork in the near future; we are planning to harvest wind and solar energy; hügelkultur beds and contour ditches are incorporated in gardening; collecting firewood is an ongoing project; and there is always need for more garden mul

name: Michel - Family home and garden - rural, Homestead, Diversified farm
description:
This is an old time, beyond organic farm, where we grow, build, and repair everything we need. I am most passionate about teaching young people to grow and preserve food and build structures using traditional methods. See the Opportunity—Current Projects section for a detailed description of the many sustaining activities here.

Organic methods: No herbicides or pesticides of any kind. The soil on this former wetland is naturally rich, and we sometimes supplement with soil from the surrounding forests, and manure from our own horses. Crop rotation, tilling, and weeding is all we need for our many crops to thrive naturally here.

Certifications: We have not made the expenditure for organic certification, but our standards are beyond organic

Current projects: We grow many types of vegetables and fruits. Our main crop is potatoes, and we sell these at the local farmers market and in the surrounding towns. We also grow garlic, beets, carrots, cabbage, zucchini, squash, onions, lettuce, kale, peas, broccoli, tomatoes, currants, haskap, blueberries, and strawberries.

Additionally, we produce up to 500 bales of hay most years, and 150lbs of honey.

Every spring we make birch syrup from our trees using a wood fire and traditional methods, and this is an involved multi-week process.

We also harvest, preserve, and sell wild foods such as chaga, fiddleheads, stinging nettle, and rose.

Outside the growing season, there is always work to be done in the woodshop and repairing machines. We have an ethos of conserving and reusing, and as a result we generate almost zero waste. Wwoofers will often be involved in harvesting standing dead timbers, operating the sawmill, and learning timber framing techniques in order to build cabins, tree stands, and other structures.

We spend many long afternoons fishing and camping at nearby lakes, and in the fall we are often out in the bush hunting moose, grouse, and sometimes bears and goats. Winters are devoted to backcountry skii

name: Frank - Biodynamic Farm in South Estonia on old farmstead.
description:
I'm Frank, I've been living on my small, fairly isolated farm for three years, growing vegetables biodynamically. I have been growing organically and biodynamically for around 20 years. Currently I'm setting up the infrastructure of a farm business and renovating an old farmstead in beautiful wild southern Estonia. The farm is mostly woodlands with 1 ha of grasslands and 1/2 ha of potential growing area.  I have a young daughter, two cats and currently two sheep.

Tasks are varied between horticultural, building tasks (a new composting toilet), looking after animals and all manner of life maintaining activities in a self reliant homestead farm.

Accommodation is in a lovely large bell tent. There is an outside earth closet, no bathroom. Water comes from our own well, we also have a pond. We will prepare meals in my kitchen and I'm happy to cater for vegetarians and vegans.

There are plenty of opportunities for hiking in the surrounding area. There is a lake nearby with a beach. The nearest town is Antsla, 14 km away. 
I love music and play guitar and of course I love the countryside and growing vegetables.
_wwoof_travel: Nearest bus stop is Urvaste, but I can pick you up from other local places such as Kuldre Rist or Kanepi.

name: Dorothée - Ferme agroécologique : production de plants et semences, agroforesterie, sol vivant
description:
Bonjour ami.e.s. des jardins,
Je m'appelle Dorothée, je suis passionnée par le Vivant sous toutes ses formes, et j'essaie de vivre et transmettre cette passion à d'autres.
Mon activité principale est la production de plants et de semences paysannes, sous mention Nature & Progrès.
Le jardin de pieds-mères est mené en agroforesterie, selon des procédés permacoles, et depuis 2020  je cultive exclusivement en sol vivant, c'est à dire sans aucun travail du sol.

Le travail est très différent d'une saison à l'autre. Ici, petite pépinière super diversifiée, il y a une grande variété de tâches selon les saisons, le temps, l'énergie.. et la Lune  (je travaille avec le rythme lunaire)
Exemples : préparation et entretien des planches permanentes, entretien et taille des vivaces, récoltes diverses, tri de graines, travail sous serre (repiquages, semis, multiplications...), sans oublier quelques travaux de nettoyage et d'entretien des structures ou du matériel..

Pour le moment je ne peux plus accueillir avec hébergement et je propose uniquement des journées d'immersion.
La nourriture est principalement bio et au maximum locale, voire autoproduite selon la saison, le régime est presque végétarien.

Un temps de prise de contact par Visio est bienvenu afin de préciser les attentes de chacun et les travaux en cours.

name: Carlos - Family home and garden - rural, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Fruit tree nursery, Mixed Farm
description:
Our project is projected as a Rural Park and Cider House from heritage Apple orcharde of 120 years old. just 15 minutes north of the city of Valdivia. The objective is to integrate and experiment with sustainable technologies that can be applied by the local community. It will be a place open to visitors with the possibility of learning about new technologies with easy access. Water purification with artificial wetlands, dry baths, green roofs, construction with straw and mud, edible forests, organic garden, etc. In addition, we want to offer cultural activities such as cinema, theater, music, talks, classes, gastronomy and health, with the collaboration of different professionals and incorporating the community into everything. Finally, we want to develop work spaces certified by the Ministry of Health where local producers can develop productive activities and have a showcase to sell their products to visitors. In fair trade and innovation. For this we are developing a rural cafeteria with space for public attention, but also community activities, events and sale of local products. Finally, we want that when the project is consolidated, it will also be transformed into a point of services such as post office, access to Internet, rural ecological recycling, space for local fairs and barter, and support for the use of water activities through the Cayumapu river and bicycles for An inner route. www.quintamichay.cl

Organic methods: We have implemented composting, recycling, rainwater harvesting design and organic garden. We do not use any type of pesticide. In addition, in terms of thermal efficiency, we have reconditioned an old abandoned house with sustainable technologies such as straw and mud walls, green roof and double glazing. In addition, a greenhouse next to the house, perimeter terraces for temperature control and 14 roof windows to capture the most light. Many coatings in the house are recycled from other buildings and materials such as mud and straw are b

name: Maria - Horse ranch and organic farm in the hills overlooking patagonian glaciers and carrera lake
description:
Hello WWOOFers!! my name is toto iam 30 years old and together with my husband - ilango (35), we run an organic farm and adventure expedition company. Iam an organic farmer and yoga instructor; ilango is a wildlife researcher, guide and horse riding expert. Our project aims at rural slow tourism where the protagonist is authentic cultural and scientific adventures. We share the ranch with 18 horses, 2 dogs and 2 cats.

On the ranch we have various activities and daily jobs as mentioned in the following:
- organic farm duties (planting, weeding, watering, processing, harvest and potential selling in the local market).
- Taking care of the horses, dogs and cats. (feeding, watering etc)
- Horse training and taming sessions
- General ranch maintenance (fencing, construction, barn cleaning etc)
- Assisting with horse rides, treks, tours, recieving guests etc.

We are great chefs! or at least believe we are. We value quality food products, therefore most veggies comes from our farm, the rest is all local. We always have 3 homecooked meals a day (Breakfast, lunch and dinner)
We have 2 options for accommodation: one is a tent with a comfortable mattress and the second option is we have a small rustic outhouse room with a comfortable bed.

Besides work we love to cook, play music (ilango is a guitarist), go for horse rides, trek, toto does yoga.  We have people over quite often and as we live in a multicultural community (from gauchos, expats, other locals) the conversations and moments are special. 
As we live in the midst of touristic attractions, there is alot to do! Here are the highlights:
- swimming, kayaking, boating on the lake.
- marble caverns exploration
- Maqui waterfalls 
- Patagonia national park (1.5 hrs drive)
- Rafting in Puerto Bertrand
- Various Trekking trails

name: Michał - Alternative co-operative community, Intentional Community, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures
description:
Start-up/open project regenerative farm focused on ancient grains and organic gardening, young orchard. Offering transformative time working together. Simple conditions, close to nature, composting toilets, communal kitchen and chill out zones, sauna, different types of accommodations are available(yurt, cabin, tent). Farm is located in a little remote place surrounded by hills and the forest. 

Organic methods: crop rotation, bio-diversity, no-till, slurry, working around moon calendar, permaculture approach.

Certifications: Its a small scale project focused on self-sufficiency and local exchange.

Current projects: chicken tractor, greenhouse, beekeeping, buckwheat, einkorn, wood construction, expanding structure and community spaces, fruit gathering, garden cultivation, work in young orchard, compost site.

Food: we eat what we grow..

I'm also open for people who would like to create own projects here or settle permanently.

Internet access available

name: Hansel and Sue - Diverse family farm in foothills of Sierra Nevada
description: The farm is located 8 miles from North Fork up Mammoth Pool Rd. within the Sierra National Forest. We're a small diverse family farm located in the foothills of the Sierra Nevada. We're on a total of 80 acres of which 10 are currently being farmed, along with 4 greenhouses and barns for goats and chickens. We produce a variety of seasonal vegetables all farmed organically and we were recently certified organic. All of our electricity comes from solar, hydro or wind with occasional generator back-up. We are working to produce healthy food for our local community as sustainably as possible. Chores include planting, weeding, harvesting, occasional farm construction and, if interested, tending to the animals, milking goats, collecting eggs, etc. In the winter there is forest management work (brushing, woodcutting and gathering, etc.)Though we've been hosting Wwoofers for both short term and long term visits, we'd prefer those who'd be willing to make a commitment of at least two months or longer (after a 2 week trial period for compatibility assessment), since we're most interested in working with those who are seriously interested in learning about sustainable agriculture, rather than those who are seeking a low cost way to see the country. We don't think you can really get a sense of what it takes to farm if you're only here for a week or two.We currently have two 2 bedroom trailers (one of which has a full kitchen and bathroom) and four RVs, available for Woofers to use as housing. We also now have a small ""cob"" structure designed by one of our wonderful ""Wwoofers"" that can be used for housing as well.  Interns are expected to prepare their own meals from food we supply, except for lunch on work days when our visitors rotate preparing a big meal for the crew in our home.   (Please note that we are a working farm and there are lots of cat and dogs around, so if you have strong allergies to furry critters, this might not be the place for you...)We are located in a
_wwoof_travel: The closest train/Bus station and airport are in Fresno, CA

name: Guy - Small Scale regenerative farm north of Hollister, growing vegetables and fruit trees.
description:
At our farm, we grow wholesome, chemical-free food using regenerative practices that restore soil and habitat while nourishing our community. We cultivate both familiar and globally inspired crops and share the joy of farm-to-table meals, learning, and connection.

Depending on the season and which projects are happening, work at the farm may include some of the following: planting, pruning, cultivating, or harvesting at the vegetable patches, berry/vine patches, or the fruit trees. Mulching, weeding, putting up or taking down trelises. Food preservation (canning, dehydrating, etc).

Snacks and meals will be provided fo volunteers. From the field if we can, or otherwise will have supplies on hand, or order in some food when neded.

Other than growing food, I deeply enjoy cooking and sharing it with friends. A farm-to-table feast with friends/volunteers at the end of a farm day, is hard to beat.
The farm is located in a beautiful rural area, less than 1 hour drive from San Jose. For great hikes, Pinnacles national park is about a 45 minutes drive south.
_wwoof_travel: Can possibly provide a ride from San Jose or along the way

name: Damian Abel - Cocoa plantation farm, livestock care, dairy production, has several fruit plants that complement th
description:
Hola WWOOFers  soy Damian,  tengo 34 años, junto a mi mama tenemos una finca donde producimos Cacao, café para el mercado además tenemos el cuidado de Ganado, caballos, cerdos y una gran variedad de aves como (gallinas pavos patos) contamos con un espacio de mucha vegetación y animales.

En la mañana recogemos a las vacas para ser ordeñadas, y luego realizamos queso, mas adelante alimentamos a los cerdos y a las aves con maiz u otros alimentos, dependiendo la época realizamos la limpieza de las plantaciones o la recolección de frutos o secado de los granos, la tarde se prepara el almuerzo y compartimos frutas de las propias plantaciones. en la tarde se guarda los animales y se revisan las plantaciones ya en la noche tomamos la merienda y descasamos.

La finca cuenta con una casa con el acceso a luz, agua y espacio para varios visitantes, contamos con 3 dormitorios  con tres camas para una persona
un baño con ducha y una letrina.
la alimentación seria cada día desayuno almuerzo y merienda.  (alimentos variados, o puede ser ajustado según requieran las personas si tuvieran alguna restricción alimenticia)

Mi mama le gusta mucho la música es una artista :)  y a mi gusta el senderismo y deportes como ciclismo MTB. 
El sector es un lugar rico en espacios verdes. cercas hay una gran variedad de rios y lugares muy bonitos por conocer. se puede hacer caminatas ciclismo pesca natación, es un lugar alejado de las ciudades por lo que es un lugar de mucha tranquilidad.

name: Emmet and Cella - Micro Dairy, CSA, and Pastured Meat farm
description:
We have a small diversified farm on 35 acres in the driftless region of WI. We grow vegetables for 80 CSA members who come pick up their share at our farm once a week, June - December. We have a small mixed herd of 6 dairy cows that we milk once a day, and we produce glass bottle milk, yogurt, and soft serve ice cream in our tiny on farm Grade A dairy plant. We sell our dairy products to our CSA members and at our local food coop and farmers market. We are also lucky to own 20 acres of woods along with a tiny creek and 12 mature and productive apple trees.

Morning milking chores start all our days, then activities vary with the season. Spring, many days spent on planting, construction projects, fencing repairs, firewood, and greenhouse work. Summer, many days spent on planting, weeding, and harvesting vegetables, as well as rotational grazing the cows. Fall, many days spent  harvesting and washing vegetables, greenhouse work, moving animals, building temporary fence, and cutting firewood. Winter, many days spent doing animal chores, maintaining equipment, and cutting firewood.

Accommodations will be in our farmhouse and include a private bathroom, attic bedroom, and private office/sitting room. We eat all our meals as a family and/or with our farm crew and will welcome any wwoofer to join us at the table. Food and kitchen space would also be available for occasional meals to be prepared by wwoofers if they prefer or we are gone.

We generally work from around 7:30-4 or 5 pm. Wwoofers would be welcome to decide which half of the day they would prefer to join us for. 

Lake Pepin is a beautiful area of WI and MN, with many scenic villages to explore. There are numerous opportunities to hike or mountain bike within a half hours drive, and rock climbing in Red Wing. Red Wing is a scenic river town with nice parks, an historic downtown shopping district, and offers the practical basics a short drive from our farm (ten minutes).
_wwoof_travel: Red Wing MN amtrak station (10 min), Minneapolis St Paul international airport (1 hr)

name: Sebastian - Certified Organic Pecan Orchard in Bastrop, Texas
description:
Ponderosa Farms is home to five thousand 8-10 year-old pecan trees (numerous varieties), a large flock of Dorper sheep, two friendly, gorgeous mares, a livestock guardian dog, one odd duck and its barn-cat companion. We also have fruit trees, a garden space, and plenty of tools and equipment for you to get your hands on.

Our sheep provide multiple vital services as we work to restore the vitality of our orchards and pasture. We maintain active silvopasture by rotationally grazing the flock through the orchard. This entails running electric fencing, moving water & mineral troughs, and some simple herding. In addition, we sell live sheep as well as processed meats.

Other work on the farm may involve use of heavy equipment (tractors, mowers, trimmers), irrigation systems, electric fence repair, general property maintenance. 

Accommodations-wise, we have a small, furnished, air-conditioned house for couples or those desiring a little more home privacy space, as well as a two-bedroom cabin, which has been known to become a fun little bunkhouse situation when we are well-staffed. No matter which house you end up calling home during your stay, you are expected to be a responsible adult about cleanliness and property maintenance. We will keep you stocked in groceries, but you should be prepared to make your own meals. In terms of internet, I have strong 5G reception out here, but ain’t no Wi-Fi right now. 

The farm is just outside Bastrop, which is both a lovely little downtown and a greater region of central Texas featuring lots of wild, wooded, and well-maintained State Park sites to explore. We also have the Colorado River right here in our backyard! Or, if city-life and live music are more your alley, Austin is an hour away as the car drives. On that note, you best come with your own set of wheels since we are rather remote from both Bastrop & Austin, and there is nothing in the way of public transport out here. 

In accordance with WWOOF, we generally expect no mor

name: Carmen - Lodge con Huerto ecologico pequeño en Cocachimba, norte del Perú , en la propiedad hay un hospedaje
description:
Hola somos Carmen, Francesco y nuestro hijo Leonardo de 12 . Vivimos con nuestra mascota Puka y algunos pocos gatos. Tenemos un alojamiento rural, donde hay un huertito donde cultivamos sobre todo ensaladas y aromáticas y algunas hortalizas, también tenemos varios frutales típicos de la zona, maracuya, granadilla, plátanos, piña, café, caña, papaya...todo en pequeña escala. Par nuestro uso y uso en las preparaciones para nuestros huéspedes. Tenemos 3 bungalows familiares, máximo 12 personas peor casi nunca está lleno

En realidad nos gustaría que nos ayuden y aprender de los WWOOFers  que tienen conocimientos básicos trabajando juntos para ayudarnos a crear pequeñas zonas de cultivos en suelos húmedos, quizá en bancales más elevados, también ayudarnos con técnicas de compostaje y abono, abono verde, riego etc, construcción de almácigos de siembra en madera

Habitación privada, baño externo, compartido si hay otra persona pero en general no. Podemos dar desayuno todos los días (auto servicio)  y almuerzo solo algunos días si hay cocinero, cena no , uso de cocina si

Nos gusta mucho la música, la cocina, la fotografía, viajar e intercambiar. Vivimos en un lugar privilegiado pro la naturaleza, suelos fértiles, mucha agua, Valle de las cataratas, incluida Gocta con 770 m, una región con una oferta inmensa de senderos, ideal para los que gustan caminar ya que está llena de rutas para caminar y paisajes bellisimos. También muy rica en historia (cultura Chachapoyas) y arqueología . estamos en el valle del rio Utcubamba en Perú, departamento de Amazonas. a 1800 msnm, clima templado. No es selva tropical
_wwoof_travel: Chachapoyas Aeropuerto- Pedro Ruiz estación de autobus

name: Edmer - A humble family Farm located in the south of Luzon.
description:
In 2009, my family envisioned a small property as a shelter for homeless children, driven by  compassionate hearts to make a positive impact in the community.  Despite facing financial challenges, our determination to create a haven for those in need was evident. Unfortunately, due to the lack of funds, the original plan to establish a shelter was put on hold.

In 2019, we decided to pivot our vision and utilize the property as a family farm. What started as a practical solution turned into an inspiring journey of transformation. The farm not only sustains the family but also exemplifies  resilience, adaptability, and dedication to making the most of available resources.

*Planting (Urban gardening, aquaponics, vertical farming and more) 
*Making organic fertilizers
*Feeding chickens and native pigs
*Pruning fruit-bearing trees

We can share dinner for most nights and forage for breakfast and lunch. WWOOFers also have the option to buy food from nearby stores. Cooking tools including stove can be provided.

Almost the entire family is musically inclined. We play a variety of musical instruments.  Near the farm's location are numerous places to visit including the Taal Volcano.

name: Sara - Fazenda Viva - Produção e venda direta de hortícolas e frutos
description:
Atividade de horto-fruticultura periurbana agroecológica numa fazenda de 1 hectare em Leiria, à borda do rio Lis, desde 2021. Cultivamos num ecosistema agroflorestal, nunca utilizamos nenhum produto químico sintético (fitossanitário ou fertilizante) e nunca lavramos o solo. Desde 2025, temos a certificação de Agricultura biológica (AB). 

Atualmente, vendemos a nossa produção de hortícolas, aromáticas e alguns frutos, em ciclo de consumo curto (venda direta e local, do produtor ao consumidor), no Mercado municipal de Leiria.

As nossas principais práticas: 
    • agricultura de conservação do solo (msv / solo vivo)
    • agrofloresta
    • low-tech

Procuramos uma ou um wwoofer : 
- para nos apoiar na nossa atividade de horticultura
- ajudar na manutenção e melhoria da estrutura e das instalações, geralmente em auto e ecoconstrução (materiais naturais e/ou reciclados)
- auxiliar-nos na confeção e processo de conservas e desidratados
- partilhar experiências e conhecimentos em agroecologia, eco-construção e low-tech
- uma atividade connosco durante 3 dias por semana (fins-de-semanas sempre livres!)

Oferecemos um quarto durante a estadia e comida vegetariana. 

Vivemos e trabalhamos na zona urbana de Leiria. As praias ficam a 30-45 minutos (nomeadamente a praia da Nazaré) e na região existem vários trilhos para caminhadas. Leiria encontra-se bem centrada em Portugal, dando acesso próximo às principais cidades e vários locais a visitar (Lisboa, Porto, Coimbra, Caldas da Rainha, Mafra, Alcobaça, Batalha, Fátima, Óbidos, Peniche). Todas as destinações estão acessíveis em transportes públicos. Podemos também pôr uma bicicleta à disposição durante a estadia. 

Somos um casal, Sara e Raphaël. Falamos português e francês fluentemente e temos boas noções de espanhol e inglês. Gostamos de partilhar uma boa refeição, compartilhar as nossas experiências e aprender de outras pessoas. Nesta época, temos geralmente muito trabalho, mas faremos o necessário para nos adaptar às capac

name: Elizabeth - Mission: grow and make own self care
description:
I am Elizabeth. 
I am living here alone with 2 cats that live outdoors but eat inside. 
 Eco Culture is a 501c3 non profit organization. Mission: make own self care and general sustainability.

1. Preparing beds
2. Compost maintenance- stirring and adding kitchen scraps
3. Weeding
4: digging out trees
5. Seed exchange events
6. Preparing seeds for exchange and storage
7. Making tinctures, emulsions teas from harvest
8. Planting beds
9. Making toxic free weed killers: vinegars and soaps 
10. Applying these applications
11. Organizing rocks for gardens
12. Organizing and designing rock gardens 
13. Sweeping raking and tidying up
14. Overseeing the essential oils manufacture. Outreach educational events for making self care. 
15. Cleaning the rooms used for gardens: tool rooms, mud rooms. 

Wwoofers will live in a carriage house and/or  home on property. 
They will have access to own kitchen and food will be supplied. I will help with food prep daily at 8:30 am Monday—Friday.  I would like help with this too, along with some shopping, and cleaning up. 

I enjoy hiking canoeing swimming playing music. 
This place is walking distance to restaurants, bars, groceries, library, book stores ect.

name: Benjamin - Organic farm On the Hills of Shool  with a magnificent lake view
description:
I run an Airbnb in a beautiful lake view in the Hills of Shool, i have a dozen chickens few ducks, rabbits, one goat and one cow. My wife and I grow organic vegetables and we offer other activities like fishing, hiking and kayaking.

WWoofers will learn how to farm, from and to take care of the farm animals.  we will teach woofers grain, vegetable and medicinal herb and spices farming .Seed saving and production . Beekeeping . Our  we also offer morrocan cuisine classes. volunteers share their farming experience with the locals, They also share the culture and the food with the most generous people in the world (the Moroccans)
we  offer trips to Rabat (the Capital) and to other famous touristic places during the days off to explore this wonderful country. 

WWoofers are free to choose what activities they need to do, from planting, feeding the animals and give us new ideas how to improve our project.

I love to travel every year my gird friend and I travel to a new destination , last year we went to turkey this April we will go to Vietnam , I spend 4 months of the year in Chicago Illinois where visit my family and work. after I spend the remaining time at the farm " making homemade wine, working and enjoying my friends and visitors.

name: Uriel - Produção Orgânica, Permacultura e Ecoturismo na Serra Fina
description:
**Atenção, recebemos apenas WWOOFs que queiram ficar 2 semana ou mais, por conta da logística da Fazenda.
Aceitamos voluntários que possuam experiência prévia com agricultura e sustentabilidade ou que realmente estejam empenhados em aprender sobre.**

Conheça agora um pouco mais sobre nós:

 A Fazenda Berço das Águas, situada no município de Itanhandu, aos pés da Serra Fina, é um verdadeiro tesouro de ambientes naturais e hidrográficos. Localizada dentro da APA da Mantiqueira, a propriedade tem mais de 90% de sua área em processo de regeneração natural, o que contribui significativamente para  a  biodiversidade  e  a  proteção  dos  recursos  hídricos.  As  principais nascentes  do  Rio  Verde,  que  abastece  diversas  cidades  da  região,  estão protegidas dentro da fazenda. Atualmente, a propriedade é um importante ponto de ecoturismo no município, possuindo infraestrutura para o recebimento de turistas e destacando-se por suas cachoeiras de beleza ímpar, com águas de coloração esverdeada.

Atualmente além da equipe de campo, a Fazenda conta com um agrônomo e um guia turístico que irão te orientar para aproveitar da melhor forma seu voluntariado. Também contamos com a Bela e o Cigano, border collies que serão companheiros durante a estadia na fazenda.

Um dia normal para aqueles envolvidos nas atividades agrícolas começa cedo pela manhã com um bom café e após a alimentação dos animais. A depender da época do ano, poderemos ir para campo para cuidar dos cultivos, realizando , capinas, limpezas, adubações e aplicações de bioinsumos. Também poderemos ir para o armazém realizar a limpeza e classificação da produção. Após completar as horas de serviço, você estará livre para explorar nossas trilhas ou tomar um belo banho de cachoeira.

O alojamento fica dentro do Ivos Hostel, espaço da fazenda onde recepcionamos turistas e atletas de montanha. O quarto é compartilhado com voluntários e turistas, e o hostel conta com cozinha equipada, água quente e um lindo quintal com

name: Robin - Farm
description:
We are a animal friendly community engaged with farming, fruit growing and forestry. 
Besides me Leonora is my agro-partner who leads permaculture farming. We are almost in the centre of sLOVEnia, near Litija. We hope to become self-sustaining because we have two springs of water, 12 ha of forest, a lot of healthy fertile land and also a big green house. 

I'm still working in Ljubljana between Monday and Thursday when you may join me on the afternoon for the ride from Ljubljana to the farm. Usually around 5pm from Litostrojska 59 in Ljubljana or in Litija train station about 40’ later. Independently from Ljubljana you may take a train to Litija and (only on a workdays catch a bus (or hitchhike) to Cerovica (direction Gabrovka) at 12.23, 14.28, 15.38 and 17.18. From Cerovica you continue 300m and walk uphill for 2km or hitchhike what is easy. 

Activities include cleaning, facility maintenance, minor repairs, especially in the summer, forest cleaning and firewood harvesting, from March to December, help in the fields and in the winter garden, from April to September, grass cutting and harvesting, in June we pick Siberian blueberries, in the summer we pick pears, and in the fall we pick apples. In November, we usually prune the blueberry orchard.

We may provide you, mostly private, accommodation and plant based food for three meals a day and expect from you to participate in the help up to 5hours a day, 5 days a week, from breakfast to the lunch. It is expected that your rest days are between Monday and Thursday.                    
LONG TERM OR PERMANENT COHABITATION IS ALSO AVAILABLE as well as the possibility of taking over the farm in a few years.

So far i traveled over150 countries, 88 on a bicycle, run 10 marathons and hosted more than 1200 guests in Ljubljana and on the farm.

I speak Croatian, English, Italian, Spanish and a bit of French, German, Portuguese and Bahasa.
DOBRODOŠLI - WELCOME - BENVENUTI - BENVENIDOS - BIENVENUE - WILLKOMMEN - BEM VIDNO - SEL

name: Thai Soon - Tiny Permaculture community farm located in a small town.
description:
ABOUT US

In 2015, we started a guesthouse and embraced a self-sufficient lifestyle. Through WWOOF, we welcomed volunteers, deepened our connection with nature, and became more aware of environmental issues like climate change, soil erosion, and species extinction. This inspired us to focus on environmental education and build a like-minded community.

In 2019, we launched a community project, but COVID-19 forced us to pause. We resumed in 2023, and from 2025 onwards, most of our WWOOF volunteer efforts will be dedicated to this project.

Our 1,400 sqm community space features a mini food forest, an ecological pond, a spice garden, a vegetable greenhouse, a workshop area, volunteer dormitories, a seedling zone, a tool storage area, rainwater collection facilities, and a café. Our goal is to show how a small piece of land can produce diverse, high-quality food while supporting biodiversity.

FOR 2026 ESTIMATED PROGRAMME TIMELINE

January, February:
Completing setting up for plants nursery center

February, March, April, May:
I will be focusing on building our community cafeteria. Volunteers who are interested in woodworking or construction are encouraged to apply.

May, June, July:
We will begin constructing a natural pond as part of our food forest system.

July, August, Sept:
Preparing for and organising a one-week festival from 28th August to 5th September, aimed at offering people an immersive experience in local culture, community connection, and sustainability awareness.

Oct, Nov, Dec: 
build a learning playground of kids

WHAT IS POSSIBLE TO EXPERIENCE DURING YOUR STAY

Here are some of the experiences you may have with us in 2026. You’re very welcome to share your interests, and I’ll do my best to shape the experience around what you’d love to learn.

🌱 Daily farm maintenance
Composting, seedling care, weeding, planting, and hands-on farm work.
For stays longer than one month, volunteers will also take part in developing our food forest using permaculture an
_wwoof_travel: Kulai bus station is 20 mins walking distance/ Kulai train station and Senai Airport only 15 mins driving

name: Katherine - Nido de pájaros es una área de conservación del bosque andino en Boyacá.  Principios Permacultura
description:
Hola somos kathe y jhon una pareja de conservaciónistas que hemos dedicado nuestra vida a la restauración de bosque nativo ,
Vivimos en el área de amortiguacion del Parque Natural Regional Serrania del Peligro con nuestros dos amigos peludos Bruno y Buco.
 Nuestro proyecto se basa en la conservación y restauración de un bosque que fue intervenido para sacar madera. Gracias a los eucaliptos y pinos pudimos costruir las bases de nuestra casa taller medicinal, en este momento estamos en la fase de paredes en técnica bareque barro y paja.

Aqui aprenderas de permacultura, un estilo de vida en armonía con el ecosistema,  tecnica de Biocostruccion bareque , agroecologia, baños secos, micoterapia y restauración .

Alimentación saludable, semi vegetariana , una o dos veces al mes comemos pollo o pescado orgánico de amigos campesinos que nos proveen en la Red de circuitos cortos.  La preparación de los alimentos se reparte entre las personas que estemos en la casa conviviendo.  El dia que cocinas prepararas desayuno y almuerzo,  la cena la hacemos entre todos. 
También repartimos el aseo de la cocina y lavado de las ollas .
Una vez a la semana se limpia el baño y zonas comunes.

Amo loa hongos!! He dedicado los últimos años a aprender sobre ellos, sus usos comunes y medicinales, soy micoterapeuta, hago acompañamiento de psicoterapia con psilocibina.  
Cada 15 días hacemos círculo de palabra alrededor del fuego, compartimos la medicina del rape del mambe y ambil. 
Jhon es amante de las plantas , sabe sus nombres, maneras de propagarse, germinacion y muchas cosas más. 
Arcabuco es un territorio de montañas y naturaleza abundante, tenemos dos paramos ( iguaque y la mesa de los indios) y vivimos en una montaña a 15 minutos hay cascadas y senderos hermosos que solemos visitar los fines de semana.

Hello, we are Kathe and Jhon, a couple of conservationists who have dedicated our lives to the restoration of native forests. We live in the buffer area of ​​the Serrania del Peligro Re

name: Violeta - Um sítio na Serra da Mantiqueira com orgânicos, animais, abelhas e turismo rural
description:
Olá! Somos uma pequena família de dois, eu Vi e meu esposo Léo, temos um lindo cachorro pastor alemão chamado Dalí que acompanha o nosso dia a dia.
Saímos da Itália e mudamos para Mantiqueira para continuar a viver imersos na natureza,  plantamos nossos alimentos e queremos ser o mais autossuficientes possível, produzindo a maior parte do que precisamos. 
Somos focados no sobrevivencialismo e preparação.
Temos alguns projetos de bioconstrução e queremos incluir nas nossas vidas pessoas que tenham afinidade com os nossos projetos, que amem a natureza e o bem viver, que gostem de animais, de compartilhar experiências e aprendizados.
Estamos ansiosos para conhecer pessoas que queiram trilhar juntos esta linda aventura que é a vida!
As terefas a realizar são:
Produzir hortaliças, plantar árvores frutíferas, flores,  roçar a grama quando precisar, projeto de irrigação para a horta, cuidar dos animais, construir o lago para criar peixe, construção de pequenas cabanas com técnicas de bioconstrução,  construção de piquetes para cabras, construir um mine teatro em ambiente natural e ajuda geral no dia a dia do sítio.

Os voluntários ficarão numa cabana charmosa independente onde terão um quarto independente com duas camas de soltero, banheiro e cozinha de uso comum para compartilhar as refeições.

Somos um casal que desfruta da natureza,  da vida na montanha  do contato com os animais e também gostamos muito de cozinhar juntos, escutar música e ler livros.
O bioma é Mata Atlântica, o sitio esta a 1500 metros de altitude e o clima é tropical de altitude, a região possui um leque muito amplo de posibilidades para se explorar, temos rios,  cachoeiras, bosques, montanhas como o Pico dos Marins 2421, Marinsinho 2432 e Pico do Itaguaré 2308, que são um ponto de referência para o montanhismo nacional.
Temos duas lindas vias de escalada em rocha na região uma com mais de 200 m.

name: Leo - Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures
description:
There is a lot to learn and do here so we only accept WWOOFers who can commit to 6 WEEKS minimum.
PLEASE DO NOT APPLY IF YOU ARE PROPOSING DATES OF LESS THAN 42 DAYS, not including arrival and departure day.

We are a boat access only eco-lodge next to the Corcovado National Park. Our mission is to help connect our guests with nature through adventure tours. We welcome travelers who want to contribute to hands-on production of organic food for our restaurant kitchen and help with dynamic tasks.

What We Offer
Volunteers stay in a shared dorm surrounded by rainforest, wildlife, and the pacific ocean. Meals are provided and enjoyed with the rest of our staff. On your time off, you’ll have access to pristine beaches, streams, and trails for swimming, kayaking, and wildlife watching.

We welcome WWOOFers who are enthusiastic, independent, and not afraid to get their hands dirty. The work is physical but rewarding. Tasks include but are not limited to:

Collecting food from the greenhouse early in the morning.
Assisting with greenhouse maintenance (pruning, planting, weeding).
Managing composting systems.
Keeping the oceanfront yard clear of palm leaves, coconuts, and other natural debris.
Maintaining the plantain field.
Unloading the boat.
Any other task that can and may arise. 

Who Fits Best Here
This placement suits people who:

Enjoy working outdoors in a tropical climate.
Are physically fit and self-motivated.
Appreciate living close to nature (expect monkeys, scarlet macaws, and the occasional tapir in the garden).
We are looking for the type of person who picks up any beach trash that they see because they genuinely care about the environment instead of walking past it and looking for a free holiday.
We are BOAT ACCESS ONLY, which means WWOOFers should be someone who truly enjoys being in nature. You will not have access to stores or any outside services, just the lodge and the last coastal primary rainforest on earth.

Extras
In exchange for your help, you’ll le

name: Brook - 244-acre working biodynamic ranch and whole systems learning center near Aspen, CO
description: Founded in 1997 , we are a 244 acre working Biodynamic ranch and Whole Systems Learning Center. We are located in the Central Rocky Mountains at 6,500 ft elevation near Carbondale 35 miles from Aspen,  and a 3 hour drive west of Denver, Colorado.We  offer several short-term WWOOF opportunities that rotate throughout the season with a two-week minimum stay.  Longer WWOOFer work/study stays can happen if it is good for all concerned.  WOOFERS should expect to pitch a tent but sometimes a temporary RV is available. Meals are provided mostly from our Biodynamic ranch production of meat, raw dairy, veggies, herbs, ...  What we do not produce we buy local organic first.  Typically for our lunch meal we cook and eat together.  We see this as an important time together for a more full experience.  Much learning happens around the table.  Breakfast and dinner  our crew & staff fends for themselves. We also offer 2-6 internships/ apprenticeships positions for a commitment of eight months to one-year long intensified learning-working in the various integrated field areas of our whole farm organism and Whole Systems Learning Center. We are currently accepting applications. Internships/ Apprenticeships typically run late March thru mid-November with other interns committing to  year round stays.  Internships/ Apprenticeships in, Rural and Urban Food Production, Livestock, Dairy, and Management Intensive Grazing(MIG),   Even with a focused study all internship/ apprenticeship work and learn across all the dynamic system layers of the ranch. Intern/ Apprentice Reflection “At Sustainable Settings I was intensely engaged in the soil fertility cycle, working daily to care for all the life on the ranch. We milked, slaughtered, planted, cultivated, harvested, maintained machinery, made premium compost, and irrigated fields, cut and put up hay and more. We studied and practiced Biodynamics, making and applying all the BD preps, and coordinated our activities with the rhythms of the year
_wwoof_travel: Train station in Glenwood Springs , RFTA Bus in Carbondale

name: César Héctor - Productos de agricultura y avez de corral en forma sana
description:
En la provincia de AMBO, departamento de Huanuco, país Perú, finca ausostenible con la crianza de animales y plantación de frutales orgánicos.

Manejo en plantaccion de frutales, podas, cosecha , plantacion y alimentación a las avez de corral.

Alimentación en base a la producción de nuestros productos que producimos  , el alojamiento es en cuarto privado para el voluntario.

Contamos con un rio al borde de la finca, cerros rodeado de vegetacion, la carretera ingresa a la misma finca.

name: Damian - Cultivo agroecologico de cítricos, bioconstrucción
description:
Somos Damián y Nadia, junto a nuestros 6 hijos vivimos en un predio rural a 7 km de Libertador San Martín, provincia de Entre Rios.
