Nos dedicamos al cultivo de cítricos agroecológicos (naranjas ombligo, de jugo, limones, mandarinas), elaboramos diversos productos como aceites esenciales, mermeladas, lemoncello, velas, etc.  Tambien habitan el espacio ovejas, algunas gallinas, guineas, un monte de árboles nativos y nuestras mascotas (tres perros y siete gatitos).
El camino hasta la localidad más cercana es de tierra, no hay transporte hasta la granja pero disponemos de vehículos.  La localidad de Libertador San Martín es una pequeña ciudad multicultural, con una Universidad, Sanatorio y Centro de Vida Sana muy reconocidos. 
Habitamos una casa autoconstruída con materiales naturales y reciclados bajo el paradigma de la bioconstrucción. El entorno es natural y el contexto es rural, llevamos adelante nuestra actividad a través de prácticas sustentables y evaluando el menor impacto posible en el ambiente.

Las actividades a realizar en la granja son las de mantenimiento (poda, preparado de biofertilizantes, corte de cesped, recolectar leña) y cuidado de la plantación de cítiricos, cosecha, elaboración de subproductos, mantenimiento del predio y la casa, actividades de construcción con materiales naturales .

Estamos abiertos al intercambio de experiencias que nos enriquezcan mutuamente. Esperamos apertura y buena predisposición al aprendizaje y la convivencia, al respeto del ambiente y quienes lo habitan (humanos, animales y naturaleza). Brindamos todas las comidas diarias y lugar para el descanso a cambio de 5 horas de trabajo diarias, 5 dias a la semana. 

Disfrutamos de charlas, comidas, fogones, guitarreadas y música con amigos y visitantes. Ofrecemos espectaculares puestas de sol, noches estrelladas, recorridos a pié o bicicleta por caminos vecinales. Visitas al río Paraná, al parque Nacional Pre-delta y playas cercanas.

name: Hélène - Off-Grid Family Life in Northern BC
description:
We are a small family of three living off-grid on a 5-acre property in northern British Columbia, about 30 km from the beautiful village of Atlin and 20 km from the Yukon border.

We enjoy a quiet life close to nature and are slowly building and improving our home and property ourselves.

Our project is mainly about creating a simple and sustainable lifestyle in a remote place. We grow some of our food in the garden, heat with firewood, collect water from our well, and run our electricity with a small solar system. We are gradually developing the property with small building projects such as cabins, a woodshed, and a sauna, while expanding our garden.

We share the land with plenty of wildlife (including bears, moose, and many birds), but we currently do not have farm animals. We do have an old cat!

We enjoy meeting people from different places and sharing this unique northern lifestyle with others who are curious about off-grid living.

Our main activities are gardening and small building projects around the property.

In the garden, we grow vegetables adapted to the northern climate. Tasks can include preparing garden beds, planting, watering, weeding, harvesting, and expanding the garden area. 

We also spend a lot of time on small building projects and general property maintenance. This can include working with wood, helping build small structures, stacking firewood, and maintaining trails around the property.

You’ll stay in a cozy guest cabin with one bedroom, a living room and a small but fully functional kitchen.
We live off-grid, so there’s no running water (outhouse + blue jugs filled from our well) and limited electricity generated by solar — more than enough in summer.
Wi-Fi is available in the main house.

We like cooking, sharing meals, and love discovering new foods. We don't eat a lot of meat, and the meat we eat is game meat.

We love hiking, canoeing, climbing, and being outdoors. There are trails around the property and near town. A small lake ne

name: Maria - Nature based educational Project.
description:
Hi! I’m Majo, mom of two curious explorers, ages 9 and 10. Together, we’re growing a dream on my family’s farm.

Roots & Boots 🌻 is our nature-based learning garden—a space where children can slow down, dig in, and build a real, heart-felt connection with the Earth 🐞. Here, learning sprouts through art, stories, free play, forest adventures, and hands-on time in the vegetable garden. 🐛🦋🍓

Life on the farm is always changing with the seasons, and so are the tasks we dive into. In our vegetable garden, we compost, nurture the soil, and grow delicious organic food. Out in the forest, we spend time clearing paths, planting new trees, and keeping the land healthy and alive.

We also care for our animal friends—rabbits, ducks, and even bees—so helping with their daily routines can become part of the experience too.

Because we’re an educational project, most of our work is done hand-in-hand with children of all ages. Throughout the year, we host a variety of nature-based programs that can include yoga, art, cooking, simple carpentry, and many other creative, earthy adventures.

We love welcoming people from different backgrounds, cultures, and walks of life. We truly value diversity and believe that sharing experiences, stories, and perspectives makes this journey richer for everyone. For us, hosting is not just about work on the farm — it’s about meaningful exchange, connection, and growing together.

As a WWOOFer, you’ll have a comfortable place to rest and recharge after your time outdoors.

We offer two types of accommodation:

🌿 Farm Stay (when available) – A cozy, more private space located directly on the farm, surrounded by nature. It’s peaceful, simple, and perfect for those who want to fully immerse themselves in the natural environment. Because it’s our most requested option, it’s not always available.

🏡 Family Home Stay – A private room with your own bathroom in our family home, just a 5-minute walk from the farm. You’ll have your own personal space while sha

name: Giuseppe - Destinazione Agrituristica
description:
L'azienda agricola si trova a Cutrofiano, nel cuore del Salento, produce olio extra vergine d'oliva e varietà antiche di grano, orzo e farro. Il patrimonio aziendale: 8 ettari di Leccino, circa  1600 piante e 3 ettari di seminativo. Il lavoro nell'olivicoltura comincia con la raccolta da ottobre a dicembre, continua con la potatura da gennaio a giugno e si conclude con la spollonatura tra agosto e settembre. Agrumeto e giardino di erbe aromatiche richiedono cure dalla primavera all'autunno. Alloggio in camera tripla, 3 SETTIMANE periodo minimo di permanenza .
lingue parlate: spagnolo e inglese.   

****************************** 

The farm is located in Cutrofiano, in the heart of Salento, producing extra virgin olive oil and ancient varieties of wheat, barley and spelt. The farm has 10 hectares of Leccino, about 1600 plants and 3 hectares of cereals/legumes. The work in olive growing begins with harvesting from October to December, continues with pruning from January to June and ends with suckering between August and September. Citrus grove and herb garden require care from spring to autumn. Accommodation in triple room, 3 weeks minimum staying.
 languages spoken: Spanish and English. 

Progetti attuali: Teaching activities, Preserving produce,Herbs, Olives,Building/maintenance,Vegetables

Accesso a Internet disponibile

name: Donald - +20 year-old 16 acre community in a desert oasis
description:
We are a +20 year-old 16 acre community--not a farm--located in a narrow valley surrounded by 3 mountain ranges. 
We use only organic methods of food production .. A canopy of thousands of fruit, nut and native trees feed and shelter us; several gardens provide us with more food. 
We compost and have several 2 chicken coups that provide us with eggs.
All this bio-mass also creates a sanctuary for a large bird population as well as for many native animals.
Caring for this 16-acre desert oasis is a lot of work. We maintain thousands of feet of irrigation lines, prune trees, plant gardens, and build structures along with new composting toilets, solar ovens, solar dehydrators, solar water distillers, etc. We generally think our work is fun, but there are other more traditionally fun things to do here also, such as enjoying the wonderful sauna and fire pit, swimming in the pool, hiking in the desert and mountains, and finding secluded places to meditate and relax..  We require a minimum one-month stay.  We are filling positions to help with building projects, cleaning, painting, basic woodworking, lots of organizing. The property needs a lot of TLC.  Workers are required to work 40 hours a week.  The work schedule can be flexible once orientation is over.Our accommodations are converted buses, RVs and lots of camping space.  Pets are okay only if they are approved in advance and are well behaved--no aggression, constant barking or chasing of our chickens and wildlife allowed.We are 20 miles away from Globe, AZ. Having your own transportation will give you the most options but not a requirement. 
We are not equipped to treat or handle serious mental conditions or addiction issues, though we have compassion for those struggling.  Alcohol/ Drug Abuse will not be tolerated.  We are looking for honest hard workers, that are will to help bring this place back to functioning order.  

Certifications: Everything here has been planted and maintained using only organic methods.

I

name: Peter - Farm
description:
we are peter(71, orig. german), pratihp(55,orig. thai),  reduced farming-activities because of healthissues. need help mainly in summer for haymaking, gardening, building/maintenance of houses. visitors wellcome anytime, also short period, but prefer wwofers to stay a while and become part of family. interested in crafts and lotec-solutions

Certifications: DEBIO

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Right in the middle of norway, 400km north of trondheim, between Mosjøen and Sandnessjøen (40km each way). follow E6 north to mosjøen, turn left towards sandnessjøen. after 40km turn right at the sign otting, follow dirtroad for 1,5km and you are there. r

name: Carmen - Oceanside organic gardening & landscaping
description:
I have a home in Halibut Cove, Alaska. It sits on Kachemak Bay. The property is large and requires much work to keep the natural vegetation under control. I plant vegetables & herbs to sustain myself here. 

WWOOFer Activities:
a. How to plant & maintain the vegetable & garden beds in an organic manner
b.  Clear brush & dispose in an ecologically manner
c. Small construction projects as the Wwoofer may enjoy. 
d. There may be some fish processing (very small scale) if Wwoofer would enjoy learning this skill. 
e.  Hauling firewood

There are 2 possibilities for accommodations. 1. In my living space. There,  I have a very comfortable loft space with 2 twin beds.  I would prepare dinner everyday. You are on your own to prepare breakfast & lunch.  You  will have access to all groceries. My kitchen is always well stocked. 

Second option - stay in one of 2 beautifully appointed guest cabins if available. Both have kitchen facilities & incredible ocean views. Often, they are rented out. But, if not, you would stay in one of the two. 

Wwoofer is welcome to work their 1/2 day (4 hours) whenever they see fit. Sometimes one needs to work around the weather. I also have kayaks, a peddle board & a paddle board to which you would be welcome to use. There is great hiking which I would ensure you had an opportunity to experience. 

I love all outdoor activities & living on the water. My day is full taking care of my place, exercising on the water, hiking & visiting with friends. I am reputed to be a good cook & enjoy preparing meals for friends and guests. My 11 year old standard poodle,Vita, is my constant companion even while doing water sports.

name: Katarina - Family home and garden with small beekeeping company
description:
Beekeeping, gardening and food foresting

Welcome to Skåne in the southern part of Sweden. We are a small family of three that consists of my wife Maria, our son Eskil and me, Katarina. Maria is Danish and I am Swedish. We live on an old farm called Norregård, built in the beginning of the ninteenth century. Parts of the farm has been demolished through the years and the land has been sold of long time ago. The house needs a lot of work still. We choose one our two projects a year and try to finish them before starting new ones. We live in the forrest with possibilities to go hiking, foraging or just strolling
It is mostly me who work at home with the bees and garden. And also take care of Eskil during the days when he is not attending preschool. Maria works as a clothing technician at Malmö stadsteater and I have a background as a classical musician. But that is a long time ago.
In the summertime I work in my own small company with bekeeping. And we sell honey and other products from our bees at local markets. I studied to be a beekeeper and company manager almost ten years ago. And have had the company since. I take care of the bees a bit differently then we did in school. Everything with nature is so complex and if your work involves nature in some way I feel you really need to think long thoughts. Many steps and observe. And my new approach is to be more disciplined so that I know in detail what I do so I can change it if I need to.

We grow our own vegetables without any pesticides, herbicides or other craziness! This year I am preparing and learning how to be a professional market gardener with hopes of also selling vegetables in small scale in the future. I am influenced especially by Elliott Coleman and J M Fortier at the moment. But there is so many! For me is quality and precision key. I like systems that are efficient and most of all saves time so that we have time to live a good life also. 

This years summer projects:
- Beekeeping as usual, collecting h
_wwoof_travel: Busstation Hörby or Sjöbo. Trainstation Lund. Airport Sturup(Malmö)/Kastrup(Copenhagen).

name: Laure - Non-profit located on the Cheyenne River Indian Reservation focused on food sovereignty
description:
We are a non-profit founded in 1988 and located on the Cheyenne River Indian Reservation. CRYP has continued to grow, educate the community and youth on food sovereignty in their 2.5 acre non-gmo garden. 

The organization has made this initiative a priority during the global pandemic to teach youth and families about food security and sustainability. CRYP continues to host Youth Internships and provide meals for the youth using the produce from the garden. 

Organic methods: no pesticides or chemicals 

Certifications: NONE 

Current projects: CRYP is currently in the middle of garden season, we have so much to harvest and then turn that into canned goods, freeze produce, cook/bake with produce and feed the youth and community in different capacities. 

Also do some upkeep of the garden, fencing area, storage areas for produce. All other tasks will depends on weather, plant growth and upcoming events involving the garden. 

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Pierre (1.5 hrs) or Rapid City (2.5 hrs)

name: Rhoda - Pistachio farm near Kingman, AZ surround by beautiful scenery and accessible mountain hiking
description:
We are located north of Kingman, Arizona. Our property is surrounded by beautiful easily accessible hills or 'mountains' for hiking. We have a mountain bike available for energetic souls that like to ride bikes on dirt roads also. Some Wwoofers have enjoyed rock climbing.   We get regular visits from wild horses, neighborhood cattle, and other desert critters. We are just over an hour away from Lake Mead, Lake Mohave, and the Colorado river that feeds both these lakes.   We are 2 hours from Las Vegas; our nearest big airport location. There is a bus that travels between Las Vegas and Kingman, and we can pick you up at the Kingman bus stop, but we prefer you have your own transportation (we are 10 miles from town you would need to plan on staying at our place for your days off since we don't go to town daily) 

WWOOFers stay in our tiny guesthouse, or pop up tent trailer,  both are located next to the orchard, You have access to a private fenced-in garden with a fire pit area. The guesthouse has a loft with a queen bed, and a single bed on first floor. Tent trailer has 3 queen size beds. You may be asked to share accommodations with other Wwoofers.  If that is a concern please let us know.   There is a small fridge/freezer, and microwave. Tiny house has heat but no AC, tent trailer has both.  Our main house is available to Wwoofers for bathroom and shower use. Laundry is available too.  You are welcome to bring your own RV, electric is available. 

We prepare dinner at the main house for everyone at the end of the day. You will also be provided with basics (bread, eggs, milk, etc) and other requests to prepare your own breakfast and lunch. We are omnivores and foodies, but happy to try to accommodate any requests.  

We have over 100 pistachio trees that are harvested and processed in September. This is our main harvest. We have 20 other fruit and nut trees, a greenhouse, chickens, and various improvement projects (painting, building structures, maintenance, etc) . W

name: Ana - Somos una pareja joven que hace poco tiempo comenzamos nuestro proyecto de vida rural y sustentable
description:
Hola! Somos Ana y Kevin, tenemos 30 y 34 años. Vivimos en la provincia de San Juan en el departamento Pocito a 25 km de la capital.
Junto a nosotros vive nuestra gata (Pati), 5 perros, 2 ovejas y varias gallinas.
Tenemos un proyecto de producción de verduras agroecologicas y estamos explorando la prácticas de la  biodinámica. También hace un año comenzamos la crianza de gallinas y producción de huevos caseros.
Nuestro hogar está dentro de un gran terreno donde hay un cultivo de alfalfa. Tenemos algunos proyectos a futuro como hacer nuevos cultivos en algunos sectores del terreno, la construcción de un reservorio de riego y la parquización de los espacios de la casa y pileta.

Los días comienzan con la planificacíón de las tareas del día mientras desayunamos, luego continuamos con la rutina diaria de alimentar perros y gallinas y controlar los bebederos y riego de almacigos.
Las tareas posibles son: siembra, trasplante y cosecha de las verduras de la huerta; desmalezado; construcción de bancales, preparación de tierra y abono; instalación y reparación de riegos.
También suelen presentarse tareas excepcionales como arreglo de gallineros, etc.

Nuestra casa es la única vivienda en el lugar, por lo tanto los Wwoofers compartirán nuestro hogar con nosotros, ofrecemos una habitación doble privada, y contamos con dos baños. Tenemos lavarropas si quieren lavar sus pertenencias.
Cada mañana compartiremos el desayuno y luego ofreceremos un almuerzo  hecho por nosotros. Sin embargo, es posible compartir la cocina e ideas de comida si les interesa.

Vivimos frente a la sierra chica de Zonda, que nos ofrece expectaculares atardeceres y posibilidades de trekking a 10 kilomentros de nuestra casa.
Además, a 5km se encuentra "Villa Aberastain", capital de Pocito. Allí se puede comprar todo tipo de viveres de cocina, insumos y articulos que se necesiten. 
Es importante destacar que hay un hospital público de atención gratuita.
Para los amantes de la panificación: Tenemos una vecina a
_wwoof_travel: Aeropuerto de San Juan , terminal de ómnibus de San Juan y Aeropuerto de Mendoza.

name: Ana Teresa - Organic farming and nature restoration project in the depths of Serra da Estrela
description:
We are Ana and André, a young couple (both 33) with a little baby named Eva. Ana studied biology and forest management, while André is an archaeologist. We have a great love for this mountain and dream to live self-sufficiently in tune with Nature and to restore the native woodland and natural pastures using ecology, permaculture and old land management techniques to create a rich, biodiverse and fire/drought resistant landscape! For this we started an ecology and heritage preservation project called "Vale Velho". It is about 100ha of abandoned land in a very remote part of the Serra da Estrela, at 1100m of altitude, where you can expect to be surrounded by mountains and enjoy stillness and the sounds of Nature. We keep heritage breeds and plant varieties, have 40 sheep, 20 beehives, 3 dogs and 2 cats, a vegetable garden, recenty planted orchard and chestnut grove, rye fields and about 80ha of land to reforest, some of which has already been planted or seeded. There is an archaeological site with ancient graves inbedded in stone.

In 2022 the land burned, and lots of our work has been destroyed. We also have a new little baby to take care of and André is finishing his master´s degree, so we need an extra need help with some farm activities, for example: collecting firewood, clearing shrubs, cleaning traditional waterways, tending the vegetable garden, looking after the animals, and seeding, planting and protecting native trees. We also have some infrastructure building and maintenance to do. We know how to use some native plants for medicine and food, and we sometimes make Serra da Estrela cheese, which we can teach. We will also share knowledge about local species and ecossystems, the shepherd culture and portuguese history and archaeology!

We have 2 tents where you can sleep and a lovely space with shade and drinking water available. Also if you come with your campervan we have a good parking spot.
In our house we have wi-fi with unlimited internet connection tha

name: Peggy - Family home and garden - rural
description:
I live on 2 1/2 acres of mostly-forested land near the Tsolum River, in the beautiful Comox Valley, near Courtenay.   Crops usually grown include a number of vegetables (tomatoes, peppers, potatoes,  kale, salad greens, etc.) as well as raspberries, apple and blueberries. Vegetables are grown primarily in raised beds.

Passionate about caring for the soil?   You'll have an opportunity to learn to a few new skills, including how to incorporate some of India's traditional Vedic practices - and to learn about some interesting East/West connections made over the past 20 years.  Other work ahead this year includes repairing fencing, maintaining trails and some basic building maintenance.  

I love sharing ideas and knowledge with people from other countries who also care deeply for the land.

The land is located about 10 minutes from the ocean and 40 minutes from Mt. Washington and Strathcona Provincial Park.  There are lots of hiking and biking trails in the area, as well as a thriving farmers market and a number of groups and organizations involved in social justice, local food security and sustainable living.

For the past 20 years, during Canada's winter season, I have worked with young farmers, tea growers and students of organic farming in northeast India.  We study concepts drawn on India's traditional Vedic teachings and organic farming practices - and support the efforts of people interested in transitioning back to traditional farming practices. 

When the timing and connections work, I periodically check in with my farming and tea growing friends in Assam - and they'd LOVE to hear from people WWOOFing here on Vancouver Island, too!

Organic methods: Composting, production and application of aerated compost "teas",  periodic application of formulas based on some of India's traditional Vedic practices 

Certifications: This is a small family-sized garden.  We follow natural practices, but do not have certification.

Current projects: fence and shed repairs, trai
_wwoof_travel: The Island Link van drops people off at the Merville Store, 5 km. from the property.   Comox airport YQQ is about 18 km - pickup might be possible.

name: George - Help us in our permaculture garden and organic shop in the Chalkidiki area, Greece
description:
Hello, 

Nice to meet you! My name is Giorgos and I live in Psakoudia in the Chalkidiki area. My daughter Nicole is now almost 4 years old. I have a bio-organic shop with seasonal fruits and vegetables, olives and olive oil from our own trees, honey, wine, cheese, pasta, and lots of other delicious local organic food. I also have a permaculture garden with vegetables, olive trees, some chickens and grapes. We cultivate with natural methods. 

I'm looking for people who love the nature and the natural way of living, who enjoy to help me in the garden and with the growth of our production. There are lots of different plants, flowers, and trees that need watering, weeding, and some love. There is also space and time to organize your own projects, I'm always open to new ideas and input from volunteers.

I can also use some extra hands in the shop: helping customers, restocking, cleaning, taking care of the plants, creative DIY projects (like painting signs), and creating a good atmosphere in general. 

The garden is a really good place to relax, enjoy nature, eat self-grown organic vegetables, and explore the surroundings such as wild beaches and beautiful mountains. The garden is 4km away from the shop and the city of Psakoudia, but both locations are only a 5-minute walk to the beach. I have some bikes that you can use for transportation. 

If you are interested in joining our family for a few weeks, send me a message and we will work something out!

Accommodation:
In the garden, there is a small house (still under construction) with a tent and a mattress inside. There is a basic kitchen, an outside shower, and a composting toilet. In the middle of the garden, you can make a small fire to enjoy the evenings under the stars. In the garden itself is no wifi but you can use the wifi in the shop. The lights are powered by small solar panels.
There are lots of vegetables and fruits in the shop which I love to share with the volunteers. Every day you can fill your basket to
_wwoof_travel: Local bus stop in Psakoudia (10 min walk), or Thessaloniki airport / Chalkidiki bus station

name: Jan - Mehrgenerationengemeinschaft und soziale Einrichtung vor den Toren Leipzigs
description:
Wir sind eine 27-köpfige Gemeinschaft, welche auf einem alten Vierseiten-Bauernhof ganz nah an Leipzig wohnt. Wir sind 4 bis 86 Jahre alt und setzen uns zusammen aus 11 Kindern und 16 Erwachsenen. Gemeinsam bewirtschaften wir einen ca. 1.200 m² großen Garten, einen ca. 600 m² großen Gemüsegarten inkl. Gewächshaus und ein, an den Hof grenzendes Waldstück, mit zwei Teichen.
Neben der gemeinschaftlichen Art des Zusammenlebens, ist der GENERATIONENHOF eine pädagogische gemeinnützige GmbH, mit der Idee Kindern und Jugendlichen, welche aus diversen Gründen nicht mehr in ihrem familiären Umfeld leben können, ein familienähnliches Leben in Wohngemeinschaften zu ermöglichen. Auf dem Hof haben wir eine Wohngemeinschaft mit 7 Kindern und Jugendlichen.
Die Vielfalt der hergestellten Produkte hängt prinzipiell von den Kapazitäten der Hofbewohner und -bewohnerinnen ab. Hier haben wir uns jedoch immer wieder erprobt in Kunsthandwerklichem, dem Brotbacken, Eiern von unseren Hühnern und Laufenten, Kräuter-Ölen, Säften oder Likören, Tee, der Gemüseernte und -Verarbeitung oder dem Keltern von Wein.

Wir experimentieren mit verschiedenen Anbaumethoden, stellen eigenen Kompost verschiedenster Art her, üben uns in der Permakultur. Verstehen uns aber mehr als Hobbygärtner und weniger als Profis ;-) Im Zentrum des Hofes steht die intergenerationale Gemeinschaft, weshalb wir zum Zweck der Inklusion aller Altersgruppen zum Beispiel auch viel mit Hochbeeten arbeiten. Unsere Gartenprojekte sind ein Teil der Tätigkeiten auf dem Hof, bei der Verwendung jeglicher Methoden und Theorien sind wir offen für Ideen und Anregungen.

Wir haben immer mehrere "Töpfe am Kochen". Neben der regulären Gartenarbeit, der Versorgung unserer Hühner bzw. Laufenten und der Pflege unserer Teiche, gibt es immer verschiedene weitere Projekte. Den kreativen Ideen sind (fast) keine Grenzen gesetzt und wir freuen uns immer über Menschen, die in den verschiedenen Bereichen "mittun" und "mitdenken" möchten.
Ende 2026 überne
_wwoof_travel: Bushaltestelle Lindennaundorf/Schmiede, Bahnhof Leipzig-Miltitz, Flughafen Leipzig/Halle

name: Srey Neang - Home Garden on the Hillside
description:
Welcome to Phnom Tob Cheang Two Sister Family Garden
Location: Sre Ambel, Koh Kong Province, Cambodia

Hello WWOOFers!
We are Sister Neang and Herby Kingston , two passionate souls rooted in the Cambodian countryside. Together we run Ark Organic Farm and the Phnom Tob Cheang Two Sister Family Garden, a beautiful collaboration that brings together organic farming, syntropic food forests, forest healing, and community-based living.

Our home is nestled between the jungle and the sea, in a peaceful rural village where nature still leads the way. We’re located in Sre Ambel, Koh Kong—a tranquil area with tropical beauty, rich culture, and warm-hearted locals.

At Ark Organic Farm, we focus on regenerative agriculture, syntropic gardening, and education for sustainable living. We've planted over 13 kinds of fruit trees, run a small organic bakery, raise tilapia and grow duckweed for natural feed. We also support local children and families by promoting home gardening, clean food access, and self-reliance.

At the Phnom Tob Cheang Family Garden, led by Sister Neang, we’ve created a Camping Garden where visitors and volunteers can connect with Mother Nature through forest healing, barefoot grounding, and simple, peaceful living. It’s a healing space open to all—locals, expats, and travelers alike.

We are a small team, but you’ll always feel like part of the family. Currently living on-site:

Sister Neang, a kind-hearted woman who left her city life behind to return to her motherland and build this healing project.

A few part-time helpers from the village

Occasional visiting friends, artists, or healers

And of course, our beloved pets—9 friendly dogs, a playful cat, and a few chickens who roam freely.

We’re looking for WWOOFers who want to get their hands dirty, connect with nature, and learn or share skills in gardening, natural building, baking, or simply being present and part of a holistic lifestyle.

We provide simple accommodation, delicious homegrown meals (vegan

name: Laura - Family home and garden - rural, herb farm, family farm
description:
Some years ago we settled in a beautiful village in the Rose Valley at the foot of the Sredna Gora range in Central Bulgaria (50 km from Plovdiv), where we are running our small organic farm and produce herbal teas, tinctures, and other herbal products. When we bought the deserted house, it was completely overgrown and needed a total renovation. Now we have completed the majority of repair work and turned our attention to our lands, which is divided over 3 nearby fields and our garden where we grow different herbs such as lavender, chamomile, echinacea, California poppy, mint species, and many others. 

We are Laura, an ethnobotanist, Andreas, a journalist and translator, and our little daughter Maxine (January 2020). Originally from the Netherlands and Germany but we have made Bulgaria our new home. We care about environmental and social justice and try to implement a more sustainable lifestyle, although we strive to move away from dogma and towards a better understanding of life in all its diversity and complexity. While we have our hands pretty full with work in and around the house and lands, in our free time we like to explore the lesser-known parts of Bulgaria and the surrounding countries. 

We grow vegetables and fruits in our garden and love cooking, baking bread, making preserves, herbal syrups, herbal teas, and spice mixtures. Laura has many years of experience with herbs and has a lot of knowledge to share about them. The herbs we use are grown on our farm and collected from our ‘wild’ surroundings, and there are plenty of wild edibles growing here too.

We welcome kind and fun spirits of all backgrounds and interests, but our place probably best suits proactive people with a keen interest in plants and/or herbs, or who want to start their own smallholding, or consider living in a remote village. It is also important for us that you like to positively interact with our daughter. Couples, small families, or two friends are very welcome. Solo travellers ar

name: Emmanuel - Help with our non-profit community project in Mfangano Island, Kenya.
activities: Unterkunft: trailer, bedroom, campsite, yurt-tipi, shack
description:
Osiepe! Hi!
My name is Emmanuel and I was born and raised in Wakinga in Mfangano Island.  I grew up in the community of peasant fishermen, small scale farmers and entrepreneurs, which inspired me to become a social and influential entrepreneur myself to support my home village.  In 2007 I started with community work as welfare officer and at the same time an assistant chair of NGOs of Mfangano Central and finally founded an organisation with my community in February 2007. The projects run by the community are multiple-community based projects, some are tackled within Mfangano Island while the rest are run in Gwassi mainland,Kenya. 

For the past 23 years I have worked both tooth and nail to support the local community.  I have a wonderful and most hospitable family, my home is very modest,however, can host upto 12 volunteers at a time or even more.

Our aim is to improve the situation of the community in different aspects.  We want to improve the lives especially from the most marginalised groups by enabling them to live a sustainable and self-reliant life. We want to train the people in Organic Farming, Bee keeping, Poultry Farming,  Bird farming, Fish farming and other skills, to fight against poverty and help to meet their daily wants. In this context, we have a small microfinance project, where we currently support vulnerable orphans, 7 in number to pursue different courses in university . We also reach out to the less privileged families in times of disasters like flooding, starving families, outbreak of diseases to give out our little support. 

Our Community-based organisation needs help. As some of our projects are still at their very beginning and our long term goal is to be a sustainable farm that produces varied crops, among others,we urgently need people who have experience or knowledge of :

—Organic Farming, agriculture and permaculture who can pass their knowledge and skills on to the locals and train them,we grow crops such as bananas,kales, spider p

name: Nicola - Developing an eco-sustainable farm in Caqueza, need of help for bilding, seeding, farming and artist
description:
I was born and raise in a small town in Italy called Fontaniva, in a area between Padova, Vicenza, Treviso, Bassano del Grappa. I got my degree in Biological Sciences at University of Padova in 2002. I then went in USA for a PhD in Physiology, which I finished in 2007.
I lived in New Jersey until 2007 and than moved to Manhattan until February 2010.
I travelled all around South America for almost an year. I was so fascinated by its different cultures. Next move was to move to Colombia and do volunteering work in Bogota ... did it for 9 months in a cultural center/library of Lisboa de Suba. Then volunteered for Sito, a wonderful project of ecological puppets with the goal of educate people (Expecially children) on ecology, sostenibility, love, true values ...
Now living in Caqueza (Cundinamarca), where I have got an organic farm that I am developing with my neighbour friends.
I'm a very easygoing person, simple and very honest. I am almost 100% vegan, eating meat only when the circumstances basically force me to do it. It is working pretty well, and I feel so much better, physically and mentally. I live with the dream of a better world, where sharing, happiness, morality and common interests are the basic roots.
I move around only by bicycle or public transportation. My biggest trip where Bogota - Cancun in 2012, Bogota - Quito - Bogota in 2013 and Venice - Bogota in 7 beautiful months in 2014. This is one of the few ways I have to oppose the wars around the world. Every time you use your car you contribute to make suffer somebody somewhere else in the world.
I don't have a cellphone ... because I am a free man!!!! (I neither have TV, radio, washing machine, refrigerator... I just have a blender to process some food).
I live day by day, living the present, the only time that matters. Sick of western society which is destroying our planet earth. I see the modern work as the ultimate form of slavery, since it is based on the idea of making money, and not to make or sup

name: Eric - Homestead transitioning to a small organic farm in NM
description:
This is an 18 year homestead transitioning to a small scale organic farm on the Gila River in Southwest New Mexico. We sell at 1-2 farmers’ market a week and at the local food co-op. We currently have 13 goats (3 goats we milk), honey bee hives, and 4 hoop houses we grow in the spring, summer, and fall. Beautiful high desert environment surrounded by nature. 35-40 miles to the nearest town which is Silver City. The land is at 5,000 feet  which makes it not so hot in the summer and pretty wintery in the winter.  Even though we are at a similar latitude as Phoenix AZ. it is nothing like that kind of heat so don't be afraid to come in the summer. Accommodations (individual rooms; 3 singles up to 3 couples), hot shower, laundry, and meals are provided. You’ll have the opportunity to  vegetable and medicinal herb growing and processing, goat care and dairy processing, and more. Open to short and long term help with farm chores. A one week trial period for all workers and assessing for longer stay after that.If it turns out you really dislike working the trial period will be shortened to three days. All buildings are hand built and homesteading skills and land setup can also be learned here. At some point I’d like to start a tree nursery for community tree planting projects. During your off days you can explore Gila National Forest, go for walks, go for a dip in the irrigation ditch, or venture out into the neighboring towns such as downtown Silver City (cafes, thrift shop, co-op, music stores, brewery, galleries). The farm is in a remote area so having your own vehicle is ideal for exploration purposes. I go to town a couple of times a week so rides can be provided. You will have the opportunity to meet a wholesome community of eclectic folks during your stay. I ask that guests please don't use strong chemical based perfumes and colognes while they are here as it contaminates the food we harvest and many people have adverse reactions to them.

Thanks! - Eric

Organic met

name: Joris - Projet de collection de fruitiers en permaculture
description:
Je m'appelle Joris et je vous  propose un mode de vie nature!  Passionné de fruit, j'ai parcouru le monde pour réaliser une collection de fruitier unique. Aujourd'hui, bananes, papayes ananas et des légumes sont déjà cultivés et au menu tout les jours. Je vous accueille dans un carbet authentique en bois local, simple mais très agréable. Il y a l'eau courante (récupération de l'eau de pluie et filtration au charbon) l'électricité par des panneaux solaires et internet. En effet le site est à 17 km de la ville et offre un cadre exceptionnel pour vivre avec la nature sauvage d'Amazonie (perroquets, singes, agoutis, jaguars...)
Je cueille beaucoup de fruits de forêt dont je me nourris beaucoup, il faut monter sur des palmiers qui font plus de 10 mètres de haut!

Concernant le travail, environ 4 heures par jour (très variable en fonction des activités) sont consacrées à développer la forêt fruitière de la graine à la plantation,  du jardin de légumes pour la vente et l'autoconsommation, également différents travaux sur la gestion et la compréhension du lieu, ainsi que de la construction et bricolage divers.  Je  propose aussi une initiation à l'apiculture. Je peux vous apprendre des choses dans de nombreux domaines bien que je ne sois pas spécialiste. 
Ce projet promeut une alimentation locale, tournée principalement vers les fruits, le partage de connaissances et mise en pratique pour une vie plus simple et saine.
Le temps libre est fait de balades en forêt, cueillette de fruits sauvages, baignade, rencontre avec les amis locaux...
Aux alentours, il est possible d'effectuer une sortie à la rencontre des ibis rouges, découverte de la mangrove et diverses criques, sentiers botaniques...
Pour le logement, un carbet est à la disposition des woofers ce qui permet de garder à chacun ses moments personnels, en particulier les soirées et les weekends. Prévoir hamac moustiquaire, duvet léger ou couverture (nuit fraîche), chaussettes hautes (je fournis les bottes),et gants de jar

name: Silvana Beatriz - Hortifruti, agroindústria, cervejaria, turismo rural,  zona rural de Porto Alegre
description:
1. Descreva seu projeto, seu lugar e seus habitantes (incluindo os animais):

Somos uma pequena propriedade familiar, adquirida em 2000, com produção agroecológica e realização de venda direta ao consumidor nas feiras orgânicas. Produzimos e processamos hortifruti, temos uma cervejaria artesanal e também trabalhamos com turismo rural. Somos 4 pessoas (todos oriundos do meio urbano), 7 cachorros, 3 gatos, 2 patos, 12 galinhas, 1 galo, uma infinidade de pássaros  e recebemos visitas recorrentes de bugios.

• Silvana Bohrer, agricultora, engenheira agrônoma, mais de 20 anos de experiência com produção orgânica.

• Zanir Bohrer (marido da Silvana), psiquiatra, mestre cervejeiro.

• Luciana (Guinha, irmã da Silvana), agricultora,  padeira, moradora do Sítio desde 2011.

• Osmar (irmão da Silvana), mecânico,  "faz tudo" do Sítio 

Além dos moradores, também temos o Daniel que é nosso ajudante para a produção. 

Já fomos membros da Woof Brazil e recebemos de 2010 a 2020 em torno de 100 voluntários. 

2. Liste as actividades em quais os WWOOFers irão participar e o que vai ensinar-lhes:

Os voluntários irão participar das rotinas do sítio: preparo de canteiros,  plantio, poda, colheita, atividades relacionadas à agroindústria (na produção de geleias, pães e conservas) e à cervejaria (fabricação,  envase, etc), recepção e atendimento aos turistas, participação da comercialização nas feiras.

3. Como alimenta e aloja os WWOOFers? Por favor, descreva as suas possibilidade de alojamento e como planeia as refeições:

O alojamento fica na parte superior da agroindústria e ao lado da moradia. Possui 4 quartos, 1 banheiro e um espaço comum. A estrutura é de madeira, banho quente e possui internet. As refeições são coletivas e realizadas na moradia dos proprietários.  O acesso é livre.

4. Como poderia ser um dia típico de um WWOOFer na sua quinta (horários das actividades, expectativas...)?

A rotina varia de acordo com as atividades e demandas. Um dia típico: café da manhã, ativid

name: Mathius - The farm works to promote organic farming by giving education, demonstration to the host community
description:
Organic Initiative Uganda was founded in 2013 as a community-based nonprofit committed to restoring degraded landscapes and improving rural livelihoods. Operating in and around the Waluleeta area, they collaborate directly with communities and schools to establish community tree nurseries, edible gardens, and classroom “hamlet organic gardens.” These gardens teach eco‑friendly practices—such as composting, green manure, and agroecological systems—to enhance food production, household income, and environmental awareness without relying on synthetic inputs  .

In addition to agroforestry and organic cultivation, the organization supports smallholder farmers—especially youth and women—with vocational skills training and sustainable livelihood alternatives. These include bamboo crafts (like bicycles), 
Activities at the farm include cultivating, slashing, mulching Banana and weeding, raising tree seedlings and out-planting, feeding birds, checking on the fish pond and feeding fish, 
community sensitization and tree planting.
In the morning 8.am break first 9-12-30 garden work, 12-2.00 lunch, 20-4.00 pm field check out and community sensitization activities

We have simple accommodation and the site and we have tents for those who prefer tents.
The farm produces the food required to make meals for the WWOOFers and the food is prepared at the site in some circumstances where special food is needed shopping is done at the trading centre

Iam fun of local music, cooking hiking, camping, cycling and football,

name: Brahim - Hi everybody:) my name is brahim, I welcomed you to my place, with your hands can make farm Green🌱
description:
Hello WWOOFers, I’m brahim I do love work in garden, but this time I’m here with your hands help your energy, your experience.. together we can make my farm alive again and green place..

You can find me watering trees and plants morning, gardening and give my time in the farming

We eat dinner together and breakfast can also cooking in the house, the lunch meal we will have it in the farm..

I enjoy the stay on garden, I like play drum, music with friends, calme time and talk story, sure i enjoy swimming, walk on desert, mountains around, and explore old places
