name: Karen - Espacio colaborativo donde hacemos compost y cultivamos en el clima extremo de la patagonia
description:
1. Describe tu proyecto, el lugar y sus habitantes (incluídos los animales):

Somos un espacio colaborativo ubicado en la ciudad de Puerto Natales a 120 kilómetros del parque nacional Torres del Paine  y nos dedicamos a la transformación de residuos orgánicos en compost el cual es usado en nuestra huerta y devuelto a las familias compostadoras que nos entregan sus residuos a través de un plan de recolección que pagan mensualmente.
Nuestra huerta orgánica utiliza este compost en la producción de hortalizas que se pueden cultivar bajo las condiciones climáticas extremas de la zona con vientos de más de 100km /hora en verano y -20°C en invierno.
En la casa habitamos entre 3 a 5 personas y tenemos gallinas, gato y perro.

2. Haz una lista de las actividades en las que participarán los WWOOFers y qué quieres enseñarles:

Actividades de compostaje (recolección de residuos orgánicos, acopios, volteo, cosecha, envasado, usos del compost)

Actividades de huerta (germinacion, almacigos, trasplante, riego, poda, nutrición del suelo, preparacion de sustratos y fertilizantes organicos).

Actividades  de bioconstruccion ( reciclaje de materiales, trabajo con barro, y otros biomateriales)

Actividades de fungicultura (preparacion de sustratos, esterilización, inoculacion, fructificacion, cosecha)

Actividades vinculadas al diario vivir que se comparten con las otras personas que habitan la casa (cocinar, mantener ordenado, lavar loza, picar leña, mantener el fuego encendido entre otras)

3. ¿Cómo sería el día de un WWOOFer en tu lugar (horario de participación en actividades, expectativas...)?

Las actividades comienzan con un desayuno donde se conversan las actividades a realizar y que comienzan en horario de  9 am a 2pm o en horario de 3 a 8pm dependiendo de la época del año,  en general no deberían superar las 5 horas por día de lunes a viernes.

Esperamos a personas que vengan con muchas ganar de compartir y vivir experiencias que vinculen la regeneración en el quehacer diario

name: AMY - A small goat-centered farmstead nestled in a 250 acre nature preserve near CLT
description:
The treehouse is available during warmer months  
Please note that we can host more than one Wwoofer and so if the Wwoof site shows booked, we can often house another. Check in about availability 

Our farm is nestled in a 250 acre nature preserve - most of the acreage is wooded and protected in a land conservancy.  There are 6 miles of gorgeous walking trails to get to know and enjoy!  We are located just three miles from the nearest grocery store, Target, Starbucks, etc. and are only 20 miles from downtown Charlotte, Charlotte Douglas International airport and even closer to the Whitewater Center (kayaking, rock climbing, ropes course and mountain biking).  Asheville and the beautiful North Carolina mountains are just a little over an hour drive, so easily explored on days off.
We are more of a hobby farm, not fully self sustaining.  We have lots of animals -bees, chickens, guinea fowl, pigs, goats, and several great pyrenese.   Mostly though, we are a goat farm.  I hand milk up to eight does each season.  We breed and sell Nubian goats, raw goat milk, raw goat milk kefir and fresh goat cheese..  I make a delicious  chèvre and will be glad to share my technique if you would like to learn to make cheese!  We usually have sourdough, kefir and kombucha in action as well.  We raise and harvest our own meat birds two or three times a year.  Every year or so we raise a few piglets to market weight and process them for our own consumption. 
Farm tasks change with the seasons.  
Fall and winter, when the goats are pregnant and I am not milking, is when we are readying the garden for the winter cold (pulling out summer crops, seeding cover crop, mulching, etc), trail making/trail maintenance, building new structures and repairing older ones, making biochar, canning bone broth and applesauce, and enjoying some down time for being creative and getting organized for crazy spring time.  
Crazy fun springtime!  January through April is kidding season which can be exhausting (as
_wwoof_travel: Charlotte Douglas International Airport is about a 20 minute drive.  Amtrak comes through Gastonia (although at 4am) and that station is just 10 minutes away.

name: Martin - Family offgrid farm by beautiful Georgian Bay, Ontario, Canada.
description:
Hello are names are Melody and Martin. We live on a small hobby farm in an straw bale house. Our farm is off grid. Our farming operation consists of growing vegetables, fruit trees, raising laying hens, wool sheep, and horses.   We try and live sustainably growing our own food. We put much effort into preserving our food so we eat much of our own produce year round. Much of our land is forest and wetland and abutts conservation land which has lots of trails for walking. The trails will take you to beautiful Georgian Bay, with about a 30 minute walk, where the swimming is wonderful. We have 3 grown children and 5 grandchildren. We have two Australian shepherd dogs who live in the house with us and three cats who live in our barn.

We garden using permaculture and regenerative soil principles and wish to teach this to as many people as possible. Our laying hens are free range and kept in a permanent coop in winter months and a movable coop in summer months. Our sheep and horses are on pasture in the summer months. We use electric fencing systems for our livestock which need to be rotated regularly. A typical day involves checking on the animals and making sure they have water and food, The garden chores vary depending on the season and may involve soil preparation, planting, weeding, and harvesting. We also have a 40 foot hoop house where we grow our tomatoes so pruning and training tomatoes is an ongoing task in the summer.

We love to cook and obviously have much homegrown produce.  We cook regular meals which we would have the WWOOFERs join us for.  We have a two storey cabin on our property that has a fold out double bed in the upper storey and a kitchen area in the lower storey.  There is also a campfire area outside the cabin. The WOOFers would also be welcome to make their own meals at the cabin if they wanted to and we could provide them with the food they would need. We will have basics like tea and coffee in the cabin also.

We both love to cook and bake. Ma
_wwoof_travel: The nearest airport is Pearson International in Toronto about 4 hours away.  The nearest bus station is in Owen Sound about 4km from our farm.

name: Christine - House and garden
description:
The renovations of the cob cottage is nearly complete, which has taken 10 years. That has meant we’ve learnt a lot about eco-materials, and are happy to share practical experience. 
There is still more to finish downstairs, i.e earth/lime plastering and carpentry. 

Also there is plenty to do in the garden, i.e weeding, brambles, pruning, mowing. Some help with finishing the porch on the cabin.

It is situated in the far south west of Cornwall, 2 miles to the beach, 6 miles to the closest big town with train and bus station, on a good bus route and 15 minute walk to the nearest village with local shops, post office and 2 pubs. 
The house is situated down a bumpy lane and is away from traffic etc. So it's very peaceful and secluded., with great walks near by.
It is not a working farm, and I have no animals. I don't usually a lot of visitors in one go. Use of a toilet and hot shower in out buildings. Some vegetarian or vegan meal will be provided, Other meals separately.

Sorry unable to provide any insurance so you would need to be covered yourself.

Organic methods: Composting

Certifications: I try to be as organic as possible 

Current projects: Lime/earth plastering, painting.
building work on cabin
Beginning a forest garden.
_wwoof_travel: Train or bus to Penzance. I can pick you up from here. Or transport can be arranged from Penzance.

name: Marie Laure - Élevage de chèvre et transformation fromagère bio
description:
J élève une cinquantaine de chèvres alpines sur 35 hectares depuis 2013. Les chèvres sortent au pâturage et leur lait me sert à faire du fromage, yaourts, crème dessert, tomme vendus dans les commerces locaux. J ai aussi des chevaux, des chiens,chats et des grands enfants ( Clémence 24 ans,  Edwin 18 ans et Thaïs sa jumelle qui sont en train de prendre leur envol). 
Nous habitons une grande maison à 6 km de la ferme.  
Je suis chrétienne, catholique pratiquante.

- Activités auxquelles les WWOOFeurs participeront et ce que vous désirez leur apprendre :
Les soins aux animaux, alimentation, paillage sortie au pâturage. La fromagerie soin des fromages préparation des commandes .livraisons 

- A quoi pourrait ressembler la journée d'un WWOOFeur chez vous (plage d'aide aux activités, vos attentes...) ?
On commence  les journées à 7h par nourrir les chèvres.  Ensuite, je trait mes chèvres et après la traite, on emmene les chèvres au champs, puis on va en fromagerie s'occupper des fromages et préparer les commandes .
Le midi préparation du repas . 
L après midi c est le temps libre pour les woofeurs à moins d organiser des chantiers, debroussaillage de clôtures ou autre parfois il reste la préparation des commandes à finir
Et pour finir, le soir on récupère les chèvres au champs et on nourri tout le monde.Les chèvres sont conduites en lactation longue et monotraite toute l année. 

- mes attentes: 
Je cherche des personnes sérieuses, dynamiques avec une réelle envie d aider. 
Idéalement une personne qui souhaiterai se tester sur une petite structure avant une formation agricole, du salariat ou ayant un projet d installation.  
Et pourquoi pas des personnes en réflexion sur les installations en collectif catholique 

- A faire aux alentours (randonnée, baignade, etc.) :
Beaucoup de randonnées à faire parmi les chemins creux et les bois autours de la ferme.
Nous sommes à 1/2 heure de la mer et vraiment tout proche du Blavet.

name: Kirsi-Maaria - Family farm
description:
Egypti farm is mostly a subsistence farm.

It exist between Tampere, Jyvaskyla and Seinajoki. The nearest railwaystation is Haapamaki.

Tucked away from the hectic pace of urban life, surrounded by pristine nature rich in wildlife, our schedule is written by the seasons.

Possible tasks for volunteers include gardening, chopping wood, clearing forests, cutting grass, caring for animals, heating the sauna as well as the house in the winter time, late summer berry picking, harvesting and processing wild edibles and medicinal plants in the nearby forests. Making food, washin up,  doing household cleaning and working with daily tasks are possible. Please tell us your talents so we can work with your abilites.

We can accommodate about 2 people at a time. 

We work during the days, and in the evenings relax in the traditional finnish sauna and swimming in the lake. We can ride a bicycle around the village, hike or walk in the forest, fish at the lake. You can relax in meditation room or in the cottage.

There is one cat and summertimechickens on a farm. You can meet as well many wild animals in the nature.

Certifications: Compost 

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: You can fly to Helsinki or Tampere and travel by public transportation from there. There is a railway station 22 km away and a bus station 30 km away. Pickup by car is possible if arranged in advance.

name: Christine - Finca dans la montagne andalouse
description:
Je m'appelle Christine, il y a 6 ans, j'ai tout quitté en Belgique pour venir vivre ici dans la montagne à 2KM d'un petit village blanc entre Malaga et Ronda (Andalousie). Depuis, mes 2chiens, mes 2chats, poules et mon canard domestique, , mes super voisins, sont ma famille espagnole mais je ne peux oublier cet esprit du village qui fait également partie de ce sentiment familial bienveillant. Les échanges, l'entraide, la proximité, la gentillesse et les sourires font partie du quotidien.
Sans oublier les nombreuses activités et petits concerts dans les bars authentiques de ce chouette village actif et rempli d'initiatives diverses, originales, artistiques.

L'endroit (finca) où j'habite a une énergie et une ambiance assez particulières. On s'y sent bien et c'est un bonheur d'y vivre paisiblement et simplement devant un tableau composé de montagnes et de nature.
Partir faire des randonnées dans la Sierra Nieves ou plonger dans les rios pour se rafraîchir... C'est juste délicieux.

Sincèrement, le lieu que je vous propose est très particulier. il est vraiment source de paix et ressourcement.... j'ai envie de vous laisser la surprise et ne pas tout dévoiler car chacun aura son propre ressenti en vivant ici.

Je souhaite lui garder ce côté simple où conserver les valeurs naturelles et y  déployer également les valeurs humaines importantes.

Ayant des formations dans le développement personnel, j'aime partager mes compétences et l'audace de faire des projets ou sortir de sa zone de confort pour vivre sa vraie vie.

J'espère pouvoir aussi partager vos expériences afin de s'enrichir mutuellement de nos apprentissages.

Le projet que j'aimerais partager maintenant est d'aménager une propriété voisine que j'ai eu la chance d'acquérir.
Un lieu ressemblant à celui où je vis, même énergie, même douceur de vivre, ressourçante.
Celle-ci se compose d'une maison en bois aménagée pour un couple et 1personne seule,  et 2 autres petits bâtiments en durs dans lesquels se dispensent 2 c

name: Raymond - Family home and garden - rural
description:
We are looking for workers for April, May, June, July, August, September .   We are quite happy to accept last minute applicants.

My wife and I live on 10 acres of land and are the sole owners and workers of our business.  We are open to hosting a single person or a couple.  We may be open to hosting a family if we can work out the accomodations. Our busy gardening season begins in May.  We would like to host WWOOFer's for at least 1- 2 months. 
 
While working on our farm there is plenty of time for relaxing, sitting around an evening fire, learning to throw pots and create works of stained glass. We operate a small market garden planting about 3 acres of  fruits and vegetables.  Much of our business is dependant on person to person relationships as the communities we sell our produce in have populations of less than 5000 people.   

In addition to our vegetables we have a pottery and glass studio.  Our buisness is very small but provides an opportunity for an individual to experience all aspects of small scale farming on less than 5 acres.  If interested there would be an opportunity for someone to learn the art of pottery (potters wheel) and working with glass.  

Over the ten years we have been hosting international travellers we have had the good fortune to become friends with quite a number of people who have helped us on our farm.   
We have had quite a few people help on our farm arriving who contacted us through a variety of platforms.  Here are some of the reviews of our place they have written.

 This review was written by Cristian who stayed with us during the 2024 season.
Winnipeg, May 2025

To whom it may concern

I am writing this letter with great enthusiasm to recommend Ray and Deb at Market Garden 434 – Food for Life to any future volunteers seeking an enriching and heartwarming experience.

Staying with Ray and Deb was one of the most rewarding and memorable experiences I’ve had. Deb is truly an exceptional cook—every meal was not only delicious
_wwoof_travel:
To find our farm enter the following coordinates:
Latitude  52.72561068840539
Longitude  -104.14498329162598
Our location will show up as Pottery and Glass Creations by Deb and Market garden 434 - Food for Life.

name: Rowan - Native Tree Nursery in the Scottish Highlands
description:
Our Farm is nestled between mountains in the beautiful and wooded Glen Beag, about 5km from the village of Glenelg, on the northwest coast of Scotland.  We run several different business from the farm, including an organic microbrewery, forestry operations including sawmill and firewood production, tree nursery and holiday accommodation.  We are also working to rewild and increase woodland cover around our glen, making the area richer in wildlife and more resilient to climate change. In total there are 9 people living here: 6 adults and 3 children (as well as a dog, a cat and 14 hens), forming a small community who work together to keep the farm running.

Accommodation is in a picturesque wooden cabin, affectionately known as the 'Yerba Loca'.  It is basic, but comfortable and consists of two bedrooms (each with two single beds), kitchen/living area, hallway and storage area.  Heating is from a small wood burning stove (firewood provided).  There is a toilet at the adjacent brewery and shower available in the office building a short walk away. 

During the summer season, we require WWOOFers to help in our native tree nursery, as well as from time to time growing vegetables in our polytunnels.  Nursery tasks vary from sowing and pricking out seedlings in the spring, to seed collecting in the late summer, and lots of weeding and watering in between!  It's a great opportunity to live in a beautiful part of the West Highlands, while learning about tree growing, horticulture and running a small farm in the modern world.

We primarily need help during the months of April to October, with preference given to those who can commit to at least 3 weeks with us.  A good level of fitness is required and some previous experience of gardening is helpful but not essential.  We typically we ask WWOOFers  to help out for 4 to 5 hours a day, with the rest of the day and weekends free for exploring the area/leisure time.

Glenelg is a small community of around 300 people on a remote pe

name: Holger - Farm in a remote silent and calm area
description:
From October onward, the growing season is over and we normally do not need help. However, if someone is interested in experiencing the Nordic winter or late winter sunlight, we may occasionally make an exception. So far we have not had requests for this. If northern Sweden appeals to you and solitude does not discourage you, feel free to get in touch.

From June 2026 the season begins again with gardening work. In August, blueberries and cloudberries ripen and are harvested for our own use. If our profile interests you, we would be happy to hear from you — especially if you include your email address for easier contact.

You would usually stay in a small two-person log cabin (please bring a sleeping bag). Work is 3–5 hours per day, depending on weather and motivation, leaving plenty of time for swimming in the lake, hiking, cycling, or occasional trips to nearby towns. The area is located in northern Sweden, surrounded by vast forests, lakes and wetlands, rich in wildlife such as moose and reindeer.

The season runs from mid-May to late September. Please bring work clothes, sturdy shoes, warm layers and rain gear. There is always weeding to be done and firewood to prepare for winter — so come and help us!

A former volunteer describes the stay as peaceful and quiet, with gardening work, shared cooking, swimming, rowing on the lake and excursions. It is not a place for action, but perfect for those who appreciate silence, nature and simplicity.

Jetzt im Oktober ist die Vegetationszeit vorüber und wir benötigen keine Hilfe. Aber in den Fällen, wo jemand den nordischen Winter oder sonnigen Spätwinter kennen lernen möchte, machen wir manchmal eine Ausnahme  Doch bis jetzt haben wir noch keine Anfragen. Also wenn Dich der Norden Schwedens reizt und Dich Einsamkeit nicht abschreckt, dann melde Dich.
      Im kommenden Jahr (-26) Geht es ab Juni wieder richtig weiter, - zunächst mit Gartenarbeit und später im August werden die Blau- und die Moltebeeren  reif, die für uns
_wwoof_travel: Am besten mit dem Zug nach Jörn, wo wir Dich dann abholen; oder mit dem Flugzeug nach Skellefteå und dann mit dem Bus nach Jörn (Richtung Arvidsjaur oder Bodö)Mit dem Wagen vom Skellefteå  Strasse -95- bis Jörn .

name: Natasa Antari - Mixed Farm, Family farm
description:
A group of people, a family, an international team with different backgrounds and various skills: teachers, farmers, handcrafters, gardeners, friends, "helping hands", dreamers. All with the common vision of returning to organic forms of farming and handcraft, aiming at achieving self-sufficiency, collectivity, solidarity, as well as a permanent culture able to inspire new generations.

Creation of a small-scale farm (55,000 m2) with edible fruits, plants and trees.

Establishing a training centre aiming at raising environmental awareness on the basis of traditional farming combined with principles of permaculture and natural farming.

We are an organic farm.

Type of Host: we are a farm open in new ideas, maybe later a community

Board: We eat vegetarian food with products from our garden, eggs also. There is a kitchenette where volunteers may prepare non-vegetarian food too.

Τypes of volunteers' tasks

- production of marmalades, tomatoes sauce, ketchup

- seasonal fruit & vegetable's cultivation

Months Needed: March to October

Organic methods: permaculture, agroforestry or traditional organic farming, save the soil, save the seeds, be sustainable,

Certifications: bio certification 

Type of work:  everything we do in the farm or in the laboratory 

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: 3km from Trikala, in the village Loggaki, near Meteora

name: Aragorn - Family farm
description:
Good Moon Farm is a family-run organic farm situated in Turnbull Estate on fertile north side of Tortola Island in the BVI. We have revitalized old terraced farmland and are planting a broad range of crops, some experimental, some staple.

We specialize in soft crops such as spinach, lettuce, arugula, mixed greens, pumpkins, tomatoes, scallions, and beans as well as herbs and bush teas.

Fruits such as mangos, papaya, passion fruit and bananas (especially our rare red bananas) are in regular supply.

Our aim is to grow a steady supply of high quality local organic produce, primarily for the marine industry, from bare boats to the super yachts and the villa renters, however we also provide for many local restaurants, resorts and families.

 

Certifications: The BVI is a elemental small society where word of mouth has more power than certification papers.

name: Gordon - Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures
description:
Thank you for your interest in volunteering at Yellow Dog Lodge, a multi-activity, remote wilderness fly-in lodge situated 20 minutes northeast of Yellowknife, Northwest Territories, Canada. THERE ARE NO ROADS HERE.

Yellow Dog Lodge, Inc. has been recognized as one of Canada's Iconic destinations.  We were awarded the Northwest Territories 2025 and 2017 Operator of the Year.

Our lodge operates entirely off-grid without road access, utilizing PV solar panels, diesel generators, and satellite communications for electricity, telephone, Internet, and WiFi. Our water supply is sourced from Graham Lake and filtered. We are committed to ethical and sustainable living, including on-site cultivation of organic produce, ethical harvesting of wild game, and gathering of wild berries, herbs, and mushrooms.

We are seeking adventurous, self-starting, and physically fit volunteers who thrive in an off-grid wilderness environment and align with our values. WWOOFers are considered volunteers and will receive mentorship from our experienced, certified staff.

The WWOOFer role is diverse, encompassing shared tasks across daily lodge operations, including:

- Production & Logistics: Garden production, fish harvesting, firewood gathering, food preparation, and managing food and fuel orders/delivery.
- Maintenance & Hospitality: Maintaining the overall cleanliness of the Lodge and assisting staff with daily operations and hospitality deliverables.

Volunteers will be trained in the use of power equipment, snowmobiles, and motor boats, with the objective of gaining experience in homesteading and off-grid living in  addition to being a tourism host.

We seek volunteers who demonstrate high energy, perform all assignments with excellence and endurance, maintain personal cleanliness, and exhibit excellent communication skills. WWOOFers are responsible for preparing their own meals, personal clean-up, and laundry during their personal time.

In exchange for your completed assignments, we w

name: Hali - Come for an adventure at this Alaskan family farm trying to be self-sufficient
description:
Updated as of 5/7/26 *Currently looking for a summer-to-fall WWOOFer.  We had someone lined up for all of summer and fall, but their plans changed, so we are back to looking. Please reach out as soon as possible to get the process and planning started. extra emphasis on horse experience, social media posters (not my strong suit), and independent workers. 

*Spring 2026 (this is all mostly started, but still have more to do): Kidding season! Baby goats are coming (expected mid-March), along with chicks, and starting the garden projects, making garden beds, putting fencing around that so the moose, bunnies, goats, and chickens leave the garden alone. Also, it's the best time to go halibut fishing!

*Summer 2026, it is recommended to have the money for your own fishing license, seeing as the state requires that to participate in many of our fun adventures!  We are in full swing and never have a shortage of things to do and light to see. We have a small garden and plenty of milk and eggs here on the homestead! Fishing season is upon us, and we look forward to sharing some fun time on the river and in the inlet to stock our freezers!

*Fall 2026: When things slow down, we start canning and preservation projects so we don't have to rely on the power grid to run our freezers. Power outages happen almost every time there is a windstorm. It is also hunting season, so hiking around is part of our regular life. We have successfully made the wood gasifier and will now be tinkering with poop brick for burning in it and making a proper cover/ shed for it to operate in, close to the barn, where we will need power!

Our small family farm is in an unsuspecting neighborhood. Our goal in this next year is to get a herd share up and running, to provide the community with local dairy goat products (6/10/2024 up and running) . For those staying with us (in summer months), we do as few grocery runs as possible, relying heavily upon our chickens, micro greens, dairy, and personally harvest

name: Adrián - Comunidad
description:
En Vea vivimos un grupo de personas que tiene como horizonte la autosuficiencia a través de la vida en común. El pueblo está ubicado en las Tierras Altas de Soria en un valle bastante aislado de las comunicaciones, bañado por el río Linares. Para llegar hasta el pueblo hay que andar 8 km por el GR-86, por lo que todo lo que obtenemos de fuera lo traemos con burros y mulos.
  Nuestra forma de funcionar consiste en que una gran parte de la vida se hace en los espacios comunes y luego cada persona tiene un espacio individual.Las actividades que llevamos a cabo a lo largo del año son muy variadas, entre ellas está la huerta, leña, construcción, carpintería, trabajo con animales, gestión del entorno, artesanía, panadería, apicultura..

Estamos interesados en personas que vengan con ganas de convivir de esta manera , con ganas de trabajar y compartir su tiempo de buen rollo, con voluntad de aprender y también de enseñar. Por otra parte, Vea es una comunidad, por lo que todas las personas que estamos allí colaboramos con las tareas diarias del lugar (limpiar, cocinar, ir a por agua a la fuente.
   Como últimas consideraciones, el lugar no tiene cobertura móvil, hay que andar 5 minutos por el monte para conectarse. El río del pueblo tiene muchas pozas para bañarse.

Organic methods: No se utiliza ningún tipo de producto químico ni ninguna herramienta con motor, ya sea eléctrico o de gasolina.

Certifications: Asociación las Tres Encinas de Vea

name: Christian - Communauté coopérative alternative, Groupe de vie communautaire, Propriété familiale
description:
À propos de nous / About Us

Nous sommes une ferme agroforestière située en Gaspésie, au Québec. Actuellement en phase de développement, nous explorons à la fois des pratiques anciennes et innovantes inspirées de la permaculture, guidées par une approche de gestion écosystémique.

Notre propriété de 87 hectares comprend des forêts, des ruisseaux et une diversité de systèmes agroforestiers : vergers, haies d’arbres et de fruitiers en champs, haies fauniques et haies comestibles. Nous avons également des jardins, des pâturages accueillant divers animaux (oies, dindes, poules, lapins, lamas, alpagas et chevaux), ainsi que des zones non aménagées dédiées à la conservation.

Notre objectif est de développer des écosystèmes sains, productifs et résilients en favorisant les synergies entre les animaux, les plantes, la vie du sol et les activités humaines.

Nous sommes largement autosuffisants sur le plan alimentaire, tout en produisant des surplus et en participant à un réseau local d’échanges de biens et de services agroécologiques.

We are an agroforestry farm located in Gaspésie, Québec. Currently in a development phase, we explore both traditional and innovative permaculture-inspired practices, guided by an ecosystem-based management approach.

Our 87-hectare land includes forests, streams, and a diversity of agroforestry systems: orchards, tree and fruit hedgerows in fields, wildlife hedges, and edible hedges. We also have gardens, pastures with animals (geese, turkeys, chickens, rabbits, llamas, alpacas, and horses), as well as unmanaged areas dedicated to conservation.

Our goal is to develop healthy, productive, and resilient ecosystems by fostering synergies between animals, plants, soil life, and human activity.

We are largely self-sufficient in food, while producing surpluses and participating in a local agroecological exchange network for goods and services.

Principales productions / Main Productions
Fruits et légumes du jardin / Garden fruits and vegetables
_wwoof_travel:
Saint-Godefroi 160 3e Rang 
prendre route de l'église monté jusqu'au 3e rang tourné a Gauche et regardé a votre droite la maison vieux jaune sur la coline

name: Jennifer - Nous sommes une petite ferme familiale diversifiée (légumes, œufs, poulet, miel)
description:
Bonjour, je me présente Jennifer. Je suis propriétaire de ma petite ferme diversifiée depuis 6 ans. J'opère la ferme avec mon conjoint, ma mère et son conjoint au quotidien. Nous produisons des légumes pour la transformation à la ferme, de la volaille pour les œufs de consommation, de fécondation et du poulet de grain, du miel et produits de la ruche. Nous avons aussi 6 chèvres miniatures pour la reproduction. Nous vivons sur les lieux de la ferme en bi-génération avec mes 3 filles de 9-14-15 ans, ma mère et son conjoint. Dans notre foyer, nous avons un élevage de Bouledogue Français comprenant 7 chiens et avons un vieux American bully de 12 ans.

Nos journées type ressemble à : S'occuper de la volaille et chèvres  (eau, nourriture, ramasser, trier et nettoyer les œufs, s'occuper des champs (arrosage, désherbage, récolte), transformation alimentaire avec nos légumes de jardin (cuisine, empotage, étiquetage, registre), entretien de la ferme, inspection des ruches et traitement nécessaire 1x par semaine et extraction du miel 3x par saison

Nous avons une belle roulotte qui a toutes les commodités ainsi que la salle de bain complète. Elle comprend 3 lits, cuisine complète, téléviseur, radio. Nous avons aussi une belle grosse tente roulotte qui a toutes les commodités pour la cuisine (lavabo avec eau froide et chaude, rond au propane, petit four, petit réfrigérateur), chauffage au propane, 2 lits (1 queen et un king), douche extérieure avec eau chaude et toilette extérieure. Pour les repas, la ferme nous fournit une bonne partie de nos repas. Nous pourrons aller à l'épicerie ensemble pour ce qui manque. Pour le partage des repas, ce sera selon un horaire variable selon mes marchés ou je dois aller à l'extérieur de la ferme (parfois en soirée ou en journée)

Pour mes loisirs, j'aime bien la lecture, faire du vélo et prendre des marches. À proximité, nous avons de beaux endroits pour la randonnée et le vélo, des vignobles... Nous nous situons à 30 minutes de Montréal, 20

name: Evarist - Farm Near Mkomazi National Park
description:
Hello WWOOFers, my name is Evarist, at the farm with my partner of WWOOF Tanzania. Crops we grow at the farm includes; maize, beans, bananas and vegetable varieties

The activities we attend normally includes; farm preparation with local tools like hand hoes (Jembe in Swahili) and panga, planting, weeding, compost manure, mulching, construction and harvesting. We want WWOOFers to learn from our activities on how to manage farms relying mainly on organic principles

We take breakfast, eat dinner and lunch together with WWOOFers at the farm whereby the house for accommodation is available, sometimes we go to the family house, a walking distance at the Pare mountains

I like the Pare mountains where I normally go for hiking, visiting my neighbour families, game drive at Mkomazi National Park which is about 1km a walking distance from the farm.

name: Devta - Back to basics, back to nature, and reconnecting with your self at this Alaskan permaculture garden
description:
We are on 11.5 acres of mostly wild native Boreal Forest in the hills outside of Ester, AK. (17 miles outside of Fairbanks). I have a home and a small Permaculture garden where I practice a sustainable lifestyle.  I am off-grid except for electricity. It is an arctic climate here; long summer days with no darkness and long winter nights with only a few hours of daylight.  This is a very pure environment so if you need to get away from EMF's and other environmental toxins this is the place for you.  I do not have cable TV, cordless phone, microwave oven...none of that.  I have a cell phone.  

When you live in the forest there is constant interaction with the indigenous plants and co-creating happening.  The garden is grown using all organic methods and employing Permaculture principles.  I harvest rain water, use grey water and have drinking water delivered by the water truck.  Being a sustainable lifestyle we are very conservative in water usage.  

The chickens help me with the compost pile which is always in multiple stages.  There is a vibrant healthy batch of earth worms for vermiculture.  There are effective micro-organisms brewing which get used in many ways, including making bokashi and biochar.  Maintaining all of these are integrated into the "farm chores" that you would be helping with.  It is a constant dance whose rhythm is mainly determined by the weather.

There is also water kefir and kombucha brewing.  I  make sauerkraut for the microbiome as well.

There are always lots of "farm chores"; the work is never done and that is what makes living here fun and exciting.  Ingenuity is essential and being willing to develop new skills helps one rise to the daily challenges of rural life surrounded by forest.  This is not a production farm, it is a survival farm and I process food for winter.  

There are many wild medicinal plants on my property and I wild harvest them and create my own medicines. 
We live in boreal blueberry country and mid to late July is
_wwoof_travel: Fairbanks International Airport.  Passenger train from Anchorage.

name: Sébastien - Ferme collective, diversifiée et agrécologique
description:
ENGLISH

The Ferme des Arondes is a collective, diversified, agroecological farm. It is located between Arbre and Saint-Gérard, in the commune of Profondeville.
The farm currently comprises a main building, former stables and 40 hectares of meadows and farmland. In April and May 2022, a major fund-raising campaign enabled us to acquire the farm in collaboration with Terre-en-Vue. 

Our collective of 6 producers is gradually setting up production and processing activities (fruit, vegetables, breads, cheeses, mushrooms, etc.) to supply the region's inhabitants with good produce!

As part of our Wwoofing program, we offer you the opportunity to 
- join a team and a collective dynamic made up of several producers with varied experience: Camille is a baker and is gradually developing cereal and flour production, Sébastien has been a diversified market gardener, Benoît has been producing 6 types of mushrooms since 2015, he is experimenting with substrate production and will also diversify his activity by planting nuts on the farm (chestnut and hazelnut trees), Simon, a fruit tree grower, Claire, a wicker specialist and Nils, a chicken master, are also arriving on the farm to carry out their project in 2025. 

- take part in different production activities depending on the season, current needs and your motivations: market gardening (sowing, transplanting, harvesting, maintenance, soil fertility management, moving greenhouses...), baking (making sourdough bread, loading wood for the oven, crop monitoring...), field crops, mushroom production and substrate, harvesting and processing wild plants, helping new projects get off the ground...

- discover the stages and challenges involved in setting up a farm and taking over a 40-hectare farm that was historically conventional and gradually becoming agroecological.

We welcome you to the farm in the main building, which we also use as our HQ. The farm is lively and productive, so there's plenty of traffic from producers and thei
_wwoof_travel: EN : The nearest train station is Lustin.  We can pick you up there. FR : La gare la plus proche est celle de Lustin. Nous pouvons venir vous chercher à cette gare. NL : Het dichtstbijzijnde treinstation is Lustin.

name: Angelune - Medicinal Herb Farm in the Wheaton River Valley, Yukon
description:
Hi! We are Jalfred  and Angelune (and Mimouna, the wild cat), and we run a 1.3 hectares off-grid property and venue where we grow our own vegetables, as well as berry bushes and medicinal plants.

Regular projects on the farm include general maintenance like garden bed preparation, planting, transplanting, weeding, pruning, harvesting and processing, as well as special projects like outdoor renovations, fencing, compost making, sod removal, terracing, etc.

We live seasonally, and the Yukon seasons are dramatically different than elsewhere in Canada. In May and June, we have school group visits, we start planting the garden and we also start going to Thursday markets in Whitehorse where we sell herbal products. July is a month of general maintenance in the garden and around the property, during which we typically don't host helpers. August, September and October are busy with harvests and processing of vegetables and herbs, and closing the garden for the winter. We typically don't host helpers from November to May as our gardens are resting under the snow. However, help with firewood or renovation projects indoors is sometimes needed, so feel free to reach out in the off-season if you're already in the Yukon and looking for a short wintry stay.

Our accommodation for helpers is a rustic cabin on the banks of the Wheaton River, about 200 feet from the main house. By rustic, we mean it is a dry cabin (no running water or toilet), heated with a wood stove, and lit with candles and a propane lamp. The cabin does not have  electricity or internet reception and there is no cell phone reception past the Annie Lake Road cut off. Cell phones can be used with WiFi calling in the summer kitchen, where devices can also be charged during the day. We provide blue water jugs for drinking and washing (a sink drains directly into a grey water pit), a camping shower (set up in an outdoor shower booth behind the cabin), a dry outhouse (close to the cabin), wood (that needs to be split

name: Nils - Former WWOOFers turned hosts in Montana!
description:
Profile description and calendar updated in March 2026! 
2024/2025 pictures are coming (also pictures of the guest room and bathroom)

Please read everything below before you write to us and submit a request.
---------------------------------------------------------------

Hi, we are Nils (38) and Lea (36), with our son Forest (4 years old) and our daughter River (2 years old). Outside of the house are our dogs Luna and Baloo, our 4 alpacas, 2 llamas, 10 chickens, 1 guinea fowl and 1 cat. 
We are french-american. We speak English and French but we have a few basics with Dutch, Spanish and Italian. 
Since we have "Wwoofed" several times around Earth, and we enjoyed the experiences, we would like to try to host a few people from time to time. 
We want to be a "self-sufficiency" property or "self-sustain farm/ranch",  trying to provide food, fleece, different kind of supplies and activities for our family. We make our own bread and yogurt. We are not a business.
Lea is working full time from home (cancer research) and Nils is a stay at home dad taking care of the kids, the land and the animals (he was a librarian for 10 years). 

For us, hosting Wwoofers is still a work in progress, so we'll try to keep this page updated as we move forward with our project and adjust our needs along the years. 

---------------------------------------------------------------

-For accommodation  : 
/guest room in the basement is now ready and can welcome 1 or 2 persons. NEW in 2025 : you'll have your own bathroom next to the bedroom. Please note that Lea has now her office in the basement so during the day you'll need to be quiet inside. 
/self-sustain vehicles on our land are welcome (you'll still eat with us and you'll be able to use our living room and bathrooms). 

-For duration : we would prefer a few days to a week. Ten days or two weeks possible depending on several things but being the maximum.

-For food : we can provide food for you to cook, or we can cook for you and you'll
_wwoof_travel: Airport : Billings in the east and Bozeman in the west

name: Eugenijus - Old schoolhouse is being renovated into a villa with self-sufficient small farm around it.
description:
Eugenijus, previously a city dweller, bought a more than 100-year-old school building and moved to the countryside, where he is trying to bring back a new life for the place, which was renamed to 'Būro kiemas' and the building -  'Villa Gladiole'. The area is surrounded by old trees, and the previous school stadium was transformed into ecologically cultivated land.
For the moment, here is a growing herd of skudde breed sheep, 6 hens and a rooster, and a sweetheart German shepherd - Gilda. 

It would be great to share knowledge and experience in the mentioned activities, but Eugenijus comes from an art and documentary photography background, where he has gathered baggage.

The living quarters are 95% completed (the entrance with the stairway is a work in progress). There is a single room with two double sofas and one room with a single two-person sofa for guests, or there is plenty of room for a camper. The villa has a fully functional shared kitchen and a bathroom.

Depending on the season, food will be from the garden and all the variations that can be made. Milk is bought from a local farmer. Whatever is needed additionally, from a grocery shop.

Breakfast for guests is self-service.

Lunch would be prepared together. 

Dinner is optional to one's needs since Eugenijus is having just a few nibbles in the evening, nothing big. But guests are welcome to prepare anything more what one is accustomed to. 

In the kitchen, Eugenijus is still learning to create more sophisticated dishes, but generally, guests are not complaining. Anyone who could teach a bit of better cooking would be much appreciated.

Evening time is free, but there is a possibility for various improvisations. Talks, books, learning... 

There are quite beautiful surroundings for a hike, so guests can enjoy them. There is an old bike (only one) for a tour around. 

Eugenijus's whole life is turning around art, with a bigger focus on documentary photography and DIY / self-sufficiency philosophy, so this

name: Tutamuzongoza - Our project deals with the community through agriculture both faming and animal rearing

3.Two hours per day

name: Lukáš - Ekologická farma a komunita hnutí Haré Kršna na Moravě
description:
Jsme živá komunita usilující o soběstačný a smysluplný život. Vaříme na dřevě, pěstujeme si vlastní jídlo, věnujeme se tradičním řemeslům a žijeme duchovně. Patříme k hnutí pro vědomí Kršny – náš každodenní život tak doprovází meditace, zpívání manter, studium písem i sdílená služba. Společně tu žije asi 15 dospělých a 7 dětí – kromě rodin tu působí také mniši v celibátu, kteří pomáhají udržovat duchovní atmosféru i rozvíjet náš projekt. Chováme 4 krávy plemene jersey, které se volně pasou na čerstvých pastvinách – staráme se o ně s láskou, jako o členy rodiny.

Zabýváme se ekologickým pěstováním zeleniny a obilí a staráme se o několik krav. Návštěvníci se mohou zapojit do různorodé práce – od péče o zahradu a sklizeň sena až po pomoc ve stáji nebo na polích.

Nabízíme čistě vegetariánskou stravu, často inspirovanou indickou kuchyní v kombinaci se západními jídly. Kršňácká kuchyně, kterou připravujeme s oddaností, je po celém světě oceňovaná pro svou chuť i duchovní hloubku.

Ubytování je jednoduché, ve venkovském stylu – skromné pokoje se společnou koupelnou a suchým kompostovacím WC. Teplá voda se ohřívá v lázeňských kamnech na dřevo. Bydlení je oddělené pro muže a ženy.

Volný čas pro nás znamená duchovní praxi – rádi zpíváme Haré Kršna mahámantru při společném kírtanu za doprovodu tradičních indických nástrojů, čteme duchovní knihy a sdílíme své poznání. Pokud vás tyto hodnoty oslovují, budeme rádi za vaši návštěvu a pomoc.

We are a small spiritual community living simply and close to nature. We cook on wood, grow our own food, develop traditional crafts and care for four Jersey cows as part of our family. Our life is inspired by Krishna consciousness – we chant together, study sacred texts, and live in harmony with traditional values. Around 15 adults and 7 children live here, including monks who help maintain the spiritual atmosphere. You’re welcome to join us and experience community life, daily meditation, honest work, and peaceful countryside living.

We a

name: Anne Sophie - Trata se de um espaço com mata Atlântica e queremos implantar um sistema agroflorestal.
description:
Olá Wwoofers! Sou Anne Sophie Bertrand, doutora em Biologia e terapeuta holística . Sou a Diretora Executiva do Centro Permacultural Fazenda Fazendo. Francesa de origem, vivo no Brasil há 20 anos e dedico minha vida à educação regenerativa e à sustentabilidade. Mãe de duas meninas (9 e 5 anos), somos uma família vegana que vive os princípios da permacultura no cotidiano.
Localizado a apenas 5 minutos do centro de Foz do Iguaçu (PR), na bacia do Arroio Pé Feio, nosso espaço é um refúgio verde de preservação ambiental no bairro Bourbon. O terreno conta com 600m² de construção colonial para atividades educativas, uma grande área verde preservada com riachinho natural correndo pelo meio, uma quadra poliesportiva e pátios para atividades comunitárias em reforma, e vários espaços destinados para sistemas agroflorestais, hortas comunitárias e canteiros educativos. 
O Centro Permacultural Fazenda Fazendo é pioneiro na região trinacional, promovendo arte, cultura e educação regenerativa para pessoas de todas as idades. Nosso projeto integra: 
🌱 Permacultura aplicada - sistemas agroflorestais, bioconstrução, saneamento ecológico 🎨 Educação através das artes - música, dança, artes visuais, circo 🌍 Consciência socioambiental - educação climática, economia circular 🤝 Integração comunitária - inclusão social, parcerias público-privadas. Quem convive na Fazenda Fazendo são as crianças e adolescentes em atividades artísticas. esportivas e culturais, famílias da comunidade local em atividades de fim de semana, educadores e permacultores da rede trinacional ORÉ, agricultores urbanos e microempreendedores locais e voluntários de diversas nacionalidades que buscam aprendizado prático.
Buscamos mãos amigas e corações alinhados para nos ajudar a desenvolver os sistemas produtivos do centro: criação de canteiros educativos, implementação de pequenos sistemas agroflorestais, cuidado com as plantas existentes, plantio, colheita e irrigação.
Oferecemos em troca: aprendizado em permacultura,
_wwoof_travel: Rodoviária de Foz do Iguaçu / aeroporto internacional de Foz do Iguaçu

name: Özge Berrin - Doğanın rehberliğinde kendi kendine yetebilen, üreten, çok çalışmalı, aşkla işlenen çiftlik.
description:
Bahçemizde agroekolojik yöntemlerle olabildiğince müdahalesiz, destekleyici, onarıcı üretim yapmaya çalışıyorum.  Yerimiz meyve bahçesi fakat direkt toprağa ekim, dikim yaptığımız alanlar da mevcut. Öncelik kendi gıdamız için üretim yapıyoruz.  İhtiyacımızdan fazlasını katma değerli ürünlere dönüştürüp paylaşıyoruz.

Yemek ve uyku saatleri dışında her an çiftliğimizi güzelleştirmek, doğallığını, sadeliğini korumak için bir şeyler yapıyorum. Çiftlikte iş hiç bitmiyor. Ben de doğadan öğreniyorum. O en büyük rehber. Bildiklerimi uygulasam da onun bana cevabı bana yol gösteriyor. Paylaşılmayan bilgi verimsiz toprak gibidir. Birlikte üretip, katkı olup öğreneceğiz. Bazen sabah erkenden kalkıp çapa yaparız, bazen bambu keser çit yaparız. Rutinimiz yalnızca çalışmak.

Çiftliğimizin içinde büyük bir evimiz var. Gönüllümüz de bizimle evimizi paylaşacak. 1 odamız gönüllülük için ayrıldı.  Biz günde 2 ana öğün, bir de atıştırma şeklinde  yemek yiyoruz. Yemekleri de birlikte yapabiliriz. Bize yardım eden başka hiç kimse yok. Ben yemek yapmayı, birlikte yemek yemeyi seviyorum. Ailecek sigara kullanmıyoruz. Ve gönüllülerimizle kendi yaşadığımız evi paylaşacağımız için sigara kullanan gönüllü başvurularını kabul edemiyorum.

Kundalini yoga eğitmeniyim. Homeopati ve agrohomeopati eğitimleri aldım. Kurumsal olarak Zeytin ve Zeytinyağı konusunda oldukça deneyimliyim. Uluslararası Zeytinyağı Tadım Uzmanıyım. Yaşadığımız yerden 2 km sonra şehir merkezi fakat bulunduğumuz yere gelen bir toplu taşıma yok. Denize uzaklık 11 km.

I am a young female farmer aged 37. I am also a kundalini yoga instructor. I am a homeopath. I am a craftsman. I am an olive oil tasting expert. We live my husband, 9 years old child and 2 cats and a do in Edremit, Balıkesir. My husband works elsewhere. He helps me on weekends. We try to produce continuously in our garden without violating agricultural ecological rights. Our place is an orchard but there are also areas where we do direct planting and sowing. We pr

name: Seth - Organic fruit, vegetable, egg, and honey farm
description:
Hi WWOOFers! My name is Seth, and my wife Mae and I own a small family farm in Orange County, NY located in the Hudson Valley. During the summer months, we primarily produce fruits, vegetables, cut flowers, eggs, and honey, which we sell through our farm stand located on our farm. We have two Labrador Retrievers who live with us (Monty and Emma). We started our farm about 5 years ago, and have slowly grown each year. From day one, we've been committed to growing everything organically. It's challenging, but our goal is to produce low-carbon, healthy, organic products for our community. As we develop our farm, we plan to add livestock, ideally grass-feed sheep, which we will allow us to close the loop and produce sufficient quantities of on-site compost, which we then incorporate back into our crop fields. We currently grow tomatoes, peppers, onions, cucumbers, zucchini, summer squash, sweet corn, potatoes, garlic, onions, shallots, green beans, kale, beets, celery, watermelon, cantaloupes, pumpkins, raspberries, blueberries, strawberries, rhubarb, asparagus, cut lavender, cut sunflowers, and other products. We have a flock of laying hens and ducks, which free-range on an approximately 1/2 acre pasture. Currently, we have approximately 1 acre in vegetable production, including a high tunnel and hoop house.  We also have a small u-pick operation we are developing, as well as a small fruit-tree orchard.

A typical day on the farm begins around 7 or 8 AM with a quick inspection of the crop fields. This often dictates what the day's tasks include, such as where weeds may need to be pulled or if pests/disease need to be addressed on the crops. Three days a week we harvest crops and then stock the farm stand (currently Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays). In the future, we may be participating in on or more of the local farmers markets as well. About once a week or every other week, I tend to our beehives. We have about a dozen beehives that help us pollinate our crops. Th
_wwoof_travel: Beacon & Campbell Hall Metro Line North train stations, Newark Airport (EWR)

name: Monika - We are an organic farm focused on regenerative and permaculture practices
description:
Our farm is located  on the beautiful Thompson River. We cultivate a large garden, 27 fruit trees and a small vineyard. We share the farm with a herd of horses, migo the dog and two cats. 


The routine of the farm revolves around the garden, orchard and caring for the animals. It would be lovely to have some help gardening (prepping garden beds, planting, weeding, watering, mowing) I'd be happy to teach basic principles on horsemanship and riding horses. 


The summers are hot here, so ideally working a short day of four hours starting early mornings would be ideal. 


I am a trained chef, we bake organic sourdough bread for the farmers market and teach food workshops here on the farm. The food in the house is mostly plant based, with a small amount of meat from the neighbors. There is always loads of fresh bread and treats. Learn as much about cooking and baking as you like!

name: Gaia - Giovane azienda agricola, animali da cortile, orto, giardino e tanta natura!
description:
Siamo Gaia (35) e Niccolò (41) , viviamo in questa casa (che per noi è il posto dei sogni) da 2 anni, ma questa proprietà è sempre stata della famiglia di Gaia ed è per noi un posto dell’anima!
Ora ci viviamo con i nostri due figli, 3 cani e 4 gatti (Gli animali per noi sono parte integrante della famiglia e sono sempre con noi) 
C’è tanto da fare tra un giardino fiorito di circa 1000mq , animali da cortile, orto (con sempre tanti progetti e sperimentazioni in corso)  e uliveto! Intorno alla casa ci sono circa 4 ettari di terreno coltivabile (per questa stagione è stato seminato grano) e un bosco che ci offre tante possibilità.
Le sistemazioni di pernotto  che possiamo offrire sono in una casetta indipendente (a 10m da casa) completa di bagno, e siamo molto felici di condividere con voi quanto più possibile

Le nostre attività giornaliere sono la cura degli animali, dell’orto (con sempre qualche progetto in costruzione) e in primavera/estate c è molto da fare anche con il giardino tra cura delle piante e del prato.. A fine pomeriggio però ci piace recuperare un po di energie con un bell’aperitivo  home made vista tramonti spaziali!
A seconda del periodo dell'anno ci prendiamo cura dell'uliveto con la produzione di olio extraergine d'oliva e la manutenzione ordinaria

Ci piacerebbe ospitare persone a cui piace la condivisione, per cenare insieme e condividere bei momenti, ma sappiamo quanto è importante un po di privacy e tranquillità quindi siamo disponibili fornendo anche soluzioni per pasti in autonomia

Ci piace tantissimo stare all'aperto e  fare lunghe passeggiate nel territorio (ci sono molti sentieri da esplorare sui monti che circondano casa) e spesso quando gli impegni lo permettono visitiamo i borghi vicini perché sono veramente meravigliosi. 
(Gaia è un’appassionata di fai da te e ogni giorno ha un nuovo progetto tra fuori e dentro casa)
Comunque è il nostro primo hobby rimane sempre cucinare!! (Seguiamo un'alimentazione vegetariana)

name: Diane - Educational Centre, Horse Ranch, Family farm, Diversified farm, Internship opportunities
description:
Volunteer With Us – 2026 Placements (Minimum 2 Weeks)

Join us on 300 acres of peaceful Alberta ranch land where horses, gardens and big skies shape daily life. Stay, work, learn and immerse yourself in authentic ranch living.

We are seeking energetic, positive helpers who enjoy meaningful work outdoors and aren’t afraid to get their hands dirty. Ranch life isn’t 9–5; weather and seasons guide our days. We have weather delays due to most of our work is outside so we quit when its raining/snowing and then go back out when it stops so days do not follow a 9 -5 schedule.

Projects include: gardening (permaculture & food forest), building raised beds, fencing, yard work, painting, general ranch chores, office help, and occasional support during retreats or camps. Experienced horse people may assist with light training and programs.

Helpers stay in our cozy bunkhouse (shared accommodations, basic food provided). English fluency is required for safety. We value kindness, initiative, teamwork, and zero drama.

If you want to live close to horses, land, and wide open skies — and ask, “What can I do next?” — we’d love to hear from you.

name: Kara - Looking for wwoofers April-November 2026
description:
Welcome to Our Farm in Southwestern Manitoba!

Hi there! I’m Kara, and I’m so glad you’ve found our page. We’re a family farm overlooking the beautiful Souris River, surrounded by oak forests, hiking trails, and a lovely river swimming spot just a short drive from our home.

We are looking for enthusiastic WWOOFers to join us during the growing season! We can host up to six people at a time and love welcoming people who are eager to learn, share experiences, and enjoy farm life.

A Bit About Us
We’re a young family of four with a deep passion for farming, sustainability, and self-sufficiency. Our farm includes a vibrant market garden where we grow vegetables and flowers. I bake sourdough for market, and my husband runs a grain farm. We also forage for wild greens and berries, tap maple trees for syrup, and preserve our harvests through freezing, dehydrating, canning, and cold storage.

Our little farm crew includes:
🐐 9 friendly pet goats
🐑 6 adorable Babydoll sheep
🐕 A medium-sized, happy dog
🐓 Free-range hens
🐈 2 sweet cats

We also love cooking over an open fire, fermenting, sprouting, woodworking, sand sewing. Hunting and fishing are part of our lifestyle, and we have experience with outdoor and winter survival.

Accommodations & Meals
We offer cozy and clean accommodations, including:
🏡 A guest house with three bedrooms
🌿 A guest cabin
🛏️ A spacious guest room with a king bed
🏕️ A well-insulated cowboy camper for a comfortable stay

We eat a plant-based diet with some meat, eggs, and dairy, always focusing on seasonal and local foods. Ww can easily accomodate vegan, vegetarian, or  meat eater diets. We provide most food for WWOOFers and share many meals together.

Our Farm Practices
🌱 We farm using organic methods, including hand weeding, tilling, and mulching. Our 2-acre garden follows a cover crop rotation every second year.
🌾 We have two yard sites and over 280 hectares of farmland that have been organically managed since 2020.

What to Expect
👩‍🌾 Our work s

name: Luciana - Transição agroecologica com cultivo de manga e tangerina em transição para pequena agrofloresta.
description:
Sou Luciana, nascida em Salvador. Cresci numa vida semi-urbana próximo ao mar e fazem 15 anos que venho caminhando em busca de viver a vida e o rítmo do campo.  Sou permacultora, formada em psicologia e apaixonada pelos processos da vida e pela vida e cultura rural. A Chácara Bem Viver está localizada em Rio de Contas, na Chapada Diamantina.  É um espaço que proposto para regeneração da vida e do ser, aberto às trocas e e experimentos de novas formas de bem viver a vida rural. 
Uma de nossas buscas é vivenciar na prática um novo paradigma, com uma visão que leve em consideração todas as possíveis relações entre os seres e elementos visíveis e invisíveis na convivência e cooperação pela construção de um espaço que favoreça os processos que beneficiem a vida.

Dentre as atividades desenvolvidas para o sustento da Chácara, estão a agricultura, que envolve o roçar, adubar, semear, transplantar, podar, colher, secar, armazenar (com produtos para consumo, venda direta e beneficiamento), artesanato com materiais naturais, que envolve coleta, secagem, produção, hospedagem familiar/camping e alimentação. Celebramos os solstícios e equinócios, promovemos círculos brincantes com as crianças da comunidade e fogueiras de histórias.

O preparo das refeições faz parte das atividades diárias da chácara para pertilha com todos. Temos desjejum, almoço, lanche e janta todos os dias.

Gosto de tomar banho nos rios próximos, compartilhar momentos na fogueira com contação de história e música e dançar!

Soy Luciana, nacida en Salvador. Crecí en una vida semiurbana cerca del mar y desde hace 15 años camino en busca de vivir la vida y el ritmo del campo. Soy permaculturista, licenciada en psicología y apasionada por los procesos de la vida. Chácara Bem Viver está ubicada en Rio de Contas, en Chapada Diamantina. Es un espacio propuesto para la regeneración de la vida y del ser, abierto a intercambios y experimentaciones sobre nuevas formas de vivir la vida rural.
Una de nuestras búsquedas e
_wwoof_travel: Agência de ônibus de Rio de Cont

name: Sebastian - Family farm
description:
Re-imagining a family farm as a self-sustainable and environmentally-conscious community. 
We are passionate about agriculture, music, surfing, travel, nature and the environment. We have dogs, cats, chickens, sheep, bees, horses and a eucalyptus forest playing host to a plethora of wildlife. We have a large and productive vegetable garden with a range of vegetables and herbs, and a variety of fruit trees.
We are looking for self-motivated individuals to help us out with daily tasks such as keeping the house and grounds clean, tending to and watering the gardens and fruit trees, taking care of the chickens and other animals, and whatever else needs to be done.

Organic methods: As natural as possible
Certifications: We only produce to consume
**Minimum stay of three months

name: Ingrid - Bonny on the Clyde is a historic farm on the beautiful Clyde River deep in the heart of Nova Scotia.
description:
Des bons vivants Québecois in Nova Scotia. 

We are situated outside Shelburne, Nova Scotia on the beautiful Clyde River (OO-NE-GUN-SOOK). Our homestead is 26 acres with river frontage and cleared trails so there are lots of things to do when you aren't helping out if you like the outdoors. South West Nova Scotia is home to some of the best stargazing in North America. We have kayaks, a paddle board, and other outdoor equipment for you to play with. We usually end the day with a dip in the river. 

We offer excellent food and usually bake our own breads. During the summer and fall we enjoy fresh veggies from the garden, and fresh, organic eggs. We are omnivores but our animals are pets. We presently have a motley crüe of chickens, Guinea fowl, rabbits, and mini goats.  

We hope to connect with people who want to learn more about or have expertise in permaculture, gardening, forest management, animal husbandry, construction, and carpentry. We have a mill and we mill lumber for our projects. Almost all tasks are physical and require a certain amount of self-motivation, strength, stamina, and focus. We are not an education centre. We teach you what you need to know to do the task at hand. 

We are off the beaten path, so couples and travelling friends enjoy the experience more. For visits of 2-3 week we can pick you up and drop you off at the bus in Bridgewater, if we have availability. For shorter visits you must have your own transportation. 

Contact us for a link to videos of the property.

Gardening methods: Composting, no-dig gardening, vermicomposting, seed saving, companion planting, permaculture, aquaculture, phytorestoration. 

Certifications: We are not certified organic but we do not use chemical herbicides, pesticides, or fertilizer on our property. We are focused on biodiversity, permaculture, and ecotherapy.

Current or on-going projects: Clearing forest of underbrush, milling lumber, building a funky greenhouse,  more raised beds and Hugelkultur garden
_wwoof_travel: Halifax airport or Bridgewater bus station

name: Fatma - Tibbi ve yenebilir yabani otlar yetiştirme/Toplama/Kurutma
description:
[ENGLISH BELOW]

Merhaba, biz Antalya’daki çiftliğimizde 7 kedi, 3 köpek, 15-20 kadar tavuk ile birlikte yaşayan 60 yaşında akademisyen evli bir çiftiz. Çiftliğimiz 2 dönümlük arazi içinde. Yakınımızda işlediğimiz 2 dönümlük bir arazi daha var. Çiftliğimizde yabani bitkilerin üretimi, tanıtımı, kurutulması, tohum alma gibi işler yapıyoruz. Otlardan ve meyvelerden kurutulmuş gıda ve yeni nesil atıştırmalıklar yapıyoruz. Deneme bahçeleri yapıyoruz. Çiçekler yetiştiriyoruz, reçel yapıyoruz, kurutulmuş domates yapıyoruz. Bakla yetiştiriyoruz. Papatya ve gelincik tarlamız var. Arazimizi sürdürmüyoruz. Doğal tarım ve permakültür prensiplerini benimsiyoruz, yaban tarımı yapıyoruz.

Köyümüz taş evleri ile ünlü, 10-12 hanelik bir köy. Antalya’ya 60 km mesafedeyiz. Arazimizde iki taş evimiz var. Birinde biz kalıyoruz, diğeri gönüllülerin konaklaması için kullanılıyor. Gönüllü konaklama alanımız bizim kaldığımız ana binanın 3 metre mesafesinde 37 metrekarelik bağımsız bir bölüm. 2 odadan oluşan bölümde 1 tuvalet/banyo var. Her odada 2 yatak ve bir raflı dolap bulunuyor. Televizyon ve çalışma masası var. Isınma için sobada odun yakıyoruz, sıcak su için de banyoda ısıtıcı var. Ana bina ve gönüllü evinde kalanlar mutfak olarak açık mutfağı kullanabiliyor.

Gönüllülerimizi genelde 1 nisandan  1 ekim'e  kadar  kabul ediyoruz.

kadınların doğal tarımda daha fazla yer almasını istediğimizden ve 
Kadınlara yönelik eğitici bir çalışma olmasını düşündüğümüzden  Sadece kadın gönüllülerin başvurmasını istiyoruz  

Gönüllü dostlardan bize sahada bitki toplama, üretim aşamasında bitki bakımı, tohum elde etmek, bitkilerin kurutulması aşaması, çiftlikte hayvanların bakımı ve günlük işler konusunda yardımcı olmalarını bekliyoruz. Sabahları erken kalkıyoruz, 7 gibi kahvaltı yapıyoruz. Programımıza göre öğlene kadar arazide oluyoruz. Öğlen birkaç saat mola verdikten sonra saha veya ana binada 18.00a kadar faaliyetlerimize devam ediyoruz.

—------

Hello, we are a married couple of 60-year-old ac

name: Lena - Family farm
description:
This is a small family farm/homestead located in a very small village in the forest. We used to have animals as you can see in the photos, but now my two children are in their 20th and have moved away from home. So I, Lena, currently live here by myself. For some years now I want to be more flexible and be able to travel, but I will eventually have animals again in the future.
I'm 53 years old and work as a high school teacher in Spanish and Social and political studies. I also work as a journalist for a regional newspaper. In the summer Hollidays I would love to have some help with gardening and cultivation. The work here this summer could be to take down old fences from corrals we don't use anymore, take in fire wood to an outhouse, clean the garden, dig the potato land, some cultivation. In the end of the summer you can also harvest potatoes and other vegetables, pick berries and do jam. It would also be nice with some help in the kitchen and other things. 

You live in a small house, a typical Swedish friggebod. It´s 10 m2 and has three beds, but we recommend you stay maximum two persons, unless you come three friends together.  In the guesthouse there is electricity, but no bathroom, so you use the bathroom, eat and use wifi in our house.

I also have a little wooden house in the Ecuadorian Amazon where you are welcome to stay if you help fixing the house and the fruit-tree garden. Together with a local man you can help fixing the paths so it will be easier and dryer to walk. My neighbours in Ecuador have a place where you can do voluntary work too. Its called Zanja Arajuno. You can google it ;)

Welcome, Lena
_wwoof_travel: Closest main airport is Arlanda, Stockholm. From there you can take a train for Ludvika and there I pick you up with my car. There is a bus stop in the village Klenshyttan and another one in the village Blötberget.

name: Patricia - Multigenerational wwoofing on a tropical flower farm and garden center, teaching floristry skills
description:
Aloha and Welcome to WNGC!!

We have restarted our hydroponic growing operation for our farm to table Bistro, and are currently looking for 2 more males and 2 females to join our summer interns who are already busy with flower farming, plant and nursery management and floristry. If interested in these areas, especially our farm to table Bistro, please read on!

In addition to shared spaces, we  also have three private rooms opening up for "mature" in age singles or any age couples. We love to host people traveling together and hope to see your applications soon!

Our farm is located on the most beautiful part of the island, right across the street from the ocean. The bus stops at our property and goes all over the island! This is truly Paradise living in an executive style home. All stays are 3 month commitments, with a few exceptions.

OUR CURRENT NEEDS: plant nursery work; help with maintaining the ornamental plants, landscape and fruit trees on the property; floristry help and help with our hydroponic and aquaponic growing systems.

You will learn seeding, propagating, pest control, fertilization and harvesting techniques, as well as floristry (if interested). 

Certificates and college credits are available in floristry, floraculture and plant nursery management.

SHARED  MALE BEDROOM:  
First floor large, very private room with bath and direct exit to a tropical backyard escape.  Queen and bunk beds with comfortable mattresses, a dresser for each wwoofer and 4 closets. 

SHARED FEMALE BEDROOM: Second floor bedroom has two bunks with comfortable bedding,  nice bathroom next door and lots of cupboard and drawer space for personals. Each wwoofer has her own dresser and bathroom drawers. Huge upstairs deck with panoramic view of mountains and ocean, a perfect place for yoga and a cup of Kona coffee. 

* We support Wwoofers who identify outside the binary categories of male or female to choose the shared bedroom where they feel most comfortable.

** Over 50. WE WELC
_wwoof_travel: Honolulu is the airport. No train yet (building a rail system now). Bus stops right in front of our property

name: Hélène - FERME EN GRANDES CULTURES DANS LA PLAINE CHAMPENOISE
description:
Bonjour amis WWOOfeurs ! Je m'appelle Hélène, j'ai 41 ans, et il y a neuf ans je suis revenue avec mes 3 enfants et mon mari  sur ma terre d'enfance pour reprendre l'exploitation agricole en grandes cultures. Mon objectif était de la convertir progressivement à  l'agriculture biologique.

Ici, il n'y a pas de journée type et nous travaillons avec la météo. En avril, mail, juin , juillet nos activités vont s'organiser autour du désherbage et notamment le désherbage manuel des betteraves sucrières, culture très pratiquée dans notre département.

Nous avons une grande maison à 4 km de la ferme. Dans cette maison, un coin un peu indépendant avec deux chambres, sanitaires et douche, permettra aux wwoofeurs d'avoir l'indépendance et un confort de vie.
Nous avons un grand salon et également une cuisine que l'on peut partager et pourquoi pas prendre, au gré des envies de chacun, nos repas ensemble !
Nous sommes idéalement situé à 5 minutes en voiture de la gare de Chalons en Champagne, donc il est possible pour nos amis wwoffeurs de se prévoir de petites excursions sur Paris qui est bien desservi et à seulement 1h40 de chez nous en train. De plus nous sommes également à proximité de la montagne de Reims et du vignoble champenois, idéal pour programmer quelques jolies randonnées, visite de caves et dégustation de notre Champagne ! 

Nous sommes une famille passionnée par le basket ; nos trois enfants le pratiquent quotidiennement et nos week-ends sont souvent remplis par leurs matchs ou ceux de l'équipe locale que l'on supporte.
Personnellement je suis également engagée dans des associations qui ont pour but de préserver la nature.

name: Jessie - By a lovely village in Clwydian Hills, N Wales. We produce veg and lamb for our local community.
description:
Hi Woofers, we are Paul and Jess and we live here with our 4 year old son, Robin. We have been here for 3 years and began veg production 2 years ago to provide wonderful organically produced veg boxes to our surrounding community. Moving into our next year we are preparing to double the size of our veg garden which has been tricky with all this wet weather but we will do it! We have 2 cats, and a herd of Lleyn sheep which have just finished lambing and a few dexter cattle.   Also a peacock which came with the house!

We could do with some help looking after the veg, among other things. We currently have more no-dig veg beds to make and existing ones to look after. Currently pulling of old plants and preparing the beds to receive this years seedlings. Sewing seeds, creating a compost system, weeding, picking veg and help with boxing up. So you will be able to see the process of how we do things here. And who knows, maybe you can teach us a thing or two as well! All ideas welcome!  We also have some small building projects we want to complete this year, a compost toilet and basic shower blocks for woofers, and a shed in the market garden. We also need to create a packing station for the veg boxes in an existing shed.

Our woofer accommodation is a caravan which has been very well adorned inside! It can sleep 2 adults and there's also potential to sleep 2 children.  Our farm is spacious and in a beautiful setting and the plan is that we work out a balance between giving you the space that you want as well as welcoming you to join us for an evening meal a couple of times a week. A casual lunch can be eaten either outside or in. Much of your vegetables (possibly all!)will be from the garden from June, with the rest sourced from our nearest town or village shop.

Our farm is in an area of Outstanding natural beauty and you will see why. There is plenty of walking to do, there's Llandegla Mountain biking forest, an outdoors activity centre, and River Dee for kayaking or sw
_wwoof_travel: Nearest train Station, Chester. Nearest bus station is Mold, Nearest airport Liverpool or Manchester

name: Jennifer Gregoria - Agritourism Destination, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat
description:
A paradise for fresh food and fit bodies, Eden's Door has two customized fitness trails on its perimeter. On July 16, 2017, Eden's Door welcomed Wwoofer, Valentina Quinti from Genoa, Italy. Valentina is incredibly helpful and is making solid contributions to Eden's Door. A great photographer as well. I am seeing many new aspects of Eden's Door through her photos. Valentina loves Tobago and the warmth of the people and is thrilled to enjoy our farm-to-table gourmet cuisine. We shall post photos and stories soon Thank you.. Eden's Door promotes healthy lifestyles and wellness in a relaxing atmosphere. At Eden's door you will: Experience a working farm Grow fruit trees in an arboretum Sow your own seeds. Catch them; plant them; nurture them Enjoy and learn to prepare farm-to-table food; Hike our self-directed trails Help us grow in our herb garden, surrounding a fountain: Basil, thyme, rosemary, Shadon Beni, Tulsie and white lavender used in local cuisine. Our food is delicious and much of it comes from the farm. We welcome volunteers with organic farming experience and any skills in the outdoors. We need help always. Thank you, Eden's Door

Organic methods: Composting, Animal manure.

Certifications: Not applicable

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: International Airport is 25 minutes from ED. Flights from the USA and EU. Caribbean Airlines (CAL) flies to Tobago. Taxis available from Airport to Eden's Door.

name: Noam - We receive volunteers for the harvest seasson fron November till May 2026
description:
The farm cradled in the San Luis valley of Monteverde cloud forest has been growing coffee for over 80 years. It grows some of Costa Rica finest coffee, but aim to do more than grow great coffee, Santuario El-Jardin is a demonstration project wich restore and enhance ecological balance through art, education and Biodynamic agriculture.
Our coffee farm is a jewel set in the cloud forest where we are restoring the forest and have planted thousands of trees reclaiming pasture and recreating home for a multitude of native and medicinal plants, trees and forest animals.
since 2012 we started working with Biodynamic methods and preparations. 

Organic methods: organic and  Biodynamic methods.
Biodynamic preparations.
moon calendar
compost, liquid fertilizers, mountain micro organism
erosion control
water management

Certifications: member of the MAOPAC organization.

Current projects: coffee farm
cacao plantation
avocado and citrus orchard
greenhouse vegetables
garden

Internet access available

name: Robert - Family home and garden - rural
description:
A small unit, peaceful surroundings in the Aconcagua valley, easy reach of  Santiago and the coast. A small scale farmlet with  a large variety of crops and animals.  Host as well as many animals including ostriches, horse, ducks, pigs rabbits, chickens and dogs. Available room in main House with own bathroom.   Hel wanted in fruit picking as well as maintenance work such as painting. No heavy duty tasks required.  To arrive,  go  ahead to midway between San Felipe and Llay llay, km 16 of   Route 60.

Certifications: Demasiado chico para certificar.

Current projects: Desarrollo de jardines y areas de recreación, meta es ser auto suficiente en alimentos.

Internet access available

name: Nicolai - Regenerative farm surrounded by majestic mountains and stormfull ocean
description:
Hello,

Ps. We are looking for longer stays in spring, summer, autum..

On the farm, it's me Nicolai (32 years), my partner Panida (30 years), our daughter Åsa (1 year), and our joyful dog Slim Shady. I am a teacher in economics at the local high school, while my partner has a master's degree in food science.

Me and my partner are engaged outdoor enthusiasts, who love to be in and understand nature. Our hobby is surfing and climbing in the local area.

We live surrounded by the sea and mountains. We live 4 minutes by bike from a beautiful surfing beach, and we have mountains with beautiful trails and good buldering rocks to climb, just outside our doorstep. We live in what we call paradise :)

We bought our farm 2 year ago. The condition of the farm was pretty bad, so the last year have been mostly renovation of the barn, getting up fences, making/planning beds and paths, and getting to known the landscape. We have planted a bunch of trees and bushes for wind protection and more fertile soil. We also  got chickens and  "old tradional viking sheep", that graze around the farm. To sum it up, we have done all to get the infrastructure of a regenerative/ permaculture farm up and going.

We have different dreams on the farm:
First is to make a forest garden, with a diversity of flowers, trees, bushes etc, that could provide health for nature and healthy food for ourselves and others.
