Secondly, we want to make a permaculture garden, with vegetable production, and chicken and sheep that provide manure, egg and meat.

Thirdly we have today 14 acres of grass production that are used for winter food for the sheep. We want to transform this production into a regenerative practice, with high biodiversity of flowers and plants, that would be better for soil, wildlife and the sheeps :)

Then lastly, we want to create a place for learning and education. Therefore, I have made a carpentry workshop, and a little library with a diversity of books, and some instruments in the barn.

name: Flavio - Sítio auto sustentável, com criação de animais, produção de frutas e hortaliças
description:
Olá wwoofers, me chamo Flávio, tenho 46 anos moramos e sobrevivemos  no sítio,  com minha esposa Cléa e nosso filho Davi, temos alguns bichos de estimação,  entre eles duas cadelas e uma mula.  As atividades desenvolvidas no sítio  é totalmente familiar. Nosso projeto é voltado para a produção agroecológica, com base na economia circular, onde nada se perde e tudo se aproveita, levamos uma vida muito simples e aproveitamos tudo o que temos e preservamos ao máximo nossos recursos naturais, garantindo os ciclos da vida, respeitando sempre a mãe natureza, dessa forma tornando nosso recanto, um organismo agrícola sustentável e integrado.

Num organismo agroecológico integrado e sustentável atividade é o que não falta. Temos criação de ovinos, aves, minhocas e apiário com abelhas com ferrão e sem ferrão e muita disposição para poder aprender e partilhar conhecimento.
Nosso dia sempre inicia com os cuidados com os animais, montando os piquetes de pastagem e limpando o aprisco, alimentando o biodigestor e as minhocas, a sequência das atividades,  depende muito da demanda e do nosso planejamento diário, 
Mas temos uma horta, que a base de nossa alimentação, onde todos os dias capinamos, plantamos, irrigamos e colhemos.

Em nossa casa temos cômodos confortáveis sobrando, com cama e armários, fazemos sempre 3 refeições ao dia fora os lanches constantes.

Temos um estilo de vida bem pacata, gosto de violão, ouvimos varios estilos de músicas, somos esportistas jogamos futebol, vôlei e futevôlei, fazemos corrida ao ar livre durante a noite após os trabalhos e nos finais de semanas temos como opções varios parque aquático para banho e natação ao nosso redor, serão sempre bem vindos em nosso recanto.

Hola, wwoofers, me llamo Flávio, tengo 46 años y vivimos y sobrevivimos en el sitio con mi esposa Cléa y nuestro hijo Davi. Tenemos dos perros y una mula como mascotas. Las actividades que realizamos en el sítio son totalmente familiares. Nuestro proyecto se centra en la producción a
_wwoof_travel: Aeroporto Santa Maria em Aracaju/Se 60km e rodoviária de salgado/Se a 4 km

name: Paulette - Wild Country Gardens is a Farm/Orchard with Vegetables and Fruit.
description:
Our gardens are planted and starting to grow , the orchard is blooming and summer is coming., this is our favourite time of year and we would like to share it with you. 
As a WOOFer you will take part in most aspects of our farming operations, along with daily chores and activities alongside us.  There is always a variety of things to do on our farm, such as; planting, picking, producing products, packaging or maintenance.  
In our busy season we are picking fruit, vegetables and producing products for sale, so its a great time to visit our farm.   
We are able to teach you:	
Freeze Drying process
Making products like jams, syrups and much more
Yard and Orchard maintenance	
Farmer Markets	
Marketing skills	
Things we produce are: jams, jellies, peppers jellies, salsas, syrups, juices, sauerkraut, herbal teas, herbs and freeze dried foods etc.  You can participate in helping with the production, packaging and labeling of all our products. New is our Freeze Dried Candy, Snack and Foods business,  There is lots of fun things to do, our WWOOFer favorite is Freeze Drying Ice Cream as we have up to 10 flavors of Ice Cream in stock, used to make the Freeze Dried Ice Cream.  
You will need to have clothes suitable for working on the farm both very warm weather and cooler days.  We will supply any tools, gloves and bug sprays etc. 
A big part of our lives are our three dogs (Miley, Issie and Maddy) and our cat Journey, the dogs love to go on daily walks through the forest and like as much attention as they can get.
If you choose to come to our farm we ask that you fit yourselves into our schedule, learn to do the daily tasks required for the day and participate in as much as possible.  We ask that you become a part of our family and that you accept our customs and traditions.  
Projects that we need help with:
-picking fruit
-cleaning and freezing fruit
-making jams & syrups, salsas, sauerkraut etc
-freeze drying and packaging foods
-weeding and maintenance of orchard and ga
_wwoof_travel: Edson or Mayerthorpe  traveling by Bus and Edmonton for traveling  by Airplane

name: Jamie - Beautiful 1700-acre sheep ranch and vegetable garden near McCall, Idaho
description:
We are four people here on the Ranch. David s a cattle rancher; Jamie is a neuroscience writer; Leo is our property manager and cattle rancher; and his wife Fer. David and Jamie come and go so you will primarily be working with us when we are around or Leo during your stay. We have 3 lovable house dogs and one outdoor sheep dog, Bear, chickens, horses, sheep, cows and a donkey and a pig. A happy family by all accounts.

We have a flock of around 200 sheep plus all the new lambs. We need help looking after them, feeding/watering, looking out for anyone in distress. We could use help building new fences in the fields for the sheep!

Our primary need for help in the spring, summer and fall will be in the vegetable garden and harvesting/maintenance of the raspberries. We participate in the local Farmer's Market every week from May to October. We also have a lot of building projects underway so handy people are welcome. 

It would be ideal if you had your own means of transportation since this is a rural area (around two hours outside of Boise.) We are happy to arrange a pickup location if you do not have a vehicle. We have bicycles available for use, and we are about four miles outside the hub of Council. The town is about three blocks long and it boasts a coffee shop, thrift store, small grocery and a lovely herb shop. Starkey Hot Springs is a 10-minute drive from the ranch, and McCall is a sweet mountain town about 45 minutes drive with weekly farmer's markets. There are plenty of hot springs in the area and national forests to go hiking and mushroom hunting. There is a reservoir nearby which will be perfect for swimming in summer or taking the kayak out. The ranch is on three miles of the Weiser River so there could be fishing and swimming here too.

Your accommodation will be your choice of one of two campers by the river (about 2-3 min walk to main house), or a cabin next to our house. There is also a rustic cabin up the hill (5 min walk to main house) with stunnin

name: Curt - Horse Ranch - Breeding registered stock, training and sales
description:
We are a family run ranch, breeding, training and selling Quarter horses. We have been doing this for over 15 years. We are located above the beautiful Peace River. At any given time we have around 50 head of horses to care for. Mares, stallions and our many young horses. We apply natural horsemanship training methods.  Spring time is a very busy time for us. We foal out around 15-20 mares every year. We are very hands on with our horses, espically our mares during this time of year. We monitor them night and day. I think its everyones favorite time of year, to see all the beautiful babies running around. After foaling season we start breeding. For breeding season, we do live cover, or A.I. Along with the horses we also put up hay. Usually over 700 acres of hay a year. Haying usually starts mid June for us, and goes till we're done. By the end of July we are normally finished haying. All the hay we produce is organic, no spraying.We are looking for someone to help us out durring foaling season, breeding season, monitoring mares and helping with the foals and chores. Other areas of help might include - working with other horses, riding, haying... and other genral duties that come with being on a horse ranch. Experience with horses is definitely a plus but not an asset if you are someone who wants to learn! Please contact us if you want to get to experience helping out on a working horse ranch.Due to our lifestyle and the choices we make, someone who is vegan will find our home to not be the right fit. We eat meat, and also enjoy hunting. While we apprecate the interest, hosting someone who has made these lifestyle/dietary choices  won’t fit well. Thank you. 

Organic methods: We don't use weed herbicides,chemicals, or fertilizers. Hay production is left natural.

We are currently in the process of becoming certified organic.

Certifications: working on becoming certified

Current projects: Hello,

On our family run ranch we have lots going on. We are looking for
_wwoof_travel: Located 21 km west of Hines Creek, AB  , location number 63076 twp 834 Please contact us for directions.

name: Dieter - Family home and garden - rural
description:
We are both in our late 60s and early70s, early retirement from forest industry in order to enjoy off grid living on 40 acres (16 ha)and starting a trial permaculture small fruit orchard. 
We now have a very nice u-pick berry orchard with, Haskap, Saskatoon berries, Sour Cherries, Josta berries, Aronia, Chokecherries, Seabuckthorn and Purple Raspberries among others 

We live in a 32' yurt with a strawbale above ground basement. Our electricity is provided by solar and wind power. We live in an area of fairly extreme conditions, near minus 40 in winter and up to +35 in summer, frost occurs some years every month. A challange to grow anything with great success, but when living outside a box makes any challenge a welcome project, if it is not somewhat of a challenge, it is hardly worth doing.

This year's projects include planting some more haskap, area has already been prepared by pigs and will get a final rake over from our chickens. planting garden and greenhouses, one is a star dome, building a chord wood chicken hotel for our working laying hens, chipping/mulching small trees for our mulch that will be used for our row covers, starting another orchard area with a couple of loaned pigs using wooden pallets for containment, dismantling pallets for other projects including firewood for our rocket stoves. 

Also on project list is converting a 15 ha wilderness on a small lake just 3.5 km east from us into a permaculture showcase. 

Presently we have a converted school bus with cooking facility if inclined to do some of your own cooking, we can supply food, has a small propane camper fridge. We also have a small wall tent with wood stove for someone who wants to have a more rustic living quarters. A 28' long motorhome round out our wwoofer accommodations. 

We have also 2 small ponds for garden, orchard and cooling off. This is a great place for someone who enjoys walking barefoot. For this coming season we require 2 wwoofers starting in the 1st week of April, wwoofi

name: Anna - Mixed Farm, Farm
description:
We are now looking for extra help in June  its really good change to try something new and see something new we are in the north of Iceland with really beautiful landscape and few kilometres away from the farm is a really nice natural hotpot! 

 We are six in the family, parents and 4 grown up kids, that live at the farm and not and some grandchildren. we have 31 milking cows (no robot),we have an old cowhouse and the cows are in a stall. We are not 100% organically certified farm. We need help with the daily work like milking and feeding the cows and cleaning.  and some more extra farm work someday that have to do. the cows are inside over the winter and until the grass will start to grow and the weather get warmer and they can stay outside over the day that is around June beginning that they go outside and until around August/September we start to have them inside again because its so cold over the winter in Iceland and not good for the milking cows to stay outside then. 

Organic methods: we are looking for wwoofers that could help us with the milking feeding and cleaning and taking care of the cows  with us. the wwoofers will never work alone. you dont have to experience of farmwork you only have to be interested to learn and help :) 

Certifications: ..

Current projects: milking and feeding the cows and always some more extra farm work someday that have to do. and sometimes some house work.

Internet access available

We eat a lot of meat from our animals in almost every meal so it would be best if the woofer also eat meat 🥩 😊
_wwoof_travel: Near Varmahlíð and not so long way from Akureyri

name: Marylynn - Market Garden Farm in Beautiful Haida Gwaii!
description:
Our farm is located on 25 acres of ocean front property on Haida Gwaii surrounded by a beautiful forest. We are a hobby farm with chickens, ducks, goats and very soon baby geese!   We can host up to two couples at a time so please feel free to inquire even if our availability shows planned visit.  We really enjoy meeting people from all over the world and accept guests all year round. 
We have immediate room for all of May.

We provide a private room and healthy meals in exchange for help on our farm and the chance to meet new friends! We sell eggs at the farmers markets and make goat cheese and yogurt to eat and to trade for fish, deer, honey, and vegetables from other farms.   We also  make soap with goats milk and are working toward self sufficiency and grow as much food as we can in the 2 gardens, and green house. 
We make sausage, sourdough and other good foods to share and sell.

Being so isolated we're affected by storms and ferry schedule changes which affect the quality and availability of produce, so we produce/harvest  eggs, vegetables, berries, jams, preserves, wines, herbs, and goat/pork/poultry/venison/salmon. There is a large garden that we fenced and plant with vegetables and berries for us and our animals.   We also have 10 fruit trees that we planted 10 years ago, looking forward to increased apple/cherry/plum harvests to come!   Some years we foster baby deer that lose their mothers and we bottle feed them when the SPCA or Conservation asks us to help.  

Living right between the ocean and forest we also dip net crab and riverside fish for salmon.  Hunting for deer is also an excellent food source for us if you are wanting to learn.

Wwoof guests are welcome to work on the farm doing jobs that can be done independently or they can choose days as their hiking and touring the island days, it's up to you and depends if you like to work alone or along side us. If you are flexible and are coming to learn or help or just see Haida Gwaii we'll do our bes

name: Mark - Small-scale farm with organically grown vegetables, a raw milk dairy, chickens & greenhouse nursery
description: We grow seasonal vegetable staples (asparagus, beets, squash, sweet potatoes, peppers, tomatoes, onions…), herbs and flowers for sale at our farmstand located on the property. We also operate a herd-share raw milk dairy comprised of A2/A2 Jersey cows; we currently have over forty members who receive a share of the milk as well as feta cheese, ricotta and Greek yogurt. We also have a chicken yard and supply eggs at the farm-stand. We have a nursery license and raise and sell plants in season. Farm includes a large high tunnel and a heated greenhouse.

name: Francisco - Proyecto agroecológico integral sostenible
description:
Aloha! amigos resido en el lago Peten Itzá, tengo 35 años me gusta viajar por el mundo conocer nuevas culturas, el proyecto de conservación de los bosques tropicales mayas y agroturismo con cacao, especies forestales y apicultura.
Observación de flora y fauna en la selva, conocer las especies de plantas medicinales, recolección de semillas para reproducción en vivero. Te alojaras en cuartos cómodos con cama y hamaca, baños con agua potable, energía eléctrica. Son 3 tiempos al día mas 2 refacciones. se dispone cocina con estufa de gas o leña.
se practica senderismo al mirador con vista al lago.
ejercicio en bicicleta MTB
conciertos de música
comida típica de la región como tamales, dulces y frutas.
río para pescar

La agricultura sostenible es importante para mí porque representa una forma responsable de producir alimentos y recursos naturales siendo amigables con los ecosistemas generando más  oportunidades para las futuras generaciones. Al haber crecido en Petén, Guatemala, una región reconocida por su riqueza de biodiversidad, desarrollé desde temprana edad un profundo respeto por la naturaleza y la necesidad de protegerla. Considero que las prácticas sostenibles permiten conservar los suelos, el agua, la flora y la fauna, al mismo tiempo que fortalecen las comunidades rurales y mejoran la calidad de vida de las personas que dependen de la agricultura.
Además, mi experiencia laboral en cultivos tropicales, flores y follajes me ha permitido comprender la importancia de implementar buenas prácticas agrícolas, el uso eficiente de los recursos y el cumplimiento de estándares ambientales de Rainforest Alliance. Por ello, estoy interesado en seguir aprendiendo métodos que contribuyan a la regeneración de los ecosistemas y a una producción más sostenible.
 Los voluntarios de WWOOF pueden aprender diferentes prácticas relacionadas con la agricultura sostenible y la conservación del medio ambiente, incluyendo la regeneración y protección de bosques, la restauración de hábi

name: Mamadou - Family farm, Community farm
description:
Notre objectif est de regrouper des jeunes dans une activité agricole. Pour le moment c'est ma famille qui a commencé le maraîchage. Après l'hivernage nous faisons des maraîchages pour planter les légumes. Nous cultivons les choux, la salade, la patate douce, les oignons ... Nous avons aussi une grande plantation d'acajou et nous vendons les fruits, mais nous avons aussi des plantations de manguiers, de bananiers et d'orangers Pendent l'hivernage nous cultivons le riz, le mil, les arachides, le sorgho, le soja. Nous avons créé un petit barrage contre la salinité mais qui commence à se dégrader.Nous pourrons héberger des woofers en famille pour l ' échange culturel avec les membres de la famille. Contacter moi par l'intermédiaire de mon email.

Organic methods: biological methods used on m'y property

Certifications: Faroto

Current projects: Récolte d'arachides pour la commercialisation,plantation d'acajou dans la ferme de manguiers d'orangers et aussi assister la jeunesse à la création de salles de classe pour mieux apprendre à l'école.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: La ferme se trouve dans un petit village à sédhiou en casamance prés du fleuve

name: Kjetil - Homestead
description:
+++ In need of help in hay-season 2026 (July / August). Here it is done with horse and manual labour. Intense and fun. +++

Øyane is a horse driven small scale farm focusing on self-sufficiency. Here is a vegetable garden, a small greenhouse, 9 sheep (with lambs from spring), one adult horse and a two year old (in training), 12 chickens and three cats. Me, Kjetil, lives here, and have been living here since 2019. What needs to be done depends on the season: maintaining the forest which gives timber and firewood, preparing the vegetable garden in spring, cutting the sheeps wool, maintaining vegetable garden, haymaking (biggest task of the year), fishing, renovating buildings, collecting the sheep in the mountains late summer or making construction materials on the sawmill. I also do handcrafts such as spinning and knitting wool from our own sheep, tanning skins, blacksmithing, making homebrew, woodwork, conserving berries and herbs. I do eat and sometimes slaughter animals. 
I try to use old and sustainable technics and methods, both in farming, in the forest and in handcraft, but still power-tools, welding machine and chainsaw is used when needed. It`s neccesary to be able to work and think both independently and collectively here, but I am patient and eager to teach and explain what needs to be done, if needed. If you are interested in a specific task or craft; please let me know! Here are no fixed working-hours, we do what is needed to be done, and every day is different. I am  not an expert but believe in learning by doing - sharing knowledge, experience and respect. This is a small mountain village, with lots of beautiful places to experience.

Organic methods: No chemicals - regenerative horse driven practice 

Certifications: No chemicals - no certification - horse driven small scale farming

Current projects: Forestwork. Making firewood. Renovating the house both inside and outside.  Gardening.

Internet access available

name: Emiliano - Bamboo, organic agroforestry, family  guinea pig farm.
description:
Welcome to El Arriendo Farm!
El Arriendo is a small family farm located in the high jungle of Cusco, Peru, in the rural community of ChilcaChaca, surrounded by mountains, rivers, coffee fields, bamboo groves, and tropical forest.
We are not a retreat center or a polished eco-project. We are a family that works, learns, makes mistakes, and slowly builds a way of life connected to the land, community, and nature. If you are looking for an authentic experience of rural life, cultural exchange, and meaningful work, you may feel at home here.
Life on the farm follows the rhythms of nature. We start early in the morning before the tropical sun becomes too strong, and our days revolve around farming, animal care, cooking, maintenance, and shared meals.
WHO WE ARE
Our family includes different generations and life experiences, including an elderly family member and my brother Avelino, who lives with an intellectual disability. Because of this, we especially value volunteers who are patient, empathetic, and comfortable sharing daily life in a diverse family environment.
Most of us speak Spanish, although some family members have a basic to intermediate level of English. Daily life on the farm offers many opportunities for cultural exchange, language practice, and mutual learning.
One of the things we value most about hosting volunteers is the opportunity to meet people from different cultures and walks of life. Good conversations, shared experiences, stories, music, and genuine human connections are just as valuable to us as the work we do together.
Volunteers become part of the daily rhythm of the farm. Everyone helps with cooking, cleaning, caring for animals, maintaining shared spaces, and the many tasks that rural life requires.
FARM LIFE
Our farm combines coffee, bamboo, vegetable production, guinea pigs, forage grasses, composting, and tree nurseries. We try to connect these activities so that each one supports the others and helps strengthen the farm over time.
Bamboo

name: Adrian - Organic Farm with beef, crops, fruit trees and hazlenut
description:
Hey there! I'm Adrian, a young farmer deeply passionate about soil biology and regenerative farming practices. I offer various courses on composting, compost teas, and permaculture techniques right here on the farm. In addition, I conduct small-scale scientific trials to enhance plant health using homemade biostimulants, and I've got some top-notch measurement tools to aid in the process.

Please Note:
We only host WWOOFers who can stay for at least one month, as it takes time to properly explain the tasks and ensure that both sides benefit from the experience.

We also host only one WWOOFer at a time, so please check in advance if your requested period is available.
In rare cases, we do accept couples – but only if at least one person has prior farm experience, can work independently, and ideally brings some technical skills.

Over the past few years, I've dedicated myself to planting numerous trees and establishing a flourishing hazelnut orchard. Among the highlights of my farm are my 15 lovely mother cows, which I graze according to the principles of mob grazing.

My wife, a friend, and I call this picturesque farm home. Nestled right in front of the majestic Alps, our hillside location offers stunning evening sunsets. When I'm not tending to the farm, you'll often find me indulging in a refreshing swim in the nearby Lake Sempachersee or venturing out for invigorating hikes or mountain biking adventures

At the farm, our activities are wonderfully diverse and, naturally, vary with the seasons. Each day typically begins with tending to the cows and preparing a fresh patch of field for grazing. Our self-sufficient vegetable garden demands daily attention, ensuring its thriving growth. We take pleasure in preparing homemade delights like pesto or chutneys, adding a flavorful touch to our meals.

We're equally dedicated to maintaining the hazelnut orchard, regularly mulching and nurturing the trees to promote their health and yield. Additionally, there are occasional

name: Lars-Göran - Hobby Farm, with pigs, vegetables, a large garden, fruit trees and original buildings 1830-1880.
description:
Skogsta is a little farm breeding the old  pig-breed, named Linderodgrisar. It is a slow growing pig which gives very good meat, and spend most of their time outdoor, the year around. On the farm there is also a small-scale growing of vegetables that has potential to expand.

It is a 19th century farm with old buildings and the house was built 1878. Everything is taken care of with the purpose to keep the old style and feeling. 

My name is Lars-Göran and I have lived and loved this farm for a little more than 15 years now. slowly but surely bringing back the place into its old style and glory. This is also in made in combination with breeding ecological pigs and  taking care of the old garden. 

Renovating or taking care of those buildings is also a part of the life in Skogsta as well as the cultural exchange. 

Located nearby the city Vasteras, but in a truly rural landscape. 

Organic methods: Pigs eat only ecologically grown food. 

Certifications: I have a very small farm and I have neither had the financial resources nor the time to undergo a certification. It is in the plan though. 

In the house there is also a small BnB, where serving traditional Swedish farm-breakfast is one of the tasks. However, this is only for information as wwoofers are not allowed to participate in businesses like BnB, Tasks such as mking breakfast for guests, cleaning, making beds are handled by the host.

Current projects: Restoration and fixing  the old barn and  some other buildings It can be painting or simpler tasks.  These kinds of work is also made in accordance with sustainable and historical methods.  There is always some work with fences  and the sheds for the pigs as well as other things regarding the pigs.

Taking care of and developing the old garden with style from 1880

A typical day, starts with feeding the pigs in the morning, checking fences and makeing sure they have a comfortable and happy situation. Then it may vary from day to day depending on weather and what
_wwoof_travel: Västerås, and also Dingtuna and Kolbäck (Bus and local Train). I can arrange pickup at Västerås train-station, Dingtuna or Kolbäck. Just let me know

name: Claudie - Ferme familiale, maraîchère biologique et laitière conventionnelle.
description:
***Description en français plus bas.***
Our piece of paradise is located a few kilometers from the little town of Amos in northern Quebec.  We are a little crazy francophone family full of energy and motivated to promote organic, environmentally friendly and sustainable agriculture on our 500 acres of land. 
Having traveled and met many wonderful people, we want to continue to embrace the world through wwoofing and share our simple way of life. Surrounded by lakes and rivers; swimming, camping and fishing can all be part of the experience "Farm Espo'Art".
With 4 children aged from five to fourteen years old, 30 dairy cows, about thirty varieties of vegetables, a greenhouse for tomatoes and cucumbers, 2 caterpillar tunnels for sensitive vegetable, 80 hens, few rabbits, a dog and cats combine with a self-serve kiosk and one public market per week, the tasks are even more diversified than the number of vegetables we are growing.
Through sowing, planting, tillage, carpentry, mechanics, construction, landscaping, feeding the animals, milking, brushing or cleaning them, we will always try to provide you with tasks that motivate you and fit your skills.

On the farm, meals are usually eaten together and it’s a real family life that we can offer you.  For your sleep, we have 5 options depending on the lenght of your stay and the availability from other wwoofers: 
1. you bring your own camper, tent, trailer, vans or other ''homes'' of your possession ...
2. We have a bunk bed in the living room.
3. We have a trailer tent with electricity, gaz and fridge close to the house.
4. For an authentic experience, we also have a small fishing hut with wood stove and solar panel good for 1 person. 
5. We can also lend you a room of one of the children, if it's not more than 2 weeks. 

In any case, you will still have access to all the amenities of the house.Your experience will be a great way to adopt a simple lifestyle, experience family life and participate in the improvements of a s
_wwoof_travel: À peu près n'importe où en Abitibi, idéalement Amos ou Val-d'Or. / Almost everywhere in Abitibi, but the best will be close to Amos or Val-d'Or.

name: Isabela - Quinta com produção de refeições, queijo e derivados
description:
Olá WWOOFers, meu nome é Isabela sou bióloga e junto do meu companheiro Marcos e meu filho Artur temos aqui a atividade de fornecimento de refeições, queijo e derivados do leite. As refeições possuem alimentos colhidos aqui, temos uma horta orgânica. Acordamos cedo para tirar leite das vacas e produzir queijos, iogurtes e outros produtos a base de leite. As atividades aqui estarão voltadas para a horta orgânica, e envolve a limpeza dos canteiros, preparação da terra com adubo orgânico oriundo dos animais daqui, plantio respeitando a época e utilizando a consorciação de espécies vegetais. Temos muito a ensinar para o anfitrião e estamos ansiosos para a troca de experiência.

Nossa rotina diária começa as 6 da manhã com nossas amigas leiteiras que generosamente nos fornecem leite para a fabricação dos queijos e derivados do leite, depois passamos uma parte da manhã em nossa horta orgânica e colhemos alimentos frescos para fazer as refeições. O anfitrião poderá escolher se nos ajudará na parte da manhã ou na parte da tarde, nas atividades por 4 horas.

Nossa casa é simples, mas bem acolhedora. O anfitrião terá um quarto para repousar e são oferecidas 03 alimentações diárias.

Estamos localizados bem próximo do centro da cidade, gostamos de atividades junto da natureza.

name: Junhyuk - <GG138>다양한 농법을 실험하는 자연농장/A natural farm exploring diverse farming techniques
description:
✅ 호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
김포 한강변에 자리한 작은 농장으로, 퍼머컬처와 자연농 철학을 바탕으로 농약과 화학비료 없이 경운하지 않고 다양한 작물을 재배합니다. ‘오공’은 아내의 오랜 반려견 이름에서 따온 이름이자 ‘하지 않는 다섯 가지(5-zero)’의 철학을 담고 있습니다. 환경오염과 농업의 악순환을 일으키는 농약과 화학비료를 거부하고, 작물 본연의 속도와 자연의 리듬에 맞춘 농사를 짓습니다. 비록 수확량이 많진 않지만 작물의 맛과 품질은 더욱 뛰어납니다.
땅을 갈지 않는 ‘노디그(No-dig)’ 방식은 토양 속 미생물과 탄소를 보호하며 기후변화 완화에도 도움이 됩니다. 영국에서 우연히 접한 책을 통해 알게 된 이 방식은, 많은 유럽 유기농 농장에서 이미 실천 중이었고, 저희도 이를 따르고 있습니다. 일부 땅은 의도적으로 쉬게 두며, 땅의 지력을 회복시키는 데 집중하고 있습니다.
또한 단일작물이 아닌 다양한 작물을 함께 심어 생물다양성을 높이려 노력합니다. 영국 헤레포드 지역에서 배운 전통 애플사이다 제조 방식은 다양한 사과 품종과 건강한 생태계가 어우러져야만 가능했으며, 이 경험은 농장 운영에도 큰 영향을 주었습니다. 다년생 식물을 함께 키우고, 미생물과 식물이 조화를 이루는 농장을 지향합니다.
저희는 유통을 거치지 않고 오직 직거래만을 고집합니다. 서울에서 열리는 마르쉐 시장 같은 장터에서 소비자와 직접 만나거나, 농장을 개방해 방문을 유도하려 합니다. 이는 포장 쓰레기를 줄이고, 생산지에 대한 신뢰를 높이며, 사람 간의 연결을 회복하기 위한 방식입니다. 앞으로는 농장에서 팜투테이블 요리와 애플사이다를 선보이며, 소비자가 머무르고 체험할 수 있는 공간도 운영할 예정입니다.
저는 서울에서 자라 식품공학을 전공하고 식품회사에 다녔지만, 이윤 중심의 삶에 염증을 느끼고 어떻게 하면 좋은 아빠가 될 수 있을지를 고민하다 아내와 함께 영국에서 새로운 삶을 배웠습니다. 귀국 후 김포의 밭을 삽으로 일구며 농장을 시작했고, 지금도 장기적인 시선으로 천천히 농장을 가꾸어가고 있습니다.

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2024년
✅ 농장 형태 : 소농
✅ 농장 주작물 : 제철 밭작물 및 다년생(허브류) 중심

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 :  농장은 크게 자연농법의 밭, 퍼머컬처 길드 개념을 따르는 숲밭, 영국 노디그(no dig) 농법의 틀밭, 가장자리(edge)를 많이 확보할 수 있는 원형밭 등 다양한 요소를 보유하고 있습니다. 400평 규모에서 시설재배는 단 6평에 불과하며, 대부분 노지재배 입니다. 재배방식은 다소 무투입에 가깝습니다. 두둑에 축분을 바로 뿌리는 행위는 지양하고 있습니다. 가급적 유기자재축분을 구하여 질소질로 활용하고 버섯폐배지나 왕겨, 폐한약재 찌꺼기를 탄소질로 활용하여 만든 자가퇴비를 3개월에서 6개월 숙성하여 밑거름으로 사용하고 있습니다.  

✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 :가족이 음식물쓰레기를 (가급적 채소서더리를 중심으로) 수거하여 곳곳의 미니키홀에서 부숙하여 자연순환하고 있습니다. 집에서 오줌을 받아 3개월 부숙하여 액비로 활용하고 있습니다.  자연농법을 실천하는 고정두둑에는 풀을 함께 키우고 있으며, 작물 주변 풀을 톱낫으로 베고 덮어두어 다시 양분이되게끔 유도하는 방식도 적극 활용중입니다. 기본적으로 혼작을 지향하고 있습니다. 당근-바질-토마토, 양파-셀러리, 마늘-비트, 완두-상추, 당근-쑥갓 등 서로 도움이 될 수 있는 작물을 함께 심고 있습니다.

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 아내
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려 동물 : 없음
✅ 우퍼가 하는일 :
-3월 : 봄 작물 파종 및 부직포(폴리스) 덮어주기, 초봄 나물 수확 
-4월 : 봄 작물 파종 및 정식, 나무심기, 다년생 작물 심기, 멀칭보완 (볏짚 덮어주거나 걷어내기), 초봄 나물 수확
-5월 : 작물관리, 완두/열무/아욱/엽채류 등 수확
-6월 : 여름작물(가지, 고추, 토마토, 오이 등) 정식, 주요작물 (감자, 양파, 마늘 등) 수확
-7월 : (새벽타임/늦은오후타임 중 택1) 낫으로 예초작업, 물주기, 가

name: Marco - Nuova azienda agricola a 300 mt dal mare di Costa rei in Sardegna
description:
Ciao, mi chiamo Marco e con la mia famiglia gestiamo 2 Hotel a Costa Rei, una splendida e rinomata località turistica nella Sardegna sud orientale, caratterizzata da una splendida spiaggia bianca e un mare tra i più belli al mondo, ma anche da una splendido territorio, ancora in parte selvaggio, caratterizzato da lagune, colline ed una riserva naturalistica nelle montagne retrostanti. Qualche anno fa abbiamo deciso di acquistare uno splendido appezzamento di terra di 6 ettari, , situato a 200 metri dal mare, ai piedi del vecchio Vulcano di Monte ferru, nella località Capo Ferrato, per dare vita al nostro sogno di impiantare una azienda agricola. 
Il terreno si trova in una zona di mezzo, a pochi minuti di auto dalla famosa località di Costa rei ed all'inizio di una splendida zona selvaggia, ricca di lagune a ridosso di spiagge deserte, in uno scenario per noi magico e ricco di energia. 
La famiglia stretta è composta da mia moglie e mio figlio, intestatario dell'azienda, quella allargata dai miei 2 fratelli e relative famiglie. 
La nostra grande ricchezza è data dal grande numero di relazioni preziose che manteniamo con tantissime persone, locali, del resto d'Italia e di altri paesi, che seguono il nostro progetto con interesse e che in varia misura partecipano alle tante attività che stiamo portando avanti e che abbiamo in previsione.
Siamo una famiglia che si è allargata per la voglia di conoscere e di conoscersi.
Amiamo vivere in serenità, gioire delle piccole cose. Amiamo la discrezione, le cose semplici e di qualità. Ci piace molto rispettare gli altri ed essere rispettati. Amiamo trattare gli altri come persone di casa: e noi a casa non facciamo complimenti. Per cui nessuno si aspetti tappeti rossi, ma si senta a casa, sicuro di trovare cordialità e sorrisi sinceri.
Ci piace conoscere, ci piace la nostra cultura e quella degli altri, amiamo viaggiare e accogliere viaggiatori, ci piacciono le relazioni di qualità, ci piace crescere imparando ad apprezzare e con

name: David - Farm/Homestead in the Finger Lakes region of upstate NY with ponds, meadows, forests, & streams
description:
–“You teach me, I forget. You show me, I remember. You involve me, I understand.” – E.O. Wilson

My wife, Maria, are continuing to rejuvenate our farm/homestead in the eastern Finger Lakes region of upstate New York. Your stay will be in a lovely house along a quiet, rural road in Marcellus, where there are hundreds of birds on 23 acres with ponds, meadows, forest, and a trout stream. We host guest workers for two to three weeks, depending on the season, with spring and fall our busiest times. 

Our chief project for the past six years has been clearing invasive shrubs and planting 3,000 young trees. It's a big, decades-long experiment: Building a diverse habitat that’s surrounded by an ocean of invasives and overun with deer. If successful, this tiny patch of vibrant acreage will help enrich the environment and offset climate change factors. 

 The next five-to-ten years will tell us a lot. We do a lot of young tree maintenance: repositioning tree cages, clearing weeds, vines and berry bushes, removing dead trees, and more. We've had volunteers from Japan, Hungary, Chile, South Africa, Kenya, Australia, India, Italy, France, Germany and the States. A New York State forester has overseen every step of the project. 

Other planned tasks this year include building an outhouse for our cabin by the stream and gates for our fenced-in garden, painting some exterior walls and possibly some indoor rooms. Additional jobs always pop up.

Our gardening involves flowers, vegetables and berries. We have a friendly, older cat. 

I am an artist, yoga teacher, journalist and an author of two biographies. Maria is a massage therapist with a beautiful, artistic side. I am a good cook, Maria is exceptional. We are both experienced travelers and have hosted for decades. We strive to see that our WWOOFers get to experience this beautiful part of the country in ways they enjoy, be it the local waterfalls, lakes, theaters and museums, thrift shops, whatever. 

We are a five to six hour bu

name: Jill - Small homestead property on .8 acres in West Michigan, near Lake Michigan, being transformed into a
description:
I’m Jill, and I live in West Michigan near Lake Michigan on a quiet .8-acre property that I’m slowly transforming into a permaculture food forest and regenerative home business. I work remotely full-time, but my real passion is creating a more connected, hands-on way of living centered around growing food, medicinal herbs, composting, creativity, and community.

The project is still in the building stage, which is part of what makes this opportunity unique. I’m looking for people who want to learn alongside me and help shape the space from the ground up. The vision includes fruit trees, vegetables, pollinator gardens, medicinal herbs, compost systems, landscape design, and the creation of products from what we grow and make.

The lifestyle here is fairly simple, quiet, and grounded. I enjoy gardening, baking, pottery, cooking, being outside, and creating meaningful spaces for people to gather and learn.

I share the home with my dog and two cats, so being comfortable around animals is important. The pets are very much part of the home and daily rhythm here.

This project is still in the early building and design phase, so the work is varied and constantly evolving. Typical activities include gardening, planting, mulching, composting, soil building, pruning, creating new garden beds, growing and drying medicinal herbs, food forest planning, light landscape and infrastructure projects, harvesting, and experimenting with products we can make and eventually sell from the property.

I’m especially interested in teaching permaculture thinking and design, how to observe land, work with natural systems, build healthy soil, grow food in layered, regenerative ways, and create spaces that are both productive and beautiful. I also want this to be a place for creativity, experimentation, and learning together rather than rigid farm labor.

An average day would vary depending on the season, weather, and projects underway. Since I work remotely during the day, WWOOFers often work

name: Laura - Family farm on 24 acres in gorgeous and vibrant foothills of Oregon Coast Range
description:
We are a small family, stewards of 24 acres in the foothills of the Oregon Coast Range. Philomath, Oregon is a small logging town with a vibrant small farm community, located 15 minutes from Corvallis, Oregon, home of Oregon State University. We are 45 minutes from the gorgeous Oregon Coast; surrounded in all directions by outdoor opportunities, including rivers, hiking trails, mountain biking, and wineries. We are a 10-minute bike ride from Philomath town center, and a 30-minute bike ride from Corvallis, with excellent bike paths connecting the towns. Our property connects to managed forests; hiking trails to the Marys River are outside our back gate. 
Our farm raises Nigerian Dwarf dairy goats, laying hens, honeybees, flowers, and produce, with an emphasis on preserving the harvest and sharing the land with the local wildlife and community. We also have two farm dogs, and two farm cats, and two livestock guardian dogs. 
Projects are constantly evolving on the farm. Daily tasks include but are not limited to: milking goats, cleaning barns, weeding, harvesting, and planting. From spring - early autumn we have a rotation of seeding in the greenhouse, hardening off, and planting. Pasture management includes mowing, weeding, seeding and moving fences. We manage 19 acres of forest with paths, moving goats through undergrowth, harvesting timber.  Other evolving projects include: mobile chicken coop; sauna; outdoor shower; outdoor kitchen. Springtime apiary chores include feeding, mite control, swarm management; honey harvest is late summer. Seasonal fiber chores include scouring, dyeing and spinning alpaca, wool & mohair. We have a weekly stand at the Philomath Farmers Market. WWOOFers can assist with harvest, prep, set up, booth management and closing. 
We have plenty of space to accommodate WWOOFers with RVs and tents. We also have a one bedroom loft above the garage with a full bath for a single or couple. 

Organic methods: Organic pesticides & fertilizer. Bio-dynami

name: Arne - Organic market garden
description:
Welcome to our old farm surrounded by quiet woods and lakes! We, Paula and Arne, aged around 40, have been living here since 2019. When we came, the farm had been uninhabited for about half a century so the first few years we focused on renovating the house, the outhouses and getting the basics up and running. In 2023 we started our commercial market garden and are now looking forward to our 4th season of producing high quality, organic vegetables which we sell directly to our customers in the nearest town, Torsby, as well as some other villages in the area.
We don’t have any animals yet, but are considering getting chickens, bees and sheep in the future, after we get the infrastructure, the knowledge and the money to get started.

The surroundings of our farm are very quiet, forests and lakes as far as the eye can see. The nearest permanent neighbours are 3 km away. The nearest bus stop is 10 km away and Torsby is 25 km away. Even though Torsby has only around 6000 inhabitants, it functions as a regional capital. There are several supermarkets, a hospital, several schools and churches, a swimming pool, small shops and big stores providing almost everything we need.
We don’t have a car. We commute by bike. One of our bikes is an electric cargo bike which we use to deliver fresh vegetables to our customers or to markets. It can transport about 100kg, so if you come by bus, we might decide to give you and your luggage a ride in the cargo bike. It depends on the circumstances. Otherwise, to get here, you will need to cycle with us either from Torsby or from the bus stop. Or walk. Or if you should choose to come by car…

We always have many projects on our farm and depending on the season, the weather, your interests and skills, we might ask you to help us with the following tasks:
preparing beds (adding manure, compost, mulch, aereation, edge maintenance)
weeding
picking slugs (we still don’t have any, but they will come)
pruning and clipping tomato and cucumber plants
_wwoof_travel: Torsby, bus to Mårbacken where we can pick you up by bike

name: Hanneke - Off the Grid wooded property  with ocean view on Read island.
description:
We are a Christian couple who live on a 40 acre forested property on an island which is boat access only. Our log cabin house is on the waterfront with ideal views of the other islands and mountains. There is quite a lot of wildlife that passes us on the water be it whales, ducks, seagulls, bald eagles and the passing traffic of boats and kayakers. The woods are ideal for bird watching and hiking.

The land is mostly undeveloped and we would like to develop parts of it. This includes starting new gardens (vegetable and flowers), making pathways, clearing areas for development, milling, building, carpentry, painting and general upkeep and maintenance of the existing cabin and surrounding buildings and vehicles. We are completely off grid and can teach WWOOFers about solar and battery systems, research into developing hydro-electric system for winter and providing water from our wells. As we are relatively new to this kind of living, we can learn together and have fun trying new things such as fishing, prawn catching and exploring some of the waterways around us. 
Breakfast is around 8:30 during which we will discuss the day’s activities and timings. This often depends on the weather. It could include planting, clearing paths and brush cutting, gathering and stacking firewood, carpentry, milling, maintenance on the buildings, maintenance on vehicles/machinery.
Lunch is around 1:00  and dinner around 6:00.

Mornings start with a breakfast consisting of cereal and some fruit, (toast and eggs) Tea/coffee
Lunch comprises cheese sandwiches and dinner is a cooked meal consisting of a starch (pasta/rice), meat (chicken/sausages, pork) and vegetables. WWOOFers can make their own breakfast and lunch (tea and coffee) and dinner will be eaten together.
Accommodation is a bedroom and lounge area in the basement of the house with a private bathroom. 
Wifi is available.

Hanneke is a trained musician and plays piano and violin. She also teaches recorder at the school and trains the

name: Jorge - Eco Aldea, sustainable, eco construction, bird farm, organic vegetables
description:
I'm a 70 year old retiree, living the dream our small community consist on 6 people Carina, Andres, Lupe, Erika, Angel, Alfonso 4 mascotas +

Feeding the birds and animals, compost, planting, building enjoying good food!

We have 6 cabins available, meals provided by Cari, Lupe and my self

Creating,  designing, learning technics to build, traveling, music, wood work
_wwoof_travel: Zacatlan bus station? Mexico City International Airport

name: Carlo - Destinazione Agrituristica, Fattoria di famiglia, Produzione ortaggi
description:
Siamo in agro di Monreale - Grisì (PA) alla C/da Cambuca, sulle colline che dominano il lago Poma e la Valle dello Jato. Per arrivare necessita una macchina. In alternativa, previo accordi, possiamo venire a prendervi alla stazione ferroviaria di Partinico o alla fermata dei bus di Partinico o di San Cipirello. Coltiviamo olivi, vigne e ortaggi di stagione. Tutte le coltivazioni sono praticate in regime di agricoltura biologica. Gli ortaggi e la frutta che raccogliamo vengono lavorati in azienda per la produzione di conserve e marmellate. Inoltre svolgiamo anche attività di ospitalità rurale. La collaborazione dei soci visitatori è richiesta principalmente per la raccolta dei prodotti agricoli ma anche per tutte le altre fasi della coltivazione o trasformazione degli stessi. Il lavoro è flessibile e può durare da poche ore a tutto il giorno in funzione dei programmi della azienda. I soci visitatori saranno seguiti nella generalità dei casi dai titolari e solo per alcune operazioni colturali potrebbero essere seguiti da un nostro operaio specializzato. I soci ospiti abitualmente condividono la casa dei proprietari e con essi si fa vita comune. Luoghi di interesse sono il parco archeologico del Monte Jato, le zone di mare e le città d'arte (Palermo, Monreale, Trapani). Inoltre è possibile percorrere sentieri naturalistici, e sul lago Poma, effettuare bird watching ed equitazione. Con i soci ospiti, desideriamo condividere l'amore per la natura, per la terra e per un sistema di vita organico. Condividiamo anche il tempo lavoro ed i giorni di libertà. Chi fosse interessato a venire per fare una vacanza può rivolgersi altrove. Per tutto il resto parliamone insieme.

******************************

Our farm is situated in Agro di Monreale (PA) in the Jato hills with the Pomo lake nearby. Reachable by bus from Palermo or train to Partinico station. We mainly cultivate olives, vines and seasonal vegetables. Everything is cultivated organically. The fruit and vegetables
_wwoof_travel: Stazione treno o bus di Partinico/Aeroporto di Palermo

name: Starlight - Regenerative oasis in the desert
description:
**We are once again accepting WWOOFers for Fall/Winter 2026 and Spring 2027!**

We are Starlight and Siin, owners of Rancho de la Libertad, a pioneering regenerative farmstead and rewilding project just north of Joshua Tree National Park. Rancho de la Libertad is just under 6 acres of slowly improving desertified grassland, decimated by the activities of the gold rush and settlers who came across this land in the last two centuries.  The Ranch is home to our family: us, our four year old daughter and our twin sons who are about two. Non-human residents currently include four rabbits, three pigs, and 20 chickens as well as our half-working dog, Oso and a plethora of returning wildlife. 

This project was inspired by our connection to this land, and our determination to engage in thoughtful land stewardship to restore its biodiversity and natural resources. We see its determination to return to a flourishing life of grass each time it rains. We are certain that with the thoughtful integration of animal, human, and plant symbiosis we can create the conditions necessary for it to do so.

Days here are variable this upcoming season: in the Fall waking will probably be around 6, with breakfast near 7am and the daily work of animal and garden tending occurring in the morning and again in the afternoon. Various infrastructure projects or more intensive management tasks such as animal breeding occur at different times, depending on the project. We tend to plan projects week-by-week, and have a running list of what needs to be done a few months in advance. Each Saturday we visit the Joshua Tree Farmer's Market, which is both our place to obtain our groceries as well as a social hub for the community. Sometimes Saturdays also entail food preservation, though this is seasonal and not always the case as most of the "extras" from vendor friends are now feeding our animals, and most of our harvest is eaten immediately or given away. 

Because Starlight works a day job in the morni
_wwoof_travel: Palm Springs Airport, 29 Palms Transit Center

name: Marcela - Homestead
description: We are a young couple  with a daughter (2016).  We do not have pets. The property  has  of two parts, one at the level of the river, where is located the camping, and another just above where the house and some trees, which is designed to place the organic garden projected. The accommodation is available in tents near the river, it  has restrooms, electricity, shower, etc. The wwoofers  can feed themselves, wash clothes and spend time at home with family.  Help expected in manual labor, which can be manufactured carpentry or at  camping, like cutting branches or taking out the trash. The campsite is near the village of  Diaguitas, 20 minutes walking. To arrive, the bus coming from La Serena leaves  you on the whereabouts of  Diaguitas  and there can call  us or send  us a WhatsApp for you to go for. We can send a map as well. Near the site there are hills for trekking very nice, also the astronomical observatory Mamalluca that you can visit. We are very happy to meet people from other cultures and to share and exchange  knowledgments.

name: Andi (Andrea) - Sustainability focused homestead near Great Lakes
description:
**2026** update: 

This season, we will only be able to host the months of April - August. We have some jobs taking us away from the Homestead quite a bit this season, so please notify us with as much advancement as possible if you want to get on our summer schedule :) 

the Homestead and the surrounding area:

Our homestead is surrounded by fresh water in northern lower Michigan: located between one Great Lake and two smaller inland lakes. The shores and harbors of these lakes support a handful of small towns, with the largest being fifteen miles/24 km away (Petoskey) and the closest approximately three miles/5km (Boyne City). These swell with tourists during the summers, which are admittedly beautiful, but the forgotten three seasons here do maintain beauty in their own ways. 

In 2019, we moved to this property + log cabin, with the intention of living as sustainable a lifestyle as we can manage. The majority of the property -- sixteen acres (six and a half hectares) -- is dense Beech-Maple forest, with two large clearings for growing on the north and south sides. We grow perennial and annual vegetables and fruits, but we also spend a lot of our time working on building projects, home improvement projects (old home), foraging mushrooms, trail maintenance, and processing wood for winter heat. We have intentions to eventually raise chickens and pigs, but all good things take time. 

Traveling here:

We will try to accommodate any wwoofers travel plans so that they can make it to us. We are within an hour of two airports: Traverse City and Pellston, MI, and would gladly make trips to these if necessary. There are also buses leaving from Detroit, Lansing and Grand Rapids that stop in nearby towns. 

Andi, Matt & Aster

Both of us, Andi and Matt, have planned for this project, this lifestyle well before we saw the property -- before we met each other, even. Andi managed large vegetable farms, small market-gardens, and labored within Michigan’s local food system for th
_wwoof_travel: Petoskey for bus, Traverse City for airport, Grand Rapids for train

name: SALVADOR - Educational Centre
description:
My name is Salvador, I am Mexican, my wife Jessica is from Spain and together we have 2 children, Cloe and Iker.
We left the city more than 15 years ago and started an environmental education center in a beautiful land on the shore of the largest natural lake in Mexico "Chapala"
in the place we apply permaculture and we are in search of self-sufficiency.
We are looking for help from a nice couple of volunteers who like nature and have an interest in permaculture, who are clean, hardworking and independent and like to talk to visitors.
We offer a nice, clean and independent place to sleep and everything you need to prepare your vegetarian meals in exchange for a minimum of 5 hours of work a day, 5 days a week.
The work is in several areas such as: care of the gardens, bioconstruction and help in the kitchen and cleaning during workshops and yoga retreats.
minimum stay 1 month!
Igloo Kokolo is in front of the lake surrounded by nature and offers an excellent opportunity to learn and enjoy nature and a simple life.

name: Elisha - Organic Airport
description:
My name is Elisha am 51 we do organic agriculture together with partners at my place.

We feed chickens in the morning then we collect manure for using it to the horticulture . Before engage in agriculture we provide skills to be used and also share some ideas on how we can do better in the project.
In extra time after farming we allow  visitors to engage in different activities such as teaching program we have classes for those who want to learn Swahili language as well as those who can teach English. Apart of that we have  children center children are taught variety skills known as curriculum Based Competency (CBC) 
By doing so we have seen people expose their talents and having enough room to share new experiences for both sides.

We provide the three meals like breakfast, lunch and dinner. We also have a special house for accommodation

My hobby is teaching  and heart to heart exchange. I also play musical instrument

name: Monica - Organic homestead on 2.25 acres surrounded by mountain views on private road
description:
Hi adventurous travelers, Welcome to my. neck of the woods, my name is Monica.  I live on a secluded, tucked away 2.25 acre  piece of land on a private road along a picturesque  view of the mountain to the east, only one neighbor to the south, the rest is open land with an apple orchard to the north.  I need help to organize the property that houses 40+ chickens of different colorful varieties, a turkey, a peking duck, 2 French guineas, a pair of Nigerian dwarf goats and a dove that adopted me a little more than a year ago.  The property is fenced but I still get  occasional wild turkeys or pheasant, maybe a rare visit from a deer, especially in the winter.  The property has fruit trees-apple, cherry, apricot, mulberry, pear, quince, current bushes, gooseberries,  almond tree and almond bushes, sea buckthorn trees, elderberry trees,  goji berry bushes, pecan tree, Siberian pea shrubs, edible bamboo, white and Concord grapes,  several garden beds, greenhouse, lots of beneficial herbs.  My goals are to live as self sustainably as possible.  My indoor pets include Diva, a beautiful long haired calico cat and Rio an adopted blue and gold macaw.

My morning tasks are of course feeding the animals and making sure they have water.  In the evening I make sure they are safely tucked in, they have electric gate door in one coop, but not the other ones.  There have been the tragic misfortunes of having raccoons or skunks think it was OK to have dinner here, but I trap them and rehome them in the mountains far, far away.  Some of the chickens are allowed to roam the property freely since my main purpose for having them is to eat grasshoppers and other pests.  You will be able to eat all the eggs you want to your hearts content.  I will need help maintaining the yard, organizing the barn and greenhouse and hopefully we can both learn together how to make some garden beds with ferrocement.

I really don't have a set schedule for eating, I eat when I feel hungry and the foods avai
_wwoof_travel: Ogden/Bus and train stations, SLC for airport

name: Danielle - Learn about growing organic Kona coffee at this farm
description:
Our next tentative availability will be mid-summer 2026 for a minimum stay of 3 months.

We are looking for hard working, fast learning candidates traveling solo. Nonsmoking.

Since we offer a shared living space and bath with different arriving and departing schedules two girls would be best suited.
No experience necessary just a lot of energy and a positive attitude.

There is a lot to learn about Kona Coffee, Specialty Coffee; you will be hosting our Farm Tours, helping package and selling our coffee. 
We need someone easy going, friendly, and articulate. If you like coffee, it is even better.

We will providing a shared room with its own kitchen and bath, internet access, one laundry per week.

Captain Cook is a small village, but we have a grocery store within walking distance from the house and a Farmers Market on Sundays.

The wwoofers will be cooking their own meals and would be responsible for their transportation.

They would be always welcome to tag along to Kona or to the beach.

Who lives at the farm? 

husband and wife.

Any animals or pets? 

3 dogs and 2 cats

Activities for your guests to do on their free time. 

hiking, swimming, visiting nearby farmer's markets, ...

How many hours a week will you ask guests to help? 
20 hours per week

What kind of tasks will you require them to do? 

Hosting Farm Tours, labeling coffee bags, packing coffee, opening and closing the visitor center and keeping the surrounding area in a pristine condition. There may be some farm work/landscaping during the slower times of the year.

What is the food that you provide for them? 

We provide a stipend of $100 per week per person. 
They will cook their own food.

Organic methods: Organic practice

Current projects: We are currently revamping our nursery, planting more coffee trees each year and replacing some old ones.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Kona International Airport - Big Island Hawaii

name: Anne - Situated at 25 km of Fagaras in Transilvania
description:
Description of land and geographic region: Our village (originally Hungarian) of 300 inhabitants is situated at 25 km of Fagaras in Transylvania, in a hilly region at 500m altitude, surrounded by forests and grassland.

Agriculture is an element within others of our life. We developed different projects in favor of the local community. For example: a sawmill, regular transport service to Fagaras, recycling of plastic waste. In development is the creation of a small fire department for the village. We organize summer camps for young people and develop with them micro projects for the local community (f.e. activities for the children of the village).

We need help in building, working with wood, in the garden, taking care of the animals and food preparations. Agriculture is an element of our life. We have developed many community projects. We also organize summer camps, for children.

Accommodation is simple houses. Shower and toilets are outside. Heating with wood.

The idea of our project can be summarized with two words: autonomy and solidarity. Ideals that we try to come close, knowing that we can never reach them, but are constantly on the move towards them. In short: life is not boring for us and we have a lot of work.

name: Lars - Living and Learning Center for Regenerative and Nonviolent living
description:
Provision is situated in the Western part of Transylvania. We are nestled in the foothills of the Western Carpathians, or Apuseni Mountains in a small oasis of fruit and walnut trees on the divide of wild mountain forest and gently rolling agricultural land. In the mountains just bordering the village to the west, bears, wolves and lynxes still roam in a vast stretch of nature preserve connected to the famous Padis plateau, where hikers and spelunkers like to explore a complex terrain of mountains, rivers and caves. The rolling hills on the Eastern side of our village is where local smallholders have been cultivating the fields for centuries.

We cultivate and grow food and beverages to feed our own family, our volunteers, friends and guests. We raise goats for milk and meat and chickens for eggs and meat.

Work on the farm: daily tasks; animal care; one draft horse, four dairy goats (milking), chickens, rabbits, dog, cat, cooking, seasonal work; haying, planting, growing, harvesting, canning & preserving, butchering, juicing & distilling… Projects like natural building, infrastructure improvement, farm upkeep… Work with the village community: Our village is a peasant community struggling for its existence in the speed of modern ‘development’. We very much encourage our interns to partake in the work of these villagers, help where it is needed. We ourselves do this as well. This is a great opportunity to do social service, learn skills first hand and have wonderful cultural exchanges. Work for our center for regenerative and nonviolent living: During summer season we provide courses and workshops at Provision. This brings along a fair amount of organization and work. Our volunteers can help to keep the farm running smoothly as these courses are happening, and help with the organisation around the courses. Work schedule: Summertime is a busy season. Some days will be fuller and longer then others. We work with the flow of season, weather, ongoing projects and persona

name: Dhala - Soul Farm – family homestead by the sea in Denmark
description:
Welcome to Soul Farm, our small family homestead close to the sea in rural Denmark. We are Oussi, Dhala,  Luna, Alma and dog Emma! After years of travelling the world ourselves we’ve landed in this beautiful place, constantly bringing our vision to life. 

Soul Farm grew slowly out of a simple idea: to create a place where food, family life and meaningful work come together. A place where things grow — vegetables, projects, ideas and hopefully also community.

We live here with our two young daughters (almost 3 and 1.5 years old), our dog, bees and many projects that keep evolving year by year.

Life here is beautiful, but also busy. Between family life, the land and the guest cabins there is always something to take care of.

⸻

The place

Our land is slowly turning into a small farm.

We grow vegetables, experiment with regenerative gardening and work with the rhythms of the seasons as much as possible. The garden is still evolving and there is always something happening there — planting, harvesting, building beds or trying new ideas.

We also keep bees and harvest honey during the season.

Another part of life here is making small farm products for our little farm shop — things like herbal teas, soaps, candles and honey.

At the same time we host guests in several small cabins on the land, which is part of how we support this project financially.

⸻

The reality of life here

Soul Farm is first and foremost a family home.

With two small children and several guest cabins, daily life includes many different tasks. Some days are relaxed, others are busy — just like in any real household and farm.

We try to keep a good rhythm between work, family life and shared meals, but this is not a retreat or a holiday place. It is a place where real life happens.

We usually ask for around 5 hours of help per day, 5 days a week.

The help is quite varied and often includes a mix of:

• spending time with the kids and helping with childcare
• cleaning and preparing the guest c
_wwoof_travel: Tingles Trainstation, Flensburg Trainstation, Aabenraa Busstop

name: Catherine - Building a Sustainable Life with Gardens & Bees
description:
Hello!  We're Cara and Frankie, we live on the South Shore of Nova Scotia in a beautiful hamlet called Mill Village along with our 3 rescue dogs- Pembie, Hazel and Pecan. We are building a life of sustainability and believe in sharing our skills to teach others and build community with our neighbours from across the road or across the world.

We have transformed the lawns of our property into gardens to grow vegetables, herbs and flowers.   Our small flock of chickens lay eggs for us and we bring in meat producing birds in the summer to raise and sustain us through the year.  We also have bee hives to  pollinate our gardens and produce honey.  We bake sourdough bread several times a week and try to avoid the big grocery store as much as possible.

Our 225 year old home is one of the first built in the area, our guests have a private bedroom overlooking one of the gardens.  All other areas of our home are shared including the two bathrooms, dining room,  kitchen and living room.  Frankie was a chef for over 20 years and prepares most of the meals in our home, but Cara also grew up in a restaurant and knows her way around the kitchen.  Meals are made with real whole ingredients.

We love hosting events for our community in our barn, whether it's live music, a workshop or feeding large groups of people.  Because we are located in such a great spot, we are only 10 minutes away from incredible beaches,  half an hour from the hiking and camping at Kejimkujik National Park, 45 minutes to the UNESCO Historical Town of Lunenberg and close to the all the amenities of Bridgewater. 

A typical day on our little farm includes taking care of the chickens and ducks, planting, weeding and watering the gardens. We enjoy working on the property and have lots of projects on the go from building and repairing to baking and creating new herbal tea blends.
_wwoof_travel: Bridgewater NS-Bus Station,  Halifax NS Airport

name: Jim and Debbie - Diverse certified organic farm and cidery 70 miles north of the Twin Cities
description:
We are a husband-and-wife team with four friendly dogs and operate a small, diverse certified organic farm and cidery 70 miles north of the Twin Cities. Our 172-acre farm is surrounded by forest, lakes, and a restored native prairie full of spectacular, blooming wildflowers. Crops include maple syrup, vegetables, fruits, and herbs, apples, ginger, plus egg-laying chickens and 10+ honeybee hives. 

We also operate a family friendly  farm winery (cidery) where we produce Yellow Belly brand hard cider which is distributed in six states throughout the upper Midwest. The farm and Cidery are open to the public on weekends and we have food trucks, music, art classes, and entertainment for the whole family.

The peak of our season is May - September, where WWOOFers will learn about growing, maintaining, and harvesting organic vegetables in our two hoop houses, and apple orchard. Other opportunities include learning about managing free-range chickens, and honeybee hives (optional), plus other organic farm-related projects during the growing season.

Our state-of-the-art Cider Barn is where we produce Yellow Belly cider and have a sampling room where we host on-farm events throughout the year.  WWOOFers have the opportunity to use their creative juices to help plan these events and can relax and join in the fun. We always look forward to hearing of new ideas our WWOOFers may have for improving our operation and new ways to help educate the public about organic farming, local food access, and native habitat.

A minimum of two-week commitment is requested, but WWOOFers can stay longer if desired. Accommodations are provided in the bunkhouse, a comfortable, rustic little cabin with primitive amenities: disposable toilet, sun-warmed water for shower. Also have a sauna available for use as well which has a shower with hot and cold running water. Also bathroom is available inside the house. Room for RV parking as well. Absolutely no smoking or tobacco use as the mosaic tobacco viru

name: Etravaille - Family home and garden - rural, Intentional Community, Aquaponics, Hydroponic,local business support
description:
Etravaille Community is an organisation, towards a sustainable life and for the protection of the environment. 
We're involved in the promotion of Biofarming. Hydroponic system of farming, Renewable energy, EcoHouse construction and #ZerowasteFood campaign in Mauritius. 
Founded and registered in Mauritius since 2018.

W'RE HOSTING:
We work closely with locals and foreign nationales in terms of sustainable living, protection of the environment, training, Hydroponic system of farming and community awareness services.

You are most welcome for a short visit (not more than 4 weeks), join us at any time of the year to share your experience with us. 
We welcome normads, students on internship, professionals and individuals interested in cultural and agricultural learning experience and knowledge exchange...

We live in Blue Bay, 50 meter away from one of the most beautiful beach on the Island, a home for 60% of tourist. We're happy to send you invitation to visit us, support or partner with us in the projects that we do. Kindly let us know 2weeks before your trip to Mauritius.

We can respond to your messages and request, within 48hours.
Send us a message on Facebook, if you don't get reply after 48hours.  

Organic methods: 1. Hydroponic kits system
2. Bio-fertilizers and plant nutrient 
3. Bio-flocs Technologies

Certifications: Certificate in view

Current projects: Our projects are;
1. Setting up a hydroponic kit system in 100 households on the Island.
2. Running a campaign and orientation against Food waste (#ZerowasteFood)
3. Construction of sustainable EcoHouse
4. Training, Campaign and Community awareness program for healthy eating habits.

Internet access available

name: Gary - Agritourism Destination
description: Living off the grid we offer a  minimum of modern conveniences, but trade them for holistic and natural conforts. We are creative with a strong disipline in sustainability. Our workshop and sawmill are now full operational. Perched on the top of Back Mountain outside of Earltown, Nova Scotia, Canada, overlooks fifty acres of blueberry fields, vast swaths of maple forest, the dramatic Northumberland Coast and the Atlantic Ocean. A year-round sustainable retreat maintained by Gary McCallum, a place of discovery, serenity, creation and rejuvenation for those who appreciate nature in all its unbounded glory. From each singular sunrise to sunset,the retreat is alive with natural beauty. Cross-country skiing, mountain biking, maple sugaring, fossil-hunting or just strolling around in its 43 acres, you will encounter innumerable species of plants, trees and fauna including deer, porcupine, bear, fox, lynx, rabbit, owl and hawk.The cozy main cabin, artists/writerâs cabin and natural teahouse are great for the solitary dreamer, the romantic couple, small groups of creative collaborators and nature-loving families. They are sustainably built, simply but lovingly decorated, woodstove-heated and run on solar energy. Bliss is the outdoor shower and clawfoot tub, tucked into the maples and overlooking the sea. Beaches, restaurants, farmers markets and museums are a short but not too shortâ drive away.The retreat is available by appointment as an eco-retreat for rental.We are a small farm. We have a small plot of cleared land with organic mixed vegetables. We have a small greenhouse. We harvest a little lumber, for building and woodworking. Fall of 2012 update; we have our Oxford rotary sawmill up and working We are looking forward to sawing this spring and summer.We are looking for help starting and maintaing our gardens, clearing brush, and building fences. We are starting to make hand carved spoons from maple, and birch.We would ask for reasonable effort per day, we are flex

name: Brenda & Gail - Family home and garden - rural
description:
Our 37 acre farm is on the beautiful Slocan River with a bus stop 1.2 km away.  Pick up is available from Nelson. BC.  Our son, his wife and their three sons share the land. We have enjoyed meeting and learning from WWOOFers and sharing our skills since 1994. Our farm has greenhouses,  vegetable and flower gardens, 7 acre hay field, a small commercial potato area, an orchard, wildlife pond & woods. Animals include chickens,  Cassie  (friendly Labradoodle) and PK, our cat. We are busy in the Spring, Summer and Fall and expect about 25 hours/week from visitors and some help in the kitchen. Guests learn about seedlings, transplanting, irrigation, growing, harvesting and preserving food. Optional activities include hay making and building projects.  Long term visits are appreciated but not required. Two guest rooms are available in our heritage log home with internet.  If you play a brass, woodwind or percussion instrument, you are invited to join the Slocan Valley Community Band (outdoor summer rehearsals). We eat what we grow and vegetarians are welcome. We live close to provincial parks & recreation includes seasonal hiking, cycling on the 50 km Slocan Valley Rail Trail, mountain biking, kayaking, canoeing, swimming, X-country skiing, and skating. We enjoy company and will introduce you to our friends and neighbors; this is a safe, artistic and environmentally aware community. Please tell us a bit about yourself when you contact us if you are considering Elderbee Greens.  We recommend early contact to reserve your preferred time.

Organic methods: We were KOGS certified organic members from 1994 until 2011 and have continued to follow organic standards in all our production.  We discontined formal certification when we sold the bedding plant business after 17 years (too much work!).

Certifications: KOGS (Kootenay Organic Grower's Society) #19 from 1999-2011

Current projects: Interface forestry (limbing & thining trees close to buildings & fence lines), building soi

name: Clare - Sustainable goat dairy producing a variety of goat milk products.
description:
Hello, I'm Clare, Chef/Operations manager and live on the farm with my partner, Cole, who is the barn manager! In conjunction with the farm owners and a small but mighty team, we run this dairy farm, NC’s pioneer farmstead goat dairy. The farm owners live on site as well in a 7 bedroom Inn that is open to the public.  We’ve been treating all our goats like celebrities since 1991 and strive to do the same for our customers! Following time-honored French farmstead techniques, we transform their milk into a  variety of goat cheese products. Its taste is inextricably tied to its place of origin – our particular herd of Alpine and Lamancha goats.

We have several seasons on our farm, beginning in winter with kidding season where our herd of 60 goats begin having babies. In conjunction with kidding, we start back up the  milking parlor with a morning and evening milking shift. We also begin bottle feeding the baby goats once become 4 days old. There is much to do during kidding season to include: monitoring pregnant moms, attending births, recording, tagging and monitoring each kid, feeding babies, etc.  Kidding season lasts up until the beginning of May, then we transition to the high milk season of summer including continuing to care for the last of the babies born.  Breeding season then begins in August and by the end of November we are no longer milking and spend our time on many farm projects in preparation for the kidding season.  In addition to all the above listed animal husbandry, we have a fully operations cheese production facility and attend 6 farmers markets a week. During kidding season we host Open Barn days every Saturday. 

Accommodations are a 3 bedroom house, with a full kitchen, bath and laundry facility. The pictures show the house in the process of being repainted and furnished and will be fully furnished before WWOOFer arrival. There is a full kitchen for exclusive WWOOFer use. We will provide a variety of ingredients and prepared foods for WWOOFers

name: Bethany - Animal sanctuary farm, Homestead, Garden, Vegetables, Permaculture, Ranch/Livestock
description:
Welcome to our farm—a magical, heart-centered sanctuary where animals are cherished, healing is honored, and the land breathes with soul.

Tucked into the stillness of Madison County in North Florida, we’re surrounded by natural treasures: the Florida Trail for hiking, crystal-clear rivers and springs, and a magical koi pond on the property—perfect for swimming, meditating, or simply letting go.

This isn’t just a farm. It’s a sacred space for regeneration—for both animals and humans. We’re co-creating something extraordinary here, and I’m looking for WWOOFers who want to live with intention, work with meaning, and help build the dream, one day at a time.

Who Lives Here:
We care for five horses, 3 pigs, chickens, cats, and 12 dogs—all rescues, each with a past of neglect, trauma, or abuse. This is their sanctuary. Volunteers must bring gentle awareness, emotional maturity, and a calm, loving presence. Healing—of all kinds—is our foundation.

Projects & Daily Rhythms:
– Build a new horse stall
– Feed and care for all animals; muck stalls; groom and play with them to bond release stored energy
– Construct and prep raised garden beds for spring planting
– Support the creation of a food forest, including fencing and layout
– Help build a water collection system—rain catchment, filtration, and storage
– Use or offer carpentry skills for small structures and beautification
– Contribute to general maintenance and land care
– I want to learn more about yoga and meditation so those practices would be greatly valued. 
Where You’ll Stay:
You’ll rest your head in one of these peaceful, high-vibe accommodations:
– A private guest house
– A charming bedroom inside the horse barn
– A guest room in the main house if overflow space is needed
