The farm has high-speed internet, shared nourishing meals, and plenty of quiet time for journaling, hiking, or deep rest. Meals with sometimes be prepared and served together.
They will also be self-prepared and self-served by the Wwoofer/gues

name: Lütfü - Ahşap işleri / Woodwork
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Asıl mesleği marangozluk olan ev sahibi, eşiyle ve çocuklarıyla birlikte Doğu Kararadenizin en güzel yerlerinden bir tanesinde yaşamaktadır. Kışın ılıman yazın sıcak olan hava koşullarıyla bölgesine göre farklı iklim koşulları sergileyen bölge, doğa turizmi açısından da çok zengindir. Yaklaşık 6 dönüme sahip aile kendi ekip biçtiği kendine ve misafirlerine yetecek her türlü sebzeyi yetiştirmektedir. Ziyaretçiler evin dışında kurulmuş olan ahşaptan yapılmış ayrı barınaklarda kalabilmektedir. Ersiz Kalesi, Dört Kilise, İşhan Kilisesi, Kaçkar Dağı ve Çoruh Vadisi gibi hem tarihi hem de doğal açıdan zengin potansiyele sahip ziyaret noktalarına yakındır. Ev sahibi Lütfü Beyden marangozluğa dair her beceri ve eşinden de tarım ve mutfak işlerine dair birçok şey öğrenilebilir.

---

The landlord, a carpenter by trade, lives in one of the most beautiful places in the Eastern Black Sea region with his wife and children. The region is very rich for nature tourism as it has warm weather in winter and hot weather in summer. The family owns approximately 6 acres of land grows nearly every vegetable for their guests needs and themselves. Visitors stay in a separate wooden lodge out of the main house building. It is close to some historical and naturally rich touristic places, such as Ersiz Castle, Four Churches, İşhan Church, Kaçkar Mountain and Çoruh Valley. Many things can be learned about carpentry from Mr. Lütfü as well as agriculture and cooking from his wife.

name: Murielle - Permaculture. Agroforestry
description:
Bonjour,
Je m'appelle Murielle.
Il y a 30 ans je suis venue m'installer au Brésil.
En 2000, nous nous sommes installés sur un petit domaine de 5 hectares de terrain avec le doux rêve de vivre en autarcie. Sans savoir que ce terrain était complètement dégradé. Il est aujourd'hui devenu un petit coin de paradis où la forêt est réapparue et où l'on plante et ramasse nos fruits et légumes grâce à la Permaculture et au système agroflorestal.

Les activités sont l'entretien de la forêt, des plantations et des constructions. 

Vous serez logés au petit chalet si il n'est pas loué, ou chez nous ou sous tente si vous le souhaitez. 
Nous assurons le petit-déjeuner et le déjeuner.

J'aime beaucoup cuisiner, ce fut ma profession.
Aujourd’hui je me consacre à l'entretien du domaine en parfaite harmonie avec la nature. 
Notre région du Cerrado est merveilleuse avec ses magnifiques cascades entre autres activités.

Hello, we are a Franco-Brazilian family. In 2000 we settled on these 5 ha of land with the sweet dream of living in autarky, it was without knowing that this land was completely degraded, today it has become a little corner of paradise where the forest has reappeared and where we plant and collect our fruits and vegetables thanks to Permaculture and the agroflorestal system. 

We had a little cottage to receive you.
_wwoof_travel: Brasília ou Goiânia airport  and after bus station in Pirenópolis

name: Ruby - Alternative co-operative community, Family farm, Community farm
description:
Oma maa (Our Land) food cooperative in Finland is warmly welcoming interested wwoofers to come work, learn and stay along with us during the upcoming season! 

Whilst cooperating with two farms located some 30 km from Helsinki, we produce year-round food bags, including fresh and ready made products, whilst we take care of our land and our animals (three Lapland cows as well as chickens), and work in our food forest, greenhouse, gardens and kitchen and, whenever we can, farm-dine together in our little membership/restaurant space in town, or on the farm. 

Our coop is in a dynamic, developing fase. Besides several producer members living/working on the farm, also increasingly coopmembers and trainees will be coming out and joining us on weekdays and saturdays as we head towards spring and summer,  

On the farm it is also  possible to have a grill together, sauna, and a dip in the "natural pool":) of the farm. Accommodation is modest but sweet, with breakfast and lunch/dinner during the weekdays included. The coopcar drives every day up from Helsinki, and as such its easy to get a ride to the city or nearby smaller towns. 

We much enjoy to be exchanging experiences, learning and doing with visitors coming for stays  - and look forward to hear of your interest to be joining us this season! 

Organic methods: Permaculture & polyculture. 

Certifications: Official organic certification/ Oma maa luomuosuskunta

name: Kat 'Katherine' - 16 acre organic food forest in the making centered around vacation rentals
description:
We have a blend of tree plantation, forest land that we are beginning a food forest in, blueberries, vineyard, orchard trees, nuttery, and herbs. We have a dozen chickens that free range currently, a rescue German Shepherd, Borzoi,and 5 rescue cats. 

We have seasonal activities: applying wood chips over bark for weed suppression, weeding, pruning and trimming, organic fertilizer application, as well as general mowing to maintain the estate, forest management ,etc.

4 to 6 hours a day of work outside is needed by experienced people only as we are not always available . Realize it is raining from October to June in the Pacific Northwest yet work and life goes on.

Our santctuary and its maintenance is a major focus alongside enjoying what we have created here. You can see a drone view on the website. It’s a stunning location and once work is done the lake and rivers are nearby to enjoy or Mount Rainier an hour away, etc. We have an optometry office that houses the NHF Memorial library with many books on agriculture and soil for you to use and refer to. Education back-and-forth between us is what we are aiming for. We need a self-motivated and experienced person to handle the estate which is also a short term rental/Airbnb, which means the property has to be maintained for instant booking at all times. Through my work at Codex alimentarius, which regulates food and nutritional supplements globally, we endeavor to create a model for self-sufficiency and small farm sales that does not include artificial fertilizers or pesticides. My family were cropdusting pilots, and are all impacted with chemical exposure so our commitment is to soil integrity and the health of the body/mind/spirit.

name: Philomena - Family home and garden - rural, smallholding, livestock farm, house and garden
description:
Overview

Meiklejohn’s Croft is a smallholding located in Easter Ross, in the north-eastern Scottish Highlands, practicing organic and ethical growing. At the present time  we grow  for our own subsistence,and have a small flock of sheep mainly for grazing. Our focus  is mainly on promoting  native biodiversity.Originally founded as an organic dairy farm in the early 1980's , we produced   a range of sheep and goat cheeses. We have experience of hosting WWOOFers since the 1980's.

We have ongoing activities depending on the time of year and offer a learning experience  where you  will live in our ( Alasdair and Philomena)  home and participate in our day to day activities and life on a croft. We are  looking for friendly volunteers who are interested  in immersing themselves  and learning what it is like to live on a croft and care for  the land.

At Meiklejohn’s Croft we aim to create a welcoming and nourishing place to live, not only for ourselves, but our sheep, wildlife, and all those who come to visit. We use very little mechanisation and no pesticides in our extensive vegetable garden, where we focus on growing tubers, brasicas, berries, beans, and root vegetables. Inside our large polytunnel, we grow a wide variety of vegetables and fruit, all organically.

Beyond this, we have around 11 acres of land, comprising a mosaic of pasture, mixed woodland of mostly native species, and scrub.  Our vision for our land is to provide an appropriate area for our sheep to graze and develop the rest to promote local biodiversity. To this end, we have been planting up areas of our croft with a mix of local tree and shrub species, and are also looking to transition some of our pasture to wildflower meadows. We also have done much work to attract a variety of birds in our garden  Over the coming years, we hope to do more to encourage local mammals in particular, such as red squirrel, pine martin, hedgehogs, and badgers. 

 We are looking for  WWOOFers who are interested in be

name: Yildiz - Regenerative farmhouse in the forest of Ibiza
description:
La Granja Farmhouse is an old farm located in the heart of the island of Ibiza.
Since the beginning of 2025, we have started implementing a new vision for La Granja. 

The project focuses on expanding the farm, always rooted in regenerative methods. The goal is for the farm to become, as beautifully described in permaculture, a closed loop system.  

We work with a close-knit team of six people at La Granja Farmhouse, with each person having their own responsibilities within the project. With three nationalities represented in the team, there is a rich diversity of backgrounds and input that contributes to the project.

Leading on the project there is the team of Kayatree, which already has the experience of hosting Wwoofers and volunteers on other projects in Europe. 
We have always done so with the philosophy of providing a fulfilling experience for the WWOOFers, ensuring that everyone leaves the project feeling empowered and ready to carry the knowledge of regenerative practices out into the world.
Within the program, alongside the work that needs to be done, we also offer WWOOFers time for self-reflection and space to reconnect with themselves through nature. Something we have found to be deeply needed in this day and age.

On the farm, we work with intensive planting beds as well as perennials such as fruit and nut trees. There are also a small number of animals live on the farm, including two horses, three goats, and dozens of chickens.

All cultivation is done using regenerative practices, drawing from a variety of methods including permaculture, syntropic farming, and successional agriculture. 

In addition, there is also an "aesthetic" garden, which we are gradually transforming to have not only visual appeal but also productive value. In this process, we are replacing certain plants and flowers with alternatives that contribute to the farm’s overall production.  

A day on the farm begins with caring for the animals, followed by work in the productive syst

name: Amanda - 80 acre farm in the remote, and beautiful countryside of Southern Mississippi
description: Our farm is 80 acres located in the quiet, remote, and beautiful countryside of Southern Mississippi.  Our farm resonates with only the sounds and lights of nature and ourselves.  Our 80 acres is a dynamic matrix of forests, pasture-lands, and homestead.  We currently have cattle,  sheep, chickens, turkeys, geese, ducks, and horses, and we utilize animals for fertilzer, grazing impact, and regenerating the landscape.  We have several different projects that are in the works.	We have established a non-profit to provide educational programs and affordable housing.  We are establishing a permanent community of residents in alternative-style homes like Yurts, tiny homes, and small cabins, etc. 	Spirituality & Personal Development: Personal and group development for the eco-village residents, visitors, and students.  We have plans to build a barn later this year that will be our Event & Retreat Center built from materials harvested here on the land.  We will host seasonal educational events as a Regenerative Agriculture and Permaculture  courses. We practice Conservation and Restoration. There will be ample opportunity to participate in all the above mentioned ‘spokes’ of our ‘wheel’, the primary responsibility of WWOOF volunteers will be maintenance and development of our permaculture and regenerative agriculture goals.  The WWOOF program is run pretty much like an internship, so we really value the education and experience that wwoofers will take away with them.  You’ll get lots of experience working with farm animals.  We are constantly working to improve our ‘food forest’; we have a sizable annual garden; and we are developing a highly diverse herbal garden around the main homestead. Wwoofers will be expected to put in 30 hours per week in exchange for room and board.  Volunteer housing is a canvas bell tents.  We also welcome other members who have their own tent or accommodation.  Bathrooms with running water and hot shower is provided via a beautiful outdoor bathr

name: Reiner - Olivenhain
description:
Ich lebe allein inmitten eines ruhigen Olivenhains auf Korfu – umgeben von Natur und mit viel Raum für kleine, nachhaltige Projekte. Das Grundstück ist etwa 0,4 Hektar groß und bietet Platz für gemeinschaftliches Arbeiten, Entspannen und ein einfaches Leben. Aktuell schlafen meine Gäste in Zelten auf einem sicheren Campingplatz. Zusätzlich entsteht gerade ein kleines Gästezimmer, mit Fokus auf naturnahes Wohnen und schöne Außenbereiche.

Ich bin 61 Jahre alt und arbeite seit vielen Jahren als Physiotherapeut. Ich lebe minimalistisch und schätze Einfachheit und Tiefe in allen Lebensbereichen. Mir ist wichtig, dass sich alle wohlfühlen und sich entsprechend ihrer Möglichkeiten einbringen können. Die Arbeit ist anfängerfreundlich und flexibel gestaltet, sodass jede:r nach dem eigenen Rhythmus teilnehmen kann.

Wie wir hier essen und kochen, richtet sich ein Stück weit nach der Mithilfe. Manche Gäste möchten vor allem entspannen und sind eher für sich – das ist völlig in Ordnung. Andere bringen sich gern mehr ein, helfen regelmäßig im Olivenhain oder bei kleinen Projekten im Haus und Garten. Dann entsteht oft auch mehr gemeinsames Kochen: gemütliche Mahlzeiten in der Runde oder kleine kulinarische Experimente. Beide Formen haben hier ihren Platz, und alles entwickelt sich ganz natürlich.

Da ich therapeutisch arbeite, kenne ich das Thema Erholung und Regeneration aus verschiedenen Kontexten. Wer sich gerade in einer körperlich oder mental sensibleren Phase befindet, findet hier eine ruhige Umgebung, in der der Tagesablauf gut anpassbar bleibt und nichts erzwungen wird.

In der Umgebung gibt es viele Möglichkeiten, die Natur zu genießen: Wanderungen durch Olivenhaine und Berge, Schwimmen im Meer oder einfach in der Stille entspannen. Mein Ziel ist es, einen offenen, freundlichen Ort zu schaffen, in dem gemeinsames Arbeiten, Lernen und Entspannen im Mittelpunkt stehen.

Da dies ein kleines Projekt ist und der Raum begrenzt, beginnen Aufenthalte in der Regel erst einmal mi
_wwoof_travel: Bushaltestelle 1,0  km / Hafen 40 km / Flughafen 42 km

name: Bård - Horse Ranch, Mixed Farm, Farm
description:
Our Farm: Nordre Hestnes Gård is a farm in Balsfjord, 60 km away from the city Tromsø. It is very close to the fjord with an amazing view across the water and mountaintops in every direction. We are operating ecologically and are certified by Debio and also as a Green Care farm. The animals we have on the farm, are horses, hens and sheep. We also have small fields with vegetables and herbs. Additionally, there are two apartments that are rented out to tourists. Nordre Hestnes Gård also works with the child welfare program of Norway, and there is a group of children staying on the farm for a weekend, approximately twice a month.

We run a continuously evolving farm, where the goals and visions vary and change. New ideas and creative ways of enhancing the work and life on the farm are welcome.

Work: The work on the farm consists of about 6 hours a day, 5 days a week and always varies, some odd work hours can be asked of you in times off need, depending on the seasons, weather and capabilities. However, there are also daily chores. These consist of:

- Taking care of the animals (feeding, giving water, collecting eggs, cleaning)

- Taking care of vegetable fields and herb garden

- Farm maintenance (repairing broken fences, lights etc.)

- Cooking (we take turns, so everyone will cook around once a week) The two apartments that are rented out to tourists also have to be prepared after a stay, which will also fall into your field of responsibilities. We use wood from our forest for heating so there might be some woodwork. We have a wood machine and will try to maintain a good production of firewood.

In Summer and Autumn there will be a lot of gardening (planting, maintaining and harvesting the vegetable fields). Also, we want to build new fences around the farm.

What we are looking for: We are looking for independent, reliable team-players that can be a valuable part of our farm. You should be honest, sincere and respectful towards the people and anim
_wwoof_travel:
It is approximately one hour from Tromsø centrum by bus. 
Take the bus from Tromsø airport to Prostneset (Tromsø sentrum). Take another bus from Prostneset to LAKSVATN (Balsfjord).  
We will pick you up at Laksvatn with our car. 

See tromskortet.no f

name: Jerry - Ferme holistique visant l'auto-suffisance. Finca holística orientada a la autosuficiencia.
description:
1. Décrivez votre projet, votre lieu, et ses habitants (y compris les animaux) :
Logé au pied de la Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta, la plupart de nos voisins sont Kogi. C'est un lieu qui offre la possibilité de connexion très profonde avec la terre. Observer, ressentir redévelopper l'instinct primaire de la connexion à la terre, dans le but de vivre simplement et sainement. On privilégie les woofers hispanophone ! Pour voir des photos de la finca, allez sur Instagram @altanohaju
Ubicados al pie de la Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta, la mayoría de nuestros vecinos son Kogi. Es un lugar que ofrece la posibilidad de una conexión muy profunda con la tierra. Observar, sentir y volver a desarrollar el instinto primario de conexión con la tierra, con el objetivo de vivir de forma sencilla y saludable. Preferimos los woofers de habla Espagnol ! Para ver fotos de la finca, vaya a Instagram @altanohaju

2. Listez les activités auxquelles les WWOOFeurs participeront et ce que vous désirez leur apprendre :
Restauration des arbres fruitiers, implantation des cultures saisonnières, entretien des chemins et des parcelles de cultures, s'occuper des semis et de la pépinière, transformation alimentaire, construction et amélioration des infrastructures existantes,... 
Restauración de arboles frutales, establecimiento de cultivos de temporada, mantenimiento de caminos y parcelas de cultivo, cuidado de las semillas y del vivero, procesamiento de alimentos, construcción y mejora de infraestructuras existentes, trabajo espiritual con plantas maestras…

3. Comment allez-vous nourrir et loger les WWOOFeurs ? Veuillez décrire votre hébergement et l'organisation des repas :
Un chalet en matériaux naturel indépendant avec un vrai lit confortable et toilette compost et évier privé, douche extérieur privé et galerie avec hamac privé.
Un chalet independiente en materiales naturales con una cama realmente cómoda y un inodoro de compost y lavabo privado, ducha privada al aire libre y galería con hamaca
_wwoof_travel: Bus de Santa Marta à Palomino. Mototaxi de Palomino ou rio Ancho jusqu'à la finca.

name: Margret - Eco Retreat
description:
We are located amidst lush tropical vegetation in the heart of Bali (20 minutes from Ubud). We provide a sanctuary in an idyllic, powerful environment where visitors can experience tranquility and re-connect to the life-giving qualities of the earth. Jiwa Damai is a meeting place for people from all over the world to study, to find and initiate peace, to learn from each other and from the land. Jiwa Damai Agro-Permaculture and Retreat Centre benefits the social educational Foundation, Lagu Damai Bali supporting action projects and study programs for Indonesian and international youths (check our YouTube channels: Jiwa Damai & Lagu Damai Bali). At Jiwa Damai, we offer a basic introduction to permaculture hands-on practicing in our garden and work with different permaculture techniques, sustainability, and how to regenerate the soil, get to know tropical plants and fauna, coconut oil-making production from our own trees. You may wish to experience working side by side with local people and participate in local events or ceremonies and the wealth of tropical vegetation. All of these are embedded in an early yoga or Qigong class (if teachers are available) and before dinner a short heart meditation.

Our volunteers generally support us 6 hours a day, 5 days a week. When you write to us, let us know the dates you would be available with two-month notice. All volunteers, in addition to their main area of support for Jiwa Damai, are invited to share in some maintenance duties during their stay. We offer our volunteers basic clean accommodation and Balinese organic food, mainly vegetarian three meals a day. All volunteer accommodations have access to electricity. We ask for a minimum period of one-month commitment, subject to a review after one week to make sure it is a positive learning experience and a win-win situation.

An additional attraction is to participate in our mountain permaculture garden design and complete an Earthship-Inspired project in North Bali where our
_wwoof_travel: 20 minute drive from Ubud and about one hour from the airport in Denpasar. Please check our latest information.

name: Saket - We are currently starting fresh in Riverview Florida. I plan aquaponics, permaculture and mud homes
description:
The farm has a pond shared amongst other homes. Total area is 1 acre, but the actual farm area is less than that, as part of it is the pond. I would like to redirect some of the greywater from the house and kitchen for farming purposes, set up an aquaponics garden, and perhaps start with multilayer permaculture farming without animals, then add ducks and goats. I would like the land to have a lot of vertical layers, and the design like a cottage garden with flowers and edibles planned based on color, edible, and profit harvesting cycles. The idea is for it to look like a great place to host events, process food in-house, and bring in raw food from outside farms to process and conduct monthly cooking and processing classes. I have done this in India, Houston, and Napa Valley.

I can teach waste water recycling, bug and worm raising, aquaponic farming of vegies and herbs to maximize growth, landscape design, crop cycle planning, food processing, mud house building, and waste recycling

There are 3 rooms and 2 baths. I would like to offer 2 rooms to 2 or up to 4 WWOOFers. I plan to have bunks for folks to live in a social community, and ideally from fall to spring. I cook meals, and I cook all three meals in the house, which is open to other woofers to share. I consider cooking and farm food processing as an integral part of organic living, and expect someone to share duties or free me up from other duties while I do the cooking

I do stained glass, product design, mud home building, sewing, food processing, board games, and landscape design, antiquing, thrifting, and cooking as a hobby. I do video editing and script writing also, but that has taken a back seat as that requires way more planning with all the farm and full-time work I do as a product designer

The farm is at a very early stage, and I would like to design the plan on paper and execute the hardscape first. Fall is when I will have animals and plants, so the initial work is mostly going to be construction

name: Kathryn - Resilient 21-acre permaculture homestead  located in St. Ignatius, Montana
description:
Are you looking for an authentic homesteading experience? Think about coming here - a resilient 21-acre permaculture homestead  located in St. Ignatius, Montana, with dreams reaching far beyond simply growing and enjoying beautiful fruits and vegetables. 

ABOUT THE PROPERTY 
Hi there, and welcome! My name is Kathy and I run the farm. Our home is built with straw bale construction.  The property, while not off the grid, is solar installed and great hydro potential. There are bees, turkeys, chickens, dogs, and cats on the farm, plenty of garden space and greenhouse space where delicious and nutritional food is grown to feed our home. I worked in animation as a background artist, and helped create such projects as Care Bears, Madeline, and Tiny Toons. I also enjoy making sculptures, jewelry, and cartoons. As such, teaching others about art, and working collaboratively on artistic projects is one of my great joys.  I love having WWOOFers here at my place and building friendships with each other as we work together to achieve our goals.  

The main goal is to get to a point where the nucleus, or core intent, of the farm -i.e. growing food, being sustainable, and having all the structures built that we need in order to survive and thrive- is completed to such an extent that the farm work is passively supportive and there exists sufficient time to dedicate to other projects. Ultimately, my goal is to create and be a part of a small, sustainable, multi-generational and healthy community that is supportive of both organic living and the arts. Every bit of work you perform on this property will contribute towards that goal, from creating beautiful handicap-accessible greenhouse living spaces to perfecting recipes incorporating a variety of natural foods... It's all immensely important - and appreciated.  

Our location is difficult to beat. On the western slope of the Mission Mountain Range, we have an excellent 360 degree view. The farm is on the Flathead Indian Reservation

name: Isabel - Agritourism Destination
description:
Welcome to our finca! Our place is a hotel and restaurant in the lush mountains of Miraflor in the north of Nicaragua. We grow many beautiful flowers on our finca as well as other crops. Most of the people in Miraflor work in agriculture. We are looking for volunteers to help with many of the projects that we have in process. More creative ideas to spend time with the children and the community are also welcome!

Wwoofers will have the opportunity to enjoy the beatiful nature of the Reserva Natural Miraflor. In their time off the volunteer will get to know life of the community. There are many activities and excursions to enjoy in your free time. Explore waterfalls, hiking trails, horseback riding to name just a few. The cloud forest of Miraflor is world renoun for its bird watching as well. In exchange for your work you are provided a comfortable bed in a room surrounded by flower gardens, fruit trees and beautiful views. You will also be provided with three meals, coffee, tea and juice. Wifi and hot water are provided, which are not always common in our country we are located in the highest zone of Miraflor, so weather can be a bit chilly and foggy, especially at night. Come spend some time teaching and learning on our beautiful finca in Nicaragua!

Organic methods: Ecological farming and gardening. Exploring permaculture.

Certifications: Organic certification in Nicaraua is uncommon and expensive. It not widely available.

Current projects: We are looking for motivated volunteers with a positive mindset and willingness to challenge yourself. 
Tasks are varied and may include gardening, farming, landscaping, painting, carpentry, building living roofs, art projects, murals, or teaching English. There are also potential opportunities to help in the kitchen including cooking, harvesting, food prep, and cleaning. Knowledge of permaculture, gardening, and/or carpentry are a plus. If you have knowledge of mushroom growing, composting, or beer brewing these are also

name: Franz - Family home and garden - rural, Mixed Farm, Farm, Family farm
description:
Finca Heidi 
We created now the ecological and cultural association Morada del Ser. Our aim is to run an ecological farm with a big diversity of different subtropical plants. We also plan to offer guided visits about avocado cultivation and more and culinary workshops.
We have been living here in our beautiful Avocado farm (17000 m2) for 10 years, during which we have renovated the buildings. Now we are changing from the Avocado monoculture to a mixed tropical and subtropical fruit forest where one can participate as an volunteer.

The minimal duration for wwoofing in our finca is five weeks.

We expect you to be able to cook for 7-8 persons once a week. 

You have to be in good condition. We have a lot of physical work and our site is steep. So physical fitness is a prerequisite. 

We don't tolerate using headphones at work.

After the first week we will have a conversation and check if further cooperation is desirable.

From November till April after heavy raining it sometimes happens that we can't cross the river by car for some days but by foot it´s always possible.

For the next years we have different projects:

- The ground has to be covered with bokashi and mulch to protect it from drying out

- We always have to clean and repair our spring which supplies us with  fresh springwater and we need to construct a grey water recycling system

- For fertilizing we make our own fish emulsion and we produce a lot of biochar and mulch with the remains of tree-cuts

- Our objective is to be mainly self sufficient so we would like to develop a bigger vegetable garden 

- Our product refinement is to dry fruits and create different jams, chutneys and mojos

We want to share our place with you and offer accomodation and good food for working for 5 hours, 5 days per week.
Once a week you help us to clean the community rooms in the afternoon, this takes about one hour.

Our place is very peaceful, quiet and special, situated at the entrance of the breathtakingly spectacular
_wwoof_travel: Os recogemos en la parada de autobus Los Llanos

name: Harry - Inicio de reconversión a finca ecológica
description:
Pequeña granja en proceso de preparación para la nueva temporada de cultivos. Durante este periodo previo a la siembra necesitamos apoyo en diferentes tareas del día a día de la granja.

Las actividades incluyen preparación de bancales, organización del huerto, pequeñas tareas de mantenimiento y el cuidado diario de nuestras aves de corral (alimentarlas, revisar agua y mantener limpio el gallinero). Es un buen momento para aprender cómo se prepara la tierra antes de plantar y participar en la vida real de una granja.

Buscamos voluntarios responsables, con buena actitud y ganas de trabajar al aire libre. Preferimos perfiles con reseñas positivas o experiencia previa, ya que algunas tareas requieren cierta autonomía y compromiso.

Si te interesa venir, preséntate en tu mensaje: cuéntanos tu experiencia previa, cuánto tiempo te gustaría quedarte y qué te motiva a participar en la vida de la granja.

Nos gusta compartir conocimientos, trabajar en equipo y mantener un ambiente tranquilo y respetuoso. Si disfrutas de la naturaleza, el trabajo con la tierra y el contacto con animales, probablemente disfrutarás la experiencia aquí.

Si nos escribes, incluye la palabra ‘gallinas’ en tu mensaje para saber que has leído el perfil completo
_wwoof_travel: Estación de FFCC Olesa de Montserrat/ Autobuses desde Barcelona

name: Soares - Family home and garden - rural
description:
You will be very welcome in Povoação: a beautiful area in the Island of S. Miguel in Azores. Here you will discover nature and simple life. We are a luso-luxemburgish couple having a project of a sustainable guest house since 2012 !! 
We speak Portuguese, Luxemburgish, French, English, Spanish, and a little bit of Italian, Finnish and Swedish! 
We need help this Spring for some house maintenance works and in our organic farm as well as to take care of our animals. We have a very comfortable house  and we grow lots of vegetables and fishing is also possible in the village. 

We have a new project for 2021 for a longer period in the in Central Portugal near Pombal in my grand-parents farm. There is a old little house for farming tools but a camping car would be recommended welcome or a good tente. 
The farm has orange, olive, cherry and fig trees and all has to be renovated and rebuilt. More details will follow. 

Thanks in advance for your help!We will wait for you!

Certifications: N/A

Type of work: Planning Spring plantation,
House maintenance,
Rebuild a tools house

Internet access available

name: Murat - Arıcılık ve arı ürünleri ile doğal ürünler
description:
Takat'ın  Merkez Aydoğdu Köyünde temel faaliyet olarak arıcılık yapmaktayız. Kardeşimle ortak evimizde ikamet ediyoruz.  Ziraat Mühendisiyim. 28 yıl İzmir'de   memur olarak çalıştıktan sonra emekli olup köyümde  arıcılık yapmaya başladım. Doğaya ve toprağa dönmek benim için bir amaçtı. Ben ve kardeşim daha doğal bir hayat yaşamak için mücadele ediyoruz.
Kardeşim Otelcilik turizm işletme fakültesi  mezunu. 
Ben bekarım. Kardeşim evli daha çok Tokat merkezde oluyor. İşler olunca Köye geliyor. Annem, kardeşimin eşi ve çocukları ile arada sırada kısa zamanlı olarak bizimle kalıyorlar. Köydeki arıcılık faaliyetini kardeşim Mutlu  ile birlikte yapıyoruz. Kendimiz için doğal sebzeler yetiştiriyoruz. O zaman annem de bize yardım ediyor.  Henüz evcil hayvanımız yok. Ancak kedi köpek at ve inek edineceğiz.

Arıların rutin dönemsel bakımları,
İlkbaharda çerçeve çakma, tel çekme vb. Arı kovanlarını açıp inceleme ve takip etme. Hastalık ve zararlı mücadelesi.  Yazın bal hasadı. Sonbaharda kışa hazırlık bakımları. Sabah kahvaltıdan sonra  yapılacak işlerin aciliyet durumuna ve hava durumuna  göre arı ile ilgili işlere başlanır. Öğlen molası  sonrası  iş planına göre devam edilir. Akşam yemeği ve serbest zaman. Müzik aletlerimiz vardır. Birlikte Müzik yaparız. Arı işleri dışında bahçede sebzelerimiz vardır.  Çapalama ve sulama,  hastalık ve zararlı mücadelesi  için doğal ilaçlar yaparız. Kül suyu ve ısırgan suyu gibi. Odun sobamız vardır soğuk havalarda yakarız.  Bazen karşı dağdaki yaylamıza ve soğuk sularımıza kır gezisi düzenleriz. Hayvanlarımızı aldıktan sonra ise ineklerin bakımı kedi ve köpeğin yemekleri ile atımızın beslenmesi  ve atla gezintiler yaparız.
Lavanta bahçemiz var 5 dönüm kadar. İş planına göre lavantanın yabancı ot mücadelesi, çoğaltmak için çeliklemeler, hasat zamanı hasadı edilmesi işlerimiz de vardır.
Doğal kuşburnu erik alıç  ve yabani meyveler, derelerde fındıklar var. Mevsimine göre bunlardan toplanır. Komposto ve kurutma gibi. Kendimize yetecek kadar yer
_wwoof_travel: Tokat otogar ve havaalanı, Turhal tren istasyonu. Sivas havaalanı,  Yıldızeli hızlı tren istasyonu

name: Jef - Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Family farm
description:
The farm is an organic olive farm located 45km from Amman, in Jerash mountains-Jordan. The farm also has citrus and fruit trees and grows seasonal vegetables. Sheep, Geese, Ducks, and Chicken are also bred on the farm and are allowed free grazing. In the Jeftlek farm we host volunteers during two main seasons through the year. “The Joy of Olive Harvesting” season which starts early September and lasts till mid December, work involves Olive picking, tree pruning, plant irrigation, animal care, cooking, and housekeeping. “Enjoy the Farm Spring” season which starts early March till mid June. Work in spring includes preparing the land for spring and summer planting, watering, weeding, field crops harvesting, shepherding and taking care of newly born sheep and chicken.

Organic methods: Rainwater harvesting, waste water treament and reuse, no pestecide, orgaing fertilizer

Certifications: working on this issue to be certified

Internet access available

name: Joe and JoAnn - Veganic gardens and orchard just outside Lawrence, Kansas
description:
We are a small veganic farm surrounded by woods, just outside of Lawrence Kansas. We are also long term vegans (entirely gluten-free here too.) We would like to share our passion for veganism and great food with woofers who could help us maintain our gardens and woods, create beautiful spaces and structures on our property.

The current projects we need help with are 1) Planting weeding and watering 2) Building  more raised garden beds from stone  3) Gathering up dead wood that has fallen in the forest and burning it to make ash that we use in our gardens and for building projects 4) Building paths and stairs on those paths through our woods, and 5) Mixing up veganic fertilizer.

The University of Kansas campus is a 20 minute car ride away, and we'd be happy to drive people into Lawrence  to see the campus or town if they don't have a car.  Clinton Lake is nearby and has some nice swimming beaches that we visit occasionally as well.    We are about a four mile hike from downtown Lecompton (former territorial capital of Kansas) which also has a couple of cool museums.  The Kaw river is just a 1/2 mile from our farm, and there is lots of hiking nearby.

Our gardens are organic -- actually veganic -- meaning we not only don't use toxic chemicals but also do not use any animal by-products from slaughtering or raising animals, so we never ever foliar feed/spray our lettuce and other greens with blood, bone meal or fish emulsion -- this additionally protects those who consume our produce and our land from all the toxic substances that bio-accumulate in the tissues and excretions of fish and farmed animals.

Our wwoofer lodging includes an off-grid cord-wood cabin that has a woodstove with a twin bed, and an off-grid tree-house with no heat that has a queen-size bed. Wwoofers have use of our indoor bathroom during the day and composting toilets at night.

One final thing we want you to know: We oppose corporate tyranny in all forms and believe that informed consent for med

name: Sisir - Our farm offers volunteers an authentic immersion into Nepal’s local traditional lifesty
description:
Nestled in the hilly area of eastern Pokhara, the home of Sisir Banstola offers volunteers an authentic immersion into Nepal’s local traditional lifestyle. The family, belonging to the Brahmin community, warmly welcomes WWOOFers to experience both cultural richness and agricultural practice in the village of Arba, Pokhara.
The farm is alive with the rhythms of banana cultivation, vegetable gardens, and goat farming, providing a hands-on opportunity to learn and contribute to sustainable rural living. Volunteers can expect good food and comfortable accommodation, with space for 2–3 participants at a time, ensuring a communal yet intimate environment.
Beyond the farm, this host location serves as a gateway to trekking routes that lead into the majestic mountains. Volunteers can enjoy sightseeing across the Pokhara valley, explore its serene lakes, and take part in hiking and camping adventures that highlight the natural beauty of the region. This host combines agricultural learning with cultural exchange and outdoor exploration, making it an ideal setting for those seeking both meaningful work and memorable experiences in Nepal.

name: Alyssa - Animal rescue and hobby farm in a forest swampland oasis
description:
UPDATE: We are hosting a FREE natural building mentorship focused on finishing our cob house with lime plastering and roofing techniques. Whether you're new to natural building or want to deepen your skills, this is a perfect opportunity to learn hands-on. We're looking for up to 5 WWOOFers for the month of November 2026! 

Our farm is our personal residence as well as headquarters to our nonprofit and campground. It is a 24-acre forest swampland oasis. It is home to our herd of beloved goats and two rescued horses, our family of humans, and often tent and RV campers who are spending a night here with us. The property boasts five ponds, three moats, cypress and citrus trees, many berry varieties, incredible wildlife spotting, and a balance of both manicured design and uninhibited nature. 

We would love to host WWOOFers who are especially interested in animal husbandry, natural resource conservation and land stewardship, beekeeping, gardening, art, and farm construction. We welcome vegans and vegetarians however we do eat meat and we milk our very spoiled goats.

No experience necessary - just show up with a caring and passionate heart and we'll train you on the rest!

Let us know what excites your curiosity and what your skills are and we’ll choose projects accordingly. :)

A typical day may include any of the following:
-Animal Care such as feeding and watering the animals, administering medicine, collecting eggs, brushing the horses, milking goats, mucking stalls. 
-Land Stewardship such as taking a kayak out to weed the ponds from invasive and overgrown plants, beekeeping, mushroom log inoculation, trail maintenance, foraging edibles and medicinal plants, and processing fallen trees into firewood
-Property Operations and Maintenance such as giving a tour to campers, painting fences, making soaps, building cabins, landscaping, and tidying public areas.

Pitch a tent, park an RV, or stay in one of the rooms in our guest house. 3 meals a day included. ~5 hours of w

name: Madhu - Our farm is in  about 37 km far from Kathmandu
description: Our farm is in Sindhupalchowk, Nepal, surrounded by quiet jungle and clean mountain air. We grow vegetables, rice, and fruits like bananas, oranges, lemons, guava, and pomegranates. We also keep cattle, buffalo, and goats. Sindhupalchowk is known for its natural beauty—green hills, terraced fields, rhododendron forests, clear rivers like the Bhote Koshi and Sunkoshi, waterfalls, and views of the Himalayan peaks. Nearby places to explore include trekking routes, Dhap Dam, tea gardens, Chisapani, local temples, and peaceful villages. You can enjoy river valleys, sunrise and sunset over the hills, and, in some areas, hot springs like Tatopani. We welcome volunteers who follow WWOOF values. Come join us for hands-on farm work, sustainable living, and a warm cultural exchange with our community.

name: Cristina - Regenerative cattle farm in the Uruguayan countryside
description:
In brief : 
We are a regenerative cattle farm in Uruguay, practicing grass-fed and animal-friendly livestock farming, If you enjoy life in the countryside and are interested in learning about soil regeneration and ranching, then this could be the place for you ! 

About us : 
We are a 280-hectare cattle farm with roughly the same number of animals. Originally from Argentina, we lived in Europe for many years before recycling us as cattle ranchers in Uruguay. We acquired the farm in 2008 and have been living on it full-time since 2021.

All our pastures are natural, our cattle is 100% grass-fed and treated with great care. We practice regenerative agriculture, more specifically Voisin grazing, a system based on moving cattle daily between paddocks to mimic the natural migrations of large herbivores. This allows soils to rest, regenerate, and promotes long-term soil health. 
The farm itself hosts rich biodiversity, with many native trees, streams, large rock formations, and a varied landscape that is home to abundant wildlife.
We also have dogs, cats, gooses, sheeps, horses and a skunk. 

Work :
We are looking for people who are interested in learning about the regeneration of soil with cattle. 
During your stay, tasks may include working with the cattle, caring for newly planted trees that provide shade, helping in the little vegetable garden, or assisting with the maintenance of buildings and infrastructure.
Working hours are adapted to the climate. In summer, work is done early in the morning and later in the afternoon, while in winter it takes place during sunnier hours of the day.

Location & accommodation
The farm is located about 30 km from the nearest village (Cardona), which is connected by a few daily bus routes. We are also relatively close to Colonia (about 45 minutes by car), a well-known coastal city in Uruguay, with access to the Río de la Plata. It can also be reached by bus.
Accommodation is provided in a refurbished container, located about 100 meter

name: Cheryl - Permaculture
description:
Hello! 

I've lived on this wonderful spot for over 35 years, just outside of Fort Collins, CO. Our property is just shy of an acre. We have an organic garden, a small orchard (apples, peaches), and we raise chickens (15 currently). We have just enough chickens to sell eggs during non-winter months informally to friends to pay for food and to have eggs for ourselves all year long. We've raised pigs in the past, although we don't have any concrete plans to get more this year. We have a wonderful livestock guardian dog named Willow who keeps the whole thing going.  The chicken run needs some work, and I want to move some fencing around.

Our main need right now is weeding. We have an infestation of leafy spurge, which we mitigating with bio control (mostly flea beetles). In the meantime, we need to keep it contained, so we are pulling weeds at property lines so it won't spread. 

I'm also open to ideas to make the land/space more sustainable. I'm a master gardener and I have a permaculture design certificate. 

Our home is LGBTQIA+ friendly!

Because most of the work on our land will involve heavy equipment, children will not be a good fit. Pets also would not work. I'm looking for a 1 week stay, but I'm open to longer or shorter. Single folks or couples are both welcome.

I have a van that I rarely drive, and I'm happy to let you take it on your days off. We are close to the Poudre Canyon for access to camping and hiking, and there are lots of additional outdoor options, depending on your interests. Rocky Mountain National Park is also fairly close.  I can provide food for a variety of dietary needs, and I'm happy to cook most meals while you are here. 

Looking forward to working together! Thanks so much for reading!

Organic methods: We use crop rotation, heavy mulching, companion planting, regenerative practices. We don't till. We integrate animals that are happy together (chickens and a livestock guardian dog), and we utilize the chicken's poop for manure. We har
_wwoof_travel: Fort Collins Transit Center (from DIA you can take the A-Train to Union Station and take the Bustang to Fort Collins

name: Muhammet Ali - Bolu Kıbrıscık’ta Hayatın Huzur Bulduğu Kırsal Ev | Kıvrak Ceviz Bahçesi
description:
🌍Merhaba sevgili WWOOF gönüllüleri 🌿
Ben Muhammet Ali, Bolu’nun sakin ilçesi Kıbrıscık’ta yaşıyorum. Doğanın içinde, ceviz ağaçlarıyla çevrili küçük ama huzurlu bir bahçem var. Burada misafir ağırlamayı, sohbet etmeyi, birlikte yemek yapmayı ve doğayı paylaşmayı seviyorum.
Bahçem ana yol üzerinde olduğu için ulaşım oldukça kolay. Ziyaretiniz boyunca birlikte vakit geçirir, Kıbrıscık’ın doğal ve kültürel güzelliklerini keşfederiz.
Buradaki asıl amaç işten çok tanışmak, kaynaşmak ve gerçek Anadolu kırsal yaşamını deneyimlemek.
Ayrıca sivil toplum çalışmalarında aktifim.
Kıbrıscık Sürdürülebilir Kalkınma Derneği Başkanıyım ve Türkiye Multipl Skleroz (MS) Derneği Bolu Şubesi Başkanlığı görevini yürütüyorum.
Merak ettiğiniz her şeyi bana gönül rahatlığıyla sorabilirsiniz 😊
Instagram: @muhammetalikivrak

------------------

🌍 Hello dear WWOOF volunteers 🌿
My name is Muhammet Ali, and I live in Kıbrıscık, a quiet district of Bolu. I have a small but peaceful garden surrounded by nature and walnut trees. I enjoy hosting guests, having conversations, cooking together, and sharing life in nature.
My garden is located next to the main road, so transportation is easy. During your visit, we will spend time together and explore the natural and cultural beauties of Kıbrıscık.
The main purpose here is not work, but getting to know each other, building connections, and experiencing authentic rural life in Anatolia.
I am also active in civil society. I serve as the President of the Kıbrıscık Sustainable Development Association and the President of the Türkiye Multiple Sclerosis (MS) Association – Bolu Branch.
Please feel free to ask me anything you are curious about 😊
Instagram: @muhammetalikivrak

name: Lisa - Market Garden, Ranch/ Livestock Farm, Mixed Farm, Family farm, CSA market garden
description:
Grovenland Farm is a great place to spend some time farming with some easy going friendly folks. We've had a few WWOOF visitors every summer for since 2012 and have enjoyed the company of every one of them!
*Looking for volunteers for one more fall job on the farm in October (asap) to help with garden clean-up and garlic planting. (And there's always animal chores too.) Warm private house to stay in. *

The farm took new form in 2011 to become a multi-generation mixed farm. We seek to grow wholesome, natural food for our community, and surrounding areas in central Saskatchewan and would love having others join us in the adventure! We have a market garden, including a CSA (approx 40 shares) and a variety of livestock. We raise pastured poultry (for both meat and eggs), pastured heritage pork, and grass-finished beef. We have also begun a small school field trip program. 

From late April to October we can use help in many different areas, including garden work, animal chores, fence building, building infrastructure around the farmyard (ie. more chicken housing, greenhouse, shop repairs,)  chicken butchering (July-Sept), and food preserving. There is something for everyone! There are 2 families living on the farmyard, Lisa and Ben and 3 children (boys ages 12-16) live in one house, and Ben's parents, John and Denise in the other. We also have 2 staff that live in campers on the farm during the spring to fall months. We are LGTBQ+ inclusive.
WWOOF accommodations would be in a camper (or possibly in Lisa and Ben's house), while meals are with either or both of the families. We have had fabulous WWWOOFers come stay with us (both with kids and without) and we enjoy showing them around the area and taking them with us for social events, outings, and local festivities. 
 
Having been interns on a farm ourselves and having very positive hosting experiences in the past, we look forward to hosting more WWOOFers on our farm! We are located 120km south-east of Saskatoon on the Y
_wwoof_travel: Saskatoon nearest airport, no public transportation

name: Silke - Homestead, Cattle Ranch, Farm, Family farm
description:
We are a small family in the north that is working towards self sufficiency on about 100 hectares of land, there are currently 2  of us working the farm, my husband and myself. We are raising a small herd of beef cattle and a small flock of 20 chickens (for eggs). Our big garden includes several large outdoor plots with raised beds and 3 greenhouses that produce enough veggies for family and friends. In the later summer there are lots of wild berries to forage and as it gets into the fall there are wild mushrooms as well. During harvest season we use preservation practices such as: canning, drying, pickling and fermenting. We sell some meat to people that appreciate the unique features of grass-fed beef as well as any excess veggies. 
Our animals are treated with love and respect and we take pride in raising them as stress-free as possible.
Our 2 dogs and 2 cats are living with us in the house so dog walks, playtime and cuddle sessions are part of our daily routine.
We love to keep farming as simple as possible by using methods like mulching, no-till gardening, composting, crop rotation, permaculture and rotational grazing. 
Our farm is situated about 8 km from a small town which has all the necessities like 2 grocery stores, hardware stores, a post office, library and recreational facilities. The surrounding area is mostly made up of farms, lakes, forests and trees and more trees which provides plenty of opportunities for camping, swimming, fishing, kayaking or just reconnecting with nature. With a little patience and good luck you may even spot local wildlife like moose, bears, coyotes, or the occasional wolf or maybe even some Northern Lights.
Living close to nature has taught us to be open-minded, to think outside the box and to improvise in unique situations   
Since we moved here from Germany 19 years ago with very little farming background we had to acquire many new skills such as bread making, preserving, seed saving, tractor driving, electrical fencing, han
_wwoof_travel: Timmins Victor M.  Power Airport (YTS) / Ontario Northland Bus Stop in Matheson

name: TONG WON - <JJ129>예술과 치유의 농장 / A Sanctuary of Art and Healing
description:
✅ 호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
대학에서 교수로 재직후  2013년 제주에 내려와 농장을 가꿨습니다.
저희농장은 "예술치유농장(Art Farm)"을 표방하고 있습니다.
정신장애인들의 예술작품(art brut, outsider art)을 보여주는 작은 전시장이 있으며, 로즈마리, 녹차, 황칠 등과 같은 약용식물을 재배하며,  약 2 Km 정도의 생태탐방로가 농장에 있으며, 풍광이 수려한 장소라서 정신장애인을 포함한 방문자들에게 "치유 프로그램을 운영"하고 있습니다. 1년에 2차례 전시회 등을 농장에서 열고 있습니다.
생태복지 강의를 본 농장에서 일반인, 사회복지사, 장애인 등을 대상으로 이미 수백명에게 강의를 하였습니다.

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2013년
✅ 농장 형태 : 체험 교육 농장
✅ 농장 주작물 : 조경수, 황칠, 로즈마리, 녹차 등

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 지난 20년 넘게 친환경(농약과 화학비료가 없는) 농장을 유지하여 왔으며, 호스트 본인은 이러한 농장운영을 "생태복지"(Ecological Welfare)라는 체계속에서 동양적 관점(예: 천지인 삼재합일사상)에서 접근하려고 노력하고 있습니다.
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 : 개인적으로도 가능한 자동차를 타지 않고 걷거나 자전거 타기를 좋아하며 육식보다 채식위주의 식단을 선호 합니다.  남의 생산물을 구매하는 소비자보다 스스로 생산하는 생산자 입장(텃밭 가꾸기 같은)에서 일상생활을 하려고 노력하고 있습니다.

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 형님 내외
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려 동물 : 강아지 2마리
✅ 우퍼가 하는일 :
봄, 여름, 가을에는 주로 예초작업과 텃밭가꾸기를 하며 겨울에는 나무를 옮기거나 심는 작업이 많음

✅ 선호하는 우퍼상 :
성실한 사람
✅ 우퍼에게 당부 메세지 :
좋은 만남이 되도록 서로 노력
✅ 숙소에서 와이파이 가능여부 : 가능
✅ 술,담배 가능여부 : 금연,금주
✅ 호스트와 식사여부 : 하루 한,두끼만 함께 먹고 나머지는 식재료를 드려 직접 해 드셔야 합니다.
✅ 영어소통 : 기본 가능
✅ 일주일 기준 우핑시간 : 하루5시간 주5일

======== English explanation ==========
✅ Introduction to the Host and the Farm :
After serving as a university professor, I moved to Jeju in 2013 to cultivate this farm.
Our farm identifies itself as an “Art Healing Farm.” We run a small exhibition space that showcases artworks by people with mental disabilities (art brut, outsider art). We also grow medicinal plants such as rosemary, green tea, and Dendropanax morbifera (Hwangchil), and maintain an ecological trail of about 2 km. With its beautiful landscape, the farm provides healing programs for visitors, including those with mental disabilities. Twice a year, we also host exhibitions on the farm.

We have offered numerous ecological welfare lectures here, reaching hundreds of people including the general public, social workers, and people with disabilities.

✅ Year Farming Started : 2013
✅ Farm Type : Educational & experiential farm
✅ Main Crops : Landscape trees, Dendropanax morb

name: Piotr - Agritourism Destination, Homestead, Hobby Farm, Mixed Farm
description:
Hello!
Quick info: it'd be great to have some volounteers in May-September 2026. Feel free to contact us. Accomodation in tents.

I've started a donkey farm in 2020,  and I'm looking to meet open-minded people who wants to learn, help or just spend some quality time in the countryside and share their skills. 
Right now it's me (Peter) and my 6 years old son Stefan who live on the farm. 

I've traveled a bit, worked here and there as volounteer, and after walking with our first three donkeys from France to Poland (>1000kms) we've finally started our own farm. I have around 6ha of land, 8 donkeys, 3 cats,  big dog (Landseer), two ducks, 3 pigs and 3 goats.

I rent the mongolian style yurt (got a lady that cleans it, dont worry), have lots of plans for the future. My work is walking donkeys with clients and telling stories about them :) Children can ride them too!

We live in oldest (and very small, highest hill is 600m) polish mountains, 40kms from Kielce. There's quite a few things to visit in the neighbourhood, and it's perfect place for walks, runs or bike rides. It's generally very calm, as we're the last house in the village. Not much a party place. More like calm campfire in the evening.

There's a huge list of things you could do, here's some of them:
- cooking
- our vegetable garden is tiny, there's just few raise beds. But it's still nice to see the vegetables grow, and to show it to children
- cleaning after donkeys ;)
- fencing (electric fense needs to be moved every now and then. It takes lots of walking to do so)
- training a donkey (walking with him on the paths around)

And many more.

We've had almost 40 different volunteers (mostly NOT from woofing but on the same rules), and we're looking for opportunities to meet new people :) Rule of thumb is that we eat lunch together, other meals usually separately. Of course we provide all the food.
For accomodation we have tents set up on a wooden platforms, with all the gears (sleeping bags, mattresses) .There

name: Alyn - Agro-ecological production farm in the beautiful island of Puerto Rico
description:
Hello, my name is Alyn and I run a small agro-ecological farm in Ceiba, Puerto Rico. This project grew out of a desire to live a simpler, more intentional lifestyle while working closely with nature and creating a space where people can come together, slow down, and explore themselves. This is not only a farming experience, but a living experience that includes cultural exchange, reflection, and learning through daily life on the land.

Every day on the farm looks different. WWOOFers help with feeding and caring for animals, cooking shared vegetarian meals (breakfast, lunch, and light dinner), seeding, transplanting, pruning, weeding, and general maintenance. Tasks may also include fencing, basic plumbing, tool care, and tending shared kitchen and living spaces. This experience is ideal for self-motivated volunteers who enjoy simple living, shared responsibility, and hands-on learning.

The farm is deeply connected to the surrounding community, with local women, farmers, and neighbors often visiting, collaborating, and sharing their knowledge. I am also actively involved in community service, including volunteering at a local feeding center every Tuesday and Thursday. These ongoing relationships create a living, evolving environment where learning and connection unfold organically. 

A small cottage is available and can house up to two people at a time. The farm also has space for camping and/or day visits. A newer addition to the property is a tiny home located in a secluded area that is available for rent, featuring a full bathroom, kitchen, and full-size bed. Time outside of work is often spent cooking together, resting, making music, or visiting nearby rivers, waterfalls, and beaches. This experience is best suited for people who are adaptable, grounded, and open to both physical work and personal growth. The maximum stay is two weeks.

We are fortunate to be surrounded by many beautiful places. A nearby bakery is just five minutes away. Rivers and waterfalls su

name: Yanick Frutueux Cadnel - L’Eco-ferme Fructueux vous fera prendre de la graine !
description:
Fondée en 2019, cette ferme a été transformée en ferme biologique en 2021 par son fondateur. Ici, tout est cultivé de manière biologique sur place, ce qui témoigne d'un engagement profond envers des pratiques agricoles durables. Mais ce n'est pas seulement une question de cultures - l'Eco Ferme Fructueux est également un lieu de communauté et d'apprentissage, où les gens sont accueillis pour partager des repas et des connaissances.
Les animaux font partie intégrante de notre écosystème à l'Eco Ferme Fructueux. Ils jouent un rôle essentiel dans la fertilisation des sols et contribuent à maintenir un équilibre naturel dans notre ferme.

Les WWOOFeurs auront la chance de participer à la culture des légumes biologiques, de prendre soin des animaux de la ferme et de découvrir les secrets de la cuisine à base de produits frais et locaux. Nous souhaitons leur transmettre des connaissances sur l'agriculture biologique, la durabilité et l'alimentation saine.

Nous nous assurons que les WWOOFeurs sont bien nourris et logés lors de leur séjour à l'Eco Ferme Fructueux. Pour l'hébergement, nous disposons de logements confortables et adaptés à leurs besoins. Cela peut inclure des chambres partagées, des dortoirs ou des tentes, en fonction des préférences et des disponibilités.
En ce qui concerne les repas, nous aimons partager nos plats avec les WWOOFeurs. Nous préparons des repas sains et délicieux à partir des produits biologiques cultivés sur place. Cela permet aux WWOOFeurs de découvrir notre cuisine locale et de profiter d'une alimentation équilibrée pendant leur séjour.
Nous accordons une grande importance à la convivialité et à la communauté, donc les repas sont souvent pris en commun, ce qui favorise les échanges et les moments de partage.

La journée d'un WWOOFeur chez nous peut être variée et enrichissante. Ils participeront à des activités agricoles telles que la plantation, la récolte et l'entretien des cultures biologiques. Nous organisons également des ateliers sur
_wwoof_travel: Aéroport International de Cotonou - Cardinal Bernardin Gantin

name: Karen - Lavender and herb farm
description:
This is a lavender/herb farm with a barn venue/teaching facility.   We utilize organic  practices.   There is a small greenhouse and this season we added a 30 x 96 foot high tunnel.  The farm is home to four cats. We just lost our magnificent Great Pyrenees to old age. I'm sure I'll be getting another at some point.   The farm is open to the public during blooming season and we do a variety of classes in the barn.  The classes include essential oil distillation, herbal salves, balms and lotions, crafts, and a variety of other subjects.  We  host  classes, reunions, meetings and weddings in the barn. 

WWOOFers will be exposed to a variety of farm activities, from weeding to tractor maintenance, making and selling lavender/herbal products both on the farm and at local markets,  welcoming and assisting farm visitors, enjoying the most amazing sunrises and sunsets. 

Days will be variable. It is best not to work lavender until the dew is off (to prevent diseases). So harvesting doesn't start til mid morning and finishes around noon due to oil dissipation in the afternoon sun.  The other herbs are not so finicky.   We cut and dry many herbs for teas, salve making etc.   On days we have classes, the barn has to be set up, swept, bathrooms cleaned, kitchen checked, etc.  Product making is done as needed to keep inventory stable. The lavender is dried for most products. In season we rotate bundles on and off the drying racks.   We host upick days in season and sell at Farmers Markets.  The addition of the high tunnel has enabled extended season in vegetable production.  So planting, weeding, watering, monitoring for infestations are all part of the day.  All of these activities and more are available.   Also, I'm a pretty good cook and hopefully the garden will be producing delicious home grown vegetables.  In addition, as of November, I have just added a 30 x 96 foot high tunnel and will be planting with winter vegetables.  So there will be a fresh addition to the food fa
_wwoof_travel: XNA airport is 1and 1/2 hours away near Fayetteville, AR. The train is 3 hours away in Little Rock. Bus station is also in Fayetteville.

name: Ricki - Family home and garden - rural, Homestead
description:
We've lived off the power grid for the past 40+ years (have a battery bank with arrays of solar panels and a backup generator that runs on recycled vegetable-oil) on 160 acres of mixed forest with several creeks flowing through it. 
Gardening is part of the spring, summer and fall routine as well as gathering firewood, trail clearing, caring for our poultry, sheep and horses, fixing fences, and picking edible wild foods and herbs etc.  
We eat a lot of what the garden has ready or what is in the root cellar. We also have a large AGS greenhouse (Annualized Geo-Solar) which gives us a longer growing season for tomatoes, cucumbers, peppers etc. and it is home to grapes and kiwi vines along with an apricot, a couple of peach trees and a few perennials. 
We completed an addition, with a 2 story turret, to our owner-built house (a passive solar design) using as much local material as possible, combining earthbag, strawbale, cob, and adobe with a post and beam frame. We are completing the building of our barn.
We are looking for wwoofers who are physically fit, (NO SMOKERS please!), hardworking, self motivated  and quick to learn new things, and able to handle remoteness, all kinds of weather situations, especially cold and snow in the winter. 
Our nearest neighbour is 600m away and the closest town is 10km from Goddess Country.
In exchange we treat you like family and welcome you into our comfortable, striving to be self sustaining lives, and look forward to sharing our meals and ideas with you!!

Organic methods: Use composted manure from our animals, humanure, etc. No pesticides, herbicides, or artificial fertilizers, just what is available on our 160 acres of land.

Certifications: use organic methods only but certification is too costly

Projects depending on the season include:

caring for our horses, sheep, ducks and chickens

working in our garden, greenhouse

getting more firewood for next winter

maintenance of our many walking trails

finishing the new barn

add
_wwoof_travel: Bus to Prince George then train to New Hazelton, also Smithers or Terrace have airports you can fly to from Vancouver

name: Eveline - Small  family farm focused on Regenerative Agriculture and Permaculture
description:
We are Jaagup (40) and Eveline (38) and we live on our small but old farmstead in the center of Estonia, together with our sons Tarmo (3) and Aare (almost 1). We aim for creative and sustainable solutions with permaculture principles. We use mob grazing/AMP grazing with our flerd (flock and herd togeher) of friendly sheep and cows to regenerate the soils and stimulate biodiversity. We also make compost with our chickens and tractor meat rabbits around in the 100+year old orchard. An ever expanding vegetablegarden provides us with food throughout the season. There is a traditional Estonian farmhouse ('longhouse') here from 1850 which houses our animals in wintertime. We have 2 dogs and a barncat to entertain us with their crazyness.

We keep ourselves busy with moving the flerd with electric fencing every day,  driving the ATV to fill up the water throughs and providing the animals with shade.
Depending on the time of year there will be work in the garden like sowing, transplanting, weeding, bed preparation, mulching, sifting compost, turning of compost and of course harvesting cooking and fermenting of produce.

Chickens need feeding and watering, rabbits need moving to fresh grass. Sometimes we are building, we like our clay and limeplasters and can show you how to work with it. 

When weather and children allow, we enjoy finishing the evening around a campfire.

We eat as much as we can from our own garden and our own meat. We will provide the food you need and there is almost always fresh home made sourdough bread (whole grain)  available. 
We will share meals in or around our small family house which is an old loghouse with clay plasters inside and limeplaster outside. We have tadelakt in our kitchen and bathroom (even a tadelakt bath!).

You will stay in our cute egg shaped sauna, where there are two single beds a washroom and a sauna. NOTE: It is very small. But if you decide to take outside showers the washroom could be converted to some storage. ;-)
The eggc

name: Barbara - Azienda Agricola e Allevamento  Cavalli a gestione naturale sull Appennino Ligure
description:
Qui nella nostra Azienda ci occupiamo principalmente di cavalli, ne abbiamo 23, di cui 3 pony e 3 asini, gestiti in modo naturale. Pratichiamo il metodo Parelli, metodo che si basa sullo studio e la comprensione degli equidi e delle loro esigenze psicofisiche. Proponiamo lezioni di  Parelli Natural Horsemanship, addestramento puledri e recupero di cavalli difficili e traumatizzati. 
In estate ospitiamo ragazzi per campi e settimane verdi e organizziamo corsi del metodo Parelli per tutti e per tutti i livelli.
Abbiamo inoltre pascolo e una parte boschiva. Frutteto e orto. 

Le nostre principali attività qui sono quindi pulire e accudire gli animali, tenere in ordine gli spazi condivisi, il frutteto e il terreno circostante e cucinare per noi stessi e per i nostri ospiti se necessario.
Inoltre, per curare di più la nostra visibilità e crescita dell'azienda, anche sui social, sono gradite figure come:
- social media manager
- business manager
- content creator
Cerchiamo persone che si adattino alla vita semplice e rurale, che amino condividere gli spazi (cucina e alloggio) e a cui piacciano i lavori manuali e all'aperto in ogni stagione; amanti degli animali.

La titolare sono io, Barbara Parziale, Parelli Professional Instructor 3 stelle, vivo qui in paese a 5 minuti dal ranch, ho un custode che vive alla fattoria e alcuni collaboratori che mi aiutano e che saranno felici di affiancare i nostri ospiti. 
Potete trovarci anche su Istagram e Facebook, sia come Parziale Barbara che come Azienda Agricola Cortin, abbiamo doppio profilo.

Siamo situati sulle alture di Genova a circa 40 minuti di auto dal mare. Siamo sull'appennino ligure a 20 minuti dall'Alta Via dei Monti Liguri, percorso panoramico da percorrere in bici, a piedi o a cavallo che congiunge La Spezia e Ventimiglia. 
Per arrivare con i mezzi pubblici si può arrivare alla stazione di Genova Pontedecimo e poi con il bus 736
, che da Genova Pontedecimo porta a Isoverde o Cravasco (Comune di Campomorone).

Accesso

name: Franko - Family Home and Organic Farm - Rural.
description:
Proyecto de agricultura familiar basada en principios de permacultura y conservación. Contamos con una pequeña Huerta en constante expansión e invernaderos de reproducción de especies nativas y ornamentales, más un bosque comestible en en proceso,  estamos constantemente experimentando y aprendiendo sobre el arte de cultivar alimentos y el mejoramiento del hábitat mediante métodos sostenibles. (Nota de oficina WWOOF Chile: Por motivos de iniciación, esta granja puede cobrar una mantención a sus invitados, prever eso antes de aceptar)

Te invitamos a que nos contactes y puedas aprender y disfrutar de este bello lugar. 
WWOOF Chile office note:  This farm, exceptionally and because it is just starting out, has been allowed to ask the wwoofers for a little money for their food.  (WWOOF Chile office note: For initiation reasons, this farm may charge a maintenance fee to its guests, anticipate this before accepting)

name: Werner - Granja a la orilla del lago General Carrera, con huerta orgánica y animales.
description:
La granja reside a  200 kilómetros al sur de Coyhaique,  region de Aysen,  Chile, a 5 kilómetros de un pueblo llamado Bahia Murta.

Todas actividades del campo, trabajos en la huerta orgánica , trabajos con cabras lecheras y mantenimento de la mini central eléctrica de la propia energia del rancho. Mantenimiento de senderos por el bosque y los cercos. 

El alojamiento en una pieza en la casa o en una cabaña.   
La mayoría de la comidas es del propio rancho como verduras, frutas , leche , queso , carne y huevos
consistimos de una propia vertiente para el agua potable.

Contamos con senderos hacía la cordillera y al lago como kayaks para la pesca 
y hay muchos bosques nativos de la zona

name: Anna - Homestead, Mixed Farm, Family farm
description:
We are a small family farm with 20 years of experience in organic farming and small scale cheese making. Our family consists of: me - Anna, 38, I'm an educated farmer, and also been working on the farm since childhood, my daughter Michalina - a very active 9 year old, my father Krzysztof (64 with 35 years experience in farming) We all speak Polish as our first language and English as second.

     We currently breed 40 East Friesian and Lacaune cross sheep and produce a few varieties of cheese, jogurt and kefir, which we sell from home and on a local farmer's market. Our farm is self sufficient in feed production, sheep feed on the grass and grain in summer months, and hay in  the winter. We also grow a vegetable garden which we plan to expand this year, as we would also like to be able to offer some greens to our cheese customers. With very little machinery, a lot of the farm work is done by hand.

     15 years ago our family moved to an 1859 farm house in the Land of Extinct Volcanos region of Lower Silesia, and we've been slowly renovating it since, trying to bring back some of its previous glory. Therefore anyone with building/renovaiting experience is also very welcome.

  During the season from March til November we need help in: machine milking, feeding the animals, making cheese, packing and selling products, making preserves, weeding, fencing pastures and sheep herding. Additionally we are haymaking 4 times a year, in the spring grow vegetables from own seedlings and  in the summer collect own fruits and harvest the grains. In the autumn we collect potatoes and stones from the fields. 

Organic methods: - we use manure for the fields and pastures

- crop rotation

- use compost in the garden

-keep biodiverse fallows

-keep buffer strips 

Certifications: Agro Bio Test

Current projects: Projects for 2025:

- expanding composting methodes: hot composting

- creating a rotation grazing system: fencing the plots

- pest control by ducks

- never ending house

name: Gabe - Hidden desert oasis ranch located in the canyonlands near Death Valley National Park
description:
Our ranch is a hidden desert oasis located in the canyonlands near Death Valley National Park. Historically it was a water stop on the Old Spanish Trail between Santa Fe and Los Angeles. Today it is a date farm and tourism destination, we also serve as a trail head for hikers exploring the Amargosa River and adjacent public lands. We are farming sustainably 25 acres of date palms, and produce over 50,000 lbs. of the sweet fruit annually. We also have a gift shop and bakery here where we sell our goods to the general public. We do it all here; start and grow our own trees, hand pollenate, tend, harvest, sort, grade, and package our dates which we sell primarily right here at the ranch.

Visitors will learn about, and help with, all aspects of date culture, as well as the realities of running a successful small farm in an isolated. rural area. We can house 6 visitors during the cool months, October through April, and 4 in the hot months, May through September. Visitors stay in a large 2 bedroom double wide mobile home and an adjacent cabin. We allow the visitors to pick out their own groceries and we pay for them, visitors can either cook together or individually if they prefer. In exchange we ask for 5 hours a day, 5 days a week of work, the rest of the time can be spent hiking and exploring our unique and beautiful setting. Visitors can either drive here or get to Las Vegas , Nevada where we will pick them up and return them on their departure day. We ask for a minimum of a 10 day stay, beyond that it is open ended, as long as both parties are content.

A stint here is real work, and may not be a good fit for some one who does not like  physical labor or is afraid to get dirty  occasionally. it may test you a bit, but you will be stronger for it and learn something about yourself.

As summer ends and we head into the harvest season, we could use at least 3 more volunteers! The weather will be cooling in September, and the dramatic and historical landscape here is wo

name: Jesse - Urban organic farm and BnB in the small town of Seaside California, on the Monterey Bay
description:
We are a family owned and operated bed and breakfast and urban organic farm, in the small town of Seaside California, on the Monterey peninsula. We are located one mile from the beach and have great views from our Craftsman style house over looking the town and Monterey Bay. since we are in town we are quite central to activities and getting around. Our organic farm consists of vegetable gardens, fruit trees, flower gardens, chickens and honey bees.

Agricultural activities include: plant, weed, feed, prune, harvest - vegetables, fruit trees and flowers. Feed/water chickens and clean coop. Projects and goals include: building planter and herb boxes, harvest/spin honey. 

The accommodation has a queen size bed, includes kitchen and bath, use of laundry facility and our beautiful garden. We provide great food. 
No children, no pets. We do not have parking for RV’s and such. If you bring a car there is plenty of street parking.

As a WWOOFer, you are expected to participate in keeping the living spaces clean. This includes house chores and laundry, which we all contribute to. 

Monterey is a vacation destination so there are lots of activities, especially for the outdoor enthusiast. Hiking, biking, kayaking, beaches, good concerts (venues are small), wine tasting, whale watching, several farmers markets.We are also located on the bus route with a stop right in front of the house.Preferably looking for long term 3-6 months.

Although experience is a bonus, No experience is necessary, we will be happy to educate.

No drugs, no smoking, especially in the accommodation. We are an Inn so quiet time after 10pm for sleeping guests. No shoes in the house.

Organic methods: Hand removal and natural product treatments of pests. 

Certifications: We are not commercial so have not sought out certified organic 

Current projects: Reformatting and redesigning vegetable garden layout, bringing in container gardening as well. 

Internet access available
Couples welcome
_wwoof_travel: Monterey regional airport, San Jose International  airport

name: Alejandra - Ferme autonome en agriculture raisonné.
description:
Bonjour les amis Wwoofeeurs. Je suis infirmiere a la retraite et avec mon mari nous construisons une ferme dans le sud de la Colombie qui se veut autonome et respectueuse de l'environnement.

L'activité quotidienne tourne autour de la plantation de citronniers, Cacao, bananiers... en pots puis en pleine terre. Nous avons aussi des poules et bientôt des porcs.

La maison construite en 2023 de 170m2 respecte les normes (eau chaude eau froide) courant 110v énergie solaire et internet Starlink. La cuisine est de type française avec les produit disponibles dans le sud de la Colombie.

Les loisirs sont autour de la pratique du yoga, de la méditation de la lecture et de l'échange avec la communauté.

name: Mary - Farm in beautiful SE Minnesota- Bluff Country in the Root River Valley near the Mississippi River
description:
Located in beautiful SE Minnesota- Bluff Country in the Root River Valley near the Mississippi River. We're 20 minutes West of LaCrosse, WI (Amtrak, bus station and airport there- we'll pick you up). 2024 will be our fifteenth year hosting WWOOFers. We thoroughly enjoy hosting and getting to know our guests. In many ways our WWOOFers become part of our family. Doug and I are retired.  Doug was a District Soil and Water Manager and I am a retired public health nurse.  We love to travel and also love to engage with people from around the country and world to share experiences.  

Our two adult daughters, Anna and Emily and their little girls are often her too, helping with our animals. We have a large vegetable garden developed to produce our own food - small orchard provides fruit in late summer.   We also have a small herd of dairy goats used to provide breeding stock. Participants may learn about milking, making fresh goat cheese, kidding and general dairy goat management.
