The time of year dictates the type of work; Spring- till and plant, kidding; Summer- cultivate, weed & maintain garden, kid-care; Fall- put-up produce and prepare planting beds for winter, goat-breeding. Year-round milking & cheese-making.  Stay a 1 week minimum - longer stays are available. Early spring into November. We have two bedrooms for WWOOFers, one has two twin beds, the other a queen and a single. You are not expected to share a room. You may have to share a bathroom with other WWOOFers. There is separate access on same level. 

Wi-Fi available.  Besides farming, bike along the Root River or canoe. We can provide meals for vegetarians if requested. We speak English and minimal French and German. There is no smoking in our home and we have a golden retriever that lives indoors as well as two large livestock guard dogs who live with the goats. We are flexible with time off. If you don't have transportation we try to get you to some of the local interesting places. Some days require long h
_wwoof_travel: LaCrosse, WI has an airport,  Amtrak stop and a bus station.

name: Jim (James) - Creek-side campground hideaway available in South Austin TX
description:
Creek-side private picnic/campground hideaway available in South Austin TX. This is a CAMPGROUND: no room or RV involved. This is a ""want to camp"" and not a ""need to camp"" situation.  If you ""need to camp"" due to financial and/or other problems in your life then this will not be a good fit. Bring a tent and gear to be comfortable. 

I have 2 acres on a secret and secluded little street 5 miles due South of the capitol of TX and even closer (2.5) to the very hip SoCo area (Continental Club, Jo's Coffee, Guero's Taco Bar, Continental Club and lots more). We have a large organic garden, 4 cats, chickens, 2 little donkeys  and 5 sweet mini goats here at my little and funky 'farmette' right on Williamson Creek and next to a huge wooded greenbelt with a quiet and unused nature trail.

We are not a 'traditional' farm here; more of an an 'unintentional' urban community/campground, with a few permanent residents, some vacation rentals, and a bit of helper/camper folks coming and going. You may find this to be a good 'in town' exchange situation if you would like to explore Austin TX as we are right here in the city. Lots of remodeling and cleanup chaos here (this great place was neglected 30+ yrs. before we got it and needs some love) but also lots of pretty and secluded nature. 

Projects include help with the garden, on-going creek/flood cleanup, land clearing to make space for camp-sites, gardens and gathering spaces, and general tidying and landscaping. Some light carpentry and painting too depending on skills you bring to the table. 

2-6hrs 'help' per day would be appreciated. Length of stay depends on how well we like each other. Everyone here speaks English only.

It is a short walk to the bus stop and historic Hills Cafe and Austin Bergstrom Airport is 9 easy highway miles away.

Rustic outdoor (hot) shower/kitchen/privy. Some groceries and shared meals may be available from time to time but mostly folks are on their own. **FREE AND CLEAR ZONE** Fragrance free

name: Giancarlo - Family farm
description:
INTRODUCTION:

We know that life in cities is stressful and hard. Every single day we have got to deal with noice, bad mood, traffic, problems and many similar things. This is the main reason we are always trying to disconnect from this reality and take a deep breathe as well as we charge energies.

We offer you the chance to scape from this situation while you are helping us with a familiar project we have got in the northern rainforest of Peru.

OUR PROJECT

We belong to a long and old coffee producer family. We have got around thirty hectares of lands of which we are working five of them with coffee, cacao and other fruits like oranges and bananas.

Our project consists to develop the farm in an sustaintable and organic way, improving quality of coffee through the right processes without using chemical products (herbicides, pesticides) and protecting the environment.

We also try to improve the family’s quality life.

The team is my family (parents, brothers and cousins) and we all work together to achieve this project.

We all like the spirit of volunteering and the strength of people who really want to discover how is the coffee process while they protect the enviroment.

LOCATION: HOW TO GET THERE

Huaquillo is small village located inTamborapa Pueblo which is a town of San Ignacio distrit, part of Cajamarca region in the north east part of Peru close to the border of Ecuador.

Huaquillo is a beautiful village surrounded by nature and wildlife, it has got amazing cascades and offers the chance of doing good trekings.

You have got two ways to get to Tamborapa Pueblo, from Piura or from Jaen

	Piura – Huancabamba: 7 hours and 30 soles ($9)Huancabamba – Tamborapa Pueblo: 3 hours and 25 soles ($7)

	Jaen – Tamborapa: 4 hours and 25 soles ($7)As son as you arrive to Tamborapa Pueblo you will contact with Victor`s family who live in the town and they will show you the way to go to Huaquillo (20 min in mototaxi and 10 soles ($3) or walkin

name: Edmon - Practicing organic and regenerative farming, mushroom culture and hydroponics
description:
Jem Home farm is located in Guimaras Island, Philippines . A 8 hectares property inspired by Permacuture ethics and design principles. We grow high value crops including fruits, vegetable and culinary  and medicinal herbs. We also culture mushrooms and livestocks including chicken, ducks and pigs.

Mushroom culture and production, Hydroponics gardening, Livestock raising, Organic farming production, Aquaculture production and Natural building activities.

Mudhouses and indigenous or native housing.  Serving 3 meals a day vegetarian and Non-vegetarian foods.

Hands-on in all farm activities. Including cooking and cleaning the room and accommodation. It is 6hours farm activities including classroom lectures. We will have a fellowship or sharing our feedback of activities after dinner. 

Dancing and playing guitar, Fishing and hiking.

name: Paula - Pequeno turismo rural com horta biológica, galinhas, cães, gatos, oliveiras e sobreiros
description:
Olá WWOOFers, chamo-me Paula Silva e vivo com o meu marido Alexandre Coutinho na Herdade da Figueirinha dos Condados, entre as aldeias de São Luís e Relíquias, mais ou menos a 25 km de Vila Nova de Milfontes, no litoral alentejano. A cadelinha Meia Leca, o cão Pirulas, as gatas Alice e Ema fazem parte da nossa família, assim como a gansa Kiwi, o galo Ernesto e as galinhas Esgroviada, Camélia, Princesa, Azeitona e Pintarolas. Temos apenas 6 alojamentos e vivemos off grid com a ajuda de um sistema de energia solar. Construímos o nosso projecto virado para a natureza e para a sustentabilidade e acrescentámos uma piscina biológica.

Alimentar os nossos animais é a primeira tarefa do dia, bem como preparar o pequeno-almoço para todas as pessoas. Depois ao longo do dia há que limpar a piscina, tratar da horta biológica - retirar ervas daninhas, podar, regar, etc. - limpar o terreno em redor das árvores que foram recentemente plantadas, cortar a relva, aprender a cozinhar pratos vegetarianos ou pratos tradicionais portugueses.

As refeições são preparadas em conjunto e são partilhadas normalmente à mesma hora. Alguns dos nossos alojamentos são em tendas - inseridas no conceito de glamping - e é numa tenda que também alojaremos os nossos voluntários, num máximo de duas pessoas.

Gostamos de ler, cozinhar, ouvir música, caminhar, viajar e ir à praia. Nos arredores existem trilhos para caminhar, pequenos restaurantes locais e uma associação na escola primária na aldeia mais próxima - Monte da Estrada, a 3 km - onde há aulas de yoga, uma biblioteca, concertos e outros eventos.

My name is Paula and I live with my husband, Alexandre. We also live with Meia Leca and Pirulas, our dogs, Ema and Alice, our cats, and also our chickens, rooster and a goose. We have 6 small lodgings, including 2 glamping tents, we do biological gardening and we grow vegetables with no chemicals. We have a biological swimming pool and we live off grid with the help of a solar system. We need help with

name: Larry and Helen - Magical self-sufficiency homestead
description:
[Update posted 08/01/25] The Crossing announces a change, a seed planted four years ago. For the past four years, the land belongs, as is fair and good, to a Tribal University and Land Grant University located on the Rosebud Reservation, home of the Sicangu Oyate, in South Dakota.  Our donation of the property was formally accepted by Sinte Gleska University during September of 2020.  Since then, the seed has sprouted and pushed roots of beginnings down into the soil and the sprout and it's cotyledons have come out into the sun. Last August, with an amazing gathering here of a group of people from The University, inipis (sweat lodge, in Lakota) were poured and prayers sent up to Creator, and the transition from private ownership to the establishment of Sinte Gleska University's welcomed and growing presence is forever engaged – the first true leaf has opened to the sunlight.  Helen and I will share more about the future of The Crossing and the direction by Sinte Gleska University when you get here, sharing for now that food sovereignty and herbal medicine, in the context of acceptance and maintenance of an indigenous way of life are what you will experience.

	After reviewing the introduction to The Crossing presented here, if your passion and experience fit the need just described, please get in touch and explore the possibilities with us.  The following three titled paragraphs describe typical activities during the coming season. The titles are from the Lakota names for the Moons associated with the coming season.

Waniyetucokan Wi   -   Middle of Winter Moon   (December)

	A couple of the wood stoves have fires 24/7 now and the sauna in The Cabin offers warmth around the clock.  Vigilance is kept for the colder nights when Mamie's Garden needs fires in her two wood stoves.  Greens, like kale, spinach, lettuce and Swiss chard, grace every meal.  The hoop house root crops, like carrots, beets and Asian radishes are coming on and are sweetened by the cool days. Repa
_wwoof_travel: Louisville, KY (airport or bus), Paoli, IN (bus), Bloomington (bus)

name: Pascal - Maison d'hôte avec 500 m2 de potager, 53 oliviers, arbres fruitiers, ruches
description:
bonjour à tous, mon nom est Pascal, 64 ans, après des années de vie à Los Angeles et Bruxelles et différents métiers, j'ai décidé de m'installer ici à Essaouira. Une maison d'hôte a été crée avec comme buts : donner du travail aux locaux, promouvoir l'écologie participative et fournir à nos clients, employés des produits bio dans un cadre enchanteur respectant au maximum les grands principes d'éco-responsabilité, à savoir, les restes sont donnés aux poules et les restes non consommables vont au compost, nos poules nous fournissent des oeufs, nos ruches du miel, nos olivier  de l'huile pour notre consommation pendant 1 an et un grand potager et une serre dédiée aux plantes tropicales. le TOUT sans aucun engrais sauf notre compost et des crottes de chèvre et moutons. Les plantes sont soignée par des solutions bio comme les olivier avec un mélange savon noir et lait aspergé. Nous avons un couple de paons et des hérissons pour manger les scorpions, beaucoup de tortues sauvages et un chien, beldi snoopy et 3 lapins, je ne sais pas pourquoi d'ailleurs ;-)

Une journée type commence à 9h par nourrir les poules et les paons ensuite le potager avec tout le travail habituel et classique d'un potager : enlever les mauvaises herbes, planter, cueillir, gérer, etc...s'occuper des abeilles. parfois des petits travaux de bricolage, ramasser les noix d'arganier, ramasser les olives,....bref une journée classique. horaire 9h à 11H  - 11H le thé avec les ouvriers ( 30 minutes)  de 11H30 à 13h30 déjeuner avec les ouvriers FIN DE JOURNEE Le dimanche il faudra simplement nourrir les poules.

il y a deux maisons : une chambre sera consacrée au WWOOFEUR avec douche et grand lit, il y a une cuisine, frigo, Airco etc...je suis seul donc on pourra cuisiner ensemble ou parfois la cuisinière de la maison d'hôte pourra le faire.

moi j'en ai pas des masses mais dans le ranch, ill y a la piscine, une piste de pétanque, jeu de fléchettes et ping pong. sinon pour 10 dirhams , le Wwoffeur peut aller

name: Igor - Meu quintal tem aproximadamente 300m². Nele tem árvores frutíferas, hortaliças etc.
description:
Olá Wwoofers! Meu nome é Igor, nasci em 1980. Sou brasileiro, professor de Agroecologia e Educação do Campo na Universidade Federal Rural do Rio de Janeiro em Seropédica-RJ. Moro sozinho (sem animais) em uma casa com quintal (terreno total de 400m²) na área urbana de Guapimirim-RJ, a cerca de 1h30min do centro da cidade do Rio de Janeiro. Eu também tenho uma propriedade coletiva na comunidade rural do Fojo, também em Guapimirim, e faço parte da Associação de Produtores Rurais da Microbacia do Fojo (AFOJO - https://www.instagram.com/afojo.guapi/).

No meu quintal, as principais tarefas são: manejos de árvores frutíferas - banana, coco, tangerina, limão, abacate, goiaba, acerola, cacau, café; manejo de hortaliças - couve, alface, tomate, temperos etc.; compostagem caseira; manejo de um tanque de água da chuva; manejo das bananeiras do tanque de evapotranspiração (fossa ecológica). Posso ensinar como manejar tudo isso, e gostaria de adquirir novos conhecimentos também! Eventualmente, os Woofers poderão participar de atividades em outros sítios, de parceiros da Afojo (a combinar previamente).

Minha casa tem 2 quartos e 2 banheiros, além de uma sala de estar com sofá-cama e é possível acampar no quintal. Tenhos uma cozinha ampla onde podemos preparar juntos refeições diversas. Eu não tenho restrições alimentares, mas em geral como pouca carne em casa. Sempre prefiro alimentação orgânica - aquilo que não é produção própria, eu compro na Feira da Agricultura Familiar de Guapimirim. Eventualmente, compro alimentos também nos mercados locais.

Guapimirim é um município de natureza exuberante, com cachoeiras, trilhas, florestas, montanhas e o espetacular Parque Nacional da Serra dos Órgãos. No tempo livre, os Woofers podem aproveitar para conhecer! Também gosto muito de música e de conversar sobre cultura, viagens etc.

¡Hola, Wwoofers! Me llamo Igor, nací en 1980. Soy brasileño y profesor de Agroecología y Educación Rural en la Universidad Federal Rural de Río de Janeiro, e
_wwoof_travel: Ponto de ônibus "Eskina do pão" (Rua Claudionor Batista, Guapimirim-RJ)

name: Štefan - Agritourism Destination
description:
We are a small eco tourist farm, adopting a holistic management style. Most of our livestock are in the mountains grazing, because of that part of the work, during the summer, is going hiking to check on them and take care of some. we want to help people create a connection with their food and understand what is needed for growing it, and gaining respect for food.
Most of the work during the summer consists of gardening a herbal garden, working with bees, doing DIY projects around the house

Certifications: Kon-cert d.o.o.

Current projects: Finishing a herbal garden
Finishing the apiary for apitherapy practices
Gathering wild and homegrown herbs for the tea
Preparing a pasture system for the laying hens
Renovating the dog house

Internet access available

name: Fook sun lee/simon - Mixed Farm
description:
My partner and I have a 5 acre undulating land that is now planted with a variety of fruit tress, vegetables and supplemented with diary goats.  We need able bodied volunteers who are interested in doing general maintenance around the farm and farm management.  There are always new things to do in the farm especially on maintenance of the premises and fruit trees, treatment of soil.  Landscaping of unexplored area within the farm will be challenging.

Organic methods: The farm is not certified organic yet.  However, we are adopting natural farming by creating the ecosystem within the farming area.  No pesticides or growth hormones are used within the farm.
_wwoof_travel: Batu Arang, Selangor.  Will pick up from nearby train station or at bus terminal at Kuala Lumpur.

name: RAMON DE JESUS - Trabajamos con abejas, tenemos varios apiarios, ruta apicola, area de ventas.San Mateo Boyaca COLOMB
description:
HOLA WWOFERS, vivimos con mi pareja en un pueblito muy chico de BOYACA, COLOMBIA. hace35 años me dedico a trabajar con las ABEJAS, tenemos granja con abejas, vacas, cabras, es lindo lugar. aparte vivimos en el pueblito y tenemos otros apiarios pequeños.

hacemos diferentes tareas de acuerdo a las necesidades del momento, a veces con abejas, otras con arreglo de la finca et otros animales.
quien este con  nosotros aprendera  variadas cosas para toda la vida.

Los WWOOF tendran alojamiento en la casa del pueblo en SAN MATEO, y en dos casas rurales. La alimentacion sera normal, y los WWOOF casi siempre COLABORARAN en hacerla y mantener todos los espacios limpios. no aceptamos ni licores, ni fumadores, ni  drogas.
jamas se quejaran de tener hambre, pues siempre habra AGUA, MIEL Y POLEN.

NOS ENCANTA  WWOOF  que sepan pintar,  cocinar ,musicos, artesanos, constructores, apicultores...este tierra es de bosques, climas variados, gentes  buenas.

name: Beatriz Stamato - Agrovila Agroecológica com práticas em permacultura e bioconstrução
description:
Nossa associação têm muita satisfação em receber pessoas interessadas em aprender mais sobre Agroecologia e a vida no campo. Somos um grupo de pessoas que decidiram apostar numa vida saudável e sustentável, mantendo um maior contacto com a natureza e as pessoas. É por isso que construimos uma comunidade produtiva no campo. Lá poderemos acomodar voluntários, que poderão ajudar nas tarefas habituais da ecovila. Atualmente existem várias casas em construção e já existem outras casas com habitantes. Entre elas está a Casa Amarela, uma casa comunitária onde os voluntários ficam alguns dias da semana e uma sede na cidade para trocas e repartos. A casa coletiva possui 2 quartos com beliches, banheiro, sala ampla e cozinha. Você terá toda a comida necessária para cozinhar. A ecovila está localizada a menos de 10 quilômetros da cidade e pode ser alcançada de carro por uma estrada de terra em boas condições.

Há culturas que já estão em fase de produção: macadâmias, caquis e pereiras. Dependendo da época do ano, serão realizadas tarefas de poda, colheita ou plantio. Há também animais: ovelhas crioulas, cavalos, coelhos e abelhas. O pastoreio rotativo é realizado para manter o crescimento da biomassa. A Escola está em processo de reforma com projeto de bioconstrução. A voluntária poderá participar nas tarefas que mais lhe interessam.

O Instituto tem sede na cidade onde acontecem semanalmente as atividades no território (áreas de assentamento do MST e comunidades rurais) e atividade de Economia Solidário como Feiras, eventos e repartos. Además del trabajo en la ecovilla, la voluntaria podrá también colaborar con la ONG, onde ocorre todo tipo de servicios, espacios culturales, de lazer e mercado.

Pedimos que as pessoas façam entre 20 e 25 horas por semana, distribuídas conforme combinado com o pessoal do local. Gostaríamos que a pessoa interessada pudesse passar pelo menos 3 meses conosco, a fim de desenvolver uma experiência completa e aprender com as diferentes áreas do noss
_wwoof_travel: A cidade é acessível por ônibus (autocarro) por rodovia expressa a partir da cidade São Paulo, aeroporto central brasileiro

name: Marcos - La finca de permaculture est situés dans les montagnes et la jungle de Osa sur la côte pacifique sud
description:
🌿 Bienvenue chez Arbol Essence 🌿
Reconnexion • Simplicité • Harmonie

Je m’appelle Marco, fondateur du projet Arbol Essence ici, nous travaillons a créé ensemble, ici,  notre vision dans la péninsule d’Osa au Costa Rica.  Celle de bâtir un lieu vivant, autonome et aligné avec la nature, où l’on peut se reconnecter à l’essentiel, apprendre, partager et évoluer simplement.

Ce projet se construit chaque jour avec les personnes qui passent ici, les volontaires, les amis, et les collaborateurs locaux et internationaux.

Nous sommes heureux de vous accueillir dans ce sanctuaire vivant. Voici l’essentiel de notre accord de vie pour un séjour harmonieux :

✨ Nos 4 Piliers (Accords Toltèques)
• Parole Impeccable : Communiquer avec bienveillance.
• Rien de Personnel : Chacun vit son propre chemin.
• Pas de Suppositions : Poser des questions, tout simplement.
• Faire de son Mieux : Contribuer positivement à l'énergie du lieu.

💧 Respect du Vivant & Écologie
• Produits 100% biodégradables : Seuls les produits fournis ou vendus à la boutique sont autorisés pour protéger notre écosystème.
• L’eau est précieuse : Fermez bien les robinets. Un oubli peut vider les réservoirs et couper l'eau pour tout le monde.
• Énergie solaire : Éteignez lumières et ventilateurs après usage.

🏠 Vie Collective & Sécurité
• Zéro nourriture en chambre : Pour éviter d'attirer les insectes et animaux.
• Pas de flammes : Bougies et encens sont interdits dans les cabinas.
• Clarté & présence : Site sans tabac, sans vapotage et sans alcool/substances.

🍲 Infos Pratiques
• Repas : Petit-déjeuner (6h–7h) | Déjeuner (12h–13h) | Dîner (17h–18h)
• Urgence : Trousse de premiers soins disponible près des sanitaires et de la cuisine

🚖 Transport (urgence)
Taxi Uvita (WhatsApp) : +506 8853 9090 / +506 8403 3629

💚 Ce que nous proposons

✅ Une expérience de vie durable en communauté
✅ Une immersion en nature sauvage et inspirante
✅ Une découverte de la permaculture et de l’autonomie

"Cultivons ensemble un espace h
_wwoof_travel: SJO santa maria Costa Rica pour l'aéroport et uvita pour le bus mais en fait il peu vous laissez au coin de la rue ici.

name: Deanna - Tiny, sustainability-focused, off-grid homestead
description:
Hello! We are a sustainability-focused, off-grid, 43-acre homestead nestled into the Colorado Rockies with breath-taking mountain views, glorious night skies, abundant wildlife, and bright wildflowers in a pinon-yucca forest. We tend to a flock of chickens for eggs and occasional meat, 2 greenhouses and some outdoor garden beds for fruits and veggies, and keep bees for pollination, honey, and wax. We sell eggs and honey to our local community.  Projects WWOOFers might be asked to help with include, but may not be limited to, growing/harvesting/preserving garden produce, seed saving, beekeeping, animal husbandry (and harvesting if you wish), construction, landscaping/trail building, vehicle/tool repair and maintenance, hunting/butchering (if you wish), and firewood harvesting/processing. We ask that you work a minimum of 24 hrs/wk if an individual, and 36 hrs/wk if two people cohabitating.

Our area is very rural so having your own vehicle is necessary. Our closest town is a 20-min drive away and boasts 2 small grocery stores (1 standard and the other emphasizing health food), a couple of gas stations, a small thrift store, a couple of restaurants, and an occasional community event. More amenities, fairs, and events are available in towns 35-70 minutes away but there is not much to be said for any kind of urban nightlife nearby. There is, however, plenty of access to hiking, camping, mountain biking, hot springs, fishing, hunting, rafting, rock climbing, star gazing (with almost no light pollution), bird watching, hunting, snow sports, etc. If you enjoy the great outdoors, you can’t be bored here!

You’re welcome to bring your own camper/van/tiny house to live in, or we can offer housing in our 35-foot camper that is insulated and heated. It has its own fully furnished kitchen with stove/oven and newer larger refrigerator/freezer. It has its own bathroom, with a shower. There is heated running water. It has a queen-sized bed and a couch that folds out into a 1-2 pers

name: Jordan - Family farm
description:
Somos una familia que nos dedicamos a cultivar y producir cafe oroganico y banano, nos preocupamos por cuidar y proteger el medio ambiente y la biodiversidad que nos rodea,

La finca esta rodeada por la naturaleza, cascadas, quebradas y bellos lugares turisticos por conocer.

Las actividades que realizamos en el campo son manualmente, actividades como: cosecha selectiva, manejo agronómico, labores culturales sin contaminación ambiental, protección de la fauna y la flora.

Tenemos nuestro objetivo como granja, nuestros conocimientos y experiencias en el campo, las ideas intercambiables, para fortalecer las capacidades y aprender más sobre la agricultura orgánica.

Organic methods: Tenemos una metodologia de controlar las plagas y enfermedades en los cultivos mediante un sistema organico,preparacion de bioles , caldos sulfocalcicos, que estan permitidos por la certificacion organica . El uso de productos quimicos esta prohibido en la finca.

Certifications: Nuestro producto tiene certificación orgánica- por medio de la CACE AP mediante IMO CONTROL

Current projects: Actualmente trabajamos  formulando proyectos productivos, por ahoar estamos ejecutando un proyecto en la erradicacion del trabajo infantil y un proyecto cafe sostenible con el soporte de una cooperativa.

Hemos constituido una Cooperativa  que se denomina Cooperativa Oxapampa de la Reserva Biosfera Asháninca Yanesha, en el cual están asociados productores de banano y café especialmente personas de las comunidades nativas ,con el fin de brindar asistencia técnica en campo, acopio y comercialización  de los productos orgánicos que cultivamos con responsabilidad social y protección del medio ambiente.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel:
La finca esta ubicada a 30 minutos de la ciudad de Villa Rica, con movilidad, taxi, motocicleta.
con un acceso favorable (carretera asfaltada) rodeado por mucha vegetación a una  altitud de 1200 a 1500 msnm

name: MERCEDES - La Aguada is a medicinal distillation laboratory in the mountanous center of Argentina.
description:
My name is Mer, I live in my family-owned land in the mountains of Argentina with my 6 years old son Samu and our dog Cane. Within this land called Villa Sol (16 hectares / 16 houses neighbourhood), I created a medicinal laboratory and an aquatics arts garden that includes a solar powered dome-pool. We harvest wild natives and plants we grow to make essential oils and hydrosols. My. next goal is to start growing food, start tending chickens, and to create a dry toilet bath house next to my dome pool. The land has a lot of space and potential for all kinds of initiatives. It is very kid friendly.

We prepare the land for planting, we plant, we harvest wild natives or whatever we have planted, we dry some of the plants when needed, we clean the laboratory, we distill the plants, we create diverse medicinal and culinary alchemies with the essential oils and the hydrosols. I would love to teach wwoofers about the magical process of distillation and how to do it in a way that respects the natural exchange within this particular ecology.

I have two rooms (1 bathroom and 1 big kitchen) in my personal home that can be used to host 2-3 people, and an extra isolated room 50mts away from my home and closer to the laboratory. I am planning to have wwoofers help me set up this room if they rather use this one (we only need to move some bunk beds around). 
My plan for meals is to shop big quantities of staples twice a week, and have wwoofers and I share and coordinate the cooking tasks.

The land is 5min walk away from a beautiful isolated river which I visit often. I can share many sweet natural spots in the area. 
I am an artist; a filmmaker, musician and dancer. So I dedicate a lot of time to these practices and have friends come over once in a while to jam. There is a big saloon in the land where we gather for dancing events as well. 
I do water somatic therapy work in my dome pool once in a while. 
I am a committed mother and love sharing about that journey. 
I love watchin
_wwoof_travel: Train station is 20min walk, bus station is 5min walk, airport is 1:30hs away

name: Christina - Offgrid  & Remote Alaskan Homestead
description:
Ever dreamed of coming to Alaska? 
We sold our 110 acre farm in VA and purchased a 4 acre property on a remote island in SE Alaska. 

Edna Bay Alaska is a very small community that consists of 40 residents. 

We are a conventional family of 4. We have goals to create a garden, build a greenhouse, build housing for rabbits, quail, and chickens. 

We have a duplex with each until consisting of 2 bedrooms and 1 bathroom on each side. We are completely offgrid and depend on solar panels and generators as well as propane for cooking. 

To get to Edna Bay, you can fly to Ketchikan then fly to Klawock. And we would bring you on boat to the island.

name: Karl - Educational Centre, Agritourism Destination, Homestead, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat
description:
Our farm is a uniquely situated eco-lodge and wildlife refuge located just 3.9 km from the Zamora terminal via a lovely winding access road to the Northwest. As one of only 36 classified biodiversity hot-spots remaining on planet earth, our permaculture farm is bordered by the massive and stunning Andes mountains and Podacarpus National Park along the southern border near Peru. Protected by conservation efforts, the park boasts an awe-inspiring and alluring alias as "The Botanical Gardens of the Americas". It is no wonder the acclaimed naturalist Alexander Von Humboldt fell enamored and spent several years in Ecuador during his exploration of the continent. 

This region of Ecuador classifies as a megadiverse "Upper Amazon Basin Andean Tropical Mountain Rainforest". As it is near the high peaks and lagoons of the chilly Loja Province, Zamora possesses an enchanting quality about it with perpetually changing cloud cover, intense amazonian sun, yet with spells of heavy rain and cool refreshing nights! Such attributes combined with the spectacular array of endemic flora and fauna only found here make our farm pretty special! It earns its name from the native and regenerative bamboo species. We strive to use all local materials in a peaceful and sustainable co-existence with the surrounding land. Here it is well worth an extended stay and visit to soak it all in and re-connect with our primitive selves. Be it hiking to the cold lakes of 3500M altitude or sipping coconuts on the beaches of the Bombuscara river or the town malecon, this truly is an outdoor adventurers or nature lovers paradise! 

Lastly and perhaps most importantly, this conservation area represents a safe space and serves as a refuge and sanctuary for both humans and all living species flora and fauna alike. Where clean air and water are at the top of the totem pole in terms of truly intrinsic value. An off grid farm to both disconnect, re-connect and re-charge simultaneously. A place to share and exchan

name: Goran - Old ways of living in a village next to the Skadar lake
description:
Hello Stranger,
My name is Goran and I would love to know you better and to get some help from you with DIY project I'm working on with my parents for couple of years now.
I'm an IT engineer who fled from digital world into the countryside with one goal in mind: to be closer to the nature and to preserve old ways of living and the life itself in the remote rural areas of our beautiful country. 
Our property was abandoned for couple of generations and everything was covered in bushes and ruined, old house, old mill on the creek, vineyards, drywalls, retaining walls..etc. 
Step by step, we are bringing life back into our countryside, doing hard work and enjoying the freedom and the beautiful nature in the national park Skadar lake. 
We aim to be eco friendly and self sustainable, we blend into the nature and we learn from the nature.
We need help from you who knows how to build things with stone and wood, how to plant a tree or cut the grass, how to take care of animals or plants, how to be the best version of yourself day by day, how to try and fail and then try again. You, who cares for others and not just for yourself, who is willing to stay for a longer period and to make an impact, to commit yourself to a common goal: bringing life and joy to our beautiful village countryside and to preserve old ways of living from oblivion.

Every season is different around here so is the work we do. Usually we talk about things that need to be done the next days in the evenings and then we get up early in the morning, we drink coffee and talk more about our tasks for that day.

We cook and eat together and have communal kitchen and barbecue area.

My hobbies and interests are vast. Some of them include mosaic making, woodworking, mushroom picking, MTB, fishing, hiking, wild swimming, cliff diving etc. All of those I do I'm close by areas of my beautiful countryside.

name: Kevin Holden and - Community of passionate farmers living & working on approximately 6 acre regenerative urban farm
description:
We are a small community of passionate and motivated farmers living and working on an approximately 6 acre regenerative urban farm located a few miles south of downtown Phoenix, Arizona. We started with nothing and over the course of a few short years have planted 800 fruit trees, built 2 tiny homes, and started a 2,000 sq ft. vegetable garden. We are looking for similar passionate and motivated individuals interested in nature and agriculture to help us on our journey while learning in the process. Daily activities will vary and will be catered to your interests and may also include working in the orchard and building infrastructure alongside the owner, an engineer and 20 year student of nature and regenerative agriculture. We back up against South Mountain Nature Preserve and are close enough to Phoenix that there is plenty to do on your time off. Come be a part of the future of farming!

We are now accepting applicants for our Fall through Early Spring season! Some planned projects include:
Growing Microgreens
Orchard care
Building a chicken run
Garden planting/maintenance
Building a pole barn
Designing, decorating and opening a farm store/stand
Building a tiny house, community center
Etc, etc, etc.

We are also open to projects that fit your skill set/interests! We have limited accommodations so wwoofers with a camper, van, RV, etc are particularly welcome. Please reach out to learn more and inquire about staying with us, and one of our community members will get back to you with a questionnaire so we can get to know you better. 

Organic methods: Regenerative
Organic
Biologic
No-till
Living with nature

Certifications: We follow the principles of regenerative organic farming; not currently certified due to cost and time.

Current projects: Growing Microgreens Mulching orchard trees
Chicken coop construction
Farm stand construction
Pole barn construction
Drip / spray irrigation
Solar power expansion
Community area design
Social media / website construction
Raisi
_wwoof_travel: Phoenix Sky Harbor Airport, Phoenix Bus Station

name: Mads - Dairy Farm
description:
Valla Farm is located on an beautiful island Handnesøya, we fantastic scenery with the nature forces close on our daily life. From sunny nights all summer long, to the cold, dark and storm full winter. And we must not forget the fantastic Aurora Borealis that often comes for a visit in the winter and late autumn. 

The farm is one of the oldest Organic Milk farms in Norway, it was totally organic until 2025. Due to changes in the dairy contract, we had to partly convert to non organic, some of the fields fare from the farm now get fertilizer. But the farm and the island was pioneers in the Organic Farming when they started up, and the knowledge they collected is still in use today. 

Mads bought the farm February 2018 and Stina moved in 2026🤗

There is around 28 milking cows on the farm, in total there is around 70 cattle on the farm at all times. We produce around 200 tons of milk a year and harvest grass on around 50 hectare. 

There is also two cats on the farm, Johnanne and Stein. 

 (Sadly enough daisy passed away in July 2023.) 

You will be joining in on the mainly tasks on an daily basis. mostly that would be doing the barn, milking, cleaning and feeding ☺️
Other than that it’s all season and weather based.
You will get the possibility to learn new skills, get more knowledge about organic farming and Norwegian farming in general.
My motivation is that you as a woofer can grow as a person and go home with new experiences and skills. 
You most likely will do stuff that you never dreamed of mastering by your own☺️ here we let everyone try something if they want to👏🏻 learning by doing, and sometimes failing. 

We eat dinner after evening barn and have Breakfast/lunch after morning barn and discuss the work of the day.  food are available in the fridge at any time. 

We are attending the local life on the island when there are things happening🤗 like 17. May, which is the national day of Norway, and is a big celebration. Or if there is a party at the local communi

name: Susan - Unique garden in stunning artists' community
description:
Our property is small and certainly does not qualify as a farm. The vegetable gardens are contained in two small boats, and the hillside is covered with perennials. No herbicides or pesticides are used on either. The location is stunning and the recreational opportunities are delightful for anyone who loves the outdoors.

Our projects include help with gardening  (we are both over 80 and not as agile as we once were!) and other projects like painting, hauling firewood, brushing out trails, etc. Now and then we need help bringing groceries from the water taxi up to our house. I (Susan) am often here alone and like to know there is a capable person nearby.

We are located in Halibut Cove, Alaska, on Kachemak Bay across from the little town of Homer. Access is by boat only. Halibut Cove is a very special community of around 150 people. There is a small restaurant, a tiny coffee house, a few small lodges and cabin rentals. There are many trails on the island including one which accommodates four-wheelers but the half-mile trail to our property is a foot trail. People get around by boat or on foot if they do not have four-wheelers (we do not!). We have several kayaks available for use.

The Cove is known as an artists’ community. There are painters and potters, jewelry makers and photographers. I (Susan) am a writer and had wonderful a career that included work in conservation, post-secondary education, and healthcare. David is a published poet and spent his working life as a bush pilot flying all over Alaska.

This part of Alaska is stunnng. The hills and mountains are heavily forested, and glaciers hang in most valleys. We have eagles nesting nearby, and often have small black bears on the island. Sea otters and whales populate the Bay.

There is always work around the island for strong young people:  sometimes construction, sometimes gardening, sometimes cleaning of rental cabins, sometimes splitting and stacking firewood, etc. I am certain there is enough to keep a W

name: Mikkel - Family home and garden - rural, Educational Centre, Hobby Farm, Flower Garden Farm, Horse Ranch
description:
A family of 2 but sometimes 3.
Me (Mikkel) and my wife Stefani. Occasionally our daughter comes home and stays.

We are a small-scale vegetable grower and mostly for private use but would like to have some small shop to sell locally. 

We also have a larger business with education of horses and horse owners so we travel quite a lot with that work.

We have 3 dogs and about 12 horses. It varies on the season.

We are both Danish but speak English and Swedish fluently also. 

Organic methods: Doing our best and mostly trial and error. So hard work... :-)

Certifications: We are not a commercial farm so we don't need it.

Current projects: there are always projects. 

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Hässleholm is the nearest town. Copenhagen is the nearest airport.

name: Eric - Essential Oil Distillation, Wild Crafting, Forestry, and Poetry
description:
Greetings WWOOFers! Welcome to our high desert mountain sanctuary in Southern Utah. House of Aromatics is a primarily a one man show, run by Eric Scott Bresselsmith, with the help of incredible WWOOFers, volunteers, friends and neighbors. The still runs year round to create wild crafted essential oils and maintain the local forests. 

The Mission of House of Aromatics is to help people to connect with their Heart Selves, their Elemental Nature & to remember "For the Benefit of All". Spiraling the greed out of Alchemy has become the primary message that the Trees & Plants have been giving as they go through the birthing process from standing in their natural community to be transformed into essential oil to move through the world of humans.

The Work 
As a member of the crew, you will participate in an alchemical collaboration with the local Plants and Trees. The work is very physical and deeply rooted in the seasons.
Primary activities include:  
- Wild-Harvesting: Collecting and hand-processing plant material, such as Pinion Pine, Utah Juniper, and White Fir, from the surrounding forests. This involves using hand loppers and saws to gather and prepare the plant material or "charge".
- The Distillation Process: Learn the entire chemical process of distilling oils and the tools to do so.
- Physical Labor: Managing the "retort" (a 50-gallon barrel) full of plant material in and out of the shed. This includes climbing up onto the retort to “dance” and pack material in, as well as loading and unloading the still.
- Forest Stewardship: Learning sustainable harvesting and forest health.
- Seasonal Markets: In season, you will join us at our community market to assist with the outreach and sale of our essential oils. You can get a great burrito and see what the rest of this group is sellin'.
- Land Projects: General maintenance, housekeeping, and other projects around our little land plot and creative distillation workspace. 

Accommodations
We are located in a remote, bea

name: Pedro Vicente - Homestead
description:
Somos una familia que vive en una granja organica , con animales , huerta , invernadero , espacios para niños, cabañas para turistas, restaurant de campo, camping, trabajamos como granja educativa, recreativa, turistica y productiva, producimos nuestros propios alimentos y para la venta, tenemos pileta de natacion y 7 has de espacio verde.

Organic methods: Abonos naturales ( humus, compost, gallinaza, bocashi ) rotacion y asociacion de cultivos, flores y hierbas aromaticas acompañantes, mulching, no dig, produccion de almacigos, desmalezado manual, etc.

Certifications: tuvimos certificacion hace 4 años atras pero dejamos de pagarla por el alto costo y debido a que la cantidad de productos que comercializabamos era pequeña y no justificaba tal costo.

Current projects: Visitas escolares, cuidado de huerta organica, cuidado del jardin , produccion de almacigos, construccion y diseño de carteles para la huerta, ayuda en grabacion de videos para redes sociales, elaboracion de abonos organicos, ayuda en construccion de nuevos corrales para animales, etc

Internet access available

name: Cristina - Family home and garden - rural
description:
Somos una pareja, Cristina y Sébastien, nuestro hogar lo bautizamos como “AlasRaices” y está ubicado en Ourol, en la Mariña Lucense, Galicia.

Detrás de nuestra vuelta al rural, hay una historia de amor de pareja y también, a la tierra, al agua, a la madera, a vivir lejos de la ciudad. Creemos en otros valores de equilibrio con uno mismo, con la naturaleza y en crear redes más humanas y profundas.

Y desde Abril de 2026 un nuevo miembro llegó en la familia de AlasRaices el bebe Noé, nuestro primer hijo que llena la casa de ternura y mucho amor!

Yo, Sébastien llego desde Francia como voluntario de Workaway a Xerdiz, Ourol, mi furgoneta se estropeo aquí y me quede en estas tierras trabajando. Yo Cristina, soy Gallega, llegue a conocer proyectos comunitarios a este lugar y aquí nos encontramos,

El nombre de nuestra casa es “AlasRaices” nombre que nace porque en su puerta hay un Angel tallado en piedra y los dos antes de conocernos, tuvimos muchas Alas, fuimos nomadas, viajamos y vivimos en varios países de Sudamérica y ahora estamos sintiendo echar Raíces en este lugar de campo cerca del mar.

Seb es Carpintero, le encanta el mar y practica KiteSurf, yo soy trabajadora Social, terapeuta Gestal, formadora, también me gusta la pintura y fotografía. 

Llegamos a esta casa en Febrero del 2021, estamos en proceso de rehabilitación hasta el momento tenemos baño, cocina y una habitación. Además de la caravana roulotte en la que vivimos el primer año.

Nos encanta conocer gente de otros países y culturas, ya que tenemos amigos de muchos lugares del mundo. Para nosotros es muy enriquecedor el intercambio cultural y conocer a gente que también valora el regreso a la naturaleza como forma de vida.

Seb, salio de Francia con su furgoneta llena de herramientas, su gato y su caravana Roulotte, desde Francia a Galicia por la costa. Y estuvo durante tres años ayudando a muchas personas haciendo su trabajo voluntario de carpintero en Workaway, Woofer, Helpx, ahora es él quién necesit
_wwoof_travel: Estación de bus y tren de Viveiro. Lugo y areropuerto de Coruña.

name: Warren - Small town urban home with organic garden close to amenities and recreational opportunities.
description:
I'm a recently retired man who lives on my own on a lovely property in the historic neighbourhood of a friendly and progressive small town on Vancouver Island. I have an active social life and love to get outdoors or in the garden as much as possible. My friends say I'm fun, relaxed and kind, and have a good sense of humour. There are no pets that live with me although there are some demanding hummingbirds living in the yard that get annoyed if I don't keep their feeders well stocked.

I'm looking for some help with a few interesting garden projects that are too much for me to do on my own (or would take me too long) and I'd love to share the learning and accomplishments with someone who is keen to learn some skills and see big results from the work we will do together. 

The main projects are to set up a greenhouse in the back yard, build some new blueberry beds in the side yard, renovate my front yard into a native plant garden, rebuild some stone pathways, and other garden planting. The greenhouse, blueberry garden and native plant garden projects will also involve setting up drip irrigation systems.

An average day would involve some focussed time on one of the main projects on the list below. These projects will provide opportunities to learn a lot of urban gardening skills and specific knowledge related to each project. If time allows there may also be some general gardening tasks such as weeding and going to nurseries. 

The specific main projects to accomplish together include:

GREENHOUSE (10' x 6.5' x 8')
-- helping with foundations, including digging some trenches and filling with gravel, and assembling a wooden foundation 12" high (this may already be done by the time a WWOOFer arrives)
-- assembling and erecting the Planta greenhouse (2-3 days work)
-- preparing raised beds inside the greenhouse for tomatoes
-- installing a drip irrigation system and hose tap
-- planting tomatoes in the beds and peppers in pots

BLUEBERRY GARDEN
-- preparing the ground
_wwoof_travel: Comox YYQ, Courtenay bus depot, IslandLink bus

name: Alexandros - Olive Grove Homestead & Creative Retreat
description:
Welcome to my sanctuary in the olive groves of Thassos.

I’m Alex — I work with weddings, the land, and tourism. In 2018, I quit an office job and life in the city and returned to our family’s old holiday house to give it and myself a new life. In this valley, the days follow the rhythm of the earth: pruning olive trees, harvesting figs and grapes, tending vegetables, or creating small spaces for guests to enjoy under the stars. It’s a simple, soulful life, rooted in the cycles of nature and the joy of shared exchange.

🌱 The Exchange

Here, contribution is not measured in hours but in balance. Some days are light — keeping the property in good order, cleaning around, garden projects — other days may ask more energy — cutting wood, tending to the earth, or helping with a creative project.

The flow is flexible, adaptive, and seasonal, and follows the needs of the earth.

Olive pruning (March & April)
Soil prep & planting (April)
Figs (drying, preserving) (August) 
Grapes & winemaking (5-15th September)
Olive harvest (10-20th October)
Wood gathering & other projects (November & December)  

Sometimes: Gardening, planting, creative or building projects around the homestead, occasional help with the glamping retreat and hosting.

🌌 Life in the Valley

In this serene homestead surrounded by nature, the days are simple - mornings of work, meals shared with fresh ingredients, evenings under vast starry skies. The sea is close for swimming, the hills offer hiking paths, and the Monday market in Prinos brings the colors and flavors of the island.

Now and then, we step out together — joining a local wedding celebration, discovering folklore, or dancing barefoot at a summer beach party. These moments of joy and community balance the calm of valley life, offering a taste of the island’s vibrant spirit.

We cook together, exchange stories, and sometimes explore healing practices, music, or photography. Volunteers often say they feel both productive and deeply rested here — as

name: Severin - Dreiseitenhof in Sachsen
description:
Wir sind eine junge Familie, Stefanie (38 Jahre) und Severin (36 Jahre) mit unseren Kindern Felix (6 Jahre) und Moritz (3 Jahre), 2 Katzen (Emil und Findus) und unserem Hund (Kira) und leben seit Juli 2022 auf dem Dreiseitenhof. Seit Sommer diesen Jahres wohnt unsere Mitbewohnerin Romy auf unserem Hof. Unser Hof liegt in einem kleinen Dorf, ca. 30 km östlich von Dresden entfernt, zwischen der Sächsischen Schweiz und der Oberlausitz. Unsere Absicht ist es, den Hof im Sinne der Permakultur als Selbstversorgerhof aufzubauen. Ganz nach dem Motto "Es ist wichtiger, etwas im Kleinen zu tun, als im Großen darüber zu reden." möchten wir Stück für Stück anfangen, unsere Ideen auszubauen und umzusetzen. 
Wir sind nicht in der Landwirtschaft groß geworden und entwickeln unsere Fähigkeiten durch lesen, ausprobieren und lernen. Auch dein Wissen kann hier Platz finden und wir können gemeinsam Pläne und Ideen erarbeiten.

Wir haben ein 1,5 ha großes Grundstück, auf dem derzeit 5 Dahomey-Rinder, ca. 90 Hühner und 6 Ziegen leben. 

Unsere aktuellen Projekte:
- Rinderstall ausmisten
- Gemüsegarten pflegen
- Ziegenstall bauen
- Holz hacken und ins Haus bringen 

Unsere täglichen bzw. wiederkehrenden Aufgaben sind:
- Tiere versorgen
- Gassi gehen

Unsere Mitbewohnerin Romy wird euch die Aufgaben zeigen und euch dabei begleiten und unterstützen. 

Nachdem wir unsere Aufgaben auf dem Hof erledigt haben, verbringen wir die Zeit gern in Gemeinschaft. Wir schätzen eine gute Work-Life-Balance sehr und versuchen sie gut umzusetzen. Die Sächsische Schweiz bietet viel Raum zum Wandern und Dresden ist ebenfalls ein Besuch wert. 

Während deiner/eurer Zeit bei uns seid ihr Teil unserer Familie und wohnt mit bei uns im Haus. Gerne können auch Wwoofer mit (Klein-)Kindern zu uns auf den Hof kommen.

Bitte beachtet das unsere Kinder sich über Besucher freuen und auch gern mit Ihnen spielen :-)

Unser Hof ist nicht geeignet für Schülerpraktikanten und/oder Minderjährige.
_wwoof_travel: Gerne können wir dich/Euch auch vom Bahnhof in Großharthau abholen.

name: Kim - Community Village School who do farming
description:
**Profile Description for WWOOF**

We are a small charity organization based in the tranquil village of Prey Sbat, Takeo Province, Cambodia. Our project focuses on providing free education, agricultural training, and community support to local children and families. The people living here include a dedicated team of educators, volunteers, and a few local families. We have no pets but take pride in our peaceful and welcoming environment.

WWOOFers will be provided with free accommodations, including a private room with a mosquito net, bedding, and two daily meals—lunch and dinner. We aim to create a family-like atmosphere where you’ll feel at home while contributing to our community.

Our farming activities involve sustainable crop cultivation, animal care, and organic gardening. WWOOFers can expect to learn about basic agriculture, animal husbandry, and eco-friendly farming techniques, as well as engage in community education programs. We look forward to teaching volunteers hands-on farming skills while sharing cultural experiences in our beautiful rural setting.

**Typical Farming Activities**

Our farming activities focus on sustainable agriculture, including growing vegetables, fruits, and herbs, as well as animal husbandry. WWOOFers will participate in daily tasks like planting, watering, weeding, and harvesting crops, as well as feeding and caring for animals. We also practice composting and natural pest control methods to maintain an eco-friendly farm.

**What We Teach WWOOFers**
You will learn how to grow food sustainably using local techniques, improve soil health through composting, and manage small-scale livestock. WWOOFers will also gain insights into how farming supports our educational and community initiatives.

**Average Day for a WWOOFer**
A typical day begins with breakfast, followed by 3-4 hours of farming tasks. In the afternoon, WWOOFers might assist with community activities or help with small construction projects. Evenings are free to explore

name: Burak - Smallholding, Family farm
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Kocaeli’ndeki organik çiftliğimizde bizimle beraber çalışacak, hevesli gönüllüler arıyoruz.

Şehirden uzak, doğayla uyumlu bir yaşam arayışının sonunda kendimizi İzmit'in el değmemiş orman köyü Fethiye'de bulduk. 2016 yılında kurulumuna başladığımız çiftliğimizde hem üretim hem de kurulum çalışmalarımız yoğun bir şekilde devam etmektedir. 66 dönüm üzerine kurulu çiftliğimizde sebze, meyve, mikro yeşillik, çiftlik hayvanları, yumurta, bal, fide, fidan ve saksı bitkileri organik tarım yönetmeliğine uygun olarak üretmekteyiz. Amacımız çiftliğimizde üretimini yaptığımız ürünleri yerel organik pazarlarda ve internet üzerinden satarak kendimize yetecek basit huzurlu bir hayat kurmaktır. Bu düşünceyi paylaşan ve doğayı seven herkesi aramızda görmekten büyük mutluluk duyarız.
Çiftlikte yapılması gereken birçok günlük işimiz bulunmaktadır. Örneğin tarlada veya seramızdaki bitkilerin ve hayvanlarımızın bakımları gibi. Bunun yanı sıra taşıma, boyama, tamir, paketleme, temizlik, düzenleme ve buna benzer birçok farklı iş de çiftliğimzde düzenli olarak yaptığımız işler arasındadır. Bu nedenle eminim ki burada herkes keyif aldığı bir iş bulacaktır. Çiftliğimizde gönüllülerimizden kendine uygun bulmadığı bir işi yapmasını zorlamıyoruz ve mümkün oldukça gönüllülerimiz için her gün farklı bir iş ayarlamaya çalışıyoruz. Çalışma saatlerimiz günde 5-6 saat ve haftada 5 gün şeklindedir. Genellikle saat 8’de işe başlanır.
Konaklama için içerisinde perdelerle ayrılmış 2 adet ranzanın bulunduğu maksimum 4 kişilik iki odamız bulunmaktadır. Gönüllüler için ayrı mutfak banyo ve oturma alanı da aynı yapı içerisinde yer almaktadır. Gönüllülerimizden kendi yemeklerini ve temizliklerini kendilerinin yapmasını beklemekteyiz. Tabi ki bu işler için gerekli bütün ürünleri çiftliğimizden veya marketlerden kendimiz tedarik etmekteyiz. Bu sayede konakladığınız sure boyunca mevsimsel organik çiftlik ürünlerin tadına da bakma şansı yakalayacaksınız.
Çiftliğimizde çevre köylerden ye

name: Samuel - Local Community Projects
description:
EXPERIENCE A UNIQUE VOLUNTEER OPPORTUNITIES 
Join us in a volunteer experience where you have complete freedom and flexibility. Engage in projects that interest you, at your own pace, within a friendly, relaxed, and meaningful atmosphere. This trust-based environment offers numerous opportunities for learning and personal growth.

SEEKING PARTNERS 
We are looking for volunteers, donors, well-wishers, organizations, and foundations to Partner with on community projects in Mfangano Island and surrounding areas in Lake Victoria, Kenya. If you are interested in partnering with us, please reach out via email.

ABOUT ME 
My name is Samuel, a 59-year-old teacher living on Mfangano Island. I enjoy meeting people from around the world and sharing enriching experiences. I'm happy to assist you during your stay and even beforehand.

I lead registered community-based organizations aimed at alleviating the suffering/the disadvantaged, fostering societal development, and restoring hope to the less fortunate in the community. Our work focuses on providing access to basic quality education, community healthcare, community food security, and environmental initiatives. We strive to empower women, youths, and local households for sustainable economic development and self-reliance.

In my home, we are over twenty people, including my wives, sons, daughters, grandchildren, and workers. My family and I are committed to offering high hospitality and integrity to all volunteers, we adjust plans to meet volunteers needs.

LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES 
Volunteers will have the opportunity to participate in various farming activities. Learn about local languages (Suba, Luo, and Swahili)) and experience the Suba and Luo cultures, including our ways of living, food preparation, eating, and governance.

 MEALS
We typically serve meals prepared from our own farm produce, including ugali, cassava, porridge, kales, cowpeas, and potatoes. For items we do not grow, such as wheat flour, fish, chicken, meat,
_wwoof_travel: The closest airport is Kisumu international airport and Mbita in Homabay County is our closest bus stop from Nairobi

name: Diana - This is a house in the mountains working on self-sufficiency and building community
description:
Finca el Romeral is a piece of mountain with an amazing view. You will find a house and a dome for camping. This is a project in phase one, in which you will be part cleaning the mountain, reforesting, painting, picking up wood, and other home activities related to the forest or the permaculture project.

Certifications: New farm

Current projects: Actually, I am running an ecoturistic project. It involves reforestation and organic farming. The idea is to bring turists to enjoy a camping day with nature conection.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Small town, 3 hours far from Bogota

name: Linda - Beef cattle raised on irrigated pasture
description:
Our farm is located on the Gila River in the Duncan Valley located in SE Arizona, USA. We raise beef cattle on irrigated pasture utilizing minimum till practices and natural fertilization on the fields.  We use a modified rotational grazing system and gentle handling practices  We also raise paint burros (donkeys) and have a beautiful Watusi steer just to show and enjoy. Our Australian Shepherd "Bear" is our faithful friend and helper.
   We are an older couple with traditional values.  We will consider you  a part of our family of children, grandchildren and great-grandchildren, who live near by. while you stay with us.

When you are with us your experience could include building, checking or maintaining fences; checking cattle; moving cattle; irrigating; brush and weed control; branding and ear tagging in season; gentling and training burros (donkeys); at times operating a utility tractor or dozier; restoring a 1915 barn.
   A typical day would include an early Breakfast followed by daily chores.  Next would be the main activity depending on the day and time of year. The large meal would be Dinner in the middle of the day followed by a rest time.  In the later afternoon, evening chores and a Supper.  
    Weekends would be a great time to enjoy some of the nearby attractions described below.  If you are interested in attending church on Sunday, our small rural valley has 11 different religious denominations.

You will be housed in one of our upstairs bedrooms or you can sleep on cots under the stars when the weather permits.  Our meals are simple, nutritious, home cooked: Breakfast, Dinner, Supper.

We feel the view from our front porch down to the river and beyond to the hills is one of the most beautiful in our valley.   
   Local attractions include the Gila River, hiking, mountain climbing, historical sites, ancient hieroglyphics and bird watching to name a few.  We are blessed with abundant wild life such as javelina, coyotes, deer, an occasional mountain lio
_wwoof_travel: Airport-Tucson, AZ (168 m. away) ; Train - Lordsburg NM (34 m. away);  Bus - Duncan, AZ

name: Naomi - Ferme biologique certifiée à Macouria en Guyane
description:
Je suis jeune agricultrice, certifiée en agriculture biologique. Mon activité de production maraîchère biologique, située sur un terrain de 4ha constitué de savane et de forêt secondaire, est en pleine expansion. Je commercialise mes produits principalement auprès d'une coopérative bio non loin de mon exploitation. 
L'exploitation est située dans une commune rurale, à 30 km de Cayenne le chef-lieu. Elle est entourée d'autres parcelles agricoles et assez proche du bourg de la commune.
J'habite seule sur place et n'ai pour l'instant pas d'animaux. Le logement dans lequel le woofer aura sa chambre dispose de l'électricité et de l'eau courante.

Je propose au woofer de participer aux activités suivantes : 
Semis
Ajustement du plan de culture au fil de l'eau en fonction des contraintes
Veille "santé et protection des végétaux". Reconnaissance des bioagresseurs
Désherbage
Repiquage
Récoltes
Irrigation
Maintenance des équipements
Amendement des planches permanentes
Planification du développement de l'exploitation : préparation des futures surfaces cultivées, 
Réflexions pour l'optimisation des tâches et recherches de solutions innovantes à des problématiques spécifiques

La journée d'un woofer, du lundi au vendredi, pourrait débuter à 7h et se terminer à 12h. Il est mieux pour lui de travailler le matin pour éviter les fortes chaleurs de l'après-midi.
Je travaille également le samedi mais le woofer est libre samedi et dimanche, sauf s'il souhaite travailler le samedi également.
Je prépare les repas d'avance dans un souci de gain de temps. Je n'attends pas spécialement d'aide pour cela :) 

J'aime lire, je suis très curieuse et aime apprendre sur différents sujets, je m'intéresse à la psychologie et je suis bénévole au sein d'un chantier d'insertion.

Loisirs :  Balades en forêt, observation nature, randonnées, séjours en carbet en forêt. Il y a non loin de l'exploitation en activités payantes, un karting, des balades en quad et à cheval. Pour les activités plus citadines,

name: Eva-Maria - Chacra con parque, huerta, frutales, monte
description:
1. Beschreibe dein Projekt, den Ort und seine Bewohner (einschließlich Tiere):
Somos Franco, Eva y Tory. 
La Posta de la Laguna es un lugar donde se viene para tratar bien a el cuerpo y la alma.
Tenemos alojamiento, un parque con piscina, un pequeno monte, una huerta y frutales.
La huerta aporta vegetales para la familia, los huespedes y para vender en la feria vecinal se sobran.
Nuestros projectos estan en fase de construction y crecimiento.
Tenemos a nuestro perro Felix y el gatto Vlady.

2. Liste die Aktivitäten auf, an denen WWOOFer teilnehmen werden, und was du ihnen beibringen möchtest:
Todo los trabajos que ocurren cuando se cultivan vegetales y frutas, como sembrar, plantar, fertilizar, cubrir con mantillo, regar, desmalezar, controlar plagas, cosechar, conservar.
También: cocinar, convertir nuevas tierras en tierras cultivables, producir abono y fertilizantes, hacer lena, recolectar semillas, segar, planear y preparar eventos,...

3. Wie könnte der Tag eines WWOOFers bei dir aussehen (Zeitfenster für die Teilnahme an Aktivitäten, Erwartungen...)?
Comemos todos juntos (desayuno, almuerzo y cena). 
Dependiendo de la época del año, del clima y de la tarea, las mañanas, y la tarde son el mejor momento para trabajar.
Nos esperamos que las personas puedan tambien trabajar de forma independiente y solo.

4. Spiele den Guide! Spreche über deine eigenen Hobbies (Musik, Kochen etc.) und die Möglichkeiten in der Umgebung (Wandern, Schwimmen etc.):
Nuestro lugar es tranquilo. Estamos a 7 km de la ciudad y 4 km del mar. 
Estamos felices de compartir todo lo que sabemos y esperamos aprender de usted también.
_wwoof_travel: La próxima parada de autobús es la terminal de La Paloma, pero el autobús a pedido también para justo en frente de nuestra puerta

name: Titus Hillary - Cocoa, coffee, fruit farming, dairy production, piggery, and poultry, forestry
description:
The family operates a mixed farm in Jinja District, the home of the source of the River Nile in Eastern Uganda, focusing on diverse agricultural activities including cocoa, coffee, fruit farming, dairy production, piggery, and poultry. We are known for innovative practices like yoghurt making, cocoa wine making, using cow dung for fertilizer and other by-products, contributing to sustainable farming.

Our farm serves as a model for other farmers, demonstrating the potential for wealth creation through diversified agriculture.
Expect to do some coffee picking, cocoa harvesting, and feeding the animals on the farm.

You will be served typical Ugandan local foods,

Guest rooms available at the farm with a shared bathroom and toilet outside

Biking, nature walks, water biking, boat rides

name: Hassan - Welcome to Whispering Palms Oasis, a serene and enchanting retreat nestled in the heart of Ktawa
description:
Nestled in the picturesque oasis of Ktawa in the Zagora region, our palm tree garden is a haven of tranquility and natural beauty. Surrounded by the stunning desert landscape, our oasis offers a lush, green contrast to the arid surroundings. Towering palm trees provide shade and shelter, creating a cool, serene environment. The air is filled with the soothing rustle of palm fronds and the occasional chirping of desert birds. 

Our oasis features a traditional Moroccan garden layout, with pathways winding through clusters of palm trees, date palms, and other native plants. Small irrigation channels bring life-giving water to the roots, ensuring the garden thrives even in the challenging desert climate. The garden is dotted with cozy seating areas where you can relax and soak in the peaceful atmosphere.

In addition to the natural beauty, our oasis is a place of cultural richness. The surrounding community is warm and welcoming, offering a glimpse into the traditional way of life in the Moroccan desert.

As a volunteer, you'll have the opportunity to learn about and participate in local customs, cuisine, and agricultural practices.

Come experience the magic of Whispering Palms Oasis, where the desert meets a verdant paradise, and every day is an invitation to reconnect with nature and nurture the land.

Feeding :
At Whispering Palms Oasis, we provide wholesome and traditional Moroccan meals to our volunteers. You’ll enjoy a variety of local dishes, including tagines, couscous, fresh salads, and fruits. Meals are prepared with love, ensuring you experience the rich flavors and culinary traditions of the region. We accommodate dietary preferences and restrictions, so please let us know if you have any special requirements.
Sleeping:
Volunteers will stay in our charming and authentic family house, currently being renovated to provide a comfortable and welcoming environment. The house is made of traditional mud bricks, offering natural cooling and a unique, rustic charm.

name: Leon - Self-sufficiency property with orchard, gardens, and chickens about 40 minutes from Asheville, NC
description:
At our property, we have chickens, an orchard and large gardens. In the winter there is pruning, in spring, planting, tilling and cultivating, in summer, there is cultivating and harvesting, and in fall, there is cider making, canning and harvesting. Ongoing activities include turning the compost piles, burning brush and pruning, mulching, staking plants, and wood cutting.

There are 5 friendly, goofy ( non guard dogs) dogs on the land.  One of us is very allergic to cats, so please no cats. We have two  2 bedroom, fully equipped Airbnb cabins and a guest room in the main house for accommodation.  If they get booked in the busy seasons, folks might have to share a cabin.  
 
We are up in the mountains 40 minutes by car outside of Asheville, NC and Bryson City, TN. Closer towns are Waynesville, NC, Gatlinburg, TN, Maggie Valley, NC and Lake Junaluska. There is the Pisgah National Forest, Biltmore House (in Asheville), restaurants and breweries galore.

We would be glad to host someone who would work half days for half days off, or full days for full days off.

Organic methods: We compost, grow crops, keep chickens, preserve food, grow grapes and fruit trees.

Certifications: We do not use any synthetic pesticides or fertilizers.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Asheville, NC with Airport, Bus Stop ... train someday.

name: Bridget - Real Organic Permaculture Food Forest Farm in Michigan Countryside
description:
We have an ambitious, cutting-edge farm project and business located in Plymouth, Michigan. It is putting into practice a regenerative, permaculture-inspired design that weaves together USDA certified organic market gardening, food forests, land regeneration and re-wilding, and is a demonstration site for these approaches. Gateway is a place of abundance and beauty; a community hub offering nutrient dense food, education, events, sanctuary, and hope for the future.

This is a unique farm business and wonderful learning opportunity for anyone interested in Permaculture, Regenerative Farming, Eco-landscaping and Rewinding. 
 
WWOOFer Daily Activities:
Farm and gardening tasks including caring for the food forest on certified  our organic farm. Help with farm events - workshops, farm to table dinners, markets. We are especially looking for international WWOOFers who like to  cook and teach us new recipes.

We have room for WWOOFers in a house on the farm where our directors Bridget & Charlie live. We also have some tents where WWOOFers can stay on the farm. We are building an out doorshower and bath. And we have a full indoor bathroom is available for use. We do have an indoor/outdoor Farm Cat named Ghost. He is not a fan or welcoming of other pets. 

WWOOFers are welcome to farm food, our kitchen, and the outdoor grill and family meals. 

We are located 10 minuets from Plymouth and Canton city centers that have all the shops that you could need. 20 min from Ypsilanti and Ann Arbor and 30 min from Detroit were you have tons of activities, arts, music, and entertainment opportunities. We are with in a 15-60  min drive of many beautiful lakes, hiking and outdoor activity areas as well.
_wwoof_travel: Ann Arbor Amtrak Station, Detroit Airport

name: Nigel - Family smallholding making Touchwood Cider
description:
We are a family run smallholding, working on organic principles for the last 30 years. We have 14 acres, with another 50 acres on the Lizard peninsula. We have orchards, a large vegetable and soft fruit garden and keep poultry, geese, horses and bees.
We are 5 minutes from the coast and close to many good surfing beaches.
Help required with all sorts of projects, woodland management, and green timber build, as well as the usual seasonal work.
Our busiest time is harvesting apples and crushing for juice/making cider, from September through to December.
We are happy for any unskilled labour, any skills a bonus.
Food is all home produced and eaten together.
Accommodation is in a caravan, and there is use of a bathroom 20m from the caravan in the main house. 

Organic methods: Our Farm operates on Organic principles for the last 30 years.

Certifications: were certified organic by soil association back in 1986 and have never had need to pay to be certified organic since!

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Redruth train station, Truro coach station

name: Alexandre & Florence - Ferme maraichère  et pépinière bio
description:
Bonjour,

Nous sommes un couple de 39 ans installés en maraichage biologique  depuis fin 2017 et pépinière depuis avril 2022, labellisés AB .
Nous avons quatre filles de 7  à 14 ans et garçon né en janvier 2025.

Notre modèle de maraîchage  est celui proposé initialement proposé (maraichage bio-intensif) par Jean-Martin Fortier et repris depuis en France, notamment par Sylvain Coudert. Nous nous inspirons de différents mouvements : maraîchage sur sol vivant, permaculture, concept de jardin forêt, agroforesterie . 
La pépinière est une pépinière de plants potagers, fleurs, aromatiques, médicinales, petits fruits et une petite gamme en diversification d'ornementales. 

La commercialisation se fait sur la ferme et en vente directe dans un rayon de 10 km.

Le travail se fait majoritairement avec des outils manuels, un motoculteur nous sert ponctuellement.
Nous avons également investi cette année dans 2 brouettes électriques.

Notre terrain s'étend 3.7 hectares, orienté plein sud avec de nombreux arbres dont des fruitiers.
 Nous cultivons environ 5000 m2 en plein champ et 900 m2 sous tunnel, au milieu desquels sont intégrés des petits fruits, des aromatiques, des fleurs...
Petite nouveauté de ce début d'année 2026: notre bassin de stockage d'eaux pluviales  sera fini avant le printemps !

Côté commercialisation, nous avons une boutique à la ferme, ouverte deux soirs par semaine où sont proposés les produits d'une quinzaine de producteurs et artisans. Nous produisons et vendons l'équivalent d'une centaine de paniers par semaine.

Cette saison 2026, nous serons cinq à travailler ici : deux personnes en maraichage, une en pépinière plus nous deux.
Et bien entendu tous les copains, amis, anciens wwoofeurs, famille qui viennent régulièrement passer du temps ici.

Nous avons des animaux : deux chats, deux chiens, des poules, des coqs et des chèvres.

Notre rythme de travail est calqué sur les horaires d'école, nous faisons en sorte d'être disponibles pour nos enfants.
 Nous n'

name: Esa Eemeli - Family farm
description:
Tammiston Kesälä is situated at Lohja municipality at the southern Finland. Normal daily routines at our home includes taking care of animals and small scale gardening, farming and forrest work. Also would be nice, if you are interested of small building work also. 

Your hosts will be me and my wife Birgit. Sometimes here stays our 19-years old daughter Vellamo, but she mostly has her own life in town. The food we make is vegetarian, but we can prepare it for vegans also.  Help in the kitchen and household will be part of the daily routines.

We are not your place, if you are politically right-winger, conspirancy theorist, anti-vaxxer or so on. 

Also we have also a naturist lifestyle, so we are not a good choice, if nudity somehow bothers you. But we are a must place for you, if you like to go to sauna and have a swim au-naturel - or just enjoy being without clothes whenever you feel so.

And we are a place for you, if you want to be part of the finnish daily family life, take hikes and boat trips into nature, like to get connected to our wonderfull local community and get into finnish culture.

Certifications: Small family farm

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: We can pick you from Helsinki. Because of work we visit Helsinki many days per week, so you can spend your free time also there some days, if you like.

name: Miguel - Communal Living Group
description:
Somos un colectivo de músicos, artistas, agricultores que nos hemos juntado para desarrollar el proyecto en común de vivir en armonía con la naturaleza desde la creatividad en la granja Vilarcangel.
 En Vilarcangel ( nombre de la granja) conviven tres proyectos, granja ecológica, centro de retiros y residencia de artistas. En la granja estamos reconvirtiendo una explotación tradicional de naranjos, en un jardín ecológico de árboles comestibles en simbiosis con un parque artístico (landart). Nuestra intención en un futuro cercano es invitar a los niños y a los vecinos de los pueblos de alrededor a que vengan a disfrutar de un entorno ecológico y estético. De vez en cuando realizamos retiros de yoga, meditación y terapias holísticas y por otro lado tenemos la residencia de artistas donde recibimos a creativos de todo el mundo  para realizar proyectos artísticos. 
Vilarcangel es un espacio creado para aprender como vivir en armonía con la naturaleza y conocer los procesos necesarios para vivir una vida más consciente y armoniosa.  

Organic methods: Realizamos todo manualmente

Current projects: Actualmente realizamos talleres de permacultura, musicoterapia, arte terapia, yoga, meditación, temascal y otras actividades relacionadas con el crecimiento personal, la conciencia ecológica y el desarrollo sostenible. Estamos diseñando una especie de bosque del alma, donde el visitante pueda a modo de viaje iniciativo, conectar con la naturaleza a través de diversos espacios que estamos creando. Estos espacios tienen una función sinestesica y tratan de aunar conceptos como la geometría sagrada, la música, la aroma terapia el arte terapia y la agricultura ecológica.

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: Estación de tren de Sueca a 3,5km y aeropuerto a 40km

name: Audrey - Mixed Farm
description:
Hello! My name is Audrey, I own and operate Fir River Ranch. If you are country at heart, enjoy wildlife, love horses and dogs, and meeting good people then this is the place for you.  We are an award winning bed and breakfast in Hudson Bay, Saskatchewan. 

Organic methods: Composting, using no chemicals in our garden and fruit trees, wild berries, natural fertilizer and lawn cutting with our horses, recycling, building sustainable log furniture
 
We have a gigantic garden and berry orchard that we preserve into hundreds of jar of salsa, pickles, carrots, beans and almost anything else you can think of.   We make wine with all our own berry syrup, jams, and pick many different kinds of wild berries.  We also make most of our own bread, all deserts, butcher our own wild meat.

There are 8 horses, 3 dogs, some are rescue.   There is two cabin for volunteers. We offer wagon and sleigh ride. Regarding horse training -  we currently have a pair of five year old's and two six year old that you can get a chance to work with. If you're comfortable being around  green horses you can work with them and ride. Our driver is a expert with many years of experience you can help harness and learn from.

I am looking for help with a wide variety of chores. The Ranch is run like a family unit.  Our main focus is on working with our animals and to care for our  property.  You would work in exchange for meals, housing and many other amenities. You would stay in a cabin with private rooms built for volunteers showers and relax area in main lodge. We try to have at least four helpers at all times so there is lots of cooking and cleaning . The chores include cleaning, computer work, fence fixing, cooking healthy meals together,  care of huge property, gardening, maintenance of our tractors, quads, truck and cars. There is also usually some kind of wood working going on at the ranch.
