A typical work day is breakfast being eaten by 8 and then start working, going through our daily chore lis

name: Gayle - Beef and Sheep farm in rural Argyll, diversified into a plant nursery and glamping pods.
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals): We are a family run farm and we currently have 20 beef cattle with calves and 400 breeding sheep and 2 horses, split over two farms (approx. 800 acres). We have a glamping site which has 3 pods. We also have a small plant nursery and these plants are sold within a large honesty box area within the farm grounds. You will help alongside Andrew and Terence (Father and Son) who are the farm workers. I (Gayle) provide assistance when family life/work life allows and i pick up/drop off, take you for food shops and act as a tour guide for the local area

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them: Some sheep and cow husbandry (depending on which season) Potting and restocking of plants needed for the plant nursery on site. We can teach you a variety of skills including the care of livestock, plant propagation, general farm skills (fence building, field maintenance etc). We can try and accommodate any specific skills volunteers would like to learn within our industry, throughout their stay however our work can be very seasonal due to the weather etc.

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals: A 2 bedroom static caravan is available with TV and Wifi, ensuit and kitchen facilities. A weekly shopping trip will be provided and the caravan has a small kitchen area that can be used to prepare meals. The caravan has a small log burner and wood will be provided throughout the full stay. 

4. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?  

The helping days can vary due to season etc. During summer, our animals are all field kept. Lambing time can mean alot of sheep work which continues into the months of May/June. 

5. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.): 

As

name: Petri - Family farm
description:
Northern summer is short and intensive. June-july sun shines night and day, midnight sun boosts us for long working days, which we then compensate during winter. If you are ready for flexible tasks and working hours (still 20-30 hours per week should be sufficient for a volunteer) and want to learn norther cropping and sheep husbandry, our farm could be a place for you. June-September our sheep are out on the pasture, small pieces of grassland in the surrounding village. We will produce roughage (hay, silage) for our sheep, grains (oats, barley, peas) they need and some vegetables as well. Vegetables are mostly for other farms, who store, package and market those to wholesale market. Helping hands are mainly needed from early may until november. Depending on your skills and interests, you can help with the sheep (feeding, pasture fencing, shearing etc.), in field work (tractor work, some hand weeding june-july) or in our private kitchen to ensure proper meals for all of us. Field operations are limited to june-october. A driving licence (car) is a big plus, as well as some experience in construction work. We, farmers are appr. 60 years old, our children are already working elsewhere. We are also running another ag-related small business (Elomestari Oy) and active in culture and politics locally. In our house, we have two extra rooms upstairs, so there should be a peaceful corner to accomodate yourself. In your own time, the biggest free (no power plants!) river in Finland offers excellent opportunities for fishing and recreational purposes. Since we can not offer independent, simple an monotonous work for totally unskilled workers, we wish you to stay few weeks or longer. Thereby you are able to learn us, our farm and surroungings to work independently, without our continous supervision.

Organic methods: Crop rotation, rotational grazing, multiple non-chemical weed control methods.

Certifications: Our national EU-compatible certification (Ruokavirasto)

Internet a
_wwoof_travel: We can pick up from Tornio/Kemi or even Rovaniemi/Oulu/Luleå

name: Ian - Family home and garden - rural
description:
A seaside country house with a vegetable garden, and lemon, mandarin, and orange trees. 

Organic methods: Application of EM (effective micro-organisms)

Certifications: Very small garden, no commercial production

Type of help needed: We are a couple who renovated a country house and we live there for half of the year. WWOOFers will live with us in the same house and will help with the vegetable garden.
_wwoof_travel: The public bus from Chania city can bring you very close to the house every 30min

name: Zauditu - Organic Family Farm in the Rainforest of the US Virgin Islands
description:
We are a Small, 2.5 acre,  Family Farm based in the Rainforest on the Island of St.Croix US Virgin Islands. 

Our farm is full of different trees bearing fruits such as: 
-Mangoes, Carambola/Starfruit, Pineapple, Tamarind, Soursop, Sugar Apple, Golden Apple, Plum, Jack Fruit, Coconut, Mulberry, Cherries, Bananas, Sorrel,  Ackee  and so much more. 

On our farm we grow vegetables such as: 
-Kale, Mustard greens, Cabbage, Corn, Sweet Potato, Sweet peppers, Hot peppers, Tomatoes, Cassava, Broccoli, and so much more. 

We also grow a large variety of Herbs, Spices, and Bush Teas! 

The produce grown on the Farm is used to feed the household as well as to sell in the markets as raw products or as processed products (I.e. Pepper Sauce, Fruit Chutneys, Fruit Preserves, Vegetable preserves, or Wine etc.). 

We are seeking help on the Farm to assist with daily farm maintenance and duties. Some of these duties include: 
-Grass Cutting 
-Garden Weeding 
-Harvesting Crops
-Watering Plants 
-Digging Holes 
-Feeding Farm Dogs 
-Laying weed control tarp 
-Planting trees 
-Planting Seedlings 
-Planting Crops
-Organic Pest control spraying 
-Trimming tree branches 

Occasionally we will need help with projects such as: 

-Building Seedling nursey tables 
-Green house 
-Cleaning Shed/Storage area 
-Assisting with water delivery 
-Wine making/Bottling 
-Pepper Sauce Making 
-Food/Fruit preservation 
-Vending at Local events/Markets

WWOOFers will be housed in either our Cabin loft guest house, our self insulated bunker studio/efficiency, or a Campsite. All areas will have access to the shared outdoor kitchen, bathroom, and shower. You will be responsible for keeping areas tidy and clean. All WWOOFers are expected to respect residents' living spaces. 

WIFI will be available. 

Living quarters are powered by Solar and Gas.

We practice only Organic methods of Farming. 

WWOOFers shouold be able to lift 50lbs.

Work hours will be 4-5 hours a day with the weekends off so that you may exp
_wwoof_travel: 2.5 Miles from town taxi services/ 15 minutes to Island Airport

name: Denise - Our small, peony farm is nestled in the forest of the tiny community of Funny River, Alaska..
description:
Hello Wwoofer. My name is Denise. My husband Dan and I own a peony farm in Alaska. We also have a dog named Rocky. We have no employees, but often have friends that like to come to help at our farm. We have 3,000 peony plants on our farm, which is considered a small farm, but it really keeps us busy. When the peonies are in bloom, there is no prettier place to be.

We work to make our farm a beautiful space. We weed around the plants when necessary. We pinch off the side buds in June, to help produce the biggest, most beautiful flowers in July. During harvest, we have to cut the peonies at just the right stage, so we can hold them in our cooler for up to 8 weeks. After the peonies are cut, they must be placed in the the cooler within 20 minutes. The lower leaves need to be trimmed from each stem. We also, have a greenhouse with many tomatoes and cucumbers, which we need to water, sucker and tie up.  Another thing on our farm is our personal vegetable garden. We care for an eat from this garden as much as we can in late summer. We have a 75 foot berry enclosure. It houses lots of raspberries, haskap berries and strawberries. We can always use help picking our berries.

We usually all make our own breakfast and lunches from the food we keep stored in our cabin. Wwoofers would be welcome to use any food in the cupboard or refrigerator to make their favorite breakfast and lunches. We all come together at dinner time for a sit down meal together. We usually have a lot of snack food on hand too.

The Kenai Peninsula, where our farm is located, is one of the the most beautiful parts of Alaska. There is salmon fishing very near our cabin (though it does require a fishing license). We have fat tire e-bikes that we would be happy to share with a Wwoofer. We have trails behind our cabin that we walk our dog on everyday. There is also scenery near the ocean, 30 minutes from our farm. If a Wwoofer has a driver's license we would consider letting them use one of our vehicles to e
_wwoof_travel: Kenai,  Alaska, though we could pick someone up in Anchorage ( 3 hrs away) if need be.

name: Christophe - Permaculture sacrée, refuge LPO
description:
Nous sommes 3 adultes et 1 âne de travail, ainsi que des bénévoles réguliers a être accueillis au sein d'un écosystème riche, fragile et protégé de 12ha, dont une partie en zone sauvage préservée + deux ha de bois vers une Réserve Biologique Intégrale. Refuge LPO depuis 2019, nous menons des actions de protection et de suivi de la faune et la flore et organisons des activités de sensibilisation  à toute forme de Vie, pour une relation consciente et aimante au Vivant. Depuis notre installation en 2016, nous allons vers l'autonomie alimentaire en permaculture ( pas de production commerciale). Les lieux sont équipés de toilettes sèches et de système de phyto-épuration, pour une gestion éco-responsable de l'eau. Aucun engin motorisé sur le lieu, nos actions se font manuellement, au rythme des saisons et de la vie du sol et de ses habitants. Nous pratiquons une convivialité sans alcool ni produits stupéfiants et partageons les valeurs d'une simplicité heureuse.

L'éco-lieu est entièrement non fumeur, à l'intérieur et à l'extérieur.

Les activités des WWOOFeurs 
priorité à l'autonomie alimentaire à partir de mars 2022. SELON LES SAISONS ET LES BESOINS DU LIEU (climat, météo, types de terre, urgences, etc) : préparation manuelle des parcelles de cultures en permaculture, semis, plantations, transplantations, arrosage, récolte suivi et soin des plantes, suivi des semences, fauchage à la faux ou à la serpette, creusement de mares, création de baissières ("ENSEMBLE COMPOSE D'UN FOSSE ET D'UNE BUTTE") et activités liées à la gestion de l'eau. Nous nous retrouvons plutôt les week-end pour travailler ensemble par équipes avec des bénévoles venus pour les chantiers participatifs.
Les fins de semaines peuvent donc être des journées complètes tandis qu'en semaine c'est plutôt des demi-journées en petites équipes ou à deux, parfois en autonomie. Les horaires dépendent beaucoup des saisons, des besoins et de la météo.

Nous sommes en bordure d'un cours d'eau magnifique et mystérieux,

name: Annie - Ferme familliale non commerciale qui vise l'autosuffisance
description:
English will  follow:

Venez apprendre l`autosuffisance en nous aidant à démarrer la saison en juin! Vous pourrez assister à des naissances, apprendre à démarrer un potager et participer à divers travaux manuels.

Venir à la Maison Verte, ce n'est pas seulement aider sur une ferme. C'est prendre part à un projet familial qui s'inscrit dans une expérience plus large, collective, avec plusieurs autres personnes de la communauté. C'est aussi des repas copieux et des soirées entre amis, au bord du feu ou autour d'un jeu, des discussions, des rires, de  la musique et des cris d'enfants. Bref, pour plusieurs, la Maison Verte devient un deuxième chez soi, ou du moins, un lieu où il fait bon vivre...autrement.

Nous sommes une famille de 4 (Joel , Annie, Lili et Félix ) qui habite une maison ancestrale entre mer et forêt. Nous avons une terre d`une trentaine d`acre, avec environ 20 acres de forêt, environ 1 acre en jardin, dans lequel nous cultivons une grande variété de légumes, petits fruits, fines herbes et plantes médicinales. Le reste du terrain est en champs de foin et céréales, arbres fruitiers, et il y a aussi une petite plantation d`érable a sucre et une serre. Nous élevons aussi une vache canadienne, 1 chèvre, une vingtaine de dindes,  autant de canards et quelques poules. Nous vivons aussi avec 3 chats et 1 chien (qui viennent dans la maison).

 Nous visons l`autosuffisance autant que possible et avons besoin d`aide pour diverses tâches. Nous aimons partager avec les gens et la maison verte offre plusieurs possibilités d`apprentissage dans divers domaines, le tout dans un paysage enchanteur. Nous avons beaucoup voyagé par le passé et sommes heureux de pouvoir maintenant recevoir des voyageurs et de leur faire connaitre le mode de vie que nous avons choisi. Nous sommes tous deux passionnés par ce que nous faisons et sommes heureux de le partager avec des gens. 

J`enseigne le français a temps partiel (donc congé l`été) et Joel a un emploi saisonnier, alors nous no

name: Lisa - Ferme communautaire, jardins et animaux (poules, lapins, chèvres, ânes
description:
Je possède une petite ferme où je travaille à l'autonomie alimentaire.
Je travaille à la maison comme acupuncteure 2 jours semaine.
Pour le moment, il y a seulement une culture biologique.
Nous faisons l'élevage de chevreaux, poules à chaire, lapins.
À partir de juin, nous allons commencer la production des produits de lait de chèvre, fromage, yogourt, beurre, etc
Il y a sur la ferme ânes qui sont les protecteurs de la ferme.
Les repas seront variés.
La durée du séjour pourrait être jusqu'à l'automne.
Le logement serait au dessus du garage, très bel espace avec vue sur les jardins, cet espace sert à la transformation de la viande, légumes, et les produits de lait de chèvre.
 Le travail à faire est l'entretien du jardin et du terrain en général. et des animaux.
Je recherche une personne manuelle, une journée débute vers 8h, nourrir les poules, ramasser les œufs, faire le tour du jardins, traire les chèvres, tondeuse et coupe bordure si nécessaire.

Méthodes biologiques: Permaculture, engrais de chevaux.

Accès Internet disponible

name: Joseph - Farm of vegetables, bananas, goats, sheep, pigs and chicken
description:
Hello, my name is Joseph and I am married to Neema. We have a family garden planting banana, vegetable, and have few goats, sheep, pigs and dogs. We are in Arusha, a place known as Sakina kwa Idi juu.

Organic farming and five hours of working. We are very flexible according to each one capacity.

We have family house with different rooms and wwoofer have rooms each and three meals.. Breakfast lunch and dinner.

In Arusha we have many possibilities,from national parks and many places to visit.

name: Jennifer - Wilderness lodge living off the land in Alaska
description:
We live 70 miles off the grid on a summer maintained road.  We are open year round.  We practice sustainable living such as berry picking, hunting and trapping.  At the lodge we have the 3 owners.  Jennifer, Claude and their son, Bob.  We have a full time cook, Chrissy.  We have up to 4 seasonal employees.

Our farming is living off what the land has to offer.  Basic hunter/gatherer lifestyle.  Your work would be involved in sustainability of resources.

We have a daily meal schedule.  You may be in a room or cabin at the lodge or one of our wall tents.

Hobbies include hiking, atving, snow mobiling, fishing, hunting, trapping and more!

No Drugs.  Drug testing may be required.  No excessive alcohol.  Customer service and people skills a must.  

We are always accepting applications and look forward to hearing from you!  Send Jennifer a visit request if interested!! 

We would love to help you experience your Alaska Dreams!

name: Ludwig - Éco lieu, basé sur la culture de fruits et légumes
description:
Bonjour à toutes et tous, 

Nous faisons appel aux wwoofing pour plusieurs raisons.

La première raison est que la ferme se situe en milieu rural et que nous aimons la compagnie et les relations sociales. Nous pensons qu'accueillir des bénévoles en quête de calme, de simplicité mais avides de nouvelles expériences, est une bonne manière de faire vivre le lieu de façon constructive. 

Ensuite, nous avons acheté la ferme il y a 4 ans et après une bonne phase d'installation/transition nous commençons à être bien organisé et Ludwig, le maraîcher, travaille à plein temps pour la ferme.  Nous sommes peu mécanisés, travaillons en certification biologique, au rythme des saisons et au gré de la météo. Beaucoup de choses peuvent encore être mise en place (communicaion, développement circuit de vente, transformation de fruits et légumes... Cette année 2026 noù nous allons  faire pousser des légumes  sur 5000 m2, forts de notre expérience de 12 années en maraîchage diversifiés.
Justement, qui sommes-nous ? 
Nous sommes 3 à avoir acheté la ferme, Ludwig, Gaëlle, et Vincent, nous la partageons avec Elsa (9ans), Zéphyr ( 2) et Fjala (le chien), nous l'avons acheté pour créer un éco lieu, basé sur la culture de fruits et légumes mais aussi nous aimerions y mener des projets culturels (fêtes, concert, projection cinéma) et sociaux (camps, animations, portes ouvertes).
Venez nous aider à créer ce lieu dynamique et éco futuriste !!
La ferme se compose d'un corps de ferme, d'une grange et d'une étable, de plusieurs bâtiments utiles au camping à la ferme,  de deux espaces préfabriqués, d'un camping de 0,5 ha, de prairies (1,8ha) et d'une surface agricole (0,7 ha). Nous comptons aussi parmi nous 8 moutons, 2 ânes , 18 poules et 1 coq.

Les travaux demandés à la ferme concerneront principalement le maraîchage (plantation, semis, construction, travaux manuels divers), et les animaux (entretien);  Nous recherchons des profils autonomes, socialement ouverts et pro actifs. 

Nous avons l'habi

name: Jens-Uwe - Farming, gardening, working, relaxing, enjoying ...
description:
Northern Blue Bird Farm is run by Jens and Nadja, a couple originally from Germany who have been living in the Yukon since 2012. We first started as Blue Bird Farm in Whitehorse in 2014, raising just 20 laying hens and 20 meat birds. In October 2018, we purchased North Wind Farm, a 160-acre farm located 15 km north of Mayo. Since March 2019, we have called our farm Northern Blue Bird Farm.

Northern Blue Bird Farm is a diverse and dynamic farm with a variety of livestock and crops. Year-round, we raise up to 600 laying hens, 30 rabbits, meat birds, and 60 geese. Seasonally, we also raise 30 pigs. Our farm includes a garden where we grow potatoes, carrots, beets, cabbage, greens, and herbs, along with small greenhouses producing tomatoes, peppers, cucumbers, zucchini, and more.

Our farm is protected by eight dedicated guardian dogs—a mix of Great Pyrenees and Maremma—who keep us and the animals safe.

Helping on the farm involves working with the animals, maintaining the garden, and tending to the greenhouses. If you have carpentry skills, there are plenty of projects to work on, such as constructing our new egg grading station and processing room.

Most of the time, you’ll be outdoors, enjoying the beautiful surroundings. From the farm, you can see Mount Haldane, the tallest mountain in the area at 1,839 meters, often covered in snow. In the summer, we use our outdoor kitchen for cooking, baking, eating, and relaxing. The farm also has a shower house with a sauna, as well as outhouses. Much of our power comes from solar panels, especially during the summer months.

There are trails leading directly from the farm into the bush, as well as nearby hiking trails. We are just seven minutes from a lake and three minutes from the river, making it easy to go fishing. The town of Mayo is 15 minutes away, where you can visit a pool, a soap-making company, a library, a gym, and a museum. There are also many activities to join, such as yoga, Tabata, and various workout session

name: Jim - Family home and garden - rural
description:
I enjoy meeting and hosting people from all around the world and sharing my property with those who appreciate nature and animals and the quiet and tranquility that is associated with this isolated spot in the Cypress Hills. My goal is to treat others as you would have them treat you and that includes all races, religions and genders in people but I would like to think we should extend that to include animals both wild and domestic. I am passionate about treating the planet with respect and I strongly endorse environmentally friendly ideas such as recycling and reclaiming and exploring new and alternative ways to heat, cool and transport ourselves around this globe. I love to learn about food, traditions and culture from my wwoofers and guests as I seldom get the chance to travel myself.The Spring Valley Guest Ranch is a working ranch with a herd of commercial cows plus a mini dairy specializing in rare and heritage breeds. The ranch also has a flock of 200 chickens made up of four breeds.The guest ranch takes in Bed and Breakfast guests as well as hosts writers retreats, weddings, reunions and also hosts musical concerts in the Saville Theatre.What's for fun? Come and enjoy a truly quiet and peaceful stay in a secluded setting! Stroll around the barnyard, lawns and gardens, or hike over prairies, hills or a wooded valley with a year-round flowing spring running through it. Relish fresh air, tranquility and beautiful views! Chill out in the loft watching TV or choosing from the vast selection of DVDs, VCRs and board games.Local attractions include the T-Rex Discovery Centre, the Cypress Hills Interprovincial Park, Fort Walsh National Historic Site, Jones Peak, Whitemund Pits, local museums, golf courses, biking, horse riding, fishing and hiking.The ranch is located in the Cypress Hills, about 50km South-west of Maple Creek and 37km North-west of Eastend. The last 15km leading to the ranch are on gravel roads.  

Certifications: Too costly to certify.

Internet acces

name: Ruairi - Family farm
description:
Greetings to woofing community!

Firstly - please note that we just do not have the spare time to process visa applications, here in Latvia it's a long and tedious process.

Start 2020 -

We began to restore a semi abandoned farm in a secluded location in Latvia. Deep countryside, fertile land, nice river running by ,20 minutes from sea and beaches.

The farm consists of a large vegetable plot, large strawberry patch, many other berry bushes, 15 apple trees, 2 pear trees, 2 plum trees, 10 acres of forest ,4 acre cereal field and 4 acres of pasture.
From this base we also travel to other countries for wild camping, hiking , kayaking and sailing trips. This year we are doing such trips on west coast Scotland.

The skills we offer to teach and the life we like to share are all connected to sustainable self sufficiency and travel adventures. Traveling, planting, harvesting, storing of many food products, working with animals including processing of sheepskins, milling wood on our sawmill for our needs, processing of firewood & mulch, welding & engineering, machinery use and servicing, in fact all technical DIY skills to keep everything running.

We are also waiting to learn from You!

So it is that we are already getting quite self sufficient regarding food which is marvellous.
Much of the real hard work is done and now we have time to create exchanges with Woofers!

Ideally we would love to host a couple for long term who have at least one child, like us. We have a 9yr old boy and we're homeschooling. Playmates are essential because actually children learn via playing , adults too, but many haved stopped playing. Please don't ask for short stays of afew nights, this doesn't work out for us, we can't do constant changeovers and repetitive learning curves.

It doesn't matter if you have no previous skills, it will be our pleasure to teach, like this you can gain the skills to have your own mini farm in the future if you wish.

Organic methods: We will not use commercial

name: Alex - Organic self sustainable paradise in the kigezi mountains
description:
I am Alexander, Irish, 19 and my friend Anthony, Ugandan, 24 are beginning to form our projects for the community, especially the batwa of bwindi. Anthony has had his project for nearly a year while mine has started in summer of 24. We live on lake Bunyonyi the southwest of Uganda and our project is aimed at self sustainability, helping the communities around us and the batwa. We are the only two permanent dwellers while volunteers come and go. As of yet we have no pets but hopefully soon we shall.

Usually there is construction to do or simple farming like picking passion fruits, onions or other foods. We wake up early and work for most of the day but as a volunteer, you will have all the time to work and enjoy the paradise.  We would like to teach you about farming, African style, how to survive in equatorial Africa and spread love, always.

As of now we have 3 beds at Anthony’s place and soon to have 2 more at mine. They are very near to each other and easy to access with our boat. Meals are breakfast, lunch and dinner. Times are usually 8am/1pm/8pm. We have a gas cooker but like to use the local methods of simple fires as much as we can. The accommodation is simple but beautiful and enough room for everything.

We have a lake on both of our doorsteps so we always swim, it is also a form of our bathing. We love football as well and play as much as we can. Cooking is an art in Africa so we will treat you to some beautiful meals! Anything you want to do here you can. We also love Reggae :).

name: David - Homestead, mixed farm in Dolný Tisovník (Slovakia) - To avoid poverty tomorrow, choose sobriety NOW.
description:
Generic messages and copy-paste applications go straight to the bin.
Long-term partnership welcome.

First, a necessary clarification:
I'm David, Belgian, fluent in French and English, learning Slovak. Before describing what I do and what I offer, I want to address something that will strike many readers as provocative, but which I consider the most important thing I can say.
From childhood, when I asked "why?" the answer was "because that's how it is." I always sought to understand, solve problems, help others see what I was seeing. For decades I tried to find my place in a system that didn't suit me. I failed.
The way I think and speak about the situation, direct, uncompromising, openly critical of how most people live, is not aggression. It is, in fact, the most genuinely CARING position available. Let me explain.
When I tell someone that their lifestyle is incoherent with the reality of what's happening, I am treating them as an intelligent adult capable of understanding difficult things and changing accordingly. I am betting on their capacity for growth. The alternative, staying silent, being pleasant, validating their choices, not wanting to create discomfort, is a form of capitulation. It says, implicitly: I don't believe you're capable of changing. I'll leave you where you are.
Most people around me choose that capitulation, and call it kindness.
I'm not harming the situation by saying uncomfortable things, but the ones who refuse to listen, refuse to understand, and refuse to change, DO! ...while expecting to be treated with respect for doing so. They are the biggest aggressors consistently presenting themselves as the victims. I've watched this mechanism operate my entire life, and I have no patience left for it.
This doesn't make me misanthropic, but someone who still believes, against considerable evidence, that people are capable of better.
I'm one of the extremely rare people who consider it both appropriate and essential to completely reorganise their
_wwoof_travel: Closest Airport: Bratislava. Closest train Station: Krivan. Closest local Bus Station: Detvianska Huta. Closest International Bus Station: Banska Bystrica .

name: Gabriel Darío - Chacra agroturistica agroflorestal. Restaurando un pedacito de selva.
description:
Somos "Chacra Atajacaminos", Contamos con un espacio de 1 Hectáreas. donde se esta restaurando un pedazo de selva y se esta trabajando el suelo con agroecologia, aplicamos la técnica agroflorestal sintrópica. También empezamos a aguardianar semillas criollas propias y de intercambio..Empezamos con apicultura siguiendo el ciclo natural de las abejas..
Lo q producimos es para el autoconsumo, cuando hay abundancia, se comparte.
Estamos a 10 km del pueblo, hacemos eco-turismo y excursiones dentro y fuera del predio.
Aqui todo esta planificado para aprovechar al máximo la observación de Flora, Fauna y Funga. Este espacio esta pensado para q puedan ser parte de otra forma de vivir. También está pensado para que los adultos que nos visiten, disfruten al máximo su paso por Misiones y sean parte de una experiencia de aprendizaje, siendo esta algo divertido, relajante, y seguro... Deseando que se sientan en sintonía con la naturaleza, con la chacra, con la calma, con la historia, con la idiosincrasia de un barrio Rural; Estarán en Sintonía con la cultura Misionera!

Hacemos turismo comunitario, colaboramos con una comunidad Guaraní, trabajamos en conjunto. Buscamos q los vecinos q todavía tienen prácticas convencionales de cultivo puedan ver otra alternativa al control de plagas y malezas q no sea el glifosato. Hay otras formas de cultivar.

La idea es q colaboren y den una mano en la agrofloresta, en las tareas diarias (podar, plantar, cosechar, regar, carpir, abonar suelo, buscar materia orgánica de la selva, etc.) y colaborar en el armado de estructuras, mantenimiento y limpieza de áreas comunes, dentro y fuera de la casa. 

Los días empiezan entre las 6 y las 8 hs y entre las 15  y 16 hs. ya que solemos trabajar en 2 turnos de 2 horas y media c/u, con la modalidad de trabajo de 3x2. 
Todo se irá programando con un día de antelación dado q se siguen los ritmos de la naturaleza (clima, luna, sol y estaciones) y van a haber dias en los que trabajaran solos/as ya que mi traba
_wwoof_travel: Desde el aeropuerto Argentino y Brasilero  se accede a la Terminal de buses de Puerto Iguazú. Ahí será nuestro punto de encuentro..

name: Fadime - Family farm
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Foça’nın Ilıpınar köyüne 2 km mesafede olan çiftlik kızılçam ormanı, zeytinlikler ve nar bahçeleri arasında bulunuyor. Eski bir Rum köyü olan Ilıpınar, ismini hala köyün içinde akan pınardan alıyor. 
Çiftlikten Foça’ya ve Menemen’e toplu taşıma ile 15 dakikada, İzmir’e ise yine toplu taşıma ile 1 saatte ulaşım sağlanabiliyor. Çiftliğe yakın olan ve Akdeniz foklarının en önemli yaşam alanlarından Foça Özel Çevre Koruma Bölgesi ve Türkiye’deki 14 Ramsar alanından Gediz Deltası kuş, kelebek gözlemciliği ve fotoğrafçılığı, balıkçılıkla ilgilenenlerin ilgi duyduğu yerlerden. Ayrıca sanat, kültür ve sosyal aktivitelerin yoğun olduğu İzmir Fuarı, kordon, Kemeraltı İzmir’in önemli noktalarından. 

Emekli öğretmen olan Fadime ve Kaplan Zülfikargil, İzmir’de 25 yıl öğretmenlik yaptıktan sonra 1998’den bu yana Ilıpınar’daki çiftliklerinde, 7 dönüm zeytinlik içinde kendi yaptıkları evlerinde 2 kişilik bir aile olarak yaşamaktadırlar. 

Çiftlikteki en önemli üretim zeytin hasadı, sofralık zeytin ve zeytinyağı olmasına karşın doğadan yana her türlü üretimi deneyerek ya da başka üreticilerden dinleyip üretmekten keyif alıyorlar. Böylece üzüm çekirdeği yağı, zeytinyağı sabunu, turşu, sirke, kendilerine yetecek kadar meyve, sebze üretimi ve yerli tohumların çoğaltılıp meraklılarla paylaşılması konularında da severek emek harcıyorlar. 

Müstakil ve bölgenin doğal mimarisine göre taş ve ahşap kullanarak yaptıkları evlerinin üst katında bulunan 2 oda, TaTuTa misafirleri için ayrıldı. Doğadan yana üretime katılmaya, evin gündelik işlerini paylaşmaya, öğrenmeye ve bildiğini paylaşmaya istekli herkesi davet ediyorlar.

Çiftlikte Türkçe ve temel düzeyde İngilizce konuşulabiliyor. 

Ekim, Kasım ve Aralık aylarında şu üretim çeşitleri için gönüllü kabul edilmektedir; zeytin hasatı, ot yolma, bahçe işleri, çevre temizliği, meyve toplama, nar ekşisi, reçel ve turşu yapımı. 

--- 

The farm is located in Ilıpınar village 2kms far from Foça, surrounded by pine forest, ol
_wwoof_travel: İzmir Otogarı, Adnan Menderes Hava Limanı, Hatundere İZBAN (İzmir Banliyö) Durağı

name: Monica - Art and Permaculture Community in Northern Vermont
description:
Greetings Woofers! Eric and Monique are lovers who are both passionate about art and natural living. They have six children and live in a forest in rural Vermont. Our goal is to create the inner and outer infrastructure of healthy joyous living. 

Eric is a sculptor who mostly works with driftwood and other pieces he finds whilst on nature walks alone or with his family. He is also a seasoned poet who performs locally. He is a full time art student at the nearby university. He has the most experience growing food here and has been successful in running an organic CSA.

Monique is a holistic practitioner, singer/song writer, mystic who you might find dancing naked or in the kitchen preparing a medicinal meal or chai to share or both! She is the heart of our social infrastructure and loves to create bridges of understanding and give permission for all the nuance involved in the process of becoming. She is studying edible plants and often forages and loves companions to gather herbs and mushrooms for making meals and medicines. She is happy to share her skills with preparing meals using whole foods.

Willow is our local preteen and force of kindness. She loves all things fashion and beauty and will likely give you a makeover if you ask nicely. She is a homeschooler and would love to meet someone who can help her learn guitar or advance in understanding academic skills.

Ena is an incredible anime artist and loves to discuss their special interests. They attend the local highschool and are in chorus.

Mireille the baby is living love!

Elowyn is three and loves to sing, talk and explore. She is great on nature hikes and fun activities!

Solomon is our sage child. He is like a cat and will come snuggle up if he feels like it.

Michael will ask you thousands of questions and will grab your hand and have you searching for frogs and salamanders in no time!

Coopers bark is worse than his bite, but don't worry, he won't bite and once he gets to know you, is one of the bigges

name: Paul - Mountain Market Farm Rooted in Regenerative Agriculture
description:
If you are interested in learning all about both indoor and outdoor growing in a cold mountainous climate year-round please reach out and tell us about yourself!                                                                                                                                                                                                             Please note we do have a requirement of one month and often give priority to those that wish to stay longer.

Our farm is located in an awe inspiringly beautiful area of the coastal mountain range in the Sea to Sky corridor.  The property is situated on 20 acres of land in a secluded forest sanctuary, but only  20 minutes from Whistler village and 1.5hrs from downtown Vancouver.

 The Brew Creek Farm was historically a high end retreat centre with catering to groups with produce right here from the farm. While we do still host retreats on site, the farm project was such a hit that it has shifted the focus of the property and we have recently become the first year-round organic production farm in the area! We focus on edible flowers, microgreens, herbs and salad greens.

Over the past three seasons we have been making this shift and are now in full operation selling to high end restaurants in Whistler, Squamish and even Vancouver. Additionally, to contribute to the community, over the summer we offer a  weekly produce donation  to the local food bank. These small steps are progressing us towards becoming a central hub for the future of environmentally conscious agriculture in the region. 

It has been a big transition process, but we are already reaping the benefits through nourishing our community, invigorating the food security of the region, and tending to the land in a regenerative manner to honour the beautiful ecosystem that we reside within. 

Here at the Brew Creek Farm we are inspired by modern farming techniques and alternative agricultural practices. Our produce is carefully cultivated using the m

name: Bar - Educational Centre, Eco Retreat, Orchard, Vegetable production
description:

12-19 April, 1-7 September, 23-30 November. Other periods may be announced in the future
Accettiamo wwoofer:
12-19 aprile, 1-7 settembre, 23-30 novembre. Altri periodi potranno essere annunciati in futuro.

- - - English below - - - 
Siamo un centro di pratica di meditazione ed ecologia, dove uniamo la permacultura e la presenza mentale (mindfulness) per prenderci cura sia della Terra che della mente. Sul sito sta crescendo una food forest e una zona di ortaggi, insieme a un compost toilet, raccolta di acqua piovana, compostaggio e altro.  Le giornate sono scandite da meditazione mindfulness, pasti in consapevolezza, lavori e tempo personale. I pasti sono vegani. Oltre ai lavori di permacultura, facciamo anche piccoli lavori di autocostruzione nella nuova casa.

Il terreno è di 12 ettari di cui molti sono boschi, accanto alla riserva naturale "il Contrafforte Pliocenico" nell'appennino bolognese. Ospitiamo giornate, fine settimana, ritiri e corsi che integrano la presenza mentale e la permacultura. Puoi raggiungerci con i mezzi pubblici sia da Bologna che da Firenze.

Orari
Gli orari delle attività variano in base alla stagione. Un orario d’esempio è:

6.15 sveglia
6.45 meditazione mattutina
7.30 colazione
8.30 aiuto con permacultura e altre attività 
12.30 pranzo
tempo personale
18.30 cena
20.30 meditazione serale
nobile silenzio (fino alle 8.30 del giorno successivo)

Per maggiori informazioni sulla vita quotidiana: https://www.pardesa.org/vita-quotidiana/
----------------
Our association is creating a centre for permaculture and mindfulness, to take care of both the Earth and the mind. On site we are creating a food forest and annual garden, plus a compost toilet, rainwater collection, composting and more. Currently we're a small operation: the project caretaker's country house also hosts volunteers and participants during our activities. Days are characterized by an atmosphere of mindfulness, with meditation, mindful meals and time for

name: Jorge - Family home and garden - rural, Livestock Farm, Orchard, Family farm
description:
Granja Familiar enfocada a la conservación de practicas agrícolas ancestrales.
Con mas de 30 años de experiencia nos hemos dedicado al cultivo de hortalizas, crianza de animales y conservación de la biodiversidad que rodea nuestra granja. 

Organic methods: Preparación de suelo con abono orgánico, rotación de cultivos, elaboración de compost y humus, abono orgánicos, agua de vertientes

Certifications: Granja vista hermosa quellon

Current projects: Granja familiar ubicada en Quellón, Chiloé, que busca rescatar la agricultura ancestral, mediante cultivos orgánicos, manejo respetuoso con el medio ambiente y los ciclos naturales.
Además contamos con animales de granja como avejas, aves de corral, bovinos, caballos.

name: Emily & Mitchell - Mixed Livestock Homestead in Northern Alberta
description:
We are a young family (two adults, two kids) in the beginning stages of building our mixed livestock homestead in Northern Alberta, close to Athabasca.  We aim to create organic, regeneratively raised food for ourselves and community. We raise cattle (dairy and beef), sheep, chickens, turkeys, pigs, goats and ducks 

The main projects this summer are building related as we work improve the farm infrastructure. This could look like work building animal shelters, fencing, watering systems, etc. Learning about renewable energy systems may also be an opportunity as we continue to install our solar system this summer. 

We also have a strong focus on organic dairy production on our farm. Helpers have the opportunity to learn about how to care for and milk cows and make cheese and other dairy products. Other tasks may include helping with the rotational grazing system, moving chicken tractors and land clearing projects.

We have a small cabin, atco trailer and converted sea can available for staying in with access to an outhouse. As well as military style tents in the summer. We also have the ability to host those who would like to bring their own camper or tent. There will always be plenty of food available, the goal will be for most meal to be shared but breakfast and lunch may be self serve sometimes to fit with our children's schedules.

In our free time we enjoy going for walks in the forested area of our property on our free time, there is lots of space to explore and we back onto 5 quarter sections of crownland. There is lots of opportunity for foraging. There are many lakes to explore within a couple hour radius. We also have lots of homestead adjacent skills to share (canning, baking, etc) for those interested in those types of skills. We aim to cook all of our food from scratch so we are usually experimenting in the kitchen!

name: Terry - Family home and garden - rural, Agritourism Destination, Flower Farm, Apiary and Willow
description:
Welcome to our Farm in Friendly Manitoba—right in the heart of Canada

Hello there from wide-open skies and big prairie sunshine! If you’re longing to be surrounded by nature’s beauty, try new things, and become part of a welcoming farm family, our place might be just the place for you.

Manitoba is known for its cold winters, but our summers are truly incredible—long sunny days, endless skies, and an astonishing abundance of things that grow beautifully here.

My husband and I live on a 3.5-acre property just south of Winnipeg. Seven years ago, I retired from my office job to follow a long-held passion. After completing my Prairie Horticulturist certification, Sweet Petals was born. The name says it all: “Sweet” represents the honey produced by our small apiary, and “Petals” reflects the thousands of flowers we grow each year. Flowers, bees, and a deep love for the land are at the heart of everything we do.

I’m a big believer in following your passion—no matter your age—and I’m currently stepping into a brand-new dream: growing willow for basket weaving. In March 2026, I’ll be traveling to England to attend a four-day willow basket-making workshop. When I return, I’ll be planting heirloom willow rods and beginning the next chapter of our farm. WWOOFers may get to be part of this exciting new venture from the very beginning!

What does WWOOFing at Sweet Petals Farm look like?

WWOOFers generally work from 8 am until 2 pm Monday to Friday. (There is a little flexibility with this schedule.)
No two days are quite the same—but early in the season, honesty is best: it starts with soil prep… which means weeding. From there, it’s seeding, watering, planting, and nurturing everything to a strong start. Most of our flowers are started right here on the farm in our studio using grow lights.

By July, we host educational garden tours, followed by bouquet-making workshops in August. Throughout the summer, we harvest and prepare weekly orders for our flower subscription custom
_wwoof_travel: Winnipeg Richardson International Airport, VIA Rail stop at Union Station

name: Flavio Emilio - Santuario De Cavalos, criaçao de pastos e areas pra Cavalos.
description:
Hello! I'm Flavio, an Italian originally from Sicily, with a passion for travel and a love for music. After years of hitchhiking and exploring various corners of the world, I’ve settled in the beautiful Vale do Capão in Bahia, Brazil, where I manage a serene horse farm. 

I speak italian, english, spanish, portuguese

About the Farm:

Nestled at the foot of mountains, my farm offers a rustic and welcoming atmosphere, with simple accommodations available for guests. The surroundings are abundant with picturesque rivers and hiking trails, making it an ideal spot for nature lovers. You can enjoy a peaceful retreat while still being just an hour's walk from the charming local town. 

I care for seven wonderful horses, and my goal is to allow them the freedom to roam and thrive in their natural environment. While I have experience offering horseback riding experiences to tourists, I’m currently focusing on creating a more tranquil and wild habitat for my horses. 

If you’re looking for a meaningful experience in nature, a chance to connect with animals, and an opportunity to learn about sustainable farming practices, I'd love to welcome you to my little paradise!

Normalmente, todas as manhas ira encontrar-me a dar de comer aos nossos cavalos, mas a nossa tarefa mais importante é fazer cercas para separar áreas de plantios. Escovar e cuidar dos cavalos, limpar as áreas dos animais pra adubar as áreas de plantios. Cortar grama pra os cavalos, treinar eles, amansar eles e criar estruturas pra melhorar a permanência dos cavalos na área. Perma-cultura e construção de ambientes  pra voluntários, melhorarias  das estruturas presentes.

Os voluntarios serao alojados numa estructuras com camas e tetos parecido a um camping, com areas ao redor pra montar barracas, e lugares pra colocar redes. Os animais domestico como cachorros e gatos se emcontram nas mesmas areas das pessoas. Tem pia, tem chuveiro, e banheiro seco em areas abertas. OS volontarios serao convidados em cada refeic

name: William - Off grid Homestead, Garden, Vegetables, Permaculture, Ranch/Livestock
description:
Our farm is a Christian homestead/farm on 55 acres of property located in the Maine farm community of Thorndike. The farm contains beautiful sugar maples, wild apple trees, ten  acres of open field, small streams and assorted cedar and fir trees. It is located on the side of a westward facing hill with a beautiful view of the Appalachian Mountains .

This farm has been established to provide an educational environment for those interested in homesteading and sustainable farming practices. We provide instruction in composting, wild edibles, renewable energy design and installation, husbandry, organic farming and permaculture. Our goal is to incorporate homesteading courses in a way that glorifies our creator by applying a Biblical world view and theological thought to the practice of self sufficiency. In this process we hope to encourage a young generation of potential farmers to support themselves and be a gracious example among the community by offering a portion of their crops to individuals in need.

Current Projects:
1. March 2026 Sugar Maple processing season by tapping trees, running line and boiling sap.
2. Daily Milking of our Jersey cow and shepherding a herd of Katahdin sheep.
3. Construct some small tiny houses for guests to reside in.
4. Care for and butcher as needed Muscovy Ducks and sheep. 
5.Clear brush and debris from silva pasture.
6. Painting trim and finishing touches to our guest house. 
7. Fall Harvest and planting cover crops
8.  Construct new green house and maintain and seed existing one.
9. Upgrade solar power system in guest house

We enjoy the Joel Salatin methods of pasturing chickens and livestock and the Sepp Holzer book on permaculture. If you are interested in organic farming, wild edibles, medicinal plants, kombucha, lacto-fermentation, permaculture, fiddles, contra dance, singing, amazing sunsets, hard work, long days, using a hand scythe, raising chickens, sheep, rabbits, pigs, pumping your own water, living off grid, limited Inte
_wwoof_travel: BGR airport, Augusta, Belfast or Bangor Concord Trailways

name: Özcan - Agritourism Destination, Farm, Family farm
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Ev sahibi Özcan Kulaksız, ceviz tohumlarını yıllar önce tek tek kendi ekmiş, onları çocuk yetiştirir gibi yetiştirmiş. Sertifikalı organik tarım yapan Özcan Bey, memleketi Yelek kasabasına örnek olmak istiyor. Ev sahibinden çiftliğe oldukça yakın olan Yelek'i gezdirmesini ve çiftliğinde bulunan tarımsal/ geleneksel eşyaların sergilendiği entnoğrafya müzesini göstermesini rica edebilirsiniz. 
Kaman'ın bozkırın ortasında olmasına aldanmayın, yüzmeye gidebileceğiniz plajları bile var! Savcılı ve Toklumen plajları çiftliğe sadece 10 km ötede bulunmaktadır. 
Çiftlikte hem konuklar ve hem de gönüllüler aynı anda bulunabileceklerinden konuklar ve gönüllüler yemeklerini ayrı ayrı yemektedir. Eğer gönüllü ve konuk sayısı az olursa birlikte yiyebilir. Gönüllüler sofrayı diğer çalışanlarla paylaşabilir veya işletme yetkilisinin vereceği malzemelerle kendileri pişirebilirler.

Özcan Kulaksız çiftliğini şöyle anlatıyor:

Çiftliğimiz, 1994 yılında ülkemizde ünüyle tanınan Kaman Cevizi üretimi amacıyla kurulmuş olup daha sonraki yıllarda ürün çeşitliliğini artırmasıyla, bugün itibariyle 260 dönümü organik meyve ve sebze üretimi için ve 600 dönümü de organik hububat, bakliyat üretimi için olmak üzere toplam 860 dönümlük araziye sahiptir. Organik tarım kapsamında yaklaşık yetişmiş ve yetişmekte olan toplam 2800 adet ceviz ağacı bulunmaktadır. Bunun yanında badem, elma, armut, kayısı, üzüm, vişne, kiraz, ayva, domates, patlıcan, biber, salatalık gibi sebzeler ve bakliyat çeşitleri gibi iklime uygun ürünler yetiştirilmektedir. Ayrıca kestane, incir, nar, böğürtlen gibi meyvelerin üretilebilmesi için gerekli çalışmalar devam ediyor. Çiftliğimizde yetişen elma başta olmak üzere tüm meyvelerden sirke üretimi ve meyve kurusu yapılmaktadır. 

Aile çiftliğimizde, sabit çalışanlar yanında ihtiyaca göre mevsimlik işçiler de çalışmaktadır. 
Evin bir tanesinde ortak kullanıma açık mutfak, gönüllülerin kalacağı iki oda, banyo, wc ve dinlenme salonu ve ayrıca evin ön tara
_wwoof_travel: Yurt dışından  ve Türkiyenin değişik şehirlerinden gelecekler için en uygun otogar Ankara "AŞTİ"Kaman otogar

name: Petra & - Farm in the mountains
description:
We are a couple from Austria and we live since january 2014 in the middle of the Swedish nature together with around 24 Alaskan Huskies.

Sometimes we expect guests from all over the world at our farm, mainly from Germany.

Our farm is in the middle of now-where at the beginning of Swedish Lapland in the wilderness and you can expect a lot of nature, lakes, woods and a lot of wild animals like bears, lynx, moose, reindeers and many different kind of birds.

We are looking for help for january with training, feeding the dogs and cleaning the kennels. You should be a teamplayer, speak german or  have at least B2 with your english skills and you should also be able to work sometimes alone. There is plenty of spare time, to explore the nature and the surrounding. 

What we offer is accommodation in a cabin with bathroom and toilet. And you will get groceries from us which you can cook for yourself. You will get a lot of informations about how it is to live in the wilderness, the nature, swedish culture and all about our dogs.

We are looking forward to hear from you / Petra & Walter

name: Peter - Connect to the land and actively engage in raising food and serving people with us
description:
This farm is a place of work and reflection. We must work to make our world a better place. We must reflect to know ourselves. We must know ourselves more clearly to work more effectively and truthfully. Mouna means inner silence. Our inner journey is all we really have in life. To allow our spirit to connect, create and serve is to go further in ourselves and our community, whatever that may mean for you. 

This farm is a space for people to connect with a deeper relationship to the “self” and Mother Earth. It is also a place where we become more connected to the land, our food, our bodies, our selves by actively engaging in raising food and serving people.  If interested, please share with us a few things about your self: who you are, what inspires you, what experience and skills you carry. What interests you about the farm and alternative living? Please provide contact information and Wwoofer ID# and/or a link to your page. Please contact us for a conversation before acceptance!  Mahalo (thank you)!

Our farm hosts 2-7 individuals interested to connect to themselves and the land. We offer an immersion based experience on an organic 5 acre farm with fruit trees, vegetables, flowers, medicinal plants. We work on the farm. We reflect on our selves.  We connect ourselves to the land and each other. We currently have spaces available throughout the year. We strive to have bananas, papayas, citrus and an array of other fruits every day (working on it!) We grow vegetables to feed ourselves and our community. We are looking for positive, motivated, self-initiated individuals to dedicate their energy and love into the land. Wwoofers will spend most of their time doing farmwork: weeding, (lots of weeding!) planting, watering, harvesting. They prep, cook, and serve food for each other, visitors, and events. They will work on projects that enhance the beauty and function of the space (construction, landscape and building art, cleanup). Wwoofers will have opportunities to lea

name: David - Lieu de vie et prochainement  de stages et formations
description:
Salut à vous!
La Chassagnade est un lieu-dit de la commune d'Albussac situé entre Tulle et Argentat dans le sud de la Corrèze. A quelques minutes à pied, les belles cascades de Murel dont l'eau va se jeter dans la Dordogne à Argentat à 15 min de voiture. Plusieurs lacs baignables à 10 et 25 minutes.
Ce territoire peu peuplé riche d'une nature généreuse, diversifiée et assez bien préservée attire de plus en plus de jeunes (et moins jeunes ) désireuses de poser les valises, planter des arbres, travailler, créer, vivre un peu à l'écart de la folie du monde.. Une dynamique est en marche, les collines se repeuplent doucement d'énergie, d'idées, de renouveau..

C'est sur une de ces collines que j'habite avec mes 2 enfants Cyl 20 ans et Basile 16 ans( de moins en moins souvent..) et en partie avec Julie e sa fille Elly qui a 10 ans.
Depuis bientôt 12 ans nous aménageons une très grande grange afin d'y organiser des stages et événements socio-culturels et créatifs en lien avec d'autres personnes et associations.
Thématiques variées autour du jardin naturel,apprentissage de savoirs faire,cuisine,autonomie alimentaire, éco-construction, pratiques artistiques et artisanat, écologie, méditation &co, réflexions autour des alternatives politiques ,..
A force de planter et planter des arbres, des plantes, des légumes, des fleurs..une petite forêt comestible est en train de voir le jour.
Jardin de beauté, jardin nourricier. Très important pour moi qui suit cuisinier nomade depuis 25 ans en quête de goûts, de textures, de couleurs, de plaisirs. Je cuisine peu de viande et aime faire découvrir le plaisir d'un repas festif végétarien.

Auto-construit essentiellement en matériaux locaux et écologiques, l'aménagement du bâtiment avance au rythme des projets, des saisons, des présences en naviguant entre chantiers solitaires  ou participatifs.
A côté du temps consacré au jardin et aux travaux, je continue à cuisiner à l'extérieur pour différents clients et projets publiques et privés. L'

name: Maisie - A biodynamic farm in the foothills of the Black Mountains, Wales
description:
Situated on the Welsh Herefordshire borders, at the foothills of The Black Mountains, our farm has been producing certified biodynamic vegetables, fruit and flowers for the past twenty-seven years.  For over a decade the farm has worked in a farm to table collaboration with a restaurant that shares our ethos. Alongside our produce, we send our flowers to the restaurant in addition to a few renowned florists. 

We are looking for dedicated and enthusiastic volunteers to help in the production of fruit, vegetables and flowers for sale; this will include the sowing of seeds, planting, weeding and cultivation right through to harvesting. We are growing on approximately ten acres. Life on the farm is communal, but there is a level of independence that's required. Once your visit is confirmed we'll send along an information pack that details what to expect and everything you might need. 

Both August and I live on the farm, alongside Tig, a talented grower, and her partner Ru, who is about to begin an MA in Landscape Architecture. The farm is also home to two dogs, Winnie and Lettuce, and two kittens Aggie and Oolie. 

For volunteers, there is a shared bathroom with a shower and a shared kitchen, dining and living room space with all cooking facilities provided. There is a large table to work and eat at, a sofa, and wifi and electricity to charge devices. For food, we will provide staple ingredients and tend to stick to a vegetarian diet for the shop. To sleep/relax, you will be in one of three small cabins. The cabins are comfortable but basic with candlelight and log burners for light and warmth. Previous volunteers have brought items from home to make their cabin feel like theirs, which we're very happy for you to do too. 

Typically, we ask for a contribution of 5hrs work per day (around 25 hrs per week), Monday-Friday. We tend to start around 8am but if the weather is hot we may start a little earlier. 

In the Golden Valley there are plenty of trails for walking, cy

name: Maxime - Jardin créole
description:
Bienvenue, je suis votre hôte, de parents bâtisseurs, marins, éleveurs et maraîchers, des études en soins et un BP agricole, plusieurs années en autodidactes nous ont menés à ce projet sur terrain familial, refuge naturel avec mare et tout le potentiel pour en faire une ferme autonome. Whitie le dog et notre trio vous accueilleront.

L'implantation d'îlots fruitier/potager, leur arrosage, les récoltes, l'entretien des outils,  la conception générale du lieu, création des structures (pépinière, bergerie, abris, poulailler...) mais aussi de réguliers séjours en bord de mer, à 2 ou 3km, font notre quotidien.

Nos repas sont majoritairement conçus avec les produits et la culture créole, préparés et pris en commun sur place ou en camp, avec les fruits de la mer.

Les échanges d'expériences en artisanat, permaculture, en sciences: botanique, astronomie, faune, archéologie... et tout le Sud de l'île avec ses sentiers vous est ouvert à vélo, en bus, voiture de location ou stop.

name: Fabian - Family farm
description:
We live in la Fabulosa. Im Fabian And Aradia my daugther. Our house is made out of mud.  It's a small place located in the ecologic community of Peñalolen, next to the Andes. Peñalolen is located 30-40 min to the city centre, so it's still possible to reach our Santiago without much hassle. 

Our place is pretty chilled, we plenty of plants,  we grow cactus, succulents  we have two dogs , chico and Bolt and our cat "Peluda". . We have wi-fi,. We always need a hand with the garden, buillding, and cleaning, are also great assets,  so we can offer to stay with us in a mezzanine, the guest room, or a bed in the studio area, according to the situation.  

The community is pretty lively and social, with cafes, pubs, shops and other activities.  Our main problem is time  I  work a lot in the Creative industry, and  to keep everything running.  So sometimes our woofers will be left alone, thats why we offer a  pretty free work schedule with tasks to be completed within reason, we share our food, although we have plenty of salad,  but please notice that we won't provide a 3 meal service as everyone deals with his own belly, but as a house rule , we cook for everyone in the house, and we go together to the market and share expenses.  This farm is a unique magic place, in here you might learn a few tricks, so write me a line and we will help if we have space. 

Organic methods: We do syntrophic agriculture, our place its a cycle that provides food.  

Certifications: NO certification for now 

Current projects: Building a new room. 

Internet access available.

name: Vebjørn Hansen - Idyllic small farm with mainly sucklercows and outdoor pigs
description:
Hi!
My name is Vebjørn and together with my wife Sunniva our son Eivind 1 year old, we are looking for people to help us with the different tasks in the farm.

Our main production is sucklercows for meat production, earlier we sold some various vegetables, fruits and berries, all directly to local customers, but we're not doing it this year because it takes to much time.

On our farm we have cows, pigs, chickens, turkey and cats. 
We live in a rural place but at the same time not to far from town centre. 

We started up this farm in 2020 with the first cows, now  we have around 10 sucklercows but 30all together. 

We have a greenhouse for mainly tomatoes and are growing some other veggies outside for our own use in the winter.
We have pigs in the forest, also have a few hens and turkey for egg production for our own needs. 🦃 

The tasks on the farm can be very varied depending when you're here, you can get some responsibility for feeding animals like hens and the pigs.

You will be living in a camping wagon 5 meters from the sea, very idyllic and I am a bit jealous about living down there myself, especially in the sunny days😅
The standards are simple, you have kitchen facilities in the wagon, shower and compost toilet outside of the wagon. There is ordinary water toilet and washing machine in the basement of the house for your use.

The placement of your living is a short walk from the farmyard.

Food and meals:
I will buy basic groceries for your need, so you can prepare your own meals and normally eat on your own or hopefully with a partner. 
I am really open for wishes since people comes from different places with different food culture, but things like alcohol you will have to buy on your own. 

We do not want volunteers who smoke or use snuff.

We use most of our time home at our farm and are happy with that, much because of lots of work but also because or lifestyle gives us (most of the time) much energy and lots of experiences with the domestic animals and n

name: Gabriel - Cultivo de Cacau & Hortaliças / Galinheiro
description:
Oi, WWOOFer! Meu nome é Gabriel e sou responsável por um pequeno terreno rural na Bahia, onde desenvolvo um projeto voltado à agricultura de base familiar, com foco principal no cultivo de cacau e coco, além da implementação gradual de uma horta. Antes de me mudar pra Itacaré eu trabalhava com teatro e outras áreas artísticas, o que ainda influencia a forma como penso, buscando sempre integrar sensibilidade, criatividade e funcionalidade no desenvolvimento do projeto.

Atualmente, nosso plantio de cacau já conta com alguns pés em produção, que já estavam aqui antes da minha chegada. A partir disso, estamos em processo de expansão, com o plantio de novas mudas e o objetivo de aumentar progressivamente a quantidade de árvores produtivas, adotando práticas com manejo sustentável. No caso do coco, por enquanto realizamos a manutenção das plantas já existentes, com planos de ampliação futura do cultivo. A horta está em fase inicial de estruturação, incluindo o planejamento e a construção de canteiros para produção de hortaliças

Moro em uma casa com dois quartos junto com um voluntário permanente que colabora em todas as atividades do dia a dia do projeto. Tem também uma segunda casa no terreno, onde vive o caseiro e sua esposa. Eles contribuem com serviços gerais, além de possuírem amplo conhecimento prático sobre a região, o que é fundamental para o funcionamento e adaptação das atividades locais.

Temos um galinheiro com cerca de 30 galinhas, além de algumas criadas soltas. Há também seis cães e dois gatos, que convivem livremente no espaço. Possuímos ainda dois porcos, mantidos atualmente em um chiqueiro provisório de pequeno porte, com planos de ampliação dessa estrutura como parte das melhorias previstas no projeto.

Nosso objetivo é realizar benfeitorias contínuas na fazenda, com foco no aumento da produção rural, melhoria das infraestruturas existentes e no desenvolvimento de um ambiente funcional, sustentável e colaborativo, especialmente por meio da participaçã
_wwoof_travel: Aeroporto de Ilhéus, rodoviária de Itacaré

name: Gail - Vineyard, Haskap Berries, Chickens and more, close to a city but feels remote
description:
This is our third year wwoofing and we look forward to welcoming some great helpers keen to enjoy the area while also supporting a young family farm. 

1.  Our farm is a picturesque property located in the countryside, surrounded by beautiful crown land forest. We have a young family and along with us, our farm is also home to various animals like dogs, chickens - and wildlife. Our main focus is on vineyard cultivation (spring to October), berry picking (in June/July), making jam for local shops with those berries, and taking care of our chickens.  We have a 6 year old son and three big dogs. Two very friendly Pyrenees live outdoors to protect the animals and farm, and our indoor loveable mutt will hug you if given the chance. They do love to get up close for pets and cuddles, so fair warning if you don't like dogs. 

2.  WWOOFers who join us will have the opportunity to participate in a range of activities. You will learn about vineyard management, including  pruning, thinning, and harvesting grapes, depending on what time of year you are here. You may also be involved in berry picking and helping to maintain the berry bushes. There are lots of repairs and improvements around the farm, and an active veggie garden for our family. Additionally, you can assist in feeding and tending to our chickens, learning about their care and the responsibilities involved in raising farm animals.

3.  We provide comfortable accommodations in our two-bedroom/ two bathroom trailer, ensuring privacy and independence. Groceries will be provided to allow you to prepare your own breakfast, lunch, dinner and we can share weekly family meals together. Join us on our patio after a long day to enjoy a drink or share experiences; or enjoy some quiet and privacy on your own deck, whatever you prefer.

4.  You can join us in the vineyard to participate in the day's tasks and help with feeding and tending to our animals. When it's quiet, we spend time managing the forest around us to prevent for
_wwoof_travel: Transit limited in/Around/To Tappen. There are some infrequent buses that can get you to Kamloops, Sorrento or Salmon Arm, and for longer stays we can try to coordinate pick up from Kelowna or Kamloops airport. It's best to have your own car

name: Mark - Large organic garden and a line of herbal products.
description:
Passionate about organic for 20 years, passionate about heatlh and wellness for 25 years ; the two fit together. Have never put manure in the greenhouse, amend the soil in different ways. A plant that struggles rather than being forced to grow provides different flavors, a different nutrient mix. There are presently no animals on the farm. With exception of the chainsaw, all equipment runs on alternative fuels. Off-grid solar, root cellar, sawmill, large garden, etc ; it’s been a journey not yet at it’s destination. Most things are built from a pile of ‘stuff’ acquired second (or third or fourth hand), with emphasis on sustainability and recycling. The farm motto : every dollar you spend is a vote for the world you want (and your children) to live in. If this level of craziness appeals to you, come on down.  I am a young fit 50+ male currently on my own. 

Currently planting seeds and soon plants started in the greenhouse to the garden ; asparagus and rhubarb are up and ready to harvest.  Always try to make use of all plant parts, carrot tops, corn silk, etc. Someone with pickling/canning experience would be beneficial but could just google it, why spoil the fun with knowing what you’re doing. 

Have a 5th wheel trailer and truck box camper for housing ; farm is not a 4 season facility but considering building a more permanent cold-hardy structure. 

A day would consist of whatever needs attention (generally something different to harvest/weed/water or build/repair every day) and prepping/eating meals. There is a 2nd solar pivot array and larger greenhouse to build, and a new sawmill to supply necessary wood, 

Nestled in the foothills of the rocky mountains, there is a river nearby, lakes and mountains beyond, wildlife, waterfalls, etc. Have multiple kayaks and some snorkel gear, but the most common activity is hiking. 

All meals are 99.9% organic, is there any other way? Always found it peculiar when going to an organic farm and seeing bought non-organic items on

name: Carmen - Garden on the lake and farm out of town (50 min away)
description:
Tansi (Hello), WWOOFers, 
We are a small family with a lakeside property in Moberly Lake BC. We have a cabin where we live recreationally and  small home with an garden where a WWOOFer is welcome. We also are part of a small first nations community and help elders with yard upkeep. As First Nations we are active in our community harvesting edible plants, collecting wood, fishing and doing activities  close to home. We live on a lake, so in the summer we love kayaking, hiking, boating and fishing! Picking berries, Atv-ing and going for trips camping is always fun too.

We have a 8 year old and would encourage families to reach out as a preference.

In a variety to yard work, My day time activities as a biologist include foraging wild foods for humans and also caribou!  We are recovering a caribou herd and pick the lichens for winter food security in the boreal forest (taiga) which is a great field trip.

Our tiny home has it own accommodation with its own kitchen. We could supply groceries and share meals. The guests would have access to our lakeside sauna, kayaks and dock. We also have a car that they could use if we arrange the right insurance. 

Our place is 25km to the nearest town of Chetwynd B.C. with local amenities.  So it is considered remote with only 1 gas station that is open part time.

name: Mikael - Horse Ranch, Farm
description: I am a man born in 1942. I live a quiet life on a rather remote farm on the North coast of Iceland. I am a retired psychological therapist. On the farm I have 6 horses that keep me company. The farm has hot water from the earth, so there is a pool all year round. The life I live is quiet, so don't expect to much action, even though sometimes nature and the horses give a little more than one does expect. My English is not the best, but my experience is that it is adequate to make a decent conversation. My written English is not good, so don't be intimidated by short replys. My computor skills are not too good either, so if there are any mistakes on my behalf I do apologise in advance. Due to the huge amount of requests I am only able to reply requests of interest. The tasks I expect you to do, depending on season: - Horse riding, and training the horses - Feeding them/ do stable work (cleaning and so on)- Basic maintenence around the farm i.e. checking the horses' shoes - Keeping the log-house that I rent to tourist tidy and nice - Going for a ride with the tourists if there's any. Cooking the meals and cleaning the kitchen and your own room and bathroom you use. You have to stay minimum 4 weeks and have good experience with horse riding. Accommodation: Main house. The farm is in the area of Tröllaskagi, a very nice area of Iceland, 10 kms from the village Ólafsfjördur. The village is visible from the window, and you can also reach it by bicycle.
_wwoof_travel: Plane or bus to Akureyri. Bus from Akureyri to Olafsfjordur no 78, I will pick you up from the bus stop in Ólafsfjordur.

name: Christine - Homestead
description:
Casa Rosa is an acreage in stunning Tlell, on Haida Gwaii, in Northern BC. It is on the traditional territory of the Haida Nation.  A sweet ferry ride across the Hecate Strait will get you here from Prince Rupert (7 hours), or you can fly up from Vancouver to either Sandspit (YZP) or Masset airports (YMT)  (2.5 hours).  Casa Rossa is located on the east coast of Graham Island, on 16 acres of mostly forested land with about 3 acres cleared. You will sleep in your own private bunkie, out on the dune, amongst coastal Sitka spruce trees, overlooking the Pacific ocean. The bunkie is simple, self-contained, with a single bed, small desk and basic kitchen supplies and equipment (kettle for coffee/ tea, toaster) Hammocks hang outside the bunkie for afternoon snoozes and there is a cute bivvy for a toilet just adjacent. The bunkie has rainwater catchment for hand-washing/ dishes (summer) and drinking water provided in a separate jug. You will look after your own breakfast and lunch, with basic groceries provided at the main house. We will compile a shopping list weekly to ensure your needs are met. There is a small country store just 2 km away where you can walk/ bike to grab a coffee or luxury items you miss.  Dinners will be together, with me, and my son (if he is around) and they will be prepared with local, seasonal ingredients as much as possible and typically are for omnivores, but we can accommodate different needs (vegetarian). Participation in dinner prep is helpful and taking turns is too if you like to cook, and even if you’re just learning. 

You will spend time helping us with a neverending list of farm chores that we will compile together depending on your experience, including: moving rounds of wood, wood splitting & stacking, gardening (weeding, planting, watering, fertilizing, mulching, harvesting, prepping, storing), brush burning, manure collecting, compost-turning, light carpentry, collecting eggs, feeding & watering chickens. 

We also have a small green
_wwoof_travel: YMT Masset Airport/ Skidegate Ferry Landing

name: Damon and Mary - Goat farm, horse and dog rescue and gardens between Austin and San Antonio in Central Texas
description:
WE ARE NOT CURRENTLY HOSTING DUE TO A FAMILY EMERGENCY AND UPCOMING HOME REPAIRS. WE WILL BE ACCEPTING EXPERIECED WWOOFERS STARTING IN AUGUST 2026. We are located between Austin and San Antonio in Central Texas. We raise ADGA PB Oberhasli goats, Registered Kinder goats and various grade dairy goats. Right now, the work that needs to be done, includes heavy spring cleaning type of work with the goat pens and the vegetable gardens. WOOFErs must be able to do heavy work . In the summer, I am only hosting WWOOFERs who can hand milk goats, or will commit to learning hand milkings. I can teach milking, home dairying, and home cheesemaking. We also have an herb garden and a small vegetable garden. We have a horse rescue and there are opportunities to ride about twice a week. 

I have a Dog Rescue and Sanctuary and so we have several dogs and several large dogs. For this reason, and because other dogs sometimes attack goats we do not welcome pets. 

Help is needed with the goat chores, horse chores, feeding dogs, goat structure building, milking, and, if interested, with rescue horse and pony training and rehabilitation. It is mainly heavy duty, dirty work. The animals need to be fed timely, on a regular basis. Animal care is serious, and a very strong work ethic is required. 

I ( Mary) have been an exchange student and I am considered fluent in German. I apprenticed on organic farms in the USA in the 1980's and I am familiar with issues related to travel and WWOOFING. 

Children are welcome and parents are responsible for their safety on the farm, around dogs, around horses and farm animals, and around standing bodies of water. If safety concerns arise and cannot be mitigated, WWOOFers with children will be asked to leave. 

There is a lot to do in the surrounding area. We are located in a major tourist area between the large cities of Austin and San Antonio. We participate in local parades with the horses. People come to the area for river recreation, camping, and Texas
_wwoof_travel: Airports: Austin or San Antonio Texas. Bus/Train: San Marcos, TX

name: Baltazar - Educational Centre, Permaculture, Fruit Trees, Reforestation, Community Farm
description:
We are conservationists, regenerative farmers, and engaged community leaders working to restore and protect the largest jungle in Central America -- the Selva Maya. This area is incredibly biodiverse and home to ancient Maya temples throughout the forest. 

We are looking for help with our Guatemalan-Honduran campesino owned permaculture project which serves as an experimental, educational model to teach the surrounding rural communities restorative methods for sustainably working and living in the jungle. We work with nature focussed youth and women's groups and are engaged in multiple community activities every month. 

The permaculture project is 2.5 hectares of native tropical forest land on the edge of the rural village of Paxcaman (Flores, Peten). We are planting a food forest of ginger, turmeric, papaya, macal, vanilla, herbs, vegetables, etc. There is also a tree nursery, a fish pond and a cacao and ramon tree based agroforestry area. We focus on education, agroforestry and bioconstruction. Currently we are on using a mix of ancestral and modern bioconstruction techniques to build a permaculture education centre from natural materials.

We invite volunteers to join our natural-material construction efforts, forest restoration, community work and teaching! We are particularly keen to host volunteers with manual labour experience. Conversational level of Spanish is an essential requirement.

Our site is located in an ecotourism region of Petén, Guatemala close to Tikal National Park. Flores Island (airport and bus terminal) is approx 15 mins away and the Belize border (Melchor crossing) is 1.5 hours away. 

Local team: Baltazar, Emanuel, Lou, Jasmine
Accommodation style: jungle camping in tent or hammock. 

Please note that we will not consider your volunteering stay confirmed until you have completed our registration form (this is sent out after we have discussed the project with you). PLEASE DO NOT SHOW UP AT THE PROJECT IF YOUR APPLICATION HAS NOT YET BEEN
_wwoof_travel: Local buses from Santa Elena (bus terminal from Guate City) and Flores (Airport) run regularly to the village of Paxcaman during daylight hours

name: Par - Organic farm in a small Gambian Village
description:
At his eco tourist camp Par is creating an oasis amid the sometimes frenetic activity of village life in West Africa.
Par's eco tourist venture has cabins for rental, a permaculture garden, a bird watching hide and the opportunity to experience life in a small village which relies on subsistence agriculture.

This is a real cultural exchange. Par would appreciate a couple of hours of your time each day helping at the camp in exchange for simple accommodation at the camp or the family compound and all meals. Activities would include assisting with  gardening, watering, and camp maintenance. There is the opportunity to learn much about the local culture, the history and the Mandinka language. Par does not read or write English very well so after initial contact through WWOOF, his preferred method of communication is via WhatsApp phone call. 

WWOOFers with experience in permaculture gardening and DIY building projects would be most welcome.
