Currently we have a wide variety of crops, including: mango, orange, lemon, avocado, soursop, coconut, guave, appel, strawberries, olives, pineapples, tomatoes, green peppers, garden eggs, carrots, yam, bitter tomatoe etc. A day-to-day of the WWOOFers could look like this: after waking up, we have some breakfast and attaya together. Then we start working together for a couple of hours. The work can range from watering crops, harvesting crops, weeding the fields, planting new crops, and helping with expanding the nersery for new crops. Then we will go to the village together for lunch with my family, and then we can maybe work a couple of more hours when we are back at the camp. If we are not working, I can take you on a village tour, the beach, the senegalese border nearby, or the snake and turtle farm. I am very passionate about Mandinka culture, so I am excited to show you around and introduce you to the local customs!

Par is building accommodations in an eco-friendly way with local materials, including palm frond roofs. Help wi

name: Shan - Small scale production of eggs, vegetables, berries, fruits and mushrooms.
Octa - Communal Living Group, Family home and garden - urban, Educational Centre, Agritourism Destination
description:
LIBERTAD   EN HUERTO COMUNITARIO Y CASA DEL ÁRBOL.
⚛️ VOLTEANDO EL SISTEMA ⚛️
Estoy feliz de guardar en mi correo principal, la cadena de actualizaciones de mis perfil en esta plataforma de woofing.  Desde el año, 2022, 23, 24,25 dónde expresaba todo lo bueno y malo que me hacian los humanos de mi alrededor ... Era un infierno total debo reconocerlo y hasta casi me envían en prisión, se que no son buenas noticias para mí perfil  así como las guerras a nivel mundial en todos los sentidos  tampoco parece importar pero cada acción cuenta  entonces siento que hicimos nuestra parte hacia el universo y este año 2026 con mucho orgullo debo simplificar diciendo que ya pasé a un nivel donde necesitaba ser yo mismo tal cual como soy en la montaña,  el mundo es una cueva o técnicamente mi habitación por qué simplemente veo el mundo como mi casa..
Entonces para no ir en prisión entro a la municipalidad de mi ciudad para poder demostrar  que no tengo sentido de propiedad y que en verdad solo quiero cuidar el planeta tierra en mi locura, más no ser dueño de un área del planeta tierra.  Pero naturalmente como vivimos en un mundo al revés tuve que vivir experiencias de traicion de mis supuestos amigos a quienes confíe el uso de los terrenos que había propuesto a alcaldes  en gestiones anteriores Pero los políticos no comprenden mi idea e intensión en estos tiempos... Ni yo mismo lo sabía  pero al invitar a los ciudadanos de mi entorno, creyendo que podría ser una idea buena dar la oportunidad de usar los espacios que había recuperado desde niño  y destinarlo para  el usufructo en bien común de todxs, pero un amigo y su familia tenían otro  plan, terrenales totalmente .. pero de todos los sujetos del planeta, uno tendría que traicionarme para yo despertar totalmente  de esta mentira  de amistad y trataron de cambiarme la realidad hacerme ver el malo de la película, loco y calumnias inimaginables con tal de lograr dejarme fuera de la carrera por un supuesto terreno de propiedad. Al f

name: Claus - Homestead, Intentional Community, Orchard, Apiary
description:
Im siebten Jahr zusammen mit Euch Wwoofern lebt der vorher gut einhundert Jahre nicht bewirtschaftete Hof in anderer Form wieder auf. Blühflächen, Hecken, Streuobst und viele andere Maßnahmen sollen/tragen dazu beitragen, einen guten Lebensraum zu schaffen.  Die biodynamische Arbeit zur Schaffung einer landwirtschaftlichen Individualität und zur Wiederbelebung von Ort und Boden ist uns ein wichtiges Anliegen. Den Mittelpunkt bilden die Bienen, denen wir mit Achtung begegnen. Mit vielen Beteiligten gestalten wir unser aller Projekt nunmehr im neunten Jahr. Ein kleines Team trifft sich regelmäßig zur Arbeit im und am Garten und den Wiesen. Wochentags bereiten wir zusammen mit euch WwooferInnen die Mahlzeiten Mittags und Abends zu und nehmen diese zumeist gemeinsam ein. Unser Demeter zertifizierter Hof (nicht kommerziell) liegt im Südwesten Deutschlands, südlich von Freiburg, ca. 25km entfernt von Basel. Kandern-Riedlingen ist mit Busverkehr angeschlossen an den öffentlichen Personennahverkehr.

Not being used as a farm for many decades, right now we are working on hedges, flower-fields, planting of fruits and trees on the grassland to establish both biotope and living environment. Our biodynamical aim is to build up a "farm individuality" and the revitalization of place and ground. The bees are in the center and we meet them with respect. In groups, friends and people related to the farm we are working on our project. Our farm is situated in the Southwest of Germany (Markgräflerland), about 50km south of Freiburg and about 25km north-east from Basel (Bale). Our place can be reached by bus.

biodynamic preparations, wild flower strips, meadows with apple-, cherry- quince-, walnut- and chestnut-trees, hedges, composting, scaled mowing, Benjes-hedges, deadwood-places.
Demeter

Wir suchen Dein Interesse und den Austausch an/über unserer bio-dynamisch und wesensgemäß geführten Imkerei (Demeter-zertifiziert). Gemeinsam begegnen wir den vielfältigen Aufgaben rund um den Hof,
_wwoof_travel: Bahnhof Bad-Bellingen oder Basel Badischer Bahnhof, Flughafen Euro-Airport Basel-Mulhouse-Freiburg

name: Linda - Heritage grain and oilseed farm near Stockholm biodiversity agroforestry climate-friendly farming.
description:
Welcome to live and work with us on Jädra Farm, 45 kilometers from Stockholm and 35 km from Uppsala!

We are Linda and Torbjörn and have lived on the farm since 1993. Our four children are now grown up but often come to visit.  We are very internationally interested and have lived and worked in Latin America, Asia and Africa. Ever since moving to our farm, we have tended to our pastures and forests and since 2020  we are also cultivating the land. 

Our vision is to create a diversified farm, with high biodiversity and as climate friendly as possible. We grow  old Swedish traditional varieties (heritage grains)of wheat, rye, spelt, oats and peas and buckwheat, We also grow plants for oil making like turnip rape, camelina and hemp. We also test different legumes like lupins and lentils. A third of the land is in green manure to enrich the soils for the future. 

Our farm is organically Swedish and EU certified. We have established agroforestry,  a wetland,  created insect habitats and restored old grazing land, among others.  We have a nine year crop rotation to grow as many crops as possible for crop and soil health, and agroforestry is ready to start. Our high biodiversity value pastures are grazed by cows, sheep and horses, owned and tended to by others. The forests are developing to resemble natural forests and we do most of that reshaping work ourselves. This is just the start and we are constantly discussing how to proceed and what the future shall look like. We believe in close contacts between people and also our customers, so we dry, clean, process (two mills and an oilpress) and package all products at home in small scale. 

When staying with us, what you do will depend on your interests and which activities are ongoing for the moment. Just to mention some areas:
•	There are building projects. You can help with painting, carpentry and repairs. 
•	Cleaning, packaging and selling our products. 
•	Planting herbs, bushes and trees for biodiversity and maintaini
_wwoof_travel: Pick up at Bro, Bålsta commuter train station or bus from Stockholm, Uppsala or Airport.

name: Nur - New hobby gardening family
description:
Welcome to Mayo Québec, 
nur and michel here, We are a couple living in a small village called Mayo. It is located northeast of the city of Gatineau. Our garden is 45 mnt to go downtown Ottawa, 43 mnt to gatineau park Visitor center. Where we live there Réserve écologique de la Forêt-la-Blanche, 10 mnt by car and the closest village is Buckingham (10 mnt) the closest bus stop from Ottawa.
we are enjoying producing food in our Gardens, it was just more of a curiosity at first for us. 
we wanted to eat all the foods that we love, find a ways  to make them available in our own garden. we are collecting our own seeds as well. 

Depending on when you will come, to our home, during spring time after the snow melt we do soil preparation, like we do aerate and add soil with compost, pruning shrubs ( black current,blueberry, sea bocktorn, haskap ), plum and grape,removing dead or damaged branches, some seedling, acclimatize seedlings before planting and Planting, watering,weeding.

During the autumn 
Harvest and remove dead plants, dehydrated herbs, store remaining corps, spread a layer of compost and cover it,  planting garlic, dividing perennial plants or cover them.

We would like to learn more and enjoy help to any project that we have in mind.
We can prepare meal and eat breakfast, lunch and dinner together.

You have your own room and bathroom.
We have a washing machine but don't have a dryer. During relaxing  hours You can swing yourself in a hammock or swim or canoe in the river in front of our home, you can use the sauna, also a yoga cabin. 

We speak English. French and Indonesian

name: Brian - <GW134>산속 퍼머컬처 와이너리 / Permaculture Winery in the Mountains
description:
✅ 호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
대한민국 청정 지역 강원도 영월 봉래산 450고지에서 유럽 양조용 포도나무를 퍼머컬처농법으로 재배하여 와인을 만들고 있습니다.
포도 품종은 피노누아, 샤르도네,청수,리슬링 등 와인 양조용 품종을 재배중이며 내추럴 와인을 양조중입니다.
포도밭은 1000평이며 나머지는 3000평은 정원 및 게스트하우스로 가꾸며 관리중입니다. 
포도 농부는 에바와 브라이언으로 도시에서 살다가 농촌으로 이주한 지는 5년이 되었고 농사 이외에도 마차리 국제 아트 페스티벌의 기획자로 지역의 문화 예술 컨텐츠를 만들어 가고 있습니다. 다양한 문화적 기반을 가진 세계 각국의 우퍼들과 교류하기를 바랍니다.

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2022년
✅ 농장 형태 : 와이너리, 양조아카데미, 와인테이스팅
✅ 농장 주작물 : 포도

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 퍼머컬처농법.
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 : 음식물 쓰레기로 퇴비 만들고 게스트들에게 쓰레기 회수를 요청하여 쓰레기 발생을 최소화하기 위해서 노력중

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 아내
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려 동물 : 경주견 1마리(별이)
✅ 우퍼가 하는일 :
-8~9월 : 포도 수확 및 양조
-11월 : 포도 가지 정리 

✅ 선호하는 우퍼상 : 자연을 사랑하고 농작물을 키우는 것을 즐기는 우퍼
✅ 우퍼에게 당부 메세지 : 즐겁게 포도 농사를 도와주시고 함께 와인도 즐길 수 있는 우퍼를 환영합니다.

✅ 숙소에서 와이파이 가능여부 : 가능
✅ 술,담배 가능여부 : 간헐적 외부 흡연, 및 음주 가능
✅ 호스트와 식사여부 : 하루 한,두 끼만 함께 먹고 나머지는 식재료를 드려 직접 해 드셔야 합니다.
✅ 영어소통 : 원활히 가능
✅ 일주일 기준 우핑시간 : 하루5시간 주5일

======== English explanation ==========

✅ Introduction to the Host and the Farm :
Our farm is located at an altitude of 450 meters on Bongnaesan Mountain in Yeongwol, a pristine region of South Korea. We grow European wine grapes using permaculture farming methods and produce natural wine.
The grape varieties we cultivate include Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Cheongsu, and Riesling, all suitable for winemaking.
Our vineyard covers approximately 1,000 pyeong, and the remaining 3,000 pyeong is managed as a garden and guesthouse.
The grape farmers, Eva and Brian, moved from the city to the countryside five years ago. In addition to farming, they are also planners of the Machari International Art Festival, creating cultural and artistic content within the local community.
We look forward to exchanging and connecting with WWOOFers from around the world who come from diverse cultural backgrounds.

✅ Year Farming Started : 2022
✅ Farm Type : Winery, Brewing Academy, Wine Tasting
✅ Main Crops : Grapes

✅ Eco-Friendly Farming Practices : Permaculture farming methods
✅ Eco-Friendly Lifestyle Practices : We compost fo

name: Marius - Permaculture farm in Castiadas, Sardinia, just 6 km from the sea
description:
Bioloco is more than a retreat centre – it is a living space based on the Advaita philosophy that opens the way to an authentic, undivided experience. Inspired by the first encounters with Sardinia over 47 years ago, when the island was still a mysterious, unspoilt paradise, the desire arose to revive the original spirit of this island. Back then, the paths were dusty, the shepherds were nomadic, and nature revealed itself in intense, mystical clarity. Today, Sardinia – with its more than 500 million-year-old geological history – symbolises both the enormous potential and the loss of this primeval state, which is visible in the rapid industrialisation and alienation of modern man.

On 25 hectares in Castiadas, at the foot of the ‘Sette Fratelli’ national park, Bioloco combines history, values and a sustainable art of living into a concept that counteracts the increasing alienation in our technical world. Thousands of fruit trees, medicinal herbs, water sources and ancient rock formations blend here with modern, nature-oriented building methods – clay buildings, terraces and a food forest create a closed, respectful cycle.

The twelve principles of permaculture – from the precise observation of nature and the use of renewable resources to the creative integration of all elements – form the basis of this project. This approach requires continuous, process-oriented learning and agile development that is aligned with the living rhythm of nature.

Based on the Advaita philosophy, which emphasises the non-duality of humans and nature, Bioloco invites you to transcend the boundaries of your own self and experience unity with everything. The aim is to question excessive consumer behaviour and the pursuit of external growth, and instead to follow the path of inner transformation. Workshops on natural building techniques, community work and the conscious experience of natural cycles open up space to recognise oneself as an integral part of life.

Bioloco is a place of self-re

name: Donald - Community Supported Agriculture farm, located in the hills of North Central Pennsylvania.
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
Our project is to continue to develop regenerative systems on our farm. Our farm grows vegetables, and free range laying hens for our CSA and local community. Kathleen and I(Farmer Don), live on the farm with one dog and three cats. WWOOFers will have the opportunity to stay with us in the house, in your own bedroom, with a full bath. We share cooking duties each night. We eat vegetarian, dairy and meat-free.

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
WWOOFers will assist the farmer with sustainable development of the farm, learning regenerative farming practices as well as building community through local agriculture.

3. What could a WWOOFers day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)? Our morning routine includes animal chores, onto daily projects. We break for a communal lunch, followed by afternoon seasonal projects. 
Dinner would probably be on the back porch, or maybe up by the spring-fed pond.

4. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.): I am Farmer Don. My life companion and partner of 38 years passed away in 2022 due to multiple chronic illnesses. I have since met a compassionate, caring and kind partner to continue our farm operations. We have a small dog, Dilly, and 3 cats. They are my world right now. We have approx. 35 laying hens, producing eggs for us and our local community. Dancing Hen Farm owns 26 acres and we are less than 10 miles from Ricketts Glen State Park. I love to hike and our proximity to parks and forests make our location prime for this activity. Waterfall hikes are the best.  The farm has a spring fed pond which is great for swimming. I enjoy acoustic music. I didn't grow up on a farm, but, since starting our farm, my passion for farming has grown each year.  We have been members of PASA,
_wwoof_travel: Bloomsburg, PA is the nearest town, Williamsport, PA nearest airport and bus station, Wilkes-Barre/Scranton is the nearest large city airport

name: Rebecca - Farm specializing in horses and horse care in Wisconsin
description:
A little about me:
My name is Becky and I am the owner of French Kiss Farm (FKF). I am 30 years old and live with my boyfriend (28), 4 year old son, and my aunt. I have lived in Normandie, France doing an entensive jumping training program. I have family in the south of France (Narbonne) and frequently visit. I own a Selle Français mare whom I imported in 2021.  FKF was originally established in Ocala, FL in 2021 and changed to Bristol, WI in 2022. 

A little about our farm:
We are looking for help around our farm with many different things. We specialize in horses and horse care. Some things we will need help with are: 
- fencing installation   
- landscaping 
- generalized horse care which include feeding the horses, maintaining the pastures, stall cleaning, etc. (this can be taught) 

**Would love to find someone who does interior design. We need to repaint our house and update flooring**

Must be willing to operate a smaller tractor 
Must be okay either working alone or with others
Hours are dependent on our current projects or what time of year it is. Approximately 6 hours of work per day with 2 days off.  For work, we start around 6:30/7am. We get the horses outside right away and do the morning feeding. After morning feeding there will be 9-11 horse stalls/box to clean. Finally, evening feeding starts about 1 hour before sun set. And horses come back inside right before the dark. Any days worked/off usually works for us! We are pretty flexible unless there’s a specific request if we need. Please be open to working weekends as well. 

*Horses are labor intensive! Feeding the horses and cleaning the stalls are labor intensive. Please be prepared for that!

As for meals, we don’t always eat together and we definitely do not eat formally. Sometimes my schedule is weird so I kind of eat whenever I get a chance. 

We have a spare bedroom in our home. It is fully furnished with a full size bed, tv, sauna, etc. Shared space includes the kitchen, bathrooms, living roo

name: GALEN - Sheep and goat farm with demonstration garden run by retired environmental studies professor.
description:
Greetings, WWOOFers. I am a (nearly) retired environmental studies professor, farm raised,  with a small herd of sheep and goats. I live on a 20 acre farm with my wife, son, a Great Pyrenees, a Boarder Collie, 20 chickens and a barn cat.   We are located just outside of Eugene, OR, at the south end of the beautiful Willamette Valley. I have traveled the world observing and participating in agriculture and food systems. As a teacher, I used the farm and kitchen as a classroom when teaching about sustainable agriculture and food. I have worked with college students for 40 years and want to continue my work in education even as I leave the formal classroom.

We bring in the herds each evening to protect them from predators and and so we can check on their general welfare. Morning chores consist largely of releasing the herds into the appropriate pastures. The dogs have a daily feeding, work and/or exercise routine as well. Other tasks vary greatly throughout the year including: basic animal care, egg gathering, planting, weeding, harvesting , food preservation, pen cleaning, assisting in lambing and goat births, forest management, wood milling, fence maintenance, landscaping, compost management, and green house work.

WWOOFers will be housed in a 600 square foot guest house with a full kitchen and hot shower.  We will provide food for cooking or will share meals when we are cooking.

I enjoy all aspects of running the farm. I enjoy listening to and performing a wide variety of music. We are avid track and field fans. I also have a wood shop where I make furniture and items for the farm.
_wwoof_travel: 8 miles from Eugene city center and University of Oregon

name: Ane Marit - Smallholding
description:
Hi! I am Ane, 46 years old and a big enthusiast of everything living and growing. I have a small farm in the hillsides of Midtre Gauldal, Norway. Here I have plenty of projects, only two hands and not enough daylight hours in the day to do them all by my self. :D Also, I love working together with others. I think this is because I grew up on a farm and the days consisted of trailing my dad around helping him with various work. Also I was fortunate enough to experience how it was like when the extended family came together to work on tasks such as "hesjing", which was the traditional way of drying grass on Norwegian farms to secure the food for the animals over winter. I have a cat, Laffen. I will in time have mohair goats and a  small flock of icelandic chickens. This year is also the small beginnings of a market garden.  I also do some foraging for plants and mushrooms for food and to use for handicraft and other purposes so this will also be an activity, depending on what is in season. I´ve worked for several years as a professional forager and I´ve held a lot of courses in foraging, so if this is one pf your interests, I´d be glad to share this activity with you. I do quite a bit of traditional and more experimental/ modern preparing and conserving of ingredients like baking of traditional flatbread, kimchi, kombucha, beer brewing and my latest try was with the slavic bread-based drink kvass, but this I have not mastered yet :) I make most of my spreads my self and all the vegetables and berries I grow and forage  have to be preserved in different ways, so I make a lot of sauces, pickles and cordial amongst other things. I am working toward so called self- sufficiency, but as someone wise so poignantly pointed out: there´s no such thing as self -sufficiency:  we will forever need the community and collaboration with others in this.

I would like to have a couple of periods this summer with people staying. I love sustainable living , foraging, food, laughing, musi
_wwoof_travel: Støren for trains and buses and Trondheim Airport

name: Paul - Homestead, Hobby Farm, Ranch/ Livestock Farm, on farm cafe/Farm store
description:
Get out of the lower mainland and Southern Interior and see some wild BC :)  Check out our local ski hill 'Hudson Bay Mountain' where there is lots of nice dry powder and no lift lines! We are a certified organic livestock farm, if you want to experience looking after a wide variety of animals using organic farming methods we are one of the very few places in this region to do it. Located 25 minutes from Smithers by car, our farm is 320 acres where we raise pigs, cows, chickens, turkeys, Katahdin sheep, alpaca's and other critters.  Our entire farm is certified organic (NOOA)  Work can involve any farm project (feeding, cleaning, fencing, firewood gathering, gardening, building, cooking, house work, etc).  WWOOF'ers cabin has two bedrooms with Queen sized beds plus a living room area with small fridge and is wood heated - also has great WIFI.   If you wanted to stay here for a longer term in the spring you could plant and maintain our large market garden which you could sell at the local farmers market in our tent for a source of income. Unlimited recreational potential in this area, we can help you get out there too!  We often cook vegetarian fare, but as a livestock farm meat is common in our meals.  We love food! - vegetarian, Indian, Asian, Thai, etc, maybe you want to come and show us how it's done?  Contact via email is preferred as we are often outside. We expect four hours per day per person for excellent room and board.   PS we don't really have a chalet for you to stay in...but the cabin is pretty sweet!

Organic methods: farming according to Canadian Organic Standards

Certifications: NOOA

Current projects: currently working on a new brooder building, our poultry processing facility and more.  Walking trail maintenance (clearing windfall, brushing as required), and expansion of same trail network. Daily chores, feeding, watering and bedding all the critters, tending to baby chicks and turkeys.  Our son's own a small cafe on our farm property as well, vot

name: Rodrigo - Permaculture, Sintropic Agroforestry & Bioconstruction demostrative Center.
description:
¡Hola! Soy Rodrigo Gonzalez. Tras años de una vida profesional en la CDMX, decidí transformar mi forma de vivir y fundar CASANA en el corazón de Tepoztlán, Morelos. CASANA es un centro de regeneración y eco-hostal donde buscamos pasar del consumo a la producción consciente. Actualmente, vivimos aquí 4 residentes permanentes (2 hombres y 2 mujeres) enfocados en un estilo de vida saludable, activo y en armonía con la tierra. Tenemos un ambiente de comunidad vibrante, compartiendo el espacio con voluntarios que buscan aprender sobre sistemas vivos.

Un día en CASANA comienza con la energía de la montaña. Las actividades principales se centran en el mantenimiento de nuestro sistema agroforestal sintrópico  y bosque comestible, así  como en proyectos diversos: bioconstrucción, elaboración de bio fertilizantes, entre otras. Los WWOOFers aprenderán sobre regeneración de suelos, poda, siembra orgánica y técnicas de construcción natural.
​
Día típico: Mañanas de trabajo físico en el huerto o construcción (4-5 horas), seguidas de un almuerzo comunitario. Las tardes son para el estudio personal, la experimentación en el taller o simplemente disfrutar del entorno de Tepoztlán.

Somos apasionados de la nutrición como medicina. Seguimos un estilo de vida ovo-lacto vegetariano, priorizando ingredientes 100% orgánicos cosechados directamente de nuestro huerto. Los voluntarios se alojan en nuestra casa de huéspedes y las comidas son compartidas y colectivas; nos encanta cocinar juntos y aprender nuevas recetas basadas en plantas que nutran el cuerpo para el trabajo físico que realizamos.

En CASANA celebramos la vida a través de la música, la buena charla y experiencias gastronómicas como nuestras noches de pizza al horno de piedra. Estamos rodeados por la mística de Tepoztlán; a pocos minutos de senderos increíbles hacia el Tepozteco y pozas naturales. Si te gusta el senderismo, la filosofía regenerativa y el arte, te sentirás en casa. Buscamos personas con iniciativa, que disfrute

name: Renate - Solidarietà oltre i confini, contro l'abbandono della campagna, per una vita viva
description:
"Solidarietà oltre i confini, contro l'abbandono della campagna, per una vita viva" - il motto che faceva nascere CCC nel 1992 (frutto della lotta contro i missili nucleari a Comiso) non ha perso la sua validità. L'appezzamento, situato sulle colline dei Monti Iblei, tra Comiso e Chiaramonte Gulfi, comprende 4 ha di terreno con caseggiati rurali, in parte ristrutturati, di cui uno adibito a  piccolo B&B e gli altri ad abitazioni private. 
Attualmente vivono a CCC Renate ('57), pittrice, che porta avanti il progetto sin dal suo inizio. E Iano ('83), musicista, che è qua dal 2024.
I wwoofers parteciperanno a tutte le faccende di vita in campagna, dalle raccolte: mandorle, carrube, olive, timo, fichi, alla cura del terreno e dell'orto, alle potature e al taglio della legna da ardere, alla preparazione del pane, marmellata e sciroppi...
Chi viene qua trova una vita semplice connessa ai ritmi della natura. 
Corrente e acqua calda ci arrivano dal sole (impianto fotovoltaico 12V non collegato alla rete). 
Crediamo che l'attenzione alle azioni quotidiane sia fondamentale per creare il mondo che desideriamo. Facciamo parte di una rete di persone che si sostengono attraverso baratto, mutuo aiuto e scambio di conoscenze.
I Wwoofer, massimo due, sono ospitati nel caravan. Preferiamo soggiorni di almeno 2 settimane. Si condividono pasti vegetariani/vegani. 
Lingue parlate: italiano, tedesco, inglese. L'email non viene letta quotidianamente.

 ****************************** 

"Solidarity across borders, against the abandonment of the countryside, for a lively life" - our motto from 1992 (the result of the struggle against the nuclear missiles in Comiso) is still valid today. The plot, located in the hills of the Monti Iblei, between Comiso and Chiaramonte Gulfi, comprises 4 hectares of land with rural houses, part of which have been renovated, one of which is used as a small B&B and the others as private homes. 
Currently living in CCC is Renate ('57), a painter, who has been run
_wwoof_travel: Fermata Bus via Gandhi, Comiso / Stazione Bus Coffa, Chiaramonte Gulfi

name: Stephan - Homestead dream slowly taking shape
description:
Our homestead is a permaculture inspired dream becoming reality on 1.5 acres just minutes from downtown San Diego. Established in September of 2020 we are in the beginning stages of rehabilitating the land, building a community space and creating a food forest. We are planning to be food independent in three years, of the grid in six and enjoying every step on the way. 
On our homestead you will learn to build with natural materials like Cob and Adobe, establish and tend a garden, create soil, digging with a backhoe (and by hand :), plumbing, framing, build water catchments, , create swells, establish a food forest and many more useful skills, probably a few silly ones too.

Organic methods: we will try any method at least once

Certifications: not certified but aspiring to be

Current projects: The main focus at this point is to establish the vegetable garden and build the facilities for guests to stay, followed by rainwater catchment systems and water storage.

Internet access available

name: Marisabel - Fundación Apachita, dedicada a la enseñanza de la Eduación Ambiental y enseñanza de horticultura org
description:
Somos una familia de 3 personas, todos ambientalistas. Fuimos productores de hortalizas orgánicas y hierbas medicinales, ahora vivimos en la ciudad de La Serena, Región de Coquimbo, norte de Chile.
Para canalizar nuestra actividad medioamambiental, fundamos Fundación Apachita, donde impartimos educación ambiental, horticultura orgánica y terapia hortícola, a diferentes públicos y personas: personas mayores, adultos, mujeres, niños y jóvenes.
Estamos buscando una nueva granja para comprar, pero eso aún no ha sido posible. Sin embargo podemos recibir uno o dos voluntarios , ellos pueden ayudar con las tareas del hogar y ser voluntarios en el trabajo de la fundación.
Acá podrás conocer la hermosa ciudad de La Serena, sus playas, el valle del Elqui y mucho más.
En el futuro, con una nueva granja, habrá mucho trabajo. 
Somos vegetarianos, pero somos tolerantes.
Como somos uno de las wwoofering más antiguas (Desde 2011), y debido a la experiencia de todos estos años, recibiendo a muchos voluntarios, nos hemos dado cuenta que queremos recibir personas a las que les guste compartir e intercambiar experiencias en temas culturales y medioambientales.
No recibimos personas que vienen de vacaciones.

We are a family of 3 people, all environmentalists. We were producers of organic vegetables and medicinal herbs, now we live in the city of La Serena, Coquimbo Region, northern Chile.
To channel our environmental activity we founded Fundación Apachita, where we provide environmental education, organic horticulture and horticultural therapy to different audiences and people: older adults, adults, women, children and young people.
We are looking for a new farm to buy, but it hasn't been possible yet. However, we can receive one or two volunteers, they can help with household chores and volunteer in the foundation's work.
Here you can get to know the beautiful city of La Serena, its beaches, the Elqui valley and much more.
In the future, with a new farm, there will be a lot of work.
W
_wwoof_travel: Aereopuerto La Florida de La Serena, Región de Coquimbo, Estación de Buses de La Serena.

name: Tayla - A sanctuary space, gathering grounds, and experiential educational institute in the UP of Michigan
description:
Hello WWOOFers, we are the Wellfolx pack - michael, tayla, ayān (6), and amagi (2), maple and birch (4-year-old shepsky sisters), and chewy and woody (1-year-old kitty siblings). We are an interracial, queer, neurodivergent family converting a 40-acre off-grid "camp" into a rewilding sanctuary at the nexus of ecology, healing, and liberation. Our mission is to foster rewilding through the operation of a seasonal retreat center that serves as a space of sanctuary for our earthly kin, as a gathering grounds for movement activists, creatives, and healers, and as an institute for experimental experiential embodiment education.

A bit about the place - Our camp is located on the waterfalls where the Yellow Dog River meets the Bushy Creek in the Huron Mountain Range of the Northwoods, Upper Peninsula of Michigan (da "UP"), Southcentral shores of Gichigami (Lake Superior), Occupied Anishinaabek 1842 Treaty lands. The camp has been here for ~100 years, and the infrastructure - cabin, sauna, outhouse, sheds, outbuildings, machinery, well, solar, septic, etc. - reflects that history. The land itself is magical. The camp sits on a flat, open plateau in the river canyon, overlooking the river, situated under a large rocky outcropping mountaintop with views of Superior, surrounded by ravines, rugged terrain, and old-growth woods. The land abuts State land, a local Community Forest, and commercial forestry land, so there is endless public access all around. We are three miles deep on a truck trail and a mile from our nearest year-round homestead neighbors, so it's very remote, and yet only 30 minutes from Marquette, the largest town in the UP, so amenities are readily accessible. 

A bit about us - We are PhDs, activist-organizers, land tenders, and healers. Michael has a PhD in ecology, spirituality, and religion with research in human rewilding, as well as a master's in Outdoor Leadership, an MBA, and a background in engineering and green building design, . Tayla has a PhD in w

name: Tumaini - We are a orphanage and education center advocating for self-sufficient help to children and widows
description:
Let me first extend our WELCOME to you! We say Karibu!

If you are comfortable working with children and widows in challenged communities, TUMAINI FOUNDATION may be a fit for you. You will have a choice of living in the compound, with a community member, self-provided lodge rental space or combining options. We are caring for widows with food, occasional visits and support. We house children, teach them, provide for their safety and show them love and affection. We are also involved in no-til, organic farming on a small plot of land. We are actively seeking more land and a variety of business opportunities. The children of the TUMAINI FOUNDATION are polite, well-mannered and loving as is most of the Dodoma community. Although there are many pleasurable activities in Dodoma and the surrounding area, there are many projects and issues that we are working on and hope to address with your help. ‘Karibu’ or feel welcomed. You can check out our missions and our past projects in https://www.tumaini-foundation.org/

The closest airport will be in Dodoma, the capital of Tanzania, we offered airport pickup service and discounted tours to the safaris. These proceedings will go back to the foundation directly. We are located in a rustic community nestled in a village of the Gogo Tribe, with infrastructure challenges such as dirt roads, power outages, occasional minor flooding and out-houses in some places. The town center provides many of the conveniences that are available in most cosmopolitan areas. 

We offer private room or shared domatory, depending on the availability. The NYUMBANI Lodge, 5 minutes away, also provides favorable accommodations. Space is limited and the regular diet is based on traditional Tanzanian foods. You can also walk 15 mins to the main town that serve a variety of western styled cuisine.

The minimum required stay for volunteers is (2) weeks. If you are unable to remain for that length of time your visit would actually be more disruptive then helpfu

name: Rebecca - 150 acre fifth generation ranch that has been operational since 1888 in heart of Tetons
description:
We are located in the heart of the Teton Range bordered by national forest and BLM lands.  The 150 acres is a fifth generation homestead ranch that has been operational since 1888.
The ranch is 6 miles from Grand Targhee Resort, Alta, Wyoming;  40 miles from Jackson Hole, WY; 90 miles from Yellowstone National Park and 45 miles to Grand Teton National Park.  
Activities include sightseeing, hiking, fishing, rock climbing, biking, river rafting, canoeing and water sports. 

Currently we are raising Alpha hay as the major crop on the ranch.
Additionally we grow raspberries, red currents, honey berries,  goji berries, gooseberries,  rhubarb, asparagus, apples, and cherries.  Seasonal planting includes beets, carrots,  beans, peas,  squash, onions and garlic. 
There is an extensive organic vegetable garden and Greenhouse.  We grow tomato and cucumber in the greenhouse and also some greens like basil, arugula, lettuce, radish, chard, and herbs.

PROJECTS WILL INCLUDE:  
Tree planting & pruning
Noxious weed abatement
Raised Bed maintenance and planting
Organic vegetable garden maintenance and planting
Grounds maintenance and planting
Firewood cutting, splitting and stacking 
Harvest and preserving vegetables and fruits

LENGTH OF STAY:
WE ARE CURRENTLY looking for WWoofers to join us for one to six  week sessions  in 2025.   We would require at least a one week stay and would accept 3-weeks to a month depending on goals, skills and compatibility.
 
DATES NEEDED:  We would like WWOOFERS to be available 
May 1 -  June 30th -  August 1- October 1, 2025. We take a break for the month of July

WORK: At the RANCH we work four or five  days a week (4 supervised five-hour shifts plus a few hours) for a total of 25 hours per week. In addition each of us work three hours per week on chores or individual ranch projects. We work efficiently and intensely with only one break so that we can finish our work in this very short amount of time.

Some of the WORK at the RANCH is physically
_wwoof_travel: Jackson Hole, WY.  (JAC). or Idaho Fall, ID. (IDA)

name: Bonnie - Cacao (Chocolate), Tropical Fruit,  Mac Nut and Coffee Orchard, Garden, Vegetables, Market, Flowers
description:
Aloha!

Our beautiful  Certified Organic farm has 8 acres of mixed exotic tropical fruit, hearts of palm, cacao (chocolate), macadamia nuts and coffee.   

We are very organized on the farm, we work in the mornings and the rest of the day is for you to enjoy the island life. The beach is just down the hill where there is great swimming, snorkeling and surfing (you may even see a dolphin, turtle or whale). 

Our farm is very close to a great small community where there are stores, Sunday farmers market, a fish market, coffee shops, local shops, yoga and many other activities. We are always in the process of learning here. We are trying to make our life and farm as sustainable as possible. We grow most of what our farm needs for food and fertilizer by using different types of cover crops, compost tea, mulching and composting (we also compost with black soldier flies). We use agroforestry techniques when possible. The soil food web is a big part of how our farm works. We feed the soil and the soil feeds the plants. We have incorporated nitrogen fixing trees and shrubs. We also have  chicken tractors that we move around the farm for bug, weed control, eggs and fertility. (they also provide a lot of entertainment!) We grow most of our own food and sell the excess at the local farmers market and local stores. You will learn the whole process of growing, processing and marketing coffee, macadamia nuts and cacao (chocolate) while you are here. We enjoy what we do and sharing it with others. If you are interested in learning where your food comes from, learn how to grow your own food in a garden and learn how a small family farm works day to day this is the farm for you.  You must be 21 or older to apply. Please do not ask us for information from our Facebook page. We do not respond to the Facebook page. You can check out pictures of our farm on Facebook and Instagram.

Organic methods: We are an certified organic farm.  We try to keep it simple and grow all the things we ne
_wwoof_travel: Kona International Airport,  Bus stop Captain Cook

name: Emilian-Paul - Life in Romania — Where the Seasons Set the Clock
description:
A Sustainable Life Project
My name is Paul, I am 49 years old, an electronics engineer by profession. Together with my wife, we have been building a project since 2016 on a piece of land in the hill-mountain area of Vrancea — a place where we set out to live as close to real autonomy as possible, decoupled as much as we can from the rhythm and dependencies of modern life.
What started as an experiment became a way of life. And now we want to take it a step further.
Where we are now
The property has a large-capacity solar photovoltaic system that provides full energy and heating autonomy. We have our own water, a garden, an orchard, and beehives. Two connected houses, with four rooms, a shared kitchen, and many places where you can sit, read, think or simply listen to the silence.
Life here follows the rhythm of the seasons, not the clock. Mornings are quiet, the air is clean, and the days are filled with simple, meaningful things — the garden, the orchard, medicinal plants, the bees, maintaining the land. We eat simply and healthily, almost entirely from what we produce or from local sources. We avoid processed food and enjoy cooking and eating without rush.
Animals are an important part of this place. We share our life with two Lagotto Romagnolo dogs, two Labradors and a few cats, who move freely and remind us every day what presence and joy truly mean.
This is not a holiday destination. It is a living, functional place where every resource has a purpose.
Where we want to go
Our vision is a community that is almost 100% sustainable — a kind of modern tribe, where people and resources support each other.
We are working towards building a fish pond, a beekeeping pavilion, additional accommodation spaces, and perhaps a woodworking workshop. The next concrete steps include:
— An electric drying oven for fruits and medicinal plants — local processing, no waste
— A sustainable irrigation system — the garden watered exclusively from our own production
— A tourism componen

name: Alexa - Transylvanian diary farm
description:
Plain area.

Farm with cows, cheese production and guest house

Taking care of the animals, seeding, harvesting, making of the cheese, taking care of the garden and the fruit trees, decorating and mentaining the guest house, cooking for guests, etc. 

A good condition room and indoor bathroom.

We are located in a open plain location, in the midle of Transylvania and we enjoy meeting and working with new people.
_wwoof_travel: Ludus train station and bus station, Cluj Napoca airport

name: Jo - Permacultuur Familie boerderij Drenthe voedselbos met koeien kippen Alternative homestead vrij leven
description:
1. Beschrijf uw project, uw plaats en zijn bewoners (inclusief dieren):
Permacultuur boerderij autarkie moestuin voedselbos, Nieuw Weerdinge, familie met 5 kinderen. Op het land leven poezen koeien kippen en bijen

2. Geef een lijst van de activiteiten waaraan WWOOFers zullen deelnemen en wat je hen wilt leren:
Klussen, verven, timmeren, hout hakken  en/of tuinieren, melken, kaas maken, bomen verplaatsen, oogsten, eten maken, onkruid wieden, dieren verzorgen, enz.

3. Hoe zou de dag van een WWOOFer er op jouw locatie uit kunnen zien (tijdspanne voor deelname aan activiteiten, verwachtingen...)?
Uitslapen, ochtend gesprek om de activiteiten te bespreken, in de middag deze activiteiten samen of individueel uitvoeren.

4. Jij bent de gids! Vertel over je eigen hobby's (muziek, koken, etc.) en die mogelijk in de buurt (wandelen, zwemmen, etc.):
Radio dj " https://eigenwijze30.nl ", muziek luisteren, dansen, concerten, natuur, planten en bomen, spiritualiteit, Germanen, Friezen, Kelten, thuisonderwijs, zweethut en sauna op eigen terrein. Overleven in de natuur, oogsten uit de natuur, kruidengeneeskunde, zelfvoorziening, permacultuur, stam.
_wwoof_travel: Bus stopt dichtbij en vertrekt vanaf treinstation Emmen

name: Gisela - Family home and garden - rural
description:
Hi there!
The former Masonic Lodge of Port Dufferin and masons' property is now turning into a beautiful oceanfront home with an extravagant garden! We are located in a small village on the Eastern Shore (Marine Drive), with gorgeous scenery and friendly people :)  Our garden is filled with flowers and herbs. 
We have an awesome river to go swimming , just minutes away, or jump into the ocean at our doorstep!

name: Dan - Orchard focused on being a place of healing (physically, spiritually and mentally) for people
description:
Our farm is situated on 36 acres 2 miles west of Taylors Falls. I found this property when I was looking for a new house to care for my  mom (who had Alzheimer's) in. Since I was looking for something new, I also wanted to look for a place where a dream of starting an orchard could be realized. While living in Bloomington, I  experimented with a system in my  backyard which proved peaches could grow successfully in a hostile winter environment with temperatures as low as -35°F. The system worked especially well organically with minimal energy input and removed 2000 miles of carbon footprint to provide the same delicious stone fruits locally! Now that I have the farm, I do all of that on a larger scale in high tunnels as well as maintain ongoing research with dwarfing pear tree rootstocks, new stone fruit varieties, fruit seedlings and cold weather nut production. I  steward the uncultivated acreage with earth friendly practices that promote a naturally healthy habitat for the creatures all around us. I traveled extensively in Europe and speak basic German, Guten Tag!!

My vision is for the farm to be a place of healing (physically, spiritually and mentally) for people of all walks of life. My family experienced a lot of loss and I have a heart for others who have as well (I endeavor to be an open, transparent person and remain open to share parts of my  story with you as we get to know each other). My vision is for people to experience something here they might not be able to otherwise. I’m not completely sure how that vision will unfold but am open to seeing what is in store for this farm. What I know for sure: I need to share what I have been so blessed with which is why I started hosting campers via Hipcamp and AirBnB.

I feel I have a gift as a teacher and love to share this knowledge. Though not academically trained in horticulture, my hands-on passion to discover and learn has contributed to the success of the orchard, especially in organic peach and apricot g

name: Grégory - Ferme de plantes médicinales et aromatiques, maraichère, pépinière en AB et apiculture
description:
Bonjour. Je m'appelle Grégory, je vis et je travaille dans une forêt en compagnie de mon chien Beethoven. Ancien menuisier, je me suis reconverti dans l'agriculture il y a 8 ans. Je suis ce qu'on appelle un paysan-herboriste.

Voici les activités que vous serez amenés à faire :
-cueillette de plantes
-maraichage
-défrichage
-plantation
-apiculture
-pépinière.

Pour votre hébergement, je vous offre une chambre avec un lit double, toilettes et douche (eau à faire chauffer pour de l'eau chaude) à proximité de l'hébergement. Concernant la gestion de l'eau, il faudra veiller à ne pas la gaspiller car elle est rare pendant la période sèche.

Je vous fournirai de quoi cuisiner car mes talents culinaires ne sont pas extraordinaires. Nous irons faire les courses ensemble et nous nourrirons des produits locaux et de saison.

Le climat est sec et chaud du mois d'octobre au mois de décembre puis pluvieux jusqu'au mois de mai. Les températures baissent drastiquement à partir du mois de mai jusqu'au mois de septembre.

Pendant la période cyclonique, nous nous autorisons à refuser des personnes en cas de cyclone proche.

Même si je travaille de 8h à 17h, vous adopterez votre rythme. Je dormirai sur place sauf 1 à 2 nuits par semaine pendant lesquelles vous serez seul sur le lieu. Le week-end, je vous ramènerai au Tampon chez moi pour y dormir car je ne suis pas sur le terrain le samedi et le dimanche.

J'aime bien le trail, la salsa, les randonnées en montagne.

Le lieu est isolé et dans le cas où vous n'êtes pas véhiculé, il vous faudra descendre au village de la Crête pour prendre le bus si besoin.

Si possible, je vous conseille de venir voir le site et l'hébergement pour être sûr que cela vous convienne.

name: Serge - Agritourism Destination
description:
Notre ferme  se consacre à la culture de bleuets, baies de sureau, camerises et à la transformation de ses fruits:  sirops, confitures et vinaigre. 
 Les woofers sont demandés de travailler quatre heures par jour, six jours par semaine.  De préférence, congé,  le dimanche . Un petit camping fait aussi partie de la ferme.   Les heures peuvent varier, mais principalement, le travail se fait de  8h à  midi. Les woofers utilisent le bloc sanitaire réservé au camping pour douche et toilette. Les responsabilités se regroupent autour du désherbage, les récoltes des petits fruits, fraises, bleuets, sureau et raisin, s'occuper parfois du magasin et toutes autres tâches reliées au travail de la ferme. Nous sommes situés tout près du Centre Éco-Irving (La dune de Bouctouche), ainsi que 10km de la ville de Bouctouche où se trouve le Pays de la Sagouine.  Le temps libre peut être réservé à la plage, en vélo sur la piste cyclable ou se promener dans la région.  Nous sommes à  la recherche d'aide de mai à la fin septembre.

name: Erin - Okanagan Lake Organic Garden
description:
Come hang out, garden, and swim 🌿

We’re a family of four (kids are 11 & 13), plus a friendly dog and a cat, living on a pretty special spot within walking distance to a very swimmable lake. Summer here is kind of magic—lake swims, sunshine, and long evenings outside.

We’re tucked between Summerland and Peachland. It’s quiet, a bit rural, and great if you like being outside more than being in town.

This isn’t a big farm—just a busy family garden that keeps us fed and slightly overwhelmed in the best way. We like to grow garlic, haskaps, raspberries, blackberries, tomatoes, peppers, squash, basil, greens, and whatever else we get excited about that year.

The work (aka life with us):

Around 20–25 hours/week
A lot of weeding (I love weeding, I hope you do to! 😄)
Planting, harvesting, general garden care
Random projects depending on the week—building pathways, organizing tools, sheet mulching new areas, etc.
We tend to work more on weekends, so days off are best taken mid-week

Food & home life:

Breakfast + lunch are do-your-own thing in our shared kitchen
We typically eat dinner together
Cooking and kitchen clean-up is shared by all
Once a week we do a quick group clean of common areas

About us:

I run a small gardening company, so I’m often off tending to other people’s gardens during the day—but I am flexible to be available when needed. 
My husband is a carpenter/builder and works in Kelowna.
Our kids are active, social, and fun to be around. They swim, do track and field, love hiking and climbing and are used to having WWOOFers around from time to time.

Getting around:
Walkable to the best swimming lake!  We’re about 8 minutes from Summerland, and 1 hour from Kelowna Airport. There’s public transit from town (not on weekends/holidays), but not right from our house. A car would be ideal for exploring, but not essential.

If you’re into dirt, fresh food, lake swims, and a relaxed family vibe, you’ll probably fit right in here.

Send us a message—we’d love to h
_wwoof_travel: Kelowna International Airport, Penticton Regional Airport, EBus terminal West Kelowna

name: Michael - Mostly orchard and permaculture work these days
description:
A small, startup farm focused on orchard work, gardening, some rotational grazing with goats and alpaca. Kindness and openness  are more important traits than farm knowledge here, and the first few Wwoofers have been of exactly that mind space. If you have a project you'd like to tackle, or just want an overview of day-to-day hobby farming, reach out.

With roughly 15 acres of grazing, orchard, and forested space, and another 9 acres of fledgling, market garden (1 acre), infrastructure, projects and ideas to follow.

Close to Frederick, MD, Washington, DC, and numerous other cities nearby, you'll have the opportunity to explore as well. I can provide transportation on pre-planned outings. LGBTQ+ friendly.

Just message me if you have any questions!

*Only fluent/conversational in English, but with limited Spanish, French, and Indonesian for simple communication

Organic methods: Permaculture, no-till, organic methods applied.

Certifications: Just started. Organic approach, not certified.

Current projects: permaculture planting, Building infrastructure and planting fruit trees.

Running large garden.

Planting perennials (fruit trees, berries, asparagus, etc.).

Updating/mending fences and infrastructure.

Internet access available

As is discussed with wwoofers before acceptance, I love off-farm and my farm partner/neighbor is the one available for 24/7 support. I am also on the farm 4-5 days a week, or when not operating the market/business side.

name: Lisa - Developing a homestead training center to share about growing organic food and medicinal herbs
description: We are developing a Homestead training center for anyone wanting to learn and grow there own organic food and medicinal herbs. We are located on the Saluda river with kayakers floating by daily in the summer. We host a monthly real food pot luck to the community with our wood fired pizza oven and screen room and tables by the river. We have many grow systems like hügelkultur, 55 27 gal wicking buckets , aeroponics, Alaska grow buckets, PVC recycle grow systems, tower gardening, Hydroponic Dutch buckets, elderberries, muscadine, blackberry, figs and blueberry bushes. Our 2 greenhouses keep us busy too. We have a campground area and 5 retreat rentals here also. some of our other projects we do are alt building, cement raised beds, herbal medicine, Our animals include many chickens , 4 geese, 5 goats and 3 rabbits.. We are newly retired and enjoy encouraging others to grow your own food and let food be your medicine (when possible ). Lisa is a retired Lic Midwife who led clinical mission trips around the world with her Midwife school students. She now teaches 1/2 way house ladies sewing and crafts free at local community  center. Chris can and does build and fix about everything and has a auto shop and newly built wood shop to prove it! We also have a desire to train missionaries how to be self sufficient when going oversees to serve the poor.  Help is much welcome in whatever projects we are working on, gardens, propagating plants, building fences, building cement raised beds (we built forms ), future dome or yurt  are in the plans for the future. Building rabbit and chicken hutches happens ofter here too! We sometimes get mushroom spores from Mushroom Mt also located in Easley! Just did mushroom  logs with the 10 trees we just cut down. We want to start  doing homestead classes, recycled arts and crafts, retreats and more in new meeting room we fixed up attached to screen room where we do our pot lucks. Oh and have plans of a commercial size greenhouse on top of hill

name: Gabriel - Family-run organic tea farm in Guria producing artisan green, black, white and wild blueberry leaf t
description:
Hello WWOOFers!

My name is Gabriel and I live with my family in the beautiful tea-growing region of Guria, western Georgia. We run TeniTea, a small family-owned organic tea farm producing artisan green, black, white and fermented wild blueberry leaf teas.

Our tea-making journey began in the 1990s. At that time we had old tea plantations but no equipment, so my father built a wooden tea rolling machine powered by water, which allowed us to produce our first black tea in 1997. Since then, we have continued preserving traditional tea craftsmanship while sharing our passion for sustainable farming and Georgian hospitality.

Our family enjoys meeting people from around the world, exchanging cultures, drinking tea together and

Tea production is our main activity. Depending on the season, WWOOFers may help with hand-picking tea leaves, processing tea, sorting and packaging, maintaining the plantation, gardening and light farm work.

We also make traditional fermented wild blueberry leaf tea collected from the nearby mountains. Volunteers can learn about tea cultivation, artisanal tea processing methods, tasting techniques and the history of Georgian tea.

A typical day usually starts with breakfast and tea, followed by 4–5 hours of farm activities in the morning. Afternoons are often free for resting, exploring the village, hiking, learning Georgian cooking or enjoying tea tastings and cultural exchange with our family.

No previous experience is required. Curiosity, a positive attitude and respect for nature are the most important qualities.

WWOOFers stay in our family home in a private or shared room with access to a bathroom, kitchen and common areas.

We provide three homemade meals per day, prepared mainly with local and seasonal ingredients whenever possible. Volunteers will have the opportunity to taste traditional Georgian dishes, fresh products from the village and our artisan teas.

We aim to create a warm family atmosphere where guests feel welcomed and beco
_wwoof_travel: Ozurgeti Bus Station / Ureki Railway Station / Batumi International Airport/Kutaisi international airport

name: Paulo - Family home and garden - rural
description:
I´m a photographer, visual artist, designer and philosopher
You are invited to live with our unique family of artists and lovers of life
in a beautiful place with a welcoming home with a great backyard
with by the exuberant sunset in the savanna of the brasilian cerrado

it would be wonderful to have you with us helping with ours art and eco projects, teaching language to our children, cooperating with our family, helping us to improve our quality of life more integrated with nature

Organic methods: as organic as possible

Certifications: because we are still in the installation phase with a very small chicken farm

Current projects: we have many artistic projects
we recently moved to this new home
with the help of the volunteers we intend to improve the raising of chickens, to produce organic food, to create bees and mushrooms and to reforest our property

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel:
We live in a nice neighborhood near the airport
and 30 minutes by bus from downtown Brasilia
around Brasilia there are numerous waterfalls and about 3 hours is the beautiful Chapada dos Veadeiros with dozens of waterfalls
Two hours away has the histori

name: Mikko - Rantala family farm
description:
In Rantala farm you get close to real Finnish countryside. Farm is near lake and it is possible to swim in summer daily and wintertime in cold water every day too.There is plenty of room for visitors. You can stay in main house or summertime there is possible to sleep in another building which is traditional place to sleep in summertime. We need help with different things, household, picking berries, taking weeds off from bushes etc. You can also take part to traditional Finnish actitivities. We are also helping our neighbors and there is different small jobs to do. Wintertime is more silent, but we will figure out something, so you will not get bored! We have some chicken, turkeys and couple of peacocks. Plus two cats and dog.
Currently I am living with my adult daughter and her two dogs. She is in charge when I am away. 

I am allergic to smells and I appreciate if you do not use fragrances. Perfumes and after shaves are worst and I can not stay inside the house when someone using those. Same with smelling hair dressing products. So using those kind of products inside the house is strictly forbidden. 

And please take care of not use perfumed washing powder or softener before coming. Sometimes woofers clothes must be washed 2-3 times before could stay inside house. Same with perfumes, after shaves and other fragrances.
It us better to not use them two weeks before arriving farm. This thing with clothes  is especially ones who are coming from France.
And take care your deodorant is without smell!
I hope you take this seriously, because I do not want to turn anyone back from front door.  Or put out to tent during stay.

Accomodation is in shared rooms, up to three people in the same room. For couples we are trying to arrange own room.  We have usually many woofers at the same time and if you need silence, you can go out to nature. Which is close. 

We have sauna almost every day and we are swimming in the lake year around. 
Wintertime we have hole in the ice, which
_wwoof_travel: From Helsinki to Kuopio by train or cheap bus. Pickup from Kuopio. From Kuopio airport ( 20 kms)  pickup.

name: Travis - Gooseberry Point Intentional Community
description:
I have two properties available where I'm needing help growing crops and maintaining the farms. There are approximately 15 minutes away from each other, and ideally someone could shift between both locations and stay on either one. one property is off grid, the other one is on grid and closer to town in Ferndale Washington. The off-grid property will require more prep than planting, some areas need to be cleared, blackberry control is a big factor and I need help removing blackberries, and growing and planting perennial berries. you would need your own accommodations, such as a tent. Both properties have outhouse and hot showers/bath. I also have a mobile sauna on site part of the time. I'm also looking for people to help build a treehouse if interested. Lots of opportunities for the right people.

2026 update: I now possibly have lodging available, a simple camper.

Certifications: We don't use chemicals but are not certified organic

Current projects: Natural building, planting perennials, mainly berries, reclaiming farmland from blackberries, compost etc.

name: Vida - Cottage,  garden and  heritage variety orchard
description:
.... Come and share the 'Good Life'! Due to last minute date changes we have availability for 1 WWOOFer from  Monday 20 July to Monday 27 July

WWOOFers  as guests are welcome to come and share  'the good life' with the hosts and experience the tasks associated with this lifestyle and sustainable living.  More recently we  are trying to make changes in the way we live to take into account the current climate change /cost of living crisis and hope you can join us in this.

I prefer WWOOFers to be flexible and adapt to my lifestyle rather than have a timetabled regime. Some days are long- some days are short - depends what is going on. People call in for coffee and we all enjoy their company.  Friends invite us for coffee. We attend community events. On a sunny day it is lovely to walk to the river. We may visit local towns or explore the local countryside. I prefer last minute WWOOFers  I often have to travel away.

Our place is a lovely property located in beautiful countryside 10 miles from Perth. The small holding has been managed  in a sustainable way for 50 years with an attempt to be as organic and self sufficient as possible and create a variety of habitats.

The 4 acre field is leased for sheep or cows. Part of it has an orchard growing heritage apple, plum and pear trees. The .75 acre garden has an organic veg patch, flower gardens, a large lawn, wildlife pond and a wooded area managed for firewood. Our hens  and ducks produce fresh eggs.

I enjoy cooking for WWOOFers. Ingredients include eggs from the hens and ducks, veg and salad in season from the garden and meat including local game.   I can't really cater for strict vegetarians  as I cook for omnivores  and use meat stock for lunchtime soups. I would hope that visiting WWOOFers would be prepared to be adaptable to what is available at the time.    No fussy eaters or smokers please.

Jobs may include
Feeding hens and ducks.
Hedgerow management.
Weeding and general estate management.
Cultivating , plantin
_wwoof_travel: Train to Perth station or bus to Perth Broxden bus stop or St Leonard's St bus station and I will collect you. PH1 4QJ sat nav.

name: Stefano - Pequeña finca alimentada por fotovoltaico en desarollo con el objectivo de autosuficiencia energetic
description:
Se trata de una finca de 4000mt2,con dos casas, el todo alimentado por energia solar fotovoltaica.  Estamos organizandonos para montar en ella una asociacion cultural por la promocion de las energias alternativas en la isla ,con un especial ojo al cultivo  biologico,autosuficiencia  y sustenibilidad.  Necesitamos compartir concimientos en temas de  horticultura biologica,permacultura,carpinteria y posiblemente pero sin ser impresindible experiencia en fotovoltaico.  Tambien se aprecia compartir experiencia en jardineria, carpinteria ,fontaneria y electricidad en general, visto,entre otros, el projecto de crear un sistema fotovoltaico para sacar el agua de un pozo ya existente y quizas encontrar maneras alternativas de desalinizar el agua del mismo que resulta ser algo salada.

It is a 4000mt2 estate with two houses, all powered by photovoltaic solar energy. We are organizing ourselves to create in  it a cultural association for the promotion of alternative energy sources on the island, with a special eye to organic farming, self-sufficiency and susteinability. We need to share knowledge on issues of organic gardening, permaculture, carpentry and possibly without being impresindible, experience in photovoltaic. Also welcom sharing experience in gardening, carpentry, plumbing and electricity generally seen, among others, the project to create a photovoltaic system to draw water from an existing well and maybe find alternative ways to desalinate water thereof is be somewhat salty.

name: Alice - 55-acre lavender farm 💜 near Chattanooga
description:
On our 55 acre Lookout Mountain farm, we specialize in growing lavender using responsible and sustainable practices. During the season of bloom, we open for events such as uPicks for both the flowers and later in July, our blueberries.​

In addition to the farm experience, our clients enjoy a limited selection of hand made lavender based products. We pledge to use only 100% organic essential oil, never synthetic fragrances, and our own farm grown lavender to produce.

We are roughly 20 miles south of Chattanooga, nearby the beautiful Cloudland Canyon State park, bouldering sites and a hang-gliding flight park.  We prefer WWOOFer keep their stay to 2-3 weeks or less during the months of April (later in month) through July.  We need help planting, pruning, weeding and harvesting, as well as help with our agri-tourism visits.  We have a vintage airstream which will accomodate an individual or couple traveling together.  We can accommodate special diets, but not pets or children.  We speak English only.  

Organic methods: We use very little, if any, fertilizers, pesticides (none) or fungicides and if so, they are OMRI certified.  Lavender enjoys poor soil, insects don't damage it and it requires little water.  Our blueberries were sprayed once 3 years ago with an organic fungicide to fight mummyberry.

Certifications: We are certified by the Certified Naturally Grown organization (CNG).

Current projects: We need help planting lavender in the spring and pruning, weeding, mowing and harvesting in the summer.  We also have events during our bloom season where we will need help with our guests and minding the farm store where our products are sold.  We currently don't do many farmer's markets because we are short staffed, but perhaps that could be added with the additional help.  

Internet access available

name: Gerlando - Siamo  Dino ed Helene, abbiamo una proprietà nella piana dei cugni ,vicino a cavagrande,Siracusa
description:
Abbiamo delle galline in libertà non accettiamo più i cani.. Dal 20 maggio al fine giugno cerchiamo aiuto  anche per l orto estivo. 
.Non siamo una azienda dunque l 'aiuto che chiediamo è per le attività varie da fare nella giornata! Cucinare, pulire interno esterno, giardinaggio e lavori fuori e dentro... 
Siamo una coppia sessantenne ,italo-francese. Dino è  restauratore al museo archeologico  Paolo Orsi  di Siracusa, ma anche realizzatore di sculture e scenografie per il teatro .Originario di un paese Madonita (centro sicilia) ,si è lanciato nel recupero di un terreno nella riserva naturale di Cavagrande del Cassibile. il progetto è quello di conservare questo angolo intatto  di paesaggio Ibleo, reimpiantando piante della macchia mediterranea. Trovare forme di sinergie tra  il luogo ed alcuni interventi  reversibili di "Land Art" con materiali naturali presenti nel luogo. 
Helene, pittrice sulla seta ,partecipa alla creazione di una vita collettiva , con attività condivise sul territorio  ..(lotta antincendio, passeggiate , ecc. 
Abbiamo un gatto.
, LE ATTIVITÀ  svolte Assieme  sono le seguenti:
Cura e raccolta delle carrube,
cura di un orto 
cura di un frutteto
lavoro di pulizia del terreno e tagliafuoco
ripristino di muretti a secco.
Lavori di rifinitura del casale {pergola, terrazza.. Ecc} 
Abbiamo bisogno di un aiuto generico  nel nostro quotidiano, cioè mantenere l insieme della proprietà funzionante, cioè anche aiutare a cucinare o pulire i spazi comuni. 

Per il mese di luglio e agosto preferiamo ospitare coppie che rimangano almeno 3 o 4 settimane.o singole persone interessate anche a fare da soli i custodi della proprietà . C'è molto caldo e bisogna innaffiare solo le piante .

Siamo flessibili negli orari, Decidiamo insieme .

Ospitiamo i nostri volontari , in una roulotte, o una casetta di legno nel bosco , anche nel casale. 

Partecipiamo al movimento Balfolk  Sicilia , Dino a costruito una bella piazza dove si può ballare.
Balliamo anche in piazza a
_wwoof_travel: La stazione dei  treni si trova a Siracusa, l'aeroporto a Catania

name: Anne Kari - Family farm, Community farm
description:
I want to share my little nice farm with others that want to live their life close to nature, producing their own food, making their  footprints as small as possible.  Surrounded by the most amazing nature with high mountains and the fjord, the location is perfect for hiking in the leisure time and you always have a nice view to rest your eyes on, while working. Building up the farm, there is plenty of work to do. 

At the time I have 15 sheep, old Norwegian breed and two cats. I also have 5 chickens. Two icelandic horses keep house in the stable. I grow vegetables, herbs and berries. I also collect wild herbs for making teas etc. 

I'm experimenting with different ways of cultivating, making different kinds of composts, using bokashi,  mulching, hugelculture; trying to make the soil as fertile and alive as possible.  

Organic methods: A kind of permaculture.

Certifications: DEBIO

Current projects: April- mai: Lambing,  making firewood, fencing, sowing and priming indoors making seedling for the vegetable garden, pruning in the berry garden, harvesting wild herbs.

May-June-July-august: Fencing, herding, making composts, preparing the soil, sowing and planting vegetables outdoor, taking the sheeps to the mountain, cutting grass, making hay. Summer is also the best time to paint  the house and the barn...

July- August-September: Harvesting vegetables, herbs, hay, and berries, painting, making firewood.

October; Making fences, working with the sheep, making firewood..

November: Making firewood, feeding the animals, making teas...

Internet access available
_wwoof_travel: With train to Narvik  and bus to Hamnvik, or with airplane to Harstad/ Narvik airport and via bus to Harstad and ferry to Sørrollnes,  or with plane to Tromsø and westamaran to Engenes + bus.

name: Mark - Established, off-grid, self-sufficient homestead forest community. Central Florida..
activities: Unterkunft: shack, bedroom, trailer, campsite, yurt-tipi
description:
Many are struggling with a challenging economy, a growing sense of loneliness and a housing market that seems impossible to break into. Intentional communal living can offer more opportunity, stability and financial freedom than most realize. 

These small, self-reliant communities have a central focus on reducing their carbon footprint, minimizing resource consumption and instilling food sovereignty by implementing communal subsistence and regenerative farming practices. 

The emphasis is more on ecocentric living* than on material recognition or monetary gain. By returning to an agrarian based lifestyle*, we strive to live within the natural balance of nature, not in opposition to it. 

Homestead Heirloom Gardens and Ecovillage is an off-grid, self-reliant community of individuals who have come together, and dedicated their lives to, simple communal living, the preservation of rare, medicinal and indigenous cultivars, and the reskilling of the lost and forgotten practices of traditional homestead living.

We are seeking those of like mind an vision, to live simply and in community, to leave the smallest carbon footprint as possible, and to not only save, but to restore the diversity and the integrity of our heritage foods and forgotten life skills and practices. 

Homestead Heirloom Gardens now has Wwoofing and work trade residency positions available. Heavily rooted in ecocentric* concepts and philosophies, we are seeking those with a background or profound interest in syntropic permaculture, agroforestry food production, heirloom subsistence farming* and simple communal living. 

Furnished apartments and cabins, all vegetarian* meals, all utilities, all essential items, toiletries, laundry and community closet are all provided. Work trade only. No costs, fees or contracts ever required. Long term/permanent residency possible. Plant based, holistic, alcohol and tobacco free communal living!

Our daily community activities and current projects include:

GARDENING

name: Lucy - Family home and garden - rural, Hobby Farm, Flower Garden Farm, Cattle Ranch, Ranch/ Livestock Farm
description:
Our property is an environmentally-conscious family operation with 40 pairs of beef cattle, 9 horses, 2 honey bee hives and 2 yearling jersey heifers, dogs and seasonal sheep and chickens (summer months).

I manage the cattle and most of the ranching operations, although my two adult daughters both live at the ranch and contribute to ranching projects when they are able to.  As a sixty-something woman I sure appreciate the help and company that WWOOFers have provided over the last 10 years we have been a host (although we took a break during Covid).

Starting in April we take on help to get ready for calving and gardening season! This includes starting more seeds indoors, keeping started plants going, sorting/moving cows and feeding them every day, prepping the calving pens and materials, getting spreadsheets ready etc. Once calving starts there is much work to do checking the herds several times a day, doctoring animals, and bottle feeding calves while keeping up with the other daily chores (feeding dogs, horses, ducks and whatever else we may have at the time)! Towards the middle of May we start planting the gardens.

In June we have to process all the cattle and get the calves tagged and branded. We do this the traditional way by roping the calves from horseback. It's hard work, but lots of fun! Neighbors come to help us, and we also go to theirs to return the favor. There is a lot of weeding and planting still happening in the garden at this time, and we also start going out to repair the fences and maybe build new ones in preparation for turning out the cattle in the middle to end of June

September is all about processing the garden harvest and gathering firewood. This is also when we will extract the honey from our bee hives. Fall can often be the nicest time of the year, but it is also not uncommon to see some snow throughout the month. You will want all the different kinds of clothes! 

This lifestyle requires a healthy sense of humour because livestock and
_wwoof_travel: For Wwoofers traveling by plane and bus, the closest bus station is Fort MacLeod. We are happy to pick you up there. For those of you with vehicles, email us and we will give you directions to our place.

name: Solene - Agritourism Destination, Eco Lodge/Wilderness Adventures, Eco Retreat, Market Garden
description:
🌱 Quiénes somos

Somos una pequeña finca ecológica de 25 hectáreas, ubicada en la naturaleza de Nicaragua, rodeada de colinas, selva y cerca del océano. La finca se cultiva sin pesticidas ni químicos, con plantaciones de moringa, cúrcuma, jengibre, pitaya, mangos, limones y otras frutas tropicales.

La finca está a cargo de un pequeño equipo local, apasionado por la agricultura natural y la permacultura, con el deseo de compartir este estilo de vida y conocimientos con voluntarios de todo el mundo.

⸻

🏡 Alojamiento y comida
	•	Los voluntarios se alojarán en una casa compartida (2 camas en la misma habitación, baño y cocina compartidos).
	•	La comida está incluida, pero cada voluntario prepara sus propios platos en la cocina de la casa.
	•	La finca es sencilla y no tiene acceso directo a internet (posible después de caminar 20–25 minutos hasta la cima o salir del pueblo en moto ~15 minutos).
	•	El ambiente es familiar, con espíritu de simplicidad y colaboración.

⸻

⏰ Horario de trabajo
	•	El trabajo se realiza solo por la mañana, de 6 am a 12 pm (6 horas por día).
	•	Las tardes son libres para descansar, explorar o participar en actividades locales.
	•	Estancia mínima: 3 semanas, estancia máxima: 3 meses.

⸻

🌿 Actividades

Según la temporada y las necesidades de la finca, los voluntarios pueden participar en:
	•	Mantenimiento de cultivos (moringa, jengibre, cúrcuma, pitaya, árboles frutales).
	•	Preparación de suelos y siembra durante la temporada de lluvias.
	•	Cosecha y transformación artesanal (polvo de cúrcuma, infusiones, frutas secas).
	•	Cuidado de animales y apoyo en la gestión de la finca.
	•	Intercambio de conocimientos sobre permacultura y vida sencilla.

⸻

🎒 Lo que hay que saber
	•	Es necesario hablar español para comunicarse con el equipo local.
	•	Se buscan voluntarios autónomos, motivados y con buena condición física para el trabajo al aire libre.
	•	El transporte no está incluido, pero es posible alquilar una moto todo terreno con contactos locale

name: Juan Angel - Espacio de crecimiento  auto suficiente , con practica de agricultura ecológica , bioconstrucción
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals)
El espacio Luna Nueva , es un espacio auto suficiente  enigmáticamente  , con algunos animales : gatos , un perro , 2 patos y  pájaros salvajes . Este proyecto quiere ofrecer un lugar sano donde las personas puedan aprender agricultura ecológica y bioconstrucción , tambien quiero que sea un espacio de crecimiento espiritual y de intercambio cultural .

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
Los voluntarios tendrían que ayudar en las tareas de construcción y transformación , creando espacios nuevos , trabajaremos la madera, la piedra , y aprenderás diferentes métodos de agricultura ..

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals:
Hay diferentes opciones para alojamiento , ahora mismo trabajo en crear nuevos espacios para voluntarios, ahora mismo la opcion seria en tienda   en una casa cueva .
La comida , aveces comeremos juntos pero los voluntarios tendrán su propio espacio para hacerlo
