4. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?
La contribución del voluntario seria un mínimo de 5 horas al día , teniendo dos dias a la semana libre 

5. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.):
Me he formado como guia y trabajado  en diferentes ocupaciones , , soy instructor de yoga ,  me  gusta los deportes de montana , practico escalada y barranquismo .

name: Marc - Casa rural sostenible y autosuficiente
description:
Somos Nuria y Marc y tenemos un alojamiento de Ecoturismo, que hemos reconstruido nosotros con mucho esfuerzo e ilusión en un entorno aislado, en plena naturaleza, en el  Montsec de Rubies, Prepirineo de Lleida, Catalunya.
Vivimos en una casa sostenible y autosuficiente, con placas fotovoltaicas para  la electricidad que consumimos, caldera de leña eficiente de doble combustión para el agua caliente y la calefacción.
Cultivamos un huerto ecológico,  cuidamos de nuestras gallinas, ovejas, y burras. Aprovechamos  todo lo que nos regala la naturaleza: hierbas, setas, etc.

Ayuda habitual de los voluntarios:

Trabajos en el exterior
•	Mantenimiento del huerto
o	Sembrar, plantar
o	Quitar hierbas
o	Recoger frutos
•	Dar de comer a animales y limpieza de corral. (gallinas, ovejas)
•	Recogida y tala de leña para calefacción.

Trabajos en la casa
•	Ayuda en la preparación y conservas i frutos del huerto....
•	Ayuda en cocina para poner lavaplatos, limpiar y ordenar materiales cocina. 
•	Ayuda en la limpieza de habitaciones cuando se necesite. 

En el pasado hemos hecho, con la ayuda de voluntarios entusiastas (gracias a tod@s :) un espacio exterior de madera para actividades de yoga y meditación, un invernadero,  un gallinero móvil,  instalar una pequeña piscina, una leñera, un pequeño bosque comestible, ,  un nuevo corral para que convivan nuestras ovejas y las burras que hemos adoptado. Una sala  polivalente de madera para realizar actividades  como  yoga, talleres, etc. i  una tiny House  en un  contenedor marítimo.

Ahora estamos en un proyecto de adecuacion de la finca con los animales, arboles frutales i arreglando cercas ademas de preparar el huerto para la plantacion de verano.
 
Hacemos un trabajo variado para que no sea monótono y la vez se puedan aprender diferentes actividades, siempre en función de la época del año y de vuestro perfil.

Alojamiento y comidas
•	Te alojarás en una tiny house  habilitada con cocina,  baño propio, agua caliente, estufa de leña y vent

name: Christian - Subtropical Fruit Garden above the Atlantic
description:
O Jardim do Araçá is a small farm with a large orchard on the Azores island of Pico. I have been living here with my cat Gilberta for four years, transforming a former cow pasture and cornfield into a regional fruit and vegetable garden, bordered by a small vineyard made of volcanic stones, which is very typical on Pico, and framed by endemic trees. Gardening here is a big challenge with the forces of the wind, as there can occur strong storms, especially in winter, which affect the plants. I try to listen carefully to the local farmers and their experiences to understand how to sow, plant, care for and harvest under these conditions. One focus of the garden is on the fruit trees that are slowly beginning to establish themselves: guavas, figs, mangoes, papayas, citrus fruits, pitangas, tamarillos, macadamias, supplemented by bananas, pineapples, passion fruit and grapes.
The principles of ecological gardening are applied: mulching (palm tree leaves, cana, bambus, etc.), ecological fertilisation (horse dung, algae) and maintenance work. In Jardim do Araçá, attempts are being made to establish traditional multi-layered windbreak systems with the succession of endemic trees in order to generate greater adaptability and knowledge in conjunction with the garden. The aim is to learn by observing the plants and to respond sensitively to this cultural mix here in the Azores.

Understanding how to promote and take into account the old endemic plant communities in the context of approximately 90 per cent immigrant plants here in the middle of the Atlantic, while at the same time not condemning existing agriculture, which still relies mainly on conventional farming, is a central undertaking and is what we can achieve together here. For it is important to preserve and enrich the convivial knowledge that is being formed in order to comprehend and understand the immense areas of friction on these islands between local, invasive plant and tourist interests. That is why Jardim do A

name: Yüksel - Family farm
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Yaklaşık 110 dönüm araziye araziye sahiptir. 10ar dönüme ekilmiş olan elma, kiraz ve ceviz ağaçlarının yanı sıra 65 dönüm üzerinde buğday, arpa ve yem bitkisi yetiştirilmektedir. Ürünler sertifikalı organik ürünler olup, genel olarak pazarlarda ve marketlerde satılmaktadır. Tarımın yanı sıra aile arıcılık ve hayvancılık da yapmaktadır. 2 katlı bir evde yaşamakta olan aile gönüllülere kendi evlerinin kapısını açmaktadır. Aynı sofranın paylaşılacağı ailede tüm işler imece usulü yapılır. Ürün hasadından sonra aile topluca kışlıklarını hazırlar. Erişteler kesilir, pekmez ve reçeller hazırlanır. Şebinkarahisar sınırları içinde yer alan çiftlik Tamzara Koruma Alanı, Aluçra Çayı ve Sayderesi gibi birçok doğal güzelliğe yakınlığı ile dikkat çekmektedir.

Mart: Ekim, bahçe hazırlığı, fide hazırlığı, ağaç dikimi
Nisan: Arıcılık, ekim, fidan dikimi, aşılama
Mayıs: Aşı, sebze dikimi, Hayvan bakımı, çobanlık, Arıcılık
Haziran: Arıclık, sebze toplama, çapalama, sulama, ot biçme, dut silkeleme, arpa,buğday hasadı
Temmuz: Arıcılık, sebze hasadı, sulama, kışlık sebze dikimi
Ağustos: Bal hasadı, hasat
Eylül: Arıcılık, hasat
Ekim: arıları kışa hazırlama ve bakımı

---

The farm is on 110 hectares of land. They plant 10 hectares each of cherries, apples and walnut trees, and wheat, barley and feed for animals on the rest of the farm. All the products are certificated and farmed organically and sold in markets and supermarkets. Alongside farming the family also keeps bees and livestock. The family has a house with two floors and accomodate their guest there. Meals are eaten with the family and everyting is done collectively. After they finish harvesting they start preparing for winter-pasta, molasses and jam making. The farm is in Sebinkarahisar and near to the protected site of Tamzara, Alucra Creek and Saydere natural beauty spots.

March: Seeding, garden preparation, seedling preparation, planting seedlings
April: Beekeeping, seeding, planti

name: Elias - Organic Farm and Campsite
description:
MARHABA! Greetings!
We're a close-knit group of Lebanese friends managing an authentic rural camping site nestled beside the river in Ain-Dara village. More than just a campsite, we're on a mission to reconnect people with their roots while preserving traditional ways of living. Our focus is on self-sustainability through organic vegetable cultivation, mushroom farming, and the application of permaculture principles.
But that's not all – we're also deeply committed to nature building, which involves constructing eco-friendly structures using locally-sourced materials like earthbags and wood.

if you're passionate about sustainability, eager to learn new skills, and ready to make a positive impact on the world, we'd love to have you join us at our campsite. Come be a part of our journey towards a greener, more sustainable future. Together, we can make a difference!

Organic methods: Hot compost, vermicomposting,  mulching, Active Charcoal, Natural spraying, companion plants .

Certifications: 1) PDC (Permaculture Design Course). 2) Earth Bag Dome Certification Course. 3) Sustainable Agriculture Farming.

Current projects: We are now preparing our garden, Mushroom farm and  setting the campsite, along with Nature Building Projects.

name: Jaclyn - Regenerative Enterprise Incubator and Urban Permaculture Farm
description:
There’s a lot going on in this little urban plot. It’s hard to know where to start, so I guess I’ll start with my background. I have a Master’s Degree from Gaia University in Regenerative Enterprise Design and a Permaculture Design Certificate from Boulder Permaculture in Colorado. I’m also really into Korean Natural Farming and High Bionutrient Farming. I previously operated a certified organic mushroom farm and was on the board of the Oklahoma Food Cooperative. I’ve worked at The Integral Center in Boulder Colorado, managed a housing cooperative, organized many community gardens, and taught regenerative gardening to middle school kids. I’ve been doing business consulting for non-profits like the Living System’s Institute in Denver, Colorado and entrepreneurs starting small businesses.

About a year and a half ago I purchased this large 4 bedroom house with a 33 foot by 10 foot grow building that was previously used to grow cannabis. I’ve transformed the grow building into a mushroom farm. It also has space to grow either plant starts or microgreens. I transformed the front yard into a permaculture food forest as a demonstration site and marketing to get regenerative landscaping clients. In the back yard I have laying hens, raised beds, rainwater catchment, and a biochar burner/rocket stove that I designed.

I’m also working with a team to start a worker-owned food cooperative. We’re doing weekly pop-up markets and just launched our online ordering system for local customers. You can check it out at https://redbudfood.coop.

I plan to use my house as a meeting and educational space. I set up my living room as a classroom with an 8 foot white board and seating for up to 20 people. I also have a dedicated meditation/workout room that can host a small class.

So, basically there are a lot of possibilities for what could be done here, and I’m more of a consultant and a teacher. I’m looking for someone who is bringing their own ideas and wants to experiment. I can sell
_wwoof_travel: Will Rogers International Airport. I can pick up if I'm not busy. Busses run from airport to near my house.

name: Bhawani - Our farm has been in operation for over 15 years.
description:
Situated at an altitude of 1,650 meters, our farm experiences an average temperature of 20–30°C. Currently, I serve as the Chairman of the Organic Coffee Cooperative in Parbat and the Vice Chairman of the Central Coffee Association in Nepal. I’ve received valuable support from the District Coffee Entrepreneurs Association. Our farm has been in operation for over 15 years. the National Tea and Coffee Development Board, and the District Development Committee of Parbat.

At our farm, we specialise in cultivating Arabica coffee, particularly the Yellow Caturra variety, which is considered superior to robusta. Since coffee is a shade-loving crop, we’ve also incorporated sweet orange cultivation on the farm, and more recently, we’ve begun planting jackfruit trees, which have shown promising results. Though I am not formally trained in agriculture, I’ve gained practical knowledge through experience and have participated in numerous coffee-related workshops and trainings.

Our nursery is one of the largest suppliers of coffee seedlings in Nepal, and we manage a total farm area of around 68 hectares, spread across different locations. The nursery itself operates on 1 ropani of land, and we have a dedicated team of 15 staff members. Internationally, our coffee has reached markets in Korea, Germany, the USA, and Australia. Domestically, it has been praised by many renowned figures. While we are close to meeting all the requirements for organic certification, we are not yet officially certified. For organic manure and fertilisers, we partner with Lumbini Agro Fertiliser and also use manure from our own goat farm.

In addition to coffee, I also rear goats using pasture management techniques inspired by Thailand, where goats are kept within fenced pasture areas. I also have a passion for writing and have authored several books on agricultural techniques tailored to the needs of general farmers in Nepal, written in simple, easy-to-understand language.

For volunteers interested in

name: Phanat - Socially & environmentally conscious urban garden operation in New Iberia, LA hosted by
description:
Welcome to the IMG, a socially and environmentally conscious privately owned urban market garden operation nestled in the city of New Iberia, Louisiana. we offer a unique blend of agriculture, horticulture, tourism, and community activism. Since our establishment in November 2014, our mission has been to provide fresh, locally grown produce to the Acadiana region through CSA programs, farmers markets, farm-to-table restaurants, and volunteer exchange.

Situated on a charming half-acre property in a low-income neighborhood, our small-scale farm encompasses two high tunnels housing a vertical hydroponic container system, numerous raised beds and ground rows, a composting heap, and beautifully landscaped ornamental flower gardens surrounding the house. We have also been slowly making our old brick garage into an off-grid granny-flat. Volunteers can expect to immerse themselves in various activities such as planting, weeding, and maintaining these areas, while getting hands-on experience in sustainable farming practices and small agri-tourism operations. 

During your stay, you will be accommodated in our guest suite, which also doubles as an Airbnb suite when not used by WWOOFers, providing a cozy and comfortable living space. As a host, I live in the main house with my boyfriend Nicholas, who works as a transportation planner. Together, we will share a laundry room and kitchen with our guests. We have no dietary restrictions. Jen, our groundskeeper, also stays in a camper on site with her little dog, Lithium. We may be open to other pets staying but do not accept Pitbull or mixed pitbull breeds due to a dangerous and costly incident we've had in the past. Safety is a number one priority. 

To ensure your well-being, basic staples like rice, flour, canned goods, seasonings, and garden vegetables are provided, and it will be the responsibility of the WWOOFers to prepare their meals. In addition to the engaging garden activities, we have an exciting project in progress—r
_wwoof_travel: There is an Amtrak(1-mile) and Greyhound (2miles) bus stop in town  that stops a few times a week. The Lafayette Regional Airport is the closest airpot (30minute drive) with commercial flights. New Orleans International Airport is a 2 hour drive from the

name: Harichandra - Farm in nature
description:
Namaste, and welcome!

We're a small, family run farm in Dharmasthali, on the outskirts of Kathmandu, surrounded by mountain views, fresh air, and friendly neighbours. We built this place from scratch, and it's grown into a peaceful spot where we grow our own food and live simply, alongside our two friendly dogs.
We love hosting travellers and volunteers from all backgrounds, especially those drawn to farming, cultural exchange, or teaching English. Days here are shaped by real farm work, watering, harvesting, weeding, planting, building beds, and seeding, along with homemade Nepali meals, quiet village walks, and mornings that start with birdsong.

This is a simple homestay, not a luxury one. Guests stay in a shared room with a shared bathroom, and while we keep things basic, we do have reliable mobile data for internet access. What we offer instead is a safe, welcoming family environment and an honest taste of everyday village life.
I can be a little shy at first, but I'm friendly, easygoing, and always glad to help. In my own free time I enjoy cooking, hiking, reading, and travelling, and I love exchanging stories, languages, and ideas with open-minded guests.

If you're looking for a genuine local experience rather than just a place to sleep, whether for a few days or longer, we'd be delighted to host you. Welcome to our farm!

name: Santiago - Emprendimiento agroecologico con olivos y cultivos de hoja verde.
description:
Mi nombre es Santiago, tengo 40 años (aunque cueste admitirlo).
Soy esposo de Ximena y papá de Josefina y Mateo. Hace algunos años dejamos la ciudad para comenzar un nuevo camino en el campo. Compramos un terreno, plantamos más de 4.000 olivos y empezamos a producir cultivos de hoja verde con prácticas agroecológicas certificadas.

Elaboramos aceite de oliva virgen extra y vendemos nuestros productos frescos a hoteles, restaurantes y servicios de catering. Trabajamos principalmente en el canal B2B.

Nuestro proyecto es estacional. Durante el invierno, las tareas son más tranquilas: poda de olivos, preparación de tierras y planificación.
Entre octubre y marzo, todo se acelera: plantamos, cosechamos, embalamos pedidos y atendemos el día a día con mucha energía. En esos meses, la ayuda de voluntarios es muy importante.

Este año queremos ampliar la plantación de tomate antiguo, un cultivo hermoso e intenso, ideal para quienes disfrutan del trabajo con la tierra y quieren aprender haciendo.

De lunes a viernes almorzamos juntos. Las cenas y desayunos también están a nuestro cargo, con alimentos de producción propia y de colegas de la zona: buena comida, leche fresca, ingredientes locales. Nuestro espacio es apto para personas veganas.

Los fines de semana siempre hay actividades para compartir: desde caminatas hasta asados, salidas o simplemente descansar.

Alojamiento: casa grande y cómoda, para que te sientas bienvenido/a y descanses como en casa.
La dinámica de la quinta es tranquila y en ciertos períodos podrán quedarse solos durante la noche en el predio. Buscamos personas cómodas con la autonomía y el ritmo simple del campo, algo que muchos wwoofers valoran especialmente.

Nos gusta subir cerros en familia, cantar, bailar, caminar, correr y escuchar a los pájaros.
Vivimos en una zona rural muy tranquila, rodeada de lindos paisajes y mucha PAZ.

Y cuando el calor aprieta, tenemos la playa a solo 40 minutos.

*********************************************************
_wwoof_travel: Aeropuerto de Carrasco a 30 mins de la farm. Omnibus pasan muy cerca del predio

name: Dannielle - Community-centered education with emphasis on respectful relationships with the land and her beings.
description:
This is a very unique multi-species farm operation on five acres in the middle of a growing county. The farm invented itself in a very organic way. We have been lucky enough to live here as the land stewards, while it continues to unfold and flourish. Recently we added a non-profit branch of our farm that focuses solely on education. The private part of the farm supplies the facility and equipment necessary to offer the educational experiences. There is a raw milk dairy with mini jersey cows where one can learn the ins and outs of raw milk processing along with important bio security practices for distribution. Colorado is a state where raw milk is illegal to consume for anyone who does not own a cow or cow share.  Nigerian dwarf goats have their own small-yard landscaping business in the summer, traveling to backyards to teach about "green-scaping". There are over 100 chickens that supply eggs and meat to the community, offering the opportunity to learn chicken stewardship from chick to adult as well as egg and meat processing in the context of respect and reciprocity. There are alpacas that offer fiber, so camelid healthcare is part of our regular work along with learning to shear and process the fiber into clothing. Geese and ducks offer eggs while bees teach us community, teamwork and offer us the wonderful honey to process.  Of course, there are dogs and a farm cat.  We also host native animals like the prairie dogs, coyotes and raptors. We view all of our more-than-human family as our teachers and mentors. Our goal is to live respectfully in the space as stewards. We invite others to also find their place and offer a positive impact to the world. We are all learning together and teaching each other. 
My families are Indigenous from both North and South America. My ancestors were the original stewards of the land that we now care for. My husband is a computer engineer who works both remotely and travels for work. My son is at university nearby and comes home on

name: Hüseyin - Family farm
description:
SCROLL DOWN FOR ENGLISH

Ev sahibi çift, memleketleri olan köyde bir köy evini restore ettirerek atölye çalışmaları ve konaklama hizmetine sunmuş. Mevsimlik bahçe bitkilerinin yanı sıra meyve ağaçları da bulunuyor. Hüseyin Bey ekolojik tarım bilincini, özellikle de Fukuoka'nın doğal tarım felsefesini benimseyip geleneksel bilgiyle örtüştürmüş olup eşiyle yetiştirdikleri ürünleri gerek kendileri tüketmekte gerekse konuklarının tüketimine sunmakta, kalanı da satmaktalar. Keziban Hanım'ın leziz yemeklerinde kullandığı malzemelerin tamamına yakını kendi ürünleri. Genç ailesi, ekolojik yöntemlerle konserve, sirke yapımı, kışlık gıda hazırlık eğitimleri de veriyor. Yakın mesafede bulunan "tuzlanın damı" adlı tuz ocağından doğal tuz elde edilmektedir. Yer yatağı ve sedir bulunan misafir odası, tuvalet ve banyo evin içindedir. İçki tüketiminin sofrayla sınırlı tutulması rica ediliyor, sigaraya ise yangın riski nedeniyle sadece dışarıda müsaade ediliyor.

Çiftlikte zaman zaman gerçek gıda konusunda atölye çalışmaları oluyor, özellikle Ankara'da yaşayan ailelerin ilgisini çekiyor.

HAZİRAN: Bahçe işleri, bağ belleme, ekim, çapalama, ot yolma EYLÜL: Bahçe işleri, meyve hasadı, ekim, çapalama, ot yolma

---

The host couple had restored an old village house in their home town. Agriculture is a common activity around the area where the farm is located and there are plenty of fruit trees as well as seasonal garden plants. Mr. Hüseyin Genç adopts Fukuoka's natural farming principles and traditional methods. Mr. and Mrs. Genç supply their needs from their own crops and also present them to their guests, and the rest is sold through a community supported agriculture project. Mrs. Keziban Genç's delicious meals are cooked using almost only their own produce. Workshops on food preservation and vinegar making are offered. Natural salt is obtained from the saltpan near the farm. Traditionally decorated guest room, toilet and bathroom are in the house. Please keep your alcohol

name: Celeste - Tangerine grove, an anthurium forest, avocados, botanical garden, and wild tropical jungle
description:
Our family property has an established 40 plus year old tangerine grove, an anthurium forest, a few avocado trees here and there, botanical gardens, and lots of wild, tropical jungle.  The lot is 35 acres. Our focus is on the nine frontmost acres. 

We are about three miles from Pahoa Village. Time seems to hold still there, somewhere in the late 60's.  A nice long walk or a short drive into town will put you in the center of a quaint community, complete with locally owned grocers, cafés, cute boutiques, restaurants from casual to fancy, and of course a few beloved watering holes to wet your whistle if you feel so inclined. Town is also where you will find internet, running water, and the coin laundromat. There is a community pool to enjoy as well with a very convenient shower house.  Though our property is on rain catchment, we have always found it helpful to bring drinking water back home from one of the coin water refill stations. Just fill up some empty gallon jugs and bring them back with you. There is also free city water fill stations you are welcome to utilize around the area as well. 

We, my sisters and I,  recently started caring for this property.  It has been deeply loved these past forty plus years by our Hanai (adopted) mother. Her gardens are something to behold, with rare species of rhododendron, orchids, anthurium, hibiscus, and many more spectacular varieties of flora to admire. There's even a giant banyan tree toward the back of the acreage. The night sky is breathtaking. You can see the milky way when the sky is clear of clouds. You can often gather a snack as you explore while walking the land. With all the tropical splendor, however, her home is something to be demolished. It was one of her last wishes to knock her house to the ground. At the moment, it's an open air structure where wild cats, chickens, and random forest animals reside. Nature has been reclaiming it for years. Even as our Hanai Mother was still residing within. 

There is so m

name: Lorna and Bill - Farm
description:
We are looking for wwoofers for this summer and fall to come join us on our working sheep farm, helping us taking care of our ewes and their lambs as they graze our 80 acre farm along with their guardian dogs.  In May we lambed our ewes and you can help with the babies, feeding and giving hugs. With summer comes working in the garden , weeding and thinning and maintenance of the yard. Bill plans on building more fences so he needs a wwoofer that has experience using a hammer, can drive a tractor and helping hauling hay. 

We have moved to a new farm in Saskatchewan and need help with building fences, improving buildings, replacing roofing material, cutting firewood and painting. Bill had a heart attack and surgery 5 years ago, so we need some strong willing helpers. 

Here you can learn about working and feeding our sheep, lambs, chickens, and our guardian dogs. Learn about using machinery, driving tractors, moving hay, carpentry work, fencing, cutting firewood and other farm chores, like feeding dogs, baby chicks, chickens and gathering eggs, each day is a new experience. 

We can pick you up at the Saskatoon airport or bus or train station. If you have your own car, we will give you the directions to the farm.

Come experience our farm, with a stay of around 3 weeks. Enjoy farm grown home cooked meals, There is painting, staining, and carpentry inside jobs and helping in the house on rainy days, working around 5 hours a day, 6 days a week.  

You can gain experience driving and working on farm machinery. We will provide coveralls, farm boots, gloves and bedding. We can arrange transport to local events, museums, malls, or places you would like to visit.  Saskatchewan has lots of interesting place to see and visit.

You can stay in bedrooms in our house and share three Homemade meals each day, the use of facilities and internet with the family. The Nutritious homemade meals include home grown chicken, lamb, and pork, and homemade desserts. I do not prepare vegetari

name: Graham - Retreat Space  and Gardens - Rural agritourism destination, Homestead, Orchard, Mixed Farm
description:
Experience  Ravenstar  (formerly known as Harvest Moon, where we welcomed guests and travellers from around the world since 1997!). 
Now we invite you to be a part of  our new vision! Ravenstar,  an exciting and immersive experiential tour centre and Garden cafe.  We are building upon the legacy of Harvest Moon Organic Bakery and Gardens and making a space to go deeper into the world of art, celebration, cuisine,  nature, spirit, and human connection.   We are transforming our 25 acre (10 ha) organic farm property into a magical project in the central Bruce Peninsula.  RavenStar is home to extensive vegetable and herb gardens,  perennial flower beds, sculpture lined nature trails, a  small maple sugar bush, wetlands, a tree-house,  a geodesic event dome, and a passive solar home, and more.   

Here, our guests will  Pause, Explore and  and Explore this ancient and sacred landscape. We want visitors to connect in an authentic way to themselves, each other, the broader community and to a sense of purpose greater than ourselves. Discover yourself immersed in magical spaces, celebrating connection and breathe in the cycles of the natural world.  RavenStar special events include music performances, markets, full moon circles, night time activities,   celebrations and more. 

WWOOF work here is varied, creative, dynamic and fun which is why many of our WWOOFers often return or even move to the neighborhood. Tasks here include all aspects of gardening from soil preparation and seeding to weeding,  pruning, and harvesting.  Occasional or seasonal activities include brewing, preserving, cider making and drying and more.   New nature trails are being established here creating opportunities for art and sculpture creation, grounds keeping, landscaping new gardens, medicinal herb harvesting and some wildcrafting. 

The Bruce Peninsula is home to two national parks, the spectacular Bruce Trail, Provincial nature reserves, rare flora and fauna, a bird observatory, a dark night sky

name: Sangkook - <GN126> 남쪽 바다 농장 /The southern sea
description:
✅호스트 소개 및 자유로운 농장소개 :
2015년에 서울에서 통영으로 이사를 와서 2017년부터 농사를 지었습니다.  저희는 직접 생산한 소규모 농산물을 게스트와 카페 손님들에게 제공하여 로컬 농산물의 부가가치를 높이는 1,3차 산업의 융합을 실천하고 있습니다
 커피업을 함께 하며 농장에서 기른 신선한 농산물로 여러가지 메뉴를 만들고 있습니다. 건강하고 맛있고 환경적으로 올바른 음식을 만드는 것이 목표입니다. 
  주 작목은 산딸기와 방풍이며 게스트하우스 조식과 까페 메뉴에 활용되고 있습니다. 저희가 먹지 않는 재료는 일체 손님들에게 판매하지 않는 자가제조 원칙을 고수하고 있습니다 서울에서 직장생활을 그만두고 통영으로 귀촌하여 <상국씨가 풍화리로 간 까닭은>으로 KBS 인간극장에 소개되기도 하였습니다. 동아시아의 최남단 바닷가 이곳에서 전 세계 여행자들과 교류하고 소통할 수 있다면 더할 나위 없는 활력소가 될 것입니다.

✅ 농업을 시작한 년도 : 2017년
✅ 농장 형태 : 가족농
✅ 농장 주작물 : 산딸기, 방풍나물

✅ 친환경 농업에 대한 실천 : 생활하면서 나오는 음식물을 컴포스팅 통에 넣어 부숙시켜 퇴비로 활용합니다. 쌀뜨물과 커피우린 물, 발효된 오줌등도 틈틈히 땅으로 되돌리고 있습니다. 농장에서 자라는 풀은 제초제나 멀칭을 써서 제거하지 않습니다. 플라스틱, 비닐 농자재를 사용하지 않습니다. 
✅ 생활에서 친환경 실천 사항 :바다와 물을 오염시킬 수 있는 합성 세제는 사용하지 않습니다. 직접 만든 비누나 생협에서 구입한 물사랑 비누(친환경 세제)만을 써서 설겆이를 합니다. 아이들이 커서 자주는 하지 못하지만 마을 바닷가에 내려가 바다쓰레기를 줍거나 등하굣길에 길가에 버려진 플라스틱을 수거하고 있습니다. 

✅ 호스트 외 만날 수 있는 구성원 : 배우자와 아이들4
✅ 농장에서 만나는 반려 동물 : 고양이, 닭, 도마뱀, 육지 거북이
✅ 우퍼가 하는일 :
3~4월 봄나물 채취
5월 보리베기
6월 산딸기, 매실 수확
7월~8월 한시적 휴경기
9~10월 동백수확
11월 보리파종
12월~2월 휴경기

✅ 선호하는 우퍼상 : 스스로 일거리를 찾아서 도와주고 에너지 절약을 실천하는 우퍼, 여가시간에 독립적으로 여행하는 우퍼
✅ 우퍼에게 당부 메세지 : 아이들과 동물을 사랑하는 분을 환영합니다. 지역주민들과의 교류에 적극적으로 참여해주세요.
✅ 숙소에서 와이파이 가능여부 : 가능
✅ 술,담배 가능여부 : 금연이나 음주는 가능
✅ 호스트와 식사여부 : 하루 한,두끼만 함께 먹고 나머지는 식재료를 드려 직접 해 드셔야 합니다.
✅ 영어소통 : 원활히 가능, 일본어
✅ 일주일 기준 우핑시간 : 하루5시간 주5일

======== English explanation ==========
✅ Host Introduction and Free Farm Introduction:
In 2015, I moved from Seoul to Tongyeong and have been farming since 2017. We provide our self-produced, small-scale agricultural products to guests and cafe customers, practicing a fusion of the primary and tertiary industries to enhance the added value of local produce.
We are also involved in the coffee business, creating various menus with the fresh agricultural products grown on our farm. Our goal is to make food that is healthy, delicious, and environmentally sound.
Our main crops are wild raspberries and bangpung (herb), which are used in the guesthouse breakfast and cafe menus. We adhe

name: Katarzyna - Organic garden with herbalism
description:
1. Describe your project, your place, and its inhabitants (including animals):
Permaculture garden:  tomatoes in a greenhouse,  vegetables, berries, fruit trees, many flowers, medical herbs.
Food preservation for winter
Herbalism: making own vinegars (many types),  tinctures, dried herbal teas, creams etc
Inhabitants: Me (woman). 1 dog and 2 cats 
Natural house: strawbale and clay

30 km away from Krakow, 8 km to railway station, train goes 25 minutes to Krakow, 5 km to bus stop, bus every 20 minutes to Krakow, goes 40 minutes. I give the ride to railway station or a bus stop.

2. List the activities that WWOOFers will participate in and what you want to teach them:
Gardening: preparing the beds for vegetable , herbs and flowers, weeding, watering tomatoes 
Picking the vegetables and fruits, food preservation for winter
Picking the herbs: drying herbs, making vinegars 
I can teach all of these activities.

3. How will you feed and house WWOOFers? Please describe your accommodations and plan for meals:
1 private bedroom for WWOOFer, I can accommodate 1 person or a couple, that is maximum.
Kitchen and bathroom shared with me.
Three main meals daily and of course snacks.
I enjoy very much meals cooked by someone, all the ingredients provided.

4. What could a WWOOFer's day look like at your site (time slot for participation in activities, expectations...)?
I expect some help in the garden: preparing beds, mulching, weeding, composting, harvesting,
food preservation, herbalism, picking herbs, drying etc  
I would like about 4-5 hours work a day, Sunday is free, maybe a bit more on Saturday 

5. Play the guide! Talk about your own hobbies (music, cooking, etc.) and those possible around (hiking, swimming, etc.):
Gardening and herbalism, and growing medical herbs, always  searching and trying new and interesting species possible to cultivate in  Central European climate.
Natural medicine, wild edible plants .
Self-sufficiency: 100% self-sufficient in water: own private we
_wwoof_travel: Słomniki train station/ Kraków KRK airport

name: Help Us Renovate a Farmhouse (western France)

location.address: France, Chemillé-en-Anjou, Pays de la Loire

description: We—Marie and Romain—are renovating a farmhouse using eco-friendly methods, with the goal of moving in within the next six months. We have two daughters, aged 9 and 4, and a cat. We live in a town of around 8,000 people, with amenities including a swimming pool, a library, shops, and an SNCF railway station. There’s also a medicinal herb garden and a shaded area with a pond and a walking path all the way around it. We’re looking for help with projects such as installing drywall, building a rammed earth wall, and plastering the interior stone walls with lime plaster. We’d be delighted to welcome you and get to know you! You’ll mainly be working with Romain, while I, Marie, take care of the family logistics. No previous experience is needed—we’ll show you how to do everything. Sometimes you’ll work independently and have the farmhouse to yourself, but most of the time you’ll be working alongside Romain. We live in a small house 2 km from the farmhouse, which is currently being renovated. To give you some independence, you’ll be staying in a shaded caravan on the farmhouse grounds. We’ll share our meals with you and introduce you to our favourite local specialities—we love cooking! If you don’t speak French, don’t worry! Romain is an English teacher, and I’m a speech therapist. We both love languages and enjoy meeting people from different backgrounds. We can communicate with you in English and Spanish, and Romain also speaks Italian and German. Our 9-year-old daughter is very chatty and will be delighted to learn a few words of your language!

name: Join Us for Salsa on the Beach: Volunteer as a Dancer (Canary Islands)
description: We are offering salsa dance volunteering opportunities for one or two weeks between 25 of october, 2026 and 30 May 2027 in La Gomera, near Tenerife in Spain. ---  We are looking for either one complete dance couple or one female dancer. Medium-level proficiency is sufficient. My dance partner and I, Harald, together with you, will be teaching everyone a new dance on the beach. The dance we call  Salsita, or  Salsa Light. A gratis dance class for everybody ! Every thursday in Valle Gran Rey, and one other day in El Cedro. --- Salsita is a simplified form of salsa. The step is not 1-2-3, pause, 5-6-7, pause, but only 1-1-1-1-1-1-1-1. Apart from this, we also teach salsa for beginners and for salsa dancers who would like to refresh their skills. This is a voluntary cultural project for people who may lack the means, time, or confidence for a dance school. People shall find their way back to partner dancing. And away from the smartphones ! You can stay for free and you may bring one more person, regardless of gender and whether they dance or not. It is free for both guests. The only requirement is that one female dancer commits to dancing for 14 hours per week.  For one or two weeks, in the time frame 25th of october 2026 until 30th of may, 2027. . It is not necessary to be a professional dance teacher; it is enough if you are a medium-level salsa dancer. Please note: Just dancing, nothing more ( !!!)    We are easy-going, fun people !   We do this since 3 years , and it is so much fun ! Nearly everybody in the salsa scene knows us as this is a  very small island. We are happy to receive your message. ---  Last update: 6th of July, 2026.

name: Experience Organic Farming and Help in the Garden (Germany)
description: We are a diverse community of about 250 people – staff, children, volunteers from all over the world, children and young people with special needs, guests, and more. We live in a small village near the River Elbe, in a nature reserve with large gardens, farmland, a children’s home, a bakery, a dairy, and much more. A construction team is always at work; we are currently building several eco-friendly wooden houses. We would greatly appreciate your help in many areas. There is always something going on here – a vibrant life with a good balance of hard work and plenty of fun. We aim to create a place where life, friendship, learning and nature come together in the most beautiful way possible. We work hard, and we expect that you enjoy putting in a good effort too. The work is satisfying and enjoyable because we do it together, and it is not for profit — it is about creating something new, meaningful and healthy. We welcome volunteers throughout the year. We ask you to stay for at least one month so you have the opportunity to settle in and get involved in the activities here. If you would like to learn or brush up on your German, staying a little longer is always worthwhile.

name: Education, Social Work & Psychology Volunteers Wanted (Ghana)

location.address: Education, Social Work & Psychology Volunteers Wanted

description: We are looking for volunteers with a background in Education, Social Work, or Psychology, not simply to lend a hand, but to help shape the future of our work. Your knowledge, training, and experience can play a vital role in developing our projects from the ground up. We are in the early stages of establishing a Social Welfare and Wellbeing Centre – a youth centre and safe, supportive space where children, young people, and their families can access guidance, learning opportunities, and emotional support. We are seeking knowledgeable and passionate individuals who can contribute to the development of our mental health and wellbeing programmes and activities. By sharing your expertise and working closely with our local team, you will help strengthen the project, ensuring it meets the needs of the community and creates a lasting, positive impact.

name: House Sit my Dogs & Maintain the Garden (Italy)
description: I have 3 adorable dogs (children to me) they hate being left behind, at home, and I need sometime for myself.  Also there is a lovely garden in need of some new bushes and flower.

name: Join our Summer Camp as an English-speaking Assistant (Italy)
description: We are looking for English-speaking Summer Camp Trainers and Teachers' Assistants aged 22–29. Volunteers should be outgoing and able to motivate young learners through enjoyable, fun and creative activities. You should also have a passion for sport and outdoor activities. In return, we provide free accommodation, meals and plenty of fun, Italian style.

name: Volunteer at a Welcoming Hostel in Fez (Morocco)
description: Our place is for volunteers. You'll live like a local, meet travellers from around the world every day, make plenty of friends, and experience the country from a local perspective. Our hostel features a sun terrace and hammam. Dar El Mizan is located in Fès, in the Fes-Boulmane Region, and we have an on-site restaurant.

name: Farmstay with Gardening, Harvesting, and Nanny Help (Oregon)

location.address: Farmstay with Gardening, Harvesting, and Nanny Help

description: There is a lot to do here and plenty of space for growth, development, and cultivation. Our vision is to gradually transform our 2.5-acre pasture into a thriving food forest guided by permaculture principles. We live a holistic lifestyle and enjoy sharing what we’ve learned about food production and selling at farmers markets with like-minded people. This season, we are seeking help with gardening, orchard work, and caring for our 2.5-year-old outdoors while we garden and complete daily chores. Assistance with harvesting for farmers markets is also needed. Tasks will change with the seasons: spring focuses on garden work, while summer and autumn involve harvesting, sorting, processing, and preserving fruit. There may also be opportunities to attend farmers markets with us. Ideally, we hope to welcome a couple interested in this lifestyle. There are also opportunities to learn about natural building. Andrew is an amateur builder with a passion for natural materials and earth construction. He has previously built a 160-square-foot adobe cabin and an earthen, wood-fired oven. His long-term dream is to create an off-grid guest house featuring an outdoor kitchen with a wood-fired pizza and bread oven, outdoor showers, and composting toilets. We are especially interested in hosting an eager couple who dreams of owning land someday, growing food, living a healthy lifestyle, and raising children. Help is needed primarily from March through November, though pruning work is often available in January through March. Late August through October is especially important for harvesting, processing, and preserving fruit. We usually work 25 hours per week, typically five hours per day for five days, with two days off. Other work may include firewood preparation, composting, pruning and thinning, forest management, and solar cooking. Helpers should be prepared for physical outdoor work and bring a hat, comfortable work clothing, sturdy shoes, rain boots for spring and winter, and glo

name: Hospitality Internship - Managing Tourist Apartments in Florence (Italy)
description: We are looking for assistance with reception duties and managing our apartments in the heart of Florence. This support is needed before and after guest check-in and check-out. While proficiency in English is very important, it is not mandatory. Living in one of the most beautiful places in the world, you will have the chance to enjoy stunning medieval architecture and art, as well as explore the nearby forests and picturesque woods. You will also meet many people from all over the globe. As a volunteer or intern in Florence, you will learn how to help me manage the apartments I rent to tourists. Your experience will include learning how to prepare an apartment for: - Photography sessions - Creating listings - Interacting with travellers - Managing the various opportunities available in the hospitality business worldwide You will have the chance to explore the beautiful city of Florence, which is surrounded by history, art, and delicious food! The actual work is divided between back-office marketing and operations, as well as direct interactions with tourists in the city centre. Back-office tasks will primarily involve managing online travel websites and marketing efforts.

name: Share Our Lives While Helping Us Tame Our Garden (France)
description: Join us in rural Deux-Sèvres with full board in exchange for 25 hours of help per week. Most of the work will be outdoors, helping us bring our overgrown garden back to life. Help us with gardening, woodcutting, organising outbuildings, renovations and general maintenance around our home in rural South West France.

name: Volunteer with Our Community: Help with Indoor & Outdoor Jobs (Germany)
description: We offer start-ups the opportunity to launch their own projects within a cooperative community in the Lusatian Lake District, such as a residential protective workshop or a permaculture project with a pedagogical smallholding. We welcome volunteers to assist with gardening, graphic design, filmmaking, and bookkeeping. Helpers receive: ✔️ Accommodation and meals ✔️ Support with language learning ✔️ Courses on innovative education (optional EU qualification) ✔️ Insights into the latest scientific discoveries, including neuro-immunology and effective microorganisms ✔️ Bikes for exploring the surrounding nature reserve

name: Volunteer at an Observatory in Corsica (France)
description: Join a cultural project centred on astronomy, immerse yourself in a pristine natural environment, and enjoy memorable experiences with fellow stargazers. Stelle Nostre is a human-focused initiative celebrating discovery, knowledge sharing, and authentic moments beneath the Corsican sky. We value genuine connections, learning, and a simple life close to nature. Based in the village of Erbajolo, Stelle Nostre is creating a cultural meeting place dedicated to astronomy, nature, and human exchange. Throughout the year, we host enthusiasts and invited astronomers who lead workshops, observation evenings, and educational activities in a friendly, engaging atmosphere. On-site accommodation welcomes visitors and contributors in a relaxed, natural setting, making Stelle Nostre a space for cultural exchange and shared learning.

name: Help out Clearing, Planting & Renovating (Australia)

location.address: Help out Clearing, Planting and Renovating

description: We have recently returned from a 5 week trip to Canada and feel renewed! Experience in outdoors work with lawn tractors, whipper snippers, saws and machetes would be a great asset. A couple of such workers would be ideal. We are presently available from end of April to mid December 2026 and from mid January 2027 onward. That said, we plan to have a great time with our helpers as always, treating you members of our family. We love conversation, playing cards and touring with our volunteers to the national parks, beaches, museums galleries etc. that our fabulous region has to offer. All the amazing flora and fauna, the flowers, trees, wallabies, goannas and birds, birds, birds are still here to delight you! Please come and HELP US! We have now had FOUR volunteers in 2026! So glad to be back in the game. After 2 years, we are really excited to welcome workers again. As we have previously said: We would love to consider COUPLES or two singles traveling together.  We hope you will stay for at least 3 weeks.  If you are a single we would like you to stay at least 4 weeks. To be considered you must be already in Australia or show that you have a valid Working Holiday visa and tickets to Australia! We love having volunteers at our place.  We need Clearing, Planting, Renovating on the edge of the rainforest in Australia's green caldera, located between Byron Bay and the Gold Coast. We welcome a couple or two singles travelling together, to help with clearing and planting a fairly steep slope of native bush in order to mitigate bush fire vulnerability for the house.  AND now we need help tidying up to sell and move! We also need clearing of feral plants (lantana, blue top, camphor laurel etc), road work & rock wall building. We are also looking for workers with carpentry and home renovation skill. Lots of work indoors when it is raining, or not!  Some help with cooking and housekeeping would be great too!

name: Join Our Resort Team: Accommodation & Surf Lessons Included (Portugal)
description: Trainees at RIDE have the opportunity to connect with people from diverse fields, building relationships that can benefit them both personally and professionally. Our dynamic hotel environment offers a chance to support our teams for 5 hours a day, while enjoying the rest of your day exploring the area, participating in activities, or relaxing. This is a great way to step out of your comfort zone, gain practical experience, and build confidence. We provide accommodation with breakfast included, access to our facilities, and free equipment rentals, allowing trainees to save money while enjoying a rewarding hands-on experience in hospitality.

name: Join Our Team at a Hostel in Thessaloniki! (North Greece)
description: Hostel life is always interesting, social, and international. You'll meet people from all over the world and share unforgettable experiences. Free walking tours are available every day, allowing you to explore the city's rich history and vibrant atmosphere. You’ll also have the chance to immerse yourself in Thessaloniki’s culture—learning about local cuisine, traditional appetisers, and iconic drinks. From an archaeological perspective, Thessaloniki offers deep insights into ancient Macedonia, the historic Greek kingdom. You can visit sites such as Ancient Pella and Vergina, home to the tomb of Philip II. The city also boasts well-preserved Roman structures, many of which are UNESCO World Heritage monuments, still standing proudly in the heart of Thessaloniki. Most importantly, you can explore the city's extensive Byzantine history through its magnificent churches, city walls, towers, and castle. Thessaloniki was the second most significant city of the Byzantine Empire, making it a treasure trove of historical sites. For day trips, two of the most remarkable destinations are: * Mount Olympus – The legendary home of the 12 gods of Greek mythology and the site of the ancient Temple of Zeus at Dion. * Meteora – A breathtaking rock formation, home to ancient Byzantine monasteries perched atop towering cliffs.

name: Female Helper: Garden, Animal Care & Home (Italy)

location.address: Female Helper: Garden, Animals & Home – Salento

description: I live, together with my 14-year-old daughter, in the beautiful Salento, in a house with about two hectares of land. We are looking for help with daily household chores, both inside the house and in the garden. We also need assistance with various maintenance tasks, gardening, painting, and caring for the animals, including cats, dogs, and chickens. The house is located near the village and a few kilometres from the sea and Gallipoli, but you will need to have your own car, as I only have one and use it for work. Here you will find yourself in a peaceful place, surrounded by olive, fig, and fruit trees. Not far from here is one of the most beautiful seas in Italy. Salento also offers many places to visit, and if there is time, we can explore them together. I will need a hand with harvesting fruit and olives, cutting the grass, and sometimes gathering firewood. You will also take care of regular cleaning, both inside and outside the house, and occasionally assist with more extensive maintenance such as painting, repairs, etc. Additionally, when I am not around, you will be responsible for looking after the animals — dogs, cats, and chickens.

name: Help with Cleaning & Front Desk (California)
description: We have a place in LA called PodShare where we’re looking for help with cleaning and front desk support. In exchange, you get a free “pod” — a custom-built bunk bed (normally $50/night for guests) — in return for helping us make beds, keep the space clean, and welcome guests with front desk check-ins. We also provide WiFi, laundry, and some paid work opportunities! Which PodShare location you’ll help at depends on your availability, but the coolest part is that you’re welcome to stay across our locations throughout the city. Join our community and experience LA while living the PodShare way!

name: Small Hobby Farm near Vancouver (Canada)
description: Small hobby farm 100 km from Vancouver with 2 mini goats, ~50 chickens, 3 turtles, geese, a few ducks, and lots of fruit trees—including half an acre of grapes and a row of blueberries. We also have a small garden and greenhouse. Helpers stay in a private cabin with Wi-Fi. Chores are about 5 hours a day, 5 days a week, covering whatever needs to be done. We work together and welcome year-round help. The area offers skiing, hiking, biking, and swimming, and we have bikes available.

name: Help on an Organic Farm in the Matanuska Valley (Alaska)
description: We're an organic farm located in the beautiful Matanuska Valley, Alaska. Farm work changes with the seasons—spring is all about preparing the fields, sowing seeds, growing and transplanting seedlings, weeding, and watering. As summer and autumn arrive, volunteers help harvest the produce and sell it at local farmers’ markets, where the fruits of months of hard work come to life.

name: Experience Life on a Family Farm (west of Ireland)
description: We are a family of four: Patrick, Biddy, and our two children. We own a 100-acre mixed farm in western Ireland, where we primarily practice traditional and organic farming. Our farm includes cattle, horses, sheep, and more. Although our children are grown, they frequently visit home. Frank enjoys traveling and gardening, while Fina has a passion for working with horses. We also cultivate our own vegetables and occasionally host Airbnb guests. We welcome help with various aspects of our farm, including farming, gardening, managing our Airbnb, and daily operations.

name: Learn to make Chocolate on a Tropical Farm (Colombia)
description: Arcapacha is a place where you can reconnect with yourself – to share, give, and receive. Here, you'll not only enjoy but also collaborate while learning about our surroundings. Situated near Yopal, a small village nestled in the mountains of the Tolima region, just 130 kilometers south of Bogotá, the capital of Colombia, we reside in the tropical humid forest, boasting rich biodiversity in both fauna and flora. Our climate is ideal for delving into cocoa cultivation. Join us in nature, surrounded by rivers and trails, to relax in a stunning environment of harmony and connect with the ecosystem. Additionally, we offer a studio for fine arts, serve healthy cuisine, and provide opportunities to practice yoga, tai chi, or meditation. You can also unwind in our chlorine-free river water pool, admire the beautiful landscapes, and stay connected with Wi-Fi. Embrace the essence of Arcapacha.

name: Assist with Bungalow renovations in Los Angeles (California)
description: We are situated on a hill overlooking Elysian Park and downtown LA on a beautiful historic property with century-old bungalows. I have restored several and need an assistant to lend a hand. We have a large house and your own bedroom as well as kitchenette and deck. We need help with carpentry, light renovations, painting, gardening, and animal care for our chickens and horses. There are always projects ongoing that I could use a hand with as well as an assistant to help me renovate one of the bungalows. Please contact me if you any questions.

name: Help Out Around the House and Explore LA & OC (California)

location.address: United States, Garden Grove, Los Angeles

description: Just a little about our home — we live in the middle of the city, just 10 minutes from Disneyland. It’s a homestay where you can explore both Los Angeles and Orange County with ease. We typically host around 3 to 4 travellers at a time. Food is included throughout the week. We enjoy preparing homemade dinners and like to have a “family-style” dinner during weekdays. That said, if you're out exploring, there’s no pressure to return for meals. We love board games, video games, and virtual reality. On weekends, we try to take our travellers to local events, sights, or other fun activities. We also offer a free trip to a nearby amusement park. There’s a back house with three rooms where our travellers stay. We aim to make everyone feel at home while staying with us. This home is centred around cultural exchange and having fun together as a group. We’re actively involved with our travellers on weekends. During the work week, the schedule is 2.5 days — two days of 8 hours and one day of 4 hours.

name: Volunteer Teaching English to Students (Vietnam)
description: Please note that we can currently welcome volunteers only from the USA, UK & Ireland, Canada, New Zealand, and Australia. We are seeking enthusiastic volunteers to assist in local primary, secondary, and preschool schools in Bac Giang, Vietnam. Bac Giang is a small city located 50 km from Hanoi. This is a fantastic opportunity to immerse yourself in the authentic culture of Vietnam, away from typical tourist spots. You'll have the chance to make a real difference in the lives of local children by providing them with access to native English-speaking teachers. You’ll be supported in planning and teaching short conversational English classes alongside a Vietnamese teaching assistant, working 12 hours per week. In return, you'll receive free accommodation in a clean private room and a $200 living allowance per month. Our friendly staff will be on hand to assist with all aspects of living in Vietnam. We want you to feel welcome and safe during your time with us and will do everything we can to ensure that. For volunteers interested in staying longer, we can offer an extended employment contract with negotiable terms.

name: Sapa, Ta Phin Village – Culture and Wine Workshop (Vietnam)

location.city: Ta Phin Village – Culture and Wine Workshop (Vietnam)
location.address: Sapa, Ta Phin Village – Culture and Wine Workshop (Vietnam)

description: Volunteers choose to stay with us because they can experience authentic local life in Sa Pa, learn about traditional rice wine making, enjoy delicious Vietnamese home-cooked food, and become part of our family while helping for just 2 hours a day. What You'll Experience: 🍶 **Traditional Rice & Fruit Wine Making** 🌾 **Farm Stay Experience** 🏡 **Authentic Village Life** 🤝 **Cultural Exchange & Local Immersion** 🍳 **Traditional Vietnamese Cooking** Each day, you'll spend around **2 hours** helping with activities at our winery. The schedule is flexible and may take place either in the morning or the afternoon, depending on the day's activities and the weather. Typical tasks may include: * Assisting with seasonal farm work * Simple gardening * Helping with traditional rice and fruit wine production * Light household or community activities No previous experience is required—we'll guide you every step of the way. After your daily contribution, the rest of the day is yours to explore and relax. You can: * Trek through beautiful rice terraces and mountain trails * Visit nearby villages * Learn to cook authentic Vietnamese dishes * Discover the traditional rice and fruit wine-making process * Meet local families and experience everyday village life * Join cultural events and festivals (when available) * Relax and enjoy the peaceful countryside We ask for a contribution of **USD 12 per day**, which covers your accommodation, home-cooked meals, and the cultural experiences we share throughout your stay. Your daily 2-hour contribution is part of our cultural exchange programme, giving you the opportunity to learn about traditional farming, rice and fruit wine production, and authentic rural life while becoming part of our local community. We look forward to welcoming you to our family and sharing the beauty, culture, and traditions of Sa Pa with you.

name: Volunteer Caring for Horses at Our Sanctuary (Greece)
description: Caring for 36 critically endangered Skyrian Horses is a non-stop job—challenging but incredibly rewarding! Since launching our volunteering programme in 2009, we’ve welcomed over 200 volunteers. A Day in the Life of a Volunteer Here’s what a typical summer day looks like (winter hours vary): * 07:30 Muck out fields, fill water buckets, assist with feeding. * 10:00 DIY breakfast and usually free time, depending on the day's tasks. * 13:00 Help with feeding and water. * 14:00 Home-cooked lunch and break/free time. * 18:00 Final muck-out, water, and feeding. * 19:30 (For skilled volunteers and if time allows) Lunging, equine agility, halter training, handling, etc. * 20:30 DIY supper. The work is straightforward but physically demanding. You’ll build strength, fitness, and a great tan if you come in summer! We muck out twice daily to keep tasks manageable rather than tackling everything in one go. What We Look for in Volunteers We seek mature, self-motivated individuals who are committed to the ethos of volunteering. Volunteers should be capable problem-solvers, enjoy solitude and hard work, and be comfortable living without many modern conveniences. Those with a rural background or horse experience will especially enjoy farm life. If you’re passionate about horses, there’s plenty to do! We have young ponies ready for training, and we follow natural horsemanship principles from various teachers.

name: Teach Languages to Students Aged 4–15 (Vietnam)
description: By joining us, you will travel to a safe destination, take part in activities with local people, and have time to explore the landscapes and regional cultural features of Vietnam. You will live with local families as well as other Vietnamese and international volunteers, while still feeling at home. If you wish to learn Vietnamese, English, or other languages, this is an excellent opportunity. You will also have the chance to meet interesting people from different countries and share knowledge, life experiences, and cultures. This experience can help you reflect on yourself, discover your own dreams, and understand the true value of your life. If you develop ideas to study, work, or invest in Vietnam or another country in the future, we are always willing to provide support, advice, and collaboration. At the end of the programme, you will receive a volunteer certificate.

name: Volunteer as a Classroom Assistant (Guatemala)
description: Do you want to make a real difference in Guatemala? If so, this could be the perfect opportunity for you. At Niños de Guatemala, we believe education is the best tool to shape a child’s future. That’s why we’re inviting you to join our Paid Volunteering Programme as a Classroom Assistant and help fight poverty in our communities. As a Classroom Assistant, you’ll support the teacher in whatever is needed and supervise the children when the teacher is away. You’ll also get the chance to be an active part of the classroom, connecting with the students and helping them see new possibilities for themselves. This isn’t just volunteering—it’s being a role model and a source of inspiration for the children. They’ll make the experience truly special, and through your time and effort, you’ll give them opportunities to learn, grow, and dream bigger. We are Niños de Guatemala, and together, we’re changing lives through education.

name: Volunteer at Our Bar, Meet People, and Immerse Yourself in Local Culture (Colombia)

location.address: Volunteer at Our Bar, Meet People, and Immerse Yourself in Local

description: Join us in our lovely bar at Cacao Hostel! We're looking for volunteers. About the conditions: - We're seeking someone with strong social skills and fluency in both English and Spanish. - Your work in the hostel bar will be in the afternoon from 5 PM to 10 PM. Your responsibilities include selling drinks, engaging with guests on the patio, being the cheerful and social face of the hostel, restocking the fridge, preparing cocktails, completing sales forms, and managing inventory. - Training will last for 1 hour. We have opened a hostel in a grand house near downtown Santa Marta with the goal of creating a meeting place for travelers from South America. The owners are a Spanish family and some friends who arrived in Colombia to embark on this exciting project! We are searching for a volunteer who is outgoing, humorous, talkative, friendly, and radiates good vibes. The volunteer's role will be to cultivate a sociable atmosphere in the hostel. Tasks include: - Engaging with all the guests. - Facilitating social activities in common areas. - Organizing fun games such as karaoke and board games. - Arranging events for the hostel, including dance classes, yoga, and sports activities. - Managing the refreshments, crafting cocktails, documenting drink sales, and maintaining inventory records.

name: Help Build a Draft Horse Farm in Central Brittany (France)
description: Our farm is located in a private valley with 45 hectares of preserved forest and pastureland. We work with draft horses and offer horse boarding facilities. We also welcome children with social, emotional, or additional needs. Tourists and families are welcome to visit the farm. We are looking for help with setting up farm fencing, trimming hedges, and mowing grass, among other tasks. Other activities may include batch cooking for preserves, building an earth-and-straw bread oven, setting up our yurt, and gardening around a newly created pond. All tasks will be carried out alongside us and under our supervision. You will not be left on your own unless you prefer to work independently.

name: Help Maintain Gardens and Stone Houses (Italy)
description: I manage six apartments in the heart of the Apuan Alps, in a medieval village off the usual tourist routes. I’m looking for help with maintaining the gardens and the houses, as well as caring for the olive groves and orchards. I’m an experienced restorer of stone houses, having worked extensively in construction, and I studied at the Carrara Academy of Fine Arts. I can also organise artistic projects for anyone interested. Alongside this exchange, I’d be delighted to learn about cultures from around the world, and to share my own culture with you, as well as show you the most adventurous corners of the Apuan Alps.

name: Marketing and general help needed in a tropical paradise (Bolivia)
description: We are a very friendly young family. You will be working closely with us. The town of Villa Tunari is very nice and touristic. You'll be expected to produce promotional short videos and photographs for our small lodge. In spare time you can also help with general maintenance tasks. The aim is for you to capture the beauty and comfort of the place on camera, and to show what an amazing experience it is for any person who visits us. The material produced should be ideal and attractive for current social media. Besides this, in spare time and depending on the length of your stay, you can help with general maintenance tasks, like gardening, fixings, painting, etc.

name: Experience Glamping Farm Life – Hospitality Help (Ireland)
description: We are Neasa and Alan, and our daughter Lena, and we run a family glamping farm offering unique accommodation and farm stays. We are looking for extra volunteer help with warmly welcoming and interacting with our guests, as well as housekeeping and cleaning. We are a friendly, easy-going family, supported by a long-standing team of staff who have worked on the farm for many years. Many volunteers return for multiple summers, which we really value. We welcome visitors from all over the world, so you will have the opportunity to experience Irish farm life and culture in a peaceful, natural setting while also meeting people from a wide range of countries and backgrounds. We have been welcoming language exchange guests and volunteers to our family farm for over 40 years (since I was a child), and we have always enjoyed sharing our stories and learning from the experiences of those who stay with us. Wicklow is one of the most beautiful parts of Ireland, with a mix of mountains and coastline and easy access to Dublin. We look forward to sharing our little slice of Wicklow wilderness with you. We are a one-hour drive from Dublin and just ten minutes from Glendalough, with its famous lakes, monastic site and wonderful walking trails. There are good bus links to Dublin from our local village of Ashford (a ten-minute drive from the farm), and we can arrange lifts to the bus stop or nearby villages.

name: Help with Dogs, Ponies & Airbnb Treehouse (England)

location.address: Help Out with Dogs, Ponies & Airbnb Treehouse

description: Are you an animal lover? We need help caring for our 5 dogs, 2 ponies and 1 cat during the day (and sometimes at night if we have puppies). The role includes pet sitting while we’re at work, some house cleaning, and changeovers for our Airbnb treehouse. You’ll also help with general household chores. We provide a very comfortable caravan where you can cater for yourself, plus £40 per week towards food.

name: Help with Check-Ins and Common Area Care in Bologna (Italy)
description: We have just opened a small hostel just outside the city centre of Bologna, offering a limited number of rooms (three private rooms and two dormitories) and a private bedroom for the hostel keeper. We are seeking someone to assist with check-ins, cleaning and handling bedding. Digital nomads are welcome, as you will have plenty of free time.

name: 'Welcome Host' Opportunity in a Homestay (Massachusetts)
description: We are an independent homestay, and we work hard to offer a safe, clean, and multicultural place for travelers to rest. We enjoy enriching our environment by hosting people from different parts of the world. The welcome host helps create a friendly and welcoming atmosphere for our guests. This role ensures that everything runs smoothly during the afternoon and evening operations. The welcome host also organizes supplies to make sure all necessary items are available for the housekeeping team. The position requires 26 hours per week, with four afternoons of work and three days off. We offer USD 30 per week to help cover food expenses for those who work 26 hours per week. The tasks are easy and not physically demanding, but they do require attention and care. Responsibilities include folding sheets, refilling toiletries, keeping bathrooms organized, welcoming guests and checking them in, explaining house rules, helping and socializing with guests, and doing light cleaning of balconies and stairs. In Boston, there are English clubs for people learning English, and we like to participate in activities together at least once a month. We are available to host for periods ranging from three to five weeks. We are located three blocks from the subway station, close to the beach, stores, and a mall. We also provide bicycles so you can get around easily and stay active.

name: Help with Gardening, Light DIY, Pool and Countryside Living (France)

location.address: Help with Gardening, Light DIY, Pool and Countryside Living

description: We are a small family living between Lyon and the countryside with our young son. We have a large family house with several bedrooms, a big garden of around 2,000 m², a swimming pool, and plenty of outdoor space. The house is a lovely place, especially during the summer, but it also needs regular care and some small improvements. As we spend time between Lyon and the house, we do not always have enough time to take care of everything ourselves. We would be happy to welcome friendly, independent and respectful people who would enjoy staying with us and giving us a hand with the house and garden. The help we are looking for is mainly light gardening, tidying outdoor areas, watering plants, helping around the house, small DIY tasks, organising spaces, and general maintenance. Nothing dangerous or too technical. We are looking for people who enjoy simple tasks, being outdoors, and sharing good moments. In exchange, we can offer a comfortable place to stay, access to the kitchen, bathroom, common areas, garden and swimming pool. We can host people in a bedroom, and we may also be able to welcome people travelling in a van, as we have outdoor space. We are looking for mature, clean, respectful and positive people. Since this is our family home and we have a young child, it is very important for us to welcome people who are trustworthy, calm and considerate. If you enjoy family life, nature, simple living, gardening, light DIY and good conversations, we would be happy to hear from you.

name: Home and Garden by the Sea in Dublin (Ireland)
description: My place offers a calm, friendly, and comfortable home in the Dublin suburbs, just a 10-minute train ride from the city centre. You’ll be surrounded by plants, nature, and good company 🌿. The tasks are light and relaxed, and you’ll also learn practical gardening tips and horticultural knowledge in a cosy, welcoming environment. It’s an ideal place for volunteers who enjoy peaceful living, meaningful conversations, and a warm home atmosphere. I am a well-organised and responsible person. I’m an easy-going and friendly host who enjoys gardening and evening chats 😊. I value respect, honesty, and a relaxed environment. Volunteers can expect to be treated kindly and fairly, with plenty of support and patience. The house also has a permanent tenant, Gloria from Italy (early 30s), who has been here for over a year. I live in a lovely home with many plants and shrubs that need regular care. Currently, there is one volunteer, but I have space for one more. Each volunteer has their own private bedroom 😊. I’m looking for an energetic and friendly volunteer, especially during winter, as the long Irish winters can be challenging. You will help with 5 shifts of 5 hours per week — a total of 25 hours. There will be some gardening work, but much of the time we will simply spend time together chatting, listening to music, playing board games, or going on long walks. There will also be some light cleaning, such as tidying the kitchen and vacuuming, but nothing too demanding. You will have 2 days off per week. Of course, you are welcome to spend time together outside of working hours as well if you wish 😊. Respectful behaviour is expected at all times. No guests are allowed, although there may be some flexibility once trust has been established 🤝.

name: Help Build a Forest Retreat in the Woods (Czech Rep)
description: Two years ago, I bought an old cottage hidden in the forest, about 30 minutes from Prague. What I thought would be a small renovation project quickly turned into a much bigger adventure. Since then, I’ve spent most of my weekends and free time bringing the place back to life—learning new skills, solving unexpected problems, and slowly turning it into a cosy retreat surrounded by nature. The cottage is already comfortable to live in, but there is still plenty to create. My vision is to build a place where people can slow down, relax, enjoy nature, sit by the fire, warm up in a sauna, play games, read books, and simply escape the stress of everyday life. I live and work in Prague, so I split my time between the city and the cottage. Because of this, I’m looking for independent and resourceful people who enjoy freedom and don’t need constant supervision. When I’m in Prague, you’ll often have the whole place to yourself. When I’m at the cottage, we’ll work together on larger projects and share ideas over a coffee or a beer. This place is perfect for people who enjoy nature, creativity, and hands-on projects. You’ll be surrounded by forest and complete peace and quiet, while still having easy access to Prague whenever you feel like exploring the city. If you’re the type of person who enjoys building things and spending time away from the crowds, I think you’ll feel right at home here.

name: Volunteer in the Heart of Edinburgh at a Backpacker Hostel (Scotland)
description: Join our hostel team and assist with all the tasks involved in running a bustling backpacker hostel. The multi-award-winning Castle Rock Hostel boasts a fantastic location right next to Edinburgh Castle! Our grand, yet laid-back hostel combines a touch of grandeur with a lively backpacker vibe. Enjoy staying in Edinburgh's prime location, at the very heart of the city. We're just a short walk from the bus and train stations, the airport bus, and only a minute's walk from Edinburgh Castle, the Royal Mile, and the Grassmarket. Situated on a relatively quiet street, we’re also just around the corner from the city's vibrant nightlife—perfect for an ideal stay!

name: Family-owned B&B Seeking Hands-On Helpers (France)
description: We run a small family-owned B&B on a large plot of land and are looking for extra hands for maintenance, gardening, and creative projects. Beyond the work, the space encourages genuine cultural exchange. Whether over shared meals, conversations around the fire, or creative sessions in the atelier or orangerie, everyone is invited to share their skills, learn from others, and find inspiration in the community. Visitors come from all walks of life, and the diversity of backgrounds makes each stay unique. Through food, language, art, or hands-on projects, this place offers space to learn, connect, and create together at a thoughtful, human pace. We need help running the everyday B&B—preparing meals, making rooms, engaging with guests, laundry, cleaning, small renovations—and hosting other events. We’re also looking to renovate the buildings surrounding the château and develop a small permaculture plot to bring the land into fuller ecological balance. If you’re passionate about sustainable agriculture and enjoy working in a creative, hands-on environment, we’d love to hear from you. Accommodation is provided on-site, with several comfortable options depending on season and availability. You may stay in private or shared rooms within the former schoolhouse, in simple but cozy spaces in the château itself, or, in warmer months, camp on the grounds surrounded by nature and under the stars. The atmosphere is peaceful and communal, with shared living areas, a kitchen, and access to showers and basic amenities. While rustic, the space is full of charm and character—ideal for those who appreciate simplicity, creativity, and connection to the land.

name: Assist with Tasks on Our Olive Oil Farm (South of France)
description: Hello! We're a family from the beautiful south of France. It's a serene and peaceful place. Our olive oil farm is located near the charming town of Capestang, in the southern part of the country. We're looking for some assistance with a variety of tasks, such as gardening, harvesting, chopping wood for winter, and building structures like a solar dryer and a greenhouse. Our aim is to create an off-grid haven for travellers. We want to establish a garden, grow our own food, filter water, and plant fruit trees and other types of trees. It’s important to note that this is not a commercial farm – it's a space for us to learn and grow our own organic produce. I'm a yoga teacher, and my income comes from teaching classes, not from the farm. Anyone helping on the farm or around the house does so on a voluntary basis, and in return, we provide accommodation, meals, and a welcoming place to stay. We have a lot to share, including the secrets of a healthy diet and knowledge about various herbs, wild fruits, and seasonal greens. You’ll also have the opportunity to experience yoga and meditation with us. As I’m also a chef and dietary expert, you’ll learn to cook delicious, nourishing meals. We'll show you how to create compost and grow food with ease, and you’ll gain valuable insights into gardening and planting fruit trees. Most importantly, you’ll get to soak up the sun and, above all, learn about olive farming – from harvesting the olives to making oil.

name: Help in a Bed & Breakfast in Brittany (northwestern France)
description: Hello, we are Frederic and Fleur who run a little Bed and Breakfast and well being centre by a magnificent beach in Brittany. Nature and beautiful views all around us. Great waves for surfing, great paths for hiking or biking. Healthy lifestyle, yoga, spirituality. Family/community atmosphere, sharing and caring! We have 2 furry cat Shaman ans Narcisse so please beware if you are allergic! In the house there is a  ping pong table, sauna, two bikes, some sport equipment. We will be happy to share with you the magic of Presquile de Crozon! Show you the trails,  beaches and incredible places.
