Volunteers work 4-5 hrs per day, mainly housekeeping: preparing the rooms and bathrooms for the guests (we have only 4), preparing breakfast, taking care of the house Gardening is also another activity- vegetable garden and maintenance. In exchange we offer  accommodation, organic vegetarian food, course of massage with possibility of a certificate, yoga, sauna...

name: Help with Renovation, Planting & General Tasks (France)

location.address: Help with Renovation, Planting and General Tasks

description: Hi, I’m Wilna. I have been a host for many years and am originally from the Netherlands, but I now spend most of my time in the south of France, where I have a large house in a small village near Carcassonne, as well as a farm with a small wooden house. I grow fruit and nut trees, and the work varies from renovation and construction to planting—there's always something to do, which can be enjoyable for both experienced volunteers and those looking to gain new skills. The south of France is one of the most beautiful regions in the country, with pleasant weather for most of the year and proximity to the Mediterranean Sea. I typically ask for help five days a week for an average of five hours each day, allowing you plenty of time to explore the region during your time off. I provide meals, accommodation, and a private room with Wi-Fi. I also have pets, including one dog, two cats, goats, ducks, geese, chickens, tuekeys and a rooster.

name: Spring Painting + Gardening at Art Centre Château near Paris (France)

location.city: Gardening and Maintenance at a Château
location.address: Painting, Gardening and Maintenance at a Château

description: We are preparing the house and gardens for the season of arts retreats and residencies. For four hours a day, six days a week, we will be painting, gardening, and carrying out general maintenance. There will be a maximum of six of us, working, eating, and enjoying our time together. Château de Sacy is an 18th-century house near Paris that has been in our family for two centuries. We host creative retreats and artist residencies here, maintaining the gardens according to permaculture principles.

name: Help Build a Recovery Retreat in the Pyrenees (France)
description: Situated in the beautiful Pyrenees mountains, right on the French-Spanish border, is Saint-Béat. My plan is to open this lovely townhouse as a not-for-profit recovery retreat. For me, it's all about giving back: providing people with a safe place to heal, reconnect with nature, receive guided support based on the 12-step approach, and focus on both physical and spiritual wellbeing within an environment of positivity, warmth, and encouragement. The house itself—both in how it feels and how it looks—will play a big part in the recovery journey. I want it to offer a sense of calm, with space for yoga and meditation, a kitchen where people can come together to cook and eat, and bedrooms that are comfortable and stylish. I am looking for a person, or ideally a couple of people, to help with the building work. A fair amount has already been completed, much of it by myself, but there is still plenty to do, including stud work, plasterboarding, laying floors, tiling, repairing wooden stairs, fitting doors, building a small outdoor terrace, repointing, painting, filling and sanding, rendering the front of the property, replacing four windows, and much more. I had absolutely no building experience before starting this project, but with a little faith, help from friendly locals, and plenty of Google searches, I have managed to complete the first-fix electrics and plumbing, level and replace the landing and several living-area floors, and finish two rooms, including one with an en-suite bathroom. When I say "I", I really mean "we", as my wife has played an equal part in the renovation so far, and I am incredibly grateful for everything she has done. However, due to family commitments and caring responsibilities for an elderly parent, Jackie will need to spend a significant amount of time in the UK next year. That is why I am reaching out for help, assistance, and support in whatever way you feel able to contribute. My plan is to dedicate a full year to the project, starting in

name: Help Out at a Historic Silk Mill in the Ardèche (southeast France)
description: Hi! We’re Luc 🇳🇱 and Raquel 🇺🇸 — a Dutch-American couple pouring our energy, creativity, and love into La Filature, a 19th-century silk mill in the beautiful Ardèche region of southern France. This isn’t a big operation — it’s just the two of us, working together on a project that’s close to our hearts. We welcome volunteers who enjoy meaningful, hands-on work, shared meals, and time in nature. Whether you’re planting lavender, painting shutters, or helping clear ivy from the old stone walls, you’ll be part of something special. We believe in creating a space where people feel at home — where stories, cultures, and laughter are shared as easily as the day’s tasks. If that sounds like your kind of place, we’d love to meet you.

name: Join Our Sicilian Tomato Farm (Italy)
description: Hi! We are looking for volunteers to join our Sicilian tomato farm, where we will be making sun-dried tomatoes and tomato sauce—a fun and interesting activity! The exchange involves 4 hours of work in the morning, leaving your afternoons free. We can go to the beach together, or you can choose to explore the beautiful island of Sicily on your own. We provide food and accommodation. Help is also needed with cooking, general cleaning and maintenance.

name: Share Skills, Mentor & Promote Cultural Exchange (Malawi)

location.address: Share Skills, Mentor and Promote Cultural Exchange

description: Our organisation welcomes volunteers who want to make a real difference while learning and growing. This programme focuses on sharing skills, supporting our local volunteers and staff, and promoting cultural exchange within the communities we serve. Volunteers work closely with local communities in areas such as community development, environmental protection, waste management, youth empowerment, livelihoods, and social support. They do not just observe — they actively participate in meaningful activities that create positive change. Volunteers work directly with local people, helping to train young people, support environmental activities such as tree planting and waste management campaigns, and assist in community development programmes. Their contributions have visible and lasting impact, benefiting both the community and the volunteers themselves. The programme is not one-way; volunteers share their knowledge in areas such as leadership, entrepreneurship, communication, agroforestry, project management, and environmental protection, while also learning about local solutions, traditional knowledge, and community-based approaches. Volunteers experience rich local culture, traditions, food, music, and daily life. Cultural exchange sessions are organised where community members and volunteers learn from one another, fostering friendship, respect, and global understanding. We provide guidance, mentorship, and clear roles for volunteers, who work alongside committed local staff and community volunteers in a welcoming, safe, and well-organised environment. Through this programme, volunteers gain practical experience, confidence, and leadership skills. They work in teams, tackle real community challenges, and strengthen their communication and problem-solving abilities. The focus is on long-term impact, as volunteers help build local capacity so that skills and knowledge remain in the community even after the programme ends. By joining, volunteers become part of a passi

name: Help Build Eco-Friendly Projects (Canary Islands)
description: Here lies a wild vision from an ex-world traveller—a dreamer, a creative, an often-lost soul—who blends expert knowledge of tropical fruit trees, permaculture, and a deep passion for nature to design a lush, self-sustaining paradise. This eccentric dream aims to inspire those who step into it. Hundreds of tropical fruit trees and plants have already been planted, and a mini-jungle is beginning to emerge from the Sub-Saharan volcanic landscape. If only there were enthusiastic people willing to help bring this vision to life! Perhaps even some with skills and practical knowledge? This is an open space to learn how to care for plants, trees, and even animals while developing or sharing skills in farming, eco-build techniques, sustainability, and personal growth. All skill levels are welcome—extra hands and expertise are always appreciated! Your contributions will help push the project towards its sustainability goals. Come and experience the challenges and rewards of being close to nature in a wild and stunning environment, nestled on the edge of a UNESCO-preserved coastline. Beyond daily farm tasks, a range of projects are available for all skill levels. On-site, the experience is akin to camping or glamping, with a few added comforts—even a touch of luxury butnin some aspects it's still very basic. We're especially looking for people to help in the permaculture garden and with eco-build techniques. If this sounds like your kind of adventure, we’d love to hear from you!

name: Come and Lend a Helping Hand on a Family Farm (Spain)
description: This chaotic, happy lot, living on a beautiful but often windy farm in south-east Galicia, are looking for volunteers. So if you feel like digging, chasing runaway animals, fencing, joking, laughing, and playing music, we would be happy to get to know you. You can learn and do almost anything here – we are experienced builders and farmers who took on a challenge that is perhaps a bit too big for just the six of us. We are rebuilding the village and could use a helping hand or two with taking care of the animals (sheep, goats, cows), reshaping the landscape, turning fallen trees into firewood, cutting brambles, managing water flow, cleaning water reserves, fencing, and general farm tasks. There are plenty of odd jobs to be done, and we can adapt to what you would like to learn.

name: Cultural Volunteer Experience: Teach, Care, and Explore (Tanzania)

location.address: Educational and Cultural Volunteer Experience: Teach, Care, and
location.country: Educational and Cultural Volunteer Experience: Teach

description: Hello everyone, my name is Ellias, and at 41 years old, together with my wife, we are genuinely excited to invite you to our project aimed at helping children around our community. We have established our project as a registered non-profit organization with the primary vision of providing free and better education to the youth. As a project organizer, I am looking for volunteers for two main activities: 1. Teaching and Childcare: Volunteers will be responsible for teaching and taking care of kids in our small organization. This involves educational activities, playtime, and providing assistance to children from 09:00 am to 01:00 pm. 2. Gardening and Animal Care (Optional): For those interested, we welcome volunteers to assist with gardening and animal care, involving tasks such as cleaning and milking for our chickens and cows. Participation in this aspect is entirely optional. Apart from volunteering activities, we also allocate time for volunteers to explore Arusha City. Travelers will have a great chance to learn various activities from both my wife and me. They can learn local languages, including Swahili and Maasai, and my wife can provide lessons on cooking Tanzanian local foods and the traditional ways of dressing in the Maasai culture.

name: Work Exchange on Our Raspberry Farm in the Algarve (Portugal)
description: We produce and export raspberries across Europe. The main tasks include picking, packing, and some general maintenance. We're happy to share our knowledge with you and would love to learn from your life experiences. We believe that learning, sharing, and creating great memories together is what makes this experience special. Accommodation and meals are provided, along with a bike for exploring nature and heading to the beach. You’re also welcome to use the swimming pool at any time. There are always people from different parts of the world helping us, creating a diverse and friendly atmosphere. Looking forward to seeing you soon! 😊

name: Volunteer Staff at a Backpacker Hostel (Northern Ireland)
description: Vagabonds Backpackers is the coolest hostel in Belfast and all of Northern Ireland. We are an independent hostel just outside the city centre, created by travellers for travellers. We are always on the lookout for other vagabonds to help us take care of the house, welcome our guests, and take the opportunity to experience the city and its life. Tara and Curt have toured the globe and have witnessed first-hand the best and worst hostel accommodation the world has to offer. Our manifesto is simple: to provide a clean, relaxed, and affordable environment for those paying a brief visit to our honest city. Situated in the Queen’s Quarter, just five minutes from the city centre, museums, train and bus stations, and the city’s best bars and cafés, we consider ourselves to have one of the best locations for travellers.

name: Stay in a Manor in Exchange for Help Maintaining the Venue (England)
description: Brympton House is a stunning historic venue set in the heart of Somerset. We love hosting guests and travellers, learning from them along the way. In exchange for 25 hours of help each week to maintain the venue, we offer free accommodation, food shopping contributions, and free Wi-Fi.

name: Experience Off-Grid Life in the Lleida Pyrenees (France)
description: In the silent majesty of the high mountains of Lleida, in a hidden corner where only the wind, birdsong, and clear-flowing rivers can be heard, our one-hectare-plus farm welcomes special volunteers. We are in the midst of transformation, with construction well underway. We are looking for people with practical land skills, a love for the earth, and a desire to contribute to a sustainable, conscious project. We seek calm, independent people who respect nature and enjoy solitude, meditation, and contemplation. Vegetarians and non-smokers are preferred. We offer a magical little cabin in the forest next to a stream, designed for two people. It has a bunk bed, a small private kitchen, and a dry toilet, creating a rustic yet comfortable space where you can wake up to sunlight and fall asleep under a blanket of stars. Lunch is provided as a shared daily meal, while we also value independence and encourage volunteers to manage their own routines with confidence. Breakfast and dinner are self-organised, and you will also have the opportunity to enjoy fresh seasonal vegetables from our garden. We ask for around four hours of work per day, Monday to Saturday, with a minimum initial stay of two weeks. Volunteers should be willing to help with a range of tasks depending on the needs of the day, including farming work, light construction, trail maintenance, water collection, land care, and general project support. Our way of life is simple and close to nature, powered entirely by solar energy and supplied with pure spring water. Access is via forest trails with no paved roads, and the surroundings offer wild meadows, high-mountain paths, and a deeply peaceful atmosphere where nature can be fully experienced. This is not a place for everyone. It is for those who feel a deep calling towards simplicity, authenticity, and the sacredness of nature. If that sounds like you, and your heart resonates with the mountains, we welcome you with open arms. 🌿 An experience to transform, heal,

name: Internship in Tuscany – Learn the Wine Tourism Business (Italy)
description: Help with our wine tourism business in Tuscany in exchange for learning the Italian language and culture, and free accommodation. Enjoy a wonderful working experience in the heart of the countryside, only 10 miles from the stunning city of Florence, while helping in the wine tourism business and learning Italian surrounded by vineyards, olive groves, forests with hiking trails, and wildlife. Castello del Trebbio was built in the late 12th century for the Pazzi family. The name “Trebbio” comes from the Latin word “trivium”, meaning “three ways”. The Baj Macario family purchased Castello del Trebbio in 1968. A programme of restoration and vineyard planting was undertaken to bring the estate to its current splendour. The castle is now owned and managed by Anna Baj Macario and her husband Stefano Casadei, the winemaker. They have since focused the estate on producing high-quality wine and extra virgin olive oil. Today, the estate produces around 350,000 bottles of wine. This includes traditional Chianti, an accessible DOCG wine for everyday enjoyment at an excellent price, as well as the high-quality Super Tuscan “Pazzesco”, a blend of Merlot and Syrah grapes, and the Chianti Rufina Riserva “Lastricato”, awarded by Wine Spectator and Gambero Rosso. Stefano and Anna have also founded other wineries, one in southern Tuscany and another in Sardinia.

name: Horse Riding & Tourism Experience near Agadir Beach (Morocco)
description: We are a small and friendly horse riding project located near Agadir beach in Morocco. We offer beautiful experiences in nature, including beach and mountain rides. We are looking for motivated volunteers to help with daily tasks such as caring for horses, assisting guests, cleaning, and providing general support. In exchange, we offer a relaxed atmosphere, accommodation, cultural exchange, and the opportunity to discover Moroccan life. Volunteers can also enjoy horse riding and explore the area during their free time. No experience is required — just motivation and a positive attitude 😊

name: Lend a Hand at an Old Manor in the Woods (Ireland)
description: Set within 12 acres of mature forest, we are a 200-year-old home currently undergoing significant transformation. As we develop our family home into something more than just ‘old and cold’, we’re looking for people to assist with gardening and construction work. We also welcome those who can help with housekeeping and ongoing maintenance of the house. In exchange, we offer the opportunity to enjoy the land, stay in a small 19th-century manor house, and experience the natural beauty of two rivers and abundant wildlife. Tasks are varied and may include anything from caring for our elderly mother to gardening or general housekeeping. If you have experience in construction and aren't afraid of physical work, we would be especially pleased to hear from you.

name: Volunteer on a Hobby Farm in the Beautiful West (Ireland)
description: We are a small hobby farm in the beautiful rural west of Ireland, surrounded by stunning scenery, sunrises, and sunsets. Our farm is home to a variety of animals, including ponies, a donkey, cows, sheep, pygmy goats, llamas, poultry, and a few reptiles. Right now, we’re welcoming baby goats, lambs, chicks, and ducklings. We need help with daily tasks such as feeding, watering, cleaning, and caring for the animals, as well as building and maintaining their enclosures. Some of the baby animals may require bottle-feeding. In addition to animal care, we’re working on building new enclosures, repairing existing ones, and doing some painting. We also take the animals to fairs and heritage days, offering the chance to meet people, browse stalls, and see crafts. Our farm is located just outside the town of Kiltimagh, which is easy to visit, and we have two bikes available for exploring the countryside.

name: Join our Sustainable Living and Permaculture Adventure (Brazil)
description: We are a permaculture community with over 30 years of history, located 7 kilometers from Vale do Capão, near the Chapada Diamantina National Park. This prime location gives us easy access to several waterfalls within walking distance, such as Cachoeira da Fumaça, Águas Claras, Lago dos Patos, and others. The best way to reach our community is through Vale do Capão, by taking a bus from Salvador to Palmeiras and then from Palmeiras to Vale do Capão. We also have an internal library open to everyone. In our free time, we enjoy making bonfires and playing music. Here, we offer the experience of living in one of Brazil's oldest alternative communities, where we practice a simple, collective way of life in harmony with nature. Our daily routine is peaceful, allowing for deep contact with the land and opportunities for self-discovery. Our team consists of residents and individuals who have embraced this lifestyle and are happy to share it with those who visit and collaborate with us. We welcome everyone as brothers and sisters who will live alongside us for a few days. We engage in activities such as agroforestry, bioconstruction, reforestation, environmental protection, gardening, herb cultivation, handmade soap-making, and running a Waldorf-inspired school, among others. Our diet is ovo-lacto vegetarian, and all meals are prepared in a wood-fired oven. We welcome visitors, volunteers, and also offer courses. We all work together from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m., Monday to Saturday. The volunteer program lasts for 15 days, with the possibility of extending for an additional 15 days. We request a contribution of R$400.00 (four hundred Brazilian reais) for each volunteer's 15-day stay in the community, which covers food, accommodation, and learning experiences. We are looking for individuals who are primarily focused on agriculture (gardening, agroforestry) and other daily tasks as needed by the community. We seek people who are eager to learn and enjoy participating in a communal ex

name: Experience Healing at Our Gardens and Retreat Centre (British Columbia)
description: Wild Heart Gardens and Retreat Centre is a non-profit project founded by Sue and Jeff Turner in the stunning foothills of the Cascade Mountains, in Tulameen, British Columbia. Sue, an experienced energy healer, has been helping people heal for over 20 years. A few years ago, she and Jeff established the gardens and retreat centre as a sanctuary where visitors can connect with the healing power of nature and spirit. Visiting Wild Heart Gardens offers a unique opportunity to immerse yourself in the wilderness and natural beauty of this untamed part of Canada. Sue and Jeff Turner are also acclaimed natural history filmmakers, with a distinguished career producing wildlife documentaries for leading broadcasters such as the BBC, PBS, CBC, Discovery Channel, Netflix, and others. Their films have been broadcast in over 80 countries, showcasing the wonders of the natural world. Their deep understanding of nature and its inhabitants underpins the retreat experience. Exploring the wild landscapes of the Cascade Mountains is an integral part of what makes a visit here so special. However, there is another equally significant dimension to the Wild Heart experience: the opportunity to engage in spiritual practices. Meditation, psychic and mediumship readings, and energy healing are core aspects of the retreats. Workers and volunteers may also have the chance to participate in these practices, should they wish to do so. The gardens embody the profound connection between the natural energy of the Earth and the healing energy of spirit.

name: Help with housekeeping duties at a Holistic Retreat (Greece)
description: We organise holiday for solo traveller where they can explore their creative potential and meet like-minded people in a stunning location.

name: Help with Our Permaculture Project by the Sea in Catalonia (Spain)
description: We are creating an oasis surrounded by nature for explorers and digital nomads, inspired by the Earth, ecology, bioconstruction and circular economy, just 6 km from the stunning beaches of the Mediterranean Sea. I’m Francesco (francescoelcormoran.com) a singer-songwriter who values simplicity and joy. No need to send a CV; just drop me an email sharing your skills, interests, and motivations. In the Montnegre Natural Park, near the beautiful beaches of Sant Pol de Mar and only 40 minutes from Barcelona, I am building “WE ARE NATURE”—a wild space dedicated to well-being, homemade and wholesome eating, and living in harmony with nature. Here, we prioritise ecology, sustainability, wilderness, simplicity, circular economy, medicinal plants, and a space to cultivate creativity and freedom. The location is well-connected, with a regular bus service linking us to the nearby train station. Surrounded by the natural beauty of Montnegre and close to the stunning beaches of Costa Brava, it’s a place to connect with nature, share with others, and embrace a spirit of care. We offer activities like yoga, spiritual music, tree meditation, and cooking. I’m Italian and have been living in Spain for eight years. Having lived in various places, I enjoy cooking with an international flair, and I make a variety of homemade products, such as liqueurs (limoncello and strawberry), jams, herbal extracts, and hearty dishes cooked over an open fire—think rich paellas, roasted chestnuts, and fresh vegetables. Following nature’s rules, here, nothing is wasted. We recover all resources, embrace circular economy principles, and find joy in simple pleasures like stargazing and tuning into the universe, learning from the fire, the trees, and the ocean. Just 10 minutes from the house lies the natural park, offering hundreds of kilometres of nature trails. Some days, we visit the beautiful beaches nearby or go chestnut gathering in the forest, maybe even enjoying a moonlit walk for unforgettable exp

name: Volunteer on a Permaculture Farm (Spain)

location.city: Dirty Hands. Playful and honest — perfect for folks
location.address: Slow Life, Dirty Hands. Playful and honest — perfect for folks

description: 🌿 Slow Life, Dirty Hands. Playful and honest—perfect for folks craving real work and real joy. Welcome to our small permaculture homestead tucked away in rural Spain, where days are shaped by the sun, the soil, and the shared rhythm of slow, intentional living. We’re a multicultural, story-rich family with plenty of dirt under our nails—and even more life in our hearts. This isn’t a retreat—it’s a place to show up, dive in, and grow through doing. We garden, build, compost, cook, care, and learn together. Depending on the season, you might help plant vegetables, repair fences, feed chickens, harvest food, create natural spaces, or prepare family meals from scratch. You might also find yourself dancing in the kitchen, fixing a leaky hose, or swapping stories around a fire with people from all over the world. We live in a historic house surrounded by gardens, apple, pear, almond, and fig trees—and a lot of peace and quiet. The land isn’t perfect, and neither are we—but we’re here with open hearts, playful energy, and a deep belief that real connection happens when people slow down and get their hands dirty. Whether you’re new to farm life, dreaming of your own future homestead, or simply need to reconnect with something real and grounded, there’s a place for you here. We love sharing what we’ve learned—and we’re always learning more. We value curiosity, honesty, good questions, and small daily acts of care. What we offer isn’t luxury, but it is nourishing: – A cozy room in the warehouse or a seasonal tent spot – Home-cooked meals shared as a family – Time to rest, reflect, or roam the land – Laughter, music, storytelling, and the occasional philosophical rabbit hole You won’t just “help”—you’ll become part of the rhythm. And in return, we hope you leave with new skills, clearer purpose, and a few great stories of your own. So if you’re ready to live slow, work with your hands, and be part of something a little muddy and a lot meaningful—pack your boots and come grow w

name: Join Our Hostel/B&B as a Volunteer (Florida)
description: We are a pirate-themed inn located in the historic city of St. Augustine, Florida. Working hours are from 9:30 am to noon, usually for about 2 hours. After 12 pm, staff are free to explore with our list of free VIP attraction access or find employment. Stays here typically range from 1 to 3 months. Duties include housekeeping work, such as changing beds and cleaning toilets. Our ideal work trader can learn quickly, is responsible and reliable, and is positive and polite with all guests, their children, and other staff members. We prefer individuals who have lived in group settings before, know the difference between a mop and a broom, and clean up after themselves. The Pirate Haus is a small hostel/B&B located in charming historic downtown St. Augustine, Florida. We have six private rooms and one hostel-style dorm room in our second-story hotel. Staff share a bunk room and have access to a fully equipped kitchen, always stocked with pancakes and spaghetti, a common area with a television (Netflix), a rooftop patio, and free Wi-Fi.

name: Join Us at Our Nature Adventure Camp as a Volunteer (Wales)
description: Our family-run centre has a friendly team of 4, which grows to up to 20 staff and volunteers in the summer, when we can host up to 50 guests. We work hard to provide nature-based adventure camps for teenagers from around the world, with a strong focus on personal development. We offer a variety of roles, from camp counsellors to gardeners, and match each volunteer with a job they enjoy. Regardless of the role, you’ll have the chance to explore the stunning Welsh landscape and participate in activities like climbing, caving, kayaking, archery, and more. Our staff house fosters a communal living environment with plenty of social time, adventures, board games, and meaningful conversations. Since our inception in 2017, we’ve had a long history of welcoming volunteers, some of whom have stayed with us for over five years.

name: Help Create a Space for Youth (Burundi)
description: At Sonship Burundi, our Youth Base regularly welcomes young people and adults from a variety of backgrounds, many of whom face significant challenges. The base provides a safe and intentional space to listen, rest, relax, learn, and experience the love of Christ. Here, we write and share stories, support one another, and heal together. We work with youth recovering from drug use, sexual abuse, abandonment, and other life challenges. We prayerfully seek warm and dynamic volunteers who are passionate about serving those who cannot give anything in return, and who are willing to join us at any time throughout the year.

name: Love Animals? Volunteer at our Animal Sanctuary (northern Spain)
description: Help us take care of 130 animals of 15 different species and enjoy a great experience. We take care of the animals all day. Never more than 7 hours of work. The work goes from (early) morning to night, although there is always room for your own activities. You will have your free time. The work is exhausting some days but you'll have energy left over to read books or spend downtime. We continue to improve our organizational processes and the help changes with the needs of the animals. The main tasks are animal care, including manure cleanup, cleaning of stables, paddocks and outside areas, gardening and lots of support tasks for our sanctuary manager. The animals feel safe and loved here, so you NEED to be an animal lover here. We will also have to work in the volunteer house keeping the common spaces clean and cooking when we have to do it.

name: Help Restore an Olive Grove and Medieval House (Italy)
description: Hi everyone, We live in a very peaceful corner of the world, yet still close to bustling cities like Monte-Carlo and Nice. We are a family of three restoring an ancient olive grove, creating a small rose garden, and renovating a medieval house in the Italian mountains right on the border with France. What we offer is your own small apartment with a balcony and private entrance, kilometres of hiking trails, and the possibility to bring friends and/or family (up to four people). We are 15 km from the nearest beach, 30 minutes from Monaco, and 1 hour from Nice and the airport. Dogs and cats are welcome and loved here; other pets can be discussed. We are looking for someone strong, as there will be plenty of physical work (carrying stones, sacks of earth, building walls, etc.). If you have a car to help transport materials, even better. Work is until midday; the rest of the day is free to explore the area. (Please note: in summer, the temperatures increase fast: working hours will be shorter) Places to visit include the Hanbury Botanical Gardens, Sanremo, Dolceacqua (painted by Monet), and many more beautiful sites.

name: House-Sitter needed for my Off Grid Home (Spain)

location.address: House-Sitter needed for my Off Grid Home in Spain, Europe
location.country: House-Sitter needed for my Off Grid Home in Spain

description: House-Sitter needed for my Off Grid Home in Spain. I just look for some responsible and nature lover who will take care and give water to my plants and trees. But I m looking during all year so do not hesitate to contact me and leave your phone and mail so that I can call you.

name: Volunteer with Rescued Horses in Mallorca (Spain)
description: My mother and I are looking for a volunteer – either an individual, a couple, or friends – to help us at our horse rescue yard. We care for around 20 horses, from yearlings to retirees, and need support with their daily care. Tasks include feeding, cleaning paddocks, brushing and showering the horses, as well as a bit of housework. Riding is available in your free time, with lessons and hacks provided. We ride bitless, and all our horses are barefoot. We are looking for someone who can help 5–6 hours a day, 5 days a week, with a few hours in the morning and a few in the afternoon. We offer two sleeping options: a room with a double bed inside our house, or a very cosy wooden tiny house with a double bed (both options share a bathroom). Food is provided. A driving licence would be helpful. Alongside the horses, we also share our home with dogs, cats, chickens, a pet pig, and a mule.

name: Live on a Vineyard and Learn the Art of Wine Production (Italy)
description: I live with my father and mother in a large modern house in Gavi, nestled in the middle of a vineyard. We share our home with 2 dogs and 1 cat – we are a close-knit family. We are an Italian countryside family striving to live in direct contact with nature through an eco-sustainable lifestyle. We generate our electricity using solar panels and produce hot water from solar panels in summer, and from wood-fired stoves and heaters in winter, ensuring all our energy comes from our property. Additionally, we produce wine from the ground to the bottle, with visitors able to witness every step of the process. We barter our wine with other local families for items we need, and also produce wine for our family and the local community. Most of the work involves tending to the fields, as we aim to minimise our impact on nature by utilising natural human energy and reducing machinery use. In the winery, the process is natural, taking place in barrels during winter using traditional methods enhanced by a few modern technologies. We operate a self-sustaining community in the countryside, offering helpers the chance to experience Italian rural life and learn about our culture. We teach Italian language, cooking, local history, and the art of winemaking. We strive to ensure our helpers feel like part of the family, sharing both responsibilities and pleasures during their stay. We need assistance with plant care, including pruning, tying, and harvesting grapes, as well as tasks in the winery such as wine production, bottling, and labelling. Helpers will also help gather wood for stoves and heaters, and may assist with building fences and digging ditches.

name: Bring Hope to Children Through Education & Care (Uganda)
description: My Shepherd Ministry Uganda is a Christian non-profit organisation dedicated to serving vulnerable children, empowering young people, and supporting families in rural communities through education, discipleship, and community development. Our ministry operates in Uganda with a vision of sharing the love of Jesus Christ while creating opportunities for children and families to thrive. Through our school, community outreach programmes, and church activities, we provide education, spiritual guidance, and practical support to those in need. We welcome volunteers from around the world who are passionate about making a positive impact. Volunteers can assist with teaching, mentoring children, organising sports and recreational activities, supporting church and youth programmes, helping with administration, photography, social media, and participating in community outreach projects. At My Shepherd Ministry, volunteers become part of our ministry family. We value cultural exchange and believe that both volunteers and local community members benefit from sharing experiences, skills, and perspectives. Volunteers have the opportunity to learn about Ugandan culture, traditions, and daily life while contributing their talents to meaningful projects. Our environment is peaceful, friendly, and community-centred. We strive to provide a safe and welcoming experience for volunteers who desire to serve, learn, and grow spiritually while making a lasting difference in the lives of children and families. Whether you are a teacher, student, healthcare worker, creative professional, ministry worker, or simply someone with a heart to serve, your contribution can help us continue our mission of bringing hope, education, and God's love to our community. If you are looking for a genuine cultural exchange, meaningful service, and personal growth, we are the perfect place for your volunteering journey. We look forward to welcoming volunteers who are willing to share their time, skills, and compa

name: Hands-On Gardening, Art, and Wildlife Conservation (England)

location.address: Hands-On Gardening, Art, and Wildlife Conservation in Lancashire

description: I am a former university lecturer in Chemistry. After retirement, I pursued a degree in Ceramics and Design. I am privileged to own an expansive, multi-level garden, some parts of which are well-tended, while others are much less so. This garden has been developed over many years with the aim of creating a place that delights visitors and helpers alike, featuring both plants and quirky structures. These include an underground grotto dedicated to alchemy and a suspension bridge across the River Darwen, which flows through the property. From the bridge, you can climb up to Rapunzel’s Tower for a commanding view. Wildlife preservation is at the heart of how I garden. My observation is that the countryside around me is now an ecological desert, dominated by monocultures. Only a fraction of the life that once thrived here remains. I feel that my garden should be as biologically diverse as possible, and I am always keen to increase the number of species it supports. I am looking at adding new nesting boxes for owls, swifts, and hedgehogs, alongside the bats—we have the largest population of pipistrelles in Lancashire, around 450—and also two jackdaws that nest with us. The pond already supports a large range of insects and amphibian life. I have only two beehives, as I do not want them to be in too much competition with wild bees. Given the state of many rivers in the UK, I am fortunate that the Darwen is in reasonably good health. Kingfishers cannot breed on my patch, as the banks are too rocky, but we frequently see them fishing up and down the river. Just upstream, there is a population of otters, and I have occasionally spotted them along my bank. To help with my projects, I have two free-standing, fully equipped workshops for pottery and woodworking.

name: Empower Through Compassion: Volunteer With Us (Uganda)
description: Kalikwani Children's Support Centre (KCSC) formly known as Kalikwani  Children's  Foundation is an established non-profit charitable foundation located in Uganda, specifically in Kamuli Municipality, Southern Division, Busota Ward, Asadhawaire Cell. At the core of our foundation lies a deeply personal story of resilience and determination. The founders, myself and my younger brother, experienced the loss of our parents at the tender ages of 12 and 8, respectively. Left with a void that was not easily filled, we faced numerous challenges and uncertainties, making our dreams seem unattainable. However, our big brother’s selfless sacrifice of his own education became our beacon of support, inspiring us to make a difference in the lives of other children facing similar obstacles. Motivated by our challenging personal journey and a profound sense of gratitude, my little brother and I embarked on a mission to establish the Kalikwani Children's Foundation which was later transformed to Kalikwani Children's Support Centre. With unwavering commitment and an unyielding spirit, our goal is to provide hope and empowerment to teenagers, vulnerable children, and those facing homelessness aged between 1 and 17 years. In terms of accommodation, we offer single private rooms to our visitors, each equipped with a bed and a mosquito net. We also provide meals, giving you the opportunity to share and enjoy local sweet dishes with us. Kalikwani Children's Support Centre is dedicated to bringing hope into children’s lives by facilitating formal education and imparting various life skills. We are actively seeking volunteers who can assist us in different capacities, including community sensitization, teaching, and skill development for the children, aiming to restore hope and bring peace to their hearts and minds. You could revise it to: If you wish to bring a gift, our current priority needs are electronic equipment that can support learning and administration both at our school and at o

name: Hands-On Learning Through Fair Tourism (Nepal)
description: Namaste and welcome! We invite travellers to come and experience Nepal in our beautiful guest house in Dhulikhel. It is located in a picturesque area of Nepal, with scenic mountain views and a traditional town atmosphere. We practise fair tourism and support rural communities through organic farming and the improvement of local living standards. We are more than happy to show you what a peaceful day in Nepal looks like, surrounded by rich flora and fauna. Our guest house is run by family members, and we offer volunteers the opportunity to learn more about Nepal and Nepali culture. We organise visits to rural villages and provide insight into local agriculture. At our guest house, you can practise yoga, meditation, and healing therapies. We also provide healing treatments offered by our experienced healing practitioner. Our volunteers work 3–4 hours a day, from Sunday to Thursday. Fridays and Saturdays are free to explore—enjoy village hikes, experience local life in Dhulikhel, or visit Kathmandu and Bhaktapur to explore ancient monuments.

name: Help an NGO Support Our Community with Projects (Tanzania)
description: Kyosei Foundation runs an English-medium day school in Arusha, Tanzania, dedicated to educating vulnerable children aged 3 to 15. Unlike public schools that teach in Swahili, we offer full English immersion — preparing students for secondary education and a brighter future. Our school, Kyosei Pre & Primary School, is an officially licensed English-medium day school offering full English immersion to vulnerable children aged 3–15. The school follows Tanzania's six-year primary programme, including national exams at grades 4 and 6, and is affiliated with MoEST, NECTA, Arusha City Council, and TIE. Ways to get involved: - Teaching & mentoring: English, French & computer lessons for vulnerable children - Farming & sustainability: Support our school farm, seedlings & permaculture programme - Sports & leadership: Football coaching & youth development for ages 6–17 - Build & develop: School construction & infrastructure improvements - Girls' Hope Initiative: Menstrual hygiene workshops & mentorship for schoolgirls - Fundraising & advocacy: Grant writing, outreach & social impact campaigns You will stay with our host family (a warm mama and four children!) in a comfortable homestay 15 minutes from the school and farm. Three meals a day are included, and yes, we will teach you to cook Tanzanian food too! 🍲 Free time: Visit Kilimanjaro & Meru trekking, Serengeti & Ngorongoro Safaris, Chemka Hot Springs, Maasai markets, and the best home-cooked Tanzanian food you will ever taste. Core team members & volunteer support - Director & Founder · mission lead · volunteer welcome & oversight - Outreach Coordinator:	First point of contact · scheduling · placement coordination · daily support - Chef/ Host: homestay host · 3 meals/day · cultural exchange · laundry service (fee) - Local driver:	24/7 airport transfers · day trips · safe transport around Arusha - Professional Guides: Safari · Kilimanjaro · cultural tours · Maasai village · excursion planning - School teachers: Daily classro

name: Practising English with Adults (Vietnam)
description: At English Leader, we believe that English is more than just a language; it is a powerful key that unlocks doors to opportunity, understanding, and personal growth. We were founded with a single purpose: to be a dedicated companion on your journey to mastering English. We offer heartfelt support that goes beyond traditional teaching. We listen and help you break down language barriers, empowering everyone to express themselves confidently in a global environment. Every lesson at English Leader is designed for practical application, helping learners improve their career performance and enrich their daily lives. By making English accessible, we aim to foster a knowledgeable community where every individual can connect globally and contribute positively to society.

name: Join the Journey of Sustainable Serenity in Cancun (Mexico)
description: I’m creating an oasis in Cancun—a sanctuary where organic farming, spiritual healing, yoga, and serene accommodations come together. As I embark on this retirement adventure, I envision a tranquil haven that blends sustainable living with heartfelt hospitality. My dream is more than just building a place; it’s about nurturing a community rooted in nature’s embrace and shared wisdom. I seek volunteer advice and insights to help harmonise spirituality with sustainable practices, cultivating not only the land but a collective spirit. With open arms, I invite kindred souls to join this sacred journey—learning, growing, and sharing as we create a mindful oasis in the heart of Cancun, where peace and purpose flourish together.

name: Care for Our Animals and Help Uphold Our Property (Florida)
description: We are now offering co-working, co-housing, and co-growing chill-out spaces in Naples, Florida. This unique opportunity allows you to experience a sharing, caring, and growing open-source lifestyle in an all-natural, sober, whimsical environment that fosters high vibrations and networking opportunities. Our property is ideally located for professionals, digital nomads, remote workers, teachers, entrepreneurs, laptop CEOs, students, artists, visionaries, dreamers, creators, inventors, healers, conscious individuals, and travelling business professionals. We provide a flexible space for you to live, work, and grow while craving a high-energy experience and environment on demand. What makes a great candidate? You should possess excellent communication skills, a positive attitude, and the ability to live and work in shared accommodations. You must be open to giving and receiving constructive feedback and willing to dedicate time to reading, writing, brainstorming, and engaging with videos and masterclasses focused on self-development, financial independence, and cultivating good habits. Great gardening skills and the ability to grow organic food are essential, as is a readiness to embrace an 18GGX lifestyle. Extras included with your stay at our property: You will enjoy a variety of complimentary amenities, including free fruits and vegetables (we visit Immokalee once a week to gather seasonal fresh produce), canned goods, and basic food items such as pasta, rice, beans, bread, cheese, and more. Free use of a washer and dryer, including laundry detergent, is also provided. Additional benefits include free secure shaded parking on a gated property, fully furnished accommodation (including sheets, blankets, towels, pillows, kitchenware, toilet paper, and basic toiletries), decorative positive affirmations and vision board inspirations, and free Wi-Fi, water, sewer, and rubbish disposal.

name: Volunteering at Our Retreat Center with Rescue Horses (Canada)
description: Epona Rise Retreat Center is an 80-acre retreat centre and home to 27 rescue horses. Our mission is to create inspiring experiences for our guests, empower healing work with horses, and support our rescue herd. We are located in the beautiful mountains of British Columbia. Our volunteers help with the daily chores involving our herd of horses, who live outside all year round. We require all volunteers to have previous horse experience. Daily tasks include feeding the horses in the mornings and afternoons, holding horses for our farrier while she works, grooming horses, feeding the chickens and ducks, and assisting with other general and seasonal ranch duties. Our help-stayers also assist with light cleaning in the lodge and cabins. When we host retreats, from April to October, volunteers are asked to heat and serve catering for our guests, so a basic knowledge of food preparation and some confidence in the kitchen are an asset. We provide accommodation and food in exchange for volunteer work. We host up to three work-stay volunteers each month. The schedule is four to five hours per day, five days a week. Each work-stay volunteer has a private room with a private bathroom, and shares a kitchen and lounge in a private apartment above the main lodge. There are plenty of hiking opportunities in the summer, and a ski hill is just fifteen minutes away.

name: Join our B&B Guest House as a Volunteer (Italy)
description: Our B&B is a charming traditional house in the heart of the historic center of Giuliano di Roma, in Ciociaria. Giuliano di Roma is a village located on the crater of an extinct volcano and the name La Preta Nera derives from the lava stone with which the territory is scattered. Just 90 km from Rome, in an area of ​​rolling hills and valleys. La Preta Nera is a place that will allow you to live your holidays in a natural environment and in contact with the inhabitants of a small lively village. Giuliano, a medieval village with characteristic corners and alleys that have kept their history, immersed in local traditions and surrounded by nature. Volunteers will be staying and sleeping at La Preta Nera B&B, they'll have their own room with private bathroom, and a wage of 200€ monthly. There is a kitchen that can be used freely, and the B&B is located in the centre of Giuliano di Roma, so it is a very good and comfortable accommodation to reach everything on foot in the village. We ask fot a minimum stay of 3 months.

name: Volunteer on an Off-Grid Nature Farm (Costa Rica)
description: At The Jungle Hub, you will experience a unique opportunity to live immersed in the Costa Rican jungle while contributing to a regenerative and creative community project surrounded by nature, mountains, rivers, and abundant wildlife. We are an off-grid retreat and permaculture-inspired space focused on sustainability, nature connection, creativity, music, spirituality, and meaningful human experiences. Our volunteers become part of a small international community where we collaborate, share meals, learn from one another, and co-create a peaceful environment for both visitors and residents. Your main area of support will be in the kitchen, including washing dishes and helping to prepare meals for up to 8 people, shared with other volunteers. Depending on current needs and your skills, you may also help with: 🔹 Gardening and land regeneration 🔹 Permaculture and food forest projects 🔹Construction and maintenance 🔹Artistic and creative projects 🔹 Hospitality and retreat preparation 🔹Cleaning and organisation 🔹 Social media, photography, or music-related projects This experience is ideal for people seeking more than conventional tourism. Many volunteers come here to disconnect from city life, reconnect with nature, learn practical skills, reflect on life, explore creativity, and experience a slower, more intentional way of living. What makes The Jungle Hub special is the atmosphere we create together. Beyond the work itself, there is space for deep conversations, music, meditation, campfires, river walks, shared meals, beautiful sunsets, and authentic community living. We especially appreciate volunteers who are independent, proactive, adaptable, respectful, emotionally mature, and genuinely interested in contributing to a positive group dynamic. In exchange for your contribution, you will enjoy: 🔹 Private accommodation immersed in nature 🔹Healthy and abundant shared meals (farm-to-table) 🔹Access to rivers, trails, and jungle landscapes 🔹A peaceful and inspiring environ

name: Experience Castle Life – Estate & Hospitality Help Needed (Ireland)
description: Lough Cutra Castle is both a family home and a unique hospitality venue, set within beautiful grounds and lakeside surroundings. One role will focus on interiors—housekeeping, day-to-day maintenance, and supporting hospitality when the castle hosts guests and events. The second role will focus on the estate and grounds, working alongside skilled professionals to help care for and maintain the outdoor spaces. In return, you'll enjoy private accommodation, including a double room with lake views, a private bathroom, a separate entrance, and access to your own kitchen facilities. Food is provided, and a modest remuneration package may be available depending on skills and experience. You'll also help care for our dogs when the owners are away. This is a great opportunity to experience life in an Irish castle, learn new skills, meet interesting people, and enjoy a memorable adventure together.

name: Join us as a Volunteer at our Multicultural Hostel in Tamarindo (Costa Rica)
description: Volunteer at a Multicultural Hostel steps away from the waves in Tamarindo, Costa Rica. We are conveniently located just 5 minutes from downtown and Tamarindo beach, which is renowned as one of Costa Rica's most beautiful beaches. Tamarindo offers a unique experience where you can witness breathtaking sunsets, embrace an endless summer lifestyle, indulge in swimming and surfing at one of our country's favorite surf spots, and connect with amazing people from around the world. It's an opportunity to practice different languages and forge friendships that transcend borders. As a volunteer, you will be expected to contribute 20 hours per week, working 4 hours a day with two days off. The minimum stay requirement is 1 month or longer, and while basic Spanish is preferred, a positive attitude, a collaborative spirit, and good performance are essential. In return, you will receive free accommodation in the Fan-room for volunteers, equipped with a private locker (bring your lock), access to common facilities, and a shared kitchen.

name: Winter 2025/26 Chalet Host & Digital Nomad Opportunity (Italy)
description: We’re preparing for an exciting winter 2025/26 season at Chalet Baita Zelda in Trepalle, Livigno, Italy, and we’re now recruiting two chalet hosts to join our small team from 1 December 2025 until the end of April 2026, with a possible extension into the first week of May. This coming winter will be especially dynamic, as Livigno will host major activities during the Winter Olympics in February 2026 and the Paralympics in March 2026. We expect a busy season and are therefore looking for two reliable and motivated hosts, ideally a couple or two friends, although individual applicants who are happy to share the work and accommodation are equally welcome. You will help ensure the smooth running of Chalet Baita Zelda, a family-run property in Trepalle, located above Livigno, which includes five self-contained apartments and one small apartment in Teola, close to Livigno’s centre. Your main responsibilities will include welcoming guests during check-ins and check-outs, cleaning and preparing apartments between stays, washing and organising linens and towels, carrying out simple maintenance and tidying tasks, coordinating with management for arrivals and schedules, and keeping digital records up to date. Basic DIY skills are a bonus. Working hours are part-time, usually mornings and evenings, averaging five to six hours per day. Solo applicants will work five days per week, while couples or joint applicants can expect to work six days per week. The position combines hands-on chalet duties with light digital reporting tasks, creating a balanced and rewarding role. You will receive fair seasonal pay and free on-site accommodation. A free ski pass for the entire winter season is also provided, whether you apply alone or as a couple. The accommodation includes access to a kitchenette, Wi-Fi, and garage or ski storage facilities. Accommodation is shared or dormitory-style, comfortably heated, and equipped with a kitchenette and bathroom. Trepalle is a peaceful hamlet above Liv

name: Be Part of Our Guesthouse: Join as a Volunteer (northwestern Italy)
description: The tasks required are those for a happy and joyful welcome of our guests who come from all over the world. Good knowledge of the English language is required. The goal is to make our guests feel at home. You will help take care of the reception of our guests, help them check in and check out, and inform them about what they can and cannot do -- the attractions and places of interest. We live in a paradise in the beautiful Liguria region that hosts the 5 Terre, and is 25 km from the enchanting Principality of Montecarlo. You will have the opportunity to get to know the authenticity of this part of Italy just a few kilometers from the Côte d'Azur. A region bathed by the splendid Ligurian sea, often classified and cataloged as the best place in Italy and protected by the Alps -- the Maritime Alps. The opportunity to have sea and mountains in the same land, something unique.

name: Volunteer Supporting Children and Caregivers (Tanzania)
description: Volunteers will have the opportunity to share their experiences with children and the wider community, helping to change mindsets about development. They will be able to teach English at primary and secondary schools located close to their accommodation. In addition, they will participate in class sessions with OVCT members, discussing various aspects of the histories of different countries and important global issues. Kiswahili will also be taught to volunteers free of charge by some OVCT members. The volunteers’ accommodation is comfortable and includes beds with mattresses, pillows, sheets, blankets and mosquito nets, as well as access to electricity and the internet. There is a piped water supply in the house, so water availability is not an issue. The weather is generally pleasant and does not pose any problems. Hospitals and dispensaries are located close to the volunteers’ accommodation, and transport is readily available. A nutritious diet is provided, with a variety of fruits such as pawpaws, avocados, bananas, mangoes and sweet melons available at reasonable prices. Volunteers will enjoy learning about the cultures of the villages they visit, as well as climbing the impressive rock formations in the area and taking in the stunning views surrounding some of the villages where they will work with children and caregivers.

name: Assist teaching at a community nursery & primary school (Uganda)
description: We need volunteers of all types and are reaching out for volunteers to assist with CCM projects and to assist teaching at a community nursery and primary school. You don't necessarily have to be a certified teacher. We are looking for individuals with good parental skills and the ability to interact with young children of all different age groups, starting from 2 1/2 years to 13 years old. It is important for our volunteers to have the ability to create and sustain good working relationships with children and young adults. You should have good communication skills and able to speak some/fluent English. Other languages in addition to that are welcome. Our teachers will be assisting you in planning lessons, translating in class if needed and in any other school activities. (You will be working together with our staff). We also need volunteers who are skilled at computer and marketing, printing and designing brochures, banners, t-shirts and other marketing and promotional materials.

name: Volunteer at a Multilingual School in Cusco (Peru)

location.address: Multilingual School Volunteer Programme: Education, Creativity and

description: We are the first English-speaking kindergarten dedicated to nurturing bilingual children from non-English-speaking families, aiming to provide them with better opportunities for their future lives. In 2012, Peru ranked last among the 65 countries participating in the Programme for International Student Assessment (PISA), which tested the educational skills of 15-year-olds worldwide. By 2015, Peru made slight progress, ranking 63rd out of 69 nations. However, this remains a critical issue for Peruvian students, with only students from the Dominican Republic performing worse in Latin America. These results stem not only from secondary education but also from inadequate early education, which fails to equip children with the necessary tools to build self-esteem and improve academic performance. At our school, we strive to raise standards and provide children with the invaluable gift of a foreign language—specifically English. English not only enriches children’s lives by opening up opportunities but also fosters self-esteem and parental pride, inspiring continuous improvement. This impact will reverberate through society, expanding and growing over time. Our vision goes beyond the city limits. We have already organised summer classes for children in an Andean community, bringing these benefits to those who have never had such opportunities before. Our unique teaching method for pre-reader children is transforming the way knowledge is shared with children under 7 years old. We invite you to be part of this ambitious project, working with the most delightful and precious age group. Our approach is playful and interactive, so you will be engaging with the children in a fun and meaningful way. You will enjoy the time spent caring for these little ones, cherishing the laughter and unforgettable moments that come with helping them learn and grow. Don’t hesitate—join us in this exciting project. You’ll support daily routines, assist teachers, and help with activities and proj

name: Sun, Sea & Cats: Help Wanted for a Cool Online Project (Italy)

location.city: Sea & Cats in Southern Italy — Help Wanted for a Cool
location.address: Sun, Sea & Cats in Southern Italy — Help Wanted for a Cool

description: This is not a big, famous Italian city, and that is exactly the point. Policoro is quiet, simple, local, and very pleasant, especially in spring and summer. It is a seaside town with nearby beaches, a relaxed atmosphere, cafés, small shops, and a slower rhythm of life. If you are curious about the less touristy side of Italy—real towns, countryside, local people, and peaceful days—this could be a good place for you. I live in the countryside, in a calm area with a nice garden and fruit trees. The house is large, and I share my space with cats, so it is very important that you genuinely like cats. Sometimes, if I am away for a few hours, I may ask you to feed them or check that they are okay. The help I need is light and practical: a little help around the house, simple daily tasks, and some support with internet research for my work. The online research part is very important for me. I work on independent creative projects, and I need someone who is comfortable using the internet, searching for information, organising simple lists, and helping me build connections online in a natural and social way. This stay would suit someone who is responsible, independent, kind, and comfortable in quiet places. I am especially looking for volunteers from the United States, because part of the online research is connected to the American market, and I need someone who understands American culture, language, and online habits. You do not need special professional skills, but you should be from or currently living in the United States, be comfortable doing internet research, be organised and reliable, and love cats. This is not a party place and not a big-city experience. It is a calm stay in Southern Italy, close to the sea, with a mix of light household help, cats, a garden, countryside life, and online work. In your free time, you can go to the beach, explore Policoro, visit nearby towns, enjoy local food, walk, relax, read, or simply experience a part of Italy that many tourist

name: Help Out at a Spiritual Retreat Centre in Nature (Scotland)
description: Lendrick Lodge is a spiritual retreat centre for transformation. Vicky and Stephen teach Reiki and Shamanism with the heartfelt mission of empowering others to live their most soul-shining life. Volunteers seeking a peaceful place to be of service and connect with heart-centred people will thrive at Lendrick. There are many scenic walks nearby, taking you through forests, to lochs, and along mountain trails. Many of our volunteers brave the cold waters of the nearby lochs or enjoy a refreshing dip in the magical Goddess Pool. We host firewalks, sweat lodges, Tai Chi classes, and offer Aikido for those staying longer term. The people drawn to Lendrick are light-hearted souls on a spiritual path. Many meet their soul family here and form lifelong friendships. The land itself holds a beautiful, nurturing energy – perfect for deepening your spiritual practice. For those drawn to meditation, we have a dedicated meditation room, along with inspiring books and oracle cards to enjoy. Visitors often leave calling Lendrick Lodge their second home. There is a vibrant spirit of giving and receiving here, rooted in guiding people closer to the heart of the Beloved. We are looking for volunteers who wish to contribute to a centre dedicated to personal development and healing. Volunteer tasks include preparing food, cleaning the centre, chopping firewood, and supporting guest needs. Every action helps create a safe, enriching space for your spiritual family here at Lendrick.

name: Enjoy a Study Holiday - Student Residential Placement (England)
description: We have hosted over 29 students from 12 countries. It's a friendly, mellow, rural atmosphere: to explore England, learn skills and enrich your outlook. Study activities include: horticulture, heritage, painting, wildlife, landscaping, crafts, conservation. Residential placements for male students age 18-28 (female accommodation dependent on local host families). And since this is a study holiday you can also be flexible in your timetable to have (for example) 3 or 4+ days long weekends away for private excursions,/visiting friends etc. WHAT WE DO: Receive visiting groups; teach horticulture & conservation; make videos; skill training for the unemployed, resource for adults with learning difficulties. Linked to 7 British universities for student conservation research articles, and for making conservation videos. TUITION: No previous experience or skills required as tuition provided. The purpose of our Trust (registered charity number 1174239) is the advancement of education in horticulture and conservation. We're a registered charity trust working for the advancement of education in horticulture and conservation.

name: Volunteer Caring for Horses and Other Animals (Austria)
description: We are looking for YOU – flexible young people who want to live and care for horses and other animals. We offer you the chance to live in harmony with horses and nature. We are an association, and our work focuses on horse riding-assisted education and therapeutic riding. We have about 3 hectares of pasture where all of our four-legged friends can be found. From our goats, we get milk and produce other goat products. Our new members in the stable are 3 donkeys, used for therapeutic riding lessons. You can learn about horse therapy, participate in training the horses, ride horses yourself or learn to ride, milk the goats, mow the meadow and make hay, muck out the barn, and help restore our historic courtyard. We are located approximately 60 km from Vienna. You can visit Vienna’s cultural scene, attend several events, or go sightseeing to historical places, old castles, or the many typical vineyards around our farm.

name: Become a Volunteer: Help Improve Animal Welfare (Caribbean)

location.address: Saint Vincent And The Grenadines, Island of Mayreau

description: We are a non-profit organisation dedicated to improving the lives of neglected, mistreated, and abandoned animals on the small island of Mayreau, located in the Southern Grenadines of St Vincent & the Grenadines. Our small team of highly committed volunteers supports the welfare of all animals on the island, including dogs, cats, goats, sheep, and any wildlife in need. We rescue, rehabilitate, and provide essential healthcare and medication, including parasite control, for island dogs. Many dogs are tied up 24/7, and with the permission of their owners, we walk them daily. We also encourage owners to have their dogs and cats sterilised and assist in facilitating this process. Through action, education, and leading by example, we strive to promote the health and wellbeing of all animals. We are seeking fit, energetic, easy-going, motivated, and reliable volunteers who will truly love and care for our rescue dogs and cats, as well as all island animals. This includes approximately 80 island dogs, goats, sheep, cats, and other animals in need. Volunteers should be willing to go the extra mile to make a real difference in their lives.

name: Care for Dogs and Enjoy a Stay in Muskoka Region (Canada)
description: We cherish the energy and creativity that helpers bring to our team. We're looking for passionate, hardworking individuals eager to dive into the rhythms of our unique, cage-free dog resort while enjoying the stunning Muskoka region. Whether you’re an outdoor enthusiast or someone who thrives in contributing to the care and upkeep of the resort, you’ll find a welcoming place here. Since 1996, we’ve been building a haven for dogs in Muskoka’s beautiful parklands, complete with fully fenced areas and spring-fed ponds. Helpers from around the world have been part of our journey since 1999, enriching our work with cultural exchange and diverse experiences. We invite people ready to grow, embrace new challenges, and thrive outdoors—no matter the season! Explore Muskoka’s Beauty Located just 15 minutes from Arrowhead Provincial Park and 45 minutes from Algonquin Provincial Park, our resort is surrounded by breathtaking trails, wildlife, and over 1,000 lakes perfect for hiking, fishing, canoeing, or simply enjoying nature. For a mix of nature and culture, we’re only 6km from Huntsville, where you can explore charming shops, restaurants, and year-round festivals. A Taste of Home Meals are an important part of our day. We take pride in preparing nutritious, delicious meals using local ingredients. You’ll enjoy breakfast, lunch, and snacks at your leisure, with an early evening dinner shared together. If you have a favourite recipe, we’d love to try it—food is one of the best ways we connect! Comfortable Accommodations You’ll stay in a private, bachelor-style apartment within our charming old brick farmhouse, equipped with air conditioning, kitchen appliances, and private bathrooms. Many helpers call our beds some of the most comfortable they’ve slept on! We also have a communal building with workout equipment, games, and a cozy space to relax. Your Role You’ll work four days a week, starting at 9 a.m. and finishing around 3 or 3:30 p.m., with three full days off to explore M

name: Conservation, Farming & Beach Life in Drake Bay (Costa Rica)

location.city: Farming & Beach Life in Drake Bay
location.address: Conservation, Farming & Beach Life in Drake Bay

description: We are a family-run project with three members, established in 2010, located on the Osa Peninsula in Drake Bay, in the town of Progreso de Drake. We work in conservation and agriculture on a farm of approximately 8 hectares. We use the land for farming and to learn more about nature. We’re only a 10-minute walk from the beach, where we play volleyball three days a week. During the rainy season, we can surf and fish. We’re also very close to Corcovado National Park and Caño Island. It’s a place surrounded by abundant nature and beautiful beaches. Is there a cost to volunteer? Yes, each volunteer pays a daily fee of $25 per person. This money helps us provide low-income individuals with a small daily meal, as well as purchase necessary items for the project, such as tools and other supplies. What benefits will volunteers receive? You will receive three meals a day: breakfast, lunch, and dinner. We can accommodate vegetarian volunteers, so there’s no need to worry about the food — it will undoubtedly be delicious. Each volunteer will stay in shared rooms with other volunteers and will have access to bedding, shared bathroom and shower facilities, bath towels, 24-hour Wi-Fi, electricity, and some free tours. Other paid activities are also available for you to enjoy in your free time. What can you do in your free time? You can go hiking, play volleyball or football, surf, fish, dive, snorkel, take horse-riding tours, ATV tours, visit Corcovado National Park, or visit Caño Island.

name: Peaceful Bay Area Home with Dogs and Creative Projects (California)

location.address: United States, San Francisco Bay Area

description: I am located in the East Bay Hills of California, USA, and I’m seeking volunteers for a minimum stay of 2 weeks, with a 3–5 day trial period. Volunteers will work approximately 25–30 hours a week with a flexible schedule. I offer a serene mountaintop home with breathtaking views of the Bay Area, and the location is close to San Francisco, Napa, Sonoma, Oakland, and Berkeley. You’ll have access to forest hiking trails and a peaceful environment, perfect for work or writing. You’ll also have the chance to care for big, affectionate dogs and contribute to meaningful, mindful, and creative work. The space is cosy and supportive, providing an opportunity to recharge and reflect. Your main tasks will include daily dog walking and care, with flexibility for morning or evening schedules. You will also assist with general upkeep of shared spaces. Depending on your skills and interests, you might help with handyman tasks, light repairs, gardening, website management, social media, video editing, writing, organising, cleaning, event preparation, and personal assistant-style duties. I’m an entrepreneur originally from New York and based in the Bay Area. I’m passionate about animal rescue, art, music, and community service. I’m also the founder of a mindful education and ed-tech company and an active volunteer with a creative spirit. You can learn more about me at citytestprep.com/gateway. In terms of house culture and expectations, I ask volunteers to keep shared spaces clean, as this is a home, not a hostel. Smoking, drugs, and loud partying are not allowed, and no guests are permitted at the house. Volunteers should clean up after themselves in the kitchen, bathroom, and bedroom, and communicate clearly about tasks—what has been done, what hasn’t, and what still needs attention. Volunteers must speak English and have a SIM card for communication outside of Wi-Fi. Most importantly, I’m looking for people who love dogs, as they are central to this home. I’m looking for voluntee

name: Speak with Students in Exchange for Accommodation (Morocco)
description: Casablanca, Fes, and Berrechid offer you the chance to discover the exciting marvels Morocco has to offer. At one of our three language schools, where students come to learn English and French, you’ll have the opportunity to immerse yourself in the culture of this vibrant society. We host native and fluent speakers to provide our pupils with an immersive experience in English conversation. Additionally, our guests are offered an enjoyable experience with real Moroccan families, traditions, and customs. Volunteers will have the chance to become familiar with life in Morocco and explore the Sahara Desert, the High Atlas Mountains, or the beautiful coastline. Take a trip to Ouarzazate to see the waterfalls, or enjoy a camel ride and witness the stars at night over the desert. The opportunities are as endless as the food is delicious. Our school is highly preferred by local students and greatly enjoyed by volunteers. In each of the three locations (Berrechid, Casablanca, and Fes), there is a shared apartment, so travellers can live with and share experiences with people from all over the world. Food is very inexpensive here, making budget travel attainable. The students are enthusiastic learners, eager to share their culture while also learning about others. We look forward to hosting you and hope to hear from you soon. Feel free to ask any questions about Islamic culture or life here in Morocco.

name: Speak English? Join Us in Running Our English Language Courses (Poland)
description: We run English language courses and are seeking English speakers to help our Polish participants practice and improve their language skills. There is no teaching involved, as the course consists of conversation sessions, group activities, games, and more. We provide accommodation throughout the course, along with all meals and return transport from Poznań. You'll stay for free at an incredible resort in Poland. We are currently looking for volunteers for the following dates: 2026: January 18-23 (Poznan) - Youth & Adult Group January 24-25 (Poznan) - Weekend Program February 08-13 (Poznan) - Youth & Adult Group February 15-20 (Poznan) - Youth & Adult Group March 22-27 (Poznan) - Adult Group March 28-29 (Poznan) - Weekend Program ..... April - June DATES WILL BE ADDED SOON..... July 19-24 (Poznan) - Youth&Junior Group August 09-14 (Poznan) - Adult Group August 16-21 (Poznan) - Youth&Junior Group September 27-October 02 (Poznan) - Adult Group October 03--28 (Poznan) - Weekend Program .....MORE DATES WILL BE ADDED SOON.....

name: Live with Locals & Speak English Every Day (Vietnam)
description: Enghouse is one of Vietnam’s leading English Homestay communities, where Vietnamese learners and international volunteers live, learn, and share daily life together in an English-speaking environment. Instead of learning English only in classrooms, our members practise English naturally throughout the day while cooking, eating, playing sports, joining discussions, travelling, and taking part in community activities. We currently have homestays in Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi, welcoming hundreds of learners every year.
