Our mission is to build an international community where language learning happens through real-life communication and meaningful cultural exchange.

name: Volunteer Teaching English to Children (Peru)
description: Come and volunteer with our non-profit organisation, Jar’kay, and help empower children to build a brighter future. We provide free English classes, surfing and sandboarding lessons, organise beach clean-ups, and run a variety of projects, events and activities that strengthen the community and protect the environment. Spend your days surfing the waves of northern Peru, try sandboarding on the nearby dunes, build meaningful relationships with local people and fellow volunteers, and use your skills and ideas to make a lasting impact. Volunteering with us gives you the opportunity to connect with children and the local community, meet volunteers from around the world, and experience a rich cultural exchange. Our programme is friendly, flexible and relaxed, allowing you to contribute in ways that match your interests and strengths while enjoying daily surfing, exciting outdoor activities and an unforgettable experience in Peru.

name: Volunteer to Practise English with Students (Ecuador)
description: In Santo Domingo, discover the heart of Ecuador — a city midway between the beaches and the Andes, with deep ancestral roots and a vibrant culinary tradition. We’re Verónica and Carlos Arciniega, siblings and founders of this educational project in Santo Domingo, Ecuador. Our initiative was created to help people aged 12 and above learn English, giving them the tools to expand their academic and professional opportunities. We’re a small team, which is why we need volunteers like you to support our students — especially by helping them practise conversation and improve their pronunciation. Volunteering at our learning centre not only allows you to make a real difference in the lives of our students, but also invites you to join a community that believes in education as a force for change. What you’ll do 🗣️ Language Exchange – Practise English conversation with our students 📚 Assist in Classes – Support English lessons with pronunciation and participation 🎲 Interactive Activities – Join or lead games, role-plays, or themed discussions 💬 Cultural Sharing – Share about your country, traditions, and experiences in relaxed sessions ⏰ Around 20–25 hours per week Join our team and be part of a meaningful journey — with purpose and heart!

name: Enrich Lives: Volunteer for Education and Community Development (Uganda)
description: Esther Links Children's Centre Organization is named after Esther, a child with Down syndrome. My wife Susan and I share a passion for restoring hope to children with Down syndrome, starting with Esther. These children are often unfairly regarded as useless, with no future. We are here to challenge that perception and affirm that they are as capable as any other children; they just need attention and support. As a result, Esther Links has initiated the following projects with the expected support outlined below: A. Education and Well-being Project: This project aims to change attitudes toward children with Down syndrome, empowering them through education and addressing the health and social-psychological effects of their living environment. Participants in this project will be able to: - Engage in singing rhymes with the children. - Assist them in learning how to read and write, providing special support. - Serve them breakfast and lunch. B. Empowering the Needy Project: This project focuses on transforming children's attitudes toward life through various activities, including: - Conducting home visits. - Organizing community walks in the evening. - Teaching children essential skills such as bathing and personal hygiene. - Instilling self-esteem in the children.

name: Gardening & General Maintenance in Crete (Greece)

location.address: Gardening and General Maintenance in Crete, Greece
location.country: Gardening and General Maintenance in Crete

description: We are a  Greek family run small business at crete island, Greece. We opened for the first time in 2 years ago and had a very positive experience working with volunteers. We are looking for  builders with experience to build an outdoor kitchen and a retaining wall We offer accommodation and salary from November to March 30hours a week of work. References available on request.

name: Experience Mindanao and Help Run Our Homestay (Philippines)
description: Casa Crespina is a family-run homestay in Barobo, Philippines, also known as a “Home with a Heart”. We are a warm and friendly family who enjoy meeting people from different cultures and sharing our way of life. The homestay is located in the centre of Barobo town, which offers a peaceful atmosphere and is close to the beach, cold springs, and local community areas. We welcome volunteers who are open-minded, helpful, and interested in experiencing authentic Filipino culture while contributing to our homestay or local community projects. You will have enough time to explore the natural attractions in the area, such as the islands, mountains, cold springs, and waterfalls, while helping us set up our homestay business. This includes supporting housekeeping and digital marketing, assisting on the farm, and engaging with local children through language exchange activities and non-formal education. Our place is also a great stopover before continuing your travels to either Siargao or Davao. You can fly or sail from Manila or Cebu.

name: Learn Permaculture & Organic Farming (Hawaii)
description: Aloha! Thank you for considering our farm. Our farm consists of two separate growing sites: a one-acre permaculture mini-farm (home site), which is a 30-year-old working permaculture system and food forest with many fruit trees, chickens, a fish pond, and some vegetable crops; and a separate three-acre organic production and training farm with an orchard. Both sites incorporate permaculture planning principles. The host completed the Permaculture Design Course (PDC) in 1993, has over 30 years of practical experience, and is also a permaculture teacher and practitioner. Apprentices will learn all aspects of organic growing in Hawaiʻi, as well as food preparation and permaculture practices, aquaculture, poultry care, and agroforestry. They will also be introduced to Polynesian foods and other useful and medicinal plants and their uses. We can accommodate up to four people at one time. The minimum stay is three months, and longer stays are preferred. Volunteers have access to produce grown on the farm, laundry (washer only), and wireless internet. Basic foodstuffs are provided, including farm produce. Meat and dairy are not provided, but volunteers are welcome to purchase and consume them at their own expense. A CV/resume with at least two work references and a completed application are required for consideration. Preferred stays are two to three months. Thank you very much for your time and consideration!

name: Heart of Atlas – Volunteer and Cultural Experience (Morocco)
description: Heart of Atlas is a local association in southeast Morocco working with children and Amazigh communities. Volunteers help with games, languages, activities, and cultural exchange while discovering authentic village life, Moroccan culture, music, and desert experiences near Merzouga.

name: Help Children Through Educational and Charitable Activities (Tanzania)

location.address: Tanzania, United Republic of, Mafinga, Iringa Region

description: A Joy Pre and Primary School is a community-based school located in Mafinga, Iringa Region, Tanzania. The school was founded to provide access to quality education for underprivileged children, while nurturing academic excellence, life skills, creativity and positive values. Our project focuses on early childhood and primary education, with growing interest in ICT exposure, reading culture, creative arts, environmental awareness and basic life skills. We believe education should be practical, inclusive and supportive, especially for children from vulnerable backgrounds. We welcome volunteers who are passionate about education, community development and cultural exchange. Volunteers may assist with: * Teaching support (English, Maths, reading and basic Science) * Playing with and engaging children * Fundraising activities * Library organisation and reading activities * ICT and digital literacy exposure * Creative activities (arts, music, sports and storytelling) * DIY projects, including gardening, building and painting Volunteers are provided with simple but safe accommodation within or near the school or community. Meals are provided and consist of local Tanzanian food such as maize flour (ugali), rice, beans, vegetables, bananas, potatoes, porridge and seasonal fruits. Living conditions are basic, with non-flushing toilets and limited amenities, offering an authentic rural African experience. In return, volunteers gain meaningful hands-on experience, cultural immersion and the opportunity to make a real impact on children’s lives. We value mutual respect, cultural exchange and teamwork, and we strive to create a welcoming and supportive environment for all volunteers.

name: Help with the Garden and Cabin Building (Chile)
description: We are a family where harmony, joy, and love for our neighbours flourish. Our home is situated on a small estate that combines both countryside and beach, surrounded by untamed nature. The native flora includes pine trees, eucalyptus, boldo, litre, doca, and various other plants, while the local fauna features wild horses, rabbits, quail, foxes, and more. From our windows, we enjoy a stunning view of the Pacific Ocean. Each year on December 31st, our terrace provides a perfect vantage point to witness the largest fireworks display in South America along the Valparaíso coastline. We are conveniently located just 1 hour and 35 minutes from Arturo Merino Benítez International Airport and only 30 minutes from Valparaíso, the capital of the 5th region. Additionally, we are 45 minutes from Viña del Mar, where the city's renowned Song Festival takes place every February. Our village, Laguna Verde, is situated on the Curaumilla estate. We are seeking volunteers to assist us in creating a community garden, finishing the construction of a wooden cabin where you will stay (it is nearly complete), and completing other tasks typical of rural coastal life. We would be delighted to have your help and will provide accommodation and meals. Please ensure you have your own funds for transportation and personal items.

name: Gardening, Community Exchange & Hands-On Projects (Oregon)
description: Welcome to SOHA — Sanctuary of Healing Arts — a developing rural community and retreat space nestled in the rolling hills of western Oregon. Our 140-acre property is an evolving project focused on nature connection, healing, creativity, food growing, and hands-on learning experiences. We welcome travellers who enjoy meaningful contribution, collaborative living, and practical projects in rural environments. This is an exchange-based experience where helpers contribute their time and energy in exchange for accommodation, shared experiences, learning opportunities, and participation in community life. SOHA is not a polished retreat centre or a large commercial farm. It is a growing and evolving space where helpers have the opportunity to participate in real-world projects and help shape the next stage of the land and community. Projects are based on your interests and skills, but may include gardening such as weeding, watering, planting, and tending; landscaping, including developing new planting areas or relocating native plants to more suitable locations; chicken care, including tending the flock and washing eggs for the farm stand; building and maintenance work; and art projects using mill ends of wood or other salvaged materials. Summer 2026 projects include: *maintaining our new garden - weeding, harvesting and planting *staffing our farmstand building (great for someone who need some "office/computer" time as there is a desk and wifi available) and helping customers as they arrive. *Skilled workers invited to help with building, maintenance and construction of various small and large projects. *harvesting and preserving apples in Late August and September. We love helping people get to know the local area.  A car may be helpful but we do go to town from time to time. Bikes are available as we are 6 miles from the nearest "store."

name: Join Our Hostel Team as a Volunteer in Arequipa (Peru)
description: Hi everyone, We need help during the mornings and afternoons, 5 days per week, 5 hours per day. During the morning, volunteers will assist with breakfast, cleaning tasks, and letting the guests know about the activities, among other things. We are all young people who love teamwork and sharing aspects of our culture and traditions. We are really glad to have people from around the world in our house, as they also become part of our family.

name: Join Our School Community: Be a Part of Something Great (northeast Vietnam)
description: Our School is located in Tuyen Quang, Vietnam. We’re looking for volunteers with an education and teaching background to assist our school in the Northeast Vietnam. If you are a self-motivated individual with an interest in teaching, learning about indigenous cultures being involved with the community, then this is the placement for you! Our school aims to help local students grow both in gaining a high level of English, traditional cultures and in life skills. The students here are very friendly and adorable, and they also like to communicate with the foreigners as well. As an Education Assistant, you will provide your knowledge as an educator, share your experiences about the world, inspire children to learn and pursue further education. You will work directly with assigned teachers (including the host teacher), participate in activities both inside and outside the classroom, and be engaged with the community through intercultural events throughout the school year. Each volunteer will be required to teach a maximum of 5 hours per day (- 8:00 am - 11:00 am and 2:00 pm - 4:00 pm) from Monday to Friday, leaving you weekends to explore surrounding areas. ESSENTIAL REQUIREMENTS: - Completed university degree or college diploma - You’ll need to commit to a minimum of one month. A long term volunteer to be a priority - Strong understanding of working with Primary and High School age students - Be culturally sensitive and have an openness to learning about different cultures - Excellent organizational, communication and interpersonal skills

name: Help Exchange at a Hotel in the Valley of the Incas (Peru)
description: Hello Volunteers! We are a hotel located in Ollantaytambo, Cusco, Peru, in the heart of the Sacred Valley of the Incas. We offer accommodation with breakfast, and lunch is often available as well, although it is not guaranteed. We are currently looking for someone to support the general management of the hotel and assist with our marketing and social media presence. Your main responsibilities will include helping in different areas of the hotel and improving the hotel's visibility on social media platforms and major booking websites. Working Hours 🔹6 hours per day 🔹5 days per week 🔹Minimum stay: 1 month Skills Required 🔹Friendly and positive attitude 🔹Clean and professional presentation 🔹Quick learner 🔹Focused and motivated 🔹Basic Spanish and good English 🔹Good knowledge of social media and website management What We Offer 🔹A private room with a bathroom in the hotel 🔹Breakfast included 🔹Lunch often available, although not guaranteed 🔹Access to an abundance of Inca archaeological sites and beautiful hiking areas to explore during your free time 🔹A vibrant and international social environment We look forward to welcoming you to our team and sharing the beauty of the Sacred Valley with you!

name: Joyful Volunteers Welcome at an Off-Grid Community (Greece)
description: Tired of the 9–5 grind? Searching for deeper meaning? You’re not alone. Many people travel or volunteer in the hope of finding purpose—only to return to the same old patterns. We know this feeling well. We’ve been there too. Then we found something different. We stepped away from the familiar cycle of job, family, and house, and began to build a life rooted in truth, unity, and transformation. Today, we live together on a Greek mountain, guided by a living Master whose timeless teachings centre on inner freedom, love, and the art of conscious living. His presence is woven into every aspect of our daily life. What we are creating is a modern Asclepieion—a sanctuary for healing and awareness. It is a space where we cultivate Εὖ Ζῆν (Ef Zin): a life of wellbeing, clarity, and purpose. We live as a large, extended family of over 50 people from diverse backgrounds. Together, we grow food, build with our hands, care for plants, sing, dance, meditate, and study. Our path is spiritual, yet deeply grounded in everyday experience. You are not expected to follow our path—only to arrive with sincerity and openness. Since 2006, the land that was once dry and barren has been transformed. We have planted more than 2,000 trees, herbs, and crops using natural farming methods such as Fukuoka, permaculture, and biodynamics. We use solar energy, work manually, and learn from the Earth each day. Volunteering here means joining a living experiment. You’ll help with planting, harvesting, and day-to-day tasks, but you’ll also have the opportunity to attend teachings, participate in community life, and explore a way of being that is rarely encountered in today’s world. You may find yourself engaging in organic cultivation, reflecting inwardly, and deepening your connection with nature and yourself. If you wish, and by application, you may attend seminars with the Master. There is also space for practices such as yoga, sound healing, and natural therapies, as well as visits to nearby waterfa

name: Join Our 4-Week Wildlife Programme (South Africa)
description: Join Our 4-Week Wildlife Conservation Programme in Greater Kruger, South Africa. Work on over 12 active conservation projects in the world-renowned Greater Kruger National Park. This programme is perfect for anyone looking to make a real difference to Africa’s wildlife, gain hands-on, transferable skills in ecology and conservation, or simply enjoy an unforgettable travel adventure. Live on a Big Five reserve, play a crucial role in our conservation efforts, and explore the best of South Africa! Volunteer Activities May Include: * White rhino behavioural research * Anti-poaching patrols * Bird ringing (catching, tagging, and releasing) * Camera trap surveys * Humane trapping and surveying of small mammals * Wildlife handling and community presentations * Wildlife rehabilitation and rural veterinary support * Invasive species removal * Indigenous tree surveying * Socio-economic development with local communities * Sustainable agriculture initiatives * Wildlife tracking and monitoring * Reserve maintenance and management * Opportunities to lead your own mini-projects * And much more! Programme Details: * Duration: 4 weeks * All-inclusive cost: £2,350 (€2,730) * Deposit: £200 (€230) to secure your place * Balance: £2,150 (€2,500) due no later than 42 days before arrival * Please message us for payment instructions. What’s Included: * Return airport transfers to and from the reserve * On-site accommodation throughout your stay * All in-country transportation * Guided excursions and group activities * All meals and drinks (excluding personal extras) * Site entry fees * All activity equipment * Wi-Fi access * Participation in over 12 conservation projects * A dedicated week of travel and adventure * UK-based meet and greet event before departure * Volunteer contact list (sent in advance) * 24/7 on-site emergency support * 100% financial protection as a registered TTA member What’s Not Included: * International flights * Travel insurance * Visas * Required vaccinations Opt

name: Sumatra Project: Empower Children & Protect Nature (Indonesia)
description: Every volunteer brings different skills and interests, so activities may vary depending on the needs of the community and each volunteer’s strengths. 📚 Education & Youth Development Volunteers support educational activities that encourage creativity, confidence, and lifelong learning. No teaching experience is required. Enthusiasm, patience, and a willingness to engage with children are the most important qualities. 🎨 Arts & Creativity Creative activities help children express themselves, build confidence, and develop communication and problem-solving skills. Volunteers are encouraged to share ideas that inspire imagination and self-expression. ⚽ Sports & Outdoor Activities Sports provide an enjoyable way for children to develop teamwork, discipline, and healthy habits. Volunteers help create a positive and inclusive environment where children can learn, grow, and have fun. 🎵 Music & Performing Arts Music is a powerful tool for learning, confidence-building, and cultural exchange. Volunteers can share musical skills, introduce new activities, or simply encourage participation. No professional musical background is required—enthusiasm and creativity are welcome. 🐢 Turtle Conservation The coastline surrounding Sungai Pinang provides important habitat for sea turtles. Volunteers have the opportunity to support conservation awareness, educational programmes, and community initiatives that help protect these remarkable marine species. 🌊 Coral Reef Restoration & Marine Conservation The marine environment of West Sumatra contains valuable coral reef ecosystems that support biodiversity and local livelihoods. Volunteers contribute to environmental education and conservation activities that promote the long-term protection of marine ecosystems and encourage sustainable stewardship of coastal resources. This is more than a volunteer placement—it is an opportunity to become part of a community, inspire young minds, and contribute to meaningful educational and conservation init

name: Talk English & Enjoy a Free Hotel Stay (Czech Republic)
description: Do you want to volunteer and help people learn English? 🇬🇧 ➡️ Are you a native English speaker? ➡️ Are you planning a trip to Europe? ➡️ Would you like to gain experience teaching English? ➡️ Do you want to volunteer and help people learn English? Then Prague is your answer! Come and volunteer in beautiful Prague, the capital of the Czech Republic, in the heart of Europe. We invite you to join our first programme in the Czech Republic. Englishstay helps students improve their English in cooperation with volunteers. The entire programme takes place in some of the most beautiful locations across the Czech Republic. If you would like to explore fascinating places and experience Czech culture, simply come and join us. You’ll have the opportunity to visit the Czech Republic’s spa regions and historic castles. Teaching English to Adults ➡️ “I’m Not Afraid – I Want to Speak Fluently” (1-to-1 sessions) This programme is designed for clients who want to commit fully to individual language learning with native speakers. Each student is paired with one English lecturer for every session. Over more than 70 hours of English conversation, students will improve both their speaking and writing skills. Through this programme, participants can prepare for English language examinations or for new career opportunities. Throughout the day, students spend time in English conversation with volunteer teachers, their mentors, and other students while working on group projects. ➡️ “I Speak Just a Little and I Want More” (2-to-1 sessions) We also offer a programme for those who are not quite sure whether a week of intensive English communication is right for them. In this version, lessons are conducted in a 2-to-1 format — one native speaker works with two students. This approach is ideal for those who know the basics of English grammar but have not yet had the opportunity to practise English with a native speaker in real-life situations. At this stage of learning, support from a fellow stude

name: Wildmex Surf & Adventure Volunteer Experience (Mexico)
description: Join Wildmex and make a positive impact just steps from the beach! As a volunteer, you’ll support daily operations at the front desk and boutique, help keep spaces clean and organised, and take part in beach clean-ups and mountain bike trail building. Beyond the work, you’ll enjoy an unforgettable experience—meeting people from around the world, embracing an active outdoor lifestyle, and having the chance to surf at one of Mexico’s most beautiful coastal destinations. Our headquarters and volunteer accommodations are located at La Lancha Beach, a stunning surfing spot 45 minutes north of Puerto Vallarta, 5 minutes from Punta Mita, and 20 minutes from Sayulita. We are not in a city, so access to bars and nightlife is limited. This location is ideal for nature and surf enthusiasts. Types of Help and Learning Opportunities: 🔹Language practice 🔹Eco projects 🔹Gardening 🔹DIY and building projects 🔹General maintenance 🔹Household assistance 🔹Hospitality/tourism 🔹General computer help 🔹Cultural exchange Benefits: 🔹Stay at one of Mexico’s best beginner and longboard surf breaks 🔹Free access to quality surf gear 🔹Opportunity to join surfing lessons (subject to availability) 🔹Connect with people from diverse countries and cultures 🔹Learn or practise Spanish 🔹Enjoy incredible weather year-round We Are Looking For: Friendly volunteers who enjoy making new friends, love nature, learning about different cultures, and adventure. We are a community focused on enjoying the outdoors, respecting nature, and living healthily.

name: Explore the Desert, Immerse Yourself in Local Traditions (Morocco)

location.address: Explore the Desert, Immerse Yourself in Local Traditions

description: I’m Hamza, a local Amazigh  from Tagounite and a desert guide with deep roots in the Sahara. I love sharing my culture, stories, and the beauty of the desert with travelers from around the world. My home is your home — a place to work and relax, connect, and experience the real Moroccan. You’ll often find me outside, watering the plants and palm trees. Our main tasks are feeding the animals and keeping the plants hydrated, as the area is very dry. We water as much as possible, and every week we plant new vegetables and herbs, depending on the season. Our day begins with breakfast, followed by three hours of work on the farm. We then take a break for lunch, prepared with natural ingredients from our land. Afterwards, you can relax, take a nap, or have a shower. In the evening, there are plenty of activities: playing games, cards, visiting abandoned kasbahs, hiking in the mountains, or even swimming, if you like. We share all our meals together, with most food coming from our farm. We have simple but comfortable facilities, including beds, a shower, kitchen, and toilet, as well as ample space for campervans and pets. I enjoy running and go hiking in the mountains twice a week to enjoy the silence. Staying here gives you the chance to experience authentic Moroccan culture and village life. You’ll learn about local traditions, customs, cuisine, and language while building connections with villagers and fellow volunteers. You’ll also explore the natural beauty and diversity of the region, from the stunning landscapes of the Sahara desert and the Atlas Mountains to the Draa Valley. Historical and cultural sites, such as ancient kasbahs, mosques, and markets, are also nearby for you to visit.

name: Volunteer for Education and Life Improvement (Peru)
description: We are a small non-profit organization dedicated to improving the quality of life and education for children, women, and communities in the central Andes of Peru. Our mission is to support these children through an alternative school, providing actions and activities aimed at enhancing their education and rebuilding self-esteem. Many of these children come from migrant Quechua families facing various challenges, including neglect and domestic violence, stemming from economic and social hardships and cultural marginalization. One of our distinctive features is that we develop and implement our projects directly within extremely impoverished populations facing economic and cultural marginalization due to their indigenous immigrant status. We invite you to join us as a volunteer in this project crafted with our own hands, driven by the ambition to conduct transparent, authentic, and direct social work within the communities. Work Details: - Duration: Minimum 4 weeks + - Location: Huancayo in the central Andes of Peru Activities Include: - Teaching English and Quechua - Dance, acting, and theater classes - Homework assistance - Drawing, painting, and crafts - Outdoor sports and educational games - Yoga and ecological tutoring - Hygiene and health education - Social skills training - And more; we're open to new ideas! Flexible Schedule: Monday to Friday, 6 to 8 hours per day, with morning options available. Requirements: - Minimum age 19 - Ability and patience to work with children and women - Teaching experience not required - Tolerance and adaptability - Teamwork skills - Ability to work and live in a diverse intercultural group - Adaptable, open, and positive mindset What's Included: - Accommodation - Personal support and assistance - Customized activities and practical courses in: - -- Spanish and Quechua languages - -- Food, dessert, and beverage exchanges - -- Excursions and group trips to various attractions in the Andes and central jungle of Peru - -Traditional a

name: Artistic Oasis: Eco Projects & Cultural Exchange (Brazil)
description: I am a photographer, visual artist, and designer. You are invited to join our unique family of artists and life enthusiasts in a stunning home set in the beautiful backdrop of the Brazilian cerrado, where vibrant sunsets paint the sky. We would love to have you with us, contributing to our art and eco projects, teaching languages to our children, collaborating within our family, assisting with household tasks, sharing your culinary specialties, and working in the garden. Your presence would greatly contribute to enhancing our quality of life and fostering a deeper connection with nature.

name: Help with a Horse Trekking Centre in Devon (England)

location.address: United Kingdom, Exmoor, West Lyn, Devon

description: We are a family run horse trekking center on beautiful Exmoor. We are fully licensed and rated as 5 stars. Our horses are part of the family and their care and welfare is the foundation of everything. We have the best location for riding over the open moors and through beautiful rivers. Accommodation will be a self contained annex, which depending on time of year possibly sharing with 1 other person. Will provide meals.

name: Live in Nature and Work with Horses in Machakos County (Kenya)
description: Experience life in a beautiful, serene environment where you can go horseback riding among wildlife. We are located just one hour from Nairobi. I am looking for someone to help care for our many animals — dogs, cats, and donkeys — but especially our horses. The role focuses on their daily care and riding, using natural horsemanship techniques. We live on a 20,000-acre wildlife conservancy, where you can enjoy the unique experience of riding among wild animals. Live close to nature while working with horses, dogs, and other rescued animals surrounded by wildlife. We rescue abused or neglected animals and rehabilitate them to their natural state, giving them the freedom and social dynamics they crave.

name: Lend a Hand on a Sicilian Family Farm (Italy)
description: I'm Edoardo, 30 years old, and the fifth generation of farmers in my family. My family and I live in a large house in a very small town in the stunning province of Messina, in north-east Sicily. We speak English and Spanish fairly well, and we're always happy to welcome open-minded travellers from around the world. We’re located 7 km from the seaside, right at the foot of the mountains, about 300 metres above sea level. It’s a perfect place for nature lovers—great for trekking and off-road adventures for all levels, and also ideal for those who enjoy the sea. Look up "Golfo di Milazzo" for a glimpse, but I’ll be happy to share some secret, lesser-known spots with you too! We have several small projects around the farm, especially focused on modernising and restoring old rural houses and traditional stone walls. In the hotter months, we focus on repairing rural buildings and keeping the plants well-watered—this is a big part of our daily routine, along with maintaining our vegetable garden, which produces food for the family all year round. In the colder seasons, the work depends a bit on the weather, but you can definitely help with daily tasks in the fields—such as harvesting, cleaning small rivers to ensure water flow, and other practical jobs. I can guarantee your hands will get dirty—in the best way! I have plenty of skills to share in this kind of work and lots of experience leading and coordinating small teams. I also love cooking and have learned all my recipes from my two grandmothers. I'm deeply interested in cultural and culinary exchange—it’s in my nature to be curious about different ways of living. If you have any questions, don’t hesitate to reach out. I’d be happy to explain everything in more detail and make sure you feel welcome and informed. We’re well connected to Catania Airport by a bus company that can take you to Milazzo Port or Messina Centrale (the main train station), which are both just 20–25 km from our home. The nearest train station is

name: Teach Alongside Locally Recruited Teachers (Honduras)
description: Cofradía's Bilingual School (CBS), located in Cofradía, Cortes, Honduras, offers a high-quality bilingual education to over 300 students from kindergarten through grade 11. Our committed team of international volunteers works alongside locally recruited Honduran teachers to provide students with an essential alternative to the public school system. Our students receive an immersion-based bilingual education from native and fluent English speakers from around the world. Graduating with English language skills opens up many more opportunities than would otherwise be available to them. Many of our students come from low socioeconomic backgrounds, and a bilingual education equips them with the tools to break the cycle of poverty for their families. Our graduates are motivated, passionate individuals, ready to build a brighter future for themselves, their community, and their country. All volunteer-led classes are conducted in English, so Spanish language skills are not required. While prior teaching experience is advantageous, it is not necessary for working at CBS. We place higher value on motivation, passion, and a strong work ethic. We are currently seeking volunteer English teachers for the 2025–2026 school year, which runs from August to June. Ideally, volunteers commit to at least three months (though exceptions may be considered). Volunteers residing in Honduras are provided with accommodation in our volunteer house, along with three meals a day and airport transfers. Visas are free upon arrival in Honduras, and we cover basic medical expenses for the duration of your volunteering with us.

name: Help Care for My Mother in Nosy Be (Madagascar)
description: My mother is in a wheelchair and needs daily support and rehabilitation. We are looking for a volunteer with experience in elderly care or therapy, or simply someone kind, hygienic, and sensible. She has hemiparesis and some difficulty chewing and swallowing (dysphagia). I live in Nosy Be, on one of the world’s most beautiful islands, where I run a small yoga centre in a village nestled between the ocean and the forest. In exchange for two hours of care or therapy, five days a week, the volunteer will receive free accommodation, Wi-Fi, pool access, and Yoga/Meditation classes. Volunteers who wish to contribute more can help with local activities such as the neighbouring vocational school or children’s library. There are also wonderful opportunities to explore the island and surrounding islets, walk in the forest and nature reserve, and swim in the ocean every day, while making a meaningful difference in my mother’s life.

name: Be a Community Champion at goSTOPS (India)
description: Duration: 15 Days - 1 Month • 5 hours a day | Day or Evening Shift  (flexible, based on activities) goVOLUTEER is our vibe-first volunteer programme for travellers who want to co-create events, host moments, and turn strangers into friends, and are looking for more than just a place to sleep. goVOLUTEER lets you trade your time and energy for stays and experiences at goSTOPS. You host, connect, and create, we take care of the rest. You host. You steady the rhythm. You connect. We take care of the rest. Delhi | Varanasi | Gurugram | Bangalore | Coorg | Amritsar | Bir | Kasol | Mcleodganj | Bir | Jaipur | Udaipur | Rishikesh | Auroville | Ooty | Nainital | Mukteshwar | Mussoorie | Alibaug | Goa | Katra - Jammu Your Role (aka Vibe Creator) Think part host, part community builder, part chaos coordinator (the fun kind). You’ll be: • Welcoming guests & giving hostel tours • Hosting social events (Karaoke, Movie Nights, Open Mics, Bonfires & more) • Engaging travellers & building the hostel vibe • Capturing fun content (photos/videos/reels) • Supporting the team during events • Collecting guest feedback Working Hours & Off Day • 5 hours a day (flexible, based on activities) • Tuesday = weekly off (explore, chill, repeat) What’s in it for you Free stay in mixed dorms All meals covered Certificate of participation Hands-on travel & hospitality experience Meet travellers from around the world Get 5 guests to book a paid experience in a day -  you get to enjoy that experience for FREE 15% discount coupons.

name: Help and Discover Nature, the Beach, and Mt. Fuji (Japan)
description: My work is running a minpaku — that means I rent out private rooms in my home. Every day, many different kinds of people stay here. If you like Mount Fuji, you can visit it in just a few hours from here. I can speak English, so I’m happy to explain about Japanese culture, and I also enjoy learning about other cultures, so we can share and exchange our experiences. I’m looking for a helper who really enjoys cultural exchange and communication, even during free time after cleaning the rooms in the evenings. I also hope you can help me with cooking dinner. I have a daughter, she would only feel comfortable with a female helper. I hope to find someone who is good at cleaning and general household tasks. I have already checked with the Japanese government, and this position does not require a working holiday visa. However, when you enter Japan, please tell immigration that you are visiting a friend — that way, you can enter smoothly.

name: Join Our Team at a Magical Hostel in San Cristóbal (Mexico)

location.address: Mexico, San Cristobal de las Casas

description: Help our team at the hostel with general activities in Mexico Our hostel is in San Cristóbal de las Casas, Chiapas, Mexico. San Cristóbal de las Casas has been recognised as the Most Magical Village in Mexico for many years, offering a vibrant blend of culture, art, music, nature, and a fantastic opportunity to meet people from around the world and share experiences. Our hostel is just two blocks from the main square and offers a variety of services both inside and outside the property, including accommodation, tours, and a temazcal (Mayan sauna). The building is a large, old neoclassical house with a beautiful garden, hammocks, and a brand-new terrace offering 360-degree views of the city. Around San Cristóbal, you’ll find fascinating cultural villages just 20 minutes away (San Juan Chamula & Zinacantán), as well as natural wonders such as rivers, waterfalls, lakes, and archaeological sites just 2-3 hours away (Lagos de Montebello, Cascada El Chiflón, Toniná, Cascadas de Agua Azul, Palenque, etc.). This project began with one simple idea: to meet travellers and share experiences. That’s why we strive to have staff who love to share and enjoy every moment. We also believe that comfort and quality are essential (if not the most important aspects) of any trip, which is why we are committed to providing these to our guests. Though our time running the hostel has been short, we’ve had incredible experiences and are confident that we’re heading in the right direction. We’ve also made many new friends, with some guests staying for over 50 days or returning at different times of the year.

name: Help and Learn as You Go (England)
description: Close to Peterborough and March railway stations. Rural setting, growing our own vegetables and fruit, and raising our own livestock. Help is needed with the garden, field fencing, and various other outdoor tasks. We keep bees, sheep, pigs, hens, quails, and rabbits. We also have a small dog and two cats. We enjoy sharing our wide range of skills, so please let us know if you’re interested. Within suitable distances for day trips are 'Sandringham', 'The Norfolk Coast', 'Cambridge College Backs' and 'Sutton Hoo' to name but a few.

name: Help Needed on a Forest Farm & Events Venue (Portugal)
description: Volunteers will be integrated into our daily tasks, feel part of the organisation, and enjoy the freedom of our spaces. There is plenty of nature, and we are not far from town. We consider volunteers a precious part of our daily lives. We enjoy teaching volunteers about our activities and learning from their personal experiences.Our website: agolada.pt There is lots of room to roam around, swimming in our lakes or pool, visiting the local town of Coruche, and enjoying meals cooked by our resident staff. Volunteers also have their own quarters with a full kitchen, four bedrooms, (2 of them are private, no bunk beds),two full bathrooms, and Wi-Fi. Whether you enjoy socialising or prefer some privacy, we provide opportunities for both. We want volunteers to have a memorable experience. For many people, this is their first volunteering experience, so we ease them into the tasks and give them time to adjust. We understand that communication and mutual respect between hosts, staff, and volunteers are key to success, so we make it easy for volunteers to voice any concerns at all times. We like to think of ourselves as open-minded and flexible. Volunteers, if they wish, can leave at any time without notice and without being asked questions. This freedom is important so that volunteers never feel stuck or obliged to justify their departure. We encourage socialising between volunteers, staff, and ourselves as hosts.

name: Experience Daily Farm Life in Sicily (Italy)
description: I am an amateur farmer living in the picturesque Madonie Park, nestled at an altitude of 1,000 meters above sea level. My home is in the countryside near the charming town of Petralia Sottana, a region known for its stunning natural beauty and rich cultural heritage. The nearby towns are full of history, with beautifully preserved stone buildings, and the locals are warm and welcoming, always ready to share a smile. I take great joy in sharing my daily life with volunteers. Together, we care for the animals on my small farm, which include goats, a donkey, chickens, dogs, and a cat. One of the highlights of our routine is milking the goats to produce homemade dairy products for everyday use. Depending on the season, we might also gather almonds from the trees or head into the woods to collect firewood. Meal preparation is another opportunity for connection, as we share culinary knowledge and enjoy creating dishes together. For those who love outdoor activities, the surrounding area offers numerous trekking trails that showcase the beauty of the Madonie Park. In winter, a short 30-minute drive up into the mountains will bring you to ski lifts and slopes, perfect for enjoying the snowy season. Whether it’s exploring nature, connecting with animals, or experiencing the simple pleasures of farm life, this corner of Sicily offers a unique and enriching experience.

name: Homestay, Nanny & Dog Carer in St Kilda (Australia)

location.city: Nanny & Dog Carer in St Kilda
location.address: Homestay, Nanny & Dog Carer in St Kilda

description: We are looking for a nanny to live with us, primarily to care for our 10-month-old Cavoodle. This will include walking him, taking him to the dog park and puppy school, and doing some training exercises. Occasionally, the role will also involve some nannying duties with our two children, aged 4 and 6, such as cooking, tidying, and accompanying us on outings. You will be warmly welcomed into our home and well looked after. We will cover all your food and most expenses, and you’ll have the chance to enjoy a wonderful lifestyle in Melbourne—meeting locals and joining us on trips to the countryside. We live in St Kilda, close to the beach, Chapel Street nightlife, and with plenty of public transport options. The city centre is just 15–20 minutes away.

name: Grow Food, Foster Inclusion, Build Community (Indonesia)

location.address: Grow Food, Foster Inclusion, Build Community

description: We are creating a living learning space where permaculture, food security and inclusive education come together. Our mission is to build a resilient community that cares for the Earth, values every person, and strengthens local food systems through education and collaboration. We believe that healthy communities begin with healthy ecosystems. By growing food sustainably, sharing knowledge and creating accessible learning opportunities for everyone, we aim to inspire positive change that will benefit generations to come.

name: Winery and tasting room / Homestead helping hand (Maine)
description: *** We won't be able to host anyone until March 31, 2024. Starting from April 1 to October 31, 2024, we'll be available for hosting. *** A working family homestead in Northern Maine, and multi-generational home. My wife and I are the kids! We have been married 29 years this coming may and have two grown children, living in other parts of the state, who visit regularly. Mt. Katahdin, and Baxter State Park are just under 45 minutes away. The coast, and Acadia National Park just a couple hours away. And tons of lighthouses around the state including Quoddy State Park and its lighthouse and Reversing Falls Park. We operate a small onsite winery, tasting room, onsite Airbnb, host RV campers through Harvest Host, and operate a small farm store. We raise Kiko goats for breeding and meat, process honey from our hives in the summer, grow ten varieties of apples, grow produce in our 20+ raised garden beds and mini-greenhouse, mostly for household consumption. We are looking for Spring through mid-fall help to support the many projects we challenge ourselves with around the homestead during the warmer parts of the year. The type of work you could do to assist us ranges from, morning and evening chores in the barn, animal husbandry, including goats and chickens, maintaining garden beds, tree and limb clean up, which the leaves and needles are fed to our goats, building and mending fences, building decks, beekeeping, general maintenance, and preparing for winter. We will be adding mushrooms, strawberries, blueberries, and more grapes to our arbor this year. We also have a bed of asparagus, rhubarb, and horseradish growing. And 10 varieties of garlic. Let's not forget about the pumpkins, gourds, tomatoes, lettuce, cucumbers, squash, and We also need help with canning vegetables, jams, and other goodies. We are looking for a Jack/Jill of all trades. A go-getter who enjoys trying new things and willing to stretch and grow with us. The opportunities for learning are endless, and new

name: Volunteer Teaching, Gardening, Social & Medical Care (Uganda)

location.address: Volunteer Teaching, Gardening, Social Work and Medical Care

description: I'm Brian, greeting you from Uganda, East Africa. I run an organization called BUTIKI Children's Ministry where I support disadvantaged children like orphans, street children and children from poor families. We have a model school where we provide education for them, we also provide feeding, clothing medical care, accomodations for these children in our community. We have a farm where we grow food to feed them and also sell some to be able to pay bills like water, electricity etc. We are seeking for volunteers in teaching, gardening, social working, medical care. *** IMPORTANT *** All volunteer applicants for this placement must undergo police vetting in their home country. This is because the role involves working with children (under 18s) and/or vulnerable adults, and their safety is our top priority. Thank you for your cooperation in ensuring a safe and secure environment for those we serve.

name: Help Rebuild an Old Farmstead (Slovakia)
description: This is a place for tough people, for those with a vision for the future… and maybe a little bit crazy, I guess. We live in a very eco-friendly environment, so our approach to daily life follows the same principles. Although we don’t hold any eco-certification or eco-label, living ecologically has long been an essential part of our adult lives. We do not use artificial fertilisers in the garden, relying instead on a mixture of water with soaked nettles or dissolved chicken droppings. For the second year in a row, we have been sorting waste on the plot and we prefer eco-friendly cosmetics. While we do have a car, we often get around by bike, and we only use mechanisation where necessary, even though we do have some power tools. Returning from a walk in the forest with a bag full of garbage—instead of a basket full of mushrooms—is second nature to us. We have often done this even in very remote places while travelling through Latin America, where we even managed to organise a mass cleanup with the local communities. In short, a long-term stay in nature leads us to coexist with it naturally, without requiring a deliberate effort or force of will. We have some animals, but we definitely want more in the future. There are plenty of deer all around us. Imagine the rut here—it’s incredible! In autumn, the singing of the deer fills the area everywhere you look.

name: Help with farming and taking care of animals (northern Norway)
description: We have a lot of horses and other animals. We need help with feeding, cleaning and taking the horses in and outside in the morning and evening. We are a family that have a farm near Tromsø city. Here we have many different kinds of animals. We seek you who like and have experience with animals, especially horses. We are looking for a person of order, is practical and can fix things, is flexible and in good mood. We need help 5-6 hours a day, 5 days in the week. We hope for someone who can stay for a longer time. The help is to take out the horses in the morning, feed and clean for animals. Take the horses inside in the evening. And other farm working things, depending on the season. Here you can explore the northern lights in the wintertime and the midnight sun in the summertime.

name: Join Us on Our Organic Olive Farm in Tuscany (Italy)
description: We have an old organic olive grove and need help caring for it. We are also renovating our old barn to create a community space for all kinds of experiences, such as cooking classes, retreats and workshops.

name: Sea Turtle Conservation Programme in Galle (Sri Lanka)
description: Sri Lanka’s southern coastline is one of Asia’s most important sea turtle nesting habitats and is home to five of the world’s seven sea turtle species. Based in the historic coastal city of Galle, this programme places volunteers at the heart of active conservation efforts to protect these endangered animals and their fragile coastal ecosystems. Volunteers work alongside our local conservation team to monitor nesting beaches, collect field data, assist with nest relocation, support hatchling releases, and take part in beach clean-ups. No previous experience is required, as full training and ongoing support are provided throughout the programme. Volunteers live with a welcoming Sri Lankan host family, enjoying home-cooked meals, cultural immersion, and a genuine connection to the local community. Accommodation is available in either a shared room, ideal for solo travellers seeking a sociable experience with fellow volunteers, or a private room for those who prefer additional comfort and privacy after a day in the field. Both options include three meals per day and full programme support. The programme is open to volunteers aged 18 and over. No specialist qualifications or conservation background are required—just a genuine passion for wildlife and environmental conservation. The minimum stay is two weeks, with longer placements and extensions welcome.

name: Hostel Volunteer – Peak District Adventures (England)
description: We're a newly refurbished hostel situated in the charming village of Hathersage, located within The Peak District National Park. We're looking for friendly and enthusiastic volunteers to help us create a fun and welcoming environment for our guests. If you have a passion for the outdoors and enjoy spending time with people in a community of like-minded individuals, then Base Camp may be the place for you! In exchange for 4 hours of work per day, 5 days per week, we provide free shared staff accommodation in the heart of one of the country's finest national parks. The work involves hosting and reception duties, cleaning, making beds, and helping to ensure our guests have a friendly and enjoyable experience at the hostel. Please get in touch if you'd like to find out more. We are accepting volunteers for immediate starts and throughout the summer!

name: Internship at Phu Quoc Resort & English Camp (Vietnam)
description: We are a 4-star boutique resort near the quiet white sands of Bai Sao in southern Phu Quoc, and we are looking for hands-on volunteers for our upcoming summer season. In June, we need maintenance volunteers willing to help with general upkeep, resort repairs, and getting the property in top shape before our peak season. In July and August, our focus shifts entirely to our summer English camp, where we need enthusiastic volunteer teachers to run classes for students and lead conversational English sessions for our resort staff. While you will be living on-site at a 4-star resort with access to our pool, gym, and spa, this is a real-world, active hotel with high standards. We are looking for adaptable, community-minded people who show up ready to be useful, dress appropriately in guest areas, and treat the space with care. A "roll-up-your-sleeves" attitude is essential, as you will be working alongside our multilingual Vietnamese, English, and Korean team to keep operations running smoothly. If you are ready to immerse yourself in island life and help us build something great this June, July, or August, we would love to have you join our team.

name: Enjoy Nature and Lovely Animals: Assist with Farm Tasks (Denmark)
description: Welcome to our beautiful farm, situated in stunning natural surroundings only 1.5 km from one of Denmark's best beaches! We are a retired couple who are still very active and have many interests. We enjoy meeting new people and cultural exchange. On our farm, we have plenty of lovely animals: ponies, donkeys, goats, rabbits, hens, geese, ducks, guinea pigs, peacocks, dogs, and cats. We live in a large house that we are currently renovating. Additionally, we have a guest house/B&B for tourists to stay in during the summer. When we are open for tourists, we usually organize pony trekking tours for children. Our farm is situated in beautiful North Jutland, between two popular tourist towns, Løkken and Lønstrup, near the seaside with a splendid broad sand beach surrounded by magnificent nature! All our animals are very tame and friendly, making anyone who loves animals happy. There are many interesting places to see in and around Løkken and Lønstrup, including good beaches, many activities for young people, and great possibilities for bike riding. You can take the bus to Løkken (5 min) or to Hjørring (15 min) and from Hjørring, the train (35 min) to Ålborg.

name: Live, Learn, and Farm with a Nepali Family (Nepal)

location.region: and Farm with a Nepali Family
location.address: Live, Learn, and Farm with a Nepali Family

description: Namaste 🙏 Welcome to Harit Agro Avocado Farm, a peaceful, family-run organic farm located in the beautiful hills of Bhumlutar-8, Kavre District, Nepal. Our farm is surrounded by green hills, fresh air, rivers, and traditional Nepali village life. We grow avocado trees along with other seasonal fruits and vegetables using natural and organic farming methods. We are a family of six—my father, mother, wife, and our two children—and we warmly welcome volunteers who are interested in farming, nature, culture, and rural community life. This is an ideal place for those who wish to experience an authentic Nepali village lifestyle while contributing to a meaningful agricultural project. Volunteers can help with avocado farming such as planting, watering, mulching, and harvesting, as well as organic vegetable gardening, farm maintenance and cleaning, tree plantation and nursery care, and helping at the local school, Prakash Mabi, with basic English or creative activities. We also enjoy cultural exchange and skill sharing, including photography, social media, and eco-project ideas. We usually ask for 4–5 hours of help per day, five days a week. In return, we provide comfortable accommodation, healthy home-cooked Nepali meals, and a friendly family atmosphere. During your stay, you will experience traditional Nepali food and culture, village festivals, and local daily life, along with beautiful hill views and a peaceful natural environment. You will also learn about organic avocado farming and have opportunities to teach and interact with local children. We believe in respect, learning, and genuine cultural exchange. Our home is simple but full of warmth and positive energy. If you love nature, farming, and meaningful human connections, Harit Agro Avocado Farm may be the perfect place for you. We look forward to welcoming you into our farm family. 🌱🙏

name: Seeking a Russian-Speaking Au Pair (Andora)
description: Living in a microstate is a unique cultural experience. You'll learn the Catalan way of life, from the local language to the rhythms of mountain living. Whether we're heading to a local village festival, going for a weekend hike, or taking a day trip across the border, you're always welcome to join us. We view hosting as a two-way street. While we hope to share our knowledge of Andorra and our various projects with you, we're equally excited to learn from you. Whether it's a recipe from your home country, a different way of approaching a problem, or simply hearing about your travels, your background enriches our home. Our son understands and speaks Russian, but neither my husband nor I do. If you speak Russian, it would be a wonderful way to communicate with him while also helping him keep his language skills fresh.

name: Help with Operations at Our Vacation Rentals (Caribbean)

location.address: Saint Vincent And The Grenadines, Canouan

description: Welcome to Bay View Apartments, the top-rated vacation rental on the stunning island of Canouan! Nestled in a tropical paradise, our accommodation offers breathtaking ocean views, world-class hospitality, and access to unforgettable island experiences. We are seeking enthusiastic volunteers in exchange for free accommodation. If you love tropical destinations, meeting new people, and embracing the dynamic island lifestyle, this is the perfect opportunity for you! Why Choose Bay View Apartments? ✅ Breathtaking Location – Wake up to magnificent sea views and enjoy easy access to the beach. ✅ Luxury Accommodation for Free – Stay in one of our comfortable, fully-equipped apartments. ✅ Authentic Caribbean Experience – Immerse yourself in the island's culture, cuisine, and daily life. ✅ Exciting Adventures – Enjoy access to snorkelling, beach excursions, and trips to the Tobago Cays. ✅ A Fun, Friendly Environment – Work alongside a welcoming team in a laid-back setting. We need an extra set of hands to assist with: 🔹 General Assistance – Light cleaning, organisation, and ensuring the apartments are guest-ready. 🔹 Guest Relations – Assisting with check-ins, answering questions, and making guests feel at home. 🔹 Experience Coordination – Helping with activities such as golf cart rentals, snorkelling, and island tours. 🔹 Social Media & Photography – If you have creative skills, help us capture and share the magic of Canouan! What You’ll Get in Return 🌴 Free Stay – No accommodation costs while you volunteer. 🌴 Local Adventures – Experience the island like a true insider. 🌴 Time to Explore – Flexible work arrangements to enjoy your surroundings. 🌴 Connections & Friendships – Meet travellers and locals from around the world. If you’re ready for a unique adventure in paradise, we’d love to hear from you!

name: Help Restore an Organic Spa and Retreat (Spain)
description: Help bring to life a magical old spa in the beautiful Cerdanya Valley in Spain. We are seeking assistance with gardening, building, and Airbnb hosting jobs. Sanillés is an organic retreat set in an old spa featuring thermal water springs and a swimming pool. The retreat has several rooms available for rent, accommodating groups or individuals on an ad-hoc basis, and also operates a bar and swimming pool area. While some buildings and spaces have been renovated, many still require attention, and we are gradually working towards renovating and decorating these areas. With two miles of riverside and gardens, there is always plenty to maintain and enhance, including the addition of steps, signs, focal points, and exterior artwork. There are various types of work to undertake, from social activities like welcoming and caring for groups and visitors to tasks such as stone wall building and artwork. We encourage you to engage in activities that you love and for which you already have skills.

name: Join Our Team at a San Diego Hostel (California)
description: Join our team as a helper—stay with us and connect with fellow travelers and visitors to our city.

name: Volunteer in a Meditation Centre in Leicester (England)
description: Short- and long-term opportunities available, from a minimum of 2 weeks ♥ Are you looking for a fun and meaningful experience while also learning a little about spirituality and meditation? We are a Buddhist Meditation Centre based in Leicester city centre, England. Our centre teaches people how to calm their minds through meditation and understand key teachings from the Buddha. We have two meditation rooms and modern en-suite bunk-bed dorms accommodating up to four people per room. Our centre also includes the World Peace Café and hosts regular meditation classes, courses, and special events promoting inner peace, compassion, and wisdom. We welcome everyone, Buddhist or non-Buddhist. We are looking for volunteers to help in our World Peace Café, with cleaning, painting, and office work. There is a mixed-age community living here, creating a vibrant and supportive environment. The Exchange: As a working volunteer, you will be asked to work 35 hours per week, generally Tuesday to Saturday, 9.30 am–4.30 pm, with Sunday and Monday off. In return for your work, we offer: * Free food (three standard meals per day) * Free tea and coffee facilities * Free accommodation, including bedding and towels * Free laundry facilities * Free access to all our meditation classes, teachings, courses, and events As a Buddhist community, we ask volunteers to observe some basic Buddhist precepts while staying at our centre: * No lying * No killing * No stealing * No intoxicants (alcohol, drugs, smoking, vaping) * No sexual misconduct We do require a reference; however, we can accept a recommendation from a previous HelpStay or HelpX host if they have posted a review on your profile. Please mention this in your application if applicable.

name: Help Us Complete Our Pond, Garden and Orchard (Australia)

location.address: Help Us Complete Our Pond, Garden and Orchard

description: Hello Travellers! We’ve recently built a wonderfully energy-efficient passive solar house, complete with off-grid solar for all our energy needs, rainwater collection for our water supply, and a worm farm waste system for natural black and grey water processing. Our goal has been to create a fossil-fuel-free, all-electric home with the smallest environmental footprint possible. We’re also fortunate to live in a beautiful rural–coastal area near Inverloch, Victoria, around 1 hour 45 minutes south-east of Melbourne. We’re close to some of Victoria’s best-known wildlife and wilderness attractions, including the Penguin Parade at Phillip Island, the Koala Conservation Reserve, and Wilson’s Promontory for hiking and camping — not to mention numerous local beaches and fishing spots only 10 minutes away! With our house complete, we’re now turning our attention to landscaping. We’d really appreciate a helping hand to develop a natural habitat pond, install a watering system, establish garden beds and vegetable plots, and plant a small orchard. If this sounds of interest, we’d love to hear from you! We’re ready for an immediate start and can host stays from a week up to a couple of months — just let us know what works for you. Guests stay in a private onsite caravan with kitchen, shower and toilet, with the option of an additional private bathroom.

name: Volunteer helping our Youths Build a Better Future (Tanzania)
description: My name is Selly, and I am the president of an association. I am 25 years old and I live in the city of Morogoro in Tanzania, where I was born. I founded this association two years ago with the aim of promoting access to education for disadvantaged and disabled children in my country and hometown. Unfortunately, despite Tanzania's exceptional wildlife and culture, access to education is marred by significant inequalities. Approximately 3.5 million school-age children and adolescents are not enrolled in school, and nearly 30% of children aged 5 to 14 are engaged in work, with 94% of them working in the agricultural sector. Additionally, child labor still affects approximately 4.2 million children aged 5 to 17. Schools for children aged 5 to 18 are fee-paying and often too expensive for the poorest families, greatly limiting their access to education. If you wish to get involved in our project, you are welcome, whether it be for the short or long term. For the past two years, we have regularly welcomed volunteers of all backgrounds to participate in our project, but also to discover our country, its exceptional wildlife and flora, and to exchange ideas about our culture. If you wish to participate in our projects, your tasks will include: - Teaching daily classes to children aged 5 to 16 in subjects such as mathematics, English, history-geography, and your main language, among others. - Participating in the educational program designed to allow each volunteer to invest in a project that corresponds to their knowledge and interests. Each person will, therefore, be free to choose the program that suits them best. However, it is important to note that each volunteer will need to share one day a week with the children in the program they designed beforehand. This day will be dedicated to educational and fun activities aimed at stimulating the curiosity and creativity of children, as well as imparting new knowledge to them. We are open to all types of learning, whether cul

name: Become Part of Our Small Hospitality Team (Newfoundland)
description: Join our coffee house and guest accommodation small team in beautiful Witless Bay on the Avalon Peninsula in Newfoundland, Canada. Located oceanside on the main road in beautiful Witless Bay on the Avalon Peninsula in Newfoundland — just a short thirty-five-minute drive from the historic city of St. John’s (Route 10). This unique coffee and tea house offers a range of services. The first thing you’ll notice, though, is the commanding view of the gorgeous Witless Bay. It’s breathtaking — and it comes with delicious food. The coffee house is conveniently located close to the spectacular East Coast Trail. Hikers can take advantage of our affordable public shower service, which includes towels, shampoo and soap. High-speed internet is also available at a reasonable rate.

name: Volunteer at our Centre of Wellness & Longevity (Peru)
description: Our centre is a great place to volunteer if you like the sea and nature. All year round we have sun and enjoy surf or snorkel with turtles and sea lions; and from june to november you can observe humpback whales from the shore! We offer our guests to live a transformative lifestyle experience through holistic treatments and curative nutrition and tools for wellness, rest and relaxation. The founders are Christian, a trained Environmental Scientist and specialist of thalassotherapy and Zinnia, Master in Environmental Engineering and coach in cellular health and longevity. Both, together with their three year old son Arik will be the ones who will receive, interchange knowledge and assist you throughout your stay. The center needs to be maintained, attained and marketed. We are always happy to receive people with all different kind of skills...be it practical as gardener, carpenter...technical as IT-specialist, communicator, administrator...artistical as sculptor, fotographer...benevolent as therapist, healer, yoga teacher...or whatever ability you wish to share with us.

name: Join Our Community Project in Gitothua (Kenya)
description: Cup of Hope is a community-based non-profit organisation located in Gitothua, Ruiru, Kiambu County, Kenya, dedicated to transforming lives through nutrition, education, sports, mentoring, and community development. Founded on the belief that lasting change begins at the grassroots level, Cup of Hope works alongside children, young people, families, volunteers, and partners to create opportunities that empower communities to thrive. We believe that hope is not simply given — it is built through action, compassion, and partnership.

name: Help in Our Beautiful Garden in Ceredigion (Wales)
description: We live in the stunning Cambrian Mountains of Wales, in a very small but historic village. Our property is surrounded by beautiful countryside, nature reserves and historic monuments. We're a retired professional couple who would love the opportunity to welcome people to this wonderful area. We're looking for volunteers who are happy to help in our one-acre garden in exchange for free accommodation and meals. At the moment, we need help with hedge maintenance, the vegetable garden, mulching and clearing a small pond. There are also plenty of logs to be split and stacked ready for winter. We share our home with a super-friendly German Shepherd and six chickens, so there's always something going on. If you think you'd enjoy spending some time with us, we'd be delighted to hear from you.

name: Living, Learning, and Helping at a Meditation Centre (Italy)

location.region: and Helping at a Meditation Centre
location.address: Living, Learning, and Helping at a Meditation Centre

description: We welcome individuals who would like to support us in all current activities. Whether helping with the houses, the land, or with group work, you will have the opportunity to put your skills into practice. Staying at Osho Nirvesha can help you discover your personal path and provide the chance to make new friends. If you're interested in sharing this experience, please contact us! Osho Nirvesha is a love story with the mystic Osho. It was founded in 1987 by a group of friends in search of the inner self, aiming to live with a new quality, support one another, and be true. This space allows you to meet yourself and others without masks, valuing human connections. Here, you will find friends and the opportunity to open yourself up. It is a place where you can care for yourself. We offer a variety of meditations for everyone, fostering an ongoing experience of growth together. Osho Nirvesha is dedicated to the development of consciousness and human potential, living in harmony with others and nature. The centre includes a small, 6-hectare farm at the foot of the Macchia della Magona, 6 km from the sea, near the village of Bibbona along the wine route to Bolgheri. We produce olive oil, jam, herb salt, and firewood for our own use. Your help is needed in the care and harvest of 250 olive trees and 50 fruit trees, tending the herb garden, and cutting firewood in our beautiful park with ancient trees. Other tasks include housekeeping and maintaining the houses and land. We offer shared accommodation and bathrooms in the houses, and in the summer, there are wooden huts and spaces for tents under the trees. Meals are vegetarian and communal. At times, you may be surrounded by many people, and at others, only a few. We have an internet connection and speak several languages. You are welcome to stay for at least two to three weeks or longer, if you enjoy being here and developing your consciousness is important to you.

name: Help at a Historic Italian Vineyard & Farm B&B (Italy)

location.address: Italy, San Giovanni a Piro Salerno

description: We offer a variety of tasks on this unique farm and Bed & Breakfast. Key responsibilities include tending to vineyards and winemaking, maintaining a large vegetable garden, and picking fruit and vegetables to make jams and preserves. You’ll also gather botanicals by the sea for our award-winning Gin Cilento Wild Coast, make liqueurs, and bottle wine, liqueurs, and aromatic vinegar. Additional tasks involve maintenance and gardening around the B&B and our 17th-century country estate, as well as assisting with guest services and kitchen support.

name: Work Stay at a Top-Rated Hostel (Costa Rica)
description: Want to spend 3 months living in paradise as part of our work-for-stay programme? If you're aged between 19 and 27, a team player, and have a guest-first attitude, we’d love to hear from you! Basic Spanish is helpful, but strong English skills are a must. A 3-month stay is preferred. What you’ll get: 🌴 24 hours of work per week 🌴 Staff dorm accommodation 🌴 Free breakfast + 50% off lunch and dinner 🌴 Free use of surfboards and bikes Room2Board is a locally owned boutique hostel and surf school located right on Jaco Beach, along Costa Rica’s stunning Central Pacific Coast. Brian, the owner, and Carolina, our general manager, are always on site to make sure everyone is happy and the hostel remains safe, clean, and full of good vibes. Our team includes both locals and like-minded world travellers, creating a welcoming environment where you’ll feel part of our global family from the moment you arrive. Room2Board was purpose-built with an eco-friendly design to offer surfers, backpackers, and adventure travellers a clean, affordable, and stylish place to stay. It combines the social vibe of a traditional hostel and surf camp with the comfort and amenities of a boutique hotel. We’re just 50 metres from the beach and a short 6-minute walk to the lively town of Jaco. On-site, we have a surf and tour shop, bar and restaurant, and a rooftop patio that’s perfect for sunset views or hammock naps. Daily yoga and surf lessons are also available at a discounted rate for volunteers.

name: Helping Out at a Buddhist Retreat Centre (England)
description: Helping 5 days a week - 2 days off every week. By helping to support the daily life of our peaceful Buddhist retreat centre, you will be involved in meaningful tasks such as working in the kitchen, assisting with household duties, and helping in our café. You will be part of a warm, international community, living and working alongside resident Buddhist monks, nuns, and lay practitioners from around the world. Beyond your volunteer hours, you are invited to join meditation classes and prayer sessions, offering a chance to deepen your own practice or simply experience a calm and reflective way of life. In your free time, you can enjoy our beautiful 38 acres of parkland and surrounding countryside, or explore nearby cities such as Derby (20 minutes away) and Birmingham (1 hour away) by public transport.

name: Our Hostel in Sofia Is Looking for Volunteers (Bulgaria)
description: Hostel №1 by Rules - multiple-time annual winner of the “Best Hostel in Bulgaria” award. Hostel N1 "No party and Many rules" is the safest and most peaceful hostel in Sofia because it is chosen by respectful and sensible guests only. Our hostel is located in the center of Sofia, close to most of the city’s attractions. We are looking for helpers (volunteers) to welcome guests, help with cleaning, entertain guests, and follow simple instructions. The most important qualities are being sociable and attentive to guests’ needs. We provide a bed, access to a kitchen, basic food, free trips, and Wi-Fi. The work is 5 hours per day, with 2 days off each week. The minimum stay is one month. There is no maximum period. Helpers will have the opportunity to visit all the sights of Sofia and travel around Bulgaria. If you don’t mind rolling up your sleeves for this unique experience, send us a message!

name: Experience a Farm Stay in the Lake District (England)

location.address: United Kingdom, Lake District National Park

description: We are fortunate to live on a farm in the beautiful Lake District, situated just on the edge of the National Park in Cumbria. The farm dates back to the mid-1600s and has seen various renovations and additions over the years, though some parts are still in need of a little TLC! My sister, my nephew and I all live and work on the farm. Together, we manage farmhouse holiday accommodation and a Friesian horse sanctuary. While we have big plans for the farm’s future, at present we could use a bit of help with the day-to-day running of things. Our farm is located on the outskirts of the charming village of Cartmel, home to a small racecourse, several excellent restaurants, Black Horses Ltd, and the famous sticky toffee pudding. It’s a 20-minute walk into the village, where you’ll also find a local grocery store and several cosy pubs frequented by friendly locals and visitors from around the world. The nearest town is Kendal, about a 20-minute drive away, though buses and trains are available from the nearby village of Grange-over-Sands. The scenery here is simply stunning. From the farm, perched high above the Cartmel Valley, we enjoy spectacular views of the surrounding countryside. The lush green pastures owe their vibrancy to the Lake District’s unpredictable weather, which ranges from sunny and dry to wet and windy. Both contribute to the soothing and restorative atmosphere of this unique landscape. We welcome couples, friends, or individual travellers. As a relaxed yet hard-working family, we do our best to ensure our guests, visitors, and helpers feel at home. We greatly value those who can follow instructions while also working independently.
