Please note that the only animals on the farm are horses, pet sheep, and dogs, though we do allow our neighbour to graze his cattle and sheep on the land. We are unable to assist with visa applications.

name: Empowering Children: Volunteer for a Brighter Future (Ghana)
description: Children here thrive when surrounded by love and care, giving their best to showcase their talents. A wonderful area is being transformed into a children’s park, which will serve as both a playground and an event space for various programmes. This vision can become a reality with your volunteering efforts, helping to make each child feel at home. We prioritise providing care and support for children and individuals with physical challenges, particularly those who have no one else to care for them. Many of these children face difficult circumstances, such as being orphaned, abandoned, neglected, or living on the streets. Our work focuses on meeting their essential needs, including food, education, and healthcare. Our primary goals include ensuring the full protection and development of every child, while encouraging their active involvement in family, school, and community life. We aim to create opportunities for sustainable livelihoods and income, establishing the foundations necessary for the survival, growth, protection, and active participation of all children in the future. Additionally, we strive to raise awareness and understanding of children’s rights, especially for those in vulnerable situations. *** IMPORTANT *** Volunteers must complete police vetting in their home country before undertaking roles involving tasks that primarily require regular access to or contact with children (under 18) or vulnerable adults.

name: Volunteer Opportunity in a Peaceful Greek Village (Greece)
description: 🌿 Volunteer Opportunity in a Peaceful Greek Village – Villa Averoff, Kirinthos 🌿 Are you looking to reconnect with nature, simplicity, and meaningful experiences? We’re inviting friendly and motivated volunteers to join us at Villa Averoff, our authentic countryside estate in Kirinthos, Greece – just a short drive from the sea and 2.5 hours from Athens. Our estate can host up to 30 guests and features charming historic buildings, beautiful gardens, a swimming pool, cosy outdoor spaces, and a variety of animals. We’re currently looking for volunteers to help with gardening, light cleaning, animal care, and general upkeep. ✨ What We Offer: – Free accommodation – Fully equipped kitchen with groceries provided – Tranquil surroundings in a picturesque village – An authentic taste of Greek countryside life 🕓 What We Ask: – Minimum stay of 10 days – 4–6 hours of help per day, 5 days per week – Basic English (Greek not required) If this sounds like your kind of experience, we’d love to hear from you! Please send us a short message with your available dates and a little bit about yourself. 📍 Location: Kirinthos village, Evia (approx. 2.5 hours from Athens) Let’s create a meaningful summer together 🌞🇬🇷

name: Experience Farm Life with Nature and Animals (Italy)
description: The Ark is a small rural community that lives by the principles of respect and sharing. We are an educational and social farm, convinced that this lifestyle—simple, close to nature, and far from the hustle and bustle of the city—can restore peace and serenity. We are located at the foot of Pollino Park, just 1.5 km from the sea, opposite the medieval village of Belvedere Marittimo. We grow crops and raise many types of animals in freedom. We work hard to ensure the survival of this project, which requires a great deal of care and attention. As a farm, our activities are varied: cultivation, animal care, educational visits for schools, childcare, cooking, cleaning, maintenance, and hosting guests in our reception area. We offer the opportunity to gain a wide range of experiences depending on the season, and to learn traditional Italian cuisine. We welcome volunteers of all ages, for a minimum stay of two weeks, in our main house where we also live. Meals are prepared and shared together, and vegetarians will be well catered for. In summer, volunteers can also enjoy the swimming pool and barbecue area. We don’t require any specific skills—just goodwill, respect, good manners, cleanliness, and a love for nature and animals. Most of all, we hope you’ll bring a joyful spirit and a desire to become part of our big family!

name: How to Become a Farmer: Learn the Essentials (Italy)
description: Our farm is very traditional and completely organic. We raise cows for milk and Parmigiano Reggiano production, and we have vineyards, fruits, and vegetables. We are focused on making our farm the first fully carbon-free farm, using only solar energy and biogas, not just for the buildings but also for the tractors and farming equipment. We aim to pass on our knowledge to young people who want to make farming their work and passion. We will teach you everything you need to start your own farm and support you with both our guidance and resources, including land and funding, to help you become an expert and independent farmer.

name: Help me build a House and Cat Sanctuary on Syros Island (Greece)
description: The cats are now becoming more accustomed to being petted and they all crave attention. It’s not possible to give them the attention they crave. So that would be your main job. Spending time with them inside and in the garden, feeding them, grooming them etc. I'm in the middle of a renovation of a traditional stone house and I look after 26 cats. I need help looking after the cats, with building works, repairs and additions, skilled or unskilled. There is a big community here that you can join in with if you wish, with swimming, clubs, parties, bbq’s etc. The house is in a peaceful rural area and traditionally Greet, but the beach is less than a minute away. Ermopouli is the year round neoclassical main town and is 10 minutes away. It is modern and lively but with a lot of history and architecture as well as a high old town. There are lots of beaches organised and natural with beautiful clear turquoise sea and Sandy beaches. Syros Island in Greece is spectacular all times of year.

name: Volunteer at HIVE Festival (eastern Germany)
description: HIVE Festival just keeps getting bigger and better every year! And yes, you guessed it — this year is no exception. Get ready for an incredible exploration of the most thrilling techno city on the map! For real: 10 different stages. Representing their genres are some of the biggest names in the world, combined with the most impressive industrial open-air location you’ve ever seen. Oh yeah — and a massive lake to swim in… and sandy beaches… at the festival and right next to the campsite. It’s insane! One important thing, though: a place like this doesn’t just appear out of thin air. It’s not created by techno fairies for everyone to consume and then disappear. To build a world of this magnitude, this city needs people — not only those who take, but those who build and maintain it, giving their sweat, love, time, and dedication to its creation and upkeep. We are not simply looking for people to work with — we are looking for citizens. Human beings who want to be part of a gathering of 30,000 freaks, not only to dance with them, but also to ensure that there is a place to dance. To provide a service to their community by working with it, supporting it, and offering assistance when needed. Do you want to experience the biggest, craziest, loudest and most unbelievable festival together with a lovely and driven community? Are you willing to be the bee that tends to the HIVE and get your hands dirty in exchange for being part of something far bigger than yourself? Here is your chance. As a crew, we all camp together and build a little pop-up community, taking care of our camp and letting connections grow between us. This makes it easy to get to know friendly people with whom you can share great moments during the festival. After arriving at our camp, we take some time to get familiar with each other and introduce everyone to the crew and our work. The next morning, we do a walk around the festival grounds to get to know the place. A shift plan will be created so that every

name: Teach English and Explore Northern Thai Culture (Thailand)
description: We are currently welcoming volunteers to assist us in several rural public schools, that are in need of support with English language teaching. Our international volunteers are placed in these schools to work alongside Thai English teachers in the classroom, helping to make a meaningful and lasting impact on the lives of students and their families. We invite you to come and teach Basic English to students from Kindergarten to Year 9 at one of our countryside schools. No knowledge of Thai is required! We are looking for fluent English speakers to help our students develop basic conversational and verbal communication skills. As a volunteer, you will engage directly with the students, with a local teacher present in the classroom to assist you whenever needed. While prior teaching experience is an advantage, it is not essential—what matters most is your enthusiasm and willingness to help! This is a wonderful opportunity to immerse yourself in Thai culture and language. You’ll have the chance to learn about local traditions, history, and cuisine while enjoying the peaceful rural setting surrounded by lush greenery, rice fields, and fruit farms. Our host community will prepare a variety of delicious Thai dishes for you, and you’ll be invited to explore historical sites, enjoy local life, and even visit nearby tourist attractions. We are more than happy to help you make the most of your experience here in Phayao Province.

name: Join Our Community Project in Trujillo (Peru)
description: Hi! We are Hilo Rojo, a small non-profit organisation based in Trujillo, a beautiful city on the northern coast of Peru. Since 2013, we have been working with children and families living in vulnerable communities, creating opportunities through education, community support and cultural exchange. We believe that every child deserves a safe place where they can learn, play and dream of a brighter future. At the heart of our work is La Escuelita, our after-school learning centre, where around 100 children come every afternoon. Here they receive homework support, take part in creative workshops, play games, practise sports, enjoy art activities and, most importantly, spend time in a safe and caring environment. One of the highlights of our programme is our English classes. Many of our children have very limited opportunities to practise English, so our international volunteers make a huge difference. You don't need to be a qualified teacher—we're simply looking for people who enjoy sharing their language, culture and enthusiasm through fun games, conversations, songs and interactive activities. Our work also extends beyond the classroom. Together with our psychologist and social worker, we regularly visit families to better understand their needs and strengthen our relationship with the community. These home visits help us support each child in a more personal and meaningful way. Every volunteer has something unique to offer. Whether you enjoy teaching, arts and crafts, sports, music, photography, social media, gardening, maintenance, administration or simply playing with children, we'll help you find the role that best matches your interests and skills. Volunteering with us is about much more than helping. It's about becoming part of our daily lives, learning about Peruvian culture, sharing meals, making friends from around the world and creating unforgettable memories together. We are proud to have welcomed volunteers from many different countries over the years. The

name: Experience African life while volunteering (Tanzania)
description: Welcome to PWM Family – Empowering Communities, Transforming Lives About Us & Our Core Mission We are a warm, loving family organization that welcomes international volunteers not as visitors, but as true members of our family. Based in Kibaha  in Coast Region and Igamba Village Mbozi district in New Songwe Region Tanzania, we provide accommodation, 3 local meals a day, and an authentic cultural experience. We believe in hard work, joy, and holistic well-being ensuring you feel mentally refreshed and spiritually inspired during your stay. ⚠️ Important Note for Applicants We seek dedicated, proactive, and passionate volunteers genuinely committed to making an impact. While we offer a welcoming home, we are NOT a free hostel for casual travelers. We invest our hearts and resources into our volunteers and expect maturity, commitment, and a true spirit of service in return. The Journey Behind Our Vision At age 15, our founder fled extreme hardship in Igamba, surviving homelessness sleeping under tree and eating on trash until arriving in Kibaha on a livestock train. Using his musical talent, he found shelter as a church pianist, where an Australian sponsor eventually funded his education. When cancer cut that sponsorship short, he didn't give up he taught himself English and computer repair, built a business, and returned to his roots to lift vulnerable youth. Thus, PWM Family was born. How You Can Make a Difference You can choose to serve in suburban Kibaha or rural Igamba Village across these key areas: Sustainable Farming: Hands-on work in our community farms to support food security. Education & Teaching: Teaching English, Math, Arts, or practical skills to children. Childcare & Recreation: Bonding, playing, and bringing joy to local youth. Fundraising & Grant Writing: Helping us secure grants and global partnerships. No Language Barrier: We welcome all languages! With open hearts and translation technology, communication is seamless. Ready to change lives? Join the

name: Help Empower Amazon Forest Communities (Peru)
description: You are welcome to share your skills and passions with a local community in the Amazon rainforest in Peru. This is a wonderful opportunity to support the local community while exploring this magnificent region. Your volunteer work will take place in Iquitos, a Peruvian port city and the gateway to jungle lodges and tribal villages of the northern Amazon. This is a personalised volunteering experience, meaning you will work with the project coordinator to find a role that best suits your skills. It’s a great chance to put your talents to use, and you are encouraged to offer new ideas and projects.

name: Help with the Restoration of a Castle (south of France)
description: Help with the restoration of an ancient small castle/farm of the 12th century in a magnificent and preserved landscape, South of France. I will be happy to welcome you and share some happy work moments in the South of France near the Pyrenees Mountains. I am restoring a building originating from the 12th century located at about 600 m altitude near the city of Foix (medieval city of about 10 000 inhabitants). It is an ancient small castle from the Cathar period transformed later on into a farm with about 20 hectares of meadows and forest in a magnificent and preserved mountainous landscape. There are many beautiful location to visit (including ancient Cathar castles) and possible activities in the surroundings (lake where you can swim or sail, mountain hiking, gliding, paragliding, rock climbing...). I definitely need help right now to restore a few wood windows (sanding and painting) and in the meadows and forest (cleaning, collecting wood...) but there are many other things to do. It can be for example helping with housework (cooking, cleaning...), with maintenance and repair of motorized tools, with construction work, with gardening or planting trees... depending on your skill and wishes. It can be useful if you have some experience with at least one of these tasks but in any case I'll be happy to teach if you are willing to learn. More important than your experience is your will to help, learn and share. Specifics are negotiable but I would generally expect 4-6 hours of work 5 days a week. It can be possible to include in your stay a few days in the city of Toulouse (regional capital of about 500 000 inhabitants, fourth largest city in France) if you are interested. It is a one hour drive by car. This is an Eco-friendly project and my family and I are vegetarian.

name: Join Semillas Life, an NGO in the Amazon (Peru)
description: Join Semillas Life, a grassroots NGO in the Peruvian Amazon, located in Codo del Pozuzo, Huánuco, where community-led conservation, culture, and creativity come together. We are seeking passionate volunteers to help protect the rainforest while making a meaningful impact in the local community. As an education volunteer, you will teach English through creative methods such as storytelling, music, and drama, inspiring local youth to value and protect their environment. Volunteers supporting the region’s unique Austro-German heritage help teach German, strengthening cultural identity and opening doors for future ecotourism opportunities. Those with artistic talents can contribute to our educational book projects by creating engaging illustrations and layouts that encourage children to care for the planet. Volunteers with digital marketing and communications skills help share our mission globally, managing social media, content creation, and strategy to expand our network of supporters. Beyond your specific role, you will immerse yourself in the “Lungs of the Earth” through guided rainforest trekking and jungle camping. You will experience authentic cultural exchange, learning to make chocolate from bean to bar, cooking traditional Peruvian dishes, and connecting with local families. We provide secure accommodation, 24/7 Wi-Fi, laundry facilities, and a motorcycle for exploring the stunning landscapes of the Pachitea River basin. Use your skills to protect the future and join the Semillas Life family today.

name: Live Off-Grid in the Beautiful South (Spain)
description: Live in the middle of nowhere in the south of Spain – no neighbours, just a few farmers passing by on their tractors during the day. My idea is to share this beautiful place with people who want to stay in the garden, in tents, vans, trailers, or in the caravan I’ve set up for visitors. It’s easy to slow down here, surrounded by raw nature, and gain a fresh perspective on everyday life. There are endless opportunities to help, be creative, and pick up new skills. Life here is simple, off the road and deeply connected with nature. The help needed can include gardening, cleaning, painting, maintenance or even building, depending on the skills you bring – there are always plenty of different tasks each day. This is an open space for open minds. You can enjoy the purity of nature, contribute to making this place even better, and perhaps even create something you’ve always dreamed of with a little support. You’ll have your own spacious caravan with a couch, kitchen, plenty of storage, and a bed.

name: Learn Organic Gardening in the Central Andes Jungle (Peru)
description: Our project aims to transform a traditional farm into a more ecological one, with a focus on sharing knowledge and raising awareness about organic farming. We want to extend this knowledge to the most vulnerable people in the surrounding area. On our farm, we currently grow organic cocoa, coffee, and bananas, which are the backbone of the local economy. We make chocolate from part of our harvest and roast coffee from our beans. Volunteers will have the opportunity to learn the entire process, ensuring there’s always a good cup of coffee or chocolate to enjoy. In addition, we maintain organic gardens for our own consumption and to learn farming techniques. We grow a variety of produce, including spinach, cabbage, lettuce, onions, peppers, beets, celery, herbs, cucumbers, caigua, pumpkins, tomatoes, and broccoli, as well as flowers. The farm also features fruit plants such as bananas, oranges, lemons, mandarins, tangelos, sweet lemons, papayas, starfruit, cocona, pineapples, as well as cassava and pituca. We have areas with natural water flow, which we plan to use for fish farming, and we also want to raise small animals like chickens, ducks, and guinea pigs. Our farm is diverse, with timber trees, bamboo, streams, large rocks, and a medium-sized river. It’s truly a blessed place. Through this project, we aim to share the culture of the central region of our country with the volunteers, creating culinary, artistic, and humanitarian fusions to help the most vulnerable members of our community. The goal of our volunteer program is to "learn to teach." Every week, we set aside one day to visit other farms in the area to help where needed and share what we’ve learned. For free time, we offer two optional outings to magical places in the area. On other days, we can also host workshops in macramé, jewellery-making (bracelets, necklaces, etc.), and other crafts. On weekends, we organise trips to more distant, beautiful villages, giving volunteers the chance to explore even m

name: House Renovation Project (Southern France)
description: Old village centre with all local services, set amidst beautiful countryside for walking and cycling. Own accommodation is 50 metres from the work site. Wi-Fi available. Work includes labouring, plasterboarding, plumbing, electrical work, flooring, lime plastering of internal and external walls, painting, and roofing.

name: Help us Create an Eco-Paradise (Wales)
description: Hello! We are seeking folks who would like to get involved and help us regenerate this small area of land (19 acres) to support a biodiverse ecosystem, grow food using permaculture and agroforestry approaches, and help humans rewild themselves (see our profile for more info). This project will need lots of help from both 'skilled' and 'unskilled' lovely humans. If you have any special skills in carpentry, organic food growing, herbal medicine, permaculture, off-grid systems, plumbing, or agroforestry we would definitely love to hear from you. If your only 'skill' is a desire to get stuck in we would love to have you too!

name: Volunteer at a Hostel Near Yosemite National Park (California)
description: We are looking for help with housekeeping and front desk duties for three hours per day in exchange for accommodation. We are located 25 miles from Yosemite National Park, in the gateway town of Groveland. The town offers a peaceful atmosphere where you can relax, unwind, and enjoy fresh air every day. There are also plenty of activities and attractions to explore in the area.

name: Eco Farm & Homestead Projects in Ardèche (France)
description: We’re Manon, Alessandro, and Brioche (I’m a dog) – a young family passionate about nature, travelling, independence, regenerative practices, agriculture, farming, gardening, trekking, and life. After almost ten years of working and travelling overseas, we felt the need to put down roots and transform our dream into reality. We bought an old farm stable in ruins (a dry-stone building) inside a very small village, along with a few hectares of old chestnut forest next to it. On one side, we are restoring the old building using eco-friendly techniques as much as possible to transform it into our home. On the other, we are developing a syntropic garden and a small orchard based on agroforestry principles, which will also be applied to parts of the chestnut forest. We’re located in South Ardèche, near the town of Joyeuse. The area is famous for its rivers, canyons, and forests, with plenty of hiking and biking trails, bathing spots, canyoning, rafting, climbing, historic villages, local markets, and – last but definitely not least – exceptionally good cheese! We would love to share this little corner of paradise with like-minded people who are keen to help us turn our dream into a tangible reality.

name: Volunteers Needed to Serve in the Church Community (Uganda)
description: Welcome to our community-based high school and outreach project near Jinja, Uganda. We work with students, young people, and families in a rural village community, helping provide education, mentorship, encouragement, and opportunities for personal growth. Our project is connected to a local Christian community that supports education and outreach activities within the surrounding area. We welcome volunteers from all backgrounds who would like to experience Ugandan culture while making a meaningful contribution to the lives of young people and the wider community. Volunteers may help with: • Spoken English and reading activities • Classroom assistance and academic support • Sports, debate, and leadership activities • Music, art, and creative programmes • Computer skills and digital learning • Photography, media, and social media • Youth mentorship and community outreach Professional teaching experience is not required. Kindness, flexibility, patience, and a willingness to learn and share experiences are the most important qualities. Volunteering with us is an opportunity to experience authentic Ugandan community life while building meaningful relationships and encouraging young people through education and cultural exchange.

name: Help students to stay safe and help them with learning (Tanzania)
description: We believe friends have a power to change and give new idea to girls who are the victim of harassment, abuse and violence and make girls to feel comfortable. Also to give them help to grow through various activities which contribute their living at the safe house, also children from the girls and from the community needs help in their learning. We are a small team and we need help to create better chain and give chance to volunteers from every corner of the World regardless their skills and experience or language to contribute to the cause there is a good and safe place to stay volunteers. *** IMPORTANT *** Volunteer applicants must undergo prior police vetting in their home country as this placement involves working/activities which includes access/contact with children (under 18s) and/or vulnerable adults.

name: Volunteer at Our Meditation and Yoga Retreat (Portugal)
description: Are you interested in contributing to Awakeland's Reforestation & Land Rehabilitation project? The weeks are structured as follows: - Week 1 - Soil regeneration project: Seeding and spreading straw bales for germination support. - Week 2 - Tree plantation in an irrigation context: Replacing lost trees with a mix of forest and fruit trees. - Week 3-4 - Wild forest and native tree plantation. Please note: The sequence of these stages is determined by weather conditions and may not follow this exact order. Some stages may also extend beyond 4 weeks. To secure your place, we require an advance payment of €70 to demonstrate your commitment. After the first week, we will review your progress. If you wish to extend your stay for another week or more, we can discuss it further. Upon completing at least one stage of the project (7 days or more), you will receive a Certificate of Participation detailing your hours and project contributions. In exchange, we request 6 hours of work, 5 days a week. We provide three vegan meals daily, accommodation in shared rooms or the option to camp with your own tent or campervan. This experience offers the opportunity to learn about reforestation and land rehabilitation using biodynamic and permaculture principles. Working hours: - 9am - 4pm - 1-2pm - lunch break - Plus regular group cleaning of communal spaces. What to bring: - Clothing suitable for warm and cold weather - Waterproof attire for rainy days - Warm bedding, a sleeping bag, and a pillow (nights can be cold in Monchique) - Sturdy boots suitable for working on the land (waterproof recommended) - Eco-friendly body products, toothpaste, etc. Please note: Our showers and toilets are ecological, and the water flows directly into our land and trees. Therefore, please refrain from using chemical products in the showers or toilets. Bring only eco-friendly products. We look forward to planting trees with you soon!

name: Nourish, Educate, and Cultivate: Investing in a Child's Future (Kenya)

location.region: and Cultivate: Investing in a Child's Future
location.address: Nourish, Educate, and Cultivate: Investing in a Child's Future

description: The Oasis Centre, situated in a slum area, serves as a beacon of hope for countless children yearning for a safe haven to play, laugh, and enjoy nourishing meals. It provides a space where they can simply be children, momentarily liberated from the challenges they face at home. Every Saturday, with the generous support of well-wishers, we are able to provide over a hundred children with affordable, nutritious meals—often the only healthy food they eagerly anticipate. The program is a joyful experience, akin to a family gathering, where children actively participate in cooking, acquiring valuable culinary skills. Additionally, they showcase their talents through music and talent shows, ensuring a day filled not only with satisfaction but also with personal growth and newfound skills. Indeed, there is no meal more gratifying than one prepared with love alongside these innocent, inspiring children. We would greatly appreciate any assistance volunteers are capable of providing, particularly with the feeding program and educating children who are unable to attend school. Our primary goal is to expand the centre to accommodate as many children as possible. *** IMPORTANT *** Prior to placement, volunteer applicants must undergo police vetting in their home country. This requirement is necessary due to the nature of the work/activities involved, which may include access to or contact with children (under 18) and/or vulnerable adults.

name: Learn about Organic Gardening & Permaculture (Canada)
description: We provide a wonderful opportunity to learn about organic gardening, permaculture, with a large variety of different food plants. We understand the need for balance between work and recreation, so we offer a different arrangement to traditional 'Helper/Apprentices'. We are asking for only 2 hours of your help per day (14 hours per week) in exchange for accommodation and utilities only (you must provide your own food). Share fully furnished kitchen and living area and laundry ,wireless Internet provided. Sorry, we can’t provide meals (except for some garden produce that you can arrange to trade extra hours of help for) you will need to have enough monetary funds to supply your own personal needs. We allow helper`s to help for 2 hrs per day or two 7-hour days if they like (or we can try and arrange for personal schedule ) and have 5 free days for exploring the Kootenays. We have lived here for 30 years and know a lot of people in the area, it seem that lots of locals look for some temporary help on & off during the season. Helper`s have a private clean room in our home or in one of three of our caravans,

name: Help with Permaculture, DIY & Farming (Portugal)
description: This is a rural farm set within 27 hectares of cork oak forest. We aim for self-sufficiency through a domestic economy based on organic family farming. We offer direct and genuine contact with nature, an organic garden, and farm animals. We are looking for people with knowledge of permaculture, bioconstruction, carpentry/joinery, agriculture, animal care, fencing, and DIY (including detergents, soaps, and salves). Lisbon, the Portuguese capital, is just a 30-minute drive away, across the Tagus River via the Vasco da Gama Bridge. Current projects include building a shelter in the pasture for the animals (goats and donkeys) to protect them during the winter. We already have a dry toilet with a shower, but we plan to build another one closer to the social and camping area. During the summer, we need help applying protective treatments to the wood in our house. We also plan to build a small greenhouse/nursery to support our garden.

name: Help and Learn with Us as We Develop a Forest Garden (Scotland)
description: We live on an isolated croft approximately 25 miles from Inverness. We can teach permaculture, foraging and food preservation skills. We are in the heart of the Highlands, so you will have plenty of free time to travel independently, but if we have time we are happy to take you on a trip or two to places you can’t easily reach by bus. I am new here, but have many years of excellent reviews on two other hosting sites, which I am very happy to share. (I will update this fully later—until then, please feel free to ask any questions.) What you can gain from us (other than English practice): country skills. 🔹We are working towards greater self-sufficiency and growing our own food wherever possible. There will be opportunities to plant, grow, tend, and harvest food, as well as learn poultry husbandry. 🔹Occasionally, there may be the opportunity to learn how to prepare a small animal (such as rabbit or chicken) for the table, if you wish. This is entirely optional, and for the most part our diet is vegetable-based. 🔹We also forage and are happy to share knowledge of wild foods, including greens, mushrooms, and berries. 🔹We make jams, chutneys, beers, wines, and more, and would love to share these skills with you.

name: Explore Busan While Helping at Our Guesthouse (Korea)

location.address: Make Friends, Party, and Explore Busan

description: Welcome to Midpoint! We are looking for social, outgoing, and fun people who enjoy hanging out together, can make every day enjoyable for everyone, and work well with the rest of the team. This is a newly opened guesthouse, and we need people who can bring a positive, energetic vibe. You will help five days a week, for a maximum of three hours a day, with cleaning. In return, we provide free ramyun, rice, WiFi, laundry, and plenty of fun. Looking for a place to build friendships, party in the city, enjoy coffee at Jeonpo Coffee Street, and live your best life? Our environment is chill and lively, with game nights, karaoke, movie nights, and dancing at the club. You’ll share bunk beds with seven other girls, with an attached private kitchen for staff use only and a private bathroom. This is the perfect place for anyone who knows how to bring a vibe!

name: Help with House Restoration and Projects (Netherlands)
description: We live in and enjoy a house from the 1920s that is in need of complete renovation. We also run several side projects, including our guest house, our country garden, and much more. We love having friendly people around who want to help make this place a pleasant living environment. We are looking for people who enjoy renovation work, cooking, and cleaning.

name: Little Paradise – Meditation & Retreat Centre (northern Germany)
description: We are a small modern monastery and meditation & retreat centre located near Hamburg, Germany. Our centre offers an environment for meditation, self-reflection, and inner stillness — a place to calm down, reconnect with oneself, and enjoy the peace of nature. It is particularly well suited for experienced meditators who value quiet surroundings. We welcome volunteers who would like to spent retreat time with us and would like to help us in any of the following areas: including cleaning and housekeeping, repair and maintenance work, gardening, administrative tasks, processing apples from the garden, among other. As a retreat center dedicated to spiritual practice, we ask that volunteering here aligns with your own longing for spiritual deepening. This should be your main purpose for coming, alongside the volunteer work. If you do not long for such activities or if you have other main purposes in your free time - like digital work, sightseeing, etc. -  we are not the right place for you. We are two permanent residents and provide three private guest rooms in our meditation center. Visitors have often shared that their time here has brought them: - a regular daily meditation routine, - a sense of inner peace and stillness, - a deepening of spiritual understanding, - inner transformation on many levels, - inspiration, clarity, and energy to pursue new projects. We will support you on this journey. Daily life here includes morning and evening group meditations, which are attended by residents, guests, and volunteers. You should only apply if you genuinely wish to take part in these shared practices. It is important that you already have your own practices or themes you wish to explore during your stay. Without this, you may quickly become bored. Beginners on the spiritual path have often found the experience challenging, as our daily life carries a strong spiritual focus with elements of monastic living.

name: Helpers Needed for Housework and Gardening (Greece)
description: We are a warm and welcoming family living in the picturesque suburbs of Kastoria, Greece. Our home is situated in a tranquil setting surrounded by nature, yet conveniently close to the charming city of Kastoria, renowned for its stunning lake, rich history, and vibrant culture. We are seeking enthusiastic and friendly volunteers to join our family and assist with housework and gardening. Whether you bring prior experience or simply a willingness to learn and contribute, we’d love to welcome you. Your responsibilities will primarily involve helping us keep our home clean and organised, as well as lending a hand in the garden. Here’s what you can expect: - Housework: Light cleaning, tidying shared spaces, doing laundry, and occasionally assisting with cooking or meal preparation. - Gardening: Watering plants, weeding, pruning, planting flowers or vegetables, and helping maintain our outdoor spaces. - Creative Projects: If you have a flair for creativity, we’d love your ideas for improving our garden or home décor. The work is not overly demanding, and we strive to make it a collaborative and enjoyable experience. Your daily schedule will be flexible, usually requiring 4–5 hours of work, with ample time to relax and explore. What We Offer in Exchange - Accommodation: A cosy private room in our home, complete with access to all essential amenities, including internet. - Meals: We’ll provide home-cooked meals and would love to share some of our favourite Greek dishes. Please let us know if you have any dietary preferences or restrictions. - Local Experiences: Kastoria is famed for its lake, Byzantine churches, and traditional architecture. We’re happy to guide you to the best spots and occasionally organise trips to nearby attractions. - Cultural Exchange: We love learning about different cultures and look forward to sharing our traditions, stories, and local customs with you. Our family consists of two adults and two young children. We’re friendly, laid-back, and value

name: Homestay in the Lovely Malvern Hills (England)
description: Hello, my partner John and I have had a wonderful time sharing our home with volunteers, many of whom have become lasting friends. We now have a vacancy, and both short and longer stays are welcome. We live as a family and enjoy many things together with our volunteers. We live in a four-bedroom modern dormer bungalow in a beautiful village, with a shop and bus stop just a three-minute walk from the house. We live with my father, who is 89 years old and has moderate memory loss. He is calm and relaxed. My father enjoys having volunteers around and likes watching television with them so that he is not alone. He also enjoys helping volunteers improve their English pronunciation. Volunteers are welcome to work on their laptops while sitting with my father. He is used to this and still enjoys their company. I also need help with keeping the house clean and with simple vegetarian cooking. We enjoy sharing simple international food with our volunteers and have plenty of vegetarian cookbooks for ideas. We have a friendly and well-mannered older lady dog, a Labrador–Springer cross called Teal. She loves going for walks with volunteers in the morning and evening. We would love to welcome someone who could spend time with my very polite and placid father, help with housework, cook a simple vegetarian evening meal, and walk our gentle dog twice a day. It is really just a matter of joining in with family life and helping with daily tasks. The accommodation offered consists of two very nice single ground-floor rooms (not shared), with an adjacent wet room. We are a vegetarian family who practise Buddhism. We live in a village at the foot of the beautiful Malvern Hills, and you would be welcome to join us on weekend visits to local places of interest, walks, kayaking, cycling around the countryside, and perhaps an evening at the Malvern Spa. There is also the opportunity to try meditation and attend talks at the Buddhist centre three miles from our home if you are staying longer.

name: Farm Stay & Holistic Retreat in the Himalayas (Nepal)
description: Welcome to Our Farm Stay and House-Sitting Experience in the Himalayas. Nestled in a serene eco-village surrounded by lush forests, our Forest Retreat House offers a unique opportunity to connect with nature, embrace holistic healing, and experience rural Nepali life. At the heart of our initiative is Mind, Movement, and Mountain (3M)—a holistic approach that integrates mental well-being, physical activity, and the majestic beauty of the Himalayas. I am a graduate of Schumacher College, UK, with a Master’s in Mind, Movement, and Ecology, as well as a second Master’s in Applied Ecology from Evenstad, Norway. My passions include big cats, holistic healing, and regenerative tourism. While I will be available on weekends and holidays, my brother Bipin, my wife Binita, and my mother Narayani will be your guides, introducing you to Nepali cuisine and traditions. You’ll also meet our playful four-year-old, Binayak, who loves catching dragonflies and grasshoppers! While our holistic Ayurvedic healing centre is still in development, we welcome volunteers for house-sitting, forest-farm management, and fish keeping. This is a chance to co-create a unique experience, blending relaxation with light farm work. Expect a rustic and reflective environment—perfect for a digital detox and immersing yourself in the tranquil rhythms of rural life. What We Offer 🏡 Stay in our eco-friendly home with a well-equipped kitchen (no refrigerator), surrounded by vegetable gardens where we grow cabbage, cauliflower, and spinach. 🌿 Engage in farm tasks, explore forests and rivers, and participate in local ceremonies and traditional Nepali cuisine. Experience the soothing sounds of nature and the earthy scent of village life. 🤝 Become part of a welcoming eco-village that is familiar with volunteers, offering a supportive space for cultural exchange and personal growth. We seek volunteers to support the development of our holistic Ayurvedic healing yoga ashram. You will have the freedom to live inde

name: Volunteer at a Buddhist Meditation Centre (England)
description: Living here is not just volunteering — for many people, it becomes a unique and meaningful life experience. Our Buddhist meditation centre is set in the peaceful countryside of the Lake District, surrounded by nature, silence, and a supportive international community. Days here are calm, simple, and often deeply refreshing — very different from everyday life. In exchange for around 5 hours of help per day (5 days per week), you will receive: 🔹 Shared accommodation in a peaceful setting 🔹 Fresh vegetarian or vegan meals 🔹Free access to meditation classes 🔹The opportunity to join daily prayers and spiritual activities 🔹Plenty of free time to rest, reflect, or explore the lakes and mountains 🔹 A chance to learn practical and creative skills Volunteers can get involved in different areas of the centre depending on their interests and current needs. You may have the opportunity to help in: 🔹Our unique and inspiring Art Studio, 🔹Spend time in our welcoming World Peace Café, 🔹Support the kitchen team, 🔹Help care for the gardens, 🔹Or assist in preparing rooms for retreat guests. A typical day might include helping in the café or garden in the morning, sharing meals with the community, and joining a meditation session or simply enjoying the peaceful surroundings in the evening. Volunteers usually spend their free days hiking, visiting nearby villages, or relaxing and enjoying the quiet atmosphere of the centre. We are looking for people who are: 🔹Open-minded and respectful 🔹Happy to be part of a shared community life 🔹Willing to help with a variety of practical tasks 🔹Curious to experience a slower, more mindful way of living You don’t need any experience with Buddhism — everyone is welcome. Some volunteers come for meditation, others for nature, and many simply need a break from busy modern life. Whatever your reason, this place offers a rare opportunity to slow down, reconnect, and experience something different.

name: Development & Cultural Centre Volunteering (Thailand)
description: 🌿 **Join Volunt2Thai – Make a Real Difference in Rural Thailand!** 🌱✨ Work alongside **Raimund**, our founder, his family, and local villagers in daily life and community projects. Every day brings hands-on learning, meaningful connections, and the chance to **support the education of underprivileged children in the Isan region**. ❤️🙏 --- ## 🌿 Volunt2Thai Campus & Cultural Centre A **non-profit village development project** in Northeast Thailand. Since 2013, we’ve focused on **educating children from rural communities** through **free English classes** and creative activities in local schools and on our campus. We are a **heart-led, grassroots project**, built on trust, cultural exchange, and long-term relationships — not a business. ❤️ **Our Mission** Education opens doors. By supporting children in rural schools and empowering volunteers to use their talents — including medical, creative, or educational expertise — we help create **brighter futures** for these children. --- ## 🌾 Experience Rural Life Volunt2Thai is **off the tourist trail** — no bars, parties, or backpacker crowds. Instead, enjoy: * 🌾 Rice fields * 🌳 Forests * 🛕 Local temples * 🏡 Authentic village life With guidance from Raimund and local leaders, you’ll be fully integrated into the community, gaining **deep insight into Thai village culture**. --- ## 🌟 What Makes Us Special We value your **unique talents** and encourage you to develop your own projects — creative, practical, musical, technical, and of course educational. We also welcome **physiotherapists and occupational therapists** to support elders, including stroke patients, improving well-being and mobility. --- ## 🏫 Support Local Schools Help children with: * 📚 Creative & conversational English * 🎨 Arts, crafts & music * ⚽ Sports & movement games * 💻 Basic computer skills * 🎬 Photography, short films & storytelling Our campus also has a **Thai boxing gym**, where sports build confidence and discipline. Volunteers with fitness or martial ar

name: Volunteering: Nursery School and Sustainability Projects (Tanzania)
description: We’re friendly, and the community around us is amazing. Mafinga is a large town in southern Tanzania. Our project is young but has grown rapidly, and we continue to develop our vision. We need support to expand and maintain the gardens, renovate and build new structures, and to learn about the latest developments in sustainability. One of our main goals is to raise awareness through education, helping to prevent hardship and eradicate poverty. When children learn important lessons from an early age, those lessons tend to stay with them for life. We also provide informal education, teaching self-reliance, the importance of recycling and sustainability, and good hygiene practices. By profession, I am a teacher, and we run the nursery on a voluntary basis. We currently work with a team of four people. Recently, we acquired three hectares of land, where we have planted potatoes, beans, peas, and will soon add avocado trees. This is a new permaculture project that will continue to expand. Our intention is to use the food we grow in the children’s meal programme and sell any surplus to support the school.

name: OpenmindProjects Welcomes Volunteers to Asia (Thailand)
description: We welcome you to volunteer, whether you’re on a career break, holiday, gap year, travelling with your family, retired, or seeking an interesting internship. We offer meaningful volunteering opportunities in Southeast Asia, where you can volunteer, teach, and learn too. Share your skills and energy to empower underprivileged young students! 🌏 Corporate Volunteering Corporate volunteering is increasingly popular among staff and employees. Make a positive impact with us as a corporate volunteer. Gain valuable skills, strengthen your company culture, and contribute to meaningful local change. 🎓 Internship Abroad Are you looking for an interesting, impactful internship abroad? Join us as an intern and share your skills while gaining valuable experience with our award-winning NGO in Thailand. Boost your CV and make a real difference — both to yourself and to the people you help! 🏖️ Career Break or Holiday Break Take a break from your career or spend part of your holiday volunteering! Make a difference in the lives of young, less privileged students. Volunteer with us in Asia, gain valuable new experiences, and help create positive change. Learn more about our career-break volunteering opportunities. 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family Volunteering Make a difference together as a family! Volunteer while travelling in Thailand or Southeast Asia. Your children can volunteer for free and gain valuable cross-cultural and life experience, while you help and teach local children. 🧳 Gap Year Students Students and graduates — make your gap year unforgettable. Volunteer with us at Openmind Projects. Earn academic credits, help local students, and develop valuable cultural and social skills that will last a lifetime. 🌟 Volunteering in Retirement Want to spend some of your retirement making a difference? Share your wisdom and experience by training underprivileged students in English or other subjects. Your skills can leave a lasting, positive impact on the lives of young learners.

name: Volunteer at a Hostel in Central Tirana (Albania)
description: Our hostel is a budget-friendly hostel with a nice terrace. We would like to connect with travellers who can help with general chores. The work is not complicated or demanding. We simply ask for a commitment of around 4 hours per day, and the rest of the day is free for you to enjoy the hostel, meet other travellers, or explore Tirana. After your volunteer stay, you will be added to our hostel friends list and can enjoy a 50% discount on shared dorms if you need an easy, affordable place to stay in Tirana in the future.

name: Vineyard Farm Stay in the Hills (Northern Italy)
description: Hello! I’m Massimiliano, and I live with my business partner, Riccardo, and our two dogs, Echo and Ettore. We are based in a beautiful location nestled in the hills of northern Italy, surrounded by nature and vineyards. We live sustainably, taking care of our animals – chickens, a bull, and two cows – as well as our vegetable patch and vineyards, where we produce our own natural wines. It’s a peaceful and isolated place, yet only a few minutes from the nearest village and less than an hour’s drive from Milan. Life here moves at a slower, more enjoyable pace. We love spending our time playing music, singing karaoke, and cooking a variety of dishes. I’m a passionate cook who enjoys experimenting with new recipes, drinking good wine, and watching wonderful sunsets. We also welcome guests to our home, offering a genuine rural hospitality experience. We cook and eat together, sharing ideas, stories, and laughter. Our volunteers help in communal areas such as the house, vegetable garden, common spaces, vineyard, and cellar. Together, we create a warm, friendly, and creative environment.

name: Support Rural Development while Embracing Nature's Beauty (Vietnam)
description: Nui Tuong is dedicated to uplifting the community in rural Vietnam. We provide educational and vocational training for local children and youth, alongside our eco-tourism initiatives which help fund our community projects. Visitors to our site can enjoy accommodation, fresh food, and guided tours. Our small garden practices sustainable farming, serving as an educational tool to teach children about sustainable agriculture. We also promote awareness of healthy lifestyles among both locals and visitors to Nui Tuong. Our farm typically hosts 4-6 people, with children often present after school. Surrounded by a river, pond, farm, rice fields, and forest, it offers a quiet and peaceful atmosphere. Volunteering here offers opportunities to immerse yourself in Vietnamese culture and cuisine, as well as engage with our team. Accommodation includes shared rooms with an English-speaking team in a rustic, cozy space with a refreshing river view. We seek volunteers who are passionate about community contribution and eager to learn. Traveling from Ho Chi Minh City is convenient by bus. Please kindly note that we are unable to accommodate strict vegan or vegetarian diets. However, in case you are vegan or vegetarian, it would be great if you are comfortable eating vegetables that may be cooked with meat. If you're interested in our projects, please get in touch with us!.

name: Help Rescue Street Dogs in Huanchaco (Peru)
description: Hello! My name is Uchi, and for more than 20 years I have dedicated my life to helping abandoned animals in Huanchaco, a beautiful beach town on the northern coast of Peru. Huanchaco al Rescate is a non-profit animal rescue project that provides shelter, food, medical care, sterilisation, and love to dogs and cats that have been abandoned, abused, or left on the streets. Currently, we care for around 40 rescued dogs, each with their own story and a second chance at life. Our mission is not only to save animals but also to promote responsible pet ownership, animal welfare education, and community support. Volunteers become part of our family and help us continue this important work. We welcome kind-hearted people who love animals and would like to contribute to a meaningful project while experiencing authentic Peruvian culture.

name: Eco-Village Requiring Marketing & Household Skills (Denmark)
description: It is a place to grow together, but also to have fun together. We are currently looking for people with the following skills: Social Media Coordinator, Content creator & Videographer, Student Coordinator, Handymen/handywomen for maintenance, Hosting, Gardening and Cleaning, People who enjoy cooking, and web designer. Our vision is a world where we compassionately connect with ourselves, each other and the environment. Our mission is to accelerate the transition needed in relation to health, work, community and resources on personal, cultural and social levels in order to secure optimal living conditions for ourselves and future generations. Our aim is to be a living example of a new paradigm and an oasis for people who want to heal, live more sustainably and contribute to a new world. We have plenty of rooms available, so everyone can enjoy a private room with an en-suite bathroom. During their free time, volunteers can enjoy a range of activities including basketball, volleyball, football, table tennis, table football and many more. There is a fitness room and there are daily yoga lessons.

name: Spanish Speakers: Help our Multi-Business Family (Finland)

location.address: Soulful Photographer,  Business Owner & Home Mom! 🦋

description: We are a warm, open-minded, and high-energy family of 4 (soon to be 5 in July! 👶) living in the beautiful lakeside city of Järvenpää, Finland. We love to CREATE — from our funky & rustic handcrafted home in Järvenpää, Finland, and our nearby creative hub @ainoastudio.fi, to our dream van @the_vani — not forgetting our magical forest cottage in Pertunmaa @biitsikeidas. Our family includes two adults, a 3-year-old daughter, a 14-year-old bonus daughter, an upcoming baby due in July plus our kind, fluffy dog (allergy-free)! Since Spain is strongly included in our next creation - we are very interested in learning Spanish from you! ¿Hablas español? ¡VAMOS! We live in a vibrant, multi-generational community in Järvenpää, filled with kids of all ages and grandparents right next door. This role is perfect for a social butterfly who enjoys being around people and thrives in a lively, child-friendly atmosphere. You will stay in a unique environment with your own room and access to a proper Finnish sauna. Our home has two floors, a front and back yard, and a rustic sauna. - 1 minute walk to a grocery store - 10 minute drive to beautiful Järvenpää - 8 minute walk to train station - within 35 minutes you can go to Helsinki City Center! We’d love to get you fired up—if you have big plans for your own life or projects, we will help you get things moving. We value kindness, respect, openness and positive energy, and we are looking for someone hands-on, reliable, and comfortable around kids. An entrepreneurial mindset is a huge plus; we love people who are proactive and can tackle challenges independently. You don’t need to be a pro at everything—a practical, ‘can-do’ mindset is what matters most. We are also very open to DIY or ‘handyman’ types who prefer focusing on technical tasks, renovations, and maintenance over childcare. Just keep in mind that a bit of help with daily household flow is needed from everyone.

name: Potato Lovers Wanted – Help in Our Potato Hotel (Germany)
description: We are a community of potato lovers based in Wendland, in the east of the Lüneburger Heide, Lower Saxony. Our region in northern Germany is well connected, with Berlin, Hamburg and Hannover just about two hours away. Our passion is growing potato varieties from around the world, cooking tasty dishes and sharing them with our guests. We also publish our favourite recipes on our blog. As Germany’s only Potato Hotel and restaurant, we are always looking for support with a wide range of tasks. Your work might include preparing breakfast, helping in the restaurant, housekeeping, and cleaning. We also need help in the garden and potato fields, cultivating rare varieties. Since we heat with wood, there is plenty to do with chopping wood and felling trees. If you enjoy cooking – especially if you know some special potato recipes – you’re very welcome to join us in the kitchen. During your stay, you can also improve your German while working with us. Gaining experience abroad is valuable for anyone wanting a career in hospitality, and we offer just that. Our helpers have created a book filled with ideas for free-time activities – from cycling and hiking tours to bus trips and local sights – which every new helper is invited to contribute to. We provide bicycles for trips to the nearby town of Lüchow (4 km) or further afield. Car rides can often be arranged to Lüchow, and sometimes to Dannenberg, Hitzacker or Lüneburg – all towns with picturesque medieval centres. The River Elbe, once part of the German-German border, is also nearby and well worth a visit for those interested in German history.

name: Immerse Yourself in a Farm Stay & Cultural Exchange (Kenya)
description: We're dedicated to building a better society across various communities in Kenya 🇰🇪, with a special focus on children, as we believe they are the future leaders. Nurturing and guiding them toward positive growth is key to achieving our goal of a thriving society. We actively support children's talents, such as football and music, to boost their confidence. By investing in books and promoting avid reading, we help foster articulate individuals who can express themselves effectively. Our weekly feeding programs involve cooking with the children, creating bonding moments. Additionally, we conduct mentorship programs that address crucial topics like menstrual health and mental well-being, particularly in communities where the needs of the girl child are often overlooked. We also have a small family farm that supports all our projects, especially by providing resources for our activities. We have established our own Library space where children have a safe space to do their homework after school.We also carry out story telling activities and skills activities for the youth. We invite you to join us on our journey of growth and the creation of beautiful moments together. Our projects always focus on unity, growth, and development as we collaboratively identify challenges and work toward solutions. Beyond our projects, we welcome you to experience our culture firsthand with our family.We welcome guest to our farm house and they get to help with poultry farming and urban farming maintainance of our garden. Note:The fee goes only towards supporting miss mary with waterbill electricity and the balance goes to the children program donation. You are all welcome! Remember, no act is too small in the service of humanity. Be part of an African experience—stay with me and my community.

name: Help with Garden & Animals (Northern Ireland)
description: I'm Johnny. I live in Castlewellan, County Down, close to the beautiful east coast and the Mourne Mountains. I love music and gardening. I live in a small, comfortable cottage near the Mourne Mountains and the seaside town of Newcastle. I enjoy wild swimming in the sea and in rivers, and—though perhaps a little less enthusiastically—walking in the mountains. I work as a wedding DJ and also play the saxophone. Musicians are especially welcome, although it’s certainly not necessary to be one. I enjoy showing visitors around my local area and hosting people from different places. I especially love it when guests share a taste of their culture by cooking traditional dishes from their country. I keep chickens and ducks for fresh eggs, and I grow vegetables and fruit in the garden. I’d appreciate some help keeping everything running smoothly and restoring my polytunnel, which was damaged in a storm. As I work quite a lot, an extra pair of hands would be very helpful. I have bicycles available for volunteers, and my cottage is just one mile from a beautiful forest park.

name: Help in Sculpting Studio, with Gardening & Harvesting Grapes (Italy)
description: Your volunteer stay will enrich you both culturally and professionally. You will enjoy good food and good wine produced by me. Hello, my name is Alberto, I have always practiced the profession of sculptor, my activity takes place in the studio near Siena, a medieval city rich in artistic complexes. My artistic activity is articulated in the use of any material capable of fulfilling the dialectical and visual expression, therefore I work in bronze, marble, wood, ferrous and non-ferrous materials, even the latest generation plastic materials, in order to learn or refine in many techniques. We often go to the foundry, or to Carrara in various marble workshops. I recommend a minimum stay of no less than a month so that you can learn many things. The help required is very varied for beginners who want to learn the rudiments of the art just help in making the pieces work, pass me the various tools, and why not under my control you try to use various electric tools, for the most experts there will be more demanding tasks, so as you can see. I also need someone (as long as  they are an art lover) to help with gardening, harvesting grapes and olives in my country house. At certain times of the year.

name: Feed Cats and look after flowers in Sicily (Italy)

location.address: Feed Cats and look after flowers in Sicily, Italy
location.country: Feed Cats and look after flowers in Sicily

description: I can host only 1 person. I am a retired University teacher of Economics in Palermo, Italy. I own a villa with a large garden: 3.200 square meters and large pool salt water. It's situated in Balestrate, close to Palermo, a beautiful countryside and beachside (km. 4 from the sea) area, close to amazing archaeological sites and interesting monumental towns: Segesta, Selinunte, Mozia, Monreale, etc. I need help to feed and take care of my friendly sweet cats (they live in their large garden kingdom): fill their bowls twice a day (morning and evening) with biscuits and gnam-gnam morsels for cats, change the water every morning. Also some of my flower plants will need your help: watering every 2 days in summer, no need or just attention in other seasons. Basic upkeep of the home is welcome. My home of residence is my villa is and my second house is in Palermo. Happens that I leave in the morning and come back in the evening if possible. It means that also when I am there I haven't enough time to take care of my cats as I would and they need. That's why your help is very important for me and them, thank you. You will have your total privacy, independence and apartment. Will be an option for you to join to my table to enjoy my Italian (excellent) cooking and anyway I will provide you all food and drinks during the stay. If you like cats and (light) gardening you will be very welcome and will have your own apartment: large bedroom and bathroom, independent access.

name: Everything You Need for a Pleasant Stay in Restelo (Portugal)
description: Working and having fun at the same time? Yes, it’s possible! Everything you need for a pleasant stay in a warm and relaxed atmosphere. Both dorms and private rooms available. High-speed Wi-Fi (500 Mbps). Fully equipped kitchen. Quiet area. Direct bus to the city centre. Near train stations, gardens, beaches, supermarkets, cafés, restaurants, and terraces. It’s up to you to choose between the sea and the countryside, the centre of Lisbon, crowded or quiet beaches, and the tranquillity of the gardens and surrounding hills. Activities available all year round!

name: Native Plant Restoration and Wildlife Habitat Management (Missouri)
description: Hamilton Native Outpost strives to provide a variety of adapted native plants and the knowledge to establish and maintain them for ecosystem restoration, wildlife habitats, grazing, and enhancing the beauty of low-maintenance landscapes. Livestock adapted to the environment is a complementary enterprise. We need help primarily with the removal of invasive species/plants using hand tools. On-the-job training will be provided for identifying native and non-native plant species. Additional tasks may include planting, hand-harvesting, working with cattle and bison herds, fence repair, vehicle maintenance, order fulfillment, and computer work. Availability is mostly from May to June, with potential for more options on an individual basis. We have space for camping and boondocking are also available. Field management involves the use of hand tools, fence mending, and possible work with bison or cattle. The Ozark National Scenic Riverways is just a short drive away! Foraging and fishing are available right on the property, and the town is nearby with plenty of options for groceries and dining.

name: Veganic Permaculture & Reforestation in the Amazon (Ecuador)
description: We welcome volunteers who: ✅ Are vegan ✅ Practice natural hygiene (no chemical products, pharmaceuticals, etc.) 🌴🌞 Welcome Jungle Friends 🌞🌴 "This is sure to be an eye-opening experience that will change your life forever." If you’re the type of person who values living freely in nature, growing an abundance of food, exploring jungle rivers and waterfalls, and being part of something truly helping to heal the earth, we invite you to learn more about our reforestation and food forest project in the Amazon of Ecuador. As you may know, the Amazon — one of the most biodiverse and sacred ecosystems on Earth — is under attack. Huge areas are being destroyed every day, mostly for animal agriculture and monoculture farming, which poison the earth and displace its wild beauty. Amongst this destruction, we are rewilding 3.5 hectares of land, transforming what used to be cattle pasture and monoculture plantations into a thriving, abundant, biodiverse organic food forest packed with tropical fruits, native plants, flowers, medicinal plants, and big jungle vibes. Think: Garden of Eden… with machetes and pineapples. There are many adventures and exploration opportunities here, connecting with the land, the locals, and yourself. This is a place where you can be free to be you, accepted for who you are, and experience a simpler way of life. This is earth-based, minimalistic living with the abundant songs of birds, heartfelt efforts planting dreams in the soil, our living energy caring for the land, and exploring the vast rainforest around us. Sharing stories, making music, and falling asleep to the orchestra of the jungle under starlit skies. The weather is warm and tropical year-round, creating ideal conditions to grow a wide variety of vegetation. We harvest bananas, pineapples, plantains, guavas, passion fruit, coconuts, oranges, mandarins, limes, sugar cane, cacao (make your own sugar cane juice or chocolate when in season), and several other rare and wonderful fruits and veget

name: Farm Stay & Cultural Exchange (Nepal)
description: Hello, my name is Deepak S., and I live with my family on a small, remote farm in the mountains of Nepal, near the Dhading district, at 3,500 metres above sea level. I share this life with my parents, my grandfather, and my younger sister, who has Down syndrome. We would be very happy to welcome foreign travellers and introduce them to our culture, our way of life, and the way we care for the farm throughout the year. Our farm is a very peaceful place, surrounded by nature, with a beautiful view over the entire valley. Here, you will find goats, a male goat, a lamb, a buffalo, a cow, small dogs, and chickens. The surroundings are also full of colourful butterflies and birds. Travellers stay in a private room with three beds, each equipped with a mattress, warm blankets, and a mosquito net. There are electrical outlets in the room, Wi-Fi on site, and the mobile signal works relatively well. From the farm, it is easy to go for walks, explore the surrounding area, and meet people from the village and local families. During your stay, it is also possible to organise a three-hour trek to a beautiful temple at the top of the mountain. A visit to the village school can also be arranged if we are informed in advance. The farm can be reached from the village in 20 minutes by scooter taxi, for around 50 to 70 rupees. You will stay in a room that can host up to three guests at the same time. Each guest has a comfortable mattress, warm blankets, and their own mosquito net. There are electrical outlets in the room, so you can charge your computer or other devices. We have Wi-Fi on site, and mobile internet also works depending on the SIM card you use; we recommend Namaste or Ncell. There is also a private shower area available for guests with full privacy. For breakfast, lunch, and dinner, we cook using only vegetables from our farm, and sometimes fruit as well, depending on the season. During your stay, you will be able to try traditional local dishes such as dal bhat, dhido, t

name: Join Us as a Community Volunteer & Enjoy a Homestay (Kenya)
description: Hello! My name is Nixon, and I would love to warmly invite you to stay with me and my family in our humble home in Homa Bay, Kenya. We are a simple but very loving family, and for the past 20 years I have dedicated myself to supporting our local community through all challenges. Our home can host up to three volunteers at a time, and we welcome anyone with a willing heart — whether you can help with building, teaching, gardening, supporting our animals, fundraising, or sharing any skill that can uplift our community. Truly, even the smallest help creates a huge impact here. At us ,volunteers play a vital role. Our community faces the pressures of climate change, weak medical and educational systems, and poor sanitation. For many years, our main lifeline was a successful tailoring project that empowered vulnerable women and helped us fund school fees, mentorship programs, hygiene trainings, and community health awareness. Sadly, we lost everything in a major fire in 2019, and we are now starting again from zero — with hope, courage, and determination. This is why we are asking for volunteers who can support us in rebuilding, fundraising, and helping revive the tailoring project. Your presence and skills can open doors that many here never imagined possible. To help us provide good meals for all volunteers, we kindly request a small contribution of 5 USD per person per day, which goes directly toward food to ensure you are well fed and comfortable during your stay. In return, you will find a second family, experience true African culture, and become part of a mission that brings dignity, opportunity, and hope to children, women, and the entire community. If you feel called to help or would like to know more, please reach out — we would be truly honored to welcome you.

name: Volunteer at Our Backpacker Hostel (northwest Scotland)
description: We are looking for volunteer help with the day-to-day tasks involved in running a busy backpacker hostel. Whether you’re passing through Oban on your way to the islands or staying for a few days to explore the historic and beautiful Argyll region, Oban Backpackers is the best place to stay in town! The name Oban, translated from its Gaelic roots, means ‘small bay’. It is a charming horseshoe-shaped bay, sheltered from the Atlantic by the Isle of Kerrera. Known as the ‘Gateway to the Isles’, Oban is the largest port in North West Scotland and offers stunning views across to the Hebridean Islands. A highlight is the view at dusk from McCaig’s Tower, where you can enjoy a sunset you’ll never forget. As with any lively port, Oban is full of activity, with fishing boats, ferries, yachts, and seabirds, making the waterfront a delightful place to relax and enjoy the atmosphere.

name: Come and help us fight world hunger in central California (United States)
description: We need more volunteers. We are a non-profit organization that processes and ships food to approximately 100 impoverished nations. We are located near Fresno, California. We are open most of the year, have a variety of jobs and are flexible regarding the length of your stay. We have plenty of great food, comfortable accommodations, phones, a public computer, free Wi-Fi and a swimming pool. For your labor, you get free room and board during your stay. We are located near Sequoia National Park, home of the largest trees on earth. Also, Yosemite National Park is not far. We provide free transportation to and from the local greyhound/Amtrak stations. Please email me only if you have specific dates in mind, and that you are pretty certain you will come. I can then check for availability. I try to respond to every email, but if, for some reason I don't reply within 6 days, it means all the beds are taken, and we cannot accommodate you. Glean is a  non-profit charity. We produce over 50 million servings of delicious and healthy dried soup mix each year. Each summer we process and dry fresh peaches – enough to fill about 10 sea containers. We produce handmade warm quilts as well – several thousand each year. The product of our labor is given to partner ministries here in the US and abroad – (in about 100 countries so far) to be distributed to hungry people. If you are interested in volunteering, please contact me with the exact dates you want to volunteer. If I don't return your message within 5 days, it means there is no availability. Please note: It is up to the individual backpacker to provide all the necessary documents to US immigration so that he or she can enter the United States. When backpackers come to volunteer with us, it is just a small part of their overall US experience. Therefore, we do not provide any documents for backpackers.

name: Plumbing, HVAC & Painting Near the Beach – Trelleborg (Sweden)
description: Hi there, fellow traveller! I’m looking for a skilled plumber/HVAC to help install two water radiators in my cosy house near the beach in Trelleborg, southern Sweden. It’s not a large job and could probably be completed in a few days, though you are welcome to stay longer. You should know how to fit pipes, and additional help with sanding and painting a few indoor walls and an outdoor concrete wall would also be appreciated. The house is currently undergoing renovations. The main sanding and painting should be finished by May, and the facade joints by April. It’s a bit messy now, but will be much improved soon. The location is fantastic—just a one-minute walk from the beach, with easy access to the city centre by bus or bicycle. You’ll also have plenty of coastal nature to explore, including trips to Ystad, Höllviken, Skanör, and Malmö, which is just a 25-minute drive or reachable by bus/train. I live in the house some days and in Malmö on others, so I’m flexible about how long you can stay. Ideally, it would be great if you could come at the end of April. Work expectations are light—around three hours per day if needed, but I’m very flexible so you have plenty of time to explore the area. I’d love to host someone and get help with this project. Looking forward to hearing from you!

name: Experience Farm Life: Help with Animals & Gardening (Victoria)
description: We are a couple in our 60s who love hosting backpackers. Our home is shared with two cats, two dogs, chickens, geese, ponies, cattle, and a vibrant orchard, veggie patch, and plant nursery. You’ll have your own room in a shared house, and Mark, a wonderful cook, will take care of meals. Our property is 4.5 km from the nearest town, but there’s a bicycle and walking track nearby. We’re also happy to drive you to the bus, which connects to Melbourne for day trips. Alternatively, I can drop you at Belgrave station for easy access to Melbourne’s metro network, allowing you to explore the city and return with me after work. During your stay, we can teach you about farm life, help with English, and share insights into the Aussie way of life. We’re more than happy to offer guidance on settling into Australia, from setting up documents to planning your travels. Our home provides a safe and welcoming environment, perfect for travellers orienting themselves while preparing for their adventures. This is a help exchange stay, offering free food and accommodation in return for four hours of assistance per day, five days a week. Join us on our beautiful 16-hectare hobby farm in South Gippsland and enjoy an authentic home environment while immersing yourself in farm life.

name: Wallace and Gromit Build: Forager’s Paradise (England)
description: I went mad, got better, got a dog. Covid. That sucked. I moved back to the UK from Europe in 2021 and decided to start a business that, to be fair, has got a bit out of hand! Working full-time around it, I need to make a real push in 2026 and need a bit of brawn for the" grumpy back" days and  someone to help get my head round social media promotion and help organise a calendar for the up coming summer. As to what there is to do? Well, I need to complete an area for a potential off-grid cabin, involving running some sort of madcap irrigation system for a “mushroom forest” and veg beds (I’m no gardener!). I have an event in early summer, running a new idea based around Taskmaster and a foraging experience, which is a lot of work for one person.
