So I need help setting up clues and traps, and a brainstormer is always welcome. We have a pack of dogs that are boisterous but very friendly, and always up for a run in the forest, usually combined with some other task. I have lots of foraged and home-grown produce to prep for resale, along with painting, boxing, and inventing ideas — both practical and arty. Accommodation is “rustic”, and I guess the best way to describe it is a posh shed/multimedia studio with bunks. There is a little fridge and stove inside, and a larger kitchen in the workshop available. I can’t provide three meals a day, but I am happy to provide essentials and a few treats. We will have a few campfire cookouts, and I want to build a kind of oven in the paddock which, although not top of the list of priorities, may one day just be the thing that triggers me — and will be thrown together. Now, it’s a bit chaotic. Some days it seems like madness, when I’m cursing at a piece of plastic pipe like it murdered my family. But if at any point I ask something you’re not comfortable with, or just really don’t want to do, I get it! There is a composting toilet and outside shower, and indoor facilities are also available, but not 24-hour. I have a shooting range to

name: Historic House Recuperation / Biodiverse Mountains (Spain)
description: Stay in a rural house dating from the 1850s in eastern Spain, with a complex and interesting geometry and various vernacular features, such as Moorish (Mudéjar) ceramic panelling. The house is currently being carefully researched, restored, and enhanced while lived in. The project involves a range of crafts, including the design of gardens and terraces, and is connected to traditional local building methods and materials. The surroundings are exceptionally rich in biodiversity, with mountain trekking routes, small rivers and cascades, dramatic cliffs, and historic villages and hamlets nearby. The area is quiet and rural, well suited to people interested in nature, architecture, bio-construction, ecological approaches, gardening, restoration, agriculture, walking, drawing, photography, or slower forms of travel. Help needed includes mainly gypsum plastering, woodwork, and lime finishes, with clay and lime plastering already ongoing on site.

name: Live in a Bedouin Camp in Wadi Rum Desert (Jordan)
description: Hello! My name is Awad, and I was born here in Wadi Rum Village. I have a Bedouin camp in the desert in Wadi Rum. As many guests come to my tents each year, I would like to have volunteers to help me in my camp and assist me with my website. I really enjoy meeting new people from all over the world and would love for you to join me! Wadi Rum Meteorite Camp is located in a special area of the desert, attached to the mountains where you can find peace and connect with nature. Our view of the mountains is unique. We have everything arranged to provide you with the best experience of desert life in Wadi Rum. Breakfast is included, along with essential amenities. We also offer various activities such as a jeep tour, camel riding, and hiking. It's not just a place to stay; it's a complete experience.

name: Volunteer at our Orphanage in Mombasa (Kenya)
description: We are looking for volunteers to come help us around the orphanage, to empower our children, to bring new ideas and cultures and to play and love our children as much as possible. The children love visitors and we can guarantee as a volunteer you will have your heart stolen by our youngsters. We are a children’s home located in Mombasa, Kenya registered as a CBO by the Kenyan Government. We are a family run organization under Risper Ayub known as “Mama”. Our family has taken charge of caring for orphans from the local community who have been abandoned or left without parents do to HIV/AIDS although the children have remained unaffected. Currently we have 38 children in our care from the ages of 2-17. Our mission as an organization is to protect, educate, and encourage orphans on the coastline and Kenya at large to feel wanted and cared for, with a bright future ahead of them and to give them the best opportunity possible to grow and flourish into successful members of society. Mombasa is a very active city with many things to do and see close by. Our location has excellent access to cheap public transport. In their free time volunteers can go to the local beaches, visit the town market, go on city tours and see the local tourist attractions. We can also make arrangements for organized trips to nearby areas. We have local contacts who can get discounts on sea excursions and Safaris. We hope to see you soon.

name: Be a Conservation Volunteer in British Columbia (Canada)
description: Opportunities from our conservation volunteering programme include gaining experience in wildlife research through data collection on wilderness horseback trips in the Canadian backcountry. Volunteers may also take part in wildlife population counts for mountain goats and California bighorn sheep, as well as collect grizzly bear hair for DNA analysis. In addition, they may gather cones from the endangered whitebark pine for replanting in affected areas. The programme also involves engaging with people and supporting conservation outreach by educating visitors and involving them in conservation projects. Volunteers may contribute to wildlife research studies that inform local regulation and management, as well as respond to consultations and referrals and help organise meetings with relevant stakeholders. There are also opportunities to assist with managing ongoing stewardship and resource management projects, planning future initiatives, and supporting efforts to secure funding for the Institute. Work may also include helping to create land management plans that guide wildlife, land, and resource use, as well as supporting educational marketing such as advertising, photography, videography, and website maintenance. Each season brings different challenges and opportunities from living alongside nature. As a ranch-based research centre, the work is varied and interconnected, contributing to a wider conservation purpose. Alongside conservation activities, volunteers learn the practical skills needed for living in a remote wilderness environment and developing self-sufficiency. Daily responsibilities include contributing to ranch chores such as cooking shared meals, carrying out general repairs and maintenance, housekeeping, and caring for animals including horses, chickens, dogs, and cats, all while supporting a self-sufficient lifestyle.

name: Volunteer Teaching Experience (Zambia)
description: The school offers volunteering opportunities in a variety of subjects, including English, Mathematics, Local Languages, Religious Education, and Art. The programme also includes extracurricular activities such as building, gardening, sports, and more. We warmly welcome suggestions from volunteers on ways to broaden the school curriculum. Volunteers are encouraged to share any skills they have, and in return, the school provides free accommodation and meals during volunteering hours, as well as pick-up and drop-off services. During their time at the school, volunteers will have the chance to immerse themselves in the Simoonga community and learn about the village’s rich culture and traditions.

name: Help Set Up a GoFundMe for Community Projects (Tanzania)
description: Warm greetings from Mufindi, Tanzania, We are seeking support with our fundraising efforts for several urgent community projects currently underway in our rural area. We are working on the following initiatives: * Building a safe house for vulnerable girls * Establishing a skills training centre to empower youth and women * Constructing a classroom for our charity school, which serves underprivileged children * Starting a vegetable garden to help feed and support low-income families in our community We are looking for a volunteer to assist us in setting up and managing a GoFundMe campaign (or a similar platform), with the aim of reaching supporters both locally and internationally. We fully understand the time and effort required for such a task. Therefore, we are prepared to offer fair compensation once the funds have been successfully raised and we have access to the donations. We would greatly appreciate help in managing the campaign transparently and overseeing the collection of funds on our behalf. Your support could make a truly meaningful difference in the lives of children and families in need here in Mufindi.

name: Teach English to Children in the Countryside of Siem Reap (Cambodia)
description: Hello! I am Pagna, founder of the school — an NGO located in the countryside near Siem Reap (about 30 km away). I believe that education is the key to breaking the cycle of poverty and giving children access to a better future. My dream is to expand the organisation to reach more children in the villages by providing vocabulary training, supporting community development, and offering extra lessons. Securing funds is a challenging task, but we do our best to help our students with the limited resources we have. That’s why we need your help! I have been teaching here for many years, but I now need volunteers to help this project grow. Everyone is welcome to join and support the school and our mission, regardless of any previous work experience. We hold classes from 8:00 am to 10:30 am, 1:00 pm to 4:00 pm, and 6:00 pm to 8:00 pm. Volunteers are asked to stay for at least one week; however, if you feel uncomfortable, you may leave at any time. Of course, we would love for you to stay as long as possible, and longer stays are preferred. We can also arrange for you or your friends to visit as guests, even if only for one night. At the end of your volunteer experience, a certificate of participation will be provided as proof of your involvement with the school. This can serve as a reference for future teaching opportunities. Our school is an ideal starting point for anyone holding a TEFL certificate, as it provides the chance to practise teaching skills and classroom management. However, everyone is welcome to join — experience or formal training is not required. In addition to the unique opportunity to help disadvantaged children build a better future, you will experience life in a modest Cambodian village. During your free time, you can explore the surrounding nature — vast rice fields, coconut trees, and water buffaloes — all far from the noise of the city. I can also take you to the best spots for sunrise or sunset, and on your days off, you can enjoy a boat tour of th

name: We're a Non-Profit Charity Organisation Helping the Poor (Uganda)
description: We are looking for volunteers to join us in our ambitious "Kigungu Kids Edu Care" project. Volunteers will be provided with a bed, hot water for showers, and potable drinking water. They will stay in an independent room equipped with a mosquito net. We are seeking individuals willing to help the poor and orphaned children of our village with their homework, studies, and lessons. This will focus on Maths, Science, English language, Geography, and History for the older children, and English writing and reading skills for the younger ones. Volunteers are free to come and go at their convenience. Kigungu is accessible by boda boda (motorcycle taxi) or matatu, and it takes 30 minutes from Entebbe town. We offer airport pick-ups to Kigungu village, and it costs only €5 from the airport. If a volunteer wishes to take a tour of Entebbe or Kampala, one of our organisation members (e.g. chairman, secretary, treasurer, and others) can act as a guide. Volunteers pay €5 for each day they stay in Kigungu, which covers meals and drinks, while accommodation is provided for free.

name: Volunteer & Internship Opportunities in Conservation (Greece)
description: The project is located on the island of Kalamos in Western Greece, in a village of the same name. Terra Sylvestris is a registered Greek NGO that works towards protecting the environment and wildlife in the Inner Ionian area, as well as addressing environmental justice issues. Terra Sylvestris is the only NGO in the area focusing on community-based conservation. We are working on biodiversity and wildlife inventories in an internationally recognised conservation area. With the help of our interns and volunteers, we have discovered colonies of endangered mammals such as bats, mapped the fishing and use rights of local communities, and accomplished much more within just three years. Our work is routinely published in peer-reviewed literature. We currently have four pre-arranged activities: 1. Building the Kalamos Island Eco-community and experimental organic fruit and vegetable farm 2. Feral cat monitoring programme for Kalamos, Kastos, and their satellite islands 3. Marine ecosystems monitoring programme 4. Ornithological habitat surveys The goal of our programme is to contribute to sustainable development in the Inner Ionian Island Archipelago in Greece, as part of the Kalamos and Kastos Sustainable Development Programme. An important aim is to help educate and inspire people from around the world regarding sustainability and nature conservation. The programme is based at the Kalamos Island Biological Field Station. Costs: There is a fee to cover accommodation and other related services such as access to WiFi, unlimited use of washing machines and clothes drying facilities. The fee also covers assistance with project development and execution, use of equipment, transportation to field sites for pre-arranged activities, and related administrative costs. The station is located in a village, so meal ingredients and other daily necessities can be purchased locally.

name: Help Building an Eco Healing Project on Andros Island (Greece)

location.address: Build a Eco Healing Project on Andros, Greece
location.country: Build a Eco Healing Project on Andros

description: As long as I remember myself I used to live like being in a fairy tale with mythic creatures and animals in a lush land where people were always happy and smiling 🧚‍♀️... Growing up I found that my life purpose is to help people to reach happiness through awareness. After several attempts at work, I have come to the conclusion that what I have been asking for and which suits me, is the holistic energy therapy in which I have been working on for 10 years. But, I have to practice my ideas in the confines of a big city that does not actually blend with my values. I hope that I manage to transform this property into a cozy and intimate home for everyone who would like to join me and my dog Arsinoe, so I can practice my beliefs, away from the busy and distracting life of a big city. My life companion and my two kids share my vision, but due to their occupations come occasionally and for a short period of time. Therefore I need some help and I would love to share my dream project with anyone who would like to be there. If you come, you will have the chance to spend some time in a eco friendly space, connect with nature and meet many other travelers or people that share the same values. At the same time you can explore the wonderful Island of Andros. Also you can join me in my daily yoga practice, and when there are yoga classes, you can participate if you like. Also meditations, silent walks in nature, pranayama, mudra and mantras will be held, and you can join any of these. And because I am an Holistic Healer, I can give you Reiki session, and/or other energy healing sessions, and of course make a lot of spiritual conversations! :-) As a person I like very much to travel around the world, meet new people, get into the local customs and philosophy so I would be very interested to learn yours... And if you have any ideas or experiences of how to build eco-structures, paint, decorate, tree trimming, I would be very delighted to listen and follow. If you like to be in nature

name: Help in an Animal Shelter, Eco Farm and Community (Greece)

location.address: Help in an Animal Shelter, Eco Farm and Community

description: From stray dogs and cats to ecological vegetables, fruits, and aromatic/healing plants growing. We are dedicated to creating a centre for human and animal welfare — feeding, sheltering, activating, inspiring, and teaching. From simple eco-building to retreats and ceremonies, this will be the centre of our work with people. Providing natural medicines for health and a vision for a better, greener planet for tomorrow. 💚

name: Join Our Hospitality Team on Santorini Island (Greece)
description: We are a small hospitality business based in Santorini, committed to providing high-quality accommodation. We are passionate about our work and take pride in making our guests feel at home, while offering them a memorable and authentic local experience. Our property is located in the village of Firostefani, a central area of Santorini, just a few minutes from the local bus stop (a 25-minute walk), which offers convenient access to all parts of the island. We are currently seeking a reliable and responsible individual (aged 28 or above) to assist with the daily cleaning of guest rooms and general housekeeping duties.

name: Help Rescued and Disabled Animals (Cambodia)
description: Our sanctuary is home to more than 140 rescued cats, dogs (including those in our paralysed care section), and rescued farm animals. We are the only sanctuary of our kind in the region. Many of our residents have been rescued from the dog meat trade and now enjoy a peaceful life at the sanctuary. Our animals thrive in a calm and supportive environment, so a positive attitude is essential. Volunteers should be happy to pick up poo, assist with health issues, and help with physiotherapy. Beyond the sanctuary, we run animal rights campaigns, provide outreach for injured animals, and operate our own clinic. Knowledge of animal health and welfare is therefore a significant advantage. The ideal applicant will be patient, as many animals need time to build trust, as well as thoughtful, resourceful and hardworking. We are primarily seeking people with veterinary, animal care or sanctuary experience, whether through work, volunteering or education. Skills in fundraising, social media, maintenance or practical repairs are also highly valued. The heat and physical nature of the work can take a few days or weeks to adjust to, so resilience and adaptability are essential qualities for any volunteer. The role can also be emotionally demanding, as working with sick, injured or traumatised animals is often unpredictable and can be stressful. If your application is successful, we will arrange an interview to learn more about you and discuss how you can contribute to our work.

name: Volunteer at a Regenerative Cloud Forest Sanctuary (Colombia)
description: We are a small, land-based sanctuary located in the Andean cloud forest of Colombia. Our mission is land stewardship, regenerative living, and cultural preservation. The project integrates permaculture, ancestral wisdom, and community building in a rural, hands-on environment. Volunteers participate in a balanced exchange of learning and contribution, including: * Gardening, planting, and caring for crops * Reforestation and maintenance of native plants * Light construction and bamboo or cob building projects * Animal care (chickens, fish, and cats) * Communal cooking and food preparation * Cultural exchange, skill sharing, and storytelling Daily life is simple, immersive, and collaborative. Helpers work for several hours per day, with time for nature walks, meditation, creative projects, and reflection. We welcome volunteers who are reliable, adaptable, and passionate about hands-on, land-based work. No prior experience is required—attitude and cooperation matter most.

name: Teach Coding, 2D Art, Farming & Tipi Building (Jamaica)

location.address: Teach Coding, 2D Art, Farming & Tipi Building

description: Hi! I run a platform that gamifies climate action, aiming to use the power of games to make the hero’s journey of helping the planet fun, rewarding, and community-driven. I have often relied on coders, but now I want to learn how to code in JavaScript so I can speed up development and support my team when we hit blocks. If you work with JavaScript, TypeScript, C#, or C++, I would love to learn from you. Please note we are not using React or Python. We also have two residences, one in Kingston and one in Portland near the beach. We can host you at either location and offer a great experience on the island in exchange for your help. We are also open to 2D game artists, web designers, WordPress specialists, and PHP developers who are willing to teach. Ideally, we would like to document or record parts of the process so others can learn as well and contribute to building games for our site. Once you collaborate with us on this project, you become a long-term friend of the project and part of our extended community. Finally, we are also planning to build bamboo teepees for camping and develop a small farm. There is a lot of exciting work to do. You may also have the opportunity to be a guest on my podcast, “Solarpunk Presents”, where we discuss the sustainable and just future we are working towards.

name: Volunteer for Sustainable Living and Community Projects (England)
description: Volunteering here offers great perks: you'll work alongside the community on amazing projects while honing your skills. Our charity focuses on educating about sustainable living. Through volunteering, you'll learn about our methods, like our Wetland system for waste water, water recycling, and solar energy. Tasks include gardening, cooking, cleaning, and upkeep. Plus, you'll get two days off to explore nearby attractions like the Jurassic coast, just 3 miles away, and Lyme Regis.

name: Learn About Horses with us as a Volunteer Helper (Italy)

location.address: Learn About Horses as a Volunteer Helper in Italy, Europe
location.country: Learn About Horses as a Volunteer Helper in Italy

description: Come learn about Horses as a volunteer in Italy. Quite place nice horses good environment. Come volunteer with us. You can ride the horses (if able too) or learn natural horsemanship, Italian cooking, french or Italian language etc

name: Education, Culture & Community Impact (Kenya)

location.address: Live and Volunteer in Kenya: Education, Culture & Community
location.country: Live and Volunteer in Kenya: Education

description: Experience Kenya from the inside while contributing to meaningful community work. Our project supports children and young people through education, mentoring, and creative development activities. Volunteers become part of a welcoming local team, assisting with learning sessions, sports, arts, and youth empowerment programmes. You will also take part in cultural exchange and gain valuable insight into everyday life within Nairobi's communities. This opportunity is ideal for travellers seeking a purpose-driven experience and meaningful connections with local people while making a positive impact in the community.

name: Help at a Historic Villa in the Tuscan Countryside (Italy)
description: Assist at a large historic villa in the beautiful Tuscan countryside. Villa Michaela is a picturesque estate, rich in history, and surrounded by nature near Lucca, where we are seeking year-round support. Your tasks will include general villa maintenance such as handyman work, decorating, painting, gardening, and cleaning, as well as helping us clear and prune our olive grove. You’ll have shared accommodation (a room for two helpers) with a private bathroom, an equipped kitchen, and  Starlink Wi-Fi. The work requires 5 hours a day, 5 days a week. Food is provided, and you’ll prepare your own meals, plus receive cash in hand each week. We are located on a bus route just 6km from Lucca train station, with easy access to the coast, Florence, Pisa, Cinque Terre, and more. We would warmly welcome a couple or 2 friends. Great scenery , tennis court, walking , hiking , easy access to Lucca train station . English language would be helpful   or French , German or Italian.

name: Help with Cleaning and Explore Edinburgh (Scotland)
description: Help us with cleaning and get to know Edinburgh! Centrally located in Edinburgh's West End, near the ancient and beautiful Dean Village and the Water of Leith, Belford Hostel is a unique and lively converted church. The hostel is situated between Edinburgh Haymarket and Waverley rail stations, both of which are about a 10-minute walk away. Our dormitory accommodation is housed within the old church hall, complete with stunning stained glass windows! Dorm sizes range from 4 to 10 beds. Belford Hostel is a great place to relax and unwind after a long day of travelling or sightseeing, and an ideal spot to meet fellow travellers.

name: Welcome to FreeSpirit Surf & Yoga Hostel (Ecuador)
description: Hi! Welcome to our little piece of paradise, where yoga and surfing are part of daily life! We truly believe that here you will find a family and friendships that last a lifetime. Our hostel is a place where you can enjoy the company of travellers and fellow volunteers from all over the world. Our wide selection of surfboards, daily yoga classes, and incredible atmosphere will make you want to stay with us forever. We aim to create memories and moments that make you say, “Here is where I belong!” We are looking for volunteers who genuinely want to get involved, use their skills, and learn new ones. Volunteers who are only looking for a cheap way to travel are not the right fit for us.

name: Cultural Exchange & Gardening in a Miami Paradise (Florida)
description: We are a young, semi-retired couple looking for someone to help out on our beautiful, quiet, lush property, which we’ve been slowly evolving over the past three years into an edible urban sanctuary. We ask for around 20 hours per week (generally 5 hours Monday–Friday, but flexible on days and times) in return for full board, including a private bedroom and private bathroom. We love meeting helpers who share our interests—learning from each other, exchanging experiences, creating memories, and trying new things. The creatives, intellectuals, artists, nature-lovers, storytellers, adventurers, free spirits, travellers, seafarers, and hippies have all inspired us, and we hope to do the same—living more freely, enjoying the moment, and saying yes to new experiences (at least twice, just to be sure!). Meals are included, and we have an open kitchen policy: whatever we grow or have available, we share. Any specific dietary requirements are usually your responsibility. You’re welcome to join us for meals (preferred) or prepare your own—extra points if you can share traditional dishes from your country. We’d appreciate help with gardening tasks such as weeding, planting, raking leaves, and tending to raised beds, as well as with our worm compost system. Stays can range from a few days to a few weeks (a minimum of 7 days is preferred, but not required). Some days you’ll have the property to yourself, as we both work as consultants in our respective fields. You’ll also have access to kayaks, bikes, a laundry room, pool, private sun deck, and possibly weekend trips around Florida. Please note—we’re not looking to be a last resort if you have nowhere else to go, nor do we want guests who remain in their room all day. We’re keen on cultural exchange, and look forward to evenings spent sharing stories, learning about each other, and hearing about your country and travels. When contacting us, it’s very helpful if you provide your intended arrival and departure dates, so we can coor

name: Help Out with a Recycling Project and Off-Grid Farm (Belize)
description: We moved here as a family 15 years ago with three small children, who have since grown up and left! In 2012, we began building Belize’s first Earthship, and ever since, recycling has become our way of life. We have plenty of projects in the pipeline and need extra hands. Learn how to repurpose plastic and glass bottles to create stunning buildings, or explore Belizean plants and wildlife while helping out on the farm. We're also looking for long-term help, so if you’re curious about off-grid, self-sufficient living, this could be the perfect opportunity to give it a try! If you want to experience an off-grid lifestyle in the middle of the jungle while doing your bit for the environment, come and see if it suits you!

name: Looking for Help in Our Walled Garden in Yorkshire (England)
description: We’re in the early stages of creating Minty’s Garden Retreat and are looking for help in the walled garden. There’s plenty to learn and contribute, allowing you to make your mark on the garden. While much renovation has been completed, it remains an ongoing project. There’s always something happening, and the tasks are varied. If you’d like to stay in the countryside, we also have dogs and opportunities to get involved in general management on the family farm. A fulle driving licence would be wonderful .

name: Looking for Sun, Community & a Great Experience (Tunisia)

location.address: Looking for Sun, Community & a Great Experience

description: We're Looking for Lovely Volunteers! We invite you to join our small and warm-hearted volunteer community at Guesthouse Al Medina, located in the charming old town of Bizerte, Tunisia. 🛏️ What’s Waiting for You: * Your own private room in the medina, just a 5-minute walk from the beach and harbour * Accommodation and meals fully covered * Keep any tips from happy guests Your home and the guesthouse are nestled in the heart of the old town, just minutes from the beach and right by the harbour. A bustling local market offers Tunisian specialities, spices, fresh fruit, vegetables, and daily catch from the sea. In the evenings, you can unwind at nearby harbourfront restaurants with beautiful terrace views. We're also close to peaceful forests for a nature escape. About us and our Team: we are a very welcoming community, working together and living together. Several times a week we go to the beach, horse riding, to a pool, or just enjoying our evenings together on the rooftops of our home or our Guesthouse.

name: Biodiversity Project: Sustainable Gardening & DIY (Spain)
description: Welcome to our home and community. We are a small group living together as a family, focused on learning, conscious living, and personal growth. Our backgrounds are diverse — yoga, psychology, martial arts, gardening, creativity, and community work — and we love sharing what we know while learning from the people who join us. This is not a work-for-accommodation project. We don’t host “volunteers.” We welcome people who want a true cultural exchange: living with us, joining our daily rhythm, sharing their ideas, skills, and culture, and learning from ours. Daily life includes gardening, cooking, caring for animals, creative projects, yoga, meditation, and meaningful conversations. Everyone participates naturally, just like in a family. To keep our space peaceful and aligned with our values, we follow a vegan/vegetarian lifestyle. No smoking, alcohol, drugs, or any kind of violence are allowed on our premises. We value clear communication, responsibility, kindness, initiative, and collaboration. If you enjoy contributing, listening, speaking openly, and taking part in a shared daily life, you will feel at home here. You can expect respect, honesty, and warmth. We listen to each other, support each other, and encourage personal expression. You won’t be treated as a worker — you will be welcomed as a  family member whose presence and culture matter. In return, we ask for sincerity, curiosity, and an active, willing attitude. Here, you can explore gardening, animal care, yoga, meditation, psychology, philosophy, martial arts, creativity, and conscious communication. You will learn from our practices, and we will learn from your culture, your experience, and your personal path. This exchange is the heart of the stay. If you are looking for a place where you can grow, connect deeply, contribute meaningfully, and live with people who care about coherence, empathy, justice, and goodness, our community may be right for you. We look forward to welcoming people who want to liv

name: Building, Gardening, Harvesting & Permaculture (France)

location.address: Building, Gardening, Harvesting and Permaculture Helping Provence

description: Our mission is to connect people who want to live in a more sustainable way and we welcome volunteers to help build a place that radiates the philosophy that we (together) can do it in a different way. Self-supporting is the goal and every project will be lead to that goal. Healing is the side-effect we want to create for everyone, the guests, the volunteers or the locals. It is a place with love for ourselves, others and the animals in the forest. On the south oriented hill site we are maintaining a permaculture garden. The many fruit trees, olive trees, almond trees gives us harvest we can use in delicious meals. Near the guest house we want to create a garden of Eden. The site is located 3 km from a picturesque village Bargemon and in the heart of the Provence. There are many beautiful things to visit in the neighborhood and the Mediterranean coast (Fréjus) is only 30 min away (by car).

name: Learn to Cook Plant-Based & Global Dishes (Belgium)
description: Welcome to our kitchen! We cook balanced, seasonal menus using the highest quality fresh, local, and organic ingredients. Our aim is to bring people together to connect over cuisine driven by love and to prove once and for all that truly healthy food can be creative — and of course, delicious! Right now, you have the opportunity to train in cooking delicious vegan meals. By doing so, you’ll help us offer the local and global community a new style of dining seven days a week: homemade, fresh, organic, plant-based food made with love. This food brings people together to be nourished and share our special four-course menu, which changes every three weeks. Our vision is unique because we want to be more than just a place serving good food. We aim to create a collaborative kitchen that both trains new chefs and employs them on a freelance basis. We are a non-profit organisation with a mission to promote healthy vegan food and to train volunteers how to cook for themselves or, if they wish, to pursue a career in cooking. We welcome travellers interested in learning more about whole food, plant-based cuisine; such individuals will gain hands-on experience cooking alongside our team. Our current head chefs, Alistair, Ruth, and Chala, are skilled in preparing plant-based, whole food, organic, vegan cuisine. The work involves learning to prepare a four-course meal that changes every three weeks. You will be trained in how to cook each element of the meal so that after three weeks, you can make it independently. This is a hands-on learning environment in the kitchen, from cleaning and chopping vegetables, to recipe and menu planning, all necessary preparations, and plating. We operate on a rotation of six shifts per week, approximately 30 hours, with two full days off. While the majority of the work involves cooking, you will also occasionally serve customers and assist with cleaning tasks. We have two en-suite bedrooms and one bedroom without a bathroom. Meals are provided th

name: Dog Sitter Needed for Two Lovely Dogs (Norway)
description: Want to spend some time in beautiful Bergen while providing company to two lovely Staffordshire terriers? If so, you have arrived at the right listing. The only thing expected is that you are more or less present between 07:00 and 15:00 on weekdays, so my dogs have company while I am at work. There is a fairly large garden, so you simply need to be around and let them out to play, etc. If you have a remote job on your PC, it is absolutely possible to combine the two. We live just a 30-minute walk or a five-minute bus ride from the heart of the city, yet still in the midst of nature, with only forest and mountains behind us. There is an exceptional view of the city here. You will have your own fully furnished room with a bed, sofa, table, and storage, and you will share the kitchen and bathroom with me. It is expected that you have at least some basic experience with dogs, but most importantly that you enjoy being around animals. You will of course be invited to join us on our many trips and hikes in the beautiful nature surrounding the area, and if you are interested, you are welcome to join my martial arts, yoga, and strength training sessions (I am a private instructor). Stays are a minimum of two weeks, preferably longer.

name: Nanny’s Rustic Retreat - Men’s Wellness Sanctuary (Pennsylvania)
description: Nanny’s Rustic Retreat – Men’s Wellness Sanctuary offers a unique volunteer opportunity for individuals seeking a peaceful, nature-oriented, wellness-focused lifestyle experience in the Laurel Highlands of Pennsylvania. This is more than simply a place to work. The retreat is designed as a relaxing and restorative environment where guests come to slow down, reconnect with nature, and enjoy a calm, adult-oriented atmosphere centred on wellness, hospitality, and meaningful human connection. The property includes rustic cabins, glamping areas, wooded surroundings, outdoor gathering spaces, seasonal pool access, a hot tub, sauna, firepit areas, and spaces intended for relaxation and reflection. The atmosphere is intentionally peaceful, laid-back, and welcoming. Volunteers are often attracted to the retreat because they appreciate slower living, natural environments, hospitality, wellness culture, and contributing to positive guest experiences. This opportunity may especially appeal to individuals interested in holistic wellness, massage, meditation, mindfulness, Reiki, bodywork, sauna traditions, spiritual reflection, eco-living, or alternative wellness-oriented lifestyles. A plus would be individuals with interest, experience, or knowledge in wellness-oriented practices, guest-centred hospitality, or nature-based relaxation environments. Formal certification is not required, but a genuine appreciation for wellness, relaxation, and creating a positive atmosphere is highly valued. Typical volunteer tasks may include: • Light groundskeeping and outdoor upkeep • Pool and hot tub maintenance • Cleaning and preparing guest areas • Guest interaction and hospitality • General retreat upkeep and organisation • Helping maintain a calm and welcoming atmosphere The ideal volunteer is mature, dependable, respectful, open-minded, self-motivated, and comfortable interacting with guests in a relaxed, wellness-oriented setting. This is not a corporate environment or traditional commerc

name: Teaching English, Nature & Comfortable Stay in Pinnawala (Sri Lanka)

location.address: Teaching English to Children, Exploring Nature, and Comfortable

description: Volunteers can experience authentic Sri Lankan culture and village life. They will have the opportunity to interact with local people, learn about daily routines, and enjoy a peaceful lifestyle close to nature. Volunteers can also teach English. We love sharing our traditional food, customs, and way of life with our volunteers. You can explore nearby areas and visit local attractions such as the Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage. Our programme: 🔹Three days a week: English teaching for underprivileged children 🔹Optional: vegetable farm work, if you are interested 🔹Two days a week: work at the elephant orphanage or zoo 🔹Activities include bird watching and identification, learning about reptile behaviour and habitats, night walks, jungle tours, hiking, and river bathing 🔹Additional activities can be added depending on your interests Weekends are free, so you can travel and explore independently at your own pace.

name: Volunteer on an Alpaca Farm (Canada)
description: Our beautiful little alpaca farm is located 20 minutes north of Parrsboro, Nova Scotia—so you’re very close to the Bay of Fundy, an inlet of the Atlantic Ocean. The farm sits along a river and includes several acres of woodland. We currently have 11 alpacas—gentle, adorable animals. They require daily feeding and barn care, and once a year we shear them. It’s a challenging but fun day. We’re also rebuilding our small flock of chickens after a weasel killed most of them. There are two gorgeous cats in the house, so if you’re allergic, this may not be a good fit. In addition to animal care, there are occasional projects such as fence-building, brush-clearing, cleaning and organizing inside the farmhouse, and painting. I try to match volunteers with tasks that suit their skills and interests, though of course some jobs simply need to be done—whether you love them or not. :-) You would work 5 hours per day, 5 days a week. On your days off, you’re free to relax here or explore beautiful Cumberland County. Your only responsibility on those days is a small amount of housecleaning, just as you would in any shared home. The Joggins Fossil Cliffs—a UNESCO World Heritage Site—are about half an hour away, and Cape d’Or, one of the most beautiful spots in Nova Scotia, is about an hour away. Over the years, we’ve hosted more than 100 volunteers from a dozen countries. With some, I’ve developed long-term friendships and meaningful connections. You’re welcome to stay for one month. If it’s a great fit and we have good chemistry, that can extend to 2–3 months. And if I ever meet someone who’s a truly good match—someone who loves both the current projects and the ones I dream of—well, it’s a very big house. :-)

name: Waitress/Waiter Wanted at Family-Run Restaurant (Italy)
description: Hi, I’m Michela. I’m looking for two young people (a couple) or a single person who is cheerful, respectful, and eager to see beautiful things! I run my family business, which offers food service and accommodation, excursions, foraging, and other experiential activities. Let’s say I work with nature in all its forms, and I’m looking for someone who can give me a hand with daily activities. I’m located in Calabria, in a forest surrounded by waterfalls and lots of wonder. For safety reasons, I prefer to share all the details in private! If you’d like to share your love for nature with me while gaining experience and earning a little something, I’m waiting for you! We can decide together on the most suitable salary and contract. Come and live an authentic experience in the wildest part of southern Italy!

name: Help with Gardening near the University of Virginia (United States)

location.city: Fun Trips and more at the University of Virginia
location.address: Home Gardens, Fun Trips and more at the University of Virginia

description: Welcome to our peaceful gardens in Charlottesville!  Located southwest of Washington, DC, in the prestigious Lewis Mountain neighborhood, surrounded by the University of Virginia, our home offers a blend of tranquil garden work and vibrant cultural life. I am a dual-national (originally from England) running a local building business and a non-profit foundation. We pride ourselves on being a warm, inclusive, and compassionate household. We believe in the power of honest, faithful companionship and welcome volunteers of any race, nationality, or sexual orientation. When you stay with us, you aren't just a helper; you are part of the family. We need assistance tending to our fruit and vegetable gardens and general landscaping. The work is rewarding and perfect for those who love being outdoors and seeing the literal fruits of their labor. We are nestled at the foot of the Blue Ridge Mountains. You’ll have access to incredible hiking trails and forests right at the end of our street. Charlottesville is a historic wine-producing region with a rich cultural scene, including excellent cafes, restaurants, and concerts. We live by the values of Psalm 101:6:  "I will look for trustworthy people so I can live with them in the land."  Thus, we build a community of integrity here.  We love hosting volunteers who are straightforward, honest, and ready to walk this journey with us.  We value open communication and complete honesty in all our interactions. I love to provide wonderful food and shared meals. Please let me know in advance if you have any specific dietary needs so I can look after you properly. You are welcome to use our fast Wi-Fi and laundry facilities. We just ask that you treat our home with the same respect and care that we show you.  Ours is a home of kindness. We expect all guests to maintain a spirit of respect and compassion for everyone they meet here. We appreciate the help in the gardens, we want you to enjoy Virginia! We encourage you to take time to expl

name: We're Looking for Help Around Our House and Garden (Ireland)
description: We're a small household. We'll offer you tasks or projects to work on, and you can manage your own time. As we rely on the local bus service, you'll probably want to plan your working hours around the bus timetables and whatever you'd like to do in your free time. The house is about a 35–45 minute walk from the main road where the bus stops, so you'll need to allow for this when travelling to nearby towns and villages. We could use some help with housekeeping, general housework, DIY repairs, house-sitting, looking after the cats, keeping the communal areas clean and tidy, wall painting and murals, and gardening. You're welcome to stay for as long or as short a time as you like. Eventually, there will be space for three or more volunteers. At the moment, there is space for one person, and we'd appreciate your help getting more spaces ready so that more volunteers can stay. It's nice when there are several people here, as volunteers can get to know one another and spend time together. We enjoy an eco-friendly lifestyle, so if you're comfortable with a simpler way of living and can do without luxury, you'll fit in well here. We live in a quiet rural area, surrounded by farms, the sea, and rolling countryside.

name: Come Share Experiences & Languages near Bilbao (northern Spain)
description: Estoy buscando a una persona que me pueda ayudar en la granja. Necesito a alguien que pueda desbrozar, cuidar la tierra, podar, y que tenga conocimientos en plantas y árboles frutales. Durango es una pequeña localidad rodeada de montañas y situada entre las ciudades de Bilbao y San Sebastián. I am looking for someone who can help me on the farm. I need someone who can clear the land, take care of the soil, prune, and has knowledge of plants and fruit trees. Durango is a small town surrounded by mountains and located between the cities of Bilbao and San Sebastián.

name: Join Our Family in Beautiful Greater Victoria (Canada)
description: Hello! We're a warm and welcoming homeschooling family of five — two parents and three children (aged 13, 11, and 5) — living in the beautiful Westshore area of Greater Victoria, BC. Our household is bilingual, as we have a blend of Canadian and South Asian cultures. We are fortunate to travel internationally each year, and when we’re back home in Canada, we love opening our home to welcome people from all corners of the world, sharing stories, cultures, and experiences. Over the past seven years, we've hosted close to 50 volunteers through various platforms and have learned what makes a genuinely great match. We truly value cultural exchanges and enjoy welcoming people who want to be part of real family life — not just passing through. 📌 Quick Snapshot 📍 Location: Greater Victoria, British Columbia (nature + city access) 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family: 2 parents + 3 children (13, 11, and 5) 🕒 Help: ~5 hours a day, 5 days a week 🍳 Main tasks: Cooking, household help, engaging with children, gardening 🛏 Accommodation: Private room and bathroom in the family home 📅 Minimum stay: 3 weeks 🚫 We are not a good fit for: Night owls or travellers looking for just a place to sleep ✅ Best fit: Responsible, proactive, enjoys children, takes initiative Our home is structured but relaxed, and we aim to create a respectful, supportive atmosphere for everyone. Note: We are not able to host solo male travellers at this time and are unable to provide invitation letters for visa purposes. 👉 Please include your favourite hobby in your message so we know you’ve read our profile. Victoria is a wonderful city to explore — beaches, trails, markets, festivals, and history, all within a convenient public bus service. With the mildest climate in Canada, Greater Victoria enjoys year-round blooms and abundant natural beauty. Surrounded by ocean and forest, the region offers outdoor adventures in every season. Longer weekend trips to Tofino, the Gulf Islands, Vancouver, Seattle, and Port Angeles are popular wit

name: Help Create a Vegetable Garden & Chicken Coop (France)
description: I'm Sophie, a young retiree from organic farming. I live in a large house which I rent out for two months a year as an eco-lodge, sharing my passion for ecology, the countryside, the biology of our natural world, and life on a former farm dating back to the 15th century. I live alone with two very affectionate elderly cats, Tina and Skaal. I would like to build a chicken coop for around ten laying hens and create permaculture mounds for my vegetable garden. Most importantly, I want to maintain my property, which is wonderfully wild! I am a biologist and ecologist by training, and I spent 25 years working as an organic farmer, independently raising horses and Gascon pigs. I am curious about everything and have a strong knowledge of agriculture and animal husbandry. I am also a specialist in hedgerows and ants, as well as an entomologist with a passion for insects, which are especially abundant here because the property has never been chemically treated — something quite rare in France. One of my daily activities is caring for my plants, trees, and flowers. My current projects include building a chicken coop and creating permaculture mounds for a vegetable garden. Life here is slow and peaceful, much like my stage of life. I cook using organic produce bought directly from farms or nearby local markets. We will prepare and share meals together, and arrangements can be made when you arrive. Your room will be in the gîte (holiday cottage). It is large, spacious, and comfortable, with a private bathroom. However, if you wish to come in July or August, during the tourist season, we will need to arrange alternative accommodation, as the gîte will be occupied by guests. There is also a swimming pool on site, as well as a gym with weight-training equipment. I practise yoga. An electric fat bike will be available for you to travel to the village, where there is a leisure centre during the summer, along with a few small shops. However, having your own car would be helpful if yo

name: Renovating an 18th-Century Mansion House (Northern France)
description: Hello kind stranger! Our names are Claire and Michiel. We're a young Franco-Belgian family living on an old farmstead near the coast of Wissant in northern France. For the past seven years, we have been restoring the old farmhouses while also raising our two daughters, Fleur (5) and Perle (3). We’re looking for help with repairing the mansion house! With previous groups, we’ve already restored the exterior—now it’s time to tackle the inside. If you're motivated, we can teach you some basic carpentry, stone masonry, and how to make and apply traditional chalk-hemp insulation on the walls. We also need help maintaining the family vegetable garden—harvesting, preserving (and tasting!) our vegetables—and trimming the hedges. The animals will need feeding too. At the moment, we have chickens and sheep, though they only need feeding in winter. Occasionally, we may also ask you to keep our daughters entertained while we’re working outside. We usually host 4 to 6 volunteers at a time, so you won’t just experience French culture and language but also those of the other nationalities present. So far, we’ve hosted 160 volunteers from 26 different countries! We're fully booked until the end of August, but we're still looking for volunteers from September 17th to December 3rd (4-5 spots available)

name: Volunteer at a Yoga Health Retreat (Australia)
description: Join us at our peaceful retreat centre in the mountains near Coffs Harbour, set on 100 hectares of pristine native gum tree forest. With stunning views, serene bush walks, and abundant native wildlife, this is a truly unique opportunity to reconnect with nature and yourself. As a volunteer, you’ll receive full accommodation and delicious vegan meals, along with free yoga and meditation sessions. You’ll also have access to the Online Ascension Yoga Studies course at no cost. This is a small retreat centre hosting only a few guests and one or two volunteers at a time. Your days will include early morning Ashtanga-Sivananda mixed yoga classes with pranayama, a nourishing vegan breakfast, a morning work session, a boutique organic vegan lunch, followed by afternoon tasks, free time, and evening herbal teas. Evening activities may include kirtan singing, chanting, music, prayer, meditation, or a satsang lecture exploring the spiritual teachings of Ascension Yoga. Zen art classes are also available. For a small additional cost, you can enjoy massages, healing sessions, kayaking on the Nymboida River—one of Australia’s best white-water rivers—or try your hand at local adventure sports like rock climbing. Long-term volunteers may be eligible for a significant discount on the UOCA one-month Yoga Teacher Training course. Our property is surrounded by breathtaking nature—mountain views, sandstone cliffs, rainforest, creeks, waterfalls, and a swimming dam. Wildlife such as kookaburras, magpies, possums, rock wallabies, goannas, and many others are often seen. Please be mindful of snakes and bull ants, and ask us to point out any known nests on arrival. This is a real opportunity to work on yourself without distraction, with personal guidance from an experienced yogi who shares your cultural background. It’s a chance to set aside the pressures of everyday life and reconnect with your primal self—your undivided nature—through the deep experience of yoga and the teachings of Purus

name: DIY & Tea Room Support at Hamwood House (Ireland)
description: Hamwood House is a Georgian property dating back to 1777. It is a unique private home which, as the owners, we have been restoring over the past ten years. The house has been in the Hamilton family for seven generations. The property consists of seven bedrooms and 120 acres of farmland and woodland. From early spring to mid-autumn, we need support to help keep the gardens and grounds in good shape. As a requirement of the State, properties such as ours must be open to the public for a significant number of days each year to allow visitors to view the house and grounds. At weekends during the season, we also open a tea room to offer refreshments to visitors. *** You must be a registered HelpStay member to contact this host. Applications sent directly by email will not be considered or answered. ***

name: We Need Volunteers to Help Out on a Hobby Farm (Norway)
description: Our farm is located near Lake Osensjøen in Nordre Osen, Åmot, Innlandet. Imke (55) and Patrick (54) live here with their three cats. Patrick is a Dutch army veteran with PTSD, and Imke is a seamstress and Reiki Master. We have a small farm (småbruk) covering 1.9 hectares. Most of it is pasture, and we have set aside part of it for growing vegetables and fruit for our own use. We are looking for enthusiastic and reliable helpers, both on the farm and in the house. We welcome active volunteers who are physically and mentally strong and not afraid to get their hands dirty. There is always something to do, with a wide variety of tasks. We can use help all year round with keeping the farm tidy, cleaning the house and guest accommodation, and maintaining the vegetable garden and greenhouse. In 2025, we installed a large greenhouse, which keeps us busy from March through October. The greenhouse will be upgraded this year with additional planters. We also maintain a vegetable garden, fruit bushes, and fruit trees, and there are always renovation, construction, or painting projects underway. For example, we are planning to build a carport for the caravan and caravan awning, including a patio. Our farm is situated in a quiet location near Lake Osensjøen. Here, you can swim at one of Norway’s most beautiful sandy beaches, and there are plenty of opportunities for fishing. There is also a ski resort near our farm that offers various activities even outside the snow season. In addition, there is a supermarket in the village where you can buy most of your daily groceries. We view hosting as a two-way exchange. Not only will you be helping us, but we also hope to share our knowledge, skills, and lifestyle with you, and to learn from your experiences and culture. With us, you can build social connections by spending time with locals, eating and cooking together, helping with creative or practical projects, and participating in small local activities. You will also learn practical s

name: Teach and Assist at a School in Kerala (India)
description: My family owns an English-medium school in India. We are primarily looking for native English speakers to help our students improve their conversational English. We can arrange excellent accommodation in the area, with a 20% contribution towards living expenses. It would be a lifetime experience to visit this small inland town in the beautiful state of Kerala and interact with our staff, pupils, and the local community. The region is known for its historic bungalows and picturesque farmlands. The school has an excellent faculty across all subject areas. We are looking forward to working with native speakers to give our pupils greater exposure to English. We welcome volunteers and tutors from all backgrounds, including languages, science, personal development, the arts, public speaking, sports, and school management. In return, our teachers can share their expertise in areas such as classical dance, music, Indian languages (Malayalam, Hindi, and Tamil), and teaching methodologies. We believe this will be an enriching and rewarding experience for everyone involved—our pupils, staff, and visitors alike.

name: Volunteer with Our Integrated Community Project (Kenya)
description: We're a community initiative organization which focuses on the child's right and upholds the well-being of the children and also protects and conserves the environment to retain the dignity of Mother Earth. We work with the orphans and vulnerable children. We exist because the children need us! We welcome volunteers and greatly value their contributions to our project. I have deep passion to work for the children and the community. All my family members are involved in the project. My wife is instrumental in forming part of the school teaching team, helping the volunteers to learn how to cook local foods and children are at different schools and grades but also help the volunteers in learning the local language (Dholuo), Kiswahili and English for those from Non-English speaking countries. People involved in our project include children (orphans), vulnerable children, women, youths and other community members who use our school as a resource center and social meeting place. We have been running the project for the last ten years and have handled volunteer for nine years from different backgrounds, this normally gives the volunteers the opportunity to enhance their multicultural understanding. The children are provided with basic education, food, clothes, medical care and psychological support. Our activities include  organic farming, environmental and Nature conservation ,  teaching, poultry keeping, tailoring and dress making for the youths (job creation) and HIV/AIDS awareness creation. Other Activities volunteers can do while here: Lake Victoria !!! Lake Victoria is ONLY minutes away from where you staying. Africa's largest lake by area, the world's largest tropical lake, and the world's second-largest fresh water lake by surface area after Lake Superior in North America. Ruma National Park !!! The volunteers can have the opportunity to visit Ruma National Park just about 27 km from our home. Ruma National Park is a home to Giraffe, Rhino, Antelopes and several ot

name: Experience a language & cultural exchange in Lima (Peru)

location.address: Experience a language and cultural exchange in Lima, Peru
location.country: Experience a language and cultural exchange in Lima

description: Welcome to the safest, most touristic and most modern place in Lima. This beautiful neighborhood is really close to the sea and next to Barranco, which is considered one the coolest neighborhoods in the whole world. This apartment has 2 bedrooms but only one of them is available, 2 shared bathrooms, good internet connection, living room, dining room, kitchen, tv cable and a garage. I'm a native Spanish speaker, lawyer, polyglot (english, italian, portuguese, german, french and currently learning dutch) and language tutor. I live with my brother. Language tutoring and tours around the city are offered in exchange of house cleaning. The experience includes breakfast while lunch and dinner can be made in the kitchen or delivered. However most of the time there is food available to give it away! There are several restaurants around the building as well as an alternative just in case.

name: House Renovation, Gardening, and Enjoying the Countryside (France)

location.address: House Renovation, Gardening, and Enjoying the Countryside

description: Hi! 😊 Looking for kind people to help me out but especially to make new friends, share stories, learn about cultures and enjoy this beautiful spot in central France. 🇫🇷 My name is Jeroen, I'm 34 years old and grew up in the center of the Netherlands, in between the forest and lake. I did a lot of watersports (like sailing) and studied architecture. I’m a social, kind and honest person and I love meeting new people and share stories. I’m a fulltime optimist, interested in almost anything and like new ideas and projects. ☀️ I build myself a camper van (2023) and drove to most Northern en Southern tips of Europe. Now (April 2024) I've bought a house in the center of France in the Bourgogne, Parc the Morvan. The house (100+ years) is in the countryside and has a beautiful views over the valley. 🏡 It comes with a big garden (5500m2). The place is very peaceful and calm. You can hear the cowes and sheep, birds and sometimes the river. My plan is to renovate the interior of the house completely (in my own pace) and give the garden some much needed attention. As said before I like to connect with people. I’ve traveled in Europe and love to use couchsurfing and similar platforms. I’m not looking for free labour but for connecting with friendly people and having a good time - and in between doing some work in and around the house. Still that doesn’t mean I don’t prefer people who are dedicated, have some experience, take responsibility, are secure and have positive attitude and reviews/feedback! During the renovation is the house still liveable. The top floor has two bedrooms and a separate shower. And can be used privately. Also the garden has space to park or to place your tent. And I have a renovated small retro caravan with one bed 185x100. I like to cook together and share meals. But nothing is mandatory. I'm a Vegetarian. I’ve a car and could pick you up in the city (15 minutes drive) from the bus stop or train station (Autun). 🚂 Dates available 2025: 26-04 / 31-05 05-0

name: Looking for someone to take care of my dog and animals (Madeira Island)
description: I'm looking for a caring individual who can look after my dog and farm animals while I'm away for 3 days. My goat and chickens just need to be fed and checked up on every now and then, but my dog needs more attention and care: feeding, walking and petting. You'd arrive a few days earlier so that I can train you and answer any additional questions you may have. My house is located in the heart of nature on the beautiful mountain tops of Madeira Island.

name: Volunteer on a Solar-Powered Sunset Cruise (Laos)

location.address: Lao People's Democratic Republic, Luang Prabang

description: Sabaidee! We’re a small, family-run eco-tourism project based in the beautiful UNESCO-listed town of Luang Prabang, Laos. For over ten years, our mission has been to share the beauty of the Mekong River through sustainable, culturally rich travel experiences. Our most popular service is the Sunset Cruise, one of the top-rated experiences in Luang Prabang, recommended by TripAdvisor, Google, and Lonely Planet. What makes our cruise truly special is that we operate the first solar-powered boat in Luang Prabang, currently running on 80% solar energy. We care deeply about the environment and are passionate about creating meaningful connections between travellers and local culture. As a helper, you’ll join a friendly and dedicated team made up mostly of local Lao staff. Many of them have been with us for years and are like family. Although most of the team speaks only basic English, they are incredibly kind, curious, and welcoming. It’s a relaxed and positive environment, perfect for cultural exchange, language practice, and mutual learning. Your main role will be to help with our daily Sunset Cruise. You may welcome guests, check tickets, serve drinks, keep things tidy, and chat with travellers. We’ll also invite you to share some of the Lao traditions we present during the cruise, and we’ll teach you how. If you’re interested, you might also help take photos and videos, create social media content, do some graphic design or website updates, or help us write or translate marketing material. No special skills are needed — just a warm attitude and a willingness to learn. You’ll stay in our bamboo-style guesthouse, located just outside the city centre and surrounded by peaceful nature. Accommodation is in comfortable dorms with good mattresses, mosquito nets, fans, and shared bathrooms. The space is simple, clean, and cosy — a great place to unwind. This is a unique opportunity to immerse yourself in Lao culture. You’ll join in traditional activities on the boat, learn a f

name: Hostel Exchange near Snow Monkey Park & Ski Resort (Japan)
description: Hello! I'm Anna, and I own a hostel in the historic town of Shibu Onsen. We're currently looking for people to join our team. Located just over an hour from Nagano City, we're the nearest hostel to the famous Jigokudani Snow Monkey Park and close to the incredible Shiga Kogen Ski fields. Our town is also renowned for Ryokan Kanaguya, which inspired the bathhouse in the anime movie Spirited Away. We offer volunteers free accommodation in cosy Ryokan-style tatami/futon rooms (sometimes shared), three meals a day, free WiFi, a ski pass, access to our in-house onsen, and the opportunity to visit the nine local onsens. In exchange, we require 5 hours of work, 5 days a week. Helpers prepare their own breakfast with provided ingredients, share a communal lunch, and assist with preparing the evening meal. Tasks may include room cleaning, maintenance, customer service, some web/computer work, and cooking. We invite you to join us in Shibu Onsen! **** Update from the HelpStay team regarding this hostel exchange: We have received mixed reports about this hostel and have considered removing the listing altogether. Therefore, please proceed with caution as it may not meet your expectations. If you search for 'Nozaru Hostel' on Google, you will also find mixed reviews from guests.****

name: Volunteer at Vegan Sanctuary Foundation (Spain)
description: 💚 Vegan Sanctuary Foundation was founded in 2011 by Laura Luengo with the mission of saving the lives of animals rejected by society who, under other circumstances, would have had no chance of survival. Through 14 years of dedicated work and public outreach, the foundation has helped transform society’s relationship with animals and created a true sanctuary where they can live free and happy lives. 🐮 Life at Vegan Sanctuary is centred around a cruelty-free lifestyle and anti-speciesist education. Through our volunteer programme, you can immerse yourself in a community where animals rescued from the livestock industry live alongside people who share a commitment to ending animal exploitation. 🐽 As a volunteer, you will learn about the true emotional lives of farmed animals living free from exploitation. At the same time, you will share daily life with people from different backgrounds and cultures who are united by their ethical commitment, living together and preparing 100% plant-based meals without using any animal-derived products. 🐐 Within our team, we place special importance on activities that foster solidarity, empathy, and commitment through an educational and meaningful experience. We are especially grateful for volunteers who genuinely wish to dedicate their time to helping the animals and supporting the sanctuary, rather than seeking a tourist experience or simply free accommodation while visiting Madrid. The sanctuary follows a comprehensive safety and well-being plan designed to protect and support volunteers through prevention, training, supervision, and effective communication. ❤️‍🩹 For this reason, the volunteer programme revolves around caring for the animals and raising awareness of their needs and dignity as sentient beings. Tasks focus on restoring their well-being and respecting their right to live their own lives. Responsibilities include feeding, hygiene and health care, ensuring their comfort according to their individual needs and limitations

name: English Camp – Volunteer Opportunity (Russia)
description: We are looking for a volunteer to join our English camp, which will take place from 1 to 7 August 2026, approximately 100 km from Moscow. We are seeking a native English speaker who can participate in camp activities with our students (children). This is a great opportunity to experience Russian culture and spend time in the Moscow region. We will provide: 🔹Transport within Russia 🔹Accommodation and meals at the camp Thank you for your attention. We look forward to hearing from you!

name: Teach, Travel & Enjoy a HomeStay in Hanoi (Vietnam)

location.city: Travel and Enjoy a HomeStay in Hanoi
location.address: Teach, Travel and Enjoy a HomeStay in Hanoi, South East Asia

description: A very warm greeting from Hanoi, the capital of Vietnam! If you plan to visit Vietnam, we would like to invite you to take part in our working holiday program in Hanoi and Northern Vietnam. Here you will gain an authentic experience of the local way of life, by making real connections with a lot of friendly and COOL people in our coffee shop, by marketing work and instructing work to our lovely customers, and on top of that, you will be enjoying a variety of entertaining activities with us. We are a dynamic and passionate social enterprise in Hanoi who are running the working holiday program to - Provide the well-friendly and cozy coffee shop where the young customer can enjoy the nice drinks and take part in many meaningful and interesting activities. - Instruct for English speaking club in our coffee shop. - Help the travelers in Hanoi in urgent and difficult situations those who are subject to scams, lost luggage or those who arrive late at night and have problems getting from A to B. We warmly welcome people who are caring and possess a strong willingness to take part in our program. Here you can live a different life that you desire, work and travel together to discover the well-rounded beauty of local culture and landscape in Hanoi. Join our team to seize the chance to be part of something real, to be useful to others and to engage in a unique adventure where tons of amusement is destined to occur!

name: Help Renovate, Learn New Skills and Have Fun (France)

location.city: Learn New Skills and Have Fun
location.address: Help Renovate, Learn New Skills and Have Fun

description: In the beautiful, relaxed surroundings of the Limousin countryside, we are bringing these lovely old stone buildings back to life, combining the best of traditional and modern building practices. We cover most aspects, offering a great way to learn new skills in a peaceful, fantastic location. We work on installing windows and doors, traditional lime pointing, creating new floors, stud work, building stone garden walls, fencing, constructing wooden garden structures, wood cutting, and much more…

name: Restoration and Cultural Immersion in Sardinia's Hidden Gem (Italy)
description: Hello, I'm Valeria, and I have recently purchased an old house on the western coast of Sardinia, Italy. I am in the process of restoring it, together with my partner, Alex. We are looking for helpers who would be interested in helping us during the renovation, both with the house and the garden. We also have two plots of land that can be cultivated or used for various purposes. All of this while staying in and exploring one of the most beautiful and unspoiled areas on the island. Our house is located in a small village, just 10 minutes away from a larger town with direct connections to the airport and major cities on the island. It's situated right next to one of Europe's largest salt-water lagoons and a Marine Natural Area with 30 kilometers of unique white quartz beaches, along with archaeological sites of international significance. The house has three double bedrooms (plus a mezzanine) and some outbuildings, providing plenty of space to host volunteers. You would be staying with us, an international couple who has traveled around the world. Alex and I have traveled extensively and speak many languages, but we are Italian, with Alex being raised in Sardinia. This means that our guests can enjoy the best of both worlds: open-mindedness and local knowledge. Sardinia is a fantastic island, ideal for trekking, snorkeling, surfing, and kitesurfing all year round, as well as birdwatching, learning local recipes, and living on an island recognized as one of the very few blue zones in the world. We'll be working on all areas of the house, from plumbing to electrics, roofing, flooring, and painting and decorating. In the garden, there is a need for redesign with local plants and stonework. Our plan also includes installing a small pool with a stone deck area. As for accommodation, we offer double or twin bedrooms inside the house with a shared bathroom. We suggest preparing and enjoying meals together, but the kitchen is available to anyone who prefers to cook their own m

name: Help With Cleaning at Our Spooky, Witch-Themed Guest House (England)

location.address: Help With Cleaning at Our Spooky, Witch-Themed Guest House
location.country: Help With Cleaning at Our Spooky

description: We are looking for volunteers to help out at our spooky, witch-themed guest house 🧹🪄. If you’re a Harry Potter fan, this could be perfect for you! In exchange for up to 25 hours of work per week, we provide food, accommodation, and a cultural exchange experience. We currently have a total of three helpers. We are located in York, one of England’s most beautiful and historic cities. There are plenty of things to do here to keep you entertained for months. It’s also an excellent base for visiting other destinations around England and Scotland on your days off.

name: Surfside Retreat: Eco-friendly Hostel Seeks On-site Volunteers (Mexico)
description: We're seeking an on-site volunteer to assist with daily tasks such as managing reservations, providing guest guidance, addressing inquiries, and promoting the hostel. In exchange, you'll enjoy complimentary accommodation in our hostel, located in a surfing paradise. Located approximately 30 km north of Ixtapa and Zihuatanejo on Mexico's picturesque Pacific coast, lies a collection of beautiful surf spots known as ondas hermosas. Nestled in this area, our retreat offers a serene escape from the tourist rush, just steps away from the sandy shores. Much like the surroundings, our hostel embodies a relaxed, natural ambiance while providing modern amenities and comprehensive comfort. We're not your average hostel. Crafted to harmonize with nature, our hostel boasts tropical landscaping and breathtaking views. The property is completely private, enclosed within grounds that incorporate natural energy features supporting the environment. Utilizing greywater systems and cutting-edge composting toilets, we prioritize sustainability. We're on the lookout for on-site volunteers for various positions: 1. Receptionist/Host: Assist with daily operations, including managing reservations, guiding guests, responding to inquiries, and promoting the hostel. 2. Bio-construction: Help construct rooms using bio-construction techniques, which involves physical challenges. In return for your contribution, we offer complimentary accommodation in a four-person dormitory and a complimentary dinner at a fantastic hidden beach. If you're looking to live by the beach while working, you've come to the perfect place.

name: Garden & Home Help by the Beach in Los Caños de Meca (Spain)

location.address: Spain, Los Caños de Meca, Cádiz

description: We are a couple with a little baby, living in a house with a garden, just a five-minute walk from the beach. We need some help with the house and garden. We are an easy-going couple with a lovely house in a beautiful setting, five minutes’ walk from the beach and the countryside.

name: Help Renovate a Farmhouse in the Mountains (Italy)
description: Hello and welcome! The main project involves the renovation of a splendid mountain house, complete with a mill for grinding chestnuts. The property is deeply immersed in the greenery of Tuscany (Garfagnana) and surrounded by over 300 chestnut trees. There are natural waterfalls of 5 and 20 metres just a 15-minute walk away, as well as many fantastic walking trails. At the moment, I have around 50 free-range chickens and several friendly German Shepherds who help me look after them. There’s also a pond with trout that I’ve restored. I keep a vegetable garden and gather many wild herbs, berries, nuts, and mushrooms to preserve. Even in winter, I can provide delicious, locally sourced food — and if you’d like, I can teach you how to cook using traditional Italian methods. Our drinking water comes from a natural spring, which I also use to make my own soap. The farm is crossed by a river, and in the future, I hope to create hydroelectric power from it. At present, I’m focusing on renovating the bathroom, though there is already a toilet, shower, and bidet with hot water heated by a wood-fired cooker. Please note that my place is quite isolated in the mountains, making it perfect for nature lovers. If you’re not arriving by car, you can take a bus or train from Lucca to Coreglia, and I’ll be happy to pick you up from there. Thanks in advance, and see you soon! — Matteo

name: Help at a Ski Resort in Nagano (Japan)

location.address: Help, Work & Enjoy Life in Yudanaka

description: We are based near Yudanaka Station in Yudanaka, Nagano. In winter, this area offers some of the best skiing in Asia, while in the summer off-season there are plenty of activities such as hiking, local events, and fishing.
