Our company living spaces require ongoing updates during the off-season, including painting, cleaning, building projects such as storage units and ski racks, washing of floors, windows, sheets and blankets, and general repairs including plumbing and electrical work.

name: Help with Renovations in Exchange for a Farmhouse Stay (Switzerland)
description: We live in a beautiful part of Switzerland with plenty to see and do in the local area. We have bicycles available for getting around, or you can use public transport. Swiss public transport is excellent, although not cheap! If we have activities planned, you are welcome to join us. We are also happy to help you plan your travels and suggest places to visit. Our project mainly involves property maintenance, although from time to time there may be building, carpentry or masonry work as well. Tasks can include tree removal, digging in the garden, planting trees, clearing paddocks and other hands-on outdoor work. On hot days, take a dip in our Brunnen to cool off, or enjoy a sunset walk up the hill behind the house. Our normal schedule is four days of work and three days off. We ask for 28 hours per week, and your days off may fall during the week rather than at weekends. This depends on when you arrive and, most importantly, what projects are underway at the time. We usually meet 10 minutes before work starts for a briefing. There is a one-hour lunch break plus an additional 30-minute break, both outside your working hours. The usual start time is 7:50 am. On warm days we often go for a swim after work, so bring your swimwear. If it's especially hot, we may start earlier so we can finish and swim sooner! We provide food and accommodation. Accommodation may be in bunk beds in the art room, a bedroom in the old apartment, or a room in the house. If you prefer, you can also sleep in our cinema barn. You will join us for some family dinners, although not every day. We provide ingredients for a variety of meals, which you prepare yourself. Regarding dietary requirements, we do not cater for vegan diets, and we eat meat, dairy and pasta. If you have specific dietary needs, please discuss them with us before applying. Many of our meals are vegetarian, but not all. The cinema barn is the main hangout space, with sofas, tables and room to relax. There is an outdoor kitchen, an

name: Teach English to Children While Living with a Local Family (Indonesia)
description: You will be teaching children aged 5–14, either in private lessons or one-to-one sessions, with around four classes per day. I will provide a laptop to support your teaching. Volunteers should be kid-friendly, creative, and patient, as some students may be shy at the beginning. Fluency in English is required to ensure correct pronunciation. During classes, please wear appropriate clothing: a shirt or T-shirt is fine, but tank tops are not allowed. Trousers or skirts should be worn; hot pants are not permitted. This dress code applies only during teaching hours — your daily clothing is otherwise up to you. In exchange, you will be provided with a private room free of charge and three meals per day. We can also go out, and I am happy to drive you around and show you my city. The accommodation is simple: there is no shower, and the toilet is a squat toilet with a water tank for bathing. There is no luxury, but I do my best to make volunteers feel comfortable here. This place is for volunteers who want to experience life like a local.

name: Help with Landscaping & Building (Ireland)
description: The work is undertaken in between periods of peak demand in our business. This project combines driving and renovation work. You need a full clean driving licence, class B, and a good level of English plus building or mechanical skills and you need to be able bodied and hardworking. We can only accept applicants who are citizens of EU/ EEA countries or in the case of non EEA citizens those who have obtained a study visa which enables them to work part time. There is a very wide range of tasks to be done. As well as production, work activities at the house and farmyard include cleaning and maintenance of the house and outbuildings, grounds, vehicles and equipment. We also have a special project for an experienced and enthusiastic HGV mechanic. It is the restoration of a classic truck. Handyman/ building or mechanical skills plus a full clean class B driving-licence would be the ideal skill set we are looking for. We have current openings for men with this skill set.

name: Community & Cultural Exchange in Livingstonia (Malawi)
description: Livingstonia Community Development Forum (LICODEF) is a community-based organisation located in Livingstonia, Rumphi District, Malawi. It was established with the aim of improving the lives of vulnerable people through community development initiatives focused on education, youth empowerment, environmental conservation, cultural exchange, and social welfare. LICODEF works closely with local communities, schools, youth groups, women, and traditional leaders to create sustainable solutions to the challenges affecting rural areas. Our organisation believes that development starts within the community itself. Therefore, we encourage participation, unity, and cooperation among community members and international volunteers. Through our activities, we promote self-reliance, creativity, skills development, and equal opportunities for all, regardless of background. LICODEF is involved in a range of community projects, including supporting schools, teaching and mentoring students, organising youth activities, environmental clean-up campaigns, tree planting, cultural exchange programmes, sports activities, community sensitisation, assisting vulnerable children and elderly people, and promoting tourism in Livingstonia and surrounding areas. We also work to preserve local culture and encourage mutual understanding between local people and international visitors. Volunteers who join LICODEF become part of the community and experience authentic village life in Malawi. They work alongside local people while sharing knowledge, experiences, and ideas that help create positive change. Volunteers may participate in teaching English, assisting in schools, organising educational activities, supporting youth and children’s programmes, environmental projects, photography, social media promotion, farming activities, and other initiatives depending on their skills and interests. What makes LICODEF special is the warm hospitality and rich cultural experience offered to volunteers. Livingston

name: Come and Experience a Lifestyle Close to Nature (Italy)
description: Hello there, I'm looking for travellers, nature lovers, people interested in permaculture and a different way of living. I'm Erik, class of ’88. I live in northern Italy, where I’ve been building a permaculture project for the last few years. I’ve always been a dreamer, a wanderer seeking adventure and a deeper connection with Nature. After a few years of working and travelling around the world, I decided to begin a different kind of journey here, about an hour and a half’s drive from my birthplace. As a sensitive person, healing has been a big part of what inspired this life choice, as I find it a gentler and more sustainable way of living — both for ourselves and for the environment. On a more practical note, the land is currently managed for self-sufficiency rather than as a business.

name: Join our hostel team as a volunteer in Swansea (Wales)
description: We are the only 5* hostel in Wales located in the very heart of Swansea - vibrant city surrounded by breathtaking nature. We are currently looking for: Volunteer Role Housekeeping & Reception Cwtsh Hostel – Castle Street, Swansea Hours: 18 hours per week In return: Free accommodation in shared dorm Trial Period: All volunteers start with a 7-day trial period to make sure it’s a good fit for both sides. Minimum stay (after trial): 1 month preferred. Duties include: • Bed making & pod cleaning • Bathroom cleaning (9 bathrooms) • Kitchen cleaning & checks • Laundry & linen organisation • Guest check-ins/check-outs • Maintaining high cleanliness standards We’re looking for someone who is: • Reliable and punctual • Proactive and hands-on • Comfortable working independently • Friendly but professional with guests

name: Language Stay with Woodwork and Home Improvement (England)
description: We live in a three-bedroom house in a well-connected part of North Bristol (UK), with direct buses to Bristol city centre just six minutes from the nearest bus stop. Ideally, we would welcome one person, but we are flexible if you would like to come with a friend or partner (please note: no under-18s). We moved into our home over two years ago and have almost finished restoring and renovating it. Over the past two summers, we hosted international students, and we greatly enjoyed the exchange as well as helping them improve their English and discover British culture. Previously, they paid for their stay, but this time we would like to try a different approach: you can stay for free, with full board, in exchange for helping us cover the extra costs (food, electricity, gas, etc.). We are looking for a dynamic young person—or people—to help us finish the house renovation. This will mainly involve woodwork (I make most of my furniture from reclaimed wood; see Facebook: "Sylvain's Reclaimed Wood Crafts") and possibly some gardening. You don’t need prior experience, but a willingness to learn and some common sense are essential. we welcome travellers as well as students needing internship, We have a single room and a double room available, both with comfortable beds. You will have access to all communal areas and facilities in our house: fully equipped kitchen, bathroom, washing machine, dishwasher, garden, broadband, TV, etc. We would consider short stays of a minimum of two weeks and up to four weeks, depending on your visa/right to stay, but we are flexible if needed. We would be looking for around 20 hours of help per week with woodwork and gardening (we are flexible about how this time is structured) and sharing household tasks. We provide bedding, towels, and a hairdryer. You only need to bring your clothes, including a set you don’t mind getting dirty during DIY tasks. We are keen cooks, and if you want to learn how to prepare British and French food, we would be de

name: Help in an Art Gallery in Francavilla d'Ete (Italy)
description: The 'Palazzo Ruudt Wackers' Art Centre is located approximately 50 kilometres (31 miles) south of Ancona in a beautiful historic area of Italy. Immerse yourself in an inspiring atmosphere filled with art, history, and knowledge in the heart of Italy. We are primarily looking for volunteers to assist with various small tasks in our studio and gallery at the Palazzo, such as preparing canvases and materials for sculpting, gallery exhibitions, conferences, and masterclasses. Additional tasks may include cooking and helping with a variety of activities. We seek volunteers who are willing to try new things, adapt quickly, and take the initiative. Whether you want to learn to draw or paint, pose in the studio, or simply cook—whatever your heart desires—you’ll have time to relax, learn, and admire the beautiful surroundings of the Palazzo. Don't forget to enjoy the stunning sunshine, landscapes, and historic Italian cities nestled in the hills, along with the warm Italian summers! This opportunity is ideally suited for those with a keen interest in the arts. Notes: We request a small contribution of €20 per week per person for house cleaning.

name: Help with Our Winery and Enjoy Yoga (Germany)
description: We are looking for helping hands during harvest, from the end of August to mid-October. We live in the heart of the Rheingau, in Oestrich-Winkel. The Rheingau is about 20 minutes from Wiesbaden/Mainz and 45 minutes from Frankfurt. We have a large house with shared rooms where four of our friends and, hopefully, you can live. There’s also a spacious courtyard and garden where we grow vegetables, and our chickens enjoy their lives. We definitely want to introduce you to organic and biodynamic wine-growing. We’ll show you the necessary vineyard work for the season, or if you come in the fall, we’ll pick grapes together! We want to expand your wine knowledge, share tastings with you, and get you excited about this amazing product. We also want to involve you in projects here on the farm, such as building a new raised bed, designing the garden and planting vegetables, caring for the chickens, renovating things around the farm, and more. Additionally, we’d love to give you the chance to explore our second passion: yoga. We offer yoga classes every evening from Monday to Friday, which you are welcome to join free of charge. Depending on the time of year, mornings are usually spent in the vineyards. Seasonal tasks might include pruning, tying, training, or harvesting. If there’s nothing to do outside, we continue with farm work. In the evenings, if you wish, there’s the opportunity to practise yoga or meditation. Nik and I love yoga and meditation alongside our passion for wine. Another big hobby of ours is climbing and bouldering — we even converted our attic into a bouldering hall last year. We’d be thrilled if you share our interests and, of course, have an enthusiasm for wine tasting. We also enjoy party evenings with our friends and acquaintances here on the farm and would be happy if you wanted to be part of that social life too.

name: Help Out at a Family B&B in the Yorkshire Dales (England)
description: Lookking for help February onwards. We have been welcoming helpers from all over the World for over 14 years and really enjoy been hosts. We love to learn and exchange ideas with helpers.  Help with English practice through daily conversations.  Plenty of free time to hike or  visit many beautiful places in the North of England and Yorkshire Dales. Helpers welcomed from all corners of the World the more unusual the better.  Please tell us your special qualities. We look for energetic, positive, intelligent and enthusiastic  people. No experience needed simple daily tasks around the b&b which are easy to pick up. Big smiles  and big hearts a must. We run a small, family-owned B&B in the beautiful Yorkshire Dales and are looking for a bit of help during busy periods. In exchange, we offer a private room with a lock, all meals, and local lifts. Our B&B has been welcoming guests from around the world since 1973. We have four en-suite rooms, each equipped with smart TVs and tea and coffee-making facilities. Located just two minutes from the Dales Way Path and right on the Way of the Roses cycle route, we offer a quiet setting with off-road parking. Dogs are welcome by prior arrangement.

name: Tourist & Artistic Assistance Volunteer Opportunity (Italy)
description: I am looking for a volunteer to help with tourist and artistic assistance (Assistenza Turistica ed Artistica) at Palazzo Vecchio in Vibonati (SA), Italy. Palazzo Vecchio is managed by the eclectic Fabrizio Vecchio, a geologist and master vintner who oversees the building’s cellars. The furniture and rooms are original and date back to 1914. The property has three bedrooms and a shared bathroom. The guest kitchen was built from scratch in one of the upper rooms and is shared by guests. In addition to staying at the palazzo, visitors can taste wines from the winery and explore the exhibition halls, where works by artists from Bellamostra are displayed. Since 2007, Palazzo Vecchio has hosted a series of solo and group exhibitions featuring national and international artists. From the terrace of the building, guests can enjoy panoramic views of the ancient village of Vibonati, perched around the bell tower that rises above the basilica. The terrace can be used for dining, relaxing, taking a nap in the cool natural cellar, and even hosting exhibitions for artists in the exhibition rooms on the first floor. On arrival, guests are welcomed into the cellar for an aperitif featuring the house wine — a natural wine made without added sulphites.

name: Life-Sharing Alongside People with Special Needs (Ireland)
description: Mountshannon Camphill Community is located in the quaint village of Mountshannon, on the shores of Lough Derg, Co. Clare. We are a small community working alongside people with special needs. We are always looking for volunteers to join us and help with everyday tasks such as workshops, outings, cooking, personal hygiene, and general housekeeping. Currently, we run a loom weaving workshop and are developing a basket-making workshop. We also offer seasonal workshops such as candle making, and we maintain a small garden and polytunnel. Your unique human qualities are more important than academic qualifications, although there are plenty of opportunities for those who bring both. Previous experience is not required — instead, we value a willingness to contribute wherever needed and an enthusiasm to learn. Training is provided through hands-on experience, with greater responsibility given as individuals develop their support skills. Adults of all ages and backgrounds are always welcome to apply to become part of our small community. After a successful application process, volunteers typically live, work, and share their lives in the Camphill Community Mountshannon for one year.

name: Tipi & Tent Camp Ground Helping in Peru

location.address: Tipi and Tent Campground Helping in Peru, South America
location.country: Tipi and Tent Campground Helping in Peru

description: YakuRumi is a 'Tipi and Tent Campground' on the edge of Huascarán National Park in the Cordillera Blanca of central Peru. We're located beside the Llullán river just outside of Caraz in a small community called Shocsha, along the road to Laguna Paron. YakuRumi is a great place to volunteer if your looking for community and love the mountains. A Prefect Place to Start Your Trip to the Cordillera Blanca... Wake up to a spectacular view of the Putaca Glacier, Sphinx and the Huandoys. Ideally located along the banks of the Yuyan River at the foot of the Huandoys, YakuRumi is only minutes from the entrance to Llanganuco, Paron and Santa Cruz valleys, making it a perfect place to launch your trip to some of Cordillera Blanca’s most sought after Outdoor Adventures. We offer our volunteers plenty of free info, maps and help with transportation, gear rental, and guided trekking and climbing trips.

name: Help on a Small Farm While Enjoying the Outdoors (Serbia)
description: There is plenty of agricultural work to be done, but don’t worry—it’s nothing intimidating! We engage in these activities for the satisfaction they bring. We grow our own vegetables and fruits, and garden tasks include planting, weeding, mulching, and watering. During the summer, we have a lot of work related to mowing and storing hay. There are also tasks involving the maintenance of buildings and fences. In the forest, we cut trees, clear undergrowth, and plant new ones. We gather fruits to make preserves, as well as wine and brandy. Additionally, we have a vineyard that requires regular care. Our farm is home to bees, chickens, pigs, sheep, cats, and dogs. We source our water from a well, and occasionally, we need to clean the barn or chicken coop. We would greatly appreciate assistance with housekeeping and preparing communal meals. If you have skills in construction or electrical work, you would be more than welcome here! --------------------------- Има  доста пољопривреде ( али не бојте се, није ништа страшно - све нам ово треба причињавати задовољство) . Поврће и воће се производимо  сами. Рад у башти обухвата,: садњу, плевљење, малчирање, окопавање.Преко лета имамо доста посла са кошењем и складиштењем сена. Постоје послови на одржавању зграда и ограда. У шуми сечемо дрва расчишћује4мо шикару и садуимо нова стабла. Скупљампо воће и правимо прерађевине , или правимо вино и ракију, имамо и виноград  који се окопава. Имамо: пчеле, кокошке, свиње, овце, мачке и псе. Вода долази из бунара. Понекад морамо да чистимо шталу или кокошињац. Добро би нам дошла помоћ и у спремању куће и заједничких оброка. Ако имате вештине у вези изградње и електруификације овде сте добро дошли.

name: Help at Our Campsite with Different Tasks (Albania)
description: Hello from Alessandra and Daniele, two Italians who moved to Albania in 2022 with the dream of opening a campsite and living close to animals and nature. Our place is located in Shënkoll, Lezhë, Albania, and we have four dogs, four cats, 30 chickens, 10 ducks, and three peacocks. We opened the campsite two years ago and, as we are still at the beginning, there’s a lot to do: gardening, cleaning, animal care, building, maintenance, DIY, and more. Enjoy nature at our campsite while helping us with a variety of tasks. Depending on the season, work may include cutting grass, planting trees, collecting fruit, or building new structures in wood, iron, or brick. The work can be hard in winter (cold) and in summer (hot), but it’s always rewarding. We also need help caring for the animals. We prefer helpers to stay for at least three weeks. We love living close to nature, but life on the farm can feel a bit like stepping back in time — simple and peaceful. Our place is a great starting point for exploring Albania: we’re between Shkodër and Tirana (connected by public bus) and just 6 km from the seaside. Our guests from around the world love watching the sunrise behind the mountains and the sunset through the trees. We need help 5 hours per day, 5 days a week, plus shared time for normal daily activities such as cooking. Days off in summer may differ from the weekend. Helpers will be accommodated in a private caravan with shared toilet and shower facilities — just like real camping life. We’ll eat and prepare meals together.

name: Volunteer with Children with Disabilities (Ghana)
description: We run a charity home and school for all categories of children. Currently we need volunteers who have passion for children of different disabilities to help them at home, teach them and make their life better. The ages of children ranges from 8 years to 15 years. The are is no qualifications needed but having specialty in the field is helpful. Other Projects: Teaching Projects: Many of the schools in Africa are understaffed and over-crowded; however, the students are keen and enthusiastic. Eduaid International therefore welcomes any assistance from volunteers to teach and / or support local teachers in subjects such as English, French, Computer Science, Mathematics or Science, from per-school, primary, junior high or senior high school. Your ideas, your energy and your presence are exciting and motivating for students as well as for teachers and parents. The students are full of curiosity about the world around them. As well as teaching, you can get involved in creative projects and non-classroom based activities, which help create a stimulating learning environment. Building and Construction Projects: We have number of construction projects to help support communities and allow them to develop a basic educational infrastructure. Volunteers will be working with locals to complete projects and so will be fully immersed in the culture and community. You may have previously participated in building work, but the projects in Africa can be quite different with respect to the resources and tools available; it is therefore a different type of experience but an exciting and fun one. Train a teacher Project: If you have a skills in education or can plan  teacher planning projects in any subjects like English,Mathematics,Science and Creative Art,we welcome you to help in our programs in Ghana and other African country we operate.

name: Help with Hotel and Outdoor Activities in Jokkmokk (Sweden)
description: We run an eco-friendly outdoor lodge, welcoming guests from around the world. We cherish our pristine nature, which is among the last remaining in Europe, right here in Jokkmokk. This is a small family-run business, and in our spare time, we strive to be in nature and embark on adventures as much as possible. If you're dreaming of experiencing the Northern Lights in the best possible way, staying here between August and May gives you the greatest chance!

name: Volunteer in a Reconstructed Viking Village Experience (Sweden)
description: From May to August, we welcome volunteers who wish to join our small community and learn more about Viking culture and history. Over the years, we have happily shared our knowledge, our rich local history and a variety of traditional skills with people from all around the world. As a volunteer, you may be introduced to the art of weaving or blacksmithing, or learn how to repair the walls of a Viking Age house using historical techniques such as clay and straw. You may also learn archery and demonstrate it to our visitors, or bake historical bread over the campfire in the reconstructed village together with children. Due to the cultural richness of the site, we welcome people from many different countries, making our community multilingual. You may have the opportunity to learn Swedish, improve your English or even practise some French. If you wish to immerse yourself in history, culture and traditional Viking Age crafts, this is the perfect place for you. Volunteers work approximately 5 to 5.5 hours per day, five days a week, usually from 10:00 to 16:00 with a lunch break included. Working hours may vary slightly depending on the season and any special events taking place at the site. You will also have two days off each week. Accommodation is provided in our summer cabin, which includes several bedrooms, two bathrooms and a large shared kitchen. Volunteers usually share a room with another volunteer, and we generally host around four volunteers at a time. Food is also provided, including three meals a day. We even provide Viking clothing for your stay.

name: Volunteer at a Horse Ranch in Antalya (Turkey)
description: Volunteers can enjoy a variety of activities, including riding horses, exploring forest trails, visiting waterfalls, swimming, and going to nearby beaches. In our free time, we guide volunteers, have fun together, and explore the area. Situated in a popular tourist region, our ranch attracts visitors from all over the world, and as locals, we never tire of the stunning scenery and beautiful beaches.

name: Under Study & Experience an Artistic Life (United States)

location.address: United States, Vallejo, Bay area, San Francisco

description: Process original artwork, catalog, curate the collection! I offer experience in all phases of producing original art for a collectors market: Inspiration, creation, interacting with the audience. I am trained in data science but my artwork is very traditional analogue technique. There is a wide range of skills you might offer: organizing the studio and holding life drawing sessions, curating the work, coding smart contracts for a print auction site, managing the CRM and running campaigns, helping at festivals, selling, modelling for drawings. There really are a LOT of ways to fit in. What I require is professionalism, a sense of urgency, and someone who allows the fun to derive from the WORK. We are close to San Francisco, just a ferry ride away. Many of my performances are there. We are geographically poised to get away from the Bay Area quickly. This means day trips to Yosemite and Tahoe, or better, overnight camping.

name: A Cultural Exchange with a Senior Lady (Malaysia)
description: We are looking to welcome a warm, mindful, and independent traveller into our home to share life, stories, and daily routines with a vibrant senior lady. She is independent and self-sufficient with her personal care, but she believes life is simply richer when shared with others. This is more than just a place to stay; it is an opportunity for a genuine cultural exchange. Whether you would like to practise a new language, share stories from your travels, or learn more about our local way of life, you will be welcomed as part of the household. If you are a calm, mature, and empathetic person who enjoys a slower pace and meaningful human connection, you will fit in well here. We would love to share around 4–5 hours of our day with you, 5 days a week, leaving you plenty of free time to explore the area, relax, or enjoy your own interests. Your help will naturally become part of her daily routine. We enjoy preparing good food and sharing meals together. You can help with simple, wholesome cooking, and we would love for you to introduce us to a favourite dish from your home country while we share some of our own local recipes with you. She enjoys staying active, so we would appreciate a companion for gentle morning or evening walks, light stretching, and spending peaceful time outdoors in nature. You can also join her for trips to local shops, markets, and places to buy fresh ingredients. It is a lovely way to experience the local pace of life, choose food together, and enjoy a coffee or conversation along the way. We also enjoy simple shared activities such as listening to music, watching films or documentaries, playing board games or cards, and having meaningful conversations over a cup of tea. We are looking for someone with a mature mindset, a warm heart, and a respectful, tidy approach to shared living. We value open communication, kindness, mutual respect, and allowing each other personal space. In return, we offer a peaceful and private bedroom, daily ingredients

name: Rajasthan Retreat: Volunteer for a Unique Village Experience (India)
description: My name is Gajendra, and I am a travel guide. I work with Tours by Locals and also serve as a tourist guide for the US Embassy. I own a small heritage home and hotel in Fort Dhamli, Pali, Rajasthan, India, with a PIN code of 306501. This is a cosy hotel with 11 rooms where European tourists typically stay for 1 or 2 nights to experience village life. Volunteers can assist with bed making, gardening, and basic cleaning while learning Indian cooking for free. They can also share their knowledge from their home countries, making this place a welcoming hub for travellers. The comfort of solo or couple guests is a priority, and our project is located in a rural village, away from the city. However, we have good train connections, and Jodhpur Airport is just a 1.5-hour drive away. Our village has a hospital, a Village Government Administrator Office, and a population of around 2500 people. It's a small agricultural village where shepherds, cow and cattle herders, bird watching for bird lovers is a paradise and farmers live here mostly. The village has two schools, including a girls' school up to the age of 12, after which students study together in high school. Wifi and mobile services are well-established here. People in this part of North India understand basic English and Hindi. My father was an Italian and Spanish guide who spent most of his life in Europe. He started this project in Rajasthan, India. Rajasthan is a must-visit destination for tourists in India, and our place offers a home away from home. In the village, you'll see camels and horses in the vicinity. The weather is sunny from September to the end of March, with temperatures ranging from around 15 degrees at night to 35 degrees during the day. We provide hot and cold water, and fans make the rooms comfortable. Tourists have been visiting us since the 1980s. The presence of organic vegetables and dairy products adds to the delight of staying here. Lastly, there are plenty of things to explore on weekends

name: Looking for Help with My B&B and Photography Tours (Canada)
description: I’m looking for one or two mature, responsible helpers, or a couple, for a long-term stay on northern Vancouver Island, ideally from May to October, although a longer stay would be even better. My B&B is currently closed, so this opportunity is not about guest check-ins or cleaning suites. The focus is instead on farm and property projects, helping with my tours, and providing a reliable presence while I’m busy. I run small, private wildlife photography tours during the season and am often out for many hours. The work itself is not extreme, but I value someone who is steady, independent, calm, and reliable. Most of the help needed is farm-related and includes gardening and food production such as planting, weeding, watering, and harvesting. There may also be opportunities to help with soil building, composting, and worm castings, depending on your interests, as well as general property upkeep, small projects, and occasional help related to my boat or tours. In return, I offer free accommodation and all meals. You are also welcome to join my tours when there is space and it fits the day. There is plenty of free time for anyone who enjoys nature, quiet, and independence. This place is best suited to someone who prefers peace, nature, and a slower rhythm, who is mature, reliable, and comfortable spending time on their own. It is not a good fit if you need nightlife, crowds, or constant social activity. I’m looking for people who value a meaningful, longer-term stay rather than short visits and quick turnover. I only accept longer-term stays, as I don’t want to change helpers mid-season. May to October is ideal.

name: Homestay

location.address: Bowen Mountain, New South Wales, Australia

description:
Hello....We have been involved with many share hosting organisations over the years. We look after some houses on a property in the foothills of the blue mountains, we grow a garden, do household maintenance, painting, cleaning, bush clearing/regeneration work. We are always happy, time permitting, to take guests out to see the Blue Mountains, maybe do a bush walk, motorbike ride, a canyon or some abseiling. We have Danish, German and Australian background and have travelled extensively. We supply accomodation and meals in exchange for 4 hrs per day helping with the upkeep and maintenance of this bushland property. Accommodation is either at Pete & Lorena's place or the BnB cabin if available, we live in different houses...We prefer Helpers to be over 25years old and be able to understand and speak English relatively well. Experience with yard machinery such as brush cutters and lawn mowers, any building, tiling etc are a plus. 
A train from Sydney to Richmond takes approx 1.5hr. We do prefer two people at a time, so send us a message and hopefully see you soon!. Thanks! p.s please make initial contact via HelpX messaging. 
Happy Travelling!
Cheers!
Pete, Lorena and Yane

name: Homestay
description:
I have only recently moved back to the Farm. I grew up on this farm but has been away for a number of years and things need doing to get it back how I like it.

I spend some time off farm  but are flexible about work hours and/or pay. Work will be varied depending on skills but could include gardening, housework, maintenance around the farm, working with sheep or beef cattle or driving tractors. Crops raised include Wheat, Peas, Ryegrass Seed & Vegetable Seed crops.The farm also runs some Sheep & Cattle. Our helpers almost always leave with new skills which often include operating Quad Bikes and Tractors. At certain times of the year I can find paid work for you.

I am easy going & well travelled, I like to enjoy life.I'm also a Couch Surfer host and like to meet people from elsewhere in the world. Interests include Mountain Biking, Fishing, Gardening, Self Sufficiency, Music, Travel, Camping & Hiking.

The farm is close to Ashburton (8km) which has all services. Rivers, Recreation Lake & the Beach are all near by. In the winter its less than an hours drive to Mt Hutt skifield.

Email me now to find out more.

name: Farmstay
description:
I can't see the world, but the world can come see me.
First and foremost, I want to thank all the helpers who came from United Kingdom-England, France, Belgium, Italy, China, Japan, Australia and New Zealand. 

Welcome Helpers. We are a family of 5 living  in the Appalachian Mountains of West Virginia. Sue is the founder of this place. Her daughter Ruth moved to the Hollow later as well as her husband Tim and their 2 daughters.  Our hollow is quite with no neighbors in sight. There are no city lights, and you can see every star on a clear night!  

Our homestead consists of Sue's workshop/cabin, Tim & Ruth's cabin and one very small guest cabin.  Also, there is a private guestroom with private entrance on the second floor above the workshop/cabin combination. .

We have a lot you can be involved in, knowledge to share, friendships to build, and opportunities for growth. Below are our  routines and some projects. We would love to involve you!

- Caring for our honey bees, chickens, cats, dogs, one registered pure american mulefoot pig, one crossbred pig   berkshire-tamworth with spots.. Sue is a fourth-generation beekeeper and has much knowledge to share.  

- General landscaping,. upkeep such as mowing, weed whacking and pulling weeds.

- Soil prep, start seeds, transplant, plant, weed, cultivate, and harvest fruits and vegetables. Assist with home-canning (preserving) of fruits and vegetables.  Care for flowers.

- Construction and home improvement projects.  Prepping & painting the cabins as well as various other painting & sealing projects.

- Helping prepare meals and housekeeping.  Ruth loves to cook. Sharing recipes from around the world is one of her favorite things. 

- Organizing and cleaning sheds

- Build bird houses with saved scrap lumber

- Clear a trail for hiking & hunting

- Assist with quilt making, sewing or anything related

We have an area for evening campfires where we sit, talk, roast hot dogs, eat s`mores, and star gaze.

Work is done accordi

name: Homestay
description:
Hello from the beautiful Brooklyn neighborhood of Park Slope!  I'm in an old, historic home in a gorgeous neighborhood, very safe, vibrant, dynamic but chill, very close to subways.  Surrounded by beautiful Prospect Park,  great restaurants, music, wonderful culture, museums, theater, fun !  There is a lot of great, low cost, or free stuff to do, especially in summer.  

I have a lovely home with a couple of rented rooms.  Occasionally I can host for a short stay, but usually, volunteers stay for a longer time at my home.   

I prefer 2 helpers (a couple, 2 friends) for help with things like :
*  House basic repairs, experience with "Handy-person" tasks (use of basic tools,  experience plumbing, fixing or building things, painting, carpentry, all very helpful).
*  There is usually outdoor work to do (including garden).   
*  Due to certain projects I seek helper/s with excellent speaking and reading English.
*  Sometimes detailed cleaning, "house organizing";  preparing for guests.  Volunteers must have schedule flexibility.
*  Possibly build a website and edit / re-design my house "Booklet".  Knowledge of technology/computers/ Windows 11 is great.  
*  Cooking is always appreciated but not part of your tasks here.

Please tell me your specific skills, abilities, work experience / background, volunteering experience or hobbies .  I would like to understand what you can do and how you can work independently  (whenever possible).  
What are your plans, dates, schedule - are you flexible?

I prefer a mature couple (or 2 people share one room),  with experience and references -- who can stay for a longer time.  New York is huge and amazing.  Most helpers stay 4 weeks - 6 months.  NYC and my house and projects will take time to learn ... so I ask for a longer time commitment so that you can do everything you want to -- while being available at my home, and also do projects independently.

If you work  PART TIME  that's no problem, but sometimes you must have daytime sche

name: Farmstay
description:
I live on an organic farm about 15km from Kerikeri. It's a short walk to Lake Manuwai which is great for swimming and kayaking. There are beautiful bushwalks close by, a canyon and the Kauri forest. 
I am developing the property by planting a native forest, a vinyard, fruit and vegetables. For around 4 hours help mostly outside (weather dependent) you get good food and accommodation with your own room and bathroom.
I love eating good food, yoga, fishing, swimming, travelling, music and good company.

name: Farmstay
description:
A Calm, Welcoming Place to Live, Learn & Contribute in the old Coast Hinterland. 

We have a vacancy for 1 one couple,  from the end of May, beginning of June.
Ideally with with a vehicle, there is no public transport  here. 

2 week minimum stay (longer stays warmly welcomed)

If you’re looking for a peaceful rural place where you’re treated as a valued part of the household — not just extra labour — you may feel very at home here.

We are a friendly farming couple in our 60s, and we’ve lived on this beautiful property for over 30 years. We’re about 35 minutes from the beach, on the ocean side of Mt Tamborine. We’ve been hosting helpers since 1998, and many travellers who stay with us become like family — while they’re here and long after they’ve gone. We genuinely enjoy hosting and are very grateful for the wonderful helpers we’ve met over the years.


🌱 What Life Is Like Here

Life on our farm is calm, grounded and collaborative. Days are varied, meals are shared, and there’s time to breathe. You’ll be living close to nature, surrounded by wildlife, gardens, horses and rainforest, with seasonal waterfalls and rock pools to relax in after the day’s work.

We also host a small Airbnb farmstay tiny home cabin, so you’ll be part of a lived-in, working environment where people come and go quietly and respectfully.


🛠️ What You’ll Help With

Projects and tasks change with the seasons and what’s happening at the time. You’ll always be working with us, not left on your own, and the pace is humane and reasonable, with a natural daily rhythm.

Tasks may include:
* Gardening and general property maintenance
* Animal care and feeding
* Housework and light cleaning
* Collecting and chopping firewood
* Weed control and tree planting in our protected Land for Wildlife areas. 

We also have some hands-on, creative projects happening at present, including:

* Setting up our apiary yard (we have 20 beehives, a new and exciting project)
* Fencing
* Finishing our stone gateway
* Re

name: Farmstay
description:
Only a three hundred meter farm walk away is the famous Miranda Holiday park with it’s many mineral hot pools, and cafe. Miranda is a unique unspoilt playground with abundant tourist opportunities and is renowned for its Godwit bird migration. We are located just 45 minutes from Auckland Airport and 20 minutes from the Coromandel Peninsula on your way to the Coromandel. We have 100m2 of vegetable garden and many other gardens around the property. Lots of animals and pet birds and life is very relaxed for us.

We have some gardening, cleaning and building type work if you are interested. We would be happy to host you from any Sunday through to Friday if you are available. Normally we would ask five hours work a day however we are happy with four hours work and help with making the evening meals and the kitchen cleaned up after meals. We will provide a friendly environment, nice meals and good conversations, a comfy bed and fast wifi. We ask that you do 4 hours work on the day you arrive as nobody really wants to work the day they are leaving. Do you have a preference of food? Let us know.

name: Homestay
description:
Hi folks, James and Rose here. We are looking for some assistance with simple landscaping, gardening, fencing, painting and household maintenance. We live on Whangarei outskirts in a nice area close to town. We would be looking to provide food and lodging in exchange for 4h/d assistance. 

We have a four year old daughter, a small border collie, ducks and soon chickens. 

We have kayaks, bikes, snorkelling and fishing equipment available for use anytime.

name: HUJ (Armenia)
description: HUJ ist eine Vollmitgliedsorganisation der Alliance of European Voluntary Service Organisations (gegründet 1965, Mitglied seit 2000). Vermittelt internationale Freiwilligeneinsätze und Workcamps.
